sanskrit_iast: manumekāgramāsīnamabhigamya maharṣayaḥ pratipūjya yathānyāyamidaṃ vacanamabruvan
meaning: मनुमेकाग्रमासीनमभिगम्य महर्षयः प्रतिपूज्य यथान्यायमिदं वचनमब्रुवन्
text: The Great Sages, having approached Manu, paid their respect to him in due form, and finding him seated with mind calm and collected, addressed him these words
iast: manumekāgramāsīnamabhigamya maharṣayaḥ pratipūjya yathānyāyamidaṃ vacanamabruvan

sanskrit_iast: bhagavan sarvavarṇānāṃ yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ antaraprabhavānāṃ ca dharmānno vaktumarhasi
meaning: भगवन् सर्ववर्णानां यथावदनुपूर्वशः अन्तरप्रभवानां च धर्मान्नो वक्तुमर्हसि
text: May Thou, O blessed One, explain to us, in due form and in proper order, the duties of all castes and intermediate castes!
iast: bhagavan sarvavarṇānāṃ yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ antaraprabhavānāṃ ca dharmānno vaktumarhasi

sanskrit_iast: tvameko hyasya sarvasya vidhānasya svayaṃbhuvaḥ acintyasyāprameyasya kāryatattvārthavit prabho
meaning: त्वमेको ह्यस्य सर्वस्य विधानस्य स्वयंभुवः अचिन्त्यस्याप्रमेयस्य कार्यतत्त्वार्थवित् प्रभो
text: Thou alone, O Lord, art conversant with what ought to be done, which forms the true import of this entire Veda—which is eternal, inconceivable and not directly cognisable.
iast: tvameko hyasya sarvasya vidhānasya svayaṃbhuvaḥ acintyasyāprameyasya kāryatattvārthavit prabho

sanskrit_iast: sa taiḥ pṛṣṭastathā samyagamitojā mahātmabhiḥ pratyuvācārcya tān sarvān maharṣīṃśrūyatāmiti
meaning: स तैः पृष्टस्तथा सम्यगमितोजा महात्मभिः प्रत्युवाचार्च्य तान् सर्वान् महर्षींश्रूयतामिति
text: Being thus questioned by the high-souled Great Sages, he, possessed op illimitable vigour, received them with reverence, and with proper courtesy answered them—‘listen.’
iast: sa taiḥ pṛṣṭastathā samyagamitojā mahātmabhiḥ pratyuvācārcya tān sarvān maharṣīṃśrūyatāmiti

sanskrit_iast: āsīdidaṃ tamobhūtamaprajñātamalakṣaṇam apratarkyamavijñeyaṃ prasuptamiva sarvataḥ
meaning: आसीदिदं तमोभूतमप्रज्ञातमलक्षणम् अप्रतर्क्यमविज्ञेयं प्रसुप्तमिव सर्वतः
text: This (World) was in existence in the form, as it were, of dense Darkness,—unperceived, undifferentiated, incogitable, (hence) incognizable; as it was wholly merged in deep sleep.
iast: āsīdidaṃ tamobhūtamaprajñātamalakṣaṇam apratarkyamavijñeyaṃ prasuptamiva sarvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tataḥ svayaṃbhūrbhagavānavyakto vyañjayannidam mahābhūtādi vṛttojāḥ prādurāsīt tamonudaḥ
meaning: ततः स्वयंभूर्भगवानव्यक्तो व्यञ्जयन्निदम् महाभूतादि वृत्तोजाः प्रादुरासीत् तमोनुदः
text: Thereafter, the supreme being Hiraṇyagarbha, self-born, unmanifest and bringing into view this (universe), appeared,—dispelling darkness and having his (creative) power operating upon the Elemental Substances and other things.
iast: tataḥ svayaṃbhūrbhagavānavyakto vyañjayannidam mahābhūtādi vṛttojāḥ prādurāsīt tamonudaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo 'sāvatīndriyagrāhyaḥ sūkṣmo 'vyaktaḥ sanātanaḥ sarvabhūtamayo 'cintyaḥ sa eva svayamudbabhau sa eṣa
meaning: यो 'सावतीन्द्रियग्राह्यः सूक्ष्मो 'व्यक्तः सनातनः सर्वभूतमयो 'चिन्त्यः स एव स्वयमुद्बभौ स एष
text: He,—who is apprehended beyond the senses, who is subtile, unmanifest and eternal, absorbed in all created things and inconceivable,—appeared by himself.
iast: yo 'sāvatīndriyagrāhyaḥ sūkṣmo 'vyaktaḥ sanātanaḥ sarvabhūtamayo 'cintyaḥ sa eva svayamudbabhau sa eṣa

sanskrit_iast: so 'bhidhyāya śarīrāt svāt sisṛkṣurvividhāḥ prajāḥ apa eva sasarjādau tāsu vīryamavāsṛjat
meaning: सो 'भिध्याय शरीरात् स्वात् सिसृक्षुर्विविधाः प्रजाः अप एव ससर्जादौ तासु वीर्यमवासृजत्
text: Desiring to create the several kinds of created things, he, in the beginning, by mere willing, produced, out op his own body, Water; and in that he threw the seed.
iast: so 'bhidhyāya śarīrāt svāt sisṛkṣurvividhāḥ prajāḥ apa eva sasarjādau tāsu vīryamavāsṛjat

sanskrit_iast: tadaṇḍamabhavaddhaimaṃ sahasrāṃśusamaprabham tasmiñjajñe svayaṃ brahmā sarvalokapitāmahaḥ
meaning: तदण्डमभवद्धैमं सहस्रांशुसमप्रभम् तस्मिञ्जज्ञे स्वयं ब्रह्मा सर्वलोकपितामहः
text: That became the golden egg, resplendent like the Sun; in that (egg) he (Hiraṇyagarbha) himself was born as Brahmā, the ‘Grand-father’ of the whole world.
iast: tadaṇḍamabhavaddhaimaṃ sahasrāṃśusamaprabham tasmiñjajñe svayaṃ brahmā sarvalokapitāmahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āpo narā iti proktā āpo vai narasūnavaḥ tā yadasyāyanaṃ pūrvaṃ tena nārāyaṇaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: आपो नरा इति प्रोक्ता आपो वै नरसूनवः ता यदस्यायनं पूर्वं तेन नारायणः स्मृतः
text: Water is called ‘nara,’—water being the offspring of nara; since water was the first thing created by (or, the original residence of) that being, he is, on that account, described as ‘nārāyaṇa.’
iast: āpo narā iti proktā āpo vai narasūnavaḥ tā yadasyāyanaṃ pūrvaṃ tena nārāyaṇaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yat tat kāraṇamavyaktaṃ nityaṃ sadasadātmakam tadvisṛṣṭaḥ sa puruṣo loke brahmaiti kīrtyate
meaning: यत् तत् कारणमव्यक्तं नित्यं सदसदात्मकम् तद्विसृष्टः स पुरुषो लोके ब्रह्मैति कीर्त्यते
text: That which is the cause—unmanifest, eternal and partaking of the nature of the existent and the non-existent,—the being produced by that (cause) is described among people as ‘brahmā.’
iast: yat tat kāraṇamavyaktaṃ nityaṃ sadasadātmakam tadvisṛṣṭaḥ sa puruṣo loke brahmaiti kīrtyate

sanskrit_iast: tasminnaṇḍe sa bhagavānuṣitvā parivatsaram svayamevātmano dhyānāt tadaṇḍamakaroddvidhā
meaning: तस्मिन्नण्डे स भगवानुषित्वा परिवत्सरम् स्वयमेवात्मनो ध्यानात् तदण्डमकरोद्द्विधा
text: That supreme lord, having dwelt in that egg for a y ear, himself, by his own thought, broke that egg into two parts.
iast: tasminnaṇḍe sa bhagavānuṣitvā parivatsaram svayamevātmano dhyānāt tadaṇḍamakaroddvidhā

sanskrit_iast: tābhyāṃ sa śakalābhyāṃ ca divaṃ bhūmiṃ ca nirmame madhye vyoma diśaścāṣṭāvapāṃ sthānaṃ ca śāśvatam
meaning: ताभ्यां स शकलाभ्यां च दिवं भूमिं च निर्ममे मध्ये व्योम दिशश्चाष्टावपां स्थानं च शाश्वतम्
text: Out of those two pieces (of the egg) he formed Heaven and Earth, and, between them, the Ākāśa, the eight quarters and the eternal receptacle of water.
iast: tābhyāṃ sa śakalābhyāṃ ca divaṃ bhūmiṃ ca nirmame madhye vyoma diśaścāṣṭāvapāṃ sthānaṃ ca śāśvatam

sanskrit_iast: udbabarhātmanaścaiva manaḥ sadasadātmakam manasaścāpyahaṅkāramabhimantāramīśvaram ahaṅkāram
meaning: उद्बबर्हात्मनश्चैव मनः सदसदात्मकम् मनसश्चाप्यहङ्कारमभिमन्तारमीश्वरम् अहङ्कारम्
text: From out of himself he brought forth the mind, which partakes of the nature of the existent and non-existent; and before the mind, he brought up the all-powerful principle of egoism, whose function consists in self-consciousness.
iast: udbabarhātmanaścaiva manaḥ sadasadātmakam manasaścāpyahaṅkāramabhimantāramīśvaram ahaṅkāram

sanskrit_iast: mahāntameva cātmānaṃ sarvāṇi triguṇāni ca viṣayāṇāṃ grahītr̥̄ṇi śanaiḥ pañcaindriyāṇi ca
meaning: महान्तमेव चात्मानं सर्वाणि त्रिगुणानि च विषयाणां ग्रहीत्र्̥̄णि शनैः पञ्चैन्द्रियाणि च
text: From out of himself he brought forth the mind, which partakes of the nature of the existent and non-existent; and before the mind, he brought up the all-powerful principle of egoism, whose function consists in self-consciousness.
iast: mahāntameva cātmānaṃ sarvāṇi triguṇāni ca viṣayāṇāṃ grahītr̥̄ṇi śanaiḥ pañcaindriyāṇi ca

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ tvavayavān sūkṣmān ṣaṇṇāmapyamitojasām saṃniveśyātmamātrāsu sarvabhūtāni nirmame sanniveśya
meaning: तेषां त्ववयवान् सूक्ष्मान् षण्णामप्यमितोजसाम् संनिवेश्यात्ममात्रासु सर्वभूतानि निर्ममे सन्निवेश्य
text: Having combined the subtile components of the said six principles of illimitable potency with their own evolutes, he created even all beings.
iast: teṣāṃ tvavayavān sūkṣmān ṣaṇṇāmapyamitojasām saṃniveśyātmamātrāsu sarvabhūtāni nirmame sanniveśya

sanskrit_iast: yan mūrtiavayavāḥ sūkṣmāstānīmānyāśrayanti ṣaṭ tasmātśarīramityāhustasya mūrtiṃ manīṣiṇaḥ
meaning: यन् मूर्तिअवयवाः सूक्ष्मास्तानीमान्याश्रयन्ति षट् तस्मात्शरीरमित्याहुस्तस्य मूर्तिं मनीषिणः
text: Because the six subtile components of the frame (of primordial matter) enter into (produce) these, therefore the wise ones have described the frame of that (primordial matter) as ‘body.’
iast: yan mūrtiavayavāḥ sūkṣmāstānīmānyāśrayanti ṣaṭ tasmātśarīramityāhustasya mūrtiṃ manīṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tadāviśanti bhūtāni mahānti saha karmabhiḥ manaścāvayavaiḥ sūkṣmaiḥ sarvabhūtakṛdavyayam
meaning: तदाविशन्ति भूतानि महान्ति सह कर्मभिः मनश्चावयवैः सूक्ष्मैः सर्वभूतकृदव्ययम्
text: The great elemental substances, along with their functions, as also the mind, along with its subtile components, enter into that which (on that account) is the generator of all things and imperishable.
iast: tadāviśanti bhūtāni mahānti saha karmabhiḥ manaścāvayavaiḥ sūkṣmaiḥ sarvabhūtakṛdavyayam

sanskrit_iast: teṣāmidaṃ tu saptānāṃ puruṣāṇāṃ mahaujasām sūkṣmābhyo mūrtimātrābhyaḥ saṃbhavatyavyayādvyayam
meaning: तेषामिदं तु सप्तानां पुरुषाणां महौजसाम् सूक्ष्माभ्यो मूर्तिमात्राभ्यः संभवत्यव्ययाद्व्ययम्
text: From out of the Subtile constituents of the frames of the said exceedingly potent principles is produced this (Gross Body)—the perishable proceeding from the imperishable.
iast: teṣāmidaṃ tu saptānāṃ puruṣāṇāṃ mahaujasām sūkṣmābhyo mūrtimātrābhyaḥ saṃbhavatyavyayādvyayam

sanskrit_iast: ādyādyasya guṇaṃ tveṣāmavāpnoti paraḥ paraḥ yo yo yāvatithaścaiṣāṃ sa sa tāvadguṇaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: आद्याद्यस्य गुणं त्वेषामवाप्नोति परः परः यो यो यावतिथश्चैषां स स तावद्गुणः स्मृतः
text: Among these (Elementary Substances), each succeeding one acquires the quality of what precedes it; and each elemental substance is endowed with as many qualities as the place it occupies (in the order in which the said substances are set forth).
iast: ādyādyasya guṇaṃ tveṣāmavāpnoti paraḥ paraḥ yo yo yāvatithaścaiṣāṃ sa sa tāvadguṇaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāṃ tu sa nāmāni karmāṇi ca pṛthak pṛthak vedaśabdebhya evādau pṛthak saṃsthāśca nirmame
meaning: सर्वेषां तु स नामानि कर्माणि च पृथक् पृथक् वेदशब्देभ्य एवादौ पृथक् संस्थाश्च निर्ममे
text: At the outset he designated distinct names for add things; and devised acts and Laws, on the basis of the words of the veda.
iast: sarveṣāṃ tu sa nāmāni karmāṇi ca pṛthak pṛthak vedaśabdebhya evādau pṛthak saṃsthāśca nirmame

sanskrit_iast: karmātmanāṃ ca devānāṃ so 'sṛjat prāṇināṃ prabhuḥ sādhyānāṃ ca gaṇaṃ sūkṣmaṃ yajñaṃ caiva sanātanam
meaning: कर्मात्मनां च देवानां सो 'सृजत् प्राणिनां प्रभुः साध्यानां च गणं सूक्ष्मं यज्ञं चैव सनातनम्
text: For the sake of living beings intent upon action, he created the eternal sacrifice; as also the host of Gods and the subtile multitude of the lesser divinities, the Sādhyas.
iast: karmātmanāṃ ca devānāṃ so 'sṛjat prāṇināṃ prabhuḥ sādhyānāṃ ca gaṇaṃ sūkṣmaṃ yajñaṃ caiva sanātanam

sanskrit_iast: agnivāyuravibhyastu trayaṃ brahma sanātanam dudoha yajñasiddhiarthaṃ ṛcyajussāmalakṣaṇam
meaning: अग्निवायुरविभ्यस्तु त्रयं ब्रह्म सनातनम् दुदोह यज्ञसिद्धिअर्थं ऋच्यजुस्सामलक्षणम्
text: From out of (the three deities) Agni, Vāyu and Ravi, he extracted, for the due fulfilment of sacrifices, the eternal Brahman, threefold, in the forms of ‘Ṛk,’ ‘Yajuṣ’ and ‘Sāman.’
iast: agnivāyuravibhyastu trayaṃ brahma sanātanam dudoha yajñasiddhiarthaṃ ṛcyajussāmalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: kālaṃ kālavibhaktīśca nakṣatrāṇi grahāṃstathā saritaḥ sāgarāṃśailān samāni viṣamāni ca
meaning: कालं कालविभक्तीश्च नक्षत्राणि ग्रहांस्तथा सरितः सागरांशैलान् समानि विषमानि च
text: [He created] also Time, the Divisions of Time, the Lunar Mansions, the Planets, the Rivers, the Oceans, the Mountains and the tracts of land, plain and rugged.
iast: kālaṃ kālavibhaktīśca nakṣatrāṇi grahāṃstathā saritaḥ sāgarāṃśailān samāni viṣamāni ca

sanskrit_iast: tapo vācaṃ ratiṃ caiva kāmaṃ ca krodhameva ca sṛṣṭiṃ sasarja caivaimāṃ sraṣṭumicchannimāḥ prajāḥ
meaning: तपो वाचं रतिं चैव कामं च क्रोधमेव च सृष्टिं ससर्ज चैवैमां स्रष्टुमिच्छन्निमाः प्रजाः
text: Being desirous of bringing into existence these creatures, he created this entire creation (comprising) austerity, speech, happiness, desire and anger.
iast: tapo vācaṃ ratiṃ caiva kāmaṃ ca krodhameva ca sṛṣṭiṃ sasarja caivaimāṃ sraṣṭumicchannimāḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: karmaṇāṃ ca vivekārthaṃ dharmādharmau vyavecayat vivekāya dvandvairayojayaccaimāḥ sukhaduḥkhādibhiḥ prajāḥ
meaning: कर्मणां च विवेकार्थं धर्माधर्मौ व्यवेचयत् विवेकाय द्वन्द्वैरयोजयच्चैमाः सुखदुःखादिभिः प्रजाः
text: For the due discrimination of actions, he differentiated Virtue and Vice; and he connected these creatures with such pairs of opposites as Pleasure-Pain and the like.
iast: karmaṇāṃ ca vivekārthaṃ dharmādharmau vyavecayat vivekāya dvandvairayojayaccaimāḥ sukhaduḥkhādibhiḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: aṇvyo mātrā vināśinyo daśārdhānāṃ tu yāḥ smṛtāḥ tābhiḥ sārdhamidaṃ sarvaṃ saṃbhavatyanupūrvaśaḥ
meaning: अण्व्यो मात्रा विनाशिन्यो दशार्धानां तु याः स्मृताः ताभिः सार्धमिदं सर्वं संभवत्यनुपूर्वशः
text: The evanescent subtile constituents of the half-ten (Elemental Substances) that have been described,—along with those, this whole (world) comes forth, in due order.
iast: aṇvyo mātrā vināśinyo daśārdhānāṃ tu yāḥ smṛtāḥ tābhiḥ sārdhamidaṃ sarvaṃ saṃbhavatyanupūrvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yaṃ tu karmaṇi yasmin sa nyayuṅkta prathamaṃ prabhuḥ sa tadeva svayaṃ bheje sṛjyamānaḥ punaḥ punaḥ
meaning: यं तु कर्मणि यस्मिन् स न्ययुङ्क्त प्रथमं प्रभुः स तदेव स्वयं भेजे सृज्यमानः पुनः पुनः
text: Each being, when created again and again, naturally conformed to that same act to which the lord had, at first, directed him.
iast: yaṃ tu karmaṇi yasmin sa nyayuṅkta prathamaṃ prabhuḥ sa tadeva svayaṃ bheje sṛjyamānaḥ punaḥ punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: hiṃsrāhiṃsre mṛdukrūre dharmādharmāvṛtānṛte yadyasya so 'dadhāt sarge tat tasya svayamāviśat
meaning: हिंस्राहिंस्रे मृदुक्रूरे धर्माधर्मावृतानृते यद्यस्य सो 'दधात् सर्गे तत् तस्य स्वयमाविशत्
text: Each being, when created again and again, naturally conformed to that same act to which the lord had, at first, directed him.
iast: hiṃsrāhiṃsre mṛdukrūre dharmādharmāvṛtānṛte yadyasya so 'dadhāt sarge tat tasya svayamāviśat

sanskrit_iast: yathāṛtuliṅgāny ṛtavaḥ svayamevaṛtuparyaye svāni svānyabhipadyante tathā karmāṇi dehinaḥ
meaning: यथाऋतुलिङ्गान्य् ऋतवः स्वयमेवऋतुपर्यये स्वानि स्वान्यभिपद्यन्ते तथा कर्माणि देहिनः
text: Just as on the approach of the turn of the Seasons, each season, by itself, acquires its own seasonal characteristics,—so in the same manner living beings take up their respective lines of action.
iast: yathāṛtuliṅgāny ṛtavaḥ svayamevaṛtuparyaye svāni svānyabhipadyante tathā karmāṇi dehinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: lokānāṃ tu vivṛddhiarthaṃ mukhabāhūrupādataḥ brāhmaṇaṃ kṣatriyaṃ vaiśyaṃ śūdraṃ ca niravartayat
meaning: लोकानां तु विवृद्धिअर्थं मुखबाहूरुपादतः ब्राह्मणं क्षत्रियं वैश्यं शूद्रं च निरवर्तयत्
text: With a view to the development of the (three) regions, He brought into existence the Brāhmaṇa, the Kṣatriya, the Vaiśya and the Śūdra, from out of His mouth, arms, thighs and feet (respectively).
iast: lokānāṃ tu vivṛddhiarthaṃ mukhabāhūrupādataḥ brāhmaṇaṃ kṣatriyaṃ vaiśyaṃ śūdraṃ ca niravartayat

sanskrit_iast: dvidhā kṛtvā 'tmano dehamardhena puruṣo 'bhavat ardhena nārī tasyāṃ sa virājamasṛjat prabhuḥ
meaning: द्विधा कृत्वा 'त्मनो देहमर्धेन पुरुषो 'भवत् अर्धेन नारी तस्यां स विराजमसृजत् प्रभुः
text: Having divided his body into two halves, with the one half, the Lord became Male, and with the other half, Female; from her he produced Virāj.
iast: dvidhā kṛtvā 'tmano dehamardhena puruṣo 'bhavat ardhena nārī tasyāṃ sa virājamasṛjat prabhuḥ

sanskrit_iast: tapastaptvā 'sṛjadyaṃ tu sa svayaṃ puruṣo virāṭ taṃ māṃ vittāsya sarvasya sraṣṭāraṃ dvijasattamāḥ
meaning: तपस्तप्त्वा 'सृजद्यं तु स स्वयं पुरुषो विराट् तं मां वित्तास्य सर्वस्य स्रष्टारं द्विजसत्तमाः
text: O best of Brāhmaṇas, know me, the creator, of this whole (would), to be that whom the said Being Virāj himself, after having performed austerities, produced.
iast: tapastaptvā 'sṛjadyaṃ tu sa svayaṃ puruṣo virāṭ taṃ māṃ vittāsya sarvasya sraṣṭāraṃ dvijasattamāḥ

sanskrit_iast: ahaṃ prajāḥ sisṛkṣustu tapastaptvā suduścaram patīn prajānāmasṛjaṃ maharṣīnādito daśa
meaning: अहं प्रजाः सिसृक्षुस्तु तपस्तप्त्वा सुदुश्चरम् पतीन् प्रजानामसृजं महर्षीनादितो दश
text: Being desirous of bringing into existence the (various kinds of) created beings, I, at the very outset, performed most arduous austerities and called into being the ten great sages, the directors of all created things; (34)—viz: Marīci, Atri, Aṅgiras, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Pracetas, Vaśiṣṭha, Bhṛgu and also Nārada.
iast: ahaṃ prajāḥ sisṛkṣustu tapastaptvā suduścaram patīn prajānāmasṛjaṃ maharṣīnādito daśa

sanskrit_iast: marīcimatriaṅgirasau pulastyaṃ pulahaṃ kratum pracetasaṃ vasiṣṭhaṃ ca bhṛguṃ nāradameva ca
meaning: मरीचिमत्रिअङ्गिरसौ पुलस्त्यं पुलहं क्रतुम् प्रचेतसं वसिष्ठं च भृगुं नारदमेव च
text: Being desirous of bringing into existence the (various kinds of) created beings, I, at the very outset, performed most arduous austerities and called into being the ten great sages, the directors of all created things; (34)—viz: Marīci, Atri, Aṅgiras, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Pracetas, Vaśiṣṭha, Bhṛgu and also Nārada.
iast: marīcimatriaṅgirasau pulastyaṃ pulahaṃ kratum pracetasaṃ vasiṣṭhaṃ ca bhṛguṃ nāradameva ca

sanskrit_iast: ete manūṃstu saptān yānasṛjan bhūritejasaḥ devān devanikāyāṃśca maharṣīṃścāmitojasaḥ
meaning: एते मनूंस्तु सप्तान् यानसृजन् भूरितेजसः देवान् देवनिकायांश्च महर्षींश्चामितोजसः
text: These mighty (Sages) called into being the seven Manus, gods and gods’ habitations, as also Great Sages,—all possessed of illimitable power.
iast: ete manūṃstu saptān yānasṛjan bhūritejasaḥ devān devanikāyāṃśca maharṣīṃścāmitojasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yakṣarakṣaspiśācāṃśca gandharvāpsaraso 'surān nāgān sarpān suparṇāṃśca pitr̥̄ṇāṃśca pṛthaggaṇam pitr̥̄ṇāṃ
meaning: यक्षरक्षस्पिशाचांश्च गन्धर्वाप्सरसो 'सुरान् नागान् सर्पान् सुपर्णांश्च पित्र्̥̄णांश्च पृथग्गणम् पित्र्̥̄णां
text: [They called into being] also Yakṣas, Rakṣasas, Piśācas, Gandharvas, Apsarases, Asuras, Nāgas, Sarpas, Suparṇas, and the several orders of Pitṛs.
iast: yakṣarakṣaspiśācāṃśca gandharvāpsaraso 'surān nāgān sarpān suparṇāṃśca pitr̥̄ṇāṃśca pṛthaggaṇam pitr̥̄ṇāṃ

sanskrit_iast: vidyuto 'śanimeghāṃśca rohitaindradhanūṃṣi ca ulkānirghātaketūṃśca jyotīṃṣyuccāvacāni ca
meaning: विद्युतो 'शनिमेघांश्च रोहितैन्द्रधनूंषि च उल्कानिर्घातकेतूंश्च ज्योतींष्युच्चावचानि च
text: [They called into being] Lightnings, Hails, Clouds, Vertical Phosphorescence, Rainbows, Meteors, Portentous Sounds, Comets, and Stars of varying magnitudes.
iast: vidyuto 'śanimeghāṃśca rohitaindradhanūṃṣi ca ulkānirghātaketūṃśca jyotīṃṣyuccāvacāni ca

sanskrit_iast: kinnarān vānarān matsyān vividhāṃśca vihaṅgamān paśūn mṛgān manuṣyāṃśca vyālāṃścaubhayatodataḥ
meaning: किन्नरान् वानरान् मत्स्यान् विविधांश्च विहङ्गमान् पशून् मृगान् मनुष्यांश्च व्यालांश्चौभयतोदतः
text: [They called into being] Kinnaras, Apes, Fishes, Birds of various kinds, Cattle, Deer, Men and wild beasts with two rows of teeth.
iast: kinnarān vānarān matsyān vividhāṃśca vihaṅgamān paśūn mṛgān manuṣyāṃśca vyālāṃścaubhayatodataḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṛmikīṭapataṅgāṃśca yūkāmakṣikamatkuṇam sarvaṃ ca daṃśamaśakaṃ sthāvaraṃ ca pṛthagvidham
meaning: कृमिकीटपतङ्गांश्च यूकामक्षिकमत्कुणम् सर्वं च दंशमशकं स्थावरं च पृथग्विधम्
text: [They called into being] Worms, Beetles and Moths; Lice, Flies and Bugs; Gadflies and Gnats; and also the entire host of the several kinds of immovable things.
iast: kṛmikīṭapataṅgāṃśca yūkāmakṣikamatkuṇam sarvaṃ ca daṃśamaśakaṃ sthāvaraṃ ca pṛthagvidham

sanskrit_iast: evametairidaṃ sarvaṃ madniyogān mahātmabhiḥ yathākarma tapoyogāt sṛṣṭaṃ sthāvarajaṅgamam
meaning: एवमेतैरिदं सर्वं मद्नियोगान् महात्मभिः यथाकर्म तपोयोगात् सृष्टं स्थावरजङ्गमम्
text: In this manner was all this, movable and immovable, called into being, through the force of austerities, by these high-souled sages, under my direction,—in accordance with their actions.
iast: evametairidaṃ sarvaṃ madniyogān mahātmabhiḥ yathākarma tapoyogāt sṛṣṭaṃ sthāvarajaṅgamam

sanskrit_iast: yeṣāṃ tu yādṛṣaṃ karma bhūtānāmiha kīrtitam tat tathā vo 'bhidhāsyāmi kramayogaṃ ca janmani
meaning: येषां तु यादृषं कर्म भूतानामिह कीर्तितम् तत् तथा वो 'भिधास्यामि क्रमयोगं च जन्मनि
text: That kind of action which belongs to the several beings has been described here. I am now going to explain the manner of their birth.
iast: yeṣāṃ tu yādṛṣaṃ karma bhūtānāmiha kīrtitam tat tathā vo 'bhidhāsyāmi kramayogaṃ ca janmani

sanskrit_iast: paśavaśca mṛgāścaiva vyālāścaubhayatodataḥ rakṣāṃsi ca piśācāśca manuṣyāśca jarāyujāḥ manuṣāśca
meaning: पशवश्च मृगाश्चैव व्यालाश्चौभयतोदतः रक्षांसि च पिशाचाश्च मनुष्याश्च जरायुजाः मनुषाश्च
text: Cattle, Deer, Wild Beasts with two rows of teeth, Rākṣasas, Piśācas and Men are viviparous.
iast: paśavaśca mṛgāścaiva vyālāścaubhayatodataḥ rakṣāṃsi ca piśācāśca manuṣyāśca jarāyujāḥ manuṣāśca

sanskrit_iast: aṇḍājāḥ pakṣiṇaḥ sarpā nakrā matsyāśca kacchapāḥ yāni caivaṃprakārāṇi sthalajānyaudakāni ca
meaning: अण्डाजाः पक्षिणः सर्पा नक्रा मत्स्याश्च कच्छपाः यानि चैवंप्रकाराणि स्थलजान्यौदकानि च
text: Birds, Serpents, Crocodiles, Fishes, Tortoises, and other animals of similar kinds, terrestrial as well as aquatic,—are oviparous.
iast: aṇḍājāḥ pakṣiṇaḥ sarpā nakrā matsyāśca kacchapāḥ yāni caivaṃprakārāṇi sthalajānyaudakāni ca

sanskrit_iast: svedajaṃ daṃśamaśakaṃ yūkāmakṣikamatkuṇam ūṣmaṇaścaupajāyante yaccānyat kiṃ cidīdṛṣam
meaning: स्वेदजं दंशमशकं यूकामक्षिकमत्कुणम् ऊष्मणश्चौपजायन्ते यच्चान्यत् किं चिदीदृषम्
text: Gadflies and Gnats, Lice, Flies and Bugs, are sweat- born; whatever else is of similar character is born from heat
iast: svedajaṃ daṃśamaśakaṃ yūkāmakṣikamatkuṇam ūṣmaṇaścaupajāyante yaccānyat kiṃ cidīdṛṣam

sanskrit_iast: udbhijjāḥ sthāvarāḥ sarve bījakāṇḍaprarohiṇaḥ oṣadhyaḥ phalapākāntā bahupuṣpaphalaupagāḥ
meaning: उद्भिज्जाः स्थावराः सर्वे बीजकाण्डप्ररोहिणः ओषध्यः फलपाकान्ता बहुपुष्पफलौपगाः
text: All those immovable brings that are produced by splitting (i.e., Plants) grow out of seeds and slips. those that, abounding in flowers, perish with the ripening of their fruit, are called ‘oṣadhis’ (‘Annuals’).
iast: udbhijjāḥ sthāvarāḥ sarve bījakāṇḍaprarohiṇaḥ oṣadhyaḥ phalapākāntā bahupuṣpaphalaupagāḥ

sanskrit_iast: apuṣpāḥ phalavanto ye te vanaspatayaḥ smṛtāḥ puṣpiṇaḥ phalinaścaiva vṛkṣāstūbhayataḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: अपुष्पाः फलवन्तो ये ते वनस्पतयः स्मृताः पुष्पिणः फलिनश्चैव वृक्षास्तूभयतः स्मृताः
text: Those trees that are called ‘vanaspati’ bear fruits without flowers; and those called ‘vṛkṣa’ bear both flowers and fruits.
iast: apuṣpāḥ phalavanto ye te vanaspatayaḥ smṛtāḥ puṣpiṇaḥ phalinaścaiva vṛkṣāstūbhayataḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: gucchagulmaṃ tu vividhaṃ tathaiva tṛṇajātayaḥ bījakāṇḍaruhāṇyeva pratānā vallya eva ca
meaning: गुच्छगुल्मं तु विविधं तथैव तृणजातयः बीजकाण्डरुहाण्येव प्रताना वल्ल्य एव च
text: The various kinds op clumps and thickets, and the other species of grass, as also low-spreading tendrils and creepers—all these grow out of seeds and slips.
iast: gucchagulmaṃ tu vividhaṃ tathaiva tṛṇajātayaḥ bījakāṇḍaruhāṇyeva pratānā vallya eva ca

sanskrit_iast: tamasā bahurūpeṇa veṣṭitāḥ karmahetunā antassaṃjñā bhavantyete sukhaduḥkhasamanvitāḥ
meaning: तमसा बहुरूपेण वेष्टिताः कर्महेतुना अन्तस्संज्ञा भवन्त्येते सुखदुःखसमन्विताः
text: All these (vegetable beings) are invested by manifold ‘darkness’ (inertia), the result of their acts; and possessing inner consciousness, they are affected by pleasure and pain.
iast: tamasā bahurūpeṇa veṣṭitāḥ karmahetunā antassaṃjñā bhavantyete sukhaduḥkhasamanvitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: etadantāstu gatayo brahmādyāḥ samudāhṛtāḥ ghore 'smin bhūtasaṃsāre nityaṃ satatayāyini
meaning: एतदन्तास्तु गतयो ब्रह्माद्याः समुदाहृताः घोरे 'स्मिन् भूतसंसारे नित्यं सततयायिनि
text: Thus have been described the conditions of life, beginning with brahmā and ending with those just mentioned, which occur in this ever frightful and constantly fluctuating cycle of births and deaths of created beings.
iast: etadantāstu gatayo brahmādyāḥ samudāhṛtāḥ ghore 'smin bhūtasaṃsāre nityaṃ satatayāyini

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sarvaṃ sa sṛṣṭvaidaṃ māṃ cācintyaparākramaḥ ātmanyantardadhe bhūyaḥ kālaṃ kālena pīḍayan
meaning: एवं सर्वं स सृष्ट्वैदं मां चाचिन्त्यपराक्रमः आत्मन्यन्तर्दधे भूयः कालं कालेन पीडयन्
text: Thus repeatedly suppressing time (of dissolution) by time (of creation and maintenance), he, of inconceivable power, created all this and also myself; [he directed me to maintain it] and then disappeared within himself.
iast: evaṃ sarvaṃ sa sṛṣṭvaidaṃ māṃ cācintyaparākramaḥ ātmanyantardadhe bhūyaḥ kālaṃ kālena pīḍayan

sanskrit_iast: yadā sa devo jāgarti tadevaṃ ceṣṭate jagat yadā svapiti śāntātmā tadā sarvaṃ nimīlati
meaning: यदा स देवो जागर्ति तदेवं चेष्टते जगत् यदा स्वपिति शान्तात्मा तदा सर्वं निमीलति
text: When that divine being is awake, then this world is active; when he slumbers, with his mind in calm repose, then all vanishes.
iast: yadā sa devo jāgarti tadevaṃ ceṣṭate jagat yadā svapiti śāntātmā tadā sarvaṃ nimīlati

sanskrit_iast: tasmin svapiti tu svasthe karmātmānaḥ śarīriṇaḥ svapati svakarmabhyo nivartante manaśca glāniṃ ṛcchati
meaning: तस्मिन् स्वपिति तु स्वस्थे कर्मात्मानः शरीरिणः स्वपति स्वकर्मभ्यो निवर्तन्ते मनश्च ग्लानिं ऋच्छति
text: When he slumbers, having retired within himself, all active embodied beings desist from their actions, and their mind falls into depression.
iast: tasmin svapiti tu svasthe karmātmānaḥ śarīriṇaḥ svapati svakarmabhyo nivartante manaśca glāniṃ ṛcchati

sanskrit_iast: yugapat tu pralīyante yadā tasmin mahātmani tadā 'yaṃ sarvabhūtātmā sukhaṃ svapiti nirvṛtaḥ
meaning: युगपत् तु प्रलीयन्ते यदा तस्मिन् महात्मनि तदा 'यं सर्वभूतात्मा सुखं स्वपिति निर्वृतः
text: When this Soul of all things sleeps happy and contented, then all things become absorbed all at once in that Great Soul.
iast: yugapat tu pralīyante yadā tasmin mahātmani tadā 'yaṃ sarvabhūtātmā sukhaṃ svapiti nirvṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tamo 'yaṃ tu samāśritya ciraṃ tiṣṭhati saindriyaḥ na ca svaṃ kurute karma tadautkrāmati mūrtitaḥ
meaning: तमो 'यं तु समाश्रित्य चिरं तिष्ठति सैन्द्रियः न च स्वं कुरुते कर्म तदौत्क्रामति मूर्तितः
text: This (individual Soul), on entering into ‘Darkness,’ remains, for a long time, equipped with the sense-organs, but does not perform its functions; then it departs from the body.
iast: tamo 'yaṃ tu samāśritya ciraṃ tiṣṭhati saindriyaḥ na ca svaṃ kurute karma tadautkrāmati mūrtitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadā 'ṇumātriko bhūtvā bījaṃ sthāsnu cariṣṇu ca samāviśati saṃsṛṣṭastadā mūrtiṃ vimuñcati
meaning: यदा 'णुमात्रिको भूत्वा बीजं स्थास्नु चरिष्णु च समाविशति संसृष्टस्तदा मूर्तिं विमुञ्चति
text: When, invested with minute particles, the Individual enters the moveable or immoveable Seed;—then, becoming united with the aforesaid (Subtile body), it assumes the (new) body.
iast: yadā 'ṇumātriko bhūtvā bījaṃ sthāsnu cariṣṇu ca samāviśati saṃsṛṣṭastadā mūrtiṃ vimuñcati

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sa jāgratsvapnābhyāmidaṃ sarvaṃ carācaram sañjīvayati cājasraṃ pramāpayati cāvyayaḥ
meaning: एवं स जाग्रत्स्वप्नाभ्यामिदं सर्वं चराचरम् सञ्जीवयति चाजस्रं प्रमापयति चाव्ययः
text: Thus by waking and sleeping, the Imperishable One incessantly brings to like and destroys all this that is moveable and immoveable.
iast: evaṃ sa jāgratsvapnābhyāmidaṃ sarvaṃ carācaram sañjīvayati cājasraṃ pramāpayati cāvyayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: idaṃ śāstraṃ tu kṛtvā 'sau māmeva svayamāditaḥ vidhivadgrāhayāmāsa marīciādīṃstvahaṃ munīn
meaning: इदं शास्त्रं तु कृत्वा 'सौ मामेव स्वयमादितः विधिवद्ग्राहयामास मरीचिआदींस्त्वहं मुनीन्
text: Having prounded this Law, he himself, first of all, taught it to me with due care; I then taught it to Marīci and other Sages.
iast: idaṃ śāstraṃ tu kṛtvā 'sau māmeva svayamāditaḥ vidhivadgrāhayāmāsa marīciādīṃstvahaṃ munīn

sanskrit_iast: etadvo 'yaṃ bhṛguḥ śāstraṃ śrāvayiṣyatyaśesataḥ etaddhi matto 'dhijage sarvameṣo 'khilaṃ muniḥ
meaning: एतद्वो 'यं भृगुः शास्त्रं श्रावयिष्यत्यशेसतः एतद्धि मत्तो 'धिजगे सर्वमेषो 'खिलं मुनिः
text: This Bhṛgu will fully describe to you this (Law); this sage has learnt the whole of this in its entirety, from me.
iast: etadvo 'yaṃ bhṛguḥ śāstraṃ śrāvayiṣyatyaśesataḥ etaddhi matto 'dhijage sarvameṣo 'khilaṃ muniḥ

sanskrit_iast: tatastathā sa tenaukto maharṣimanunā bhṛguḥ tānabravīdṛṣīn sarvān prītātmā śrūyatāmiti
meaning: ततस्तथा स तेनौक्तो महर्षिमनुना भृगुः तानब्रवीदृषीन् सर्वान् प्रीतात्मा श्रूयतामिति
text: Thereupon, being thus directed by Manu, the great sage Bhṛgu, with a gladdened heart, said to the sages—‘Listen.’
iast: tatastathā sa tenaukto maharṣimanunā bhṛguḥ tānabravīdṛṣīn sarvān prītātmā śrūyatāmiti

sanskrit_iast: svāyaṃbhuvasyāsya manoḥ ṣaḍvaṃśyā manavo 'pare sṛṣṭavantaḥ prajāḥ svāḥ svā mahātmāno mahaujasaḥ
meaning: स्वायंभुवस्यास्य मनोः षड्वंश्या मनवो 'परे सृष्टवन्तः प्रजाः स्वाः स्वा महात्मानो महौजसः
text: There are Six other Manus, high-souled and mighty, who belong to the same race as this Svāyambhuva Manu, and have called into being, each his own offsprings.
iast: svāyaṃbhuvasyāsya manoḥ ṣaḍvaṃśyā manavo 'pare sṛṣṭavantaḥ prajāḥ svāḥ svā mahātmāno mahaujasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: svārociṣaścauttamaśca tāmaso raivatastathā cākṣuṣaśca mahātejā vivasvatsuta eva ca
meaning: स्वारोचिषश्चौत्तमश्च तामसो रैवतस्तथा चाक्षुषश्च महातेजा विवस्वत्सुत एव च
text: [These six Manus are]—Svārociṣa, Uttama, Tāmasa, Raivata, Cākṣuṣa, and the glorious Vivasvat-suta (the Son of Vivasvat).
iast: svārociṣaścauttamaśca tāmaso raivatastathā cākṣuṣaśca mahātejā vivasvatsuta eva ca

sanskrit_iast: svāyaṃbhuvādyāḥ saptaite manavo bhūritejasaḥ sve sve 'ntare sarvamidamutpādyāpuścarācaram
meaning: स्वायंभुवाद्याः सप्तैते मनवो भूरितेजसः स्वे स्वे 'न्तरे सर्वमिदमुत्पाद्यापुश्चराचरम्
text: These seven almighty Manus, of whom Svāyambhuva is the first, having called into existence the whole of this world, consisting of movable and immovable beings, sustained it, each during his own regime.
iast: svāyaṃbhuvādyāḥ saptaite manavo bhūritejasaḥ sve sve 'ntare sarvamidamutpādyāpuścarācaram

sanskrit_iast: nimeṣā daśa cāṣṭau ca kāṣṭhā triṃśat tu tāḥ kalā triṃśat kalā muhūrtaḥ syādahorātraṃ tu tāvataḥ
meaning: निमेषा दश चाष्टौ च काष्ठा त्रिंशत् तु ताः कला त्रिंशत् कला मुहूर्तः स्यादहोरात्रं तु तावतः
text: Ten and eight ‘nimeṣas’ (should be known as) one ‘Kāṣṭhā’; thirty such (Kāṣṭhās) one ‘Kalā’; thirty ‘Kalās’ one ‘muhūrta’; and as many ‘muhūrtas’ one ‘Ahorātra’ (Day and Night).
iast: nimeṣā daśa cāṣṭau ca kāṣṭhā triṃśat tu tāḥ kalā triṃśat kalā muhūrtaḥ syādahorātraṃ tu tāvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: ahorātre vibhajate sūryo mānuṣadaivike rātriḥ svapnāya bhūtānāṃ ceṣṭāyai karmaṇāmahaḥ
meaning: अहोरात्रे विभजते सूर्यो मानुषदैविके रात्रिः स्वप्नाय भूतानां चेष्टायै कर्मणामहः
text: The Sun divides the ‘Day’ and ‘Night’ of Men and Gods; [of others] what is conducive to the repose of beings is ‘Night,’ and what is conducive to activity is ‘Day.’
iast: ahorātre vibhajate sūryo mānuṣadaivike rātriḥ svapnāya bhūtānāṃ ceṣṭāyai karmaṇāmahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pitrye rātriahanī māsaḥ pravibhāgastu pakṣayoḥ karmaceṣṭāsvahaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ śuklaḥ svapnāya śarvarī
meaning: पित्र्ये रात्रिअहनी मासः प्रविभागस्तु पक्षयोः कर्मचेष्टास्वहः कृष्णः शुक्लः स्वप्नाय शर्वरी
text: One month (of men) forms the ‘day and night’ of the ‘Pitṛs’; and their division is by fortnights: the darker fortnight, conducive to activity, is ‘Day,’ and the lighter fortnight, conducive to repose, is ‘Night.’
iast: pitrye rātriahanī māsaḥ pravibhāgastu pakṣayoḥ karmaceṣṭāsvahaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ śuklaḥ svapnāya śarvarī

sanskrit_iast: daive rātriahanī varṣaṃ pravibhāgastayoḥ punaḥ ahastatraudagayanaṃ rātriḥ syāddakṣiṇāyanam
meaning: दैवे रात्रिअहनी वर्षं प्रविभागस्तयोः पुनः अहस्तत्रौदगयनं रात्रिः स्याद्दक्षिणायनम्
text: One ‘year’ (of men) forms the ‘Day and Night’ of the Gods; and the division of these is that the ‘Northern course’ is the ‘Day,’ and the ‘Southern Course’ the ‘Night.’
iast: daive rātriahanī varṣaṃ pravibhāgastayoḥ punaḥ ahastatraudagayanaṃ rātriḥ syāddakṣiṇāyanam

sanskrit_iast: brāhmasya tu kṣapāhasya yat pramāṇaṃ samāsataḥ ekaikaśo yugānāṃ tu kramaśastannibodhata
meaning: ब्राह्मस्य तु क्षपाहस्य यत् प्रमाणं समासतः एकैकशो युगानां तु क्रमशस्तन्निबोधत
text: Learn in brief, in due order, the measure of the Brahmic ‘day and night,’ as also that of the ‘Time-cycles’ (Yugas) one by one.
iast: brāhmasya tu kṣapāhasya yat pramāṇaṃ samāsataḥ ekaikaśo yugānāṃ tu kramaśastannibodhata

sanskrit_iast: catvāryāhuḥ sahasrāṇi varsāṇāṃ tat kṛtaṃ yugam tasya tāvatśatī saṃdhyā saṃdhyāṃśaśca tathāvidhaḥ
meaning: चत्वार्याहुः सहस्राणि वर्साणां तत् कृतं युगम् तस्य तावत्शती संध्या संध्यांशश्च तथाविधः
text: They say that four thousnd ‘years’ are what is the ‘Kṛta-cycle’; as many hundred ‘years’ form the ‘Juncture’ (Morning); and of equal measure is the ‘Juncture-end’ (Evening).
iast: catvāryāhuḥ sahasrāṇi varsāṇāṃ tat kṛtaṃ yugam tasya tāvatśatī saṃdhyā saṃdhyāṃśaśca tathāvidhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: itareṣu sasaṃdhyeṣu sasaṃdhyāṃśeṣu ca triṣu ekāpāyena vartante sahasrāṇi śatāni ca
meaning: इतरेषु ससंध्येषु ससंध्यांशेषु च त्रिषु एकापायेन वर्तन्ते सहस्राणि शतानि च
text: In each of the other time-cycles, along with their ‘junctures’ and ‘juncture-ends’, the ‘thousands’ and ‘hundreds’ are reduced by one.
iast: itareṣu sasaṃdhyeṣu sasaṃdhyāṃśeṣu ca triṣu ekāpāyena vartante sahasrāṇi śatāni ca

sanskrit_iast: yadetat parisaṅkhyātamādāveva caturyugam etaddvādaśasāhasraṃ devānāṃ yugamucyate
meaning: यदेतत् परिसङ्ख्यातमादावेव चतुर्युगम् एतद्द्वादशसाहस्रं देवानां युगमुच्यते
text: This period of the four time-cycles that have been just computed,—twelve thousand such periods are called the ‘time-cycle of the gods.’
iast: yadetat parisaṅkhyātamādāveva caturyugam etaddvādaśasāhasraṃ devānāṃ yugamucyate

sanskrit_iast: daivikānāṃ yugānāṃ tu sahasraṃ parisaṅkhyayā brāhmamekamaharjñeyaṃ tāvatīṃ rātrimeva ca tāvatī rātrireva ca
meaning: दैविकानां युगानां तु सहस्रं परिसङ्ख्यया ब्राह्ममेकमहर्ज्ञेयं तावतीं रात्रिमेव च तावती रात्रिरेव च
text: The ‘Time-cycles’ of the Gods, one thousand in number, should be regarded as one ‘day’ of brahmā; and (his) ‘night’ also is of the same extent.
iast: daivikānāṃ yugānāṃ tu sahasraṃ parisaṅkhyayā brāhmamekamaharjñeyaṃ tāvatīṃ rātrimeva ca tāvatī rātrireva ca

sanskrit_iast: tadvai yugasahasrāntaṃ brāhmaṃ puṇyamaharviduḥ rātriṃ ca tāvatīmeva te 'horātravido janāḥ
meaning: तद्वै युगसहस्रान्तं ब्राह्मं पुण्यमहर्विदुः रात्रिं च तावतीमेव ते 'होरात्रविदो जनाः
text: Those who know the ‘Day of Brahmā’ as ending with the (said) thousand ‘time-cycles,’ and the ‘night’ also as of the same extent,—are people who alone know what is ‘day and night’, and acquire merit.
iast: tadvai yugasahasrāntaṃ brāhmaṃ puṇyamaharviduḥ rātriṃ ca tāvatīmeva te 'horātravido janāḥ

sanskrit_iast: tasya so 'harṃiśasyānte prasuptaḥ pratibudhyate pratibuddhaśca sṛjati manaḥ sadasadātmakam
meaning: तस्य सो 'हर्ंइशस्यान्ते प्रसुप्तः प्रतिबुध्यते प्रतिबुद्धश्च सृजति मनः सदसदात्मकम्
text: At the end of the said ‘Day and Night,’ Brahmā, who was asleep, wakes up, and ha vino woken up, he creates mind, which partakes op the nature op the existent and the non-existent.
iast: tasya so 'harṃiśasyānte prasuptaḥ pratibudhyate pratibuddhaśca sṛjati manaḥ sadasadātmakam

sanskrit_iast: manaḥ sṛṣṭiṃ vikurute codyamānaṃ sisṛkṣayā ākāśaṃ jāyate tasmāt tasya śabdaṃ guṇaṃ viduḥ
meaning: मनः सृष्टिं विकुरुते चोद्यमानं सिसृक्षया आकाशं जायते तस्मात् तस्य शब्दं गुणं विदुः
text: The ‘Mind’ impelled by (Brahmā’s) desire to create, evolves creation;—from out of that (Mind) is produced Ākāśa; of this they know sound to be the quality.
iast: manaḥ sṛṣṭiṃ vikurute codyamānaṃ sisṛkṣayā ākāśaṃ jāyate tasmāt tasya śabdaṃ guṇaṃ viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: ākāśāt tu vikurvāṇāt sarvagandhavahaḥ śuciḥ balavāñjāyate vāyuḥ sa vai sparśaguṇo mataḥ
meaning: आकाशात् तु विकुर्वाणात् सर्वगन्धवहः शुचिः बलवाञ्जायते वायुः स वै स्पर्शगुणो मतः
text: After Ākāśa, from out of the same evolvent [‘Mind’], there comes into existence the pure and potent Wind, the vehicle of all odours; and it is held to be endowed with the quality of Touch.
iast: ākāśāt tu vikurvāṇāt sarvagandhavahaḥ śuciḥ balavāñjāyate vāyuḥ sa vai sparśaguṇo mataḥ

sanskrit_iast: vāyorapi vikurvāṇādvirociṣṇu tamonudam jyotirutpadyate bhāsvat tadrūpaguṇamucyate
meaning: वायोरपि विकुर्वाणाद्विरोचिष्णु तमोनुदम् ज्योतिरुत्पद्यते भास्वत् तद्रूपगुणमुच्यते
text: After wind, from out of the same Evolvent, emanates the bright and radiant light, the dispeller of darkness; it is said to be endowed with the quality of colour.
iast: vāyorapi vikurvāṇādvirociṣṇu tamonudam jyotirutpadyate bhāsvat tadrūpaguṇamucyate

sanskrit_iast: jyotiṣaśca vikurvāṇādāpo rasaguṇāḥ smṛtāḥ adbhyo gandhaguṇā bhūmirityeṣā sṛṣṭirāditaḥ
meaning: ज्योतिषश्च विकुर्वाणादापो रसगुणाः स्मृताः अद्भ्यो गन्धगुणा भूमिरित्येषा सृष्टिरादितः
text: After light, from out of the same Evolvent, emanates water, which has been declared to be endowed with the quality of taste. and after water, comes earth, endowed with the quality of odour.—Such is creation at the outset.
iast: jyotiṣaśca vikurvāṇādāpo rasaguṇāḥ smṛtāḥ adbhyo gandhaguṇā bhūmirityeṣā sṛṣṭirāditaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadprāg dvādaśasāhasramuditaṃ daivikaṃ yugam tadekasaptatiguṇaṃ manvantaramihaucyate
meaning: यद्प्राग् द्वादशसाहस्रमुदितं दैविकं युगम् तदेकसप्ततिगुणं मन्वन्तरमिहौच्यते
text: The ‘Time-cycle of the Gods’ which has been described above as consisting of ‘twelve thousand periods,’—this multiplied by ‘seventy-one’ forms what is known here as ‘Manvantara’ (Regime of a Manu).
iast: yadprāg dvādaśasāhasramuditaṃ daivikaṃ yugam tadekasaptatiguṇaṃ manvantaramihaucyate

sanskrit_iast: manvantarāṇyasaṅkhyāni sargaḥ saṃhāra eva ca krīḍannivaitat kurute parameṣṭhī punaḥ punaḥ
meaning: मन्वन्तराण्यसङ्ख्यानि सर्गः संहार एव च क्रीडन्निवैतत् कुरुते परमेष्ठी पुनः पुनः
text: Innumerable Manvantaras, as also Creation and Dissolution—all this the supreme lord calls into being again and again, as if in amusement
iast: manvantarāṇyasaṅkhyāni sargaḥ saṃhāra eva ca krīḍannivaitat kurute parameṣṭhī punaḥ punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: catuṣpāt sakalo dharmaḥ satyaṃ caiva kṛte yuge nādharmeṇāgamaḥ kaścin manuṣyān prati vartate upavartate
meaning: चतुष्पात् सकलो धर्मः सत्यं चैव कृते युगे नाधर्मेणागमः कश्चिन् मनुष्यान् प्रति वर्तते उपवर्तते
text: In the Kṛta Cycle Virtue exists in its perfect form, with all its four feet; and so does Truth;—No benefit accrues to men by vice
iast: catuṣpāt sakalo dharmaḥ satyaṃ caiva kṛte yuge nādharmeṇāgamaḥ kaścin manuṣyān prati vartate upavartate

sanskrit_iast: itareṣvāgamāddharmaḥ pādaśastvavaropitaḥ caurikānṛtamāyābhirdharmaścāpaiti pādaśaḥ
meaning: इतरेष्वागमाद्धर्मः पादशस्त्ववरोपितः चौरिकानृतमायाभिर्धर्मश्चापैति पादशः
text: In the other Cycles, virtue fell off from the scriptures, foot by foot; and on account of theft, falsehood and fraud, virtuous acts deteriorated foot by foot
iast: itareṣvāgamāddharmaḥ pādaśastvavaropitaḥ caurikānṛtamāyābhirdharmaścāpaiti pādaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: arogāḥ sarvasiddhārthāścaturvarṣaśatāyuṣaḥ kṛte tretādiṣu hyeṣāmāyurhrasati pādaśaḥ vayo hrasati
meaning: अरोगाः सर्वसिद्धार्थाश्चतुर्वर्षशतायुषः कृते त्रेतादिषु ह्येषामायुर्ह्रसति पादशः वयो ह्रसति
text: During the Kṛta Cycle, men are free from disease, they have all their aims fulfilled, and their life lasts trhough four hundred years;—During the Tretā and other Cycles, their life becomes shortened, quarter by quarter.
iast: arogāḥ sarvasiddhārthāścaturvarṣaśatāyuṣaḥ kṛte tretādiṣu hyeṣāmāyurhrasati pādaśaḥ vayo hrasati

sanskrit_iast: vedauktamāyurmartyānāmāśiṣaścaiva karmaṇām phalantyanuyugaṃ loke prabhāvaśca śarīriṇām
meaning: वेदौक्तमायुर्मर्त्यानामाशिषश्चैव कर्मणाम् फलन्त्यनुयुगं लोके प्रभावश्च शरीरिणाम्
text: The full age of mortals spoken of in the Veda, the results of actions and the powers of embodied beings,—are obtained in accordance with the character of the Cycle.
iast: vedauktamāyurmartyānāmāśiṣaścaiva karmaṇām phalantyanuyugaṃ loke prabhāvaśca śarīriṇām

sanskrit_iast: anye kṛtayuge dharmāstretāyāṃ dvāpare 'pare pare anye kaliyuge nr̥̄ṇāṃ yugahrāsānurūpataḥ
meaning: अन्ये कृतयुगे धर्मास्त्रेतायां द्वापरे 'परे परे अन्ये कलियुगे न्र्̥̄णां युगह्रासानुरूपतः
text: During the Kṛta-cycle, the characteristics of men are of one kind,—of different kinds during the Tretā and the dvāpara,—and of yet another kind during the Kali-cycle;—this being due to the deterioration of each suceeding Cycle.
iast: anye kṛtayuge dharmāstretāyāṃ dvāpare 'pare pare anye kaliyuge nr̥̄ṇāṃ yugahrāsānurūpataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tapaḥ paraṃ kṛtayuge tretāyāṃ jñānamucyate dvāpare yajñamevāhurdānamekaṃ kalau yuge
meaning: तपः परं कृतयुगे त्रेतायां ज्ञानमुच्यते द्वापरे यज्ञमेवाहुर्दानमेकं कलौ युगे
text: In the Kṛta Cycle, ‘Austerity’ is the highest; in the Tretā ‘knowledge’ is described as such; in the Dvāpara they call the ‘Sacrifice’ the highest, and ‘Charity’ alone in the Kali-Cycle
iast: tapaḥ paraṃ kṛtayuge tretāyāṃ jñānamucyate dvāpare yajñamevāhurdānamekaṃ kalau yuge

sanskrit_iast: sarvasyāsya tu sargasya guptiarthaṃ sa mahādyutiḥ mukhabāhūrupajjānāṃ pṛthakkarmāṇyakalpayat
meaning: सर्वस्यास्य तु सर्गस्य गुप्तिअर्थं स महाद्युतिः मुखबाहूरुपज्जानां पृथक्कर्माण्यकल्पयत्
text: With a view to the protection of this entire creation, the Resplendent One ordained the distinct functions of those who sprang from the mouth, the arms, the thighs and the feet
iast: sarvasyāsya tu sargasya guptiarthaṃ sa mahādyutiḥ mukhabāhūrupajjānāṃ pṛthakkarmāṇyakalpayat

sanskrit_iast: adhyāpanamadhyayanaṃ yajanaṃ yājanaṃ tathā dānaṃ pratigrahaṃ caiva brāhmaṇānāmakalpayat
meaning: अध्यापनमध्ययनं यजनं याजनं तथा दानं प्रतिग्रहं चैव ब्राह्मणानामकल्पयत्
text: For the Brāhmaṇas he ordained teaching, studying, sacrificing and officiating at sacrifices, as also the giving and accepting of gifts.
iast: adhyāpanamadhyayanaṃ yajanaṃ yājanaṃ tathā dānaṃ pratigrahaṃ caiva brāhmaṇānāmakalpayat

sanskrit_iast: prajānāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ dānamijyā 'dhyayanameva ca viṣayeṣvaprasaktiśca kṣatriyasya samāsataḥ samādiśat
meaning: प्रजानां रक्षणं दानमिज्या 'ध्ययनमेव च विषयेष्वप्रसक्तिश्च क्षत्रियस्य समासतः समादिशत्
text: For the Kṣatriya he ordained protecting of the people, giving of gifts, sacrificing and studying, as also abstaining prom being addicted to the objects of sense.
iast: prajānāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ dānamijyā 'dhyayanameva ca viṣayeṣvaprasaktiśca kṣatriyasya samāsataḥ samādiśat

sanskrit_iast: paśūnāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ dānamijyā 'dhyayanameva ca vaṇikpathaṃ kusīdaṃ ca vaiśyasya kṛṣimeva ca
meaning: पशूनां रक्षणं दानमिज्या 'ध्ययनमेव च वणिक्पथं कुसीदं च वैश्यस्य कृषिमेव च
text: For the Vaiśya, tending of cattle, giving of gifts, sacrificing and studying; as also trade, money-lending and cultivating of land.
iast: paśūnāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ dānamijyā 'dhyayanameva ca vaṇikpathaṃ kusīdaṃ ca vaiśyasya kṛṣimeva ca

sanskrit_iast: ekameva tu śūdrasya prabhuḥ karma samādiśat eteṣāmeva varṇānāṃ śuśrūṣāmanasūyayā
meaning: एकमेव तु शूद्रस्य प्रभुः कर्म समादिशत् एतेषामेव वर्णानां शुश्रूषामनसूयया
text: For the Śūdra the Lord ordained only one function: the ungrudging service of the said castes.
iast: ekameva tu śūdrasya prabhuḥ karma samādiśat eteṣāmeva varṇānāṃ śuśrūṣāmanasūyayā

sanskrit_iast: ūrdhvaṃ nābhermedhyataraḥ puruṣaḥ parikīrtitaḥ tasmān medhyatamaṃ tvasya mukhamuktaṃ svayaṃbhuvā
meaning: ऊर्ध्वं नाभेर्मेध्यतरः पुरुषः परिकीर्तितः तस्मान् मेध्यतमं त्वस्य मुखमुक्तं स्वयंभुवा
text: Man is described as purer above his naval; hence the Self-existent One has declared the mouth to be his purest part.
iast: ūrdhvaṃ nābhermedhyataraḥ puruṣaḥ parikīrtitaḥ tasmān medhyatamaṃ tvasya mukhamuktaṃ svayaṃbhuvā

sanskrit_iast: uttamāṅgaudbhavāj jyeṣṭhyādbrahmaṇaścaiva dhāraṇāt jyaiṣṭhyād sarvasyaivāsya sargasya dharmato brāhmaṇaḥ prabhuḥ
meaning: उत्तमाङ्गौद्भवाज् ज्येष्ठ्याद्ब्रह्मणश्चैव धारणात् ज्यैष्ठ्याद् सर्वस्यैवास्य सर्गस्य धर्मतो ब्राह्मणः प्रभुः
text: In matters regauding ‘Dharma’, the Brāhmaṇa is the Lord of this whole world;—because he sprang out of the best part of (Prajāpati’s) body, because he is the eldest ok all, and because he upholds the Veda.
iast: uttamāṅgaudbhavāj jyeṣṭhyādbrahmaṇaścaiva dhāraṇāt jyaiṣṭhyād sarvasyaivāsya sargasya dharmato brāhmaṇaḥ prabhuḥ

sanskrit_iast: taṃ hi svayaṃbhūḥ svādāsyāt tapastaptvā 'dito 'sṛjat havyakavyābhivāhyāya sarvasyāsya ca guptaye
meaning: तं हि स्वयंभूः स्वादास्यात् तपस्तप्त्वा 'दितो 'सृजत् हव्यकव्याभिवाह्याय सर्वस्यास्य च गुप्तये
text: Him the Sself-existent one, after performing austerities, created, in the beginning, out of his own mouth, for the conveying of offerings (to the gods) and of oblations (to the Pitṛs), and for the preservation of this entire creation.
iast: taṃ hi svayaṃbhūḥ svādāsyāt tapastaptvā 'dito 'sṛjat havyakavyābhivāhyāya sarvasyāsya ca guptaye

sanskrit_iast: yasyāsyena sadā 'śnanti havyāni tridivokasaḥ kavyāni caiva pitaraḥ kiṃ bhūtamadhikaṃ tataḥ
meaning: यस्यास्येन सदा 'श्नन्ति हव्यानि त्रिदिवोकसः कव्यानि चैव पितरः किं भूतमधिकं ततः
text: What being is superior to him through whose mouth the gods always eat the offerings and the Pitṛs the oblations?
iast: yasyāsyena sadā 'śnanti havyāni tridivokasaḥ kavyāni caiva pitaraḥ kiṃ bhūtamadhikaṃ tataḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhūtānāṃ prāṇinaḥ śreṣṭhāḥ prāṇināṃ buddhijīvinaḥ buddhimatsu narāḥ śreṣṭhā nareṣu brāhmaṇāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: भूतानां प्राणिनः श्रेष्ठाः प्राणिनां बुद्धिजीविनः बुद्धिमत्सु नराः श्रेष्ठा नरेषु ब्राह्मणाः स्मृताः
text: Among beings, aminated ones are regarded as foremost; among aminated ones, those that subsist by reason: among rational beings men are foremost; and among men, Brāhmaṇas.
iast: bhūtānāṃ prāṇinaḥ śreṣṭhāḥ prāṇināṃ buddhijīvinaḥ buddhimatsu narāḥ śreṣṭhā nareṣu brāhmaṇāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇeṣu ca vidvāṃso vidvatsu kṛtabuddhayaḥ kṛtabuddhiṣu kartāraḥ kartṛṣu brahmavedinaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणेषु च विद्वांसो विद्वत्सु कृतबुद्धयः कृतबुद्धिषु कर्तारः कर्तृषु ब्रह्मवेदिनः
text: Among Brāhmaṇas, the learned are the best, among the learned, those with firm convictions, among the men with firm convictions, those that act up to them; and among the actors, those that know Brahman.
iast: brāhmaṇeṣu ca vidvāṃso vidvatsu kṛtabuddhayaḥ kṛtabuddhiṣu kartāraḥ kartṛṣu brahmavedinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: utpattireva viprasya mūrtirdharmasya śāśvatī sa hi dharmārthamutpanno brahmabhūyāya kalpate
meaning: उत्पत्तिरेव विप्रस्य मूर्तिर्धर्मस्य शाश्वती स हि धर्मार्थमुत्पन्नो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते
text: The very genesis of the Brāhmaṇa is the eternal incarnation of Virtue; for he is born for the sake of Virtue; and this (birth) leads to the state of Brahman.
iast: utpattireva viprasya mūrtirdharmasya śāśvatī sa hi dharmārthamutpanno brahmabhūyāya kalpate

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇo jāyamāno hi pṛthivyāmadhijāyate īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ dharmakośasya guptaye
meaning: ब्राह्मणो जायमानो हि पृथिव्यामधिजायते ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां धर्मकोशस्य गुप्तये
text: The Brāhmaṇa, on coming into existence, becomes supreme on earth; he is the supreme lord of all beings, serving the purpose of guarding the treasure of Virtue.
iast: brāhmaṇo jāyamāno hi pṛthivyāmadhijāyate īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ dharmakośasya guptaye

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ svaṃ brāhmaṇasyaidaṃ yat kiṃ citjagatīgatam śraiṣṭhyenābhijanenaidaṃ sarvaṃ vai brāhmaṇo 'rhati
meaning: सर्वं स्वं ब्राह्मणस्यैदं यत् किं चित्जगतीगतम् श्रैष्ठ्येनाभिजनेनैदं सर्वं वै ब्राह्मणो 'र्हति
text: Whatever is contained in this world is all the property of the Brāhmaṇa; the Brāhmaṇa verily deserves all by virtue of his superiority and noble birth.
iast: sarvaṃ svaṃ brāhmaṇasyaidaṃ yat kiṃ citjagatīgatam śraiṣṭhyenābhijanenaidaṃ sarvaṃ vai brāhmaṇo 'rhati

sanskrit_iast: svameva brāhmaṇo bhuṅkte svaṃ vaste svaṃ dadāti ca ānṛśaṃsyādbrāhmaṇasya bhuñjate hītare janāḥ
meaning: स्वमेव ब्राह्मणो भुङ्क्ते स्वं वस्ते स्वं ददाति च आनृशंस्याद्ब्राह्मणस्य भुञ्जते हीतरे जनाः
text: What the Brāhmana eats is his own; his own what he wears and his own also what he gives; it ts due to the good will of the Brāhmaṇa that other people enjoy (things).
iast: svameva brāhmaṇo bhuṅkte svaṃ vaste svaṃ dadāti ca ānṛśaṃsyādbrāhmaṇasya bhuñjate hītare janāḥ

sanskrit_iast: tasya karmavivekārthaṃ śeṣāṇāmanupūrvaśaḥ svāyaṃbhuvo manurdhīmānidaṃ śāstramakalpayat
meaning: तस्य कर्मविवेकार्थं शेषाणामनुपूर्वशः स्वायंभुवो मनुर्धीमानिदं शास्त्रमकल्पयत्
text: It was for the purpose of regulating the actions of the Brāhmaṇa,—and incidentally of others also,—that the wise Manu Svāyambhuva Elaborated these institutes.
iast: tasya karmavivekārthaṃ śeṣāṇāmanupūrvaśaḥ svāyaṃbhuvo manurdhīmānidaṃ śāstramakalpayat

sanskrit_iast: viduṣā brāhmaṇenaidamadhyetavyaṃ prayatnataḥ śiśyebhyaśca pravaktavyaṃ samyalg nānyena kena cit
meaning: विदुषा ब्राह्मणेनैदमध्येतव्यं प्रयत्नतः शिश्येभ्यश्च प्रवक्तव्यं सम्यल्ग् नान्येन केन चित्
text: This may he studied with care, and duly taught to pupils, by the learned Brāhmaṇa,—not by any one else.
iast: viduṣā brāhmaṇenaidamadhyetavyaṃ prayatnataḥ śiśyebhyaśca pravaktavyaṃ samyalg nānyena kena cit

sanskrit_iast: idaṃ śāstram adhīyāno brāhmaṇaḥ saṃśitavrataḥ manasvācdehajairnityaṃ karmadoṣairna lipyate
meaning: इदं शास्त्रम् अधीयानो ब्राह्मणः संशितव्रतः मनस्वाच्देहजैर्नित्यं कर्मदोषैर्न लिप्यते
text: The Brāhmaṇa studying these institutes, and (thence) discharging all prescribed duties, is never defiled by sins of commission (or omission), proceeding from mind, speech or body.
iast: idaṃ śāstram adhīyāno brāhmaṇaḥ saṃśitavrataḥ manasvācdehajairnityaṃ karmadoṣairna lipyate

sanskrit_iast: punāti paṅktiṃ vaṃśyāṃśca saptasapta parāvarān pṛthivīmapi caivaimāṃ kṛtsnāmeko 'pi so 'rhati
meaning: पुनाति पङ्क्तिं वंश्यांश्च सप्तसप्त परावरान् पृथिवीमपि चैवैमां कृत्स्नामेको 'पि सो 'र्हति
text: He purifies his company, and also his kindreds—seven higher (ancestors) and seven lower (descendants). he alone deserves this entire earth.
iast: punāti paṅktiṃ vaṃśyāṃśca saptasapta parāvarān pṛthivīmapi caivaimāṃ kṛtsnāmeko 'pi so 'rhati

sanskrit_iast: idaṃ svastyayanaṃ śreṣṭhamidaṃ buddhivivardhanam idaṃ yaśasyamāyuṣyaṃ idaṃ niḥśreyasaṃ param idaṃ yaśasyaṃ satatam
meaning: इदं स्वस्त्ययनं श्रेष्ठमिदं बुद्धिविवर्धनम् इदं यशस्यमायुष्यं इदं निःश्रेयसं परम् इदं यशस्यं सततम्
text: This (treatise) is ever conducive to welfare; it is most excellent; it expands the understanding brings fame and constitutes the highest good.
iast: idaṃ svastyayanaṃ śreṣṭhamidaṃ buddhivivardhanam idaṃ yaśasyamāyuṣyaṃ idaṃ niḥśreyasaṃ param idaṃ yaśasyaṃ satatam

sanskrit_iast: asmin dharmo 'khilenaukto guṇadoṣau ca karmaṇām caturṇāmapi varṇānāmācāraścaiva śāśvataḥ
meaning: अस्मिन् धर्मो 'खिलेनौक्तो गुणदोषौ च कर्मणाम् चतुर्णामपि वर्णानामाचारश्चैव शाश्वतः
text: Herein has been expounded Dharma in its entirety: the good and bad features of actions of all the four castes; as also eternal Morality.
iast: asmin dharmo 'khilenaukto guṇadoṣau ca karmaṇām caturṇāmapi varṇānāmācāraścaiva śāśvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācāraḥ paramo dharmaḥ śrutyoktaḥ smārta eva ca tasmādasmin sadā yukto nityaṃ syādātmavān dvijaḥ
meaning: आचारः परमो धर्मः श्रुत्योक्तः स्मार्त एव च तस्मादस्मिन् सदा युक्तो नित्यं स्यादात्मवान् द्विजः
text: Morality [Right Behaviour] is highest Dharma; that which is prescribed in the śruti and laid down in the Smṛti. hence the twice-born person, desiring the welfare of his soul, should be always intent upon Right Behaviour.
iast: ācāraḥ paramo dharmaḥ śrutyoktaḥ smārta eva ca tasmādasmin sadā yukto nityaṃ syādātmavān dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācārādvicyuto vipro na vedaphalamaśnute ācāreṇa tu saṃyuktaḥ sampūrṇaphalabhāj bhavet saṃpūrṇaphalabhāk smṛtaḥ
meaning: आचाराद्विच्युतो विप्रो न वेदफलमश्नुते आचारेण तु संयुक्तः सम्पूर्णफलभाज् भवेत् संपूर्णफलभाक् स्मृतः
text: The Brāhmaṇa who departs from Right Behaviour, does not obtain the fruit of the Veda; he however who is equipped with Right Behaviour obtains the full reward.
iast: ācārādvicyuto vipro na vedaphalamaśnute ācāreṇa tu saṃyuktaḥ sampūrṇaphalabhāj bhavet saṃpūrṇaphalabhāk smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: evamācārato dṛṣṭvā dharmasya munayo gatim sarvasya tapaso mūlamācāraṃ jagṛhuḥ param
meaning: एवमाचारतो दृष्ट्वा धर्मस्य मुनयो गतिम् सर्वस्य तपसो मूलमाचारं जगृहुः परम्
text: Having thus seen that virtue is got at from Right Behaviour, the sages regarded Right Behaviour as the very root of all Austerity.
iast: evamācārato dṛṣṭvā dharmasya munayo gatim sarvasya tapaso mūlamācāraṃ jagṛhuḥ param

sanskrit_iast: jagataśca samutpattiṃ saṃskāravidhimeva ca vratacaryaupacāraṃ ca snānasya ca paraṃ vidhim
meaning: जगतश्च समुत्पत्तिं संस्कारविधिमेव च व्रतचर्यौपचारं च स्नानस्य च परं विधिम्
text: The coming into existence of the world,—the rule relating to the sacramental Rites,—the method of keeping observances,—as also the excellent rules bearing upon the Final Ablution.
iast: jagataśca samutpattiṃ saṃskāravidhimeva ca vratacaryaupacāraṃ ca snānasya ca paraṃ vidhim

sanskrit_iast: dārādhigamanaṃ caiva vivāhānāṃ ca lakṣaṇam mahāyajñavidhānaṃ ca śrāddhakalpaṃ ca śāśvatam
meaning: दाराधिगमनं चैव विवाहानां च लक्षणम् महायज्ञविधानं च श्राद्धकल्पं च शाश्वतम्
text: The taking of wife,—the definition of the several forms of Marriage,—the method of the Great Sacrifices,—the eternal regulations relating to the offering toPitṛs.
iast: dārādhigamanaṃ caiva vivāhānāṃ ca lakṣaṇam mahāyajñavidhānaṃ ca śrāddhakalpaṃ ca śāśvatam

sanskrit_iast: vṛttīnāṃ lakṣaṇaṃ caiva snātakasya vratāni ca bhakṣyābhakṣyaṃ ca śaucaṃ ca dravyāṇāṃ śuddhimeva ca
meaning: वृत्तीनां लक्षणं चैव स्नातकस्य व्रतानि च भक्ष्याभक्ष्यं च शौचं च द्रव्याणां शुद्धिमेव च
text: The duty of the Vaiśya and the Śūdra,—the birth of the mixed castes—the duties of all castes during times of distress—the method of expiation.
iast: vṛttīnāṃ lakṣaṇaṃ caiva snātakasya vratāni ca bhakṣyābhakṣyaṃ ca śaucaṃ ca dravyāṇāṃ śuddhimeva ca

sanskrit_iast: strīdharmayogaṃ tāpasyaṃ mokṣaṃ saṃnyāsameva ca rājñaśca dharmamakhilaṃ kāryāṇāṃ ca vinirṇayam
meaning: स्त्रीधर्मयोगं तापस्यं मोक्षं संन्यासमेव च राज्ञश्च धर्ममखिलं कार्याणां च विनिर्णयम्
text: The duty of the Vaiśya and the Śūdra,—the birth of the mixed castes—the duties of all castes during times of distress—the method of expiation.
iast: strīdharmayogaṃ tāpasyaṃ mokṣaṃ saṃnyāsameva ca rājñaśca dharmamakhilaṃ kāryāṇāṃ ca vinirṇayam

sanskrit_iast: sākṣipraśnavidhānaṃ ca dharmaṃ strīpuṃsayorapi vibhāgadharmaṃ dyūtaṃ ca kaṇṭakānāṃ ca śodhanam
meaning: साक्षिप्रश्नविधानं च धर्मं स्त्रीपुंसयोरपि विभागधर्मं द्यूतं च कण्टकानां च शोधनम्
text: The duty of the Vaiśya and the Śūdra,—the birth of the mixed castes—the duties of all castes during times of distress—the method of expiation.
iast: sākṣipraśnavidhānaṃ ca dharmaṃ strīpuṃsayorapi vibhāgadharmaṃ dyūtaṃ ca kaṇṭakānāṃ ca śodhanam

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyaśūdraupacāraṃ ca saṅkīrṇānāṃ ca saṃbhavam āpaddharmaṃ ca varṇānāṃ prāyaścittavidhiṃ tathā
meaning: वैश्यशूद्रौपचारं च सङ्कीर्णानां च संभवम् आपद्धर्मं च वर्णानां प्रायश्चित्तविधिं तथा
text: The duty of the Vaiśya and the Śūdra,—the birth of the mixed castes—the duties of all castes during times of distress—the method of expiation.
iast: vaiśyaśūdraupacāraṃ ca saṅkīrṇānāṃ ca saṃbhavam āpaddharmaṃ ca varṇānāṃ prāyaścittavidhiṃ tathā

sanskrit_iast: saṃsāragamanaṃ caiva trividhaṃ karmasaṃbhavam niḥśreyasaṃ karmaṇāṃ ca guṇadoṣaparīkṣaṇam
meaning: संसारगमनं चैव त्रिविधं कर्मसंभवम् निःश्रेयसं कर्मणां च गुणदोषपरीक्षणम्
text: The threefold transmigration of the Soul, arising from actions,—the highest good,—and the examination of the good and bad features of actions.
iast: saṃsāragamanaṃ caiva trividhaṃ karmasaṃbhavam niḥśreyasaṃ karmaṇāṃ ca guṇadoṣaparīkṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: deśadharmānjātidharmān kuladharmāṃśca śāśvatān pāṣaṇḍagaṇadharmāṃśca śāstre 'sminnuktavān manuḥ
meaning: देशधर्मान्जातिधर्मान् कुलधर्मांश्च शाश्वतान् पाषण्डगणधर्मांश्च शास्त्रे 'स्मिन्नुक्तवान् मनुः
text: The eternal laws op countries, duties op castes and laws of dynasties,—also the laws relating to heretics and to guilds,—all this manu has expounded in these Institutes.
iast: deśadharmānjātidharmān kuladharmāṃśca śāśvatān pāṣaṇḍagaṇadharmāṃśca śāstre 'sminnuktavān manuḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathaidamuktavāṃśāstraṃ purā pṛṣṭo manurmayā tathaidaṃ yūyamapyadya matsakāśātnibodhata
meaning: यथैदमुक्तवांशास्त्रं पुरा पृष्टो मनुर्मया तथैदं यूयमप्यद्य मत्सकाशात्निबोधत
text: One should not sit with a superior upon the couch or seat prepared for him. and if he himself should happen to be seated on a couch or skat, he should rise to meet (the superior) and salute him.
iast: yathaidamuktavāṃśāstraṃ purā pṛṣṭo manurmayā tathaidaṃ yūyamapyadya matsakāśātnibodhata

sanskrit_iast: vidvadbhiḥ sevitaḥ sadbhirnityamadveṣarāgibhiḥ hṛdayenābhyanujñāto yo dharmastaṃ nibodhata
meaning: विद्वद्भिः सेवितः सद्भिर्नित्यमद्वेषरागिभिः हृदयेनाभ्यनुज्ञातो यो धर्मस्तं निबोधत
text: Learn that Dharma, which has been ever followed by, and sanctioned by the heart of, the learned and the good, who are free from love and hate.
iast: vidvadbhiḥ sevitaḥ sadbhirnityamadveṣarāgibhiḥ hṛdayenābhyanujñāto yo dharmastaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: kāmātmatā na praśastā na caivaihāstyakāmatā kāmyo hi vedādhigamaḥ karmayogaśca vaidikaḥ
meaning: कामात्मता न प्रशस्ता न चैवैहास्त्यकामता काम्यो हि वेदाधिगमः कर्मयोगश्च वैदिकः
text: It is not right to be absorbed in desires—“But there is in this world, no absolute absence of desire; for the study of the Vedas itself is prompted by desire, as also every act prescribed in the Veda.”
iast: kāmātmatā na praśastā na caivaihāstyakāmatā kāmyo hi vedādhigamaḥ karmayogaśca vaidikaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṅkalpamūlaḥ kāmo vai yajñāḥ saṅkalpasaṃbhavāḥ vratāni yamadharmāśca sarve saṅkalpajāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: सङ्कल्पमूलः कामो वै यज्ञाः सङ्कल्पसंभवाः व्रतानि यमधर्माश्च सर्वे सङ्कल्पजाः स्मृताः
text: Desire has its root in Thought; Sacrifices proceed from Thought; Vows and Restraints—all these have been described as originating in Thought.
iast: saṅkalpamūlaḥ kāmo vai yajñāḥ saṅkalpasaṃbhavāḥ vratāni yamadharmāśca sarve saṅkalpajāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: akāmasya kriyā kā ciddṛśyate naiha karhi cit yadyaddhi kurute kiṃ cit tat tat kāmasya ceṣṭitam
meaning: अकामस्य क्रिया का चिद्दृश्यते नैह कर्हि चित् यद्यद्धि कुरुते किं चित् तत् तत् कामस्य चेष्टितम्
text: No action is ever found in this world to be done by a man entirely without desires; whatever a man does is. the outcome of desire.
iast: akāmasya kriyā kā ciddṛśyate naiha karhi cit yadyaddhi kurute kiṃ cit tat tat kāmasya ceṣṭitam

sanskrit_iast: teṣu samyag vartamāno gacchatyamaralokatām yathā saṅkalpitāṃścaiha sarvān kāmān samaśnute
meaning: तेषु सम्यग् वर्तमानो गच्छत्यमरलोकताम् यथा सङ्कल्पितांश्चैह सर्वान् कामान् समश्नुते
text: Behaving in the right manner, in regard to these (desires), a man attains the position of Immortals; and even in this world he obtains all the desires that he may have thought of.
iast: teṣu samyag vartamāno gacchatyamaralokatām yathā saṅkalpitāṃścaiha sarvān kāmān samaśnute

sanskrit_iast: vedo 'khilo dharmamūlaṃ smṛtiśīle ca tadvidām ācāraścaiva sādhūnāmātmanastuṣṭireva ca
meaning: वेदो 'खिलो धर्ममूलं स्मृतिशीले च तद्विदाम् आचारश्चैव साधूनामात्मनस्तुष्टिरेव च
text: The entire Veda is the root-source of Dharma; also the Conscientious Recollection of righteous persons versed in the Veda, the Practice of Good (and learned) Men, and their self-satisfaction.
iast: vedo 'khilo dharmamūlaṃ smṛtiśīle ca tadvidām ācāraścaiva sādhūnāmātmanastuṣṭireva ca

sanskrit_iast: yaḥ kaścit kasya ciddharmo manunā parikīrtitaḥ sa sarvo 'bhihito vede sarvajñānamayo hi saḥ
meaning: यः कश्चित् कस्य चिद्धर्मो मनुना परिकीर्तितः स सर्वो 'भिहितो वेदे सर्वज्ञानमयो हि सः
text: Whatever Dharma for whatever person has been described by Manu,—all this is declared in the Veda; since the Veda embodies all knowledge.
iast: yaḥ kaścit kasya ciddharmo manunā parikīrtitaḥ sa sarvo 'bhihito vede sarvajñānamayo hi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ tu samavekṣyaidaṃ nikhilaṃ jñānacakṣuṣā śrutiprāmāṇyato vidvān svadharme niviśeta vai
meaning: सर्वं तु समवेक्ष्यैदं निखिलं ज्ञानचक्षुषा श्रुतिप्रामाण्यतो विद्वान् स्वधर्मे निविशेत वै
text: Having fully perceived all this with the eye of knowledge, the learned man should enter upon his own duties, resting upon the authority of the Revealed Word.
iast: sarvaṃ tu samavekṣyaidaṃ nikhilaṃ jñānacakṣuṣā śrutiprāmāṇyato vidvān svadharme niviśeta vai

sanskrit_iast: śrutismṛtyoditaṃ dharmamanutiṣṭhan hi mānavaḥ iha kīrtimavāpnoti pretya cānuttamaṃ sukham
meaning: श्रुतिस्मृत्योदितं धर्ममनुतिष्ठन् हि मानवः इह कीर्तिमवाप्नोति प्रेत्य चानुत्तमं सुखम्
text: For the man performing the duty laid down by the revealed word and the recollections obtains fame here, and after death, unsurpassed happiness.
iast: śrutismṛtyoditaṃ dharmamanutiṣṭhan hi mānavaḥ iha kīrtimavāpnoti pretya cānuttamaṃ sukham

sanskrit_iast: śrutistu vedo vijñeyo dharmaśāstraṃ tu vai smṛtiḥ te sarvārtheṣvamīmāṃsye tābhyāṃ dharmo hi nirbabhau
meaning: श्रुतिस्तु वेदो विज्ञेयो धर्मशास्त्रं तु वै स्मृतिः ते सर्वार्थेष्वमीमांस्ये ताभ्यां धर्मो हि निर्बभौ
text: The Veda should be known as the ‘revealed word,’ and the Dharmaśāstra as the ‘recollections’; in all matters, these two do not deserve to be criticised, as it is out op these that Dharma shone forth.
iast: śrutistu vedo vijñeyo dharmaśāstraṃ tu vai smṛtiḥ te sarvārtheṣvamīmāṃsye tābhyāṃ dharmo hi nirbabhau

sanskrit_iast: yo 'vamanyeta te mūle hetuśāstrāśrayāddvijaḥ sa sādhubhirbahiṣkāryo nāstiko vedanindakaḥ
meaning: यो 'वमन्येत ते मूले हेतुशास्त्राश्रयाद्द्विजः स साधुभिर्बहिष्कार्यो नास्तिको वेदनिन्दकः
text: If a twice-born person, relying upon the science of dialectics, should disregard these two sources, he should be cast out by good men,—the detractor of the Veda being an infidel.
iast: yo 'vamanyeta te mūle hetuśāstrāśrayāddvijaḥ sa sādhubhirbahiṣkāryo nāstiko vedanindakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vedaḥ smṛtiḥ sadācāraḥ svasya ca priyamātmanaḥ etaccaturvidhaṃ prāhuḥ sākṣāddharmasya lakṣaṇam
meaning: वेदः स्मृतिः सदाचारः स्वस्य च प्रियमात्मनः एतच्चतुर्विधं प्राहुः साक्षाद्धर्मस्य लक्षणम्
text: The Veda, the Smṛti, the Practice of cultured Men, and what is agreeable to oneself—these directly constitute the fourfold means of knowing Dharma.
iast: vedaḥ smṛtiḥ sadācāraḥ svasya ca priyamātmanaḥ etaccaturvidhaṃ prāhuḥ sākṣāddharmasya lakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: arthakāmeṣvasaktānāṃ dharmajñānaṃ vidhīyate dharmaṃ jijñāsamānānāṃ pramāṇaṃ paramaṃ śrutiḥ
meaning: अर्थकामेष्वसक्तानां धर्मज्ञानं विधीयते धर्मं जिज्ञासमानानां प्रमाणं परमं श्रुतिः
text: The knowledge of Dharma is ordained for those who are not addicted to the pursuit of wealth and pleasures; and for those seeking for the knowledge of Dharma, the Revealed Word is the highest authority.
iast: arthakāmeṣvasaktānāṃ dharmajñānaṃ vidhīyate dharmaṃ jijñāsamānānāṃ pramāṇaṃ paramaṃ śrutiḥ

sanskrit_iast: śrutidvaidhaṃ tu yatra syāt tatra dharmāvubhau smṛtau ubhāvapi hi tau dharmau samyaguktau manīṣibhiḥ
meaning: श्रुतिद्वैधं तु यत्र स्यात् तत्र धर्मावुभौ स्मृतौ उभावपि हि तौ धर्मौ सम्यगुक्तौ मनीषिभिः
text: Where there is conflict between two Vedic texts, both are held to be Dharma; both have been rightly pronounced by the wise to be Dharma.
iast: śrutidvaidhaṃ tu yatra syāt tatra dharmāvubhau smṛtau ubhāvapi hi tau dharmau samyaguktau manīṣibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: udite 'nudite caiva samayādhyuṣite tathā sarvathā vartate yajña itīyaṃ vaidikī śrutiḥ
meaning: उदिते 'नुदिते चैव समयाध्युषिते तथा सर्वथा वर्तते यज्ञ इतीयं वैदिकी श्रुतिः
text: At sunrise, or before sunrise, or at early dawn,—the sacrificial act may be performed at any time,—such is the pronouncement of the Veda.
iast: udite 'nudite caiva samayādhyuṣite tathā sarvathā vartate yajña itīyaṃ vaidikī śrutiḥ

sanskrit_iast: niṣekādiśmaśānānto mantrairyasyaudito vidhiḥ tasya śāstre 'dhikāro 'smiñ jñeyo nānyasya kasya cit
meaning: निषेकादिश्मशानान्तो मन्त्रैर्यस्यौदितो विधिः तस्य शास्त्रे 'धिकारो 'स्मिञ् ज्ञेयो नान्यस्य कस्य चित्
text: That person alone, and none other, should be regarded as entitled to the scripture, for whom the sacraments beginning with conception and ending with the crematorium, are prescribed as to be done with mantras.
iast: niṣekādiśmaśānānto mantrairyasyaudito vidhiḥ tasya śāstre 'dhikāro 'smiñ jñeyo nānyasya kasya cit

sanskrit_iast: sarasvatīdṛśadvatyordevanadyoryadantaram taṃ devanirmitaṃ deśaṃ brahmāvartaṃ pracakṣate
meaning: सरस्वतीदृशद्वत्योर्देवनद्योर्यदन्तरम् तं देवनिर्मितं देशं ब्रह्मावर्तं प्रचक्षते
text: The region lying between the divine rivers Sarasvati and Dṛṣasvatī which has been created by the gods,—they call ‘Brahmāvarta’
iast: sarasvatīdṛśadvatyordevanadyoryadantaram taṃ devanirmitaṃ deśaṃ brahmāvartaṃ pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: tasmin deśe ya ācāraḥ pāramparyakramāgataḥ varṇānāṃ sāntarālānāṃ sa sadācāra ucyate
meaning: तस्मिन् देशे य आचारः पारम्पर्यक्रमागतः वर्णानां सान्तरालानां स सदाचार उच्यते
text: That practice, which has comb down through an unbroken line op tradition among the several castes and subcastes in that country, is called the ‘Practice of Good Men.’
iast: tasmin deśe ya ācāraḥ pāramparyakramāgataḥ varṇānāṃ sāntarālānāṃ sa sadācāra ucyate

sanskrit_iast: kurukṣetraṃ ca matsyāśca pañcālāḥ śūrasenakāḥ eṣa brahmarṣideśo vai brahmāvartādanantaraḥ
meaning: कुरुक्षेत्रं च मत्स्याश्च पञ्चालाः शूरसेनकाः एष ब्रह्मर्षिदेशो वै ब्रह्मावर्तादनन्तरः
text: Next to Brahmāvarta is the ‘Brahmarṣideśa,’ comprising the regions op Kurukṣetra, Matsyas, Pañchālas and Śukasenakas.
iast: kurukṣetraṃ ca matsyāśca pañcālāḥ śūrasenakāḥ eṣa brahmarṣideśo vai brahmāvartādanantaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: etaddeśaprasūtasya sakāśādagrajanmanaḥ svaṃ svaṃ caritraṃ śikṣeran pṛthivyāṃ sarvamānavāḥ
meaning: एतद्देशप्रसूतस्य सकाशादग्रजन्मनः स्वं स्वं चरित्रं शिक्षेरन् पृथिव्यां सर्वमानवाः
text: All men on the earth may learn their respective duties from the Brāhmaṇa born in these countries.
iast: etaddeśaprasūtasya sakāśādagrajanmanaḥ svaṃ svaṃ caritraṃ śikṣeran pṛthivyāṃ sarvamānavāḥ

sanskrit_iast: himavadvindhyayormadhyaṃ yat prāg vinaśanādapi pratyageva prayāgācca madhyadeśaḥ prakīrtitaḥ
meaning: हिमवद्विन्ध्ययोर्मध्यं यत् प्राग् विनशनादपि प्रत्यगेव प्रयागाच्च मध्यदेशः प्रकीर्तितः
text: The country lying between the Himālaya and the Vindhya, to the east of Vinaśana and to the west of Prayāga, is called the ‘Madhyadeśa,’ the ‘Middle Country.’
iast: himavadvindhyayormadhyaṃ yat prāg vinaśanādapi pratyageva prayāgācca madhyadeśaḥ prakīrtitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ā samudrāt tu vai pūrvādā samudrācca paścimāt tayorevāntaraṃ giryorāryāvartaṃ vidurbudhāḥ
meaning: आ समुद्रात् तु वै पूर्वादा समुद्राच्च पश्चिमात् तयोरेवान्तरं गिर्योरार्यावर्तं विदुर्बुधाः
text: The country extending as far as the Eastern Ocean and as far as the Western Ocean, and lying between the same two mountains,—the learned know as ‘Āryāvarta.’
iast: ā samudrāt tu vai pūrvādā samudrācca paścimāt tayorevāntaraṃ giryorāryāvartaṃ vidurbudhāḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṛṣṇasārastu carati mṛgo yatra svabhāvataḥ sa jñeyo yajñiyo deśo mlecchadeśastvataḥ paraḥ
meaning: कृष्णसारस्तु चरति मृगो यत्र स्वभावतः स ज्ञेयो यज्ञियो देशो म्लेच्छदेशस्त्वतः परः
text: But the region where the spotted deer roams by nature is to be known as the ‘land fit for sacrificial acts’; beyond that is the ‘land op the Mlecchas.’
iast: kṛṣṇasārastu carati mṛgo yatra svabhāvataḥ sa jñeyo yajñiyo deśo mlecchadeśastvataḥ paraḥ

sanskrit_iast: etāṇ dvijātayo deśān saṃśrayeran prayatnataḥ śūdrastu yasmin kasmin vā nivasedvṛttikarśitaḥ yasmiṃstasmin vā
meaning: एताण् द्विजातयो देशान् संश्रयेरन् प्रयत्नतः शूद्रस्तु यस्मिन् कस्मिन् वा निवसेद्वृत्तिकर्शितः यस्मिंस्तस्मिन् वा
text: The twice-born people should seek to resort to these countries; the Śūdra may however, when distressed for a living, reside in any land.
iast: etāṇ dvijātayo deśān saṃśrayeran prayatnataḥ śūdrastu yasmin kasmin vā nivasedvṛttikarśitaḥ yasmiṃstasmin vā

sanskrit_iast: eṣā dharmasya vo yoniḥ samāsena prakīrtitā saṃbhavaścāsya sarvasya varṇadharmānnibodhata
meaning: एषा धर्मस्य वो योनिः समासेन प्रकीर्तिता संभवश्चास्य सर्वस्य वर्णधर्मान्निबोधत
text: Thus has the source of Dharma been briefly explained to you, as also the origin of all this (world). Learn now the duties of the several castes.
iast: eṣā dharmasya vo yoniḥ samāsena prakīrtitā saṃbhavaścāsya sarvasya varṇadharmānnibodhata

sanskrit_iast: vaidikaiḥ karmabhiḥ puṇyairniṣekādirdvijanmanām kāryaḥ śarīrasaṃskāraḥ pāvanaḥ pretya caiha ca
meaning: वैदिकैः कर्मभिः पुण्यैर्निषेकादिर्द्विजन्मनाम् कार्यः शरीरसंस्कारः पावनः प्रेत्य चैह च
text: For the twice-born persons corporeal consecration, beginning with ‘Conception,’ should be performed with auspicious Vedic rites; it purifies in this world and also after death.
iast: vaidikaiḥ karmabhiḥ puṇyairniṣekādirdvijanmanām kāryaḥ śarīrasaṃskāraḥ pāvanaḥ pretya caiha ca

sanskrit_iast: gārbhairhomairjātakarmacauḍamauñjīnibandhanaiḥ baijikaṃ gārbhikaṃ caino dvijānāmapamṛjyate
meaning: गार्भैर्होमैर्जातकर्मचौडमौञ्जीनिबन्धनैः बैजिकं गार्भिकं चैनो द्विजानामपमृज्यते
text: Of twice-born men the taint of seed and womb is removed by the ‘Libations in connection with Pregnancy’ and by ‘Jātakarman’ (Rites attendant upon birth), ‘Chauḍa’ (Tonsure) and ‘Mauñjībandhana’ (Tying of the grass-girdle).
iast: gārbhairhomairjātakarmacauḍamauñjīnibandhanaiḥ baijikaṃ gārbhikaṃ caino dvijānāmapamṛjyate

sanskrit_iast: svādhyāyena vratairhomaistraividyenaijyayā sutaiḥ mahāyajñaiśca yajñaiśca brāhmīiyaṃ kriyate tanuḥ
meaning: स्वाध्यायेन व्रतैर्होमैस्त्रैविद्येनैज्यया सुतैः महायज्ञैश्च यज्ञैश्च ब्राह्मीइयं क्रियते तनुः
text: This body is made godly,—by a thorough study of the three Vedas, by Observances, by libations, by offerings, by children, by the Great Sacrifices and by the Sacrifices.
iast: svādhyāyena vratairhomaistraividyenaijyayā sutaiḥ mahāyajñaiśca yajñaiśca brāhmīiyaṃ kriyate tanuḥ

sanskrit_iast: prālg nābhivardhanāt puṃso jātakarma vidhīyate mantravat prāśanaṃ cāsya hiraṇyamadhusarpiṣām
meaning: प्राल्ग् नाभिवर्धनात् पुंसो जातकर्म विधीयते मन्त्रवत् प्राशनं चास्य हिरण्यमधुसर्पिषाम्
text: For the male child, before the cutting of the umbilical cord the performance of the Jāta-karma (Birth-rite) has been ordained: (it consists of) the feeding of him with gold, honey and butter, to the accompaniment of Mantras.
iast: prālg nābhivardhanāt puṃso jātakarma vidhīyate mantravat prāśanaṃ cāsya hiraṇyamadhusarpiṣām

sanskrit_iast: nāmadheyaṃ daśamyāṃ tu dvādaśyāṃ vā 'sya kārayet puṇye tithau muhūrte vā nakṣatre vā guṇānvite
meaning: नामधेयं दशम्यां तु द्वादश्यां वा 'स्य कारयेत् पुण्ये तिथौ मुहूर्ते वा नक्षत्रे वा गुणान्विते
text: One should have his ‘naming’ (Nāmadheya) done on the tenth or the twelfth (day), on an auspicious lunar date and at an auspicious moment. and under a propitious lunar asterism.
iast: nāmadheyaṃ daśamyāṃ tu dvādaśyāṃ vā 'sya kārayet puṇye tithau muhūrte vā nakṣatre vā guṇānvite

sanskrit_iast: maṅgalyaṃ brāhmaṇasya syāt kṣatriyasya balānvitam vaiśyasya dhanasaṃyuktaṃ śūdrasya tu jugupsitam
meaning: मङ्गल्यं ब्राह्मणस्य स्यात् क्षत्रियस्य बलान्वितम् वैश्यस्य धनसंयुक्तं शूद्रस्य तु जुगुप्सितम्
text: The name of the Brāhmaṇa should be auspicious, that of the Kṣatriya connected with power, that of the Vaiśya associated with wealth; while that of the Śūdra contemptible.
iast: maṅgalyaṃ brāhmaṇasya syāt kṣatriyasya balānvitam vaiśyasya dhanasaṃyuktaṃ śūdrasya tu jugupsitam

sanskrit_iast: śarmavadbrāhmaṇasya syādrājño rakṣāsamanvitam rājñā ? vaiśyasya puṣṭisaṃyuktaṃ śūdrasya preṣyasaṃyutam
meaning: शर्मवद्ब्राह्मणस्य स्याद्राज्ञो रक्षासमन्वितम् राज्ञा ? वैश्यस्य पुष्टिसंयुक्तं शूद्रस्य प्रेष्यसंयुतम्
text: The name of the Brāhmaṇa should be expressive of ‘peace,’ that of the Kṣatriya, of ‘protection’; that ot the Vaiśya, of ‘prosperity,’ and that of the Śūdra, of ‘submissiveness.’
iast: śarmavadbrāhmaṇasya syādrājño rakṣāsamanvitam rājñā ? vaiśyasya puṣṭisaṃyuktaṃ śūdrasya preṣyasaṃyutam

sanskrit_iast: strīṇāṃ sukhaudyamakrūraṃ vispaṣṭārthaṃ manoharam maṅgalyaṃ dīrghavarṇāntamāśīrvādābhidhānavat
meaning: स्त्रीणां सुखौद्यमक्रूरं विस्पष्टार्थं मनोहरम् मङ्गल्यं दीर्घवर्णान्तमाशीर्वादाभिधानवत्
text: That of women should be easily pronouncible, not harsh, of plain meaning, heart-captivating and auspicious; it should end in a long vowel and contain a benedictory term.
iast: strīṇāṃ sukhaudyamakrūraṃ vispaṣṭārthaṃ manoharam maṅgalyaṃ dīrghavarṇāntamāśīrvādābhidhānavat

sanskrit_iast: caturthe māsi kartavyaṃ śiśorniṣkramaṇaṃ gṛhāt ṣaṣṭhe 'nnaprāśanaṃ māsi yadvaiṣṭaṃ maṅgalaṃ kule
meaning: चतुर्थे मासि कर्तव्यं शिशोर्निष्क्रमणं गृहात् षष्ठे 'न्नप्राशनं मासि यद्वैष्टं मङ्गलं कुले
text: In the fourth month should be performed the ceremony of the child’s ‘Egress’ from the room; and in the sixth month the ceremony of ‘Feeding’; or, whatever might be regarded as auspicious in the family.
iast: caturthe māsi kartavyaṃ śiśorniṣkramaṇaṃ gṛhāt ṣaṣṭhe 'nnaprāśanaṃ māsi yadvaiṣṭaṃ maṅgalaṃ kule

sanskrit_iast: cūḍākarma dvijātīnāṃ sarveṣāmeva dharmataḥ prathame 'bde tṛtīye vā kartavyaṃ śruticodanāt śrutinodanāt
meaning: चूडाकर्म द्विजातीनां सर्वेषामेव धर्मतः प्रथमे 'ब्दे तृतीये वा कर्तव्यं श्रुतिचोदनात् श्रुतिनोदनात्
text: In view or the injunctions or the Veda, the Tonsure-ceremony of all twice-born children should be performed, according to law, in the first year or the third.
iast: cūḍākarma dvijātīnāṃ sarveṣāmeva dharmataḥ prathame 'bde tṛtīye vā kartavyaṃ śruticodanāt śrutinodanāt

sanskrit_iast: garbhāṣṭame 'bde kurvīta brāhmaṇasyaupanāyanam garbhādekādaśe rājño garbhāt tu dvādaśe viśaḥ
meaning: गर्भाष्टमे 'ब्दे कुर्वीत ब्राह्मणस्यौपनायनम् गर्भादेकादशे राज्ञो गर्भात् तु द्वादशे विशः
text: In the eighth year from conception one should perform the initiation of the Brāhmaṇa; of the king in the eleventh year from conception; and of the Vaiśya in the twelfth.
iast: garbhāṣṭame 'bde kurvīta brāhmaṇasyaupanāyanam garbhādekādaśe rājño garbhāt tu dvādaśe viśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brahmavarcasakāmasya kāryo viprasya pañcame rājño balārthinaḥ ṣaṣṭhe vaiśyasyaihārthino 'ṣṭame
meaning: ब्रह्मवर्चसकामस्य कार्यो विप्रस्य पञ्चमे राज्ञो बलार्थिनः षष्ठे वैश्यस्यैहार्थिनो 'ष्टमे
text: For the Brāhmaṇa desirous of Brahmic glory, it should be done in the fifth year; for the ‘King’ desirous of power, in the sixth; and for the Vaiśya desirous of business, in the eighth.
iast: brahmavarcasakāmasya kāryo viprasya pañcame rājño balārthinaḥ ṣaṣṭhe vaiśyasyaihārthino 'ṣṭame

sanskrit_iast: ā ṣodaśādbrāhmaṇasya sāvitrī nātivartate ā dvāviṃśāt kṣatrabandhorā caturviṃśaterviśaḥ
meaning: आ षोदशाद्ब्राह्मणस्य सावित्री नातिवर्तते आ द्वाविंशात् क्षत्रबन्धोरा चतुर्विंशतेर्विशः
text: For the Brāhmaṇa the Sāvitrī does not lapse till the sixteenth year; for the Kṣatriya till the twenty-second year; and for the Vaiśya till the twenty-fourth year.
iast: ā ṣodaśādbrāhmaṇasya sāvitrī nātivartate ā dvāviṃśāt kṣatrabandhorā caturviṃśaterviśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ata ūrdhvaṃ trayo 'pyete yathākālamasaṃskṛtāḥ sāvitrīpatitā vrātyā bhavantyāryavigarhitāḥ
meaning: अत ऊर्ध्वं त्रयो 'प्येते यथाकालमसंस्कृताः सावित्रीपतिता व्रात्या भवन्त्यार्यविगर्हिताः
text: Beyond this, all these three, not having received the sacrament at the proper time, become excluded from Sāvitrī (initiation), and thereby come to be known as ‘Vrātyas’ (apostates), despised by all good men.
iast: ata ūrdhvaṃ trayo 'pyete yathākālamasaṃskṛtāḥ sāvitrīpatitā vrātyā bhavantyāryavigarhitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: naitairapūtairvidhivadāpadyapi hi karhi cit brāhmān yaunāṃśca saṃbandhānnācaredbrāhmaṇaḥ saha brāhmaṇaiḥ saha
meaning: नैतैरपूतैर्विधिवदापद्यपि हि कर्हि चित् ब्राह्मान् यौनांश्च संबन्धान्नाचरेद्ब्राह्मणः सह ब्राह्मणैः सह
text: The Brāhmaṇa should not in ant case, even in times of distress, establish spiritual or uterine relationship with these persons, until they have been duly purified.
iast: naitairapūtairvidhivadāpadyapi hi karhi cit brāhmān yaunāṃśca saṃbandhānnācaredbrāhmaṇaḥ saha brāhmaṇaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: kārṣṇarauravabāstāni carmāṇi brahmacāriṇaḥ vasīrannānupūrvyeṇa śāṇakṣaumāvikāni ca
meaning: कार्ष्णरौरवबास्तानि चर्माणि ब्रह्मचारिणः वसीरन्नानुपूर्व्येण शाणक्षौमाविकानि च
text: Brahmacāris should wear the skin of the black (deer), of the Ruru deer and of the goat respectively; and also the cloth of hemp, flax and wool.’
iast: kārṣṇarauravabāstāni carmāṇi brahmacāriṇaḥ vasīrannānupūrvyeṇa śāṇakṣaumāvikāni ca

sanskrit_iast: mauñjī trivṛt samā ślakṣṇā kāryā viprasya mekhalā kṣatriyasya tu maurvī jyā vaiśyasya śaṇatāntavī
meaning: मौञ्जी त्रिवृत् समा श्लक्ष्णा कार्या विप्रस्य मेखला क्षत्रियस्य तु मौर्वी ज्या वैश्यस्य शणतान्तवी
text: For, the Brāhmaṇa the girdle should be threefold, of even thickness, soft and smooth, made of muñja grass; for the Kṣatriya it should be the bow-string made of murvā grass; and for the Vaiśya the cord made of hempen fibres.”
iast: mauñjī trivṛt samā ślakṣṇā kāryā viprasya mekhalā kṣatriyasya tu maurvī jyā vaiśyasya śaṇatāntavī

sanskrit_iast: muñjālābhe tu kartavyāḥ kuśāśmantakabalvajaiḥ trivṛtā granthinaikena tribhiḥ pañcabhireva vā
meaning: मुञ्जालाभे तु कर्तव्याः कुशाश्मन्तकबल्वजैः त्रिवृता ग्रन्थिनैकेन त्रिभिः पञ्चभिरेव वा
text: In the event of Muñja (and thb rest) being not available, they should be made of Kuśa, Ashmantaka and Balvaja,—triplicated with one, three or four knots.
iast: muñjālābhe tu kartavyāḥ kuśāśmantakabalvajaiḥ trivṛtā granthinaikena tribhiḥ pañcabhireva vā

sanskrit_iast: kārpāsamupavītaṃ syādviprasyaurdhvavṛtaṃ trivṛt śaṇasūtramayaṃ rājño vaiśyasyāvikasautrikam
meaning: कार्पासमुपवीतं स्याद्विप्रस्यौर्ध्ववृतं त्रिवृत् शणसूत्रमयं राज्ञो वैश्यस्याविकसौत्रिकम्
text: The Sacrificial Thread worn over the shoulder,—which is triple and twisted upwards,—should be hade op cotton for the Brāhmaṇa, of hempen fibres for the Kṣatriya, and op woolen fibres for the Vaiśya.
iast: kārpāsamupavītaṃ syādviprasyaurdhvavṛtaṃ trivṛt śaṇasūtramayaṃ rājño vaiśyasyāvikasautrikam

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇo bailvapālāśau kṣatriyo vāṭakhādirau pailavaudumbarau vaiśyo daṇḍānarhanti dharmataḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणो बैल्वपालाशौ क्षत्रियो वाटखादिरौ पैलवौदुम्बरौ वैश्यो दण्डानर्हन्ति धर्मतः
text: The Brāhmaṇa should, by law, have staves of Bilva and Palāśa wood; the Kṣatriya those of Vaṭa and Khadira; and the Vaiśya those of Pīlu and Udumbara.
iast: brāhmaṇo bailvapālāśau kṣatriyo vāṭakhādirau pailavaudumbarau vaiśyo daṇḍānarhanti dharmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: keśāntiko brāhmaṇasya daṇḍaḥ kāryaḥ pramāṇataḥ lalāṭasammito rājñaḥ syāt tu nāsāntiko viśaḥ
meaning: केशान्तिको ब्राह्मणस्य दण्डः कार्यः प्रमाणतः ललाटसम्मितो राज्ञः स्यात् तु नासान्तिको विशः
text: For the Brāhmaṇa the staff should be made in size reaching up to the end of his hair, for the Kṣatriya it should reach up to the forehead, and for the Vaiśya to the nose;
iast: keśāntiko brāhmaṇasya daṇḍaḥ kāryaḥ pramāṇataḥ lalāṭasammito rājñaḥ syāt tu nāsāntiko viśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṛjavaste tu sarve syuravraṇāḥ saumyadarśanāḥ anudvegakarā nr̥̄ṇāṃ satvaco 'nagnidūṣitāḥ
meaning: ऋजवस्ते तु सर्वे स्युरव्रणाः सौम्यदर्शनाः अनुद्वेगकरा न्र्̥̄णां सत्वचो 'नग्निदूषिताः
text: All these should be straight, unimpaired, handsome-looking, not frightening to men, with dark and unspoilt by fire
iast: ṛjavaste tu sarve syuravraṇāḥ saumyadarśanāḥ anudvegakarā nr̥̄ṇāṃ satvaco 'nagnidūṣitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: pratigṛhyaipsitaṃ daṇḍamupasthāya ca bhāskaram pradakṣiṇaṃ parītyāgniṃ caredbhaikṣaṃ yathāvidhi
meaning: प्रतिगृह्यैप्सितं दण्डमुपस्थाय च भास्करम् प्रदक्षिणं परीत्याग्निं चरेद्भैक्षं यथाविधि
text: Taking up the staff of his liking, having worshipped the sun and walked round the fire to his right, he should beg for alms according to the prescribed rule.
iast: pratigṛhyaipsitaṃ daṇḍamupasthāya ca bhāskaram pradakṣiṇaṃ parītyāgniṃ caredbhaikṣaṃ yathāvidhi

sanskrit_iast: bhavatpūrvaṃ caredbhaikṣamupanīto dvijottamaḥ bhavanmadhyaṃ tu rājanyo vaiśyastu bhavaduttaram
meaning: भवत्पूर्वं चरेद्भैक्षमुपनीतो द्विजोत्तमः भवन्मध्यं तु राजन्यो वैश्यस्तु भवदुत्तरम्
text: The Brāhmaṇa, having undergone Initiation, should beg for food with words of which ‘bhavat’ (‘Lady’) forms the beginning; the Kṣatriya with words of which ‘bhavat’ forms the middle; and the Vaiśya with words of which ‘bhavat’ forms the end.
iast: bhavatpūrvaṃ caredbhaikṣamupanīto dvijottamaḥ bhavanmadhyaṃ tu rājanyo vaiśyastu bhavaduttaram

sanskrit_iast: mātaraṃ vā svasāraṃ vā māturvā bhaginīṃ nijām bhikṣeta bhikṣāṃ prathamaṃ yā cainaṃ nāvamānayet
meaning: मातरं वा स्वसारं वा मातुर्वा भगिनीं निजाम् भिक्षेत भिक्षां प्रथमं या चैनं नावमानयेत्
text: First of all he should beg food of his mother, or of his sister, or of his mother’s own sister, or of such another lady as may not insult him.
iast: mātaraṃ vā svasāraṃ vā māturvā bhaginīṃ nijām bhikṣeta bhikṣāṃ prathamaṃ yā cainaṃ nāvamānayet
