sanskrit_iast: samāhṛtya tu tadbhaikṣaṃ yāvadannamamāyayā yāvadarthaṃ nivedya gurave 'śnīyādācamya prālgmukhaḥ śuciḥ
meaning: समाहृत्य तु तद्भैक्षं यावदन्नममायया यावदर्थं निवेद्य गुरवे 'श्नीयादाचम्य प्राल्ग्मुखः शुचिः
text: Having collected as much food as may be needed, and having offered it, without guile, to his Teacher, he should eat it, with his face to the east, after having sipped water and become pure.
iast: samāhṛtya tu tadbhaikṣaṃ yāvadannamamāyayā yāvadarthaṃ nivedya gurave 'śnīyādācamya prālgmukhaḥ śuciḥ

sanskrit_iast: āyuṣyaṃ prālgmukho bhuṅkte yaśasyaṃ dakṣiṇāmukhaḥ śriyaṃ pratyalgmukho bhuṅkte ṛtaṃ bhuṅkte hyudalgmukhaḥ
meaning: आयुष्यं प्राल्ग्मुखो भुङ्क्ते यशस्यं दक्षिणामुखः श्रियं प्रत्यल्ग्मुखो भुङ्क्ते ऋतं भुङ्क्ते ह्युदल्ग्मुखः
text: Eating with face to the East, he does what is conducive to longevity; eating with face to the South, he does what brings fame; eating with face to the West, he does what brings prosperity; and eating with face to the North, he does what leads to the true.
iast: āyuṣyaṃ prālgmukho bhuṅkte yaśasyaṃ dakṣiṇāmukhaḥ śriyaṃ pratyalgmukho bhuṅkte ṛtaṃ bhuṅkte hyudalgmukhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: upaspṛśya dvijo nityamannamadyāt samāhitaḥ bhuktvā caupaspṛśet samyagadbhiḥ khāni ca saṃspṛśet
meaning: उपस्पृश्य द्विजो नित्यमन्नमद्यात् समाहितः भुक्त्वा चौपस्पृशेत् सम्यगद्भिः खानि च संस्पृशेत्
text: The twice-b orn person should always take his food after having sipped water and with due care; and after having eaten, he should rinse his mouth in the proper manner and touch the cavities with water.
iast: upaspṛśya dvijo nityamannamadyāt samāhitaḥ bhuktvā caupaspṛśet samyagadbhiḥ khāni ca saṃspṛśet

sanskrit_iast: pūjayedaśanaṃ nityamadyāccaitadakutsayan dṛṣṭvā hṛṣyet prasīdecca pratinandecca sarvaśaḥ
meaning: पूजयेदशनं नित्यमद्याच्चैतदकुत्सयन् दृष्ट्वा हृष्येत् प्रसीदेच्च प्रतिनन्देच्च सर्वशः
text: He should always worship the food and eat it without disparaging it. When he sees it, he should rejoice and peel gratified, and he should always welcome it.
iast: pūjayedaśanaṃ nityamadyāccaitadakutsayan dṛṣṭvā hṛṣyet prasīdecca pratinandecca sarvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pūjitaṃ hyaśanaṃ nityaṃ balamūrjaṃ ca yacchati apūjitaṃ tu tadbhuktamubhayaṃ nāśayedidam
meaning: पूजितं ह्यशनं नित्यं बलमूर्जं च यच्छति अपूजितं तु तद्भुक्तमुभयं नाशयेदिदम्
text: The food, thus worshipped always, imparts strength and vigour. If eaten irreverently, it destroys them both.
iast: pūjitaṃ hyaśanaṃ nityaṃ balamūrjaṃ ca yacchati apūjitaṃ tu tadbhuktamubhayaṃ nāśayedidam

sanskrit_iast: naucchiṣṭaṃ kasya ciddadyānnādyādetat tathā 'ntarā na caivātyaśanaṃ kuryānna caucchiṣṭaḥ kva cidvrajet
meaning: नौच्छिष्टं कस्य चिद्दद्यान्नाद्यादेतत् तथा 'न्तरा न चैवात्यशनं कुर्यान्न चौच्छिष्टः क्व चिद्व्रजेत्
text: He should not give the leavings to anyone; he should not eat in between; he should not do over-rating; and he should not go any-where with particles of food still on him.
iast: naucchiṣṭaṃ kasya ciddadyānnādyādetat tathā 'ntarā na caivātyaśanaṃ kuryānna caucchiṣṭaḥ kva cidvrajet

sanskrit_iast: anārogyamanāyuṣyamasvargyaṃ cātibhojanam apuṇyaṃ lokavidviṣṭaṃ tasmāt tat parivarjayet
meaning: अनारोग्यमनायुष्यमस्वर्ग्यं चातिभोजनम् अपुण्यं लोकविद्विष्टं तस्मात् तत् परिवर्जयेत्
text: Over-eating destroys health, cuts off life and bars heaven; it is unrighteous and detested by people; for these reasons one should avoid it.
iast: anārogyamanāyuṣyamasvargyaṃ cātibhojanam apuṇyaṃ lokavidviṣṭaṃ tasmāt tat parivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: brāhmeṇa viprastīrthena nityakālamupaspṛśet kāyatraidaśikābhyāṃ vā na pitryeṇa kadā cana
meaning: ब्राह्मेण विप्रस्तीर्थेन नित्यकालमुपस्पृशेत् कायत्रैदशिकाभ्यां वा न पित्र्येण कदा चन
text: Every time, the Brāhmana should sip water, either though the receptacle dedicated to Brahmā, or through that dedicated to Prajāpati, or through that dedicated to the ‘Thrice-ten’ (Gods); but never through that dedicated to the Pitṛs.
iast: brāhmeṇa viprastīrthena nityakālamupaspṛśet kāyatraidaśikābhyāṃ vā na pitryeṇa kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: aṅguṣṭhamūlasya tale brāhmaṃ tīrthaṃ pracakṣate kāyamaṅgulimūle 'gre devaṃ pitryaṃ tayoradhaḥ
meaning: अङ्गुष्ठमूलस्य तले ब्राह्मं तीर्थं प्रचक्षते कायमङ्गुलिमूले 'ग्रे देवं पित्र्यं तयोरधः
text: The part of the palm at the root of the thumb they call ‘the receptacle dedicated to Brahmā’; that which is at the root of the finger ‘Sacred to Prajāpati’; that on the finger-tops ‘dedicated to the Gods’; and that which is below these two ‘dedicated to the Pitṛs.’
iast: aṅguṣṭhamūlasya tale brāhmaṃ tīrthaṃ pracakṣate kāyamaṅgulimūle 'gre devaṃ pitryaṃ tayoradhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: trirācāmedapaḥ pūrvaṃ dviḥ pramṛjyāt tato mukham khāni caiva spṛśedadbhirātmānaṃ śira eva ca
meaning: त्रिराचामेदपः पूर्वं द्विः प्रमृज्यात् ततो मुखम् खानि चैव स्पृशेदद्भिरात्मानं शिर एव च
text: First of all he should sip water thrice; then he should wipe his mouth twice, and touch with water the cavities, the soul and also the head.
iast: trirācāmedapaḥ pūrvaṃ dviḥ pramṛjyāt tato mukham khāni caiva spṛśedadbhirātmānaṃ śira eva ca

sanskrit_iast: anuṣṇābhiraphenābhiradbhistīrthena dharmavit śaucaipsuḥ sarvadā 'cāmedekānte prāgudalgmukhaḥ
meaning: अनुष्णाभिरफेनाभिरद्भिस्तीर्थेन धर्मवित् शौचैप्सुः सर्वदा 'चामेदेकान्ते प्रागुदल्ग्मुखः
text: One who knows his duties, when desiring cleanliness, should always sip, through the proper receptacle, water which is neither hot nor frothy,—in solitude, with his face towards the North or the East.
iast: anuṣṇābhiraphenābhiradbhistīrthena dharmavit śaucaipsuḥ sarvadā 'cāmedekānte prāgudalgmukhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: hṛdgābhiḥ pūyate vipraḥ kaṇṭhagābhistu bhūmipaḥ vaiśyo 'dbhiḥ prāśitābhistu śūdraḥ spṛṣṭābhirantataḥ
meaning: हृद्गाभिः पूयते विप्रः कण्ठगाभिस्तु भूमिपः वैश्यो 'द्भिः प्राशिताभिस्तु शूद्रः स्पृष्टाभिरन्ततः
text: The Brāhmaṇa is purified by the water reaching the heart; the Kṣatriya by that reaching the throat; the Vaiśya by water that is just sipped, and the Śūdra by what is touched with the end.
iast: hṛdgābhiḥ pūyate vipraḥ kaṇṭhagābhistu bhūmipaḥ vaiśyo 'dbhiḥ prāśitābhistu śūdraḥ spṛṣṭābhirantataḥ

sanskrit_iast: uddhṛte dakṣine pāṇāvupavītyaucyate dvijaḥ savye prācīnāvītī nivītī kaṇṭhasajjane
meaning: उद्धृते दक्षिने पाणावुपवीत्यौच्यते द्विजः सव्ये प्राचीनावीती निवीती कण्ठसज्जने
text: When the right hand is held above (the sacred thread etc.), the twice-born person is described as ‘Upavītin’; when the left hand is held above, ‘Prācīnāvītin [Prachinaviti]’, and on its hanging by the neck, ‘Nivītin.’
iast: uddhṛte dakṣine pāṇāvupavītyaucyate dvijaḥ savye prācīnāvītī nivītī kaṇṭhasajjane

sanskrit_iast: mekhalāmajinaṃ daṇḍamupavītaṃ kamaṇḍalum apsu prāsya vinaṣṭāni gṛhṇītānyāni mantravat
meaning: मेखलामजिनं दण्डमुपवीतं कमण्डलुम् अप्सु प्रास्य विनष्टानि गृह्णीतान्यानि मन्त्रवत्
text: When the girdle, the skin, the staff, the sacred thread or the water-pot becomes damaged, he should throw it into the water and take up another with the proper formulas.
iast: mekhalāmajinaṃ daṇḍamupavītaṃ kamaṇḍalum apsu prāsya vinaṣṭāni gṛhṇītānyāni mantravat

sanskrit_iast: keśāntaḥ ṣoḍaśe varṣe brāhmaṇasya vidhīyate rājanyabandhordvāviṃśe vaiśyasya dvyadhike mataḥ
meaning: केशान्तः षोडशे वर्षे ब्राह्मणस्य विधीयते राजन्यबन्धोर्द्वाविंशे वैश्यस्य द्व्यधिके मतः
text: The sacrament of Keśānta is ordained for the Brāhmaṇa in his sixteenth year; for the Kṣatriya in his twenty-second year, and for the Vaiśya two years later.
iast: keśāntaḥ ṣoḍaśe varṣe brāhmaṇasya vidhīyate rājanyabandhordvāviṃśe vaiśyasya dvyadhike mataḥ

sanskrit_iast: amantrikā tu kāryaiyaṃ strīṇāmāvṛdaśeṣataḥ saṃskārārthaṃ śarīrasya yathākālaṃ yathākramam
meaning: अमन्त्रिका तु कार्यैयं स्त्रीणामावृदशेषतः संस्कारार्थं शरीरस्य यथाकालं यथाक्रमम्
text: For females, this whole series should be performed at the right time and in the proper order, for the purpose of sanctifying the body; but without the Vedic formulas
iast: amantrikā tu kāryaiyaṃ strīṇāmāvṛdaśeṣataḥ saṃskārārthaṃ śarīrasya yathākālaṃ yathākramam

sanskrit_iast: vaivāhiko vidhiḥ strīṇāṃ saṃskāro vaidikaḥ smṛtaḥ patisevā gurau vāso gṛhārtho 'gniparikriyā
meaning: वैवाहिको विधिः स्त्रीणां संस्कारो वैदिकः स्मृतः पतिसेवा गुरौ वासो गृहार्थो 'ग्निपरिक्रिया
text: For females the Rites of marriage have been ordained to be their ‘Vedic Sacrament,’ the serving of the husbands their ‘residence with the Teacher,’ and the household-duties their ‘tending of fire.’
iast: vaivāhiko vidhiḥ strīṇāṃ saṃskāro vaidikaḥ smṛtaḥ patisevā gurau vāso gṛhārtho 'gniparikriyā

sanskrit_iast: eṣa prokto dvijātīnāmaupanāyaniko vidhiḥ utpattivyañjakaḥ puṇyaḥ karmayogaṃ nibodhata
meaning: एष प्रोक्तो द्विजातीनामौपनायनिको विधिः उत्पत्तिव्यञ्जकः पुण्यः कर्मयोगं निबोधत
text: Thus has been described the Initiatory Ceremony of twice-born men,—that which sanctifies them and marks their (real) birth. Learn now the duties to which they should apply themselves.
iast: eṣa prokto dvijātīnāmaupanāyaniko vidhiḥ utpattivyañjakaḥ puṇyaḥ karmayogaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: upanīya guruḥ śiṣyaṃ śikṣayetśaucamāditaḥ ācāramagnikāryaṃ ca saṃdhyaupāsanameva ca
meaning: उपनीय गुरुः शिष्यं शिक्षयेत्शौचमादितः आचारमग्निकार्यं च संध्यौपासनमेव च
text: Having initiated the pupil, the Teacher should, first of all, teach him cleanliness, right behaviour, firetending and also the twilight-devotions.
iast: upanīya guruḥ śiṣyaṃ śikṣayetśaucamāditaḥ ācāramagnikāryaṃ ca saṃdhyaupāsanameva ca

sanskrit_iast: adhyeṣyamāṇastvācānto yathāśāstramudalgmukhaḥ brahmāñjalikṛto 'dhyāpyo laghuvāsā jitaindriyaḥ
meaning: अध्येष्यमाणस्त्वाचान्तो यथाशास्त्रमुदल्ग्मुखः ब्रह्माञ्जलिकृतो 'ध्याप्यो लघुवासा जितैन्द्रियः
text: When about to study, he shall be taught with his face to the north, when he has sipped water according to law, has formed the Brahmāñjali (with his hands), wears light clothing and has the organs under full control.
iast: adhyeṣyamāṇastvācānto yathāśāstramudalgmukhaḥ brahmāñjalikṛto 'dhyāpyo laghuvāsā jitaindriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brahmārambhe 'vasāne ca pādau grāhyau guroḥ sadā saṃhatya hastāvadhyeyaṃ sa hi brahmāñjaliḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: ब्रह्मारम्भे 'वसाने च पादौ ग्राह्यौ गुरोः सदा संहत्य हस्तावध्येयं स हि ब्रह्माञ्जलिः स्मृतः
text: At the beginning and at the end op the (study of) the Veda, the feet of the Teacher should always be clasped; and the veda should me studied with joined palms; this is what has been called the ‘Brahmāñjali.’
iast: brahmārambhe 'vasāne ca pādau grāhyau guroḥ sadā saṃhatya hastāvadhyeyaṃ sa hi brahmāñjaliḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vyatyastapāṇinā kāryamupasaṅgrahaṇaṃ guroḥ savyena savyaḥ spraṣṭavyo dakṣiṇena ca dakṣiṇaḥ
meaning: व्यत्यस्तपाणिना कार्यमुपसङ्ग्रहणं गुरोः सव्येन सव्यः स्प्रष्टव्यो दक्षिणेन च दक्षिणः
text: The clasping op the teacher’s feet should be done with crossed hands: the left should be touched with the left and the right with the right.
iast: vyatyastapāṇinā kāryamupasaṅgrahaṇaṃ guroḥ savyena savyaḥ spraṣṭavyo dakṣiṇena ca dakṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: adhyeṣyamāṇaṃ tu gururnityakālamatandritaḥ adhīṣva bho iti brūyādvirāmo 'stviti cāramet
meaning: अध्येष्यमाणं तु गुरुर्नित्यकालमतन्द्रितः अधीष्व भो इति ब्रूयाद्विरामो 'स्त्विति चारमेत्
text: When the pupil is going to study, the Teacher, ever free from indolence, should say—‘ho, read!’ and he should cease when he says ‘let there be a stop!’
iast: adhyeṣyamāṇaṃ tu gururnityakālamatandritaḥ adhīṣva bho iti brūyādvirāmo 'stviti cāramet

sanskrit_iast: brahmanaḥ praṇavaṃ kuryādādāvante ca sarvadā sravatyanoṅkṛtaṃ pūrvaṃ parastācca viśīryati
meaning: ब्रह्मनः प्रणवं कुर्यादादावन्ते च सर्वदा स्रवत्यनोङ्कृतं पूर्वं परस्ताच्च विशीर्यति
text: One should always pronounce the Praṇava in the beginning and at the end of the Veda. if it is not accompanied by the syllable ‘oṃ’ in the beginning, it trickles away; and (if it is not accompanied by it) at the end, it becomes shattered.
iast: brahmanaḥ praṇavaṃ kuryādādāvante ca sarvadā sravatyanoṅkṛtaṃ pūrvaṃ parastācca viśīryati

sanskrit_iast: prākkūlān paryupāsīnaḥ pavitraiścaiva pāvitaḥ prāṇāyāmaistribhiḥ pūtastata oṃkāramarhati
meaning: प्राक्कूलान् पर्युपासीनः पवित्रैश्चैव पावितः प्राणायामैस्त्रिभिः पूतस्तत ओंकारमर्हति
text: Seated upon Kuśa-blades pointing eastwards and sanctified by Kuśa-blades, when one has purified himself by means of three ‘breath-suspensions,’—then alone he becomes entitled to the syllable ‘oṃ.’
iast: prākkūlān paryupāsīnaḥ pavitraiścaiva pāvitaḥ prāṇāyāmaistribhiḥ pūtastata oṃkāramarhati

sanskrit_iast: akāraṃ cāpyukāraṃ ca makāraṃ ca prajāpatiḥ vedatrayātniraduhadbhūrbhuvaḥ svaritīti ca
meaning: अकारं चाप्युकारं च मकारं च प्रजापतिः वेदत्रयात्निरदुहद्भूर्भुवः स्वरितीति च
text: Out of the three Vedas, Prajāpati milked the letter ‘a,’ the letter ‘u’ and the letter ‘m’; as also the syllables ‘bhūḥ-bhuvaḥ-svaḥ.’
iast: akāraṃ cāpyukāraṃ ca makāraṃ ca prajāpatiḥ vedatrayātniraduhadbhūrbhuvaḥ svaritīti ca

sanskrit_iast: tribhya eva tu vedebhyaḥ pādaṃ pādamadūduhat tadity ṛco 'syāḥ sāvitryāḥ parameṣṭhī prajāpatiḥ
meaning: त्रिभ्य एव तु वेदेभ्यः पादं पादमदूदुहत् तदित्य् ऋचो 'स्याः सावित्र्याः परमेष्ठी प्रजापतिः
text: Out of the three Vedas again, the Supreme Prajāpati milked each foot of the Sāvitrī verse beginning with ‘tat.’
iast: tribhya eva tu vedebhyaḥ pādaṃ pādamadūduhat tadity ṛco 'syāḥ sāvitryāḥ parameṣṭhī prajāpatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: etadakṣarametāṃ ca japan vyāhṛtipūrvikām saṃdhyayorvedavidvipro vedapuṇyena yujyate
meaning: एतदक्षरमेतां च जपन् व्याहृतिपूर्विकाम् संध्ययोर्वेदविद्विप्रो वेदपुण्येन युज्यते
text: Reciting, at the two twilights, this syllable and this verse, preceded by the Vyāhṛtis, the Brāhmaṇa, learned in the Veda, becomes endowed with Vedic merit.
iast: etadakṣarametāṃ ca japan vyāhṛtipūrvikām saṃdhyayorvedavidvipro vedapuṇyena yujyate

sanskrit_iast: sahasrakṛtvastvabhyasya bahiretat trikaṃ dvijaḥ mahato 'pyenaso māsāt tvacaivāhirvimucyate
meaning: सहस्रकृत्वस्त्वभ्यस्य बहिरेतत् त्रिकं द्विजः महतो 'प्येनसो मासात् त्वचैवाहिर्विमुच्यते
text: Repeating this Triad a thousand times in open air, the twice-born person becomes freed, in a month, from even great sin, as the snake from its slough.
iast: sahasrakṛtvastvabhyasya bahiretat trikaṃ dvijaḥ mahato 'pyenaso māsāt tvacaivāhirvimucyate

sanskrit_iast: etayāṛcā visaṃyuktaḥ kāle ca kriyayā svayā brahmakṣatriyaviśyonirgarhaṇāṃ yāti sādhuṣu
meaning: एतयाऋचा विसंयुक्तः काले च क्रियया स्वया ब्रह्मक्षत्रियविश्योनिर्गर्हणां याति साधुषु
text: Bereft of this verse, and of the timely performance of his own duty, a person of Brāhmaṇa, Kṣatriya or Vaiśya birth incurs the odium of good men.
iast: etayāṛcā visaṃyuktaḥ kāle ca kriyayā svayā brahmakṣatriyaviśyonirgarhaṇāṃ yāti sādhuṣu

sanskrit_iast: oṃkārapūrvikāstisro mahāvyāhṛtayo 'vyayāḥ oṅkāra tripadā caiva sāvitrī vijñeyaṃ brahmaṇo mukham
meaning: ओंकारपूर्विकास्तिस्रो महाव्याहृतयो 'व्ययाः ओङ्कार त्रिपदा चैव सावित्री विज्ञेयं ब्रह्मणो मुखम्
text: The three imperishable Mahāvyāhṛtis preceded by the syllable ‘oṃ,’ and the three-footed Sāvitrī,—these should be regarded as the ‘Mouth of Brahman.’
iast: oṃkārapūrvikāstisro mahāvyāhṛtayo 'vyayāḥ oṅkāra tripadā caiva sāvitrī vijñeyaṃ brahmaṇo mukham

sanskrit_iast: yo 'dhīte 'hanyahanyetāṃ trīṇi varṣāṇyatandritaḥ sa brahma paramabhyeti vāyubhūtaḥ khamūrtimān
meaning: यो 'धीते 'हन्यहन्येतां त्रीणि वर्षाण्यतन्द्रितः स ब्रह्म परमभ्येति वायुभूतः खमूर्तिमान्
text: He who, untired, recites this, day after day, for three years, turns into air, and becoming transfigured into Ākāśa, reaches the supreme brahman.
iast: yo 'dhīte 'hanyahanyetāṃ trīṇi varṣāṇyatandritaḥ sa brahma paramabhyeti vāyubhūtaḥ khamūrtimān

sanskrit_iast: ekākṣaraṃ paraṃ brahma prāṇāyāmaḥ paraṃ tapaḥ sāvitryāstu paraṃ nāsti maunāt satyaṃ viśiṣyate
meaning: एकाक्षरं परं ब्रह्म प्राणायामः परं तपः सावित्र्यास्तु परं नास्ति मौनात् सत्यं विशिष्यते
text: The mono-syllable is the highest Brahman; Breath-suspensions are the highest austerity; nothing is higher than the Sāvitrī verse; truth is better than Silence.
iast: ekākṣaraṃ paraṃ brahma prāṇāyāmaḥ paraṃ tapaḥ sāvitryāstu paraṃ nāsti maunāt satyaṃ viśiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: kṣaranti sarvā vaidikyo juhotiyajatikriyāḥ akṣaraṃ duṣkaraṃ jñeyaṃ brahma caiva prajāpatiḥ akṣaraṃ tvakṣaraṃ jñeyaṃ
meaning: क्षरन्ति सर्वा वैदिक्यो जुहोतियजतिक्रियाः अक्षरं दुष्करं ज्ञेयं ब्रह्म चैव प्रजापतिः अक्षरं त्वक्षरं ज्ञेयं
text: All vedic acts of oblation and sacrifice pass away; while this syllable (om) is to be regarded as imperishable; and it is Brahman, and also Prajāpati
iast: kṣaranti sarvā vaidikyo juhotiyajatikriyāḥ akṣaraṃ duṣkaraṃ jñeyaṃ brahma caiva prajāpatiḥ akṣaraṃ tvakṣaraṃ jñeyaṃ

sanskrit_iast: vidhiyajñāj japayajño viśiṣṭo daśabhirguṇaiḥ upāṃśuḥ syātśataguṇaḥ sāhasro mānasaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: विधियज्ञाज् जपयज्ञो विशिष्टो दशभिर्गुणैः उपांशुः स्यात्शतगुणः साहस्रो मानसः स्मृतः
text: The offering that consists in the repeating of mantras excels the enjoined (ritualistic) offering ten times; the inaudible (repeating) excels this latter a hundred times; and the Mental (repeating) excels it a thousandfold.
iast: vidhiyajñāj japayajño viśiṣṭo daśabhirguṇaiḥ upāṃśuḥ syātśataguṇaḥ sāhasro mānasaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ye pākayajñāḥ catvāro vidhiyajñasamanvitāḥ sarve te japayajñasya kalāṃ nārhanti ṣoḍaśīm
meaning: ये पाकयज्ञाः चत्वारो विधियज्ञसमन्विताः सर्वे ते जपयज्ञस्य कलां नार्हन्ति षोडशीम्
text: The four cooked offerings, along with the enjoined sacrifices,—all these are not worth the sixteenth part of the offering that consists in the repeating of mantras.
iast: ye pākayajñāḥ catvāro vidhiyajñasamanvitāḥ sarve te japayajñasya kalāṃ nārhanti ṣoḍaśīm

sanskrit_iast: japyenaiva tu saṃsidhyedbrāhmaṇo nātra saṃśayaḥ kuryādanyanna vā kuryān maitro brāhmaṇa ucyate
meaning: जप्येनैव तु संसिध्येद्ब्राह्मणो नात्र संशयः कुर्यादन्यन्न वा कुर्यान् मैत्रो ब्राह्मण उच्यते
text: It is by means of repeating mantras that the Brāhmaṇa succeeds;—there is no doubt in this. he may, or may not, do anything else, one comes to be called a Brāhmaṇa if he is of a friendly disposition.
iast: japyenaiva tu saṃsidhyedbrāhmaṇo nātra saṃśayaḥ kuryādanyanna vā kuryān maitro brāhmaṇa ucyate

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇāṃ vicaratāṃ viṣayeṣvapahāriṣu saṃyame yatnamātiṣṭhedvidvān yantaiva vājinām
meaning: इन्द्रियाणां विचरतां विषयेष्वपहारिषु संयमे यत्नमातिष्ठेद्विद्वान् यन्तैव वाजिनाम्
text: The wise man should put forth an effort to restrain his organs roaming among alluring objects; just as the driver restrains the horses.
iast: indriyāṇāṃ vicaratāṃ viṣayeṣvapahāriṣu saṃyame yatnamātiṣṭhedvidvān yantaiva vājinām

sanskrit_iast: ekādaśaindriyāṇyāhuryāni pūrve manīṣiṇaḥ tāni samyak pravakṣyāmi yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ
meaning: एकादशैन्द्रियाण्याहुर्यानि पूर्वे मनीषिणः तानि सम्यक् प्रवक्ष्यामि यथावदनुपूर्वशः
text: Those eleven organs which the ancient sages have named I shall now fully describe in due order.
iast: ekādaśaindriyāṇyāhuryāni pūrve manīṣiṇaḥ tāni samyak pravakṣyāmi yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: śrotraṃ tvak cakṣuṣī jihvā nāsikā caiva pañcamī pāyūpasthaṃ hastapādaṃ vāk caiva daśamī smṛtā
meaning: श्रोत्रं त्वक् चक्षुषी जिह्वा नासिका चैव पञ्चमी पायूपस्थं हस्तपादं वाक् चैव दशमी स्मृता
text: (They are) the Ear, the Skin, the Eyes, the Tongue and the Nose as the fifth; the Anus, the Generative Organ, the Hands and Feet, and Speech described as the tenth.
iast: śrotraṃ tvak cakṣuṣī jihvā nāsikā caiva pañcamī pāyūpasthaṃ hastapādaṃ vāk caiva daśamī smṛtā

sanskrit_iast: buddhīndriyāṇi pañcaiṣāṃ śrotrādīnyanupūrvaśaḥ karmaindriyāṇi pañcaiṣāṃ pāyuādīni pracakṣate
meaning: बुद्धीन्द्रियाणि पञ्चैषां श्रोत्रादीन्यनुपूर्वशः कर्मैन्द्रियाणि पञ्चैषां पायुआदीनि प्रचक्षते
text: Of these the five beginning with the Ear in due order they call “Organs of Sensation’; and five of these, beginning with the Anus, “Organs of Action.”
iast: buddhīndriyāṇi pañcaiṣāṃ śrotrādīnyanupūrvaśaḥ karmaindriyāṇi pañcaiṣāṃ pāyuādīni pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: ekādaśaṃ mano jñeyaṃ svaguṇenaubhayātmakam yasmin jite jitāvetau bhavataḥ pañcakau gaṇau
meaning: एकादशं मनो ज्ञेयं स्वगुणेनौभयात्मकम् यस्मिन् जिते जितावेतौ भवतः पञ्चकौ गणौ
text: The Mind is to be regarded as the eleventh, which, by its own quality, is of two-fold nature; and on this being subdued, both the aforesaid five-mentioned groups become subdued.
iast: ekādaśaṃ mano jñeyaṃ svaguṇenaubhayātmakam yasmin jite jitāvetau bhavataḥ pañcakau gaṇau

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇāṃ prasaṅgena doṣaṃ ṛcchatyasaṃśayam saṃniyamya tu tānyeva tataḥ siddhiṃ nigacchati
meaning: इन्द्रियाणां प्रसङ्गेन दोषं ऋच्छत्यसंशयम् संनियम्य तु तान्येव ततः सिद्धिं निगच्छति
text: By attachment to the organs one incurs evil, without doubt; while by subjugating those same he attains success.
iast: indriyāṇāṃ prasaṅgena doṣaṃ ṛcchatyasaṃśayam saṃniyamya tu tānyeva tataḥ siddhiṃ nigacchati

sanskrit_iast: na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānāmupabhogena śāmyati haviṣā kṛṣṇavartmaiva bhūya evābhivardhate
meaning: न जातु कामः कामानामुपभोगेन शाम्यति हविषा कृष्णवर्त्मैव भूय एवाभिवर्धते
text: Never is desire appeased by the enjoyment of desires; it only waxes stronger, like fire by clarified butter.
iast: na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānāmupabhogena śāmyati haviṣā kṛṣṇavartmaiva bhūya evābhivardhate

sanskrit_iast: yaścaitān prāpnuyāt sarvān yaścaitān kevalāṃstyajet prāpaṇāt sarvakāmānāṃ parityāgo viśiṣyate
meaning: यश्चैतान् प्राप्नुयात् सर्वान् यश्चैतान् केवलांस्त्यजेत् प्रापणात् सर्वकामानां परित्यागो विशिष्यते
text: As between one who would attain all these, and another who would renounce them all, the renunciation of desires surf asses the attainment of
iast: yaścaitān prāpnuyāt sarvān yaścaitān kevalāṃstyajet prāpaṇāt sarvakāmānāṃ parityāgo viśiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: na tathaitāni śakyante saṃniyantumasevayā viṣayeṣu prajuṣṭāni yathā jñānena nityaśaḥ
meaning: न तथैतानि शक्यन्ते संनियन्तुमसेवया विषयेषु प्रजुष्टानि यथा ज्ञानेन नित्यशः
text: These (organs), being contaminated with objects, are not capable of being subjugated by mere abstinence, as they are by ever present knowledge.
iast: na tathaitāni śakyante saṃniyantumasevayā viṣayeṣu prajuṣṭāni yathā jñānena nityaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vedāstyāgaśca yajñāśca niyamāśca tapāṃsi ca na vipraduṣṭabhāvasya siddhiṃ gacchati karhi cit
meaning: वेदास्त्यागश्च यज्ञाश्च नियमाश्च तपांसि च न विप्रदुष्टभावस्य सिद्धिं गच्छति कर्हि चित्
text: The Vedas, Renunciation, Sacrifices, Restraints and Austerities never attain fulfilment for one whose disposition is vitiated.
iast: vedāstyāgaśca yajñāśca niyamāśca tapāṃsi ca na vipraduṣṭabhāvasya siddhiṃ gacchati karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: śrutvā spṛṣṭvā ca dṛṣṭvā ca bhuktvā ghrātvā ca yo naraḥ na hṛṣyati glāyati vā sa vijñeyo jitaindriyaḥ
meaning: श्रुत्वा स्पृष्ट्वा च दृष्ट्वा च भुक्त्वा घ्रात्वा च यो नरः न हृष्यति ग्लायति वा स विज्ञेयो जितैन्द्रियः
text: That man is to be known as having subjugated his sense-organs, who, on having heard, or touched, or seen, or tasted, or smelt, anything, neither rejoices nor grieves.
iast: śrutvā spṛṣṭvā ca dṛṣṭvā ca bhuktvā ghrātvā ca yo naraḥ na hṛṣyati glāyati vā sa vijñeyo jitaindriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇāṃ tu sarveṣāṃ yadyekaṃ kṣaratīndriyam tenāsya kṣarati prajñā dṛteḥ pādādivaudakam
meaning: इन्द्रियाणां तु सर्वेषां यद्येकं क्षरतीन्द्रियम् तेनास्य क्षरति प्रज्ञा दृतेः पादादिवौदकम्
text: From among all the organs, if one happens to ooze out, then thereby his wisdom oozes out, just like water from one part of the leathern bag.
iast: indriyāṇāṃ tu sarveṣāṃ yadyekaṃ kṣaratīndriyam tenāsya kṣarati prajñā dṛteḥ pādādivaudakam

sanskrit_iast: vaśe kṛtvaindriyagrāmaṃ saṃyamya ca manastathā sarvān saṃsādhayedarthānakṣiṇvan yogatastanum
meaning: वशे कृत्वैन्द्रियग्रामं संयम्य च मनस्तथा सर्वान् संसाधयेदर्थानक्षिण्वन् योगतस्तनुम्
text: Having brought the host of organs under control, and having also subdued the mind, one should accomplish all his purposes, taking care not to injure his body.
iast: vaśe kṛtvaindriyagrāmaṃ saṃyamya ca manastathā sarvān saṃsādhayedarthānakṣiṇvan yogatastanum

sanskrit_iast: pūrvāṃ saṃdhyāṃ japāṃstiṣṭhet sāvitrīmā 'rkadarśanāt paścimāṃ tu samāsīnaḥ samyag ṛkṣavibhāvanāt paścimāṃ tu sadāsīta
meaning: पूर्वां संध्यां जपांस्तिष्ठेत् सावित्रीमा 'र्कदर्शनात् पश्चिमां तु समासीनः सम्यग् ऋक्षविभावनात् पश्चिमां तु सदासीत
text: Everyday during the earlier twilight one should stand repeating the Sāvitrī, till the sun becomes distinctly visible; and during the later twilight he should sit till the stars ark clearly seen.
iast: pūrvāṃ saṃdhyāṃ japāṃstiṣṭhet sāvitrīmā 'rkadarśanāt paścimāṃ tu samāsīnaḥ samyag ṛkṣavibhāvanāt paścimāṃ tu sadāsīta

sanskrit_iast: pūrvāṃ saṃdhyāṃ japaṃstiṣṭhannaiśameno vyapohati paścimāṃ tu samāsīno malaṃ hanti divākṛtam
meaning: पूर्वां संध्यां जपंस्तिष्ठन्नैशमेनो व्यपोहति पश्चिमां तु समासीनो मलं हन्ति दिवाकृतम्
text: One who, during the morning-twilight, repeats (the Sāvitrī) standing, removes the sin of the (preceding) night; while he who, during the evening-Twilight, repeats it seated, destroys the sin committed during the day.
iast: pūrvāṃ saṃdhyāṃ japaṃstiṣṭhannaiśameno vyapohati paścimāṃ tu samāsīno malaṃ hanti divākṛtam

sanskrit_iast: na tiṣṭhati tu yaḥ pūrvāṃ naupāste yaśca paścimām sa śūdravadbahiṣkāryaḥ sarvasmāddvijakarmaṇaḥ
meaning: न तिष्ठति तु यः पूर्वां नौपास्ते यश्च पश्चिमाम् स शूद्रवद्बहिष्कार्यः सर्वस्माद्द्विजकर्मणः
text: But he who does not stand during the morning-twilight, and who does not sit through the evening-twilight, should be excluded, like the Sśūdra, from all that is due to twice-born persons.
iast: na tiṣṭhati tu yaḥ pūrvāṃ naupāste yaśca paścimām sa śūdravadbahiṣkāryaḥ sarvasmāddvijakarmaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: apāṃ samīpe niyato naityakaṃ vidhimāsthitaḥ sāvitrīmapyadhīyīta gatvā 'raṇyaṃ samāhitaḥ
meaning: अपां समीपे नियतो नैत्यकं विधिमास्थितः सावित्रीमप्यधीयीत गत्वा 'रण्यं समाहितः
text: Convinced of the necessary character of the injunction, and retiring to the forest on a spot near water, one may even recite the Sāvitrī only, with a clean body and a collected mind.
iast: apāṃ samīpe niyato naityakaṃ vidhimāsthitaḥ sāvitrīmapyadhīyīta gatvā 'raṇyaṃ samāhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vedaupakaraṇe caiva svādhyāye caiva naityake nānurodho 'styanadhyāye homamantreṣu caiva hi
meaning: वेदौपकरणे चैव स्वाध्याये चैव नैत्यके नानुरोधो 'स्त्यनध्याये होममन्त्रेषु चैव हि
text: There is no regard for (observance of) days forbidden for study in connection with the appurtenances to the Veda, and with the mantras recited during oblations.
iast: vedaupakaraṇe caiva svādhyāye caiva naityake nānurodho 'styanadhyāye homamantreṣu caiva hi

sanskrit_iast: naityake nāstyanadhyāyo brahmasattraṃ hi tat smṛtam brahmāhutihutaṃ puṇyamanadhyāyavaṣaṭkṛtam
meaning: नैत्यके नास्त्यनध्यायो ब्रह्मसत्त्रं हि तत् स्मृतम् ब्रह्माहुतिहुतं पुण्यमनध्यायवषट्कृतम्
text: There is no “day forbidden for study” in regard to the daily recitation; since this has been called “Brahmasatra”; it is meritorious, being offered with the offering of study, and being maintained by the syllable “vaṣaṭ” in the shape of the recitation made on forbidden days.
iast: naityake nāstyanadhyāyo brahmasattraṃ hi tat smṛtam brahmāhutihutaṃ puṇyamanadhyāyavaṣaṭkṛtam

sanskrit_iast: yaḥ svādhyāyamadhīte 'bdaṃ vidhinā niyataḥ śuciḥ tasya nityaṃ kṣaratyeṣa payo dadhi ghṛtaṃ madhu
meaning: यः स्वाध्यायमधीते 'ब्दं विधिना नियतः शुचिः तस्य नित्यं क्षरत्येष पयो दधि घृतं मधु
text: He who, clean and self-controlled, recites the Veda, in due form, for one year,—for him this constantly fours out milk, curd, clarified butter and honey.
iast: yaḥ svādhyāyamadhīte 'bdaṃ vidhinā niyataḥ śuciḥ tasya nityaṃ kṣaratyeṣa payo dadhi ghṛtaṃ madhu

sanskrit_iast: agnīndhanaṃ bhaikṣacaryāmadhaḥśayyāṃ gurorhitam ā samāvartanāt kuryāt kṛtaupanayano dvijaḥ
meaning: अग्नीन्धनं भैक्षचर्यामधःशय्यां गुरोर्हितम् आ समावर्तनात् कुर्यात् कृतौपनयनो द्विजः
text: The twice-born person, whose Initiation has been performed, should continue to do, till the final Bath of “Return” (Samāvartana), tub kindling of fire, the begging of food, the sleeping on the ground and the acting for the teacher’s well- being.
iast: agnīndhanaṃ bhaikṣacaryāmadhaḥśayyāṃ gurorhitam ā samāvartanāt kuryāt kṛtaupanayano dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācāryaputraḥ śuśrūṣurjñānado dhārmikaḥ śuciḥ āptaḥ śakto 'rthadaḥ sādhuḥ svo 'dhyāpyā daśa dharmataḥ
meaning: आचार्यपुत्रः शुश्रूषुर्ज्ञानदो धार्मिकः शुचिः आप्तः शक्तो 'र्थदः साधुः स्वो 'ध्याप्या दश धर्मतः
text: The teacher’s son, one who is eager to do service, one who imparts knowledge, one who is righteous, one who is clean, a near relative, one who is competent, one who gives monet, one who is gentle, and one’s own (son)—these ten should be taught for the sake of merit.
iast: ācāryaputraḥ śuśrūṣurjñānado dhārmikaḥ śuciḥ āptaḥ śakto 'rthadaḥ sādhuḥ svo 'dhyāpyā daśa dharmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāpṛṣṭaḥ kasya cidbrūyānna cānyāyena pṛcchataḥ jānannapi hi medhāvī jaḍavalloka ācaret
meaning: नापृष्टः कस्य चिद्ब्रूयान्न चान्यायेन पृच्छतः जानन्नपि हि मेधावी जडवल्लोक आचरेत्
text: One should not instruct any one unless he is asked; nor any one who asks in an improper manner. even though knowing (the truth), the wise man should behave, among men, as if ignorant.
iast: nāpṛṣṭaḥ kasya cidbrūyānna cānyāyena pṛcchataḥ jānannapi hi medhāvī jaḍavalloka ācaret

sanskrit_iast: adharmeṇa ca yaḥ prāha yaścādharmeṇa pṛcchati tayoranyataraḥ praiti vidveṣaṃ vā 'dhigacchati
meaning: अधर्मेण च यः प्राह यश्चाधर्मेण पृच्छति तयोरन्यतरः प्रैति विद्वेषं वा 'धिगच्छति
text: He who instructs in an unlawful manner, and he who asks in an unlawful manner,—of those two one or the other either dies (untimely), or incurs the ill-will (of the people).
iast: adharmeṇa ca yaḥ prāha yaścādharmeṇa pṛcchati tayoranyataraḥ praiti vidveṣaṃ vā 'dhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: dharmārthau yatra na syātāṃ śuśrūṣā vā 'pi tadvidhā tatra vidyā na vaptavyā śubhaṃ bījamivauṣare
meaning: धर्मार्थौ यत्र न स्यातां शुश्रूषा वा 'पि तद्विधा तत्र विद्या न वप्तव्या शुभं बीजमिवौषरे
text: Where merit and wealth are not possible, nor is there an adequate desire to serve, there knowledge should not be imparted; just as healthy seed (is not sown) on barren land.
iast: dharmārthau yatra na syātāṃ śuśrūṣā vā 'pi tadvidhā tatra vidyā na vaptavyā śubhaṃ bījamivauṣare

sanskrit_iast: vidyayaiva samaṃ kāmaṃ martavyaṃ brahmavādinā āpadyapi hi ghorāyāṃ na tvenāmiriṇe vapet
meaning: विद्ययैव समं कामं मर्तव्यं ब्रह्मवादिना आपद्यपि हि घोरायां न त्वेनामिरिणे वपेत्
text: The expounder of the Veda may rather perish along with his knowledge; hut he should never sow it on barren soil, even in dike distress.
iast: vidyayaiva samaṃ kāmaṃ martavyaṃ brahmavādinā āpadyapi hi ghorāyāṃ na tvenāmiriṇe vapet

sanskrit_iast: vidyā brāhmaṇametyāha śevadhiste 'smi rakṣa mām śevadhiṣ ṭe asūyakāya māṃ mādāstathā syāṃ vīryavattamā
meaning: विद्या ब्राह्मणमेत्याह शेवधिस्ते 'स्मि रक्ष माम् शेवधिष् टे असूयकाय मां मादास्तथा स्यां वीर्यवत्तमा
text: Learning having approached the Brāhmaṇa said to him—“I am thy wealth, guard me; impart me not unto a scorner; thus may I become extremely powerful.”
iast: vidyā brāhmaṇametyāha śevadhiste 'smi rakṣa mām śevadhiṣ ṭe asūyakāya māṃ mādāstathā syāṃ vīryavattamā

sanskrit_iast: yameva tu śuciṃ vidyānniyatabrahmacāriṇam vidyā niyataṃ brahmacāriṇam tasmai māṃ brūhi viprāya nidhipāyāpramādine
meaning: यमेव तु शुचिं विद्यान्नियतब्रह्मचारिणम् विद्या नियतं ब्रह्मचारिणम् तस्मै मां ब्रूहि विप्राय निधिपायाप्रमादिने
text: “Expound me unto the Brāhmaṇa who guards his treasures and is never careless,—and whom thou knowest to be pure, self-controlled and a duly qualified student.”
iast: yameva tu śuciṃ vidyānniyatabrahmacāriṇam vidyā niyataṃ brahmacāriṇam tasmai māṃ brūhi viprāya nidhipāyāpramādine

sanskrit_iast: brahma yastvananujñātamadhīyānādavāpnuyāt sa brahmasteyasaṃyukto narakaṃ pratipadyate
meaning: ब्रह्म यस्त्वननुज्ञातमधीयानादवाप्नुयात् स ब्रह्मस्तेयसंयुक्तो नरकं प्रतिपद्यते
text: He, who may acquire Veda, without his permission, from one who is reciting it, becomes corrupted with the sin of stealing the Veda, and falls into hell.
iast: brahma yastvananujñātamadhīyānādavāpnuyāt sa brahmasteyasaṃyukto narakaṃ pratipadyate

sanskrit_iast: laukikaṃ vaidikaṃ vā 'pi tathā 'dhyātmikameva vā ādadīta yato jñānaṃ taṃ pūrvamabhivādayet
meaning: लौकिकं वैदिकं वा 'पि तथा 'ध्यात्मिकमेव वा आददीत यतो ज्ञानं तं पूर्वमभिवादयेत्
text: One should first salute him from whom he receives knowledge, either temporal or scriptural or spiritual.
iast: laukikaṃ vaidikaṃ vā 'pi tathā 'dhyātmikameva vā ādadīta yato jñānaṃ taṃ pūrvamabhivādayet

sanskrit_iast: sāvitrīmātrasāro 'pi varaṃ vipraḥ suyantritaḥ nāyantritastrivedo 'pi sarvāśī sarvavikrayī
meaning: सावित्रीमात्रसारो 'पि वरं विप्रः सुयन्त्रितः नायन्त्रितस्त्रिवेदो 'पि सर्वाशी सर्वविक्रयी
text: Better the Brāhmaṇa knowing the Sāvitrī alone, if he is thoroughly self-controlled,—and not he who knows all the three Vedas, but is not self-controlled, and eats all things and sells all things.
iast: sāvitrīmātrasāro 'pi varaṃ vipraḥ suyantritaḥ nāyantritastrivedo 'pi sarvāśī sarvavikrayī

sanskrit_iast: śayyā ''sane 'dhyācarite śreyasā na samāviśet śayyā ''sanasthaścaivainaṃ pratyutthāyābhivādayet
meaning: शय्या ''सने 'ध्याचरिते श्रेयसा न समाविशेत् शय्या ''सनस्थश्चैवैनं प्रत्युत्थायाभिवादयेत्
text: One should not sit with a superior upon the couch or seat prepared for him. and if he himself should happen to be seated on a couch or skat, he should rise to meet (the superior) and salute him.
iast: śayyā ''sane 'dhyācarite śreyasā na samāviśet śayyā ''sanasthaścaivainaṃ pratyutthāyābhivādayet

sanskrit_iast: ūrdhvaṃ prāṇā hyutkrāmanti yūnaḥ sthavira āyati pratyutthānābhivādābhyāṃ punastān pratipadyate
meaning: ऊर्ध्वं प्राणा ह्युत्क्रामन्ति यूनः स्थविर आयति प्रत्युत्थानाभिवादाभ्यां पुनस्तान् प्रतिपद्यते
text: On the elder approaching, the vital breaths of the younger rush outward; and he recovers them by the acts of rising to meet and salute him.
iast: ūrdhvaṃ prāṇā hyutkrāmanti yūnaḥ sthavira āyati pratyutthānābhivādābhyāṃ punastān pratipadyate

sanskrit_iast: abhivādanaśīlasya nityaṃ vṛddhaupasevinaḥ catvāri tasya vardhante āyurdharmo yaśo balam catvāri saṃpravardhante
meaning: अभिवादनशीलस्य नित्यं वृद्धौपसेविनः चत्वारि तस्य वर्धन्ते आयुर्धर्मो यशो बलम् चत्वारि संप्रवर्धन्ते
text: For one who is in the habit of saluting and constantly revering elders,—four things prosper:viz., longevity, merit, fame and strength.
iast: abhivādanaśīlasya nityaṃ vṛddhaupasevinaḥ catvāri tasya vardhante āyurdharmo yaśo balam catvāri saṃpravardhante

sanskrit_iast: abhivādāt paraṃ vipro jyāyāṃsamabhivādayan asau nāmāhamasmīti svaṃ nāma parikīrtayet
meaning: अभिवादात् परं विप्रो ज्यायांसमभिवादयन् असौ नामाहमस्मीति स्वं नाम परिकीर्तयेत्
text: The Brāhmaṇa, when greeting an elder, should, after the accosting, pronounce his name, (saying) “this here, I am named so and so,”
iast: abhivādāt paraṃ vipro jyāyāṃsamabhivādayan asau nāmāhamasmīti svaṃ nāma parikīrtayet

sanskrit_iast: nāmadheyasya ye ke cidabhivādaṃ na jānate tān prājño 'hamiti brūyāt striyaḥ sarvāstathaiva ca
meaning: नामधेयस्य ये के चिदभिवादं न जानते तान् प्राज्ञो 'हमिति ब्रूयात् स्त्रियः सर्वास्तथैव च
text: To those persons who do not comprehend the (significance of the) name (pronounced) in the words of greeting, the wise one should say ‘I’; similarly to all, women.
iast: nāmadheyasya ye ke cidabhivādaṃ na jānate tān prājño 'hamiti brūyāt striyaḥ sarvāstathaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: bhoḥśabdaṃ kīrtayedante svasya nāmno 'bhivādane nāmnāṃ svarūpabhāvo hi bhobhāva ṛṣibhiḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: भोःशब्दं कीर्तयेदन्ते स्वस्य नाम्नो 'भिवादने नाम्नां स्वरूपभावो हि भोभाव ऋषिभिः स्मृतः
text: In saluting, one should pronounce the term “Oh, Sir” at the end op his own name; since it has been declared by the sages that the form “Oh, Sir” represents the form of all names.
iast: bhoḥśabdaṃ kīrtayedante svasya nāmno 'bhivādane nāmnāṃ svarūpabhāvo hi bhobhāva ṛṣibhiḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āyuṣmān bhava saumyaiti vācyo vipro 'bhivādane akāraścāsya nāmno 'nte vācyaḥ pūrvākṣaraḥ plutaḥ
meaning: आयुष्मान् भव सौम्यैति वाच्यो विप्रो 'भिवादने अकारश्चास्य नाम्नो 'न्ते वाच्यः पूर्वाक्षरः प्लुतः
text: On saluting, the Brāhmaṇa should he answered with the words ‘Be long lived, O Gentle One’; and at the end of his name the vowel “a,” which occurs at the end of the consonant, should be pronounced ultra long.
iast: āyuṣmān bhava saumyaiti vācyo vipro 'bhivādane akāraścāsya nāmno 'nte vācyaḥ pūrvākṣaraḥ plutaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo na vettyabhivādasya vipraḥ pratyabhivādanam nābhivādyaḥ sa viduṣā yathā śūdrastathaiva saḥ
meaning: यो न वेत्त्यभिवादस्य विप्रः प्रत्यभिवादनम् नाभिवाद्यः स विदुषा यथा शूद्रस्तथैव सः
text: The Brāhmaṇa who knows not the return-greeting of the greeting of salutation does not deserve to be saluted by the learned; he is exactly as the śūdra is.
iast: yo na vettyabhivādasya vipraḥ pratyabhivādanam nābhivādyaḥ sa viduṣā yathā śūdrastathaiva saḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇaṃ kuśalaṃ pṛcchet kṣatrabandhumanāmayam vaiśyaṃ kṣemaṃ samāgamya śūdramārogyameva ca
meaning: ब्राह्मणं कुशलं पृच्छेत् क्षत्रबन्धुमनामयम् वैश्यं क्षेमं समागम्य शूद्रमारोग्यमेव च
text: Having met a Brāhmaṇa, one should ask him his “welfare,” a Kṣatriya his “freedom from distemper,” a Vaiśya his “prosperity,” and a Shudra his “freedom from disease.”
iast: brāhmaṇaṃ kuśalaṃ pṛcchet kṣatrabandhumanāmayam vaiśyaṃ kṣemaṃ samāgamya śūdramārogyameva ca

sanskrit_iast: avācyo dīkṣito nāmnā yavīyānapi yo bhavet bhobhavatpūrvakaṃ tvenamabhibhāṣeta dharmavit
meaning: अवाच्यो दीक्षितो नाम्ना यवीयानपि यो भवेत् भोभवत्पूर्वकं त्वेनमभिभाषेत धर्मवित्
text: A person who has been initiated (for a rite), even though he be younger, should not be addressed by name; one who knows the law should address him beginning with such terms as “sir” and “your worship.”
iast: avācyo dīkṣito nāmnā yavīyānapi yo bhavet bhobhavatpūrvakaṃ tvenamabhibhāṣeta dharmavit

sanskrit_iast: parapatnī tu yā strī syādasaṃbandhā ca yonitaḥ tāṃ brūyādbhavatītyevaṃ subhage bhaginīti ca
meaning: परपत्नी तु या स्त्री स्यादसंबन्धा च योनितः तां ब्रूयाद्भवतीत्येवं सुभगे भगिनीति च
text: A female who is the wife of another person, and is not related to one by birth, should be addressed as “Lady,” and also “blessed,” or “sister.”
iast: parapatnī tu yā strī syādasaṃbandhā ca yonitaḥ tāṃ brūyādbhavatītyevaṃ subhage bhaginīti ca

sanskrit_iast: mātulāṃśca pitṛvyāṃśca śvaśurān ṛtvijo gurūn asāvahamiti brūyāt pratyutthāya yavīyasaḥ
meaning: मातुलांश्च पितृव्यांश्च श्वशुरान् ऋत्विजो गुरून् असावहमिति ब्रूयात् प्रत्युत्थाय यवीयसः
text: One should rise and say ‘here (so and so) I am,’ to such maternal uncles, paternal uncles, fathers-in-law and superiors as happen to be younger.
iast: mātulāṃśca pitṛvyāṃśca śvaśurān ṛtvijo gurūn asāvahamiti brūyāt pratyutthāya yavīyasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mātṛśvasā mātulānī śvaśrūratha pitṛśvasā saṃpūjyā gurupatnīvat samāstā gurubhāryayā
meaning: मातृश्वसा मातुलानी श्वश्रूरथ पितृश्वसा संपूज्या गुरुपत्नीवत् समास्ता गुरुभार्यया
text: Mother’s sister, maternal uncle’s wife, mother-in-law, and father’s sister deserve to re honoured like the teacher’s wife; all these are equal to the wife of the Teacher.
iast: mātṛśvasā mātulānī śvaśrūratha pitṛśvasā saṃpūjyā gurupatnīvat samāstā gurubhāryayā

sanskrit_iast: bhrāturbhāryaupasaṅgrāhyā savarṇā 'hanyahanyapi viproṣya tūpasaṅgrāhyā jñātisaṃbandhiyoṣitaḥ
meaning: भ्रातुर्भार्यौपसङ्ग्राह्या सवर्णा 'हन्यहन्यपि विप्रोष्य तूपसङ्ग्राह्या ज्ञातिसंबन्धियोषितः
text: The brother’s wife, if of the same caste, should be clasped in the feet day by day; but the wives of other paternal, maternal and other relatives should be so clasped only when one has gone on a journey.
iast: bhrāturbhāryaupasaṅgrāhyā savarṇā 'hanyahanyapi viproṣya tūpasaṅgrāhyā jñātisaṃbandhiyoṣitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: piturbhaginyāṃ mātuśca jyāyasyāṃ ca svasaryapi mātṛvadvṛttimātiṣṭhen mātā tābhyo garīyasī
meaning: पितुर्भगिन्यां मातुश्च ज्यायस्यां च स्वसर्यपि मातृवद्वृत्तिमातिष्ठेन् माता ताभ्यो गरीयसी
text: Towards his father’s sister, his mother’s sister, and his own elder sister, one should adopt the same behaviour as towards his mother; but the mother is more venerable than these.
iast: piturbhaginyāṃ mātuśca jyāyasyāṃ ca svasaryapi mātṛvadvṛttimātiṣṭhen mātā tābhyo garīyasī

sanskrit_iast: daśābdākhyaṃ paurasakhyaṃ pañcābdākhyaṃ kalābhṛtām tryabdapūrvaṃ śrotriyāṇāṃ svalpenāpi svayoniṣu
meaning: दशाब्दाख्यं पौरसख्यं पञ्चाब्दाख्यं कलाभृताम् त्र्यब्दपूर्वं श्रोत्रियाणां स्वल्पेनापि स्वयोनिषु
text: Among citizens friendship and equality are regarded as ranging within ten years (of age-difference); among artists, it is regarded as ranging within five years; among learned men, it proceeds up to three years; and among blood-relations, it ranges only within a very short period of time.
iast: daśābdākhyaṃ paurasakhyaṃ pañcābdākhyaṃ kalābhṛtām tryabdapūrvaṃ śrotriyāṇāṃ svalpenāpi svayoniṣu

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇaṃ daśavarṣaṃ tu śatavarṣaṃ tu bhūmipam pitāputrau vijānīyādbrāhmaṇastu tayoḥ pitā
meaning: ब्राह्मणं दशवर्षं तु शतवर्षं तु भूमिपम् पितापुत्रौ विजानीयाद्ब्राह्मणस्तु तयोः पिता
text: The Brāhmaṇa or ten years and the Kṣatriya or a hundred years should be known as father and son; and of the two this Brāhmaṇa is the father.
iast: brāhmaṇaṃ daśavarṣaṃ tu śatavarṣaṃ tu bhūmipam pitāputrau vijānīyādbrāhmaṇastu tayoḥ pitā

sanskrit_iast: vittaṃ bandhurvayaḥ karma vidyā bhavati pañcamī etāni mānyasthānāni garīyo yadyaduttaram mānasthānāni
meaning: वित्तं बन्धुर्वयः कर्म विद्या भवति पञ्चमी एतानि मान्यस्थानानि गरीयो यद्यदुत्तरम् मानस्थानानि
text: Wealth, Relation, Age, Action and Learning, as the fifth,—these are the grounds of respect; (among them) that which follows is weightier (than that which goes before it).
iast: vittaṃ bandhurvayaḥ karma vidyā bhavati pañcamī etāni mānyasthānāni garīyo yadyaduttaram mānasthānāni

sanskrit_iast: pañcānāṃ triṣu varṇeṣu bhūyāṃsi guṇavanti ca yatra syuḥ so 'tra mānārhaḥ śūdro 'pi daśamīṃ gataḥ
meaning: पञ्चानां त्रिषु वर्णेषु भूयांसि गुणवन्ति च यत्र स्युः सो 'त्र मानार्हः शूद्रो 'पि दशमीं गतः
text: Among the three (higher) castes, he, in whom there are present most op these five, and of high degree, deserves (greater) respect; as also the Śūdra who has reached the tenth stage (of life).
iast: pañcānāṃ triṣu varṇeṣu bhūyāṃsi guṇavanti ca yatra syuḥ so 'tra mānārhaḥ śūdro 'pi daśamīṃ gataḥ

sanskrit_iast: cakriṇo daśamīsthasya rogiṇo bhāriṇaḥ striyāḥ snātakasya ca rājñaśca panthā deyo varasya ca
meaning: चक्रिणो दशमीस्थस्य रोगिणो भारिणः स्त्रियाः स्नातकस्य च राज्ञश्च पन्था देयो वरस्य च
text: Way should be made for one in a chariot, for one who is in the tenth stage of life, for one suffering from disease, for one carrying a burden, for a woman, for the person who has just passed out of studentship, for the king and for the bridegroom.
iast: cakriṇo daśamīsthasya rogiṇo bhāriṇaḥ striyāḥ snātakasya ca rājñaśca panthā deyo varasya ca

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ tu samāvetānāṃ mānyau snātakapārthivau rājasnātakayoścaiva snātako nṛpamānabhāk
meaning: तेषां तु समावेतानां मान्यौ स्नातकपार्थिवौ राजस्नातकयोश्चैव स्नातको नृपमानभाक्
text: Among these, when they come together, the man who has just passed his studentship and the King deserve to be honoured; and between the person just passed his studentship and the King, the person just passed his studentship receives the respects op the King.
iast: teṣāṃ tu samāvetānāṃ mānyau snātakapārthivau rājasnātakayoścaiva snātako nṛpamānabhāk

sanskrit_iast: upanīya tu yaḥ śiṣyaṃ vedamadhyāpayeddvijaḥ sakalpaṃ sarahasyaṃ ca tamācāryaṃ pracakṣate
meaning: उपनीय तु यः शिष्यं वेदमध्यापयेद्द्विजः सकल्पं सरहस्यं च तमाचार्यं प्रचक्षते
text: The Brāhmana who, having initiated a pupil, teaches him the Veda along with the Ritualistic and esoteric treatises,—him they call, ‘Ācārya,’ ‘Preceptor’
iast: upanīya tu yaḥ śiṣyaṃ vedamadhyāpayeddvijaḥ sakalpaṃ sarahasyaṃ ca tamācāryaṃ pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: ekadeśaṃ tu vedasya vedāṅgānyapi vā punaḥ yo 'dhyāpayati vṛttyarthamupādhyāyaḥ sa ucyate
meaning: एकदेशं तु वेदस्य वेदाङ्गान्यपि वा पुनः यो 'ध्यापयति वृत्त्यर्थमुपाध्यायः स उच्यते
text: He is called “Upādhyāya,” “Sub-teacher,” who teaches, for a living, only a part of the Veda, or only the Vedic subsidiary sciences.
iast: ekadeśaṃ tu vedasya vedāṅgānyapi vā punaḥ yo 'dhyāpayati vṛttyarthamupādhyāyaḥ sa ucyate

sanskrit_iast: niṣekādīni karmāṇi yaḥ karoti yathāvidhi saṃbhāvayati cānnena sa vipro gururucyate
meaning: निषेकादीनि कर्माणि यः करोति यथाविधि संभावयति चान्नेन स विप्रो गुरुरुच्यते
text: That Brāhmaṇa, who performs, in the prescribed manner, one’s sacramental rites beginning with the rites of impregnation, and supports him with food, is called the “Guru,” “Mentor.”
iast: niṣekādīni karmāṇi yaḥ karoti yathāvidhi saṃbhāvayati cānnena sa vipro gururucyate

sanskrit_iast: agnyādheyaṃ pākayajñānagniṣṭomādikān makhān yaḥ karoti vṛto yasya sa tasyaṛtvigihaucyate
meaning: अग्न्याधेयं पाकयज्ञानग्निष्टोमादिकान् मखान् यः करोति वृतो यस्य स तस्यऋत्विगिहौच्यते
text: He who, being duly appointed, performs, for one the Fire-laying rite, the Cooked Sacrifices and the Agniṣṭoma and other sacrifices,—is called his “officiating priest.”
iast: agnyādheyaṃ pākayajñānagniṣṭomādikān makhān yaḥ karoti vṛto yasya sa tasyaṛtvigihaucyate

sanskrit_iast: ya āvṛṇotyavitathaṃ brahmaṇā śravaṇāvubhau sa mātā sa pitā jñeyastaṃ na druhyet kadā cana
meaning: य आवृणोत्यवितथं ब्रह्मणा श्रवणावुभौ स माता स पिता ज्ञेयस्तं न द्रुह्येत् कदा चन
text: He who rightly fills one’s both ears with the Veda should be regarded as his Father and Mother; one should not, at any time, do him harm.
iast: ya āvṛṇotyavitathaṃ brahmaṇā śravaṇāvubhau sa mātā sa pitā jñeyastaṃ na druhyet kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: upādhyāyān daśācārya ācāryāṇāṃ śataṃ pitā sahasraṃ tu pitr̥̄n mātā gauraveṇātiricyate
meaning: उपाध्यायान् दशाचार्य आचार्याणां शतं पिता सहस्रं तु पित्र्̥̄न् माता गौरवेणातिरिच्यते
text: In veneration, the Preceptor excels ten Sub-teachers; the Father a hundred preceptors, and the Mother a thousand Fathers.
iast: upādhyāyān daśācārya ācāryāṇāṃ śataṃ pitā sahasraṃ tu pitr̥̄n mātā gauraveṇātiricyate

sanskrit_iast: utpādakabrahmadātrorgarīyān brahmadaḥ pitā brahmajanma hi viprasya pretya caiha ca śāśvatam
meaning: उत्पादकब्रह्मदात्रोर्गरीयान् ब्रह्मदः पिता ब्रह्मजन्म हि विप्रस्य प्रेत्य चैह च शाश्वतम्
text: Between the progenitor and the imparter of the veda, the imparter op the veda is the more venerable father; for the brāhmaṇa’s “birth” is the veda, eternally,—here as well as after death.
iast: utpādakabrahmadātrorgarīyān brahmadaḥ pitā brahmajanma hi viprasya pretya caiha ca śāśvatam

sanskrit_iast: kāmān mātā pitā cainaṃ yadutpādayato mithaḥ saṃbhūtiṃ tasya tāṃ vidyādyadyonāvabhijāyate
meaning: कामान् माता पिता चैनं यदुत्पादयतो मिथः संभूतिं तस्य तां विद्याद्यद्योनावभिजायते
text: When the Father and Mother beget one through mutual desire,—this that he is born in the womb is to be regarded as his “production.”
iast: kāmān mātā pitā cainaṃ yadutpādayato mithaḥ saṃbhūtiṃ tasya tāṃ vidyādyadyonāvabhijāyate

sanskrit_iast: ācāryastvasya yāṃ jātiṃ vidhivadvedapāragaḥ utpādayati sāvitryā sā satyā sā 'jarā 'marā
meaning: आचार्यस्त्वस्य यां जातिं विधिवद्वेदपारगः उत्पादयति सावित्र्या सा सत्या सा 'जरा 'मरा
text: But the “birth” which the Preceptor, well-versed in the Veda, brings about for him, in the lawful manner, by means of the Sāvitrī,—that is real, imperishable, immortal.
iast: ācāryastvasya yāṃ jātiṃ vidhivadvedapāragaḥ utpādayati sāvitryā sā satyā sā 'jarā 'marā

sanskrit_iast: alpaṃ vā bahu vā yasya śrutasyaupakaroti yaḥ tamapīha guruṃ vidyātśrutaupakriyayā tayā
meaning: अल्पं वा बहु वा यस्य श्रुतस्यौपकरोति यः तमपीह गुरुं विद्यात्श्रुतौपक्रियया तया
text: If one benefits him by means of knowledge, more or less,—him also one should regard herb as the “mentor,” by virtue of that benefit of knowledge.
iast: alpaṃ vā bahu vā yasya śrutasyaupakaroti yaḥ tamapīha guruṃ vidyātśrutaupakriyayā tayā

sanskrit_iast: brāhmasya janmanaḥ kartā svadharmasya ca śāsitā bālo 'pi vipro vṛddhasya pitā bhavati dharmataḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मस्य जन्मनः कर्ता स्वधर्मस्य च शासिता बालो 'पि विप्रो वृद्धस्य पिता भवति धर्मतः
text: The Brāhmaṇa, who brings about his Vedic birth, and teaches him his duty,—even though he be a mere child,—becomes in law the father of the old man (whom he teaches).
iast: brāhmasya janmanaḥ kartā svadharmasya ca śāsitā bālo 'pi vipro vṛddhasya pitā bhavati dharmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: adhyāpayāmāsa pitr̥̄n śiśurāṅgirasaḥ kaviḥ putrakā iti hauvāca jñānena parigṛhya tān
meaning: अध्यापयामास पित्र्̥̄न् शिशुराङ्गिरसः कविः पुत्रका इति हौवाच ज्ञानेन परिगृह्य तान्
text: The child Kavi, the son of Aṅgiras, taught his fathers; and having received and trained them by knowledge, he called them “little sons.”
iast: adhyāpayāmāsa pitr̥̄n śiśurāṅgirasaḥ kaviḥ putrakā iti hauvāca jñānena parigṛhya tān

sanskrit_iast: te tamarthamapṛcchanta devānāgatamanyavaḥ devāścaitān sametyaucurnyāyyaṃ vaḥ śiśuruktavān
meaning: ते तमर्थमपृच्छन्त देवानागतमन्यवः देवाश्चैतान् समेत्यौचुर्न्याय्यं वः शिशुरुक्तवान्
text: They, having their anger aroused, questioned the gods about this matter; and the gods, having met together, said to them—“the child has addressed you in the lawful manner.”
iast: te tamarthamapṛcchanta devānāgatamanyavaḥ devāścaitān sametyaucurnyāyyaṃ vaḥ śiśuruktavān

sanskrit_iast: ajño bhavati vai bālaḥ pitā bhavati mantradaḥ ajñaṃ hi bālamityāhuḥ pitaityeva tu mantradam
meaning: अज्ञो भवति वै बालः पिता भवति मन्त्रदः अज्ञं हि बालमित्याहुः पितैत्येव तु मन्त्रदम्
text: The ignorant person is verily a “child,” while the imparted of mantras is the “father.” They have called the ignorant man “child,” and the imparter of mantras, “father.”
iast: ajño bhavati vai bālaḥ pitā bhavati mantradaḥ ajñaṃ hi bālamityāhuḥ pitaityeva tu mantradam

sanskrit_iast: na hāyanairna palitairna vittena na bandhubhiḥ ṛṣayaścakrire dharmaṃ yo 'nūcānaḥ sa no mahān
meaning: न हायनैर्न पलितैर्न वित्तेन न बन्धुभिः ऋषयश्चक्रिरे धर्मं यो 'नूचानः स नो महान्
text: Neither by years, nor by grey hair, nor by wealth, nor by relatives (is greatness attained); since the sages have made the law that ‘he who teaches is the greatest among us.’
iast: na hāyanairna palitairna vittena na bandhubhiḥ ṛṣayaścakrire dharmaṃ yo 'nūcānaḥ sa no mahān

sanskrit_iast: viprāṇāṃ jñānato jyaiṣṭhyaṃ kṣatriyāṇāṃ tu vīryataḥ vaiśyānāṃ dhānyadhanataḥ śūdrāṇāmeva janmataḥ
meaning: विप्राणां ज्ञानतो ज्यैष्ठ्यं क्षत्रियाणां तु वीर्यतः वैश्यानां धान्यधनतः शूद्राणामेव जन्मतः
text: Among Brāhmaṇas seniority is by knowledge; among Kṣatriyas by valour; and among Vaiśyas by grains and riches; among Shudras alone it is by age.
iast: viprāṇāṃ jñānato jyaiṣṭhyaṃ kṣatriyāṇāṃ tu vīryataḥ vaiśyānāṃ dhānyadhanataḥ śūdrāṇāmeva janmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: na tena vṛddho bhavati yenāsya palitaṃ śiraḥ yo vai yuvā 'pyadhīyānastaṃ devāḥ sthaviraṃ viduḥ
meaning: न तेन वृद्धो भवति येनास्य पलितं शिरः यो वै युवा 'प्यधीयानस्तं देवाः स्थविरं विदुः
text: One does not become venerable by the fact that his hair has turned grey; the gods know him to be venerable who, though young, continues to study.
iast: na tena vṛddho bhavati yenāsya palitaṃ śiraḥ yo vai yuvā 'pyadhīyānastaṃ devāḥ sthaviraṃ viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathā kāṣṭhamayo hastī yathā carmamayo mṛgaḥ yaśca vipro 'nadhīyānastrayaste nāma bibhrati
meaning: यथा काष्ठमयो हस्ती यथा चर्ममयो मृगः यश्च विप्रो 'नधीयानस्त्रयस्ते नाम बिभ्रति
text: As the elephant made of wood, as the deer made of leather, so the non-learning Brāhmaṇa,—these three merely bear their names.
iast: yathā kāṣṭhamayo hastī yathā carmamayo mṛgaḥ yaśca vipro 'nadhīyānastrayaste nāma bibhrati

sanskrit_iast: yathā ṣaṇḍho 'phalaḥ strīṣu yathā gaurgavi cāphalā yathā cājñe 'phalaṃ dānaṃ tathā vipro 'nṛco 'phalaḥ
meaning: यथा षण्ढो 'फलः स्त्रीषु यथा गौर्गवि चाफला यथा चाज्ञे 'फलं दानं तथा विप्रो 'नृचो 'फलः
text: As the eunuch is useless among women, as the cow is useless among cows, as a gift to the ignorant person is useless, even so is the Bbrāhmaṇa useless who is devoid of the Veda.
iast: yathā ṣaṇḍho 'phalaḥ strīṣu yathā gaurgavi cāphalā yathā cājñe 'phalaṃ dānaṃ tathā vipro 'nṛco 'phalaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ahiṃsayaiva bhūtānāṃ kāryaṃ śreyo 'nuśāsanam vāk caiva madhurā ślakṣṇā prayojyā dharmamicchatā
meaning: अहिंसयैव भूतानां कार्यं श्रेयो 'नुशासनम् वाक् चैव मधुरा श्लक्ष्णा प्रयोज्या धर्ममिच्छता
text: Teaching for good should be imparted to living beings, without injury to them; and sweet and soft words should be employed by one who seeks for merit.
iast: ahiṃsayaiva bhūtānāṃ kāryaṃ śreyo 'nuśāsanam vāk caiva madhurā ślakṣṇā prayojyā dharmamicchatā

sanskrit_iast: yasya vācmanasī śuddhe samyaggupte ca sarvadā sa vai sarvamavāpnoti vedāntaupagataṃ phalam
meaning: यस्य वाच्मनसी शुद्धे सम्यग्गुप्ते च सर्वदा स वै सर्वमवाप्नोति वेदान्तौपगतं फलम्
text: He, whose speech and mind are pure and ever properly guarded, obtains the whole reward recognised by the canons of the Veda.
iast: yasya vācmanasī śuddhe samyaggupte ca sarvadā sa vai sarvamavāpnoti vedāntaupagataṃ phalam

sanskrit_iast: nāruṃtudaḥ syādārto 'pi na paradrohakarmadhīḥ yayā 'syaudvijate vācā nālokyāṃ tāmudīrayet
meaning: नारुंतुदः स्यादार्तो 'पि न परद्रोहकर्मधीः यया 'स्यौद्विजते वाचा नालोक्यां तामुदीरयेत्
text: Even though pained, one should not (use such words as) cut to the quick; he should not do, or think of, injury to others; he should not utter words by which others are pained, and which (therefore) will obstruct his passage to (higher) regions.
iast: nāruṃtudaḥ syādārto 'pi na paradrohakarmadhīḥ yayā 'syaudvijate vācā nālokyāṃ tāmudīrayet

sanskrit_iast: sammānādbrāhmaṇo nityamudvijeta viṣādiva amṛtasyaiva cākāṅkṣedavamānasya sarvadā
meaning: सम्मानाद्ब्राह्मणो नित्यमुद्विजेत विषादिव अमृतस्यैव चाकाङ्क्षेदवमानस्य सर्वदा
text: The Brāhmaṇa should ever shrink from reverence, as from poison; and he should always seek for disrespect, as for nectar.
iast: sammānādbrāhmaṇo nityamudvijeta viṣādiva amṛtasyaiva cākāṅkṣedavamānasya sarvadā

sanskrit_iast: sukhaṃ hyavamataḥ śete sukhaṃ ca pratibudhyate sukhaṃ carati loke 'sminnavamantā vinaśyati
meaning: सुखं ह्यवमतः शेते सुखं च प्रतिबुध्यते सुखं चरति लोके 'स्मिन्नवमन्ता विनश्यति
text: One who is scorned sleeps in comport and wakes up in comfort and goes about in the world in comport; it is the scorner that perishes.
iast: sukhaṃ hyavamataḥ śete sukhaṃ ca pratibudhyate sukhaṃ carati loke 'sminnavamantā vinaśyati

sanskrit_iast: anena kramayogena saṃskṛtātmā dvijaḥ śanaiḥ gurau vasan sañcinuyādbrahmādhigamikaṃ tapaḥ
meaning: अनेन क्रमयोगेन संस्कृतात्मा द्विजः शनैः गुरौ वसन् सञ्चिनुयाद्ब्रह्माधिगमिकं तपः
text: Sanctified in self, the twice-born man, while dwelling with his Teacher, should, by the adoption of this course, generally accumulate sanctity for the learning of the Veda.
iast: anena kramayogena saṃskṛtātmā dvijaḥ śanaiḥ gurau vasan sañcinuyādbrahmādhigamikaṃ tapaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tapoviśeṣairvividhairvrataiśca vidhicoditaiḥ vedaḥ kṛtsno 'dhigantavyaḥ sarahasyo dvijanmanā
meaning: तपोविशेषैर्विविधैर्व्रतैश्च विधिचोदितैः वेदः कृत्स्नो 'धिगन्तव्यः सरहस्यो द्विजन्मना
text: The entire Veda, along with the Esoteric Treatises, should be learnt by the twice-born person,—by means of various kinds of austerities and observances prescribed by rule.
iast: tapoviśeṣairvividhairvrataiśca vidhicoditaiḥ vedaḥ kṛtsno 'dhigantavyaḥ sarahasyo dvijanmanā

sanskrit_iast: vedameva sadā 'bhyasyet tapastapsyan dvijottamaḥ vedābhyāso hi viprasya tapaḥ paramihaucyate
meaning: वेदमेव सदा 'भ्यस्येत् तपस्तप्स्यन् द्विजोत्तमः वेदाभ्यासो हि विप्रस्य तपः परमिहौच्यते
text: The best of Brāhmaṇas, desiring to acquire piety, should constantly repeat the Veda; because for the Brāhmaṇa, Veda-repeating is declared to be the highest penance on earth.
iast: vedameva sadā 'bhyasyet tapastapsyan dvijottamaḥ vedābhyāso hi viprasya tapaḥ paramihaucyate

sanskrit_iast: ā haiva sa nakhāgrebhyaḥ paramaṃ tapyate tapaḥ yaḥ sragvyapi dvijo 'dhīte svādhyāyaṃ śaktito 'nvaham
meaning: आ हैव स नखाग्रेभ्यः परमं तप्यते तपः यः स्रग्व्यपि द्विजो 'धीते स्वाध्यायं शक्तितो 'न्वहम्
text: It is said that that twice-born man, who, even though garlanded, recites the Veda daily to the best of his capacity, undergoes the highest penance to his very nail-tips.
iast: ā haiva sa nakhāgrebhyaḥ paramaṃ tapyate tapaḥ yaḥ sragvyapi dvijo 'dhīte svādhyāyaṃ śaktito 'nvaham

sanskrit_iast: yo 'nadhītya dvijo vedamanyatra kurute śramam sa jīvanneva śūdratvamāśu gacchati sānvayaḥ
meaning: यो 'नधीत्य द्विजो वेदमन्यत्र कुरुते श्रमम् स जीवन्नेव शूद्रत्वमाशु गच्छति सान्वयः
text: The twice-born man, who, not having learnt the veda, labours over other things, soon falls, along with his descendants, even while living, to the state of the śūdra.
iast: yo 'nadhītya dvijo vedamanyatra kurute śramam sa jīvanneva śūdratvamāśu gacchati sānvayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: māturagre 'dhijananaṃ dvitīyaṃ mauñjibandhane tṛtīyaṃ yajñadīkṣāyāṃ dvijasya śruticodanāt
meaning: मातुरग्रे 'धिजननं द्वितीयं मौञ्जिबन्धने तृतीयं यज्ञदीक्षायां द्विजस्य श्रुतिचोदनात्
text: According to the directions of the Revealed Word, the first birth of the twice-born man is from the mother, the second, after the Ggirdle-tying ceremony, and the third, after sacrificial initiation.
iast: māturagre 'dhijananaṃ dvitīyaṃ mauñjibandhane tṛtīyaṃ yajñadīkṣāyāṃ dvijasya śruticodanāt

sanskrit_iast: tatra yadbrahmajanmāsya mauñjībandhanacihnitam tatrāsya mātā sāvitrī pitā tvācārya ucyate
meaning: तत्र यद्ब्रह्मजन्मास्य मौञ्जीबन्धनचिह्नितम् तत्रास्य माता सावित्री पिता त्वाचार्य उच्यते
text: Among these, at that Brahmic birth which is. marked by the tying of the girdle, the Sāvitrī has been declared to be his “Mother,” and the Teacher his “Father.”
iast: tatra yadbrahmajanmāsya mauñjībandhanacihnitam tatrāsya mātā sāvitrī pitā tvācārya ucyate

sanskrit_iast: vedapradānādācāryaṃ pitaraṃ paricakṣate na hyasmin yujyate karma kiñcidā mauñjibandhanāt
meaning: वेदप्रदानादाचार्यं पितरं परिचक्षते न ह्यस्मिन् युज्यते कर्म किञ्चिदा मौञ्जिबन्धनात्
text: They call the Teacher “father,” on account of his imparting the Veda. Before the tying of the girdle, the performance of no religious act is proper for him.
iast: vedapradānādācāryaṃ pitaraṃ paricakṣate na hyasmin yujyate karma kiñcidā mauñjibandhanāt

sanskrit_iast: nābhivyāhārayedbrahma svadhāninayanādṛte śūdreṇa hi samastāvadyāvadvede na jāyate
meaning: नाभिव्याहारयेद्ब्रह्म स्वधानिनयनादृते शूद्रेण हि समस्तावद्यावद्वेदे न जायते
text: He should hot pronounce Vedic texts, apart from the Svadhā-offering; because so long as he is not born in the Veda, he is equal to a śūdra.
iast: nābhivyāhārayedbrahma svadhāninayanādṛte śūdreṇa hi samastāvadyāvadvede na jāyate

sanskrit_iast: kṛtaupanayanasyāsya vratādeśanamiṣyate brahmaṇo grahaṇaṃ caiva krameṇa vidhipūrvakam
meaning: कृतौपनयनस्यास्य व्रतादेशनमिष्यते ब्रह्मणो ग्रहणं चैव क्रमेण विधिपूर्वकम्
text: For the boy whose initiatory rite has been performed, instruction regarding; observances is considered desirable; as also the getting up of the Veda, in due course, according to the prescribed rule.
iast: kṛtaupanayanasyāsya vratādeśanamiṣyate brahmaṇo grahaṇaṃ caiva krameṇa vidhipūrvakam

sanskrit_iast: yadyasya vihitaṃ carma yat sūtraṃ yā ca mekhalā yo daṇḍo yatca vasanaṃ tat tadasya vrateṣvapi
meaning: यद्यस्य विहितं चर्म यत् सूत्रं या च मेखला यो दण्डो यत्च वसनं तत् तदस्य व्रतेष्वपि
text: That skin, that sac red thread, that girdle, that staff and that garment, which has been prescribed for one, stand during the observances also.
iast: yadyasya vihitaṃ carma yat sūtraṃ yā ca mekhalā yo daṇḍo yatca vasanaṃ tat tadasya vrateṣvapi

sanskrit_iast: sevetaimāṃstu niyamān brahmacārī gurau vasan sanniyamyaindriyagrāmaṃ tapovṛddhiarthamātmanaḥ
meaning: सेवेतैमांस्तु नियमान् ब्रह्मचारी गुरौ वसन् सन्नियम्यैन्द्रियग्रामं तपोवृद्धिअर्थमात्मनः
text: With a view to enhancing his own piety, the Rreligious Student should, while living with his Teacher, observe all these rules,—having fully subjugated all his organs.
iast: sevetaimāṃstu niyamān brahmacārī gurau vasan sanniyamyaindriyagrāmaṃ tapovṛddhiarthamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nityaṃ snātvā śuciḥ kuryāddevaṛṣipitṛtarpaṇam devatābhyarcanaṃ caiva samidādhānameva ca
meaning: नित्यं स्नात्वा शुचिः कुर्याद्देवऋषिपितृतर्पणम् देवताभ्यर्चनं चैव समिदाधानमेव च
text: Every day, having bathed and become clean, he should make offerings to deities, Sages and Fathers, and do the worshipping of the deities and the placing of fuel.
iast: nityaṃ snātvā śuciḥ kuryāddevaṛṣipitṛtarpaṇam devatābhyarcanaṃ caiva samidādhānameva ca

sanskrit_iast: varjayen madhu māṃsaṃ ca gandhaṃ mālyaṃ rasān striyaḥ śuktāni yāni sarvāṇi prāṇināṃ caiva hiṃsanam
meaning: वर्जयेन् मधु मांसं च गन्धं माल्यं रसान् स्त्रियः शुक्तानि यानि सर्वाणि प्राणिनां चैव हिंसनम्
text: He should abstain from honey, meat, scent, garland, flavours, women, all fermented acids and also the killing of living creatures.
iast: varjayen madhu māṃsaṃ ca gandhaṃ mālyaṃ rasān striyaḥ śuktāni yāni sarvāṇi prāṇināṃ caiva hiṃsanam

sanskrit_iast: abhyaṅgamañjanaṃ cākṣṇorupānahchatradhāraṇam kāmaṃ krodhaṃ ca lobhaṃ ca nartanaṃ gītavādanam
meaning: अभ्यङ्गमञ्जनं चाक्ष्णोरुपानह्छत्रधारणम् कामं क्रोधं च लोभं च नर्तनं गीतवादनम्
text: From anointing, applying collyrium to the eyes, shoes, holding the umbrella, attachment, anger, avarice, dancing, singing and playing on musical instruments.
iast: abhyaṅgamañjanaṃ cākṣṇorupānahchatradhāraṇam kāmaṃ krodhaṃ ca lobhaṃ ca nartanaṃ gītavādanam

sanskrit_iast: dyūtaṃ ca janavādaṃ ca parivādaṃ tathā 'nṛtam strīṇāṃ ca prekṣaṇālambhamupaghātaṃ parasya ca ālambhā
meaning: द्यूतं च जनवादं च परिवादं तथा 'नृतम् स्त्रीणां च प्रेक्षणालम्भमुपघातं परस्य च आलम्भा
text: From gambling, quarelling with people, calumniating, and also lying; prom gazing at and touching of women, and prom the injuring op others.
iast: dyūtaṃ ca janavādaṃ ca parivādaṃ tathā 'nṛtam strīṇāṃ ca prekṣaṇālambhamupaghātaṃ parasya ca ālambhā

sanskrit_iast: ekaḥ śayīta sarvatra na retaḥ skandayet kva cit kāmāddhi skandayan reto hinasti vratamātmanaḥ
meaning: एकः शयीत सर्वत्र न रेतः स्कन्दयेत् क्व चित् कामाद्धि स्कन्दयन् रेतो हिनस्ति व्रतमात्मनः
text: He should always sleep alone; nowhere should he allow his manhood to run out; by intentionally allowing his manhood to run out, hr breaks his vow.
iast: ekaḥ śayīta sarvatra na retaḥ skandayet kva cit kāmāddhi skandayan reto hinasti vratamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: svapne siktvā brahmacārī dvijaḥ śukramakāmataḥ snātvā 'rkamarcayitvā triḥ punarmāmity ṛcaṃ japet
meaning: स्वप्ने सिक्त्वा ब्रह्मचारी द्विजः शुक्रमकामतः स्नात्वा 'र्कमर्चयित्वा त्रिः पुनर्मामित्य् ऋचं जपेत्
text: The twice-born religious student, haying unintentionally dropped his manhood during a dream, should thrice recite the three vedic verses beginning with “punarmām,” after having bathed and worshipped the sun.
iast: svapne siktvā brahmacārī dvijaḥ śukramakāmataḥ snātvā 'rkamarcayitvā triḥ punarmāmity ṛcaṃ japet

sanskrit_iast: udakumbhaṃ sumanaso gośakṛtmṛttikākuśān āharedyāvadarthāni bhaikṣaṃ cāhar 'haścaret
meaning: उदकुम्भं सुमनसो गोशकृत्मृत्तिकाकुशान् आहरेद्यावदर्थानि भैक्षं चाहर् 'हश्चरेत्
text: He should fetch the jar of water, flowers, cowdung, earth and kuśa-grass,—as much as may be required; and day by day he should beg for alms.
iast: udakumbhaṃ sumanaso gośakṛtmṛttikākuśān āharedyāvadarthāni bhaikṣaṃ cāhar 'haścaret

sanskrit_iast: vedayajñairahīnānāṃ praśastānāṃ svakarmasu brahmacāryāharedbhaikṣaṃ gṛhebhyaḥ prayato 'nvaham
meaning: वेदयज्ञैरहीनानां प्रशस्तानां स्वकर्मसु ब्रह्मचार्याहरेद्भैक्षं गृहेभ्यः प्रयतो 'न्वहम्
text: The Religious Student, being pure, should fetch alms daily from the houses of persons who are not devoid of Veda and sacrifices, and who are famed for their deeds.
iast: vedayajñairahīnānāṃ praśastānāṃ svakarmasu brahmacāryāharedbhaikṣaṃ gṛhebhyaḥ prayato 'nvaham

sanskrit_iast: guroḥ kule na bhikṣeta na jñātikulabandhuṣu alābhe tvanyagehānāṃ pūrvaṃ pūrvaṃ vivarjayet
meaning: गुरोः कुले न भिक्षेत न ज्ञातिकुलबन्धुषु अलाभे त्वन्यगेहानां पूर्वं पूर्वं विवर्जयेत्
text: He should not beg for food from his Teacher’s family; nor from the family of his paternal or maternal relations. But when other houses are not available, he should avoid the preceding in preference to the succeeding.
iast: guroḥ kule na bhikṣeta na jñātikulabandhuṣu alābhe tvanyagehānāṃ pūrvaṃ pūrvaṃ vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ vāpi caredgrāmaṃ pūrvauktānāmasaṃbhave niyamya prayato vācamabhiśastāṃstu varjayet
meaning: सर्वं वापि चरेद्ग्रामं पूर्वौक्तानामसंभवे नियम्य प्रयतो वाचमभिशस्तांस्तु वर्जयेत्
text: In the event of al the afouesaid being not available, he may wander over the whole village, remaining pure and having his speech well-controlled; but he should avoid persons of ill-repute.
iast: sarvaṃ vāpi caredgrāmaṃ pūrvauktānāmasaṃbhave niyamya prayato vācamabhiśastāṃstu varjayet

sanskrit_iast: dūrādāhṛtya samidhaḥ sannidadhyādvihāyasi sāyaṃprātaśca juhuyāt tābhiragnimatandritaḥ
meaning: दूरादाहृत्य समिधः सन्निदध्याद्विहायसि सायंप्रातश्च जुहुयात् ताभिरग्निमतन्द्रितः
text: Having fetched fuel-sticks from a distance, he should place them in the air; and with these he should, without fail, make offerings to the fire, morning and evening.
iast: dūrādāhṛtya samidhaḥ sannidadhyādvihāyasi sāyaṃprātaśca juhuyāt tābhiragnimatandritaḥ

sanskrit_iast: akṛtvā bhaikṣacaraṇamasamidhya ca pāvakam anāturaḥ saptarātramavakīrṇivrataṃ caret
meaning: अकृत्वा भैक्षचरणमसमिध्य च पावकम् अनातुरः सप्तरात्रमवकीर्णिव्रतं चरेत्
text: He who, without being ill, omits for seven days, to beg alms and to offer fuel to the fire, shall perform the rites prescribed for the Avakīrṇin.
iast: akṛtvā bhaikṣacaraṇamasamidhya ca pāvakam anāturaḥ saptarātramavakīrṇivrataṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: bhaikṣeṇa vartayennityaṃ naikānnādī bhavedvratī bhaikṣeṇa vratino vṛttirupavāsasamā smṛtā
meaning: भैक्षेण वर्तयेन्नित्यं नैकान्नादी भवेद्व्रती भैक्षेण व्रतिनो वृत्तिरुपवाससमा स्मृता
text: The avowed student should subsist on alms; he should not (habitually) eat the food given by one person. for the student, subsisting on alms has been declared to be equal to fasting.
iast: bhaikṣeṇa vartayennityaṃ naikānnādī bhavedvratī bhaikṣeṇa vratino vṛttirupavāsasamā smṛtā

sanskrit_iast: vratavaddevadaivatye pitrye karmaṇyathaṛṣivat kāmamabhyarthito 'śnīyādvratamasya na lupyate
meaning: व्रतवद्देवदैवत्ये पित्र्ये कर्मण्यथऋषिवत् काममभ्यर्थितो 'श्नीयाद्व्रतमस्य न लुप्यते
text: During a performance in honour of gods and in honour of ancestors, he may, when invited, eat freely, like an ascetic, in due accordance with his observances. by this his observances do not suffer.
iast: vratavaddevadaivatye pitrye karmaṇyathaṛṣivat kāmamabhyarthito 'śnīyādvratamasya na lupyate

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇasyaiva karmaitadupadiṣṭaṃ manīṣibhiḥ rājanyavaiśyayostvevaṃ naitat karma vidhīyate
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्यैव कर्मैतदुपदिष्टं मनीषिभिः राजन्यवैश्ययोस्त्वेवं नैतत् कर्म विधीयते
text: This duty has been prescribed by the sages for the Brāhmaṇa only; this duty has not been so ordained for the Kṣatriya and the Vaiśya.
iast: brāhmaṇasyaiva karmaitadupadiṣṭaṃ manīṣibhiḥ rājanyavaiśyayostvevaṃ naitat karma vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: codito guruṇā nityamapracodita eva vā kuryādadhyayane yatnamācāryasya hiteṣu ca 'dhyayane yogam
meaning: चोदितो गुरुणा नित्यमप्रचोदित एव वा कुर्यादध्ययने यत्नमाचार्यस्य हितेषु च 'ध्ययने योगम्
text: Prompted by the Teacher, or even when not prompted, he should put forth his exertion to study, and also to doing what is helpful to the teacher.
iast: codito guruṇā nityamapracodita eva vā kuryādadhyayane yatnamācāryasya hiteṣu ca 'dhyayane yogam

sanskrit_iast: śarīraṃ caiva vācaṃ ca buddhīndriyamanāṃsi ca niyamya prāñjalistiṣṭhedvīkṣamāṇo gurormukham
meaning: शरीरं चैव वाचं च बुद्धीन्द्रियमनांसि च नियम्य प्राञ्जलिस्तिष्ठेद्वीक्षमाणो गुरोर्मुखम्
text: Having under control his body and his speech, as also his organs of sensation and his hind, he should stand with joined palms, looking at the face of his Teacher.
iast: śarīraṃ caiva vācaṃ ca buddhīndriyamanāṃsi ca niyamya prāñjalistiṣṭhedvīkṣamāṇo gurormukham

sanskrit_iast: nityamuddhṛtapāṇiḥ syāt sādhuācāraḥ susaṃvṛtaḥ āsyatāmiti cauktaḥ sannāsītābhimukhaṃ guroḥ
meaning: नित्यमुद्धृतपाणिः स्यात् साधुआचारः सुसंवृतः आस्यतामिति चौक्तः सन्नासीताभिमुखं गुरोः
text: He should always have his arm raised, remain well behaved, and well-guarded; when addressed with the words “be seated,” he should sit facing his teacher.
iast: nityamuddhṛtapāṇiḥ syāt sādhuācāraḥ susaṃvṛtaḥ āsyatāmiti cauktaḥ sannāsītābhimukhaṃ guroḥ

sanskrit_iast: hīnānnavastraveṣaḥ syāt sarvadā gurusannidhau uttiṣṭhet prathamaṃ cāsya caramaṃ caiva saṃviśet
meaning: हीनान्नवस्त्रवेषः स्यात् सर्वदा गुरुसन्निधौ उत्तिष्ठेत् प्रथमं चास्य चरमं चैव संविशेत्
text: In the peesenge of his Teacher, he should always have inferior food, dress and apparel; he should rise before him, and go to sleep later.
iast: hīnānnavastraveṣaḥ syāt sarvadā gurusannidhau uttiṣṭhet prathamaṃ cāsya caramaṃ caiva saṃviśet

sanskrit_iast: pratiśrāvaṇasaṃbhāṣe śayāno na samācaret nāsīno na ca bhuñjāno na tiṣṭhanna parālgmukhaḥ
meaning: प्रतिश्रावणसंभाषे शयानो न समाचरेत् नासीनो न च भुञ्जानो न तिष्ठन्न पराल्ग्मुखः
text: He should not listen to and converse with (his Teacher), while lying down; nor while seated, nor while eating, nor while standing, nor with his face turned away.
iast: pratiśrāvaṇasaṃbhāṣe śayāno na samācaret nāsīno na ca bhuñjāno na tiṣṭhanna parālgmukhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āsīnasya sthitaḥ kuryādabhigacchaṃstu tiṣṭhataḥ pratyudgamya tvāvrajataḥ paścāddhāvaṃstu dhāvataḥ
meaning: आसीनस्य स्थितः कुर्यादभिगच्छंस्तु तिष्ठतः प्रत्युद्गम्य त्वाव्रजतः पश्चाद्धावंस्तु धावतः
text: He should do (these) standing, when the teacher is seated; advancing towards him, when he is standing; going forward to him, when he advances; and running after him, when he runs;
iast: āsīnasya sthitaḥ kuryādabhigacchaṃstu tiṣṭhataḥ pratyudgamya tvāvrajataḥ paścāddhāvaṃstu dhāvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: parālgmukhasyābhimukho dūrasthasyaitya cāntikam praṇamya tu śayānasya nideśe caiva tiṣṭhataḥ
meaning: पराल्ग्मुखस्याभिमुखो दूरस्थस्यैत्य चान्तिकम् प्रणम्य तु शयानस्य निदेशे चैव तिष्ठतः
text: Facing him, when the teacher has his pace averted; going near him, when the teacher is at a distance; bowing low, when the teacher is lying down, as also if he is standing close by.
iast: parālgmukhasyābhimukho dūrasthasyaitya cāntikam praṇamya tu śayānasya nideśe caiva tiṣṭhataḥ

sanskrit_iast: nīcaṃ śayyā ''sanaṃ cāsya nityaṃ syādgurusannidhau gurostu cakṣurviṣaye na yathaiṣṭāsano bhavet
meaning: नीचं शय्या ''सनं चास्य नित्यं स्याद्गुरुसन्निधौ गुरोस्तु चक्षुर्विषये न यथैष्टासनो भवेत्
text: When near his Teacher, his bed or seat should always be low; and within sight op his Teacher, he should not sit at ease.
iast: nīcaṃ śayyā ''sanaṃ cāsya nityaṃ syādgurusannidhau gurostu cakṣurviṣaye na yathaiṣṭāsano bhavet

sanskrit_iast: naudāharedasya nāma parokṣamapi kevalam na caivāsyānukurvīta gatibhāṣitaceṣṭitam
meaning: नौदाहरेदस्य नाम परोक्षमपि केवलम् न चैवास्यानुकुर्वीत गतिभाषितचेष्टितम्
text: Even behind the Teacher’s back, he should not pronounce his mere name; nor should he mimic his gait, speech or deportment.
iast: naudāharedasya nāma parokṣamapi kevalam na caivāsyānukurvīta gatibhāṣitaceṣṭitam

sanskrit_iast: guroryatra parivādo nindā vā 'pi pravartate karṇau tatra pidhātavyau gantavyaṃ vā tato 'nyataḥ
meaning: गुरोर्यत्र परिवादो निन्दा वा 'पि प्रवर्तते कर्णौ तत्र पिधातव्यौ गन्तव्यं वा ततो 'न्यतः
text: Where the censuring or defaming of his Teacher is going on, there he should either close his ears, or go away thence to another place.
iast: guroryatra parivādo nindā vā 'pi pravartate karṇau tatra pidhātavyau gantavyaṃ vā tato 'nyataḥ

sanskrit_iast: parīvādāt kharo bhavati śvā vai bhavati nindakaḥ paribhoktā kṛmirbhavati kīṭo bhavati matsarī
meaning: परीवादात् खरो भवति श्वा वै भवति निन्दकः परिभोक्ता कृमिर्भवति कीटो भवति मत्सरी
text: Through censure one becomes an ass, and the defamer becomes a dog; he who lives on him becomes a worm, and he who is jealous becomes an insect.
iast: parīvādāt kharo bhavati śvā vai bhavati nindakaḥ paribhoktā kṛmirbhavati kīṭo bhavati matsarī

sanskrit_iast: dūrastho nārcayedenaṃ na kruddho nāntike striyāḥ yānāsanasthaścaivainamavaruhyābhivādayet
meaning: दूरस्थो नार्चयेदेनं न क्रुद्धो नान्तिके स्त्रियाः यानासनस्थश्चैवैनमवरुह्याभिवादयेत्
text: He should not offer his worship to the teacher while at a distance from him; nor when angry, nor near a woman. If seated upon a conveyance or a seat, he should come down and then salute him.
iast: dūrastho nārcayedenaṃ na kruddho nāntike striyāḥ yānāsanasthaścaivainamavaruhyābhivādayet

sanskrit_iast: prativāte 'nuvāte ca nāsīta guruṇā saha prativātānuvāte asaṃśrave caiva gurorna kiṃ cidapi kīrtayet
meaning: प्रतिवाते 'नुवाते च नासीत गुरुणा सह प्रतिवातानुवाते असंश्रवे चैव गुरोर्न किं चिदपि कीर्तयेत्
text: When sitting in the company of his teacher, uk should not sit either to the lee-ward or to the wind-ward of him; nor should he say anything beyond the hearing of his teacher.
iast: prativāte 'nuvāte ca nāsīta guruṇā saha prativātānuvāte asaṃśrave caiva gurorna kiṃ cidapi kīrtayet

sanskrit_iast: go 'śvauṣṭrayānaprāsādaprastareṣu kaṭeṣu ca āsīta guruṇā sārdhaṃ śilāphalakanauṣu ca
meaning: गो 'श्वौष्ट्रयानप्रासादप्रस्तरेषु कटेषु च आसीत गुरुणा सार्धं शिलाफलकनौषु च
text: He may sit with the teacher on carts drawn by oxen, horses and camels, on terraces and on grass-mats; as also on reedmats, rocks, benches and boats.
iast: go 'śvauṣṭrayānaprāsādaprastareṣu kaṭeṣu ca āsīta guruṇā sārdhaṃ śilāphalakanauṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: gurorgurau sannihite guruvadvṛttimācaret na cānisṛṣṭo guruṇā svān gurūnabhivādayet
meaning: गुरोर्गुरौ सन्निहिते गुरुवद्वृत्तिमाचरेत् न चानिसृष्टो गुरुणा स्वान् गुरूनभिवादयेत्
text: When the Teacher’s teacher happens to be close by, he should adopt towards him the same behaviour as towards his own teacher; but until permitted by his teacher, he should not pay respects to his own elders.
iast: gurorgurau sannihite guruvadvṛttimācaret na cānisṛṣṭo guruṇā svān gurūnabhivādayet

sanskrit_iast: vidyāguruṣvevameva nityā vṛttiḥ svayoniṣu pratiṣedhatsu cādharmāddhitaṃ caupadiśatsvapi
meaning: विद्यागुरुष्वेवमेव नित्या वृत्तिः स्वयोनिषु प्रतिषेधत्सु चाधर्माद्धितं चौपदिशत्स्वपि
text: This same shall be his constant behaviour towards his intellectual teachers, towards his blood-relations, towards persons restraining him from sin and towards those who give him salutary advice.
iast: vidyāguruṣvevameva nityā vṛttiḥ svayoniṣu pratiṣedhatsu cādharmāddhitaṃ caupadiśatsvapi

sanskrit_iast: śreyaḥsu guruvadvṛttiṃ nityameva samācaret guruputreṣu cāryeṣu guroścaiva svabandhuṣu guruputre tathācārye
meaning: श्रेयःसु गुरुवद्वृत्तिं नित्यमेव समाचरेत् गुरुपुत्रेषु चार्येषु गुरोश्चैव स्वबन्धुषु गुरुपुत्रे तथाचार्ये
text: Towards superiors he should always behave as towards the Teacher, as also towards the Teacher’s son who has acquired the position of the Teacher, and towards the Teacher’s own blood-relations.
iast: śreyaḥsu guruvadvṛttiṃ nityameva samācaret guruputreṣu cāryeṣu guroścaiva svabandhuṣu guruputre tathācārye

sanskrit_iast: bālaḥ samānajanmā vā śiṣyo vā yajñakarmaṇi adhyāpayan gurusuto guruvatmānamarhati
meaning: बालः समानजन्मा वा शिष्यो वा यज्ञकर्मणि अध्यापयन् गुरुसुतो गुरुवत्मानमर्हति
text: Whether he be younger, or of equal age, or a student of sacrificial ritual,—the Teacher’s son, imparting instruction, dfserves the same honour as the Teacher.
iast: bālaḥ samānajanmā vā śiṣyo vā yajñakarmaṇi adhyāpayan gurusuto guruvatmānamarhati

sanskrit_iast: utsādanaṃ ca gātrāṇāṃ snāpanaucchiṣṭabhojane na kuryādguruputrasya pādayoścāvanejanam
meaning: उत्सादनं च गात्राणां स्नापनौच्छिष्टभोजने न कुर्याद्गुरुपुत्रस्य पादयोश्चावनेजनम्
text: He shall not shampoo the limbs of his teacher’s son, nor assist him in bathing, nor eat of the food left by him; nor should he wash his feet.
iast: utsādanaṃ ca gātrāṇāṃ snāpanaucchiṣṭabhojane na kuryādguruputrasya pādayoścāvanejanam

sanskrit_iast: guruvat pratipūjyāḥ syuḥ savarṇā guruyoṣitaḥ asavarṇāstu sampūjyāḥ pratyutthānābhivādanaiḥ
meaning: गुरुवत् प्रतिपूज्याः स्युः सवर्णा गुरुयोषितः असवर्णास्तु सम्पूज्याः प्रत्युत्थानाभिवादनैः
text: The teacher’s ladies belonging to the same caste should be honoured like the teacher himself; those not belonging to the same caste should be honoured with rising and salutations.
iast: guruvat pratipūjyāḥ syuḥ savarṇā guruyoṣitaḥ asavarṇāstu sampūjyāḥ pratyutthānābhivādanaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: abhyañjanaṃ snāpanaṃ ca gātrautsādanameva ca gurupatnyā na kāryāṇi keśānāṃ ca prasādhanam
meaning: अभ्यञ्जनं स्नापनं च गात्रौत्सादनमेव च गुरुपत्न्या न कार्याणि केशानां च प्रसाधनम्
text: Annointing, assisting at bath, shampooing of limbs and dressing of the hair should not be done for the teacher’s wife.
iast: abhyañjanaṃ snāpanaṃ ca gātrautsādanameva ca gurupatnyā na kāryāṇi keśānāṃ ca prasādhanam

sanskrit_iast: gurupatnī tu yuvatirnābhivādyaiha pādayoḥ pūrṇaviṃśativarṣeṇa guṇadoṣau vijānatā
meaning: गुरुपत्नी तु युवतिर्नाभिवाद्यैह पादयोः पूर्णविंशतिवर्षेण गुणदोषौ विजानता
text: The teacher’s wife, when young, shall not be saluted at her feet by a pupil who is full twenty years old, and who is conscious of what is good and what is bad.
iast: gurupatnī tu yuvatirnābhivādyaiha pādayoḥ pūrṇaviṃśativarṣeṇa guṇadoṣau vijānatā

sanskrit_iast: svabhāva eṣa nārīṇāṃ narāṇāmiha dūṣaṇam ato 'rthānna pramādyanti pramadāsu vipaścitaḥ
meaning: स्वभाव एष नारीणां नराणामिह दूषणम् अतो 'र्थान्न प्रमाद्यन्ति प्रमदासु विपश्चितः
text: It is the very nature of women to corrupt men. It is for this reason that the wise are never unguarded regarding women.
iast: svabhāva eṣa nārīṇāṃ narāṇāmiha dūṣaṇam ato 'rthānna pramādyanti pramadāsu vipaścitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: avidvāṃsamalaṃ loke vidvāṃsamapi vā punaḥ pramadā hyutpathaṃ netuṃ kāmakrodhavaśānugam
meaning: अविद्वांसमलं लोके विद्वांसमपि वा पुनः प्रमदा ह्युत्पथं नेतुं कामक्रोधवशानुगम्
text: In this world women are capable op leading astray the ignorant, as well as the learned, who becomes a servile follower of desire and passion.
iast: avidvāṃsamalaṃ loke vidvāṃsamapi vā punaḥ pramadā hyutpathaṃ netuṃ kāmakrodhavaśānugam

sanskrit_iast: mātrā svasrā duhitrā vā na viviktāsano bhavet balavānindriyagrāmo vidvāṃsamapi karṣati
meaning: मात्रा स्वस्रा दुहित्रा वा न विविक्तासनो भवेत् बलवानिन्द्रियग्रामो विद्वांसमपि कर्षति
text: One should not sit alone with his mother, sister or daughter. The powerful host of sense-organs overpowers even the learned.
iast: mātrā svasrā duhitrā vā na viviktāsano bhavet balavānindriyagrāmo vidvāṃsamapi karṣati

sanskrit_iast: kāmaṃ tu gurupatnīnāṃ yuvatīnāṃ yuvā bhuvi vidhivadvandanaṃ kuryādasāvahamiti bruvan
meaning: कामं तु गुरुपत्नीनां युवतीनां युवा भुवि विधिवद्वन्दनं कुर्यादसावहमिति ब्रुवन्
text: The young man may perform the proper salutation on the ground to the young wives of his Teacher,—saying “here i am.”
iast: kāmaṃ tu gurupatnīnāṃ yuvatīnāṃ yuvā bhuvi vidhivadvandanaṃ kuryādasāvahamiti bruvan

sanskrit_iast: viproṣya pādagrahaṇamanvahaṃ cābhivādanam gurudāreṣu kurvīta satāṃ dharmamanusmaran
meaning: विप्रोष्य पादग्रहणमन्वहं चाभिवादनम् गुरुदारेषु कुर्वीत सतां धर्ममनुस्मरन्
text: Having returned from a journey, he should clasp the feet of his Teacher’s wife, and daily he should salute her, bearing in mind the duty of the righteous.
iast: viproṣya pādagrahaṇamanvahaṃ cābhivādanam gurudāreṣu kurvīta satāṃ dharmamanusmaran

sanskrit_iast: yathā khanan khanitreṇa naro vāryadhigacchati tathā gurugatāṃ vidyāṃ śuśrūṣuradhigacchati
meaning: यथा खनन् खनित्रेण नरो वार्यधिगच्छति तथा गुरुगतां विद्यां शुश्रूषुरधिगच्छति
text: Just as a man digging with the spade obtains water,—even so one who is eager to serve acquires the learning that is in the Teacher.
iast: yathā khanan khanitreṇa naro vāryadhigacchati tathā gurugatāṃ vidyāṃ śuśrūṣuradhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: muṇḍo vā jaṭilo vā syādatha vā syātśikhājaṭaḥ nainaṃ grāme 'bhinimlocet sūryo nābhyudiyāt kva cit
meaning: मुण्डो वा जटिलो वा स्यादथ वा स्यात्शिखाजटः नैनं ग्रामे 'भिनिम्लोचेत् सूर्यो नाभ्युदियात् क्व चित्
text: He may have his head shaved, or wear his hair in braids, or have only the top-hair braided. The sun should never set, nor should it rise, while he is still in the village.
iast: muṇḍo vā jaṭilo vā syādatha vā syātśikhājaṭaḥ nainaṃ grāme 'bhinimlocet sūryo nābhyudiyāt kva cit
