sanskrit_iast: taṃ cedabhyudiyāt sūryaḥ śayānaṃ kāmacārataḥ nimlocedvā 'pyavijñānāj japannupavaseddinam
meaning: तं चेदभ्युदियात् सूर्यः शयानं कामचारतः निम्लोचेद्वा 'प्यविज्ञानाज् जपन्नुपवसेद्दिनम्
text: If the sun should rise or set while he is still sleeping, either intentionally or unintentionally, he should fast during the day, reciting. (the Sāvitrī).
iast: taṃ cedabhyudiyāt sūryaḥ śayānaṃ kāmacārataḥ nimlocedvā 'pyavijñānāj japannupavaseddinam

sanskrit_iast: sūryeṇa hyabhinirmuktaḥ śayāno 'bhyuditaśca yaḥ 'bhinimluktaḥ prāyaścittamakurvāṇo yuktaḥ syān mahatainasā
meaning: सूर्येण ह्यभिनिर्मुक्तः शयानो 'भ्युदितश्च यः 'भिनिम्लुक्तः प्रायश्चित्तमकुर्वाणो युक्तः स्यान् महतैनसा
text: If during one’s sleep the sun has set, and if during one’s sleep the sun has risen,—if he does not perform the Expiatory Rite, he becomes tainted by grievous sin.
iast: sūryeṇa hyabhinirmuktaḥ śayāno 'bhyuditaśca yaḥ 'bhinimluktaḥ prāyaścittamakurvāṇo yuktaḥ syān mahatainasā

sanskrit_iast: ācamya prayato nityamubhe saṃdhye samāhitaḥ śucau deśe japañjapyamupāsīta yathāvidhi
meaning: आचम्य प्रयतो नित्यमुभे संध्ये समाहितः शुचौ देशे जपञ्जप्यमुपासीत यथाविधि
text: Having sipped water, with calm and collected mind, he shall daily attend upon the two twilights, in a clean place, reciting the mantras to be recited, according to rule.
iast: ācamya prayato nityamubhe saṃdhye samāhitaḥ śucau deśe japañjapyamupāsīta yathāvidhi

sanskrit_iast: yadi strī yadyavarajaḥ śreyaḥ kiṃ cit samācaret tat sarvamācaredyukto yatra cāsya ramen manaḥ
meaning: यदि स्त्री यद्यवरजः श्रेयः किं चित् समाचरेत् तत् सर्वमाचरेद्युक्तो यत्र चास्य रमेन् मनः
text: If either a woman; or a junior person, do something good, he should faithfully perform all that; as also that in which his mind finds satisfaction.
iast: yadi strī yadyavarajaḥ śreyaḥ kiṃ cit samācaret tat sarvamācaredyukto yatra cāsya ramen manaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dharmārthāvucyate śreyaḥ kāmārthau dharma eva ca artha evaiha vā śreyastrivarga iti tu sthitiḥ
meaning: धर्मार्थावुच्यते श्रेयः कामार्थौ धर्म एव च अर्थ एवैह वा श्रेयस्त्रिवर्ग इति तु स्थितिः
text: Spiritual merit and wealth are called “good”; or pleasure and wealth; or spiritual merit alone, or wealth alone is “good”; but the truth is that it is the aggregate of the three.
iast: dharmārthāvucyate śreyaḥ kāmārthau dharma eva ca artha evaiha vā śreyastrivarga iti tu sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācāryaśca pitā caiva mātā bhrātā ca pūrvajaḥ nārtenāpyavamantavyā brāhmaṇena viśeṣataḥ
meaning: आचार्यश्च पिता चैव माता भ्राता च पूर्वजः नार्तेनाप्यवमन्तव्या ब्राह्मणेन विशेषतः
text: The preceptor, the father, the mother and the elder brother should not be treated with disrespect, especially by a Brāhmaṇa,—even though he be distressed
iast: ācāryaśca pitā caiva mātā bhrātā ca pūrvajaḥ nārtenāpyavamantavyā brāhmaṇena viśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācāryo brahmaṇo mūrtiḥ pitā mūrtiḥ prajāpateḥ mātā pṛthivyā mūrtistu bhrātā svo mūrtirātmanaḥ
meaning: आचार्यो ब्रह्मणो मूर्तिः पिता मूर्तिः प्रजापतेः माता पृथिव्या मूर्तिस्तु भ्राता स्वो मूर्तिरात्मनः
text: The preceptor is the embodiment of Brahman; the father is the embodiment of Prajāpati; the Mother is the embodiment of the earth, and one’s own brother is the embodiment of the self.
iast: ācāryo brahmaṇo mūrtiḥ pitā mūrtiḥ prajāpateḥ mātā pṛthivyā mūrtistu bhrātā svo mūrtirātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yaṃ mātāpitarau kleśaṃ sahete saṃbhave nṛṇām na tasya niṣkṛtiḥ śakyā kartuṃ varṣaśatairapi
meaning: यं मातापितरौ क्लेशं सहेते संभवे नृणाम् न तस्य निष्कृतिः शक्या कर्तुं वर्षशतैरपि
text: The trouble that the parents undergo in the birth of children,—for that there can be no compensation even in a hundred years.
iast: yaṃ mātāpitarau kleśaṃ sahete saṃbhave nṛṇām na tasya niṣkṛtiḥ śakyā kartuṃ varṣaśatairapi

sanskrit_iast: tayornityaṃ priyaṃ kuryādācāryasya ca sarvadā teṣveva triṣu tuṣṭeṣu tapaḥ sarvaṃ samāpyate
meaning: तयोर्नित्यं प्रियं कुर्यादाचार्यस्य च सर्वदा तेष्वेव त्रिषु तुष्टेषु तपः सर्वं समाप्यते
text: He should always do what is pleasing to those two and to the preceptor; on these three being satisfied, all austerity becomes completed.
iast: tayornityaṃ priyaṃ kuryādācāryasya ca sarvadā teṣveva triṣu tuṣṭeṣu tapaḥ sarvaṃ samāpyate

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ trayāṇāṃ śuśrūṣā paramaṃ tapa ucyate na tairanabhyanujñāto dharmamanyaṃ samācaret
meaning: तेषां त्रयाणां शुश्रूषा परमं तप उच्यते न तैरनभ्यनुज्ञातो धर्ममन्यं समाचरेत्
text: The service of these three is declared to be the highest austerity; until permitted by them, one should not perform any other meritorious act.
iast: teṣāṃ trayāṇāṃ śuśrūṣā paramaṃ tapa ucyate na tairanabhyanujñāto dharmamanyaṃ samācaret

sanskrit_iast: ta eva hi trayo lokāsta eva traya āśramāḥ ta eva hi trayo vedāsta evauktāstrayo 'gnayaḥ
meaning: त एव हि त्रयो लोकास्त एव त्रय आश्रमाः त एव हि त्रयो वेदास्त एवौक्तास्त्रयो 'ग्नयः
text: These have been declared to be the three regions, these the three life-stages, these the three Vedas and these the three fires.
iast: ta eva hi trayo lokāsta eva traya āśramāḥ ta eva hi trayo vedāsta evauktāstrayo 'gnayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pitā vai gārhapatyo 'gnirmātā 'gnirdakṣiṇaḥ smṛtaḥ gururāhavanīyastu sā 'gnitretā garīyasī
meaning: पिता वै गार्हपत्यो 'ग्निर्माता 'ग्निर्दक्षिणः स्मृतः गुरुराहवनीयस्तु सा 'ग्नित्रेता गरीयसी
text: The Father has been declared to be the Gārhapatya Fire, the Mother the Dakṣiṇa Fire, and the Preceptor the Āhavanīya fire; and this Triad of fires is highly important.
iast: pitā vai gārhapatyo 'gnirmātā 'gnirdakṣiṇaḥ smṛtaḥ gururāhavanīyastu sā 'gnitretā garīyasī

sanskrit_iast: triṣvapramādyanneteṣu trīn lokān vijayedgṛhī dīpyamānaḥ svavapuṣā devavaddivi modate
meaning: त्रिष्वप्रमाद्यन्नेतेषु त्रीन् लोकान् विजयेद्गृही दीप्यमानः स्ववपुषा देववद्दिवि मोदते
text: The Householder who fails not towards these three would win the three regions, and rejoice in heaven, radiant in body, like a God.
iast: triṣvapramādyanneteṣu trīn lokān vijayedgṛhī dīpyamānaḥ svavapuṣā devavaddivi modate

sanskrit_iast: imaṃ lokaṃ mātṛbhaktyā pitṛbhaktyā tu madhyamam guruśuśrūṣayā tvevaṃ brahmalokaṃ samaśnute
meaning: इमं लोकं मातृभक्त्या पितृभक्त्या तु मध्यमम् गुरुशुश्रूषया त्वेवं ब्रह्मलोकं समश्नुते
text: He acquires this region by devotion to his Mother, the middle region by devotion to his Father, and the region of Brahman by serving his Preceptor.
iast: imaṃ lokaṃ mātṛbhaktyā pitṛbhaktyā tu madhyamam guruśuśrūṣayā tvevaṃ brahmalokaṃ samaśnute

sanskrit_iast: sarve tasyādṛtā dharmā yasyaite traya ādṛtāḥ anādṛtāstu yasyaite sarvāstasyāphalāḥ kriyāḥ
meaning: सर्वे तस्यादृता धर्मा यस्यैते त्रय आदृताः अनादृतास्तु यस्यैते सर्वास्तस्याफलाः क्रियाः
text: All the duties have been honoured by him who has honoured these three; and all acts remain fruitless for him who does not honour them.
iast: sarve tasyādṛtā dharmā yasyaite traya ādṛtāḥ anādṛtāstu yasyaite sarvāstasyāphalāḥ kriyāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāvat trayaste jīveyustāvatnānyaṃ samācaret teṣveva nityaṃ śuśrūṣāṃ kuryāt priyahite rataḥ
meaning: यावत् त्रयस्ते जीवेयुस्तावत्नान्यं समाचरेत् तेष्वेव नित्यं शुश्रूषां कुर्यात् प्रियहिते रतः
text: So long as these three live, he should not do anything else; he should always bender service unto them, rejoicing in what is pleasing and beneficial to them.
iast: yāvat trayaste jīveyustāvatnānyaṃ samācaret teṣveva nityaṃ śuśrūṣāṃ kuryāt priyahite rataḥ

sanskrit_iast: teṣāmanuparodhena pāratryaṃ yadyadācaret tat tannivedayet tebhyo manovacanakarmabhiḥ
meaning: तेषामनुपरोधेन पारत्र्यं यद्यदाचरेत् तत् तन्निवेदयेत् तेभ्यो मनोवचनकर्मभिः
text: He should communicate to them by thought, word or deed whatever he may do without injury to them, for the sake of the next life.
iast: teṣāmanuparodhena pāratryaṃ yadyadācaret tat tannivedayet tebhyo manovacanakarmabhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: triṣveteṣvitikṛtyaṃ hi puruṣasya samāpyate eṣa dharmaḥ paraḥ sākṣādupadharmo 'nya ucyate
meaning: त्रिष्वेतेष्वितिकृत्यं हि पुरुषस्य समाप्यते एष धर्मः परः साक्षादुपधर्मो 'न्य उच्यते
text: All that ought to be done by man is finished on these three; this is the highest direct duty; every other is a subordinate duty.
iast: triṣveteṣvitikṛtyaṃ hi puruṣasya samāpyate eṣa dharmaḥ paraḥ sākṣādupadharmo 'nya ucyate

sanskrit_iast: śraddadhānaḥ śubhāṃ vidyāmādadītāvarādapi anyādapi paraṃ dharmaṃ strīratnaṃ duṣkulādapi
meaning: श्रद्दधानः शुभां विद्यामाददीतावरादपि अन्यादपि परं धर्मं स्त्रीरत्नं दुष्कुलादपि
text: One imbued with faith may acquire excellent learning even from a lowly person, special law even from the lowest, and the gem of a wife even from a base family.
iast: śraddadhānaḥ śubhāṃ vidyāmādadītāvarādapi anyādapi paraṃ dharmaṃ strīratnaṃ duṣkulādapi

sanskrit_iast: viṣādapyamṛtaṃ grāhyaṃ bālādapi subhāṣitam amitrādapi sadvṛttamamedhyādapi kāñcanam
meaning: विषादप्यमृतं ग्राह्यं बालादपि सुभाषितम् अमित्रादपि सद्वृत्तममेध्यादपि काञ्चनम्
text: Nectar mat be taken even from poison, good advice even prom a child; good conduct (may be learnt) even from a foe; and gold (mat be taken) even from an impure source.
iast: viṣādapyamṛtaṃ grāhyaṃ bālādapi subhāṣitam amitrādapi sadvṛttamamedhyādapi kāñcanam

sanskrit_iast: striyo ratnānyatho vidyā dharmaḥ śaucaṃ subhāṣitam vividhāni ca śīlpāni samādeyāni sarvataḥ
meaning: स्त्रियो रत्नान्यथो विद्या धर्मः शौचं सुभाषितम् विविधानि च शील्पानि समादेयानि सर्वतः
text: Wives, gems, learning, virtue, purity, wise saying and the various arts may be obtained from all sources.
iast: striyo ratnānyatho vidyā dharmaḥ śaucaṃ subhāṣitam vividhāni ca śīlpāni samādeyāni sarvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: abrāhmaṇādadhyāyanamāpatkāle vidhīyate anuvrajyā ca śuśrūṣā yāvadadhyāyanaṃ guroḥ
meaning: अब्राह्मणादध्यायनमापत्काले विधीयते अनुव्रज्या च शुश्रूषा यावदध्यायनं गुरोः
text: In abnormal times of difficulty learning from a non-Brāhmaṇa has been enjoined, as also the serving of such a teacher, in the shape of following him, during the course of study.
iast: abrāhmaṇādadhyāyanamāpatkāle vidhīyate anuvrajyā ca śuśrūṣā yāvadadhyāyanaṃ guroḥ

sanskrit_iast: nābrāhmaṇe gurau śiṣyo vāsamātyantikaṃ vaset brāhmaṇe vā 'nanūcāne kāṅkṣan gatimanuttamām
meaning: नाब्राह्मणे गुरौ शिष्यो वासमात्यन्तिकं वसेत् ब्राह्मणे वा 'ननूचाने काङ्क्षन् गतिमनुत्तमाम्
text: A pupil, desiring an unsurpassable state, shall not be in life-long residence with a non-Brāhhaṇa teacher; or with a Brāhmaṇa-teacher who is not an expounder.
iast: nābrāhmaṇe gurau śiṣyo vāsamātyantikaṃ vaset brāhmaṇe vā 'nanūcāne kāṅkṣan gatimanuttamām

sanskrit_iast: yadi tvātyantikaṃ vāsaṃ rocayeta guroḥ kule yuktaḥ paricaredenamā śarīravimokṣaṇāt
meaning: यदि त्वात्यन्तिकं वासं रोचयेत गुरोः कुले युक्तः परिचरेदेनमा शरीरविमोक्षणात्
text: If one likes to live in life-long residence in the teacher’s house, he should, intently serve him till he becomes freed from his body.
iast: yadi tvātyantikaṃ vāsaṃ rocayeta guroḥ kule yuktaḥ paricaredenamā śarīravimokṣaṇāt

sanskrit_iast: ā samāpteḥ śarīrasya yastu śuśrūṣate gurum sa gacchatyañjasā vipro brahmaṇaḥ sadma śāśvatam
meaning: आ समाप्तेः शरीरस्य यस्तु शुश्रूषते गुरुम् स गच्छत्यञ्जसा विप्रो ब्रह्मणः सद्म शाश्वतम्
text: The Brāhmaṇa who, till the dissolution of his body, serves his teacher, goes forthwith to the eternal abode of brahman.
iast: ā samāpteḥ śarīrasya yastu śuśrūṣate gurum sa gacchatyañjasā vipro brahmaṇaḥ sadma śāśvatam

sanskrit_iast: na pūrvaṃ gurave kiṃ cidupakurvīta dharmavit snāsyaṃstu guruṇā 'jñaptaḥ śaktyā guruarthamāharet
meaning: न पूर्वं गुरवे किं चिदुपकुर्वीत धर्मवित् स्नास्यंस्तु गुरुणा 'ज्ञप्तः शक्त्या गुरुअर्थमाहरेत्
text: At first the pupil, knowing his duties, should not give anything to his teacher; but when going to take the final bath, he should, on being ordered by the teacher, present something for him, to the best of his capacity;
iast: na pūrvaṃ gurave kiṃ cidupakurvīta dharmavit snāsyaṃstu guruṇā 'jñaptaḥ śaktyā guruarthamāharet

sanskrit_iast: kṣetraṃ hiraṇyaṃ gāmaśvaṃ chatraupānahamāsanam chatropānahamantataḥ dhānyaṃ śākaṃ ca vāsāṃsi gurave prītimāvahet dhānyaṃ vāsāṃsi śākaṃ vā gurave prītimāharan
meaning: क्षेत्रं हिरण्यं गामश्वं छत्रौपानहमासनम् छत्रोपानहमन्ततः धान्यं शाकं च वासांसि गुरवे प्रीतिमावहेत् धान्यं वासांसि शाकं वा गुरवे प्रीतिमाहरन्
text: Joyfully bringing to the teacher, a field, or gold, or a cow, or a horse, or at least an umbrella and a fair of shoes, grain, vegetables or clothes.
iast: kṣetraṃ hiraṇyaṃ gāmaśvaṃ chatraupānahamāsanam chatropānahamantataḥ dhānyaṃ śākaṃ ca vāsāṃsi gurave prītimāvahet dhānyaṃ vāsāṃsi śākaṃ vā gurave prītimāharan

sanskrit_iast: ācārye tu khalu prete guruputre guṇānvite gurudāre sapiṇḍe vā guruvadvṛttimācaret
meaning: आचार्ये तु खलु प्रेते गुरुपुत्रे गुणान्विते गुरुदारे सपिण्डे वा गुरुवद्वृत्तिमाचरेत्
text: The Preceptor having died, he should serve, in the same manner as the Preceptor, the qualified son of the Preceptor, or the Preceptor’s wife, or his Sapiṇḍa.
iast: ācārye tu khalu prete guruputre guṇānvite gurudāre sapiṇḍe vā guruvadvṛttimācaret

sanskrit_iast: eteṣvavidyamāneṣu sthānāsanavihāravān prayuñjāno 'gniśuśrūṣāṃ sādhayeddehamātmanaḥ
meaning: एतेष्वविद्यमानेषु स्थानासनविहारवान् प्रयुञ्जानो 'ग्निशुश्रूषां साधयेद्देहमात्मनः
text: When all these are non-existent, he should perfect his body, while he carries on the tending of the Fire, with only such diversions as standing and sitting.
iast: eteṣvavidyamāneṣu sthānāsanavihāravān prayuñjāno 'gniśuśrūṣāṃ sādhayeddehamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ carati yo vipro brahmacaryamaviplutaḥ sa gacchatyuttamasthānaṃ na caiha jāyate punaḥ
meaning: एवं चरति यो विप्रो ब्रह्मचर्यमविप्लुतः स गच्छत्युत्तमस्थानं न चैह जायते पुनः
text: He who, having eaten at a śrāddha, gives the leavings to a śūdra,—this foolish man falls headlong into the Kālasūtra hell.
iast: evaṃ carati yo vipro brahmacaryamaviplutaḥ sa gacchatyuttamasthānaṃ na caiha jāyate punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṭtriṃśadābdikaṃ caryaṃ gurau traivedikaṃ vratam tadardhikaṃ pādikaṃ vā grahaṇāntikameva vā
meaning: षट्त्रिंशदाब्दिकं चर्यं गुरौ त्रैवेदिकं व्रतम् तदर्धिकं पादिकं वा ग्रहणान्तिकमेव वा
text: Duties relating to the Three Vedas should be observed under the Preceptor for thirty-six years, or for half that period, or for a quarter, or precisely till they have been got up.
iast: ṣaṭtriṃśadābdikaṃ caryaṃ gurau traivedikaṃ vratam tadardhikaṃ pādikaṃ vā grahaṇāntikameva vā

sanskrit_iast: vedānadhītya vedau vā vedaṃ vā 'pi yathākramam aviplutabrahmacaryo gṛhasthāśramamāvaset
meaning: वेदानधीत्य वेदौ वा वेदं वा 'पि यथाक्रमम् अविप्लुतब्रह्मचर्यो गृहस्थाश्रममावसेत्
text: Having learnt, in due course, three Vedas, or two Vedas, or one Veda, he should enter upon the state of the householder, having never deviated from the vows of studentship.
iast: vedānadhītya vedau vā vedaṃ vā 'pi yathākramam aviplutabrahmacaryo gṛhasthāśramamāvaset

sanskrit_iast: taṃ pratītaṃ svadharmeṇa brahmadāyaharaṃ pituḥ sragviṇaṃ talpa āsīnamarhayet prathamaṃ gavā
meaning: तं प्रतीतं स्वधर्मेण ब्रह्मदायहरं पितुः स्रग्विणं तल्प आसीनमर्हयेत् प्रथमं गवा
text: When, by the due observance of his duties, he has acquired the Veda and his heritage from his father, and is so inclined,—his father shall first honour him, adorned with garlands and seated upon a couch, with the “Cow.”
iast: taṃ pratītaṃ svadharmeṇa brahmadāyaharaṃ pituḥ sragviṇaṃ talpa āsīnamarhayet prathamaṃ gavā

sanskrit_iast: guruṇānumataḥ snātvā samāvṛtto yathāvidhi udvaheta dvijo bhāryāṃ savarṇāṃ lakṣaṇānvitām
meaning: गुरुणानुमतः स्नात्वा समावृत्तो यथाविधि उद्वहेत द्विजो भार्यां सवर्णां लक्षणान्विताम्
text: The twice-born person, having, on being permitted by the Preceptor, “bathed” and “returned” according to rule, shall take a wife of the same caste as himself, who is endowed with signs.
iast: guruṇānumataḥ snātvā samāvṛtto yathāvidhi udvaheta dvijo bhāryāṃ savarṇāṃ lakṣaṇānvitām

sanskrit_iast: asapiṇḍā ca yā māturasagotrā ca yā pituḥ sā praśastā dvijātīnāṃ dārakarmaṇi maithune 'maithinī
meaning: असपिण्डा च या मातुरसगोत्रा च या पितुः सा प्रशस्ता द्विजातीनां दारकर्मणि मैथुने 'मैथिनी
text: She who is not a “sapiṇḍa” of one’s mother, not of the same “Gotra” as his Father, and who is not born of (unlawful) intercourse—has been recommended for marriage.
iast: asapiṇḍā ca yā māturasagotrā ca yā pituḥ sā praśastā dvijātīnāṃ dārakarmaṇi maithune 'maithinī

sanskrit_iast: mahāntyapi samṛddhāni go 'jāvidhanadhānyataḥ strīsaṃbandhe daśaitāni kulāni parivarjayet
meaning: महान्त्यपि समृद्धानि गो 'जाविधनधान्यतः स्त्रीसंबन्धे दशैतानि कुलानि परिवर्जयेत्
text: In female connection one should avoid these (following) ten families,—even though they be great and rich in the possession of cattle, goat and sheep and grain.
iast: mahāntyapi samṛddhāni go 'jāvidhanadhānyataḥ strīsaṃbandhe daśaitāni kulāni parivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: hīnakriyaṃ niṣpuruṣaṃ niśchando romaśārśasam kṣayāmayāvy 'pasmāriśvitrikuṣṭhikulāni ca
meaning: हीनक्रियं निष्पुरुषं निश्छन्दो रोमशार्शसम् क्षयामयाव्य् 'पस्मारिश्वित्रिकुष्ठिकुलानि च
text: Such families as—(1) that in which the sacred rites have been abandoned, (2) which is male-less, (3) which is devoid of the Veda, (4) members of which are woolly and subject to (5) piles, (6) phthisis, (7) dyspepsia, (8) epilepsy, (9) leucoderma, and (10) leprosy.
iast: hīnakriyaṃ niṣpuruṣaṃ niśchando romaśārśasam kṣayāmayāvy 'pasmāriśvitrikuṣṭhikulāni ca

sanskrit_iast: naudvahet kapilāṃ kanyāṃ nādhikāṅgīṃ na rogiṇīm nālomikāṃ nātilomāṃ na vācāṭāṃ na piṅgalām vācālāṃ
meaning: नौद्वहेत् कपिलां कन्यां नाधिकाङ्गीं न रोगिणीम् नालोमिकां नातिलोमां न वाचाटां न पिङ्गलाम् वाचालां
text: He should hot marry a maiden with tawny hair, nor one with superfluous limbs, nor one who has disease, nor one who has either no hair or too much hair, nor one who is garrulous, nor one with reddish eyes.
iast: naudvahet kapilāṃ kanyāṃ nādhikāṅgīṃ na rogiṇīm nālomikāṃ nātilomāṃ na vācāṭāṃ na piṅgalām vācālāṃ

sanskrit_iast: naṛkṣavṛkṣanadīnāmnīṃ nāntyaparvatanāmikām na pakṣiahipreṣyanāmnīṃ na ca bhīṣananāmikām
meaning: नऋक्षवृक्षनदीनाम्नीं नान्त्यपर्वतनामिकाम् न पक्षिअहिप्रेष्यनाम्नीं न च भीषननामिकाम्
text: Nor one bearing the name of an asterism, or a tree, or a river; nob one having her hame after a low caste or a mountain; nor one named after a bird, a serpent or a slave; nor one with a hame inspiring terror.
iast: naṛkṣavṛkṣanadīnāmnīṃ nāntyaparvatanāmikām na pakṣiahipreṣyanāmnīṃ na ca bhīṣananāmikām

sanskrit_iast: avyaṅgāṅgīṃ saumyanāmnīṃ haṃsavāraṇagāminīm tanulomakeśadaśanāṃ mṛdv 'ṅgīmudvahet striyam
meaning: अव्यङ्गाङ्गीं सौम्यनाम्नीं हंसवारणगामिनीम् तनुलोमकेशदशनां मृद्व् 'ङ्गीमुद्वहेत् स्त्रियम्
text: One should marry a female with a faultless body, bearing an agreeable name, having her gait like that of the swan or the elephant, having fine hair on the body and the head, and fine teeth, and with tender limbs.
iast: avyaṅgāṅgīṃ saumyanāmnīṃ haṃsavāraṇagāminīm tanulomakeśadaśanāṃ mṛdv 'ṅgīmudvahet striyam

sanskrit_iast: yasyāstu na bhavedbhrātā na vijñāyeta vā pitā vai(vā pitā naupayaccheta tāṃ prājñaḥ putrikā 'dharmaśaṅkayā
meaning: यस्यास्तु न भवेद्भ्राता न विज्ञायेत वा पिता वै(वा पिता नौपयच्छेत तां प्राज्ञः पुत्रिका 'धर्मशङ्कया
text: The wise man shall not mabry one who has no brother, or whose father is not known; for fear of her having the character of the “appointed daughter.”
iast: yasyāstu na bhavedbhrātā na vijñāyeta vā pitā vai(vā pitā naupayaccheta tāṃ prājñaḥ putrikā 'dharmaśaṅkayā

sanskrit_iast: savarṇā 'gre dvijātīnāṃ praśastā dārakarmaṇi kāmatastu pravṛttānāmimāḥ syuḥ kramaśo 'varāḥ
meaning: सवर्णा 'ग्रे द्विजातीनां प्रशस्ता दारकर्मणि कामतस्तु प्रवृत्तानामिमाः स्युः क्रमशो 'वराः
text: For ‘twice-born men’ a girl of equal caste has been recommended for the first marriage-sacrament. For those, however, who take to it through mere desire, these (following) should be regarded as preferable in due order.
iast: savarṇā 'gre dvijātīnāṃ praśastā dārakarmaṇi kāmatastu pravṛttānāmimāḥ syuḥ kramaśo 'varāḥ

sanskrit_iast: śūdraiva bhāryā śūdrasya sā ca svā ca viśaḥ smṛte te ca svā caiva rājñaśca tāśca svā cāgrajanmanaḥ
meaning: शूद्रैव भार्या शूद्रस्य सा च स्वा च विशः स्मृते ते च स्वा चैव राज्ञश्च ताश्च स्वा चाग्रजन्मनः
text: For the Śūdra, the Śūdra girl. alone has been ordained to be the wife; for the Vaiśya, she as also the girl of his own caste; for the Kṣatriya, those two as also the girl of his own caste; and for the Brāhmaṇa those three as also the girl of his own caste
iast: śūdraiva bhāryā śūdrasya sā ca svā ca viśaḥ smṛte te ca svā caiva rājñaśca tāśca svā cāgrajanmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na brāhmaṇakṣatriyayorāpadyapi hi tiṣṭhatoḥ kasmiṃścidapi vṛttānte śūdrā bhāryaupadiśyate
meaning: न ब्राह्मणक्षत्रिययोरापद्यपि हि तिष्ठतोः कस्मिंश्चिदपि वृत्तान्ते शूद्रा भार्यौपदिश्यते
text: Under no circumstance whatsoever has a Śūdra wife been ordained for the Brāhmaṇa and the Kṣatriya,—even though these be placed in peril.
iast: na brāhmaṇakṣatriyayorāpadyapi hi tiṣṭhatoḥ kasmiṃścidapi vṛttānte śūdrā bhāryaupadiśyate

sanskrit_iast: hīnajātistriyaṃ mohādudvahanto dvijātayaḥ kulānyeva nayantyāśu sasantānāni śūdratām
meaning: हीनजातिस्त्रियं मोहादुद्वहन्तो द्विजातयः कुलान्येव नयन्त्याशु ससन्तानानि शूद्रताम्
text: Twice-born men, marrying, through infatuation, a girl of the low caste, quickly reduce their families, along with their offspring, to the position of the Śūdra.
iast: hīnajātistriyaṃ mohādudvahanto dvijātayaḥ kulānyeva nayantyāśu sasantānāni śūdratām

sanskrit_iast: śūdrāvedī patatyatrerutathyatanayasya ca śaunakasya sutautpattyā tadapatyatayā bhṛgoḥ
meaning: शूद्रावेदी पतत्यत्रेरुतथ्यतनयस्य च शौनकस्य सुतौत्पत्त्या तदपत्यतया भृगोः
text: One who marries a Śūdra girl becomes an outcastk,—according to atri and to the son of Utathya; according to Śaunaka, by the birth of a son; and according to Bhṛgu, by having children from her (alone).
iast: śūdrāvedī patatyatrerutathyatanayasya ca śaunakasya sutautpattyā tadapatyatayā bhṛgoḥ

sanskrit_iast: śūdrāṃ śayanamāropya brāhmaṇo yātyadhogatim janayitvā sutaṃ tasyāṃ brāhmaṇyādeva hīyate
meaning: शूद्रां शयनमारोप्य ब्राह्मणो यात्यधोगतिम् जनयित्वा सुतं तस्यां ब्राह्मण्यादेव हीयते
text: Having placed a Śūdrā woman on his bed, the Brāhmaṇa goes to perdition; and having begotten a son by her, he falls from Brāhmaṇahood itself.
iast: śūdrāṃ śayanamāropya brāhmaṇo yātyadhogatim janayitvā sutaṃ tasyāṃ brāhmaṇyādeva hīyate

sanskrit_iast: daivapitryātitheyāni tatpradhānāni yasya tu nāśnanti pitṛdevāstanna ca svargaṃ sa gacchati
meaning: दैवपित्र्यातिथेयानि तत्प्रधानानि यस्य तु नाश्नन्ति पितृदेवास्तन्न च स्वर्गं स गच्छति
text: If the rites performed by one in honour of deities, Pitṛs and Guests are dominated by her (his Śūdra wife), then the Pitṛs and the Gods do not eat of them; and the man does not go to heaven.
iast: daivapitryātitheyāni tatpradhānāni yasya tu nāśnanti pitṛdevāstanna ca svargaṃ sa gacchati

sanskrit_iast: vṛṣalīphenapītasya niḥśvāsaupahatasya ca tasyāṃ caiva prasūtasya niṣkṛtirna vidhīyate
meaning: वृषलीफेनपीतस्य निःश्वासौपहतस्य च तस्यां चैव प्रसूतस्य निष्कृतिर्न विधीयते
text: There is ho expiation for him who has drunk the moisture of the mouth of a Śūdra woman, who has been tainted by her breath, and who has begotten children on her.
iast: vṛṣalīphenapītasya niḥśvāsaupahatasya ca tasyāṃ caiva prasūtasya niṣkṛtirna vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: caturṇāmapi varṇānaṃ pretya caiha hitāhitān aṣṭāvimān samāsena strīvivāhānnibodhata
meaning: चतुर्णामपि वर्णानं प्रेत्य चैह हिताहितान् अष्टाविमान् समासेन स्त्रीविवाहान्निबोधत
text: Understand briefly these (following) eight forms of marriage of girls, among the four castes,—which are beneficial and not-beneficial here (in this life) and also after death.
iast: caturṇāmapi varṇānaṃ pretya caiha hitāhitān aṣṭāvimān samāsena strīvivāhānnibodhata

sanskrit_iast: brāhmo daivastathaivārṣaḥ prājāpatyastathā 'suraḥ gāndharvo rākṣasaścaiva paiśācaścāṣṭamo 'dhamaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मो दैवस्तथैवार्षः प्राजापत्यस्तथा 'सुरः गान्धर्वो राक्षसश्चैव पैशाचश्चाष्टमो 'धमः
text: (1) The Brāhma, (2) the Daiva, (3) the Ārṣa, (1) the Prājāpatya, (5) the Āsura, (6) the Gāndharva, (7) the Rākṣasa and (8) the Paiśāca, which is the eighth and the lowest.
iast: brāhmo daivastathaivārṣaḥ prājāpatyastathā 'suraḥ gāndharvo rākṣasaścaiva paiśācaścāṣṭamo 'dhamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo yasya dharmyo varṇasya guṇadoṣau ca yasya yau tadvaḥ sarvaṃ pravakṣyāmi prasave ca guṇāguṇān
meaning: यो यस्य धर्म्यो वर्णस्य गुणदोषौ च यस्य यौ तद्वः सर्वं प्रवक्ष्यामि प्रसवे च गुणागुणान्
text: Which (of these) is lawful for which caste, what are the good and bad points of each, the good and bad effects of each upon the offspring,—all this I shall explain to you.
iast: yo yasya dharmyo varṇasya guṇadoṣau ca yasya yau tadvaḥ sarvaṃ pravakṣyāmi prasave ca guṇāguṇān

sanskrit_iast: ṣaḍānupūrvyā viprasya kṣatrasya caturo 'varān viśśūdrayostu tāneva vidyāddharmyānarākṣasān dharmyānna rākṣasān
meaning: षडानुपूर्व्या विप्रस्य क्षत्रस्य चतुरो 'वरान् विश्शूद्रयोस्तु तानेव विद्याद्धर्म्यानराक्षसान् धर्म्यान्न राक्षसान्
text: One should know the first six in the order stated as lawful for the Brāhmaṇa, the last four for the Kṣatriya and those same, excepting the “Rākṣasa,” for the Vaiśya and the Śūdra.
iast: ṣaḍānupūrvyā viprasya kṣatrasya caturo 'varān viśśūdrayostu tāneva vidyāddharmyānarākṣasān dharmyānna rākṣasān

sanskrit_iast: caturo brāhmaṇasyādyān praśastān kavayo viduḥ rākṣasaṃ kṣatriyasyaikamāsuraṃ vaiśyaśūdrayoḥ
meaning: चतुरो ब्राह्मणस्याद्यान् प्रशस्तान् कवयो विदुः राक्षसं क्षत्रियस्यैकमासुरं वैश्यशूद्रयोः
text: The wise ones have regarded the first four as commended for the Brāhmaṇa, the Rākṣasa alone for the Kṣatriya and the Āsura for the Vaiśva and the Śūdra.
iast: caturo brāhmaṇasyādyān praśastān kavayo viduḥ rākṣasaṃ kṣatriyasyaikamāsuraṃ vaiśyaśūdrayoḥ

sanskrit_iast: pañcānāṃ tu trayo dharmyā dvāvadharmyau smṛtāviha paiśācaścāsuraścaiva na kartavyau kadā cana
meaning: पञ्चानां तु त्रयो धर्म्या द्वावधर्म्यौ स्मृताविह पैशाचश्चासुरश्चैव न कर्तव्यौ कदा चन
text: Of the five, three have been declared to be lawful and two unlawful, in this treatise; the Paiśāca and the Āsura forms should never be adopted.’
iast: pañcānāṃ tu trayo dharmyā dvāvadharmyau smṛtāviha paiśācaścāsuraścaiva na kartavyau kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: pṛthak pṛthag vā miśrau vā vivāhau pūrvacoditau gāndharvo rākṣasaścaiva dharmyau kṣatrasya tau smṛtau
meaning: पृथक् पृथग् वा मिश्रौ वा विवाहौ पूर्वचोदितौ गान्धर्वो राक्षसश्चैव धर्म्यौ क्षत्रस्य तौ स्मृतौ
text: The two forms of marriage mentioned before—i.e., the Gāndharva and the Rākṣasa—have been declared, whether separately or mixed, to be lawful for the Kṣatriya.
iast: pṛthak pṛthag vā miśrau vā vivāhau pūrvacoditau gāndharvo rākṣasaścaiva dharmyau kṣatrasya tau smṛtau

sanskrit_iast: ācchādya cārcayitvā ca śrutaśīlavate svayam āhūya dānaṃ kanyāyā brāhmo dharmaḥ prakīrtitaḥ
meaning: आच्छाद्य चार्चयित्वा च श्रुतशीलवते स्वयम् आहूय दानं कन्याया ब्राह्मो धर्मः प्रकीर्तितः
text: When one himself invites a man endowed with learning and character and gives to him his daughter, after having dressed and worshipped (them),—this is called the “Brāhma” form.
iast: ācchādya cārcayitvā ca śrutaśīlavate svayam āhūya dānaṃ kanyāyā brāhmo dharmaḥ prakīrtitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yajñe tu vitate samyag ṛtvije karma kurvate alaṅkṛtya sutādānaṃ daivaṃ dharmaṃ pracakṣate
meaning: यज्ञे तु वितते सम्यग् ऋत्विजे कर्म कुर्वते अलङ्कृत्य सुतादानं दैवं धर्मं प्रचक्षते
text: While a sacrifice is being performed, if one gives away his daughter, after having decked her, to the priest who is officiating at it,—this they call the “Daiva” form.
iast: yajñe tu vitate samyag ṛtvije karma kurvate alaṅkṛtya sutādānaṃ daivaṃ dharmaṃ pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: ekaṃ gomithunaṃ dve vā varādādāya dharmataḥ kanyāpradānaṃ vidhivadārṣo dharmaḥ sa ucyate
meaning: एकं गोमिथुनं द्वे वा वरादादाय धर्मतः कन्याप्रदानं विधिवदार्षो धर्मः स उच्यते
text: ‘When the maiden is given away in due accordance with rule, after taking, in odedience to law, from the bridegroom, one or two pairs of cow and bull,—this is called the “Ārṣa” form.’
iast: ekaṃ gomithunaṃ dve vā varādādāya dharmataḥ kanyāpradānaṃ vidhivadārṣo dharmaḥ sa ucyate

sanskrit_iast: sahaubhau caratāṃ dharmamiti vācā 'nubhāṣya ca kanyāpradānamabhyarcya prājāpatyo vidhiḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: सहौभौ चरतां धर्ममिति वाचा 'नुभाष्य च कन्याप्रदानमभ्यर्च्य प्राजापत्यो विधिः स्मृतः
text: When the Father, having decked them, gives away the daughter with the words, “may you both together perform your duty,” making them also repeat them,—this is called the “Prājāpatya” form.
iast: sahaubhau caratāṃ dharmamiti vācā 'nubhāṣya ca kanyāpradānamabhyarcya prājāpatyo vidhiḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jñātibhyo draviṇaṃ dattvā kanyāyai caiva śaktitaḥ kanyāpradānaṃ svācchandyādāsuro dharma ucyate
meaning: ज्ञातिभ्यो द्रविणं दत्त्वा कन्यायै चैव शक्तितः कन्याप्रदानं स्वाच्छन्द्यादासुरो धर्म उच्यते
text: When one carries away the maiden, after having given, of his own will, as much wealth as the (he?) can to the kinsmen, as well as to the bride herself, this is called the “Āsura” form.
iast: jñātibhyo draviṇaṃ dattvā kanyāyai caiva śaktitaḥ kanyāpradānaṃ svācchandyādāsuro dharma ucyate

sanskrit_iast: icchayā 'nyonyasaṃyogaḥ kanyāyāśca varasya ca gāndharvaḥ sa tu vijñeyo maithunyaḥ kāmasaṃbhavaḥ
meaning: इच्छया 'न्योन्यसंयोगः कन्यायाश्च वरस्य च गान्धर्वः स तु विज्ञेयो मैथुन्यः कामसंभवः
text: The mutual union of the bride and bridegroom, through love is to be known as the “Gāndharva” form; it has sexual intercourse for its end and it has its source in lust.
iast: icchayā 'nyonyasaṃyogaḥ kanyāyāśca varasya ca gāndharvaḥ sa tu vijñeyo maithunyaḥ kāmasaṃbhavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: hatvā chittvā ca bhittvā ca krośantīṃ rudantīṃ gṛhāt prasahya kanyāharaṇaṃ rākṣaso vidhirucyate
meaning: हत्वा छित्त्वा च भित्त्वा च क्रोशन्तीं रुदन्तीं गृहात् प्रसह्य कन्याहरणं राक्षसो विधिरुच्यते
text: The forcible abduction of the maiden from her home, while she is crying out and weeping, after having beaten and wounded and pierced,—is called the “Rākṣasa” form.
iast: hatvā chittvā ca bhittvā ca krośantīṃ rudantīṃ gṛhāt prasahya kanyāharaṇaṃ rākṣaso vidhirucyate

sanskrit_iast: suptāṃ mattāṃ pramattāṃ vā raho yatraupagacchati sa pāpiṣṭho vivāhānāṃ paiśācaścāṣṭamo 'dhamaḥ paiśācaḥ prathito 'dhamaḥ
meaning: सुप्तां मत्तां प्रमत्तां वा रहो यत्रौपगच्छति स पापिष्ठो विवाहानां पैशाचश्चाष्टमो 'धमः पैशाचः प्रथितो 'धमः
text: When the man approaches the girl by stealth, while she is asleep, or intoxicated or unconscious,—it is the “Paiśāca” form, the wickedest and the basest of marriages.
iast: suptāṃ mattāṃ pramattāṃ vā raho yatraupagacchati sa pāpiṣṭho vivāhānāṃ paiśācaścāṣṭamo 'dhamaḥ paiśācaḥ prathito 'dhamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: adbhireva dvijāgryāṇāṃ kanyādānaṃ viśiṣyate itareṣāṃ tu varṇānāmitaretarakāmyayā
meaning: अद्भिरेव द्विजाग्र्याणां कन्यादानं विशिष्यते इतरेषां तु वर्णानामितरेतरकाम्यया
text: ‘For the chief of twice-born men the giving away of one’s daughter with water alone is commended; but for the other castes it is with mutual desire.’
iast: adbhireva dvijāgryāṇāṃ kanyādānaṃ viśiṣyate itareṣāṃ tu varṇānāmitaretarakāmyayā

sanskrit_iast: yo yasyaiṣāṃ vivāhānāṃ manunā kīrtito guṇaḥ sarvaṃ śṛṇuta taṃ viprāḥ sarvaṃ kīrtayato mama samyak kīrtayato
meaning: यो यस्यैषां विवाहानां मनुना कीर्तितो गुणः सर्वं शृणुत तं विप्राः सर्वं कीर्तयतो मम सम्यक् कीर्तयतो
text: The quality that has been ascribed by Manu to each of these forms of marriage—listen to all that, O Brāhmaṇas, from me, as I proceed to describe them correctly.
iast: yo yasyaiṣāṃ vivāhānāṃ manunā kīrtito guṇaḥ sarvaṃ śṛṇuta taṃ viprāḥ sarvaṃ kīrtayato mama samyak kīrtayato

sanskrit_iast: daśa pūrvān parān vaṃśyānātmānaṃ caikaviṃśakam brāhmīputraḥ sukṛtakṛtmocayatyenasaḥ pitr̥̄n
meaning: दश पूर्वान् परान् वंश्यानात्मानं चैकविंशकम् ब्राह्मीपुत्रः सुकृतकृत्मोचयत्येनसः पित्र्̥̄न्
text: The son born of the wife married by the Brāhma form is a performer of righteous acts, absolves from sins ten Pitṛs on the ascending side and ten on the descending side of his family, as also himself as the twenty-first.
iast: daśa pūrvān parān vaṃśyānātmānaṃ caikaviṃśakam brāhmīputraḥ sukṛtakṛtmocayatyenasaḥ pitr̥̄n

sanskrit_iast: daivauḍhājaḥ sutaścaiva sapta sapta parāvarān ārṣauḍhājaḥ sutastrīṃstrīn ṣaṭ ṣaṭ kāyauḍhajaḥ sutaḥ
meaning: दैवौढाजः सुतश्चैव सप्त सप्त परावरान् आर्षौढाजः सुतस्त्रींस्त्रीन् षट् षट् कायौढजः सुतः
text: The son born of the wife married by the Daiva form (absolves from sin) seven ancestors and seven des cendants; the son born of the wife married by the Ārṣa form three of each; and the son born of the wife married by the Prājāpatya form six of each.
iast: daivauḍhājaḥ sutaścaiva sapta sapta parāvarān ārṣauḍhājaḥ sutastrīṃstrīn ṣaṭ ṣaṭ kāyauḍhajaḥ sutaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmādiṣu vivāheṣu caturṣvevānupūrvaśaḥ brahmavarcasvinaḥ putrā jāyante śiṣṭasammatāḥ brahmavarcasinaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मादिषु विवाहेषु चतुर्ष्वेवानुपूर्वशः ब्रह्मवर्चस्विनः पुत्रा जायन्ते शिष्टसम्मताः ब्रह्मवर्चसिनः
text: Only from the four marriages mentioned successively, beginning with the Brāhma, are born sons endowed with Brahmic glory and respected by cultured persons.
iast: brāhmādiṣu vivāheṣu caturṣvevānupūrvaśaḥ brahmavarcasvinaḥ putrā jāyante śiṣṭasammatāḥ brahmavarcasinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: rūpasattvaguṇaupetā dhanavanto yaśasvinaḥ paryāptabhogā dharmiṣṭhā jīvanti ca śataṃ samāḥ
meaning: रूपसत्त्वगुणौपेता धनवन्तो यशस्विनः पर्याप्तभोगा धर्मिष्ठा जीवन्ति च शतं समाः
text: Endowed with beauty and the quality of goodness, possessing wealth, and fame, with full enjoyment and righteous, they live for a hundred years.
iast: rūpasattvaguṇaupetā dhanavanto yaśasvinaḥ paryāptabhogā dharmiṣṭhā jīvanti ca śataṃ samāḥ

sanskrit_iast: itareṣu tu śiṣṭeṣu nṛśaṃsā 'nṛtavādinaḥ jāyante durvivāheṣu brahmadharmadviṣaḥ sutāḥ
meaning: इतरेषु तु शिष्टेषु नृशंसा 'नृतवादिनः जायन्ते दुर्विवाहेषु ब्रह्मधर्मद्विषः सुताः
text: From the other remaining inferior marriages are born sons, addicted to saying harsh and untrue words, and despisers of the Vedic Dharma.
iast: itareṣu tu śiṣṭeṣu nṛśaṃsā 'nṛtavādinaḥ jāyante durvivāheṣu brahmadharmadviṣaḥ sutāḥ

sanskrit_iast: aninditaiḥ strīvivāhairanindyā bhavati prajā ninditairninditā nr̥̄ṇāṃ tasmānnindyān vivarjayet
meaning: अनिन्दितैः स्त्रीविवाहैरनिन्द्या भवति प्रजा निन्दितैर्निन्दिता न्र्̥̄णां तस्मान्निन्द्यान् विवर्जयेत्
text: From unblamable marriages unblamable offspring is born to men; and from blameworthy marriages blameworthy child. One should therefore avoid the blamable marriages.
iast: aninditaiḥ strīvivāhairanindyā bhavati prajā ninditairninditā nr̥̄ṇāṃ tasmānnindyān vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: pāṇigrahaṇasaṃskāraḥ savarṇāsūpadiśyate asavarṇāsvayaṃ jñeyo vidhirudvāhakarmaṇi
meaning: पाणिग्रहणसंस्कारः सवर्णासूपदिश्यते असवर्णास्वयं ज्ञेयो विधिरुद्वाहकर्मणि
text: In the case of girls of the same caste (as the bridegroom) the sacramental rite of “taking the hand” has been prescribed; and in that of the marriage of girls of different castes, this (following) should be known as the right procedure.
iast: pāṇigrahaṇasaṃskāraḥ savarṇāsūpadiśyate asavarṇāsvayaṃ jñeyo vidhirudvāhakarmaṇi

sanskrit_iast: śaraḥ kṣatriyayā grāhyaḥ pratodo vaiśyakanyayā vasanasya daśā grāhyā śūdrayautkṛṣṭavedane
meaning: शरः क्षत्रियया ग्राह्यः प्रतोदो वैश्यकन्यया वसनस्य दशा ग्राह्या शूद्रयौत्कृष्टवेदने
text: When being married to a man of higher caste, the Kṣatriya girl should take hold of the arrow, the Vaiśya girl of the goad and the Śūdra girl of the hem of the garment.
iast: śaraḥ kṣatriyayā grāhyaḥ pratodo vaiśyakanyayā vasanasya daśā grāhyā śūdrayautkṛṣṭavedane

sanskrit_iast: ṛtukālābhigāmī syāt svadāranirataḥ sadā parvavarjaṃ vrajeccaināṃ tadvrato ratikāmyayā
meaning: ऋतुकालाभिगामी स्यात् स्वदारनिरतः सदा पर्ववर्जं व्रजेच्चैनां तद्व्रतो रतिकाम्यया
text: One should observe the rule of approaching (one’s wife) during the period of her season,—ever attached to his own wife. In consideration of her he may approach her with a desire for sexual intercourse, except on the sacred days.
iast: ṛtukālābhigāmī syāt svadāranirataḥ sadā parvavarjaṃ vrajeccaināṃ tadvrato ratikāmyayā

sanskrit_iast: ṛtuḥ svābhāvikaḥ strīṇāṃ rātrayaḥ ṣoḍaśa smṛtāḥ caturbhiritaraiḥ sārdhamahobhiḥ sadvigarhitaiḥ
meaning: ऋतुः स्वाभाविकः स्त्रीणां रात्रयः षोडश स्मृताः चतुर्भिरितरैः सार्धमहोभिः सद्विगर्हितैः
text: Sixteen days, including the four days that are censured by good men, have been declared to be the normal “season” for women.
iast: ṛtuḥ svābhāvikaḥ strīṇāṃ rātrayaḥ ṣoḍaśa smṛtāḥ caturbhiritaraiḥ sārdhamahobhiḥ sadvigarhitaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: tāsāmādyāścatasrastu ninditaikādaśī ca yā trayodaśī ca śeṣāstu praśastā daśarātrayaḥ
meaning: तासामाद्याश्चतस्रस्तु निन्दितैकादशी च या त्रयोदशी च शेषास्तु प्रशस्ता दशरात्रयः
text: Of these the first four days have been deprecated, as also the eleventh and the thirteenth, the remaining ten days have been recommended.
iast: tāsāmādyāścatasrastu ninditaikādaśī ca yā trayodaśī ca śeṣāstu praśastā daśarātrayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yugmāsu putrā jāyante striyo 'yugmāsu rātriṣu tasmādyugmāsu putrārthī saṃviśedārtave striyam
meaning: युग्मासु पुत्रा जायन्ते स्त्रियो 'युग्मासु रात्रिषु तस्माद्युग्मासु पुत्रार्थी संविशेदार्तवे स्त्रियम्
text: On the even days male children are conceived, and female ones on the uneven days; therefore one who desires a son should have recourse to ones wife on the even days of her “season.”
iast: yugmāsu putrā jāyante striyo 'yugmāsu rātriṣu tasmādyugmāsu putrārthī saṃviśedārtave striyam

sanskrit_iast: pumān puṃso 'dhike śukre strī bhavatyadhike striyāḥ same 'pumān puṃstriyau vā kṣīṇe 'lpe ca viparyayaḥ
meaning: पुमान् पुंसो 'धिके शुक्रे स्त्री भवत्यधिके स्त्रियाः समे 'पुमान् पुंस्त्रियौ वा क्षीणे 'ल्पे च विपर्ययः
text: A male child is born when the man’s seed is in excess, and a female child when the woman’s (is in excess); when the two are equal, there is born either a non-male or a boy and a girl; when it is weak and small in quantity, there is failure.
iast: pumān puṃso 'dhike śukre strī bhavatyadhike striyāḥ same 'pumān puṃstriyau vā kṣīṇe 'lpe ca viparyayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nindyāsvaṣṭāsu cānyāsu striyo rātriṣu varjayan brahmacāryeva bhavati yatra tatrāśrame vasan
meaning: निन्द्यास्वष्टासु चान्यासु स्त्रियो रात्रिषु वर्जयन् ब्रह्मचार्येव भवति यत्र तत्राश्रमे वसन्
text: ‘By avoiding women on the forbidden days and also on the eight other days, one remains a “religious student” (observing the vow of continence), in whatever stage of life he may happen to be.”
iast: nindyāsvaṣṭāsu cānyāsu striyo rātriṣu varjayan brahmacāryeva bhavati yatra tatrāśrame vasan

sanskrit_iast: na kanyāyāḥ pitā vidvān gṛhṇīyātśulkamaṇuapi gṛhṇaṃśulkaṃ hi lobhena syānnaro 'patyavikrayī
meaning: न कन्यायाः पिता विद्वान् गृह्णीयात्शुल्कमणुअपि गृह्णंशुल्कं हि लोभेन स्यान्नरो 'पत्यविक्रयी
text: The girl’s father, if wise, should not accept even a small consideration; by accepting a consideration, through greed, the man becomes a child-seller.
iast: na kanyāyāḥ pitā vidvān gṛhṇīyātśulkamaṇuapi gṛhṇaṃśulkaṃ hi lobhena syānnaro 'patyavikrayī

sanskrit_iast: strīdhanāni tu ye mohādupajīvanti bāndhavāḥ nārī yānāni vastraṃ vā te pāpā yāntyadhogatim
meaning: स्त्रीधनानि तु ये मोहादुपजीवन्ति बान्धवाः नारी यानानि वस्त्रं वा ते पापा यान्त्यधोगतिम्
text: Those relations who, through folly, live upon the bride’s properties—even the bride’s conveyances and clothes—are sinners and fall into the lowest state.
iast: strīdhanāni tu ye mohādupajīvanti bāndhavāḥ nārī yānāni vastraṃ vā te pāpā yāntyadhogatim

sanskrit_iast: ārṣe gomithunaṃ śulkaṃ ke cidāhurmṛṣaiva tat alpo 'pyevaṃ mahān vā 'pi vikrayastāvadeva saḥ tāvāneva sa vikrayaḥ
meaning: आर्षे गोमिथुनं शुल्कं के चिदाहुर्मृषैव तत् अल्पो 'प्येवं महान् वा 'पि विक्रयस्तावदेव सः तावानेव स विक्रयः
text: Some people declare that the bovine pair are the “consideration” (to be accepted) in the Ārṣa form of marriage. This is not true; for small or large, the act becomes a ‘selling’ all the same.
iast: ārṣe gomithunaṃ śulkaṃ ke cidāhurmṛṣaiva tat alpo 'pyevaṃ mahān vā 'pi vikrayastāvadeva saḥ tāvāneva sa vikrayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāsāṃ nādadate śulkaṃ jñātayo na sa vikrayaḥ arhaṇaṃ tat kumārīṇāmānṛśaṃsyaṃ ca kevalam na kevalam
meaning: यासां नाददते शुल्कं ज्ञातयो न स विक्रयः अर्हणं तत् कुमारीणामानृशंस्यं च केवलम् न केवलम्
text: In the case of girls whose relations do not appropriate the bride’s gift, it is not “selling;” It is only a means of honouring the maidens and is entirely harmless.
iast: yāsāṃ nādadate śulkaṃ jñātayo na sa vikrayaḥ arhaṇaṃ tat kumārīṇāmānṛśaṃsyaṃ ca kevalam na kevalam

sanskrit_iast: pitṛbhirbhrātṛbhiścaitāḥ patibhirdevaraistathā pūjyā bhūṣayitavyāśca bahukalyāṇamīpsubhiḥ
meaning: पितृभिर्भ्रातृभिश्चैताः पतिभिर्देवरैस्तथा पूज्या भूषयितव्याश्च बहुकल्याणमीप्सुभिः
text: These shall be honoured and adorned by their fathers and brothers, husbands and brothers-in-law, who are desirous of their own welfare.
iast: pitṛbhirbhrātṛbhiścaitāḥ patibhirdevaraistathā pūjyā bhūṣayitavyāśca bahukalyāṇamīpsubhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yatra nāryastu pūjyante ramante tatra devatāḥ yatraitāstu na pūjyante sarvāstatrāphalāḥ kriyāḥ
meaning: यत्र नार्यस्तु पूज्यन्ते रमन्ते तत्र देवताः यत्रैतास्तु न पूज्यन्ते सर्वास्तत्राफलाः क्रियाः
text: Where women are honoured, there the gods rejoice; where, on the other hand, they are not honoured, there all rites are fruitless.
iast: yatra nāryastu pūjyante ramante tatra devatāḥ yatraitāstu na pūjyante sarvāstatrāphalāḥ kriyāḥ

sanskrit_iast: śocanti jāmayo yatra vinaśyatyāśu tat kulam na śocanti tu yatraitā vardhate taddhi sarvadā
meaning: शोचन्ति जामयो यत्र विनश्यत्याशु तत् कुलम् न शोचन्ति तु यत्रैता वर्धते तद्धि सर्वदा
text: Where the female relations live in grief, the family soon wholly perishes; but that family where they are not unhappy ever prospers.
iast: śocanti jāmayo yatra vinaśyatyāśu tat kulam na śocanti tu yatraitā vardhate taddhi sarvadā

sanskrit_iast: jāmayo yāni gehāni śapantyapratipūjitāḥ tāni kṛtyāhatānīva vinaśyanti samantataḥ
meaning: जामयो यानि गेहानि शपन्त्यप्रतिपूजिताः तानि कृत्याहतानीव विनश्यन्ति समन्ततः
text: The houses on which female relations, not being duly honoured, pronounce a curse, perish completely, as if destroyed by magic.
iast: jāmayo yāni gehāni śapantyapratipūjitāḥ tāni kṛtyāhatānīva vinaśyanti samantataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tasmādetāḥ sadā pūjyā bhūṣaṇācchādanāśanaiḥ bhūtikāmairnarairnityaṃ satkareṣūtsaveṣu ca
meaning: तस्मादेताः सदा पूज्या भूषणाच्छादनाशनैः भूतिकामैर्नरैर्नित्यं सत्करेषूत्सवेषु च
text: Hence men who seek (their own) welfare, should always honour women on holidays and festivals with (gifts of) ornaments, clothes, and (dainty) food.
iast: tasmādetāḥ sadā pūjyā bhūṣaṇācchādanāśanaiḥ bhūtikāmairnarairnityaṃ satkareṣūtsaveṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: saṃtuṣṭo bhāryayā bhartā bhartrā bhāryā tathaiva ca yasminneva kule nityaṃ kalyāṇaṃ tatra vai dhruvam
meaning: संतुष्टो भार्यया भर्ता भर्त्रा भार्या तथैव च यस्मिन्नेव कुले नित्यं कल्याणं तत्र वै ध्रुवम्
text: In that family, where the husband is pleased with his wife and the wife with her husband, happiness will assuredly be lasting.
iast: saṃtuṣṭo bhāryayā bhartā bhartrā bhāryā tathaiva ca yasminneva kule nityaṃ kalyāṇaṃ tatra vai dhruvam

sanskrit_iast: yadi hi strī na roceta pumāṃsaṃ na pramodayet apramodāt punaḥ puṃsaḥ prajanaṃ na pravartate
meaning: यदि हि स्त्री न रोचेत पुमांसं न प्रमोदयेत् अप्रमोदात् पुनः पुंसः प्रजनं न प्रवर्तते
text: For if the wife is not radiant with beauty, she will not attract her husband; but if she has no attractions for him, no children will be born.
iast: yadi hi strī na roceta pumāṃsaṃ na pramodayet apramodāt punaḥ puṃsaḥ prajanaṃ na pravartate

sanskrit_iast: striyāṃ tu rocamānāyāṃ sarvaṃ tadrocate kulam tasyāṃ tvarocamānāyāṃ sarvameva na rocate
meaning: स्त्रियां तु रोचमानायां सर्वं तद्रोचते कुलम् तस्यां त्वरोचमानायां सर्वमेव न रोचते
text: If the wife is radiant with beauty, the whole house is bright; but if she is destitute of beauty, all will appear dismal.
iast: striyāṃ tu rocamānāyāṃ sarvaṃ tadrocate kulam tasyāṃ tvarocamānāyāṃ sarvameva na rocate

sanskrit_iast: kuvivāhaiḥ kriyālopairvedānadhyayanena ca kulānyakulatāṃ yānti brāhmaṇātikrameṇa ca
meaning: कुविवाहैः क्रियालोपैर्वेदानध्ययनेन च कुलान्यकुलतां यान्ति ब्राह्मणातिक्रमेण च
text: By low marriages, by omitting (the performance of) sacred rites, by neglecting the study of the Veda, and by irreverence towards Brahmanas, (great) families sink low.
iast: kuvivāhaiḥ kriyālopairvedānadhyayanena ca kulānyakulatāṃ yānti brāhmaṇātikrameṇa ca

sanskrit_iast: śilpena vyavahāreṇa śūdrāpatyaiśca kevalaiḥ gobhiraśvaiśca yānaiśca kṛṣyā rājaupasevayā
meaning: शिल्पेन व्यवहारेण शूद्रापत्यैश्च केवलैः गोभिरश्वैश्च यानैश्च कृष्या राजौपसेवया
text: By (practising) handicrafts, by pecuniary transactions, by (begetting) children on Sudra females only, by (trading in) cows, horses, and carriages, by (the pursuit of) agriculture and by taking service under a king.
iast: śilpena vyavahāreṇa śūdrāpatyaiśca kevalaiḥ gobhiraśvaiśca yānaiśca kṛṣyā rājaupasevayā

sanskrit_iast: ayājyayājanaiścaiva nāstikyena ca karmaṇām kulānyāśu vinaśyanti yāni hīnāni mantrataḥ
meaning: अयाज्ययाजनैश्चैव नास्तिक्येन च कर्मणाम् कुलान्याशु विनश्यन्ति यानि हीनानि मन्त्रतः
text: By sacrificing for men unworthy to offer sacrifices and by denying (the future rewards for good) works, families, deficient in the (knowledge of the) Veda, quickly perish.
iast: ayājyayājanaiścaiva nāstikyena ca karmaṇām kulānyāśu vinaśyanti yāni hīnāni mantrataḥ

sanskrit_iast: mantratastu samṛddhāni kulānyalpadhanānyapi kulasaṅkhyāṃ ca gacchanti karṣanti ca mahadyaśaḥ
meaning: मन्त्रतस्तु समृद्धानि कुलान्यल्पधनान्यपि कुलसङ्ख्यां च गच्छन्ति कर्षन्ति च महद्यशः
text: But families that are rich in the knowledge of the Veda, though possessing little wealth, are numbered among the great, and acquire great fame.
iast: mantratastu samṛddhāni kulānyalpadhanānyapi kulasaṅkhyāṃ ca gacchanti karṣanti ca mahadyaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaivāhike 'gnau kurvīta gṛhyaṃ karma yathāvidhi pañcayajñavidhānaṃ ca paktiṃ cānvāhikīṃ gṛhī
meaning: वैवाहिके 'ग्नौ कुर्वीत गृह्यं कर्म यथाविधि पञ्चयज्ञविधानं च पक्तिं चान्वाहिकीं गृही
text: In the marriage-fire the householder should perform the ‘gṛhya’ rites; as also the rite of the ‘five sacrifices’ and the daily cooking.
iast: vaivāhike 'gnau kurvīta gṛhyaṃ karma yathāvidhi pañcayajñavidhānaṃ ca paktiṃ cānvāhikīṃ gṛhī

sanskrit_iast: pañca sūnā gṛhasthasya cullī peṣaṇyupaskaraḥ kaṇḍanī caudakumbhaśca badhyate yāstu vāhayan vadhyate
meaning: पञ्च सूना गृहस्थस्य चुल्ली पेषण्युपस्करः कण्डनी चौदकुम्भश्च बध्यते यास्तु वाहयन् वध्यते
text: For the householder there are five slaughter-houses: the hearth, the grinding-stone, household implements, mortar and pestle and water-jar;—by using which he becomes stricken.
iast: pañca sūnā gṛhasthasya cullī peṣaṇyupaskaraḥ kaṇḍanī caudakumbhaśca badhyate yāstu vāhayan vadhyate

sanskrit_iast: tāsāṃ krameṇa sarvāsāṃ niṣkṛtyarthaṃ maharṣibhiḥ pañca kḷptā mahāyajñāḥ pratyahaṃ gṛhamedhinām
meaning: तासां क्रमेण सर्वासां निष्कृत्यर्थं महर्षिभिः पञ्च कॢप्ता महायज्ञाः प्रत्यहं गृहमेधिनाम्
text: For the purpose of expiating all these in their course, the five great sacrifices have been ordained by the great sages, for householders (to be performed) daily.
iast: tāsāṃ krameṇa sarvāsāṃ niṣkṛtyarthaṃ maharṣibhiḥ pañca kḷptā mahāyajñāḥ pratyahaṃ gṛhamedhinām

sanskrit_iast: adhyāpanaṃ brahmayajñaḥ pitṛyajñastu tarpaṇam homo daivo balirbhauto nṛyajño 'tithipūjanam
meaning: अध्यापनं ब्रह्मयज्ञः पितृयज्ञस्तु तर्पणम् होमो दैवो बलिर्भौतो नृयज्ञो 'तिथिपूजनम्
text: Teaching is the ‘offering to Brahma;’ the Tarpaṇa is the ‘offering to Pitṛs;’ the Homa is ‘offering to Gods;’ the Bali is ‘offering to elementals;’ and the honouring of Guests is ‘offering to men.’
iast: adhyāpanaṃ brahmayajñaḥ pitṛyajñastu tarpaṇam homo daivo balirbhauto nṛyajño 'tithipūjanam

sanskrit_iast: pañcaitān yo mahā 'yajñānna hāpayati śaktitaḥ sa gṛhe 'pi vasannityaṃ sūnādoṣairna lipyate
meaning: पञ्चैतान् यो महा 'यज्ञान्न हापयति शक्तितः स गृहे 'पि वसन्नित्यं सूनादोषैर्न लिप्यते
text: He who omits not these sacrifices, to the best of his capacity, on any day, does not become tainted by the sin of the ‘slaughter-house,’ even though living in the house.
iast: pañcaitān yo mahā 'yajñānna hāpayati śaktitaḥ sa gṛhe 'pi vasannityaṃ sūnādoṣairna lipyate

sanskrit_iast: devatā 'tithibhṛtyānāṃ pitr̥̄ṇāmātmanaśca yaḥ na nirvapati pañcānāmucchvasanna sa jīvati
meaning: देवता 'तिथिभृत्यानां पित्र्̥̄णामात्मनश्च यः न निर्वपति पञ्चानामुच्छ्वसन्न स जीवति
text: He who does not make offerings to the five—viz., gods, guests, dependents, Pitṛs and himself,—does not live, even though breathing.
iast: devatā 'tithibhṛtyānāṃ pitr̥̄ṇāmātmanaśca yaḥ na nirvapati pañcānāmucchvasanna sa jīvati

sanskrit_iast: ahutaṃ ca hutaṃ caiva tathā prahutameva ca brāhmyaṃ hutaṃ prāśitaṃ ca pañcayajñān pracakṣate
meaning: अहुतं च हुतं चैव तथा प्रहुतमेव च ब्राह्म्यं हुतं प्राशितं च पञ्चयज्ञान् प्रचक्षते
text: ‘They also call these five sacrifices—(1) “Ahuta,” (2) “Huta,” (3) “Prahuta,” (4) “Brāhmya-huta,” and (5) “Prāśita.”’
iast: ahutaṃ ca hutaṃ caiva tathā prahutameva ca brāhmyaṃ hutaṃ prāśitaṃ ca pañcayajñān pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: japo 'huto huto homaḥ prahuto bhautiko baliḥ brāhmyaṃ hutaṃ dvijāgryārcā prāśitaṃ pitṛtarpaṇam
meaning: जपो 'हुतो हुतो होमः प्रहुतो भौतिको बलिः ब्राह्म्यं हुतं द्विजाग्र्यार्चा प्राशितं पितृतर्पणम्
text: (1) Japa is ‘Ahuta,’ (2) Offering into fire is ‘Huta,’ (3) Offering to elementals is ‘Prahuta,’ (4) The honouring of Brāhmaṇas is ‘Brāhmya-huta,’ and (5) Water-offering to Pitṛs is ‘Prāśita.’
iast: japo 'huto huto homaḥ prahuto bhautiko baliḥ brāhmyaṃ hutaṃ dvijāgryārcā prāśitaṃ pitṛtarpaṇam

sanskrit_iast: svādhyāye nityayuktaḥ syāddaive caivaiha karmaṇi daivakarmaṇi yukto hi bibhartīdaṃ carācaram
meaning: स्वाध्याये नित्ययुक्तः स्याद्दैवे चैवैह कर्मणि दैवकर्मणि युक्तो हि बिभर्तीदं चराचरम्
text: One should be constantly engaged in Vedic Study, as also in acts in honour of the gods; one who is engaged in acts in honour of the gods upholds the world, moveable as well as immoveable.
iast: svādhyāye nityayuktaḥ syāddaive caivaiha karmaṇi daivakarmaṇi yukto hi bibhartīdaṃ carācaram

sanskrit_iast: agnau prāstā 'hutiḥ samyagādityamupatiṣṭhate ādityāj jāyate vṛṣtirvṛṣterannaṃ tataḥ prajāḥ
meaning: अग्नौ प्रास्ता 'हुतिः सम्यगादित्यमुपतिष्ठते आदित्याज् जायते वृष्तिर्वृष्तेरन्नं ततः प्रजाः
text: An oblation duly thrown into the fire reaches the sun; from the sun proceeds rain from, rain food, and from food, the creatures.
iast: agnau prāstā 'hutiḥ samyagādityamupatiṣṭhate ādityāj jāyate vṛṣtirvṛṣterannaṃ tataḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathā vāyuṃ samāśritya vartante sarvajantavaḥ sarve jīvanti jantavaḥ tathā gṛhasthamāśritya vartante sarva āśramāḥ vartanta itarāśramaḥ
meaning: यथा वायुं समाश्रित्य वर्तन्ते सर्वजन्तवः सर्वे जीवन्ति जन्तवः तथा गृहस्थमाश्रित्य वर्तन्ते सर्व आश्रमाः वर्तन्त इतराश्रमः
text: Just as all Creatures subsist by deriving support from air, so do the other states subsist by deriving support from the Housohelder.
iast: yathā vāyuṃ samāśritya vartante sarvajantavaḥ sarve jīvanti jantavaḥ tathā gṛhasthamāśritya vartante sarva āśramāḥ vartanta itarāśramaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasmāt trayo 'pyāśramiṇo jñānenānnena cānvaham gṛhasthenaiva dhāryante tasmāj jyeṣṭhāśramo gṛhī gṛhaṃ
meaning: यस्मात् त्रयो 'प्याश्रमिणो ज्ञानेनान्नेन चान्वहम् गृहस्थेनैव धार्यन्ते तस्माज् ज्येष्ठाश्रमो गृही गृहं
text: Because men in all the three states are sustained by householders only, with knowledge and food, therefore the householder’s is the highest state.
iast: yasmāt trayo 'pyāśramiṇo jñānenānnena cānvaham gṛhasthenaiva dhāryante tasmāj jyeṣṭhāśramo gṛhī gṛhaṃ

sanskrit_iast: sa saṃdhāryaḥ prayatnena svargamakṣayamicchatā sukhaṃ caihaichhatā 'tyantaṃ yo 'dhāryo durbalaindriyaiḥ
meaning: स संधार्यः प्रयत्नेन स्वर्गमक्षयमिच्छता सुखं चैहैछ्हता 'त्यन्तं यो 'धार्यो दुर्बलैन्द्रियैः
text: That state, which cannot be maintained by men with weak organs, should be kept up with care by one desiring imperishable heaven and also undying happiness in this world.
iast: sa saṃdhāryaḥ prayatnena svargamakṣayamicchatā sukhaṃ caihaichhatā 'tyantaṃ yo 'dhāryo durbalaindriyaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṛṣayaḥ pitaro devā bhūtānyatithayastathā āśāsate kuṭumbibhyastebhyaḥ kāryaṃ vijānatā
meaning: ऋषयः पितरो देवा भूतान्यतिथयस्तथा आशासते कुटुम्बिभ्यस्तेभ्यः कार्यं विजानता
text: Sages, Pitṛs, gods, elementals and guests have expectations from family-men; one who knows should fulfil (one’s duty) towards them.’
iast: ṛṣayaḥ pitaro devā bhūtānyatithayastathā āśāsate kuṭumbibhyastebhyaḥ kāryaṃ vijānatā

sanskrit_iast: svādhyāyenārcayetaṛṣīn homairdevān yathāvidhi pitr̥̄ṃśrāddhaiśca nr̥̄nannairbhūtāni balikarmaṇā
meaning: स्वाध्यायेनार्चयेतऋषीन् होमैर्देवान् यथाविधि पित्र्̥̄ंश्राद्धैश्च न्र्̥̄नन्नैर्भूतानि बलिकर्मणा
text: One should worship, according to law, the Sages by Vedic Study, the Gods by Homa-offerings, the Pitṛs by Śrāddha offerings, the Men by food and the Elementals by the offering of Bali.’
iast: svādhyāyenārcayetaṛṣīn homairdevān yathāvidhi pitr̥̄ṃśrāddhaiśca nr̥̄nannairbhūtāni balikarmaṇā

sanskrit_iast: kuryādahar 'haḥ śrāddhamannādyenaudakena vā dadyādahar 'haḥ payomūlaphalairvā 'pi pitṛbhyaḥ prītimāvahan
meaning: कुर्यादहर् 'हः श्राद्धमन्नाद्येनौदकेन वा दद्यादहर् 'हः पयोमूलफलैर्वा 'पि पितृभ्यः प्रीतिमावहन्
text: One should daily offer Śrāddha with food, or with water, or with milk, roots and fruits,—(thereby) bringing pleasure to the Pitṛs.
iast: kuryādahar 'haḥ śrāddhamannādyenaudakena vā dadyādahar 'haḥ payomūlaphalairvā 'pi pitṛbhyaḥ prītimāvahan

sanskrit_iast: ekamapyāśayedvipraṃ pitryarthe pāñcayajñike pitryarthaṃ na caivātrāśayet kiṃ cidvaiśvadevaṃ prati dvijam
meaning: एकमप्याशयेद्विप्रं पित्र्यर्थे पाञ्चयज्ञिके पित्र्यर्थं न चैवात्राशयेत् किं चिद्वैश्वदेवं प्रति द्विजम्
text: At that (Śrāddha) which forms part of the Five Sacrifices, one should feed even one Brāhmaṇa in honour of the pitṛs; and on this occasion he shall not feed any Brāhmaṇa in honour of the Viśve-devas.
iast: ekamapyāśayedvipraṃ pitryarthe pāñcayajñike pitryarthaṃ na caivātrāśayet kiṃ cidvaiśvadevaṃ prati dvijam

sanskrit_iast: vaiśvadevasya siddhasya gṛhye 'gnau vidhipūrvakam ābhyaḥ kuryāddevatābhyo brāhmaṇo homamanvaham
meaning: वैश्वदेवस्य सिद्धस्य गृह्ये 'ग्नौ विधिपूर्वकम् आभ्यः कुर्याद्देवताभ्यो ब्राह्मणो होममन्वहम्
text: Out of the food cooked in the domestic fire, for the Viśvedevas, the Brāhmaṇa shall every day offer, according to rule, Homa to these deities,’
iast: vaiśvadevasya siddhasya gṛhye 'gnau vidhipūrvakam ābhyaḥ kuryāddevatābhyo brāhmaṇo homamanvaham

sanskrit_iast: agneḥ somasya caivādau tayoścaiva samastayoḥ viśvebhyaścaiva devebhyo dhanvantaraya eva ca
meaning: अग्नेः सोमस्य चैवादौ तयोश्चैव समस्तयोः विश्वेभ्यश्चैव देवेभ्यो धन्वन्तरय एव च
text: First to Agni and to Soma, then to both these together, then to the Viśve-devas and to Dhanvantari.
iast: agneḥ somasya caivādau tayoścaiva samastayoḥ viśvebhyaścaiva devebhyo dhanvantaraya eva ca

sanskrit_iast: kuhvai caivānumatyai ca prajāpataya eva ca saha dyāvāpṛthivyośca tathā sviṣṭakṛte 'ntataḥ
meaning: कुह्वै चैवानुमत्यै च प्रजापतय एव च सह द्यावापृथिव्योश्च तथा स्विष्टकृते 'न्ततः
text: To Kuhū, to Anumati, and to Prajāpati; then to Dyauḥ—Pṛthivī jointly, and finally to Sviṣṭakṛt,’
iast: kuhvai caivānumatyai ca prajāpataya eva ca saha dyāvāpṛthivyośca tathā sviṣṭakṛte 'ntataḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ samyagg havirhutvā sarvadikṣu pradakṣiṇam indrāntakāppatīndubhyaḥ sānugebhyo baliṃ haret
meaning: एवं सम्यग्ग् हविर्हुत्वा सर्वदिक्षु प्रदक्षिणम् इन्द्रान्तकाप्पतीन्दुभ्यः सानुगेभ्यो बलिं हरेत्
text: Having thus duly offered the oblation into fire, he should place Bali-offerings in all directions, proceeding towards his right to Indra, Antaka, Ap-pati and Indu, along with their followers.
iast: evaṃ samyagg havirhutvā sarvadikṣu pradakṣiṇam indrāntakāppatīndubhyaḥ sānugebhyo baliṃ haret

sanskrit_iast: marudbhya iti tu dvāri kṣipedapsvadbhya ityapi vanaspatibhya ityevaṃ musalaulūkhale haret
meaning: मरुद्भ्य इति तु द्वारि क्षिपेदप्स्वद्भ्य इत्यपि वनस्पतिभ्य इत्येवं मुसलौलूखले हरेत्
text: Saying “this to the Maruts,” he should make an offering at the door; also one in water, saying, “this to the Apas;” and he should hake an offering on the pestle and mortar, saying, “this to the Vanaspatis.”
iast: marudbhya iti tu dvāri kṣipedapsvadbhya ityapi vanaspatibhya ityevaṃ musalaulūkhale haret

sanskrit_iast: ucchīrṣake śriyai kuryādbhadrakālyai ca pādataḥ brahmavāstoṣpatibhyāṃ tu vāstumadhye baliṃ haret
meaning: उच्छीर्षके श्रियै कुर्याद्भद्रकाल्यै च पादतः ब्रह्मवास्तोष्पतिभ्यां तु वास्तुमध्ये बलिं हरेत्
text: He should hake an offering to Śrī on the “head” and to Bhadrakālī on the “feet;” for Brahman and Vāstoṣpati, he should place an offering in the centre of the homestead.
iast: ucchīrṣake śriyai kuryādbhadrakālyai ca pādataḥ brahmavāstoṣpatibhyāṃ tu vāstumadhye baliṃ haret

sanskrit_iast: viśvebhyaścaiva devebhyo balimākāśa utkṣipet divācarebhyo bhūtebhyo naktañcāribhya eva ca
meaning: विश्वेभ्यश्चैव देवेभ्यो बलिमाकाश उत्क्षिपेत् दिवाचरेभ्यो भूतेभ्यो नक्तञ्चारिभ्य एव च
text: The offering to the Viśvedevas he should throw into the sky; as also to the Elementals roaming in the day and to those roaming at night.
iast: viśvebhyaścaiva devebhyo balimākāśa utkṣipet divācarebhyo bhūtebhyo naktañcāribhya eva ca

sanskrit_iast: pṛṣṭhavāstuni kurvīta baliṃ sarvātmabhūtaye sarvānnabhūtaye pitṛbhyo baliśeṣaṃ tu sarvaṃ dakṣiṇato haret
meaning: पृष्ठवास्तुनि कुर्वीत बलिं सर्वात्मभूतये सर्वान्नभूतये पितृभ्यो बलिशेषं तु सर्वं दक्षिणतो हरेत्
text: These offerings one should make in the upper dwelling, for the purpose of acquiring all kinds of food. The entire remnant of the offerings he should offer towards the South, to the pitṛs.
iast: pṛṣṭhavāstuni kurvīta baliṃ sarvātmabhūtaye sarvānnabhūtaye pitṛbhyo baliśeṣaṃ tu sarvaṃ dakṣiṇato haret

sanskrit_iast: śūnāṃ ca patitānāṃ ca śvapacāṃ pāparogiṇām vayasānāṃ kṛmīṇāṃ ca śanakairnirvapedbhuvi vayasāṃ ca
meaning: शूनां च पतितानां च श्वपचां पापरोगिणाम् वयसानां कृमीणां च शनकैर्निर्वपेद्भुवि वयसां च
text: He should gently place on the ground food for dogs, outcasts, Cāṇḍālas, persons afflicted with filthy diseases, birds and insects.
iast: śūnāṃ ca patitānāṃ ca śvapacāṃ pāparogiṇām vayasānāṃ kṛmīṇāṃ ca śanakairnirvapedbhuvi vayasāṃ ca

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ yaḥ sarvabhūtāni brāhmaṇo nityamarcati sa gacchati paraṃ sthānaṃ tejomūrtiḥ pathāṛjunā
meaning: एवं यः सर्वभूतानि ब्राह्मणो नित्यमर्चति स गच्छति परं स्थानं तेजोमूर्तिः पथाऋजुना
text: The Brāhmaṇa who thus daily honours all beings, becomes endowed with a body of light, and goes to the highest place, by the straight path.
iast: evaṃ yaḥ sarvabhūtāni brāhmaṇo nityamarcati sa gacchati paraṃ sthānaṃ tejomūrtiḥ pathāṛjunā

sanskrit_iast: kṛtvaitadbalikarmaivamatithiṃ pūrvamāśayet bhikṣāṃ ca bhikṣave dadyādvidhivadbrahmacāriṇe
meaning: कृत्वैतद्बलिकर्मैवमतिथिं पूर्वमाशयेत् भिक्षां च भिक्षवे दद्याद्विधिवद्ब्रह्मचारिणे
text: Having performed this rite of offerings, he should first feed his guest and then give alms in the proper form, to one who is mendicant and a ‘Brahmacārin’
iast: kṛtvaitadbalikarmaivamatithiṃ pūrvamāśayet bhikṣāṃ ca bhikṣave dadyādvidhivadbrahmacāriṇe

sanskrit_iast: yat puṇyaphalamāpnoti gāṃ dattvā vidhivadguroḥ tat puṇyaphalamāpnoti bhikṣāṃ dattvā dvijo gṛhī
meaning: यत् पुण्यफलमाप्नोति गां दत्त्वा विधिवद्गुरोः तत् पुण्यफलमाप्नोति भिक्षां दत्त्वा द्विजो गृही
text: The twice-born householder, giving alms, obtains the same reward for merit which reward for merit one obtains by giving a cow, in the proper form, to his Teacher.
iast: yat puṇyaphalamāpnoti gāṃ dattvā vidhivadguroḥ tat puṇyaphalamāpnoti bhikṣāṃ dattvā dvijo gṛhī

sanskrit_iast: bhikṣāmapyudapātraṃ vā satkṛtya vidhipūrvakam vedatattvārthaviduṣe brāhmaṇāyaupapādayet
meaning: भिक्षामप्युदपात्रं वा सत्कृत्य विधिपूर्वकम् वेदतत्त्वार्थविदुषे ब्राह्मणायौपपादयेत्
text: In accordance with scriptural injunctions, one should make over to the Brāhmaṇa knowing the true meaning of the Veda even alms and a water-put, after having honoured him.
iast: bhikṣāmapyudapātraṃ vā satkṛtya vidhipūrvakam vedatattvārthaviduṣe brāhmaṇāyaupapādayet

sanskrit_iast: naśyanti havyakavyāni narāṇāmavijānatām bhasmībhūteṣu vipreṣu mohāddattāni dātṛbhiḥ bhasmabhūteṣu
meaning: नश्यन्ति हव्यकव्यानि नराणामविजानताम् भस्मीभूतेषु विप्रेषु मोहाद्दत्तानि दातृभिः भस्मभूतेषु
text: Rites in honour of the gods and those in honour of the Pitṛs performed by ignorant men become lost, when they are presented by the givers, through folly, to ash-like Brāhmaṇas.
iast: naśyanti havyakavyāni narāṇāmavijānatām bhasmībhūteṣu vipreṣu mohāddattāni dātṛbhiḥ bhasmabhūteṣu

sanskrit_iast: vidyātapassamṛddheṣu hutaṃ vipramukhāgniṣu nistārayati durgācca mahataścaiva kilbiṣāt
meaning: विद्यातपस्समृद्धेषु हुतं विप्रमुखाग्निषु निस्तारयति दुर्गाच्च महतश्चैव किल्बिषात्
text: An oblation thrown into the mouth-fire of Brāhmaṇas, effulgent with learning and austerities, saves from trouble, and also from great sin.
iast: vidyātapassamṛddheṣu hutaṃ vipramukhāgniṣu nistārayati durgācca mahataścaiva kilbiṣāt

sanskrit_iast: saṃprāptāya tvatithaye pradadyādāsanaudake annaṃ caiva yathāśakti satkṛtya vidhipūrvakam saṃskṛtya
meaning: संप्राप्ताय त्वतिथये प्रदद्यादासनौदके अन्नं चैव यथाशक्ति सत्कृत्य विधिपूर्वकम् संस्कृत्य
text: To the guest that has happened to come, he should offer; according to rule, water and seat, and also food prepared to the best of his abilitv.
iast: saṃprāptāya tvatithaye pradadyādāsanaudake annaṃ caiva yathāśakti satkṛtya vidhipūrvakam saṃskṛtya

sanskrit_iast: śilānapyuñchato nityaṃ pañcāgnīnapi juhvataḥ sarvaṃ sukṛtamādatte brāhmaṇo 'narcito vasan
meaning: शिलानप्युञ्छतो नित्यं पञ्चाग्नीनपि जुह्वतः सर्वं सुकृतमादत्ते ब्राह्मणो 'नर्चितो वसन्
text: A Brāhmaṇa staying unhonoured (in one’s house) takes away all his merit, even though he be one who subsists by gathering harvest-droppings, or offers oblations into the five fires.
iast: śilānapyuñchato nityaṃ pañcāgnīnapi juhvataḥ sarvaṃ sukṛtamādatte brāhmaṇo 'narcito vasan

sanskrit_iast: tṛṇāni bhūmirudakaṃ vāk caturthī ca sūnṛtā etānyapi satāṃ gehe naucchidyante kadā cana
meaning: तृणानि भूमिरुदकं वाक् चतुर्थी च सूनृता एतान्यपि सतां गेहे नौच्छिद्यन्ते कदा चन
text: Grasses, place, water and kind word as the fourth,—even these never fail in the house of good people.
iast: tṛṇāni bhūmirudakaṃ vāk caturthī ca sūnṛtā etānyapi satāṃ gehe naucchidyante kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: ekarātraṃ tu nivasannatithirbrāhmaṇaḥ smṛtaḥ anityaṃ hi sthito yasmāt tasmādatithirucyate
meaning: एकरात्रं तु निवसन्नतिथिर्ब्राह्मणः स्मृतः अनित्यं हि स्थितो यस्मात् तस्मादतिथिरुच्यते
text: A Brāhmaṇa staying for a single night has been declared to be a “guest” (Atithi). Because his stay is not long, therefore he is called “Atithi” (guest).
iast: ekarātraṃ tu nivasannatithirbrāhmaṇaḥ smṛtaḥ anityaṃ hi sthito yasmāt tasmādatithirucyate

sanskrit_iast: naikagrāmīṇamatithiṃ vipraṃ sāṅgatikaṃ tathā upasthitaṃ gṛhe vidyādbhāryā yatrāgnayo 'pi vā
meaning: नैकग्रामीणमतिथिं विप्रं साङ्गतिकं तथा उपस्थितं गृहे विद्याद्भार्या यत्राग्नयो 'पि वा
text: One should not regard as “guest” a Brāhmaṇa who lives in the same village or who is a companion. He should regard him as such when he arrives at his house, or where the wife and the fires are at the time.
iast: naikagrāmīṇamatithiṃ vipraṃ sāṅgatikaṃ tathā upasthitaṃ gṛhe vidyādbhāryā yatrāgnayo 'pi vā

sanskrit_iast: upāsate ye gṛhasthāḥ parapākamabuddhayaḥ tena te pretya paśutāṃ vrajantyannādidāyinaḥ
meaning: उपासते ये गृहस्थाः परपाकमबुद्धयः तेन ते प्रेत्य पशुतां व्रजन्त्यन्नादिदायिनः
text: Those foolish householders who wait upon the food cooked by others, become, after death, on that account, cattle belonging to the givers of food.
iast: upāsate ye gṛhasthāḥ parapākamabuddhayaḥ tena te pretya paśutāṃ vrajantyannādidāyinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: apraṇodyo 'tithiḥ sāyaṃ sūryauḍho gṛhamedhinā kāle prāptastvakāle vā nāsyānaśnan gṛhe vaset
meaning: अप्रणोद्यो 'तिथिः सायं सूर्यौढो गृहमेधिना काले प्राप्तस्त्वकाले वा नास्यानश्नन् गृहे वसेत्
text: The guest brought by the sun in the evening should not be driven away by the house-holder. Arrived in time, or not in time, he shall not stay in his house without taking food.
iast: apraṇodyo 'tithiḥ sāyaṃ sūryauḍho gṛhamedhinā kāle prāptastvakāle vā nāsyānaśnan gṛhe vaset

sanskrit_iast: na vai svayaṃ tadaśnīyādatithiṃ yanna bhojayet dhanyaṃ yaśasyamāyuṣyaṃ svargyaṃ vā 'tithipūjanam
meaning: न वै स्वयं तदश्नीयादतिथिं यन्न भोजयेत् धन्यं यशस्यमायुष्यं स्वर्ग्यं वा 'तिथिपूजनम्
text: He himself should not eat what he does not offer to his guest. The honouring of guests is conducive to wealth, fame, longevity and heaven.
iast: na vai svayaṃ tadaśnīyādatithiṃ yanna bhojayet dhanyaṃ yaśasyamāyuṣyaṃ svargyaṃ vā 'tithipūjanam

sanskrit_iast: āsanāvasathau śayyāmanuvrajyāmupāsanām uttameṣūttamaṃ kuryāddhīine hīnaṃ same samam
meaning: आसनावसथौ शय्यामनुव्रज्यामुपासनाम् उत्तमेषूत्तमं कुर्याद्धीइने हीनं समे समम्
text: He should offer seat, room, bed, foliowing and attendance of the best kind to superiors, of the inferior kind to inferiors and of the equal (ordinary) kind to equals.
iast: āsanāvasathau śayyāmanuvrajyāmupāsanām uttameṣūttamaṃ kuryāddhīine hīnaṃ same samam

sanskrit_iast: vaiśvadeve tu nirvṛtte yadyanyo 'tithirāvrajet tasyāpyannaṃ yathāśakti pradadyānna baliṃ haret
meaning: वैश्वदेवे तु निर्वृत्ते यद्यन्यो 'तिथिराव्रजेत् तस्याप्यन्नं यथाशक्ति प्रदद्यान्न बलिं हरेत्
text: On the Vaiśvadeva having been finished, if another guest should happen to arrive,—for him also he should provide food to the best of his ability; but he shall not make any offering (out of that food).
iast: vaiśvadeve tu nirvṛtte yadyanyo 'tithirāvrajet tasyāpyannaṃ yathāśakti pradadyānna baliṃ haret

sanskrit_iast: na bhojanārthaṃ sve vipraḥ kulagotre nivedayet bhojanārthaṃ hi te śaṃsan vāntāśīityucyate budhaiḥ
meaning: न भोजनार्थं स्वे विप्रः कुलगोत्रे निवेदयेत् भोजनार्थं हि ते शंसन् वान्ताशीइत्युच्यते बुधैः
text: A Brāhmaṇa should not advertise his family and Gotra for the purpose of obtaining a meal. Bragging about these, for the purpose of obtaining a meal, he comes to be called a “feeder on filth” by the wise.
iast: na bhojanārthaṃ sve vipraḥ kulagotre nivedayet bhojanārthaṃ hi te śaṃsan vāntāśīityucyate budhaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: na brāhmaṇasya tvatithirgṛhe rājanya ucyate vaiśyaśūdrau sakhā caiva jñātayo gurureva ca
meaning: न ब्राह्मणस्य त्वतिथिर्गृहे राजन्य उच्यते वैश्यशूद्रौ सखा चैव ज्ञातयो गुरुरेव च
text: In a Brāhmaṇa’s house, the Kṣatriya is not called a ‘guest;’ nor the Vaiśya or the Śūdra, nor his friends or relations, or his Teacher.
iast: na brāhmaṇasya tvatithirgṛhe rājanya ucyate vaiśyaśūdrau sakhā caiva jñātayo gurureva ca

sanskrit_iast: yadi tvatithidharmeṇa kṣatriyo gṛhamāvrajet bhuktavatsu ca vipreṣu kāmaṃ tamapi bhojayet
meaning: यदि त्वतिथिधर्मेण क्षत्रियो गृहमाव्रजेत् भुक्तवत्सु च विप्रेषु कामं तमपि भोजयेत्
text: If a Kṣatriya should happen to come to one’s house in the character of a guest, one may feed him also, after the Brāhmaṇas have eaten.
iast: yadi tvatithidharmeṇa kṣatriyo gṛhamāvrajet bhuktavatsu ca vipreṣu kāmaṃ tamapi bhojayet

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyaśūdrāvapi prāptau kuṭumbe 'tithidharmiṇau bhojayet saha bhṛtyaistāvānṛśaṃsyaṃ prayojayan
meaning: वैश्यशूद्रावपि प्राप्तौ कुटुम्बे 'तिथिधर्मिणौ भोजयेत् सह भृत्यैस्तावानृशंस्यं प्रयोजयन्
text: The Vaiśya and the Śūdra also, when arrived in the family in the character of guests, he should feed, along with his servants,—showing his compassionate disposition.
iast: vaiśyaśūdrāvapi prāptau kuṭumbe 'tithidharmiṇau bhojayet saha bhṛtyaistāvānṛśaṃsyaṃ prayojayan

sanskrit_iast: itarānapi sakhiādīn samprītyā gṛhamāgatān prakṛtyānnaṃ yathāśakti bhojayet saha bhāryayā
meaning: इतरानपि सखिआदीन् सम्प्रीत्या गृहमागतान् प्रकृत्यान्नं यथाशक्ति भोजयेत् सह भार्यया
text: Others also, friends and the rest, that may come to his house out of affection, he should feed on food specially prepared, to the best of his ability, together with his wife.
iast: itarānapi sakhiādīn samprītyā gṛhamāgatān prakṛtyānnaṃ yathāśakti bhojayet saha bhāryayā

sanskrit_iast: suvāsinīḥ kumārīśca rogiṇo garbhiṇīḥ striyaḥ atithibhyo 'gra evaitān bhojayedavicārayan 'tithibhyo 'nvagevaitān
meaning: सुवासिनीः कुमारीश्च रोगिणो गर्भिणीः स्त्रियः अतिथिभ्यो 'ग्र एवैतान् भोजयेदविचारयन् 'तिथिभ्यो 'न्वगेवैतान्
text: Newly married girls, maidens, sick persons and pregnant women,—these he should, without hesitation, feed immediately after the guests.
iast: suvāsinīḥ kumārīśca rogiṇo garbhiṇīḥ striyaḥ atithibhyo 'gra evaitān bhojayedavicārayan 'tithibhyo 'nvagevaitān

sanskrit_iast: adattvā tu ya etebhyaḥ pūrvaṃ bhuṅkte 'vicakṣaṇaḥ sa bhuñjāno na jānāti śvagṛdhrairjagdhimātmanaḥ
meaning: अदत्त्वा तु य एतेभ्यः पूर्वं भुङ्क्ते 'विचक्षणः स भुञ्जानो न जानाति श्वगृध्रैर्जग्धिमात्मनः
text: The foolish man, who eats before giving food to these, does not understand, that, in thus eating, he is himself devoured by dogs and vultures.
iast: adattvā tu ya etebhyaḥ pūrvaṃ bhuṅkte 'vicakṣaṇaḥ sa bhuñjāno na jānāti śvagṛdhrairjagdhimātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhuktavatsvatha vipreṣu sveṣu bhṛtyeṣu caiva hi bhuñjīyātāṃ tataḥ paścādavaśiṣṭaṃ tu dampatī
meaning: भुक्तवत्स्वथ विप्रेषु स्वेषु भृत्येषु चैव हि भुञ्जीयातां ततः पश्चादवशिष्टं तु दम्पती
text: After the Brāhmaṇas, his own people and servants have dined,—the husband and wife should afterwards eat what is left.
iast: bhuktavatsvatha vipreṣu sveṣu bhṛtyeṣu caiva hi bhuñjīyātāṃ tataḥ paścādavaśiṣṭaṃ tu dampatī

sanskrit_iast: devān ṛṣīn manuṣyāṃśca pitr̥̄n gṛhyāśca devatāḥ pūjayitvā tataḥ paścādgṛhasthaḥ śeṣabhug bhavet
meaning: देवान् ऋषीन् मनुष्यांश्च पित्र्̥̄न् गृह्याश्च देवताः पूजयित्वा ततः पश्चाद्गृहस्थः शेषभुग् भवेत्
text: Having worshipped the gods, sages, men, the Pitṛs and the household deities, the Householder shall eat afterwards what remains.
iast: devān ṛṣīn manuṣyāṃśca pitr̥̄n gṛhyāśca devatāḥ pūjayitvā tataḥ paścādgṛhasthaḥ śeṣabhug bhavet

sanskrit_iast: aghaṃ sa kevalaṃ bhuṅkte yaḥ pacatyātmakāraṇāt yajñaśiṣṭāśanaṃ hyetat satāmannaṃ vidhīyate
meaning: अघं स केवलं भुङ्क्ते यः पचत्यात्मकारणात् यज्ञशिष्टाशनं ह्येतत् सतामन्नं विधीयते
text: He who cooks for himself eats only sin; for the eating of good mem has been described as the eating of the remains of sacrifices.
iast: aghaṃ sa kevalaṃ bhuṅkte yaḥ pacatyātmakāraṇāt yajñaśiṣṭāśanaṃ hyetat satāmannaṃ vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: rājaṛtvijsnātakagurūn priyaśvaśuramātulān arhayen madhuparkeṇa parisaṃvatsarāt punaḥ
meaning: राजऋत्विज्स्नातकगुरून् प्रियश्वशुरमातुलान् अर्हयेन् मधुपर्केण परिसंवत्सरात् पुनः
text: He should receive, with the “honey-mixture,” the king, the priest, the accomplished Student, the Teacher, the Son-in-law, the Father-in-law and the Maternal uncle,—coming again after a year.
iast: rājaṛtvijsnātakagurūn priyaśvaśuramātulān arhayen madhuparkeṇa parisaṃvatsarāt punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: rājā ca śrotriyaścaiva yajñakarmaṇyupasthitau upasthite madhuparkeṇa saṃpūjyau na tvayajña iti sthitiḥ
meaning: राजा च श्रोत्रियश्चैव यज्ञकर्मण्युपस्थितौ उपस्थिते मधुपर्केण संपूज्यौ न त्वयज्ञ इति स्थितिः
text: The king and the Learned Man should be honoured with the Honey-mixture, at the approach of a sacrificial performance,—not if there is no sacrifice (going to be performed).
iast: rājā ca śrotriyaścaiva yajñakarmaṇyupasthitau upasthite madhuparkeṇa saṃpūjyau na tvayajña iti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: sāyaṃ tvannasya siddhasya patnyamantraṃ baliṃ haret vaiśvadevaṃ hi nāmaitat sāyaṃ prātarvidhīyate
meaning: सायं त्वन्नस्य सिद्धस्य पत्न्यमन्त्रं बलिं हरेत् वैश्वदेवं हि नामैतत् सायं प्रातर्विधीयते
text: Out of the food cooked in the evening the wife should offer the Bali-oblation, without sacred formulas. This is the “Vaiśvadeva” rite which has been enjoined for both Morning and evening.
iast: sāyaṃ tvannasya siddhasya patnyamantraṃ baliṃ haret vaiśvadevaṃ hi nāmaitat sāyaṃ prātarvidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: pitṛyajñaṃ tu nirvartya vipraścandrakṣaye 'gnimān caindukṣaye piṇḍānvāhāryakaṃ śrāddhaṃ kuryān māsānumāsikam
meaning: पितृयज्ञं तु निर्वर्त्य विप्रश्चन्द्रक्षये 'ग्निमान् चैन्दुक्षये पिण्डान्वाहार्यकं श्राद्धं कुर्यान् मासानुमासिकम्
text: Month after Month, on the moonless day, the Brāhmaṇa with the Fire shall, after having performed the Pitṛyajña, offer the “Piṇḍānvāhāryaka.”
iast: pitṛyajñaṃ tu nirvartya vipraścandrakṣaye 'gnimān caindukṣaye piṇḍānvāhāryakaṃ śrāddhaṃ kuryān māsānumāsikam

sanskrit_iast: pitr̥̄ṇāṃ māsikaṃ śrāddhamanvāhāryaṃ vidurbudhāḥ taccāmiṣeṇā kartavyaṃ praśastena prayatnataḥ
meaning: पित्र्̥̄णां मासिकं श्राद्धमन्वाहार्यं विदुर्बुधाः तच्चामिषेणा कर्तव्यं प्रशस्तेन प्रयत्नतः
text: The monthly śrāddha to the Pitṛs the wise call “anvāhārya;” and it should be carefully performed with such meat as has been commended.
iast: pitr̥̄ṇāṃ māsikaṃ śrāddhamanvāhāryaṃ vidurbudhāḥ taccāmiṣeṇā kartavyaṃ praśastena prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tatra ye bhojanīyāḥ syurye ca varjyā dvijottamāḥ yāvantaścaiva yaiścānnaistān pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ
meaning: तत्र ये भोजनीयाः स्युर्ये च वर्ज्या द्विजोत्तमाः यावन्तश्चैव यैश्चान्नैस्तान् प्रवक्ष्याम्यशेषतः
text: I am now going to describe fully which and how many Brāhmaṇas should be fed at it, and on what food,—as also those that should be avoided.
iast: tatra ye bhojanīyāḥ syurye ca varjyā dvijottamāḥ yāvantaścaiva yaiścānnaistān pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: dvau daive pitṛkārye trīnekaikamubhayatra vā pitṛkṛtye bhojayet susamṛddho 'pi na prasajjeta vistare na pravarteta
meaning: द्वौ दैवे पितृकार्ये त्रीनेकैकमुभयत्र वा पितृकृत्ये भोजयेत् सुसमृद्धो 'पि न प्रसज्जेत विस्तरे न प्रवर्तेत
text: Even though wealthy, one should feed two at the rite performed in honour of the gods, and three at that in honour of the Pitṛs; or one only at each of the two rites he should not indulge in large company.
iast: dvau daive pitṛkārye trīnekaikamubhayatra vā pitṛkṛtye bhojayet susamṛddho 'pi na prasajjeta vistare na pravarteta

sanskrit_iast: satkriyāṃ deśakālau ca śaucaṃ brāhmaṇasaṃpadaḥ pañcaitān vistaro hanti tasmānnaiheta vistaram
meaning: सत्क्रियां देशकालौ च शौचं ब्राह्मणसंपदः पञ्चैतान् विस्तरो हन्ति तस्मान्नैहेत विस्तरम्
text: Respectful treatment, place and time, purity and the qualities of Brāhmaṇas,—a large company hampers these five; therefore one shall not seek a large company.
iast: satkriyāṃ deśakālau ca śaucaṃ brāhmaṇasaṃpadaḥ pañcaitān vistaro hanti tasmānnaiheta vistaram

sanskrit_iast: prathitā pretakṛtyaiṣā pitryaṃ nāma vidhukṣaye tasmin yuktasyaiti nityaṃ pretakṛtyaiva laukikī
meaning: प्रथिता प्रेतकृत्यैषा पित्र्यं नाम विधुक्षये तस्मिन् युक्तस्यैति नित्यं प्रेतकृत्यैव लौकिकी
text: This rite named “Pitrya,” performed on the Moonless Day is known as beneficial to the dead. To him who is intent on performing it, there always accrues benefit after death, offered according to human ordinances.
iast: prathitā pretakṛtyaiṣā pitryaṃ nāma vidhukṣaye tasmin yuktasyaiti nityaṃ pretakṛtyaiva laukikī

sanskrit_iast: śrotriyāyaiva deyāni havyakavyāni dātṛbhiḥ arhattamāya viprāya tasmai dattaṃ mahāphalam
meaning: श्रोत्रियायैव देयानि हव्यकव्यानि दातृभिः अर्हत्तमाय विप्राय तस्मै दत्तं महाफलम्
text: Food offered to the gods and that offered in the Pitṛs are to be given to the most deserving; Brāhmaṇa learned in the Vedas. What is given to him is conducive to great results.
iast: śrotriyāyaiva deyāni havyakavyāni dātṛbhiḥ arhattamāya viprāya tasmai dattaṃ mahāphalam

sanskrit_iast: ekaikamapi vidvāṃsaṃ daive pitrye ca bhojayet bhojayan puṣkalaṃ phalamāpnoti nāmantrajñān bahūnapi
meaning: एकैकमपि विद्वांसं दैवे पित्र्ये च भोजयेत् भोजयन् पुष्कलं फलमाप्नोति नामन्त्रज्ञान् बहूनपि
text: By feeding at least one learned brāhmaṇa each at the rite performed in honour of the gods and that performed in honour of the ancestors,—one obtains a full reward; and not by feeding even many men ignorant of the Veda.’
iast: ekaikamapi vidvāṃsaṃ daive pitrye ca bhojayet bhojayan puṣkalaṃ phalamāpnoti nāmantrajñān bahūnapi

sanskrit_iast: dūrādeva parīkṣeta brāhmaṇaṃ vedapāragam tīrthaṃ taddhavyakavyānāṃ pradāne so 'tithiḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: दूरादेव परीक्षेत ब्राह्मणं वेदपारगम् तीर्थं तद्धव्यकव्यानां प्रदाने सो 'तिथिः स्मृतः
text: From far off one should examine the Brāhmaṇa thoroughly versed in the Veda; such a one is the proper channel for offerings to gods and Pitṛ and in the matter of gifts he has been declared to be the guest.
iast: dūrādeva parīkṣeta brāhmaṇaṃ vedapāragam tīrthaṃ taddhavyakavyānāṃ pradāne so 'tithiḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sahasraṃ hi sahasrāṇāmanṛcāṃ yatra bhuñjate ekastān mantravit prītaḥ sarvānarhati dharmataḥ
meaning: सहस्रं हि सहस्राणामनृचां यत्र भुञ्जते एकस्तान् मन्त्रवित् प्रीतः सर्वानर्हति धर्मतः
text: Where one thousand persons ignorant of the Veda eat, all those a single man learned in the Veda, on being satisfied, absorbs, in point of merit.
iast: sahasraṃ hi sahasrāṇāmanṛcāṃ yatra bhuñjate ekastān mantravit prītaḥ sarvānarhati dharmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: jñānautkṛṣṭāya deyāni kavyāni ca havīṃṣi ca na hi hastāvasṛgdigdhau rudhireṇaiva śudhyataḥ
meaning: ज्ञानौत्कृष्टाय देयानि कव्यानि च हवींषि च न हि हस्तावसृग्दिग्धौ रुधिरेणैव शुध्यतः
text: Offerings for gods and Pitṛs should be given to one who is distinguished by knowledge; for hands smeared with blood are not cleansed by blood.
iast: jñānautkṛṣṭāya deyāni kavyāni ca havīṃṣi ca na hi hastāvasṛgdigdhau rudhireṇaiva śudhyataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāvato grasate grāsān havyakavyeṣvamantravit tāvato grasate preto dīptaśūlaṛṣṭiayoguḍān
meaning: यावतो ग्रसते ग्रासान् हव्यकव्येष्वमन्त्रवित् तावतो ग्रसते प्रेतो दीप्तशूलऋष्टिअयोगुडान्
text: As many mouthfuls as the person ignorant of the Veda swallows out of the offerings to gods and Pitṛs, so many flaming spikes, spears and iron-balls does the man swallow after death.
iast: yāvato grasate grāsān havyakavyeṣvamantravit tāvato grasate preto dīptaśūlaṛṣṭiayoguḍān

sanskrit_iast: jñānaniṣṭhā dvijāḥ ke cit taponiṣṭhāstathā 'pare tapassvādhyāyaniṣṭhāśca karmaniṣṭhāstathā 'pare
meaning: ज्ञाननिष्ठा द्विजाः के चित् तपोनिष्ठास्तथा 'परे तपस्स्वाध्यायनिष्ठाश्च कर्मनिष्ठास्तथा 'परे
text: Some twice-born persons excel in learning; others excel in austerities; some others excel in austerities and Vedic study, and others again excel in rites.
iast: jñānaniṣṭhā dvijāḥ ke cit taponiṣṭhāstathā 'pare tapassvādhyāyaniṣṭhāśca karmaniṣṭhāstathā 'pare

sanskrit_iast: jñānaniṣṭheṣu kavyāni pratiṣṭhāpyāni yatnataḥ havyāni tu yathānyāyaṃ sarveṣveva caturṣvapi
meaning: ज्ञाननिष्ठेषु कव्यानि प्रतिष्ठाप्यानि यत्नतः हव्यानि तु यथान्यायं सर्वेष्वेव चतुर्ष्वपि
text: The offerings to the pitṛs should be carefully presented to those excelling in learning; and the offerings to gods to all the four, according to law.
iast: jñānaniṣṭheṣu kavyāni pratiṣṭhāpyāni yatnataḥ havyāni tu yathānyāyaṃ sarveṣveva caturṣvapi

sanskrit_iast: aśrotriyaḥ pitā yasya putraḥ syādvedapāragaḥ aśrotriyo vā putraḥ syāt pitā syādvedapāragaḥ
meaning: अश्रोत्रियः पिता यस्य पुत्रः स्याद्वेदपारगः अश्रोत्रियो वा पुत्रः स्यात् पिता स्याद्वेदपारगः
text: If a father happen to be ignorant of the Veda, whose son is thoroughly versed in the Veda,—and if the son happen to be ignorant of the Veda and the father is t horoughly versed in the Veda;
iast: aśrotriyaḥ pitā yasya putraḥ syādvedapāragaḥ aśrotriyo vā putraḥ syāt pitā syādvedapāragaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jyāyāṃsamanayorvidyādyasya syātśrotriyaḥ pitā mantrasaṃpūjanārthaṃ tu satkāramitaro 'rhati
meaning: ज्यायांसमनयोर्विद्याद्यस्य स्यात्श्रोत्रियः पिता मन्त्रसंपूजनार्थं तु सत्कारमितरो 'र्हति
text: ‘Of these two one should regard him as superior whose father is learned in the Veda; while the other deserves honour for the sake of the veneration due to the Veda.
iast: jyāyāṃsamanayorvidyādyasya syātśrotriyaḥ pitā mantrasaṃpūjanārthaṃ tu satkāramitaro 'rhati

sanskrit_iast: na śrāddhe bhojayen mitraṃ dhanaiḥ kāryo 'sya saṅgrahaḥ nāriṃ na mitraṃ yaṃ vidyāt taṃ śrāddhe bhojayeddvijam
meaning: न श्राद्धे भोजयेन् मित्रं धनैः कार्यो 'स्य सङ्ग्रहः नारिं न मित्रं यं विद्यात् तं श्राद्धे भोजयेद्द्विजम्
text: At a Śrāddha one should not feed a friend; his acquisition shall be made by means of riches. At a Śrāddha one should feed him whom he regards neither as friend nor as foe.
iast: na śrāddhe bhojayen mitraṃ dhanaiḥ kāryo 'sya saṅgrahaḥ nāriṃ na mitraṃ yaṃ vidyāt taṃ śrāddhe bhojayeddvijam
