sanskrit_iast: yasya mitrapradhānāni śrāddhāni ca havīṃṣi ca tasya pretya phalaṃ nāsti śrāddheṣu ca haviḥṣu ca
meaning: यस्य मित्रप्रधानानि श्राद्धानि च हवींषि च तस्य प्रेत्य फलं नास्ति श्राद्धेषु च हविःषु च
text: He at whose Śrāddhas and sacrifices, the friend forms the principal factor,—for him, after death, there is no reward, either for Śrāddhas or for sacrifices.
iast: yasya mitrapradhānāni śrāddhāni ca havīṃṣi ca tasya pretya phalaṃ nāsti śrāddheṣu ca haviḥṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: yaḥ saṅgatāni kurute mohātśrāddhena mānavaḥ sa svargāccyavate lokātśrāddhamitro dvijādhamaḥ
meaning: यः सङ्गतानि कुरुते मोहात्श्राद्धेन मानवः स स्वर्गाच्च्यवते लोकात्श्राद्धमित्रो द्विजाधमः
text: The man who, through folly, makes friendships by means of Śrāddhas,—that meanest of twice-born men, having the Śrāddha for his friend, falls from the regions of heaven.
iast: yaḥ saṅgatāni kurute mohātśrāddhena mānavaḥ sa svargāccyavate lokātśrāddhamitro dvijādhamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃbhojāni sā 'bhihitā paiśācī dakṣiṇā dvijaiḥ ihaivāste tu sā loke gaurandhaivaikaveśmani
meaning: संभोजानि सा 'भिहिता पैशाची दक्षिणा द्विजैः इहैवास्ते तु सा लोके गौरन्धैवैकवेश्मनि
text: This convivial dinner has been called by twice-born people the “gift of devils.” It remains in this world alone, like the blind cow tied in a single room.
iast: saṃbhojāni sā 'bhihitā paiśācī dakṣiṇā dvijaiḥ ihaivāste tu sā loke gaurandhaivaikaveśmani

sanskrit_iast: yathairiṇe bījamuptvā na vaptā labhate phalam tathā 'nṛce havirdattvā na dātā labhate phalam
meaning: यथैरिणे बीजमुप्त्वा न वप्ता लभते फलम् तथा 'नृचे हविर्दत्त्वा न दाता लभते फलम्
text: Just as having sown the seed in barren soil, the sower reaps no harvest,—even so, having given the offerings to one ignorant of the Veda, the giver obtains no reward.
iast: yathairiṇe bījamuptvā na vaptā labhate phalam tathā 'nṛce havirdattvā na dātā labhate phalam

sanskrit_iast: dātr̥̄n pratigrahītr̥̄ṃśca kurute phalabhāginaḥ viduṣe dakṣiṇāṃ dattvā vidhivat pretya caiha ca
meaning: दात्र्̥̄न् प्रतिग्रहीत्र्̥̄ंश्च कुरुते फलभागिनः विदुषे दक्षिणां दत्त्वा विधिवत् प्रेत्य चैह च
text: The presenting of the gift, according to rule, to the learned makes the givers and receivers partakers of reward, here as well as after death.
iast: dātr̥̄n pratigrahītr̥̄ṃśca kurute phalabhāginaḥ viduṣe dakṣiṇāṃ dattvā vidhivat pretya caiha ca

sanskrit_iast: kāmaṃ śrāddhe 'rcayen mitraṃ nābhirūpamapi tvarim dviṣatā hi havirbhuktaṃ bhavati pretya niṣphalam
meaning: कामं श्राद्धे 'र्चयेन् मित्रं नाभिरूपमपि त्वरिम् द्विषता हि हविर्भुक्तं भवति प्रेत्य निष्फलम्
text: One may entertain a friend at Śrāddhas, but never a foe, even though qualified. The offering eaten by the enemy becomes futile after death.
iast: kāmaṃ śrāddhe 'rcayen mitraṃ nābhirūpamapi tvarim dviṣatā hi havirbhuktaṃ bhavati pretya niṣphalam

sanskrit_iast: yatnena bhojayetśrāddhe bahvṛcaṃ vedapāragam śākhāntagamathādhvaryuṃ chandogaṃ tu samāptikam
meaning: यत्नेन भोजयेत्श्राद्धे बह्वृचं वेदपारगम् शाखान्तगमथाध्वर्युं छन्दोगं तु समाप्तिकम्
text: With great care one should feed at a Śrāddha the adherent of the Ṛgveda who is thoroughly versed in his Veda, or the adherent of the Yajurveda who has finished the entire recensional text, or the adherent of the Sāmaveda who has reached the end of it.
iast: yatnena bhojayetśrāddhe bahvṛcaṃ vedapāragam śākhāntagamathādhvaryuṃ chandogaṃ tu samāptikam

sanskrit_iast: eṣāmanyatamo yasya bhuñjīta śrāddhamarcitaḥ pitr̥̄ṇāṃ tasya tṛptiḥ syātśāśvatī sāptapauruṣī
meaning: एषामन्यतमो यस्य भुञ्जीत श्राद्धमर्चितः पित्र्̥̄णां तस्य तृप्तिः स्यात्शाश्वती साप्तपौरुषी
text: If any one of these should dine, duly honoured, at the Śrāddha performed by a certain person, there would be ever-lasting satisfaction for his ancestors, lasting till the seventh degree (of descendants).
iast: eṣāmanyatamo yasya bhuñjīta śrāddhamarcitaḥ pitr̥̄ṇāṃ tasya tṛptiḥ syātśāśvatī sāptapauruṣī

sanskrit_iast: eṣa vai prathamaḥ kalpaḥ pradāne havyakavyayoḥ anukalpastvayaṃ jñeyaḥ sadā sadbhiranuṣṭhitaḥ
meaning: एष वै प्रथमः कल्पः प्रदाने हव्यकव्ययोः अनुकल्पस्त्वयं ज्ञेयः सदा सद्भिरनुष्ठितः
text: This is the first course to be adopted in the presenting of the offerings made to Gods and Pitṛs. This (following) is to be regarded as the secondary course always adopted by the good.’
iast: eṣa vai prathamaḥ kalpaḥ pradāne havyakavyayoḥ anukalpastvayaṃ jñeyaḥ sadā sadbhiranuṣṭhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mātāmahaṃ mātulaṃ ca svasrīyaṃ śvaśuraṃ gurum dauhitraṃ viṭpatiṃ bandhuṃ ṛtvig yājyau ca bhojayet
meaning: मातामहं मातुलं च स्वस्रीयं श्वशुरं गुरुम् दौहित्रं विट्पतिं बन्धुं ऋत्विग् याज्यौ च भोजयेत्
text: One may feed the maternal grandfather, the maternal uncle, the sister’s son, father-in-law, the teacher, the daughter’s son, the son-in-law, a relative, the priest and him at whose sacrifices the performer officiates.
iast: mātāmahaṃ mātulaṃ ca svasrīyaṃ śvaśuraṃ gurum dauhitraṃ viṭpatiṃ bandhuṃ ṛtvig yājyau ca bhojayet

sanskrit_iast: na brāhmaṇaṃ parīkṣeta daive karmaṇi dharmavit pitrye karmaṇi tu prāpte parīkṣeta prayatnataḥ
meaning: न ब्राह्मणं परीक्षेत दैवे कर्मणि धर्मवित् पित्र्ये कर्मणि तु प्राप्ते परीक्षेत प्रयत्नतः
text: At the rite in honour of the gods, the man knowing the law shall not examine the Brāhmaṇa. But when the rite in honour of the Pitṛs comes to be performed, he shall examine him carefullv.
iast: na brāhmaṇaṃ parīkṣeta daive karmaṇi dharmavit pitrye karmaṇi tu prāpte parīkṣeta prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: ye stenapatitaklībā ye ca nāstikavṛttayaḥ tān havyakavyayorviprānanarhān manurabravīt
meaning: ये स्तेनपतितक्लीबा ये च नास्तिकवृत्तयः तान् हव्यकव्ययोर्विप्राननर्हान् मनुरब्रवीत्
text: Manu has declared those Brāhmaṇas undeserving of (receiving) the offerings to gods and Pitṛs who are thieves, outcasts and eunuchs, as also those that have the behaviour of atheists.
iast: ye stenapatitaklībā ye ca nāstikavṛttayaḥ tān havyakavyayorviprānanarhān manurabravīt

sanskrit_iast: jaṭilaṃ cānadhīyānaṃ durbālaṃ kitavaṃ tathā yājayanti ca ye pūgāṃstāṃśca śrāddhe na bhojayet
meaning: जटिलं चानधीयानं दुर्बालं कितवं तथा याजयन्ति च ये पूगांस्तांश्च श्राद्धे न भोजयेत्
text: One should not feed, at a Śrāddha, one with braided hair, who is not learned, one who is hairless, the gambler, and those who sacrifice for hosts.
iast: jaṭilaṃ cānadhīyānaṃ durbālaṃ kitavaṃ tathā yājayanti ca ye pūgāṃstāṃśca śrāddhe na bhojayet

sanskrit_iast: cikitsakān devalakān māṃsavikrayiṇastathā cikitsakādevalakāmāṃsavikrayiṇastathā vipaṇena ca jīvanto varjyāḥ syurhavyakavyayoḥ
meaning: चिकित्सकान् देवलकान् मांसविक्रयिणस्तथा चिकित्सकादेवलकामांसविक्रयिणस्तथा विपणेन च जीवन्तो वर्ज्याः स्युर्हव्यकव्ययोः
text: Healers, temple-attendants, meat-sellers and those living by trade,—these should be avoided at rites performed in honour of gods and Pitṛs.
iast: cikitsakān devalakān māṃsavikrayiṇastathā cikitsakādevalakāmāṃsavikrayiṇastathā vipaṇena ca jīvanto varjyāḥ syurhavyakavyayoḥ

sanskrit_iast: preṣyo grāmasya rājñaśca kunakhī śyāvadantakaḥ pratiroddhā guroścaiva tyaktāgnirvārdhuṣistathā
meaning: प्रेष्यो ग्रामस्य राज्ञश्च कुनखी श्यावदन्तकः प्रतिरोद्धा गुरोश्चैव त्यक्ताग्निर्वार्धुषिस्तथा
text: The servant of a village and of the king, one with deformed nails, one with black teeth, the opposer of his superior, one who has forsaken the Fire and the usurer.
iast: preṣyo grāmasya rājñaśca kunakhī śyāvadantakaḥ pratiroddhā guroścaiva tyaktāgnirvārdhuṣistathā

sanskrit_iast: yakṣmī ca paśupālaśca parivettā nirākṛtiḥ brahmadviṣparivittiśca gaṇābhyantara eva ca
meaning: यक्ष्मी च पशुपालश्च परिवेत्ता निराकृतिः ब्रह्मद्विष्परिवित्तिश्च गणाभ्यन्तर एव च
text: The invalid, the cattle-tender, one who has superseded h is elder brother, he who neglects the Great Sacrifices, one who is inimical to Brāhmaṇas, he who has been superseded by his younger brother, and one who is member of a company.
iast: yakṣmī ca paśupālaśca parivettā nirākṛtiḥ brahmadviṣparivittiśca gaṇābhyantara eva ca

sanskrit_iast: kuśīlavo 'vakīrṇī ca vṛṣalīpatireva ca paunarbhavaśca kāṇaśca yasya caupapatirgṛhe
meaning: कुशीलवो 'वकीर्णी च वृषलीपतिरेव च पौनर्भवश्च काणश्च यस्य चौपपतिर्गृहे
text: An actor, one who has broken the vows of continence, the husband of a Śūdra woman, the son of a re-married woman, one who has only one eye, and he in whose house lives the paramour.
iast: kuśīlavo 'vakīrṇī ca vṛṣalīpatireva ca paunarbhavaśca kāṇaśca yasya caupapatirgṛhe

sanskrit_iast: bhṛtakādhyāpako yaśca bhṛtakādhyāpitastathā śūdraśiṣyo guruścaiva vāgduṣṭaḥ kuṇḍagolakau
meaning: भृतकाध्यापको यश्च भृतकाध्यापितस्तथा शूद्रशिष्यो गुरुश्चैव वाग्दुष्टः कुण्डगोलकौ
text: One who teaches for a stipulated fee, he who is taught by one who teaches for a stipulated fee, the pupil and also the teacher of a Śūdra, one who is reprehensible in speech, the son of an adulteress and the son of a widow.
iast: bhṛtakādhyāpako yaśca bhṛtakādhyāpitastathā śūdraśiṣyo guruścaiva vāgduṣṭaḥ kuṇḍagolakau

sanskrit_iast: akāraṇe parityaktā mātāpitrorgurostathā akāraṇaparityaktā brāhmairyaunaiśca saṃbandhaiḥ saṃyogaṃ patitairgataḥ
meaning: अकारणे परित्यक्ता मातापित्रोर्गुरोस्तथा अकारणपरित्यक्ता ब्राह्मैर्यौनैश्च संबन्धैः संयोगं पतितैर्गतः
text: The forsaker, without cause, of his mother, father and superior; and he who has formed a connection, through the relationship of either Veda or marriage, with outcasts.
iast: akāraṇe parityaktā mātāpitrorgurostathā akāraṇaparityaktā brāhmairyaunaiśca saṃbandhaiḥ saṃyogaṃ patitairgataḥ

sanskrit_iast: agāradāhī garadaḥ kuṇḍāśī somavikrayī samudrayāyī bandī ca tailikaḥ kūṭakārakaḥ
meaning: अगारदाही गरदः कुण्डाशी सोमविक्रयी समुद्रयायी बन्दी च तैलिकः कूटकारकः
text: The house-burner, the poisoner, one who eats the food of an adulteress’ son, the seller of Soma, the sea-voyager, the bard, the dealer in oils, and the perjuror.
iast: agāradāhī garadaḥ kuṇḍāśī somavikrayī samudrayāyī bandī ca tailikaḥ kūṭakārakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pitrā vivadamānaśca kitavo madyapastathā pāparogyabhiśastaśca dāmbhiko rasavikrayī
meaning: पित्रा विवदमानश्च कितवो मद्यपस्तथा पापरोग्यभिशस्तश्च दाम्भिको रसविक्रयी
text: One quarrelling with one’s father, the keeper of a gambling house, the drunkard, one afflicted with a foul disease, one accused of sins, the hypocrite, and the dealer in essence.
iast: pitrā vivadamānaśca kitavo madyapastathā pāparogyabhiśastaśca dāmbhiko rasavikrayī

sanskrit_iast: dhanusśarāṇāṃ kartā ca yaścāgredidhiṣūpatiḥ mitradhrug dyūtavṛttiśca putrācāryastathaiva ca
meaning: धनुस्शराणां कर्ता च यश्चाग्रेदिधिषूपतिः मित्रध्रुग् द्यूतवृत्तिश्च पुत्राचार्यस्तथैव च
text: The maker of bows and arrows, he whose wife dallies with another person and he who makes love to his brother’s widow, he who injures a friend, he who subsists by gambling and he who has his own son for his teacher.
iast: dhanusśarāṇāṃ kartā ca yaścāgredidhiṣūpatiḥ mitradhrug dyūtavṛttiśca putrācāryastathaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: bhrāmarī ganḍamālī ca śvitryatho piśunastathā unmatto 'ndhaśca varjyāḥ syurvedanindaka eva ca
meaning: भ्रामरी गन्डमाली च श्वित्र्यथो पिशुनस्तथा उन्मत्तो 'न्धश्च वर्ज्याः स्युर्वेदनिन्दक एव च
text: An epileptic, one having a string of scrofulous swellings, one who suffers from leucoderma, the backbiter, the lunatic, the blind man, and the derider of the Veda—all these should be avoided.
iast: bhrāmarī ganḍamālī ca śvitryatho piśunastathā unmatto 'ndhaśca varjyāḥ syurvedanindaka eva ca

sanskrit_iast: hastigo 'śvauṣṭradamako nakṣatrairyaśca jīvati pakṣiṇāṃ poṣako yaśca yuddhācāryastathaiva ca
meaning: हस्तिगो 'श्वौष्ट्रदमको नक्षत्रैर्यश्च जीवति पक्षिणां पोषको यश्च युद्धाचार्यस्तथैव च
text: The tamer of elephants, bulls, horses or camels, one who subsists on stars, bird-keeper and the teacher of warfare.
iast: hastigo 'śvauṣṭradamako nakṣatrairyaśca jīvati pakṣiṇāṃ poṣako yaśca yuddhācāryastathaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: srotasāṃ bhedako yaśca teṣāṃ cāvaraṇe rataḥ gṛhasaṃveśako dūto vṛkṣāropaka eva ca
meaning: स्रोतसां भेदको यश्च तेषां चावरणे रतः गृहसंवेशको दूतो वृक्षारोपक एव च
text: He who diverts water-courses, he who is addicted to obstructing them, the house-planner, the messenger and
iast: srotasāṃ bhedako yaśca teṣāṃ cāvaraṇe rataḥ gṛhasaṃveśako dūto vṛkṣāropaka eva ca

sanskrit_iast: śvakrīḍī śyenajīvī ca kanyādūṣaka eva ca hiṃsro vṛṣalavṛttiśca gaṇānāṃ caiva yājakaḥ
meaning: श्वक्रीडी श्येनजीवी च कन्यादूषक एव च हिंस्रो वृषलवृत्तिश्च गणानां चैव याजकः
text: He who sports with dogs, the falconer, the defiler of virgins, the cruel man, he who derives his subsistence from Śūdras, and he who sacrifices to the Gaṇas.
iast: śvakrīḍī śyenajīvī ca kanyādūṣaka eva ca hiṃsro vṛṣalavṛttiśca gaṇānāṃ caiva yājakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācārahīnaḥ klībaśca nityaṃ yācanakastathā kṛṣijīvī ślīpadī ca sadbhirnindita eva ca
meaning: आचारहीनः क्लीबश्च नित्यं याचनकस्तथा कृषिजीवी श्लीपदी च सद्भिर्निन्दित एव च
text: One devoid of right conduct, the man without vigour, the constant beggar, he who lives by agriculture, one suffering from elephantiasis, and he who is spoken ill of by good men.
iast: ācārahīnaḥ klībaśca nityaṃ yācanakastathā kṛṣijīvī ślīpadī ca sadbhirnindita eva ca

sanskrit_iast: aurabhriko māhiṣikaḥ parapūrvāpatistathā pretaniryāpakaścaiva varjanīyāḥ prayatnataḥ
meaning: औरभ्रिको माहिषिकः परपूर्वापतिस्तथा प्रेतनिर्यापकश्चैव वर्जनीयाः प्रयत्नतः
text: The sheep-dealer, the buffalo-keeper, the husband of a woman who had another previous husband, the carrier of the dead—all these should be avoided with care.
iast: aurabhriko māhiṣikaḥ parapūrvāpatistathā pretaniryāpakaścaiva varjanīyāḥ prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: etān vigarhitācārānapāṅkteyān dvijādhamān dvijātipravaro vidvānubhayatra vivarjayet
meaning: एतान् विगर्हिताचारानपाङ्क्तेयान् द्विजाधमान् द्विजातिप्रवरो विद्वानुभयत्र विवर्जयेत्
text: The learned Brāhmaṇa should avoid at both (rites) these l owest of twice-born men, who are of reprehensible conduct and who are unworthy of company.
iast: etān vigarhitācārānapāṅkteyān dvijādhamān dvijātipravaro vidvānubhayatra vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇo tvanadhīyānastṛṇāgniriva śāmyati brāhmaṇaḥ hyanadhīyānaḥ tasmai havyaṃ na dātavyaṃ na hi bhasmani hūyate
meaning: ब्राह्मणो त्वनधीयानस्तृणाग्निरिव शाम्यति ब्राह्मणः ह्यनधीयानः तस्मै हव्यं न दातव्यं न हि भस्मनि हूयते
text: The unlearned Brāhmaṇa becomes quenched in the same manner as the fire of dry grass. The sacrificial offering should not be presented to him; as no libation is poured upon ashes.
iast: brāhmaṇo tvanadhīyānastṛṇāgniriva śāmyati brāhmaṇaḥ hyanadhīyānaḥ tasmai havyaṃ na dātavyaṃ na hi bhasmani hūyate

sanskrit_iast: apāṅktadāne yo dāturbhavatyūrdhvaṃ phalaudayaḥ 'paṅktyadāne daive haviṣi pitrye vā taṃ pravaksyāmyaśeṣataḥ daive karmaṇi
meaning: अपाङ्क्तदाने यो दातुर्भवत्यूर्ध्वं फलौदयः 'पङ्क्त्यदाने दैवे हविषि पित्र्ये वा तं प्रवक्स्याम्यशेषतः दैवे कर्मणि
text: I am going to describe fully the results that afterwards accrue to the giver from giving, out of the offering to gods and to Pitṛs, to one who is unworthy of the line.
iast: apāṅktadāne yo dāturbhavatyūrdhvaṃ phalaudayaḥ 'paṅktyadāne daive haviṣi pitrye vā taṃ pravaksyāmyaśeṣataḥ daive karmaṇi

sanskrit_iast: avratairyaddvijairbhuktaṃ parivetryādibhistathā apāṅkteyairyadanyaiśca tadvai rakṣāṃsi bhuñjate
meaning: अव्रतैर्यद्द्विजैर्भुक्तं परिवेत्र्यादिभिस्तथा अपाङ्क्तेयैर्यदन्यैश्च तद्वै रक्षांसि भुञ्जते
text: Demons indeed consume the food that is eaten by Brāhmaṇas deviod of self-restraint, by such as those who have superseded their elder brother and the like, or by others that are unworthy of company.
iast: avratairyaddvijairbhuktaṃ parivetryādibhistathā apāṅkteyairyadanyaiśca tadvai rakṣāṃsi bhuñjate

sanskrit_iast: dārāgnihotrasaṃyogaṃ kurute yo 'graje sthite parivettā sa vijñeyaḥ parivittistu pūrvajaḥ
meaning: दाराग्निहोत्रसंयोगं कुरुते यो 'ग्रजे स्थिते परिवेत्ता स विज्ञेयः परिवित्तिस्तु पूर्वजः
text: He who unites himself with “wife” and “Agnihotra,” while his elder remains, is to be regarded as the “superseder of his elder;” and the elder is to be regarded as “one who is superseded.”’
iast: dārāgnihotrasaṃyogaṃ kurute yo 'graje sthite parivettā sa vijñeyaḥ parivittistu pūrvajaḥ

sanskrit_iast: parivittiḥ parivettā yayā ca parividyate sarve te narakaṃ yānti dātṛyājakapañcamāḥ
meaning: परिवित्तिः परिवेत्ता यया च परिविद्यते सर्वे ते नरकं यान्ति दातृयाजकपञ्चमाः
text: The superseded elder brother, the superseding younger brother, and she through whom the superseding is done,—all these go to hell, along with the giver and the officiating priest as the fifth.
iast: parivittiḥ parivettā yayā ca parividyate sarve te narakaṃ yānti dātṛyājakapañcamāḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhrāturmṛtasya bhāryāyāṃ yo 'nurajyeta kāmataḥ dharmeṇāpi niyuktāyāṃ sa jñeyo didhiṣūpatiḥ
meaning: भ्रातुर्मृतस्य भार्यायां यो 'नुरज्येत कामतः धर्मेणापि नियुक्तायां स ज्ञेयो दिधिषूपतिः
text: He who would lasciviously make love to the wife of his dead brother, even though she may have been appointed according to law (to bear a child by him), should be known as the “didhiṣūpati.”
iast: bhrāturmṛtasya bhāryāyāṃ yo 'nurajyeta kāmataḥ dharmeṇāpi niyuktāyāṃ sa jñeyo didhiṣūpatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: paradāreṣu jāyete dvau sutau kuṇḍagolakau patyau jīvati kuṇḍaḥ syān mṛte bhartari golakaḥ
meaning: परदारेषु जायेते द्वौ सुतौ कुण्डगोलकौ पत्यौ जीवति कुण्डः स्यान् मृते भर्तरि गोलकः
text: By the wives of other men two kinds of sons are born: the “Kuṇḍa” and the “Golaka;” he who is born while the husband is alive is the “Kuṇḍa,” and one born after the death of the husband is the “Golaka.”
iast: paradāreṣu jāyete dvau sutau kuṇḍagolakau patyau jīvati kuṇḍaḥ syān mṛte bhartari golakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tau tu jātau parakṣetre prāṇinau pretya caiha ca te tu jātāḥ parakṣetre prāṇinaḥ dattāni havyakavyāni nāśayanti pradāyinām
meaning: तौ तु जातौ परक्षेत्रे प्राणिनौ प्रेत्य चैह च ते तु जाताः परक्षेत्रे प्राणिनः दत्तानि हव्यकव्यानि नाशयन्ति प्रदायिनाम्
text: These creatures, born of other men’s wives, cause, for the giver, the destruction, in this life as well as after death, of their offerings to gods and pitṛs that have been presented to them.
iast: tau tu jātau parakṣetre prāṇinau pretya caiha ca te tu jātāḥ parakṣetre prāṇinaḥ dattāni havyakavyāni nāśayanti pradāyinām

sanskrit_iast: apāṅktyo yāvataḥ paṅktyān bhuñjānānanupaśyati 'paṅktyo yāvataḥ tāvatāṃ na phalaṃ tatra dātā prāpnoti bāliśaḥ
meaning: अपाङ्क्त्यो यावतः पङ्क्त्यान् भुञ्जानाननुपश्यति 'पङ्क्त्यो यावतः तावतां न फलं तत्र दाता प्राप्नोति बालिशः
text: If one who is unworthy of company happen to look upon a number of those that are worthy of company, while those are eating, then the foolish giver (of food) does not obtain the reward of feeding so many men.
iast: apāṅktyo yāvataḥ paṅktyān bhuñjānānanupaśyati 'paṅktyo yāvataḥ tāvatāṃ na phalaṃ tatra dātā prāpnoti bāliśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vīkṣyāndho navateḥ kāṇaḥ ṣaṣṭeḥ śvitrī śatasya tu śatasya ca pāparogī sahasrasya dāturnāśayate phalam
meaning: वीक्ष्यान्धो नवतेः काणः षष्टेः श्वित्री शतस्य तु शतस्य च पापरोगी सहस्रस्य दातुर्नाशयते फलम्
text: The blind man, by looking, destroys the feeder’s reward for feeding ninety men, the one-eyed man of sixty, the leper of one hundred, and the man afflicted with a foul disease of a thousand.
iast: vīkṣyāndho navateḥ kāṇaḥ ṣaṣṭeḥ śvitrī śatasya tu śatasya ca pāparogī sahasrasya dāturnāśayate phalam

sanskrit_iast: yāvataḥ saṃspṛśedaṅgairbrāhmaṇāṃśūdrayājakaḥ tāvatāṃ na bhaveddātuḥ phalaṃ dānasya paurtikam
meaning: यावतः संस्पृशेदङ्गैर्ब्राह्मणांशूद्रयाजकः तावतां न भवेद्दातुः फलं दानस्य पौर्तिकम्
text: As many Brāhmaṇas the officiator at the sacrifices performed by Śūdras may happen to touch with his limbs,—the reward relating to charity in connection with the gift to so many Brāhmaṇas fails to accrue to the giver.
iast: yāvataḥ saṃspṛśedaṅgairbrāhmaṇāṃśūdrayājakaḥ tāvatāṃ na bhaveddātuḥ phalaṃ dānasya paurtikam

sanskrit_iast: vedavidcāpi vipro 'sya lobhāt kṛtvā pratigraham vināśaṃ vrajati kṣipramāmapātramivāmbhasi
meaning: वेदविद्चापि विप्रो 'स्य लोभात् कृत्वा प्रतिग्रहम् विनाशं व्रजति क्षिप्रमामपात्रमिवाम्भसि
text: If a Brāhmaṇa, though learned in the Veda, should, through covetousness, accept a gift from him,—he quickly perishes; just like the unbaked vessel in water.
iast: vedavidcāpi vipro 'sya lobhāt kṛtvā pratigraham vināśaṃ vrajati kṣipramāmapātramivāmbhasi

sanskrit_iast: somavikrayiṇe viṣṭhā bhiṣaje pūyaśoṇitam naṣṭaṃ devalake dattamapratiṣṭhaṃ tu vārdhuṣau
meaning: सोमविक्रयिणे विष्ठा भिषजे पूयशोणितम् नष्टं देवलके दत्तमप्रतिष्ठं तु वार्धुषौ
text: What is given to the Soma-seller becomes ordure; to the physician, pus and blood; that to the temple-attendant becomes lost; and that to the usurer has no place.
iast: somavikrayiṇe viṣṭhā bhiṣaje pūyaśoṇitam naṣṭaṃ devalake dattamapratiṣṭhaṃ tu vārdhuṣau

sanskrit_iast: yat tu vāṇijake dattaṃ naiha nāmutra tadbhavet bhasmanīva hutaṃ dravyaṃ tathā paunarbhave dvije
meaning: यत् तु वाणिजके दत्तं नैह नामुत्र तद्भवेत् भस्मनीव हुतं द्रव्यं तथा पौनर्भवे द्विजे
text: That which is given to the trader is neither here nor there. Similarly, what is given to the Brāhmaṇa born of a remarried woman is like a libation poured on ashes.
iast: yat tu vāṇijake dattaṃ naiha nāmutra tadbhavet bhasmanīva hutaṃ dravyaṃ tathā paunarbhave dvije

sanskrit_iast: itareṣu tvapāṅktyeṣu yathauddiṣṭeṣvasādhuṣu medo 'sṛlgmāṃsamajjā 'sthi vadantyannaṃ manīṣiṇaḥ
meaning: इतरेषु त्वपाङ्क्त्येषु यथौद्दिष्टेष्वसाधुषु मेदो 'सृल्ग्मांसमज्जा 'स्थि वदन्त्यन्नं मनीषिणः
text: The wise ones declare that food given to the other unfit persons unworthy of company, described above, becomes fat, blood, flesh, marrow and bone.
iast: itareṣu tvapāṅktyeṣu yathauddiṣṭeṣvasādhuṣu medo 'sṛlgmāṃsamajjā 'sthi vadantyannaṃ manīṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: apāṅktyaupahatā paṅktiḥ pāvyate yairdvijottamaiḥ apaṅktyaupahatā paṅktiḥ tānnibodhata kārtsnyena dvijāgryān paṅktipāvanān
meaning: अपाङ्क्त्यौपहता पङ्क्तिः पाव्यते यैर्द्विजोत्तमैः अपङ्क्त्यौपहता पङ्क्तिः तान्निबोधत कार्त्स्न्येन द्विजाग्र्यान् पङ्क्तिपावनान्
text: Now listen to the full description of those chief of twice-born men, the sanctifiers of company, by which best of the twice-born a company defiled by men unworthy of company becomes purified.
iast: apāṅktyaupahatā paṅktiḥ pāvyate yairdvijottamaiḥ apaṅktyaupahatā paṅktiḥ tānnibodhata kārtsnyena dvijāgryān paṅktipāvanān

sanskrit_iast: agryāḥ sarveṣu vedeṣu sarvapravacaneṣu ca śrotriyānvayajāścaiva vijñeyāḥ paṅktipāvanāḥ
meaning: अग्र्याः सर्वेषु वेदेषु सर्वप्रवचनेषु च श्रोत्रियान्वयजाश्चैव विज्ञेयाः पङ्क्तिपावनाः
text: Those persons should be known as “sanctifiers of company” who are foremost in all the vedas and in all the explanatory sciences, and who are born in the family of men learned in the veda.
iast: agryāḥ sarveṣu vedeṣu sarvapravacaneṣu ca śrotriyānvayajāścaiva vijñeyāḥ paṅktipāvanāḥ

sanskrit_iast: triṇāciketaḥ pañcāgnistrisuparṇaḥ ṣaḍaṅgavit brahmadeyātmasantāno jyeṣṭhasāmaga eva ca brahmadeyānusantāno
meaning: त्रिणाचिकेतः पञ्चाग्निस्त्रिसुपर्णः षडङ्गवित् ब्रह्मदेयात्मसन्तानो ज्येष्ठसामग एव च ब्रह्मदेयानुसन्तानो
text: One who has learnt the “Triṇāciketa,” one who knows the science of the five fires, one who has learnt the “Trisuparṇa,” one who knows the six-limbed science, one who is born of a woman married in the “Brahma (Brāhma)” form, one who sings the Jyeṣṭha-sāmas.
iast: triṇāciketaḥ pañcāgnistrisuparṇaḥ ṣaḍaṅgavit brahmadeyātmasantāno jyeṣṭhasāmaga eva ca brahmadeyānusantāno

sanskrit_iast: vedārthavit pravaktā ca brahmacārī sahasradaḥ śatāyuścaiva vijñeyā brāhmaṇāḥ paṅktipāvanāḥ
meaning: वेदार्थवित् प्रवक्ता च ब्रह्मचारी सहस्रदः शतायुश्चैव विज्ञेया ब्राह्मणाः पङ्क्तिपावनाः
text: He who understands the meaning of the Veda, he who expounds it, the Student, the giver of a thousand, the centenarian;—these Brāhmaṇas should be known as “Sanctifiers of Company.”
iast: vedārthavit pravaktā ca brahmacārī sahasradaḥ śatāyuścaiva vijñeyā brāhmaṇāḥ paṅktipāvanāḥ

sanskrit_iast: pūrvedyuraparedyurvā śrāddhakarmaṇyupasthite nimantrayeta try 'varān samyag viprān yathauditān nimantrayīta
meaning: पूर्वेद्युरपरेद्युर्वा श्राद्धकर्मण्युपस्थिते निमन्त्रयेत त्र्य् 'वरान् सम्यग् विप्रान् यथौदितान् निमन्त्रयीत
text: When the śrāddha-performance has approaohed, one should invite, either on the preceding day or the next day, in the proper manner, at least three brāhmaṇas, such as have been described.
iast: pūrvedyuraparedyurvā śrāddhakarmaṇyupasthite nimantrayeta try 'varān samyag viprān yathauditān nimantrayīta

sanskrit_iast: nimantrito dvijaḥ pitrye niyatātmā bhavet sadā na ca chandāṃsyadhīyīta yasya śrāddhaṃ ca tadbhavet
meaning: निमन्त्रितो द्विजः पित्र्ये नियतात्मा भवेत् सदा न च छन्दांस्यधीयीत यस्य श्राद्धं च तद्भवेत्
text: The brāhmaṇa invited at a rite in honor of Pitṛs shall remain self-controlled and shall not recite the Veda; so also the man who performs the śrāddha.
iast: nimantrito dvijaḥ pitrye niyatātmā bhavet sadā na ca chandāṃsyadhīyīta yasya śrāddhaṃ ca tadbhavet

sanskrit_iast: nimantritān hi pitara upatiṣṭhanti tān dvijān vāyuvatcānugacchanti tathā 'sīnānupāsate
meaning: निमन्त्रितान् हि पितर उपतिष्ठन्ति तान् द्विजान् वायुवत्चानुगच्छन्ति तथा 'सीनानुपासते
text: The Pitṛs attend upon those invited Brāhmaṇas; like the wind, they follow them and sit down when they are seated.
iast: nimantritān hi pitara upatiṣṭhanti tān dvijān vāyuvatcānugacchanti tathā 'sīnānupāsate

sanskrit_iast: ketitastu yathānyāyaṃ havye kavye dvijottamaḥ kathaṃ cidapyatikrāman pāpaḥ sūkaratāṃ vrajet
meaning: केतितस्तु यथान्यायं हव्ये कव्ये द्विजोत्तमः कथं चिदप्यतिक्रामन् पापः सूकरतां व्रजेत्
text: The rest of Brāhmaṇas, who, when duly invited at the rite in honour of Gods and Pitṛs, happens, somehow, to neglect it, incurs sin and becomes a hog.’
iast: ketitastu yathānyāyaṃ havye kavye dvijottamaḥ kathaṃ cidapyatikrāman pāpaḥ sūkaratāṃ vrajet

sanskrit_iast: āmantritastu yaḥ śrāddhe vṛśalyā saha modate dāturyadduṣkṛtaṃ kiṃ cit tat sarvaṃ pratipadyate
meaning: आमन्त्रितस्तु यः श्राद्धे वृशल्या सह मोदते दातुर्यद्दुष्कृतं किं चित् तत् सर्वं प्रतिपद्यते
text: If the man invited at the śrāddha dallies with a woman, he takes upon himself all the sin that there may be in the giver.
iast: āmantritastu yaḥ śrāddhe vṛśalyā saha modate dāturyadduṣkṛtaṃ kiṃ cit tat sarvaṃ pratipadyate

sanskrit_iast: akrodhanāḥ śaucaparāḥ satataṃ brahmacāriṇaḥ nyastaśastrā mahābhāgāḥ pitaraḥ pūrvadevatāḥ
meaning: अक्रोधनाः शौचपराः सततं ब्रह्मचारिणः न्यस्तशस्त्रा महाभागाः पितरः पूर्वदेवताः
text: The Pitṛs are the foremost gods, free from anger, ever intent on purity, chaste, rid of all means of offence, and supremely blessed.
iast: akrodhanāḥ śaucaparāḥ satataṃ brahmacāriṇaḥ nyastaśastrā mahābhāgāḥ pitaraḥ pūrvadevatāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasmādutpattireteṣāṃ sarveṣāmapyaśeṣataḥ ye ca yairupacaryāḥ syurniyamaistānnibodhata
meaning: यस्मादुत्पत्तिरेतेषां सर्वेषामप्यशेषतः ये च यैरुपचर्याः स्युर्नियमैस्तान्निबोधत
text: Now fully learn from whom all these have their origin and who are to be worshipped, by whom and by what roles.
iast: yasmādutpattireteṣāṃ sarveṣāmapyaśeṣataḥ ye ca yairupacaryāḥ syurniyamaistānnibodhata

sanskrit_iast: manorhairaṇyagarbhasya ye marīciādayaḥ sutāḥ teṣāṃ ṛṣīṇāṃ sarveṣāṃ putrāḥ pitṛgaṇāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: मनोर्हैरण्यगर्भस्य ये मरीचिआदयः सुताः तेषां ऋषीणां सर्वेषां पुत्राः पितृगणाः स्मृताः
text: Of Manu, the son of Hiraṇyagarbha, Marīci and the rest were sons; and the sons of all these sages have been declared to constitute the “Body of Pitṛs.”
iast: manorhairaṇyagarbhasya ye marīciādayaḥ sutāḥ teṣāṃ ṛṣīṇāṃ sarveṣāṃ putrāḥ pitṛgaṇāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: virājsutāḥ somasadaḥ sādhyānāṃ pitaraḥ smṛtāḥ agniṣvāttāśca devānāṃ mārīcā lokaviśrutāḥ
meaning: विराज्सुताः सोमसदः साध्यानां पितरः स्मृताः अग्निष्वात्ताश्च देवानां मारीचा लोकविश्रुताः
text: The Somasads, the sons of Virāj, have been declared to be the Pitṛs of the Sādhyas; and the Agnisvāttas, the sons of Marīci, are famed in the world as the Pitṛs of the Gods.
iast: virājsutāḥ somasadaḥ sādhyānāṃ pitaraḥ smṛtāḥ agniṣvāttāśca devānāṃ mārīcā lokaviśrutāḥ

sanskrit_iast: daityadānavayakṣāṇāṃ gandharvauragarakṣasām suparṇakinnarāṇāṃ ca smṛtā barhiṣado 'trijāḥ
meaning: दैत्यदानवयक्षाणां गन्धर्वौरगरक्षसाम् सुपर्णकिन्नराणां च स्मृता बर्हिषदो 'त्रिजाः
text: The Barhisads, the sons of Atri, are declared to be the Pitṛs of Daityas, Dānavas, Yakṣas, Gandharvas, Uragas, Rākṣasas, Suparṇas and Kinnaras.
iast: daityadānavayakṣāṇāṃ gandharvauragarakṣasām suparṇakinnarāṇāṃ ca smṛtā barhiṣado 'trijāḥ

sanskrit_iast: somapā nāma viprāṇāṃ kṣatriyāṇāṃ havirbhujaḥ vaiśyānāmājyapā nāma śūdrāṇāṃ tu sukālinaḥ
meaning: सोमपा नाम विप्राणां क्षत्रियाणां हविर्भुजः वैश्यानामाज्यपा नाम शूद्राणां तु सुकालिनः
text: Those by name Somapās are the Pitṛs of Brāhmaṇas; the Havirbhujs are the Pitṛs of Kṣatriyas; those by name Ājyapas, of Vaiśyas; and of Śūdras, Sukālins.
iast: somapā nāma viprāṇāṃ kṣatriyāṇāṃ havirbhujaḥ vaiśyānāmājyapā nāma śūdrāṇāṃ tu sukālinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: somapāstu kaveḥ putrā haviṣmanto 'ṅgiraḥsutāḥ pulastyasyājyapāḥ putrā vasiṣṭhasya sukālinaḥ
meaning: सोमपास्तु कवेः पुत्रा हविष्मन्तो 'ङ्गिरःसुताः पुलस्त्यस्याज्यपाः पुत्रा वसिष्ठस्य सुकालिनः
text: Somapas are the sons of Kavi; Haviṣmats are the sons of Aṅgiras; Ājyapās are the sons of Pulastya, and Sukālins, of Vaśiṣṭha.
iast: somapāstu kaveḥ putrā haviṣmanto 'ṅgiraḥsutāḥ pulastyasyājyapāḥ putrā vasiṣṭhasya sukālinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: agnidagdhānagnidagdhān kāvyān barhiṣadastathā 'nagnidagdhānagnidagdhān agniṣvāttāṃśca saumyāṃśca viprāṇāmeva nirdiśet
meaning: अग्निदग्धानग्निदग्धान् काव्यान् बर्हिषदस्तथा 'नग्निदग्धानग्निदग्धान् अग्निष्वात्तांश्च सौम्यांश्च विप्राणामेव निर्दिशेत्
text: ‘The Anagnidagdhas, the Agnidagdhas, the Kāvyas, the Barhiṣads, the Agniṣvāttas, and the Saumyas,—these one should regard as the Pitṛs of Brāhmaṇas.
iast: agnidagdhānagnidagdhān kāvyān barhiṣadastathā 'nagnidagdhānagnidagdhān agniṣvāttāṃśca saumyāṃśca viprāṇāmeva nirdiśet

sanskrit_iast: ya ete tu gaṇā mukhyāḥ pitr̥̄ṇāṃ parikīrtitāḥ teṣāmapīha vijñeyaṃ putrapautramanantakam
meaning: य एते तु गणा मुख्याः पित्र्̥̄णां परिकीर्तिताः तेषामपीह विज्ञेयं पुत्रपौत्रमनन्तकम्
text: Of the principal bodies of Pitṛs that have been described, know that there are endless sons and grandsons in this world.
iast: ya ete tu gaṇā mukhyāḥ pitr̥̄ṇāṃ parikīrtitāḥ teṣāmapīha vijñeyaṃ putrapautramanantakam

sanskrit_iast: ṛṣibhyaḥ pitaro jātāḥ pitṛbhyo devamānavāḥ devebhyastu jagat sarvaṃ caraṃ sthāṇvanupūrvaśaḥ
meaning: ऋषिभ्यः पितरो जाताः पितृभ्यो देवमानवाः देवेभ्यस्तु जगत् सर्वं चरं स्थाण्वनुपूर्वशः
text: From the sages were born the Pitṛs, Gods and Men; and from the gods the entire world, moveable and immoveable, in due order.
iast: ṛṣibhyaḥ pitaro jātāḥ pitṛbhyo devamānavāḥ devebhyastu jagat sarvaṃ caraṃ sthāṇvanupūrvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: rājatairbhājanaireṣāmatho vā rajatānvitaiḥ vāryapi śraddhayā dattamakṣayāyaupakalpate
meaning: राजतैर्भाजनैरेषामथो वा रजतान्वितैः वार्यपि श्रद्धया दत्तमक्षयायौपकल्पते
text: Even water offered to these with faith, in vessels, either made of silver or connected with silver, is conducive to imperishability.
iast: rājatairbhājanaireṣāmatho vā rajatānvitaiḥ vāryapi śraddhayā dattamakṣayāyaupakalpate

sanskrit_iast: daivakāryāddvijātīnāṃ pitṛkāryaṃ viśiṣyate daivaṃ hi pitṛkāryasya pūrvamāpyāyanaṃ smṛtam
meaning: दैवकार्याद्द्विजातीनां पितृकार्यं विशिष्यते दैवं हि पितृकार्यस्य पूर्वमाप्यायनं स्मृतम्
text: For twice-born men, the rite in honour of the Pitṛs excels that in honour of the gods; the rite in honour of the gods has been declared to be the previous sustainer of the rite in honour of the Pitṛs.
iast: daivakāryāddvijātīnāṃ pitṛkāryaṃ viśiṣyate daivaṃ hi pitṛkāryasya pūrvamāpyāyanaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: teṣāmārakṣabhūtaṃ tu pūrvaṃ daivaṃ niyojayet raksāṃsi vipralumpanti śrāddhamārakṣavarjitam
meaning: तेषामारक्षभूतं तु पूर्वं दैवं नियोजयेत् रक्सांसि विप्रलुम्पन्ति श्राद्धमारक्षवर्जितम्
text: One should first engage the Brāhmaṇa in honour of the gods, as a protection to these (offerings to) Pitṛs; for the Rākṣasas take away the śrāddha that is devoid of protection.
iast: teṣāmārakṣabhūtaṃ tu pūrvaṃ daivaṃ niyojayet raksāṃsi vipralumpanti śrāddhamārakṣavarjitam

sanskrit_iast: daivādyantaṃ tadīheta pitryādyantaṃ na tadbhavet pitryādyantaṃ tvīhamānaḥ kṣipraṃ naśyati sānvayaḥ
meaning: दैवाद्यन्तं तदीहेत पित्र्याद्यन्तं न तद्भवेत् पित्र्याद्यन्तं त्वीहमानः क्षिप्रं नश्यति सान्वयः
text: One should first engage the Brāhmaṇa in honour of the gods, as a protection to these (offerings to) Pitṛs; for the Rākṣasas take away the śrāddha that is devoid of protection.
iast: daivādyantaṃ tadīheta pitryādyantaṃ na tadbhavet pitryādyantaṃ tvīhamānaḥ kṣipraṃ naśyati sānvayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: śuciṃ deśaṃ viviktaṃ ca gomayenaupalepayet dakṣināpravaṇaṃ caiva prayatnenaupapādayet
meaning: शुचिं देशं विविक्तं च गोमयेनौपलेपयेत् दक्षिनाप्रवणं चैव प्रयत्नेनौपपादयेत्
text: One should prepare with care a clean and secluded place Sloping towards the south, and smear it with cowdung.
iast: śuciṃ deśaṃ viviktaṃ ca gomayenaupalepayet dakṣināpravaṇaṃ caiva prayatnenaupapādayet

sanskrit_iast: avakāśeṣu cokṣeṣu jalatīreṣu caiva hi vivikteṣu ca tuṣyanti dattena pitaraḥ sadā
meaning: अवकाशेषु चोक्षेषु जलतीरेषु चैव हि विविक्तेषु च तुष्यन्ति दत्तेन पितरः सदा
text: The Pitṛs are always pleased with what is offered in clean places, on water-banks and in secluded places.
iast: avakāśeṣu cokṣeṣu jalatīreṣu caiva hi vivikteṣu ca tuṣyanti dattena pitaraḥ sadā

sanskrit_iast: āsaneṣūpakḷpteṣu barhiṣmatsu pṛthakpṛthak upaspṛṣṭaudakān samyag viprāṃstānupaveśayet
meaning: आसनेषूपकॢप्तेषु बर्हिष्मत्सु पृथक्पृथक् उपस्पृष्टौदकान् सम्यग् विप्रांस्तानुपवेशयेत्
text: Seats with kuśa grass having been separately placed, he should seat the said Brāhmaṇas who have performed their ablutions.
iast: āsaneṣūpakḷpteṣu barhiṣmatsu pṛthakpṛthak upaspṛṣṭaudakān samyag viprāṃstānupaveśayet

sanskrit_iast: upaveśya tu tān viprānāsaneṣvajugupsitān gandhamālyaiḥ surabhibhirarcayeddaivapūrvakam
meaning: उपवेश्य तु तान् विप्रानासनेष्वजुगुप्सितान् गन्धमाल्यैः सुरभिभिरर्चयेद्दैवपूर्वकम्
text: Having seated those unreproached Brāhmaṇas on those seats, he should worship them with sweet-smelling perfumes and Garlands, beginning with those invited in honour of the gods.
iast: upaveśya tu tān viprānāsaneṣvajugupsitān gandhamālyaiḥ surabhibhirarcayeddaivapūrvakam

sanskrit_iast: teṣāmudakamānīya sapavitrāṃstilānapi agnau kuryādanujñāto brāhmaṇo brāhmaṇaiḥ saha
meaning: तेषामुदकमानीय सपवित्रांस्तिलानपि अग्नौ कुर्यादनुज्ञातो ब्राह्मणो ब्राह्मणैः सह
text: Having presented to them water, and also sesamum along with kuśa-blade, the Brāhmaṇa, permitted by the Brāhmaṇas collectively, should make an offering into fire.
iast: teṣāmudakamānīya sapavitrāṃstilānapi agnau kuryādanujñāto brāhmaṇo brāhmaṇaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: agneḥ somayamābhyāṃ ca kṛtvā 'pyāyanamāditaḥ havirdānena vidhivat paścāt saṃtarpayet pitr̥̄n
meaning: अग्नेः सोमयमाभ्यां च कृत्वा 'प्यायनमादितः हविर्दानेन विधिवत् पश्चात् संतर्पयेत् पित्र्̥̄न्
text: Having at fir st brought about the satisfaction of Agni and Soma-Yama by the offering of sacrificial food, he should afterwards satisfy, according to rule, the Pitṛs.
iast: agneḥ somayamābhyāṃ ca kṛtvā 'pyāyanamāditaḥ havirdānena vidhivat paścāt saṃtarpayet pitr̥̄n

sanskrit_iast: agniabhāve tu viprasya pāṇāvevaupapādayet yo hyagniḥ sa dvijo viprairmantradarśibhirucyate
meaning: अग्निअभावे तु विप्रस्य पाणावेवौपपादयेत् यो ह्यग्निः स द्विजो विप्रैर्मन्त्रदर्शिभिरुच्यते
text: In the absence of Fire, he shall make the offering into the Brāhmaṇa’s hand; for it has been declared by the Brāhmaṇa seers of Vedic texts that what the Fire is, that same is the Brāhmaṇa.
iast: agniabhāve tu viprasya pāṇāvevaupapādayet yo hyagniḥ sa dvijo viprairmantradarśibhirucyate

sanskrit_iast: akrodhanān suprasādān vadantyetān purātanān lokasyāpyāyane yuktān śrāddhadevān dvijottamān śrāddhe devān dvijottamān
meaning: अक्रोधनान् सुप्रसादान् वदन्त्येतान् पुरातनान् लोकस्याप्यायने युक्तान् श्राद्धदेवान् द्विजोत्तमान् श्राद्धे देवान् द्विजोत्तमान्
text: The ancients describe these good Brāhmaṇas as the “Gods of Śrāddha,” free from anger, easily satisfied, intent upon sustaining the universe.
iast: akrodhanān suprasādān vadantyetān purātanān lokasyāpyāyane yuktān śrāddhadevān dvijottamān śrāddhe devān dvijottamān

sanskrit_iast: apasavyamagnau kṛtvā sarvamāvṛtya vikramam āvṛtparikramaṃ apasavyena hastena nirvapedudakaṃ bhuvi
meaning: अपसव्यमग्नौ कृत्वा सर्वमावृत्य विक्रमम् आवृत्परिक्रमं अपसव्येन हस्तेन निर्वपेदुदकं भुवि
text: Having done the entire serial performance in Fire, in the “Apasavya” form, he should offer water on the ground with the hand in the “Apasavya” position.
iast: apasavyamagnau kṛtvā sarvamāvṛtya vikramam āvṛtparikramaṃ apasavyena hastena nirvapedudakaṃ bhuvi

sanskrit_iast: trīṃstu tasmāddhaviḥśeṣāt piṇḍān kṛtvā samāhitaḥ audakenaiva vidhinā nirvapeddakṣiṇāmukhaḥ
meaning: त्रींस्तु तस्माद्धविःशेषात् पिण्डान् कृत्वा समाहितः औदकेनैव विधिना निर्वपेद्दक्षिणामुखः
text: Having made these balls out of the remnant of the said sacrificial material, he should, with collected mind and facing the South, offer them in the manner of the water-libations.
iast: trīṃstu tasmāddhaviḥśeṣāt piṇḍān kṛtvā samāhitaḥ audakenaiva vidhinā nirvapeddakṣiṇāmukhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nyupya piṇḍāṃstatastāṃstu prayato vidhipūrvakam teṣu darbheṣu taṃ hastaṃ nirmṛjyātlepabhāginām
meaning: न्युप्य पिण्डांस्ततस्तांस्तु प्रयतो विधिपूर्वकम् तेषु दर्भेषु तं हस्तं निर्मृज्यात्लेपभागिनाम्
text: Self-controlled, he should, after having offered those balls on Kuśa-blades, according to rule, wipe that hand on those same (Kuśa-blades), for the sake of the “Partakers of Smearings.”
iast: nyupya piṇḍāṃstatastāṃstu prayato vidhipūrvakam teṣu darbheṣu taṃ hastaṃ nirmṛjyātlepabhāginām

sanskrit_iast: ācamyaudakparāvṛtya trirāyamya śanairasūn ṣaḍ ṛtūṃśca namaskuryāt pitr̥̄neva ca mantravat
meaning: आचम्यौदक्परावृत्य त्रिरायम्य शनैरसून् षड् ऋतूंश्च नमस्कुर्यात् पित्र्̥̄नेव च मन्त्रवत्
text: Turning to the north, having sipped water and having gently suppressed his breath three times, he shall salute, with proper formulas, the six seasons and also the Pitṛs.
iast: ācamyaudakparāvṛtya trirāyamya śanairasūn ṣaḍ ṛtūṃśca namaskuryāt pitr̥̄neva ca mantravat

sanskrit_iast: udakaṃ ninayetśeṣaṃ śanaiḥ piṇḍāntike punaḥ avajighrecca tān piṇḍān yathānyuptān samāhitaḥ
meaning: उदकं निनयेत्शेषं शनैः पिण्डान्तिके पुनः अवजिघ्रेच्च तान् पिण्डान् यथान्युप्तान् समाहितः
text: The remaining water he should gently pour near the balls; and with collected mind he should smell those halls in the order in which they were offered.
iast: udakaṃ ninayetśeṣaṃ śanaiḥ piṇḍāntike punaḥ avajighrecca tān piṇḍān yathānyuptān samāhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: piṇḍebhyastvalpikāṃ mātrāṃ samādāyānupūrvaśaḥ piṇḍebhyaḥ svalpikāṃ tāneva viprānāsīnān vidhivat pūrvamāśayet
meaning: पिण्डेभ्यस्त्वल्पिकां मात्रां समादायानुपूर्वशः पिण्डेभ्यः स्वल्पिकां तानेव विप्रानासीनान् विधिवत् पूर्वमाशयेत्
text: Having, in due order of sequence, taken very small portions out of the halls, he shall first feed those same seated Brāhmaṇas with them, in accordance with role.
iast: piṇḍebhyastvalpikāṃ mātrāṃ samādāyānupūrvaśaḥ piṇḍebhyaḥ svalpikāṃ tāneva viprānāsīnān vidhivat pūrvamāśayet

sanskrit_iast: dhriyamāṇe tu pitari pūrveṣāmeva nirvapet vipravadvā 'pi taṃ śrāddhe svakaṃ pitaramāśayet śrāddhaṃ
meaning: ध्रियमाणे तु पितरि पूर्वेषामेव निर्वपेत् विप्रवद्वा 'पि तं श्राद्धे स्वकं पितरमाशयेत् श्राद्धं
text: While his father holds, one should make the offering to the previous ancestors; or, he may feed his own father at the Śrāddha as a Brāhmana.
iast: dhriyamāṇe tu pitari pūrveṣāmeva nirvapet vipravadvā 'pi taṃ śrāddhe svakaṃ pitaramāśayet śrāddhaṃ

sanskrit_iast: pitā yasya nivṛttaḥ syāj jīveccāpi pitāmahaḥ pitā yasya tu vṛttaḥ syāj pituḥ sa nāma saṅkīrtya kīrtayet prapitāmaham
meaning: पिता यस्य निवृत्तः स्याज् जीवेच्चापि पितामहः पिता यस्य तु वृत्तः स्याज् पितुः स नाम सङ्कीर्त्य कीर्तयेत् प्रपितामहम्
text: He whose father is dead, but grandfather is living, should mention the great-grandfather after having pronounced the name of the father.
iast: pitā yasya nivṛttaḥ syāj jīveccāpi pitāmahaḥ pitā yasya tu vṛttaḥ syāj pituḥ sa nāma saṅkīrtya kīrtayet prapitāmaham

sanskrit_iast: pitāmaho vā tatśrāddhaṃ bhuñjītaityabravīn manuḥ kāmaṃ vā samanujñātaḥ svayameva samācaret
meaning: पितामहो वा तत्श्राद्धं भुञ्जीतैत्यब्रवीन् मनुः कामं वा समनुज्ञातः स्वयमेव समाचरेत्
text: Manu has declared that either the grandfather may eat at that Śrāddha, or the man himself may, according to his will, perform it, after being permitted by him.
iast: pitāmaho vā tatśrāddhaṃ bhuñjītaityabravīn manuḥ kāmaṃ vā samanujñātaḥ svayameva samācaret

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ dattvā tu hasteṣu sapavitraṃ tilaudakam tatpiṇḍāgraṃ prayaccheta svadhaiṣāmastviti bruvan prayacchet tu
meaning: तेषां दत्त्वा तु हस्तेषु सपवित्रं तिलौदकम् तत्पिण्डाग्रं प्रयच्छेत स्वधैषामस्त्विति ब्रुवन् प्रयच्छेत् तु
text: Having poured into their hands water and sesamum along with kuśa blades, he should offer the top portion of the ball, saying “May this be svadhā to these.”
iast: teṣāṃ dattvā tu hasteṣu sapavitraṃ tilaudakam tatpiṇḍāgraṃ prayaccheta svadhaiṣāmastviti bruvan prayacchet tu

sanskrit_iast: pāṇibhyāṃ tūpasaṅgṛhya svayamannasya vardhitam varddhitam viprāntike pitr̥̄n dhyāyan śanakairupanikṣipet
meaning: पाणिभ्यां तूपसङ्गृह्य स्वयमन्नस्य वर्धितम् वर्द्धितम् विप्रान्तिके पित्र्̥̄न् ध्यायन् शनकैरुपनिक्षिपेत्
text: Taking up with his hands the supply of food, he shall himself gently place it near the Brāhmaṇas, thinking of his Pitṛs (all the time).
iast: pāṇibhyāṃ tūpasaṅgṛhya svayamannasya vardhitam varddhitam viprāntike pitr̥̄n dhyāyan śanakairupanikṣipet

sanskrit_iast: ubhayorhastayormuktaṃ yadannamupanīyate tadvipralumpantyasurāḥ sahasā duṣṭacetasaḥ
meaning: उभयोर्हस्तयोर्मुक्तं यदन्नमुपनीयते तद्विप्रलुम्पन्त्यसुराः सहसा दुष्टचेतसः
text: The wicked-minded demons forcibly destroy that food which is abandoned by both hands.
iast: ubhayorhastayormuktaṃ yadannamupanīyate tadvipralumpantyasurāḥ sahasā duṣṭacetasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: guṇāṃśca sūpaśākādyān payo dadhi ghṛtaṃ madhu vinyaset prayataḥ pūrvaṃ bhūmāveva samāhitaḥ
meaning: गुणांश्च सूपशाकाद्यान् पयो दधि घृतं मधु विन्यसेत् प्रयतः पूर्वं भूमावेव समाहितः
text: Pure and with collected mind, he shall first place on the ground the accessories, such as soups and vegetaui.es, &c., milk, curd, butter and honey.
iast: guṇāṃśca sūpaśākādyān payo dadhi ghṛtaṃ madhu vinyaset prayataḥ pūrvaṃ bhūmāveva samāhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhakṣyaṃ bhojyaṃ ca vividhaṃ mūlāni ca phalāni ca hṛdyāni caiva māṃsāni pānāni surabhīṇi ca
meaning: भक्ष्यं भोज्यं च विविधं मूलानि च फलानि च हृद्यानि चैव मांसानि पानानि सुरभीणि च
text: Also hard food and soft food of various kinds, roots, fruits, savoury meat and sweet-smelling drinks.
iast: bhakṣyaṃ bhojyaṃ ca vividhaṃ mūlāni ca phalāni ca hṛdyāni caiva māṃsāni pānāni surabhīṇi ca

sanskrit_iast: upanīya tu tat sarvaṃ śanakaiḥ susamāhitaḥ pariveṣayeta prayato guṇān sarvān pracodayan
meaning: उपनीय तु तत् सर्वं शनकैः सुसमाहितः परिवेषयेत प्रयतो गुणान् सर्वान् प्रचोदयन्
text: Having gently brought up all this, he shall, with collected mind and pure, serve it,—describing all its qualities.
iast: upanīya tu tat sarvaṃ śanakaiḥ susamāhitaḥ pariveṣayeta prayato guṇān sarvān pracodayan

sanskrit_iast: nāsramāpātayej jātu na kupyennānṛtaṃ vadet na pādena spṛśedannaṃ na caitadavadhūnayet
meaning: नास्रमापातयेज् जातु न कुप्येन्नानृतं वदेत् न पादेन स्पृशेदन्नं न चैतदवधूनयेत्
text: Never should he shed tears; nor should he become angry; nor tell a lie. He should not touch the food with his foot, nob shake it.
iast: nāsramāpātayej jātu na kupyennānṛtaṃ vadet na pādena spṛśedannaṃ na caitadavadhūnayet

sanskrit_iast: asraṃ gamayati pretān kopo 'rīnanṛtaṃ śunaḥ pādasparśastu rakṣāṃsi duṣkṛtīnavadhūnanam
meaning: अस्रं गमयति प्रेतान् कोपो 'रीननृतं शुनः पादस्पर्शस्तु रक्षांसि दुष्कृतीनवधूननम्
text: Tears make the food go to ghosts, anger to enemies, lie to dogs, touching with the foot to demons, and shaking to sinners.
iast: asraṃ gamayati pretān kopo 'rīnanṛtaṃ śunaḥ pādasparśastu rakṣāṃsi duṣkṛtīnavadhūnanam

sanskrit_iast: yadyadroceta viprebhyastat taddadyādamatsaraḥ brahmodyāśca kathāḥ kuryāt pitr̥̄ṇāmetadīpsitam
meaning: यद्यद्रोचेत विप्रेभ्यस्तत् तद्दद्यादमत्सरः ब्रह्मोद्याश्च कथाः कुर्यात् पित्र्̥̄णामेतदीप्सितम्
text: Whatever may be agreeable to the Brāhmaṇas, that he shall give ungrudgingly. He shall relate stories told in the Veda; as this is liked by the Pitṛs.
iast: yadyadroceta viprebhyastat taddadyādamatsaraḥ brahmodyāśca kathāḥ kuryāt pitr̥̄ṇāmetadīpsitam

sanskrit_iast: svādhyāyaṃ śrāvayet pitrye dharmaśāstrāṇi caiva hi ākhyānānītihāsāṃśca purāṇāni khilāni ca
meaning: स्वाध्यायं श्रावयेत् पित्र्ये धर्मशास्त्राणि चैव हि आख्यानानीतिहासांश्च पुराणानि खिलानि च
text: At the rite in honour of the Pitṛs, one should recite the Vedic text, Legal Institutes, Stories, Histories, Legends and supplementary Texts.
iast: svādhyāyaṃ śrāvayet pitrye dharmaśāstrāṇi caiva hi ākhyānānītihāsāṃśca purāṇāni khilāni ca

sanskrit_iast: harṣayedbrāhmaṇāṃstuṣṭo bhojayecca śanaisśanaiḥ annādyenāsakṛccaitān guṇaiśca paricodayet
meaning: हर्षयेद्ब्राह्मणांस्तुष्टो भोजयेच्च शनैस्शनैः अन्नाद्येनासकृच्चैतान् गुणैश्च परिचोदयेत्
text: Being happy himself, he shall bring delight to the Brāhmaṇas; he shall feed them, gently and slowly, with dishes, and urge them repeatedly by means of seasonings.
iast: harṣayedbrāhmaṇāṃstuṣṭo bhojayecca śanaisśanaiḥ annādyenāsakṛccaitān guṇaiśca paricodayet

sanskrit_iast: vratasthamapi dauhitraṃ śrāddhe yatnena bhojayet kutapaṃ cāsanaṃ dadyāt tilaiśca vikiren mahīm
meaning: व्रतस्थमपि दौहित्रं श्राद्धे यत्नेन भोजयेत् कुतपं चासनं दद्यात् तिलैश्च विकिरेन् महीम्
text: At a śrāddha he should, with special care, feed the daughter’s son, even though he be still under vows (of studentship). He should offer the blanket as seat and scatter sesamum on the ground.
iast: vratasthamapi dauhitraṃ śrāddhe yatnena bhojayet kutapaṃ cāsanaṃ dadyāt tilaiśca vikiren mahīm

sanskrit_iast: trīṇi śrāddhe pavitrāṇi dauhitraḥ kutapastilāḥ trīṇi cātra praśaṃsanti śaucamakrodhamatvarām
meaning: त्रीणि श्राद्धे पवित्राणि दौहित्रः कुतपस्तिलाः त्रीणि चात्र प्रशंसन्ति शौचमक्रोधमत्वराम्
text: Three things are sacred at Śrāddhas—the daughter’s son, blanket and sesamum, and they commend three things in this connection—cleanliness, absence of anger and absence of haste.
iast: trīṇi śrāddhe pavitrāṇi dauhitraḥ kutapastilāḥ trīṇi cātra praśaṃsanti śaucamakrodhamatvarām

sanskrit_iast: atyuṣṇaṃ sarvamannaṃ syādbhuñjīraṃste ca vāgyatāḥ na ca dvijātayo brūyurdātrā pṛṣṭā havirguṇān
meaning: अत्युष्णं सर्वमन्नं स्याद्भुञ्जीरंस्ते च वाग्यताः न च द्विजातयो ब्रूयुर्दात्रा पृष्टा हविर्गुणान्
text: All the food should be very hot, and they should eat with controlled speech; when asked by the giver, the Brāhmaṇas should not describe the qualities of the sacrificial food.
iast: atyuṣṇaṃ sarvamannaṃ syādbhuñjīraṃste ca vāgyatāḥ na ca dvijātayo brūyurdātrā pṛṣṭā havirguṇān

sanskrit_iast: yāvaduṣmā bhavatyannaṃ yāvadaśnanti vāgyatāḥ pitarastāvadaśnanti yāvatnauktā havirguṇāḥ
meaning: यावदुष्मा भवत्यन्नं यावदश्नन्ति वाग्यताः पितरस्तावदश्नन्ति यावत्नौक्ता हविर्गुणाः
text: As long as the food is steaming, as long as they eat with speech controlled, so long do the Pitṛs eat, as long as the qualities of the food are not described.
iast: yāvaduṣmā bhavatyannaṃ yāvadaśnanti vāgyatāḥ pitarastāvadaśnanti yāvatnauktā havirguṇāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadveṣṭitaśirā bhuṅkte yadbhuṅkte dakṣiṇāmukhaḥ saupānatkaśca yadbhuṅkte tadvai rakṣāṃsi bhuñjate
meaning: यद्वेष्टितशिरा भुङ्क्ते यद्भुङ्क्ते दक्षिणामुखः सौपानत्कश्च यद्भुङ्क्ते तद्वै रक्षांसि भुञ्जते
text: What the guest eats with his head wound up, what he eats with his face towards the south, what he eats with shoes on, all this verily the demons eat.
iast: yadveṣṭitaśirā bhuṅkte yadbhuṅkte dakṣiṇāmukhaḥ saupānatkaśca yadbhuṅkte tadvai rakṣāṃsi bhuñjate

sanskrit_iast: cāṇḍālaśca varāhaśca kukkuṭaḥ śvā tathaiva ca rajasvalā ca ṣaṇḍhaśca naikṣerannaśnato dvijān
meaning: चाण्डालश्च वराहश्च कुक्कुटः श्वा तथैव च रजस्वला च षण्ढश्च नैक्षेरन्नश्नतो द्विजान्
text: The Cāṇdāla, the pig, the cock, as also the dog, the unclean woman and the eunuch should not look at the Brāhmaṇas while eating.
iast: cāṇḍālaśca varāhaśca kukkuṭaḥ śvā tathaiva ca rajasvalā ca ṣaṇḍhaśca naikṣerannaśnato dvijān

sanskrit_iast: home pradāne bhojye ca yadebhirabhivīkṣyate daive haviṣi pitrye vā tadgacchatyayathātatham
meaning: होमे प्रदाने भोज्ये च यदेभिरभिवीक्ष्यते दैवे हविषि पित्र्ये वा तद्गच्छत्ययथातथम्
text: At the offering into Fire, at gifts, at feeding, or at any rite in honour of the Gods or of the Pitṛs,—whatever is seen by these goes wrong.
iast: home pradāne bhojye ca yadebhirabhivīkṣyate daive haviṣi pitrye vā tadgacchatyayathātatham

sanskrit_iast: ghrāṇena sūkaro hanti pakṣavātena kukkuṭaḥ śūkaro śvā tu dṛṣṭinipātena sparśeṇāvaravarṇajaḥ
meaning: घ्राणेन सूकरो हन्ति पक्षवातेन कुक्कुटः शूकरो श्वा तु दृष्टिनिपातेन स्पर्शेणावरवर्णजः
text: The pig defiles by sniffing, the cock by wind raised by its wings, the dog by casting his eye, and the man born of the low caste by touch.
iast: ghrāṇena sūkaro hanti pakṣavātena kukkuṭaḥ śūkaro śvā tu dṛṣṭinipātena sparśeṇāvaravarṇajaḥ

sanskrit_iast: khañjo vā yadi vā kāṇo dātuḥ preṣyo 'pi vā bhavet hīnātiriktagātro vā tamapyapanayet punaḥ
meaning: खञ्जो वा यदि वा काणो दातुः प्रेष्यो 'पि वा भवेत् हीनातिरिक्तगात्रो वा तमप्यपनयेत् पुनः
text: The cripple, or the one-eyed man, the man without a limb, or the man with a redundant limb,—even if he be the offerer’s servant—he should remove from there.
iast: khañjo vā yadi vā kāṇo dātuḥ preṣyo 'pi vā bhavet hīnātiriktagātro vā tamapyapanayet punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇaṃ bhikṣukaṃ vā 'pi bhojanārthamupasthitam brāhmaṇairabhyanujñātaḥ śaktitaḥ pratipūjayet
meaning: ब्राह्मणं भिक्षुकं वा 'पि भोजनार्थमुपस्थितम् ब्राह्मणैरभ्यनुज्ञातः शक्तितः प्रतिपूजयेत्
text: The Brāhmaṇa or the mendicant who comes seeking for food, he shall entertain, to the best of his ability, on being permitted by the Brāhmaṇas.
iast: brāhmaṇaṃ bhikṣukaṃ vā 'pi bhojanārthamupasthitam brāhmaṇairabhyanujñātaḥ śaktitaḥ pratipūjayet

sanskrit_iast: sārvavarṇikamannādyaṃ saṃnīyāplāvya vāriṇā samutsṛjedbhuktavatāmagrato vikiran bhuvi
meaning: सार्ववर्णिकमन्नाद्यं संनीयाप्लाव्य वारिणा समुत्सृजेद्भुक्तवतामग्रतो विकिरन् भुवि
text: Having mixed up the food of all kinds and wetted it with water, he should throw it before the Brāhmaṇas who have eaten, scattering it on the ground.
iast: sārvavarṇikamannādyaṃ saṃnīyāplāvya vāriṇā samutsṛjedbhuktavatāmagrato vikiran bhuvi

sanskrit_iast: asaṃskṛtapramītānāṃ tyāgināṃ kulayoṣitām ucchiṣṭaṃ bhāgadheyaṃ syāddarbheṣu vikiraśca yaḥ
meaning: असंस्कृतप्रमीतानां त्यागिनां कुलयोषिताम् उच्छिष्टं भागधेयं स्याद्दर्भेषु विकिरश्च यः
text: The remnant and that which has been scattered on the Kuśa grass form the share of those who have died without sacraments, and of those who have abandoned family ladies.
iast: asaṃskṛtapramītānāṃ tyāgināṃ kulayoṣitām ucchiṣṭaṃ bhāgadheyaṃ syāddarbheṣu vikiraśca yaḥ

sanskrit_iast: uccheṣaṇāṃ bhūmigatamajihmasyāśaṭhasya ca dāsavargasya tat pitrye bhāgadheyaṃ pracakṣate
meaning: उच्छेषणां भूमिगतमजिह्मस्याशठस्य च दासवर्गस्य तत् पित्र्ये भागधेयं प्रचक्षते
text: At the rite in honour of the Pitṛs, the remnant fallen on the ground is regarded as the share of straightforward, dutiful servants.
iast: uccheṣaṇāṃ bhūmigatamajihmasyāśaṭhasya ca dāsavargasya tat pitrye bhāgadheyaṃ pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: āsapiṇḍakriyākarma dvijāteḥ saṃsthitasya tu adaivaṃ bhojayetśrāddhaṃ piṇḍamekaṃ ca nirvapet
meaning: आसपिण्डक्रियाकर्म द्विजातेः संस्थितस्य तु अदैवं भोजयेत्श्राद्धं पिण्डमेकं च निर्वपेत्
text: For the twice-born person just dead, there should be (performed) the rite up to the ‘Sapiṇḍīkaraṇa’: one should do the feeding at his Śrāddha without any in honour of the gods, and he shall offer one ball.
iast: āsapiṇḍakriyākarma dvijāteḥ saṃsthitasya tu adaivaṃ bhojayetśrāddhaṃ piṇḍamekaṃ ca nirvapet

sanskrit_iast: sahapiṇḍakriyāyāṃ tu kṛtāyāmasya dharmataḥ anayaivāvṛtā kāryaṃ piṇḍanirvapanaṃ sutaiḥ
meaning: सहपिण्डक्रियायां तु कृतायामस्य धर्मतः अनयैवावृता कार्यं पिण्डनिर्वपनं सुतैः
text: But after the Amalgamating Rite has been duly performed, the offering of balls shall be done by the sons, by this same method.
iast: sahapiṇḍakriyāyāṃ tu kṛtāyāmasya dharmataḥ anayaivāvṛtā kāryaṃ piṇḍanirvapanaṃ sutaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: śrāddhaṃ bhuktvā ya ucchiṣṭaṃ vṛṣalāya prayacchati sa mūḍho narakaṃ yāti kālasūtramavākṣirāḥ
meaning: श्राद्धं भुक्त्वा य उच्छिष्टं वृषलाय प्रयच्छति स मूढो नरकं याति कालसूत्रमवाक्षिराः
text: He who, having eaten at a śrāddha, gives the leavings to a śūdra,—this foolish man falls headlong into the Kālasūtra hell.
iast: śrāddhaṃ bhuktvā ya ucchiṣṭaṃ vṛṣalāya prayacchati sa mūḍho narakaṃ yāti kālasūtramavākṣirāḥ

sanskrit_iast: śrāddhabhug vṛṣalītalpaṃ tadaharyo 'dhigacchati tasyāḥ purīṣe taṃ māsaṃ pitarastasya śerate
meaning: श्राद्धभुग् वृषलीतल्पं तदहर्यो 'धिगच्छति तस्याः पुरीषे तं मासं पितरस्तस्य शेरते
text: Having eaten at a Śrāddha, if one enters the bed of a woman on that day, his ancestors lie in her ordure for the whole of that month.
iast: śrāddhabhug vṛṣalītalpaṃ tadaharyo 'dhigacchati tasyāḥ purīṣe taṃ māsaṃ pitarastasya śerate

sanskrit_iast: pṛṣṭvā svaditamityevaṃ tṛptānācāmayet tataḥ ācāntāṃścānujānīyādabhito ramyatāmiti
meaning: पृष्ट्वा स्वदितमित्येवं तृप्तानाचामयेत् ततः आचान्तांश्चानुजानीयादभितो रम्यतामिति
text: Having asked—“Have you dined well?”—he shall, after they have been fully satisfied, make them wash; and when they have washed, he shall say—“You may rest where you choose.
iast: pṛṣṭvā svaditamityevaṃ tṛptānācāmayet tataḥ ācāntāṃścānujānīyādabhito ramyatāmiti

sanskrit_iast: svadhā 'stvityeva taṃ brūyurbrāhmaṇāstadanantaram svadhākāraḥ parā hyāṣīḥ sarveṣu pitṛkarmasu
meaning: स्वधा 'स्त्वित्येव तं ब्रूयुर्ब्राह्मणास्तदनन्तरम् स्वधाकारः परा ह्याषीः सर्वेषु पितृकर्मसु
text: The Brāhmaṇas should thereupon tell him—“May there be svadhā.” at all rites performed in honour of the Pitṛs, the syllable “Svadhā” constitutes the highest benediction.
iast: svadhā 'stvityeva taṃ brūyurbrāhmaṇāstadanantaram svadhākāraḥ parā hyāṣīḥ sarveṣu pitṛkarmasu

sanskrit_iast: tato bhuktavatāṃ teṣāmannaśeṣaṃ nivedayet yathā brūyustathā kuryādanujñātastato dvijaiḥ
meaning: ततो भुक्तवतां तेषामन्नशेषं निवेदयेत् यथा ब्रूयुस्तथा कुर्यादनुज्ञातस्ततो द्विजैः
text: He shall then inform them of the food that may be left after they have eaten; being permitted by the Brāhmaṇas he shall do as they tell him.
iast: tato bhuktavatāṃ teṣāmannaśeṣaṃ nivedayet yathā brūyustathā kuryādanujñātastato dvijaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: pitrye svaditamityeva vācyaṃ goṣṭhe tu suśṛtam saṃpannamityabhyudaye daive rucitamityapi saṃpannam
meaning: पित्र्ये स्वदितमित्येव वाच्यं गोष्ठे तु सुशृतम् संपन्नमित्यभ्युदये दैवे रुचितमित्यपि संपन्नम्
text: At the rite in honour of the Pitṛs, one should say “svaditam” (well-dined); at the Goṣṭha, “suśṛtam” (well-cooked); at the Ābhyudayika rite, “sampannam” (accomplished); and at the rite in honour of the gods, “rucitam” (agreeable).
iast: pitrye svaditamityeva vācyaṃ goṣṭhe tu suśṛtam saṃpannamityabhyudaye daive rucitamityapi saṃpannam

sanskrit_iast: aparāhṇastathā darbhā vāstusaṃpādanaṃ tilāḥ saṃpādanaṃ sṛṣṭirmṛṣṭirdvijāścāgryāḥ śrāddhakarmasu saṃpadaḥ
meaning: अपराह्णस्तथा दर्भा वास्तुसंपादनं तिलाः संपादनं सृष्टिर्मृष्टिर्द्विजाश्चाग्र्याः श्राद्धकर्मसु संपदः
text: The afternoon, Kuśa-Grass, setting up of the dwelling, sesamum grains, liberality, cleaning and superior Brāhmaṇas;—these are the essentials of Śrāddha-rites.
iast: aparāhṇastathā darbhā vāstusaṃpādanaṃ tilāḥ saṃpādanaṃ sṛṣṭirmṛṣṭirdvijāścāgryāḥ śrāddhakarmasu saṃpadaḥ

sanskrit_iast: darbhāḥ pavitraṃ pūrvāhṇo haviṣyāṇi ca sarvaśaḥ pavitraṃ yacca pūrvauktaṃ vijñeyā havyasaṃpadaḥ
meaning: दर्भाः पवित्रं पूर्वाह्णो हविष्याणि च सर्वशः पवित्रं यच्च पूर्वौक्तं विज्ञेया हव्यसंपदः
text: Kuśa-Grass, the sanctificatory texts, the forenoon, all kinds of sacrificial food, purity and also the afore-mentioned these should be regarded as the essentials of a sacrifice.
iast: darbhāḥ pavitraṃ pūrvāhṇo haviṣyāṇi ca sarvaśaḥ pavitraṃ yacca pūrvauktaṃ vijñeyā havyasaṃpadaḥ

sanskrit_iast: muniannāni payaḥ somo māṃsaṃ yaccānupaskṛtam aksāralavaṇaṃ caiva prakṛtyā havirucyate
meaning: मुनिअन्नानि पयः सोमो मांसं यच्चानुपस्कृतम् अक्सारलवणं चैव प्रकृत्या हविरुच्यते
text: The food of hermits, milk, the soma-herb, meat that is not forbidden, salt other than alkaline. are, by nature, called “sacrificial food.”
iast: muniannāni payaḥ somo māṃsaṃ yaccānupaskṛtam aksāralavaṇaṃ caiva prakṛtyā havirucyate

sanskrit_iast: visṛjya brāhmaṇāṃstāṃstu niyato vāgyataḥ śuciḥ visarjya brāhmanāṃstāṃstu prayato vidhipūrvakam dakṣiṇāṃ diśamākāṅkṣan yācetaimān varān pitr̥̄n
meaning: विसृज्य ब्राह्मणांस्तांस्तु नियतो वाग्यतः शुचिः विसर्ज्य ब्राह्मनांस्तांस्तु प्रयतो विधिपूर्वकम् दक्षिणां दिशमाकाङ्क्षन् याचेतैमान् वरान् पित्र्̥̄न्
text: Having dismissed those Brāhmaṇas, he, with collected mind, controlled in speech and pure, turning towards the southern quarter, shall beg these boons of his ancestors.
iast: visṛjya brāhmaṇāṃstāṃstu niyato vāgyataḥ śuciḥ visarjya brāhmanāṃstāṃstu prayato vidhipūrvakam dakṣiṇāṃ diśamākāṅkṣan yācetaimān varān pitr̥̄n

sanskrit_iast: dātāro no 'bhivardhantāṃ vedāḥ saṃtatireva ca śraddhā ca no mā vyagamadbahudeyaṃ ca no 'stviti
meaning: दातारो नो 'भिवर्धन्तां वेदाः संततिरेव च श्रद्धा च नो मा व्यगमद्बहुदेयं च नो 'स्त्विति
text: ‘May our benefactors prosper! As also the Vedas and our progeny! May our faith never waver! May there be much for us to give away!’
iast: dātāro no 'bhivardhantāṃ vedāḥ saṃtatireva ca śraddhā ca no mā vyagamadbahudeyaṃ ca no 'stviti

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ nirvapaṇaṃ kṛtvā piṇḍāṃstāṃstadanantaram gāṃ vipramajamagniṃ vā prāśayedapsu vā kṣipet
meaning: एवं निर्वपणं कृत्वा पिण्डांस्तांस्तदनन्तरम् गां विप्रमजमग्निं वा प्राशयेदप्सु वा क्षिपेत्
text: Having made the offering thus, he should, after this, either make the cow or the Brāhmaṇa or the goat or the fire to eat the balls, or throw them into water.
iast: evaṃ nirvapaṇaṃ kṛtvā piṇḍāṃstāṃstadanantaram gāṃ vipramajamagniṃ vā prāśayedapsu vā kṣipet

sanskrit_iast: piṇḍanirvapaṇaṃ ke cit parastādeva kurvate vayobhiḥ khādayantyanye prakṣipantyanale 'psu vā
meaning: पिण्डनिर्वपणं के चित् परस्तादेव कुर्वते वयोभिः खादयन्त्यन्ये प्रक्षिपन्त्यनले 'प्सु वा
text: Some people do the ball-offering afterwards, others cause them to be eaten by birds, or throw them into fire or water.
iast: piṇḍanirvapaṇaṃ ke cit parastādeva kurvate vayobhiḥ khādayantyanye prakṣipantyanale 'psu vā

sanskrit_iast: pativratā dharmapatnī pitṛpūjanatatparā madhyamaṃ tu tataḥ piṇḍamadyāt samyak sutārthinī
meaning: पतिव्रता धर्मपत्नी पितृपूजनतत्परा मध्यमं तु ततः पिण्डमद्यात् सम्यक् सुतार्थिनी
text: The lawful wife, devoted to her husband and intent upon the worshipping of the Pitṛs, should, if desirous of a son, eat in the proper manner, the middlemost ball.
iast: pativratā dharmapatnī pitṛpūjanatatparā madhyamaṃ tu tataḥ piṇḍamadyāt samyak sutārthinī

sanskrit_iast: āyuṣmantaṃ sutaṃ sūte yaśomedhāsamanvitam dhanavantaṃ prajāvantaṃ sāttvikaṃ dhārmikaṃ tathā
meaning: आयुष्मन्तं सुतं सूते यशोमेधासमन्वितम् धनवन्तं प्रजावन्तं सात्त्विकं धार्मिकं तथा
text: She brings forth a long-lived son, endowed with fame and intelligence, wealthy, with numerous offspring, good and righteous.
iast: āyuṣmantaṃ sutaṃ sūte yaśomedhāsamanvitam dhanavantaṃ prajāvantaṃ sāttvikaṃ dhārmikaṃ tathā

sanskrit_iast: praksālya hastāvācāmya jñātiprāyaṃ prakalpayet jñātibhyaḥ satkṛtaṃ dattvā bāndhavānapi bhojayet datvā
meaning: प्रक्साल्य हस्तावाचाम्य ज्ञातिप्रायं प्रकल्पयेत् ज्ञातिभ्यः सत्कृतं दत्त्वा बान्धवानपि भोजयेत् दत्वा
text: Having washed the hands and sipped water, he should make offerings to his paternal relations; and having treated his paternal relations well, he should feed his other relations also.
iast: praksālya hastāvācāmya jñātiprāyaṃ prakalpayet jñātibhyaḥ satkṛtaṃ dattvā bāndhavānapi bhojayet datvā

sanskrit_iast: uccheṣaṇaṃ tu tat tiṣṭhedyāvadviprā visarjitāḥ yat tiṣṭhed tato gṛhabaliṃ kuryāditi dharmo vyavasthitaḥ
meaning: उच्छेषणं तु तत् तिष्ठेद्यावद्विप्रा विसर्जिताः यत् तिष्ठेद् ततो गृहबलिं कुर्यादिति धर्मो व्यवस्थितः
text: The leavings shall remain until the Brāhmaṇas have been dismissed. After that, he should make the domestic offerings. Such is the established law.
iast: uccheṣaṇaṃ tu tat tiṣṭhedyāvadviprā visarjitāḥ yat tiṣṭhed tato gṛhabaliṃ kuryāditi dharmo vyavasthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: haviryaccirarātrāya yaccānantyāya kalpate pitṛbhyo vidhivaddattaṃ tat pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ
meaning: हविर्यच्चिररात्राय यच्चानन्त्याय कल्पते पितृभ्यो विधिवद्दत्तं तत् प्रवक्ष्याम्यशेषतः
text: I shall now fully describe what offering-material, on being offered to the Pitṛs, according to role, serves for a long time, and for eternity.
iast: haviryaccirarātrāya yaccānantyāya kalpate pitṛbhyo vidhivaddattaṃ tat pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tilairvrīhiyavairmāṣairadbhirmūlaphalena vā dattena māsaṃ tṛpyanti vidhivat pitaro nṛnām
meaning: तिलैर्व्रीहियवैर्माषैरद्भिर्मूलफलेन वा दत्तेन मासं तृप्यन्ति विधिवत् पितरो नृनाम्
text: By the proper offering of sesamum, rice and barley, māṣa, water, roots and fruits, men’s ancestors are satisfied for one month.
iast: tilairvrīhiyavairmāṣairadbhirmūlaphalena vā dattena māsaṃ tṛpyanti vidhivat pitaro nṛnām

sanskrit_iast: dvau māsau matsyamāṃsena trīn māsān hāriṇena tu aurabhreṇātha caturaḥ śākunenātha pañca vai
meaning: द्वौ मासौ मत्स्यमांसेन त्रीन् मासान् हारिणेन तु औरभ्रेणाथ चतुरः शाकुनेनाथ पञ्च वै
text: For two months by fish-meat; for three months by the meat of deer; for four by that of sheep, and for five by that of birds.
iast: dvau māsau matsyamāṃsena trīn māsān hāriṇena tu aurabhreṇātha caturaḥ śākunenātha pañca vai

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṇmāsāṃśchāgamāṃsena pārṣatena ca sapta vai aṣṭāvenasya māṃsena rauraveṇa navaiva tu 'iṇeyamāṃsena
meaning: षण्मासांश्छागमांसेन पार्षतेन च सप्त वै अष्टावेनस्य मांसेन रौरवेण नवैव तु 'इणेयमांसेन
text: For six months by the meat of goat; for seven by that of spotted deer; for eight by that of the black antelope, and for nine by that of the Ruru deer.
iast: ṣaṇmāsāṃśchāgamāṃsena pārṣatena ca sapta vai aṣṭāvenasya māṃsena rauraveṇa navaiva tu 'iṇeyamāṃsena

sanskrit_iast: daśamāsāṃstu tṛpyanti varāhamahiṣāmiṣaiḥ śaśakūrmayostu māṃsena māsānekādaśaiva tu
meaning: दशमासांस्तु तृप्यन्ति वराहमहिषामिषैः शशकूर्मयोस्तु मांसेन मासानेकादशैव तु
text: They are satisfied for ten months by the meat of boars and buffaloes; and for eleven months by the meat of the hare and the tortoise.’
iast: daśamāsāṃstu tṛpyanti varāhamahiṣāmiṣaiḥ śaśakūrmayostu māṃsena māsānekādaśaiva tu

sanskrit_iast: saṃvatsaraṃ tu gavyena payasā pāyasena ca saṃvatsare vārdhrīṇasasya māṃsena tṛptirdvādaśavārṣikī
meaning: संवत्सरं तु गव्येन पयसा पायसेन च संवत्सरे वार्ध्रीणसस्य मांसेन तृप्तिर्द्वादशवार्षिकी
text: For one year by cow’s milk and milk-preparations; and by the meat of old goat there is satisfaction lasting for twelve years.
iast: saṃvatsaraṃ tu gavyena payasā pāyasena ca saṃvatsare vārdhrīṇasasya māṃsena tṛptirdvādaśavārṣikī

sanskrit_iast: kālaśākaṃ mahāśalkāḥ khaṅgalohāmiṣaṃ madhu ānantyāyaiva kalpyante muniannāni ca sarvaśaḥ
meaning: कालशाकं महाशल्काः खङ्गलोहामिषं मधु आनन्त्यायैव कल्प्यन्ते मुनिअन्नानि च सर्वशः
text: The “Kālaśāka,” the porcupine, the meat of the rhinoceros and the red goat, and honey serve for endless time; as also all kinds ok her mit’s food.
iast: kālaśākaṃ mahāśalkāḥ khaṅgalohāmiṣaṃ madhu ānantyāyaiva kalpyante muniannāni ca sarvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yat kiṃ cin madhunā miśraṃ pradadyāt tu trayodaśīm tadapyakṣayameva syādvarṣāsu ca maghāsu ca
meaning: यत् किं चिन् मधुना मिश्रं प्रदद्यात् तु त्रयोदशीम् तदप्यक्षयमेव स्याद्वर्षासु च मघासु च
text: Whatever thing, mixed with honey, one might offer on the thirteenth day of the month, during the rains, under the asterism of Maghā,—that also would be imperishable.
iast: yat kiṃ cin madhunā miśraṃ pradadyāt tu trayodaśīm tadapyakṣayameva syādvarṣāsu ca maghāsu ca

sanskrit_iast: api naḥ sa kule bhūyādyo no dadyāt trayodaśīm pāyasaṃ madhusarpirbhyāṃ prāk chāye kuñjarasya ca
meaning: अपि नः स कुले भूयाद्यो नो दद्यात् त्रयोदशीम् पायसं मधुसर्पिर्भ्यां प्राक् छाये कुञ्जरस्य च
text: ‘May there be one in our family, who may offer milk-rice mixed with honey and butter on the thirteenth, and when the shadow of the elephant falls towards the east.’
iast: api naḥ sa kule bhūyādyo no dadyāt trayodaśīm pāyasaṃ madhusarpirbhyāṃ prāk chāye kuñjarasya ca

sanskrit_iast: yadyaddadāti vidhivat samyak śraddhāsamanvitaḥ tat tat pitr̥̄ṇāṃ bhavati paratrānantamakṣayam
meaning: यद्यद्ददाति विधिवत् सम्यक् श्रद्धासमन्वितः तत् तत् पित्र्̥̄णां भवति परत्रानन्तमक्षयम्
text: Whatever one endowed with faith, offers, according to rule and in the right manner,—that becomes endless and inexhaustible for the Pitṛs in the other world.
iast: yadyaddadāti vidhivat samyak śraddhāsamanvitaḥ tat tat pitr̥̄ṇāṃ bhavati paratrānantamakṣayam

sanskrit_iast: kṛṣṇapakṣe daśamyādau varjayitvā caturdaśīm śrāddhe praśastāstithayo yathaitā na tathaitarāḥ
meaning: कृष्णपक्षे दशम्यादौ वर्जयित्वा चतुर्दशीम् श्राद्धे प्रशस्तास्तिथयो यथैता न तथैतराः
text: ‘No dates are so commended for the offering of śrāddha as those of the dark fortnight, beginning with the tenth day, leaving out the fourteenth.
iast: kṛṣṇapakṣe daśamyādau varjayitvā caturdaśīm śrāddhe praśastāstithayo yathaitā na tathaitarāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yukṣu kurvan dinaṛkṣeṣu sarvān kāmān samaśnute ayukṣu tu pitr̥̄n sarvān prajāṃ prāpnoti puṣkalām
meaning: युक्षु कुर्वन् दिनऋक्षेषु सर्वान् कामान् समश्नुते अयुक्षु तु पित्र्̥̄न् सर्वान् प्रजां प्राप्नोति पुष्कलाम्
text: Performing (the śrāddha) on the even dates and under the even asterisms, one obtains all desires; and honouring the Pitṛs on the odd ones, he obtains prosperous offspring.
iast: yukṣu kurvan dinaṛkṣeṣu sarvān kāmān samaśnute ayukṣu tu pitr̥̄n sarvān prajāṃ prāpnoti puṣkalām

sanskrit_iast: yathā caivāparaḥ pakṣaḥ pūrvapakṣādviśiṣyate tathā śrāddhasya pūrvāhṇādaparāhṇo viśiṣyate
meaning: यथा चैवापरः पक्षः पूर्वपक्षाद्विशिष्यते तथा श्राद्धस्य पूर्वाह्णादपराह्णो विशिष्यते
text: Just as, for purposes of śrāddha, the latter half of the month is preferable to the former half, so also, the afternoon is preferable to the forenoon.
iast: yathā caivāparaḥ pakṣaḥ pūrvapakṣādviśiṣyate tathā śrāddhasya pūrvāhṇādaparāhṇo viśiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: prācīnāvītinā samyagapasavyamatandriṇā pitryamā nidhanāt kāryaṃ vidhivaddarbhapāṇinā
meaning: प्राचीनावीतिना सम्यगपसव्यमतन्द्रिणा पित्र्यमा निधनात् कार्यं विधिवद्दर्भपाणिना
text: Until death, one shall perform, with assiduity, the rite in honour of the Pitṛs, according to rule, with the sacred thread passing over the right shoulder, making offerings “from off the left” (to the right) with kuśa-grass in his hand.
iast: prācīnāvītinā samyagapasavyamatandriṇā pitryamā nidhanāt kāryaṃ vidhivaddarbhapāṇinā

sanskrit_iast: rātrau śrāddhaṃ na kurvīta rākṣasī kīrtitā hi sā saṃdhyayorubhayoścaiva sūrye caivāciraudite
meaning: रात्रौ श्राद्धं न कुर्वीत राक्षसी कीर्तिता हि सा संध्ययोरुभयोश्चैव सूर्ये चैवाचिरौदिते
text: One should not perform Śrāddha at night; for the night has been declared to be ‘fit for demons;’—nor at the two twilights, nor when the sun has just risen.
iast: rātrau śrāddhaṃ na kurvīta rākṣasī kīrtitā hi sā saṃdhyayorubhayoścaiva sūrye caivāciraudite

sanskrit_iast: anena vidhinā śrāddhaṃ trirabdasyaiha nirvapet hemantagrīṣmavarṣāsu pāñcayajñikamanvaham
meaning: अनेन विधिना श्राद्धं त्रिरब्दस्यैह निर्वपेत् हेमन्तग्रीष्मवर्षासु पाञ्चयज्ञिकमन्वहम्
text: In accordance with this rule, one should offer Śrāddha thrice in the year—during winter, summer and the rain; and that which forms part of the “five sacrifices” should be done every day.
iast: anena vidhinā śrāddhaṃ trirabdasyaiha nirvapet hemantagrīṣmavarṣāsu pāñcayajñikamanvaham

sanskrit_iast: na paitṛyajñiyo homo laukike 'gnau vidhīyate na darśena vinā śrāddhamāhitāgnerdvijanmanaḥ
meaning: न पैतृयज्ञियो होमो लौकिके 'ग्नौ विधीयते न दर्शेन विना श्राद्धमाहिताग्नेर्द्विजन्मनः
text: The oblation into fire made in connection with the rite in honour of the Pitṛs has not been prescribed as to be offered into the common fire; and for the Brāhmaṇa who has set up the fire, there is no Śrāddha apart from the Moonless day.
iast: na paitṛyajñiyo homo laukike 'gnau vidhīyate na darśena vinā śrāddhamāhitāgnerdvijanmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadeva tarpayatyadbhiḥ pitr̥̄n snātvā dvijottamaḥ tenaiva kṛtsnamāpnoti pitṛyajñakriyāphalam
meaning: यदेव तर्पयत्यद्भिः पित्र्̥̄न् स्नात्वा द्विजोत्तमः तेनैव कृत्स्नमाप्नोति पितृयज्ञक्रियाफलम्
text: When the best of Brāhmaṇas, having bathed, satisfies the Pitṛs with water,—by that alone he obtains the whole reward of the performance of the offering to the Pitṛs.
iast: yadeva tarpayatyadbhiḥ pitr̥̄n snātvā dvijottamaḥ tenaiva kṛtsnamāpnoti pitṛyajñakriyāphalam

sanskrit_iast: vasūn vadanti tu pitr̥̄n rudrāṃścaiva pitāmahān prapitāmahāṃstathā 'dityān śrutireṣā sanātanī
meaning: वसून् वदन्ति तु पित्र्̥̄न् रुद्रांश्चैव पितामहान् प्रपितामहांस्तथा 'दित्यान् श्रुतिरेषा सनातनी
text: They call the Fathers “Vasus;” the grandfathers they call “Rudras,” and the great-grandfathers they call “ādityas” such is the ancient text.
iast: vasūn vadanti tu pitr̥̄n rudrāṃścaiva pitāmahān prapitāmahāṃstathā 'dityān śrutireṣā sanātanī

sanskrit_iast: vighasāśī bhavennityaṃ nityaṃ vā 'mṛtabhojanaḥ vighaso bhuktaśeṣaṃ tu yajñaśeṣaṃ tathā 'mṛtam
meaning: विघसाशी भवेन्नित्यं नित्यं वा 'मृतभोजनः विघसो भुक्तशेषं तु यज्ञशेषं तथा 'मृतम्
text: One should daily live upon “vighasa,” and daily he should eat “amṛta.” “Vighasa” is that which is left by those who must be fed; and “amṛta” is the remnant of sacrific es.
iast: vighasāśī bhavennityaṃ nityaṃ vā 'mṛtabhojanaḥ vighaso bhuktaśeṣaṃ tu yajñaśeṣaṃ tathā 'mṛtam

sanskrit_iast: etadvo 'bhihitaṃ sarvaṃ vidhānaṃ pāñcayajñikam dvijātimukhyavṛttīnāṃ vidhānaṃ śrūyatāmiti
meaning: एतद्वो 'भिहितं सर्वं विधानं पाञ्चयज्ञिकम् द्विजातिमुख्यवृत्तीनां विधानं श्रूयतामिति
iast: etadvo 'bhihitaṃ sarvaṃ vidhānaṃ pāñcayajñikam dvijātimukhyavṛttīnāṃ vidhānaṃ śrūyatāmiti

sanskrit_iast: caturthamāyuṣo bhāgamuṣitvā 'dyaṃ gurau dvijāḥ dvitīyamāyuṣo bhāgaṃ kṛtadāro gṛhe vaset
meaning: चतुर्थमायुषो भागमुषित्वा 'द्यं गुरौ द्विजाः द्वितीयमायुषो भागं कृतदारो गृहे वसेत्
text: Having dwelt, during the first quarter of his life, with the Teacher, the Brāhmaṇa shall, during the second quarter of his life, live in his house, after having taken a wife.
iast: caturthamāyuṣo bhāgamuṣitvā 'dyaṃ gurau dvijāḥ dvitīyamāyuṣo bhāgaṃ kṛtadāro gṛhe vaset

sanskrit_iast: adroheṇaiva bhūtānāmalpadroheṇa vā punaḥ yā vṛttistāṃ samāsthāya vipro jīvedanāpadi
meaning: अद्रोहेणैव भूतानामल्पद्रोहेण वा पुनः या वृत्तिस्तां समास्थाय विप्रो जीवेदनापदि
text: [While living in the house], The Brāhmaṇa shall, in normal times, subsist by taking to that means of livelihood which involves no trouble—or very little trouble—to living beings.
iast: adroheṇaiva bhūtānāmalpadroheṇa vā punaḥ yā vṛttistāṃ samāsthāya vipro jīvedanāpadi

sanskrit_iast: yātrāmātraprasiddhiarthaṃ svaiḥ karmabhiragarhitaiḥ akleśena śarīrasya kurvīta dhanasañcayam
meaning: यात्रामात्रप्रसिद्धिअर्थं स्वैः कर्मभिरगर्हितैः अक्लेशेन शरीरस्य कुर्वीत धनसञ्चयम्
text: For the accomplishment of bare maintenance, one shall accumulate wealth by means of one’s own irreproachable occupations, without causing trouble to the body.
iast: yātrāmātraprasiddhiarthaṃ svaiḥ karmabhiragarhitaiḥ akleśena śarīrasya kurvīta dhanasañcayam

sanskrit_iast: ṛtāmṛtābhyāṃ jīvet tu mṛtena pramṛtena vā satyānṛtābhyāmapi vā na śvavṛttyā kadā cana
meaning: ऋतामृताभ्यां जीवेत् तु मृतेन प्रमृतेन वा सत्यानृताभ्यामपि वा न श्ववृत्त्या कदा चन
text: He shall live in “Truth” and “Nectar,” or by “Death” and “Super-death;” or, even by “Truth and Falsehood;” but never by the “living of the dog.”
iast: ṛtāmṛtābhyāṃ jīvet tu mṛtena pramṛtena vā satyānṛtābhyāmapi vā na śvavṛttyā kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: ṛtamuñchaśilaṃ jñeyamamṛtaṃ syādayācitam mṛtaṃ tu yācitaṃ bhaikṣaṃ pramṛtaṃ karṣaṇaṃ smṛtam
meaning: ऋतमुञ्छशिलं ज्ञेयममृतं स्यादयाचितम् मृतं तु याचितं भैक्षं प्रमृतं कर्षणं स्मृतम्
text: Gleaning and picking is to be known as “Truth;” and what is obtained unasked, “Nectar”; alms obtained by begging is “Death,” and cultivation is declared to be “Super- death.”
iast: ṛtamuñchaśilaṃ jñeyamamṛtaṃ syādayācitam mṛtaṃ tu yācitaṃ bhaikṣaṃ pramṛtaṃ karṣaṇaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: satyānṛtaṃ tu vāṇijyaṃ tena caivāpi jīvyate sevā śvavṛttirākhyātā tasmāt tāṃ parivarjayet
meaning: सत्यानृतं तु वाणिज्यं तेन चैवापि जीव्यते सेवा श्ववृत्तिराख्याता तस्मात् तां परिवर्जयेत्
text: Trade is “Truth and Falsehood;” even by that one may live. Service has been declared, to be the “living of dogs;” hence one should avoid it.
iast: satyānṛtaṃ tu vāṇijyaṃ tena caivāpi jīvyate sevā śvavṛttirākhyātā tasmāt tāṃ parivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: kusūladhānyako vā syāt kumbhīdhānyaka eva vā tryahehiko vā 'pi bhavedaśvastanika eva vā
meaning: कुसूलधान्यको वा स्यात् कुम्भीधान्यक एव वा त्र्यहेहिको वा 'पि भवेदश्वस्तनिक एव वा
text: He shall be either one possessing a granary full of grains, or one possessing a jar full of grains; he may be one possessing what is wanted for three days, or one who does not possess enough for the morrow.
iast: kusūladhānyako vā syāt kumbhīdhānyaka eva vā tryahehiko vā 'pi bhavedaśvastanika eva vā

sanskrit_iast: caturṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ dvijānāṃ gṛhamedhinām jyāyān paraḥ paro jñeyo dharmato lokajittamaḥ
meaning: चतुर्णामपि चैतेषां द्विजानां गृहमेधिनाम् ज्यायान् परः परो ज्ञेयो धर्मतो लोकजित्तमः
text: Among these four Brāhmaṇa-householders, each later should be regarded as superior, and a superior winner of worlds by virtue of his merit.
iast: caturṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ dvijānāṃ gṛhamedhinām jyāyān paraḥ paro jñeyo dharmato lokajittamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṭkarmaiko bhavatyeṣāṃ tribhiranyaḥ pravartate dvābhyāmekaścaturthastu brahmasattreṇa jīvati
meaning: षट्कर्मैको भवत्येषां त्रिभिरन्यः प्रवर्तते द्वाभ्यामेकश्चतुर्थस्तु ब्रह्मसत्त्रेण जीवति
text: From among these one follows the six occupations; another lives by three; one again with two; while the fourth lives by “Brahmasattra.’
iast: ṣaṭkarmaiko bhavatyeṣāṃ tribhiranyaḥ pravartate dvābhyāmekaścaturthastu brahmasattreṇa jīvati

sanskrit_iast: vartayaṃśca śilauñchābhyāmagnihotraparāyaṇaḥ iṣṭīḥ pārvāyaṇāntīyāḥ kevalā nirvapet sadā
meaning: वर्तयंश्च शिलौञ्छाभ्यामग्निहोत्रपरायणः इष्टीः पार्वायणान्तीयाः केवला निर्वपेत् सदा
text: ‘Living by gleanings and pickings, intent upon the performance of Agnihotra, one should constantly offer only those Iṣṭi-sacrificer that pertain to the moonless and full-moon days and to the solstices.—(10);
iast: vartayaṃśca śilauñchābhyāmagnihotraparāyaṇaḥ iṣṭīḥ pārvāyaṇāntīyāḥ kevalā nirvapet sadā

sanskrit_iast: na lokavṛttaṃ varteta vṛttihetoḥ kathaṃ cana ajihmāmaśathāṃ śuddhāṃ jīvedbrāhmaṇajīvikām
meaning: न लोकवृत्तं वर्तेत वृत्तिहेतोः कथं चन अजिह्मामशथां शुद्धां जीवेद्ब्राह्मणजीविकाम्
text: He shall never follow the worldly way, for the sake of subsistence; he shall live the straightforward, sincere and pure life of the Brāhmaṇa.
iast: na lokavṛttaṃ varteta vṛttihetoḥ kathaṃ cana ajihmāmaśathāṃ śuddhāṃ jīvedbrāhmaṇajīvikām

sanskrit_iast: saṃtoṣaṃ paramāsthāya sukhārthī saṃyato bhavet saṃtoṣamūlaṃ hi sukhaṃ duḥkhamūlaṃ viparyayaḥ
meaning: संतोषं परमास्थाय सुखार्थी संयतो भवेत् संतोषमूलं हि सुखं दुःखमूलं विपर्ययः
text: He who wants happiness should adopt perfect contentment and remain self-controlled. Happiness has its root in contentment, and its opposite is the root of unhappiness.
iast: saṃtoṣaṃ paramāsthāya sukhārthī saṃyato bhavet saṃtoṣamūlaṃ hi sukhaṃ duḥkhamūlaṃ viparyayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ato 'nyatamayā vṛttyā jīvaṃstu snātako dvijaḥ svargāyuṣyayaśasyāni vratāṇīmāni dhārayet svargyāyuṣya
meaning: अतो 'न्यतमया वृत्त्या जीवंस्तु स्नातको द्विजः स्वर्गायुष्ययशस्यानि व्रताणीमानि धारयेत् स्वर्ग्यायुष्य
text: The twice-born Accomplished Student, living by any one of these means of livelihood, should keep these (following) observances, which are conducive to heaven, longevity and fame.
iast: ato 'nyatamayā vṛttyā jīvaṃstu snātako dvijaḥ svargāyuṣyayaśasyāni vratāṇīmāni dhārayet svargyāyuṣya

sanskrit_iast: vedauditaṃ svakaṃ karma nityaṃ kuryādatandritaḥ taddhi kurvan yathāśakti prāpnoti paramāṃ gatim
meaning: वेदौदितं स्वकं कर्म नित्यं कुर्यादतन्द्रितः तद्धि कुर्वन् यथाशक्ति प्राप्नोति परमां गतिम्
text: He shall, without sloth, always perform his own duty as prescribed in the Veda. Performing that, to the best of his ability, he attains the highest state.
iast: vedauditaṃ svakaṃ karma nityaṃ kuryādatandritaḥ taddhi kurvan yathāśakti prāpnoti paramāṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: naihetārthān prasaṅgena na viruddhena karmaṇā na vidyamāneṣvartheṣu nārtyāmapi yatastataḥ na kalpamāneṣvartheṣu
meaning: नैहेतार्थान् प्रसङ्गेन न विरुद्धेन कर्मणा न विद्यमानेष्वर्थेषु नार्त्यामपि यतस्ततः न कल्पमानेष्वर्थेषु
text: He shall not seek wealth by clinging pursuits, or by contrary acts; nor when wealth is already there; nor from here and there, even in times of distress.
iast: naihetārthān prasaṅgena na viruddhena karmaṇā na vidyamāneṣvartheṣu nārtyāmapi yatastataḥ na kalpamāneṣvartheṣu

sanskrit_iast: indriyārtheṣu sarveṣu na prasajyeta kāmataḥ atiprasaktiṃ caiteṣāṃ manasā saṃnivartayet
meaning: इन्द्रियार्थेषु सर्वेषु न प्रसज्येत कामतः अतिप्रसक्तिं चैतेषां मनसा संनिवर्तयेत्
text: He shall not, through desires, become addicted to any sensual objects; excessive addiction to these, he shall avoid by mental reflection.
iast: indriyārtheṣu sarveṣu na prasajyeta kāmataḥ atiprasaktiṃ caiteṣāṃ manasā saṃnivartayet

sanskrit_iast: sarvān parityajedarthān svādhyāyasya virodhinaḥ yathā tathā 'dhyāpayaṃstu sā hyasya kṛtakṛtyatā
meaning: सर्वान् परित्यजेदर्थान् स्वाध्यायस्य विरोधिनः यथा तथा 'ध्यापयंस्तु सा ह्यस्य कृतकृत्यता
text: He shall relinquish all things impeding study, maintaining himself somehow. This is what constitutes the accomplish ment of his aims.
iast: sarvān parityajedarthān svādhyāyasya virodhinaḥ yathā tathā 'dhyāpayaṃstu sā hyasya kṛtakṛtyatā

sanskrit_iast: vayasaḥ karmaṇo 'rthasya śrutasyābhijanasya ca veṣavācbuddhisārūpyamācaran vicarediha
meaning: वयसः कर्मणो 'र्थस्य श्रुतस्याभिजनस्य च वेषवाच्बुद्धिसारूप्यमाचरन् विचरेदिह
text: He should wander about in this world, keeping his dress, speech and thoughts in conformity with his age, occupation, wealth, learning and family.
iast: vayasaḥ karmaṇo 'rthasya śrutasyābhijanasya ca veṣavācbuddhisārūpyamācaran vicarediha

sanskrit_iast: buddhivṛddhikarāṇyāśu dhanyāni ca hitāni ca nityaṃ śāstrāṇyavekṣeta nigamāṃścaiva vaidikān
meaning: बुद्धिवृद्धिकराण्याशु धन्यानि च हितानि च नित्यं शास्त्राण्यवेक्षेत निगमांश्चैव वैदिकान्
text: He shall always pore over such treatises as quickly enliven the intelligence, are conducive to wealth and are beneficial,—as also over the Vedic Scriptures.
iast: buddhivṛddhikarāṇyāśu dhanyāni ca hitāni ca nityaṃ śāstrāṇyavekṣeta nigamāṃścaiva vaidikān

sanskrit_iast: yathā yathā hi puruṣaḥ śāstraṃ samadhigacchati tathā tathā vijānāti vijñānaṃ cāsya rocate
meaning: यथा यथा हि पुरुषः शास्त्रं समधिगच्छति तथा तथा विजानाति विज्ञानं चास्य रोचते
text: For, as the man goes on studying a science, so does he go on understanding, and then his knowledge shines forth.
iast: yathā yathā hi puruṣaḥ śāstraṃ samadhigacchati tathā tathā vijānāti vijñānaṃ cāsya rocate

sanskrit_iast: ṛṣiyajñaṃ devayajñaṃ bhūtayajñaṃ ca sarvadā nṛyajñaṃ pitṛyajñaṃ ca yathāśakti na hāpayet
meaning: ऋषियज्ञं देवयज्ञं भूतयज्ञं च सर्वदा नृयज्ञं पितृयज्ञं च यथाशक्ति न हापयेत्
text: To the rest of his power, he shall never omit the sacrifice to the sages, the sacrifice to the gods, the sacrifice to elementals, the sacrifice to men and the sacrifice to the Pitṛs.
iast: ṛṣiyajñaṃ devayajñaṃ bhūtayajñaṃ ca sarvadā nṛyajñaṃ pitṛyajñaṃ ca yathāśakti na hāpayet

sanskrit_iast: etāneke mahāyajñān yajñaśāstravido janāḥ anīhamānāḥ satatamindriyeṣveva juhvati
meaning: एतानेके महायज्ञान् यज्ञशास्त्रविदो जनाः अनीहमानाः सततमिन्द्रियेष्वेव जुह्वति
