text: Some persons, conversant with the ordinances relating to sacrifices, who do not cherish ant desires, regularly offer these great sacrifices into the sense-organs.
iast: etāneke mahāyajñān yajñaśāstravido janāḥ anīhamānāḥ satatamindriyeṣveva juhvati

sanskrit_iast: vācyeke juhvati prāṇaṃ prāṇe vācaṃ ca sarvadā vāci prāṇe ca paśyanto yajñanirvṛttimakṣayām
meaning: वाच्येके जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणे वाचं च सर्वदा वाचि प्राणे च पश्यन्तो यज्ञनिर्वृत्तिमक्षयाम्
text: Some people constantly offer their life-breath into speech, and their speech into the life-breath,—knowing that in speech and in life-breath rests the imperishable fulfilment of sacrifices.
iast: vācyeke juhvati prāṇaṃ prāṇe vācaṃ ca sarvadā vāci prāṇe ca paśyanto yajñanirvṛttimakṣayām

sanskrit_iast: jñānenaivāpare viprā yajantyetairmakhaiḥ sadā yajante tairmakhaiḥ sadā jñānamūlāṃ kriyāmeṣāṃ paśyanto jñānacakṣuṣā
meaning: ज्ञानेनैवापरे विप्रा यजन्त्येतैर्मखैः सदा यजन्ते तैर्मखैः सदा ज्ञानमूलां क्रियामेषां पश्यन्तो ज्ञानचक्षुषा
text: Other Brāhmaṇas, looking, with the eye of knowledge, upon this act as having its root in knowledge, always sacrifice with these sacrifices, by means of knowledge.
iast: jñānenaivāpare viprā yajantyetairmakhaiḥ sadā yajante tairmakhaiḥ sadā jñānamūlāṃ kriyāmeṣāṃ paśyanto jñānacakṣuṣā

sanskrit_iast: agnihotraṃ ca juhuyādādiante dyuniśoḥ sadā darśena cārdhamāsānte paurṇāmāsena caiva hi
meaning: अग्निहोत्रं च जुहुयादादिअन्ते द्युनिशोः सदा दर्शेन चार्धमासान्ते पौर्णामासेन चैव हि
text: He shall always offer the Agnihotra, either at the beginning, or, at the end, of day and night, as also the “Darśa” and the “Paurṇamāsa” at the end of each half-month.
iast: agnihotraṃ ca juhuyādādiante dyuniśoḥ sadā darśena cārdhamāsānte paurṇāmāsena caiva hi

sanskrit_iast: sasyānte navasasyaiṣṭyā tathāṛtuante dvijo 'dhvaraiḥ paśunā tvayanasyādau samānte saumikairmakhaiḥ 'yanānte tu samāṃte
meaning: सस्यान्ते नवसस्यैष्ट्या तथाऋतुअन्ते द्विजो 'ध्वरैः पशुना त्वयनस्यादौ समान्ते सौमिकैर्मखैः 'यनान्ते तु समांते
text: At the end of the grains, the Brāhmaṇa shall perform the “New-Harvest sacrifice;” at the end of the seasons, the “Adhvara-sacrifices;” at the end of the solstices the “Animal-sacrifice;” and at the end of the year the “Soma-sacrifices.”
iast: sasyānte navasasyaiṣṭyā tathāṛtuante dvijo 'dhvaraiḥ paśunā tvayanasyādau samānte saumikairmakhaiḥ 'yanānte tu samāṃte

sanskrit_iast: nāniṣṭvā navasasyaiṣṭyā paśunā cāgnimān dvijaḥ navānnamadyātmāṃsaṃ vā dīrghamāyurjijīviṣuḥ
meaning: नानिष्ट्वा नवसस्यैष्ट्या पशुना चाग्निमान् द्विजः नवान्नमद्यात्मांसं वा दीर्घमायुर्जिजीविषुः
text: Without having performed the New-Harvest sacrifice, and the animal Sacrifice, the Brāhmaṇa, who has set up the fire, shall not eat new grain or meat,—if he desires to live a long life.
iast: nāniṣṭvā navasasyaiṣṭyā paśunā cāgnimān dvijaḥ navānnamadyātmāṃsaṃ vā dīrghamāyurjijīviṣuḥ

sanskrit_iast: navenānarcitā hyasya paśuhavyena cāgnayaḥ prāṇānevāttumicchanti navānnāmiṣagardhinaḥ
meaning: नवेनानर्चिता ह्यस्य पशुहव्येन चाग्नयः प्राणानेवात्तुमिच्छन्ति नवान्नामिषगर्धिनः
text: For his Fires, greedy or n ew grains and meat, seek to devour his very life, if they are not worshipped with new grains and with meat.
iast: navenānarcitā hyasya paśuhavyena cāgnayaḥ prāṇānevāttumicchanti navānnāmiṣagardhinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āsanāśanaśayyābhiradbhirmūlaphalena vā nāsya kaścidvasedgehe śaktito 'narcito 'tithiḥ
meaning: आसनाशनशय्याभिरद्भिर्मूलफलेन वा नास्य कश्चिद्वसेद्गेहे शक्तितो 'नर्चितो 'तिथिः
text: No guest shall dwell in his house without being honoured, to the best of his ability, with seat, food, bed, or with water, fruits and roots.
iast: āsanāśanaśayyābhiradbhirmūlaphalena vā nāsya kaścidvasedgehe śaktito 'narcito 'tithiḥ

sanskrit_iast: pāṣāṇḍino vikarmasthān baiḍālavratikāṃśaṭhān haitukān bakavṛttīṃśca vācmātreṇāpi nārcayet
meaning: पाषाण्डिनो विकर्मस्थान् बैडालव्रतिकांशठान् हैतुकान् बकवृत्तींश्च वाच्मात्रेणापि नार्चयेत्
text: He shall not honour, even with speech, impostors, those who follow improper occupations, those who are cat-like in their behaviour, hypocrites, logicians, and those who behave like herons.
iast: pāṣāṇḍino vikarmasthān baiḍālavratikāṃśaṭhān haitukān bakavṛttīṃśca vācmātreṇāpi nārcayet

sanskrit_iast: vedavidyāvratasnātāṃśrotriyān gṛhamedhinaḥ pūjayeddhavyakavyena viparītāṃśca varjayet
meaning: वेदविद्याव्रतस्नातांश्रोत्रियान् गृहमेधिनः पूजयेद्धव्यकव्येन विपरीतांश्च वर्जयेत्
text: He shall honour with offerings to gods and to Pitṛs, those Vedic scholars, who have become accomplished in the Veda, in learning and in the observances, as also house-holders; He shall, however, avoid those who are otherwise.
iast: vedavidyāvratasnātāṃśrotriyān gṛhamedhinaḥ pūjayeddhavyakavyena viparītāṃśca varjayet

sanskrit_iast: śaktito 'pacamānebhyo dātavyaṃ gṛhamedhinā saṃvibhāgaśca bhūtebhyaḥ kartavyo 'nuparodhataḥ
meaning: शक्तितो 'पचमानेभ्यो दातव्यं गृहमेधिना संविभागश्च भूतेभ्यः कर्तव्यो 'नुपरोधतः
text: The Householder shall give as much as he can to those who do not cook their own food; and, for the sake of all living beings, he shall make apportionment, without detriment (to himself).
iast: śaktito 'pacamānebhyo dātavyaṃ gṛhamedhinā saṃvibhāgaśca bhūtebhyaḥ kartavyo 'nuparodhataḥ

sanskrit_iast: rājato dhanamanvicchet saṃsīdan snātakaḥ kṣudhā yājyāntevāsinorvā 'pi na tvanyata iti sthitiḥ
meaning: राजतो धनमन्विच्छेत् संसीदन् स्नातकः क्षुधा याज्यान्तेवासिनोर्वा 'पि न त्वन्यत इति स्थितिः
text: Suffering from hunger, the accomplished student should seek for wealth from the king, or from one at whose sacrificial rites he officiates, or from his pupil; and not from others; such is the rule.
iast: rājato dhanamanvicchet saṃsīdan snātakaḥ kṣudhā yājyāntevāsinorvā 'pi na tvanyata iti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: na sīdet snātako vipraḥ kṣudhā śaktaḥ kathaṃ cana na jīrṇamalavadvāsā bhavecca vibhave sati
meaning: न सीदेत् स्नातको विप्रः क्षुधा शक्तः कथं चन न जीर्णमलवद्वासा भवेच्च विभवे सति
text: The Accomplished Brāhmaṇa shall not, if he can, suffer from hunger; nor shall he wear torn or dirty clothes, so long as he has any property.
iast: na sīdet snātako vipraḥ kṣudhā śaktaḥ kathaṃ cana na jīrṇamalavadvāsā bhavecca vibhave sati

sanskrit_iast: kḷptakeśanakhaśmaśrurdāntaḥ śuklāmbaraḥ śuciḥ svādhyāye caiva yuktaḥ syānnityamātmahiteṣu ca
meaning: कॢप्तकेशनखश्मश्रुर्दान्तः शुक्लाम्बरः शुचिः स्वाध्याये चैव युक्तः स्यान्नित्यमात्महितेषु च
text: Keeping his hair, nails and beard clipped, subdued, wearing white clothes, pure,—he shall always remain engaged in Vedic study, also in what may be conducive to his welfare.
iast: kḷptakeśanakhaśmaśrurdāntaḥ śuklāmbaraḥ śuciḥ svādhyāye caiva yuktaḥ syānnityamātmahiteṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: vaiṇavīṃ dhārayedyaṣṭiṃ saudakaṃ ca kamaṇḍalum yajñopavītaṃ vedaṃ ca śubhaṃ raukme ca kuṇḍale
meaning: वैणवीं धारयेद्यष्टिं सौदकं च कमण्डलुम् यज्ञोपवीतं वेदं च शुभं रौक्मे च कुण्डले
text: He shall hold a stick of bamboo, and also a water-pot full of water, the sacred thread, a handful of kuśa-grass and a pair of bright golden ear-rings.
iast: vaiṇavīṃ dhārayedyaṣṭiṃ saudakaṃ ca kamaṇḍalum yajñopavītaṃ vedaṃ ca śubhaṃ raukme ca kuṇḍale

sanskrit_iast: naikṣetaudyantamādityaṃ nāstaṃ yāntaṃ kadā cana naupasṛṣṭaṃ na vāristhaṃ na madhyaṃ nabhaso gatam
meaning: नैक्षेतौद्यन्तमादित्यं नास्तं यान्तं कदा चन नौपसृष्टं न वारिस्थं न मध्यं नभसो गतम्
text: He shall not look at the Sun when rising, nor when setting, nor when it is eclipsed, nor when it is in water, nor when it has reached the middle of the sky.
iast: naikṣetaudyantamādityaṃ nāstaṃ yāntaṃ kadā cana naupasṛṣṭaṃ na vāristhaṃ na madhyaṃ nabhaso gatam

sanskrit_iast: na laṅghayedvatsatantrīṃ na pradhāvecca varṣati na caudake nirīkṣeta svarūpamiti dhāraṇā
meaning: न लङ्घयेद्वत्सतन्त्रीं न प्रधावेच्च वर्षति न चौदके निरीक्षेत स्वरूपमिति धारणा
text: He shall not step over the rope to which a calf is tied; he shall not run when it is raining; he shall not look at his own figure in water; such is the established rule.
iast: na laṅghayedvatsatantrīṃ na pradhāvecca varṣati na caudake nirīkṣeta svarūpamiti dhāraṇā

sanskrit_iast: mṛdaṃ gāṃ daivataṃ vipraṃ ghṛtaṃ madhu catuṣpatham pradakṣiṇāni kurvīta prajñātāṃśca vanaspatīn
meaning: मृदं गां दैवतं विप्रं घृतं मधु चतुष्पथम् प्रदक्षिणानि कुर्वीत प्रज्ञातांश्च वनस्पतीन्
text: By a mound of clay, a cow, a deity, a Brāhmaṇa, clarified butter, honey, a cross-way and the well-known trees—he shall pass in such a manner as to leave them on his right.
iast: mṛdaṃ gāṃ daivataṃ vipraṃ ghṛtaṃ madhu catuṣpatham pradakṣiṇāni kurvīta prajñātāṃśca vanaspatīn

sanskrit_iast: naupagacchet pramatto 'pi striyamārtavadarśane samānaśayane caiva na śayīta tayā saha
meaning: नौपगच्छेत् प्रमत्तो 'पि स्त्रियमार्तवदर्शने समानशयने चैव न शयीत तया सह
text: Even though mad, he shall not approach a woman during her courses; nor shall he sleep on the same bed with her.
iast: naupagacchet pramatto 'pi striyamārtavadarśane samānaśayane caiva na śayīta tayā saha

sanskrit_iast: rajasā 'bhiplutāṃ nārīṃ narasya hyupagacchataḥ prajñā tejo balaṃ cakṣurāyuścaiva prahīyate
meaning: रजसा 'भिप्लुतां नारीं नरस्य ह्युपगच्छतः प्रज्ञा तेजो बलं चक्षुरायुश्चैव प्रहीयते
text: Of the man who approaches a woman covered with impurity,—intelligence, vitality, strength, sight and longevity wear off.
iast: rajasā 'bhiplutāṃ nārīṃ narasya hyupagacchataḥ prajñā tejo balaṃ cakṣurāyuścaiva prahīyate

sanskrit_iast: tāṃ vivarjayatastasya rajasā samabhiplutām prajñā tejo balaṃ cakṣurāyuścaiva pravardhate
meaning: तां विवर्जयतस्तस्य रजसा समभिप्लुताम् प्रज्ञा तेजो बलं चक्षुरायुश्चैव प्रवर्धते
text: Of that same man, if he avoids the woman covered with impurity, intelligence, vitality, strength, sight and longevity flourish.
iast: tāṃ vivarjayatastasya rajasā samabhiplutām prajñā tejo balaṃ cakṣurāyuścaiva pravardhate

sanskrit_iast: nāśnīyādbhāryayā sārdhaṃ naināmīkṣeta cāśnatīm kṣuvatīṃ jṛmbhamāṇāṃ vā na cāsīnāṃ yathāsukham
meaning: नाश्नीयाद्भार्यया सार्धं नैनामीक्षेत चाश्नतीम् क्षुवतीं जृम्भमाणां वा न चासीनां यथासुखम्
text: He shall not eat with his wife; nor shall he look at her while she is eating, or snoring, or yawning, or sitting at her ease.
iast: nāśnīyādbhāryayā sārdhaṃ naināmīkṣeta cāśnatīm kṣuvatīṃ jṛmbhamāṇāṃ vā na cāsīnāṃ yathāsukham

sanskrit_iast: nāñjayantīṃ svake netre na cābhyaktāmanāvṛtām na paśyet prasavantīṃ ca tejaskāmo dvijottamaḥ
meaning: नाञ्जयन्तीं स्वके नेत्रे न चाभ्यक्तामनावृताम् न पश्येत् प्रसवन्तीं च तेजस्कामो द्विजोत्तमः
text: The brāhmaṇa, desiring brightness, shall not look at a woman who is a pplying collyrium to her own eyes, or who has anointed herself, or who is uncovered, or is bringing forth a child.
iast: nāñjayantīṃ svake netre na cābhyaktāmanāvṛtām na paśyet prasavantīṃ ca tejaskāmo dvijottamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nānnamadyādekavāsā na nagnaḥ snānamācaret na mūtraṃ pathi kurvīta na bhasmani na govraje
meaning: नान्नमद्यादेकवासा न नग्नः स्नानमाचरेत् न मूत्रं पथि कुर्वीत न भस्मनि न गोव्रजे
text: He shall not eat food with only one piece of cloth on him; he shall not bathe naked; he shall not pass urine on the road, nor on ashes, nor on the haunts of cows.
iast: nānnamadyādekavāsā na nagnaḥ snānamācaret na mūtraṃ pathi kurvīta na bhasmani na govraje

sanskrit_iast: na phālakṛṣṭe na jale na cityāṃ na ca parvate na jīrṇadevāyatane na valmīke kadā cana
meaning: न फालकृष्टे न जले न चित्यां न च पर्वते न जीर्णदेवायतने न वल्मीके कदा चन
text: Nor on ploughed land, nor in water, nor on an oven, nor on a mountain, nor in a ruined temple, nor on an ant-hill.
iast: na phālakṛṣṭe na jale na cityāṃ na ca parvate na jīrṇadevāyatane na valmīke kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: na sasattveṣu garteṣu na gacchannapi na sthitaḥ na nadītīramāsādya na ca parvatamastake
meaning: न ससत्त्वेषु गर्तेषु न गच्छन्नपि न स्थितः न नदीतीरमासाद्य न च पर्वतमस्तके
text: Nor in holes inhabited by living creatures, nor walking, nor standing, nor on reaching the banks of a river, nor on the mountain-top.
iast: na sasattveṣu garteṣu na gacchannapi na sthitaḥ na nadītīramāsādya na ca parvatamastake

sanskrit_iast: vāyuagnivipramādityamapaḥ paśyaṃstathaiva gāḥ na kadā cana kurvīta viṣmūtrasya visarjanam
meaning: वायुअग्निविप्रमादित्यमपः पश्यंस्तथैव गाः न कदा चन कुर्वीत विष्मूत्रस्य विसर्जनम्
text: One should never pass faeces or urine, while looking at the wind or fire, or a Brāhmaṇa, or the sun, or water, or cows.
iast: vāyuagnivipramādityamapaḥ paśyaṃstathaiva gāḥ na kadā cana kurvīta viṣmūtrasya visarjanam

sanskrit_iast: tiraskṛtyauccaret kāṣṭhaloṣṭhapatratṛṇādinā tṛṇādi ca niyamya prayato vācaṃ saṃvītāṅgo 'vaguṇṭhitaḥ
meaning: तिरस्कृत्यौच्चरेत् काष्ठलोष्ठपत्रतृणादिना तृणादि च नियम्य प्रयतो वाचं संवीताङ्गो 'वगुण्ठितः
text: प्रत्यग्निṁ प्रतिसूर्यṁ च प्रतिस्ōम्ōदकद्विजम् ।प्रतिगु प्रतिवातṁ च प्रज्ञा नश्यति म्ēहतः ॥ ४९ ॥
iast: tiraskṛtyauccaret kāṣṭhaloṣṭhapatratṛṇādinā tṛṇādi ca niyamya prayato vācaṃ saṃvītāṅgo 'vaguṇṭhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mūtrauccārasamutsargaṃ divā kuryādudalgmukhaḥ dakṣiṇā 'bhimukho rātrau saṃdhyāyośca yathā divā
meaning: मूत्रौच्चारसमुत्सर्गं दिवा कुर्यादुदल्ग्मुखः दक्षिणा 'भिमुखो रात्रौ संध्यायोश्च यथा दिवा
text: He shall pass it after placing a stick, or a clod, or leaves, or grass, or some such thing, restraining his speech, clean, his b ody wrapped and covered.
iast: mūtrauccārasamutsargaṃ divā kuryādudalgmukhaḥ dakṣiṇā 'bhimukho rātrau saṃdhyāyośca yathā divā

sanskrit_iast: chāyāyāmandhakāre vā rātrāvahani vā dvijaḥ yathāsukhamukhaḥ kuryāt prāṇabādhabhayeṣu ca
meaning: छायायामन्धकारे वा रात्रावहनि वा द्विजः यथासुखमुखः कुर्यात् प्राणबाधभयेषु च
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: chāyāyāmandhakāre vā rātrāvahani vā dvijaḥ yathāsukhamukhaḥ kuryāt prāṇabādhabhayeṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: pratiagniṃ pratisūryaṃ ca pratisomaudakadvijam pratigu prativātaṃ ca prajñā naśyati mehataḥ pratigāṃ prativātaṃ
meaning: प्रतिअग्निं प्रतिसूर्यं च प्रतिसोमौदकद्विजम् प्रतिगु प्रतिवातं च प्रज्ञा नश्यति मेहतः प्रतिगां प्रतिवातं
text: In the shade, or in darkness, the Brāhmaṇa may—during the day or the night—do it, with his face towards any direction he pleases; as also where there is danger to life, and when there is fear.’
iast: pratiagniṃ pratisūryaṃ ca pratisomaudakadvijam pratigu prativātaṃ ca prajñā naśyati mehataḥ pratigāṃ prativātaṃ

sanskrit_iast: nāgniṃ mukhenaupadhamennagnāṃ naikṣeta ca striyam nāmedhyaṃ prakṣipedagnau na ca pādau pratāpayet
meaning: नाग्निं मुखेनौपधमेन्नग्नां नैक्षेत च स्त्रियम् नामेध्यं प्रक्षिपेदग्नौ न च पादौ प्रतापयेत्
text: He shall not blow fire with the mouth; nor shall he look at a naked woman. He shall not throw an unclean thing into fire; nor shall he warm his feet at it.
iast: nāgniṃ mukhenaupadhamennagnāṃ naikṣeta ca striyam nāmedhyaṃ prakṣipedagnau na ca pādau pratāpayet

sanskrit_iast: adhastānnaupadadhyācca na cainamabhilaṅghayet na cainaṃ pādataḥ kuryānna prāṇābādhamācaret
meaning: अधस्तान्नौपदध्याच्च न चैनमभिलङ्घयेत् न चैनं पादतः कुर्यान्न प्राणाबाधमाचरेत्
text: He shall not place fire under himself; nor shall he step over it; he shall not place it under his feet. He shall not do anything dangerous to life.
iast: adhastānnaupadadhyācca na cainamabhilaṅghayet na cainaṃ pādataḥ kuryānna prāṇābādhamācaret

sanskrit_iast: nāśnīyāt saṃdhivelāyāṃ na gacchennāpi saṃviśet na caiva pralikhedbhūmiṃ nātmano 'paharet srajam
meaning: नाश्नीयात् संधिवेलायां न गच्छेन्नापि संविशेत् न चैव प्रलिखेद्भूमिं नात्मनो 'पहरेत् स्रजम्
text: At junction-time, he shall not eat, nor travel, nor sleep. He shall not scratch the ground; and he shall not remove his own garland.
iast: nāśnīyāt saṃdhivelāyāṃ na gacchennāpi saṃviśet na caiva pralikhedbhūmiṃ nātmano 'paharet srajam

sanskrit_iast: nāpsu mūtraṃ purīṣaṃ vā ṣṭhīvanaṃ vā samutsṛjet amedhyaliptamanyadvā lohitaṃ vā viṣāṇi vā
meaning: नाप्सु मूत्रं पुरीषं वा ष्ठीवनं वा समुत्सृजेत् अमेध्यलिप्तमन्यद्वा लोहितं वा विषाणि वा
text: He shall not throw into water urine, or faeces, or spittings, or anything else contaminated by unclean things, or blood or poisons.
iast: nāpsu mūtraṃ purīṣaṃ vā ṣṭhīvanaṃ vā samutsṛjet amedhyaliptamanyadvā lohitaṃ vā viṣāṇi vā

sanskrit_iast: naikaḥ supyātśūnyagehe na śreyāṃsaṃ prabodhayet [śūnyagṛhe svapyān naudakyayā 'bhibhāṣeta yajñaṃ gacchenna cāvṛtaḥ
meaning: नैकः सुप्यात्शून्यगेहे न श्रेयांसं प्रबोधयेत् [शून्यगृहे स्वप्यान् नौदक्यया 'भिभाषेत यज्ञं गच्छेन्न चावृतः
text: Alone he shall not sleep in a deserted house. He shall not offer advice to his superior. He shall not converse with a woman in her courses. He shall not go to a sacrifice uninvited.
iast: naikaḥ supyātśūnyagehe na śreyāṃsaṃ prabodhayet [śūnyagṛhe svapyān naudakyayā 'bhibhāṣeta yajñaṃ gacchenna cāvṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: agnyagāre gavāṃ goṣṭhe brāhmaṇānāṃ ca saṃnidhau svādhyāye bhojane caiva dakṣinaṃ pāṇimuddharet
meaning: अग्न्यगारे गवां गोष्ठे ब्राह्मणानां च संनिधौ स्वाध्याये भोजने चैव दक्षिनं पाणिमुद्धरेत्
text: In the abode of fire, in the cow-pen, in the presence of Brāhmaṇas, during the reading of Vedas, at the time of eating, he shall uncover his right hand.
iast: agnyagāre gavāṃ goṣṭhe brāhmaṇānāṃ ca saṃnidhau svādhyāye bhojane caiva dakṣinaṃ pāṇimuddharet

sanskrit_iast: na vārayedgāṃ dhayantīṃ na cācakṣīta kasya cit na divīndrāyudhaṃ dṛṣṭvā kasya ciddarśayedbudhaḥ
meaning: न वारयेद्गां धयन्तीं न चाचक्षीत कस्य चित् न दिवीन्द्रायुधं दृष्ट्वा कस्य चिद्दर्शयेद्बुधः
text: He shall not prevent a heifer while she is drinking, nor shall he point her out to anybody. Having seen the rainbow in the sky, the wise man shall not show it to any person.
iast: na vārayedgāṃ dhayantīṃ na cācakṣīta kasya cit na divīndrāyudhaṃ dṛṣṭvā kasya ciddarśayedbudhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nādharmike vasedgrāme na vyādhibahule bhṛśam naikaḥ prapadyetādhvānaṃ na ciraṃ parvate vaset
meaning: नाधर्मिके वसेद्ग्रामे न व्याधिबहुले भृशम् नैकः प्रपद्येताध्वानं न चिरं पर्वते वसेत्
text: He shall not dwell long in an unrighteous village, nor in one abounding in sickness. Alone, he shall not undertake a journey; nor shall he reside for long on a mountain.
iast: nādharmike vasedgrāme na vyādhibahule bhṛśam naikaḥ prapadyetādhvānaṃ na ciraṃ parvate vaset

sanskrit_iast: na śūdrarājye nivasennādhārmikajanāvṛte na pāṣaṇḍigaṇākrānte naupasṣṛṭe 'ntyajairnṛbhiḥ
meaning: न शूद्रराज्ये निवसेन्नाधार्मिकजनावृते न पाषण्डिगणाक्रान्ते नौपस्षृटे 'न्त्यजैर्नृभिः
text: He shall not dwell in a country with a Śūdra King; nor in one surrounded by unrighteous persons; nor in one occupied by impostors;. nor in one frequented by men of the lowest castes.
iast: na śūdrarājye nivasennādhārmikajanāvṛte na pāṣaṇḍigaṇākrānte naupasṣṛṭe 'ntyajairnṛbhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: na bhuñjītauddhṛtasnehaṃ nātisauhityamācaret nātiprage nātisāyaṃ na sāyaṃ prātarāśitaḥ
meaning: न भुञ्जीतौद्धृतस्नेहं नातिसौहित्यमाचरेत् नातिप्रगे नातिसायं न सायं प्रातराशितः
text: He shall not eat anything from which oil has been extract ed; he shall not commit gluttony; he shall not eat very early in the morning, nor very late in the evening; nor in the evening, if he has eaten in the morning.
iast: na bhuñjītauddhṛtasnehaṃ nātisauhityamācaret nātiprage nātisāyaṃ na sāyaṃ prātarāśitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na kurvīta vṛthāceṣṭāṃ na vāryañjalinā pibet nautsaṅge bhakṣayedbhakṣyānna jātu syāt kutūhalī
meaning: न कुर्वीत वृथाचेष्टां न वार्यञ्जलिना पिबेत् नौत्सङ्गे भक्षयेद्भक्ष्यान्न जातु स्यात् कुतूहली
text: He shall not put forth any exertion without a purpose. He shall not drink water with joined hands. He shall not eat articles of food in his lap. He shall never be too curious.
iast: na kurvīta vṛthāceṣṭāṃ na vāryañjalinā pibet nautsaṅge bhakṣayedbhakṣyānna jātu syāt kutūhalī

sanskrit_iast: na nṛtyedatha vā gāyenna vāditrāṇi vādayet na nṛtyennaiva gāyecca na vāditrāṇi vādayet nāsphoṭayenna ca kṣveḍenna ca rakto virāvayet na ca rakto virodhayet
meaning: न नृत्येदथ वा गायेन्न वादित्राणि वादयेत् न नृत्येन्नैव गायेच्च न वादित्राणि वादयेत् नास्फोटयेन्न च क्ष्वेडेन्न च रक्तो विरावयेत् न च रक्तो विरोधयेत्
text: He shall not dance, nor sing, nor play upon musical instruments, nor clap, nor grind his teeth, nor, when satisfied, shall he create enmity.
iast: na nṛtyedatha vā gāyenna vāditrāṇi vādayet na nṛtyennaiva gāyecca na vāditrāṇi vādayet nāsphoṭayenna ca kṣveḍenna ca rakto virāvayet na ca rakto virodhayet

sanskrit_iast: na pādau dhāvayet kāṃsye kadā cidapi bhājane na bhinnabhāṇḍe bhuñjīta na bhāvapratidūṣite
meaning: न पादौ धावयेत् कांस्ये कदा चिदपि भाजने न भिन्नभाण्डे भुञ्जीत न भावप्रतिदूषिते
text: He shall never wash his feet in a vessel of white brass. He shall not eat out of a broken dish; nor out of one that is felt to be defiled.
iast: na pādau dhāvayet kāṃsye kadā cidapi bhājane na bhinnabhāṇḍe bhuñjīta na bhāvapratidūṣite

sanskrit_iast: upānahau ca vāsaśca dhṛtamanyairna dhārayet upavītamalaṅkāraṃ srajaṃ karakameva ca
meaning: उपानहौ च वासश्च धृतमन्यैर्न धारयेत् उपवीतमलङ्कारं स्रजं करकमेव च
text: He shall not use shoes, on clothes, or sacred thread, or ornament, or garland, or water-pot, which has been used by others.
iast: upānahau ca vāsaśca dhṛtamanyairna dhārayet upavītamalaṅkāraṃ srajaṃ karakameva ca

sanskrit_iast: nāvinītairbhajeddhuryairna ca kṣudhvyādhipīḍitaiḥ nāvinītairvrajed na bhinnaśṛṅgākṣikhurairna vāladhivirūpitaiḥ
meaning: नाविनीतैर्भजेद्धुर्यैर्न च क्षुध्व्याधिपीडितैः नाविनीतैर्व्रजेद् न भिन्नशृङ्गाक्षिखुरैर्न वालधिविरूपितैः
text: He shall not travel with untrained beasts of burden; nor with such as are suffering from hunger or disease; nor with those whose horns, eyes or hoofs are injured; nor with those that are disfigured by their tails.
iast: nāvinītairbhajeddhuryairna ca kṣudhvyādhipīḍitaiḥ nāvinītairvrajed na bhinnaśṛṅgākṣikhurairna vāladhivirūpitaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: vinītaistu vrajennityamāśugairlakṣaṇānvitaiḥ varṇarūpaupasaṃpannaiḥ pratodenātudan bhṛśam pratodenākṣipan
meaning: विनीतैस्तु व्रजेन्नित्यमाशुगैर्लक्षणान्वितैः वर्णरूपौपसंपन्नैः प्रतोदेनातुदन् भृशम् प्रतोदेनाक्षिपन्
text: He should always travel with beasts that are trained, fast, equipped with signs, well endowed with colour and figure,—without striking them much with the goad.
iast: vinītaistu vrajennityamāśugairlakṣaṇānvitaiḥ varṇarūpaupasaṃpannaiḥ pratodenātudan bhṛśam pratodenākṣipan

sanskrit_iast: bālātapaḥ pretadhūmo varjyaṃ bhinnaṃ tathā 'sanam na chindyānnakharomāṇi dantairnautpāṭayennakhān na cchindyān
meaning: बालातपः प्रेतधूमो वर्ज्यं भिन्नं तथा 'सनम् न छिन्द्यान्नखरोमाणि दन्तैर्नौत्पाटयेन्नखान् न च्छिन्द्यान्
text: The young sun and the smoke from the dead body, should be avoided, as also a broken seat. He shall not cut his nails and hair, nob shall he tear his nails with his teeth.
iast: bālātapaḥ pretadhūmo varjyaṃ bhinnaṃ tathā 'sanam na chindyānnakharomāṇi dantairnautpāṭayennakhān na cchindyān

sanskrit_iast: na mṛtloṣṭhaṃ ca mṛdnīyānna chindyāt karajaistṛṇam cchindyāt na karma niṣphalaṃ kuryānnāyatyāmasukhaudayam
meaning: न मृत्लोष्ठं च मृद्नीयान्न छिन्द्यात् करजैस्तृणम् च्छिन्द्यात् न कर्म निष्फलं कुर्यान्नायत्यामसुखौदयम्
text: He shall not crush clods of earth; nor shall he cut grass with hi s nails. He shall not do an aimless act, nor one that is likely to lead to disagreeable results.
iast: na mṛtloṣṭhaṃ ca mṛdnīyānna chindyāt karajaistṛṇam cchindyāt na karma niṣphalaṃ kuryānnāyatyāmasukhaudayam

sanskrit_iast: loṣṭhamardī tṛṇacchedī nakhakhādī ca yo naraḥ sa vināśaṃ vrajatyāśu sūcakā 'śucireva ca sūcako 'śucireva ca
meaning: लोष्ठमर्दी तृणच्छेदी नखखादी च यो नरः स विनाशं व्रजत्याशु सूचका 'शुचिरेव च सूचको 'शुचिरेव च
text: A man who crushes clods, cuts grass or bites his nails, quickly goes to perdition; so also the back-biter and the unclean man.
iast: loṣṭhamardī tṛṇacchedī nakhakhādī ca yo naraḥ sa vināśaṃ vrajatyāśu sūcakā 'śucireva ca sūcako 'śucireva ca

sanskrit_iast: na vigarhya kathāṃ kuryādbahirmālyaṃ na dhārayet na vigṛhya kathāṃ kuryād gavāṃ ca yānaṃ pṛṣṭhena sarvathaiva vigarhitam
meaning: न विगर्ह्य कथां कुर्याद्बहिर्माल्यं न धारयेत् न विगृह्य कथां कुर्याद् गवां च यानं पृष्ठेन सर्वथैव विगर्हितम्
text: He shall hot carry on a wrangling conversatioh. He shall not wear a garland outside. Riding on the back of cows and oxen is altogether deprecated.
iast: na vigarhya kathāṃ kuryādbahirmālyaṃ na dhārayet na vigṛhya kathāṃ kuryād gavāṃ ca yānaṃ pṛṣṭhena sarvathaiva vigarhitam

sanskrit_iast: advāreṇa ca nātīyādgrāmaṃ vā veśma vā 'vṛtam rātrau ca vṛkṣamūlāni dūrataḥ parivarjayet
meaning: अद्वारेण च नातीयाद्ग्रामं वा वेश्म वा 'वृतम् रात्रौ च वृक्षमूलानि दूरतः परिवर्जयेत्
text: He shall hot enter a walled village or house, except through the gate. At night, he shalt, keep away, at a long distance, from the roots of trees.
iast: advāreṇa ca nātīyādgrāmaṃ vā veśma vā 'vṛtam rātrau ca vṛkṣamūlāni dūrataḥ parivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: nākṣairdīvyet kadā cit tu svayaṃ naupānahau haret śayanastho na bhuñjīta na pāṇisthaṃ na cāsane
meaning: नाक्षैर्दीव्येत् कदा चित् तु स्वयं नौपानहौ हरेत् शयनस्थो न भुञ्जीत न पाणिस्थं न चासने
text: He shall never gamble with dice; he shall not himself carry his shoes; he shall not eat, seated on a bed; nor what has been placed in his hand or on the seat.
iast: nākṣairdīvyet kadā cit tu svayaṃ naupānahau haret śayanastho na bhuñjīta na pāṇisthaṃ na cāsane

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ ca tilasaṃbaddhaṃ nādyādastamite ravau na ca nagnaḥ śayītaiha na caucchiṣṭaḥ kva cidvrajet
meaning: सर्वं च तिलसंबद्धं नाद्यादस्तमिते रवौ न च नग्नः शयीतैह न चौच्छिष्टः क्व चिद्व्रजेत्
text: Any food containing sesamum, he shall not eat after sunset; he shall never sleep naked; nor go anywhere with mouth unwashed after meals.
iast: sarvaṃ ca tilasaṃbaddhaṃ nādyādastamite ravau na ca nagnaḥ śayītaiha na caucchiṣṭaḥ kva cidvrajet

sanskrit_iast: ārdrapādastu bhuñjīta nārdrapādastu saṃviśet ārdrapādastu bhuñjāno dīrghamāyuravāpnuyāt
meaning: आर्द्रपादस्तु भुञ्जीत नार्द्रपादस्तु संविशेत् आर्द्रपादस्तु भुञ्जानो दीर्घमायुरवाप्नुयात्
text: He shall eat with wet feet; but he shall not sleep with his feet wet. By eating with wet feet, one would attain long life.
iast: ārdrapādastu bhuñjīta nārdrapādastu saṃviśet ārdrapādastu bhuñjāno dīrghamāyuravāpnuyāt

sanskrit_iast: acakṣurviṣayaṃ durgaṃ na prapadyeta karhi cit na viṣmūtramudīkṣeta na bāhubhyāṃ nadīṃ taret
meaning: अचक्षुर्विषयं दुर्गं न प्रपद्येत कर्हि चित् न विष्मूत्रमुदीक्षेत न बाहुभ्यां नदीं तरेत्
text: He shall never approach a place difficult of access, which is not within range of his vision; he shall not look at urine or excreta; nor shall he cross a river with his arms
iast: acakṣurviṣayaṃ durgaṃ na prapadyeta karhi cit na viṣmūtramudīkṣeta na bāhubhyāṃ nadīṃ taret

sanskrit_iast: adhitiṣṭhenna keśāṃstu na bhasmāsthikapālikāḥ na kārpāsāsthi na tuṣān dīrghamāyurjijīviṣuḥ
meaning: अधितिष्ठेन्न केशांस्तु न भस्मास्थिकपालिकाः न कार्पासास्थि न तुषान् दीर्घमायुर्जिजीविषुः
text: One who is desirous of living a long life, shall not step on hair, nor on ashes, bones and potsherds; or on cotton-seed or chaff.
iast: adhitiṣṭhenna keśāṃstu na bhasmāsthikapālikāḥ na kārpāsāsthi na tuṣān dīrghamāyurjijīviṣuḥ

sanskrit_iast: na saṃvasecca patitairna cāṇḍālairna pulkasaiḥ na mūrkhairnāvaliptaiśca nāntyairnāntyāvasāyibhiḥ
meaning: न संवसेच्च पतितैर्न चाण्डालैर्न पुल्कसैः न मूर्खैर्नावलिप्तैश्च नान्त्यैर्नान्त्यावसायिभिः
text: He shall not associate with outcasts, nor with Cāṇḍālas, nor with Pulkasas; nor with the illiterate; nor with the haughty; nor with Antyas; nor with Antyāvasāyins.
iast: na saṃvasecca patitairna cāṇḍālairna pulkasaiḥ na mūrkhairnāvaliptaiśca nāntyairnāntyāvasāyibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: na śūdrāya matiṃ dadyānnaucchiṣṭaṃ na haviṣkṛtam na cāsyaupadiśeddharmaṃ na cāsya vratamādiśet
meaning: न शूद्राय मतिं दद्यान्नौच्छिष्टं न हविष्कृतम् न चास्यौपदिशेद्धर्मं न चास्य व्रतमादिशेत्
text: He shall not offer advice to a Śūdra, nor the leavings, nor what has been prepared as an offering to the Gods. He shall hot expound the law to him; nor shall he indicate to him any penance.
iast: na śūdrāya matiṃ dadyānnaucchiṣṭaṃ na haviṣkṛtam na cāsyaupadiśeddharmaṃ na cāsya vratamādiśet

sanskrit_iast: yo hyasya dharmamācaṣṭe yaścaivādiśati vratam so 'saṃvṛtaṃ nāma tamaḥ saha tenaiva majjati
meaning: यो ह्यस्य धर्ममाचष्टे यश्चैवादिशति व्रतम् सो 'संवृतं नाम तमः सह तेनैव मज्जति
text: He who expounds to him the law, and he who indicates the penance to him, will sink, along with him, into the hell called “asaṃvṛta.”
iast: yo hyasya dharmamācaṣṭe yaścaivādiśati vratam so 'saṃvṛtaṃ nāma tamaḥ saha tenaiva majjati

sanskrit_iast: na saṃhatābhyāṃ pāṇibhyāṃ kaṇḍūyedātmanaḥ śiraḥ na spṛśeccaitaducchiṣṭo na ca snāyādvinā tataḥ
meaning: न संहताभ्यां पाणिभ्यां कण्डूयेदात्मनः शिरः न स्पृशेच्चैतदुच्छिष्टो न च स्नायाद्विना ततः
text: He shall not scratch his own head with both hands joined together; he shall not touch it while unclean; and he shall not bathe without it.
iast: na saṃhatābhyāṃ pāṇibhyāṃ kaṇḍūyedātmanaḥ śiraḥ na spṛśeccaitaducchiṣṭo na ca snāyādvinā tataḥ

sanskrit_iast: keśagrahān prahārāṃśca śirasyetān vivarjayet śiraḥsnātaśca tailena nāṅgaṃ kiṃ cidapi spṛśet
meaning: केशग्रहान् प्रहारांश्च शिरस्येतान् विवर्जयेत् शिरःस्नातश्च तैलेन नाङ्गं किं चिदपि स्पृशेत्
text: Catching of the hair, as also striking on the head,—these he shall avoid; having his head bathed, he shall touch no limb with oil.
iast: keśagrahān prahārāṃśca śirasyetān vivarjayet śiraḥsnātaśca tailena nāṅgaṃ kiṃ cidapi spṛśet

sanskrit_iast: na rājñaḥ pratigṛhṇīyādarājanyaprasūtitaḥ sūnācakradhvajavatāṃ veśenaiva ca jīvatām
meaning: न राज्ञः प्रतिगृह्णीयादराजन्यप्रसूतितः सूनाचक्रध्वजवतां वेशेनैव च जीवताम्
text: He shall not accept gifts from a king not born of the Kṣatriya caste; nor from the keepers of slaughterhouses, oil-presses or grog-shops; nor from those who live on brothels.
iast: na rājñaḥ pratigṛhṇīyādarājanyaprasūtitaḥ sūnācakradhvajavatāṃ veśenaiva ca jīvatām

sanskrit_iast: daśasūnāsamaṃ cakraṃ daśacakrasamo dhvajaḥ daśadhvajasamo veśo daśaveśasamo nṛpaḥ
meaning: दशसूनासमं चक्रं दशचक्रसमो ध्वजः दशध्वजसमो वेशो दशवेशसमो नृपः
text: One oil-press is equal to ten slaughter-houses; one grog-shop is equal to ten oil-presses; one brothel is equal to ten grog-shops; and one king is equal to ten brothels.
iast: daśasūnāsamaṃ cakraṃ daśacakrasamo dhvajaḥ daśadhvajasamo veśo daśaveśasamo nṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: daśa sūṇāsahasrāṇi yo vāhayati saunikaḥ tena tulyaḥ smṛto rājā ghorastasya pratigrahaḥ
meaning: दश सूणासहस्राणि यो वाहयति सौनिकः तेन तुल्यः स्मृतो राजा घोरस्तस्य प्रतिग्रहः
text: A king has been declared to be equal to a butcher who maintains ten thousand slaughter-houses; and terrible is the receiving of gifts from him.
iast: daśa sūṇāsahasrāṇi yo vāhayati saunikaḥ tena tulyaḥ smṛto rājā ghorastasya pratigrahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo rājñaḥ pratigṛhṇāti lubdhasyaucchāstravartinaḥ sa paryāyeṇa yātīmānnarakānekaviṃśatim
meaning: यो राज्ञः प्रतिगृह्णाति लुब्धस्यौच्छास्त्रवर्तिनः स पर्यायेण यातीमान्नरकानेकविंशतिम्
text: He who accepts gifts from a king who is avaricious and behaves contrary to the scriptures, goes, in succession, to these twenty-one hells:
iast: yo rājñaḥ pratigṛhṇāti lubdhasyaucchāstravartinaḥ sa paryāyeṇa yātīmānnarakānekaviṃśatim

sanskrit_iast: tāmisramandhatāmisraṃ mahārauravarauravau narakaṃ kālasūtraṃ ca mahānarakameva ca
meaning: तामिस्रमन्धतामिस्रं महारौरवरौरवौ नरकं कालसूत्रं च महानरकमेव च
text: (1) Tāmisra, (2) Andhatāmisra, (3) Mahāraurava, (4) Raurava, (5) Kālasūtra-Naraka, (6) Mahānaraka, (7) Sañjīvana, (8) Mahāvīci, (9) Tāpana, (10) Sampatāpana, (11) Saṃhāta, (12) Sakākola, (13) Kuḍmala, (14) Pūtimṛttika, (15) Lohaśaṅku, (16) Ṛjīṣa, (17) Pathin, (18) Śālmalī, (19) Nadī, (20) Asipatravana and (21) Lohadāraka.
iast: tāmisramandhatāmisraṃ mahārauravarauravau narakaṃ kālasūtraṃ ca mahānarakameva ca

sanskrit_iast: sañjīvanaṃ mahāvīciṃ tapanaṃ saṃpratāpanam saṃhātaṃ ca sakākolaṃ kuḍmalaṃ pratimūrtikam pūtimṛttikaṃ
meaning: सञ्जीवनं महावीचिं तपनं संप्रतापनम् संहातं च सकाकोलं कुड्मलं प्रतिमूर्तिकम् पूतिमृत्तिकं
text: (1) Tāmisra, (2) Andhatāmisra, (3) Mahāraurava, (4) Raurava, (5) Kālasūtra-Naraka, (6) Mahānaraka, (7) Sañjīvana, (8) Mahāvīci, (9) Tāpana, (10) Sampatāpana, (11) Saṃhāta, (12) Sakākola, (13) Kuḍmala, (14) Pūtimṛttika, (15) Lohaśaṅku, (16) Ṛjīṣa, (17) Pathin, (18) Śālmalī, (19) Nadī, (20) Asipatravana and (21) Lohadāraka.
iast: sañjīvanaṃ mahāvīciṃ tapanaṃ saṃpratāpanam saṃhātaṃ ca sakākolaṃ kuḍmalaṃ pratimūrtikam pūtimṛttikaṃ

sanskrit_iast: lohaśaṅkuṃ ṛjīṣaṃ ca panthānaṃ śālmalīṃ nadīm asipatravanaṃ caiva lohadārakameva ca
meaning: लोहशङ्कुं ऋजीषं च पन्थानं शाल्मलीं नदीम् असिपत्रवनं चैव लोहदारकमेव च
text: (1) Tāmisra, (2) Andhatāmisra, (3) Mahāraurava, (4) Raurava, (5) Kālasūtra-Naraka, (6) Mahānaraka, (7) Sañjīvana, (8) Mahāvīci, (9) Tāpana, (10) Sampatāpana, (11) Saṃhāta, (12) Sakākola, (13) Kuḍmala, (14) Pūtimṛttika, (15) Lohaśaṅku, (16) Ṛjīṣa, (17) Pathin, (18) Śālmalī, (19) Nadī, (20) Asipatravana and (21) Lohadāraka.
iast: lohaśaṅkuṃ ṛjīṣaṃ ca panthānaṃ śālmalīṃ nadīm asipatravanaṃ caiva lohadārakameva ca

sanskrit_iast: etadvidanto vidvāṃso brāhmaṇā brahmavādinaḥ na rājñaḥ pratigṛhṇanti pretya śreyo 'bhikāṅkṣiṇaḥ
meaning: एतद्विदन्तो विद्वांसो ब्राह्मणा ब्रह्मवादिनः न राज्ञः प्रतिगृह्णन्ति प्रेत्य श्रेयो 'भिकाङ्क्षिणः
text: Knowing this, the learned Brāhmaṇas reading the Vedas do not accept gifts from a king, if they desire to secure welfare after death.
iast: etadvidanto vidvāṃso brāhmaṇā brahmavādinaḥ na rājñaḥ pratigṛhṇanti pretya śreyo 'bhikāṅkṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhme muhūrte budhyeta dharmārthau cānucintayet kāyakleśāṃśca tanmūlān vedatattvārthameva ca
meaning: ब्राह्मे मुहूर्ते बुध्येत धर्मार्थौ चानुचिन्तयेत् कायक्लेशांश्च तन्मूलान् वेदतत्त्वार्थमेव च
text: He shall wake up at the point of time sacred to Brahman, and then think over the means of acquiring Merit and wealth, of the bodily troubles involved therein, and also of the true meaning of the Veda.
iast: brāhme muhūrte budhyeta dharmārthau cānucintayet kāyakleśāṃśca tanmūlān vedatattvārthameva ca

sanskrit_iast: utthāyāvaśyakaṃ kṛtvā kṛtaśaucaḥ samāhitaḥ pūrvāṃ saṃdhyāṃ japaṃstiṣṭhet svakāle cāparāṃ ciram
meaning: उत्थायावश्यकं कृत्वा कृतशौचः समाहितः पूर्वां संध्यां जपंस्तिष्ठेत् स्वकाले चापरां चिरम्
text: Having risen, and saving accomplished the necessities of nature, he shall perform the purifications, and, with collected mind, he shall stand, repeating for a long time (the Sāvitrī), during the morning-twilight, as also during the evening-twilight, at its proper time.’
iast: utthāyāvaśyakaṃ kṛtvā kṛtaśaucaḥ samāhitaḥ pūrvāṃ saṃdhyāṃ japaṃstiṣṭhet svakāle cāparāṃ ciram

sanskrit_iast: ṛṣayo dīrghasaṃdhyatvāddīrghamāyuravāpnuyuḥ prajñāṃ yaśaśca kīrtiṃ ca brahmavarcasameva ca
meaning: ऋषयो दीर्घसंध्यत्वाद्दीर्घमायुरवाप्नुयुः प्रज्ञां यशश्च कीर्तिं च ब्रह्मवर्चसमेव च
text: It was by reason or their prolonged twilight-devotions that the sages obtained long life, wisdom, fame, refutation and Brahmic glory.
iast: ṛṣayo dīrghasaṃdhyatvāddīrghamāyuravāpnuyuḥ prajñāṃ yaśaśca kīrtiṃ ca brahmavarcasameva ca

sanskrit_iast: śrāvaṇyāṃ prauṣṭhapadyāṃ vā 'pyupākṛtya yathāvidhi yuktaśchandāṃsyadhīyīta māsān vipro 'rdhapañcamān
meaning: श्रावण्यां प्रौष्ठपद्यां वा 'प्युपाकृत्य यथाविधि युक्तश्छन्दांस्यधीयीत मासान् विप्रो 'र्धपञ्चमान्
text: Having perfomed the “Upākarma” (starting rite) on the full- moon day in the month of Śrāvaṇa or of Bhādrapada, the Brāhmaṇa shall, with due diligence, study the vedas, according to rule, during four months and a half.
iast: śrāvaṇyāṃ prauṣṭhapadyāṃ vā 'pyupākṛtya yathāvidhi yuktaśchandāṃsyadhīyīta māsān vipro 'rdhapañcamān

sanskrit_iast: puṣye tu chandasāṃ kuryādbahirutsarjanaṃ dvijaḥ māghaśuklasya vā prāpte pūrvāhṇe prathame 'hani
meaning: पुष्ये तु छन्दसां कुर्याद्बहिरुत्सर्जनं द्विजः माघशुक्लस्य वा प्राप्ते पूर्वाह्णे प्रथमे 'हनि
text: The Brāhmaṇa shall perform, outside, the “Utsarjana” (Suspension) of the Vedas on the day of the Puṣya asterism, or on the forenoon of the first day of the bright fortnight of the month of Māgha.
iast: puṣye tu chandasāṃ kuryādbahirutsarjanaṃ dvijaḥ māghaśuklasya vā prāpte pūrvāhṇe prathame 'hani

sanskrit_iast: yathāśāstraṃ tu kṛtvaivamutsargaṃ chandasāṃ bahiḥ viramet pakṣiṇīṃ rātriṃ tadevaikamaharṃiśam
meaning: यथाशास्त्रं तु कृत्वैवमुत्सर्गं छन्दसां बहिः विरमेत् पक्षिणीं रात्रिं तदेवैकमहर्ंइशम्
text: Having performed, according to law, the “Utsarga” of the Vedas, he shall cease for the night with its two wings, including the same day and night.
iast: yathāśāstraṃ tu kṛtvaivamutsargaṃ chandasāṃ bahiḥ viramet pakṣiṇīṃ rātriṃ tadevaikamaharṃiśam

sanskrit_iast: ata ūrdhvaṃ tu chandāṃsi śukleṣu niyataḥ paṭhet vedāṅgāni ca sarvāṇi kṛṣṇapakṣeṣu saṃpaṭhet
meaning: अत ऊर्ध्वं तु छन्दांसि शुक्लेषु नियतः पठेत् वेदाङ्गानि च सर्वाणि कृष्णपक्षेषु संपठेत्
text: After this, he shall diligently read the Vedas during the bright fortnights, and all the subsidiary sciences during the dark fortnights.
iast: ata ūrdhvaṃ tu chandāṃsi śukleṣu niyataḥ paṭhet vedāṅgāni ca sarvāṇi kṛṣṇapakṣeṣu saṃpaṭhet

sanskrit_iast: nāvispaṣṭamadhīyīta na śūdrajanasannidhau na niśānte pariśrānto brahmādhītya punaḥ svapet
meaning: नाविस्पष्टमधीयीत न शूद्रजनसन्निधौ न निशान्ते परिश्रान्तो ब्रह्माधीत्य पुनः स्वपेत्
text: He shall recite, not indistinctly, nor in the proximity of Śūdras; nor shall he go to sleep again, at the end of night, when he is tired after having recited the Veda.
iast: nāvispaṣṭamadhīyīta na śūdrajanasannidhau na niśānte pariśrānto brahmādhītya punaḥ svapet

sanskrit_iast: yathauditena vidhinā nityaṃ chandaskṛtaṃ paṭhet brahma chandaskṛtaṃ caiva dvijo yukto hyanāpadi
meaning: यथौदितेन विधिना नित्यं छन्दस्कृतं पठेत् ब्रह्म छन्दस्कृतं चैव द्विजो युक्तो ह्यनापदि
text: According to the prescribed rule, the Brāhmaṇa shall, every day, during normal times, diligently recite the Veda in verse, as also the Veda in verse and prose.
iast: yathauditena vidhinā nityaṃ chandaskṛtaṃ paṭhet brahma chandaskṛtaṃ caiva dvijo yukto hyanāpadi

sanskrit_iast: imānnityamanadhyāyānadhīyāno vivarjayet adhyāpanaṃ ca kurvāṇaḥ śiṣyāṇāṃ vidhipūrvakam
meaning: इमान्नित्यमनध्यायानधीयानो विवर्जयेत् अध्यापनं च कुर्वाणः शिष्याणां विधिपूर्वकम्
text: One who is studying the Veda should always avoid these days, as unfit for study; so also the person who is doing the teaching of pupils according to rule.
iast: imānnityamanadhyāyānadhīyāno vivarjayet adhyāpanaṃ ca kurvāṇaḥ śiṣyāṇāṃ vidhipūrvakam

sanskrit_iast: karṇaśrave 'nile rātrau divā pāṃsusamūhane etau varṣāsvanadhyāyāvadhyāyajñāḥ pracakṣate
meaning: कर्णश्रवे 'निले रात्रौ दिवा पांसुसमूहने एतौ वर्षास्वनध्यायावध्यायज्ञाः प्रचक्षते
text: (a) At night, when air is audible by the ear, and (b) in the day when there is dust-whorl,—these two occasions during the rains are declared by those versed in the rules of teaching to be unfit for study.
iast: karṇaśrave 'nile rātrau divā pāṃsusamūhane etau varṣāsvanadhyāyāvadhyāyajñāḥ pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: vidyutstanitavarṣeṣu mahaulkānāṃ ca saṃplave ākālikamanadhyāyameteṣu manurabravīt
meaning: विद्युत्स्तनितवर्षेषु महौल्कानां च संप्लवे आकालिकमनध्यायमेतेषु मनुरब्रवीत्
text: When there is lightning, thunder and rain,—when there is promiscuous falling of meteors;—on these occasions, there shall be no study till the same time (next day):—thus has Manu declared.
iast: vidyutstanitavarṣeṣu mahaulkānāṃ ca saṃplave ākālikamanadhyāyameteṣu manurabravīt

sanskrit_iast: etāṃstvabhyuditān vidyādyadā prāduṣkṛtāgniṣu tadā vidyādanadhyāyamanṛtau cābhradarśane
meaning: एतांस्त्वभ्युदितान् विद्याद्यदा प्रादुष्कृताग्निषु तदा विद्यादनध्यायमनृतौ चाभ्रदर्शने
text: When these appear after the Fires have been lighted, then is it to be regarded as time unfit for study; as also when clouds are seen out of season.
iast: etāṃstvabhyuditān vidyādyadā prāduṣkṛtāgniṣu tadā vidyādanadhyāyamanṛtau cābhradarśane

sanskrit_iast: nirghāte bhūmicalane jyotiṣāṃ caupasarjane etānākālikān vidyādanadhyāyān ṛtāvapi
meaning: निर्घाते भूमिचलने ज्योतिषां चौपसर्जने एतानाकालिकान् विद्यादनध्यायान् ऋतावपि
text: When there is preter-natural sound, when there is earthquake, and when there is an impact of planets,—these are to be regarded as time unfit for study, until the same hour next day,—even during the season.
iast: nirghāte bhūmicalane jyotiṣāṃ caupasarjane etānākālikān vidyādanadhyāyān ṛtāvapi

sanskrit_iast: prāduṣkṛteṣvagniṣu tu vidyutstanitaniḥsvane sajyotiḥ syādanadhyāyaḥ śeṣe rātrau yathā divā
meaning: प्रादुष्कृतेष्वग्निषु तु विद्युत्स्तनितनिःस्वने सज्योतिः स्यादनध्यायः शेषे रात्रौ यथा दिवा
text: When lightning and the roar of thunder appear after the Fires have been lighted, the time shall continue to be unfit for study, till the lights are there; and when the remaining (phenomenon) ocours, it is unfit for study during the night, as also during the day.
iast: prāduṣkṛteṣvagniṣu tu vidyutstanitaniḥsvane sajyotiḥ syādanadhyāyaḥ śeṣe rātrau yathā divā

sanskrit_iast: nityānadhyāya eva syādgrāmeṣu nagareṣu ca dharmanaipuṇyakāmānāṃ pūtigandhe ca sarvadā sarvaśaḥ
meaning: नित्यानध्याय एव स्याद्ग्रामेषु नगरेषु च धर्मनैपुण्यकामानां पूतिगन्धे च सर्वदा सर्वशः
text: In villages and cities, it is always unfit for study, for those who seek for perfect merit; as also in foul-shelling places.
iast: nityānadhyāya eva syādgrāmeṣu nagareṣu ca dharmanaipuṇyakāmānāṃ pūtigandhe ca sarvadā sarvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: antargataśave grāme vṛṣalasya ca sannidhau anadhyāyo rudyamāne samavāye janasya ca
meaning: अन्तर्गतशवे ग्रामे वृषलस्य च सन्निधौ अनध्यायो रुद्यमाने समवाये जनस्य च
text: In a village where a corpse still lies, in the presence of low people, during weeping, and in a crowd of hen,—it is unfit for study.
iast: antargataśave grāme vṛṣalasya ca sannidhau anadhyāyo rudyamāne samavāye janasya ca

sanskrit_iast: udake madhyarātre ca viṣmūtrasya visarjane ucchiṣṭaḥ śrāddhabhuk caiva manasā 'pi na cintayet
meaning: उदके मध्यरात्रे च विष्मूत्रस्य विसर्जने उच्छिष्टः श्राद्धभुक् चैव मनसा 'पि न चिन्तयेत्
text: In water, at midnight, during the evacuation of the bladder and the bowels, while one is unclean, when one has eaten at a śrāddha, one shall not even think in his mind (of the Veda).
iast: udake madhyarātre ca viṣmūtrasya visarjane ucchiṣṭaḥ śrāddhabhuk caiva manasā 'pi na cintayet

sanskrit_iast: pratigṛhya dvijo vidvānekoddiṣṭasya ketanam ekoddiṣṭaniketanam tryahaṃ na kīrtayedbrahma rājño rāhośca sūtake
meaning: प्रतिगृह्य द्विजो विद्वानेकोद्दिष्टस्य केतनम् एकोद्दिष्टनिकेतनम् त्र्यहं न कीर्तयेद्ब्रह्म राज्ञो राहोश्च सूतके
text: After having accepted invitation to a unitary funeral rite, the learned Brāhmaṇa shall not recite the Veda for three days; as also during the impurity of the king and also of Rāhu.
iast: pratigṛhya dvijo vidvānekoddiṣṭasya ketanam ekoddiṣṭaniketanam tryahaṃ na kīrtayedbrahma rājño rāhośca sūtake

sanskrit_iast: yāvadekānudiṣṭasya gandho lepaśca tiṣṭhati viprasya viduṣo dehe tāvadbrahma na kīrtayet
meaning: यावदेकानुदिष्टस्य गन्धो लेपश्च तिष्ठति विप्रस्य विदुषो देहे तावद्ब्रह्म न कीर्तयेत्
text: While the odour and stain of a funeral rite not offered to his own ancestor remain upon the body of a learned Brāhmaṇa, he shall not recite the Veda.
iast: yāvadekānudiṣṭasya gandho lepaśca tiṣṭhati viprasya viduṣo dehe tāvadbrahma na kīrtayet

sanskrit_iast: śayānaḥ prauḍhapādaśca kṛtvā caivāvasakthikām nādhīyītāmiṣaṃ jagdhvā sūtakānnādyameva ca
meaning: शयानः प्रौढपादश्च कृत्वा चैवावसक्थिकाम् नाधीयीतामिषं जग्ध्वा सूतकान्नाद्यमेव च
text: While lying down, while his feet are prominently thrust forward, and while he is seated with his knees tied together, he shall not study; as also after having eaten meat or food given by a person during impurity.
iast: śayānaḥ prauḍhapādaśca kṛtvā caivāvasakthikām nādhīyītāmiṣaṃ jagdhvā sūtakānnādyameva ca

sanskrit_iast: nīhāre bāṇaśabde ca saṃdhyayoreva caubhayoḥ amāvāsyācaturdaśyoḥ paurṇamāsy 'ṣṭakāsu ca
meaning: नीहारे बाणशब्दे च संध्ययोरेव चौभयोः अमावास्याचतुर्दश्योः पौर्णमास्य् 'ष्टकासु च
text: Nor during a fog, nor during the sound of arrows, nor at the two twilights, nor on the Moonless Day, nor on the fourteenth day, nor on the Full Moon Day, nor on the eighth day.
iast: nīhāre bāṇaśabde ca saṃdhyayoreva caubhayoḥ amāvāsyācaturdaśyoḥ paurṇamāsy 'ṣṭakāsu ca

sanskrit_iast: amāvāsyā guruṃ hanti śiṣyaṃ hanti caturdaśī brahmāṣṭakapaurṇamāsyau tasmāt tāḥ parivarjayet
meaning: अमावास्या गुरुं हन्ति शिष्यं हन्ति चतुर्दशी ब्रह्माष्टकपौर्णमास्यौ तस्मात् ताः परिवर्जयेत्
text: The Moonless Day destroys the Teacher, the Fourteenth Day destroys the pupil, and the Eighth Day and the Full-moon days destroy the Veda; hence these he shall avoid.
iast: amāvāsyā guruṃ hanti śiṣyaṃ hanti caturdaśī brahmāṣṭakapaurṇamāsyau tasmāt tāḥ parivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: pāṃsuvarṣe diśāṃ dāhe gomāyuvirute tathā śvakharauṣṭre ca ruvati paṅktau ca na paṭheddvijaḥ
meaning: पांसुवर्षे दिशां दाहे गोमायुविरुते तथा श्वखरौष्ट्रे च रुवति पङ्क्तौ च न पठेद्द्विजः
text: The Brāhmaṇa shall not read during a dust-storm, or while the sky is burning, or while jackals are howling, or while dogs ur donkeys or camels are crying in a line.
iast: pāṃsuvarṣe diśāṃ dāhe gomāyuvirute tathā śvakharauṣṭre ca ruvati paṅktau ca na paṭheddvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nādhīyīta śmaśānānte grāmānte govraje 'pi vā vasitvā maithunaṃ vāsaḥ śrāddhikaṃ pratigṛhya ca
meaning: नाधीयीत श्मशानान्ते ग्रामान्ते गोव्रजे 'पि वा वसित्वा मैथुनं वासः श्राद्धिकं प्रतिगृह्य च
text: He shall not study near the cremation-ground, nor near the village, nor in the cow-pasture, nor while dressed in the garment worn during sexual intercourse, nor after receiving presents in connection with funeral rites.
iast: nādhīyīta śmaśānānte grāmānte govraje 'pi vā vasitvā maithunaṃ vāsaḥ śrāddhikaṃ pratigṛhya ca

sanskrit_iast: prāṇi vā yadi vā 'prāṇi yat kiṃ citśrāddhikaṃ bhavet tadālabhyāpyanadhyāyaḥ pāṇyāsyo hi dvijaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: प्राणि वा यदि वा 'प्राणि यत् किं चित्श्राद्धिकं भवेत् तदालभ्याप्यनध्यायः पाण्यास्यो हि द्विजः स्मृतः
text: Any śrāddha-gift, be it animate or inanimate, if the Brāhmaṇa accepts, it becomes an occasion unfit for study; because the Brāhmaṇa has been declared as having the hand for his mouth.
iast: prāṇi vā yadi vā 'prāṇi yat kiṃ citśrāddhikaṃ bhavet tadālabhyāpyanadhyāyaḥ pāṇyāsyo hi dvijaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: corairupadrute grāme saṃbhrame cāgnikārite caurairupaplute , saṃbhrame ākālikamanadhyāyaṃ vidyāt sarvādbhuteṣu ca
meaning: चोरैरुपद्रुते ग्रामे संभ्रमे चाग्निकारिते चौरैरुपप्लुते , संभ्रमे आकालिकमनध्यायं विद्यात् सर्वाद्भुतेषु च
text: When the village has bees beset with thieves, when alarm has been caused by fire, it is unfit for study, till the same time next day; as also at the appearance of all portents.
iast: corairupadrute grāme saṃbhrame cāgnikārite caurairupaplute , saṃbhrame ākālikamanadhyāyaṃ vidyāt sarvādbhuteṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: upākarmaṇi cautsarge trirātraṃ kṣepaṇaṃ smṛtam aṣṭakāsu tvahorātraṃ ṛtvantāsu ca rātriṣu
meaning: उपाकर्मणि चौत्सर्गे त्रिरात्रं क्षेपणं स्मृतम् अष्टकासु त्वहोरात्रं ऋत्वन्तासु च रात्रिषु
text: At the ceremony of upākarma and at that of utsarjana, omission of study has been prescribed for three days, but at the Aṣṭakāṣ for one day and night; as also on the last nights of the seasons.
iast: upākarmaṇi cautsarge trirātraṃ kṣepaṇaṃ smṛtam aṣṭakāsu tvahorātraṃ ṛtvantāsu ca rātriṣu

sanskrit_iast: nādhīyītāśvamārūḍho na vṛkṣaṃ na ca hastinam na nāvaṃ na kharaṃ nauṣṭraṃ nairiṇastho na yānagaḥ
meaning: नाधीयीताश्वमारूढो न वृक्षं न च हस्तिनम् न नावं न खरं नौष्ट्रं नैरिणस्थो न यानगः
text: He shall not recite the Veda while seated on a horse, or a tree, or an elephant, or a boat, or a donkey, or a camel; nor when seated on barren ground, nor when going on a conveyance.
iast: nādhīyītāśvamārūḍho na vṛkṣaṃ na ca hastinam na nāvaṃ na kharaṃ nauṣṭraṃ nairiṇastho na yānagaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na vivāde na kalahe na senāyāṃ na saṅgare na bhuktamātre nājīrṇe na vamitvā na śuktake
meaning: न विवादे न कलहे न सेनायां न सङ्गरे न भुक्तमात्रे नाजीर्णे न वमित्वा न शुक्तके
text: Non during an al tercation, ok during a fight; nor in the midst of an army, nor during a battle, nor when he has just eaten, nor during indigestion, nor after vomiting, nor when there is eructation.
iast: na vivāde na kalahe na senāyāṃ na saṅgare na bhuktamātre nājīrṇe na vamitvā na śuktake

sanskrit_iast: atithiṃ cānanujñāpya mārute vāti vā bhṛśam rudhire ca srute gātrātśastreṇa ca parikṣate
meaning: अतिथिं चाननुज्ञाप्य मारुते वाति वा भृशम् रुधिरे च स्रुते गात्रात्शस्त्रेण च परिक्षते
text: Not without having obtained the permission of his guest, nor while the wind blows vehemently; nor when blood has flowed from his body, or his body has been wounded by a weapon.
iast: atithiṃ cānanujñāpya mārute vāti vā bhṛśam rudhire ca srute gātrātśastreṇa ca parikṣate

sanskrit_iast: sāmadhvanāvṛcyajuṣī nādhīyīta kadā cana vedasyādhītya vā 'pyantamāraṇyakamadhītya ca
meaning: सामध्वनावृच्यजुषी नाधीयीत कदा चन वेदस्याधीत्य वा 'प्यन्तमारण्यकमधीत्य च
text: He shall never recite the Ṛk or the Yajuṣ during a Sāma-chant; nor after having read the end of the Veda, or after having read the Āraṇyaka.
iast: sāmadhvanāvṛcyajuṣī nādhīyīta kadā cana vedasyādhītya vā 'pyantamāraṇyakamadhītya ca

sanskrit_iast: ṛgvedo devadaivatyo yajurvedastu mānuṣaḥ sāmavedaḥ smṛtaḥ pitryastasmāt tasyāśucirdhvaniḥ
meaning: ऋग्वेदो देवदैवत्यो यजुर्वेदस्तु मानुषः सामवेदः स्मृतः पित्र्यस्तस्मात् तस्याशुचिर्ध्वनिः
text: The Ṛgveda is sacred to the gods and the Yajurveda is human; the Sāmaveda has been declared to be related to the Pitṛs; hence its sound is impure.
iast: ṛgvedo devadaivatyo yajurvedastu mānuṣaḥ sāmavedaḥ smṛtaḥ pitryastasmāt tasyāśucirdhvaniḥ

sanskrit_iast: etadvidvanto vidvāṃsastrayīniṣkarṣamanvaham kramataḥ pūrvamabhyasya paścādvedamadhīyate
meaning: एतद्विद्वन्तो विद्वांसस्त्रयीनिष्कर्षमन्वहम् क्रमतः पूर्वमभ्यस्य पश्चाद्वेदमधीयते
text: Knowing this, the learned dally recite first the essence of the three Vedas, in due order; and it is only after this that they recite the Veda.
iast: etadvidvanto vidvāṃsastrayīniṣkarṣamanvaham kramataḥ pūrvamabhyasya paścādvedamadhīyate

sanskrit_iast: paśumaṇḍūkamārjāraśvasarpanakulākhubhiḥ antarāgamane vidyādanadhyāyamaharṃiśam
meaning: पशुमण्डूकमार्जारश्वसर्पनकुलाखुभिः अन्तरागमने विद्यादनध्यायमहर्ंइशम्
text: When cattle, a frog, a cat, a dog, a snake, a mongoose or a rat come between,—one should regard the day and night as unfit for study.
iast: paśumaṇḍūkamārjāraśvasarpanakulākhubhiḥ antarāgamane vidyādanadhyāyamaharṃiśam

sanskrit_iast: dvāveva varjayennityamanadhyāyau prayatnataḥ svādhyāyabhūmiṃ cāśuddhamātmānaṃ cāśuciṃ dvijaḥ
meaning: द्वावेव वर्जयेन्नित्यमनध्यायौ प्रयत्नतः स्वाध्यायभूमिं चाशुद्धमात्मानं चाशुचिं द्विजः
text: The twice-born man shall always carefully avoid only two conditions as unfit for study: viz. an unclean place of study and his own unclean condition.
iast: dvāveva varjayennityamanadhyāyau prayatnataḥ svādhyāyabhūmiṃ cāśuddhamātmānaṃ cāśuciṃ dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: amāvāsyāmaṣṭamīṃ ca paurṇamāsīṃ caturdaśīm brahmacārī bhavennityamapy ṛtau snātako dvijaḥ
meaning: अमावास्यामष्टमीं च पौर्णमासीं चतुर्दशीम् ब्रह्मचारी भवेन्नित्यमप्य् ऋतौ स्नातको द्विजः
text: On the Moonless Day, on the eighth, on the Full Moon Day, and on the Fourteenth, the twice-born Accomplished Student shall always remain a “Religious Student,” even during the “season.”
iast: amāvāsyāmaṣṭamīṃ ca paurṇamāsīṃ caturdaśīm brahmacārī bhavennityamapy ṛtau snātako dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na snānamācaredbhuktvā nāturo na mahāniśi na vāsobhiḥ sahājasraṃ nāvijñāte jalāśaye
meaning: न स्नानमाचरेद्भुक्त्वा नातुरो न महानिशि न वासोभिः सहाजस्रं नाविज्ञाते जलाशये
text: He shall not bathe after a meal, nor when he is ill, nor at midnight, nor along with his garments; and never in an unknown water-reservoir.
iast: na snānamācaredbhuktvā nāturo na mahāniśi na vāsobhiḥ sahājasraṃ nāvijñāte jalāśaye

sanskrit_iast: devatānāṃ guro rājñaḥ snātakācāryayostathā nākrāmet kāmataśchāyāṃ babhruṇo dīkṣitasya ca
meaning: देवतानां गुरो राज्ञः स्नातकाचार्ययोस्तथा नाक्रामेत् कामतश्छायां बभ्रुणो दीक्षितस्य च
text: He shall not intentionally step over the shadow of the gods, of a superior, of the king, of an Accomplished Student, of his own Preceptor, of the tawny thing, or of the Initiated Person.
iast: devatānāṃ guro rājñaḥ snātakācāryayostathā nākrāmet kāmataśchāyāṃ babhruṇo dīkṣitasya ca

sanskrit_iast: madhyaṃdine 'rdharātre ca śrāddhaṃ bhuktvā ca sāmiṣam saṃdhyayorubhayoścaiva na seveta catuṣpatham
meaning: मध्यंदिने 'र्धरात्रे च श्राद्धं भुक्त्वा च सामिषम् संध्ययोरुभयोश्चैव न सेवेत चतुष्पथम्
text: He shall not have recourse to a cross-road at midday, or at midnight, or after having partaken of meat-food at a śrāddha, or at the two twilights.
iast: madhyaṃdine 'rdharātre ca śrāddhaṃ bhuktvā ca sāmiṣam saṃdhyayorubhayoścaiva na seveta catuṣpatham

sanskrit_iast: udvartanamapasnānaṃ viṣmūtre raktameva ca śleśmaniṣṭhyūtavāntāni nādhitiṣṭhet tu kāmataḥ
meaning: उद्वर्तनमपस्नानं विष्मूत्रे रक्तमेव च श्लेश्मनिष्ठ्यूतवान्तानि नाधितिष्ठेत् तु कामतः
text: He shall not intentionally stand upon unguent-powder, or upon used water, or on urine or ordure, or on blood, or on mucus, spittings and vomitings.
iast: udvartanamapasnānaṃ viṣmūtre raktameva ca śleśmaniṣṭhyūtavāntāni nādhitiṣṭhet tu kāmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: vairiṇaṃ naupaseveta sahāyaṃ caiva vairiṇaḥ adhārmikaṃ taskaraṃ ca parasyaiva ca yoṣitam
meaning: वैरिणं नौपसेवेत सहायं चैव वैरिणः अधार्मिकं तस्करं च परस्यैव च योषितम्
text: He shall not pay attention to his enemy, or to his enemy’s friend, or to an unrighteous person, or to a thief, or to the lady of another person.
iast: vairiṇaṃ naupaseveta sahāyaṃ caiva vairiṇaḥ adhārmikaṃ taskaraṃ ca parasyaiva ca yoṣitam

sanskrit_iast: na hīidṛśamanāyuṣyaṃ loke kiṃ cana vidyate yādṛśaṃ puruṣasyaiha paradāraupasevanam
meaning: न हीइदृशमनायुष्यं लोके किं चन विद्यते यादृशं पुरुषस्यैह परदारौपसेवनम्
text: In this world, there is nothing so detrimental to a man’s longevity as paying attention to the wife of another person.
iast: na hīidṛśamanāyuṣyaṃ loke kiṃ cana vidyate yādṛśaṃ puruṣasyaiha paradāraupasevanam

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyaṃ caiva sarpaṃ ca brāhmaṇaṃ ca bahuśrutam nāvamanyeta vai bhūṣṇuḥ kṛśānapi kadā cana
meaning: क्षत्रियं चैव सर्पं च ब्राह्मणं च बहुश्रुतम् नावमन्येत वै भूष्णुः कृशानपि कदा चन
text: Desiring his own prosperity, he shall never despise a Kṣatriya, a serpent, a learned Brāhmaṇa,—even if they be feeble.
iast: kṣatriyaṃ caiva sarpaṃ ca brāhmaṇaṃ ca bahuśrutam nāvamanyeta vai bhūṣṇuḥ kṛśānapi kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: etat trayaṃ hi puruṣaṃ nirdahedavamānitam tasmādetat trayaṃ nityaṃ nāvamanyeta buddhimān
meaning: एतत् त्रयं हि पुरुषं निर्दहेदवमानितम् तस्मादेतत् त्रयं नित्यं नावमन्येत बुद्धिमान्
text: Because these three, when despised, may destroy the man,—therefore, the intelligent man shall never despise these three.
iast: etat trayaṃ hi puruṣaṃ nirdahedavamānitam tasmādetat trayaṃ nityaṃ nāvamanyeta buddhimān

sanskrit_iast: nātmānamavamanyeta purvābhirasamṛddhibhiḥ ā mṛtyoḥ śriyamanvicchennaināṃ manyeta durlabhām
meaning: नात्मानमवमन्येत पुर्वाभिरसमृद्धिभिः आ मृत्योः श्रियमन्विच्छेन्नैनां मन्येत दुर्लभाम्
text: He shall not despise himself by reason of former failures. Until death, he should seek fortune, and he should never think her unattainable.
iast: nātmānamavamanyeta purvābhirasamṛddhibhiḥ ā mṛtyoḥ śriyamanvicchennaināṃ manyeta durlabhām

sanskrit_iast: satyaṃ brūyāt priyaṃ brūyānna brūyāt satyamapriyam priyaṃ ca nānṛtaṃ brūyādeṣa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ
meaning: सत्यं ब्रूयात् प्रियं ब्रूयान्न ब्रूयात् सत्यमप्रियम् प्रियं च नानृतं ब्रूयादेष धर्मः सनातनः
text: He shall say what is true; and he shall say what is agreeable; he shall not say what is true, but disagreeale; nor shall he say what is agreeable, but untrue; this is the eternal law.
iast: satyaṃ brūyāt priyaṃ brūyānna brūyāt satyamapriyam priyaṃ ca nānṛtaṃ brūyādeṣa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhadraṃ bhadramiti brūyādbhadramityeva vā vadet śuṣkavairaṃ vivādaṃ ca na kuryāt kena cit saha
meaning: भद्रं भद्रमिति ब्रूयाद्भद्रमित्येव वा वदेत् शुष्कवैरं विवादं च न कुर्यात् केन चित् सह
text: What is (not) well, he shall call “well”; or, he shall simply say “well;” he shall not create needless enmity or dispute with any one.
iast: bhadraṃ bhadramiti brūyādbhadramityeva vā vadet śuṣkavairaṃ vivādaṃ ca na kuryāt kena cit saha

sanskrit_iast: nātikalyaṃ nātisāyaṃ nātimadhyaṃdine sthite nājñātena samaṃ gacchennaiko na vṛṣalaiḥ saha
meaning: नातिकल्यं नातिसायं नातिमध्यंदिने स्थिते नाज्ञातेन समं गच्छेन्नैको न वृषलैः सह
text: He shall not go about either too early in the morning or too late in the evening, or just at midday; nor with an unknown person, nor alone, nor with Śūdras.
iast: nātikalyaṃ nātisāyaṃ nātimadhyaṃdine sthite nājñātena samaṃ gacchennaiko na vṛṣalaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: hīnāṅgānatiriktāṅgān vidyāhīnān vayo 'dhikān vayo'atigān rūpadraviṇahīnāṃśca jātihīnāṃśca nākṣipet rūpadravyahīnāṃśca
meaning: हीनाङ्गानतिरिक्ताङ्गान् विद्याहीनान् वयो 'धिकान् वयो'अतिगान् रूपद्रविणहीनांश्च जातिहीनांश्च नाक्षिपेत् रूपद्रव्यहीनांश्च
text: He shall not insult those who have redundant limbs, or those who are deficient in limbs, or those destitute of learning, or those who are far advanced in age, or those destitute of beauty or wealth, or those of low birth.
iast: hīnāṅgānatiriktāṅgān vidyāhīnān vayo 'dhikān vayo'atigān rūpadraviṇahīnāṃśca jātihīnāṃśca nākṣipet rūpadravyahīnāṃśca

sanskrit_iast: na spṛśet pāṇinaucchiṣṭo vipro gobrāhmaṇānalāṇ na cāpi paśyedaśuciḥ sustho jyotirgaṇān divā svastho jyotirgaṇān divi
meaning: न स्पृशेत् पाणिनौच्छिष्टो विप्रो गोब्राह्मणानलाण् न चापि पश्येदशुचिः सुस्थो ज्योतिर्गणान् दिवा स्वस्थो ज्योतिर्गणान् दिवि
text: A Brāhmaṇa, while unclean, shall hot touch, with his hand, a cow, a Brāhmaṇa or fire. Nor shall he, being in good health, look at the luminaries in the sky, while he is impure.
iast: na spṛśet pāṇinaucchiṣṭo vipro gobrāhmaṇānalāṇ na cāpi paśyedaśuciḥ sustho jyotirgaṇān divā svastho jyotirgaṇān divi

sanskrit_iast: spṛṣṭvaitānaśucirnityamadbhiḥ prāṇānupaspṛśet gātrāṇi caiva sarvāṇi nābhiṃ pāṇitalena tu
meaning: स्पृष्ट्वैतानशुचिर्नित्यमद्भिः प्राणानुपस्पृशेत् गात्राणि चैव सर्वाणि नाभिं पाणितलेन तु
text: Having touched these, while impure, he shall always wipe with water, with his palm, his sense-organs, as also his limbs and the navel.
iast: spṛṣṭvaitānaśucirnityamadbhiḥ prāṇānupaspṛśet gātrāṇi caiva sarvāṇi nābhiṃ pāṇitalena tu

sanskrit_iast: anāturaḥ svāni khāni na spṛśedanimittataḥ romāṇi ca rahasyāni sarvāṇyeva vivarjayet
meaning: अनातुरः स्वानि खानि न स्पृशेदनिमित्ततः रोमाणि च रहस्यानि सर्वाण्येव विवर्जयेत्
text: Unless he be in trouble, he shall not, without cause, touch his cavities. He shall also avoid all secret hairs.
iast: anāturaḥ svāni khāni na spṛśedanimittataḥ romāṇi ca rahasyāni sarvāṇyeva vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: maṅgalācārayuktaḥ syāt prayatātmā jitaindriyaḥ japecca juhuyāccaiva nityamagnimatandritaḥ
meaning: मङ्गलाचारयुक्तः स्यात् प्रयतात्मा जितैन्द्रियः जपेच्च जुहुयाच्चैव नित्यमग्निमतन्द्रितः
text: He shall be intent upon auspicious customs, with his mind under control and the senses subjugated; and he shall, untired, daily recite prayers and offer oblations into the fire.
iast: maṅgalācārayuktaḥ syāt prayatātmā jitaindriyaḥ japecca juhuyāccaiva nityamagnimatandritaḥ

sanskrit_iast: maṅgalācārayuktānāṃ nityaṃ ca prayatātmanām japatāṃ juhvatāṃ caiva vinipāto na vidyate
meaning: मङ्गलाचारयुक्तानां नित्यं च प्रयतात्मनाम् जपतां जुह्वतां चैव विनिपातो न विद्यते
text: For those who are ever intent upon auspicious customs, who have their mind under control, and who go on reciting prayers and offering oblations,—there is no calamity.
iast: maṅgalācārayuktānāṃ nityaṃ ca prayatātmanām japatāṃ juhvatāṃ caiva vinipāto na vidyate

sanskrit_iast: vedamevābhyasennityaṃ yathākālamatandritaḥ vedameva japen taṃ hyasyāhuḥ paraṃ dharmamupadharmo 'nya ucyate
meaning: वेदमेवाभ्यसेन्नित्यं यथाकालमतन्द्रितः वेदमेव जपेन् तं ह्यस्याहुः परं धर्ममुपधर्मो 'न्य उच्यते
text: He shall diligently recite the Veda, whenever he finds time. They declare this to be his primary duty; everything else is declared to be his secondary duty.
iast: vedamevābhyasennityaṃ yathākālamatandritaḥ vedameva japen taṃ hyasyāhuḥ paraṃ dharmamupadharmo 'nya ucyate

sanskrit_iast: vedābhyāsena satataṃ śaucena tapasaiva ca adroheṇa ca bhūtānāṃ jātiṃ smarati paurvikīm
meaning: वेदाभ्यासेन सततं शौचेन तपसैव च अद्रोहेण च भूतानां जातिं स्मरति पौर्विकीम्
text: By the constant recitation of the Veda, by purification, by austerity, and by doing no harm to living beings, he remembers his previous birth.
iast: vedābhyāsena satataṃ śaucena tapasaiva ca adroheṇa ca bhūtānāṃ jātiṃ smarati paurvikīm

sanskrit_iast: paurvikīṃ saṃsmaran jātiṃ brahmaivābhyasyate punaḥ dvijaḥ brahmābhyāsena cājasramanantaṃ sukhamaśnute
meaning: पौर्विकीं संस्मरन् जातिं ब्रह्मैवाभ्यस्यते पुनः द्विजः ब्रह्माभ्यासेन चाजस्रमनन्तं सुखमश्नुते
text: Recollecting his former births, the twice-born person continues to study the Veda; and by his continuous study of the Veda, he obtains absolute, endless bliss.
iast: paurvikīṃ saṃsmaran jātiṃ brahmaivābhyasyate punaḥ dvijaḥ brahmābhyāsena cājasramanantaṃ sukhamaśnute

sanskrit_iast: sāvitrāṃśāntihomāṃśca kuryāt parvasu nityaśaḥ sāvitrān śāntihomāṃś pitr̥̄ṃścaivāṣṭakāsvarcennityamanvaṣṭakāsu ca
meaning: सावित्रांशान्तिहोमांश्च कुर्यात् पर्वसु नित्यशः सावित्रान् शान्तिहोमांश् पित्र्̥̄ंश्चैवाष्टकास्वर्चेन्नित्यमन्वष्टकासु च
text: On special days, he shall always offer the oblations to Savitṛ, and also the Pacificatory Oblations; on the “Aṣtakas” and on the “Anvaṣṭakas” he shall always worship the Pitṛs.’
iast: sāvitrāṃśāntihomāṃśca kuryāt parvasu nityaśaḥ sāvitrān śāntihomāṃś pitr̥̄ṃścaivāṣṭakāsvarcennityamanvaṣṭakāsu ca

sanskrit_iast: dūrādāvasathān mūtraṃ dūrāt pādāvasecanam ucchiṣtānnaniṣekaṃ ca dūrādeva samācaret
meaning: दूरादावसथान् मूत्रं दूरात् पादावसेचनम् उच्छिष्तान्ननिषेकं च दूरादेव समाचरेत्
text: Far from the dwelling-place shall he perform urination, far off he shall perform feet-washing, and far away he shall do the throwing of the leavings of food.
iast: dūrādāvasathān mūtraṃ dūrāt pādāvasecanam ucchiṣtānnaniṣekaṃ ca dūrādeva samācaret

sanskrit_iast: maitraṃ prasādhanaṃ snānaṃ dantadhāvanamañjanam pūrvāhṇa eva kurvīta devatānāṃ ca pūjanam
meaning: मैत्रं प्रसाधनं स्नानं दन्तधावनमञ्जनम् पूर्वाह्ण एव कुर्वीत देवतानां च पूजनम्
text: In the forenoon, he shall perform the evacuation of bow els, toilette, bath, cleaning of the teeth, dying of the eyes, and the worship of the gods.
iast: maitraṃ prasādhanaṃ snānaṃ dantadhāvanamañjanam pūrvāhṇa eva kurvīta devatānāṃ ca pūjanam

sanskrit_iast: daivatānyabhigacchet tu dhārmikāṃśca dvijottamān īśvaraṃ caiva rakṣārthaṃ gurūneva ca parvasu
meaning: दैवतान्यभिगच्छेत् तु धार्मिकांश्च द्विजोत्तमान् ईश्वरं चैव रक्षार्थं गुरूनेव च पर्वसु
text: He shall, in his action, diligently attend to right conduct which is the root of Righteousness ordained in the Śruti and Smṛti.
iast: daivatānyabhigacchet tu dhārmikāṃśca dvijottamān īśvaraṃ caiva rakṣārthaṃ gurūneva ca parvasu

sanskrit_iast: abhivādayedvṛddhāṃśca dadyāccaivāsanaṃ svakam kṛtāñjalirupāsīta gacchataḥ pṛṣṭhato 'nviyāt
meaning: अभिवादयेद्वृद्धांश्च दद्याच्चैवासनं स्वकम् कृताञ्जलिरुपासीत गच्छतः पृष्ठतो 'न्वियात्
text: He shall, in his action, diligently attend to right conduct which is the root of Righteousness ordained in the Śruti and Smṛti.
iast: abhivādayedvṛddhāṃśca dadyāccaivāsanaṃ svakam kṛtāñjalirupāsīta gacchataḥ pṛṣṭhato 'nviyāt

sanskrit_iast: śrutismṛtyoditaṃ samyalg nibaddhaṃ sveṣu karmasu dharmamūlaṃ niṣeveta sadācāramatandritaḥ
meaning: श्रुतिस्मृत्योदितं सम्यल्ग् निबद्धं स्वेषु कर्मसु धर्ममूलं निषेवेत सदाचारमतन्द्रितः
text: He shall, in his action, diligently attend to right conduct which is the root of Righteousness ordained in the Śruti and Smṛti.
iast: śrutismṛtyoditaṃ samyalg nibaddhaṃ sveṣu karmasu dharmamūlaṃ niṣeveta sadācāramatandritaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ācārātlabhate hyāyurācārādīpsitāḥ prajāḥ ācārāddhanamakṣayyamācāro hantyalakṣaṇam
meaning: आचारात्लभते ह्यायुराचारादीप्सिताः प्रजाः आचाराद्धनमक्षय्यमाचारो हन्त्यलक्षणम्
text: By Right Conduct he attains longevity; by Right Conduct he obtains desirable children; by Right Conduct he obtains inexhaustible wealth; and Right Conduct destroys everything inauspicious.
iast: ācārātlabhate hyāyurācārādīpsitāḥ prajāḥ ācārāddhanamakṣayyamācāro hantyalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: durācāro hi puruṣo loke bhavati ninditaḥ duḥkhabhāgī ca satataṃ vyādhito 'lpāyureva ca
meaning: दुराचारो हि पुरुषो लोके भवति निन्दितः दुःखभागी च सततं व्याधितो 'ल्पायुरेव च
text: Even though devoid of all auspicious marks, the man who follows Right Conduct, has faith and is free from jealousy, lives for a hundred years
iast: durācāro hi puruṣo loke bhavati ninditaḥ duḥkhabhāgī ca satataṃ vyādhito 'lpāyureva ca

sanskrit_iast: sarvalakṣaṇahīno 'pi yaḥ sadācāravānnaraḥ śraddadhāno 'nasūyaśca śataṃ varṣāṇi jīvati
meaning: सर्वलक्षणहीनो 'पि यः सदाचारवान्नरः श्रद्दधानो 'नसूयश्च शतं वर्षाणि जीवति
text: Even though devoid of all auspicious marks, the man who follows Right Conduct, has faith and is free from jealousy, lives for a hundred years
iast: sarvalakṣaṇahīno 'pi yaḥ sadācāravānnaraḥ śraddadhāno 'nasūyaśca śataṃ varṣāṇi jīvati

sanskrit_iast: yadyat paravaśaṃ karma tat tadyatnena varjayet yadyadātmavaśaṃ tu syāt tat tat seveta yatnataḥ
meaning: यद्यत् परवशं कर्म तत् तद्यत्नेन वर्जयेत् यद्यदात्मवशं तु स्यात् तत् तत् सेवेत यत्नतः
text: He shall carefully avoid every such act as is dependent upon others; such acts as are dependent upon himself, every one of these he shall eagerly pursue.
iast: yadyat paravaśaṃ karma tat tadyatnena varjayet yadyadātmavaśaṃ tu syāt tat tat seveta yatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ paravaśaṃ duḥkhaṃ sarvamātmavaśaṃ sukham etadvidyāt samāsena lakṣaṇaṃ sukhaduḥkhayoḥ
meaning: सर्वं परवशं दुःखं सर्वमात्मवशं सुखम् एतद्विद्यात् समासेन लक्षणं सुखदुःखयोः
text: All that is dependent on others is painful; all that is dependent on oneself is pleasing; he shall know this to be, in short, the definition of pleasure and pain.
iast: sarvaṃ paravaśaṃ duḥkhaṃ sarvamātmavaśaṃ sukham etadvidyāt samāsena lakṣaṇaṃ sukhaduḥkhayoḥ

sanskrit_iast: yat karma kurvato 'sya syāt paritoṣo 'ntarātmanaḥ tat prayatnena kurvīta viparītaṃ tu varjayet
meaning: यत् कर्म कुर्वतो 'स्य स्यात् परितोषो 'न्तरात्मनः तत् प्रयत्नेन कुर्वीत विपरीतं तु वर्जयेत्
text: He shall perform, with diligence that act, by performing which there arises satisfaction in his inner soul; and he shall avoid the contrary.
iast: yat karma kurvato 'sya syāt paritoṣo 'ntarātmanaḥ tat prayatnena kurvīta viparītaṃ tu varjayet

sanskrit_iast: ācāryaṃ ca pravaktāraṃ pitaraṃ mātaraṃ gurum na hiṃsyādbrāhmaṇān gāśca sarvāṃścaiva tapasvinaḥ
meaning: आचार्यं च प्रवक्तारं पितरं मातरं गुरुम् न हिंस्याद्ब्राह्मणान् गाश्च सर्वांश्चैव तपस्विनः
text: He shall not injure his Preceptor, or Teacher or Father, or mother, or another elder, or Brāhmaṇas, or Cows, or any persons performing austerities.
iast: ācāryaṃ ca pravaktāraṃ pitaraṃ mātaraṃ gurum na hiṃsyādbrāhmaṇān gāśca sarvāṃścaiva tapasvinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāstikyaṃ vedanindāṃ ca devatānāṃ ca kutsanam dveṣaṃ dambhaṃ ca mānaṃ ca krodhaṃ taikṣṇyaṃ ca varjayet dveṣaṃ stambhaṃ ca
meaning: नास्तिक्यं वेदनिन्दां च देवतानां च कुत्सनम् द्वेषं दम्भं च मानं च क्रोधं तैक्ष्ण्यं च वर्जयेत् द्वेषं स्तम्भं च
text: He shall, avoid atheism, cavilling at the Vedas, abusing of the gods, hatred, haughtiness, pride, anger and hastiness.
iast: nāstikyaṃ vedanindāṃ ca devatānāṃ ca kutsanam dveṣaṃ dambhaṃ ca mānaṃ ca krodhaṃ taikṣṇyaṃ ca varjayet dveṣaṃ stambhaṃ ca

sanskrit_iast: parasya daṇḍaṃ naudyacchet kruddho nainaṃ nipātayet anyatra putrātśiṣyādvā śiṣṭiarthaṃ tāḍayet tu tau
meaning: परस्य दण्डं नौद्यच्छेत् क्रुद्धो नैनं निपातयेत् अन्यत्र पुत्रात्शिष्याद्वा शिष्टिअर्थं ताडयेत् तु तौ
text: He shall not, when angry, raise the rod against another person, nor shall he let it fall; except in the case of the son or the pupil; these two he may beat with a view to correction.
iast: parasya daṇḍaṃ naudyacchet kruddho nainaṃ nipātayet anyatra putrātśiṣyādvā śiṣṭiarthaṃ tāḍayet tu tau

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇāyāvaguryaiva dvijātirvadhakāmyayā śataṃ varṣāṇi tāmisre narake parivartate
meaning: ब्राह्मणायावगुर्यैव द्विजातिर्वधकाम्यया शतं वर्षाणि तामिस्रे नरके परिवर्तते
text: The twice-born person who threatens a Brāhmaṇa, with the intention of striking him, wanders about in the tāmisra hell for a hundred years.
iast: brāhmaṇāyāvaguryaiva dvijātirvadhakāmyayā śataṃ varṣāṇi tāmisre narake parivartate

sanskrit_iast: tāḍayitvā tṛṇenāpi saṃrambhātmatipūrvakam ekaviṃśatīmājātīḥ pāpayoniṣu jāyate
meaning: ताडयित्वा तृणेनापि संरम्भात्मतिपूर्वकम् एकविंशतीमाजातीः पापयोनिषु जायते
text: Having, in anger, struck him intentionally, even with a straw, he is born, during twenty-one births, in sinful wombs.
iast: tāḍayitvā tṛṇenāpi saṃrambhātmatipūrvakam ekaviṃśatīmājātīḥ pāpayoniṣu jāyate

sanskrit_iast: ayudhyamānasyautpādya brāhmaṇasyāsṛgaṅgataḥ duḥkhaṃ sumahadāpnoti pretyāprājñatayā naraḥ
meaning: अयुध्यमानस्यौत्पाद्य ब्राह्मणस्यासृगङ्गतः दुःखं सुमहदाप्नोति प्रेत्याप्राज्ञतया नरः
text: The man, who, through folly, causes blood to flow from the body or a Brāhmaṇa who is not fighting, undergoes, after death, very great suffrring.
iast: ayudhyamānasyautpādya brāhmaṇasyāsṛgaṅgataḥ duḥkhaṃ sumahadāpnoti pretyāprājñatayā naraḥ

sanskrit_iast: śoṇitaṃ yāvataḥ pāṃsūn saṅgṛhṇāti mahītalāt tāvato 'bdānamutrānyaiḥ śoṇitautpādako 'dyate
meaning: शोणितं यावतः पांसून् सङ्गृह्णाति महीतलात् तावतो 'ब्दानमुत्रान्यैः शोणितौत्पादको 'द्यते
text: As many particles of dust the blood takes up from the ground, during so many years is the blood-spiller devoured by others in the other world.
iast: śoṇitaṃ yāvataḥ pāṃsūn saṅgṛhṇāti mahītalāt tāvato 'bdānamutrānyaiḥ śoṇitautpādako 'dyate

sanskrit_iast: na kadā ciddvije tasmādvidvānavaguredapi na tāḍayet tṛṇenāpi na gātrāt srāvayedasṛk
meaning: न कदा चिद्द्विजे तस्माद्विद्वानवगुरेदपि न ताडयेत् तृणेनापि न गात्रात् स्रावयेदसृक्
text: For these reasons, the learned man shall never even threaten a twice-born person; he shall not strike him even with a straw; and he shall not spill blood from his body.
iast: na kadā ciddvije tasmādvidvānavaguredapi na tāḍayet tṛṇenāpi na gātrāt srāvayedasṛk

sanskrit_iast: adhārmiko naro yo hi yasya cāpyanṛtaṃ dhanam hiṃsārataśca yo nityaṃ naihāsau sukhamedhate iṃsāratiś
meaning: अधार्मिको नरो यो हि यस्य चाप्यनृतं धनम् हिंसारतश्च यो नित्यं नैहासौ सुखमेधते इंसारतिश्
text: The man who is unrighteous, he whose wealth is mis-begotten, and he who is always addicted to injuring, never obtains happiness in this world.
iast: adhārmiko naro yo hi yasya cāpyanṛtaṃ dhanam hiṃsārataśca yo nityaṃ naihāsau sukhamedhate iṃsāratiś

sanskrit_iast: na sīdannapi dharmeṇa mano 'dharme niveśayet adhārmikānāṃ pāpānāmāśu paśyan viparyayam
meaning: न सीदन्नपि धर्मेण मनो 'धर्मे निवेशयेत् अधार्मिकानां पापानामाशु पश्यन् विपर्ययम्
text: Perceiving the quick overthrow of unrighteous sinners, he shall not turn his mind towards unrighteousness, even though suffering in consequence of righteonsness.
iast: na sīdannapi dharmeṇa mano 'dharme niveśayet adhārmikānāṃ pāpānāmāśu paśyan viparyayam

sanskrit_iast: nādharmaścarito loke sadyaḥ phalati gauriva śanairāvartyamānastu karturmūlāni kṛntati
meaning: नाधर्मश्चरितो लोके सद्यः फलति गौरिव शनैरावर्त्यमानस्तु कर्तुर्मूलानि कृन्तति
text: Unrighteousness, practised in this world, does not, like the Earth (or the Cow) bring its fruit immediately; but, accumulating gradually, it outs off the roots of the perpetrator.
iast: nādharmaścarito loke sadyaḥ phalati gauriva śanairāvartyamānastu karturmūlāni kṛntati

sanskrit_iast: yadi nātmani putreṣu na cet putreṣu naptṛṣu na tveva tu kṛto 'dharmaḥ karturbhavati niṣphalaḥ kṛto dharmaḥ ?
meaning: यदि नात्मनि पुत्रेषु न चेत् पुत्रेषु नप्तृषु न त्वेव तु कृतो 'धर्मः कर्तुर्भवति निष्फलः कृतो धर्मः ?
text: If not on himself, then on his sons,—if not on his sons, then on his grandsons (falls the punishment); an unrighteousness, once committed, never fails to bring its consequences to the perpetrator.
iast: yadi nātmani putreṣu na cet putreṣu naptṛṣu na tveva tu kṛto 'dharmaḥ karturbhavati niṣphalaḥ kṛto dharmaḥ ?

sanskrit_iast: adharmeṇaidhate tāvat tato bhadrāṇi paśyati tataḥ sapatnān jayati samūlastu vinaśyati
meaning: अधर्मेणैधते तावत् ततो भद्राणि पश्यति ततः सपत्नान् जयति समूलस्तु विनश्यति
text: For a time one prospers through unrighteousness, for a while he experiences good things, and for a time he conquers his enemies; but, after all, he perishes root and branch.
iast: adharmeṇaidhate tāvat tato bhadrāṇi paśyati tataḥ sapatnān jayati samūlastu vinaśyati

sanskrit_iast: satyadharmāryavṛtteṣu śauce caivāramet sadā śiṣyāṃśca śiṣyāddharmeṇa vācbāhūdarasaṃyataḥ
meaning: सत्यधर्मार्यवृत्तेषु शौचे चैवारमेत् सदा शिष्यांश्च शिष्याद्धर्मेण वाच्बाहूदरसंयतः
text: He shall always delight in truthfulness, Law and right conduct, as also in ceanliness (cleanliness?); he shall govern his pupils in accordance with law,—with his speech arms, and belly duly controlled.
iast: satyadharmāryavṛtteṣu śauce caivāramet sadā śiṣyāṃśca śiṣyāddharmeṇa vācbāhūdarasaṃyataḥ

sanskrit_iast: parityajedarthakāmau yau syātāṃ dharmavarjitau dharmaṃ cāpyasukhaudarkaṃ lokasaṅkruṣṭameva ca
meaning: परित्यजेदर्थकामौ यौ स्यातां धर्मवर्जितौ धर्मं चाप्यसुखौदर्कं लोकसङ्क्रुष्टमेव च
text: He shall, avoid such wealth and pleasures as are opposed to righteousness, as also righteousness if it be conducive to unhappiness, or disapproved by the people.
iast: parityajedarthakāmau yau syātāṃ dharmavarjitau dharmaṃ cāpyasukhaudarkaṃ lokasaṅkruṣṭameva ca

sanskrit_iast: na pāṇipādacapalo na netracapalo 'nṛjuḥ na syādvākcapalaścaiva na paradrohakarmadhīḥ
meaning: न पाणिपादचपलो न नेत्रचपलो 'नृजुः न स्याद्वाक्चपलश्चैव न परद्रोहकर्मधीः
text: He shall not be fickle with his hands and feet, nor fickle with his eyes, nor crooked, nor fickle with his speech, nor harm others by deed or thought.
iast: na pāṇipādacapalo na netracapalo 'nṛjuḥ na syādvākcapalaścaiva na paradrohakarmadhīḥ

sanskrit_iast: yenāsya pitaro yātā yena yātāḥ pitāmahāḥ tena yāyāt satāṃ mārgaṃ tena gacchanna riṣyati
meaning: येनास्य पितरो याता येन याताः पितामहाः तेन यायात् सतां मार्गं तेन गच्छन्न रिष्यति
text: He shall tread the path of the righteous by the same way in which his fathers and grandfathers have trodden; going by that way, he shall not suffer.
iast: yenāsya pitaro yātā yena yātāḥ pitāmahāḥ tena yāyāt satāṃ mārgaṃ tena gacchanna riṣyati

sanskrit_iast: ṛtvikpurohitācāryairmātulātithisaṃśritaiḥ bālavṛddhāturairvaidyairjñātisaṃbandhibāndhavaiḥ
meaning: ऋत्विक्पुरोहिताचार्यैर्मातुलातिथिसंश्रितैः बालवृद्धातुरैर्वैद्यैर्ज्ञातिसंबन्धिबान्धवैः
text: He shall not have a quarrel with the officiating priest, the priest, or his Teacher, or with his maternal uncle, or his guests or dependants, with children, or with old or sick persons, or with doctors, with his paternal relations or marriage relations, with his parents, or female relations, or brother or son, or wife, or daughter, or with his slaves.
iast: ṛtvikpurohitācāryairmātulātithisaṃśritaiḥ bālavṛddhāturairvaidyairjñātisaṃbandhibāndhavaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: mātāpitṛbhyāṃ jāmībhirbhrātrā putreṇa bhāryayā duhitrā dāsavargeṇa vivādaṃ na samācaret
meaning: मातापितृभ्यां जामीभिर्भ्रात्रा पुत्रेण भार्यया दुहित्रा दासवर्गेण विवादं न समाचरेत्
text: He shall not have a quarrel with the officiating priest, the priest, or his Teacher, or with his maternal uncle, or his guests or dependants, with children, or with old or sick persons, or with doctors, with his paternal relations or marriage relations, with his parents, or female relations, or brother or son, or wife, or daughter, or with his slaves.
iast: mātāpitṛbhyāṃ jāmībhirbhrātrā putreṇa bhāryayā duhitrā dāsavargeṇa vivādaṃ na samācaret

sanskrit_iast: etairvivādān saṃtyajya sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate etairjitaiśca jayati sarvānlokānimān gṛhī
meaning: एतैर्विवादान् संत्यज्य सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते एतैर्जितैश्च जयति सर्वान्लोकानिमान् गृही
text: Having renounced quarrel with these, the householder becomes freed from all sins; and, by ignoring them, he wins all these worlds.
iast: etairvivādān saṃtyajya sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate etairjitaiśca jayati sarvānlokānimān gṛhī

sanskrit_iast: ācāryo brahmalokaiśaḥ prājāpatye pitā prabhuḥ atithistvindralokaiśo devalokasya caṛtvijaḥ
meaning: आचार्यो ब्रह्मलोकैशः प्राजापत्ये पिता प्रभुः अतिथिस्त्विन्द्रलोकैशो देवलोकस्य चऋत्विजः
text: The teacher is the lord of the world of Brahmā; the Father is sovereign of the world of Prajāpati; the Guest is the master of the world of Indra; and the Priests are the lords of the world of gods.
iast: ācāryo brahmalokaiśaḥ prājāpatye pitā prabhuḥ atithistvindralokaiśo devalokasya caṛtvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jāmayo 'psarasāṃ loke vaiśvadevasya bāndhavāḥ saṃbandhino hyapāṃ loke pṛthivyāṃ mātṛmātulau
meaning: जामयो 'प्सरसां लोके वैश्वदेवस्य बान्धवाः संबन्धिनो ह्यपां लोके पृथिव्यां मातृमातुलौ
text: Female relatives (have power) over the region of the Heavenly nymphs; maternal relations, over that of the Viśvedevas; marriage-relations, over the region of the Waters; the mother and maternal uncle, over the Earth; the children, the aged, the emaciated and the sick should be regarded as the lords of Ākāśa; the elder brother is equal to the Father; the wife and the son are one’s own body.
iast: jāmayo 'psarasāṃ loke vaiśvadevasya bāndhavāḥ saṃbandhino hyapāṃ loke pṛthivyāṃ mātṛmātulau

sanskrit_iast: ākāśaiśāstu vijñeyā bālavṛddhakṛśāturāḥ bhrātā jyeṣṭhaḥ samaḥ pitrā bhāryā putraḥ svakā tanuḥ
meaning: आकाशैशास्तु विज्ञेया बालवृद्धकृशातुराः भ्राता ज्येष्ठः समः पित्रा भार्या पुत्रः स्वका तनुः
text: Female relatives (have power) over the region of the Heavenly nymphs; maternal relations, over that of the Viśvedevas; marriage-relations, over the region of the Waters; the mother and maternal uncle, over the Earth; the children, the aged, the emaciated and the sick should be regarded as the lords of Ākāśa; the elder brother is equal to the Father; the wife and the son are one’s own body.
iast: ākāśaiśāstu vijñeyā bālavṛddhakṛśāturāḥ bhrātā jyeṣṭhaḥ samaḥ pitrā bhāryā putraḥ svakā tanuḥ

sanskrit_iast: chāyā svo dāsavargaśca duhitā kṛpaṇaṃ param tasmādetairadhikṣiptaḥ sahetāsañjvaraḥ sadā
meaning: छाया स्वो दासवर्गश्च दुहिता कृपणं परम् तस्मादेतैरधिक्षिप्तः सहेतासञ्ज्वरः सदा
text: Slaves are one’s own shadow; the daughter is the highest object of tenderness. For these reasons, when offended by these, he shall always bear it without heat.
iast: chāyā svo dāsavargaśca duhitā kṛpaṇaṃ param tasmādetairadhikṣiptaḥ sahetāsañjvaraḥ sadā

sanskrit_iast: pratigrahasamartho 'pi prasaṅgaṃ tatra varjayet pratigraheṇa hyasyāśu brāhmaṃ tejaḥ praśāmyati
meaning: प्रतिग्रहसमर्थो 'पि प्रसङ्गं तत्र वर्जयेत् प्रतिग्रहेण ह्यस्याशु ब्राह्मं तेजः प्रशाम्यति
text: Though entitled to accept presents, he shall avoid addiction to it; because, by receiving gifts, his spiritual light is quickly extinguished.
iast: pratigrahasamartho 'pi prasaṅgaṃ tatra varjayet pratigraheṇa hyasyāśu brāhmaṃ tejaḥ praśāmyati

sanskrit_iast: na dravyāṇāmavijñāya vidhiṃ dharmyaṃ pratigrahe prājñaḥ pratigrahaṃ kuryādavasīdannapi kṣudhā
meaning: न द्रव्याणामविज्ञाय विधिं धर्म्यं प्रतिग्रहे प्राज्ञः प्रतिग्रहं कुर्यादवसीदन्नपि क्षुधा
text: Withuot knowing the lawful method of receiving gifts, the wise man shall not accept any gifts; even though he may be pining with hunger.
iast: na dravyāṇāmavijñāya vidhiṃ dharmyaṃ pratigrahe prājñaḥ pratigrahaṃ kuryādavasīdannapi kṣudhā

sanskrit_iast: hiraṇyaṃ bhūmimaśvaṃ gāmannaṃ vāsastilān ghṛtam pratigṛhṇannavidvāṃstu bhasmībhavati dāruvat
meaning: हिरण्यं भूमिमश्वं गामन्नं वासस्तिलान् घृतम् प्रतिगृह्णन्नविद्वांस्तु भस्मीभवति दारुवत्
text: The illiterate person, accepting gold, land, horse, cow food, clothing, sesamum and clarified butter, becomes reduced to ashes, like wood.
iast: hiraṇyaṃ bhūmimaśvaṃ gāmannaṃ vāsastilān ghṛtam pratigṛhṇannavidvāṃstu bhasmībhavati dāruvat

sanskrit_iast: hiraṇyamāyurannaṃ ca bhūrgauścāpyoṣatastanum aśvaścakṣustvacaṃ vāso ghṛtaṃ tejastilāḥ prajāḥ
meaning: हिरण्यमायुरन्नं च भूर्गौश्चाप्योषतस्तनुम् अश्वश्चक्षुस्त्वचं वासो घृतं तेजस्तिलाः प्रजाः
text: Gold and food destroy longevity; land and cow destroy the body; the horse destroys the eye; clothing destroys the skin; clarified butter destroys energy; and sesamum destroys offspring.
