iast: hiraṇyamāyurannaṃ ca bhūrgauścāpyoṣatastanum aśvaścakṣustvacaṃ vāso ghṛtaṃ tejastilāḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: atapāstvanadhīyānaḥ pratigraharucirdvijaḥ ambhasyaśmaplavenaiva saha tenaiva majjati
meaning: अतपास्त्वनधीयानः प्रतिग्रहरुचिर्द्विजः अम्भस्यश्मप्लवेनैव सह तेनैव मज्जति
text: If the twice-born person,who is without austerities and does not study the Veda, seeks for gifts, he sinks along with him into water; just like one who sinks along with the stone-raft.
iast: atapāstvanadhīyānaḥ pratigraharucirdvijaḥ ambhasyaśmaplavenaiva saha tenaiva majjati

sanskrit_iast: tasmādavidvān bibhiyādyasmāt tasmāt pratigrahāt svalpakenāpyavidvān hi paṅke gauriva sīdati
meaning: तस्मादविद्वान् बिभियाद्यस्मात् तस्मात् प्रतिग्रहात् स्वल्पकेनाप्यविद्वान् हि पङ्के गौरिव सीदति
text: For these reasons, the illiterate man should fight shy of accepting each and every gift. By receiving even a small gift, the illiterate person sinks, like the cow in a morass.
iast: tasmādavidvān bibhiyādyasmāt tasmāt pratigrahāt svalpakenāpyavidvān hi paṅke gauriva sīdati

sanskrit_iast: na vāryapi prayacchet tu baiḍālavratike dvije na bakavratike pāpe nāvedavidi dharmavit
meaning: न वार्यपि प्रयच्छेत् तु बैडालव्रतिके द्विजे न बकव्रतिके पापे नावेदविदि धर्मवित्
text: The man knowing the Law, shall not offer even water to the Brāhmaṇa who behaves like a cat; nor to the wicked Brāhmaṇa who behaves like a heron, nor to one ignorant of the Veda.
iast: na vāryapi prayacchet tu baiḍālavratike dvije na bakavratike pāpe nāvedavidi dharmavit

sanskrit_iast: triṣvapyeteṣu dattaṃ hi vidhinā 'pyarjitaṃ dhanam dāturbhavatyanarthāya paratrādātureva ca
meaning: त्रिष्वप्येतेषु दत्तं हि विधिना 'प्यर्जितं धनम् दातुर्भवत्यनर्थाय परत्रादातुरेव च
text: For property, even though acquired by lawful means,—if given to these three persons,—brings calamity, in the next world, to the giver, as also to the receiver.
iast: triṣvapyeteṣu dattaṃ hi vidhinā 'pyarjitaṃ dhanam dāturbhavatyanarthāya paratrādātureva ca

sanskrit_iast: yathā plavenaupalena nimajjatyudake taran tathā nimajjato 'dhastādajñau dātṛpratīcchakau
meaning: यथा प्लवेनौपलेन निमज्जत्युदके तरन् तथा निमज्जतो 'धस्तादज्ञौ दातृप्रतीच्छकौ
text: Just as a man crossing water by means of a stone-raft sinks down, so also sink downwards the ignorant giver and receiver.
iast: yathā plavenaupalena nimajjatyudake taran tathā nimajjato 'dhastādajñau dātṛpratīcchakau

sanskrit_iast: dharmadhvajī sadā lubdhaśchādmiko lokadambhakaḥ baiḍālavratiko jñeyo hiṃsraḥ sarvābhisaṃdhakaḥ
meaning: धर्मध्वजी सदा लुब्धश्छाद्मिको लोकदम्भकः बैडालव्रतिको ज्ञेयो हिंस्रः सर्वाभिसंधकः
text: He who displays his flag of virtue, is ever covetous, a cheat and a hypocrite, intent on doing harm, and the traducer of all persons, is to be known as “one behaving like the cat.”
iast: dharmadhvajī sadā lubdhaśchādmiko lokadambhakaḥ baiḍālavratiko jñeyo hiṃsraḥ sarvābhisaṃdhakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: adhodṛṣṭirnaiṣkṛtikaḥ svārthasādhanatatparaḥ śaṭho mithyāvinītaśca bakavratacaro dvijaḥ
meaning: अधोदृष्टिर्नैष्कृतिकः स्वार्थसाधनतत्परः शठो मिथ्याविनीतश्च बकव्रतचरो द्विजः
text: With eyes cast downwards, of cruel disposition, intent upon the accomplishment of his own ends, dishonest and falsely humble;—such is the Brāhmaṇa ‘who behaves like the heron’.
iast: adhodṛṣṭirnaiṣkṛtikaḥ svārthasādhanatatparaḥ śaṭho mithyāvinītaśca bakavratacaro dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ye bakavratino viprā ye ca mārjāraliṅginaḥ te patantyandhatāmisre tena pāpena karmaṇā
meaning: ये बकव्रतिनो विप्रा ये च मार्जारलिङ्गिनः ते पतन्त्यन्धतामिस्रे तेन पापेन कर्मणा
text: Those Brāhmaṇas who are heron-like in their behaviour, and those who are cat-like, fall, by that sinful act, into the Andhatāmisra hell.
iast: ye bakavratino viprā ye ca mārjāraliṅginaḥ te patantyandhatāmisre tena pāpena karmaṇā

sanskrit_iast: na dharmasyāpadeśena pāpaṃ kṛtvā vrataṃ caret vratena pāpaṃ pracchādya kurvan strīśūdradambhanam
meaning: न धर्मस्यापदेशेन पापं कृत्वा व्रतं चरेत् व्रतेन पापं प्रच्छाद्य कुर्वन् स्त्रीशूद्रदम्भनम्
text: Having committed a sin, he shall not perform penance un der the pretext of doing a righteous act,—deceiving women and Śūdras by thus covering his sin by a penance.
iast: na dharmasyāpadeśena pāpaṃ kṛtvā vrataṃ caret vratena pāpaṃ pracchādya kurvan strīśūdradambhanam

sanskrit_iast: pretyaiha caidṛśā viprā garhyante brahmavādibhiḥ chadmanā caritaṃ yacca vrataṃ rakṣāṃsi gacchati
meaning: प्रेत्यैह चैदृशा विप्रा गर्ह्यन्ते ब्रह्मवादिभिः छद्मना चरितं यच्च व्रतं रक्षांसि गच्छति
text: Such Brāhmaṇas are condemned after death, and also here (in this life) by the expounders of the Veda; and penance done under a false pretence goes to the evil spirits.
iast: pretyaiha caidṛśā viprā garhyante brahmavādibhiḥ chadmanā caritaṃ yacca vrataṃ rakṣāṃsi gacchati

sanskrit_iast: aliṅgī liṅgiveṣeṇa yo vṛttimupajīvati sa liṅgināṃ haratyenastiryagyonau ca jāyate
meaning: अलिङ्गी लिङ्गिवेषेण यो वृत्तिमुपजीवति स लिङ्गिनां हरत्येनस्तिर्यग्योनौ च जायते
text: One who, though not entitled to the wearing of a certain badge, gains one’s living by wearing that badge, takes off the sins of persons entitled to that badge, and is born in the womb of a lower animal.
iast: aliṅgī liṅgiveṣeṇa yo vṛttimupajīvati sa liṅgināṃ haratyenastiryagyonau ca jāyate

sanskrit_iast: parakīyanipāneṣu na snāyāddhi kadā cana snāyācca kadā cana nipānakartuḥ snātvā tu duṣkṛtāṃśena lipyate
meaning: परकीयनिपानेषु न स्नायाद्धि कदा चन स्नायाच्च कदा चन निपानकर्तुः स्नात्वा तु दुष्कृतांशेन लिप्यते
text: He shall never bathe in the tanks belonging to other persons. Having bathed there, he becomes tainted with a part of the tank-digger’s sin.
iast: parakīyanipāneṣu na snāyāddhi kadā cana snāyācca kadā cana nipānakartuḥ snātvā tu duṣkṛtāṃśena lipyate

sanskrit_iast: yānaśayyā ''sanānyasya kūpaudyānagṛhāṇi ca adattānyupayuñjāna enasaḥ syāt turīyabhāk
meaning: यानशय्या ''सनान्यस्य कूपौद्यानगृहाणि च अदत्तान्युपयुञ्जान एनसः स्यात् तुरीयभाक्
text: By using another person’s conveyance, couch, seat, well, garden or house,—when these have not been given,—one becomes the partaker of the fourth part of that person’s sins.
iast: yānaśayyā ''sanānyasya kūpaudyānagṛhāṇi ca adattānyupayuñjāna enasaḥ syāt turīyabhāk

sanskrit_iast: nadīṣu devakhāteṣu taḍāgeṣu saraḥsu ca snānaṃ samācarennityaṃ gartaprasravaṇeṣu ca
meaning: नदीषु देवखातेषु तडागेषु सरःसु च स्नानं समाचरेन्नित्यं गर्तप्रस्रवणेषु च
text: He shall always bathe in rivers and in tanks and lakes dug by the gods; as also in water-holes and springs.
iast: nadīṣu devakhāteṣu taḍāgeṣu saraḥsu ca snānaṃ samācarennityaṃ gartaprasravaṇeṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: yamān seveta satataṃ na nityaṃ niyamān budhaḥ yamān patatyakurvāṇo niyamān kevalān bhajan
meaning: यमान् सेवेत सततं न नित्यं नियमान् बुधः यमान् पतत्यकुर्वाणो नियमान् केवलान् भजन्
text: The wise man shall always observe the Restraints, but not necessarily the Observances. Not observing the Restraints, and keeping the Observances alone, he falls.
iast: yamān seveta satataṃ na nityaṃ niyamān budhaḥ yamān patatyakurvāṇo niyamān kevalān bhajan

sanskrit_iast: nāśrotriyatate yajñe grāmayājikṛte tathā striyā klībena ca hute bhuñjīta brāhmaṇaḥ kva cit
meaning: नाश्रोत्रियतते यज्ञे ग्रामयाजिकृते तथा स्त्रिया क्लीबेन च हुते भुञ्जीत ब्राह्मणः क्व चित्
text: The Brāhmaṇa shall never eat at a sacrifice performed by one who has not learnt the Veda; or at one performed by a village-priest, or at one offered by a woman or a eunuch.
iast: nāśrotriyatate yajñe grāmayājikṛte tathā striyā klībena ca hute bhuñjīta brāhmaṇaḥ kva cit

sanskrit_iast: aślīkametat sādhūnāṃ yatra juhvatyamī haviḥ 'ślīlam pratīpametaddevānāṃ tasmāt tat parivarjayet
meaning: अश्लीकमेतत् साधूनां यत्र जुह्वत्यमी हविः 'श्लीलम् प्रतीपमेतद्देवानां तस्मात् तत् परिवर्जयेत्
text: Where such persons pour the oblations, it is considered indecent by all good men; it is disagreeable to the gods; hence, one should avoid it
iast: aślīkametat sādhūnāṃ yatra juhvatyamī haviḥ 'ślīlam pratīpametaddevānāṃ tasmāt tat parivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: mattakruddhāturāṇāṃ ca na bhuñjīta kadā cana keśakīṭāvapannaṃ ca padā spṛṣṭaṃ ca kāmataḥ
meaning: मत्तक्रुद्धातुराणां च न भुञ्जीत कदा चन केशकीटावपन्नं च पदा स्पृष्टं च कामतः
text: He shalll never eat food offered by intoxicated or angry or sick persons; nor that which is contaminated by hair or insects, or that which has been intentionally touched with the foot.
iast: mattakruddhāturāṇāṃ ca na bhuñjīta kadā cana keśakīṭāvapannaṃ ca padā spṛṣṭaṃ ca kāmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhrūṇaghnāvekṣitaṃ caiva saṃspṛṣṭaṃ cāpyudakyayā patatriṇāvalīḍhaṃ ca śunā saṃspṛṣṭameva ca
meaning: भ्रूणघ्नावेक्षितं चैव संस्पृष्टं चाप्युदक्यया पतत्रिणावलीढं च शुना संस्पृष्टमेव च
text: Nor that which has been looked at by the Brāhmaṇa-slayer, or what has been toughed by a woman in her courses, or what has been pecked by the birds, or what has been touched by a dog.
iast: bhrūṇaghnāvekṣitaṃ caiva saṃspṛṣṭaṃ cāpyudakyayā patatriṇāvalīḍhaṃ ca śunā saṃspṛṣṭameva ca

sanskrit_iast: gavā cānnamupaghrātaṃ ghuṣṭānnaṃ ca viśeṣataḥ gaṇānnaṃ gaṇikānnaṃ ca viduṣā ca jugupsitam
meaning: गवा चान्नमुपघ्रातं घुष्टान्नं च विशेषतः गणान्नं गणिकान्नं च विदुषा च जुगुप्सितम्
text: Nor the food that has been smelt by the cow, nor particularly that food which has been publicly offered, nor the food that belongs to a multitude, nor the food of the harlot, nor that which has been censured by the learned.
iast: gavā cānnamupaghrātaṃ ghuṣṭānnaṃ ca viśeṣataḥ gaṇānnaṃ gaṇikānnaṃ ca viduṣā ca jugupsitam

sanskrit_iast: stenagāyanayoścānnaṃ takṣṇo vārdhuṣikasya ca dīkṣitasya kadaryasya baddhasya nigaḍasya ca
meaning: स्तेनगायनयोश्चान्नं तक्ष्णो वार्धुषिकस्य च दीक्षितस्य कदर्यस्य बद्धस्य निगडस्य च
text: Nor the food of the thief or the singer, nor of the carpenter, the usurer, of the initiated person, of the miser, the prisoner and the fettered.
iast: stenagāyanayoścānnaṃ takṣṇo vārdhuṣikasya ca dīkṣitasya kadaryasya baddhasya nigaḍasya ca

sanskrit_iast: abhiśastasya ṣaṇḍhasya puṃścalyā dāmbhikasya ca śuktaṃ paryuṣitaṃ caiva śūdrasyaucchiṣṭameva ca
meaning: अभिशस्तस्य षण्ढस्य पुंश्चल्या दाम्भिकस्य च शुक्तं पर्युषितं चैव शूद्रस्यौच्छिष्टमेव च
text: Nor the food of an accused person, or of the hermaphrodite, or the unchaste woman, or the hypocrite; nor the food turned sour, or that kept overnight, or what forms the leavings of the Śūdra.
iast: abhiśastasya ṣaṇḍhasya puṃścalyā dāmbhikasya ca śuktaṃ paryuṣitaṃ caiva śūdrasyaucchiṣṭameva ca

sanskrit_iast: cikitsakasya mṛgayoḥ krūrasyaucchiṣṭabhojinaḥ ugrānnaṃ sūtikānnaṃ ca paryācāntamanirdaśam
meaning: चिकित्सकस्य मृगयोः क्रूरस्यौच्छिष्टभोजिनः उग्रान्नं सूतिकान्नं च पर्याचान्तमनिर्दशम्
text: Nor the food of this physician, or of the hunter, or of a cruel person, or of one who lives on leavings; nor the food of the “Ugra;” nor what has been cooked for the newly-delivered woman, until ten days have passed; nor that which has been interrupted by washing.
iast: cikitsakasya mṛgayoḥ krūrasyaucchiṣṭabhojinaḥ ugrānnaṃ sūtikānnaṃ ca paryācāntamanirdaśam

sanskrit_iast: anarcitaṃ vṛthāmāṃsamavīrāyāśca yoṣitaḥ dviṣadannaṃ nagarīannaṃ patitānnamavakṣutam
meaning: अनर्चितं वृथामांसमवीरायाश्च योषितः द्विषदन्नं नगरीअन्नं पतितान्नमवक्षुतम्
text: Nor what is offered without respect, nor improper meat, nor food belonging to a male-less female, nor the food of an eneny, nor the food of the city-lord, nor the food of an outcast, nor that which has been sneezed at.
iast: anarcitaṃ vṛthāmāṃsamavīrāyāśca yoṣitaḥ dviṣadannaṃ nagarīannaṃ patitānnamavakṣutam

sanskrit_iast: piśunānṛtinoścānnaṃ kratuvikrayiṇastathā kratuvikrayakasya ca śailūṣatunnavāyānnaṃ kṛtaghnasyānnameva ca
meaning: पिशुनानृतिनोश्चान्नं क्रतुविक्रयिणस्तथा क्रतुविक्रयकस्य च शैलूषतुन्नवायान्नं कृतघ्नस्यान्नमेव च
text: Nor the food of the informer and the perjurer, or of the seller of sacrifices; nor the food of the actor or the tailor; nor the food of the ungrateful person.
iast: piśunānṛtinoścānnaṃ kratuvikrayiṇastathā kratuvikrayakasya ca śailūṣatunnavāyānnaṃ kṛtaghnasyānnameva ca

sanskrit_iast: karmārasya niṣādasya raṅgāvatārakasya ca suvarṇakarturveṇasya śastravikrayiṇastathā
meaning: कर्मारस्य निषादस्य रङ्गावतारकस्य च सुवर्णकर्तुर्वेणस्य शस्त्रविक्रयिणस्तथा
text: ‘Nor or the blacksmith, of the Niṣāda, of the stage-player, of the goldsmith, of the plater of musical instruments, or of the dealer in weapons,—(215).’
iast: karmārasya niṣādasya raṅgāvatārakasya ca suvarṇakarturveṇasya śastravikrayiṇastathā

sanskrit_iast: śvavatāṃ śauṇḍikānāṃ ca cailanirṇejakasya ca rañjakasya nṛśaṃsasya yasya caupapatirgṛhe rajakasya
meaning: श्ववतां शौण्डिकानां च चैलनिर्णेजकस्य च रञ्जकस्य नृशंसस्य यस्य चौपपतिर्गृहे रजकस्य
text: Nor of dog-keepers, or of wine-sellers or of the clothes-washer, or of the dyer, or of the bard, or of the man in whose house there is a paramour.
iast: śvavatāṃ śauṇḍikānāṃ ca cailanirṇejakasya ca rañjakasya nṛśaṃsasya yasya caupapatirgṛhe rajakasya

sanskrit_iast: mṛṣyanti ye caupapatiṃ strījitānāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ anirdaśaṃ ca pretānnamatuṣṭikarameva ca
meaning: मृष्यन्ति ये चौपपतिं स्त्रीजितानां च सर्वशः अनिर्दशं च प्रेतान्नमतुष्टिकरमेव च
text: Nor of thos e who bear the presence of the paramour, or of those who are entirely ruled b y women; nor the food of those in whose house death has occurred and the ten days have not passed; nor that which is disagree able.
iast: mṛṣyanti ye caupapatiṃ strījitānāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ anirdaśaṃ ca pretānnamatuṣṭikarameva ca

sanskrit_iast: rājānnaṃ teja ādatte śūdrānnaṃ brahmavarcasam āyuḥ suvarṇakārānnaṃ yaśaścarmāvakartinaḥ
meaning: राजान्नं तेज आदत्ते शूद्रान्नं ब्रह्मवर्चसम् आयुः सुवर्णकारान्नं यशश्चर्मावकर्तिनः
text: The king’s food takes off one’s vigour and the Śūdra’s food his Brahmic glory; the goldsmith’s food his longevity, and the l eather-cutter’s food hi s fame.
iast: rājānnaṃ teja ādatte śūdrānnaṃ brahmavarcasam āyuḥ suvarṇakārānnaṃ yaśaścarmāvakartinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kārukānnaṃ prajāṃ hanti balaṃ nirṇejakasya ca gaṇānnaṃ gaṇikānnaṃ ca lokebhyaḥ parikṛntati
meaning: कारुकान्नं प्रजां हन्ति बलं निर्णेजकस्य च गणान्नं गणिकान्नं च लोकेभ्यः परिकृन्तति
text: The food of artisans impairs the offspring, that of the clothes-washer impairs strength; the food of a multitude and the food of the harlot out off the max from the regions.
iast: kārukānnaṃ prajāṃ hanti balaṃ nirṇejakasya ca gaṇānnaṃ gaṇikānnaṃ ca lokebhyaḥ parikṛntati

sanskrit_iast: pūyaṃ cikitsakasyānnaṃ puṃścalyāstvannamindriyam viṣṭhā vārdhuṣikasyānnaṃ śastravikrayiṇo malam
meaning: पूयं चिकित्सकस्यान्नं पुंश्चल्यास्त्वन्नमिन्द्रियम् विष्ठा वार्धुषिकस्यान्नं शस्त्रविक्रयिणो मलम्
text: The food of the physician is pus; the food of the unchaste woman is semen; the food of the usurer is ordure, and that of the dealer in weapons is dirt.—(220.)
iast: pūyaṃ cikitsakasyānnaṃ puṃścalyāstvannamindriyam viṣṭhā vārdhuṣikasyānnaṃ śastravikrayiṇo malam

sanskrit_iast: ya ete 'nye tvabhojyānnāḥ kramaśaḥ parikīrtitāḥ teṣāṃ tvagasthiromāṇi vadantyannaṃ manīṣiṇaḥ
meaning: य एते 'न्ये त्वभोज्यान्नाः क्रमशः परिकीर्तिताः तेषां त्वगस्थिरोमाणि वदन्त्यन्नं मनीषिणः
text: The food of those other persons who have been successively mentioned as those whose food should not be eaten,—the wise men describe as skin, bones and hairs.
iast: ya ete 'nye tvabhojyānnāḥ kramaśaḥ parikīrtitāḥ teṣāṃ tvagasthiromāṇi vadantyannaṃ manīṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhuktvā 'to 'nyatamasyānnamamatyā kṣapaṇaṃ tryaham matyā bhuktvā 'caret kṛcchraṃ retasviṣmūtrameva ca
meaning: भुक्त्वा 'तो 'न्यतमस्यान्नममत्या क्षपणं त्र्यहम् मत्या भुक्त्वा 'चरेत् कृच्छ्रं रेतस्विष्मूत्रमेव च
text: After unknowingly eating the food of any one of these, there should be a three days’ fast. Having eaten it knowingly, as also on eating semen, ordure and urine, one should perform the “Kṛcchra” penance.
iast: bhuktvā 'to 'nyatamasyānnamamatyā kṣapaṇaṃ tryaham matyā bhuktvā 'caret kṛcchraṃ retasviṣmūtrameva ca

sanskrit_iast: nādyātśūdrasya pakvānnaṃ vidvānaśrāddhino dvijaḥ ādadītāmamevāsmādavṛttāvekarātrikam
meaning: नाद्यात्शूद्रस्य पक्वान्नं विद्वानश्राद्धिनो द्विजः आददीताममेवास्मादवृत्तावेकरात्रिकम्
text: The learned Brāhmaṇa shall, not eat the cooked food of the Śūdra who performs no śrāddhas. In the absence of livelihood, he may receive from him raw grain sufficient for one day.
iast: nādyātśūdrasya pakvānnaṃ vidvānaśrāddhino dvijaḥ ādadītāmamevāsmādavṛttāvekarātrikam

sanskrit_iast: śrotriyasya kadaryasya vadānyasya ca vārdhuṣeḥ mīmāṃsitvaubhayaṃ devāḥ samamannamakalpayan
meaning: श्रोत्रियस्य कदर्यस्य वदान्यस्य च वार्धुषेः मीमांसित्वौभयं देवाः सममन्नमकल्पयन्
text: The gods having compared the food of the miserly Vedic Scholar and that of the liberal usurer, ordained the food of both to be equal.
iast: śrotriyasya kadaryasya vadānyasya ca vārdhuṣeḥ mīmāṃsitvaubhayaṃ devāḥ samamannamakalpayan

sanskrit_iast: tān prajāpatirāhaitya mā kṛdhvaṃ viṣamaṃ samam śraddhāpūtaṃ vadānyasya hatamaśraddhayaitarat
meaning: तान् प्रजापतिराहैत्य मा कृध्वं विषमं समम् श्रद्धापूतं वदान्यस्य हतमश्रद्धयैतरत्
text: Thereupon Prajāpati came to them and said—“Do not make the unequal equal: what belongs to the liberal man is purified by faith, while the other is defiled by want of faith.
iast: tān prajāpatirāhaitya mā kṛdhvaṃ viṣamaṃ samam śraddhāpūtaṃ vadānyasya hatamaśraddhayaitarat

sanskrit_iast: śraddhayaiṣṭaṃ ca pūrtaṃ ca nityaṃ kuryādatandritaḥ śraddhākṛte hyakṣaye te bhavataḥ svāgatairdhanaiḥ
meaning: श्रद्धयैष्टं च पूर्तं च नित्यं कुर्यादतन्द्रितः श्रद्धाकृते ह्यक्षये ते भवतः स्वागतैर्धनैः
text: He shall always diliqenty perform, with faith, sacrifices and other religious acts; done with faith, and with well-begotten wealth, they become imperishable.
iast: śraddhayaiṣṭaṃ ca pūrtaṃ ca nityaṃ kuryādatandritaḥ śraddhākṛte hyakṣaye te bhavataḥ svāgatairdhanaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: dānadharmaṃ niṣeveta nityamaiṣṭikapaurtikam parituṣṭena bhāvena pātramāsādya śaktitaḥ
meaning: दानधर्मं निषेवेत नित्यमैष्टिकपौर्तिकम् परितुष्टेन भावेन पात्रमासाद्य शक्तितः
text: He shall practise, to the best of his ability, charity and righteousness in connection with sacrifices and acts of piety with a cheerful heart, if he finds a suitable recipient.
iast: dānadharmaṃ niṣeveta nityamaiṣṭikapaurtikam parituṣṭena bhāvena pātramāsādya śaktitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yat kiṃ cidapi dātavyaṃ yācitenānasūyayā 'nasūyatā ? utpatsyate hi tat pātraṃ yat tārayati sarvataḥ
meaning: यत् किं चिदपि दातव्यं याचितेनानसूयया 'नसूयता ? उत्पत्स्यते हि तत् पात्रं यत् तारयति सर्वतः
text: When asked, he should give something, without showing any displeasure. Because he may turn out to be a worthy recipient who will save him from everything.
iast: yat kiṃ cidapi dātavyaṃ yācitenānasūyayā 'nasūyatā ? utpatsyate hi tat pātraṃ yat tārayati sarvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: vāridastṛptimāpnoti sukhamakṣayyamannadaḥ 'kṣayam tilapradaḥ prajāmiṣṭāṃ dīpadaścakṣuruttamam
meaning: वारिदस्तृप्तिमाप्नोति सुखमक्षय्यमन्नदः 'क्षयम् तिलप्रदः प्रजामिष्टां दीपदश्चक्षुरुत्तमम्
text: The giver of water obtains satisfaction, the giver of food imperishable happiness, the giver of sesamum desirable offspring, and this giver of lamp most excellent eyesight.
iast: vāridastṛptimāpnoti sukhamakṣayyamannadaḥ 'kṣayam tilapradaḥ prajāmiṣṭāṃ dīpadaścakṣuruttamam

sanskrit_iast: bhūmido bhūmimāpnoti dīrghamāyurhiraṇyadaḥ gṛhado 'gryāṇi veśmāni rūpyado rūpamuttamam
meaning: भूमिदो भूमिमाप्नोति दीर्घमायुर्हिरण्यदः गृहदो 'ग्र्याणि वेश्मानि रूप्यदो रूपमुत्तमम्
text: The giver of land obtains land, the giver of gold long life, the giver of house excellent mansions, and the giver of silver exquisite beauty.
iast: bhūmido bhūmimāpnoti dīrghamāyurhiraṇyadaḥ gṛhado 'gryāṇi veśmāni rūpyado rūpamuttamam

sanskrit_iast: vāsodaścandrasālokyamaśvisālokyamaśvadaḥ anaḍuhaḥ śriyaṃ puṣṭāṃ godo bradhnasya viṣṭapam
meaning: वासोदश्चन्द्रसालोक्यमश्विसालोक्यमश्वदः अनडुहः श्रियं पुष्टां गोदो ब्रध्नस्य विष्टपम्
text: The giver of cloth obtains resemblance to the Moon, the giver of horse resemblance to horse-owners, the giver of the ox great good fortune, and the giver of the cow toe region of the Sun.
iast: vāsodaścandrasālokyamaśvisālokyamaśvadaḥ anaḍuhaḥ śriyaṃ puṣṭāṃ godo bradhnasya viṣṭapam

sanskrit_iast: yānaśayyāprado bhāryāmaiśvaryamabhayapradaḥ dhānyadaḥ śāśvataṃ saukhyaṃ brahmado brahmasārṣṭitām
meaning: यानशय्याप्रदो भार्यामैश्वर्यमभयप्रदः धान्यदः शाश्वतं सौख्यं ब्रह्मदो ब्रह्मसार्ष्टिताम्
text: The giver or carriage and couch obtains a wife, the giver of fearlessness sovereignty, the giver of grains eternal happiness, and the giver of the Veda equality with Brahman.
iast: yānaśayyāprado bhāryāmaiśvaryamabhayapradaḥ dhānyadaḥ śāśvataṃ saukhyaṃ brahmado brahmasārṣṭitām

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmeva dānānāṃ brahmadānaṃ viśiṣyate vāriannagomahīvāsastilakāñcanasarpiṣām
meaning: सर्वेषामेव दानानां ब्रह्मदानं विशिष्यते वारिअन्नगोमहीवासस्तिलकाञ्चनसर्पिषाम्
text: The giving or Veda surpasses all gifts or water, food, cows, buffaloes, clothes, sesamum, gold and clarified butter.
iast: sarveṣāmeva dānānāṃ brahmadānaṃ viśiṣyate vāriannagomahīvāsastilakāñcanasarpiṣām

sanskrit_iast: yena yena tu bhāvena yadyaddānaṃ prayacchati tat tat tenaiva bhāvena prāpnoti pratipūjitaḥ
meaning: येन येन तु भावेन यद्यद्दानं प्रयच्छति तत् तत् तेनैव भावेन प्राप्नोति प्रतिपूजितः
text: In whatever spirit a man bestows a gift, in that same spirit he himself receives it with due honour.
iast: yena yena tu bhāvena yadyaddānaṃ prayacchati tat tat tenaiva bhāvena prāpnoti pratipūjitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo 'rcitaṃ pratigṛhṇāti dadātyarcitameva vā tāvubhau gacchataḥ svargaṃ narakaṃ tu viparyaye
meaning: यो 'र्चितं प्रतिगृह्णाति ददात्यर्चितमेव वा तावुभौ गच्छतः स्वर्गं नरकं तु विपर्यये
text: He who receives respectfully, as well as he who gives respectfully,—both these go to heaven; but to hell, in the contrary case.
iast: yo 'rcitaṃ pratigṛhṇāti dadātyarcitameva vā tāvubhau gacchataḥ svargaṃ narakaṃ tu viparyaye

sanskrit_iast: na vismayeta tapasā vadediṣṭvā ca nānṛtam nārto 'pyapavadedviprānna dattvā parikīrtayet datvā
meaning: न विस्मयेत तपसा वदेदिष्ट्वा च नानृतम् नार्तो 'प्यपवदेद्विप्रान्न दत्त्वा परिकीर्तयेत् दत्वा
text: He shall not be proud of his austerities; having offered a sacrifice, he shall not utter a lie; even when tormented, he shall not revile the brāhmaṇas; having bestowed a gift, he shall not advertise it.
iast: na vismayeta tapasā vadediṣṭvā ca nānṛtam nārto 'pyapavadedviprānna dattvā parikīrtayet datvā

sanskrit_iast: yajño 'nṛtena kṣarati tapaḥ kṣarati vismayāt āyurviprāpavādena dānaṃ ca parikīrtanāt
meaning: यज्ञो 'नृतेन क्षरति तपः क्षरति विस्मयात् आयुर्विप्रापवादेन दानं च परिकीर्तनात्
text: The sacrifice trickles away by falsehood, the austerity trickles away by vanity, longevity by the reviling of Brāhmaṇas, and gifts by advertisement.
iast: yajño 'nṛtena kṣarati tapaḥ kṣarati vismayāt āyurviprāpavādena dānaṃ ca parikīrtanāt

sanskrit_iast: dharmaṃ śanaiḥ sañcinuyādvalmīkamiva puttikāḥ sañcinuyād paralokasahāyārthaṃ sarvabhūtānyapīḍayan
meaning: धर्मं शनैः सञ्चिनुयाद्वल्मीकमिव पुत्तिकाः सञ्चिनुयाद् परलोकसहायार्थं सर्वभूतान्यपीडयन्
text: Without causing pain to any beings, he shall, for the purpose of obtaining a companion in the other world, accumulate spiritual merit, slowly; just as the white ants accumulate the ant-hill.
iast: dharmaṃ śanaiḥ sañcinuyādvalmīkamiva puttikāḥ sañcinuyād paralokasahāyārthaṃ sarvabhūtānyapīḍayan

sanskrit_iast: nāmutra hi sahāyārthaṃ pitā mātā ca tiṣṭhataḥ na putradāraṃ na jñātirdharmastiṣṭhati kevalaḥ
meaning: नामुत्र हि सहायार्थं पिता माता च तिष्ठतः न पुत्रदारं न ज्ञातिर्धर्मस्तिष्ठति केवलः
text: There, neither father, nor mother, nor wife, nor sons, nor relations stay as companions; spiritual merit alone remains.
iast: nāmutra hi sahāyārthaṃ pitā mātā ca tiṣṭhataḥ na putradāraṃ na jñātirdharmastiṣṭhati kevalaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ekaḥ prajāyate jantureka eva pralīyate eko 'nubhuṅkte sukṛtameka eva ca duṣkṛtam
meaning: एकः प्रजायते जन्तुरेक एव प्रलीयते एको 'नुभुङ्क्ते सुकृतमेक एव च दुष्कृतम्
text: Alone is a creature born, and alone does it cease to be; alone it enjoys its good deeds and also its evil deeds.
iast: ekaḥ prajāyate jantureka eva pralīyate eko 'nubhuṅkte sukṛtameka eva ca duṣkṛtam

sanskrit_iast: mṛtaṃ śarīramutsṛjya kāṣṭhaloṣṭasamaṃ kṣitau vimukhā bāndhavā yānti dharmastamanugacchati
meaning: मृतं शरीरमुत्सृज्य काष्ठलोष्टसमं क्षितौ विमुखा बान्धवा यान्ति धर्मस्तमनुगच्छति
text: Leaving the dead body on the ground, like a log of wood or a clod of earth, the relations depart with averted faces; spiritual merit alone follows him.
iast: mṛtaṃ śarīramutsṛjya kāṣṭhaloṣṭasamaṃ kṣitau vimukhā bāndhavā yānti dharmastamanugacchati

sanskrit_iast: tasmāddharmaṃ sahāyārthaṃ nityaṃ sañcinuyātśanaiḥ dharmeṇa hi sahāyena tamastarati dustaram
meaning: तस्माद्धर्मं सहायार्थं नित्यं सञ्चिनुयात्शनैः धर्मेण हि सहायेन तमस्तरति दुस्तरम्
text: He shall, therefore, slowly accumulate Spiritual Merit, for the purpose of securing a companion; for, with Merit as his companion, the man crosses over unfordable darkness.
iast: tasmāddharmaṃ sahāyārthaṃ nityaṃ sañcinuyātśanaiḥ dharmeṇa hi sahāyena tamastarati dustaram

sanskrit_iast: dharmapradhānaṃ puruṣaṃ tapasā hatakilbiṣam paralokaṃ nayatyāśu bhāsvantaṃ khaśarīriṇam
meaning: धर्मप्रधानं पुरुषं तपसा हतकिल्बिषम् परलोकं नयत्याशु भास्वन्तं खशरीरिणम्
text: It speedily carries the man, who is devoted to duty and has his sins destroyed by austerities, clothed in his own (spiritual) body, to the brighter regions above.
iast: dharmapradhānaṃ puruṣaṃ tapasā hatakilbiṣam paralokaṃ nayatyāśu bhāsvantaṃ khaśarīriṇam

sanskrit_iast: uttamairuttamairnityaṃ saṃbandhānācaret saha saṃbhandhān ninīṣuḥ kulamutkarṣamadhamānadhamāṃstyajet
meaning: उत्तमैरुत्तमैर्नित्यं संबन्धानाचरेत् सह संभन्धान् निनीषुः कुलमुत्कर्षमधमानधमांस्त्यजेत्
text: Being desirous of raising his family to eminence, he shall always form connections with superior kinds of people; he shalt, avoid all inferior ones.
iast: uttamairuttamairnityaṃ saṃbandhānācaret saha saṃbhandhān ninīṣuḥ kulamutkarṣamadhamānadhamāṃstyajet

sanskrit_iast: uttamānuttamāneva gacchan hīnāṃstu varjayan brāhmaṇaḥ śreṣṭhatāmeti pratyavāyena śūdratām
meaning: उत्तमानुत्तमानेव गच्छन् हीनांस्तु वर्जयन् ब्राह्मणः श्रेष्ठतामेति प्रत्यवायेन शूद्रताम्
text: Having recourse to superior kinds of people, and avoiding the inferior kinds, the Brāhmaṇa attains eminence; by the contrary procedure, he becomes a Śūdra.
iast: uttamānuttamāneva gacchan hīnāṃstu varjayan brāhmaṇaḥ śreṣṭhatāmeti pratyavāyena śūdratām

sanskrit_iast: dṛḍhakārī mṛdurdāntaḥ krūrācārairasaṃvasan ahiṃsro damadānābhyāṃ jayet svargaṃ tathāvrataḥ
meaning: दृढकारी मृदुर्दान्तः क्रूराचारैरसंवसन् अहिंस्रो दमदानाभ्यां जयेत् स्वर्गं तथाव्रतः
text: He who acts resolutely, is gentle and tolerant, who does not associate with persons of cruel disposition, and who does no injury to others, shall win heaven by his self-control and liberality, if he sticks to this practice.
iast: dṛḍhakārī mṛdurdāntaḥ krūrācārairasaṃvasan ahiṃsro damadānābhyāṃ jayet svargaṃ tathāvrataḥ

sanskrit_iast: edhaudakaṃ mūlaphalamannamabhyudyataṃ ca yat sarvataḥ pratigṛhṇīyātmadhuathābhayadakṣiṇām
meaning: एधौदकं मूलफलमन्नमभ्युद्यतं च यत् सर्वतः प्रतिगृह्णीयात्मधुअथाभयदक्षिणाम्
text: He may accept from all persons fuel, water, roots, and fruits, and food that is offered, as also honey and the gift of fearlessness.’
iast: edhaudakaṃ mūlaphalamannamabhyudyataṃ ca yat sarvataḥ pratigṛhṇīyātmadhuathābhayadakṣiṇām

sanskrit_iast: āhṛtābhyudyatāṃ bhikṣāṃ purastādapracoditām mene prajāpatirgrāhyāmapi duṣkṛtakarmaṇaḥ
meaning: आहृताभ्युद्यतां भिक्षां पुरस्तादप्रचोदिताम् मेने प्रजापतिर्ग्राह्यामपि दुष्कृतकर्मणः
text: Alms brougt forward and offered, and not previously begged, Prajāpati has declared to be acceptable, even from a sinful person.
iast: āhṛtābhyudyatāṃ bhikṣāṃ purastādapracoditām mene prajāpatirgrāhyāmapi duṣkṛtakarmaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāśnanti pitarastasya daśavarṣāṇi pañca ca na ca havyaṃ vahatyagniryastāmabhyavamanyate
meaning: नाश्नन्ति पितरस्तस्य दशवर्षाणि पञ्च च न च हव्यं वहत्यग्निर्यस्तामभ्यवमन्यते
text: Of the man that disdains it, the Pitṛs do no eat any food for ten and five years; nor does fire convey his offering to the gods.
iast: nāśnanti pitarastasya daśavarṣāṇi pañca ca na ca havyaṃ vahatyagniryastāmabhyavamanyate

sanskrit_iast: śayyāṃ gṛhān kuśān gandhānapaḥ puṣpaṃ maṇīn dadhi dhānā matsyān payo māṃsaṃ śākaṃ caiva na nirṇudet
meaning: शय्यां गृहान् कुशान् गन्धानपः पुष्पं मणीन् दधि धाना मत्स्यान् पयो मांसं शाकं चैव न निर्णुदेत्
text: He shall not refuse a couch, a house, kuśa grass, perfumes, water, flowers, jewels, curd, grain, fish, milk, meat and vegetables.
iast: śayyāṃ gṛhān kuśān gandhānapaḥ puṣpaṃ maṇīn dadhi dhānā matsyān payo māṃsaṃ śākaṃ caiva na nirṇudet

sanskrit_iast: gurūn bhṛtyāṃścaujjihīrṣannarciṣyan devatā 'tithīn sarvataḥ pratigṛhṇīyānna tu tṛpyet svayaṃ tataḥ
meaning: गुरून् भृत्यांश्चौज्जिहीर्षन्नर्चिष्यन् देवता 'तिथीन् सर्वतः प्रतिगृह्णीयान्न तु तृप्येत् स्वयं ततः
text: For the purpose of bringing relief to his teachers and dependents, and for the purpose of honoring the gods and guests, he may accept gifts from all persons; but he shall not derive from them satisfaction for himself.
iast: gurūn bhṛtyāṃścaujjihīrṣannarciṣyan devatā 'tithīn sarvataḥ pratigṛhṇīyānna tu tṛpyet svayaṃ tataḥ

sanskrit_iast: guruṣu tvabhyatīteṣu vinā vā tairgṛhe vasan ātmano vṛttimanvicchan gṛhṇīyāt sādhutaḥ sadā
meaning: गुरुषु त्वभ्यतीतेषु विना वा तैर्गृहे वसन् आत्मनो वृत्तिमन्विच्छन् गृह्णीयात् साधुतः सदा
text: When, however, the Teachers are dead, or when one lives in his house without them,—then, seeking for his own subsistence, he may always accept gifts from good persons.
iast: guruṣu tvabhyatīteṣu vinā vā tairgṛhe vasan ātmano vṛttimanvicchan gṛhṇīyāt sādhutaḥ sadā

sanskrit_iast: ārdhikaḥ kulamitraṃ ca gopālo dāsanāpitau ete śūdreṣu bhojyānnā yāścātmānaṃ nivedayet
meaning: आर्धिकः कुलमित्रं च गोपालो दासनापितौ एते शूद्रेषु भोज्यान्ना याश्चात्मानं निवेदयेत्
text: His own ploughman, hereditary friend, cowherd, slave and barder,—among Śūdras; these are persons whose food may be eaten; as also one who offers himself.
iast: ārdhikaḥ kulamitraṃ ca gopālo dāsanāpitau ete śūdreṣu bhojyānnā yāścātmānaṃ nivedayet

sanskrit_iast: yādṛśo 'sya bhavedātmā yādṛśaṃ ca cikīrṣitam yathā caupacaredenaṃ tathā 'tmānaṃ nivedayet
meaning: यादृशो 'स्य भवेदात्मा यादृशं च चिकीर्षितम् यथा चौपचरेदेनं तथा 'त्मानं निवेदयेत्
text: As the man’s character may be, as may be the nature of the work he wishes to do, and as may be the manner in which he may serve,—even so must he offer himself.
iast: yādṛśo 'sya bhavedātmā yādṛśaṃ ca cikīrṣitam yathā caupacaredenaṃ tathā 'tmānaṃ nivedayet

sanskrit_iast: yo 'nyathā santamātmānamanyathā satsu bhāṣate sa pāpakṛttamo loke stena ātmāpahārakaḥ
meaning: यो 'न्यथा सन्तमात्मानमन्यथा सत्सु भाषते स पापकृत्तमो लोके स्तेन आत्मापहारकः
text: He, who describes himself to good men otherwise than what he is, is the worst sinner in the world, a thief stealing himself.
iast: yo 'nyathā santamātmānamanyathā satsu bhāṣate sa pāpakṛttamo loke stena ātmāpahārakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vācyarthā niyatāḥ sarve vācmūlā vācviniḥsṛtāḥ tāṃstu yaḥ stenayedvācaṃ sa sarvasteyakṛtnaraḥ tān tu ?
meaning: वाच्यर्था नियताः सर्वे वाच्मूला वाच्विनिःसृताः तांस्तु यः स्तेनयेद्वाचं स सर्वस्तेयकृत्नरः तान् तु ?
text: All things are regulated by speech, have their root in speech, and emanate from speech. The man who steals such speech is the stealer of all things.
iast: vācyarthā niyatāḥ sarve vācmūlā vācviniḥsṛtāḥ tāṃstu yaḥ stenayedvācaṃ sa sarvasteyakṛtnaraḥ tān tu ?

sanskrit_iast: maharṣipitṛdevānāṃ gatvā 'nṛṇyaṃ yathāvidhi putre sarvaṃ samāsajya vasen mādhyasthyamāsśritaḥ āsthitaḥ
meaning: महर्षिपितृदेवानां गत्वा 'नृण्यं यथाविधि पुत्रे सर्वं समासज्य वसेन् माध्यस्थ्यमास्श्रितः आस्थितः
text: Having paid off, according to law, the debts owing to the Great Sages, to the Pitṛs and to the Gods, he shall consign everything to his son and remain fixed in neutrality.
iast: maharṣipitṛdevānāṃ gatvā 'nṛṇyaṃ yathāvidhi putre sarvaṃ samāsajya vasen mādhyasthyamāsśritaḥ āsthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ekākī cintayennityaṃ vivikte hitamātmanaḥ itamātmani ekākī cintayāno hi paraṃ śreyo 'dhigacchati
meaning: एकाकी चिन्तयेन्नित्यं विविक्ते हितमात्मनः इतमात्मनि एकाकी चिन्तयानो हि परं श्रेयो 'धिगच्छति
text: Alone, in solitude, he shall meditate upon what is good for himself. By meditating in solitude, he attains the highest good.
iast: ekākī cintayennityaṃ vivikte hitamātmanaḥ itamātmani ekākī cintayāno hi paraṃ śreyo 'dhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: eṣauditā gṛhasthasya vṛttirviprasya śāśvatī snātakavratakalpaśca sattvavṛddhikaraḥ śubhaḥ
meaning: एषौदिता गृहस्थस्य वृत्तिर्विप्रस्य शाश्वती स्नातकव्रतकल्पश्च सत्त्ववृद्धिकरः शुभः
text: Thus has been described the eternal course of life of the Brāhmaṇa-Householder, as also the series of observances for the Accomplished Student, which is conducive to the increase of the quality of goodness, and is praiseworthy.
iast: eṣauditā gṛhasthasya vṛttirviprasya śāśvatī snātakavratakalpaśca sattvavṛddhikaraḥ śubhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: anena vipro vṛttena vartayan vedaśāstravit vyapetakalmaṣo nityaṃ brahmaloke mahīyate
meaning: अनेन विप्रो वृत्तेन वर्तयन् वेदशास्त्रवित् व्यपेतकल्मषो नित्यं ब्रह्मलोके महीयते
iast: anena vipro vṛttena vartayan vedaśāstravit vyapetakalmaṣo nityaṃ brahmaloke mahīyate

sanskrit_iast: śrutvaitān ṛṣayo dharmān snātakasya yathauditān idamūcurmahātmānamanalaprabhavaṃ bhṛgum
meaning: श्रुत्वैतान् ऋषयो धर्मान् स्नातकस्य यथौदितान् इदमूचुर्महात्मानमनलप्रभवं भृगुम्
text: The sages, having heard the duties of the Accomplished Student as just described, said this to the high-souled Bhṛgu, who sprang from fire.
iast: śrutvaitān ṛṣayo dharmān snātakasya yathauditān idamūcurmahātmānamanalaprabhavaṃ bhṛgum

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ yathauktaṃ viprāṇāṃ svadharmamanutiṣṭhatām kathaṃ mṛtyuḥ prabhavati vedaśāstravidāṃ prabho
meaning: एवं यथौक्तं विप्राणां स्वधर्ममनुतिष्ठताम् कथं मृत्युः प्रभवति वेदशास्त्रविदां प्रभो
text: “How is it, O Lord, that Death overpowers the Brāhmaṇas who are learned in the Vedic lore, and who perform their duty exactly as it has been thus described?”
iast: evaṃ yathauktaṃ viprāṇāṃ svadharmamanutiṣṭhatām kathaṃ mṛtyuḥ prabhavati vedaśāstravidāṃ prabho

sanskrit_iast: sa tānuvāca dharmātmā maharṣīn mānavo bhṛguḥ śrūyatāṃ yena doṣeṇa mṛtyurviprān jighāṃsati
meaning: स तानुवाच धर्मात्मा महर्षीन् मानवो भृगुः श्रूयतां येन दोषेण मृत्युर्विप्रान् जिघांसति
text: Death seeks to destroy the Brāhmaṇas on account of their omitting the study of the Vedas, on account of neglect of right conduct, on account of slothfulness and on account of the defects of food.
iast: sa tānuvāca dharmātmā maharṣīn mānavo bhṛguḥ śrūyatāṃ yena doṣeṇa mṛtyurviprān jighāṃsati

sanskrit_iast: anabhyāsena vedānāmācārasya ca varjanāt ālasyādannadoṣācca mṛtyurviprāñjighāṃsati viprān
meaning: अनभ्यासेन वेदानामाचारस्य च वर्जनात् आलस्यादन्नदोषाच्च मृत्युर्विप्राञ्जिघांसति विप्रान्
text: Death seeks to destroy the Brāhmaṇas on account of their omitting the study of the Vedas, on account of neglect of right conduct, on account of slothfulness and on account of the defects of food.
iast: anabhyāsena vedānāmācārasya ca varjanāt ālasyādannadoṣācca mṛtyurviprāñjighāṃsati viprān

sanskrit_iast: laśunaṃ gṛñjanaṃ caiva palāṇḍuṃ kavakāni ca abhakṣyāṇi dvijātīnāmamedhyaprabhavāni ca
meaning: लशुनं गृञ्जनं चैव पलाण्डुं कवकानि च अभक्ष्याणि द्विजातीनाममेध्यप्रभवानि च
text: Garlic, leeks and onions, mushrooms and all that proceeds from impure things, are unfit to re eaten by twice-born men.
iast: laśunaṃ gṛñjanaṃ caiva palāṇḍuṃ kavakāni ca abhakṣyāṇi dvijātīnāmamedhyaprabhavāni ca

sanskrit_iast: lohitān vṛkṣaniryāsān vṛścanaprabhavāṃstathā vraścanaprabhavāṃḥ śeluṃ gavyaṃ ca peyūṣaṃ prayatnena vivarjayet pīyūṣaṃ
meaning: लोहितान् वृक्षनिर्यासान् वृश्चनप्रभवांस्तथा व्रश्चनप्रभवांः शेलुं गव्यं च पेयूषं प्रयत्नेन विवर्जयेत् पीयूषं
text: He shall carefully avoid the red exudation from trees, as also those flowing from incisions, the śelu berries, and ‘curdled milk’ of the cow.
iast: lohitān vṛkṣaniryāsān vṛścanaprabhavāṃstathā vraścanaprabhavāṃḥ śeluṃ gavyaṃ ca peyūṣaṃ prayatnena vivarjayet pīyūṣaṃ

sanskrit_iast: vṛthā kṛsarasaṃyāvaṃ pāyasāpūpameva ca anupākṛtamāṃsāni devānnāni havīṃṣi ca
meaning: वृथा कृसरसंयावं पायसापूपमेव च अनुपाकृतमांसानि देवान्नानि हवींषि च
text: Needlessly cooked Rice-sesamum and Butter-sugar-sesamum, milk-rice and flour-cakes, unconsecrated meat, food of the gods and sacrificial viands;
iast: vṛthā kṛsarasaṃyāvaṃ pāyasāpūpameva ca anupākṛtamāṃsāni devānnāni havīṃṣi ca

sanskrit_iast: anirdaśāyā goḥ kṣīramauṣṭramaikaśaphaṃ tathā āvikaṃ saṃdhinīkṣīraṃ vivatsāyāśca goḥ payaḥ sandhinīkṣīraṃ
meaning: अनिर्दशाया गोः क्षीरमौष्ट्रमैकशफं तथा आविकं संधिनीक्षीरं विवत्सायाश्च गोः पयः सन्धिनीक्षीरं
text: The milk of the cow that has not passed its ten days, as also that of the camel and of one-hoofed animals and of sheep; the milk of the irregular cow, as also the milk of the cow without her calf.
iast: anirdaśāyā goḥ kṣīramauṣṭramaikaśaphaṃ tathā āvikaṃ saṃdhinīkṣīraṃ vivatsāyāśca goḥ payaḥ sandhinīkṣīraṃ

sanskrit_iast: āraṇyānāṃ ca sarveṣāṃ mṛgāṇāṃ māhiṣaṃ vinā strīkṣīraṃ caiva varjyāni sarvaśuktāni caiva hi
meaning: आरण्यानां च सर्वेषां मृगाणां माहिषं विना स्त्रीक्षीरं चैव वर्ज्यानि सर्वशुक्तानि चैव हि
text: That of all wild animals, except the buffalo; the milk of females (women) and all soured substances should be avoided.
iast: āraṇyānāṃ ca sarveṣāṃ mṛgāṇāṃ māhiṣaṃ vinā strīkṣīraṃ caiva varjyāni sarvaśuktāni caiva hi

sanskrit_iast: dadhi bhakṣyaṃ ca śukteṣu sarvaṃ ca dadhisaṃbhavam dadhisaṃbhavam yāni caivābhiṣūyante puṣpamūlaphalaiḥ śubhaiḥ
meaning: दधि भक्ष्यं च शुक्तेषु सर्वं च दधिसंभवम् दधिसंभवम् यानि चैवाभिषूयन्ते पुष्पमूलफलैः शुभैः
text: Among Soured Substances, the curd is fit to be eaten, and all that is prepared out of it; as also all that is distilled from pure flowers, roots and fruits;
iast: dadhi bhakṣyaṃ ca śukteṣu sarvaṃ ca dadhisaṃbhavam dadhisaṃbhavam yāni caivābhiṣūyante puṣpamūlaphalaiḥ śubhaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: kravyādāñ śakunān sarvāntathā grāmanivāsinaḥ kravyādaḥ śakunīn anirdiṣṭāṃścaikaśaphāṃṣ ṭiṭṭibhaṃ ca vivarjayet
meaning: क्रव्यादाञ् शकुनान् सर्वान्तथा ग्रामनिवासिनः क्रव्यादः शकुनीन् अनिर्दिष्टांश्चैकशफांष् टिट्टिभं च विवर्जयेत्
text: He shall avoid all carnivorous birds, and also those living in villages, the one-hoofed animals not specified, and also the Ṭiṭṭibha.
iast: kravyādāñ śakunān sarvāntathā grāmanivāsinaḥ kravyādaḥ śakunīn anirdiṣṭāṃścaikaśaphāṃṣ ṭiṭṭibhaṃ ca vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: kalaviṅkaṃ plavaṃ haṃsaṃ cakrāhvaṃ grāmakukkuṭam sārasaṃ rajjuvālaṃ ca dātyūhaṃ śukasārike rajjudālaṃ
meaning: कलविङ्कं प्लवं हंसं चक्राह्वं ग्रामकुक्कुटम् सारसं रज्जुवालं च दात्यूहं शुकसारिके रज्जुदालं
text: The Sparrow, the Plava, the Haṃsa, the Cakravāka; the village-cock, the Crane, the Rajjudāla, the Dātyūha, the Parrot and the Starling.
iast: kalaviṅkaṃ plavaṃ haṃsaṃ cakrāhvaṃ grāmakukkuṭam sārasaṃ rajjuvālaṃ ca dātyūhaṃ śukasārike rajjudālaṃ

sanskrit_iast: pratudāñjālapādāṃśca koyaṣṭinakhaviṣkirān pratudān nimajjataśca matsyādān saunaṃ vallūrameva ca
meaning: प्रतुदाञ्जालपादांश्च कोयष्टिनखविष्किरान् प्रतुदान् निमज्जतश्च मत्स्यादान् सौनं वल्लूरमेव च
text: Those birds that feed by striking with their beaks, those that are web-footed, the koyaṣṭi, those that scratch with their nails, those that dive and eat fish, slaughter-house meat, and dried meat.
iast: pratudāñjālapādāṃśca koyaṣṭinakhaviṣkirān pratudān nimajjataśca matsyādān saunaṃ vallūrameva ca

sanskrit_iast: bakaṃ caiva balākāṃ ca kākolaṃ khañjarīṭakam matsyādān viḍvarāhāṃśca matsyāneva ca sarvaśaḥ
meaning: बकं चैव बलाकां च काकोलं खञ्जरीटकम् मत्स्यादान् विड्वराहांश्च मत्स्यानेव च सर्वशः
text: The Baka, the Balākā, the Kākola, the Khañjarīṭa, the fish-eaters, and village pigs; as also fish always.
iast: bakaṃ caiva balākāṃ ca kākolaṃ khañjarīṭakam matsyādān viḍvarāhāṃśca matsyāneva ca sarvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo yasya māṃsamaśnāti sa tanmāṃsāda ucyate matsyādaḥ sarvamāṃsādastasmān matsyān vivarjayet
meaning: यो यस्य मांसमश्नाति स तन्मांसाद उच्यते मत्स्यादः सर्वमांसादस्तस्मान् मत्स्यान् विवर्जयेत्
text: He who eats the flesh of an animal, is called the ‘eater of its flesh’; he who eats fish is the ‘eater of all kinds of flesh’; hence one shall avoid fish.
iast: yo yasya māṃsamaśnāti sa tanmāṃsāda ucyate matsyādaḥ sarvamāṃsādastasmān matsyān vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: pāṭhīnarohitāvādyau niyuktau havyakavyayoḥ rājīvān siṃhatuṇḍāśca saśalkāścaiva sarvaśaḥ rājīvāḥ
meaning: पाठीनरोहितावाद्यौ नियुक्तौ हव्यकव्ययोः राजीवान् सिंहतुण्डाश्च सशल्काश्चैव सर्वशः राजीवाः
text: The ‘Pāṭhīna’ and the ‘Rohita’ are fit to be eaten when used as offerings to gods or Pitṛs; the ‘Rājīva’, the ‘Siṃhatuṇḍa’ and the ‘Saśalka,’ (one may eaṭ) on all occasions
iast: pāṭhīnarohitāvādyau niyuktau havyakavyayoḥ rājīvān siṃhatuṇḍāśca saśalkāścaiva sarvaśaḥ rājīvāḥ

sanskrit_iast: na bhakṣayedekacarānajñātāṃśca mṛgadvijān bhakṣyeṣvapi samuddiṣṭān sarvān pañcanakhāṃstathā
meaning: न भक्षयेदेकचरानज्ञातांश्च मृगद्विजान् भक्ष्येष्वपि समुद्दिष्टान् सर्वान् पञ्चनखांस्तथा
text: He shall not eat solitary animals, nor unknown beasts and birds, even though indicated among those fit to be eaten; nor any five-nailed animals.
iast: na bhakṣayedekacarānajñātāṃśca mṛgadvijān bhakṣyeṣvapi samuddiṣṭān sarvān pañcanakhāṃstathā

sanskrit_iast: śvāvidhaṃ śalyakaṃ godhāṃ khaḍgakūrmaśaśāṃstathā bhakṣyān pañcanakheṣvāhuranuṣṭrāṃścaikatodataḥ
meaning: श्वाविधं शल्यकं गोधां खड्गकूर्मशशांस्तथा भक्ष्यान् पञ्चनखेष्वाहुरनुष्ट्रांश्चैकतोदतः
text: Among five-nailed animals they declare the porcupine, the hedge-hog, the alligator, the rhinoceros, the tortoise and the hare, as fit to be eaten; as also all animals having one line of teeth. except the camel.
iast: śvāvidhaṃ śalyakaṃ godhāṃ khaḍgakūrmaśaśāṃstathā bhakṣyān pañcanakheṣvāhuranuṣṭrāṃścaikatodataḥ

sanskrit_iast: chatrākaṃ viḍvarāhaṃ ca laśunaṃ grāmakukkuṭam palāṇḍuṃ gṛñjanaṃ caiva matyā jagdhvā pateddvijaḥ
meaning: छत्राकं विड्वराहं च लशुनं ग्रामकुक्कुटम् पलाण्डुं गृञ्जनं चैव मत्या जग्ध्वा पतेद्द्विजः
text: The mushroom, the village-pig, garlic, the village-cock, onions and leeks,—the twice-born man eating these intentionally would become an ou tcast.
iast: chatrākaṃ viḍvarāhaṃ ca laśunaṃ grāmakukkuṭam palāṇḍuṃ gṛñjanaṃ caiva matyā jagdhvā pateddvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: amatyaitāni ṣaḍ jagdhvā kṛcchraṃ sāntapanaṃ caret yaticāndrāyāṇaṃ vā 'pi śeṣeṣūpavasedahaḥ
meaning: अमत्यैतानि षड् जग्ध्वा कृच्छ्रं सान्तपनं चरेत् यतिचान्द्रायाणं वा 'पि शेषेषूपवसेदहः
text: Having eaten these unintentionally, he should perform the ‘Kṛcchra Sāntapana’, or the ‘Yati-Cāndrāyaṇa’; and in the case of the rest one should fast for a day.
iast: amatyaitāni ṣaḍ jagdhvā kṛcchraṃ sāntapanaṃ caret yaticāndrāyāṇaṃ vā 'pi śeṣeṣūpavasedahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃvatsarasyaikamapi caret kṛcchraṃ dvijottamaḥ ajñātabhuktaśuddhiarthaṃ jñātasya tu viṣeśataḥ
meaning: संवत्सरस्यैकमपि चरेत् कृच्छ्रं द्विजोत्तमः अज्ञातभुक्तशुद्धिअर्थं ज्ञातस्य तु विषेशतः
text: Once a year the Brāhmaṇa shall perform the ‘Kṛcchra’ penance, in order to atone for unintentional eating; but for intentional eating, special ones.
iast: saṃvatsarasyaikamapi caret kṛcchraṃ dvijottamaḥ ajñātabhuktaśuddhiarthaṃ jñātasya tu viṣeśataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yajñārthaṃ brāhmaṇairvadhyāḥ praśastā mṛgapakṣiṇaḥ bhṛtyānāṃ caiva vṛttiarthamagastyo hyācarat purā
meaning: यज्ञार्थं ब्राह्मणैर्वध्याः प्रशस्ता मृगपक्षिणः भृत्यानां चैव वृत्तिअर्थमगस्त्यो ह्याचरत् पुरा
text: The commended beasts and birds may be killed by Brāhmaṇas for the purpose of sacrifice, and for the purpose of feeding their dependents; as Agastya did this of old.
iast: yajñārthaṃ brāhmaṇairvadhyāḥ praśastā mṛgapakṣiṇaḥ bhṛtyānāṃ caiva vṛttiarthamagastyo hyācarat purā

sanskrit_iast: babhūvurhi puroḍāśā bhakṣyāṇāṃ mṛgapakṣiṇām purāṇeṣvapi yajñeṣu brahmakṣatrasaveṣu ca purāṇeṣvṛṣiyajñeṣu
meaning: बभूवुर्हि पुरोडाशा भक्ष्याणां मृगपक्षिणाम् पुराणेष्वपि यज्ञेषु ब्रह्मक्षत्रसवेषु च पुराणेष्वृषियज्ञेषु
text: In ancient times, at sacrifices performed by the sages, as also at sacrifices performed by Brāhmaṇas and Kṣatriyas, the sacrificial cakes were made of eatable beasts and birds.
iast: babhūvurhi puroḍāśā bhakṣyāṇāṃ mṛgapakṣiṇām purāṇeṣvapi yajñeṣu brahmakṣatrasaveṣu ca purāṇeṣvṛṣiyajñeṣu

sanskrit_iast: yat kiṃ cit snehasaṃyuktaṃ bhakṣyaṃ bhojyamagarhitam tat paryuṣitamapyādyaṃ haviḥśeṣaṃ ca yadbhavet
meaning: यत् किं चित् स्नेहसंयुक्तं भक्ष्यं भोज्यमगर्हितम् तत् पर्युषितमप्याद्यं हविःशेषं च यद्भवेत्
text: Such Food and eatables as are mixed with oils may be eaten though stale, if unspoilt; so also what may be the remnant of a sacrificial offering.
iast: yat kiṃ cit snehasaṃyuktaṃ bhakṣyaṃ bhojyamagarhitam tat paryuṣitamapyādyaṃ haviḥśeṣaṃ ca yadbhavet

sanskrit_iast: cirasthitamapi tvādyamasnehāktaṃ dvijātibhiḥ yavagodhūmajaṃ sarvaṃ payasaścaiva vikriyā
meaning: चिरस्थितमपि त्वाद्यमस्नेहाक्तं द्विजातिभिः यवगोधूमजं सर्वं पयसश्चैव विक्रिया
text: All that is made of barley and wheat, as also all preparations of milk, may be eaten by twice-born men, without being mixed with oils, even though they may have been kept long
iast: cirasthitamapi tvādyamasnehāktaṃ dvijātibhiḥ yavagodhūmajaṃ sarvaṃ payasaścaiva vikriyā

sanskrit_iast: etaduktaṃ dvijātīnāṃ bhakṣyābhakṣyamaśeṣataḥ māṃsasyātaḥ pravakṣyāmi vidhiṃ bhakṣaṇavarjane
meaning: एतदुक्तं द्विजातीनां भक्ष्याभक्ष्यमशेषतः मांसस्यातः प्रवक्ष्यामि विधिं भक्षणवर्जने
text: Thus has been described is full what is fit and what unfit to be eaten by twice-born men. Next I am going to explain the rule regarding the eating and avoiding of meat.
iast: etaduktaṃ dvijātīnāṃ bhakṣyābhakṣyamaśeṣataḥ māṃsasyātaḥ pravakṣyāmi vidhiṃ bhakṣaṇavarjane

sanskrit_iast: prokṣitaṃ bhakṣayen māṃsaṃ brāhmaṇānāṃ ca kāmyayā yathāvidhi niyuktastu prāṇānāmeva cātyaye
meaning: प्रोक्षितं भक्षयेन् मांसं ब्राह्मणानां च काम्यया यथाविधि नियुक्तस्तु प्राणानामेव चात्यये
text: He may eat meat that has been consecrated; also at the wish of Brāhmaṇas; and when invited according to law; and when his life is in danger.
iast: prokṣitaṃ bhakṣayen māṃsaṃ brāhmaṇānāṃ ca kāmyayā yathāvidhi niyuktastu prāṇānāmeva cātyaye

sanskrit_iast: prāṇasyānnamidaṃ sarvaṃ prajāpatirakalpayat sthāvaraṃ jaṅgamaṃ caiva sarvaṃ prāṇasya bhojanam
meaning: प्राणस्यान्नमिदं सर्वं प्रजापतिरकल्पयत् स्थावरं जङ्गमं चैव सर्वं प्राणस्य भोजनम्
text: Prajāpati created all this as food for the vital spirit; and all that is movable or immovable is the food of the vital spirit
iast: prāṇasyānnamidaṃ sarvaṃ prajāpatirakalpayat sthāvaraṃ jaṅgamaṃ caiva sarvaṃ prāṇasya bhojanam

sanskrit_iast: carāṇāmannamacarā daṃṣṭriṇāmapyadaṃṣṭriṇaḥ ahastāśca sahastānāṃ śūrāṇāṃ caiva bhīravaḥ
meaning: चराणामन्नमचरा दंष्ट्रिणामप्यदंष्ट्रिणः अहस्ताश्च सहस्तानां शूराणां चैव भीरवः
text: The immobile is the food of the mobile; those devoid of fangs are the food of those endowed with fangs; those without hands are the food of those with hands; and cowards are the food of the brave.
iast: carāṇāmannamacarā daṃṣṭriṇāmapyadaṃṣṭriṇaḥ ahastāśca sahastānāṃ śūrāṇāṃ caiva bhīravaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāttā duṣyatyadannādyān prāṇino 'hany 'hanyapi dhātraiva sṛṣṭā hyādyāśca prāṇino 'ttāra eva ca
meaning: नात्ता दुष्यत्यदन्नाद्यान् प्राणिनो 'हन्य् 'हन्यपि धात्रैव सृष्टा ह्याद्याश्च प्राणिनो 'त्तार एव च
text: The eater incurs no sin by eating, even daily, such animals as are eatable: since the eater as well as the eaten animals have been created by the creator himself
iast: nāttā duṣyatyadannādyān prāṇino 'hany 'hanyapi dhātraiva sṛṣṭā hyādyāśca prāṇino 'ttāra eva ca

sanskrit_iast: yajñāya jagdhirmāṃsasyaityeṣa daivo vidhiḥ smṛtaḥ ato 'nyathā pravṛttistu rākṣaso vidhirucyate
meaning: यज्ञाय जग्धिर्मांसस्यैत्येष दैवो विधिः स्मृतः अतो 'न्यथा प्रवृत्तिस्तु राक्षसो विधिरुच्यते
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: yajñāya jagdhirmāṃsasyaityeṣa daivo vidhiḥ smṛtaḥ ato 'nyathā pravṛttistu rākṣaso vidhirucyate

sanskrit_iast: krītvā svayaṃ vā 'pyutpādya paraupakṛtameva vā devān pitr̥̄ṃścārcayitvā khādan māṃsaṃ na duṣyati
meaning: क्रीत्वा स्वयं वा 'प्युत्पाद्य परौपकृतमेव वा देवान् पित्र्̥̄ंश्चार्चयित्वा खादन् मांसं न दुष्यति
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: krītvā svayaṃ vā 'pyutpādya paraupakṛtameva vā devān pitr̥̄ṃścārcayitvā khādan māṃsaṃ na duṣyati

sanskrit_iast: nādyādavidhinā māṃsaṃ vidhijño 'nāpadi dvijaḥ jagdhvā hyavidhinā māṃsaṃ pretastairadyate 'vaśaḥ
meaning: नाद्यादविधिना मांसं विधिज्ञो 'नापदि द्विजः जग्ध्वा ह्यविधिना मांसं प्रेतस्तैरद्यते 'वशः
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: nādyādavidhinā māṃsaṃ vidhijño 'nāpadi dvijaḥ jagdhvā hyavidhinā māṃsaṃ pretastairadyate 'vaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na tādṛśaṃ bhavatyeno mṛgahanturdhanārthinaḥ yādṛśaṃ bhavati pretya vṛthāmāṃsāni khādataḥ
meaning: न तादृशं भवत्येनो मृगहन्तुर्धनार्थिनः यादृशं भवति प्रेत्य वृथामांसानि खादतः
text: The sin of the man who kills animals for gain is not so great, after death, as that of the man who eats needlessly-prepared meat.
iast: na tādṛśaṃ bhavatyeno mṛgahanturdhanārthinaḥ yādṛśaṃ bhavati pretya vṛthāmāṃsāni khādataḥ

sanskrit_iast: niyuktastu yathānyāyaṃ yo māṃsaṃ nātti mānavaḥ sa pretya paśutāṃ yāti saṃbhavānekaviṃśatim
meaning: नियुक्तस्तु यथान्यायं यो मांसं नात्ति मानवः स प्रेत्य पशुतां याति संभवानेकविंशतिम्
text: But when invited according to law, if a man does not e at meat, he becomes, after death, a beast, during twenty- one births.
iast: niyuktastu yathānyāyaṃ yo māṃsaṃ nātti mānavaḥ sa pretya paśutāṃ yāti saṃbhavānekaviṃśatim

sanskrit_iast: asaṃskṛtān paśūn mantrairnādyādvipraḥ kadā cana mantraistu saṃskṛtānadyātśāśvataṃ vidhimāsthitaḥ
meaning: असंस्कृतान् पशून् मन्त्रैर्नाद्याद्विप्रः कदा चन मन्त्रैस्तु संस्कृतानद्यात्शाश्वतं विधिमास्थितः
text: The Brāhmaṇa shall never eat animals that have not been consecrated with sacred texts; but those that have been consecrated with sacred texts, he shall eat, taking, his stand upon the eternal law.
iast: asaṃskṛtān paśūn mantrairnādyādvipraḥ kadā cana mantraistu saṃskṛtānadyātśāśvataṃ vidhimāsthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kuryādghṛtapaśuṃ saṅge kuryāt piṣṭapaśuṃ tathā na tveva tu vṛthā hantuṃ paśumicchet kadā cana
meaning: कुर्याद्घृतपशुं सङ्गे कुर्यात् पिष्टपशुं तथा न त्वेव तु वृथा हन्तुं पशुमिच्छेत् कदा चन
text: If there is occasion, he shall make an animal of clarified butter, or an animal of flour; but he shall never seek to kill an animal needlessly.
iast: kuryādghṛtapaśuṃ saṅge kuryāt piṣṭapaśuṃ tathā na tveva tu vṛthā hantuṃ paśumicchet kadā cana

sanskrit_iast: yāvanti paśuromāṇi tāvatkṛtvo ha māraṇam vṛthāpaśughnaḥ prāpnoti pretya janmani janmani
meaning: यावन्ति पशुरोमाणि तावत्कृत्वो ह मारणम् वृथापशुघ्नः प्राप्नोति प्रेत्य जन्मनि जन्मनि
text: As many hairs there are on the body of the animal, so m any times after dying does its needless killer suffer violent death, birth after birth.—(38.)
iast: yāvanti paśuromāṇi tāvatkṛtvo ha māraṇam vṛthāpaśughnaḥ prāpnoti pretya janmani janmani

sanskrit_iast: yajñārthaṃ paśavaḥ sṛṣṭāḥ svayameva svayaṃbhuvā yajño 'sya bhūtyai sarvasya tasmādyajñe vadho 'vadhaḥ
meaning: यज्ञार्थं पशवः सृष्टाः स्वयमेव स्वयंभुवा यज्ञो 'स्य भूत्यै सर्वस्य तस्माद्यज्ञे वधो 'वधः
text: Animals have been created by the Self-born God himself for the purpose of sacrifice: sacrifice is conducive to the well-being of all this would; hence killing at a sacrifice is no ‘killing’ at all
iast: yajñārthaṃ paśavaḥ sṛṣṭāḥ svayameva svayaṃbhuvā yajño 'sya bhūtyai sarvasya tasmādyajñe vadho 'vadhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: oṣadhyaḥ paśavo vṛkṣāstiryañcaḥ pakṣiṇastathā yajñārthaṃ nidhanaṃ prāptāḥ prāpnuvantyutsṛtīḥ punaḥ ucchritīḥ
meaning: ओषध्यः पशवो वृक्षास्तिर्यञ्चः पक्षिणस्तथा यज्ञार्थं निधनं प्राप्ताः प्राप्नुवन्त्युत्सृतीः पुनः उच्छ्रितीः
text: Herbs, animals, trees, beasts and birds, reaching death for the sake of sacrifices, attain advancements.
iast: oṣadhyaḥ paśavo vṛkṣāstiryañcaḥ pakṣiṇastathā yajñārthaṃ nidhanaṃ prāptāḥ prāpnuvantyutsṛtīḥ punaḥ ucchritīḥ

sanskrit_iast: madhuparke ca yajñe ca pitṛdaivatakarmaṇi atraiva paśavo hiṃsyā nānyatraityabravīn manuḥ
meaning: मधुपर्के च यज्ञे च पितृदैवतकर्मणि अत्रैव पशवो हिंस्या नान्यत्रैत्यब्रवीन् मनुः
text: The twice-born person, knowing the real import of the Veda, killing animals on these occasions, carries himself and the animal to the most excellent state.
iast: madhuparke ca yajñe ca pitṛdaivatakarmaṇi atraiva paśavo hiṃsyā nānyatraityabravīn manuḥ

sanskrit_iast: eṣvartheṣu paśūn hiṃsan vedatattvārthaviddvijaḥ ātmānaṃ ca paśuṃ caiva gamayatyuttamaṃ gatim
meaning: एष्वर्थेषु पशून् हिंसन् वेदतत्त्वार्थविद्द्विजः आत्मानं च पशुं चैव गमयत्युत्तमं गतिम्
text: The twice-born person, knowing the real import of the Veda, killing animals on these occasions, carries himself and the animal to the most excellent state.
iast: eṣvartheṣu paśūn hiṃsan vedatattvārthaviddvijaḥ ātmānaṃ ca paśuṃ caiva gamayatyuttamaṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: gṛhe gurāvaraṇye vā nivasannātmavān dvijaḥ nāvedavihitāṃ hiṃsāmāpadyapi samācaret
meaning: गृहे गुरावरण्ये वा निवसन्नात्मवान् द्विजः नावेदविहितां हिंसामापद्यपि समाचरेत्
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: gṛhe gurāvaraṇye vā nivasannātmavān dvijaḥ nāvedavihitāṃ hiṃsāmāpadyapi samācaret

sanskrit_iast: yā vedavihitā hiṃsā niyatā 'smiṃścarācare ahiṃsāmeva tāṃ vidyādvedāddharmo hi nirbabhau
meaning: या वेदविहिता हिंसा नियता 'स्मिंश्चराचरे अहिंसामेव तां विद्याद्वेदाद्धर्मो हि निर्बभौ
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: yā vedavihitā hiṃsā niyatā 'smiṃścarācare ahiṃsāmeva tāṃ vidyādvedāddharmo hi nirbabhau

sanskrit_iast: yo 'hiṃsakāni bhūtāni hinastyātmasukhaicchayā sa jīvāṃśca mṛtaścaiva na kva cit sukhamedhate
meaning: यो 'हिंसकानि भूतानि हिनस्त्यात्मसुखैच्छया स जीवांश्च मृतश्चैव न क्व चित् सुखमेधते
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: yo 'hiṃsakāni bhūtāni hinastyātmasukhaicchayā sa jīvāṃśca mṛtaścaiva na kva cit sukhamedhate

sanskrit_iast: yo bandhanavadhakleśān prāṇināṃ na cikīrṣati sa sarvasya hitaprepsuḥ sukhamatyantamaśnute
meaning: यो बन्धनवधक्लेशान् प्राणिनां न चिकीर्षति स सर्वस्य हितप्रेप्सुः सुखमत्यन्तमश्नुते
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: yo bandhanavadhakleśān prāṇināṃ na cikīrṣati sa sarvasya hitaprepsuḥ sukhamatyantamaśnute

sanskrit_iast: yaddhyāyati yat kurute ratiṃ badhnāti yatra ca tadavāpnotyayatnena yo hinasti na kiṃ cana
meaning: यद्ध्यायति यत् कुरुते रतिं बध्नाति यत्र च तदवाप्नोत्ययत्नेन यो हिनस्ति न किं चन
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: yaddhyāyati yat kurute ratiṃ badhnāti yatra ca tadavāpnotyayatnena yo hinasti na kiṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: nākṛtvā prāṇināṃ hiṃsāṃ māṃsamutpadyate kva cit na ca prāṇivadhaḥ svargyastasmān māṃsaṃ vivarjayet
meaning: नाकृत्वा प्राणिनां हिंसां मांसमुत्पद्यते क्व चित् न च प्राणिवधः स्वर्ग्यस्तस्मान् मांसं विवर्जयेत्
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: nākṛtvā prāṇināṃ hiṃsāṃ māṃsamutpadyate kva cit na ca prāṇivadhaḥ svargyastasmān māṃsaṃ vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: samutpattiṃ ca māṃsasya vadhabandhau ca dehinām prasamīkṣya nivarteta sarvamāṃsasya bhakṣaṇāt
meaning: समुत्पत्तिं च मांसस्य वधबन्धौ च देहिनाम् प्रसमीक्ष्य निवर्तेत सर्वमांसस्य भक्षणात्
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: samutpattiṃ ca māṃsasya vadhabandhau ca dehinām prasamīkṣya nivarteta sarvamāṃsasya bhakṣaṇāt

sanskrit_iast: na bhakṣayati yo māṃsaṃ vidhiṃ hitvā piśācavat na loke priyatāṃ yāti vyādhibhiśca na pīḍyate
meaning: न भक्षयति यो मांसं विधिं हित्वा पिशाचवत् न लोके प्रियतां याति व्याधिभिश्च न पीड्यते
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: na bhakṣayati yo māṃsaṃ vidhiṃ hitvā piśācavat na loke priyatāṃ yāti vyādhibhiśca na pīḍyate

sanskrit_iast: anumantā viśasitā nihantā krayavikrayī saṃskartā caupahartā ca khādakaścaiti ghātakāḥ
meaning: अनुमन्ता विशसिता निहन्ता क्रयविक्रयी संस्कर्ता चौपहर्ता च खादकश्चैति घातकाः
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: anumantā viśasitā nihantā krayavikrayī saṃskartā caupahartā ca khādakaścaiti ghātakāḥ

sanskrit_iast: svamāṃsaṃ paramāṃsena yo vardhayitumicchati anabhyarcya pitr̥̄n devāṃstato 'nyo nāstyapuṇyakṛt
meaning: स्वमांसं परमांसेन यो वर्धयितुमिच्छति अनभ्यर्च्य पित्र्̥̄न् देवांस्ततो 'न्यो नास्त्यपुण्यकृत्
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: svamāṃsaṃ paramāṃsena yo vardhayitumicchati anabhyarcya pitr̥̄n devāṃstato 'nyo nāstyapuṇyakṛt

sanskrit_iast: varṣe varṣe 'śvamedhena yo yajeta śataṃ samāḥ māṃsāni ca na khādedyastayoḥ puṇyaphalaṃ samam
meaning: वर्षे वर्षे 'श्वमेधेन यो यजेत शतं समाः मांसानि च न खादेद्यस्तयोः पुण्यफलं समम्
text: If a man performs the Aśvamedha Sacrifice every year, for a hundred tears,—and another does not eat meat,—the merit and reward of both these are the same.—(53.)
iast: varṣe varṣe 'śvamedhena yo yajeta śataṃ samāḥ māṃsāni ca na khādedyastayoḥ puṇyaphalaṃ samam

sanskrit_iast: phalamūlāśanairmedhyairmuniannānāṃ ca bhojanaiḥ na tat phalamavāpnoti yatmāṃsaparivarjanāt
meaning: फलमूलाशनैर्मेध्यैर्मुनिअन्नानां च भोजनैः न तत् फलमवाप्नोति यत्मांसपरिवर्जनात्
text: By subsisting upon sacred fruits and roots, and by eating the food of hermits, one does not obtain that reward which he does by abstaining from meat
iast: phalamūlāśanairmedhyairmuniannānāṃ ca bhojanaiḥ na tat phalamavāpnoti yatmāṃsaparivarjanāt

sanskrit_iast: māṃ sa bhakṣayitā 'mutra yasya māṃsamihādmyaham etatmāṃsasya māṃsatvaṃ pravadanti manīṣiṇaḥ
meaning: मां स भक्षयिता 'मुत्र यस्य मांसमिहाद्म्यहम् एतत्मांसस्य मांसत्वं प्रवदन्ति मनीषिणः
text: ‘Me he (māṃ-sa) will devour in the next world, whose meat I eat in this’—this is the ‘meatness’ (māṃsatva) of the ‘meat’ (māṃsa), as the wise ones declare.
iast: māṃ sa bhakṣayitā 'mutra yasya māṃsamihādmyaham etatmāṃsasya māṃsatvaṃ pravadanti manīṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na māṃsabhakṣaṇe doṣo na madye na ca maithune pravṛttireṣā bhūtānāṃ nivṛttistu mahāphalā
meaning: न मांसभक्षणे दोषो न मद्ये न च मैथुने प्रवृत्तिरेषा भूतानां निवृत्तिस्तु महाफला
text: There is no sin in the eating of meat, nor in wine, nor in sexual intercourse. Such is the natural way of living beings; but abstention is conducive to great rewards.
iast: na māṃsabhakṣaṇe doṣo na madye na ca maithune pravṛttireṣā bhūtānāṃ nivṛttistu mahāphalā

sanskrit_iast: pretaśuddhiṃ pravakṣyāmi dravyaśuddhiṃ tathaiva ca caturṇāmapi varṇānāṃ yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ
meaning: प्रेतशुद्धिं प्रवक्ष्यामि द्रव्यशुद्धिं तथैव च चतुर्णामपि वर्णानां यथावदनुपूर्वशः
text: I am going to describe, in due order, purification on death, as also purification of substances, as prescribed for all the four castes.
iast: pretaśuddhiṃ pravakṣyāmi dravyaśuddhiṃ tathaiva ca caturṇāmapi varṇānāṃ yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dantajāte 'nujāte ca kṛtacūḍe ca saṃsthite aśuddhā bāndhavāḥ sarve sūtake ca tathaucyate
meaning: दन्तजाते 'नुजाते च कृतचूडे च संस्थिते अशुद्धा बान्धवाः सर्वे सूतके च तथौच्यते
text: When a child dies that has teethed, or one younger than it when its tonsure has been performed, all its relatives are ‘impure’. The same is declared to be the case with births also.
iast: dantajāte 'nujāte ca kṛtacūḍe ca saṃsthite aśuddhā bāndhavāḥ sarve sūtake ca tathaucyate

sanskrit_iast: daśāhaṃ śāvamāśaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu vidhīyate arvāk sañcayanādasthnāṃ tryahamekāhameva vā
meaning: दशाहं शावमाशौचं सपिण्डेषु विधीयते अर्वाक् सञ्चयनादस्थ्नां त्र्यहमेकाहमेव वा
text: Among ‘Sapiṇḍas’. the period of impurity due to death is ordained to last for ten days; or till the collecting of the bones, or for three days, or for one day only
iast: daśāhaṃ śāvamāśaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu vidhīyate arvāk sañcayanādasthnāṃ tryahamekāhameva vā

sanskrit_iast: sapiṇḍatā tu puruṣe saptame vinivartate samānodakabhāvastu janmanāmnoravedane
meaning: सपिण्डता तु पुरुषे सप्तमे विनिवर्तते समानोदकभावस्तु जन्मनाम्नोरवेदने
text: The ‘Sapiṇḍa-relationship’ ceases with the person in the seventh-degree and the ‘Samānodaka-relationship,’ when the origin and the name become unrecognisable
iast: sapiṇḍatā tu puruṣe saptame vinivartate samānodakabhāvastu janmanāmnoravedane

sanskrit_iast: yathaidaṃ śāvamāśaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu vidhīyate janane 'pyevameva syātnipuṇaṃ śuddhimicchatām
meaning: यथैदं शावमाशौचं सपिण्डेषु विधीयते जनने 'प्येवमेव स्यात्निपुणं शुद्धिमिच्छताम्
text: Thus also should it be at a birth; but the parturient disability attaches to the parents only; or, the parturient disability would attach to the mother alone, and the father would become purified by bathing.
iast: yathaidaṃ śāvamāśaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu vidhīyate janane 'pyevameva syātnipuṇaṃ śuddhimicchatām

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāṃ śāvamāśaucaṃ mātāpitrostu sūtakam janane 'pyevameva syān mātāpitrostu sūtakaṃ sūtakaṃ mātureva syādupaspṛśya pitā śuciḥ
meaning: सर्वेषां शावमाशौचं मातापित्रोस्तु सूतकम् जनने 'प्येवमेव स्यान् मातापित्रोस्तु सूतकं सूतकं मातुरेव स्यादुपस्पृश्य पिता शुचिः
text: The man, having emitted semen becomes pure by bathing; hence, on account of seminal filiation he should observe impurity for these days.
iast: sarveṣāṃ śāvamāśaucaṃ mātāpitrostu sūtakam janane 'pyevameva syān mātāpitrostu sūtakaṃ sūtakaṃ mātureva syādupaspṛśya pitā śuciḥ

sanskrit_iast: nirasya tu pumāṃśukramupaspṛsyaiva śudhyati baijikādabhisaṃbandhādanurundhyādaghaṃ tryaham
meaning: निरस्य तु पुमांशुक्रमुपस्पृस्यैव शुध्यति बैजिकादभिसंबन्धादनुरुन्ध्यादघं त्र्यहम्
text: Those who touch this corpse become pure after one day and one day along with three three-day periods; those who offer water, after three days.
iast: nirasya tu pumāṃśukramupaspṛsyaiva śudhyati baijikādabhisaṃbandhādanurundhyādaghaṃ tryaham

sanskrit_iast: ahnā caikena rātryā ca trirātraireva ca tribhiḥ śavaspṛśo viśudhyanti tryahādudakadāyinaḥ
meaning: अह्ना चैकेन रात्र्या च त्रिरात्रैरेव च त्रिभिः शवस्पृशो विशुध्यन्ति त्र्यहादुदकदायिनः
text: The pupil performing the ‘Pitṛmedha’ for his dead Teacher becomes purified in ten days; just as those who carry the dead body.
iast: ahnā caikena rātryā ca trirātraireva ca tribhiḥ śavaspṛśo viśudhyanti tryahādudakadāyinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: guroḥ pretasya śiṣyastu pitṛmedhaṃ samācaran pretahāraiḥ samaṃ tatra daśarātreṇa śudhyati pretāhāraiḥ
meaning: गुरोः प्रेतस्य शिष्यस्तु पितृमेधं समाचरन् प्रेतहारैः समं तत्र दशरात्रेण शुध्यति प्रेताहारैः
text: In the case of miscarriage, the woman becomes pure in so mamy days as there have been months; and the woman in her courses becomes fit by bathing after the ceasing of the menstrual flow.
iast: guroḥ pretasya śiṣyastu pitṛmedhaṃ samācaran pretahāraiḥ samaṃ tatra daśarātreṇa śudhyati pretāhāraiḥ

sanskrit_iast: rātribhirmāsatulyābhirgarbhasrāve viśudhyati rajasyuparate sādhvī snānena strī rajasvalā
meaning: रात्रिभिर्मासतुल्याभिर्गर्भस्रावे विशुध्यति रजस्युपरते साध्वी स्नानेन स्त्री रजस्वला
text: In the case of persons whose tonsure has not been performed purification has been declared to come after a night; but in the case of those whose tonsure has been performed, purification is held to come after three days.
iast: rātribhirmāsatulyābhirgarbhasrāve viśudhyati rajasyuparate sādhvī snānena strī rajasvalā

sanskrit_iast: nṛṇāmakṛtacūḍānāṃ viśuddhirnaiśikī smṛtā nirvṛttacūḍakānāṃ tu trirātrātśuddhiriṣyate nirvṛttamuṇḍakānāṃ
meaning: नृणामकृतचूडानां विशुद्धिर्नैशिकी स्मृता निर्वृत्तचूडकानां तु त्रिरात्रात्शुद्धिरिष्यते निर्वृत्तमुण्डकानां
text: The child that dies while less than two years old, the relations should, after having decked it, place outside, under the ground that is clean and not defiled by heaps of bones.
iast: nṛṇāmakṛtacūḍānāṃ viśuddhirnaiśikī smṛtā nirvṛttacūḍakānāṃ tu trirātrātśuddhiriṣyate nirvṛttamuṇḍakānāṃ

sanskrit_iast: ūnadvivārṣikaṃ pretaṃ nidadhyurbāndhavā bahiḥ alaṅkṛtya śucau bhūmāvasthisañcayanādṛte
meaning: ऊनद्विवार्षिकं प्रेतं निदध्युर्बान्धवा बहिः अलङ्कृत्य शुचौ भूमावस्थिसञ्चयनादृते
text: For this child no sanctification by fire shall be performed; nor shall water-offering he made to it; having left it like a log of wood, in the forest, one shall keep aloof for three days.
iast: ūnadvivārṣikaṃ pretaṃ nidadhyurbāndhavā bahiḥ alaṅkṛtya śucau bhūmāvasthisañcayanādṛte

sanskrit_iast: nāsya kāryo 'gnisaṃskāro na ca kāryaudakakriyā araṇye kāṣṭhavat tyaktvā kṣapeyustryahameva tu kṣapeta tryahameva ca
meaning: नास्य कार्यो 'ग्निसंस्कारो न च कार्यौदकक्रिया अरण्ये काष्ठवत् त्यक्त्वा क्षपेयुस्त्र्यहमेव तु क्षपेत त्र्यहमेव च
text: For the child up to three years of age, the relations shall not make water-offerings; but for one whose teeth had appeared, or whose naming had been done, it may be done optionally.
iast: nāsya kāryo 'gnisaṃskāro na ca kāryaudakakriyā araṇye kāṣṭhavat tyaktvā kṣapeyustryahameva tu kṣapeta tryahameva ca

sanskrit_iast: nātrivarṣasya kartavyā bāndhavairudakakriyā jātadantasya vā kuryurnāmni vā 'pi kṛte sati
meaning: नात्रिवर्षस्य कर्तव्या बान्धवैरुदकक्रिया जातदन्तस्य वा कुर्युर्नाम्नि वा 'पि कृते सति
text: On the death of a fellow-student, the impurity has been declared to last for one day. In the case of a birth, the purity of the ‘Samānopaka’ relations is held to come after three pays.
iast: nātrivarṣasya kartavyā bāndhavairudakakriyā jātadantasya vā kuryurnāmni vā 'pi kṛte sati

sanskrit_iast: sabrahmacāriṇyekāhamatīte kṣapaṇaṃ smṛtam janmanyekaudakānāṃ tu trirātrātśuddhiriṣyate
meaning: सब्रह्मचारिण्येकाहमतीते क्षपणं स्मृतम् जन्मन्येकौदकानां तु त्रिरात्रात्शुद्धिरिष्यते
text: In the case of women whose sacramentary rite has not been performed, the marital relations become pure after three days; and their paternal relations also become pure according to the rule prescribed before
iast: sabrahmacāriṇyekāhamatīte kṣapaṇaṃ smṛtam janmanyekaudakānāṃ tu trirātrātśuddhiriṣyate

sanskrit_iast: strīṇāmasaṃskṛtānāṃ tu tryahātśudhyanti bāndhavāḥ yathauktenaiva kalpena śudhyanti tu sanābhayaḥ
meaning: स्त्रीणामसंस्कृतानां तु त्र्यहात्शुध्यन्ति बान्धवाः यथौक्तेनैव कल्पेन शुध्यन्ति तु सनाभयः
text: For three days they should eat food free from salines and salts, should bathe, should not eat meat-food and should sleep apart on the ground.
iast: strīṇāmasaṃskṛtānāṃ tu tryahātśudhyanti bāndhavāḥ yathauktenaiva kalpena śudhyanti tu sanābhayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: akṣāralavaṇānnāḥ syurnimajjeyuśca te tryaham māṃsāśanaṃ ca nāśnīyuḥ śayīraṃśca pṛthak kṣitau
meaning: अक्षारलवणान्नाः स्युर्निमज्जेयुश्च ते त्र्यहम् मांसाशनं च नाश्नीयुः शयीरंश्च पृथक् क्षितौ
text: This rule regarding Impurity due to death has been described in reference to cases where the parties are near one another. In reference to cases where they are not near, Kinsmen and relations should observe the following rule.
iast: akṣāralavaṇānnāḥ syurnimajjeyuśca te tryaham māṃsāśanaṃ ca nāśnīyuḥ śayīraṃśca pṛthak kṣitau

sanskrit_iast: saṃnidhāveṣa vai kalpaḥ śāvāśaucasya kīrtitaḥ asaṃnidhāvayaṃ jñeyo vidhiḥ saṃbandhibāndhavaiḥ
meaning: संनिधावेष वै कल्पः शावाशौचस्य कीर्तितः असंनिधावयं ज्ञेयो विधिः संबन्धिबान्धवैः
text: He who hears, within ten days, of a relation resident in a foreign land having died, shall, remain impure for the remainder of the period of ten days.
iast: saṃnidhāveṣa vai kalpaḥ śāvāśaucasya kīrtitaḥ asaṃnidhāvayaṃ jñeyo vidhiḥ saṃbandhibāndhavaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: vigataṃ tu videśasthaṃ śṛṇuyādyo hyanirdaśam yatśeṣaṃ daśarātrasya tāvadevāśucirbhavet
meaning: विगतं तु विदेशस्थं शृणुयाद्यो ह्यनिर्दशम् यत्शेषं दशरात्रस्य तावदेवाशुचिर्भवेत्
text: If the period of ten days has elapsed, he shall remain impure for three days; but when a year has elapsed, he becomes pure by merely touching water.
iast: vigataṃ tu videśasthaṃ śṛṇuyādyo hyanirdaśam yatśeṣaṃ daśarātrasya tāvadevāśucirbhavet

sanskrit_iast: atikrānte daśāhe ca trirātramaśucirbhavet saṃvatsare vyatīte tu spṛṣṭvaivāpo viśudhyati
meaning: अतिक्रान्ते दशाहे च त्रिरात्रमशुचिर्भवेत् संवत्सरे व्यतीते तु स्पृष्ट्वैवापो विशुध्यति
text: Hearing of the death of a kinsman, or of the birth of a son, after the ten days have elapsed, the man becomes pure by plunging into water with his clothes
iast: atikrānte daśāhe ca trirātramaśucirbhavet saṃvatsare vyatīte tu spṛṣṭvaivāpo viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: nirdaśaṃ jñātimaraṇaṃ śrutvā putrasya janma ca savāsā jalamāplutya śuddho bhavati mānavaḥ
meaning: निर्दशं ज्ञातिमरणं श्रुत्वा पुत्रस्य जन्म च सवासा जलमाप्लुत्य शुद्धो भवति मानवः
text: In the event of a child, resident in a foreign country, who is a non-sapiṇḍa relation, dying,—one becomes pure immediately, by plunging into water wth his clothes on.
iast: nirdaśaṃ jñātimaraṇaṃ śrutvā putrasya janma ca savāsā jalamāplutya śuddho bhavati mānavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bāle deśāntarasthe ca pṛthakpiṇḍe ca saṃsthite savāsā jalamāplutya sadya eva viśudhyati
meaning: बाले देशान्तरस्थे च पृथक्पिण्डे च संस्थिते सवासा जलमाप्लुत्य सद्य एव विशुध्यति
text: If, within ten days, another birth or death happen to befall, the Brāhmaṇa shall remain impure until that period of ten days shall have elapsed.
iast: bāle deśāntarasthe ca pṛthakpiṇḍe ca saṃsthite savāsā jalamāplutya sadya eva viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: antardaśāhe syātāṃ cet punarmaraṇajanmanī cet syātāṃ tāvat syādaśucirvipro yāvat tat syādanirdaśam
meaning: अन्तर्दशाहे स्यातां चेत् पुनर्मरणजन्मनी चेत् स्यातां तावत् स्यादशुचिर्विप्रो यावत् तत् स्यादनिर्दशम्
text: On the death of the Teacher, they declare the impurity to last for three days; on the death of his son or wife, it lasts for one day and night; such is the law.
iast: antardaśāhe syātāṃ cet punarmaraṇajanmanī cet syātāṃ tāvat syādaśucirvipro yāvat tat syādanirdaśam

sanskrit_iast: trirātramāhurāśaucamācārye saṃsthite sati tasya putre ca patnyāṃ ca divārātramiti sthitiḥ
meaning: त्रिरात्रमाहुराशौचमाचार्ये संस्थिते सति तस्य पुत्रे च पत्न्यां च दिवारात्रमिति स्थितिः
text: In the case of a learned companion, one shall remain impure for three days; in the case of a maternal uncle, a pupil, an officiating priest and relation, for a night along with the two days (preceding and following it).
iast: trirātramāhurāśaucamācārye saṃsthite sati tasya putre ca patnyāṃ ca divārātramiti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: śrotriye tūpasaṃpanne trirātramaśucirbhavet mātule pakṣiṇīṃ rātriṃ śiṣyaṛtvigbāndhaveṣu ca
meaning: श्रोत्रिये तूपसंपन्ने त्रिरात्रमशुचिर्भवेत् मातुले पक्षिणीं रात्रिं शिष्यऋत्विग्बान्धवेषु च
text: On the death of the King in whose realm he lives, it lasts till the light; in the case of a non-learned teacher, for the whole day; as also in the case of the (ordinary) teacher.
iast: śrotriye tūpasaṃpanne trirātramaśucirbhavet mātule pakṣiṇīṃ rātriṃ śiṣyaṛtvigbāndhaveṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: prete rājani sajyotiryasya syādviṣaye sthitaḥ aśrotriye tvahaḥ kṛtsnamanūcāne tathā gurau kṛtsnāṃ
meaning: प्रेते राजनि सज्योतिर्यस्य स्याद्विषये स्थितः अश्रोत्रिये त्वहः कृत्स्नमनूचाने तथा गुरौ कृत्स्नां
text: The Brāhmaṇa becomes pure in the days, the Kṣatriya in twelve days, the Vaiśya in fifteen days and the Śūdra in a month.
iast: prete rājani sajyotiryasya syādviṣaye sthitaḥ aśrotriye tvahaḥ kṛtsnamanūcāne tathā gurau kṛtsnāṃ

sanskrit_iast: śuddhyedvipro daśāhena dvādaśāhena bhūmipaḥ vaiśyaḥ pañcadaśāhena śūdro māsena śudhyati
meaning: शुद्ध्येद्विप्रो दशाहेन द्वादशाहेन भूमिपः वैश्यः पञ्चदशाहेन शूद्रो मासेन शुध्यति
text: One should not prolong the days of impurity; nor should he interrupt the rites performed in the fires; because he who performs those rites, even if he be a Sapiṇḍa, would never he impure.
iast: śuddhyedvipro daśāhena dvādaśāhena bhūmipaḥ vaiśyaḥ pañcadaśāhena śūdro māsena śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: na vardhayedaghāhāni pratyūhennāgniṣu kriyāḥ na ca tatkarma kurvāṇaḥ sanābhyo 'pyaśucirbhavet
meaning: न वर्धयेदघाहानि प्रत्यूहेन्नाग्निषु क्रियाः न च तत्कर्म कुर्वाणः सनाभ्यो 'प्यशुचिर्भवेत्
text: After having touched the Cāndāla, the menstruating woman, the outcast, the woman in child-bed, the dead body, or toucher thereof—one becomes pure by bathing.
iast: na vardhayedaghāhāni pratyūhennāgniṣu kriyāḥ na ca tatkarma kurvāṇaḥ sanābhyo 'pyaśucirbhavet

sanskrit_iast: divākīrtimudakyāṃ ca patitaṃ sūtikāṃ tathā śavaṃ tatspṛṣṭinaṃ caiva spṛṣṭvā snānena śudhyati
meaning: दिवाकीर्तिमुदक्यां च पतितं सूतिकां तथा शवं तत्स्पृष्टिनं चैव स्पृष्ट्वा स्नानेन शुध्यति
text: On seeing unclean things, the man, after having sipped water, shall always attentively recite the Solar Mantras according to his inclination, as also the Pāvamānī verses, according to his capacity.
iast: divākīrtimudakyāṃ ca patitaṃ sūtikāṃ tathā śavaṃ tatspṛṣṭinaṃ caiva spṛṣṭvā snānena śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: ācamya prayato nityaṃ japedaśucidarśane saurān mantrān yathautsāhaṃ pāvamānīśca śaktitaḥ
meaning: आचम्य प्रयतो नित्यं जपेदशुचिदर्शने सौरान् मन्त्रान् यथौत्साहं पावमानीश्च शक्तितः
text: Having touched a fatty human bone, the Brāhmaṇa becomes pure by bathing, but if it be free from fat, then by sipping water and touching a cow, or looking at the sun
iast: ācamya prayato nityaṃ japedaśucidarśane saurān mantrān yathautsāhaṃ pāvamānīśca śaktitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāraṃ spṛṣṭvā 'sthi sasnehaṃ snātvā vipro viśudhyati ācamyaiva tu niḥsnehaṃ gāmālabhyārkamīkṣya vā
meaning: नारं स्पृष्ट्वा 'स्थि सस्नेहं स्नात्वा विप्रो विशुध्यति आचम्यैव तु निःस्नेहं गामालभ्यार्कमीक्ष्य वा
text: The person under instruction shall not make water-offerings till the completion of his pupilage; at its completion, he becomes pure in three days, after having made the water-offerings.
iast: nāraṃ spṛṣṭvā 'sthi sasnehaṃ snātvā vipro viśudhyati ācamyaiva tu niḥsnehaṃ gāmālabhyārkamīkṣya vā

sanskrit_iast: ādiṣṭī naudakaṃ kuryādā vratasya samāpanāt samāpte tūdakaṃ kṛtvā trirātreṇaiva śudhyati
meaning: आदिष्टी नौदकं कुर्यादा व्रतस्य समापनात् समाप्ते तूदकं कृत्वा त्रिरात्रेणैव शुध्यति
text: The water-offering is withdrawn from those born in vain and from intermixture, from those who are addicted to asceticism and from those who have abandoned themselves.
iast: ādiṣṭī naudakaṃ kuryādā vratasya samāpanāt samāpte tūdakaṃ kṛtvā trirātreṇaiva śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: vṛthāsaṅkarajātānāṃ pravrajyāsu ca tiṣṭhatām ātmanastyāgināṃ caiva nivartetaudakakriyā
meaning: वृथासङ्करजातानां प्रव्रज्यासु च तिष्ठताम् आत्मनस्त्यागिनां चैव निवर्तेतौदकक्रिया
text: Also from women, who have joined a heretic, who behave too freely, who have injured a child in their womb or their husband, and those who drink wine
iast: vṛthāsaṅkarajātānāṃ pravrajyāsu ca tiṣṭhatām ātmanastyāgināṃ caiva nivartetaudakakriyā

sanskrit_iast: pāṣaṇḍamāśritānāṃ ca carantīnāṃ ca kāmataḥ garbhabhartṛdruhāṃ caiva surāpīnāṃ ca yoṣitām
meaning: पाषण्डमाश्रितानां च चरन्तीनां च कामतः गर्भभर्तृद्रुहां चैव सुरापीनां च योषिताम्
text: The student, carrying his own dead Teacher, or tutor, or father, or mother, or monitor,—does not suffer in his observances.
iast: pāṣaṇḍamāśritānāṃ ca carantīnāṃ ca kāmataḥ garbhabhartṛdruhāṃ caiva surāpīnāṃ ca yoṣitām

sanskrit_iast: ācāryaṃ svamupādhyāyaṃ pitaraṃ mātaraṃ gurum nirhṛtya tu vratī pretānna vratena viyujyate
meaning: आचार्यं स्वमुपाध्यायं पितरं मातरं गुरुम् निर्हृत्य तु व्रती प्रेतान्न व्रतेन वियुज्यते
text: One should carry the dead śūdra by the southern gate of the city; but the twice-born persons by the western, northern and eastern gates respectively
iast: ācāryaṃ svamupādhyāyaṃ pitaraṃ mātaraṃ gurum nirhṛtya tu vratī pretānna vratena viyujyate

sanskrit_iast: dakṣiṇena mṛtaṃ śūdraṃ puradvāreṇa nirharet paścimauttarapūrvaistu yathāyogaṃ dvijanmanaḥ
meaning: दक्षिणेन मृतं शूद्रं पुरद्वारेण निर्हरेत् पश्चिमौत्तरपूर्वैस्तु यथायोगं द्विजन्मनः
text: This taint of uncleanliness does not attach to Kings, or to those keeping a vow, or to the performers of sacrificial sessions; because they occupy the position of sovereigns and are ever of the nature of Brahman.
iast: dakṣiṇena mṛtaṃ śūdraṃ puradvāreṇa nirharet paścimauttarapūrvaistu yathāyogaṃ dvijanmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na rājñāmaghadoṣo 'sti vratināṃ na ca sattriṇām aindraṃ sthānamupāsīnā brahmabhūtā hi te sadā
meaning: न राज्ञामघदोषो 'स्ति व्रतिनां न च सत्त्रिणाम् ऐन्द्रं स्थानमुपासीना ब्रह्मभूता हि ते सदा
text: Immediate purification has been ordained for the king on the majestic throne; and the reason for this lies in his occupying that position for the protection of the people.
iast: na rājñāmaghadoṣo 'sti vratināṃ na ca sattriṇām aindraṃ sthānamupāsīnā brahmabhūtā hi te sadā

sanskrit_iast: rājño mahātmike sthāne sadyaḥśaucaṃ vidhīyate prajānāṃ parirakṣārthamāsanaṃ cātra kāraṇam
meaning: राज्ञो महात्मिके स्थाने सद्यःशौचं विधीयते प्रजानां परिरक्षार्थमासनं चात्र कारणम्
text: Also in the case of those killed in a riot or Battle or by lightning or by the king; and of those who have died for the sake of cows and Brāhmaṇas; as also for the person for whom the king desires it.
iast: rājño mahātmike sthāne sadyaḥśaucaṃ vidhīyate prajānāṃ parirakṣārthamāsanaṃ cātra kāraṇam

sanskrit_iast: ḍimbhāhavahatānāṃ ca vidyutā pārthivena ca ḍimbāhavahatānāṃ gobrāhmaṇasya caivārthe yasya caicchati pārthivaḥ
meaning: डिम्भाहवहतानां च विद्युता पार्थिवेन च डिम्बाहवहतानां गोब्राह्मणस्य चैवार्थे यस्य चैच्छति पार्थिवः
text: The King holds in himself the body of the eight guardian deities of the world, of the Moon, the Fire, the Sun, the Wind, Indra, the Lord of Wealth, the Lord of Water, and of Yama.
iast: ḍimbhāhavahatānāṃ ca vidyutā pārthivena ca ḍimbāhavahatānāṃ gobrāhmaṇasya caivārthe yasya caicchati pārthivaḥ

sanskrit_iast: somāgniarkānilaindrāṇāṃ vittāppatyoryamasya ca aṣṭānāṃ lokapālānāṃ vapurdhārayate nṛpaḥ
meaning: सोमाग्निअर्कानिलैन्द्राणां वित्ताप्पत्योर्यमस्य च अष्टानां लोकपालानां वपुर्धारयते नृपः
text: The King is possessed by the Lords of the World; no im purity, therefore, has been ordained for him; for the purity and impurity affect mortals and have their origin and end in the worldly regions.
iast: somāgniarkānilaindrāṇāṃ vittāppatyoryamasya ca aṣṭānāṃ lokapālānāṃ vapurdhārayate nṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: lokeśādhiṣṭhito rājā nāsyāśaucaṃ vidhīyate śaucāśaucaṃ hi martyānāṃ lokebhyaḥ prabhavāpyayau
meaning: लोकेशाधिष्ठितो राजा नास्याशौचं विधीयते शौचाशौचं हि मर्त्यानां लोकेभ्यः प्रभवाप्ययौ
text: For one who is killed in battle with brandished weapons, in the manner befitting the kṣatriya, sacrificial performances become instantly completed; and so also is the impurity; such is the established law
iast: lokeśādhiṣṭhito rājā nāsyāśaucaṃ vidhīyate śaucāśaucaṃ hi martyānāṃ lokebhyaḥ prabhavāpyayau

sanskrit_iast: udyatairāhave śastraiḥ kṣatradharmahatasya ca sadyaḥ saṃtiṣṭhate yajñastathā 'śaucamiti sthitiḥ
meaning: उद्यतैराहवे शस्त्रैः क्षत्रधर्महतस्य च सद्यः संतिष्ठते यज्ञस्तथा 'शौचमिति स्थितिः
text: The Brāhmaṇa becomes purified by touching water; the Kṣatriya by touching his conveyance and weapons; the vaiśya by touching either the goad or the leading-strings; and the Śūdra by touching the stick,—after he has performed the rite
iast: udyatairāhave śastraiḥ kṣatradharmahatasya ca sadyaḥ saṃtiṣṭhate yajñastathā 'śaucamiti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: vipraḥ śudhyatyapaḥ spṛṣṭvā kṣatriyo vāhanāyudham vaiśyaḥ pratodaṃ raśmīn vā yaṣṭiṃ śūdraḥ kṛtakriyaḥ
meaning: विप्रः शुध्यत्यपः स्पृष्ट्वा क्षत्रियो वाहनायुधम् वैश्यः प्रतोदं रश्मीन् वा यष्टिं शूद्रः कृतक्रियः
text: O Best or Brāhmaṇas, thus has been described to you the purification necesssary in the case of ‘Sapiṇḍa-relations.’
iast: vipraḥ śudhyatyapaḥ spṛṣṭvā kṣatriyo vāhanāyudham vaiśyaḥ pratodaṃ raśmīn vā yaṣṭiṃ śūdraḥ kṛtakriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etadvo 'bhihitaṃ śaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu dvijottamāḥ asapiṇḍeṣu sarveṣu pretaśuddhiṃ nibodhata
meaning: एतद्वो 'भिहितं शौचं सपिण्डेषु द्विजोत्तमाः असपिण्डेषु सर्वेषु प्रेतशुद्धिं निबोधत
text: A Brāhmaṇa, having carried, like a relation, a dead Brāhmaṇa who is not his ‘Sapiṇḍa’ relation,—or the near relatives of his mother,—becomes pure in three days.
iast: etadvo 'bhihitaṃ śaucaṃ sapiṇḍeṣu dvijottamāḥ asapiṇḍeṣu sarveṣu pretaśuddhiṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: asapiṇḍaṃ dvijaṃ pretaṃ vipro nirhṛtya bandhuvat viśudhyati trirātreṇa māturāptāṃśca bāndhavān
meaning: असपिण्डं द्विजं प्रेतं विप्रो निर्हृत्य बन्धुवत् विशुध्यति त्रिरात्रेण मातुराप्तांश्च बान्धवान्
