text: But if he eats their food, he becomes pure in ten days; if however he does not eat their food, he is purified in one day, if he does not dwell in that house.
iast: asapiṇḍaṃ dvijaṃ pretaṃ vipro nirhṛtya bandhuvat viśudhyati trirātreṇa māturāptāṃśca bāndhavān

sanskrit_iast: yadyannamatti teṣāṃ tu daśāhenaiva śudhyati anadannannamahnaiva na cet tasmin gṛhe vaset
meaning: यद्यन्नमत्ति तेषां तु दशाहेनैव शुध्यति अनदन्नन्नमह्नैव न चेत् तस्मिन् गृहे वसेत्
text: Having voluntarily followed a dead person, whether he be a relation or not, he becomes pure by bathing with his clothes on, touching fire and eating clarified butter.
iast: yadyannamatti teṣāṃ tu daśāhenaiva śudhyati anadannannamahnaiva na cet tasmin gṛhe vaset

sanskrit_iast: anugamyaicchayā pretaṃ jñātimajñātimeva ca ajñātimeva vā snātvā sacailaḥ spṛṣṭvā 'gniṃ ghṛtaṃ prāśya viśudhyati sacailaṃ , viśuddhyati
meaning: अनुगम्यैच्छया प्रेतं ज्ञातिमज्ञातिमेव च अज्ञातिमेव वा स्नात्वा सचैलः स्पृष्ट्वा 'ग्निं घृतं प्राश्य विशुध्यति सचैलं , विशुद्ध्यति
text: One should not have a dead Brāhmaṇa carried by a Śūdra, while his own people are there. For it would be an oblation into fire, defiled by the touch of the Śūdra, and as such not conducive to heaven.—(l 03).
iast: anugamyaicchayā pretaṃ jñātimajñātimeva ca ajñātimeva vā snātvā sacailaḥ spṛṣṭvā 'gniṃ ghṛtaṃ prāśya viśudhyati sacailaṃ , viśuddhyati

sanskrit_iast: na vipraṃ sveṣu tiṣṭhatsu mṛtaṃ śūdreṇa nāyayet asvargyā hyāhutiḥ sā syātśūdrasaṃsparśadūṣitā
meaning: न विप्रं स्वेषु तिष्ठत्सु मृतं शूद्रेण नाययेत् अस्वर्ग्या ह्याहुतिः सा स्यात्शूद्रसंस्पर्शदूषिता
text: Wisdom, austerity, fire, food, clay, mind, water, smearing, wind, action, the sun and time are means of purification for corporeal beings.
iast: na vipraṃ sveṣu tiṣṭhatsu mṛtaṃ śūdreṇa nāyayet asvargyā hyāhutiḥ sā syātśūdrasaṃsparśadūṣitā

sanskrit_iast: jñānaṃ tapo 'gnirāhāro mṛtmano vāryupāñjanam vāyuḥ karmārkakālau ca śuddheḥ kartr̥̄ṇi dehinām
meaning: ज्ञानं तपो 'ग्निराहारो मृत्मनो वार्युपाञ्जनम् वायुः कर्मार्ककालौ च शुद्धेः कर्त्र्̥̄णि देहिनाम्
text: Among all modes of purification, purity in regard to wealth has been ordained to be the most important; for he who is pure in regard to wealth is really pure, and he is not pure who is merely purified by clay and water.
iast: jñānaṃ tapo 'gnirāhāro mṛtmano vāryupāñjanam vāyuḥ karmārkakālau ca śuddheḥ kartr̥̄ṇi dehinām

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmeva śaucānāmarthaśaucaṃ paraṃ smṛtam yo 'rthe śucirhi sa śucirna mṛtvāriśuciḥ śuciḥ
meaning: सर्वेषामेव शौचानामर्थशौचं परं स्मृतम् यो 'र्थे शुचिर्हि स शुचिर्न मृत्वारिशुचिः शुचिः
text: Learned men become pure by tolerance; by liberality those who have done what should not be done; secret sinners by the repeating of sacred texts; and by austerity those who are well-versed in the Veda.
iast: sarveṣāmeva śaucānāmarthaśaucaṃ paraṃ smṛtam yo 'rthe śucirhi sa śucirna mṛtvāriśuciḥ śuciḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣāntyā śudhyanti vidvāṃso dānenākāryakāriṇaḥ śuddhyanti pracchannapāpā japyena tapasā vedavittamāḥ
meaning: क्षान्त्या शुध्यन्ति विद्वांसो दानेनाकार्यकारिणः शुद्ध्यन्ति प्रच्छन्नपापा जप्येन तपसा वेदवित्तमाः
text: What needs purification is purified by clay and water; the river is purified by its current; the woman of uhclean mind by menstruation; and Brāhmaṇas by renunciation.
iast: kṣāntyā śudhyanti vidvāṃso dānenākāryakāriṇaḥ śuddhyanti pracchannapāpā japyena tapasā vedavittamāḥ

sanskrit_iast: mṛttoyaiḥ śudhyate śodhyaṃ nadī vegena śudhyati rajasā strī manoduṣṭā saṃnyāsena dvijottamāḥ
meaning: मृत्तोयैः शुध्यते शोध्यं नदी वेगेन शुध्यति रजसा स्त्री मनोदुष्टा संन्यासेन द्विजोत्तमाः
text: The limbs are purified by water; the mind is purified by truthfulness; the soul proper by learning and austerity; and cognition is purified by knowledge.
iast: mṛttoyaiḥ śudhyate śodhyaṃ nadī vegena śudhyati rajasā strī manoduṣṭā saṃnyāsena dvijottamāḥ

sanskrit_iast: adbhirgātrāṇi śudhyanti manaḥ satyena śudhyati vidyātapobhyāṃ bhūtātmā buddhirjñānena śudhyati śuddhyati
meaning: अद्भिर्गात्राणि शुध्यन्ति मनः सत्येन शुध्यति विद्यातपोभ्यां भूतात्मा बुद्धिर्ज्ञानेन शुध्यति शुद्ध्यति
text: Thus has been explained to you the rule regarding bodily purification; listen now to the rule regarding the purification of various substances.—(109.)
iast: adbhirgātrāṇi śudhyanti manaḥ satyena śudhyati vidyātapobhyāṃ bhūtātmā buddhirjñānena śudhyati śuddhyati

sanskrit_iast: eṣa śaucasya vaḥ proktaḥ śarīrasya vinirṇayaḥ nānāvidhānāṃ dravyāṇāṃ śuddheḥ śṛṇuta nirṇayam
meaning: एष शौचस्य वः प्रोक्तः शरीरस्य विनिर्णयः नानाविधानां द्रव्याणां शुद्धेः शृणुत निर्णयम्
text: Of igneous substances, of gems and of everything made of stone,—the purification has been ordained to be accomplished by means of ash, by water and by clay.
iast: eṣa śaucasya vaḥ proktaḥ śarīrasya vinirṇayaḥ nānāvidhānāṃ dravyāṇāṃ śuddheḥ śṛṇuta nirṇayam

sanskrit_iast: taijasānāṃ maṇīnāṃ ca sarvasyāśmamayasya ca bhasmanā 'dbhirmṛdā caiva śuddhiruktā manīṣibhiḥ
meaning: तैजसानां मणीनां च सर्वस्याश्ममयस्य च भस्मना 'द्भिर्मृदा चैव शुद्धिरुक्ता मनीषिभिः
text: A golden vessel, free from stains, becomes pure by water alone; so also what is produced in water, what is made of stone and what is made of silver, if it is not enchased (or verse much defiled).
iast: taijasānāṃ maṇīnāṃ ca sarvasyāśmamayasya ca bhasmanā 'dbhirmṛdā caiva śuddhiruktā manīṣibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: nirlepaṃ kāñcanaṃ bhāṇḍamadbhireva viśudhyati viśuddhyati apjamaśmamayaṃ caiva rājataṃ cānupaskṛtam
meaning: निर्लेपं काञ्चनं भाण्डमद्भिरेव विशुध्यति विशुद्ध्यति अप्जमश्ममयं चैव राजतं चानुपस्कृतम्
text: Gold and silver sprang out of the union of water and fire; for these reasons the purification of these two is best done by means of their source.—(112.)
iast: nirlepaṃ kāñcanaṃ bhāṇḍamadbhireva viśudhyati viśuddhyati apjamaśmamayaṃ caiva rājataṃ cānupaskṛtam

sanskrit_iast: apāmagneśca saṃyogāddhaimaṃ raupyaṃ ca nirbabhau tasmāt tayoḥ svayonyaiva nirṇeko guṇavattaraḥ
meaning: अपामग्नेश्च संयोगाद्धैमं रौप्यं च निर्बभौ तस्मात् तयोः स्वयोन्यैव निर्णेको गुणवत्तरः
text: Of copper, iron, brass, pewter and tin, the purification should be done, according to suitability, by means of alkaline substances, of liquid acids and of water.
iast: apāmagneśca saṃyogāddhaimaṃ raupyaṃ ca nirbabhau tasmāt tayoḥ svayonyaiva nirṇeko guṇavattaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: tāmrāyaskāṃsyaraityānāṃ trapuṇaḥ sīsakasya ca śaucaṃ yathārhaṃ kartavyaṃ kṣārāmlodakavāribhiḥ
meaning: ताम्रायस्कांस्यरैत्यानां त्रपुणः सीसकस्य च शौचं यथार्हं कर्तव्यं क्षाराम्लोदकवारिभिः
text: For all liquids, purification has been declared to consist in throwing out a little; for solids, in sprinkling; and for wooden articles, in scraping.
iast: tāmrāyaskāṃsyaraityānāṃ trapuṇaḥ sīsakasya ca śaucaṃ yathārhaṃ kartavyaṃ kṣārāmlodakavāribhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: dravāṇāṃ caiva sarveṣāṃ śuddhirutpavanaṃ smṛtam prokṣaṇaṃ saṃhatānāṃ ca dāravāṇāṃ ca takṣaṇam
meaning: द्रवाणां चैव सर्वेषां शुद्धिरुत्पवनं स्मृतम् प्रोक्षणं संहतानां च दारवाणां च तक्षणम्
text: The purification of the ‘Caru’, the ‘Sruk’ and the ‘Sruva’ is done by means of hot water; as also of the ‘Sphya’, the winnowing basket, the cart, the pestle and the mortar.
iast: dravāṇāṃ caiva sarveṣāṃ śuddhirutpavanaṃ smṛtam prokṣaṇaṃ saṃhatānāṃ ca dāravāṇāṃ ca takṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: mārjanaṃ yajñapātrāṇāṃ pāṇinā yajñakarmaṇi camasānāṃ grahāṇāṃ ca śuddhiḥ prakṣālanena tu
meaning: मार्जनं यज्ञपात्राणां पाणिना यज्ञकर्मणि चमसानां ग्रहाणां च शुद्धिः प्रक्षालनेन तु
text: The purification of the ‘Caru’, the ‘Sruk’ and the ‘Sruva’ is done by means of hot water; as also of the ‘Sphya’, the winnowing basket, the cart, the pestle and the mortar.
iast: mārjanaṃ yajñapātrāṇāṃ pāṇinā yajñakarmaṇi camasānāṃ grahāṇāṃ ca śuddhiḥ prakṣālanena tu

sanskrit_iast: carūṇāṃ sruksruvāṇāṃ ca śuddhiruṣṇena vāriṇā sphyaśūrpaśakaṭānāṃ ca musalaulūkhalasya ca
meaning: चरूणां स्रुक्स्रुवाणां च शुद्धिरुष्णेन वारिणा स्फ्यशूर्पशकटानां च मुसलौलूखलस्य च
text: Of grains and cloth, in large quantities, there is sprinkling with water; and in small quantities, their purification has been ordained to be secured by means of washing with water.
iast: carūṇāṃ sruksruvāṇāṃ ca śuddhiruṣṇena vāriṇā sphyaśūrpaśakaṭānāṃ ca musalaulūkhalasya ca

sanskrit_iast: adbhistu prokṣaṇaṃ śaucaṃ bahūnāṃ dhānyavāsasām prakṣālanena tvalpānāmadbhiḥ śaucaṃ vidhīyate
meaning: अद्भिस्तु प्रोक्षणं शौचं बहूनां धान्यवाससाम् प्रक्षालनेन त्वल्पानामद्भिः शौचं विधीयते
text: The method of purifying leather and tree-barks is similar to that of clothes; and for vegetables, roots and fruits, the purification is like that of grains.
iast: adbhistu prokṣaṇaṃ śaucaṃ bahūnāṃ dhānyavāsasām prakṣālanena tvalpānāmadbhiḥ śaucaṃ vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: cailavatcarmaṇāṃ śuddhirvaidalānāṃ tathaiva ca śākamūlaphalānāṃ ca dhānyavatśuddhiriṣyate tu
meaning: चैलवत्चर्मणां शुद्धिर्वैदलानां तथैव च शाकमूलफलानां च धान्यवत्शुद्धिरिष्यते तु
text: Of Silken and woolen stuffs, by means of saline earth; of blankets by soap-berries; of ‘aṃśupaṭṭa,’ by the Bel-fruit; and of linen by white mustard.
iast: cailavatcarmaṇāṃ śuddhirvaidalānāṃ tathaiva ca śākamūlaphalānāṃ ca dhānyavatśuddhiriṣyate tu

sanskrit_iast: kauśeyāvikayorūṣaiḥ kutapānāmariṣṭakaiḥ śrīphalairaṃśupaṭṭānāṃ kṣaumāṇāṃ gaurasarṣapaiḥ
meaning: कौशेयाविकयोरूषैः कुतपानामरिष्टकैः श्रीफलैरंशुपट्टानां क्षौमाणां गौरसर्षपैः
text: The learned man should purify conch-shells, horn and things mads of bone and tusk, like linen; and by c ow’s urine or water.
iast: kauśeyāvikayorūṣaiḥ kutapānāmariṣṭakaiḥ śrīphalairaṃśupaṭṭānāṃ kṣaumāṇāṃ gaurasarṣapaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣaumavatśaṅkhaśṛṅgāṇāmasthidantamayasya ca śuddhirvijānatā kāryā gomūtreṇaudakena vā
meaning: क्षौमवत्शङ्खशृङ्गाणामस्थिदन्तमयस्य च शुद्धिर्विजानता कार्या गोमूत्रेणौदकेन वा
text: Grass and wood and straw become pure by sprinkling; the house by sweeping and sprinkling; and an earthen pot by re-baking.
iast: kṣaumavatśaṅkhaśṛṅgāṇāmasthidantamayasya ca śuddhirvijānatā kāryā gomūtreṇaudakena vā

sanskrit_iast: prokṣaṇāt tṛṇakāṣṭhaṃ ca palālaṃ caiva śudhyati mārjanaupāñjanairveśma punaḥpākena mṛtmayam
meaning: प्रोक्षणात् तृणकाष्ठं च पलालं चैव शुध्यति मार्जनौपाञ्जनैर्वेश्म पुनःपाकेन मृत्मयम्
text: An earthen vessel which has been defiled by spirituous liquor, urine, ordure, saliva, pus or blood cannot be purified by another burning.—(122)
iast: prokṣaṇāt tṛṇakāṣṭhaṃ ca palālaṃ caiva śudhyati mārjanaupāñjanairveśma punaḥpākena mṛtmayam

sanskrit_iast: madyairmūtraiḥ purīṣairvā ṣṭhīvanaiḥ pūyaśoṇitaiḥ saṃspṛṣṭaṃ naiva śuddhyeta punaḥpākena mṛtmayam
meaning: मद्यैर्मूत्रैः पुरीषैर्वा ष्ठीवनैः पूयशोणितैः संस्पृष्टं नैव शुद्ध्येत पुनःपाकेन मृत्मयम्
text: What has been eaten by a bird, what has been smelt by a cow, blown upon, or sneezed at, or defiled by hair and insects, becomes pure by scattering earth.
iast: madyairmūtraiḥ purīṣairvā ṣṭhīvanaiḥ pūyaśoṇitaiḥ saṃspṛṣṭaṃ naiva śuddhyeta punaḥpākena mṛtmayam

sanskrit_iast: saṃmārjanaupāñjanena sekenaullekhanena ca gavāṃ ca parivāsena bhūmiḥ śudhyati pañcabhiḥ śuddhyati
meaning: संमार्जनौपाञ्जनेन सेकेनौल्लेखनेन च गवां च परिवासेन भूमिः शुध्यति पञ्चभिः शुद्ध्यति
text: From an object tainted by an unclean substance, as long as the smell and the stain caused by it do not disappear, so long should earth and water be applied to it,—in all cases of the purification of things.
iast: saṃmārjanaupāñjanena sekenaullekhanena ca gavāṃ ca parivāsena bhūmiḥ śudhyati pañcabhiḥ śuddhyati

sanskrit_iast: pakṣijagdhaṃ gavā ghrātamavadhūtamavakṣutam dūṣitaṃ keśakīṭaiśca mṛtprakṣepeṇa śudhyati
meaning: पक्षिजग्धं गवा घ्रातमवधूतमवक्षुतम् दूषितं केशकीटैश्च मृत्प्रक्षेपेण शुध्यति
text: The gods ordained three things pure for the Brāhmaṇas: what is not seen, what is washed with water and what is commended by word.
iast: pakṣijagdhaṃ gavā ghrātamavadhūtamavakṣutam dūṣitaṃ keśakīṭaiśca mṛtprakṣepeṇa śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: yāvatnāpaityamedhyāktādgandho lepaśca tatkṛtaḥ tāvan mṛdvāri cādeyaṃ sarvāsu dravyaśuddhiṣu
meaning: यावत्नापैत्यमेध्याक्ताद्गन्धो लेपश्च तत्कृतः तावन् मृद्वारि चादेयं सर्वासु द्रव्यशुद्धिषु
text: Water collected on the ground is pure, if it is sufficient to allay the thirst of the cow; but only if it is not contaminated by any unclean thing, becoming affected by with its smell, colour and taste.
iast: yāvatnāpaityamedhyāktādgandho lepaśca tatkṛtaḥ tāvan mṛdvāri cādeyaṃ sarvāsu dravyaśuddhiṣu

sanskrit_iast: trīṇi devāḥ pavitrāṇi brāhmaṇānāmakalpayan adṛṣṭamadbhirnirṇiktaṃ yacca vācā praśasyate
meaning: त्रीणि देवाः पवित्राणि ब्राह्मणानामकल्पयन् अदृष्टमद्भिर्निर्णिक्तं यच्च वाचा प्रशस्यते
text: The artisan’s hand is always pure; so also is merchandise spread out for sale; the food begged and held by the student is ever sacred; such is the established rule
iast: trīṇi devāḥ pavitrāṇi brāhmaṇānāmakalpayan adṛṣṭamadbhirnirṇiktaṃ yacca vācā praśasyate

sanskrit_iast: āpaḥ śuddhā bhūmigatā vaitṛṣṇyaṃ yāsu gorbhavet avyāptāścedamedhyena gandhavarṇarasānvitāḥ
meaning: आपः शुद्धा भूमिगता वैतृष्ण्यं यासु गोर्भवेत् अव्याप्ताश्चेदमेध्येन गन्धवर्णरसान्विताः
text: The mouth of women is always pure; as also the bird in the dropping of fruits; the calf is pure in causing the flow (of milk); and the dog is pure in the catching of deer
iast: āpaḥ śuddhā bhūmigatā vaitṛṣṇyaṃ yāsu gorbhavet avyāptāścedamedhyena gandhavarṇarasānvitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: nityaṃ śuddhaḥ kāruhastaḥ paṇye yacca prasāritam paṇyaṃ brahmacārigataṃ bhaikṣyaṃ nityaṃ medhyamiti sthitiḥ
meaning: नित्यं शुद्धः कारुहस्तः पण्ये यच्च प्रसारितम् पण्यं ब्रह्मचारिगतं भैक्ष्यं नित्यं मेध्यमिति स्थितिः
text: The flesh of the animal killed by dogs Manu has declared to be pure; as also that of the animal killed by other carnivorous animals and by the Cāṇḍāla and other low castes.
iast: nityaṃ śuddhaḥ kāruhastaḥ paṇye yacca prasāritam paṇyaṃ brahmacārigataṃ bhaikṣyaṃ nityaṃ medhyamiti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: nityamāsyaṃ śuci strīṇāṃ śakuniḥ phalapātane prasrave ca śucirvatsaḥ śvā mṛgagrahaṇe śuciḥ
meaning: नित्यमास्यं शुचि स्त्रीणां शकुनिः फलपातने प्रस्रवे च शुचिर्वत्सः श्वा मृगग्रहणे शुचिः
text: The cavities that are above the navel are all pure; those that are below it are impure; as, also are all excretions dropped fr om the body.
iast: nityamāsyaṃ śuci strīṇāṃ śakuniḥ phalapātane prasrave ca śucirvatsaḥ śvā mṛgagrahaṇe śuciḥ

sanskrit_iast: śvabhirhatasya yan māṃsaṃ śuci tan manurabravīt kravyādbhiśca hatasyānyaiścaṇḍālādyaiśca dasyubhiḥ
meaning: श्वभिर्हतस्य यन् मांसं शुचि तन् मनुरब्रवीत् क्रव्याद्भिश्च हतस्यान्यैश्चण्डालाद्यैश्च दस्युभिः
text: Flies, water-drops, shadow, the cow, the horse, the sun’s rays, dust, earth, air and fire—should be regarded as pure to the touch.
iast: śvabhirhatasya yan māṃsaṃ śuci tan manurabravīt kravyādbhiśca hatasyānyaiścaṇḍālādyaiśca dasyubhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: ūrdhvaṃ nābheryāni khāni tāni medhyāni sarvaśaḥ yānyadhastānyamedhyāni dehāccaiva malāścyutāḥ
meaning: ऊर्ध्वं नाभेर्यानि खानि तानि मेध्यानि सर्वशः यान्यधस्तान्यमेध्यानि देहाच्चैव मलाश्च्युताः
text: For the cleansing of the ejectors or urine and faeces, earth and water should be used, as much as may be necessary; as also in the twelve cleansings of the bodily excretions.
iast: ūrdhvaṃ nābheryāni khāni tāni medhyāni sarvaśaḥ yānyadhastānyamedhyāni dehāccaiva malāścyutāḥ

sanskrit_iast: makṣikā vipruṣaśchāyā gauraśvaḥ sūryaraśmayaḥ rajo bhūrvāyuragniśca sparśe medhyāni nirdiśet
meaning: मक्षिका विप्रुषश्छाया गौरश्वः सूर्यरश्मयः रजो भूर्वायुरग्निश्च स्पर्शे मेध्यानि निर्दिशेत्
text: Fat, man, blood, marrow, urine, ordure, nasal excretion, eab-wax, phlegm, tears, rheum of the eyes and perspiration,—these twelve are the ‘excretions’ of human beings.
iast: makṣikā vipruṣaśchāyā gauraśvaḥ sūryaraśmayaḥ rajo bhūrvāyuragniśca sparśe medhyāni nirdiśet

sanskrit_iast: viṣmūtrautsargaśuddhiarthaṃ mṛtvāryādeyamarthavat daihikānāṃ malānāṃ ca śuddhiṣu dvādaśasvapi
meaning: विष्मूत्रौत्सर्गशुद्धिअर्थं मृत्वार्यादेयमर्थवत् दैहिकानां मलानां च शुद्धिषु द्वादशस्वपि
text: One who desires cleanliness should apply earth—once to the urinary organ, thrice to the anus, ten times to one hand and seven times to both hands.
iast: viṣmūtrautsargaśuddhiarthaṃ mṛtvāryādeyamarthavat daihikānāṃ malānāṃ ca śuddhiṣu dvādaśasvapi

sanskrit_iast: vasā śukramasṛjmajjā mūtraviṣghrāṇakarṇaviṣ śleśma aśru dūṣikā svedo dvādaśaite nṛṇāṃ malāḥ
meaning: वसा शुक्रमसृज्मज्जा मूत्रविष्घ्राणकर्णविष् श्लेश्म अश्रु दूषिका स्वेदो द्वादशैते नृणां मलाः
text: Such is the purification for householders; double of this for students, treble for hermits, and quadruple for renunciates.
iast: vasā śukramasṛjmajjā mūtraviṣghrāṇakarṇaviṣ śleśma aśru dūṣikā svedo dvādaśaite nṛṇāṃ malāḥ

sanskrit_iast: ekā liṅge gude tisrastathaikatra kare daśa ubhayoḥ sapta dātavyā mṛdaḥ śuddhimabhīpsatā
meaning: एका लिङ्गे गुदे तिस्रस्तथैकत्र करे दश उभयोः सप्त दातव्या मृदः शुद्धिमभीप्सता
text: Having passed urine or faeces, and sipped water, one should touch. with water the cavities; also when he may be going to read the Veda, and always when going to take food.
iast: ekā liṅge gude tisrastathaikatra kare daśa ubhayoḥ sapta dātavyā mṛdaḥ śuddhimabhīpsatā

sanskrit_iast: etatśaucaṃ gṛhasthānāṃ dviguṇaṃ brahmacāriṇām triguṇaṃ syādvanasthānāṃ yatīnāṃ tu caturguṇam
meaning: एतत्शौचं गृहस्थानां द्विगुणं ब्रह्मचारिणाम् त्रिगुणं स्याद्वनस्थानां यतीनां तु चतुर्गुणम्
text: Desiring bodily purification, one should sip water thrice; then he should twice wipe his mouth; but the woman and the śūdra should each do it once only.
iast: etatśaucaṃ gṛhasthānāṃ dviguṇaṃ brahmacāriṇām triguṇaṃ syādvanasthānāṃ yatīnāṃ tu caturguṇam

sanskrit_iast: kṛtvā mūtraṃ purīṣaṃ vā khānyācānta upaspṛśet vedamadhyeṣyamāṇaśca annamaśnaṃśca sarvadā
meaning: कृत्वा मूत्रं पुरीषं वा खान्याचान्त उपस्पृशेत् वेदमध्येष्यमाणश्च अन्नमश्नंश्च सर्वदा
text: By śūdras living according to law, shaving should be done every month; thus manner of purification should be like that of the Vaiśya; and their food shall consist of the leavings of twice-born men.
iast: kṛtvā mūtraṃ purīṣaṃ vā khānyācānta upaspṛśet vedamadhyeṣyamāṇaśca annamaśnaṃśca sarvadā

sanskrit_iast: trirācāmedapaḥ pūrvaṃ dviḥ pramṛjyāt tato mukham śarīraṃ śaucamicchan hi strī śūdrastu sakṛt sakṛt
meaning: त्रिराचामेदपः पूर्वं द्विः प्रमृज्यात् ततो मुखम् शरीरं शौचमिच्छन् हि स्त्री शूद्रस्तु सकृत् सकृत्
text: Drops from the mouth, if they do not reach the body, do not make one impure; nor the hairs of the beard that enter the mouth; nor what adheres to the teeth.
iast: trirācāmedapaḥ pūrvaṃ dviḥ pramṛjyāt tato mukham śarīraṃ śaucamicchan hi strī śūdrastu sakṛt sakṛt

sanskrit_iast: śūdrāṇāṃ māsikaṃ kāryaṃ vapanaṃ nyāyavartinām vaiśyavatśaucakalpaśca dvijaucchiṣṭaṃ ca bhojanam
meaning: शूद्राणां मासिकं कार्यं वपनं न्यायवर्तिनाम् वैश्यवत्शौचकल्पश्च द्विजौच्छिष्टं च भोजनम्
text: The drops that touch the feet of one who is helping others to wash should be regarded as on the same footing as those on the ground; and he is not rendered impure by them.
iast: śūdrāṇāṃ māsikaṃ kāryaṃ vapanaṃ nyāyavartinām vaiśyavatśaucakalpaśca dvijaucchiṣṭaṃ ca bhojanam

sanskrit_iast: naucchiṣṭaṃ kurvate mukhyā vipruṣo 'ṅgaṃ na yānti yāḥ na śmaśrūṇi gatānyāsyaṃ na dantāntar 'dhiṣṭhitam
meaning: नौच्छिष्टं कुर्वते मुख्या विप्रुषो 'ङ्गं न यान्ति याः न श्मश्रूणि गतान्यास्यं न दन्तान्तर् 'धिष्ठितम्
text: He who, with some substance in hand, happens to be touched by an unclean object, becomes pure by washing, without laying down that substance.
iast: naucchiṣṭaṃ kurvate mukhyā vipruṣo 'ṅgaṃ na yānti yāḥ na śmaśrūṇi gatānyāsyaṃ na dantāntar 'dhiṣṭhitam

sanskrit_iast: spṛśanti bindavaḥ pādau ya ācāmayataḥ parān bhaumikaiste samā jñeyā na tairāprayato bhavet aprayato
meaning: स्पृशन्ति बिन्दवः पादौ य आचामयतः परान् भौमिकैस्ते समा ज्ञेया न तैराप्रयतो भवेत् अप्रयतो
text: Having vomitted or purged, one should bath and then eat clarified butter. After having eaten his food, he should only sip water. For one who has copulated bathing has been ordained.
iast: spṛśanti bindavaḥ pādau ya ācāmayataḥ parān bhaumikaiste samā jñeyā na tairāprayato bhavet aprayato

sanskrit_iast: ucchiṣṭena tu saṃspṛṣṭo dravyahastaḥ kathaṃ cana anidhāyaiva taddravyamācāntaḥ śucitāmiyāt
meaning: उच्छिष्टेन तु संस्पृष्टो द्रव्यहस्तः कथं चन अनिधायैव तद्द्रव्यमाचान्तः शुचितामियात्
text: One should sip water after having slept, after having sneezed, after having eaten, after having spat, after having told a lie, after having drunk water, and when going to read the Veda, even though he may be quite pure.
iast: ucchiṣṭena tu saṃspṛṣṭo dravyahastaḥ kathaṃ cana anidhāyaiva taddravyamācāntaḥ śucitāmiyāt

sanskrit_iast: vānto viriktaḥ snātvā tu ghṛtaprāśanamācaret ācāmedeva bhuktvā 'nnaṃ snānaṃ maithuninaḥ smṛtam
meaning: वान्तो विरिक्तः स्नात्वा तु घृतप्राशनमाचरेत् आचामेदेव भुक्त्वा 'न्नं स्नानं मैथुनिनः स्मृतम्
text: Thus has the whole rule regarding cleanliness and purification of substances for all castes been expounded to you; listen now to the duties of women.
iast: vānto viriktaḥ snātvā tu ghṛtaprāśanamācaret ācāmedeva bhuktvā 'nnaṃ snānaṃ maithuninaḥ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: suptvā kṣutvā ca bhuktvā ca niṣṭhīvyauktvā 'nṛtāni ca pītvā 'po 'dhyeṣyamāṇaśca ācāmet prayato 'pi san
meaning: सुप्त्वा क्षुत्वा च भुक्त्वा च निष्ठीव्यौक्त्वा 'नृतानि च पीत्वा 'पो 'ध्येष्यमाणश्च आचामेत् प्रयतो 'पि सन्
text: Whether she be a child, or a young woman, or an aged woman, she should not do any act by herself, even in the house.
iast: suptvā kṣutvā ca bhuktvā ca niṣṭhīvyauktvā 'nṛtāni ca pītvā 'po 'dhyeṣyamāṇaśca ācāmet prayato 'pi san

sanskrit_iast: eṣāṃ śaucavidhiḥ kṛtsno dravyaśuddhistathaiva ca eṣa ukto vaḥ sarvavarṇānāṃ strīṇāṃ dharmānnibodhata
meaning: एषां शौचविधिः कृत्स्नो द्रव्यशुद्धिस्तथैव च एष उक्तो वः सर्ववर्णानां स्त्रीणां धर्मान्निबोधत
text: In childhood she should remain under the control of her father, in youth under that of her husband, and on the husband’s death under that of her sons; the woman should never have recourse to independence.
iast: eṣāṃ śaucavidhiḥ kṛtsno dravyaśuddhistathaiva ca eṣa ukto vaḥ sarvavarṇānāṃ strīṇāṃ dharmānnibodhata

sanskrit_iast: bālayā vā yuvatyā vā vṛddhayā vā 'pi yoṣitā na svātantryeṇa kartavyaṃ kiṃ cidkāryaṃ gṛheṣvapi
meaning: बालया वा युवत्या वा वृद्धया वा 'पि योषिता न स्वातन्त्र्येण कर्तव्यं किं चिद्कार्यं गृहेष्वपि
text: She should not seek separation from her father, husband or sons: by separating, the woman would render both families disreputable.
iast: bālayā vā yuvatyā vā vṛddhayā vā 'pi yoṣitā na svātantryeṇa kartavyaṃ kiṃ cidkāryaṃ gṛheṣvapi

sanskrit_iast: bālye piturvaśe tiṣṭhet pāṇigrāhasya yauvane putrāṇāṃ bhartari prete na bhajet strī svatantratām
meaning: बाल्ये पितुर्वशे तिष्ठेत् पाणिग्राहस्य यौवने पुत्राणां भर्तरि प्रेते न भजेत् स्त्री स्वतन्त्रताम्
text: She should be always cheerful and alert in household-work; she should have the utensils well-cleaned and in spending she should be close-fisted.
iast: bālye piturvaśe tiṣṭhet pāṇigrāhasya yauvane putrāṇāṃ bhartari prete na bhajet strī svatantratām

sanskrit_iast: pitrā bhartrā sutairvā 'pi naicchedvirahamātmanaḥ eṣāṃ hi viraheṇa strī garhye kuryādubhe kule
meaning: पित्रा भर्त्रा सुतैर्वा 'पि नैच्छेद्विरहमात्मनः एषां हि विरहेण स्त्री गर्ह्ये कुर्यादुभे कुले
text: Him to whom her father may give her,—or her brother with the father’s permission,—she shall attend upon as long as he lives, and shall not disregard him when he is dead.
iast: pitrā bhartrā sutairvā 'pi naicchedvirahamātmanaḥ eṣāṃ hi viraheṇa strī garhye kuryādubhe kule

sanskrit_iast: sadā prahṛṣṭayā bhāvyaṃ gṛhakārye ca dakṣayā susaṃskṛtaupaskarayā vyaye cāmuktahastayā
meaning: सदा प्रहृष्टया भाव्यं गृहकार्ये च दक्षया सुसंस्कृतौपस्करया व्यये चामुक्तहस्तया
text: At their wedding, the sacrifice to Prajāpati, which is the means of securing welfare, is performed for the purpose of procuring good fortune; it is the giving away that is the source of ownership.
iast: sadā prahṛṣṭayā bhāvyaṃ gṛhakārye ca dakṣayā susaṃskṛtaupaskarayā vyaye cāmuktahastayā

sanskrit_iast: yasmai dadyāt pitā tvenāṃ bhrātā vā 'numate pituḥ taṃ śuśrūṣeta jīvantaṃ saṃsthitaṃ ca na laṅghayet
meaning: यस्मै दद्यात् पिता त्वेनां भ्राता वा 'नुमते पितुः तं शुश्रूषेत जीवन्तं संस्थितं च न लङ्घयेत्
text: The husband who has performed the mantric sacramental rites for women is the imparter of happiness to them both in season and out of season, here as well as in the next world.
iast: yasmai dadyāt pitā tvenāṃ bhrātā vā 'numate pituḥ taṃ śuśrūṣeta jīvantaṃ saṃsthitaṃ ca na laṅghayet

sanskrit_iast: maṅgalārthaṃ svastyayanaṃ yajñaścāsāṃ prajāpateḥ prayujyate vivāhe tu pradānaṃ svāmyakāraṇam
meaning: मङ्गलार्थं स्वस्त्ययनं यज्ञश्चासां प्रजापतेः प्रयुज्यते विवाहे तु प्रदानं स्वाम्यकारणम्
text: Be he ill-mannered or of licentious habits or destitute of good qualities,—the husband should always be attended upon like a god by the true wive.
iast: maṅgalārthaṃ svastyayanaṃ yajñaścāsāṃ prajāpateḥ prayujyate vivāhe tu pradānaṃ svāmyakāraṇam

sanskrit_iast: anṛtāvṛtukāle ca mantrasaṃskārakṛt patiḥ sukhasya nityaṃ dātaiha paraloke ca yoṣitaḥ
meaning: अनृतावृतुकाले च मन्त्रसंस्कारकृत् पतिः सुखस्य नित्यं दातैह परलोके च योषितः
text: There is no separate sacrificing for women, no observances, no fastings; it is by means of serving her husband that she becomes exalted in heaven.
iast: anṛtāvṛtukāle ca mantrasaṃskārakṛt patiḥ sukhasya nityaṃ dātaiha paraloke ca yoṣitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: viśīlaḥ kāmavṛtto vā guṇairvā parivarjitaḥ upacāryaḥ striyā sādhvyā satataṃ devavat patiḥ
meaning: विशीलः कामवृत्तो वा गुणैर्वा परिवर्जितः उपचार्यः स्त्रिया साध्व्या सततं देववत् पतिः
text: The good wife, desirous of reaching her husband’s regions, should never do anything that m ay be disagreeable to her husband, alive or dead.
iast: viśīlaḥ kāmavṛtto vā guṇairvā parivarjitaḥ upacāryaḥ striyā sādhvyā satataṃ devavat patiḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāsti strīṇāṃ pṛthag yajño na vrataṃ nāpyupoṣaṇam upoṣitam patiṃ śuśrūṣate yena tena svarge mahīyate
meaning: नास्ति स्त्रीणां पृथग् यज्ञो न व्रतं नाप्युपोषणम् उपोषितम् पतिं शुश्रूषते येन तेन स्वर्गे महीयते
text: Well might she macerate her body by means of pure flowers, roots and fruits; but she should not even mention the name of another man, after her husband is dead
iast: nāsti strīṇāṃ pṛthag yajño na vrataṃ nāpyupoṣaṇam upoṣitam patiṃ śuśrūṣate yena tena svarge mahīyate

sanskrit_iast: pāṇigrāhasya sādhvī strī jīvato vā mṛtasya vā patilokamabhīpsantī nācaret kiṃ cidapriyam
meaning: पाणिग्राहस्य साध्वी स्त्री जीवतो वा मृतस्य वा पतिलोकमभीप्सन्ती नाचरेत् किं चिदप्रियम्
text: Till her death, she should remain patient, self-controlled and chaste,—seeking that most excellent merit that accrues to women having a single husband.
iast: pāṇigrāhasya sādhvī strī jīvato vā mṛtasya vā patilokamabhīpsantī nācaret kiṃ cidapriyam

sanskrit_iast: kāmaṃ tu ksapayeddehaṃ puṣpamūlaphalaiḥ śubhaiḥ na tu nāmāpi gṛhṇīyāt patyau prete parasya tu
meaning: कामं तु क्सपयेद्देहं पुष्पमूलफलैः शुभैः न तु नामापि गृह्णीयात् पत्यौ प्रेते परस्य तु
text: Many thousands of unmarried Brāhmaṇa students have gone to heaven, without having perpetuated their race
iast: kāmaṃ tu ksapayeddehaṃ puṣpamūlaphalaiḥ śubhaiḥ na tu nāmāpi gṛhṇīyāt patyau prete parasya tu

sanskrit_iast: āsītā maraṇāt ksāntā niyatā brahmacāriṇī yo dharma ekapatnīnāṃ kāṅkṣantī tamanuttamam
meaning: आसीता मरणात् क्सान्ता नियता ब्रह्मचारिणी यो धर्म एकपत्नीनां काङ्क्षन्ती तमनुत्तमम्
text: On the death of her husband, the good wife who remains firm, goes to heaven, even though childless; just like those students
iast: āsītā maraṇāt ksāntā niyatā brahmacāriṇī yo dharma ekapatnīnāṃ kāṅkṣantī tamanuttamam

sanskrit_iast: anekāni sahasrāṇi kumārabrahmacāriṇām divaṃ gatāni viprāṇāmakṛtvā kulasaṃtatim
meaning: अनेकानि सहस्राणि कुमारब्रह्मचारिणाम् दिवं गतानि विप्राणामकृत्वा कुलसंततिम्
text: That woman, however, who from a longing for a child, disregards her husband, brings disgrace to herself in this world and falls off from her place in the other world.
iast: anekāni sahasrāṇi kumārabrahmacāriṇām divaṃ gatāni viprāṇāmakṛtvā kulasaṃtatim

sanskrit_iast: mṛte bhartari sāḍhvī strī brahmacarye vyavasthitā svargaṃ gacchatyaputrā 'pi yathā te brahmacāriṇaḥ
meaning: मृते भर्तरि साढ्वी स्त्री ब्रह्मचर्ये व्यवस्थिता स्वर्गं गच्छत्यपुत्रा 'पि यथा ते ब्रह्मचारिणः
text: What is born of another is not a ‘child’: nor is one begotten on another man’s wife; for good women a second husband is nowhere ordained.
iast: mṛte bhartari sāḍhvī strī brahmacarye vyavasthitā svargaṃ gacchatyaputrā 'pi yathā te brahmacāriṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: apatyalobhādyā tu strī bhartāramativartate saiha nindāmavāpnoti paralokācca hīyate
meaning: अपत्यलोभाद्या तु स्त्री भर्तारमतिवर्तते सैह निन्दामवाप्नोति परलोकाच्च हीयते
text: She, who, having abandoned her own husband who is inerior, has recourse to another person who is superior, becomes contemptible in this world and is called a ‘remarried woman.’
iast: apatyalobhādyā tu strī bhartāramativartate saiha nindāmavāpnoti paralokācca hīyate

sanskrit_iast: nānyautpannā prajā 'stīha na cāpyanyaparigrahe na cānyasya parigrahe na dvitīyaśca sādhvīnāṃ kva cidbhartaupadiśyate
meaning: नान्यौत्पन्ना प्रजा 'स्तीह न चाप्यन्यपरिग्रहे न चान्यस्य परिग्रहे न द्वितीयश्च साध्वीनां क्व चिद्भर्तौपदिश्यते
text: The woman, who, through failure in her duty to her husband, becomes an object of contempt in the world, comes to be born as a jackal and is tormented by foul diseases.
iast: nānyautpannā prajā 'stīha na cāpyanyaparigrahe na cānyasya parigrahe na dvitīyaśca sādhvīnāṃ kva cidbhartaupadiśyate

sanskrit_iast: patiṃ hitvā 'pakṛṣṭaṃ svamutkṛṣṭaṃ yā niṣevate itvā 'vakṛṣṭaṃ nindyaiva sā bhavelloke parapūrvaiti caucyate
meaning: पतिं हित्वा 'पकृष्टं स्वमुत्कृष्टं या निषेवते इत्वा 'वकृष्टं निन्द्यैव सा भवेल्लोके परपूर्वैति चौच्यते
text: By such conduct, the woman, having her thought, word and body well controlled, obtains excellent fame in this world, and also her husband’s region in the other world.
iast: patiṃ hitvā 'pakṛṣṭaṃ svamutkṛṣṭaṃ yā niṣevate itvā 'vakṛṣṭaṃ nindyaiva sā bhavelloke parapūrvaiti caucyate

sanskrit_iast: vyabhicārāt tu bhartuḥ strī loke prāpnoti nindyatām vyabhicāre tu śṛgālayoniṃ prāpnoti pāparogaiśca pīḍyate
meaning: व्यभिचारात् तु भर्तुः स्त्री लोके प्राप्नोति निन्द्यताम् व्यभिचारे तु शृगालयोनिं प्राप्नोति पापरोगैश्च पीड्यते
text: By such conduct, the woman, having her thought, word and body well controlled, obtains excellent fame in this world, and also her husband’s region in the other world.
iast: vyabhicārāt tu bhartuḥ strī loke prāpnoti nindyatām vyabhicāre tu śṛgālayoniṃ prāpnoti pāparogaiśca pīḍyate

sanskrit_iast: patiṃ yā nābhicarati manovāgdehasaṃyutā dehasaṃyatā sā bhartṛlokamāpnoti sadbhiḥ sādhvīiti caucyate
meaning: पतिं या नाभिचरति मनोवाग्देहसंयुता देहसंयता सा भर्तृलोकमाप्नोति सद्भिः साध्वीइति चौच्यते
text: The twice-born man, knowing the law, should cremate the wife of his own caste,—who behaves herself in the said manner, and dies before him,—with the sacred fire and along with the sacrificial implements.
iast: patiṃ yā nābhicarati manovāgdehasaṃyutā dehasaṃyatā sā bhartṛlokamāpnoti sadbhiḥ sādhvīiti caucyate

sanskrit_iast: anena nārī vṛttena manovāgdehasaṃyatā ihāgryāṃ kīrtimāpnoti patilokaṃ paratra ca
meaning: अनेन नारी वृत्तेन मनोवाग्देहसंयता इहाग्र्यां कीर्तिमाप्नोति पतिलोकं परत्र च
text: Having, during the last rites, given away the sacred fires to his wife who died before him, he may marry again and kindle the fires again.
iast: anena nārī vṛttena manovāgdehasaṃyatā ihāgryāṃ kīrtimāpnoti patilokaṃ paratra ca

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ vṛttāṃ savarṇāṃ strīṃ dvijātiḥ pūrvamāriṇīm dāhayedagnihotreṇa yajñapātraiśca dharmavit
meaning: एवं वृत्तां सवर्णां स्त्रीं द्विजातिः पूर्वमारिणीम् दाहयेदग्निहोत्रेण यज्ञपात्रैश्च धर्मवित्
text: In this manner, one shall not omit the Five Sacrifices; and during the second part of his life, he shall take to a wife and dwell in the house.
iast: evaṃ vṛttāṃ savarṇāṃ strīṃ dvijātiḥ pūrvamāriṇīm dāhayedagnihotreṇa yajñapātraiśca dharmavit

sanskrit_iast: bhāryāyai pūrvamāriṇyai dattvā 'gnīnantyakarmaṇi punardārakriyāṃ kuryāt punarādhānameva ca
meaning: भार्यायै पूर्वमारिण्यै दत्त्वा 'ग्नीनन्त्यकर्मणि पुनर्दारक्रियां कुर्यात् पुनराधानमेव च
iast: bhāryāyai pūrvamāriṇyai dattvā 'gnīnantyakarmaṇi punardārakriyāṃ kuryāt punarādhānameva ca

sanskrit_iast: anena vidhinā nityaṃ pañcayajñānna hāpayet dvitīyamāyuṣo bhāgaṃ kṛtadāro gṛhe vaset
meaning: अनेन विधिना नित्यं पञ्चयज्ञान्न हापयेत् द्वितीयमायुषो भागं कृतदारो गृहे वसेत्
iast: anena vidhinā nityaṃ pañcayajñānna hāpayet dvitīyamāyuṣo bhāgaṃ kṛtadāro gṛhe vaset

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ gṛhāśrame sthitvā vidhivat snātako dvijaḥ vane vaset tu niyato yathāvadvijitaindriyaḥ
meaning: एवं गृहाश्रमे स्थित्वा विधिवत् स्नातको द्विजः वने वसेत् तु नियतो यथावद्विजितैन्द्रियः
text: The twice-born accomplished student, having, in the afore- said manner, lived, according to law, the life of the householder, should dwell in the forest, in the proper manner, self-controlled and with his organs under subjection
iast: evaṃ gṛhāśrame sthitvā vidhivat snātako dvijaḥ vane vaset tu niyato yathāvadvijitaindriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: gṛhasthastu yathā paśyedvalīpalitamātmanaḥ apatyasyaiva cāpatyaṃ tadā 'raṇyaṃ samāśrayet
meaning: गृहस्थस्तु यथा पश्येद्वलीपलितमात्मनः अपत्यस्यैव चापत्यं तदा 'रण्यं समाश्रयेत्
text: When the householder notices his wrinkles and greyness, and sees his child’s child,—then he should r etire to the forest,
iast: gṛhasthastu yathā paśyedvalīpalitamātmanaḥ apatyasyaiva cāpatyaṃ tadā 'raṇyaṃ samāśrayet

sanskrit_iast: saṃtyajya grāmyamāhāraṃ sarvaṃ caiva paricchadam putreṣu bhāryāṃ nikṣipya vanaṃ gacchet sahaiva vā
meaning: संत्यज्य ग्राम्यमाहारं सर्वं चैव परिच्छदम् पुत्रेषु भार्यां निक्षिप्य वनं गच्छेत् सहैव वा
text: Having given up cultivated food and all his belongings, he shall repair to the forest, either making over his wife to his sons, or along with her.
iast: saṃtyajya grāmyamāhāraṃ sarvaṃ caiva paricchadam putreṣu bhāryāṃ nikṣipya vanaṃ gacchet sahaiva vā

sanskrit_iast: agnihotraṃ samādāya gṛhyaṃ cāgniparicchadam grāmādaraṇyaṃ niḥsṛtya nivasenniyataindriyaḥ niṣkramya
meaning: अग्निहोत्रं समादाय गृह्यं चाग्निपरिच्छदम् ग्रामादरण्यं निःसृत्य निवसेन्नियतैन्द्रियः निष्क्रम्य
text: Taking with him the Sacred Fire, as also all the ritualistic appurtenances of the Fire, the man shall go forth from the village to the forest and live there, with his senses under control.
iast: agnihotraṃ samādāya gṛhyaṃ cāgniparicchadam grāmādaraṇyaṃ niḥsṛtya nivasenniyataindriyaḥ niṣkramya

sanskrit_iast: muniannairvividhairmedhyaiḥ śākamūlaphalena vā etāneva mahāyajñānnirvapedvidhipūrvakam
meaning: मुनिअन्नैर्विविधैर्मेध्यैः शाकमूलफलेन वा एतानेव महायज्ञान्निर्वपेद्विधिपूर्वकम्
text: These same ‘Great Sacrifices’ he should offer, according to rule, with various kinds of pure food fit for hermits, or with herbs, roots and fruits.
iast: muniannairvividhairmedhyaiḥ śākamūlaphalena vā etāneva mahāyajñānnirvapedvidhipūrvakam

sanskrit_iast: vasīta carma cīraṃ vā sāyaṃ snāyāt prage tathā jaṭāśca bibhṛyānnityaṃ śmaśrulomanakhāni ca
meaning: वसीत चर्म चीरं वा सायं स्नायात् प्रगे तथा जटाश्च बिभृयान्नित्यं श्मश्रुलोमनखानि च
text: He should wear either skin or a bit of cloth; he shall bathe in the evening, as also in the meaning; he shall always wear matted locks, as also beard, hair on his body and nails.
iast: vasīta carma cīraṃ vā sāyaṃ snāyāt prage tathā jaṭāśca bibhṛyānnityaṃ śmaśrulomanakhāni ca

sanskrit_iast: yadbhakṣyaṃ syādtato dadyādbaliṃ bhikṣāṃ ca śaktitaḥ yadbhakṣaḥ apmūlaphalabhikṣābhirarcayedāśramāgatān :āśramāgataṃ
meaning: यद्भक्ष्यं स्याद्ततो दद्याद्बलिं भिक्षां च शक्तितः यद्भक्षः अप्मूलफलभिक्षाभिरर्चयेदाश्रमागतान् :आश्रमागतं
text: What he eats, cut of that he should make the offerings and give alms, according to his capacity; and those who come to his hermitage he should honour with water, roots and fruits and alms
iast: yadbhakṣyaṃ syādtato dadyādbaliṃ bhikṣāṃ ca śaktitaḥ yadbhakṣaḥ apmūlaphalabhikṣābhirarcayedāśramāgatān :āśramāgataṃ

sanskrit_iast: svādhyāye nityayuktaḥ syāddānto maitraḥ samāhitaḥ dātā nityamanādātā sarvabhūtānukampakaḥ
meaning: स्वाध्याये नित्ययुक्तः स्याद्दान्तो मैत्रः समाहितः दाता नित्यमनादाता सर्वभूतानुकम्पकः
text: He should be always engaged in Vedic study, meek, conciliatory, quiet, ever liberal, not accepting any gifts, and compassionate towards all living beings.
iast: svādhyāye nityayuktaḥ syāddānto maitraḥ samāhitaḥ dātā nityamanādātā sarvabhūtānukampakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaitānikaṃ ca juhuyādagnihotraṃ yathāvidhi darśamaskandayan parva paurṇamāsaṃ ca yogataḥ
meaning: वैतानिकं च जुहुयादग्निहोत्रं यथाविधि दर्शमस्कन्दयन् पर्व पौर्णमासं च योगतः
text: He shall offer, according to rule, the sacrificial oblations, taking case not to omit the ‘Darśa’ and the ‘Paurnamāsa’ sacrifice.
iast: vaitānikaṃ ca juhuyādagnihotraṃ yathāvidhi darśamaskandayan parva paurṇamāsaṃ ca yogataḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṛkṣeṣṭyāgrayaṇaṃ caiva cāturmāsyāni cāharet darśeṣṭy āgrayaṇaṃ turāyaṇaṃ ca kramaśo dakṣasyāyanameva ca dākṣasyāyanaṃ
meaning: ऋक्षेष्ट्याग्रयणं चैव चातुर्मास्यानि चाहरेत् दर्शेष्ट्य् आग्रयणं तुरायणं च क्रमशो दक्षस्यायनमेव च दाक्षस्यायनं
text: He shall perform the Darśeṣṭi and the Āgrayaṇa; as also the Cāturmāsya sacrifices, and the Turāyaṇa and Dākṣāyaṇa in due order.
iast: ṛkṣeṣṭyāgrayaṇaṃ caiva cāturmāsyāni cāharet darśeṣṭy āgrayaṇaṃ turāyaṇaṃ ca kramaśo dakṣasyāyanameva ca dākṣasyāyanaṃ

sanskrit_iast: vāsantaśāradairmedhyairmuniannaiḥ svayamāhṛtaiḥ puroḍāśāṃścarūṃścaiva vidhivatnirvapet pṛthak
meaning: वासन्तशारदैर्मेध्यैर्मुनिअन्नैः स्वयमाहृतैः पुरोडाशांश्चरूंश्चैव विधिवत्निर्वपेत् पृथक्
text: With the pure grains fit for hermits, which grow in spring and in autumn, and which he has himself gathered, he shall severally prepare cakes and boiled messes, according to law
iast: vāsantaśāradairmedhyairmuniannaiḥ svayamāhṛtaiḥ puroḍāśāṃścarūṃścaiva vidhivatnirvapet pṛthak

sanskrit_iast: devatābhyastu taddhutvā vanyaṃ medhyataraṃ haviḥ śeṣamātmani yuñjīta lavaṇaṃ ca svayaṃ kṛtam
meaning: देवताभ्यस्तु तद्धुत्वा वन्यं मेध्यतरं हविः शेषमात्मनि युञ्जीत लवणं च स्वयं कृतम्
text: Having offered to the gods that most pure offering consisting of wild-growing things, he shall take to himself the remnant, as also the salt prepared by himself
iast: devatābhyastu taddhutvā vanyaṃ medhyataraṃ haviḥ śeṣamātmani yuñjīta lavaṇaṃ ca svayaṃ kṛtam

sanskrit_iast: sthalajaudakaśākāni puṣpamūlaphalāni ca medhyavṛkṣaudbhavānyadyāt snehāṃśca phalasaṃbhavān
meaning: स्थलजौदकशाकानि पुष्पमूलफलानि च मेध्यवृक्षौद्भवान्यद्यात् स्नेहांश्च फलसंभवान्
text: He should eat the vegetables that grow on land and in water; also flowers, roots and fruits, the productions of pure trees; as well as oils produced from fruits.
iast: sthalajaudakaśākāni puṣpamūlaphalāni ca medhyavṛkṣaudbhavānyadyāt snehāṃśca phalasaṃbhavān

sanskrit_iast: varjayen madhu māṃsaṃ ca bhaumāni kavakāni ca bhūstṛṇaṃ śigrukaṃ caiva śleśmātakaphalāni ca
meaning: वर्जयेन् मधु मांसं च भौमानि कवकानि च भूस्तृणं शिग्रुकं चैव श्लेश्मातकफलानि च
text: He shall avoid honey, meat, cabbages, mushrooms, the fragrant grass, the pot-herb and the ‘Śleṣmātaka (Śleśmātaka?)’ fruits.
iast: varjayen madhu māṃsaṃ ca bhaumāni kavakāni ca bhūstṛṇaṃ śigrukaṃ caiva śleśmātakaphalāni ca

sanskrit_iast: tyajedāśvayuje māsi muniannaṃ pūrvasañcitam jīrṇāni caiva vāsāṃsi śākamūlaphalāni ca
meaning: त्यजेदाश्वयुजे मासि मुनिअन्नं पूर्वसञ्चितम् जीर्णानि चैव वासांसि शाकमूलफलानि च
text: In the month of Āśvina he shall throw away the formerly-gathered ‘hermit’s food’, as also the worn-out clothes and the herbs, roots and fruits.
iast: tyajedāśvayuje māsi muniannaṃ pūrvasañcitam jīrṇāni caiva vāsāṃsi śākamūlaphalāni ca

sanskrit_iast: na phālakṛṣṭamaśnīyādutsṛṣṭamapi kena cit na grāmajātānyārto 'pi mūlāṇi ca phalāni ca puṣpāni ca phalāni ca
meaning: न फालकृष्टमश्नीयादुत्सृष्टमपि केन चित् न ग्रामजातान्यार्तो 'पि मूलाणि च फलानि च पुष्पानि च फलानि च
text: He shall not eat anything produced by ploughing, even though it mat have been thrown away by some one; nor such flowers and fruits as are grown in villages, even though he in be in distress.
iast: na phālakṛṣṭamaśnīyādutsṛṣṭamapi kena cit na grāmajātānyārto 'pi mūlāṇi ca phalāni ca puṣpāni ca phalāni ca

sanskrit_iast: agnipakvāśano vā syāt kālapakvabhujeva vā aśmakuṭṭo bhavedvā 'pi dantaulūkhaliko 'pi vā
meaning: अग्निपक्वाशनो वा स्यात् कालपक्वभुजेव वा अश्मकुट्टो भवेद्वा 'पि दन्तौलूखलिको 'पि वा
text: He may be one living on food cooked by fire, or one eating only what Ripens in its own time; he may use the stone for grinding or he may use his teeth as the mortar.
iast: agnipakvāśano vā syāt kālapakvabhujeva vā aśmakuṭṭo bhavedvā 'pi dantaulūkhaliko 'pi vā

sanskrit_iast: sadyaḥ prakṣālako vā syān māsasañcayiko 'pi vā ṣaṇmāsanicayo vā syāt samānicaya eva vā
meaning: सद्यः प्रक्षालको वा स्यान् माससञ्चयिको 'पि वा षण्मासनिचयो वा स्यात् समानिचय एव वा
text: He may be either one who washes off immediately, or one who lays by for a month, or one who lays by for six months, or one who lays by for a year.
iast: sadyaḥ prakṣālako vā syān māsasañcayiko 'pi vā ṣaṇmāsanicayo vā syāt samānicaya eva vā

sanskrit_iast: naktaṃ cānnaṃ samaśnīyāddivā vā 'hṛtya śaktitaḥ caturthakāliko vā syāt syādvā 'pyaṣṭamakālikaḥ
meaning: नक्तं चान्नं समश्नीयाद्दिवा वा 'हृत्य शक्तितः चतुर्थकालिको वा स्यात् स्याद्वा 'प्यष्टमकालिकः
text: Having collected food to the best of his ability, he should eat it at night, or during the day; or he may do it at every fourth time, or at every eighth time.
iast: naktaṃ cānnaṃ samaśnīyāddivā vā 'hṛtya śaktitaḥ caturthakāliko vā syāt syādvā 'pyaṣṭamakālikaḥ

sanskrit_iast: cāndrāyaṇavidhānairvā śuklakṛṣṇe ca vartayet pakṣāntayorvā 'pyaśnīyādyavāgūṃ kvathitāṃ sakṛt
meaning: चान्द्रायणविधानैर्वा शुक्लकृष्णे च वर्तयेत् पक्षान्तयोर्वा 'प्यश्नीयाद्यवागूं क्वथितां सकृत्
text: Or he may live during the bright and dark fortnights in th e manner of the ‘Cāndrāyaṇa’ penance; or he may eat once at the end of each of the two fortnights, boiled barley-gruel.
iast: cāndrāyaṇavidhānairvā śuklakṛṣṇe ca vartayet pakṣāntayorvā 'pyaśnīyādyavāgūṃ kvathitāṃ sakṛt

sanskrit_iast: puṣpamūlaphalairvā 'pi kevalairvartayet sadā kālapakvaiḥ svayaṃ śīrṇairvaikhānasamate sthitaḥ
meaning: पुष्पमूलफलैर्वा 'पि केवलैर्वर्तयेत् सदा कालपक्वैः स्वयं शीर्णैर्वैखानसमते स्थितः
text: Or, he may always subsist only on flowers, roots and fruits, which have ripened in their own season and fallen down spontaneously,—keeping firm in the ways of life prescribed in the ‘Vaikhānasa’ Institutes.
iast: puṣpamūlaphalairvā 'pi kevalairvartayet sadā kālapakvaiḥ svayaṃ śīrṇairvaikhānasamate sthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhūmau viparivarteta tiṣṭhedvā prapadairdinam sthānāsanābhyāṃ viharet savaneṣūpayannapaḥ
meaning: भूमौ विपरिवर्तेत तिष्ठेद्वा प्रपदैर्दिनम् स्थानासनाभ्यां विहरेत् सवनेषूपयन्नपः
text: He shall roll about on the ground, or stand on tip-toe during the day; he shall beguile his time by standing and sitting, going to water at the ‘Savanas’.
iast: bhūmau viparivarteta tiṣṭhedvā prapadairdinam sthānāsanābhyāṃ viharet savaneṣūpayannapaḥ

sanskrit_iast: grīṣme pañcatapāstu syādvarṣāsvabhrāvakāśikaḥ ārdravāsāstu hemante kramaśo vardhayaṃstapaḥ
meaning: ग्रीष्मे पञ्चतपास्तु स्याद्वर्षास्वभ्रावकाशिकः आर्द्रवासास्तु हेमन्ते क्रमशो वर्धयंस्तपः
text: During summer he shall keep five fires; during the rains, he shall have the sky for his shelter; and during the winter he shall keep wet clothes: gradually increasing his austerities.—(23 ).
iast: grīṣme pañcatapāstu syādvarṣāsvabhrāvakāśikaḥ ārdravāsāstu hemante kramaśo vardhayaṃstapaḥ

sanskrit_iast: upaspṛśaṃstriṣavaṇaṃ pitr̥̄n devāṃśca tarpayet tapascaraṃścaugrataraṃ śoṣayeddehamātmanaḥ
meaning: उपस्पृशंस्त्रिषवणं पित्र्̥̄न् देवांश्च तर्पयेत् तपस्चरंश्चौग्रतरं शोषयेद्देहमात्मनः
text: Bathing at the three Savanas, he shall offer libations to the gods and Pitṛs; and practising harsher and harsher austerities, he shall emaciate his body.
iast: upaspṛśaṃstriṣavaṇaṃ pitr̥̄n devāṃśca tarpayet tapascaraṃścaugrataraṃ śoṣayeddehamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: agnīnātmani vaitānān samāropya yathāvidhi anagniraniketaḥ syān munirmūlaphalāśanaḥ
meaning: अग्नीनात्मनि वैतानान् समारोप्य यथाविधि अनग्निरनिकेतः स्यान् मुनिर्मूलफलाशनः
text: Haying reposited, according to rule, the Śrauta Fires within himself, he shall be a silent hermit, without fires and without a house, living upon roots and fruits.
iast: agnīnātmani vaitānān samāropya yathāvidhi anagniraniketaḥ syān munirmūlaphalāśanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: aprayatnaḥ sukhārtheṣu brahmacārī dharā ''śayaḥ śaraṇeṣvamamaścaiva vṛkṣamūlaniketanaḥ
meaning: अप्रयत्नः सुखार्थेषु ब्रह्मचारी धरा ''शयः शरणेष्वममश्चैव वृक्षमूलनिकेतनः
text: Making no effort to obtain pleasure-giving objects, and maintaining celibacy, he shall sleep on the ground; and not caring for shelter, he shall have the roots of trees for his dwelling.
iast: aprayatnaḥ sukhārtheṣu brahmacārī dharā ''śayaḥ śaraṇeṣvamamaścaiva vṛkṣamūlaniketanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tāpaseṣveva vipreṣu yātrikaṃ bhaikṣamāharet gṛhamedhiṣu cānyeṣu dvijeṣu vanavāsiṣu
meaning: तापसेष्वेव विप्रेषु यात्रिकं भैक्षमाहरेत् गृहमेधिषु चान्येषु द्विजेषु वनवासिषु
text: He shall receive alms just enough for subsistence, only from brāhmaṇa-hermits, or from such twice-born householders as live in the forests.
iast: tāpaseṣveva vipreṣu yātrikaṃ bhaikṣamāharet gṛhamedhiṣu cānyeṣu dvijeṣu vanavāsiṣu

sanskrit_iast: grāmādāhṛtya vā 'śnīyādaṣṭau grāsān vane vasan pratigṛhya puṭenaiva pāṇinā śakalena vā
meaning: ग्रामादाहृत्य वा 'श्नीयादष्टौ ग्रासान् वने वसन् प्रतिगृह्य पुटेनैव पाणिना शकलेन वा
text: While dwelling in the forest, he may bring food from the village,—receiving it either in his hollowed hand or in a potsherd,—and eat eight morsels.
iast: grāmādāhṛtya vā 'śnīyādaṣṭau grāsān vane vasan pratigṛhya puṭenaiva pāṇinā śakalena vā

sanskrit_iast: etāścānyāśca seveta dīkṣā vipro vane vasan vividhāścaupaniṣadīrātmasaṃsiddhaye śrutīḥ
meaning: एताश्चान्याश्च सेवेत दीक्षा विप्रो वने वसन् विविधाश्चौपनिषदीरात्मसंसिद्धये श्रुतीः
text: The Brāhmaṇa dwelling in the forest shall attend to these and other restraints; and also to the several Vedic texts contained in the Upaniṣads, in order to attain the Self.
iast: etāścānyāśca seveta dīkṣā vipro vane vasan vividhāścaupaniṣadīrātmasaṃsiddhaye śrutīḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṛṣibhirbrāhmaṇaiścaiva gṛhasthaireva sevitāḥ vidyātapovivṛddhyarthaṃ śarīrasya ca śuddhaye
meaning: ऋषिभिर्ब्राह्मणैश्चैव गृहस्थैरेव सेविताः विद्यातपोविवृद्ध्यर्थं शरीरस्य च शुद्धये
text: Such of these as have been attended to by sages and Brāhmaṇa householders, for the advancement of knowledge and austerities, and also for the purification of the body
iast: ṛṣibhirbrāhmaṇaiścaiva gṛhasthaireva sevitāḥ vidyātapovivṛddhyarthaṃ śarīrasya ca śuddhaye

sanskrit_iast: aparājitāṃ vā 'sthāya vrajeddiśamajihmagaḥ ā nipātātśarīrasya yukto vārianilāśanaḥ
meaning: अपराजितां वा 'स्थाय व्रजेद्दिशमजिह्मगः आ निपातात्शरीरस्य युक्तो वारिअनिलाशनः
text: Or, having fixed upon the North-Easterly direction, he shall go forward, moving straight on, intent and living upon water and air,—till the falling off of his body.
iast: aparājitāṃ vā 'sthāya vrajeddiśamajihmagaḥ ā nipātātśarīrasya yukto vārianilāśanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āsāṃ maharṣicaryāṇāṃ tyaktvā 'nyatamayā tanum vītaśokabhayo vipro brahmaloke mahīyate
meaning: आसां महर्षिचर्याणां त्यक्त्वा 'न्यतमया तनुम् वीतशोकभयो विप्रो ब्रह्मलोके महीयते
text: Having discarded his body by one of these methods adopted by the Great Sages, the Brāhmaṇa, with sorrow and fear departed, becomes exalted in the region of Brahman.
iast: āsāṃ maharṣicaryāṇāṃ tyaktvā 'nyatamayā tanum vītaśokabhayo vipro brahmaloke mahīyate

sanskrit_iast: vaneṣu ca vihṛtyaivaṃ tṛtīyaṃ bhāgamāyuṣaḥ caturthamāyuṣo bhāgaṃ tyakvā saṅgān parivrajet
meaning: वनेषु च विहृत्यैवं तृतीयं भागमायुषः चतुर्थमायुषो भागं त्यक्वा सङ्गान् परिव्रजेत्
text: Having thus passed the third part of his life in the forest, the man shall, during the fourth part, renounce all attachments and go forth (a wandering mendicant).
iast: vaneṣu ca vihṛtyaivaṃ tṛtīyaṃ bhāgamāyuṣaḥ caturthamāyuṣo bhāgaṃ tyakvā saṅgān parivrajet

sanskrit_iast: āśramādāśramaṃ gatvā hutahomo jitaindriyaḥ bhikṣābalipariśrāntaḥ pravrajan pretya vardhate
meaning: आश्रमादाश्रमं गत्वा हुतहोमो जितैन्द्रियः भिक्षाबलिपरिश्रान्तः प्रव्रजन् प्रेत्य वर्धते
text: If one, after passing from stage to stage and after offering the sacrifices, with senses subdued, tired of alms and offerings,—goes forth as a wandering mendicant, and then dies, then he prospers.
iast: āśramādāśramaṃ gatvā hutahomo jitaindriyaḥ bhikṣābalipariśrāntaḥ pravrajan pretya vardhate

sanskrit_iast: ṛṇāni trīṇyapākṛtya mano mokṣe niveśayet anapākṛtya mokṣaṃ tu sevamāno vrajatyadhaḥ
meaning: ऋणानि त्रीण्यपाकृत्य मनो मोक्षे निवेशयेत् अनपाकृत्य मोक्षं तु सेवमानो व्रजत्यधः
text: One shall turn his mind towards Liberation only after having paid off the three debts; without having paid them, if he seeks for Liberation, he sinks downwards.
iast: ṛṇāni trīṇyapākṛtya mano mokṣe niveśayet anapākṛtya mokṣaṃ tu sevamāno vrajatyadhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: adhītya vidhivadvedān putrāṃścautpādya dharmataḥ iṣṭvā ca śaktito yajñairmano mokṣe niveśayet
meaning: अधीत्य विधिवद्वेदान् पुत्रांश्चौत्पाद्य धर्मतः इष्ट्वा च शक्तितो यज्ञैर्मनो मोक्षे निवेशयेत्
text: After having studied the Vedas according to rule, having begotten sons in the rightful manner, and having offered sacrifices to the rest of his ability,—he shall turn his mind towards Liberation.
iast: adhītya vidhivadvedān putrāṃścautpādya dharmataḥ iṣṭvā ca śaktito yajñairmano mokṣe niveśayet

sanskrit_iast: anadhītya dvijo vedānanutpādya tathā sutān tathā prajāṃ aniṣṭvā caiva yajñaiśca mokṣamicchan vrajatyadhaḥ
meaning: अनधीत्य द्विजो वेदाननुत्पाद्य तथा सुतान् तथा प्रजां अनिष्ट्वा चैव यज्ञैश्च मोक्षमिच्छन् व्रजत्यधः
text: The twice-born person, who seeks Liberation, without having studied the Vedas, without having begotten offspring, and without having offered sacrificis, sinks downwards.
iast: anadhītya dvijo vedānanutpādya tathā sutān tathā prajāṃ aniṣṭvā caiva yajñaiśca mokṣamicchan vrajatyadhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: prājāpatyaṃ nirupyaiṣṭiṃ sarvavedasadakṣiṇām sārvavedasadakṣiṇām ātmanyagnīn samāropya brāhmaṇaḥ pravrajedgṛhāt
meaning: प्राजापत्यं निरुप्यैष्टिं सर्ववेदसदक्षिणाम् सार्ववेदसदक्षिणाम् आत्मन्यग्नीन् समारोप्य ब्राह्मणः प्रव्रजेद्गृहात्
text: Having performed the Prājāpatya Sacrifice, wherein all his belongings are given away as the sacrificial fee,—and having reposited the Fires within himself,—the Brāhmaṇa should go forth from his house.
iast: prājāpatyaṃ nirupyaiṣṭiṃ sarvavedasadakṣiṇām sārvavedasadakṣiṇām ātmanyagnīn samāropya brāhmaṇaḥ pravrajedgṛhāt

sanskrit_iast: yo dattvā sarvabhūtebhyaḥ pravrajatyabhayaṃ gṛhāt tasya tejomayā lokā bhavanti brahmavādinaḥ
meaning: यो दत्त्वा सर्वभूतेभ्यः प्रव्रजत्यभयं गृहात् तस्य तेजोमया लोका भवन्ति ब्रह्मवादिनः
text: He who goes forth from home after having granted freedom from all fear to all beings,—to that expounder of the Veda belong regions of light.
iast: yo dattvā sarvabhūtebhyaḥ pravrajatyabhayaṃ gṛhāt tasya tejomayā lokā bhavanti brahmavādinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasmādaṇuapi bhūtānāṃ dvijānnautpadyate bhayam tasya dehādvimuktasya bhayaṃ nāsti kutaścana
meaning: यस्मादणुअपि भूतानां द्विजान्नौत्पद्यते भयम् तस्य देहाद्विमुक्तस्य भयं नास्ति कुतश्चन
text: The twice-born person, from whom not the slightest danger arises to living beings, suffers no danger from any source, when he has become freed from his body.
iast: yasmādaṇuapi bhūtānāṃ dvijānnautpadyate bhayam tasya dehādvimuktasya bhayaṃ nāsti kutaścana

sanskrit_iast: agārādabhiniṣkrāntaḥ pavitraupacito muniḥ samupoḍheṣu kāmeṣu nir 'pekṣaḥ parivrajet
meaning: अगारादभिनिष्क्रान्तः पवित्रौपचितो मुनिः समुपोढेषु कामेषु निर् 'पेक्षः परिव्रजेत्
text: Having departed from his house, fully equipped with the sacred things, he shall go forth, silent and wholly indifferent towards pleasures that may be presented to him.—(41.)
iast: agārādabhiniṣkrāntaḥ pavitraupacito muniḥ samupoḍheṣu kāmeṣu nir 'pekṣaḥ parivrajet

sanskrit_iast: eka eva carennityaṃ siddhyarthamasahāyavān siddhimekasya saṃpaśyanna jahāti na hīyate siddham
meaning: एक एव चरेन्नित्यं सिद्ध्यर्थमसहायवान् सिद्धिमेकस्य संपश्यन्न जहाति न हीयते सिद्धम्
text: He shall always wander about alone, without a companion, in order to attain success; when one realises that success accrues to the solitary man, he neither forsakes nor becomes forsaken.
iast: eka eva carennityaṃ siddhyarthamasahāyavān siddhimekasya saṃpaśyanna jahāti na hīyate siddham

sanskrit_iast: anagniraniketaḥ syādgrāmamannārthamāśrayet upekṣako 'saṅkusuko munirbhāvasamāhitaḥ 'sāṅkusuko
meaning: अनग्निरनिकेतः स्याद्ग्राममन्नार्थमाश्रयेत् उपेक्षको 'सङ्कुसुको मुनिर्भावसमाहितः 'साङ्कुसुको
text: He shall be without fires and without home; he may go to a village for food;—disinterested, steady, silent and calmly-disposed.
iast: anagniraniketaḥ syādgrāmamannārthamāśrayet upekṣako 'saṅkusuko munirbhāvasamāhitaḥ 'sāṅkusuko

sanskrit_iast: kapālaṃ vṛkṣamūlāni kucelamasahāyatā kucailaṃ samatā caiva sarvasminnetatmuktasya lakṣaṇam
meaning: कपालं वृक्षमूलानि कुचेलमसहायता कुचैलं समता चैव सर्वस्मिन्नेतत्मुक्तस्य लक्षणम्
text: The potsherd, the roots of trees, coarse cloth, solitude, e quality towards all,—are the mark of the liberated man.
iast: kapālaṃ vṛkṣamūlāni kucelamasahāyatā kucailaṃ samatā caiva sarvasminnetatmuktasya lakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: nābhinandeta maraṇaṃ nābhinandeta jīvitam kālameva pratīkṣeta nirveśaṃ bhṛtako yathā
meaning: नाभिनन्देत मरणं नाभिनन्देत जीवितम् कालमेव प्रतीक्षेत निर्वेशं भृतको यथा
text: He shall not rejoice at death; nor shall he rejoice at life; he shall await his time, just as the servant awaits the fulfilment of his contract
iast: nābhinandeta maraṇaṃ nābhinandeta jīvitam kālameva pratīkṣeta nirveśaṃ bhṛtako yathā

sanskrit_iast: dṛṣṭipūtaṃ nyaset pādaṃ vastrapūtaṃ jalaṃ pibet satyapūtāṃ vadedvācaṃ manaḥpūtaṃ samācaret
meaning: दृष्टिपूतं न्यसेत् पादं वस्त्रपूतं जलं पिबेत् सत्यपूतां वदेद्वाचं मनःपूतं समाचरेत्
text: He shall place his foot sight-purified, drink water cloth-clarified, utter speech truth-sanctified and act with pure mind
iast: dṛṣṭipūtaṃ nyaset pādaṃ vastrapūtaṃ jalaṃ pibet satyapūtāṃ vadedvācaṃ manaḥpūtaṃ samācaret

sanskrit_iast: ativādāṃstitikṣeta nāvamanyeta kaṃ cana na caimaṃ dehamāśritya vairaṃ kurvīta kena cit
meaning: अतिवादांस्तितिक्षेत नावमन्येत कं चन न चैमं देहमाश्रित्य वैरं कुर्वीत केन चित्
text: He shall patiently bear improper words, and shall not insult anyone; and he shall not make enmity with any one, for the sake of his present body
iast: ativādāṃstitikṣeta nāvamanyeta kaṃ cana na caimaṃ dehamāśritya vairaṃ kurvīta kena cit

sanskrit_iast: kruddhyantaṃ na pratikrudhyedākruṣṭaḥ kuśalaṃ vadet saptadvārāvakīrṇāṃ ca na vācamanṛtāṃ vadet
meaning: क्रुद्ध्यन्तं न प्रतिक्रुध्येदाक्रुष्टः कुशलं वदेत् सप्तद्वारावकीर्णां च न वाचमनृतां वदेत्
text: Towards an angry man he shall not retort in anger; when he is cursed, he shall pronounce a blessing; and he shall not utter an untrue word, spreading over the seven openings.
iast: kruddhyantaṃ na pratikrudhyedākruṣṭaḥ kuśalaṃ vadet saptadvārāvakīrṇāṃ ca na vācamanṛtāṃ vadet

sanskrit_iast: adhyātmaratirāsīno nir 'pekṣo nirāmiṣaḥ ātmanaiva sahāyena sukhārthī vicarediha
meaning: अध्यात्मरतिरासीनो निर् 'पेक्षो निरामिषः आत्मनैव सहायेन सुखार्थी विचरेदिह
text: Centered in spirituality, disinterested, free from longings, with himself as his sole companion, he shall wander forth in the world, seeking bliss.
iast: adhyātmaratirāsīno nir 'pekṣo nirāmiṣaḥ ātmanaiva sahāyena sukhārthī vicarediha

sanskrit_iast: na cautpātanimittābhyāṃ na nakṣatrāṅgavidyayā nānuśāsanavādābhyāṃ bhikṣāṃ lipseta karhi cit
meaning: न चौत्पातनिमित्ताभ्यां न नक्षत्राङ्गविद्यया नानुशासनवादाभ्यां भिक्षां लिप्सेत कर्हि चित्
text: He shall never obtain alms either by means of prodigies and portents, or by means of the science of astrology and palmistry, or by means of counsel and discussion.
iast: na cautpātanimittābhyāṃ na nakṣatrāṅgavidyayā nānuśāsanavādābhyāṃ bhikṣāṃ lipseta karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: na tāpasairbrāhmaṇairvā vayobhirapi vā śvabhiḥ ākīrṇaṃ bhikṣukairvā 'nyairagāramupasaṃvrajet
meaning: न तापसैर्ब्राह्मणैर्वा वयोभिरपि वा श्वभिः आकीर्णं भिक्षुकैर्वा 'न्यैरगारमुपसंव्रजेत्
text: He shall not go near a house that is filled by hermits, brāhmaṇas, birds, dogs or other mendicants
iast: na tāpasairbrāhmaṇairvā vayobhirapi vā śvabhiḥ ākīrṇaṃ bhikṣukairvā 'nyairagāramupasaṃvrajet

sanskrit_iast: kḷptakeśanakhaśmaśruḥ pātrī daṇḍī kusumbhavān vicarenniyato nityaṃ sarvabhūtānyapīḍayan
meaning: कॢप्तकेशनखश्मश्रुः पात्री दण्डी कुसुम्भवान् विचरेन्नियतो नित्यं सर्वभूतान्यपीडयन्
text: His hair, nails and beard clipped, equipped with vessels, staffs and water-pot, he shall constantly wander about, self-controlled and not causing pain to any living brings.
iast: kḷptakeśanakhaśmaśruḥ pātrī daṇḍī kusumbhavān vicarenniyato nityaṃ sarvabhūtānyapīḍayan

sanskrit_iast: ataijasāni pātrāṇi tasya syurnirvraṇāni ca teṣāmadbhiḥ smṛtaṃ śaucaṃ camasānāmivādhvare
meaning: अतैजसानि पात्राणि तस्य स्युर्निर्व्रणानि च तेषामद्भिः स्मृतं शौचं चमसानामिवाध्वरे
text: His vessels shall be non-metallic and free from holes; the cleansing of there has been ordained to be done by water, just like that of the vessels at a sacrifice.
iast: ataijasāni pātrāṇi tasya syurnirvraṇāni ca teṣāmadbhiḥ smṛtaṃ śaucaṃ camasānāmivādhvare

sanskrit_iast: alābuṃ dārupātraṃ ca mṛṇmayaṃ vaidalaṃ tathā etāṇi yatipātrāṇi manuḥ svāyaṃbhuvo 'bravīt
meaning: अलाबुं दारुपात्रं च मृण्मयं वैदलं तथा एताणि यतिपात्राणि मनुः स्वायंभुवो 'ब्रवीत्
text: Manu, the son of Svayambhu, has declared that the vessels of the renunciate shall be a gourd, a vessel of wood or of earthenware, or of splits.
iast: alābuṃ dārupātraṃ ca mṛṇmayaṃ vaidalaṃ tathā etāṇi yatipātrāṇi manuḥ svāyaṃbhuvo 'bravīt

sanskrit_iast: ekakālaṃ caredbhaikṣaṃ na prasajjeta vistare bhaikṣe prasakto hi yatirviṣayeṣvapi sajjati
meaning: एककालं चरेद्भैक्षं न प्रसज्जेत विस्तरे भैक्षे प्रसक्तो हि यतिर्विषयेष्वपि सज्जति
text: He shall go for alms only once, and shall not seek for a large quantity; because the renunciate who becomes addicted to collecting alms becomes attached to sensual objects also.
iast: ekakālaṃ caredbhaikṣaṃ na prasajjeta vistare bhaikṣe prasakto hi yatirviṣayeṣvapi sajjati

sanskrit_iast: vidhūme sannamusale vy 'ṅgāre bhuktavajjane vṛtte śarāvasaṃpāte bhikṣāṃ nityaṃ yatiścaret
meaning: विधूमे सन्नमुसले व्य् 'ङ्गारे भुक्तवज्जने वृत्ते शरावसंपाते भिक्षां नित्यं यतिश्चरेत्
text: The renunciate shall go for begging alms at a time when there is no smoke issuing, when the pestle has ceased to ply, when fire embers have been extinguished, when people have eaten, and when the removal of the dishes has been finished.
iast: vidhūme sannamusale vy 'ṅgāre bhuktavajjane vṛtte śarāvasaṃpāte bhikṣāṃ nityaṃ yatiścaret

sanskrit_iast: alābhe na viṣadī syātlābhe caiva na harṣayet prāṇayātrikamātraḥ syātmātrāsaṅgādvinirgataḥ
meaning: अलाभे न विषदी स्यात्लाभे चैव न हर्षयेत् प्राणयात्रिकमात्रः स्यात्मात्रासङ्गाद्विनिर्गतः
text: He shall not be sorry at not obtaining alms; nor shall he rejoice at obtaining it; he shall have only what suffices to sustain his life, and be free from all attachment to his accessories.
iast: alābhe na viṣadī syātlābhe caiva na harṣayet prāṇayātrikamātraḥ syātmātrāsaṅgādvinirgataḥ

sanskrit_iast: abhipūjitalābhāṃstu jugupsetaiva sarvaśaḥ abhipūjitalābhaiśca yatirmukto 'pi badhyate
meaning: अभिपूजितलाभांस्तु जुगुप्सेतैव सर्वशः अभिपूजितलाभैश्च यतिर्मुक्तो 'पि बध्यते
text: He shall disdain all honorific presents; by honorific presents the Renunciate, even though liberated, becomes fettered
iast: abhipūjitalābhāṃstu jugupsetaiva sarvaśaḥ abhipūjitalābhaiśca yatirmukto 'pi badhyate

sanskrit_iast: alpānnābhyavahāreṇa rahaḥsthānāsanena ca hriyamāṇāni viṣayairindriyāṇi nivartayet
meaning: अल्पान्नाभ्यवहारेण रहःस्थानासनेन च ह्रियमाणानि विषयैरिन्द्रियाणि निवर्तयेत्
text: By eating little food and by standing and sitting in solitude, he shall restrain his senses, when attracted by sensual objects.
iast: alpānnābhyavahāreṇa rahaḥsthānāsanena ca hriyamāṇāni viṣayairindriyāṇi nivartayet

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇāṃ nirodhena rāgadveśakṣayeṇa ca ahiṃsayā ca bhūtānāmamṛtatvāya kalpate
meaning: इन्द्रियाणां निरोधेन रागद्वेशक्षयेण च अहिंसया च भूतानाममृतत्वाय कल्पते
text: By the restraining of the senses, by the destruction of love and hatred, and by not injuring living beings, he becomes fit for immortality.
iast: indriyāṇāṃ nirodhena rāgadveśakṣayeṇa ca ahiṃsayā ca bhūtānāmamṛtatvāya kalpate

sanskrit_iast: avekṣeta gatīrnr̥̄ṇāṃ karmadoṣasamudbhavāḥ niraye caiva patanaṃ yātanāśca yamakṣaye
meaning: अवेक्षेत गतीर्न्र्̥̄णां कर्मदोषसमुद्भवाः निरये चैव पतनं यातनाश्च यमक्षये
text: He should reflect upon the conditions of men, arising from the defects of their deeds, their falling into hell and their sufferings in the abode of the death-god.
iast: avekṣeta gatīrnr̥̄ṇāṃ karmadoṣasamudbhavāḥ niraye caiva patanaṃ yātanāśca yamakṣaye

sanskrit_iast: viprayogaṃ priyaiścaiva saṃyogaṃ ca tathā 'priyaiḥ jarayā cābhibhavanaṃ vyādhibhiścaupapīḍanam
meaning: विप्रयोगं प्रियैश्चैव संयोगं च तथा 'प्रियैः जरया चाभिभवनं व्याधिभिश्चौपपीडनम्
text: On the separation of loved ones and the meeting of hated persons; on being beset with decrepitude and suffering from diseases.
iast: viprayogaṃ priyaiścaiva saṃyogaṃ ca tathā 'priyaiḥ jarayā cābhibhavanaṃ vyādhibhiścaupapīḍanam

sanskrit_iast: dehādutkramaṇaṃ cāṣmāt punargarbhe ca saṃbhavam yonikoṭisahasreṣu sṛtīścāsyāntarātmanaḥ
meaning: देहादुत्क्रमणं चाष्मात् पुनर्गर्भे च संभवम् योनिकोटिसहस्रेषु सृतीश्चास्यान्तरात्मनः
text: There is for his Inner Soul departure from the body, then again birth in the womb, and transmigrations among millions of life-forms.
iast: dehādutkramaṇaṃ cāṣmāt punargarbhe ca saṃbhavam yonikoṭisahasreṣu sṛtīścāsyāntarātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: adharmaprabhavaṃ caiva duḥkhayogaṃ śarīriṇām dharmārthaprabhavaṃ caiva sukhasaṃyogamakṣayam
meaning: अधर्मप्रभवं चैव दुःखयोगं शरीरिणाम् धर्मार्थप्रभवं चैव सुखसंयोगमक्षयम्
text: On the infliction of pain upon living beings, caused by demerit; as also upon the imperishable union with happiness proceeding from the essence of merit.
iast: adharmaprabhavaṃ caiva duḥkhayogaṃ śarīriṇām dharmārthaprabhavaṃ caiva sukhasaṃyogamakṣayam

sanskrit_iast: sūkṣmatāṃ cānvavekṣeta yogena paramātmanaḥ deheṣu ca samutpattimuttameṣvadhameṣu ca deheṣu caivopapattim
meaning: सूक्ष्मतां चान्ववेक्षेत योगेन परमात्मनः देहेषु च समुत्पत्तिमुत्तमेष्वधमेषु च देहेषु चैवोपपत्तिम्
text: By meditation he shall recognise the subtile character of the Higher Self, as also the possibility of its presence in all organisms, high and low.
iast: sūkṣmatāṃ cānvavekṣeta yogena paramātmanaḥ deheṣu ca samutpattimuttameṣvadhameṣu ca deheṣu caivopapattim

sanskrit_iast: dūṣito 'pi careddharmaṃ yatra tatrāśrame rataḥ bhūṣito 'pi samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu na liṅgaṃ dharmakāraṇam
meaning: दूषितो 'पि चरेद्धर्मं यत्र तत्राश्रमे रतः भूषितो 'पि समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु न लिङ्गं धर्मकारणम्
text: Even though he be adorned, the man should fulfil his duty, to whichever order he may belong. He should be equal to all beings; mere external marks are not conducive to merit.
iast: dūṣito 'pi careddharmaṃ yatra tatrāśrame rataḥ bhūṣito 'pi samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu na liṅgaṃ dharmakāraṇam

sanskrit_iast: phalaṃ katakavṛkṣasya yadyapyambuprasādakam na nāmagrahaṇādeva tasya vāri prasīdati
meaning: फलं कतकवृक्षस्य यद्यप्यम्बुप्रसादकम् न नामग्रहणादेव तस्य वारि प्रसीदति
text: Though the fruit of the Kataka tree clarifies water, yet water does not become clear by the mere mention of its name.
iast: phalaṃ katakavṛkṣasya yadyapyambuprasādakam na nāmagrahaṇādeva tasya vāri prasīdati

sanskrit_iast: saṃrakṣaṇārthaṃ jantūnāṃ rātrāvahani vā sadā śarīrasyātyaye caiva samīkṣya vasudhāṃ caret
meaning: संरक्षणार्थं जन्तूनां रात्रावहनि वा सदा शरीरस्यात्यये चैव समीक्ष्य वसुधां चरेत्
text: With a view to the safety of living beings, he shall always, during day and night, even during bodily illness, walk after having scanned the ground.
iast: saṃrakṣaṇārthaṃ jantūnāṃ rātrāvahani vā sadā śarīrasyātyaye caiva samīkṣya vasudhāṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: ahnā rātryā ca yāñjantūn hinastyajñānato yatiḥ teṣāṃ snātvā viśuddhyarthaṃ prāṇāyāmān ṣaḍācaret
meaning: अह्ना रात्र्या च याञ्जन्तून् हिनस्त्यज्ञानतो यतिः तेषां स्नात्वा विशुद्ध्यर्थं प्राणायामान् षडाचरेत्
text: By day and by night, if the Renunciate unintentionally injures some living creatures, he shall, for the purpose of expiating it, bathe and then perform six ‘breath-suspensions.’
iast: ahnā rātryā ca yāñjantūn hinastyajñānato yatiḥ teṣāṃ snātvā viśuddhyarthaṃ prāṇāyāmān ṣaḍācaret

sanskrit_iast: prāṇāyāmā brāhmaṇasya trayo 'pi vidhivat kṛtāḥ vyāhṛtipraṇavairyuktā vijñeyaṃ paramaṃ tapaḥ
meaning: प्राणायामा ब्राह्मणस्य त्रयो 'पि विधिवत् कृताः व्याहृतिप्रणवैर्युक्ता विज्ञेयं परमं तपः
text: Even three ‘breath-suspensions,’ accompanied by the three ‘vyāhṛti’-syllables and the syllable ‘om’, when duly performed, should be regarded as the highest austerity for the Brāhmaṇa.
iast: prāṇāyāmā brāhmaṇasya trayo 'pi vidhivat kṛtāḥ vyāhṛtipraṇavairyuktā vijñeyaṃ paramaṃ tapaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dahyante dhmāyamānānāṃ dhātūnāṃ hi yathā malāḥ tathaindriyāṇāṃ dahyante doṣāḥ prāṇasya nigrahāt
meaning: दह्यन्ते ध्मायमानानां धातूनां हि यथा मलाः तथैन्द्रियाणां दह्यन्ते दोषाः प्राणस्य निग्रहात्
text: Just as the impurities of metallic ores are consumed when they are blasted, even so are the taints of the senses consumed through the suspension of breath.
iast: dahyante dhmāyamānānāṃ dhātūnāṃ hi yathā malāḥ tathaindriyāṇāṃ dahyante doṣāḥ prāṇasya nigrahāt

sanskrit_iast: prāṇāyamairdaheddoṣān dhāraṇābhiśca kilbiṣam pratyāhāreṇa saṃsargān dhyānenānīśvarān guṇān
meaning: प्राणायमैर्दहेद्दोषान् धारणाभिश्च किल्बिषम् प्रत्याहारेण संसर्गान् ध्यानेनानीश्वरान् गुणान्
text: By means of ‘Breath-suspension’ he shall destroy the taints; and by means of ‘Concentration,’ all sin; all attachments by means of ‘Abstraction,’ and by means of ‘Contemplation,’ those attributes that are not independent.
iast: prāṇāyamairdaheddoṣān dhāraṇābhiśca kilbiṣam pratyāhāreṇa saṃsargān dhyānenānīśvarān guṇān

sanskrit_iast: uccāvaceṣu bhūteṣu durjñeyāmakṛtātmabhiḥ dhyānayogena saṃpaśyedgatimasyāntarātmanaḥ
meaning: उच्चावचेषु भूतेषु दुर्ज्ञेयामकृतात्मभिः ध्यानयोगेन संपश्येद्गतिमस्यान्तरात्मनः
text: By the practice of meditation he shall recognise the presence of this Inner Soul in all beings, high and low,—which is difficult to understand by unregenerate people.
iast: uccāvaceṣu bhūteṣu durjñeyāmakṛtātmabhiḥ dhyānayogena saṃpaśyedgatimasyāntarātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: samyagdarśanasaṃpannaḥ karmabhirna nibadhyate darśanena vihīnastu saṃsāraṃ pratipadyate
meaning: सम्यग्दर्शनसंपन्नः कर्मभिर्न निबध्यते दर्शनेन विहीनस्तु संसारं प्रतिपद्यते
text: Equipped with true insight, he is no longer fettered by his acts; but destitute of insight, he falls into the cycle of births and deaths.
iast: samyagdarśanasaṃpannaḥ karmabhirna nibadhyate darśanena vihīnastu saṃsāraṃ pratipadyate

sanskrit_iast: ahiṃsayaindriyāsaṅgairvaidikaiścaiva karmabhiḥ tapasaścaraṇaiścaugraiḥ sādhayantīha tatpadam
meaning: अहिंसयैन्द्रियासङ्गैर्वैदिकैश्चैव कर्मभिः तपसश्चरणैश्चौग्रैः साधयन्तीह तत्पदम्
text: By abstention from injuring, by the non-attachment of the senses, by the acts prescribed in the Veda, by the rigorous practising of austerities, they attain the position of That Being.
iast: ahiṃsayaindriyāsaṅgairvaidikaiścaiva karmabhiḥ tapasaścaraṇaiścaugraiḥ sādhayantīha tatpadam

sanskrit_iast: asthisthūṇaṃ snāyuyutaṃ māṃsaśoṇitalepanam carmāvanaddhaṃ durgandhi pūrṇaṃ mūtrapurīṣayoḥ
meaning: अस्थिस्थूणं स्नायुयुतं मांसशोणितलेपनम् चर्मावनद्धं दुर्गन्धि पूर्णं मूत्रपुरीषयोः
text: He shall discard this abode of material substances, where the bones are the pillars, which is held together by the tendons, plastered with flesh and blood; covered with the skin, foul-smelling, and full of urine and ordure;—(76) beset with wrinkles and sorrow, the seat of disease, harassed, sullied with passions and perishable.
iast: asthisthūṇaṃ snāyuyutaṃ māṃsaśoṇitalepanam carmāvanaddhaṃ durgandhi pūrṇaṃ mūtrapurīṣayoḥ

sanskrit_iast: jarāśokasamāviṣṭaṃ rogāyatanamāturam rajasvalamanityaṃ ca bhūtāvāsamimaṃ tyajet
meaning: जराशोकसमाविष्टं रोगायतनमातुरम् रजस्वलमनित्यं च भूतावासमिमं त्यजेत्
text: He shall discard this abode of material substances, where the bones are the pillars, which is held together by the tendons, plastered with flesh and blood; covered with the skin, foul-smelling, and full of urine and ordure;—(76) beset with wrinkles and sorrow, the seat of disease, harassed, sullied with passions and perishable.
iast: jarāśokasamāviṣṭaṃ rogāyatanamāturam rajasvalamanityaṃ ca bhūtāvāsamimaṃ tyajet

sanskrit_iast: nadīkūlaṃ yathā vṛkṣo vṛkṣaṃ vā śakuniryathā tathā tyajannimaṃ dehaṃ kṛcchrādgrāhādvimucyate
meaning: नदीकूलं यथा वृक्षो वृक्षं वा शकुनिर्यथा तथा त्यजन्निमं देहं कृच्छ्राद्ग्राहाद्विमुच्यते
text: He, who leaves this body, either as the tree leaves the bank, or as the bird leaves the tree, becomes freed from the shark of misery.—(78.)
iast: nadīkūlaṃ yathā vṛkṣo vṛkṣaṃ vā śakuniryathā tathā tyajannimaṃ dehaṃ kṛcchrādgrāhādvimucyate

sanskrit_iast: priyeṣu sveṣu sukṛtamapriyeṣu ca duṣkṛtam visṛjya dhyānayogena brahmābhyeti sanātanam
meaning: प्रियेषु स्वेषु सुकृतमप्रियेषु च दुष्कृतम् विसृज्य ध्यानयोगेन ब्रह्माभ्येति सनातनम्
text: Having, by the practice of meditation, attributed what is agreeable to him, to his good acts, and what is disagreeable, to his evil acts, he reaches the eternal Brahman.
iast: priyeṣu sveṣu sukṛtamapriyeṣu ca duṣkṛtam visṛjya dhyānayogena brahmābhyeti sanātanam

sanskrit_iast: yadā bhāvena bhavati sarvabhāveṣu niḥspṛhaḥ tadā sukhamavāpnoti pretya caiha ca śāśvatam
meaning: यदा भावेन भवति सर्वभावेषु निःस्पृहः तदा सुखमवाप्नोति प्रेत्य चैह च शाश्वतम्
text: When, by disposition, he becomes free from longing for all things, then he obtains lasting happiness in this world, as also after death.
iast: yadā bhāvena bhavati sarvabhāveṣu niḥspṛhaḥ tadā sukhamavāpnoti pretya caiha ca śāśvatam

sanskrit_iast: anena vidhinā sarvāṃstyaktvā saṅgāṃśanaiḥ śanaiḥ sarvadvandvavinirmukto brahmaṇyevāvatiṣṭhate
meaning: अनेन विधिना सर्वांस्त्यक्त्वा सङ्गांशनैः शनैः सर्वद्वन्द्वविनिर्मुक्तो ब्रह्मण्येवावतिष्ठते
text: Having, in this manner, gradually renounced all attachments, he becomes freed from all pairs of opposites, and reposes in Brahman alone.
iast: anena vidhinā sarvāṃstyaktvā saṅgāṃśanaiḥ śanaiḥ sarvadvandvavinirmukto brahmaṇyevāvatiṣṭhate

sanskrit_iast: dhyānikaṃ sarvamevaitadyadetadabhiśabditam na hyanadhyātmavit kaścit kriyāphalamupāśnute
meaning: ध्यानिकं सर्वमेवैतद्यदेतदभिशब्दितम् न ह्यनध्यात्मवित् कश्चित् क्रियाफलमुपाश्नुते
text: All this that has been declared here is appurtenant to Meditation; he who does not realise and cultivate the said mental attitude does not obtain the reward of the acts.
iast: dhyānikaṃ sarvamevaitadyadetadabhiśabditam na hyanadhyātmavit kaścit kriyāphalamupāśnute

sanskrit_iast: adhiyajñaṃ brahma japedādhidaivikameva ca ādhyātmikaṃ ca satataṃ vedāntābhihitaṃ ca yat
meaning: अधियज्ञं ब्रह्म जपेदाधिदैविकमेव च आध्यात्मिकं च सततं वेदान्ताभिहितं च यत्
text: He shall constantly recite Vedic texts bearing upon sacrifices, those dealing with deities and those dealing with the Soul, which have been called ‘Vedānta.’
iast: adhiyajñaṃ brahma japedādhidaivikameva ca ādhyātmikaṃ ca satataṃ vedāntābhihitaṃ ca yat

sanskrit_iast: idaṃ śaraṇamajñānāmidameva vijānatām idamanvicchatāṃ svargamidamānantyamicchatām
meaning: इदं शरणमज्ञानामिदमेव विजानताम् इदमन्विच्छतां स्वर्गमिदमानन्त्यमिच्छताम्
text: This is the refuge for the ignorant, this for the learned; this for those seeking heaven, and this also for those desiring immortality.
iast: idaṃ śaraṇamajñānāmidameva vijānatām idamanvicchatāṃ svargamidamānantyamicchatām

sanskrit_iast: anena kramayogena parivrajati yo dvijaḥ sa vidhūyaiha pāpmānaṃ paraṃ brahmādhigacchati
meaning: अनेन क्रमयोगेन परिव्रजति यो द्विजः स विधूयैह पाप्मानं परं ब्रह्माधिगच्छति
text: The twice-born person, who, by this successive process, goes forth (as a mendicant), shakes off evil and attains the Supreme Brahman.
iast: anena kramayogena parivrajati yo dvijaḥ sa vidhūyaiha pāpmānaṃ paraṃ brahmādhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: eṣa dharmo 'nuśiṣṭo vo yatīnāṃ niyatātmanām vedasaṃnyāsikānāṃ tu karmayogaṃ nibodhata
meaning: एष धर्मो 'नुशिष्टो वो यतीनां नियतात्मनाम् वेदसंन्यासिकानां तु कर्मयोगं निबोधत
text: Thus have the duties of the self-controlled Renunciates been expounded to you. Listen now to the duties of the ‘renouncers of the Veda.’
iast: eṣa dharmo 'nuśiṣṭo vo yatīnāṃ niyatātmanām vedasaṃnyāsikānāṃ tu karmayogaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: brahmacārī gṛhasthaśca vānaprastho yatistathā ete gṛhasthaprabhavāścatvāraḥ pṛthagāśramāḥ
meaning: ब्रह्मचारी गृहस्थश्च वानप्रस्थो यतिस्तथा एते गृहस्थप्रभवाश्चत्वारः पृथगाश्रमाः
text: The student, the Householder, the Hermit and the Renunciate,—all these, several stages emanate from the Householder.—(87). But all these, when observed in due order, a ccording to the scriptures lead the Brāhmaṇa who has (thus) acted according to the law, to the highest state.
iast: brahmacārī gṛhasthaśca vānaprastho yatistathā ete gṛhasthaprabhavāścatvāraḥ pṛthagāśramāḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarve 'pi kramaśastvete yathāśāstraṃ niṣevitāḥ yathauktakāriṇaṃ vipraṃ nayanti paramāṃ gatim
meaning: सर्वे 'पि क्रमशस्त्वेते यथाशास्त्रं निषेविताः यथौक्तकारिणं विप्रं नयन्ति परमां गतिम्
text: The student, the Householder, the Hermit and the Renunciate,—all these, several stages emanate from the Householder.—(87). But all these, when observed in due order, a ccording to the scriptures lead the Brāhmaṇa who has (thus) acted according to the law, to the highest state.
iast: sarve 'pi kramaśastvete yathāśāstraṃ niṣevitāḥ yathauktakāriṇaṃ vipraṃ nayanti paramāṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmapi caiteṣāṃ vedasmṛtividhānataḥ vedaśrutividhānataḥ gṛhastha ucyate śreṣṭhaḥ sa trīnetān bibharti hi
meaning: सर्वेषामपि चैतेषां वेदस्मृतिविधानतः वेदश्रुतिविधानतः गृहस्थ उच्यते श्रेष्ठः स त्रीनेतान् बिभर्ति हि
text: Among all these however, in accordance with the injunction or the direct Vedic text, the Householder is declared to be the best; because he supports the other three.
iast: sarveṣāmapi caiteṣāṃ vedasmṛtividhānataḥ vedaśrutividhānataḥ gṛhastha ucyate śreṣṭhaḥ sa trīnetān bibharti hi

sanskrit_iast: yathā nadīnadāḥ sarve sāgare yānti saṃsthitim tathaivāśramiṇaḥ sarve gṛhasthe yānti saṃsthitim
meaning: यथा नदीनदाः सर्वे सागरे यान्ति संस्थितिम् तथैवाश्रमिणः सर्वे गृहस्थे यान्ति संस्थितिम्
text: Just as rivers and rivulets attain their resting-places in the Ocean, so do men of all other orders obtain support in the Householder.
iast: yathā nadīnadāḥ sarve sāgare yānti saṃsthitim tathaivāśramiṇaḥ sarve gṛhasthe yānti saṃsthitim

sanskrit_iast: caturbhirapi caivaitairnityamāśramibhirdvijaiḥ daśalakṣaṇako dharmaḥ sevitavyaḥ prayatnataḥ
meaning: चतुर्भिरपि चैवैतैर्नित्यमाश्रमिभिर्द्विजैः दशलक्षणको धर्मः सेवितव्यः प्रयत्नतः
text: By twice-born men belonging to all these four orders this ten-fold duty shall always be assiduously observed.
iast: caturbhirapi caivaitairnityamāśramibhirdvijaiḥ daśalakṣaṇako dharmaḥ sevitavyaḥ prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: dhṛtiḥ kṣamā damo 'steyaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ dhīrvidyā satyamakrodho daśakaṃ dharmalakṣaṇam
meaning: धृतिः क्षमा दमो 'स्तेयं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः धीर्विद्या सत्यमक्रोधो दशकं धर्मलक्षणम्
text: (1) Steadiness (2) Forgiveness, (3) Self-control, (4) Abstention from unrighteous appropriation, (5) Purity, (6) Control of the Sense-organs, (7) Discrimination, (8) Knowledge, (9) Truthfulness, and (10) Absence of anger,—these are the ten-fold forms of duty.
iast: dhṛtiḥ kṣamā damo 'steyaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ dhīrvidyā satyamakrodho daśakaṃ dharmalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: daśa lakṣaṇāni dharmasya ye viprāḥ samadhīyate adhītya cānuvartante te yānti paramāṃ gatim
meaning: दश लक्षणानि धर्मस्य ये विप्राः समधीयते अधीत्य चानुवर्तन्ते ते यान्ति परमां गतिम्
text: Those Brāhmaṇas, who properly study the ten forms of duty, and having studied them, follow them in practice, reach the highest state.
iast: daśa lakṣaṇāni dharmasya ye viprāḥ samadhīyate adhītya cānuvartante te yānti paramāṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: daśalakṣaṇakaṃ dharmamanutiṣṭhan samāhitaḥ vedāntaṃ vidhivatśrutvā saṃnyasedanṛṇo dvijaḥ
meaning: दशलक्षणकं धर्ममनुतिष्ठन् समाहितः वेदान्तं विधिवत्श्रुत्वा संन्यसेदनृणो द्विजः
text: The twice-born person, performing, with collected mind, the ten-fold Duty, and having duly learnt the Vedānta texts, and become free from debts, should take to Renunciation.
iast: daśalakṣaṇakaṃ dharmamanutiṣṭhan samāhitaḥ vedāntaṃ vidhivatśrutvā saṃnyasedanṛṇo dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃnyasya sarvakarmāṇi karmadoṣānapānudan niyato vedamabhyasya putraiśvarye sukhaṃ vaset
meaning: संन्यस्य सर्वकर्माणि कर्मदोषानपानुदन् नियतो वेदमभ्यस्य पुत्रैश्वर्ये सुखं वसेत्
text: Having renounced all acts, and thrown off the taint of his acts, and studied the Veda with mind self-oontrolled, he shall live at ease under the protection of his son.
iast: saṃnyasya sarvakarmāṇi karmadoṣānapānudan niyato vedamabhyasya putraiśvarye sukhaṃ vaset

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ saṃnyasya karmāṇi svakāryaparamo 'spṛhaḥ saṃnyāsenāpahatyainaḥ prāpnoti paramaṃ gatim
meaning: एवं संन्यस्य कर्माणि स्वकार्यपरमो 'स्पृहः संन्यासेनापहत्यैनः प्राप्नोति परमं गतिम्
text: Having thus renounced all rites, intent upon his own duty, free from longings, he destroys sin by his renunciation and attains the highest state.
iast: evaṃ saṃnyasya karmāṇi svakāryaparamo 'spṛhaḥ saṃnyāsenāpahatyainaḥ prāpnoti paramaṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: eṣa vo 'bhihito dharmo brāhmaṇasya caturvidhaḥ puṇyo 'kṣayaphalaḥ pretya rājñāṃ dharmaṃ nibodhata
meaning: एष वो 'भिहितो धर्मो ब्राह्मणस्य चतुर्विधः पुण्यो 'क्षयफलः प्रेत्य राज्ञां धर्मं निबोधत
text: They shall present to the King the choice portion,—such is the Vedic declaration. what has not been won individually shall be distributed by the king among all the soldiers.
iast: eṣa vo 'bhihito dharmo brāhmaṇasya caturvidhaḥ puṇyo 'kṣayaphalaḥ pretya rājñāṃ dharmaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: rājadharmān pravakṣyāmi yathāvṛtto bhavennṛpaḥ saṃbhavaśca yathā tasya siddhiśca paramā yathā
meaning: राजधर्मान् प्रवक्ष्यामि यथावृत्तो भवेन्नृपः संभवश्च यथा तस्य सिद्धिश्च परमा यथा
text: I am going to expound the duties of Kings; how the Lord of Men should conduct himself, how he came into existence and how excellent success accrues to him.
iast: rājadharmān pravakṣyāmi yathāvṛtto bhavennṛpaḥ saṃbhavaśca yathā tasya siddhiśca paramā yathā

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṃ prāptena saṃskāraṃ kṣatriyeṇa yathāvidhi sarvasyāsya yathānyāyaṃ kartavyaṃ parirakṣaṇam
meaning: ब्राह्मं प्राप्तेन संस्कारं क्षत्रियेण यथाविधि सर्वस्यास्य यथान्यायं कर्तव्यं परिरक्षणम्
text: The protection of all this shall be done according to law, by the Kṣatriya who has received the Vedic training in due form.
iast: brāhmaṃ prāptena saṃskāraṃ kṣatriyeṇa yathāvidhi sarvasyāsya yathānyāyaṃ kartavyaṃ parirakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: arājake hi loke 'smin sarvato vidruto bhayāt rakṣārthamasya sarvasya rājānamasṛjat prabhuḥ
meaning: अराजके हि लोके 'स्मिन् सर्वतो विद्रुतो भयात् रक्षार्थमस्य सर्वस्य राजानमसृजत् प्रभुः
text: At a time when the people were without a King, and were utterly perturbed through fear, the Lord created the King for the protection of all this;—(3) taking out the essential constituents of Indra, Vāyu, Yama, Sūrya, Varuṇa, Chandra and Kubera
iast: arājake hi loke 'smin sarvato vidruto bhayāt rakṣārthamasya sarvasya rājānamasṛjat prabhuḥ

sanskrit_iast: indrānilayamārkāṇāmagneśca varuṇasya ca candravitteśayoścaiva mātrā nirhṛtya śāśvatīḥ
meaning: इन्द्रानिलयमार्काणामग्नेश्च वरुणस्य च चन्द्रवित्तेशयोश्चैव मात्रा निर्हृत्य शाश्वतीः
text: At a time when the people were without a King, and were utterly perturbed through fear, the Lord created the King for the protection of all this;—(3) taking out the essential constituents of Indra, Vāyu, Yama, Sūrya, Varuṇa, Chandra and Kubera
iast: indrānilayamārkāṇāmagneśca varuṇasya ca candravitteśayoścaiva mātrā nirhṛtya śāśvatīḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasmādeṣāṃ surendrāṇāṃ mātrābhyo nirmito nṛpaḥ tasmādabhibhavatyeṣa sarvabhūtāni tejasā
