meaning: यस्मादेषां सुरेन्द्राणां मात्राभ्यो निर्मितो नृपः तस्मादभिभवत्येष सर्वभूतानि तेजसा
text: In as much as the King was created with the constituent elements of these principal Gods, he surpasses all living beings by his glory.
iast: yasmādeṣāṃ surendrāṇāṃ mātrābhyo nirmito nṛpaḥ tasmādabhibhavatyeṣa sarvabhūtāni tejasā

sanskrit_iast: tapatyādityavaccaiṣa cakṣūṃṣi ca manāṃsi ca na cainaṃ bhuvi śaknoti kaścidapyabhivīkṣitum
meaning: तपत्यादित्यवच्चैष चक्षूंषि च मनांसि च न चैनं भुवि शक्नोति कश्चिदप्यभिवीक्षितुम्
text: Like the sun, he burns the eyes and minds (of man); no one on the earth can even gaze at him.
iast: tapatyādityavaccaiṣa cakṣūṃṣi ca manāṃsi ca na cainaṃ bhuvi śaknoti kaścidapyabhivīkṣitum

sanskrit_iast: so 'gnirbhavati vāyuśca so 'rkaḥ somaḥ sa dharmarāṭ sa kuberaḥ sa varuṇaḥ sa mahendraḥ prabhāvataḥ sa caindraḥ svaprabhāvataḥ]
meaning: सो 'ग्निर्भवति वायुश्च सो 'र्कः सोमः स धर्मराट् स कुबेरः स वरुणः स महेन्द्रः प्रभावतः स चैन्द्रः स्वप्रभावतः]
text: On account of his puissance he is Agni, Vāyu and Sūrya; he is Soma and Yama; he is Kubera, he is Varuṇa and he is Indra.
iast: so 'gnirbhavati vāyuśca so 'rkaḥ somaḥ sa dharmarāṭ sa kuberaḥ sa varuṇaḥ sa mahendraḥ prabhāvataḥ sa caindraḥ svaprabhāvataḥ]

sanskrit_iast: bālo 'pi nāvamāntavyo manuṣya iti bhūmipaḥ mahatī devatā hyeṣā nararūpeṇa tiṣṭhati
meaning: बालो 'पि नावमान्तव्यो मनुष्य इति भूमिपः महती देवता ह्येषा नररूपेण तिष्ठति
text: Even though an infant, the King shall not be despised as if he were merely human; because he is a great divinity in human form.
iast: bālo 'pi nāvamāntavyo manuṣya iti bhūmipaḥ mahatī devatā hyeṣā nararūpeṇa tiṣṭhati

sanskrit_iast: ekameva dahatyagnirnaraṃ durupasarpiṇam kulaṃ dahati rājā 'gniḥ sapaśudravyasañcayam
meaning: एकमेव दहत्यग्निर्नरं दुरुपसर्पिणम् कुलं दहति राजा 'ग्निः सपशुद्रव्यसञ्चयम्
text: Fire burns only one man who may happen to approach it carelessly; the fire of the King, on the other hand, consumes the entire family, along with its cattle and hoard of wealth.
iast: ekameva dahatyagnirnaraṃ durupasarpiṇam kulaṃ dahati rājā 'gniḥ sapaśudravyasañcayam

sanskrit_iast: kāryaṃ so 'vekṣya śaktiṃ ca deśakālau ca tattvataḥ kurute dharmasiddhyarthaṃ viśvarūpaṃ punaḥ punaḥ
meaning: कार्यं सो 'वेक्ष्य शक्तिं च देशकालौ च तत्त्वतः कुरुते धर्मसिद्ध्यर्थं विश्वरूपं पुनः पुनः
text: For the proper fulfilment of his duty, he assumes many forms re peatedly, after having carefully considered the nature of his business, his power and the conditions of time and place.
iast: kāryaṃ so 'vekṣya śaktiṃ ca deśakālau ca tattvataḥ kurute dharmasiddhyarthaṃ viśvarūpaṃ punaḥ punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasya prasāde padmā śrīrvijayaśca parākrame mṛtyuśca vasati krodhe sarvatejomayo hi saḥ
meaning: यस्य प्रसादे पद्मा श्रीर्विजयश्च पराक्रमे मृत्युश्च वसति क्रोधे सर्वतेजोमयो हि सः
text: He indeed contains in himself the splendour of all, in whose favour dwells the Goddess of Fortune, in whose valour rests victory and in whose anger abides death.
iast: yasya prasāde padmā śrīrvijayaśca parākrame mṛtyuśca vasati krodhe sarvatejomayo hi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: taṃ yastu dveṣṭi saṃmohāt sa vinaśyatyasaṃśayam tasya hyāśu vināśāya rājā prakurute manaḥ
meaning: तं यस्तु द्वेष्टि संमोहात् स विनश्यत्यसंशयम् तस्य ह्याशु विनाशाय राजा प्रकुरुते मनः
text: He, who, through folly, is hostile towards him, doubtlessly perishes; because the King makes up his mind for his quick destruction.
iast: taṃ yastu dveṣṭi saṃmohāt sa vinaśyatyasaṃśayam tasya hyāśu vināśāya rājā prakurute manaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tasmāddharmaṃ yamiṣṭeṣu sa vyavasyennarādhipaḥ aniṣṭaṃ cāpyaniṣṭeṣu taṃ dharmaṃ na vicālayet
meaning: तस्माद्धर्मं यमिष्टेषु स व्यवस्येन्नराधिपः अनिष्टं चाप्यनिष्टेषु तं धर्मं न विचालयेत्
text: For this reason no one should transgress that favourable decree which the King should ordain in favour of his favourites, or that unfavourable decree that he should ordain against those in his disfavour.
iast: tasmāddharmaṃ yamiṣṭeṣu sa vyavasyennarādhipaḥ aniṣṭaṃ cāpyaniṣṭeṣu taṃ dharmaṃ na vicālayet

sanskrit_iast: tasyārthe sarvabhūtānāṃ goptāraṃ dharmamātmajam tadarthaṃ brahmatejomayaṃ daṇḍamasṛjat pūrvamīśvaraḥ
meaning: तस्यार्थे सर्वभूतानां गोप्तारं धर्ममात्मजम् तदर्थं ब्रह्मतेजोमयं दण्डमसृजत् पूर्वमीश्वरः
text: For his sake, the Lord, at first, created Punishment, which is Law born of the Lord Himself, an incarnation of divine glory and the protector of all creatures.
iast: tasyārthe sarvabhūtānāṃ goptāraṃ dharmamātmajam tadarthaṃ brahmatejomayaṃ daṇḍamasṛjat pūrvamīśvaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: tasya sarvāṇi bhūtāni sthāvarāṇi carāṇi ca bhayādbhogāya kalpante svadharmātna calanti ca
meaning: तस्य सर्वाणि भूतानि स्थावराणि चराणि च भयाद्भोगाय कल्पन्ते स्वधर्मात्न चलन्ति च
text: It is through fear of him that all living beings, movable as well as immovable, go to subserve the experiences (of men) and do not swerve from their duties.
iast: tasya sarvāṇi bhūtāni sthāvarāṇi carāṇi ca bhayādbhogāya kalpante svadharmātna calanti ca

sanskrit_iast: taṃ deśakālau śaktiṃ ca vidyāṃ cāvekṣya tattvataḥ yathārhataḥ saṃpraṇayennareṣvanyāyavartiṣu
meaning: तं देशकालौ शक्तिं च विद्यां चावेक्ष्य तत्त्वतः यथार्हतः संप्रणयेन्नरेष्वन्यायवर्तिषु
text: To men who act unlawfully, he shall mete it out appropriately, having carefully considered the time and place, as also the strength and learning.
iast: taṃ deśakālau śaktiṃ ca vidyāṃ cāvekṣya tattvataḥ yathārhataḥ saṃpraṇayennareṣvanyāyavartiṣu

sanskrit_iast: sa rājā puruṣo daṇḍaḥ sa netā śāsitā ca saḥ caturṇāmāśramāṇāṃ ca dharmasya pratibhūḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: स राजा पुरुषो दण्डः स नेता शासिता च सः चतुर्णामाश्रमाणां च धर्मस्य प्रतिभूः स्मृतः
text: That punishment is the ‘King’, the ‘Man’; that is the ‘Leader’ and the ‘Ruler’ and that has been declared to be the ‘surety’ for the Law of the Four Stages.
iast: sa rājā puruṣo daṇḍaḥ sa netā śāsitā ca saḥ caturṇāmāśramāṇāṃ ca dharmasya pratibhūḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: daṇḍaḥ śāsti prajāḥ sarvā daṇḍa evābhirakṣati daṇḍaḥ supteṣu jāgarti daṇḍaṃ dharmaṃ vidurbudhāḥ
meaning: दण्डः शास्ति प्रजाः सर्वा दण्ड एवाभिरक्षति दण्डः सुप्तेषु जागर्ति दण्डं धर्मं विदुर्बुधाः
text: Punishment governs all creatures; Punishment alone protects them; Punishment lies awake while all are asleep; the wise regard Punishment as Law itself.
iast: daṇḍaḥ śāsti prajāḥ sarvā daṇḍa evābhirakṣati daṇḍaḥ supteṣu jāgarti daṇḍaṃ dharmaṃ vidurbudhāḥ

sanskrit_iast: samīkṣya sa dhṛtaḥ samyak sarvā rañjayati prajāḥ asamīkṣya praṇītastu vināśayati sarvataḥ
meaning: समीक्ष्य स धृतः सम्यक् सर्वा रञ्जयति प्रजाः असमीक्ष्य प्रणीतस्तु विनाशयति सर्वतः
text: When meted out properly after due investigation, it makes all people happy; but when meted out without due investigation, it destroys all things.
iast: samīkṣya sa dhṛtaḥ samyak sarvā rañjayati prajāḥ asamīkṣya praṇītastu vināśayati sarvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadi na praṇayedrājā daṇḍaṃ daṇḍyeṣvatandritaḥ śūle matsyānivāpakṣyan durbalān balavattarāḥ
meaning: यदि न प्रणयेद्राजा दण्डं दण्ड्येष्वतन्द्रितः शूले मत्स्यानिवापक्ष्यन् दुर्बलान् बलवत्तराः
text: If the King did not untiringly mete out punishment to those that deserve punishment, the stronger would have boasted the weaker, like fish, on the spit;
iast: yadi na praṇayedrājā daṇḍaṃ daṇḍyeṣvatandritaḥ śūle matsyānivāpakṣyan durbalān balavattarāḥ

sanskrit_iast: adyāt kākaḥ puroḍāśaṃ śvā ca lihyāddhavistathā śvā 'valihyādd svāmyaṃ ca na syāt kasmiṃścit pravartetādharauttaram
meaning: अद्यात् काकः पुरोडाशं श्वा च लिह्याद्धविस्तथा श्वा 'वलिह्याद्द् स्वाम्यं च न स्यात् कस्मिंश्चित् प्रवर्तेताधरौत्तरम्
text: The crow would eat the Sacrificial Cake, and the dog would lick the offering-materials; rights of ownership would not remain with any one and there would be a confusion among the high and low.
iast: adyāt kākaḥ puroḍāśaṃ śvā ca lihyāddhavistathā śvā 'valihyādd svāmyaṃ ca na syāt kasmiṃścit pravartetādharauttaram

sanskrit_iast: sarvo daṇḍajito loko durlabho hi śucirnaraḥ daṇḍasya hi bhayāt sarvaṃ jagadbhogāya kalpate
meaning: सर्वो दण्डजितो लोको दुर्लभो हि शुचिर्नरः दण्डस्य हि भयात् सर्वं जगद्भोगाय कल्पते
text: It is by punishment that all people are kept under control; for an absolutely guileless man is hard to find; it is through fear of punishment that the world subserves the experiences (of men).
iast: sarvo daṇḍajito loko durlabho hi śucirnaraḥ daṇḍasya hi bhayāt sarvaṃ jagadbhogāya kalpate

sanskrit_iast: devadānavagandharvā rakṣāṃsi patagauragāḥ te 'pi bhogāya kalpante daṇḍenaiva nipīḍitāḥ
meaning: देवदानवगन्धर्वा रक्षांसि पतगौरगाः ते 'पि भोगाय कल्पन्ते दण्डेनैव निपीडिताः
text: It is only when pressed by Punishment that Devas, Dānavas, Gandharvas, Rākṣasas, Birds and Reptiles subserve the experiences (of others).
iast: devadānavagandharvā rakṣāṃsi patagauragāḥ te 'pi bhogāya kalpante daṇḍenaiva nipīḍitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: duṣyeyuḥ sarvavarṇāśca bhidyeran sarvasetavaḥ sarvalokaprakopaśca bhaveddaṇḍasya vibhramāt
meaning: दुष्येयुः सर्ववर्णाश्च भिद्येरन् सर्वसेतवः सर्वलोकप्रकोपश्च भवेद्दण्डस्य विभ्रमात्
text: All the castes would become corrupt, all barriers would be broken through, and there would be disruption among all the regions,—if there were any mistakes in regard to punishment.
iast: duṣyeyuḥ sarvavarṇāśca bhidyeran sarvasetavaḥ sarvalokaprakopaśca bhaveddaṇḍasya vibhramāt

sanskrit_iast: yatra śyāmo lohitākṣo daṇḍaścarati pāpahā prajāstatra na muhyanti netā cet sādhu paśyati
meaning: यत्र श्यामो लोहिताक्षो दण्डश्चरति पापहा प्रजास्तत्र न मुह्यन्ति नेता चेत् साधु पश्यति
text: Where dark-complexioned and red-eyed Punishment stalks about, destroying sins, there the people are not misled, provided that the Governor discerns rightly.
iast: yatra śyāmo lohitākṣo daṇḍaścarati pāpahā prajāstatra na muhyanti netā cet sādhu paśyati

sanskrit_iast: tasyāhuḥ saṃpraṇetāraṃ rājānaṃ satyavādinam samīkṣyakāriṇaṃ prājñaṃ dharmakāmārthakovidam
meaning: तस्याहुः संप्रणेतारं राजानं सत्यवादिनम् समीक्ष्यकारिणं प्राज्ञं धर्मकामार्थकोविदम्
text: They declare that King to be the just governor who is truth ful of speech, who acts after due consideration, who is wise and who knows the essence of virtue, pleasure and wealth.
iast: tasyāhuḥ saṃpraṇetāraṃ rājānaṃ satyavādinam samīkṣyakāriṇaṃ prājñaṃ dharmakāmārthakovidam

sanskrit_iast: taṃ rājā praṇayan samyak trivargeṇābhivardhate kāmātmā viṣamaḥ kṣudro daṇḍenaiva nihanyate kāmāndho
meaning: तं राजा प्रणयन् सम्यक् त्रिवर्गेणाभिवर्धते कामात्मा विषमः क्षुद्रो दण्डेनैव निहन्यते कामान्धो
text: The King who metes out punishment in the proper manner prospers in respect of his three aims; he who is blinded by affection, unfair, or mean is destroyed by that same punishment.
iast: taṃ rājā praṇayan samyak trivargeṇābhivardhate kāmātmā viṣamaḥ kṣudro daṇḍenaiva nihanyate kāmāndho

sanskrit_iast: daṇḍo hi sumahattejo durdharaścākṛtātmabhiḥ dharmādvicalitaṃ hanti nṛpameva sabāndhavam
meaning: दण्डो हि सुमहत्तेजो दुर्धरश्चाकृतात्मभिः धर्माद्विचलितं हन्ति नृपमेव सबान्धवम्
text: Punishment, which is a tremendous force, hard to be controlled by persons with undisciplined minds, destroys the King who has swerved from duty, along with his relatives.
iast: daṇḍo hi sumahattejo durdharaścākṛtātmabhiḥ dharmādvicalitaṃ hanti nṛpameva sabāndhavam

sanskrit_iast: tato durgaṃ ca rāṣṭraṃ ca lokaṃ ca sacarācaram antarikṣagatāṃścaiva munīn devāṃśca pīḍayet
meaning: ततो दुर्गं च राष्ट्रं च लोकं च सचराचरम् अन्तरिक्षगतांश्चैव मुनीन् देवांश्च पीडयेत्
text: Then it will afflict his fortress and kingdom, the world along with movable and immovable things, as also the sages and the gods inhabiting the heavenly regions.
iast: tato durgaṃ ca rāṣṭraṃ ca lokaṃ ca sacarācaram antarikṣagatāṃścaiva munīn devāṃśca pīḍayet

sanskrit_iast: so 'sahāyena mūḍhena lubdhenākṛtabuddhinā na śakyo nyāyato netuṃ saktena viṣayeṣu ca
meaning: सो 'सहायेन मूढेन लुब्धेनाकृतबुद्धिना न शक्यो न्यायतो नेतुं सक्तेन विषयेषु च
text: Punishment cannot be justly administered by one who has no assistant, or who is demented, or who is avaricious, or whose mind is not disciplined, or who is addicted to sensual objects.
iast: so 'sahāyena mūḍhena lubdhenākṛtabuddhinā na śakyo nyāyato netuṃ saktena viṣayeṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: śucinā satyasaṃdhena yathāśāstrānusāriṇā praṇetuṃ śakyate daṇḍaḥ susahāyena dhīmatā
meaning: शुचिना सत्यसंधेन यथाशास्त्रानुसारिणा प्रणेतुं शक्यते दण्डः सुसहायेन धीमता
text: Punishment can be administered by one who is pube, who is true to his word, who acts according to the Law, who has good assistants and is wise.
iast: śucinā satyasaṃdhena yathāśāstrānusāriṇā praṇetuṃ śakyate daṇḍaḥ susahāyena dhīmatā

sanskrit_iast: svarāṣṭre nyāyavṛttaḥ syādbhṛśadaṇḍaśca śatruṣu suhṛtsvajihmaḥ snigdheṣu brāhmaṇeṣu kṣamānvitaḥ
meaning: स्वराष्ट्रे न्यायवृत्तः स्याद्भृशदण्डश्च शत्रुषु सुहृत्स्वजिह्मः स्निग्धेषु ब्राह्मणेषु क्षमान्वितः
text: In his own kingdom he shall be of just behaviour, and on his enemies he shall inflict rigorous chastisement; with loved friends he shall be straightforward and towards Brāhmaṇas tolerant.
iast: svarāṣṭre nyāyavṛttaḥ syādbhṛśadaṇḍaśca śatruṣu suhṛtsvajihmaḥ snigdheṣu brāhmaṇeṣu kṣamānvitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃvṛttasya nṛpateḥ śilauñchenāpi jīvataḥ vistīryate yaśo loke tailabindurivāmbhasi
meaning: एवंवृत्तस्य नृपतेः शिलौञ्छेनापि जीवतः विस्तीर्यते यशो लोके तैलबिन्दुरिवाम्भसि
text: For the King who behaves thus, even though he may subsist up on gleanings, his fame spreads in the world, like the drops of oil on water.
iast: evaṃvṛttasya nṛpateḥ śilauñchenāpi jīvataḥ vistīryate yaśo loke tailabindurivāmbhasi

sanskrit_iast: atastu viparītasya nṛpaterajitātmanaḥ saṅkṣipyate yaśo loke ghṛtabindurivāmbhasi
meaning: अतस्तु विपरीतस्य नृपतेरजितात्मनः सङ्क्षिप्यते यशो लोके घृतबिन्दुरिवाम्भसि
text: But for the King who deviates from it having no control over himself, his fame diminishes in the world, like the drop of clarified butter on water.
iast: atastu viparītasya nṛpaterajitātmanaḥ saṅkṣipyate yaśo loke ghṛtabindurivāmbhasi

sanskrit_iast: sve sve dharme niviṣṭānāṃ sarveṣāmanupūrvaśaḥ varṇānāmāśramāṇāṃ ca rājā sṛṣṭo 'bhirakṣitā
meaning: स्वे स्वे धर्मे निविष्टानां सर्वेषामनुपूर्वशः वर्णानामाश्रमाणां च राजा सृष्टो 'भिरक्षिता
text: The King has been created the protector of all castes and orders, who, in due order, are intent upon their respective duties.
iast: sve sve dharme niviṣṭānāṃ sarveṣāmanupūrvaśaḥ varṇānāmāśramāṇāṃ ca rājā sṛṣṭo 'bhirakṣitā

sanskrit_iast: tena yadyat sabhṛtyena kartavyaṃ rakṣatā prajāḥ tat tadvo 'haṃ pravakṣyāmi yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ
meaning: तेन यद्यत् सभृत्येन कर्तव्यं रक्षता प्रजाः तत् तद्वो 'हं प्रवक्ष्यामि यथावदनुपूर्वशः
text: Whatever should be done by him and his servants, for the protecting of his people, all that I am going to explain to you precisely and in due order
iast: tena yadyat sabhṛtyena kartavyaṃ rakṣatā prajāḥ tat tadvo 'haṃ pravakṣyāmi yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇān paryupāsīta prātarutthāya pārthivaḥ traividyavṛddhān viduṣastiṣṭhet teṣāṃ ca śāsane
meaning: ब्राह्मणान् पर्युपासीत प्रातरुत्थाय पार्थिवः त्रैविद्यवृद्धान् विदुषस्तिष्ठेत् तेषां च शासने
text: After rising in the morning, the King shall wait upon the Brāhmaṇas, who are accomplished students of the Threefold Science and learned; and shall follow their advice.
iast: brāhmaṇān paryupāsīta prātarutthāya pārthivaḥ traividyavṛddhān viduṣastiṣṭhet teṣāṃ ca śāsane

sanskrit_iast: vṛddhāṃśca nityaṃ seveta viprān vedavidaḥ śucīn vṛddhasevī hi satataṃ rakṣobhirapi pūjyate
meaning: वृद्धांश्च नित्यं सेवेत विप्रान् वेदविदः शुचीन् वृद्धसेवी हि सततं रक्षोभिरपि पूज्यते
text: Every day he shall wait upon elderly persons, Brāhmaṇas, pure and learned in the Vedas; he who constantly waits upon elderly persons is honoured even by Rākṣasas.
iast: vṛddhāṃśca nityaṃ seveta viprān vedavidaḥ śucīn vṛddhasevī hi satataṃ rakṣobhirapi pūjyate

sanskrit_iast: tebhyo 'dhigacchedvinayaṃ vinītātmā 'pi nityaśaḥ vinītātmā hi nṛpatirna vinaśyati karhi cit
meaning: तेभ्यो 'धिगच्छेद्विनयं विनीतात्मा 'पि नित्यशः विनीतात्मा हि नृपतिर्न विनश्यति कर्हि चित्
text: Though his mind be already disciplined, he shall always learn discipline from them; the King with a disciplined mind never perishes.
iast: tebhyo 'dhigacchedvinayaṃ vinītātmā 'pi nityaśaḥ vinītātmā hi nṛpatirna vinaśyati karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: bahavo 'vinayātnaṣṭā rājānaḥ saparicchadāḥ saparigrahāḥ vanasthā api rājyāni vinayāt pratipedire
meaning: बहवो 'विनयात्नष्टा राजानः सपरिच्छदाः सपरिग्रहाः वनस्था अपि राज्यानि विनयात् प्रतिपेदिरे
text: Many Kings, along with their belongings, have perished through want of discipline; while, on account of discipline, many, even though living in forests, have obtained Kingdoms.
iast: bahavo 'vinayātnaṣṭā rājānaḥ saparicchadāḥ saparigrahāḥ vanasthā api rājyāni vinayāt pratipedire

sanskrit_iast: veno vinaṣṭo 'vinayātnahuṣaścaiva pārthivaḥ sudāḥ paijavanaścaiva sumukho nimireva ca
meaning: वेनो विनष्टो 'विनयात्नहुषश्चैव पार्थिवः सुदाः पैजवनश्चैव सुमुखो निमिरेव च
text: It was through want of discipline that Vena perished, as also King Nahuṣa, Sudās, Paijavana, Sumukha and Nimi.
iast: veno vinaṣṭo 'vinayātnahuṣaścaiva pārthivaḥ sudāḥ paijavanaścaiva sumukho nimireva ca

sanskrit_iast: pṛthustu vinayādrājyaṃ prāptavān manureva ca kuberaśca dhanaiśvaryaṃ brāhmaṇyaṃ caiva gādhijaḥ
meaning: पृथुस्तु विनयाद्राज्यं प्राप्तवान् मनुरेव च कुबेरश्च धनैश्वर्यं ब्राह्मण्यं चैव गाधिजः
text: But through discipline Pṛthu and Manu obtained kingdoms, Kubera obtained the lordship of wealth and the son of Gādhi attained Brāhmaṇahood.
iast: pṛthustu vinayādrājyaṃ prāptavān manureva ca kuberaśca dhanaiśvaryaṃ brāhmaṇyaṃ caiva gādhijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: traividyebhyastrayīṃ vidyāṃ daṇḍanītiṃ ca śāśvatīm trayīṃ vidyāt ānvīkṣikīṃ cātmavidyāṃ vārtārambhāṃśca lokataḥ
meaning: त्रैविद्येभ्यस्त्रयीं विद्यां दण्डनीतिं च शाश्वतीम् त्रयीं विद्यात् आन्वीक्षिकीं चात्मविद्यां वार्तारम्भांश्च लोकतः
text: From persons learned in the Threefold Science he shall learn the Triad; as also the ancient Science of Government, the Science of Reasoning and the Science of the Soul; and also the art of commerce from experts
iast: traividyebhyastrayīṃ vidyāṃ daṇḍanītiṃ ca śāśvatīm trayīṃ vidyāt ānvīkṣikīṃ cātmavidyāṃ vārtārambhāṃśca lokataḥ

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇāṃ jaye yogaṃ samātiṣṭheddivāniśam jitaindriyo hi śaknoti vaśe sthāpayituṃ prajāḥ
meaning: इन्द्रियाणां जये योगं समातिष्ठेद्दिवानिशम् जितैन्द्रियो हि शक्नोति वशे स्थापयितुं प्रजाः
text: Day and night he shall put forth an effort to subdue his senses; because he whose senses are subjugated is capable of keeping his subjects under control.
iast: indriyāṇāṃ jaye yogaṃ samātiṣṭheddivāniśam jitaindriyo hi śaknoti vaśe sthāpayituṃ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: daśa kāmasamutthāni tathā 'ṣṭau krodhajāni ca vyasanāni dur 'ntāni prayatnena vivarjayet
meaning: दश कामसमुत्थानि तथा 'ष्टौ क्रोधजानि च व्यसनानि दुर् 'न्तानि प्रयत्नेन विवर्जयेत्
text: He shall shun the ten ruinous vices springing from love of plea sure, as also the eight arising from anger.
iast: daśa kāmasamutthāni tathā 'ṣṭau krodhajāni ca vyasanāni dur 'ntāni prayatnena vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: kāmajeṣu prasakto hi vyasaneṣu mahīpatiḥ viyujyate 'rthadharmābhyāṃ krodhajeṣvātmanaiva tu
meaning: कामजेषु प्रसक्तो हि व्यसनेषु महीपतिः वियुज्यते 'र्थधर्माभ्यां क्रोधजेष्वात्मनैव तु
text: The king who is addicted to vices springing from the love of pleasure becomes deprived of wealth and virtue; while he who is addicted to those proceeding from anger becomes bereft of his very soul.
iast: kāmajeṣu prasakto hi vyasaneṣu mahīpatiḥ viyujyate 'rthadharmābhyāṃ krodhajeṣvātmanaiva tu

sanskrit_iast: mṛgayā 'kṣo divāsvapnaḥ parivādaḥ striyo madaḥ tauryatrikaṃ vṛthāṭyā ca kāmajo daśako gaṇaḥ
meaning: मृगया 'क्षो दिवास्वप्नः परिवादः स्त्रियो मदः तौर्यत्रिकं वृथाट्या च कामजो दशको गणः
text: Hunting, dice, sleeping during the day, censoriousness, women, intoxication, musical triad and listless wandering constitute the ten-fold set arising from the love of pleasure.
iast: mṛgayā 'kṣo divāsvapnaḥ parivādaḥ striyo madaḥ tauryatrikaṃ vṛthāṭyā ca kāmajo daśako gaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: paiśunyaṃ sāhasaṃ droha īrṣyā 'sūyā 'rthadūṣaṇam vāgdaṇḍajaṃ ca pāruṣyaṃ krodhajo 'pi gaṇo 'ṣṭakaḥ
meaning: पैशुन्यं साहसं द्रोह ईर्ष्या 'सूया 'र्थदूषणम् वाग्दण्डजं च पारुष्यं क्रोधजो 'पि गणो 'ष्टकः
text: Tale-bearing, Treachery, Envy, Slandering, Misappropriation of property, Cruelty of speech and of Assault;—these constitute the eightfold set born of Anger.
iast: paiśunyaṃ sāhasaṃ droha īrṣyā 'sūyā 'rthadūṣaṇam vāgdaṇḍajaṃ ca pāruṣyaṃ krodhajo 'pi gaṇo 'ṣṭakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dvayorapyetayormūlaṃ yaṃ sarve kavayo viduḥ taṃ yatnena jayetlobhaṃ tajjāvetāvubhau gaṇau
meaning: द्वयोरप्येतयोर्मूलं यं सर्वे कवयो विदुः तं यत्नेन जयेत्लोभं तज्जावेतावुभौ गणौ
text: With great effort he shall subdue that Greediness which all wise men regard as the root of both these; both these sets arise out of that.
iast: dvayorapyetayormūlaṃ yaṃ sarve kavayo viduḥ taṃ yatnena jayetlobhaṃ tajjāvetāvubhau gaṇau

sanskrit_iast: pānamakṣāḥ striyaścaiva mṛgayā ca yathākramam etat kaṣṭatamaṃ vidyātcatuṣkaṃ kāmaje gaṇe
meaning: पानमक्षाः स्त्रियश्चैव मृगया च यथाक्रमम् एतत् कष्टतमं विद्यात्चतुष्कं कामजे गणे
text: In the set arising from love of pleasure,—drinking, dice, women and hunting are to be regarded as the four most pernicious, in the order in which they are named.
iast: pānamakṣāḥ striyaścaiva mṛgayā ca yathākramam etat kaṣṭatamaṃ vidyātcatuṣkaṃ kāmaje gaṇe

sanskrit_iast: daṇḍasya pātanaṃ caiva vākpāruṣyārthadūṣaṇe krodhaje 'pi gaṇe vidyāt kaṣṭametat trikaṃ sadā
meaning: दण्डस्य पातनं चैव वाक्पारुष्यार्थदूषणे क्रोधजे 'पि गणे विद्यात् कष्टमेतत् त्रिकं सदा
text: In the set born of Anger,—Assault, Cruelty of speech and Misappropriation of property,—are to be regarded as the three most pernicious.
iast: daṇḍasya pātanaṃ caiva vākpāruṣyārthadūṣaṇe krodhaje 'pi gaṇe vidyāt kaṣṭametat trikaṃ sadā

sanskrit_iast: saptakasyāsya vargasya sarvatraivānuṣaṅgiṇaḥ pūrvaṃ pūrvaṃ gurutaraṃ vidyādvyasanamātmavān
meaning: सप्तकस्यास्य वर्गस्य सर्वत्रैवानुषङ्गिणः पूर्वं पूर्वं गुरुतरं विद्याद्व्यसनमात्मवान्
text: The self-disciplined man should know that in this set of seven which spreads everywhere, each preceding vice is more serious than the succeeding one.
iast: saptakasyāsya vargasya sarvatraivānuṣaṅgiṇaḥ pūrvaṃ pūrvaṃ gurutaraṃ vidyādvyasanamātmavān

sanskrit_iast: vyasanasya ca mṛtyośca vyasanaṃ kaṣṭamucyate vyasanyadho 'dho vrajati svaryātyavyasanī mṛtaḥ
meaning: व्यसनस्य च मृत्योश्च व्यसनं कष्टमुच्यते व्यसन्यधो 'धो व्रजति स्वर्यात्यव्यसनी मृतः
text: Between Vice & Death, Vice is said to be more harmful; the vicious man sinks down and down; but the dead man, without vices, ascends to heaven.
iast: vyasanasya ca mṛtyośca vyasanaṃ kaṣṭamucyate vyasanyadho 'dho vrajati svaryātyavyasanī mṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: maulāṃśāstravidaḥ śūrānlabdhalakṣān kulaudbhavān kulaudgatān sacivān sapta cāṣṭau vā prakurvīta parīkṣitān kurvīta suparīkṣitān
meaning: मौलांशास्त्रविदः शूरान्लब्धलक्षान् कुलौद्भवान् कुलौद्गतान् सचिवान् सप्त चाष्टौ वा प्रकुर्वीत परीक्षितान् कुर्वीत सुपरीक्षितान्
text: He shall appoint seven or eight ministers, with respectable status, versed in law, of heroic temperament, experienced in business, born of noble families, and thoroughly tested.
iast: maulāṃśāstravidaḥ śūrānlabdhalakṣān kulaudbhavān kulaudgatān sacivān sapta cāṣṭau vā prakurvīta parīkṣitān kurvīta suparīkṣitān

sanskrit_iast: api yat sukaraṃ karma tadapyekena duṣkaram viśeṣato 'sahāyena kiṃ tu rājyaṃ mahaudayam kiṃ nu
meaning: अपि यत् सुकरं कर्म तदप्येकेन दुष्करम् विशेषतो 'सहायेन किं तु राज्यं महौदयम् किं नु
text: Even an undertaking that is easy is difficult to be accomplished by a single man, specially by one who has no assistant; how much more so is the work of the king, which involves great issues!
iast: api yat sukaraṃ karma tadapyekena duṣkaram viśeṣato 'sahāyena kiṃ tu rājyaṃ mahaudayam kiṃ nu

sanskrit_iast: taiḥ sārdhaṃ cintayennityaṃ sāmānyaṃ saṃdhivigraham sthānaṃ samudayaṃ guptiṃ labdhapraśamanāni ca
meaning: तैः सार्धं चिन्तयेन्नित्यं सामान्यं संधिविग्रहम् स्थानं समुदयं गुप्तिं लब्धप्रशमनानि च
text: With these he shall always discuss all ordinary business relating to peace and war, as also the ‘state’, the sources of revenue, the means of protection, and the consolidation of what has been acquired.
iast: taiḥ sārdhaṃ cintayennityaṃ sāmānyaṃ saṃdhivigraham sthānaṃ samudayaṃ guptiṃ labdhapraśamanāni ca

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ svaṃ svamabhiprāyamupalabhya pṛthak pṛthak samastānāṃ ca kāryeṣu vidadhyāddhitamātmanaḥ
meaning: तेषां स्वं स्वमभिप्रायमुपलभ्य पृथक् पृथक् समस्तानां च कार्येषु विदध्याद्धितमात्मनः
text: Having ascertained the opinion of each of the ministers individually and also collectively, he shall, in his affairs, do what is beneficial to himself.
iast: teṣāṃ svaṃ svamabhiprāyamupalabhya pṛthak pṛthak samastānāṃ ca kāryeṣu vidadhyāddhitamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāṃ tu viśiṣṭena brāhmaṇena vipaścitā mantrayet paramaṃ mantraṃ rājā ṣāḍguṇyasaṃyutam
meaning: सर्वेषां तु विशिष्टेन ब्राह्मणेन विपश्चिता मन्त्रयेत् परमं मन्त्रं राजा षाड्गुण्यसंयुतम्
text: With the learned Brāhmaṇa, however, who is the most distinguished of them all, the king shall discuss the highest secrets pertaining to the six-fold state-craft.
iast: sarveṣāṃ tu viśiṣṭena brāhmaṇena vipaścitā mantrayet paramaṃ mantraṃ rājā ṣāḍguṇyasaṃyutam

sanskrit_iast: nityaṃ tasmin samāśvastaḥ sarvakāryāṇi niḥkṣipet nikṣipet tena sārdhaṃ viniścitya tataḥ karma samārabhet
meaning: नित्यं तस्मिन् समाश्वस्तः सर्वकार्याणि निःक्षिपेत् निक्षिपेत् तेन सार्धं विनिश्चित्य ततः कर्म समारभेत्
text: He shall always, in full confidence, entrust all business to him; and having, in consultation with him, formed his resolution, he shall do what has to be done.
iast: nityaṃ tasmin samāśvastaḥ sarvakāryāṇi niḥkṣipet nikṣipet tena sārdhaṃ viniścitya tataḥ karma samārabhet

sanskrit_iast: anyānapi prakurvīta śucīn prājñānavasthitān samyagarthasamāhartr̥̄namātyān suparīkṣitān
meaning: अन्यानपि प्रकुर्वीत शुचीन् प्राज्ञानवस्थितान् सम्यगर्थसमाहर्त्र्̥̄नमात्यान् सुपरीक्षितान्
text: He shall also appoint other ministers, who are pure, wise, firm, experts in collecting revenue and thoroughly tested.
iast: anyānapi prakurvīta śucīn prājñānavasthitān samyagarthasamāhartr̥̄namātyān suparīkṣitān

sanskrit_iast: nirvartetāsya yāvadbhiritikartavyatā nṛbhiḥ tāvato 'tandritān dakṣān prakurvīta vicakṣaṇān
meaning: निर्वर्तेतास्य यावद्भिरितिकर्तव्यता नृभिः तावतो 'तन्द्रितान् दक्षान् प्रकुर्वीत विचक्षणान्
text: He shall appoint as many industrious, clever and skilful men as may be required for the accomplishment of his business.
iast: nirvartetāsya yāvadbhiritikartavyatā nṛbhiḥ tāvato 'tandritān dakṣān prakurvīta vicakṣaṇān

sanskrit_iast: teṣāmarthe niyuñjīta śūrān dakṣān kulaudgatān śucīnākarakarmānte bhīrūnantarniveśane
meaning: तेषामर्थे नियुञ्जीत शूरान् दक्षान् कुलौद्गतान् शुचीनाकरकर्मान्ते भीरूनन्तर्निवेशने
text: From among them he shall employ the brave, the expert, the high-born and the honest ones in work relating to finance,—such as mines and stores—and timid ones in the interior of the palace.
iast: teṣāmarthe niyuñjīta śūrān dakṣān kulaudgatān śucīnākarakarmānte bhīrūnantarniveśane

sanskrit_iast: dūtaṃ caiva prakurvīta sarvaśāstraviśāradam iṅgitākāraceṣṭajñaṃ śuciṃ dakṣaṃ kulaudgatam
meaning: दूतं चैव प्रकुर्वीत सर्वशास्त्रविशारदम् इङ्गिताकारचेष्टज्ञं शुचिं दक्षं कुलौद्गतम्
text: As ambassador he shall appoint one who is well versed in all the sciences, who understands hints, expressions and gestures, who is honest, expert and born of a noble family.
iast: dūtaṃ caiva prakurvīta sarvaśāstraviśāradam iṅgitākāraceṣṭajñaṃ śuciṃ dakṣaṃ kulaudgatam

sanskrit_iast: anuraktaḥ śucirdakṣaḥ smṛtimān deśakālavit vapuṣmān vītabhīrvāgmī dūto rājñaḥ praśasyate
meaning: अनुरक्तः शुचिर्दक्षः स्मृतिमान् देशकालवित् वपुष्मान् वीतभीर्वाग्मी दूतो राज्ञः प्रशस्यते
text: That royal ambassador is commended who is loyal, honest, clever, possessed of good memory, conversant with place and time, handsome of body, fearless and eloquent.
iast: anuraktaḥ śucirdakṣaḥ smṛtimān deśakālavit vapuṣmān vītabhīrvāgmī dūto rājñaḥ praśasyate

sanskrit_iast: amātye daṇḍa āyatto daṇḍe vainayikī kriyā nṛpatau kośarāṣṭre ca dūte saṃdhiviparyayau
meaning: अमात्ये दण्ड आयत्तो दण्डे वैनयिकी क्रिया नृपतौ कोशराष्ट्रे च दूते संधिविपर्ययौ
text: The army is dependent upon the minister (of war); on the army rests the act of ruling; the treasury and the realm are dependent upon the king, and upon the ambassador depend peace and its opposite.
iast: amātye daṇḍa āyatto daṇḍe vainayikī kriyā nṛpatau kośarāṣṭre ca dūte saṃdhiviparyayau

sanskrit_iast: dūta eva hi saṃdhatte bhinattyeva ca saṃhatān dūtastat kurute karma bhidyante yena mānavaḥ
meaning: दूत एव हि संधत्ते भिनत्त्येव च संहतान् दूतस्तत् कुरुते कर्म भिद्यन्ते येन मानवः
text: For it is the Ambassador alone who brings together allies and also alienates them; the Ambassador transacts that business by which people become disunited
iast: dūta eva hi saṃdhatte bhinattyeva ca saṃhatān dūtastat kurute karma bhidyante yena mānavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sa vidyādasya kṛtyeṣu nirgūḍhaiṅgitaceṣṭitaiḥ ākāramiṅgitaṃ ceṣṭāṃ bhṛtyeṣu ca cikīrṣitam
meaning: स विद्यादस्य कृत्येषु निर्गूढैङ्गितचेष्टितैः आकारमिङ्गितं चेष्टां भृत्येषु च चिकीर्षितम्
text: He shall take up residence in a country which is open, fully supplied with grains, inhabited almost entirely by men of gentle birth, free from diseases, pleasant, where the vassals are obedient and where living is easily found.
iast: sa vidyādasya kṛtyeṣu nirgūḍhaiṅgitaceṣṭitaiḥ ākāramiṅgitaṃ ceṣṭāṃ bhṛtyeṣu ca cikīrṣitam

sanskrit_iast: buddhvā ca sarvaṃ tattvena pararājacikīrṣitam tathā prayatnamātiṣṭhedyathā 'tmānaṃ na pīḍayet
meaning: बुद्ध्वा च सर्वं तत्त्वेन परराजचिकीर्षितम् तथा प्रयत्नमातिष्ठेद्यथा 'त्मानं न पीडयेत्
text: He shall take up residence in a country which is open, fully supplied with grains, inhabited almost entirely by men of gentle birth, free from diseases, pleasant, where the vassals are obedient and where living is easily found.
iast: buddhvā ca sarvaṃ tattvena pararājacikīrṣitam tathā prayatnamātiṣṭhedyathā 'tmānaṃ na pīḍayet

sanskrit_iast: jāṅgalaṃ sasyasaṃpannamāryaprāyamanāvilam ramyamānatasāmantaṃ svājīvyaṃ deśamāvaset
meaning: जाङ्गलं सस्यसंपन्नमार्यप्रायमनाविलम् रम्यमानतसामन्तं स्वाजीव्यं देशमावसेत्
text: He shall take up residence in a country which is open, fully supplied with grains, inhabited almost entirely by men of gentle birth, free from diseases, pleasant, where the vassals are obedient and where living is easily found.
iast: jāṅgalaṃ sasyasaṃpannamāryaprāyamanāvilam ramyamānatasāmantaṃ svājīvyaṃ deśamāvaset

sanskrit_iast: dhanvadurgaṃ mahīdurgamabdurgaṃ vārkṣameva vā nṛdurgaṃ giridurgaṃ vā samāśritya vaset puram
meaning: धन्वदुर्गं महीदुर्गमब्दुर्गं वार्क्षमेव वा नृदुर्गं गिरिदुर्गं वा समाश्रित्य वसेत् पुरम्
text: He shall live in a city, taking up his residence either in a ‘bow-fort’, or in an ‘earthen fort’, or in an ‘aquatic fort,’ or in an ‘arborial fort,’ or in a ‘human fort,’ or in a ‘hilly fort.’
iast: dhanvadurgaṃ mahīdurgamabdurgaṃ vārkṣameva vā nṛdurgaṃ giridurgaṃ vā samāśritya vaset puram

sanskrit_iast: sarveṇa tu prayatnena giridurgaṃ samāśrayet eṣāṃ hi bāhuguṇyena giridurgaṃ viśiṣyate
meaning: सर्वेण तु प्रयत्नेन गिरिदुर्गं समाश्रयेत् एषां हि बाहुगुण्येन गिरिदुर्गं विशिष्यते
text: The first three of these are inhabited by deer, by animals living underground and by aquatic animals, and the last three by monkeys, men and gods.
iast: sarveṇa tu prayatnena giridurgaṃ samāśrayet eṣāṃ hi bāhuguṇyena giridurgaṃ viśiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: triṇyādyānyāśritāstveṣāṃ mṛgagartāśrayāpcarāḥ trīṇyuttarāṇi kramaśaḥ plavaṅgamanarāmarāḥ
meaning: त्रिण्याद्यान्याश्रितास्त्वेषां मृगगर्ताश्रयाप्चराः त्रीण्युत्तराणि क्रमशः प्लवङ्गमनरामराः
text: The first three of these are inhabited by deer, by animals living underground and by aquatic animals, and the last three by monkeys, men and gods.
iast: triṇyādyānyāśritāstveṣāṃ mṛgagartāśrayāpcarāḥ trīṇyuttarāṇi kramaśaḥ plavaṅgamanarāmarāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathā durgāśritānetānnaupahiṃsanti śatravaḥ tathā 'rayo na hiṃsanti nṛpaṃ durgasamāśritam
meaning: यथा दुर्गाश्रितानेतान्नौपहिंसन्ति शत्रवः तथा 'रयो न हिंसन्ति नृपं दुर्गसमाश्रितम्
text: Just as enemies do not hurt these beings sheltered in their fortresses, so also the enemies do not injure the king protected by his fort.
iast: yathā durgāśritānetānnaupahiṃsanti śatravaḥ tathā 'rayo na hiṃsanti nṛpaṃ durgasamāśritam

sanskrit_iast: ekaḥ śataṃ yodhayati prākārastho dhanurdharaḥ śataṃ daśasahasrāṇi tasmāddurgaṃ vidhīyate
meaning: एकः शतं योधयति प्राकारस्थो धनुर्धरः शतं दशसहस्राणि तस्माद्दुर्गं विधीयते
text: A single bow-man, standing on a rampart, can fight against a hundred; and a hundred can fight against ten thousand; it is for the season that fortification has been enjoined.
iast: ekaḥ śataṃ yodhayati prākārastho dhanurdharaḥ śataṃ daśasahasrāṇi tasmāddurgaṃ vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: tat syādāyudhasaṃpannaṃ dhanadhānyena vāhanaiḥ brāhmaṇaiḥ śilpibhiryantrairyavasenaudakena ca
meaning: तत् स्यादायुधसंपन्नं धनधान्येन वाहनैः ब्राह्मणैः शिल्पिभिर्यन्त्रैर्यवसेनौदकेन च
text: It should be folly equipped with weapons, with money and grain, with conveyances, with Brāhmaṇas, with artisans, with machines, with fodder and with water,
iast: tat syādāyudhasaṃpannaṃ dhanadhānyena vāhanaiḥ brāhmaṇaiḥ śilpibhiryantrairyavasenaudakena ca

sanskrit_iast: tasya madhye suparyāptaṃ kārayedgṛhamātmanaḥ guptaṃ sarvaṛtukaṃ śubhraṃ jalavṛkṣasamanvitam
meaning: तस्य मध्ये सुपर्याप्तं कारयेद्गृहमात्मनः गुप्तं सर्वऋतुकं शुभ्रं जलवृक्षसमन्वितम्
text: In the centre of the fort, he shall get built for himsrlf a spacious palace, well guarded, equipped with all seasons, resplendent, and supplied with water and trees.
iast: tasya madhye suparyāptaṃ kārayedgṛhamātmanaḥ guptaṃ sarvaṛtukaṃ śubhraṃ jalavṛkṣasamanvitam

sanskrit_iast: tadadhyāsyaudvahedbhāryāṃ savarṇāṃ lakṣaṇānvitām kule mahati saṃbhūtāṃ hṛdyāṃ rūpaguṇānvītām
meaning: तदध्यास्यौद्वहेद्भार्यां सवर्णां लक्षणान्विताम् कुले महति संभूतां हृद्यां रूपगुणान्वीताम्
text: Having occupied it, he shall wed a wife of the same caste as himself, who is equipped with auspicious signs, born in a noble family, charming, and possessed of beauty and excellent qualities.
iast: tadadhyāsyaudvahedbhāryāṃ savarṇāṃ lakṣaṇānvitām kule mahati saṃbhūtāṃ hṛdyāṃ rūpaguṇānvītām

sanskrit_iast: purohitaṃ ca kurvīta vṛṇuyādeva caṛtvijaḥ te 'sya gṛhyāṇi karmāṇi kuryurvaitānikāni ca
meaning: पुरोहितं च कुर्वीत वृणुयादेव चऋत्विजः ते 'स्य गृह्याणि कर्माणि कुर्युर्वैतानिकानि च
text: He shall appoint a household priest and select officiating priests; they shall perform his domestic rites, as also the fire-sacrifices.
iast: purohitaṃ ca kurvīta vṛṇuyādeva caṛtvijaḥ te 'sya gṛhyāṇi karmāṇi kuryurvaitānikāni ca

sanskrit_iast: yajeta rājā kratubhirvividhairāptadakṣiṇaiḥ dharmārthaṃ caiva viprebhyo dadyādbhogān dhanāni ca
meaning: यजेत राजा क्रतुभिर्विविधैराप्तदक्षिणैः धर्मार्थं चैव विप्रेभ्यो दद्याद्भोगान् धनानि च
text: The King shall offer various sacrifices at which large sacrificial fees are paid; and for the purpose of acquiring merit, he shall provide for Brāhmaṇas luxuries and riches.
iast: yajeta rājā kratubhirvividhairāptadakṣiṇaiḥ dharmārthaṃ caiva viprebhyo dadyādbhogān dhanāni ca

sanskrit_iast: sāṃvatsarikamāptaiśca rāṣṭrādāhārayedbalim syāccāmnāyaparo loke varteta pitṛvatnṛṣu
meaning: सांवत्सरिकमाप्तैश्च राष्ट्रादाहारयेद्बलिम् स्याच्चाम्नायपरो लोके वर्तेत पितृवत्नृषु
text: He should cause the yearly revenue to be collectbd by trusted men. In his business he shall stick to the scriptures; and towards the people he shall behave like a father.
iast: sāṃvatsarikamāptaiśca rāṣṭrādāhārayedbalim syāccāmnāyaparo loke varteta pitṛvatnṛṣu

sanskrit_iast: adhyakṣān vividhān kuryāt tatra tatra vipaścitaḥ te 'sya sarvāṇyavekṣerannṛṇāṃ kāryāṇi kurvatām
meaning: अध्यक्षान् विविधान् कुर्यात् तत्र तत्र विपश्चितः ते 'स्य सर्वाण्यवेक्षेरन्नृणां कार्याणि कुर्वताम्
text: Here and there he shall appoint several proficient inspectors; they shall supervise all the acts of men working for him.
iast: adhyakṣān vividhān kuryāt tatra tatra vipaścitaḥ te 'sya sarvāṇyavekṣerannṛṇāṃ kāryāṇi kurvatām

sanskrit_iast: āvṛttānāṃ gurukulādviprāṇāṃ pūjako bhavet nṛpāṇāmakṣayo hyeṣa nidhirbrāhmo 'bhidhīyate
meaning: आवृत्तानां गुरुकुलाद्विप्राणां पूजको भवेत् नृपाणामक्षयो ह्येष निधिर्ब्राह्मो 'भिधीयते
text: He shall do honour to those Brāhmaṇas who have returned from their teacher’s house; for kings, this is interminable; and has been called ‘Brahmic treasure’.
iast: āvṛttānāṃ gurukulādviprāṇāṃ pūjako bhavet nṛpāṇāmakṣayo hyeṣa nidhirbrāhmo 'bhidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: na taṃ stenā na cāmitrā haranti na ca naśyati tasmādrājñā nidhātavyo brāhmaṇeṣvakṣayo nidhiḥ
meaning: न तं स्तेना न चामित्रा हरन्ति न च नश्यति तस्माद्राज्ञा निधातव्यो ब्राह्मणेष्वक्षयो निधिः
text: Neither thieves, nor enemies take it away; nor does it perish; hence this inexhaustible treasure shall be deposited by the king with the Brāhmaṇas.
iast: na taṃ stenā na cāmitrā haranti na ca naśyati tasmādrājñā nidhātavyo brāhmaṇeṣvakṣayo nidhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: na skandate na vyathate na vinaśyati karhi cit na skandati na cyavate variṣṭhamagnihotrebhyo brāhmaṇasya mukhe hutam
meaning: न स्कन्दते न व्यथते न विनश्यति कर्हि चित् न स्कन्दति न च्यवते वरिष्ठमग्निहोत्रेभ्यो ब्राह्मणस्य मुखे हुतम्
text: What is offered into the mouth of the Brāhmaṇa, which is neither spilt nor spoilt, nor wasted, is far superior to the Fire-offerings.
iast: na skandate na vyathate na vinaśyati karhi cit na skandati na cyavate variṣṭhamagnihotrebhyo brāhmaṇasya mukhe hutam

sanskrit_iast: samamabrāhmaṇe dānaṃ dviguṇaṃ brāhmaṇabruve prādhīte śatasāhasramanantaṃ vedapārage ācārye śatasāhasram] sahasraguṇamācārye
meaning: सममब्राह्मणे दानं द्विगुणं ब्राह्मणब्रुवे प्राधीते शतसाहस्रमनन्तं वेदपारगे आचार्ये शतसाहस्रम्] सहस्रगुणमाचार्ये
text: The gift to a non-Brāhmaṇa is equable; that to a nominal Brāhmaṇa is twofold; that to the Teacher, a hundred-thousand-fold and that to a person thoroughly learned in the Veda, endless.
iast: samamabrāhmaṇe dānaṃ dviguṇaṃ brāhmaṇabruve prādhīte śatasāhasramanantaṃ vedapārage ācārye śatasāhasram] sahasraguṇamācārye

sanskrit_iast: pātrasya hi viśeṣeṇa śraddadhānatayaiva ca alpaṃ vā bahu vā pretya dānasya phalamaśnute [deśakālavidhānena dravyaṃ śraddhāsamanvitam ] [pātre pradīyate yat tu taddharmasya prasādhanam ]
meaning: पात्रस्य हि विशेषेण श्रद्दधानतयैव च अल्पं वा बहु वा प्रेत्य दानस्य फलमश्नुते [देशकालविधानेन द्रव्यं श्रद्धासमन्वितम् ] [पात्रे प्रदीयते यत् तु तद्धर्मस्य प्रसाधनम् ]
text: For one obtains, after death, the reward, small or great, of his gifts,—according to the peculiar character of the recipient, and also according to his own faith.
iast: pātrasya hi viśeṣeṇa śraddadhānatayaiva ca alpaṃ vā bahu vā pretya dānasya phalamaśnute [deśakālavidhānena dravyaṃ śraddhāsamanvitam ] [pātre pradīyate yat tu taddharmasya prasādhanam ]

sanskrit_iast: samauttamādhamai rājā tvāhūtaḥ pālayan prajāḥ na nivarteta saṅgrāmāt kṣātraṃ dharmamanusmaran
meaning: समौत्तमाधमै राजा त्वाहूतः पालयन् प्रजाः न निवर्तेत सङ्ग्रामात् क्षात्रं धर्ममनुस्मरन्
text: While protecting his people, if the king is challenged by enemies, either equal in strength, or stronger, or weaker, he shall not shrink from battle, bearing in mind the duty of the kṣatriya.
iast: samauttamādhamai rājā tvāhūtaḥ pālayan prajāḥ na nivarteta saṅgrāmāt kṣātraṃ dharmamanusmaran

sanskrit_iast: saṅgrāmeṣvanivartitvaṃ prajānāṃ caiva pālanam śuśrūṣā brāhmaṇānāṃ ca rājñāṃ śreyaskaraṃ param
meaning: सङ्ग्रामेष्वनिवर्तित्वं प्रजानां चैव पालनम् शुश्रूषा ब्राह्मणानां च राज्ञां श्रेयस्करं परम्
text: Not shrinking from battle, protecting the people, and attending on brāhmaṇas,—is the best means of securing happiness for kings.
iast: saṅgrāmeṣvanivartitvaṃ prajānāṃ caiva pālanam śuśrūṣā brāhmaṇānāṃ ca rājñāṃ śreyaskaraṃ param

sanskrit_iast: āhaveṣu mitho 'nyonyaṃ jighāṃsanto mahīkṣitaḥ yudhyamānāḥ paraṃ śaktyā svargaṃ yāntyaparālgmukhāḥ
meaning: आहवेषु मिथो 'न्योन्यं जिघांसन्तो महीक्षितः युध्यमानाः परं शक्त्या स्वर्गं यान्त्यपराल्ग्मुखाः
text: Kings, seeking to slay each other in battle and fighting with gheat energy, without turning back, proceed to heaven.
iast: āhaveṣu mitho 'nyonyaṃ jighāṃsanto mahīkṣitaḥ yudhyamānāḥ paraṃ śaktyā svargaṃ yāntyaparālgmukhāḥ

sanskrit_iast: na kūṭairāyudhairhanyādyudhyamāno raṇe ripūn na karṇibhirnāpi digdhairnāgnijvalitatejanaiḥ
meaning: न कूटैरायुधैर्हन्याद्युध्यमानो रणे रिपून् न कर्णिभिर्नापि दिग्धैर्नाग्निज्वलिततेजनैः
text: While fighting his enemies in battle, he shall hot strike with concealed weapons; nor with arrows that are poisoned, or barbed, or with flaming shafts.
iast: na kūṭairāyudhairhanyādyudhyamāno raṇe ripūn na karṇibhirnāpi digdhairnāgnijvalitatejanaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: na ca hanyāt sthalārūḍhaṃ na klībaṃ na kṛtāñjalim na muktakeśaṃ nāsīnaṃ na tavāsmīti vādinam
meaning: न च हन्यात् स्थलारूढं न क्लीबं न कृताञ्जलिम् न मुक्तकेशं नासीनं न तवास्मीति वादिनम्
text: He shall hot strike one who is standing on the ground, nor one who is a eunuch, nor the supp?icant (supplicant?) with joined palms, nor one with loosened hair, nor one who is seated, nor one who says ‘i am yours;’
iast: na ca hanyāt sthalārūḍhaṃ na klībaṃ na kṛtāñjalim na muktakeśaṃ nāsīnaṃ na tavāsmīti vādinam

sanskrit_iast: na suptaṃ na visaṃnāhaṃ na nagnaṃ na nirāyudham nāyudhyamānaṃ paśyantaṃ na pareṇa samāgatam
meaning: न सुप्तं न विसंनाहं न नग्नं न निरायुधम् नायुध्यमानं पश्यन्तं न परेण समागतम्
text: Nor one who is sleeping, nor him who is without his armour, nor one who is naked, nor one deprived of his weapons, nor one who is only looking on and not fighting, nor one who is engaged in fighting with ahother person;
iast: na suptaṃ na visaṃnāhaṃ na nagnaṃ na nirāyudham nāyudhyamānaṃ paśyantaṃ na pareṇa samāgatam

sanskrit_iast: nāyudhavyasanaprāptaṃ nārtaṃ nātiparikṣatam na bhītaṃ na parāvṛttaṃ satāṃ dharmamanusmaran
meaning: नायुधव्यसनप्राप्तं नार्तं नातिपरिक्षतम् न भीतं न परावृत्तं सतां धर्ममनुस्मरन्
text: Nor one who has fallen in difficulties regarding weapons; nor one in distress, nor one severely wounded, nor one who is frightened, nor one who has turned back;—the king remembering the duties of honourable men.
iast: nāyudhavyasanaprāptaṃ nārtaṃ nātiparikṣatam na bhītaṃ na parāvṛttaṃ satāṃ dharmamanusmaran

sanskrit_iast: yastu bhītaḥ parāvṛttaḥ saṅgrāme hanyate paraiḥ bharturyadduṣkṛtaṃ kiṃ cit tat sarvaṃ pratipadyate
meaning: यस्तु भीतः परावृत्तः सङ्ग्रामे हन्यते परैः भर्तुर्यद्दुष्कृतं किं चित् तत् सर्वं प्रतिपद्यते
text: But the Kṣatriya who, frightened and turned back, is slain by the enemies, takes upon himself all the sin that there may be of his master.
iast: yastu bhītaḥ parāvṛttaḥ saṅgrāme hanyate paraiḥ bharturyadduṣkṛtaṃ kiṃ cit tat sarvaṃ pratipadyate

sanskrit_iast: yatcāsya sukṛtaṃ kiṃ cidamutrārthamupārjitam bhartā tat sarvamādatte parāvṛttahatasya tu
meaning: यत्चास्य सुकृतं किं चिदमुत्रार्थमुपार्जितम् भर्ता तत् सर्वमादत्ते परावृत्तहतस्य तु
text: And whatever merit the man slain after having turned back may have earned for the next world,—all that his master takes off.
iast: yatcāsya sukṛtaṃ kiṃ cidamutrārthamupārjitam bhartā tat sarvamādatte parāvṛttahatasya tu

sanskrit_iast: rathāśvaṃ hastinaṃ chatraṃ dhanaṃ dhānyaṃ paśūn striyaḥ sarvadravyāṇi kupyaṃ ca yo yaj jayati tasya tat
meaning: रथाश्वं हस्तिनं छत्रं धनं धान्यं पशून् स्त्रियः सर्वद्रव्याणि कुप्यं च यो यज् जयति तस्य तत्
text: Chariots and horses, elephants, umbrellas, wealth, grains, animals, women, all goods and baser metals belong to him who wins them.
iast: rathāśvaṃ hastinaṃ chatraṃ dhanaṃ dhānyaṃ paśūn striyaḥ sarvadravyāṇi kupyaṃ ca yo yaj jayati tasya tat

sanskrit_iast: rājñaśca dadyuruddhāramityeṣā vaidikī śrutiḥ rājñā ca sarvayodhebhyo dātavyamapṛthagjitam
meaning: राज्ञश्च दद्युरुद्धारमित्येषा वैदिकी श्रुतिः राज्ञा च सर्वयोधेभ्यो दातव्यमपृथग्जितम्
text: They shall present to the King the choice portion,—such is the Vedic declaration. what has not been won individually shall be distributed by the king among all the soldiers.
iast: rājñaśca dadyuruddhāramityeṣā vaidikī śrutiḥ rājñā ca sarvayodhebhyo dātavyamapṛthagjitam

sanskrit_iast: eṣo 'nupaskṛtaḥ prokto yodhadharmaḥ sanātanaḥ asmāddharmānna cyaveta kṣatriyo ghnan raṇe ripūn
meaning: एषो 'नुपस्कृतः प्रोक्तो योधधर्मः सनातनः अस्माद्धर्मान्न च्यवेत क्षत्रियो घ्नन् रणे रिपून्
text: Thus has been declared the blameless eternal law of warriors; the Kṣatriya, striking his enemies in battle, shall not deviate from this law.
iast: eṣo 'nupaskṛtaḥ prokto yodhadharmaḥ sanātanaḥ asmāddharmānna cyaveta kṣatriyo ghnan raṇe ripūn

sanskrit_iast: alabdhaṃ caiva lipseta labdhaṃ rakṣet prayatnataḥ rakṣitaṃ vardhayeccaiva vṛddhaṃ pātreṣu nikṣipet
meaning: अलब्धं चैव लिप्सेत लब्धं रक्षेत् प्रयत्नतः रक्षितं वर्धयेच्चैव वृद्धं पात्रेषु निक्षिपेत्
text: He shall strive to obtain what has not been obtained; what he has gained he shall preserve with care; he shall augment what has been preserved and what has been augmented he shall bestow upon suitable recipients
iast: alabdhaṃ caiva lipseta labdhaṃ rakṣet prayatnataḥ rakṣitaṃ vardhayeccaiva vṛddhaṃ pātreṣu nikṣipet

sanskrit_iast: etaccaturvidhaṃ vidyāt puruṣārthaprayojanam asya nityamanuṣṭhānaṃ samyak kuryādatandritaḥ
meaning: एतच्चतुर्विधं विद्यात् पुरुषार्थप्रयोजनम् अस्य नित्यमनुष्ठानं सम्यक् कुर्यादतन्द्रितः
text: What has not been gained he shall seek to obtain by means of force; what has been gained he shall save with careful attention; what has been saved he shall augment by adding to it; and what has been augmented he shall bestow on suitable recipients.
iast: etaccaturvidhaṃ vidyāt puruṣārthaprayojanam asya nityamanuṣṭhānaṃ samyak kuryādatandritaḥ

sanskrit_iast: alabdhamiccheddaṇḍena labdhaṃ rakṣedavekṣayā rakṣitaṃ vardhayedvṛddhyā vṛddhaṃ pātreṣu nikṣipet
meaning: अलब्धमिच्छेद्दण्डेन लब्धं रक्षेदवेक्षया रक्षितं वर्धयेद्वृद्ध्या वृद्धं पात्रेषु निक्षिपेत्
text: What has not been gained he shall seek to obtain by means of force; what has been gained he shall save with careful attention; what has been saved he shall augment by adding to it; and what has been augmented he shall bestow on suitable recipients.
iast: alabdhamiccheddaṇḍena labdhaṃ rakṣedavekṣayā rakṣitaṃ vardhayedvṛddhyā vṛddhaṃ pātreṣu nikṣipet

sanskrit_iast: nityamudyatadaṇḍaḥ syānnityaṃ vivṛtapauruṣaḥ nityaṃ saṃvṛtasaṃvāryo nityaṃ chidrānusāryareḥ
meaning: नित्यमुद्यतदण्डः स्यान्नित्यं विवृतपौरुषः नित्यं संवृतसंवार्यो नित्यं छिद्रानुसार्यरेः
text: He shall have his force always operative; his manliness always displayed, his secrets constantly concealed, ever following up the weak points of his enemy.
iast: nityamudyatadaṇḍaḥ syānnityaṃ vivṛtapauruṣaḥ nityaṃ saṃvṛtasaṃvāryo nityaṃ chidrānusāryareḥ

sanskrit_iast: nityamudyatadaṇḍasya kṛtsnamudvijate jagat tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni daṇḍenaiva prasādhayet
meaning: नित्यमुद्यतदण्डस्य कृत्स्नमुद्विजते जगत् तस्मात् सर्वाणि भूतानि दण्डेनैव प्रसाधयेत्
text: Of him who has his force constantly operative, the whole world stands in awe. He shall, therefore, subdue all men by means of force
iast: nityamudyatadaṇḍasya kṛtsnamudvijate jagat tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni daṇḍenaiva prasādhayet

sanskrit_iast: amāyayaiva varteta na kathaṃ cana māyayā budhyetāriprayuktāṃ ca māyāṃ nityaṃ susaṃvṛtaḥ
meaning: अमाययैव वर्तेत न कथं चन मायया बुध्येतारिप्रयुक्तां च मायां नित्यं सुसंवृतः
text: He shall always behave without guile, and never with guile; well protected himself, he shall fathom the guiles employed by his enemies.
iast: amāyayaiva varteta na kathaṃ cana māyayā budhyetāriprayuktāṃ ca māyāṃ nityaṃ susaṃvṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nāsya chidraṃ paro vidyādvidyātchidraṃ parasya ca gūhet kūrma ivāṅgāni rakṣedvivaramātmanaḥ
meaning: नास्य छिद्रं परो विद्याद्विद्यात्छिद्रं परस्य च गूहेत् कूर्म इवाङ्गानि रक्षेद्विवरमात्मनः
text: His enemy should hot know his weak points, but he must know the weak points of the enemy; he should hide the departments (of government) as the tortoise does its limbs; and he should guard his own weak points.
iast: nāsya chidraṃ paro vidyādvidyātchidraṃ parasya ca gūhet kūrma ivāṅgāni rakṣedvivaramātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bakavatcintayedarthān siṃhavatca parākrame vṛkavatcāvalumpeta śaśavatca viniṣpatet
meaning: बकवत्चिन्तयेदर्थान् सिंहवत्च पराक्रमे वृकवत्चावलुम्पेत शशवत्च विनिष्पतेत्
text: He shall ponder over his plans like the heron, and like the lion he shall exert his power; he shall snatch like the wolf, and like the hare he shall double in retreat.
iast: bakavatcintayedarthān siṃhavatca parākrame vṛkavatcāvalumpeta śaśavatca viniṣpatet

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ vijayamānasya ye 'sya syuḥ paripanthinaḥ tānānayedvaśaṃ sarvān sāmādibhirupakramaiḥ
meaning: एवं विजयमानस्य ये 'स्य स्युः परिपन्थिनः तानानयेद्वशं सर्वान् सामादिभिरुपक्रमैः
text: While he is thus engaged in conquest, if there should be any opponents for him, all these he shall bring under subjugation by means of conciliation and other expedients.
iast: evaṃ vijayamānasya ye 'sya syuḥ paripanthinaḥ tānānayedvaśaṃ sarvān sāmādibhirupakramaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadi te tu na tiṣṭheyurupāyaiḥ prathamaistribhiḥ daṇḍenaiva prasahyaitāṃśanakairvaśamānayet
meaning: यदि ते तु न तिष्ठेयुरुपायैः प्रथमैस्त्रिभिः दण्डेनैव प्रसह्यैतांशनकैर्वशमानयेत्
text: If however they should not be stopped by means of the first three expedients, then he shall gradually bring them under subjection by force.
iast: yadi te tu na tiṣṭheyurupāyaiḥ prathamaistribhiḥ daṇḍenaiva prasahyaitāṃśanakairvaśamānayet

sanskrit_iast: sāmādīnāmupāyānāṃ caturṇāmapi paṇḍitāḥ sāmadaṇḍau praśaṃsanti nityaṃ rāṣṭrābhivṛddhaye
meaning: सामादीनामुपायानां चतुर्णामपि पण्डिताः सामदण्डौ प्रशंसन्ति नित्यं राष्ट्राभिवृद्धये
text: For the prosperity of kingdoms the wise ones always recommend Conciliation and Force from among the four expedients, conciliation and the rest.
iast: sāmādīnāmupāyānāṃ caturṇāmapi paṇḍitāḥ sāmadaṇḍau praśaṃsanti nityaṃ rāṣṭrābhivṛddhaye

sanskrit_iast: yathauddharati nirdātā kakṣaṃ dhānyaṃ ca rakṣati tathā rakṣennṛpo rāṣṭraṃ hanyācca paripanthinaḥ
meaning: यथौद्धरति निर्दाता कक्षं धान्यं च रक्षति तथा रक्षेन्नृपो राष्ट्रं हन्याच्च परिपन्थिनः
text: Just as the weeder plucks out the weed and preserves the corn, so shall the King preserve his kingdom and destroy his opponents.
iast: yathauddharati nirdātā kakṣaṃ dhānyaṃ ca rakṣati tathā rakṣennṛpo rāṣṭraṃ hanyācca paripanthinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mohādrājā svarāṣṭraṃ yaḥ karṣayatyanavekṣayā so 'cirādbhraśyate rājyātjīvitātca sabāndhavaḥ
meaning: मोहाद्राजा स्वराष्ट्रं यः कर्षयत्यनवेक्षया सो 'चिराद्भ्रश्यते राज्यात्जीवितात्च सबान्धवः
text: The King, who, through folly, thoughtlessly oppresses his kingdom, becomes, ‘along with his relations’, deprived, without delay, of his kingdom and life.
iast: mohādrājā svarāṣṭraṃ yaḥ karṣayatyanavekṣayā so 'cirādbhraśyate rājyātjīvitātca sabāndhavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: śarīrakarṣaṇāt prāṇāḥ kṣīyante prāṇināṃ yathā tathā rājñāmapi prāṇāḥ kṣīyante rāṣṭrakarṣaṇāt
meaning: शरीरकर्षणात् प्राणाः क्षीयन्ते प्राणिनां यथा तथा राज्ञामपि प्राणाः क्षीयन्ते राष्ट्रकर्षणात्
text: As the lives of living beings perish by the emasciation of their bodies, so do the lives of Kings perish by oppressing their kingdom
iast: śarīrakarṣaṇāt prāṇāḥ kṣīyante prāṇināṃ yathā tathā rājñāmapi prāṇāḥ kṣīyante rāṣṭrakarṣaṇāt

sanskrit_iast: rāṣṭrasya saṅgrahe nityaṃ vidhānamidamācaret susaṅgṛhītarāṣṭre hi pārthivaḥ sukhamedhate
meaning: राष्ट्रस्य सङ्ग्रहे नित्यं विधानमिदमाचरेत् सुसङ्गृहीतराष्ट्रे हि पार्थिवः सुखमेधते
text: In the administration of his kingdom he shall adopt the procedure described below; for the king, whose kingdom is properly administered, prospers easily.
iast: rāṣṭrasya saṅgrahe nityaṃ vidhānamidamācaret susaṅgṛhītarāṣṭre hi pārthivaḥ sukhamedhate

sanskrit_iast: dvayostrayāṇāṃ pañcānāṃ madhye gulmamadhiṣṭhitam tathā grāmaśatānāṃ ca kuryādrāṣṭrasya saṅgraham
meaning: द्वयोस्त्रयाणां पञ्चानां मध्ये गुल्ममधिष्ठितम् तथा ग्रामशतानां च कुर्याद्राष्ट्रस्य सङ्ग्रहम्
text: In the midst of two, three, five or hundred villages he shall appoint an administrator of state supplied with a picket of guards.
iast: dvayostrayāṇāṃ pañcānāṃ madhye gulmamadhiṣṭhitam tathā grāmaśatānāṃ ca kuryādrāṣṭrasya saṅgraham

sanskrit_iast: grāmasyādhipatiṃ kuryāddaśagrāmapatiṃ tathā viṃśatīśaṃ śataiśaṃ ca sahasrapatimeva ca
meaning: ग्रामस्याधिपतिं कुर्याद्दशग्रामपतिं तथा विंशतीशं शतैशं च सहस्रपतिमेव च
text: He shall appoint the lord of one village, as also the lord of ten villages, the lord of twenty, the lord of hundred and the lord of thousand villages.
iast: grāmasyādhipatiṃ kuryāddaśagrāmapatiṃ tathā viṃśatīśaṃ śataiśaṃ ca sahasrapatimeva ca

sanskrit_iast: grāmadoṣān samutpannān grāmikaḥ śanakaiḥ svayam śaṃsedgrāmadaśaiśāya daśaiśo viṃśatīśine
meaning: ग्रामदोषान् समुत्पन्नान् ग्रामिकः शनकैः स्वयम् शंसेद्ग्रामदशैशाय दशैशो विंशतीशिने
text: Troubles arising in the village, the Village-lord shall himself gently report to the Lord of Ten villages; and the Lord of Ten villages to the Lord of Twenty;—(116)—the Lord of Twenty shall communicate it all to the Lord of Hundred; and the Lord of Hundred himself shall report it to the Lord of Thousand.
iast: grāmadoṣān samutpannān grāmikaḥ śanakaiḥ svayam śaṃsedgrāmadaśaiśāya daśaiśo viṃśatīśine

sanskrit_iast: viṃśatīśastu tat sarvaṃ śataiśāya nivedayet śaṃsedgrāmaśataiśastu sahasrapataye svayam
meaning: विंशतीशस्तु तत् सर्वं शतैशाय निवेदयेत् शंसेद्ग्रामशतैशस्तु सहस्रपतये स्वयम्
text: Troubles arising in the village, the Village-lord shall himself gently report to the Lord of Ten villages; and the Lord of Ten villages to the Lord of Twenty;—(116)—the Lord of Twenty shall communicate it all to the Lord of Hundred; and the Lord of Hundred himself shall report it to the Lord of Thousand.
iast: viṃśatīśastu tat sarvaṃ śataiśāya nivedayet śaṃsedgrāmaśataiśastu sahasrapataye svayam

sanskrit_iast: yāni rājapradeyāni pratyahaṃ grāmavāsibhiḥ annapānaindhanādīni grāmikastānyavāpnuyāt
meaning: यानि राजप्रदेयानि प्रत्यहं ग्रामवासिभिः अन्नपानैन्धनादीनि ग्रामिकस्तान्यवाप्नुयात्
text: The Village-lord shall get daily those things that ought to be furnished to the King by the villagers, in the shape of food, drink, fuel and the rest.
iast: yāni rājapradeyāni pratyahaṃ grāmavāsibhiḥ annapānaindhanādīni grāmikastānyavāpnuyāt

sanskrit_iast: daśī kulaṃ tu bhuñjīta viṃśī pañca kulāni ca grāmaṃ grāmaśatādhyakṣaḥ sahasrādhipatiḥ puram
meaning: दशी कुलं तु भुञ्जीत विंशी पञ्च कुलानि च ग्रामं ग्रामशताध्यक्षः सहस्राधिपतिः पुरम्
text: The Lord of Ten villages shall enjoy one Kula and the Lord of Twenty villages twenty Kulas; the Lord of Hundred villages one whole village and the Lord of Thousand villages one town.
iast: daśī kulaṃ tu bhuñjīta viṃśī pañca kulāni ca grāmaṃ grāmaśatādhyakṣaḥ sahasrādhipatiḥ puram

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ grāmyāṇi kāryāni pṛthakkāryāṇi caiva hi rājño 'nyaḥ sacivaḥ snigdhastāni paśyedatandritaḥ
meaning: तेषां ग्राम्याणि कार्यानि पृथक्कार्याणि चैव हि राज्ञो 'न्यः सचिवः स्निग्धस्तानि पश्येदतन्द्रितः
text: The affairs of these officers pertaining to the villages, as also their individual affairs another minister of the King shall inspect, who is loyal and never idle.
iast: teṣāṃ grāmyāṇi kāryāni pṛthakkāryāṇi caiva hi rājño 'nyaḥ sacivaḥ snigdhastāni paśyedatandritaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nagare nagare caikaṃ kuryāt sarvārthacintakam uccaiḥsthānaṃ ghorarūpaṃ nakṣatrāṇāmiva graham
meaning: नगरे नगरे चैकं कुर्यात् सर्वार्थचिन्तकम् उच्चैःस्थानं घोररूपं नक्षत्राणामिव ग्रहम्
text: In each town he shall appoint one Superintendent of all works, of high status and awe-inspiring appearance,—he being like a planet among stars.
iast: nagare nagare caikaṃ kuryāt sarvārthacintakam uccaiḥsthānaṃ ghorarūpaṃ nakṣatrāṇāmiva graham

sanskrit_iast: sa tānanuparikrāmet sarvāneva sadā svayam teṣāṃ vṛttaṃ pariṇayet samyag rāṣṭreṣu tatcaraiḥ
meaning: स ताननुपरिक्रामेत् सर्वानेव सदा स्वयम् तेषां वृत्तं परिणयेत् सम्यग् राष्ट्रेषु तत्चरैः
text: This officer shall always personally supervise in turn all those officers, and thoroughly acquaint himself, through the King’s spies, with their behaviour in their respective jurisdictions.
iast: sa tānanuparikrāmet sarvāneva sadā svayam teṣāṃ vṛttaṃ pariṇayet samyag rāṣṭreṣu tatcaraiḥ

sanskrit_iast: rājño hi rakṣādhikṛtāḥ parasvādāyinaḥ śaṭhāḥ bhṛtyā bhavanti prāyeṇa tebhyo rakṣedimāḥ prajāḥ
meaning: राज्ञो हि रक्षाधिकृताः परस्वादायिनः शठाः भृत्या भवन्ति प्रायेण तेभ्यो रक्षेदिमाः प्रजाः
text: As the king’s servants, appointed to protect the people, generally become knaves, bent upon seizing the property of others,—he shall protect his people against them
iast: rājño hi rakṣādhikṛtāḥ parasvādāyinaḥ śaṭhāḥ bhṛtyā bhavanti prāyeṇa tebhyo rakṣedimāḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: ye kāryikebhyo 'rthameva gṛhṇīyuḥ pāpacetasaḥ teṣāṃ sarvasvamādāya rājā kuryāt pravāsanam
meaning: ये कार्यिकेभ्यो 'र्थमेव गृह्णीयुः पापचेतसः तेषां सर्वस्वमादाय राजा कुर्यात् प्रवासनम्
text: Those evil-minded persons who would take money from men engaged in business—of these the King shall confiscate the whole property and ordain banishment.
iast: ye kāryikebhyo 'rthameva gṛhṇīyuḥ pāpacetasaḥ teṣāṃ sarvasvamādāya rājā kuryāt pravāsanam

sanskrit_iast: rājā karmasu yuktānāṃ strīṇāṃ preṣyajanasya ca rājakarmasu pratyahaṃ kalpayedvṛttiṃ sthānaṃ karmānurūpataḥ sthānakarmānurūpataḥ
meaning: राजा कर्मसु युक्तानां स्त्रीणां प्रेष्यजनस्य च राजकर्मसु प्रत्यहं कल्पयेद्वृत्तिं स्थानं कर्मानुरूपतः स्थानकर्मानुरूपतः
text: For women employed in the King’s service and also for menial servants, he shall fix daily wages, in proportion to their position and work.
iast: rājā karmasu yuktānāṃ strīṇāṃ preṣyajanasya ca rājakarmasu pratyahaṃ kalpayedvṛttiṃ sthānaṃ karmānurūpataḥ sthānakarmānurūpataḥ

sanskrit_iast: paṇo deyo 'vakṛṣṭasya ṣaḍutkṛṣṭasya vetanam ṣāṇmāsikastathā 'cchādo dhānyadroṇastu māsikaḥ
meaning: पणो देयो 'वकृष्टस्य षडुत्कृष्टस्य वेतनम् षाण्मासिकस्तथा 'च्छादो धान्यद्रोणस्तु मासिकः
text: One paṇa shall be paid as the wages of the inferior, and six to the superior servant; as also clothing every sixth month and a Droṇa of grain every month.
iast: paṇo deyo 'vakṛṣṭasya ṣaḍutkṛṣṭasya vetanam ṣāṇmāsikastathā 'cchādo dhānyadroṇastu māsikaḥ

sanskrit_iast: krayavikrayamadhvānaṃ bhaktaṃ ca saparivyayam yogakṣemaṃ ca saṃprekṣya vaṇijo dāpayet karān
meaning: क्रयविक्रयमध्वानं भक्तं च सपरिव्ययम् योगक्षेमं च संप्रेक्ष्य वणिजो दापयेत् करान्
text: He should make the traders pay duties, after due investigation of the details of buying and selling, the journey involved, fooding along with its accessories, and the measures of safety.
iast: krayavikrayamadhvānaṃ bhaktaṃ ca saparivyayam yogakṣemaṃ ca saṃprekṣya vaṇijo dāpayet karān

sanskrit_iast: yathā phalena yujyeta rājā kartā ca karmaṇām tathā 'vekṣya nṛpo rāṣṭre kalpayet satataṃ karān
meaning: यथा फलेन युज्येत राजा कर्ता च कर्मणाम् तथा 'वेक्ष्य नृपो राष्ट्रे कल्पयेत् सततं करान्
text: यथाऽल्पाल्पमदन्त्याद्यं वार्योकोवत्सषट्पदाः ।तथाऽल्पाल्पो ग्रहीतव्यो राष्ट्राद् राज्ञाब्दिकः करः ॥ १२८ (१२९) ॥
iast: yathā phalena yujyeta rājā kartā ca karmaṇām tathā 'vekṣya nṛpo rāṣṭre kalpayet satataṃ karān

sanskrit_iast: yathā 'lpālpamadantyādyaṃ vāryokovatsaṣaṭpadāḥ tathā 'lpālpo grahītavyo rāṣṭrādrājñābdikaḥ karaḥ
meaning: यथा 'ल्पाल्पमदन्त्याद्यं वार्योकोवत्सषट्पदाः तथा 'ल्पाल्पो ग्रहीतव्यो राष्ट्राद्राज्ञाब्दिकः करः
text: After due investigation the King shall always levy taxes in his kingdom in such a way that he himself and the man who carries on the business shall both receive their reward.
iast: yathā 'lpālpamadantyādyaṃ vāryokovatsaṣaṭpadāḥ tathā 'lpālpo grahītavyo rāṣṭrādrājñābdikaḥ karaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pañcāśadbhāga ādeyo rājñā paśuhiraṇyayoḥ dhānyānāmaṣṭamo bhāgaḥ ṣaṣṭho dvādaśa eva vā
meaning: पञ्चाशद्भाग आदेयो राज्ञा पशुहिरण्ययोः धान्यानामष्टमो भागः षष्ठो द्वादश एव वा
text: In the case of cattle and gold the fiftieth part shall be taken by the King; and in the case of grains, the eighth, sixth or twelfth part.
iast: pañcāśadbhāga ādeyo rājñā paśuhiraṇyayoḥ dhānyānāmaṣṭamo bhāgaḥ ṣaṣṭho dvādaśa eva vā

sanskrit_iast: ādadītātha ṣaḍbhāgaṃ drumāṃsamadhusarpiṣām gandhoṣadhirasānāṃ ca puṣpamūlaphalasya ca
meaning: आददीताथ षड्भागं द्रुमांसमधुसर्पिषाम् गन्धोषधिरसानां च पुष्पमूलफलस्य च
text: He shall take the sixth part in the case of trees, meat, honey and clarified butter, of perfumes, medicinal herbs and poisons, of flowers, roots and fruits;—(131) of leaves, vegetables and grasses, of skins and cane, of earthenware vessels, and of all things made of stone.
iast: ādadītātha ṣaḍbhāgaṃ drumāṃsamadhusarpiṣām gandhoṣadhirasānāṃ ca puṣpamūlaphalasya ca

sanskrit_iast: patraśākatṛṇānāṃ ca carmaṇāṃ vaidalasya ca mṛnmayānāṃ ca bhāṇḍānāṃ sarvasyāśmamayasya ca
meaning: पत्रशाकतृणानां च चर्मणां वैदलस्य च मृन्मयानां च भाण्डानां सर्वस्याश्ममयस्य च
text: He shall take the sixth part in the case of trees, meat, honey and clarified butter, of perfumes, medicinal herbs and poisons, of flowers, roots and fruits;—(131) of leaves, vegetables and grasses, of skins and cane, of earthenware vessels, and of all things made of stone.
iast: patraśākatṛṇānāṃ ca carmaṇāṃ vaidalasya ca mṛnmayānāṃ ca bhāṇḍānāṃ sarvasyāśmamayasya ca

sanskrit_iast: mriyamāṇo 'pyādadīta na rājā śrotriyāt karam na ca kṣudhā 'sya saṃsīdetśrotriyo viṣaye vasan
meaning: म्रियमाणो 'प्याददीत न राजा श्रोत्रियात् करम् न च क्षुधा 'स्य संसीदेत्श्रोत्रियो विषये वसन्
text: Even though dying, the King shall not levy a tax on the Śrotriya; and no Śrotriya living in his kingdom shall suffer from hunger.
iast: mriyamāṇo 'pyādadīta na rājā śrotriyāt karam na ca kṣudhā 'sya saṃsīdetśrotriyo viṣaye vasan

sanskrit_iast: yasya rājñastu viṣaye śrotriyaḥ sīdati kṣudhā tasyāpi tat kṣudhā rāṣṭramacirenaiva sīdati
meaning: यस्य राज्ञस्तु विषये श्रोत्रियः सीदति क्षुधा तस्यापि तत् क्षुधा राष्ट्रमचिरेनैव सीदति
text: The kingdom of that King in whose realm the Śrotriya suffers from hunger, shall, ere long, pine with hunger.
iast: yasya rājñastu viṣaye śrotriyaḥ sīdati kṣudhā tasyāpi tat kṣudhā rāṣṭramacirenaiva sīdati

sanskrit_iast: śrutavṛtte viditvā 'sya vṛttiṃ dharmyāṃ prakalpayet saṃrakṣet sarvataścainaṃ pitā putramivaurasam
meaning: श्रुतवृत्ते विदित्वा 'स्य वृत्तिं धर्म्यां प्रकल्पयेत् संरक्षेत् सर्वतश्चैनं पिता पुत्रमिवौरसम्
text: Having ascertained his learning and character, he shall provide for him a fair living; and he shall protect him against all things, even as the father protects his lawful son.
iast: śrutavṛtte viditvā 'sya vṛttiṃ dharmyāṃ prakalpayet saṃrakṣet sarvataścainaṃ pitā putramivaurasam

sanskrit_iast: saṃrakṣyamāṇo rājñā yaṃ kurute dharmamanvaham rājñā 'yaṃ tenāyurvardhate rājño draviṇaṃ rāṣṭrameva ca
meaning: संरक्ष्यमाणो राज्ञा यं कुरुते धर्ममन्वहम् राज्ञा 'यं तेनायुर्वर्धते राज्ञो द्रविणं राष्ट्रमेव च
text: Protected by the King he performs meritorious acts day after dat; and by that the King’s life, wealth and kingdom become augmented.
iast: saṃrakṣyamāṇo rājñā yaṃ kurute dharmamanvaham rājñā 'yaṃ tenāyurvardhate rājño draviṇaṃ rāṣṭrameva ca

sanskrit_iast: yat kiṃ cidapi varṣasya dāpayet karasaṃjñitam vyavahāreṇa jīvantaṃ rājā rāṣṭre pṛthagjanam
meaning: यत् किं चिदपि वर्षस्य दापयेत् करसंज्ञितम् व्यवहारेण जीवन्तं राजा राष्ट्रे पृथग्जनम्
text: The King shall make the other people living by business p ay during the year something under the name of “tax”.
iast: yat kiṃ cidapi varṣasya dāpayet karasaṃjñitam vyavahāreṇa jīvantaṃ rājā rāṣṭre pṛthagjanam

sanskrit_iast: kārukāṃśilpinaścaiva śūdrāṃḥ cātmaupajīvinaḥ ekaikaṃ kārayet karma māsi māsi mahīpatiḥ
meaning: कारुकांशिल्पिनश्चैव शूद्रांः चात्मौपजीविनः एकैकं कारयेत् कर्म मासि मासि महीपतिः
text: Mechanics and Artisans, as also shudras who subsist by bodily labour,—the King shall make each of these work for one day every month.
iast: kārukāṃśilpinaścaiva śūdrāṃḥ cātmaupajīvinaḥ ekaikaṃ kārayet karma māsi māsi mahīpatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: naucchindyādātmano mūlaṃ pareṣāṃ cātitṛṣṇayā ucchindan hyātmano mūlamāṭmānaṃ tāṃśca pīdayet
meaning: नौच्छिन्द्यादात्मनो मूलं परेषां चातितृष्णया उच्छिन्दन् ह्यात्मनो मूलमाट्मानं तांश्च पीदयेत्
text: He shall not cut off his own root, nor that of others, through excessive greed; by cutting off his own root he causes suffering to himself as well as to others.
iast: naucchindyādātmano mūlaṃ pareṣāṃ cātitṛṣṇayā ucchindan hyātmano mūlamāṭmānaṃ tāṃśca pīdayet

sanskrit_iast: tīkṣṇaścaiva mṛduśca syāt kāryaṃ vīkṣya mahīpatiḥ tīkṣṇaścaiva mṛduścaiva rāja bhavati sammataḥ
meaning: तीक्ष्णश्चैव मृदुश्च स्यात् कार्यं वीक्ष्य महीपतिः तीक्ष्णश्चैव मृदुश्चैव राज भवति सम्मतः
text: The King shall be severe and mild, after having duly examined the work (of each man); it is only the severe-mild King who is highly respected.
iast: tīkṣṇaścaiva mṛduśca syāt kāryaṃ vīkṣya mahīpatiḥ tīkṣṇaścaiva mṛduścaiva rāja bhavati sammataḥ

sanskrit_iast: amātyamukhyaṃ dharmajñaṃ prājñaṃ dāntaṃ kulaudgatam sthāpayedāsane tasmin khinnaḥ kāryaikṣaṇe nṛṇām
meaning: अमात्यमुख्यं धर्मज्ञं प्राज्ञं दान्तं कुलौद्गतम् स्थापयेदासने तस्मिन् खिन्नः कार्यैक्षणे नृणाम्
text: When tired with looking after the affairs of men, he shall place in that place his chief minister, who is conversant with the law, wise, self-controlled, and born of a noble family.
iast: amātyamukhyaṃ dharmajñaṃ prājñaṃ dāntaṃ kulaudgatam sthāpayedāsane tasmin khinnaḥ kāryaikṣaṇe nṛṇām

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sarvaṃ vidhāyaidamitikartavyamātmanaḥ yuktaścaivāpramattaśca parirakṣedimāḥ prajāḥ
meaning: एवं सर्वं विधायैदमितिकर्तव्यमात्मनः युक्तश्चैवाप्रमत्तश्च परिरक्षेदिमाः प्रजाः
text: Having thus arranged all his business, he shall protect his people, ever intent and watchful.
iast: evaṃ sarvaṃ vidhāyaidamitikartavyamātmanaḥ yuktaścaivāpramattaśca parirakṣedimāḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: vikrośantyo yasya rāṣṭrādhriyante dasyubhiḥ prajāḥ saṃpaśyataḥ sabhṛtyasya mṛtaḥ sa na tu jīvati
meaning: विक्रोशन्त्यो यस्य राष्ट्राध्रियन्ते दस्युभिः प्रजाः संपश्यतः सभृत्यस्य मृतः स न तु जीवति
text: He, from whose territories people are carried off, screaming, by robbers, while he himself, along with his servants is looking on, is dead, not alive.
iast: vikrośantyo yasya rāṣṭrādhriyante dasyubhiḥ prajāḥ saṃpaśyataḥ sabhṛtyasya mṛtaḥ sa na tu jīvati

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyasya paro dharmaḥ prājānāmeva pālanam nirdiṣṭaphalabhoktā hi rājā dharmeṇa yujyate
meaning: क्षत्रियस्य परो धर्मः प्राजानामेव पालनम् निर्दिष्टफलभोक्ता हि राजा धर्मेण युज्यते
text: The protection of the people is the Kṣatriya’s highest duty; the King, who enjoys the fruits mentioned, becomes endowed with merit.
iast: kṣatriyasya paro dharmaḥ prājānāmeva pālanam nirdiṣṭaphalabhoktā hi rājā dharmeṇa yujyate

sanskrit_iast: utthāya paścime yāme kṛtaśaucaḥ samāhitaḥ hutāgnirbrāhmaṇāṃścārcya praviśet sa śubhāṃ sabhām
meaning: उत्थाय पश्चिमे यामे कृतशौचः समाहितः हुताग्निर्ब्राह्मणांश्चार्च्य प्रविशेत् स शुभां सभाम्
text: Having risen during the last watch of the night and performed his ablutions, with collected mind, having poured libations into the Fire, and honoured the Brāhmaṇas, he shall enter the auspicious Hall of Audience.
iast: utthāya paścime yāme kṛtaśaucaḥ samāhitaḥ hutāgnirbrāhmaṇāṃścārcya praviśet sa śubhāṃ sabhām

sanskrit_iast: tatra sthitaḥ prajāḥ sarvāḥ pratinandya visarjayet visṛjya ca prajāḥ sarvā mantrayet saha mantribhiḥ
meaning: तत्र स्थितः प्रजाः सर्वाः प्रतिनन्द्य विसर्जयेत् विसृज्य च प्रजाः सर्वा मन्त्रयेत् सह मन्त्रिभिः
text: Staying there, he shall welcome all his subjects and then send them away. Having sent off all the people, he shall take counsel with his minister.
iast: tatra sthitaḥ prajāḥ sarvāḥ pratinandya visarjayet visṛjya ca prajāḥ sarvā mantrayet saha mantribhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: giripṛṣṭhaṃ samāruhya prasādaṃ vā rahogataḥ araṇye nisśalāke vā mantrayedavibhāvitaḥ
meaning: गिरिपृष्ठं समारुह्य प्रसादं वा रहोगतः अरण्ये निस्शलाके वा मन्त्रयेदविभावितः
text: Having ascended the top of a hill, or a house, and retiring into solitude,—or in a desolate forst he shall hold counsel, unobserved.
iast: giripṛṣṭhaṃ samāruhya prasādaṃ vā rahogataḥ araṇye nisśalāke vā mantrayedavibhāvitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasya mantraṃ na jānanti samāgamya pṛthagjanāḥ sa kṛtsnāṃ pṛthivīṃ bhuṅkte kośahīno 'pi pārthivaḥ
meaning: यस्य मन्त्रं न जानन्ति समागम्य पृथग्जनाः स कृत्स्नां पृथिवीं भुङ्क्ते कोशहीनो 'पि पार्थिवः
text: That King, whose secret plans other people, coming together, do not know, enjoys the whole earth, even though he be poor in treasure.
iast: yasya mantraṃ na jānanti samāgamya pṛthagjanāḥ sa kṛtsnāṃ pṛthivīṃ bhuṅkte kośahīno 'pi pārthivaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jaḍamūkāndhabadhirāṃstairyagyonān vayo 'tigān strīmlecchavyādhitavyaṅgān mantrakāle 'pasārayet
meaning: जडमूकान्धबधिरांस्तैर्यग्योनान् वयो 'तिगान् स्त्रीम्लेच्छव्याधितव्यङ्गान् मन्त्रकाले 'पसारयेत्
text: At the time of taking counsel, he shall send away the idiot, the dumb and the deaf, animals, very aged persons, women, foreigners, the sick and the maimed.
iast: jaḍamūkāndhabadhirāṃstairyagyonān vayo 'tigān strīmlecchavyādhitavyaṅgān mantrakāle 'pasārayet

sanskrit_iast: bhindantyavamatā mantraṃ tairyagyonāstathaiva ca striyaścaiva viśeṣeṇa tasmāt tatrādṛto bhavet
meaning: भिन्दन्त्यवमता मन्त्रं तैर्यग्योनास्तथैव च स्त्रियश्चैव विशेषेण तस्मात् तत्रादृतो भवेत्
text: Persons who have been disgraced, animals, and particularly women betray secret plans; hence he shall be careful with regard to them.
iast: bhindantyavamatā mantraṃ tairyagyonāstathaiva ca striyaścaiva viśeṣeṇa tasmāt tatrādṛto bhavet

sanskrit_iast: madhyaṃdine 'rdharātre vā viśrānto vigataklamaḥ cintayeddharmakāmārthān sārdhaṃ taireka eva vā sārthaṃ
meaning: मध्यंदिने 'र्धरात्रे वा विश्रान्तो विगतक्लमः चिन्तयेद्धर्मकामार्थान् सार्धं तैरेक एव वा सार्थं
text: At mid-day or at mid-night, free from fatigue and dulness, he shall deliberate on matters relating to morality, pleasure and wealth, either with them, or alone by himself;
iast: madhyaṃdine 'rdharātre vā viśrānto vigataklamaḥ cintayeddharmakāmārthān sārdhaṃ taireka eva vā sārthaṃ

sanskrit_iast: parasparaviruddhānāṃ teṣāṃ ca samupārjanam kanyānāṃ saṃpradānaṃ ca kumārāṇāṃ ca rakṣaṇam
meaning: परस्परविरुद्धानां तेषां च समुपार्जनम् कन्यानां संप्रदानं च कुमाराणां च रक्षणम्
text: —also on the attainment of these, mutually irreconcilable as they are, on the giving away of daughters and on the guardianship of sons;
iast: parasparaviruddhānāṃ teṣāṃ ca samupārjanam kanyānāṃ saṃpradānaṃ ca kumārāṇāṃ ca rakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: dūtasaṃpreṣaṇaṃ caiva kāryaśeṣaṃ tathaiva ca antaḥpurapracāraṃ ca praṇidhīnāṃ ca ceṣṭitam
meaning: दूतसंप्रेषणं चैव कार्यशेषं तथैव च अन्तःपुरप्रचारं च प्रणिधीनां च चेष्टितम्
text: —or the sending of ambassadors, the remaining details of undertakings, on the affairs of the harem, and on the work of spies;
iast: dūtasaṃpreṣaṇaṃ caiva kāryaśeṣaṃ tathaiva ca antaḥpurapracāraṃ ca praṇidhīnāṃ ca ceṣṭitam

sanskrit_iast: kṛtsnaṃ cāṣṭavidhaṃ karma pañcavargaṃ ca tattvataḥ anurāgāparāgau ca pracāraṃ maṇḍalasya ca
meaning: कृत्स्नं चाष्टविधं कर्म पञ्चवर्गं च तत्त्वतः अनुरागापरागौ च प्रचारं मण्डलस्य च
text: —also upon the entire ‘eight-fold business,’ and on the ‘five-fold group’ in its real character, on affection and disaffection, and on the conduct of his ‘circle’.
iast: kṛtsnaṃ cāṣṭavidhaṃ karma pañcavargaṃ ca tattvataḥ anurāgāparāgau ca pracāraṃ maṇḍalasya ca

sanskrit_iast: madhyamasya pracāraṃ ca vijīgiṣośca ceṣṭitam udāsīnapracāraṃ ca śatroścaiva prayatnataḥ
meaning: मध्यमस्य प्रचारं च विजीगिषोश्च चेष्टितम् उदासीनप्रचारं च शत्रोश्चैव प्रयत्नतः
text: —on the conduct of the ‘intermediary’ oh the doings of the king bent upon conquest, on the action of the neutral king, as also that of his enemy, with special care.
iast: madhyamasya pracāraṃ ca vijīgiṣośca ceṣṭitam udāsīnapracāraṃ ca śatroścaiva prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: etāḥ prakṛtayo mūlaṃ maṇḍalasya samāsataḥ aṣṭau cānyāḥ samākhyātā dvādaśaiva tu tāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: एताः प्रकृतयो मूलं मण्डलस्य समासतः अष्टौ चान्याः समाख्याता द्वादशैव तु ताः स्मृताः
text: These four components are, in brief, the root of the circle; eight others also have been described; these being the twelve that have been spoken op.
iast: etāḥ prakṛtayo mūlaṃ maṇḍalasya samāsataḥ aṣṭau cānyāḥ samākhyātā dvādaśaiva tu tāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: amātyarāṣṭradurgārthadaṇḍākhyāḥ pañca cāparāḥ pratyekaṃ kathitā hyetāḥ saṅkṣepeṇa dvisaptatiḥ
meaning: अमात्यराष्ट्रदुर्गार्थदण्डाख्याः पञ्च चापराः प्रत्येकं कथिता ह्येताः सङ्क्षेपेण द्विसप्ततिः
text: There are five others, (1) the minister, (2) the kingdom, (3) the fortress, (4) the treasury and (5) the army—described in connection with each (of the above twelve); these then, briefly, being seventy-two.
iast: amātyarāṣṭradurgārthadaṇḍākhyāḥ pañca cāparāḥ pratyekaṃ kathitā hyetāḥ saṅkṣepeṇa dvisaptatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: anantaramariṃ vidyādarisevinameva ca areranantaraṃ mitramudāsīnaṃ tayoḥ param
meaning: अनन्तरमरिं विद्यादरिसेविनमेव च अरेरनन्तरं मित्रमुदासीनं तयोः परम्
text: He shall regard, as ‘enemy’, his immediate neighbour, as also the person who helps his enemy; the immediate neighbour of his enemy he shall regard as his ‘friend’; and as ‘neutral’ the king who is beyond those two.
iast: anantaramariṃ vidyādarisevinameva ca areranantaraṃ mitramudāsīnaṃ tayoḥ param

sanskrit_iast: tān sarvānabhisaṃdadhyāt sāmādibhirupakramaiḥ vyastaiścaiva samastaiśca pauruṣeṇa nayena ca
meaning: तान् सर्वानभिसंदध्यात् सामादिभिरुपक्रमैः व्यस्तैश्चैव समस्तैश्च पौरुषेण नयेन च
text: All these he shall win over by means of conciliation and the other expedients, severally as well as collectively, as also by prowess and policy.
iast: tān sarvānabhisaṃdadhyāt sāmādibhirupakramaiḥ vyastaiścaiva samastaiśca pauruṣeṇa nayena ca

sanskrit_iast: saṃdhiṃ ca vigrahaṃ caiva yānamāsanameva ca dvaidhībhāvaṃ saṃśrayaṃ ca ṣaḍguṇāṃścintayet sadā
meaning: संधिं च विग्रहं चैव यानमासनमेव च द्वैधीभावं संश्रयं च षड्गुणांश्चिन्तयेत् सदा
text: Alliance, War, March, Halt, Bifurcation and seeking shelter—these six measures of policy he shall constantly ponder over.
iast: saṃdhiṃ ca vigrahaṃ caiva yānamāsanameva ca dvaidhībhāvaṃ saṃśrayaṃ ca ṣaḍguṇāṃścintayet sadā

sanskrit_iast: āsanaṃ caiva yānaṃ ca saṃdhiṃ vigrahameva ca kāryaṃ vīkṣya prayuñjīta dvaidhaṃ saṃśrayameva ca
meaning: आसनं चैव यानं च संधिं विग्रहमेव च कार्यं वीक्ष्य प्रयुञ्जीत द्वैधं संश्रयमेव च
text: He shall have recourse to Halting, to Marching, to Alliance, to War, to Bifurcation or to Seeking shelter, after having fully considered his business.
iast: āsanaṃ caiva yānaṃ ca saṃdhiṃ vigrahameva ca kāryaṃ vīkṣya prayuñjīta dvaidhaṃ saṃśrayameva ca

sanskrit_iast: saṃdhiṃ tu dvividhaṃ vidyādrājā vigrahameva ca ubhe yānāsane caiva dvividhaḥ saṃśrayaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: संधिं तु द्विविधं विद्याद्राजा विग्रहमेव च उभे यानासने चैव द्विविधः संश्रयः स्मृतः
text: Alliance, endowed with future possibilities, is of two kinds—(1) that in which the act of marching is undertaken in common and (2) that; in which it is otherwise.
iast: saṃdhiṃ tu dvividhaṃ vidyādrājā vigrahameva ca ubhe yānāsane caiva dvividhaḥ saṃśrayaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: samānayānakarmā ca viparītastathaiva ca tadā tvāyatisaṃyuktaḥ saṃdhirjñeyo dvilakṣaṇaḥ
meaning: समानयानकर्मा च विपरीतस्तथैव च तदा त्वायतिसंयुक्तः संधिर्ज्ञेयो द्विलक्षणः
text: Alliance, endowed with future possibilities, is of two kinds—(1) that in which the act of marching is undertaken in common and (2) that; in which it is otherwise.
iast: samānayānakarmā ca viparītastathaiva ca tadā tvāyatisaṃyuktaḥ saṃdhirjñeyo dvilakṣaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: svayaṅkṛtaśca kāryārthamakāle kāla eva vā mitrasya caivāpakṛte dvividho vigrahaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: स्वयङ्कृतश्च कार्यार्थमकाले काल एव वा मित्रस्य चैवापकृते द्विविधो विग्रहः स्मृतः
text: War has been declared to be of two kinds:—(1) that which is waged, in season or out of season, by oneself, for his own purpose, and (2) that which is waged on some wrong done to an ally.
iast: svayaṅkṛtaśca kāryārthamakāle kāla eva vā mitrasya caivāpakṛte dvividho vigrahaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ekākinaścātyayike kārye prāpte yadṛcchayā saṃhatasya ca mitreṇa dvividhaṃ yānamucyate
meaning: एकाकिनश्चात्ययिके कार्ये प्राप्ते यदृच्छया संहतस्य च मित्रेण द्विविधं यानमुच्यते
text: Marching is said to be of two kinds—(1) that undertaken by the king by himself alone, on the sudden approach of an emergent occasion, and (2) that undertaken by him accompanied by his ally.
iast: ekākinaścātyayike kārye prāpte yadṛcchayā saṃhatasya ca mitreṇa dvividhaṃ yānamucyate

sanskrit_iast: kṣīṇasya caiva kramaśo daivāt pūrvakṛtena vā mitrasya cānurodhena dvividhaṃ smṛtamāsanam
meaning: क्षीणस्य चैव क्रमशो दैवात् पूर्वकृतेन वा मित्रस्य चानुरोधेन द्विविधं स्मृतमासनम्
text: Halting has been declared to be of two kinds:—(1) that which is necessary for one who has become gradually weakened, either by chance or through previous acts, and (2) that which is necessitated by considerations for his ally.
iast: kṣīṇasya caiva kramaśo daivāt pūrvakṛtena vā mitrasya cānurodhena dvividhaṃ smṛtamāsanam

sanskrit_iast: balasya svāminaścaiva sthitiḥ kāryārthasiddhaye dvividhaṃ kīrtyate dvaidhaṃ ṣāḍguṇyaguṇavedibhiḥ
meaning: बलस्य स्वामिनश्चैव स्थितिः कार्यार्थसिद्धये द्विविधं कीर्त्यते द्वैधं षाड्गुण्यगुणवेदिभिः
text: When, for the accomplishment of some purpose, the Master takes up one position and the Force another,—this is what is described as ‘Bifurcation’ by those conversant with the details of the six measures of policy.
iast: balasya svāminaścaiva sthitiḥ kāryārthasiddhaye dvividhaṃ kīrtyate dvaidhaṃ ṣāḍguṇyaguṇavedibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: arthasaṃpādanārthaṃ ca pīḍyamānasya śatrubhiḥ sādhuṣu vyapadeśaśca dvividhaḥ saṃśrayaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: अर्थसंपादनार्थं च पीड्यमानस्य शत्रुभिः साधुषु व्यपदेशश्च द्विविधः संश्रयः स्मृतः
text: ‘Seeking shelter’, with noble people, has been declared to be of two kinds:—(1) that which is done for the sake of accomplishing a useful purpose, when harassed by enemies, and (2) that in the form of a status.
iast: arthasaṃpādanārthaṃ ca pīḍyamānasya śatrubhiḥ sādhuṣu vyapadeśaśca dvividhaḥ saṃśrayaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadā 'vagacchedāyatyāmādhikyaṃ dhruvamātmanaḥ tadātve cālpikāṃ pīḍāṃ tadā saṃdhiṃ samāśrayet
meaning: यदा 'वगच्छेदायत्यामाधिक्यं ध्रुवमात्मनः तदात्वे चाल्पिकां पीडां तदा संधिं समाश्रयेत्
text: When he knows that his superiority is certain in the future, and at the time there is but little harm done—then he should resort to peace.
iast: yadā 'vagacchedāyatyāmādhikyaṃ dhruvamātmanaḥ tadātve cālpikāṃ pīḍāṃ tadā saṃdhiṃ samāśrayet

sanskrit_iast: yadā prahṛṣṭā manyeta sarvāstu prakṛtīrbhṛśam atyucchritaṃ tathātmānaṃ tadā kurvīta vigraham
meaning: यदा प्रहृष्टा मन्येत सर्वास्तु प्रकृतीर्भृशम् अत्युच्छ्रितं तथात्मानं तदा कुर्वीत विग्रहम्
text: But when he thinks all his people to be highly contented, and himself to be exceedingly prosperous,—then he shall make war.
iast: yadā prahṛṣṭā manyeta sarvāstu prakṛtīrbhṛśam atyucchritaṃ tathātmānaṃ tadā kurvīta vigraham

sanskrit_iast: yadā manyeta bhāvena hṛṣṭaṃ puṣṭaṃ balaṃ svakam parasya viparītaṃ ca tadā yāyādripuṃ prati
meaning: यदा मन्येत भावेन हृष्टं पुष्टं बलं स्वकम् परस्य विपरीतं च तदा यायाद्रिपुं प्रति
text: When he thinks that his own army is happy and strong in condition, and that of the enemy is the reverse, then shall he march against the enemy
iast: yadā manyeta bhāvena hṛṣṭaṃ puṣṭaṃ balaṃ svakam parasya viparītaṃ ca tadā yāyādripuṃ prati

sanskrit_iast: yadā tu syāt parikṣīṇo vāhanena balena ca tadāsīta prayatnena śanakaiḥ sāntvayannarīn
meaning: यदा तु स्यात् परिक्षीणो वाहनेन बलेन च तदासीत प्रयत्नेन शनकैः सान्त्वयन्नरीन्
text: But when he happens to be weak in conveyances and soldiers, then he shall sit quiet, gradually conciliating his enemies with special care.
iast: yadā tu syāt parikṣīṇo vāhanena balena ca tadāsīta prayatnena śanakaiḥ sāntvayannarīn

sanskrit_iast: manyetāriṃ yadā rājā sarvathā balavattaram tadā dvidhā balaṃ kṛtvā sādhayet kāryamātmanaḥ
meaning: मन्येतारिं यदा राजा सर्वथा बलवत्तरम् तदा द्विधा बलं कृत्वा साधयेत् कार्यमात्मनः
text: When the king thinks his enemy to be stronger in every respect, then he should bifurcate his force and thus accomplish his own purpose.
iast: manyetāriṃ yadā rājā sarvathā balavattaram tadā dvidhā balaṃ kṛtvā sādhayet kāryamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadā parabalānāṃ tu gamanīyatamo bhavet tadā tu saṃśrayet kṣipraṃ dhārmikaṃ balinaṃ nṛpam
