meaning: यदा परबलानां तु गमनीयतमो भवेत् तदा तु संश्रयेत् क्षिप्रं धार्मिकं बलिनं नृपम्
text: When he happens to be very much open to attack by the enemy’s forces, then he shall seek shelter with a r ighte ous and powerful King.
iast: yadā parabalānāṃ tu gamanīyatamo bhavet tadā tu saṃśrayet kṣipraṃ dhārmikaṃ balinaṃ nṛpam

sanskrit_iast: nigrahaṃ prakṛtīnāṃ ca kuryādyo 'ribalasya ca upaseveta taṃ nityaṃ sarvayatnairguruṃ yathā
meaning: निग्रहं प्रकृतीनां च कुर्याद्यो 'रिबलस्य च उपसेवेत तं नित्यं सर्वयत्नैर्गुरुं यथा
text: That king who does the chastisement of his people as also of his enemy’s army,—him he shall ever serve with every effort, like a preceptor.
iast: nigrahaṃ prakṛtīnāṃ ca kuryādyo 'ribalasya ca upaseveta taṃ nityaṃ sarvayatnairguruṃ yathā

sanskrit_iast: yadi tatrāpi saṃpaśyeddoṣaṃ saṃśrayakāritam suyuddhameva tatrāpi nirviśaṅkaḥ samācaret
meaning: यदि तत्रापि संपश्येद्दोषं संश्रयकारितम् सुयुद्धमेव तत्रापि निर्विशङ्कः समाचरेत्
text: If even there he should perceive something wrong on the part of his shelterer, then, even in that condition he shall, without hesitation, resort to war.
iast: yadi tatrāpi saṃpaśyeddoṣaṃ saṃśrayakāritam suyuddhameva tatrāpi nirviśaṅkaḥ samācaret

sanskrit_iast: sarvaupāyaistathā kuryānnītijñaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ yathā 'syābhyadhikā na syurmitraudāsīnaśatravaḥ
meaning: सर्वौपायैस्तथा कुर्यान्नीतिज्ञः पृथिवीपतिः यथा 'स्याभ्यधिका न स्युर्मित्रौदासीनशत्रवः
text: By means of all the expedients, the politic king shall act in such a manner that his allies, neutrals and enemies do not become superior to himself.
iast: sarvaupāyaistathā kuryānnītijñaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ yathā 'syābhyadhikā na syurmitraudāsīnaśatravaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āyatiṃ sarvakāryāṇāṃ tadātvaṃ ca vicārayet atītānāṃ ca sarveṣāṃ guṇadoṣau ca tattvataḥ
meaning: आयतिं सर्वकार्याणां तदात्वं च विचारयेत् अतीतानां च सर्वेषां गुणदोषौ च तत्त्वतः
text: He shall fully think over the future and the present condition of all undertakings, as also the good and bad points of all past ones.
iast: āyatiṃ sarvakāryāṇāṃ tadātvaṃ ca vicārayet atītānāṃ ca sarveṣāṃ guṇadoṣau ca tattvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: āyatyāṃ guṇadoṣajñastadātve kṣipraniścayaḥ atīte kāryaśeṣajñaḥ śatrubhirnābhibhūyate
meaning: आयत्यां गुणदोषज्ञस्तदात्वे क्षिप्रनिश्चयः अतीते कार्यशेषज्ञः शत्रुभिर्नाभिभूयते
text: He, who is alive to the good and bad points in re gard to the future, is quick in his decisions relating to the present, and understands the consequences of his acts in the past, is never overpowered by his enemies.
iast: āyatyāṃ guṇadoṣajñastadātve kṣipraniścayaḥ atīte kāryaśeṣajñaḥ śatrubhirnābhibhūyate

sanskrit_iast: yathainaṃ nābhisaṃdadhyurmitraudāsīnaśatravaḥ tathā sarvaṃ saṃvidadhyādeṣa sāmāsiko nayaḥ
meaning: यथैनं नाभिसंदध्युर्मित्रौदासीनशत्रवः तथा सर्वं संविदध्यादेष सामासिको नयः
text: He shall arrange everything in such a manner that his allies or neutrals or enemies may not get the better of him; this is the sum-total of state-policy.
iast: yathainaṃ nābhisaṃdadhyurmitraudāsīnaśatravaḥ tathā sarvaṃ saṃvidadhyādeṣa sāmāsiko nayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tadā tu yānamātiṣṭhedarirāṣṭraṃ prati prabhuḥ tadānena vidhānena yāyādaripuraṃ śanaiḥ
meaning: तदा तु यानमातिष्ठेदरिराष्ट्रं प्रति प्रभुः तदानेन विधानेन यायादरिपुरं शनैः
text: When the king undertakes an expedition against the enemy’s kingdom, he shall advance slowly towards the enemy’s capital, in the following manner.
iast: tadā tu yānamātiṣṭhedarirāṣṭraṃ prati prabhuḥ tadānena vidhānena yāyādaripuraṃ śanaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: mārgaśīrṣe śubhe māsi yāyādyātrāṃ mahīpatiḥ phālgunaṃ vātha caitraṃ vā māsau prati yathābalam
meaning: मार्गशीर्षे शुभे मासि यायाद्यात्रां महीपतिः फाल्गुनं वाथ चैत्रं वा मासौ प्रति यथाबलम्
text: The king shall start on his expedition in the auspicious month of Mārgaśīrṣa, or towards the months of Phalguna and Caitra, according to the condition of his forces.
iast: mārgaśīrṣe śubhe māsi yāyādyātrāṃ mahīpatiḥ phālgunaṃ vātha caitraṃ vā māsau prati yathābalam

sanskrit_iast: anyeṣvapi tu kāleṣu yadā paśyeddhruvaṃ jayam tadā yāyādvigṛhyaiva vyasane cautthite ripoḥ
meaning: अन्येष्वपि तु कालेषु यदा पश्येद्ध्रुवं जयम् तदा यायाद्विगृह्यैव व्यसने चौत्थिते रिपोः
text: At other times also, if he perceives certain victory, then he shall pick up a quarrel and march forward; also when some trouble has arisen for the enemy.
iast: anyeṣvapi tu kāleṣu yadā paśyeddhruvaṃ jayam tadā yāyādvigṛhyaiva vyasane cautthite ripoḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṛtvā vidhānaṃ mūle tu yātrikaṃ ca yathāvidhi upagṛhyāspadaṃ caiva cārān samyag vidhāya ca
meaning: कृत्वा विधानं मूले तु यात्रिकं च यथाविधि उपगृह्यास्पदं चैव चारान् सम्यग् विधाय च
text: Having duly made arrangements at the base, as also those pertaining to the expedition, having secured a basis, and having duly deputed his spies,—having cleared the three kinds of roads, and having equipped his own six-fold force,—he shall advance against the enemy’s capital in the manner prescribed for warfare.
iast: kṛtvā vidhānaṃ mūle tu yātrikaṃ ca yathāvidhi upagṛhyāspadaṃ caiva cārān samyag vidhāya ca

sanskrit_iast: saṃśodhya trividhaṃ mārgaṃ ṣaḍvidhaṃ ca balaṃ svakam sāṃparāyikakalpena yāyādaripuraṃ prati
meaning: संशोध्य त्रिविधं मार्गं षड्विधं च बलं स्वकम् सांपरायिककल्पेन यायादरिपुरं प्रति
text: Having duly made arrangements at the base, as also those pertaining to the expedition, having secured a basis, and having duly deputed his spies,—having cleared the three kinds of roads, and having equipped his own six-fold force,—he shall advance against the enemy’s capital in the manner prescribed for warfare.
iast: saṃśodhya trividhaṃ mārgaṃ ṣaḍvidhaṃ ca balaṃ svakam sāṃparāyikakalpena yāyādaripuraṃ prati

sanskrit_iast: śatrusevini mitre ca gūḍhe yuktataro bhavet gatapratyāgate caiva sa hi kaṣṭataro ripuḥ
meaning: शत्रुसेविनि मित्रे च गूढे युक्ततरो भवेत् गतप्रत्यागते चैव स हि कष्टतरो रिपुः
text: He shall be very much on his guard against an ally who may be secretly serving the enemy, as also against one who has gone away and returned; as he is the more dangerous enemy.—(1 86)
iast: śatrusevini mitre ca gūḍhe yuktataro bhavet gatapratyāgate caiva sa hi kaṣṭataro ripuḥ

sanskrit_iast: daṇḍavyūhena tan mārgaṃ yāyāt tu śakaṭena vā varāhamakarābhyāṃ vā sūcyā vā garuḍena vā
meaning: दण्डव्यूहेन तन् मार्गं यायात् तु शकटेन वा वराहमकराभ्यां वा सूच्या वा गरुडेन वा
text: He shall march on this road arraying his army in the form of a staff, or in that of a cart, or a boar, or an alligator, or a needle or the Garuḍa-bird.
iast: daṇḍavyūhena tan mārgaṃ yāyāt tu śakaṭena vā varāhamakarābhyāṃ vā sūcyā vā garuḍena vā

sanskrit_iast: yataśca bhayamāśaṅket tato vistārayedbalam padmena caiva vyūhena niviśeta sadā svayam
meaning: यतश्च भयमाशङ्केत् ततो विस्तारयेद्बलम् पद्मेन चैव व्यूहेन निविशेत सदा स्वयम्
text: From where he apprehends danger there he shall extend his forces; and he himself shall always encamp in the ‘lotus-array’
iast: yataśca bhayamāśaṅket tato vistārayedbalam padmena caiva vyūhena niviśeta sadā svayam

sanskrit_iast: senāpatibalādhyakṣau sarvadikṣu niveśayet yataśca bhayamāśaṅket prācīṃ tāṃ kalpayeddiśam
meaning: सेनापतिबलाध्यक्षौ सर्वदिक्षु निवेशयेत् यतश्च भयमाशङ्केत् प्राचीं तां कल्पयेद्दिशम्
text: The Commander-in-Chief and the General he shall station in all directions; the quarter from which he apprehends danger, that he shall regard as the ‘East’ (Front).
iast: senāpatibalādhyakṣau sarvadikṣu niveśayet yataśca bhayamāśaṅket prācīṃ tāṃ kalpayeddiśam

sanskrit_iast: gulmāṃśca sthāpayedāptān kṛtasaṃjñān samantataḥ sthāne yuddhe ca kuśalānabhīrūnavikāriṇaḥ
meaning: गुल्मांश्च स्थापयेदाप्तान् कृतसंज्ञान् समन्ततः स्थाने युद्धे च कुशलानभीरूनविकारिणः
text: On all sides he shall station reliable pickets, with whom signals have been arranged, who are experts in standing firm as also in charging, fearless and loyal.
iast: gulmāṃśca sthāpayedāptān kṛtasaṃjñān samantataḥ sthāne yuddhe ca kuśalānabhīrūnavikāriṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃhatān yodhayedalpān kāmaṃ vistārayedbahūn sūcyā vajreṇa caivaitān vyūhena vyūhya yodhayet
meaning: संहतान् योधयेदल्पान् कामं विस्तारयेद्बहून् सूच्या वज्रेण चैवैतान् व्यूहेन व्यूह्य योधयेत्
text: He shall make a small number of men fight in close formation; but a large number he may extend as he likes. He shall make them fight, arraying them in the form of the ‘needle’ and the ‘thunderbolt.’
iast: saṃhatān yodhayedalpān kāmaṃ vistārayedbahūn sūcyā vajreṇa caivaitān vyūhena vyūhya yodhayet

sanskrit_iast: syandanāśvaiḥ same yudhyedanūpe nau dvipaistathā vṛkṣagulmāvṛte cāpairasicarmāyudhaiḥ sthale
meaning: स्यन्दनाश्वैः समे युध्येदनूपे नौ द्विपैस्तथा वृक्षगुल्मावृते चापैरसिचर्मायुधैः स्थले
text: On even ground he shall fight with chariots and horses; on marshy ground with boats and elephants; on ground covered with trees and thickets with bows; and on firm ground with swords and shields and other weapons.
iast: syandanāśvaiḥ same yudhyedanūpe nau dvipaistathā vṛkṣagulmāvṛte cāpairasicarmāyudhaiḥ sthale

sanskrit_iast: kurukṣetrāṃśca matsyāṃśca pañcālāṃśūrasenajān :kaurakṣetrāṃśca dīrghāṃllaghūṃścaiva narānagrānīkeṣu yojayet
meaning: कुरुक्षेत्रांश्च मत्स्यांश्च पञ्चालांशूरसेनजान् :कौरक्षेत्रांश्च दीर्घांल्लघूंश्चैव नरानग्रानीकेषु योजयेत्
text: Men born in the countries of Kurukṣhtra, Matsya, Pañcāla, and Śūrasena.—he shall make these fight in the vanguard; as also those that are tall and light.
iast: kurukṣetrāṃśca matsyāṃśca pañcālāṃśūrasenajān :kaurakṣetrāṃśca dīrghāṃllaghūṃścaiva narānagrānīkeṣu yojayet

sanskrit_iast: praharṣayedbalaṃ vyūhya tāṃśca samyak parīkṣayet bhṛśaṃ parīkṣayet ceṣṭāścaiva vijānīyādarīn yodhayatāmapi
meaning: प्रहर्षयेद्बलं व्यूह्य तांश्च सम्यक् परीक्षयेत् भृशं परीक्षयेत् चेष्टाश्चैव विजानीयादरीन् योधयतामपि
text: Having arrayed his forces, he shall encourage them and thoroughly test them; even while they are engaging the enemy, he shall mark their behaviour.
iast: praharṣayedbalaṃ vyūhya tāṃśca samyak parīkṣayet bhṛśaṃ parīkṣayet ceṣṭāścaiva vijānīyādarīn yodhayatāmapi

sanskrit_iast: uparudhyārimāsīta rāṣṭraṃ cāsyaupapīḍayet dūṣayeccāsya satataṃ yavasānnaudakaindhanam
meaning: उपरुध्यारिमासीत राष्ट्रं चास्यौपपीडयेत् दूषयेच्चास्य सततं यवसान्नौदकैन्धनम्
text: After having besieged the foe, he shall halt, and proceed to harass his kingdom and continually vitiate his supply of fodder, food, water and fuel.
iast: uparudhyārimāsīta rāṣṭraṃ cāsyaupapīḍayet dūṣayeccāsya satataṃ yavasānnaudakaindhanam

sanskrit_iast: bhindyāccaiva taḍāgāni prākāraparikhāstathā samavaskandayeccainaṃ rātrau vitrāsayet tathā
meaning: भिन्द्याच्चैव तडागानि प्राकारपरिखास्तथा समवस्कन्दयेच्चैनं रात्रौ वित्रासयेत् तथा
text: He shall destroy the tanks, as also walls and ditches; he shall assail the enemy and shall frighten him—during the night.
iast: bhindyāccaiva taḍāgāni prākāraparikhāstathā samavaskandayeccainaṃ rātrau vitrāsayet tathā

sanskrit_iast: upajapyānupajapedbudhyetaiva ca tatkṛtam yukte ca daive yudhyeta jayaprepsurapetabhīḥ
meaning: उपजप्यानुपजपेद्बुध्येतैव च तत्कृतम् युक्ते च दैवे युध्येत जयप्रेप्सुरपेतभीः
text: He shall alienate all who are alienable, keep himself informed of the enemy’s doings, and when fate is propitious, he shall fight, devoid of fear and determined to co nquer.
iast: upajapyānupajapedbudhyetaiva ca tatkṛtam yukte ca daive yudhyeta jayaprepsurapetabhīḥ

sanskrit_iast: sāmnā dānena bhedena samastairatha vā pṛthak vijetuṃ prayatetārīnna yuddhena kadā cana ariṃ
meaning: साम्ना दानेन भेदेन समस्तैरथ वा पृथक् विजेतुं प्रयतेतारीन्न युद्धेन कदा चन अरिं
text: By conciliation, by gifts and by dissension,—either severally or collectively,—he shall try to conquer his enemy,—never by war.
iast: sāmnā dānena bhedena samastairatha vā pṛthak vijetuṃ prayatetārīnna yuddhena kadā cana ariṃ

sanskrit_iast: anityo vijayo yasmāddṛśyate yudhyamānayoḥ parājayaśca saṅgrāme tasmādyuddhaṃ vivarjayet
meaning: अनित्यो विजयो यस्माद्दृश्यते युध्यमानयोः पराजयश्च सङ्ग्रामे तस्माद्युद्धं विवर्जयेत्
text: Since between two combatants victory is found to be uncertain, as also defeat,—therefore he shall avoid fighting
iast: anityo vijayo yasmāddṛśyate yudhyamānayoḥ parājayaśca saṅgrāme tasmādyuddhaṃ vivarjayet

sanskrit_iast: trayāṇāmapyupāyānāṃ pūrvauktānāmasaṃbhave tathā yudhyeta saṃpanno vijayeta ripūn yathā
meaning: त्रयाणामप्युपायानां पूर्वौक्तानामसंभवे तथा युध्येत संपन्नो विजयेत रिपून् यथा
text: But in the event of the three aforesaid expedients failing, he shall fight in such a manner as to conquer his enemies completely.
iast: trayāṇāmapyupāyānāṃ pūrvauktānāmasaṃbhave tathā yudhyeta saṃpanno vijayeta ripūn yathā

sanskrit_iast: jitvā saṃpūjayeddevān brāhmaṇāṃścaiva dhārmikān pradadyāt parihārārthaṃ khyāpayedabhayāni ca
meaning: जित्वा संपूजयेद्देवान् ब्राह्मणांश्चैव धार्मिकान् प्रदद्यात् परिहारार्थं ख्यापयेदभयानि च
text: Having gained victory, he shall worship the gods and the righteous Brāhmaṇas, grant remissions and proclaim amnesties.
iast: jitvā saṃpūjayeddevān brāhmaṇāṃścaiva dhārmikān pradadyāt parihārārthaṃ khyāpayedabhayāni ca

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāṃ tu viditvaiṣāṃ samāsena cikīrṣitam sthāpayet tatra tadvaṃśyaṃ kuryācca samayakriyām
meaning: सर्वेषां तु विदित्वैषां समासेन चिकीर्षितम् स्थापयेत् तत्र तद्वंश्यं कुर्याच्च समयक्रियाम्
text: Having briefly ascertained the wishes of all the people, he shall set up there a member of the same family and then conclude the treaty.
iast: sarveṣāṃ tu viditvaiṣāṃ samāsena cikīrṣitam sthāpayet tatra tadvaṃśyaṃ kuryācca samayakriyām

sanskrit_iast: pramāṇāni ca kurvīta teṣāṃ dharmān yathauditān ratnaiśca pūjayedenaṃ pradhānapuruṣaiḥ saha
meaning: प्रमाणानि च कुर्वीत तेषां धर्मान् यथौदितान् रत्नैश्च पूजयेदेनं प्रधानपुरुषैः सह
text: He shall make authoritative all that is declared to have been lawful (in the kingdom), and shall honor with precious gifts the king along with the leading men.
iast: pramāṇāni ca kurvīta teṣāṃ dharmān yathauditān ratnaiśca pūjayedenaṃ pradhānapuruṣaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: ādānamapriyakaraṃ dānaṃ ca priyakārakam abhīpsitānāmarthānāṃ kāle yuktam kālayuktaṃ praśasyate
meaning: आदानमप्रियकरं दानं च प्रियकारकम् अभीप्सितानामर्थानां काले युक्तम् कालयुक्तं प्रशस्यते
text: The seizing of desirable property is productive of displeasure, and the giving of it is productive of pleasure; each is commended if done at the proper time.
iast: ādānamapriyakaraṃ dānaṃ ca priyakārakam abhīpsitānāmarthānāṃ kāle yuktam kālayuktaṃ praśasyate

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ karmaidamāyattaṃ vidhāne daivamānuṣe tayordaivamacintyaṃ tu mānuṣe vidyate kriyā [daivena vidhinā yuktaṃ mānuṣyaṃ yat pravartate ] yuktaṃ [parikleśena mahatā tadarthasya samādhakam ] [saṃyuktasyāpi daivena puruṣakāreṇa varjitam ] [vinā puruṣakāreṇa phalaṃ kṣetraṃ prayacchati ] [candrārkādyā grahā vāyuragnirāpastathaiva ca ] [iha daivena sādhyante pauruṣeṇa prayatnataḥ ]
meaning: सर्वं कर्मैदमायत्तं विधाने दैवमानुषे तयोर्दैवमचिन्त्यं तु मानुषे विद्यते क्रिया [दैवेन विधिना युक्तं मानुष्यं यत् प्रवर्तते ] युक्तं [परिक्लेशेन महता तदर्थस्य समाधकम् ] [संयुक्तस्यापि दैवेन पुरुषकारेण वर्जितम् ] [विना पुरुषकारेण फलं क्षेत्रं प्रयच्छति ] [चन्द्रार्काद्या ग्रहा वायुरग्निरापस्तथैव च ] [इह दैवेन साध्यन्ते पौरुषेण प्रयत्नतः ]
text: All this undertaking is dependent upon the ordering of Destiny and of Human Exertion; of these two, Destiny is incomprehensible, and action is possible only in regard to Human Exertion.
iast: sarvaṃ karmaidamāyattaṃ vidhāne daivamānuṣe tayordaivamacintyaṃ tu mānuṣe vidyate kriyā [daivena vidhinā yuktaṃ mānuṣyaṃ yat pravartate ] yuktaṃ [parikleśena mahatā tadarthasya samādhakam ] [saṃyuktasyāpi daivena puruṣakāreṇa varjitam ] [vinā puruṣakāreṇa phalaṃ kṣetraṃ prayacchati ] [candrārkādyā grahā vāyuragnirāpastathaiva ca ] [iha daivena sādhyante pauruṣeṇa prayatnataḥ ]

sanskrit_iast: saha vā 'pi vrajedyuktaḥ saṃdhiṃ kṛtvā prayatnataḥ mitraṃ hiraṇyaṃ bhūmiṃ vā saṃpaśyaṃstrividhaṃ phalam
meaning: सह वा 'पि व्रजेद्युक्तः संधिं कृत्वा प्रयत्नतः मित्रं हिरण्यं भूमिं वा संपश्यंस्त्रिविधं फलम्
text: Gentlemanliness, knowledge of men, bravery, compassionate disposition, and constant liberality are the qualities to be sought for in a Neutral.
iast: saha vā 'pi vrajedyuktaḥ saṃdhiṃ kṛtvā prayatnataḥ mitraṃ hiraṇyaṃ bhūmiṃ vā saṃpaśyaṃstrividhaṃ phalam

sanskrit_iast: pārṣṇigrāhaṃ ca saṃprekṣya tathākrandaṃ ca maṇḍale mitrādathāpyamitrādvā yātrāphalamavāpnuyāt
meaning: पार्ष्णिग्राहं च संप्रेक्ष्य तथाक्रन्दं च मण्डले मित्रादथाप्यमित्राद्वा यात्राफलमवाप्नुयात्
text: Gentlemanliness, knowledge of men, bravery, compassionate disposition, and constant liberality are the qualities to be sought for in a Neutral.
iast: pārṣṇigrāhaṃ ca saṃprekṣya tathākrandaṃ ca maṇḍale mitrādathāpyamitrādvā yātrāphalamavāpnuyāt

sanskrit_iast: hiraṇyabhūmisaṃprāptyā pārthivo na tathaidhate yathā mitraṃ dhruvaṃ labdhvā kṛśamapyāyatikṣamam
meaning: हिरण्यभूमिसंप्राप्त्या पार्थिवो न तथैधते यथा मित्रं ध्रुवं लब्ध्वा कृशमप्यायतिक्षमम्
text: Gentlemanliness, knowledge of men, bravery, compassionate disposition, and constant liberality are the qualities to be sought for in a Neutral.
iast: hiraṇyabhūmisaṃprāptyā pārthivo na tathaidhate yathā mitraṃ dhruvaṃ labdhvā kṛśamapyāyatikṣamam

sanskrit_iast: dharmajñaṃ ca kṛtajñaṃ ca tuṣṭaprakṛtimeva ca anuraktaṃ sthirārambhaṃ laghumitraṃ praśasyate
meaning: धर्मज्ञं च कृतज्ञं च तुष्टप्रकृतिमेव च अनुरक्तं स्थिरारम्भं लघुमित्रं प्रशस्यते
text: Gentlemanliness, knowledge of men, bravery, compassionate disposition, and constant liberality are the qualities to be sought for in a Neutral.
iast: dharmajñaṃ ca kṛtajñaṃ ca tuṣṭaprakṛtimeva ca anuraktaṃ sthirārambhaṃ laghumitraṃ praśasyate

sanskrit_iast: prājñaṃ kulīnaṃ śūraṃ ca dakṣaṃ dātārameva ca kṛtajñaṃ dhṛtimantaṃ ca kaṣṭamāhurariṃ budhāḥ
meaning: प्राज्ञं कुलीनं शूरं च दक्षं दातारमेव च कृतज्ञं धृतिमन्तं च कष्टमाहुररिं बुधाः
text: Gentlemanliness, knowledge of men, bravery, compassionate disposition, and constant liberality are the qualities to be sought for in a Neutral.
iast: prājñaṃ kulīnaṃ śūraṃ ca dakṣaṃ dātārameva ca kṛtajñaṃ dhṛtimantaṃ ca kaṣṭamāhurariṃ budhāḥ

sanskrit_iast: āryatā puruṣajñānaṃ śauryaṃ karuṇaveditā sthaulalakṣyaṃ ca satatamudāsīnaguṇaudayaḥ
meaning: आर्यता पुरुषज्ञानं शौर्यं करुणवेदिता स्थौललक्ष्यं च सततमुदासीनगुणौदयः
text: Gentlemanliness, knowledge of men, bravery, compassionate disposition, and constant liberality are the qualities to be sought for in a Neutral.
iast: āryatā puruṣajñānaṃ śauryaṃ karuṇaveditā sthaulalakṣyaṃ ca satatamudāsīnaguṇaudayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ksemyāṃ sasyapradāṃ nityaṃ paśuvṛddhikarīmapi parityajennṛpo bhūmimātmārthamavicārayan
meaning: क्सेम्यां सस्यप्रदां नित्यं पशुवृद्धिकरीमपि परित्यजेन्नृपो भूमिमात्मार्थमविचारयन्
text: Even though the land (occupied by him) be safe, fertile and conducive to the increase of cattle, yet he shall quit it,—not minding his own selfish interests.
iast: ksemyāṃ sasyapradāṃ nityaṃ paśuvṛddhikarīmapi parityajennṛpo bhūmimātmārthamavicārayan

sanskrit_iast: āpadarthaṃ dhanaṃ rakṣeddārān rakṣeddhanairapi āpadarthe ātmānaṃ satataṃ rakṣeddārairapi dhanairapi
meaning: आपदर्थं धनं रक्षेद्दारान् रक्षेद्धनैरपि आपदर्थे आत्मानं सततं रक्षेद्दारैरपि धनैरपि
text: He shall save his wealth for the sake of trouble; his wife he shall protect even with his wealth; and himself he shall constantly protect, even with his wife and his wealth.
iast: āpadarthaṃ dhanaṃ rakṣeddārān rakṣeddhanairapi āpadarthe ātmānaṃ satataṃ rakṣeddārairapi dhanairapi

sanskrit_iast: saha sarvāḥ samutpannāḥ prasamīkṣyāpado bhṛśam saṃyuktāṃśca viyuktāṃśca sarvaupāyān sṛjedbudhaḥ
meaning: सह सर्वाः समुत्पन्नाः प्रसमीक्ष्यापदो भृशम् संयुक्तांश्च वियुक्तांश्च सर्वौपायान् सृजेद्बुधः
text: Seeing all kinds of troubles frequently cropping up simutaneously, the wise person shall employ all the expedients, collectively as well as severally.
iast: saha sarvāḥ samutpannāḥ prasamīkṣyāpado bhṛśam saṃyuktāṃśca viyuktāṃśca sarvaupāyān sṛjedbudhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: upetāramupeyaṃ ca sarvaupāyāṃśca kṛtsnaśaḥ etat trayaṃ samāśritya prayatetārthasiddhaye
meaning: उपेतारमुपेयं च सर्वौपायांश्च कृत्स्नशः एतत् त्रयं समाश्रित्य प्रयतेतार्थसिद्धये
text: (a) The employer of the expedients, (b) the end to be attained by the expedients and (c) the expedients themselves,—taking his stand upon all these three, he shall strive for the accomplishment of his purpose.
iast: upetāramupeyaṃ ca sarvaupāyāṃśca kṛtsnaśaḥ etat trayaṃ samāśritya prayatetārthasiddhaye

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sarvamidaṃ rājā saha sammantrya mantribhiḥ vyāyamyāplutya madhyāhne bhoktumantaḥpuraṃ viśet
meaning: एवं सर्वमिदं राजा सह सम्मन्त्र्य मन्त्रिभिः व्यायम्याप्लुत्य मध्याह्ने भोक्तुमन्तःपुरं विशेत्
text: Having thus discussed all this with his ministers, the king shall take exercise at midday; and having bathed, shall enter the inner apartment for the purpose of taking his food.
iast: evaṃ sarvamidaṃ rājā saha sammantrya mantribhiḥ vyāyamyāplutya madhyāhne bhoktumantaḥpuraṃ viśet

sanskrit_iast: tatrātmabhūtaiḥ kālajñairahāryaiḥ paricārakaiḥ suparīkṣitamannādyamadyān mantrairviṣāpahaiḥ
meaning: तत्रात्मभूतैः कालज्ञैरहार्यैः परिचारकैः सुपरीक्षितमन्नाद्यमद्यान् मन्त्रैर्विषापहैः
text: There he shall eat the food that has been thoroughly tested by such servants as are his own very self, as are conversant with the peculiarities of time, and are uncorruptible,—with such sacred texts as are destructive of poison,
iast: tatrātmabhūtaiḥ kālajñairahāryaiḥ paricārakaiḥ suparīkṣitamannādyamadyān mantrairviṣāpahaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: viṣaghnairagadaiścāsya sarvadravyāṇi yojayet viṣaghnairudakaiścāsya sarvadravyāṇi śodhayet viṣaghnāni ca ratnāni niyato dhārayet sadā
meaning: विषघ्नैरगदैश्चास्य सर्वद्रव्याणि योजयेत् विषघ्नैरुदकैश्चास्य सर्वद्रव्याणि शोधयेत् विषघ्नानि च रत्नानि नियतो धारयेत् सदा
text: He shall purify all his things by means of liquids destructive of poisons; and he shall be careful to wear always such gems as are antidotes against poison.
iast: viṣaghnairagadaiścāsya sarvadravyāṇi yojayet viṣaghnairudakaiścāsya sarvadravyāṇi śodhayet viṣaghnāni ca ratnāni niyato dhārayet sadā

sanskrit_iast: parīkṣitāḥ striyaścainaṃ vyajanaudakadhūpanaiḥ veṣābharaṇasaṃśuddhāḥ spṛśeyuḥ susamāhitāḥ
meaning: परीक्षिताः स्त्रियश्चैनं व्यजनौदकधूपनैः वेषाभरणसंशुद्धाः स्पृशेयुः सुसमाहिताः
text: Thoroughly tested women, whose toilet and ornaments have been examined, shall serve him attentively with fans, water and incense.
iast: parīkṣitāḥ striyaścainaṃ vyajanaudakadhūpanaiḥ veṣābharaṇasaṃśuddhāḥ spṛśeyuḥ susamāhitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ prayatnaṃ kurvīta yānaśayyā ''sanāśane snāne prasādhane caiva sarvālaṅkārakeṣu ca
meaning: एवं प्रयत्नं कुर्वीत यानशय्या ''सनाशने स्नाने प्रसाधने चैव सर्वालङ्कारकेषु च
text: He shall exercise similar caution with regard to conveyances, beds, seats and food, as also to bath, toilet and all kinds of ornaments
iast: evaṃ prayatnaṃ kurvīta yānaśayyā ''sanāśane snāne prasādhane caiva sarvālaṅkārakeṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: bhuktavān vihareccaiva strībhirantaḥpure saha vihṛtya tu yathākālaṃ punaḥ kāryāṇi cintayet
meaning: भुक्तवान् विहरेच्चैव स्त्रीभिरन्तःपुरे सह विहृत्य तु यथाकालं पुनः कार्याणि चिन्तयेत्
text: Having dined, he shall amuse himself in the inner apartment, in the company of the ladies; and having amused himself, he shall in due time again attend to business.
iast: bhuktavān vihareccaiva strībhirantaḥpure saha vihṛtya tu yathākālaṃ punaḥ kāryāṇi cintayet

sanskrit_iast: alaṅkṛtaśca saṃpaśyedāyudhīyaṃ punarjanam vāhanāni ca sarvāṇi śastrāṇyābharaṇāni ca
meaning: अलङ्कृतश्च संपश्येदायुधीयं पुनर्जनम् वाहनानि च सर्वाणि शस्त्राण्याभरणानि च
text: Duly robed, he shall again inspect the fighting men, as also all kinds of conveyances, weapons and accoutrements.
iast: alaṅkṛtaśca saṃpaśyedāyudhīyaṃ punarjanam vāhanāni ca sarvāṇi śastrāṇyābharaṇāni ca

sanskrit_iast: saṃdhyāṃ caupāsya śṛṇuyādantarveśmani śastrabhṛt rahasyākhyāyināṃ caiva praṇidhīnāṃ ca ceṣṭitam
meaning: संध्यां चौपास्य शृणुयादन्तर्वेश्मनि शस्त्रभृत् रहस्याख्यायिनां चैव प्रणिधीनां च चेष्टितम्
text: Having attended to his twilight devotions, he shall, well- armed, listen, in an inner room, to the doings of persons making eecret reports, and also of his spies.
iast: saṃdhyāṃ caupāsya śṛṇuyādantarveśmani śastrabhṛt rahasyākhyāyināṃ caiva praṇidhīnāṃ ca ceṣṭitam

sanskrit_iast: gatvā kakṣāntaraṃ tvanyat samanujñāpya taṃ janam praviśedbhojanārthaṃ ca strīvṛto 'ntaḥpuraṃ punaḥ
meaning: गत्वा कक्षान्तरं त्वन्यत् समनुज्ञाप्य तं जनम् प्रविशेद्भोजनार्थं च स्त्रीवृतो 'न्तःपुरं पुनः
text: Repairing to another apartment, and having dismissed those people, he shall again enter the inner apartment, surrounded by the women, for the purpose of taking his food.
iast: gatvā kakṣāntaraṃ tvanyat samanujñāpya taṃ janam praviśedbhojanārthaṃ ca strīvṛto 'ntaḥpuraṃ punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tatra bhuktvā punaḥ kiṃ cit tūryaghoṣaiḥ praharṣitaḥ saṃviśet taṃ yathākālamuttiṣṭhecca gataklamaḥ
meaning: तत्र भुक्त्वा पुनः किं चित् तूर्यघोषैः प्रहर्षितः संविशेत् तं यथाकालमुत्तिष्ठेच्च गतक्लमः
text: Then, having eaten a little again, and having been recreated by the sound of musical instruments, he shall sleep and rise at the proper time, freed from fatigue.
iast: tatra bhuktvā punaḥ kiṃ cit tūryaghoṣaiḥ praharṣitaḥ saṃviśet taṃ yathākālamuttiṣṭhecca gataklamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etadvidhānamātiṣṭhedarogaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ asvasthaḥ sarvametat tu bhṛtyeṣu viniyojayet
meaning: एतद्विधानमातिष्ठेदरोगः पृथिवीपतिः अस्वस्थः सर्वमेतत् तु भृत्येषु विनियोजयेत्
text: The marriage-ritual texts are applicable to virgins only, and nowhere among men, to non-virgins; and this because these latter are excluded from religious acts.
iast: etadvidhānamātiṣṭhedarogaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ asvasthaḥ sarvametat tu bhṛtyeṣu viniyojayet

sanskrit_iast: vyavahārān didṛkṣustu brāhmaṇaiḥ saha pārthivaḥ mantrajñairmantribhiścaiva vinītaḥ praviśet sabhām
meaning: व्यवहारान् दिदृक्षुस्तु ब्राह्मणैः सह पार्थिवः मन्त्रज्ञैर्मन्त्रिभिश्चैव विनीतः प्रविशेत् सभाम्
text: Desirous of investigating cases, the king shall enter the court, with a dignified demeanour, along with Brāhmaṇas and councillors, versed in counsel.
iast: vyavahārān didṛkṣustu brāhmaṇaiḥ saha pārthivaḥ mantrajñairmantribhiścaiva vinītaḥ praviśet sabhām

sanskrit_iast: tatrāsīnaḥ sthito vā 'pi pāṇimudyamya dakṣiṇam vinītaveṣābharaṇaḥ paśyet kāryāṇi kāryiṇām
meaning: तत्रासीनः स्थितो वा 'पि पाणिमुद्यम्य दक्षिणम् विनीतवेषाभरणः पश्येत् कार्याणि कार्यिणाम्
text: There, either seated or standing, raising his right hand, subdued in dress and ornaments, he shall look into the suits of the suitors.
iast: tatrāsīnaḥ sthito vā 'pi pāṇimudyamya dakṣiṇam vinītaveṣābharaṇaḥ paśyet kāryāṇi kāryiṇām

sanskrit_iast: pratyahaṃ deśadṛṣṭaiśca śāstradṛṣṭaiśca hetubhiḥ aṣṭādaśasu mārgeṣu nibaddhāni pṛthak pṛthak
meaning: प्रत्यहं देशदृष्टैश्च शास्त्रदृष्टैश्च हेतुभिः अष्टादशसु मार्गेषु निबद्धानि पृथक् पृथक्
text: [He shall look into the suits]—day after day, one by one,—falling as they do under eighteen heads,—according to principles deduced from local usage and from the scriptures.
iast: pratyahaṃ deśadṛṣṭaiśca śāstradṛṣṭaiśca hetubhiḥ aṣṭādaśasu mārgeṣu nibaddhāni pṛthak pṛthak

sanskrit_iast: teṣāmādyaṃ ṛṇādānaṃ nikṣepo 'svāmivikrayaḥ saṃbhūya ca samutthānaṃ dattasyānapakarma ca
meaning: तेषामाद्यं ऋणादानं निक्षेपो 'स्वामिविक्रयः संभूय च समुत्थानं दत्तस्यानपकर्म च
text: Of these the first is (1) Non-payment of Debt; (then) (2) Deposits, (3) Selling without ownership, (4) Joint concerns, (5) Non-delivery of what has been given away,—[4]—(6) Non-payment of wages, (7) Breach of Contract, (8) Recision of Sale and Purchase, (9) Dispute between the Owner and the Keeper,—[5]—(10) Disputes regarding Boundaries, (11) and (12) Assault, physical and verbal, (13) Theft, (14) Violence, (15) Adultery,—[6]—(16) Duties of man and wife, (17) Partition, and (18) Gambling and Betting;—these are the eighteen topics that form the basis of law-suits.
iast: teṣāmādyaṃ ṛṇādānaṃ nikṣepo 'svāmivikrayaḥ saṃbhūya ca samutthānaṃ dattasyānapakarma ca

sanskrit_iast: vetanasyaiva cādānaṃ saṃvidaśca vyatikramaḥ krayavikrayānuśayo vivādaḥ svāmipālayoḥ
meaning: वेतनस्यैव चादानं संविदश्च व्यतिक्रमः क्रयविक्रयानुशयो विवादः स्वामिपालयोः
text: Of these the first is (1) Non-payment of Debt; (then) (2) Deposits, (3) Selling without ownership, (4) Joint concerns, (5) Non-delivery of what has been given away,—[4]—(6) Non-payment of wages, (7) Breach of Contract, (8) Recision of Sale and Purchase, (9) Dispute between the Owner and the Keeper,—[5]—(10) Disputes regarding Boundaries, (11) and (12) Assault, physical and verbal, (13) Theft, (14) Violence, (15) Adultery,—[6]—(16) Duties of man and wife, (17) Partition, and (18) Gambling and Betting;—these are the eighteen topics that form the basis of law-suits.
iast: vetanasyaiva cādānaṃ saṃvidaśca vyatikramaḥ krayavikrayānuśayo vivādaḥ svāmipālayoḥ

sanskrit_iast: sīmāvivādadharmaśca pāruṣye daṇḍavācike steyaṃ ca sāhasaṃ caiva strīsaṅgrahaṇameva ca
meaning: सीमाविवादधर्मश्च पारुष्ये दण्डवाचिके स्तेयं च साहसं चैव स्त्रीसङ्ग्रहणमेव च
text: Of these the first is (1) Non-payment of Debt; (then) (2) Deposits, (3) Selling without ownership, (4) Joint concerns, (5) Non-delivery of what has been given away,—[4]—(6) Non-payment of wages, (7) Breach of Contract, (8) Recision of Sale and Purchase, (9) Dispute between the Owner and the Keeper,—[5]—(10) Disputes regarding Boundaries, (11) and (12) Assault, physical and verbal, (13) Theft, (14) Violence, (15) Adultery,—[6]—(16) Duties of man and wife, (17) Partition, and (18) Gambling and Betting;—these are the eighteen topics that form the basis of law-suits.
iast: sīmāvivādadharmaśca pāruṣye daṇḍavācike steyaṃ ca sāhasaṃ caiva strīsaṅgrahaṇameva ca

sanskrit_iast: strīpuṃdharmo vibhāgaśca dyūtamāhvaya eva ca padānyaṣṭādaśaitāni vyavahārasthitāviha
meaning: स्त्रीपुंधर्मो विभागश्च द्यूतमाह्वय एव च पदान्यष्टादशैतानि व्यवहारस्थिताविह
text: Of these the first is (1) Non-payment of Debt; (then) (2) Deposits, (3) Selling without ownership, (4) Joint concerns, (5) Non-delivery of what has been given away,—[4]—(6) Non-payment of wages, (7) Breach of Contract, (8) Recision of Sale and Purchase, (9) Dispute between the Owner and the Keeper,—[5]—(10) Disputes regarding Boundaries, (11) and (12) Assault, physical and verbal, (13) Theft, (14) Violence, (15) Adultery,—[6]—(16) Duties of man and wife, (17) Partition, and (18) Gambling and Betting;—these are the eighteen topics that form the basis of law-suits.
iast: strīpuṃdharmo vibhāgaśca dyūtamāhvaya eva ca padānyaṣṭādaśaitāni vyavahārasthitāviha

sanskrit_iast: eṣu sthāneṣu bhūyiṣṭhaṃ vivādaṃ caratāṃ nṛṇām dharmaṃ śāśvatamāśritya kuryāt kāryavinirṇayam
meaning: एषु स्थानेषु भूयिष्ठं विवादं चरतां नृणाम् धर्मं शाश्वतमाश्रित्य कुर्यात् कार्यविनिर्णयम्
text: Taking his stand upon eternal morality, he shall form his decision on the suits of men who mostly carry on disputes in regard to the aforesaid points.
iast: eṣu sthāneṣu bhūyiṣṭhaṃ vivādaṃ caratāṃ nṛṇām dharmaṃ śāśvatamāśritya kuryāt kāryavinirṇayam

sanskrit_iast: yadā svayaṃ na kuryāt tu nṛpatiḥ kāryadarśanam tadā niyuñjyādvidvāṃsaṃ brāhmaṇaṃ kāryadarśane
meaning: यदा स्वयं न कुर्यात् तु नृपतिः कार्यदर्शनम् तदा नियुञ्ज्याद्विद्वांसं ब्राह्मणं कार्यदर्शने
text: When he himself may not carry on the investigation of suits, he shall appoint a learned Brāhmaṇa to do the work of investigation.
iast: yadā svayaṃ na kuryāt tu nṛpatiḥ kāryadarśanam tadā niyuñjyādvidvāṃsaṃ brāhmaṇaṃ kāryadarśane

sanskrit_iast: so 'sya kāryāṇi saṃpaśyet sabhyaireva tribhirvṛtaḥ sabhāmeva praviśyāgryāmāsīnaḥ sthita eva vā
meaning: सो 'स्य कार्याणि संपश्येत् सभ्यैरेव त्रिभिर्वृतः सभामेव प्रविश्याग्र्यामासीनः स्थित एव वा
text: That man, accompanied by three assessors, shall enter the excellent Court, and either seated or standing, shall investigate the suits on behalf of the king.
iast: so 'sya kāryāṇi saṃpaśyet sabhyaireva tribhirvṛtaḥ sabhāmeva praviśyāgryāmāsīnaḥ sthita eva vā

sanskrit_iast: yasmin deśe niṣīdanti viprā vedavidastrayaḥ rājñaścādhikṛto vidvān brahmaṇastāṃ sabhāṃ viduḥ
meaning: यस्मिन् देशे निषीदन्ति विप्रा वेदविदस्त्रयः राज्ञश्चाधिकृतो विद्वान् ब्रह्मणस्तां सभां विदुः
text: That place, where three Brāhmaṇas learned in the Veda sit, as also the learned Brāhmaṇa appointed by the king,—they regard as the ‘Court of Brahman.’
iast: yasmin deśe niṣīdanti viprā vedavidastrayaḥ rājñaścādhikṛto vidvān brahmaṇastāṃ sabhāṃ viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: dharmo viddhastvadharmeṇa sabhāṃ yatraupatiṣṭhate śalyaṃ cāsya na kṛntanti viddhāstatra sabhāsadaḥ
meaning: धर्मो विद्धस्त्वधर्मेण सभां यत्रौपतिष्ठते शल्यं चास्य न कृन्तन्ति विद्धास्तत्र सभासदः
text: In a court where Justice is pierced by Injustice, and the members of the Court do not remove that dart, these members also become pierced.
iast: dharmo viddhastvadharmeṇa sabhāṃ yatraupatiṣṭhate śalyaṃ cāsya na kṛntanti viddhāstatra sabhāsadaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sabhāṃ vā na praveṣṭavyaṃ vaktavyaṃ vā samañjasam sabhā vā na praveṣṭavyā abruvan vibruvan vā 'pi naro bhavati kilbiṣī
meaning: सभां वा न प्रवेष्टव्यं वक्तव्यं वा समञ्जसम् सभा वा न प्रवेष्टव्या अब्रुवन् विब्रुवन् वा 'पि नरो भवति किल्बिषी
text: One should either not enter the Court at all, or he should speak out what is equitable; one who either spe aks nothing, or speaks falsely, becomes tainted with sin.
iast: sabhāṃ vā na praveṣṭavyaṃ vaktavyaṃ vā samañjasam sabhā vā na praveṣṭavyā abruvan vibruvan vā 'pi naro bhavati kilbiṣī

sanskrit_iast: yatra dharmo hyadharmeṇa satyaṃ yatrānṛtena ca hanyate prekṣamāṇānāṃ hatāstatra sabhāsadaḥ
meaning: यत्र धर्मो ह्यधर्मेण सत्यं यत्रानृतेन च हन्यते प्रेक्षमाणानां हतास्तत्र सभासदः
text: Where justice is destroyed by injustice, or truth by falsehood, while people are looking on,—there the members of the court also are destroyed.
iast: yatra dharmo hyadharmeṇa satyaṃ yatrānṛtena ca hanyate prekṣamāṇānāṃ hatāstatra sabhāsadaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dharma eva hato hanti dharmo rakṣati rakṣitaḥ tasmāddharmo na hantavyo mā no dharmo hato 'vadhīt vadhīt
meaning: धर्म एव हतो हन्ति धर्मो रक्षति रक्षितः तस्माद्धर्मो न हन्तव्यो मा नो धर्मो हतो 'वधीत् वधीत्
text: Justice, blighted, blights; and justice, preserved, preserves; hence justice should not be blighted, lest blighted justice blight us.
iast: dharma eva hato hanti dharmo rakṣati rakṣitaḥ tasmāddharmo na hantavyo mā no dharmo hato 'vadhīt vadhīt

sanskrit_iast: vṛṣo hi bhagavān dharmastasya yaḥ kurute hyalam tvalam vṛṣalaṃ taṃ vidurdevāstasmāddharmaṃ na lopayet
meaning: वृषो हि भगवान् धर्मस्तस्य यः कुरुते ह्यलम् त्वलम् वृषलं तं विदुर्देवास्तस्माद्धर्मं न लोपयेत्
text: For Justice is the revered ‘Vṛṣa,’ Bull; and he who commits the violation, ‘alam,’ of it, him the gods regard as ‘vṛṣala,’ low-born; hence one shall not violate Justice.
iast: vṛṣo hi bhagavān dharmastasya yaḥ kurute hyalam tvalam vṛṣalaṃ taṃ vidurdevāstasmāddharmaṃ na lopayet

sanskrit_iast: eka eva suhṛddharmo nidhāne 'pyanuyāti yaḥ śarīreṇa samaṃ nāśaṃ sarvamanyaddhi gacchati
meaning: एक एव सुहृद्धर्मो निधाने 'प्यनुयाति यः शरीरेण समं नाशं सर्वमन्यद्धि गच्छति
text: Morality (Justice) is the only friend who follows one even after death; everything else perishes along with the body.
iast: eka eva suhṛddharmo nidhāne 'pyanuyāti yaḥ śarīreṇa samaṃ nāśaṃ sarvamanyaddhi gacchati

sanskrit_iast: pādo 'dharmasya kartāraṃ pādaḥ sākṣiṇaṃ ṛcchati pādaḥ sabhāsadaḥ sarvān pādo rājānaṃ ṛcchati
meaning: पादो 'धर्मस्य कर्तारं पादः साक्षिणं ऋच्छति पादः सभासदः सर्वान् पादो राजानं ऋच्छति
text: One quarter of the Injustice falls on the man who commits it, one quarter on the witness, one quarter on the members of the Court and one quarter on the king.
iast: pādo 'dharmasya kartāraṃ pādaḥ sākṣiṇaṃ ṛcchati pādaḥ sabhāsadaḥ sarvān pādo rājānaṃ ṛcchati

sanskrit_iast: rājā bhavatyanenāstu mucyante ca sabhāsadaḥ eno gacchati kartāraṃ nindā 'rho yatra nindyate
meaning: राजा भवत्यनेनास्तु मुच्यन्ते च सभासदः एनो गच्छति कर्तारं निन्दा 'र्हो यत्र निन्द्यते
text: Where, however, the person deserving of censure is actually censured, there the king becomes sinless, the members of the court become freed, and the sin falls upon the perpetrator.
iast: rājā bhavatyanenāstu mucyante ca sabhāsadaḥ eno gacchati kartāraṃ nindā 'rho yatra nindyate

sanskrit_iast: jātimātraupajīvī vā kāmaṃ syādbrāhmaṇabruvaḥ dharmapravaktā nṛpaterna śūdraḥ kathaṃ cana
meaning: जातिमात्रौपजीवी वा कामं स्याद्ब्राह्मणब्रुवः धर्मप्रवक्ता नृपतेर्न शूद्रः कथं चन
text: Even a so-called Brāhmaṇa, who makes a living by his caste only, may, at pleasure be the propounder of the Law for the king,—but not a Śūdra under any circumtsances.
iast: jātimātraupajīvī vā kāmaṃ syādbrāhmaṇabruvaḥ dharmapravaktā nṛpaterna śūdraḥ kathaṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: yasya śūdrastu kurute rājño dharmavivecanam tasya sīdati tadrāṣṭraṃ paṅke gauriva paśyataḥ
meaning: यस्य शूद्रस्तु कुरुते राज्ञो धर्मविवेचनम् तस्य सीदति तद्राष्ट्रं पङ्के गौरिव पश्यतः
text: The kingdom or that king for whom the investigation of Law is done by a Śūdra, while he himself is looking on, suffers, like the cow in a morass.
iast: yasya śūdrastu kurute rājño dharmavivecanam tasya sīdati tadrāṣṭraṃ paṅke gauriva paśyataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadrāṣṭraṃ śūdrabhūyiṣṭhaṃ nāstikākrāntamadvijam vinaśyatyāśu tat kṛtsnaṃ durbhikṣavyādhipīḍitam
meaning: यद्राष्ट्रं शूद्रभूयिष्ठं नास्तिकाक्रान्तमद्विजम् विनश्यत्याशु तत् कृत्स्नं दुर्भिक्षव्याधिपीडितम्
text: That kingdom where there is a majority of Śūdras, which is infested with non-believers and destitute of twice-born people, quickly perishes entirely, becoming afflicted by famine and disease.
iast: yadrāṣṭraṃ śūdrabhūyiṣṭhaṃ nāstikākrāntamadvijam vinaśyatyāśu tat kṛtsnaṃ durbhikṣavyādhipīḍitam

sanskrit_iast: dharmāsanamadhiṣṭhāya saṃvītāṅgaḥ samāhitaḥ praṇamya lokapālebhyaḥ kāryadarśanamārabhet
meaning: धर्मासनमधिष्ठाय संवीताङ्गः समाहितः प्रणम्य लोकपालेभ्यः कार्यदर्शनमारभेत्
text: Having occupied the Judgment-seat, with his body covered and mind collected, he shall salute the Guardian-Deities, and then proceed with the investigation of suits.
iast: dharmāsanamadhiṣṭhāya saṃvītāṅgaḥ samāhitaḥ praṇamya lokapālebhyaḥ kāryadarśanamārabhet

sanskrit_iast: arthānarthāvubhau buddhvā dharmādharmau ca kevalau varṇakrameṇa sarvāṇi paśyet kāryāṇi kāryiṇām
meaning: अर्थानर्थावुभौ बुद्ध्वा धर्माधर्मौ च केवलौ वर्णक्रमेण सर्वाणि पश्येत् कार्याणि कार्यिणाम्
text: Understanding both ‘desirable’ and ‘undesirable’ to be only ‘Justice’ and ‘Injustice,’ he shall look into all the suits of the suitors, according to the order of the castes.
iast: arthānarthāvubhau buddhvā dharmādharmau ca kevalau varṇakrameṇa sarvāṇi paśyet kāryāṇi kāryiṇām

sanskrit_iast: bāhyairvibhāvayetliṅgairbhāvamantargataṃ nṛṇām svaravarṇaiṅgitākāraiścakṣuṣā ceṣṭitena ca
meaning: बाह्यैर्विभावयेत्लिङ्गैर्भावमन्तर्गतं नृणाम् स्वरवर्णैङ्गिताकारैश्चक्षुषा चेष्टितेन च
text: He shall discover the internal disposition of men by external signs: by variations in their voice, colour and aspect, as also by means of the eye and by gestures.
iast: bāhyairvibhāvayetliṅgairbhāvamantargataṃ nṛṇām svaravarṇaiṅgitākāraiścakṣuṣā ceṣṭitena ca

sanskrit_iast: ākārairiṅgitairgatyā ceṣṭayā bhāṣitena ca netravaktravikāraiśca gṛhyate 'ntargataṃ manaḥ
meaning: आकारैरिङ्गितैर्गत्या चेष्टया भाषितेन च नेत्रवक्त्रविकारैश्च गृह्यते 'न्तर्गतं मनः
text: The inner mind is indicated by such variations as those of aspect, gait, gesture, speech, and by changes in the eye and the face.
iast: ākārairiṅgitairgatyā ceṣṭayā bhāṣitena ca netravaktravikāraiśca gṛhyate 'ntargataṃ manaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bāladāyādikaṃ rikthaṃ tāvadrājā 'nupālayet yāvat sa syāt samāvṛtto yāvatcātītaśaiśavaḥ yāvadvā 'tītaśaiśavaḥ
meaning: बालदायादिकं रिक्थं तावद्राजा 'नुपालयेत् यावत् स स्यात् समावृत्तो यावत्चातीतशैशवः यावद्वा 'तीतशैशवः
text: The king shall take care of the property owned by a minor, till such time as he may return from the teacher’s house, or till he may have passed his minority.
iast: bāladāyādikaṃ rikthaṃ tāvadrājā 'nupālayet yāvat sa syāt samāvṛtto yāvatcātītaśaiśavaḥ yāvadvā 'tītaśaiśavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaśā 'putrāsu caivaṃ syādrakṣaṇaṃ niṣkulāsu ca pativratāsu ca strīṣu vidhavāsvāturāsu ca
meaning: वशा 'पुत्रासु चैवं स्याद्रक्षणं निष्कुलासु च पतिव्रतासु च स्त्रीषु विधवास्वातुरासु च
text: There shall be similar protection in the case of barren women, of son-less women, of women devoted to their husbands, and of widows faithful to their husbands,—when their family is extinct, and when they are in distress.
iast: vaśā 'putrāsu caivaṃ syādrakṣaṇaṃ niṣkulāsu ca pativratāsu ca strīṣu vidhavāsvāturāsu ca

sanskrit_iast: jīvantīnāṃ tu tāsāṃ ye taddhareyuḥ svabāndhavāḥ tāṃśiṣyātcauradaṇḍena dhārmikaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ
meaning: जीवन्तीनां तु तासां ये तद्धरेयुः स्वबान्धवाः तांशिष्यात्चौरदण्डेन धार्मिकः पृथिवीपतिः
text: While these women are alive, if their relatives should appropriate their property,—on them the righteous king shall inflict the punishment of thieves.
iast: jīvantīnāṃ tu tāsāṃ ye taddhareyuḥ svabāndhavāḥ tāṃśiṣyātcauradaṇḍena dhārmikaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: praṇaṣṭasvāmikaṃ rikthaṃ rājā tryabdaṃ nidhāpayet arvāk tryabdāddharet svāmī pareṇa nṛpatirharet
meaning: प्रणष्टस्वामिकं रिक्थं राजा त्र्यब्दं निधापयेत् अर्वाक् त्र्यब्दाद्धरेत् स्वामी परेण नृपतिर्हरेत्
text: Property, the owner whereof has disappeared, the King shall keep for three years; up to three years the owner may receive it; but after that the King (shall take it).
iast: praṇaṣṭasvāmikaṃ rikthaṃ rājā tryabdaṃ nidhāpayet arvāk tryabdāddharet svāmī pareṇa nṛpatirharet

sanskrit_iast: mamaidamiti yo brūyāt so 'nuyojyo yathāvidhi saṃvādya rūpasaṅkhyādīn svāmī taddravyamarhati
meaning: ममैदमिति यो ब्रूयात् सो 'नुयोज्यो यथाविधि संवाद्य रूपसङ्ख्यादीन् स्वामी तद्द्रव्यमर्हति
text: He who says ‘this is mine’ should be questioned in proper form; and the owner ought to receive the property after having correctly described the colour, the number and other details regarding it.
iast: mamaidamiti yo brūyāt so 'nuyojyo yathāvidhi saṃvādya rūpasaṅkhyādīn svāmī taddravyamarhati

sanskrit_iast: avedayāno naṣṭasya deśaṃ kālaṃ ca tattvataḥ varṇaṃ rūpaṃ pramāṇaṃ ca tatsamaṃ daṇḍamarhati
meaning: अवेदयानो नष्टस्य देशं कालं च तत्त्वतः वर्णं रूपं प्रमाणं च तत्समं दण्डमर्हति
text: If he does not provide a correct account of the place and time, and also the colour, form and size of the lost article, he deserves a fine equal to that article.
iast: avedayāno naṣṭasya deśaṃ kālaṃ ca tattvataḥ varṇaṃ rūpaṃ pramāṇaṃ ca tatsamaṃ daṇḍamarhati

sanskrit_iast: ādadītātha ṣaḍbhāgaṃ pranaṣṭādhigatānnṛpaḥ daśamaṃ dvādaśaṃ vā 'pi satāṃ dharmamanusmaran
meaning: आददीताथ षड्भागं प्रनष्टाधिगतान्नृपः दशमं द्वादशं वा 'पि सतां धर्ममनुस्मरन्
text: Property that has been lost and found should remain in the charge of specially deputed (officials); and the thieves that he may detect in connection with this, the king shall cause to be killed by an elephant.
iast: ādadītātha ṣaḍbhāgaṃ pranaṣṭādhigatānnṛpaḥ daśamaṃ dvādaśaṃ vā 'pi satāṃ dharmamanusmaran

sanskrit_iast: pranaṣṭādhigataṃ dravyaṃ tiṣṭhedyuktairadhiṣṭhitam yāṃstatra caurān gṛhṇīyāt tān rājaibhena ghātayet
meaning: प्रनष्टाधिगतं द्रव्यं तिष्ठेद्युक्तैरधिष्ठितम् यांस्तत्र चौरान् गृह्णीयात् तान् राजैभेन घातयेत्
text: Out of the property that has been lost and found, the king, remembering the duty of good men, shall take the sixth part, or the tenth, or the twelfth.
iast: pranaṣṭādhigataṃ dravyaṃ tiṣṭhedyuktairadhiṣṭhitam yāṃstatra caurān gṛhṇīyāt tān rājaibhena ghātayet

sanskrit_iast: mamāyamiti yo brūyānnidhiṃ satyena mānavaḥ tasyādadīta ṣaḍbhāgaṃ rājā dvādaśameva vā
meaning: ममायमिति यो ब्रूयान्निधिं सत्येन मानवः तस्याददीत षड्भागं राजा द्वादशमेव वा
text: In regard to a treasure-trove, if a man says truly ‘this is mine,’—from him the king shall take the sixth part, or only the twelfth part.
iast: mamāyamiti yo brūyānnidhiṃ satyena mānavaḥ tasyādadīta ṣaḍbhāgaṃ rājā dvādaśameva vā

sanskrit_iast: anṛtaṃ tu vadan daṇḍyaḥ svavittasyāṃśamaṣṭamam tasyaiva vā nidhānasya saṅkhyayā 'lpīyasīṃ kalām
meaning: अनृतं तु वदन् दण्ड्यः स्ववित्तस्यांशमष्टमम् तस्यैव वा निधानस्य सङ्ख्यया 'ल्पीयसीं कलाम्
text: But he who speaks falsely shall, be fined the eighth part of his property, or a smaller fraction, on calculation, of that same treasure-trove.
iast: anṛtaṃ tu vadan daṇḍyaḥ svavittasyāṃśamaṣṭamam tasyaiva vā nidhānasya saṅkhyayā 'lpīyasīṃ kalām

sanskrit_iast: vidvāṃstu brāhmaṇo dṛṣṭvā pūrvaupanihitaṃ nidhim aśeṣato 'pyādadīta sarvasyādhipatirhi saḥ
meaning: विद्वांस्तु ब्राह्मणो दृष्ट्वा पूर्वौपनिहितं निधिम् अशेषतो 'प्याददीत सर्वस्याधिपतिर्हि सः
text: A learned Brāhmaṇa, having found treasure buried by his forefathers, shall take it wholly; as he is the master of everything.
iast: vidvāṃstu brāhmaṇo dṛṣṭvā pūrvaupanihitaṃ nidhim aśeṣato 'pyādadīta sarvasyādhipatirhi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: yaṃ tu paśyennidhiṃ rājā purāṇaṃ nihitaṃ kṣitau tasmāddvijebhyo dattvā 'rdhamardhaṃ kośe praveśayet
meaning: यं तु पश्येन्निधिं राजा पुराणं निहितं क्षितौ तस्माद्द्विजेभ्यो दत्त्वा 'र्धमर्धं कोशे प्रवेशयेत्
text: When the king himself finds a hoard buried of old under the ground, he shall give one-half of it to the Brāhmaṇas and have the other half put in his treasury.
iast: yaṃ tu paśyennidhiṃ rājā purāṇaṃ nihitaṃ kṣitau tasmāddvijebhyo dattvā 'rdhamardhaṃ kośe praveśayet

sanskrit_iast: nidhīnāṃ tu purāṇānāṃ dhātūnāmeva ca kṣitau ardhabhāg rakṣaṇādrājā bhūmeradhipatirhi saḥ
meaning: निधीनां तु पुराणानां धातूनामेव च क्षितौ अर्धभाग् रक्षणाद्राजा भूमेरधिपतिर्हि सः
text: Of ancient hoards, as also of minerals under the ground, the king is entitled to his share, by reason of his protecting them,—he being the lord of the soil.
iast: nidhīnāṃ tu purāṇānāṃ dhātūnāmeva ca kṣitau ardhabhāg rakṣaṇādrājā bhūmeradhipatirhi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: dātavyaṃ sarvavarṇebhyo rājñā caurairhṛtaṃ dhanam rājā tadupayuñjānaścaurasyāpnoti kilbiṣam
meaning: दातव्यं सर्ववर्णेभ्यो राज्ञा चौरैर्हृतं धनम् राजा तदुपयुञ्जानश्चौरस्याप्नोति किल्बिषम्
text: Property stolen by thieves should be restored by the king to men of all castes; by retaining such property, the king imbibes the sin of the thief.
iast: dātavyaṃ sarvavarṇebhyo rājñā caurairhṛtaṃ dhanam rājā tadupayuñjānaścaurasyāpnoti kilbiṣam

sanskrit_iast: jātijānapadān dharmān śreṇīdharmāṃśca dharmavit samīkṣya kuladharmāṃśca svadharmaṃ pratipādayet
meaning: जातिजानपदान् धर्मान् श्रेणीधर्मांश्च धर्मवित् समीक्ष्य कुलधर्मांश्च स्वधर्मं प्रतिपादयेत्
text: The king knowing his duty shall determine the law for each man, after haying duly examined the provincial laws pertaining to each caste, the law’s of guilds, as also the laws of families.
iast: jātijānapadān dharmān śreṇīdharmāṃśca dharmavit samīkṣya kuladharmāṃśca svadharmaṃ pratipādayet

sanskrit_iast: svāni karmāṇi kurvāṇā dūre santo 'pi mānavāḥ priyā bhavanti lokasya sve sve karmaṇyavasthitāḥ
meaning: स्वानि कर्माणि कुर्वाणा दूरे सन्तो 'पि मानवाः प्रिया भवन्ति लोकस्य स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यवस्थिताः
text: For men following their respective occupations,—even though living at a distance,—come to be liked by the people, while they remain firm in their own duties.
iast: svāni karmāṇi kurvāṇā dūre santo 'pi mānavāḥ priyā bhavanti lokasya sve sve karmaṇyavasthitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: nautpādayet svayaṃ kāryaṃ rājā nāpyasya puruṣaḥ na ca prāpitamanyena grasedarthaṃ kathaṃ cana grasetārthaṃ
meaning: नौत्पादयेत् स्वयं कार्यं राजा नाप्यस्य पुरुषः न च प्रापितमन्येन ग्रसेदर्थं कथं चन ग्रसेतार्थं
text: Neither the king himself nor any servant of his shall promote a suit; nor shall he suppress a suit that has been brought up by another person.
iast: nautpādayet svayaṃ kāryaṃ rājā nāpyasya puruṣaḥ na ca prāpitamanyena grasedarthaṃ kathaṃ cana grasetārthaṃ

sanskrit_iast: yathā nayatyasṛkpātairmṛgasya mṛgayuḥ padam nayet tathā 'numānena dharmasya nṛpatiḥ padam
meaning: यथा नयत्यसृक्पातैर्मृगस्य मृगयुः पदम् नयेत् तथा 'नुमानेन धर्मस्य नृपतिः पदम्
text: Just as the hunter discovers the foot-print of the deer by the drops of blood, so should the king discover the right by means of inference.
iast: yathā nayatyasṛkpātairmṛgasya mṛgayuḥ padam nayet tathā 'numānena dharmasya nṛpatiḥ padam

sanskrit_iast: satyamarthaṃ ca saṃpaśyedātmānamatha sākṣiṇaḥ sākṣiṇam deśaṃ rūpaṃ ca kālaṃ ca vyavahāravidhau sthitaḥ
meaning: सत्यमर्थं च संपश्येदात्मानमथ साक्षिणः साक्षिणम् देशं रूपं च कालं च व्यवहारविधौ स्थितः
text: When engaged in judicial proceedings, the king shall keep his eye upon the truth, upon the object, upon himself, the witness and upon the place, the time and the aspect.
iast: satyamarthaṃ ca saṃpaśyedātmānamatha sākṣiṇaḥ sākṣiṇam deśaṃ rūpaṃ ca kālaṃ ca vyavahāravidhau sthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sadbhirācaritaṃ yat syāddhārmikaiśca dvijātibhiḥ taddeśakulajātīnāmaviruddhaṃ prakalpayet
meaning: सद्भिराचरितं यत् स्याद्धार्मिकैश्च द्विजातिभिः तद्देशकुलजातीनामविरुद्धं प्रकल्पयेत्
text: What may be found to have been observed in practice by the good and the righteous twice-born men, that he shall ordain for countries, families and castes,—provided that it is not antagonistic.
iast: sadbhirācaritaṃ yat syāddhārmikaiśca dvijātibhiḥ taddeśakulajātīnāmaviruddhaṃ prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: adhamarṇārthasiddhyarthamuttamarṇena coditaḥ dāpayeddhanikasyārthamadhamarṇādvibhāvitam
meaning: अधमर्णार्थसिद्ध्यर्थमुत्तमर्णेन चोदितः दापयेद्धनिकस्यार्थमधमर्णाद्विभावितम्
text: On being prayed by the creditor for the recovery of money from the debtor, he shall make the debtor pay to the creditor the money proved to be due.
iast: adhamarṇārthasiddhyarthamuttamarṇena coditaḥ dāpayeddhanikasyārthamadhamarṇādvibhāvitam

sanskrit_iast: yairyairupāyairarthaṃ svaṃ prāpnuyāduttamarṇikaḥ tairtairupāyaiḥ saṅgṛhya dāpayedadhamarṇikam
meaning: यैर्यैरुपायैरर्थं स्वं प्राप्नुयादुत्तमर्णिकः तैर्तैरुपायैः सङ्गृह्य दापयेदधमर्णिकम्
text: Having determined the means by which the debtor may be able to get his money, he shall, by those same means, make the debtor pay up.
iast: yairyairupāyairarthaṃ svaṃ prāpnuyāduttamarṇikaḥ tairtairupāyaiḥ saṅgṛhya dāpayedadhamarṇikam

sanskrit_iast: dharmeṇa vyavahāreṇa chalenācaritena ca prayuktaṃ sādhayedarthaṃ pañcamena balena ca
meaning: धर्मेण व्यवहारेण छलेनाचरितेन च प्रयुक्तं साधयेदर्थं पञ्चमेन बलेन च
text: He shall make the advanced money repaid by means of (a) good faith, (b) tactful transaction, (c) trick, (d) moral pressure, and (c) force, the fifth.
iast: dharmeṇa vyavahāreṇa chalenācaritena ca prayuktaṃ sādhayedarthaṃ pañcamena balena ca

sanskrit_iast: yaḥ svayaṃ sādhayedarthamuttamarṇo 'dhamarṇikāt na sa rājñā 'bhiyoktavyaḥ svakaṃ saṃsādhayan dhanam
meaning: यः स्वयं साधयेदर्थमुत्तमर्णो 'धमर्णिकात् न स राज्ञा 'भियोक्तव्यः स्वकं संसाधयन् धनम्
text: The creditor who shall himself recover his money from the debtor should not be prosecuted by the king, for recovering what is his own property.
iast: yaḥ svayaṃ sādhayedarthamuttamarṇo 'dhamarṇikāt na sa rājñā 'bhiyoktavyaḥ svakaṃ saṃsādhayan dhanam

sanskrit_iast: arthe 'pavyayamānaṃ tu karaṇena vibhāvitam dāpayeddhanikasyārthaṃ daṇḍaleśaṃ ca śaktitaḥ
meaning: अर्थे 'पव्ययमानं तु करणेन विभावितम् दापयेद्धनिकस्यार्थं दण्डलेशं च शक्तितः
text: The man who denies a debt shall be made to pay the creditor’s due, proved by evidence, as also a small fine, according to his means.
iast: arthe 'pavyayamānaṃ tu karaṇena vibhāvitam dāpayeddhanikasyārthaṃ daṇḍaleśaṃ ca śaktitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: apahnave 'dhamarṇasya dehītyuktasya saṃsadi abhiyoktā diśeddeśyaṃ karaṇaṃ vā 'nyaduddiśet
meaning: अपह्नवे 'धमर्णस्य देहीत्युक्तस्य संसदि अभियोक्ता दिशेद्देश्यं करणं वा 'न्यदुद्दिशेत्
text: On denial by the debtor, when asked in court to pay the debt, the complainant shall produce a witness, or adduce (other) evidence.
iast: apahnave 'dhamarṇasya dehītyuktasya saṃsadi abhiyoktā diśeddeśyaṃ karaṇaṃ vā 'nyaduddiśet

sanskrit_iast: adeśyaṃ yaśca diśati nirdiśyāpahnute ca yaḥ yaścādharauttarānarthān vigītānnāvabudhyate
meaning: अदेश्यं यश्च दिशति निर्दिश्यापह्नुते च यः यश्चाधरौत्तरानर्थान् विगीतान्नावबुध्यते
text: He who mentions the wrong place,—or who, having mentioned it, retracts,—or who does not understand that his previous and subsequent statements are contradictory;
iast: adeśyaṃ yaśca diśati nirdiśyāpahnute ca yaḥ yaścādharauttarānarthān vigītānnāvabudhyate

sanskrit_iast: apadiśyāpadeśyaṃ ca punaryastvapadhāvati 'padiśyāpadeśaṃ samyak praṇihitaṃ cārthaṃ pṛṣṭaḥ sannābhinandati
meaning: अपदिश्यापदेश्यं च पुनर्यस्त्वपधावति 'पदिश्यापदेशं सम्यक् प्रणिहितं चार्थं पृष्टः सन्नाभिनन्दति
text: He who, having put forward a statement, subsequently retracts; and who on being questioned regarding a fact (previously) duly alleged, does not support it;
iast: apadiśyāpadeśyaṃ ca punaryastvapadhāvati 'padiśyāpadeśaṃ samyak praṇihitaṃ cārthaṃ pṛṣṭaḥ sannābhinandati

sanskrit_iast: asaṃbhāṣye sākṣibhiśca deśe saṃbhāṣate mithaḥ nirucyamānaṃ praśnaṃ ca naicchedyaścāpi niṣpatet
meaning: असंभाष्ये साक्षिभिश्च देशे संभाषते मिथः निरुच्यमानं प्रश्नं च नैच्छेद्यश्चापि निष्पतेत्
text: He who secretly converses with the witnesses in a place not fit for conversation, or who does not like the question being investigated, or who falls back;
iast: asaṃbhāṣye sākṣibhiśca deśe saṃbhāṣate mithaḥ nirucyamānaṃ praśnaṃ ca naicchedyaścāpi niṣpatet

sanskrit_iast: brūhītyuktaśca na brūyāduktaṃ ca na vibhāvayet na ca pūrvāparaṃ vidyāt tasmādarthāt sa hīyate
meaning: ब्रूहीत्युक्तश्च न ब्रूयादुक्तं च न विभावयेत् न च पूर्वापरं विद्यात् तस्मादर्थात् स हीयते
text: —He who, on being ordered to speak, does not speak; or who does not prove what he has asserted;—or who does not grasp the previous and subsequent statements;—such a person fails in that suit.
iast: brūhītyuktaśca na brūyāduktaṃ ca na vibhāvayet na ca pūrvāparaṃ vidyāt tasmādarthāt sa hīyate

sanskrit_iast: sākṣiṇaḥ santi metyuktvā diśaityukto diśenna yaḥ jñātāraḥ santi mety*] * dharmasthaḥ kāraṇairetairhīnaṃ tamapi nirdiśet tamiti nirdiśet
meaning: साक्षिणः सन्ति मेत्युक्त्वा दिशैत्युक्तो दिशेन्न यः ज्ञातारः सन्ति मेत्य्*] * धर्मस्थः कारणैरेतैर्हीनं तमपि निर्दिशेत् तमिति निर्दिशेत्
text: Having asserted that he has witnesses, and on being asked to name them, if he does not name them,—him also, on these grounds, the judge shall declare to have failed in his suit.
iast: sākṣiṇaḥ santi metyuktvā diśaityukto diśenna yaḥ jñātāraḥ santi mety*] * dharmasthaḥ kāraṇairetairhīnaṃ tamapi nirdiśet tamiti nirdiśet

sanskrit_iast: abhiyoktā na cedbrūyādbadhyo daṇḍyaśca dharmataḥ bandhyo daṇḍyaśca na cet tripakṣāt prabrūyāddharmaṃ prati parājitaḥ
meaning: अभियोक्ता न चेद्ब्रूयाद्बध्यो दण्ड्यश्च धर्मतः बन्ध्यो दण्ड्यश्च न चेत् त्रिपक्षात् प्रब्रूयाद्धर्मं प्रति पराजितः
text: If the complainant does not speak out, he shall be imprisoned and pined, according to law. If the other party does not answer within three fortnights, he becomes defeated according to law.
iast: abhiyoktā na cedbrūyādbadhyo daṇḍyaśca dharmataḥ bandhyo daṇḍyaśca na cet tripakṣāt prabrūyāddharmaṃ prati parājitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo yāvatnihnuvītārthaṃ mithyā yāvati vā vadet tau nṛpeṇa hyadharmajñau dāpyo taddviguṇaṃ damam
meaning: यो यावत्निह्नुवीतार्थं मिथ्या यावति वा वदेत् तौ नृपेण ह्यधर्मज्ञौ दाप्यो तद्द्विगुणं दमम्
text: If one falsely denies a debt, or if the other falsely demands it,—these two, proficient in dishonesty, should be made by the king to pay a fine double that sum.
iast: yo yāvatnihnuvītārthaṃ mithyā yāvati vā vadet tau nṛpeṇa hyadharmajñau dāpyo taddviguṇaṃ damam

sanskrit_iast: pṛṣṭo 'pavyayamānastu kṛtāvastho dhaneṣiṇā tryavaraiḥ sākṣibhirbhāvyo nṛpabrāhmaṇasaṃnidhau
meaning: पृष्टो 'पव्ययमानस्तु कृतावस्थो धनेषिणा त्र्यवरैः साक्षिभिर्भाव्यो नृपब्राह्मणसंनिधौ
text: On having been summoned and questioned, if one denies it,—then he siiall be convicted by the man seeking for his due by means of at least three witnesses, in the presence of the king and the brāhmaṇas.
iast: pṛṣṭo 'pavyayamānastu kṛtāvastho dhaneṣiṇā tryavaraiḥ sākṣibhirbhāvyo nṛpabrāhmaṇasaṃnidhau

sanskrit_iast: yādṛśā dhanibhiḥ kāryā vyavahāreṣu sākṣiṇaḥ tādṛśān saṃpravakṣyāmi yathā vācyaṃ ṛtaṃ ca taiḥ
meaning: यादृशा धनिभिः कार्या व्यवहारेषु साक्षिणः तादृशान् संप्रवक्ष्यामि यथा वाच्यं ऋतं च तैः
text: I shall declare now what sort of persons should be made witnesses in suits by wealthy men, and how the truth should be told by them.
iast: yādṛśā dhanibhiḥ kāryā vyavahāreṣu sākṣiṇaḥ tādṛśān saṃpravakṣyāmi yathā vācyaṃ ṛtaṃ ca taiḥ

sanskrit_iast: gṛhiṇaḥ putriṇo maulāḥ kṣatraviśśūdrayonayaḥ arthyuktāḥ sākṣyamarhanti na ye ke cidanāpadi
meaning: गृहिणः पुत्रिणो मौलाः क्षत्रविश्शूद्रयोनयः अर्थ्युक्ताः साक्ष्यमर्हन्ति न ये के चिदनापदि
text: Householders, men with sons, respectable natives, and men of the kṣatriya, vaiśya and śūdra castes are competent to act as witnesses, when cited by suitors;—and not any and every person, except in emergencies.
iast: gṛhiṇaḥ putriṇo maulāḥ kṣatraviśśūdrayonayaḥ arthyuktāḥ sākṣyamarhanti na ye ke cidanāpadi

sanskrit_iast: āptāḥ sarveṣu varṇeṣu kāryāḥ kāryeṣu sākṣiṇaḥ sarvadharmavido 'lubdhā viparītāṃstu varjayet
meaning: आप्ताः सर्वेषु वर्णेषु कार्याः कार्येषु साक्षिणः सर्वधर्मविदो 'लुब्धा विपरीतांस्तु वर्जयेत्
text: In all law-suits trustworthy men of all the castes, fully conversant with morality and free from avarice, should be made witnesses; the reverse of these should be avoided.
iast: āptāḥ sarveṣu varṇeṣu kāryāḥ kāryeṣu sākṣiṇaḥ sarvadharmavido 'lubdhā viparītāṃstu varjayet

sanskrit_iast: nārthasaṃbandhino nāptā na sahāyā na vairiṇaḥ na dṛṣṭadoṣāḥ kartavyā na vyādhyārtā na dūṣitāḥ
meaning: नार्थसंबन्धिनो नाप्ता न सहाया न वैरिणः न दृष्टदोषाः कर्तव्या न व्याध्यार्ता न दूषिताः
text: Neither interested persons, nor relations, nor helpers, nor enemies, nor persons of proved corruption, nor those afflicted with disease, nor the corrupted should be made witnesses.
iast: nārthasaṃbandhino nāptā na sahāyā na vairiṇaḥ na dṛṣṭadoṣāḥ kartavyā na vyādhyārtā na dūṣitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: na sākṣī nṛpatiḥ kāryo na kārukakuśīlavau na śrotriyo na liṅgastho na saṅgebhyo vinirgataḥ
meaning: न साक्षी नृपतिः कार्यो न कारुककुशीलवौ न श्रोत्रियो न लिङ्गस्थो न सङ्गेभ्यो विनिर्गतः
text: The king should not be made a witness; nor craftsmen, nor actors, nor a Vedic scholar, nor one in holy orders, nor one who has renounced all attachments.
iast: na sākṣī nṛpatiḥ kāryo na kārukakuśīlavau na śrotriyo na liṅgastho na saṅgebhyo vinirgataḥ

sanskrit_iast: nādhyadhīno na vaktavyo na dasyurna vikarmakṛt na vṛddho na śiśurnaiko nāntyo na vikalaindriyaḥ
meaning: नाध्यधीनो न वक्तव्यो न दस्युर्न विकर्मकृत् न वृद्धो न शिशुर्नैको नान्त्यो न विकलैन्द्रियः
text: —Not one wholly dependent, nor one under pupilage, nor a paid servant, nor one who adopts forbidden occupations, nor one too old, nor a minor, nor a single person, nor one belonging to the lowest class, nor one with defective organs;
iast: nādhyadhīno na vaktavyo na dasyurna vikarmakṛt na vṛddho na śiśurnaiko nāntyo na vikalaindriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nārto na matto naunmatto na kṣuttṛṣṇaupapīḍitaḥ na śramārto na kāmārto na kruddho nāpi taskaraḥ
meaning: नार्तो न मत्तो नौन्मत्तो न क्षुत्तृष्णौपपीडितः न श्रमार्तो न कामार्तो न क्रुद्धो नापि तस्करः
text: —Nor one afflicted, nor one intoxicated, nor one demented, nor one tormented by hunger and thirst, nor one oppressed by fatigue, nor one tormented by love, nor one who is in a rage, nor a thief.
iast: nārto na matto naunmatto na kṣuttṛṣṇaupapīḍitaḥ na śramārto na kāmārto na kruddho nāpi taskaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: strīṇāṃ sākṣyaṃ striyaḥ kuryurdvijānāṃ sadṛśā dvijāḥ śūdrāśca santaḥ śūdrāṇāṃ antyānāmantyayonayaḥ
meaning: स्त्रीणां साक्ष्यं स्त्रियः कुर्युर्द्विजानां सदृशा द्विजाः शूद्राश्च सन्तः शूद्राणां अन्त्यानामन्त्ययोनयः
text: —Woman should give evidence for women; and for twice-born persons similar twice-born men, virtuous śūdhas for śūdras, and men of the lowest caste for the lowest men.
iast: strīṇāṃ sākṣyaṃ striyaḥ kuryurdvijānāṃ sadṛśā dvijāḥ śūdrāśca santaḥ śūdrāṇāṃ antyānāmantyayonayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: anubhāvī tu yaḥ kaścit kuryāt sākṣyaṃ vivādinām antarveśmanyaraṇye vā śarīrasyāpi cātyaye
meaning: अनुभावी तु यः कश्चित् कुर्यात् साक्ष्यं विवादिनाम् अन्तर्वेश्मन्यरण्ये वा शरीरस्यापि चात्यये
text: In the case of anything done in the interior of a house, or in a forest, or in the case of injury to the body,—any person who may be cognisant of the facts may give evidence on behalf of the parties to the suit.
iast: anubhāvī tu yaḥ kaścit kuryāt sākṣyaṃ vivādinām antarveśmanyaraṇye vā śarīrasyāpi cātyaye

sanskrit_iast: striyā 'pyasaṃbhāve kāryaṃ bālena sthavireṇa vā śiṣyeṇa bandhunā vā 'pi dāsena bhṛtakena vā
meaning: स्त्रिया 'प्यसंभावे कार्यं बालेन स्थविरेण वा शिष्येण बन्धुना वा 'पि दासेन भृतकेन वा
text: In the event of (proper witnesses) not forthcoming, evidence may be given by a woman, by a minor, by an aged person, by a pupil, by a relative, by a slave, or by a servant.
iast: striyā 'pyasaṃbhāve kāryaṃ bālena sthavireṇa vā śiṣyeṇa bandhunā vā 'pi dāsena bhṛtakena vā

sanskrit_iast: bālavṛddhāturāṇāṃ ca sākṣyeṣu vadatāṃ mṛṣā jānīyādasthirāṃ vācamutsiktamanasāṃ tathā
meaning: बालवृद्धातुराणां च साक्ष्येषु वदतां मृषा जानीयादस्थिरां वाचमुत्सिक्तमनसां तथा
text: In the event of minors, aged and diseased persons deposing falsely in their evidence, the Judge should make up his mind regarding the speech being irregular; so also in the case of men with disordered minds.
iast: bālavṛddhāturāṇāṃ ca sākṣyeṣu vadatāṃ mṛṣā jānīyādasthirāṃ vācamutsiktamanasāṃ tathā

sanskrit_iast: sāhaseṣu ca sarveṣu steyasaṅgrahaṇeṣu ca vāgdaṇḍayośca pāruṣye na parīkṣeta sākṣiṇaḥ
meaning: साहसेषु च सर्वेषु स्तेयसङ्ग्रहणेषु च वाग्दण्डयोश्च पारुष्ये न परीक्षेत साक्षिणः
text: In all cases of violence, of theft and adultery, and of assault, verbal and corporeal,—he shall not investigate the character of the witnesses.
iast: sāhaseṣu ca sarveṣu steyasaṅgrahaṇeṣu ca vāgdaṇḍayośca pāruṣye na parīkṣeta sākṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bahutvaṃ parigṛhṇīyāt sākṣidvaidhe narādhipaḥ sameṣu tu guṇautkṛṣṭān guṇidvaidhe dvijottamān
meaning: बहुत्वं परिगृह्णीयात् साक्षिद्वैधे नराधिपः समेषु तु गुणौत्कृष्टान् गुणिद्वैधे द्विजोत्तमान्
text: On a conflict among witnesses, the king shall accept the majority; in the case of equality (of number) those possessed of superior qualifications; and in the case of conflict between equally qualified witnesses, the best among the twice-born.
iast: bahutvaṃ parigṛhṇīyāt sākṣidvaidhe narādhipaḥ sameṣu tu guṇautkṛṣṭān guṇidvaidhe dvijottamān

sanskrit_iast: samakṣadarśanāt sākṣyaṃ śravaṇāccaiva sidhyati tatra satyaṃ bruvan sākṣī dharmārthābhyāṃ na hīyate
meaning: समक्षदर्शनात् साक्ष्यं श्रवणाच्चैव सिध्यति तत्र सत्यं ब्रुवन् साक्षी धर्मार्थाभ्यां न हीयते
text: Evidence based upon what is directly seen and is heard is admissible; and a witness, telling the truth in such cases, does not fall off from spiritual merit or worldly prosperity.
iast: samakṣadarśanāt sākṣyaṃ śravaṇāccaiva sidhyati tatra satyaṃ bruvan sākṣī dharmārthābhyāṃ na hīyate

sanskrit_iast: sākṣī dṛṣṭaśrutādanyadvibruvannāryasaṃsadi avālg narakamabhyeti pretya svargācca hīyate
meaning: साक्षी दृष्टश्रुतादन्यद्विब्रुवन्नार्यसंसदि अवाल्ग् नरकमभ्येति प्रेत्य स्वर्गाच्च हीयते
text: A witness asserting, in an assembly of noble men, anything apart from what he has seen and heard, falls downwards into hell after death and becomes shut out from heaven.
iast: sākṣī dṛṣṭaśrutādanyadvibruvannāryasaṃsadi avālg narakamabhyeti pretya svargācca hīyate

sanskrit_iast: yatrānibaddho 'pīkṣeta śṛṇuyādvā 'pi kiṃ cana pṛṣṭastatrāpi tadbrūyādyathādṛṣṭaṃ yathāśrutam
meaning: यत्रानिबद्धो 'पीक्षेत शृणुयाद्वा 'पि किं चन पृष्टस्तत्रापि तद्ब्रूयाद्यथादृष्टं यथाश्रुतम्
text: Even though not put down as a witness, if a person happens to see or hear anything in regard to a case,—when he comes to be questioned about it, he should speak out exactly as he has seen or heard it.
iast: yatrānibaddho 'pīkṣeta śṛṇuyādvā 'pi kiṃ cana pṛṣṭastatrāpi tadbrūyādyathādṛṣṭaṃ yathāśrutam

sanskrit_iast: eko 'lubdhastu sākṣī syādbahvyaḥ śucyo 'pi na striyaḥ tvasākṣī strībuddherasthiratvāt tu doṣaiścānye 'pi ye vṛtāḥ
meaning: एको 'लुब्धस्तु साक्षी स्याद्बह्व्यः शुच्यो 'पि न स्त्रियः त्वसाक्षी स्त्रीबुद्धेरस्थिरत्वात् तु दोषैश्चान्ये 'पि ये वृताः
text: A single man, free from covetousness, may be a witness, but not many women, even though pure,—because the understanding of women is not steady,—nor other men who are tainted with defects.
iast: eko 'lubdhastu sākṣī syādbahvyaḥ śucyo 'pi na striyaḥ tvasākṣī strībuddherasthiratvāt tu doṣaiścānye 'pi ye vṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: svabhāvenaiva yadbrūyustadgrāhyaṃ vyāvahārikam ato yadanyadvibrūyurdharmārthaṃ tadapārthakam
meaning: स्वभावेनैव यद्ब्रूयुस्तद्ग्राह्यं व्यावहारिकम् अतो यदन्यद्विब्रूयुर्धर्मार्थं तदपार्थकम्
text: What the witnesses state naturally, in relation to the case, should be accepted; apart from this what they state from considerations of righteousness, is useless.
iast: svabhāvenaiva yadbrūyustadgrāhyaṃ vyāvahārikam ato yadanyadvibrūyurdharmārthaṃ tadapārthakam

sanskrit_iast: sabhāntaḥ sākṣiṇaḥ prāptānarthipratyarthisaṃnidhau prāḍvivāko 'nuyuñjīta vidhinā 'nena sāntvayan
meaning: सभान्तः साक्षिणः प्राप्तानर्थिप्रत्यर्थिसंनिधौ प्राड्विवाको 'नुयुञ्जीत विधिना 'नेन सान्त्वयन्
text: The investigating Judge shall question the witnesses assembled in the court, in the presence of the plaintiff and the defendant, gently exhorting them in the following manner.
iast: sabhāntaḥ sākṣiṇaḥ prāptānarthipratyarthisaṃnidhau prāḍvivāko 'nuyuñjīta vidhinā 'nena sāntvayan

sanskrit_iast: yaddvayoranayorvettha kārye 'smiṃśceṣṭitaṃ mithaḥ tadbrūta sarvaṃ satyena yuṣmākaṃ hyatra sākṣitā
meaning: यद्द्वयोरनयोर्वेत्थ कार्ये 'स्मिंश्चेष्टितं मिथः तद्ब्रूत सर्वं सत्येन युष्माकं ह्यत्र साक्षिता
text: ‘What you know of the mutual transaction between these two persons regarding this suit,—all that may you declare freely; since you are witnesses in this matter.’
iast: yaddvayoranayorvettha kārye 'smiṃśceṣṭitaṃ mithaḥ tadbrūta sarvaṃ satyena yuṣmākaṃ hyatra sākṣitā

sanskrit_iast: satyaṃ sākṣye bruvan sākṣī lokān āpnoty puṣkalān āpnotyaninditān iha cānuttamāṃ kīrtiṃ vāgeṣā brahmapūjitā
meaning: सत्यं साक्ष्ये ब्रुवन् साक्षी लोकान् आप्नोत्य् पुष्कलान् आप्नोत्यनिन्दितान् इह चानुत्तमां कीर्तिं वागेषा ब्रह्मपूजिता
text: ‘The witness, telling the truth in his evidence, attains irreproachable regions, also unsurpassable fame; such speech is honoured by Brahmā himself.—(81)’
iast: satyaṃ sākṣye bruvan sākṣī lokān āpnoty puṣkalān āpnotyaninditān iha cānuttamāṃ kīrtiṃ vāgeṣā brahmapūjitā

sanskrit_iast: sākṣye 'nṛtaṃ vadan pāśairbadhyate vāruṇairbhṛśam vivaśaḥ śatamājātīstasmāt sākṣyaṃ vadedṛtam
meaning: साक्ष्ये 'नृतं वदन् पाशैर्बध्यते वारुणैर्भृशम् विवशः शतमाजातीस्तस्मात् साक्ष्यं वदेदृतम्
text: ‘Stating the untruth in his evidence, he becomes firmly bound in Varuṇa’s fetters, helpless during a hundred births. One should, therefore, give true evidence.’
iast: sākṣye 'nṛtaṃ vadan pāśairbadhyate vāruṇairbhṛśam vivaśaḥ śatamājātīstasmāt sākṣyaṃ vadedṛtam

sanskrit_iast: satyena pūyate sākṣī dharmaḥ satyena vardhate tasmāt satyaṃ hi vaktavyaṃ sarvavarṇeṣu sākṣibhiḥ
meaning: सत्येन पूयते साक्षी धर्मः सत्येन वर्धते तस्मात् सत्यं हि वक्तव्यं सर्ववर्णेषु साक्षिभिः
text: ‘By truth is the witness purified, by truth does merit grow: hence the truth should be spoken by witnesses of all castes.’
iast: satyena pūyate sākṣī dharmaḥ satyena vardhate tasmāt satyaṃ hi vaktavyaṃ sarvavarṇeṣu sākṣibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: ātmaiva hyātmanaḥ sākṣī gatirātmā tathā 'tmanaḥ mā 'vamaṃsthāḥ svamātmānaṃ nṛṇāṃ sākṣiṇamuttamam
meaning: आत्मैव ह्यात्मनः साक्षी गतिरात्मा तथा 'त्मनः मा 'वमंस्थाः स्वमात्मानं नृणां साक्षिणमुत्तमम्
text: ‘The soul itself is the soul’s witness, and the soul itself is the soul’s refuge; disregard not your soul, the best witness of man.’
iast: ātmaiva hyātmanaḥ sākṣī gatirātmā tathā 'tmanaḥ mā 'vamaṃsthāḥ svamātmānaṃ nṛṇāṃ sākṣiṇamuttamam

sanskrit_iast: manyante vai pāpakṛto na kaścit paśyatīti naḥ tāṃstu devāḥ prapaśyanti svasyaivāntarapūruṣaḥ
meaning: मन्यन्ते वै पापकृतो न कश्चित् पश्यतीति नः तांस्तु देवाः प्रपश्यन्ति स्वस्यैवान्तरपूरुषः
text: ‘The sinners indeed think that “no one sees us”; but the gods see them, as also their own inner personality.’
iast: manyante vai pāpakṛto na kaścit paśyatīti naḥ tāṃstu devāḥ prapaśyanti svasyaivāntarapūruṣaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dyaurbhūmirāpo hṛdayaṃ candrārkāgniyamānilāḥ rātriḥ saṃdhye ca dharmaśca vṛttajñāḥ sarvadehinām
meaning: द्यौर्भूमिरापो हृदयं चन्द्रार्काग्नियमानिलाः रात्रिः संध्ये च धर्मश्च वृत्तज्ञाः सर्वदेहिनाम्
text: ‘Heaven, earth, water, heart, moon, sun, fire, death-god, wind, night, the two twilights, and morality know the conduct of all corporeal beings.’
iast: dyaurbhūmirāpo hṛdayaṃ candrārkāgniyamānilāḥ rātriḥ saṃdhye ca dharmaśca vṛttajñāḥ sarvadehinām

sanskrit_iast: devabrāhmaṇasāṃnidhye sākṣyaṃ pṛcchedṛtaṃ dvijān udalgmukhān prālgmukhān vā pūrvāhṇe vai śuciḥ śucīn
meaning: देवब्राह्मणसांनिध्ये साक्ष्यं पृच्छेदृतं द्विजान् उदल्ग्मुखान् प्राल्ग्मुखान् वा पूर्वाह्णे वै शुचिः शुचीन्
text: In the presence of gods and Brāhmaṇas, during forenoon, the judge, pure himself, shall ask the twice-born persons, who have been purified and are facing either the north or the east, to give evidence.
iast: devabrāhmaṇasāṃnidhye sākṣyaṃ pṛcchedṛtaṃ dvijān udalgmukhān prālgmukhān vā pūrvāhṇe vai śuciḥ śucīn

sanskrit_iast: brūhīti brāhmaṇaṃ pṛcchet satyaṃ brūhīti pārthivam gobījakāñcanairvaiśyaṃ śūdraṃ sarvaistu pātakaiḥ
meaning: ब्रूहीति ब्राह्मणं पृच्छेत् सत्यं ब्रूहीति पार्थिवम् गोबीजकाञ्चनैर्वैश्यं शूद्रं सर्वैस्तु पातकैः
text: He shall question the Brāhmaṇa with the word ‘speak,’ the Kṣatriya with ‘speak out the truth,’ the Vaiśya by sins pertaining to kine, grain and food, and the Śūdra by all the sins.
iast: brūhīti brāhmaṇaṃ pṛcchet satyaṃ brūhīti pārthivam gobījakāñcanairvaiśyaṃ śūdraṃ sarvaistu pātakaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: brahmaghno ye smṛtā lokā ye ca strībālaghātinaḥ mitradruhaḥ kṛtaghnasya te te syurbruvato mṛṣā
meaning: ब्रह्मघ्नो ये स्मृता लोका ये च स्त्रीबालघातिनः मित्रद्रुहः कृतघ्नस्य ते ते स्युर्ब्रुवतो मृषा
text: ‘Whatever regions have been assigned to the slayer of the Brāhmaṇa, to the murderer of women and children, to the betrayer of friends and to the ingrate,—those same shall be thine if thou speakest falsely.’
iast: brahmaghno ye smṛtā lokā ye ca strībālaghātinaḥ mitradruhaḥ kṛtaghnasya te te syurbruvato mṛṣā

sanskrit_iast: janmaprabhṛti yat kiṃ cit puṇyaṃ bhadra tvayā kṛtam tat te sarvaṃ śuno gacchedyadi brūyāstvamanyathā
meaning: जन्मप्रभृति यत् किं चित् पुण्यं भद्र त्वया कृतम् तत् ते सर्वं शुनो गच्छेद्यदि ब्रूयास्त्वमन्यथा
text: ‘Whatever merit, good man, you may have acquired since your birth, would go to the dogs, if you speak falsely.’
iast: janmaprabhṛti yat kiṃ cit puṇyaṃ bhadra tvayā kṛtam tat te sarvaṃ śuno gacchedyadi brūyāstvamanyathā

sanskrit_iast: eko 'hamasmītyātmānaṃ yastvaṃ kalyāṇa manyase nityaṃ sthitaste hṛdyeṣa puṇyapāpaikṣitā muniḥ
meaning: एको 'हमस्मीत्यात्मानं यस्त्वं कल्याण मन्यसे नित्यं स्थितस्ते हृद्येष पुण्यपापैक्षिता मुनिः
text: ‘You think yourself, blessed man, that “I am alone”; but there ever sits in your heart the silent watcher of virtue and vice.’
iast: eko 'hamasmītyātmānaṃ yastvaṃ kalyāṇa manyase nityaṃ sthitaste hṛdyeṣa puṇyapāpaikṣitā muniḥ

sanskrit_iast: yamo vaivasvato devo yastavaiṣa hṛdi sthitaḥ tena cedavivādaste mā gaṅgāṃ mā kurūn gamaḥ
meaning: यमो वैवस्वतो देवो यस्तवैष हृदि स्थितः तेन चेदविवादस्ते मा गङ्गां मा कुरून् गमः
text: ‘The, god Yama, the son of Vivasvat, who sits in your heart,—if you have no quarrel with him, you need not visit the Gaṅgā, nor the Kurus.
iast: yamo vaivasvato devo yastavaiṣa hṛdi sthitaḥ tena cedavivādaste mā gaṅgāṃ mā kurūn gamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: nagno muṇḍaḥ kapālena ca bhikṣārthī kṣutpipāsitaḥ kapālī andhaḥ śatrukulaṃ gacchedyaḥ sākṣyamanṛtaṃ vadet
meaning: नग्नो मुण्डः कपालेन च भिक्षार्थी क्षुत्पिपासितः कपाली अन्धः शत्रुकुलं गच्छेद्यः साक्ष्यमनृतं वदेत्
text: ‘He who gives false evidence shall go for alms, with a potsherd, to the house of his enemy,—naked and shorn, tormented with hunger and thirst, and blind.’
iast: nagno muṇḍaḥ kapālena ca bhikṣārthī kṣutpipāsitaḥ kapālī andhaḥ śatrukulaṃ gacchedyaḥ sākṣyamanṛtaṃ vadet

sanskrit_iast: avākṣirāstamasyandhe kilbiṣī narakaṃ vrajet yaḥ praśnaṃ vitathaṃ brūyāt pṛṣṭaḥ san dharmaniścaye
meaning: अवाक्षिरास्तमस्यन्धे किल्बिषी नरकं व्रजेत् यः प्रश्नं वितथं ब्रूयात् पृष्टः सन् धर्मनिश्चये
text: ‘Headlong, in blind darkness shall the sinner fall into hell, who, on being interrogated in the course of a judicial investigation, answers the question falsely.
iast: avākṣirāstamasyandhe kilbiṣī narakaṃ vrajet yaḥ praśnaṃ vitathaṃ brūyāt pṛṣṭaḥ san dharmaniścaye

sanskrit_iast: andho matsyānivāśnāti sa naraḥ kaṇṭakaiḥ saha yo bhāṣate 'rthavaikalyamapratyakṣaṃ sabhāṃ gataḥ
meaning: अन्धो मत्स्यानिवाश्नाति स नरः कण्टकैः सह यो भाषते 'र्थवैकल्यमप्रत्यक्षं सभां गतः
text: ‘He who, having entered the court, bears testimony to what is contrary to facts and what he has not seen, swallows fish along with the bones,—just like a blind man.’
iast: andho matsyānivāśnāti sa naraḥ kaṇṭakaiḥ saha yo bhāṣate 'rthavaikalyamapratyakṣaṃ sabhāṃ gataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasya vidvān hi vadataḥ kṣetrajño nābhiśaṅkate nātiśaṅkate tasmānna devāḥ śreyāṃsaṃ loke 'nyaṃ puruṣaṃ viduḥ
meaning: यस्य विद्वान् हि वदतः क्षेत्रज्ञो नाभिशङ्कते नातिशङ्कते तस्मान्न देवाः श्रेयांसं लोके 'न्यं पुरुषं विदुः
text: ‘The gods do not regard any person in this world as superior to him, whom his knowing soul does not distrust, while he is speaking.’
iast: yasya vidvān hi vadataḥ kṣetrajño nābhiśaṅkate nātiśaṅkate tasmānna devāḥ śreyāṃsaṃ loke 'nyaṃ puruṣaṃ viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāvato bāndhavān yasmin hanti sākṣye 'nṛtaṃ vadan tāvataḥ saṅkhyayā tasmin śṛṇu saumyānupūrvaśaḥ
meaning: यावतो बान्धवान् यस्मिन् हन्ति साक्ष्ये 'नृतं वदन् तावतः सङ्ख्यया तस्मिन् शृणु सौम्यानुपूर्वशः
text: ‘Listen now, gentle friend, in due order, how many relatives, by number, one destroys by giving false evidence, in what cases.’
iast: yāvato bāndhavān yasmin hanti sākṣye 'nṛtaṃ vadan tāvataḥ saṅkhyayā tasmin śṛṇu saumyānupūrvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pañca paśuanṛte hanti daśa hanti gavānṛte śatamaśvānṛte hanti sahasraṃ puruṣānṛte
meaning: पञ्च पशुअनृते हन्ति दश हन्ति गवानृते शतमश्वानृते हन्ति सहस्रं पुरुषानृते
text: ‘He destroys five by false evidence regarding animals; he destroys ten by false evidence regarding kine; he destroys a hundred by false evidence regarding horses, and a thousand by false evidence regarding men.’
iast: pañca paśuanṛte hanti daśa hanti gavānṛte śatamaśvānṛte hanti sahasraṃ puruṣānṛte

sanskrit_iast: hanti jātānajātāṃśca hiraṇyārthe 'nṛtaṃ vadan sarvaṃ bhūmianṛte hanti mā sma bhūmianṛtaṃ vadīḥ
meaning: हन्ति जातानजातांश्च हिरण्यार्थे 'नृतं वदन् सर्वं भूमिअनृते हन्ति मा स्म भूमिअनृतं वदीः
text: ‘Deposing falsely in regard to gold, he kills the born as well as the unborn; by false evidence regarding land, he kills all; never tell a lie regarding land.’
iast: hanti jātānajātāṃśca hiraṇyārthe 'nṛtaṃ vadan sarvaṃ bhūmianṛte hanti mā sma bhūmianṛtaṃ vadīḥ

sanskrit_iast: apsu bhūmivadityāhuḥ strīṇāṃ bhoge ca maithune abjeṣu caiva ratneṣu sarveṣvaśmamayeṣu ca
meaning: अप्सु भूमिवदित्याहुः स्त्रीणां भोगे च मैथुने अब्जेषु चैव रत्नेषु सर्वेष्वश्ममयेषु च
text: ‘That concerning water they declare to be similar to that concerning land; as also that relating to the sexual enjoyment of women, and to gems, water-born as well as granitic.’
iast: apsu bhūmivadityāhuḥ strīṇāṃ bhoge ca maithune abjeṣu caiva ratneṣu sarveṣvaśmamayeṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: etān doṣānavekṣya tvaṃ sarvānanṛtabhāṣaṇe yathāśrutaṃ yathādṛṣṭaṃ sarvamevāñjasā vada
meaning: एतान् दोषानवेक्ष्य त्वं सर्वाननृतभाषणे यथाश्रुतं यथादृष्टं सर्वमेवाञ्जसा वद
text: ‘Having noticed all these evils proceeding prom perjury, speak out directly everything exactly what you have seen and heard.’
iast: etān doṣānavekṣya tvaṃ sarvānanṛtabhāṣaṇe yathāśrutaṃ yathādṛṣṭaṃ sarvamevāñjasā vada

sanskrit_iast: gorakṣakān vāṇijikāṃḥ tathā kārukuśīlavān vāṇijakāṃs preṣyān vārdhuṣikāṃścaiva viprān śūdravadācaret
meaning: गोरक्षकान् वाणिजिकांः तथा कारुकुशीलवान् वाणिजकांस् प्रेष्यान् वार्धुषिकांश्चैव विप्रान् शूद्रवदाचरेत्
text: ‘He shall treat like Śūdras the Brāhmaṇas who tend cattle, who engage in trade, and who are craftsmen, actors, menial servants ok money-lenders.’
iast: gorakṣakān vāṇijikāṃḥ tathā kārukuśīlavān vāṇijakāṃs preṣyān vārdhuṣikāṃścaiva viprān śūdravadācaret

sanskrit_iast: tadvadan dharmato 'rtheṣu jānannapyanythā naraḥ na svargāccyavate lokāddaivīṃ vācaṃ vadanti tām
meaning: तद्वदन् धर्मतो 'र्थेषु जानन्नप्यन्य्था नरः न स्वर्गाच्च्यवते लोकाद्दैवीं वाचं वदन्ति ताम्
text: In some cases, a man who, though knowing the truth, deposes otherwise, through piety, does not fall off from heaven. This is a divine assertion that they reproduce.
iast: tadvadan dharmato 'rtheṣu jānannapyanythā naraḥ na svargāccyavate lokāddaivīṃ vācaṃ vadanti tām

sanskrit_iast: śūdraviśkṣatraviprāṇāṃ yatraṛtauktau bhavedvadhaḥ tatra vaktavyamanṛtaṃ taddhi satyādviśiṣyate
meaning: शूद्रविश्क्षत्रविप्राणां यत्रऋतौक्तौ भवेद्वधः तत्र वक्तव्यमनृतं तद्धि सत्याद्विशिष्यते
text: Where the telling of the truth would lead to the death of a Śūdra, a Vaiśva, a Kṣatriya or a Brāhmaṇa,—in that case falsehood should be spoken; as that is preferable to truth.
iast: śūdraviśkṣatraviprāṇāṃ yatraṛtauktau bhavedvadhaḥ tatra vaktavyamanṛtaṃ taddhi satyādviśiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: vācdaivatyaiśca carubhiryajeraṃste sarasvatīm anṛtasyainasastasya kurvāṇā niṣkṛtiṃ parām
meaning: वाच्दैवत्यैश्च चरुभिर्यजेरंस्ते सरस्वतीम् अनृतस्यैनसस्तस्य कुर्वाणा निष्कृतिं पराम्
text: They should offer sacrifices to Sarasvati with half-boiled rice dedicated to the speech-goddess,—doing the best expiation for the sin of untruthfulness.
iast: vācdaivatyaiśca carubhiryajeraṃste sarasvatīm anṛtasyainasastasya kurvāṇā niṣkṛtiṃ parām

sanskrit_iast: kūṣmāṇḍairvā 'pi juhuyādghṛtamagnau yathāvidhi udity ṛcā vā vāruṇyā tṛcenāpdaivatena vā
meaning: कूष्माण्डैर्वा 'पि जुहुयाद्घृतमग्नौ यथाविधि उदित्य् ऋचा वा वारुण्या तृचेनाप्दैवतेन वा
text: Or he shall offer according to rule, clarified butter into the fire, with the ‘kuṣmāṇḍa’-texts or with the verse ‘ut, etc.’ sacred to varuṇa, or with the three verses sacred to the waters.
iast: kūṣmāṇḍairvā 'pi juhuyādghṛtamagnau yathāvidhi udity ṛcā vā vāruṇyā tṛcenāpdaivatena vā

sanskrit_iast: tripakṣādabruvan sākṣyaṃ ṛṇādiṣu naro 'gadaḥ tadṛṇaṃ prāpnuyāt sarvaṃ daśabandhaṃ ca sarvataḥ
meaning: त्रिपक्षादब्रुवन् साक्ष्यं ऋणादिषु नरो 'गदः तदृणं प्राप्नुयात् सर्वं दशबन्धं च सर्वतः
text: The man, who, without being ill, does not give evidence for three fortnights, in regard to debts and other matters, should hear that entire debt, as also a penalty of the tenth part in all cases.
iast: tripakṣādabruvan sākṣyaṃ ṛṇādiṣu naro 'gadaḥ tadṛṇaṃ prāpnuyāt sarvaṃ daśabandhaṃ ca sarvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasya dṛśyeta saptāhāduktavākyasya sākṣiṇaḥ rogo 'gnirjñātimaraṇaṃ ṛṇaṃ dāpyo damaṃ ca saḥ
meaning: यस्य दृश्येत सप्ताहादुक्तवाक्यस्य साक्षिणः रोगो 'ग्निर्ज्ञातिमरणं ऋणं दाप्यो दमं च सः
text: That witness,—who may be found, within a week of having given evidence, to suffer from sickness, fire or the death of a relative,—should be made to pay the debt and also the penalty.
iast: yasya dṛśyeta saptāhāduktavākyasya sākṣiṇaḥ rogo 'gnirjñātimaraṇaṃ ṛṇaṃ dāpyo damaṃ ca saḥ

sanskrit_iast: asākṣikeṣu tvartheṣu mitho vivādamānayoḥ avindaṃstattvataḥ satyaṃ śapathenāpi lambhayet
meaning: असाक्षिकेषु त्वर्थेषु मिथो विवादमानयोः अविन्दंस्तत्त्वतः सत्यं शपथेनापि लम्भयेत्
text: In witness-less cases, if he cannot get at the truth between the two disputants by any means, he should discover it by means of oath.
iast: asākṣikeṣu tvartheṣu mitho vivādamānayoḥ avindaṃstattvataḥ satyaṃ śapathenāpi lambhayet

sanskrit_iast: maharṣibhiśca devaiśca kāryārthaṃ śapathāḥ kṛtāḥ vasiṣṭhaścāpi śapathaṃ śepe paijavane nṛpe
meaning: महर्षिभिश्च देवैश्च कार्यार्थं शपथाः कृताः वसिष्ठश्चापि शपथं शेपे पैजवने नृपे
text: By the great sages, as well as by the Gods, oaths have been taken for the purposes of a case; Vasiṣṭha even swore an oath before the King Paijavana.
iast: maharṣibhiśca devaiśca kāryārthaṃ śapathāḥ kṛtāḥ vasiṣṭhaścāpi śapathaṃ śepe paijavane nṛpe

sanskrit_iast: na vṛthā śapathaṃ kuryāt svalpe 'pyarthe naro budhaḥ vṛthā hi śapathaṃ kurvan pretya caiha ca naśyati
meaning: न वृथा शपथं कुर्यात् स्वल्पे 'प्यर्थे नरो बुधः वृथा हि शपथं कुर्वन् प्रेत्य चैह च नश्यति
