text: If he catches hold of the hair, the king shall unhesitatingly have his hands out off; also if he lays hold of the feet, the beard, the neck, or the scrotum.
iast: keśeṣu gṛhṇato hastau chedayedavicārayan pādayordāḍhikāyāṃ ca grīvāyāṃ vṛṣaṇeṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: tvagbhedakaḥ śataṃ daṇḍyo lohitasya ca darśakaḥ māṃsabhettā tu ṣaṭniṣkān pravāsyastvasthibhedakaḥ
meaning: त्वग्भेदकः शतं दण्ड्यो लोहितस्य च दर्शकः मांसभेत्ता तु षट्निष्कान् प्रवास्यस्त्वस्थिभेदकः
text: One who bruises the skin should be fined one hundred; as also one who fetches blood; he who cuts the flesh, six ‘niṣkas’ and the bone-breaker should be banished.
iast: tvagbhedakaḥ śataṃ daṇḍyo lohitasya ca darśakaḥ māṃsabhettā tu ṣaṭniṣkān pravāsyastvasthibhedakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vanaspatīnāṃ sarveṣāmupabhogo yathā yathā yathā tathā damaḥ kāryo hiṃsāyāmiti dhāraṇā
meaning: वनस्पतीनां सर्वेषामुपभोगो यथा यथा यथा तथा दमः कार्यो हिंसायामिति धारणा
text: In the case of all trees, as their usefulness so should be the punishment inflicted for injuring them; this is the settled rule.
iast: vanaspatīnāṃ sarveṣāmupabhogo yathā yathā yathā tathā damaḥ kāryo hiṃsāyāmiti dhāraṇā

sanskrit_iast: manuṣyāṇāṃ paśūnāṃ ca duḥkhāya prahṛte sati yathā yathā mahadduḥkhaṃ daṇḍaṃ kuryāt tathā tathā
meaning: मनुष्याणां पशूनां च दुःखाय प्रहृते सति यथा यथा महद्दुःखं दण्डं कुर्यात् तथा तथा
text: When a hurt has been inflicted on men or animals, with the motive of causing pain, the king shall inflict punishment in proportion to the greatness of the pain caused.
iast: manuṣyāṇāṃ paśūnāṃ ca duḥkhāya prahṛte sati yathā yathā mahadduḥkhaṃ daṇḍaṃ kuryāt tathā tathā

sanskrit_iast: aṅgāvapīḍanāyāṃ ca vraṇaśonitayostathā prāṇaśonitayoḥ samutthānavyayaṃ dāpyaḥ sarvadaṇḍamathāpi vā
meaning: अङ्गावपीडनायां च व्रणशोनितयोस्तथा प्राणशोनितयोः समुत्थानव्ययं दाप्यः सर्वदण्डमथापि वा
text: In the case of injury to limbs, as also of strength and of blood,—the man should be made to pay the expenses of recovery, or the whole amount as ‘fine.’
iast: aṅgāvapīḍanāyāṃ ca vraṇaśonitayostathā prāṇaśonitayoḥ samutthānavyayaṃ dāpyaḥ sarvadaṇḍamathāpi vā

sanskrit_iast: dravyāṇi hiṃsyādyo yasya jñānato 'jñānato 'pi vā sa tasyautpādayet tuṣṭiṃ rājñe dadyācca tatsamam
meaning: द्रव्याणि हिंस्याद्यो यस्य ज्ञानतो 'ज्ञानतो 'पि वा स तस्यौत्पादयेत् तुष्टिं राज्ञे दद्याच्च तत्समम्
text: When a man, either intentionally or unintentionally, damages the goods of another, he shall give satisfaction to him and pay to the king a fine equal to it.
iast: dravyāṇi hiṃsyādyo yasya jñānato 'jñānato 'pi vā sa tasyautpādayet tuṣṭiṃ rājñe dadyācca tatsamam

sanskrit_iast: carmacārmikabhāṇḍeṣu kāṣṭhaloṣṭamayeṣu mūlyāt pañcaguṇo daṇḍaḥ puṣpamūlaphaleṣu ca
meaning: चर्मचार्मिकभाण्डेषु काष्ठलोष्टमयेषु मूल्यात् पञ्चगुणो दण्डः पुष्पमूलफलेषु च
text: In the case or leather and leathern vessels, and in that of those made of wood or clay, the fine shall be five times their value; as also in the case of flowers, roots and fruits.
iast: carmacārmikabhāṇḍeṣu kāṣṭhaloṣṭamayeṣu mūlyāt pañcaguṇo daṇḍaḥ puṣpamūlaphaleṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: yānasya caiva yātuśca yānasvāmina eva ca daśātivartanānyāhuḥ śeṣe daṇḍo vidhīyate
meaning: यानस्य चैव यातुश्च यानस्वामिन एव च दशातिवर्तनान्याहुः शेषे दण्डो विधीयते
text: In the case of the conveyance, its rider and its owner, they lay down ten exclusions; for the rest penalties are prescribed.
iast: yānasya caiva yātuśca yānasvāmina eva ca daśātivartanānyāhuḥ śeṣe daṇḍo vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: chinnanāsye bhagnayuge tiryakpratimukhāgate akṣabhaṅge ca yānasya cakrabhaṅge tathaiva ca
meaning: छिन्ननास्ये भग्नयुगे तिर्यक्प्रतिमुखागते अक्षभङ्गे च यानस्य चक्रभङ्गे तथैव च
text: When there is snapping of the nose-string, when the yoke is broken, when it turns sideways or backwards, when the axle breaks, and when the wheel is broken; (291) when the fittings or the yoking strap or the bridle are torn, and when there has been the loud cry ‘get out of the way,’—there is no punishment; so has Manu declared
iast: chinnanāsye bhagnayuge tiryakpratimukhāgate akṣabhaṅge ca yānasya cakrabhaṅge tathaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: chedane caiva yantrāṇāṃ yoktraraśmyostathaiva ca ākrande cāpyapaihīti na daṇḍaṃ manurabravīt
meaning: छेदने चैव यन्त्राणां योक्त्ररश्म्योस्तथैव च आक्रन्दे चाप्यपैहीति न दण्डं मनुरब्रवीत्
text: When there is snapping of the nose-string, when the yoke is broken, when it turns sideways or backwards, when the axle breaks, and when the wheel is broken; (291) when the fittings or the yoking strap or the bridle are torn, and when there has been the loud cry ‘get out of the way,’—there is no punishment; so has Manu declared
iast: chedane caiva yantrāṇāṃ yoktraraśmyostathaiva ca ākrande cāpyapaihīti na daṇḍaṃ manurabravīt

sanskrit_iast: yatrāpavartate yugyaṃ vaiguṇyāt prājakasya tu tatra svāmī bhaveddaṇḍyo hiṃsāyāṃ dviśataṃ damam
meaning: यत्रापवर्तते युग्यं वैगुण्यात् प्राजकस्य तु तत्र स्वामी भवेद्दण्ड्यो हिंसायां द्विशतं दमम्
text: When however, on account of the driver’s ineptitude, the cart turns off and causes injury, the owner shall be punished with a fine of two hundred.—(293) If the driver is a trained one, it is he that should be punished; if the driver is untrained, all the occupants of the cart should be fined a hundred each.
iast: yatrāpavartate yugyaṃ vaiguṇyāt prājakasya tu tatra svāmī bhaveddaṇḍyo hiṃsāyāṃ dviśataṃ damam

sanskrit_iast: prājakaścedbhavedāptaḥ prājako daṇḍamarhati yugyasthāḥ prājake 'nāpte sarve daṇḍyāḥ śataṃ śatam
meaning: प्राजकश्चेद्भवेदाप्तः प्राजको दण्डमर्हति युग्यस्थाः प्राजके 'नाप्ते सर्वे दण्ड्याः शतं शतम्
text: When however, on account of the driver’s ineptitude, the cart turns off and causes injury, the owner shall be punished with a fine of two hundred.—(293) If the driver is a trained one, it is he that should be punished; if the driver is untrained, all the occupants of the cart should be fined a hundred each.
iast: prājakaścedbhavedāptaḥ prājako daṇḍamarhati yugyasthāḥ prājake 'nāpte sarve daṇḍyāḥ śataṃ śatam

sanskrit_iast: sa cet tu pathi saṃruddhaḥ paśubhirvā rathena vā pramāpayet prāṇabhṛtastatra daṇḍo 'vicāritaḥ
meaning: स चेत् तु पथि संरुद्धः पशुभिर्वा रथेन वा प्रमापयेत् प्राणभृतस्तत्र दण्डो 'विचारितः
text: But if he happens to be stopped on the road and causes the death of a living being, either through animals or through the cart,—in such a case there is no doubt regarding punishment.
iast: sa cet tu pathi saṃruddhaḥ paśubhirvā rathena vā pramāpayet prāṇabhṛtastatra daṇḍo 'vicāritaḥ

sanskrit_iast: manuṣyamāraṇe kṣipraṃ cauravat kilbiṣaṃ bhavet prāṇabhṛtsu mahatsvardhaṃ gogajauṣṭrahayādiṣu
meaning: मनुष्यमारणे क्षिप्रं चौरवत् किल्बिषं भवेत् प्राणभृत्सु महत्स्वर्धं गोगजौष्ट्रहयादिषु
text: If the case of a man being killed, on the spot, the guilt would be similar to that of the thief; and half of that in the case of the larger animals, such as cows, elephants, camels, horses and the like.
iast: manuṣyamāraṇe kṣipraṃ cauravat kilbiṣaṃ bhavet prāṇabhṛtsu mahatsvardhaṃ gogajauṣṭrahayādiṣu

sanskrit_iast: kṣudrakāṇāṃ paśūnāṃ tu hiṃsāyāṃ dviśato damaḥ pañcāśat tu bhaveddaṇḍaḥ śubheṣu mṛgapakṣiṣu
meaning: क्षुद्रकाणां पशूनां तु हिंसायां द्विशतो दमः पञ्चाशत् तु भवेद्दण्डः शुभेषु मृगपक्षिषु
text: In the case of hurting petty animals, the fine is two h undred; and fifty in the case of the auspicious quadrupeds and birds.
iast: kṣudrakāṇāṃ paśūnāṃ tu hiṃsāyāṃ dviśato damaḥ pañcāśat tu bhaveddaṇḍaḥ śubheṣu mṛgapakṣiṣu

sanskrit_iast: gardhabhājāvikānāṃ tu daṇḍaḥ syāt pañcamāṣikaḥ pāñcamāṣikaḥ māṣikastu bhaveddaṇḍaḥ śvasūkaranipātane
meaning: गर्धभाजाविकानां तु दण्डः स्यात् पञ्चमाषिकः पाञ्चमाषिकः माषिकस्तु भवेद्दण्डः श्वसूकरनिपातने
text: In the case of donkeys, goat and sheep the fine shall consist of five ‘māṣas’; and the fine shall be one ‘māṣa’ for the killing of a dog ok a pig.
iast: gardhabhājāvikānāṃ tu daṇḍaḥ syāt pañcamāṣikaḥ pāñcamāṣikaḥ māṣikastu bhaveddaṇḍaḥ śvasūkaranipātane

sanskrit_iast: bhāryā putraśca dāsaśca preṣyo bhrātrā ca saudaraḥ prāptāparādhāstāḍyāḥ syū rajjvā veṇudalena vā
meaning: भार्या पुत्रश्च दासश्च प्रेष्यो भ्रात्रा च सौदरः प्राप्तापराधास्ताड्याः स्यू रज्ज्वा वेणुदलेन वा
text: The wife, the son, the slave, the servant and the uterine brother shall be beaten with a rope or a split bamboo, when they have committed a fault.
iast: bhāryā putraśca dāsaśca preṣyo bhrātrā ca saudaraḥ prāptāparādhāstāḍyāḥ syū rajjvā veṇudalena vā

sanskrit_iast: pṛṣṭhatastu śarīrasya nauttamāṅge kathaṃ cana ato 'nyathā tu praharan prāptaḥ syāccaurakilbiṣam
meaning: पृष्ठतस्तु शरीरस्य नौत्तमाङ्गे कथं चन अतो 'न्यथा तु प्रहरन् प्राप्तः स्याच्चौरकिल्बिषम्
text: But only on the back part of the body, and never on the upper part; he who strikes otherwise than this incurs the guilt of a thief.
iast: pṛṣṭhatastu śarīrasya nauttamāṅge kathaṃ cana ato 'nyathā tu praharan prāptaḥ syāccaurakilbiṣam

sanskrit_iast: eṣo 'khilenābhihito daṇḍapāruṣyanirṇayaḥ stenasyātaḥ pravakṣyāmi vidhiṃ daṇḍavinirṇaye
meaning: एषो 'खिलेनाभिहितो दण्डपारुष्यनिर्णयः स्तेनस्यातः प्रवक्ष्यामि विधिं दण्डविनिर्णये
text: Thus has the law relating to physical assault been fully explained; after this I am going to expound the law for the regulating of punishments in cases of theft.
iast: eṣo 'khilenābhihito daṇḍapāruṣyanirṇayaḥ stenasyātaḥ pravakṣyāmi vidhiṃ daṇḍavinirṇaye

sanskrit_iast: paramaṃ yatnamātiṣṭhet stenānāṃ nigrahe nṛpaḥ stenānāṃ nigrahādasya yaśo rāṣṭraṃ ca vardhate
meaning: परमं यत्नमातिष्ठेत् स्तेनानां निग्रहे नृपः स्तेनानां निग्रहादस्य यशो राष्ट्रं च वर्धते
text: The King shall, make the best efforts for suppressing thieves; by the suppression of thieves comes fame and the kingdom prospers.
iast: paramaṃ yatnamātiṣṭhet stenānāṃ nigrahe nṛpaḥ stenānāṃ nigrahādasya yaśo rāṣṭraṃ ca vardhate

sanskrit_iast: abhayasya hi yo dātā sa pūjyaḥ satataṃ nṛpaḥ sattraṃ hi vardhate tasya sadaivābhayadakṣiṇam
meaning: अभयस्य हि यो दाता स पूज्यः सततं नृपः सत्त्रं हि वर्धते तस्य सदैवाभयदक्षिणम्
text: The King who imparts security is ever to be honoured; his sacrificial session constantly prospers, accompanied as it is by the gift of ‘security.’
iast: abhayasya hi yo dātā sa pūjyaḥ satataṃ nṛpaḥ sattraṃ hi vardhate tasya sadaivābhayadakṣiṇam

sanskrit_iast: sarvato dharmaṣaḍbhāgo rājño bhavati rakṣataḥ adharmādapi ṣaḍbhāgo bhavatyasya hyarakṣataḥ
meaning: सर्वतो धर्मषड्भागो राज्ञो भवति रक्षतः अधर्मादपि षड्भागो भवत्यस्य ह्यरक्षतः
text: To the King who protects (his people) accrues the sixth part of the spiritual merit of all persons; and the sixth of their demerit also accrues to him, if he protects them not
iast: sarvato dharmaṣaḍbhāgo rājño bhavati rakṣataḥ adharmādapi ṣaḍbhāgo bhavatyasya hyarakṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadadhīte yadyajate yaddadāti yadarcati tasya ṣaḍbhāgabhāg rājā samyag bhavati rakṣaṇāt
meaning: यदधीते यद्यजते यद्ददाति यदर्चति तस्य षड्भागभाग् राजा सम्यग् भवति रक्षणात्
text: When one reads the Veda, when one performs a sacrifice, when one makes gifts, when one worships,—to the sixth part of each of those the king becomes entitled, in consequence of properly protecting the people
iast: yadadhīte yadyajate yaddadāti yadarcati tasya ṣaḍbhāgabhāg rājā samyag bhavati rakṣaṇāt

sanskrit_iast: rakṣan dharmeṇa bhūtāni rājā vadhyāṃśca ghātayan yajate 'haraharyajñaiḥ sahasraśatadakṣiṇaiḥ
meaning: रक्षन् धर्मेण भूतानि राजा वध्यांश्च घातयन् यजते 'हरहर्यज्ञैः सहस्रशतदक्षिणैः
text: The king who, according to the law, protects all creatures and strikes them who deserve to be struck, offers, day by day, sacrifices at which hundreds of thousands are given away.
iast: rakṣan dharmeṇa bhūtāni rājā vadhyāṃśca ghātayan yajate 'haraharyajñaiḥ sahasraśatadakṣiṇaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo 'rakṣan balimādatte karaṃ śulkaṃ ca pārthivaḥ pratibhāgaṃ ca daṇḍaṃ ca sa sadyo narakaṃ vrajet
meaning: यो 'रक्षन् बलिमादत्ते करं शुल्कं च पार्थिवः प्रतिभागं च दण्डं च स सद्यो नरकं व्रजेत्
text: The king, who, without affording protection, takes tributes, taxes, duties, presents and fines, would immediately sink into hell.
iast: yo 'rakṣan balimādatte karaṃ śulkaṃ ca pārthivaḥ pratibhāgaṃ ca daṇḍaṃ ca sa sadyo narakaṃ vrajet

sanskrit_iast: arakṣitāraṃ rājānaṃ baliṣaḍbhāgahāriṇam arakṣitāramattāraṃ tamāhuḥ sarvalokasya samagramalahārakam
meaning: अरक्षितारं राजानं बलिषड्भागहारिणम् अरक्षितारमत्तारं तमाहुः सर्वलोकस्य समग्रमलहारकम्
text: He who affords no protection and devours the people, grabbing his tribute of the sixth part of the produce,—him they declare to be the imbiber of the filth of the whole people.
iast: arakṣitāraṃ rājānaṃ baliṣaḍbhāgahāriṇam arakṣitāramattāraṃ tamāhuḥ sarvalokasya samagramalahārakam

sanskrit_iast: anapekṣitamaryādaṃ nāstikaṃ vipraluṃpakam 'navekṣitamaryādaṃ arakṣitāramattāraṃ nṛpaṃ vidyādadhogatim
meaning: अनपेक्षितमर्यादं नास्तिकं विप्रलुंपकम् 'नवेक्षितमर्यादं अरक्षितारमत्तारं नृपं विद्यादधोगतिम्
text: He who heeds not the bounds of morality, who is a disbeliever, who is extortionate, who does not afford protection, and is grabbing,—such a king one should regard as doomed to perdition.
iast: anapekṣitamaryādaṃ nāstikaṃ vipraluṃpakam 'navekṣitamaryādaṃ arakṣitāramattāraṃ nṛpaṃ vidyādadhogatim

sanskrit_iast: adhārmikaṃ tribhirnyāyairnigṛhṇīyāt prayatnataḥ nirodhanena bandhena vividhena vadhena ca
meaning: अधार्मिकं त्रिभिर्न्यायैर्निगृह्णीयात् प्रयत्नतः निरोधनेन बन्धेन विविधेन वधेन च
text: He shall carefully suppress the unrighteous by three modes (of restraint)—by imprisonment, by enchaining and by various forms of ‘immolation.’
iast: adhārmikaṃ tribhirnyāyairnigṛhṇīyāt prayatnataḥ nirodhanena bandhena vividhena vadhena ca

sanskrit_iast: nigraheṇa hi pāpānāṃ sādhūnāṃ saṅgraheṇa ca dvijātaya ivaijyābhiḥ pūyante satataṃ nṛpāḥ
meaning: निग्रहेण हि पापानां साधूनां सङ्ग्रहेण च द्विजातय इवैज्याभिः पूयन्ते सततं नृपाः
text: For by suppressing the vicious and postering the virtuous, kings become purified, just as twice-born men by the daily sacrifices.
iast: nigraheṇa hi pāpānāṃ sādhūnāṃ saṅgraheṇa ca dvijātaya ivaijyābhiḥ pūyante satataṃ nṛpāḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣantavyaṃ prabhuṇā nityaṃ kṣipatāṃ kāryiṇāṃ nṛṇām bālavṛddhāturāṇāṃ ca kurvatā hitamātmanaḥ
meaning: क्षन्तव्यं प्रभुणा नित्यं क्षिपतां कार्यिणां नृणाम् बालवृद्धातुराणां च कुर्वता हितमात्मनः
text: The king should always forgive the partisans of litigants who abuse him, as also the young, the aged and the infirm,—thereby accomplishing his own welfare.
iast: kṣantavyaṃ prabhuṇā nityaṃ kṣipatāṃ kāryiṇāṃ nṛṇām bālavṛddhāturāṇāṃ ca kurvatā hitamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yaḥ kṣipto marṣayatyārtaistena svarge mahīyate yastvaiśvaryānna kṣamate narakaṃ tena gacchati
meaning: यः क्षिप्तो मर्षयत्यार्तैस्तेन स्वर्गे महीयते यस्त्वैश्वर्यान्न क्षमते नरकं तेन गच्छति
text: He who, on being abused by men in distress, forgives, becomes exalted to heaven, by that act; while he who, through kingly pride, does not forgive, goes, by that act, to hell.
iast: yaḥ kṣipto marṣayatyārtaistena svarge mahīyate yastvaiśvaryānna kṣamate narakaṃ tena gacchati

sanskrit_iast: rājā stenena gantavyo muktakeśena dhāvatā dhīmatā ācakṣāṇena tat steyamevaṅkarmā 'smi śādhi mām
meaning: राजा स्तेनेन गन्तव्यो मुक्तकेशेन धावता धीमता आचक्षाणेन तत् स्तेयमेवङ्कर्मा 'स्मि शाधि माम्
text: The wise thief shall approach the king, with flying hair, confessing the theft, with the words—‘I have done this, punish me’;
iast: rājā stenena gantavyo muktakeśena dhāvatā dhīmatā ācakṣāṇena tat steyamevaṅkarmā 'smi śādhi mām

sanskrit_iast: skandhenādāya musalaṃ laguḍaṃ vā 'pi khādiram muśalaṃ śaktiṃ caubhayatastīkṣṇāmāyasaṃ daṇḍameva vā
meaning: स्कन्धेनादाय मुसलं लगुडं वा 'पि खादिरम् मुशलं शक्तिं चौभयतस्तीक्ष्णामायसं दण्डमेव वा
text: —Carrying on his shoulder a pestle, or a Club of khadira wood, or a spear sharp at both ends, or an iron staff.
iast: skandhenādāya musalaṃ laguḍaṃ vā 'pi khādiram muśalaṃ śaktiṃ caubhayatastīkṣṇāmāyasaṃ daṇḍameva vā

sanskrit_iast: śāsanādvā vimokṣādvā stenaḥ steyādvimucyate aśāsitvā tu taṃ rājā stenasyāpnoti kilbiṣam
meaning: शासनाद्वा विमोक्षाद्वा स्तेनः स्तेयाद्विमुच्यते अशासित्वा तु तं राजा स्तेनस्याप्नोति किल्बिषम्
text: The thief becomes absolved from the theft, either through punishment or through acquittal. By not punishing the thief, the king imbibes the guilt of the thief.
iast: śāsanādvā vimokṣādvā stenaḥ steyādvimucyate aśāsitvā tu taṃ rājā stenasyāpnoti kilbiṣam

sanskrit_iast: annāde bhrūṇahā mārṣṭi patyau bhāryā 'pacāriṇī gurau śiṣyaśca yājyaśca steno rājani kilbiṣam
meaning: अन्नादे भ्रूणहा मार्ष्टि पत्यौ भार्या 'पचारिणी गुरौ शिष्यश्च याज्यश्च स्तेनो राजनि किल्बिषम्
text: The Embryo-killer expurgates his guilt on him who eats his food, the misbehaving wife on her husband, the disciple and the sacrificer on the preceptor, and the thief on the king.
iast: annāde bhrūṇahā mārṣṭi patyau bhāryā 'pacāriṇī gurau śiṣyaśca yājyaśca steno rājani kilbiṣam

sanskrit_iast: rājabhiḥ kṛtadaṇḍāstu kṛtvā pāpāni mānavāḥ rājabhirdhṛtadaṇḍāstu nirmalāḥ svargamāyānti santaḥ sukṛtino yathā
meaning: राजभिः कृतदण्डास्तु कृत्वा पापानि मानवाः राजभिर्धृतदण्डास्तु निर्मलाः स्वर्गमायान्ति सन्तः सुकृतिनो यथा
text: Men who, having committed crimes, have been punished by Kings, become freed from guilt and go to heaven, just like well-behaved good men.
iast: rājabhiḥ kṛtadaṇḍāstu kṛtvā pāpāni mānavāḥ rājabhirdhṛtadaṇḍāstu nirmalāḥ svargamāyānti santaḥ sukṛtino yathā

sanskrit_iast: yastu rajjuṃ ghaṭaṃ kūpāddharedbhindyācca yaḥ prapām sa daṇḍaṃ prāpnuyān māṣaṃ tacca tasmin samāharet
meaning: यस्तु रज्जुं घटं कूपाद्धरेद्भिन्द्याच्च यः प्रपाम् स दण्डं प्राप्नुयान् माषं तच्च तस्मिन् समाहरेत्
text: When one steals the rope or the water-pot from the well, or damages a water-drinking establishment, he should be punished with a fine of one ‘māṣa,’ and should restore the article to the place.
iast: yastu rajjuṃ ghaṭaṃ kūpāddharedbhindyācca yaḥ prapām sa daṇḍaṃ prāpnuyān māṣaṃ tacca tasmin samāharet

sanskrit_iast: dhānyaṃ daśabhyaḥ kumbhebhyo harato 'bhyadhikaṃ vadhaḥ śeṣe 'pyekādaśaguṇaṃ dāpyastasya ca taddhanam
meaning: धान्यं दशभ्यः कुम्भेभ्यो हरतो 'भ्यधिकं वधः शेषे 'प्येकादशगुणं दाप्यस्तस्य च तद्धनम्
text: There shall be ‘immolation’ for one who steals more than ten jars of grain; in other cases he should he made to pay eleven times as much, as also make good the property to the owner.
iast: dhānyaṃ daśabhyaḥ kumbhebhyo harato 'bhyadhikaṃ vadhaḥ śeṣe 'pyekādaśaguṇaṃ dāpyastasya ca taddhanam

sanskrit_iast: tathā dharimameyānāṃ śatādabhyadhike vadhaḥ suvarṇarajatādīnāmuttamānāṃ ca vāsasām
meaning: तथा धरिममेयानां शतादभ्यधिके वधः सुवर्णरजतादीनामुत्तमानां च वाससाम्
text: In the case or articles weighed by scales,—gold, silver and the rest,—if more than a hundred (are stolen),
iast: tathā dharimameyānāṃ śatādabhyadhike vadhaḥ suvarṇarajatādīnāmuttamānāṃ ca vāsasām

sanskrit_iast: pañcāśatastvabhyadhike hastacchedanamiṣyate śeṣe tvekādaśaguṇaṃ mūlyāddaṇḍaṃ prakalpayet
meaning: पञ्चाशतस्त्वभ्यधिके हस्तच्छेदनमिष्यते शेषे त्वेकादशगुणं मूल्याद्दण्डं प्रकल्पयेत्
text: In the case of more than fifty, the cutting off of the hands is prescribed. In other cases, the king shall inflict a fine of eleven times the value.
iast: pañcāśatastvabhyadhike hastacchedanamiṣyate śeṣe tvekādaśaguṇaṃ mūlyāddaṇḍaṃ prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: puruṣāṇāṃ kulīnānāṃ nārīṇāṃ ca viśeṣataḥ mukhyānāṃ caiva ratnānāṃ haraṇe vadhamarhati
meaning: पुरुषाणां कुलीनानां नारीणां च विशेषतः मुख्यानां चैव रत्नानां हरणे वधमर्हति
text: For stealing noble men, and specially women, and the precious gems, the thief deserves ‘immolation.’
iast: puruṣāṇāṃ kulīnānāṃ nārīṇāṃ ca viśeṣataḥ mukhyānāṃ caiva ratnānāṃ haraṇe vadhamarhati

sanskrit_iast: mahāpaśūnāṃ haraṇe śastrāṇāmauṣadhasya ca kālamāsādya kāryaṃ ca daṇḍaṃ rājā prakalpayet
meaning: महापशूनां हरणे शस्त्राणामौषधस्य च कालमासाद्य कार्यं च दण्डं राजा प्रकल्पयेत्
text: For the stealing of large animals, of weapons or medicines, the king shall determine the punishment, after considering the time and the purpose.
iast: mahāpaśūnāṃ haraṇe śastrāṇāmauṣadhasya ca kālamāsādya kāryaṃ ca daṇḍaṃ rājā prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: goṣu brāhmaṇasaṃsthāsu churikāyāśca bhedane kharikāyāśca paśūnāṃ haraṇe caiva sadyaḥ kāryo 'rdhapādikaḥ
meaning: गोषु ब्राह्मणसंस्थासु छुरिकायाश्च भेदने खरिकायाश्च पशूनां हरणे चैव सद्यः कार्यो 'र्धपादिकः
text: For stealing cows belonging to a Brāhmaṇa, and for piercing them with the goad, and for stealing animals, the thief should be immediately made half-footed.
iast: goṣu brāhmaṇasaṃsthāsu churikāyāśca bhedane kharikāyāśca paśūnāṃ haraṇe caiva sadyaḥ kāryo 'rdhapādikaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sūtrakārpāsakiṇvānāṃ gomayasya guḍasya ca dadhnaḥ kṣīrasya takrasya pānīyasya tṛṇasya ca
meaning: सूत्रकार्पासकिण्वानां गोमयस्य गुडस्य च दध्नः क्षीरस्य तक्रस्य पानीयस्य तृणस्य च
text: In the case of the theft of yarns, cotton, fermenting drug, cowdung, molasses, curds, milk, skimmed curd, water and grass (326),—of vessels made of bamboo or cane, as also of salts, earthenware, earth and ashes (327)—of fish, birds, oil, clarified butter, meat, honey, and other animal-products (328)—of other things of this kind, spirituous liquors, cooked rice and all kinds of cooked food,—the fine shall be double the value of the thing (stolen).
iast: sūtrakārpāsakiṇvānāṃ gomayasya guḍasya ca dadhnaḥ kṣīrasya takrasya pānīyasya tṛṇasya ca

sanskrit_iast: veṇuvaidalabhāṇḍānāṃ lavaṇānāṃ tathaiva ca mṛṇmayānāṃ ca haraṇe mṛdo bhasmana eva ca
meaning: वेणुवैदलभाण्डानां लवणानां तथैव च मृण्मयानां च हरणे मृदो भस्मन एव च
text: In the case of the theft of yarns, cotton, fermenting drug, cowdung, molasses, curds, milk, skimmed curd, water and grass (326),—of vessels made of bamboo or cane, as also of salts, earthenware, earth and ashes (327)—of fish, birds, oil, clarified butter, meat, honey, and other animal-products (328)—of other things of this kind, spirituous liquors, cooked rice and all kinds of cooked food,—the fine shall be double the value of the thing (stolen).
iast: veṇuvaidalabhāṇḍānāṃ lavaṇānāṃ tathaiva ca mṛṇmayānāṃ ca haraṇe mṛdo bhasmana eva ca

sanskrit_iast: matsyānāṃ pakṣiṇāṃ caiva tailasya ca ghṛtasya ca māṃsasya madhunaścaiva yaccānyat paśusaṃbhavam
meaning: मत्स्यानां पक्षिणां चैव तैलस्य च घृतस्य च मांसस्य मधुनश्चैव यच्चान्यत् पशुसंभवम्
text: In the case of the theft of yarns, cotton, fermenting drug, cowdung, molasses, curds, milk, skimmed curd, water and grass (326),—of vessels made of bamboo or cane, as also of salts, earthenware, earth and ashes (327)—of fish, birds, oil, clarified butter, meat, honey, and other animal-products (328)—of other things of this kind, spirituous liquors, cooked rice and all kinds of cooked food,—the fine shall be double the value of the thing (stolen).
iast: matsyānāṃ pakṣiṇāṃ caiva tailasya ca ghṛtasya ca māṃsasya madhunaścaiva yaccānyat paśusaṃbhavam

sanskrit_iast: anyeṣāṃ caivamādīnāṃ madyānāmodanasya ca caivamādīnāmadyānām pakvānnānāṃ ca sarveṣāṃ tanmulyāddviguṇo damaḥ
meaning: अन्येषां चैवमादीनां मद्यानामोदनस्य च चैवमादीनामद्यानाम् पक्वान्नानां च सर्वेषां तन्मुल्याद्द्विगुणो दमः
text: In the case of the theft of yarns, cotton, fermenting drug, cowdung, molasses, curds, milk, skimmed curd, water and grass (326),—of vessels made of bamboo or cane, as also of salts, earthenware, earth and ashes (327)—of fish, birds, oil, clarified butter, meat, honey, and other animal-products (328)—of other things of this kind, spirituous liquors, cooked rice and all kinds of cooked food,—the fine shall be double the value of the thing (stolen).
iast: anyeṣāṃ caivamādīnāṃ madyānāmodanasya ca caivamādīnāmadyānām pakvānnānāṃ ca sarveṣāṃ tanmulyāddviguṇo damaḥ

sanskrit_iast: puṣpeṣu harite dhānye gulmavallīnageṣu ca anyeṣvaparipūteṣu daṇḍaḥ syāt pañcakṛṣṇalaḥ
meaning: पुष्पेषु हरिते धान्ये गुल्मवल्लीनगेषु च अन्येष्वपरिपूतेषु दण्डः स्यात् पञ्चकृष्णलः
text: For flowers, green corns, shrubs, creepers, trees and other unhusked (grains), the fine shall consist of five ‘kṛṣṇalas.’
iast: puṣpeṣu harite dhānye gulmavallīnageṣu ca anyeṣvaparipūteṣu daṇḍaḥ syāt pañcakṛṣṇalaḥ

sanskrit_iast: paripūteṣu dhānyeṣu śākamūlaphaleṣu ca niranvaye śataṃ daṇḍaḥ sānvaye 'rdhaśataṃ damaḥ
meaning: परिपूतेषु धान्येषु शाकमूलफलेषु च निरन्वये शतं दण्डः सान्वये 'र्धशतं दमः
text: For husked grains, for vegetables, roots and fruits, there shall be a fine of a hundred, in a case where there has been no propitiation; and fifty, where there has been propitiation.
iast: paripūteṣu dhānyeṣu śākamūlaphaleṣu ca niranvaye śataṃ daṇḍaḥ sānvaye 'rdhaśataṃ damaḥ

sanskrit_iast: syāt sāhasaṃ tvanvayavat prasabhaṃ karma yat kṛtam niranvayaṃ bhavet steyaṃ hṛtvā 'pavyayate ca yat
meaning: स्यात् साहसं त्वन्वयवत् प्रसभं कर्म यत् कृतम् निरन्वयं भवेत् स्तेयं हृत्वा 'पव्ययते च यत्
text: If the act is committed with violence and in the presence of men, it is ‘robbery’; it is ‘theft’ when done in the absence of men, and when it is denied after having been done.
iast: syāt sāhasaṃ tvanvayavat prasabhaṃ karma yat kṛtam niranvayaṃ bhavet steyaṃ hṛtvā 'pavyayate ca yat

sanskrit_iast: yastvetānyupakḷptāni dravyāṇi stenayennaraḥ tamādyaṃ daṇḍayedrājā yaścāgniṃ corayedgṛhāt taṃ śataṃ
meaning: यस्त्वेतान्युपकॢप्तानि द्रव्याणि स्तेनयेन्नरः तमाद्यं दण्डयेद्राजा यश्चाग्निं चोरयेद्गृहात् तं शतं
text: If a man steals these things when they have been prepared, the king should fine him one hundred; as also him who steals the fire from the house.
iast: yastvetānyupakḷptāni dravyāṇi stenayennaraḥ tamādyaṃ daṇḍayedrājā yaścāgniṃ corayedgṛhāt taṃ śataṃ

sanskrit_iast: yena yena yathāṅgena steno nṛṣu viceṣṭate tat tadeva haret tasya pratyādeśāya pārthivaḥ
meaning: येन येन यथाङ्गेन स्तेनो नृषु विचेष्टते तत् तदेव हरेत् तस्य प्रत्यादेशाय पार्थिवः
text: By whatever limb the thief operates against men, that shall the king take off, by way of retribution.
iast: yena yena yathāṅgena steno nṛṣu viceṣṭate tat tadeva haret tasya pratyādeśāya pārthivaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pitā 'cāryaḥ suhṛtmātā bhāryā putraḥ purohitaḥ nādaṇḍyo nāma rājño 'sti yaḥ svadharme na tiṣṭhati
meaning: पिता 'चार्यः सुहृत्माता भार्या पुत्रः पुरोहितः नादण्ड्यो नाम राज्ञो 'स्ति यः स्वधर्मे न तिष्ठति
text: Neither the father or the preceptor or the friend or the mother or the wife or the son or the priest is unpunishable for the King, when they do not keep within their duty.
iast: pitā 'cāryaḥ suhṛtmātā bhāryā putraḥ purohitaḥ nādaṇḍyo nāma rājño 'sti yaḥ svadharme na tiṣṭhati

sanskrit_iast: kārṣāpaṇaṃ bhaveddaṇḍyo yatrānyaḥ prākṛto janaḥ tatra rājā bhaveddaṇḍyaḥ sahasramiti dhāraṇā
meaning: कार्षापणं भवेद्दण्ड्यो यत्रान्यः प्राकृतो जनः तत्र राजा भवेद्दण्ड्यः सहस्रमिति धारणा
text: When an ordinary man would be fined one ‘Kārṣāpaṇa,’ the king should be fined one thousand; such is the established rule.
iast: kārṣāpaṇaṃ bhaveddaṇḍyo yatrānyaḥ prākṛto janaḥ tatra rājā bhaveddaṇḍyaḥ sahasramiti dhāraṇā

sanskrit_iast: aṣṭāpādyaṃ tu śūdrasya steye bhavati kilbiṣam ṣoḍaśaiva tu vaiśyasya dvātriṃśat kṣatriyasya ca
meaning: अष्टापाद्यं तु शूद्रस्य स्तेये भवति किल्बिषम् षोडशैव तु वैश्यस्य द्वात्रिंशत् क्षत्रियस्य च
text: In the case of theft, the guilt of a Śūdra is eightfold, that of the Vaiśya sixteen-fold, and that of the Kṣatriya thirty-two-fold;—(337) that of the Brāhmaṇa sixty-four-fold, or fully hundred-fold, or twice sixty-four-fold; when he is cognisant of the good or bad quality of the act.
iast: aṣṭāpādyaṃ tu śūdrasya steye bhavati kilbiṣam ṣoḍaśaiva tu vaiśyasya dvātriṃśat kṣatriyasya ca

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇasya catuḥṣaṣṭiḥ pūrṇaṃ vā 'pi śataṃ bhavet dviguṇā vā catuḥṣaṣṭistaddoṣaguṇaviddhi saḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्य चतुःषष्टिः पूर्णं वा 'पि शतं भवेत् द्विगुणा वा चतुःषष्टिस्तद्दोषगुणविद्धि सः
text: In the case of theft, the guilt of a Śūdra is eightfold, that of the Vaiśya sixteen-fold, and that of the Kṣatriya thirty-two-fold;—(337) that of the Brāhmaṇa sixty-four-fold, or fully hundred-fold, or twice sixty-four-fold; when he is cognisant of the good or bad quality of the act.
iast: brāhmaṇasya catuḥṣaṣṭiḥ pūrṇaṃ vā 'pi śataṃ bhavet dviguṇā vā catuḥṣaṣṭistaddoṣaguṇaviddhi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: vānaspatyaṃ mūlaphalaṃ dāruagny 'rthaṃ tathaiva ca tṛṇaṃ ca gobhyo grāsārthamasteyaṃ manurabravīt
meaning: वानस्पत्यं मूलफलं दारुअग्न्य् 'र्थं तथैव च तृणं च गोभ्यो ग्रासार्थमस्तेयं मनुरब्रवीत्
text: Fuel for fire and trees, roots and fruits, and grass, for feeding cows,—the taking of these Manu has declared to be no theft.
iast: vānaspatyaṃ mūlaphalaṃ dāruagny 'rthaṃ tathaiva ca tṛṇaṃ ca gobhyo grāsārthamasteyaṃ manurabravīt

sanskrit_iast: yo 'dattādāyino hastātlipseta brāhmaṇo dhanam yājanādhyāpanenāpi yathā stenastathaiva saḥ
meaning: यो 'दत्तादायिनो हस्तात्लिप्सेत ब्राह्मणो धनम् याजनाध्यापनेनापि यथा स्तेनस्तथैव सः
text: If a Brāhmaṇa seeks, even by sacrificing and teaching, to obtain wealth from one who has taken what has not been given to him,—he is just like a thief.
iast: yo 'dattādāyino hastātlipseta brāhmaṇo dhanam yājanādhyāpanenāpi yathā stenastathaiva saḥ

sanskrit_iast: dvijo 'dhvagaḥ kṣīṇavṛttirdvāvikṣū dve ca mūlake ādadānaḥ parakṣetrātna daṇḍaṃ dātumarhati
meaning: द्विजो 'ध्वगः क्षीणवृत्तिर्द्वाविक्षू द्वे च मूलके आददानः परक्षेत्रात्न दण्डं दातुमर्हति
text: If a twice-born person, running short of provisions while on a journey, takes two sugar-cane stalks, or two roots, from another man’s field, he does not deserve to be made to pay a fine.
iast: dvijo 'dhvagaḥ kṣīṇavṛttirdvāvikṣū dve ca mūlake ādadānaḥ parakṣetrātna daṇḍaṃ dātumarhati

sanskrit_iast: asaṃditānāṃ saṃdātā saṃditānāṃ ca mokṣakaḥ dāsāśvarathahartā ca prāptaḥ syāccorakilbiṣam
meaning: असंदितानां संदाता संदितानां च मोक्षकः दासाश्वरथहर्ता च प्राप्तः स्याच्चोरकिल्बिषम्
text: One who enchains the unchained, or sets free the enchained, as also one who takes away a slave, a horse or a chariot, incurs the guilt of the thief.
iast: asaṃditānāṃ saṃdātā saṃditānāṃ ca mokṣakaḥ dāsāśvarathahartā ca prāptaḥ syāccorakilbiṣam

sanskrit_iast: anena vidhinā rājā kurvāṇaḥ stenanigraham yaśo 'smin prāpnuyātloke pretya cānuttamaṃ sukham
meaning: अनेन विधिना राजा कुर्वाणः स्तेननिग्रहम् यशो 'स्मिन् प्राप्नुयात्लोके प्रेत्य चानुत्तमं सुखम्
text: By punishing thieves in accordance with th is law, the king obtains fame in this world, and after death, unsurpassable bliss.
iast: anena vidhinā rājā kurvāṇaḥ stenanigraham yaśo 'smin prāpnuyātloke pretya cānuttamaṃ sukham

sanskrit_iast: aindraṃ sthānamabhiprepsuryaśaścākṣayamavyayam naupekṣeta kṣaṇamapi rājā sāhasikaṃ naram
meaning: ऐन्द्रं स्थानमभिप्रेप्सुर्यशश्चाक्षयमव्ययम् नौपेक्षेत क्षणमपि राजा साहसिकं नरम्
text: The king who is desirous of indra’s eternal place, as also of imperishable fame, shall not ignore the desperado even for a moment.
iast: aindraṃ sthānamabhiprepsuryaśaścākṣayamavyayam naupekṣeta kṣaṇamapi rājā sāhasikaṃ naram

sanskrit_iast: vāgduṣṭāt taskarāccaiva daṇḍenaiva ca hiṃsataḥ sāhasasya naraḥ kartā vijñeyaḥ pāpakṛttamaḥ
meaning: वाग्दुष्टात् तस्कराच्चैव दण्डेनैव च हिंसतः साहसस्य नरः कर्ता विज्ञेयः पापकृत्तमः
text: He who commits violence is to be regarded as the worst offender, as compared to one who is wicked of speech, to a thief and to one who hurts with a staff.
iast: vāgduṣṭāt taskarāccaiva daṇḍenaiva ca hiṃsataḥ sāhasasya naraḥ kartā vijñeyaḥ pāpakṛttamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sāhase vartamānaṃ tu yo marṣayati pārthivaḥ sa vināśaṃ vrajatyāśu vidveṣaṃ cādhigacchati
meaning: साहसे वर्तमानं तु यो मर्षयति पार्थिवः स विनाशं व्रजत्याशु विद्वेषं चाधिगच्छति
text: The king who condones the perpetrator of violence quickly falls into destruction and incurs hatred.
iast: sāhase vartamānaṃ tu yo marṣayati pārthivaḥ sa vināśaṃ vrajatyāśu vidveṣaṃ cādhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: na mitrakāraṇādrājā vipulādvā dhanāgamāt samutsṛjet sāhasikān sarvabhūtabhayāvahān
meaning: न मित्रकारणाद्राजा विपुलाद्वा धनागमात् समुत्सृजेत् साहसिकान् सर्वभूतभयावहान्
text: Neither for the sake of friendship, nor for the sake of a large gain of money, should the king let off the perpetrators of violence, who cause terror to all living beings.
iast: na mitrakāraṇādrājā vipulādvā dhanāgamāt samutsṛjet sāhasikān sarvabhūtabhayāvahān

sanskrit_iast: śastraṃ dvijātibhirgrāhyaṃ dharmo yatrauparudhyate dvijātīnāṃ ca varṇānāṃ viplave kālakārite
meaning: शस्त्रं द्विजातिभिर्ग्राह्यं धर्मो यत्रौपरुध्यते द्विजातीनां च वर्णानां विप्लवे कालकारिते
text: Twice-born persons shall carry arms: When religion is interfered with, when there is confusion among the twice-born castes caused by the exigencies of time,—(348) in his own defence, in cases of hindrance of sacrificial fees, in the case of outrages upon Brāhmaṇas and women,—if one strikes in the cause of right, he incurs no sin.
iast: śastraṃ dvijātibhirgrāhyaṃ dharmo yatrauparudhyate dvijātīnāṃ ca varṇānāṃ viplave kālakārite

sanskrit_iast: ātmanaśca paritrāṇe dakṣiṇānāṃ ca saṅgare strīviprābhyupapattau ca ghnan dharmeṇa na duṣyati
meaning: आत्मनश्च परित्राणे दक्षिणानां च सङ्गरे स्त्रीविप्राभ्युपपत्तौ च घ्नन् धर्मेण न दुष्यति
text: Twice-born persons shall carry arms: When religion is interfered with, when there is confusion among the twice-born castes caused by the exigencies of time,—(348) in his own defence, in cases of hindrance of sacrificial fees, in the case of outrages upon Brāhmaṇas and women,—if one strikes in the cause of right, he incurs no sin.
iast: ātmanaśca paritrāṇe dakṣiṇānāṃ ca saṅgare strīviprābhyupapattau ca ghnan dharmeṇa na duṣyati

sanskrit_iast: guruṃ vā bālavṛddhau vā brāhmaṇaṃ vā bahuśrutam ātatāyinamāyāntaṃ hanyādevāvicārayan
meaning: गुरुं वा बालवृद्धौ वा ब्राह्मणं वा बहुश्रुतम् आततायिनमायान्तं हन्यादेवाविचारयन्
text: Without hesitation one should strike an approaching desperado,—be he a preceptor, a child, or an aged man, or a highly learned Brāhmaṇa.
iast: guruṃ vā bālavṛddhau vā brāhmaṇaṃ vā bahuśrutam ātatāyinamāyāntaṃ hanyādevāvicārayan

sanskrit_iast: nātatāyivadhe doṣo hanturbhavati kaścana prakāśaṃ vā 'prakāśaṃ vā manyustaṃ manyuṃ ṛcchati
meaning: नाततायिवधे दोषो हन्तुर्भवति कश्चन प्रकाशं वा 'प्रकाशं वा मन्युस्तं मन्युं ऋच्छति
text: No evil of any kind accrues to the slayer for killing a desperado, either openly or secretly; as it is only Fury recoiling upon Fury.
iast: nātatāyivadhe doṣo hanturbhavati kaścana prakāśaṃ vā 'prakāśaṃ vā manyustaṃ manyuṃ ṛcchati

sanskrit_iast: paradārābhimarśeṣu pravṛttānnr̥̄n mahīpatiḥ udvejanakarairdaṇḍaiśchinnayitvā pravāsayet cihnayitvā
meaning: परदाराभिमर्शेषु प्रवृत्तान्न्र्̥̄न् महीपतिः उद्वेजनकरैर्दण्डैश्छिन्नयित्वा प्रवासयेत् चिह्नयित्वा
text: Those men who are addicted to intercourse with the wives of other men, the king shall banish after having branded them with terror-inspiring punishments.
iast: paradārābhimarśeṣu pravṛttānnr̥̄n mahīpatiḥ udvejanakarairdaṇḍaiśchinnayitvā pravāsayet cihnayitvā

sanskrit_iast: tatsamuttho hi lokasya jāyate varṇasaṅkaraḥ yena mūlaharo 'dharmaḥ sarvanāśāya kalpate
meaning: तत्समुत्थो हि लोकस्य जायते वर्णसङ्करः येन मूलहरो 'धर्मः सर्वनाशाय कल्पते
text: For out of that arises the admixture of castes among people;—whence follows root-rending unrighteousness, tending to total destruction.
iast: tatsamuttho hi lokasya jāyate varṇasaṅkaraḥ yena mūlaharo 'dharmaḥ sarvanāśāya kalpate

sanskrit_iast: parasya patnyā puruṣaḥ saṃbhāṣāṃ yojayan rahaḥ pūrvamākṣārito doṣaiḥ prāpnuyāt pūrvasāhasam
meaning: परस्य पत्न्या पुरुषः संभाषां योजयन् रहः पूर्वमाक्षारितो दोषैः प्राप्नुयात् पूर्वसाहसम्
text: A man who engages in secret conversation with the wife of another person,—if he is one who has been previously accused of similar offences,—should receive the penalty of the ‘first amercement.’
iast: parasya patnyā puruṣaḥ saṃbhāṣāṃ yojayan rahaḥ pūrvamākṣārito doṣaiḥ prāpnuyāt pūrvasāhasam

sanskrit_iast: yastvanākṣāritaḥ pūrvamabhibhāṣate kāraṇāt na doṣaṃ prāpnuyāt kiṃ cinna hi tasya vyatikramaḥ
meaning: यस्त्वनाक्षारितः पूर्वमभिभाषते कारणात् न दोषं प्राप्नुयात् किं चिन्न हि तस्य व्यतिक्रमः
text: If, however, he is one who has not been previously accused, and converses with her for some good reason, he does not incur any guilt; as in his case there has been no transgression.
iast: yastvanākṣāritaḥ pūrvamabhibhāṣate kāraṇāt na doṣaṃ prāpnuyāt kiṃ cinna hi tasya vyatikramaḥ

sanskrit_iast: parastriyaṃ yo 'bhivadet tīrthe 'raṇye vane 'pi vā nadīnāṃ vā 'pi saṃbhede sa saṅgrahaṇamāpnuyāt
meaning: परस्त्रियं यो 'भिवदेत् तीर्थे 'रण्ये वने 'पि वा नदीनां वा 'पि संभेदे स सङ्ग्रहणमाप्नुयात्
text: He who converses with ‘another’s woman’ at a watering place, or in a wilderness, or in a forest, or at the confluence of rivers,—incurs the guilt of ‘adultery.’
iast: parastriyaṃ yo 'bhivadet tīrthe 'raṇye vane 'pi vā nadīnāṃ vā 'pi saṃbhede sa saṅgrahaṇamāpnuyāt

sanskrit_iast: upacārakriyā keliḥ sparśo bhūṣaṇavāsasām upakārakriyā saha khaṭvā 'sanaṃ caiva sarvaṃ saṅgrahaṇaṃ smṛtam
meaning: उपचारक्रिया केलिः स्पर्शो भूषणवाससाम् उपकारक्रिया सह खट्वा 'सनं चैव सर्वं सङ्ग्रहणं स्मृतम्
text: Offering help, flirting, touching of ornaments and clothes, sitting on the same bed,—all this has been declared to be ‘adultery.’
iast: upacārakriyā keliḥ sparśo bhūṣaṇavāsasām upakārakriyā saha khaṭvā 'sanaṃ caiva sarvaṃ saṅgrahaṇaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: striyaṃ spṛśedadeśe yaḥ spṛṣṭo vā marṣayet tayā parasparasyānumate sarvaṃ saṅgrahaṇaṃ smṛtam
meaning: स्त्रियं स्पृशेददेशे यः स्पृष्टो वा मर्षयेत् तया परस्परस्यानुमते सर्वं सङ्ग्रहणं स्मृतम्
text: If one touches a woman in an improper place, or condones it when touched by her,—all this, when done with mutual consent, has been declared to be ‘adultery’
iast: striyaṃ spṛśedadeśe yaḥ spṛṣṭo vā marṣayet tayā parasparasyānumate sarvaṃ saṅgrahaṇaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: abrāhmaṇaḥ saṅgrahaṇe prāṇāntaṃ daṇḍamarhati caturṇāmapi varṇānāṃ dārā rakṣyatamāḥ sadā
meaning: अब्राह्मणः सङ्ग्रहणे प्राणान्तं दण्डमर्हति चतुर्णामपि वर्णानां दारा रक्ष्यतमाः सदा
text: In a case of adultery, a non-Brāhmaṇa deserves the penalty ending in death; as the wives of all the four castes are always the most deserving of protection.
iast: abrāhmaṇaḥ saṅgrahaṇe prāṇāntaṃ daṇḍamarhati caturṇāmapi varṇānāṃ dārā rakṣyatamāḥ sadā

sanskrit_iast: bhikṣukā bandinaścaiva dīkṣitāḥ kāravastathā saṃbhāṣanaṃ saha strībhiḥ kuryuraprativāritāḥ
meaning: भिक्षुका बन्दिनश्चैव दीक्षिताः कारवस्तथा संभाषनं सह स्त्रीभिः कुर्युरप्रतिवारिताः
text: Mendicants, bards, persons initiated for a rite and craftsmen may converse with women, unchecked.
iast: bhikṣukā bandinaścaiva dīkṣitāḥ kāravastathā saṃbhāṣanaṃ saha strībhiḥ kuryuraprativāritāḥ

sanskrit_iast: na saṃbhāṣāṃ parastrībhiḥ pratiṣiddhaḥ samācaret niṣiddho bhāṣamāṇastu suvarṇaṃ daṇḍamarhati
meaning: न संभाषां परस्त्रीभिः प्रतिषिद्धः समाचरेत् निषिद्धो भाषमाणस्तु सुवर्णं दण्डमर्हति
text: One should not converse with the wives of other men, when forbidden. If, on being forbidden, he does converse, he becomes liable to be fined one ‘suvarṇa.’
iast: na saṃbhāṣāṃ parastrībhiḥ pratiṣiddhaḥ samācaret niṣiddho bhāṣamāṇastu suvarṇaṃ daṇḍamarhati

sanskrit_iast: naiṣa cāraṇadāreṣu vidhirnātmaupajīviṣu sajjayanti hi te nārīrnigūḍhāścārayanti ca
meaning: नैष चारणदारेषु विधिर्नात्मौपजीविषु सज्जयन्ति हि ते नारीर्निगूढाश्चारयन्ति च
text: This rule does not apply to the case of the wives of dancers and singers, or of those who make a living of themselves; for these men secretly bring their women into contact (with other men), and tempt them on.
iast: naiṣa cāraṇadāreṣu vidhirnātmaupajīviṣu sajjayanti hi te nārīrnigūḍhāścārayanti ca

sanskrit_iast: kiṃ cideva tu dāpyaḥ syāt saṃbhāṣāṃ tābhirācaran praiṣyāsu caikabhaktāsu rahaḥ pravrajitāsu ca preṣyāsu
meaning: किं चिदेव तु दाप्यः स्यात् संभाषां ताभिराचरन् प्रैष्यासु चैकभक्तासु रहः प्रव्रजितासु च प्रेष्यासु
text: Yet he who secretly carries on conversation with these women, or with maidservants devoted to one master, or with female ascetics, should be made to pay something.
iast: kiṃ cideva tu dāpyaḥ syāt saṃbhāṣāṃ tābhirācaran praiṣyāsu caikabhaktāsu rahaḥ pravrajitāsu ca preṣyāsu

sanskrit_iast: yo 'kāmāṃ dūṣayet kanyāṃ sa sadyo vadhamarhati sakāmāṃ dūṣayaṃstulyo na vadhaṃ prāpnuyānnaraḥ
meaning: यो 'कामां दूषयेत् कन्यां स सद्यो वधमर्हति सकामां दूषयंस्तुल्यो न वधं प्राप्नुयान्नरः
text: If a man of equal status violates an unwilling maiden, he deserves immediate death; but if he violates a willing one, he shall not suffer death.
iast: yo 'kāmāṃ dūṣayet kanyāṃ sa sadyo vadhamarhati sakāmāṃ dūṣayaṃstulyo na vadhaṃ prāpnuyānnaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: kanyāṃ bhajantīmutkṛṣṭaṃ na kiṃ cidapi dāpayet jaghanyaṃ sevamānāṃ tu saṃyatāṃ vāsayedgṛhe
meaning: कन्यां भजन्तीमुत्कृष्टं न किं चिदपि दापयेत् जघन्यं सेवमानां तु संयतां वासयेद्गृहे
text: If a maiden approaches a superior person, she shall not be made to pay anything; if however she courts an inferior person, she shall be kept confined in the house.
iast: kanyāṃ bhajantīmutkṛṣṭaṃ na kiṃ cidapi dāpayet jaghanyaṃ sevamānāṃ tu saṃyatāṃ vāsayedgṛhe

sanskrit_iast: uttamāṃ sevamānastu jaghanyo vadhamarhati śulkaṃ dadyāt sevamānaḥ samāmicchet pitā yadi
meaning: उत्तमां सेवमानस्तु जघन्यो वधमर्हति शुल्कं दद्यात् सेवमानः समामिच्छेत् पिता यदि
text: An inferior man courting a superior maiden deserves death; he who courts a maiden of equal status, shall pay the nuptial fee, if her father so wishes.
iast: uttamāṃ sevamānastu jaghanyo vadhamarhati śulkaṃ dadyāt sevamānaḥ samāmicchet pitā yadi

sanskrit_iast: abhiṣahya tu yaḥ kanyāṃ kuryāddarpeṇa mānavaḥ tasyāśu kartye aṅgulyau daṇḍaṃ cārhati ṣaṭśatam kartyā aṅgulyo
meaning: अभिषह्य तु यः कन्यां कुर्याद्दर्पेण मानवः तस्याशु कर्त्ये अङ्गुल्यौ दण्डं चार्हति षट्शतम् कर्त्या अङ्गुल्यो
text: But if any man wantonly defiles a maiden through sheer audacity, his fingers should be instantly clipped off, or he should be fined six hundred.
iast: abhiṣahya tu yaḥ kanyāṃ kuryāddarpeṇa mānavaḥ tasyāśu kartye aṅgulyau daṇḍaṃ cārhati ṣaṭśatam kartyā aṅgulyo

sanskrit_iast: sakāmāṃ dūṣayaṃstulyo nāṅgulichedamāpnuyāt dviśataṃ tu damaṃ dāpyaḥ prasaṅgavinivṛttaye
meaning: सकामां दूषयंस्तुल्यो नाङ्गुलिछेदमाप्नुयात् द्विशतं तु दमं दाप्यः प्रसङ्गविनिवृत्तये
text: A man of equal status defiling a willing maiden shall not suffer amputation of fingers; he should be made to pay the fine of two hundred with a view to prevent repetition.
iast: sakāmāṃ dūṣayaṃstulyo nāṅgulichedamāpnuyāt dviśataṃ tu damaṃ dāpyaḥ prasaṅgavinivṛttaye

sanskrit_iast: kanyaiva kanyāṃ yā kuryāt tasyāḥ syāddviśato damaḥ śulkaṃ ca dviguṇaṃ dadyātśiphāścaivāpnuyāddaśa
meaning: कन्यैव कन्यां या कुर्यात् तस्याः स्याद्द्विशतो दमः शुल्कं च द्विगुणं दद्यात्शिफाश्चैवाप्नुयाद्दश
text: If a maiden pollutes another maiden, her fine shall be two hundred; she shall also pay the double of her nuptial fee and shall receive ten lashes.
iast: kanyaiva kanyāṃ yā kuryāt tasyāḥ syāddviśato damaḥ śulkaṃ ca dviguṇaṃ dadyātśiphāścaivāpnuyāddaśa

sanskrit_iast: yā tu kanyāṃ prakuryāt strī sā sadyo mauṇḍyamarhati aṅgulyoreva vā chedaṃ khareṇaudvahanaṃ tathā
meaning: या तु कन्यां प्रकुर्यात् स्त्री सा सद्यो मौण्ड्यमर्हति अङ्गुल्योरेव वा छेदं खरेणौद्वहनं तथा
text: But if a woman pollutes a maiden, she deserves immediate shaving off, or the amputation of two fingers, and also being carried by a donkey.
iast: yā tu kanyāṃ prakuryāt strī sā sadyo mauṇḍyamarhati aṅgulyoreva vā chedaṃ khareṇaudvahanaṃ tathā

sanskrit_iast: bhartāraṃ laṅghayedyā tu strī jñātiguṇadarpitā tāṃ śvabhiḥ khādayedrājā saṃsthāne bahusaṃsthite
meaning: भर्तारं लङ्घयेद्या तु स्त्री ज्ञातिगुणदर्पिता तां श्वभिः खादयेद्राजा संस्थाने बहुसंस्थिते
text: If a woman, proud of relations and her qualities, passes over her husband, the king shall have her devoured by dogs in a place frequented by many.
iast: bhartāraṃ laṅghayedyā tu strī jñātiguṇadarpitā tāṃ śvabhiḥ khādayedrājā saṃsthāne bahusaṃsthite

sanskrit_iast: pumāṃsaṃ dāhayet pāpaṃ śayane tapta āyase abhyādadhyuśca kāṣṭhāni tatra dahyeta pāpakṛt
meaning: पुमांसं दाहयेत् पापं शयने तप्त आयसे अभ्यादध्युश्च काष्ठानि तत्र दह्येत पापकृत्
text: The offending male he should make to lie down upon a redhot iron bed; they shall put wooden-logs over him, so that the sinner may be burnt.
iast: pumāṃsaṃ dāhayet pāpaṃ śayane tapta āyase abhyādadhyuśca kāṣṭhāni tatra dahyeta pāpakṛt

sanskrit_iast: saṃvatsarābhiśastasya duṣṭasya dviguṇo damaḥ saṃvatsare 'bhiśastasya vrātyayā saha saṃvāse cāṇḍālyā tāvadeva tu
meaning: संवत्सराभिशस्तस्य दुष्टस्य द्विगुणो दमः संवत्सरे 'भिशस्तस्य व्रात्यया सह संवासे चाण्डाल्या तावदेव तु
text: If the convicted man is accused again within a year, he shall be punished with a double fine. the same also in the case of intercourse with a ‘vrātyā’ or a ‘chāṇḍālī.’
iast: saṃvatsarābhiśastasya duṣṭasya dviguṇo damaḥ saṃvatsare 'bhiśastasya vrātyayā saha saṃvāse cāṇḍālyā tāvadeva tu

sanskrit_iast: śūdro guptamaguptaṃ vā dvaijātaṃ varṇamāvasan aguptamaṅgasarvasvairguptaṃ sarveṇa hīyate 'ṅgasarvasvī
meaning: शूद्रो गुप्तमगुप्तं वा द्वैजातं वर्णमावसन् अगुप्तमङ्गसर्वस्वैर्गुप्तं सर्वेण हीयते 'ङ्गसर्वस्वी
text: A śūdra having intercourse with a twice-born woman, protected or unprotected, shall be deprived of his limb and his whole property, in the case of the unprotected woman, and of everything in that of the protected.
iast: śūdro guptamaguptaṃ vā dvaijātaṃ varṇamāvasan aguptamaṅgasarvasvairguptaṃ sarveṇa hīyate 'ṅgasarvasvī

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyaḥ sarvasvadaṇḍaḥ syāt saṃvatsaranirodhataḥ sahasraṃ kṣatriyo daṇḍyo mauṇḍyaṃ mūtreṇa cārhati
meaning: वैश्यः सर्वस्वदण्डः स्यात् संवत्सरनिरोधतः सहस्रं क्षत्रियो दण्ड्यो मौण्ड्यं मूत्रेण चार्हति
text: The Vaiśya should be fined his entire property after a year’s imprisonment; the Kṣatriya is to be fined one thousand, and be shaved with urine.
iast: vaiśyaḥ sarvasvadaṇḍaḥ syāt saṃvatsaranirodhataḥ sahasraṃ kṣatriyo daṇḍyo mauṇḍyaṃ mūtreṇa cārhati

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇīṃ yadyaguptāṃ tu gacchetāṃ vaiśyapārthivau vaiśyaṃ pañcaśataṃ kuryāt kṣatriyaṃ tu sahasriṇam
meaning: ब्राह्मणीं यद्यगुप्तां तु गच्छेतां वैश्यपार्थिवौ वैश्यं पञ्चशतं कुर्यात् क्षत्रियं तु सहस्रिणम्
text: If the Vaiśya and the Kṣatriya have intercourse with an unprotected Brāhmaṇa woman, the Vaiśya should be committed with five hundred and the Kṣatriya with one thousand.
iast: brāhmaṇīṃ yadyaguptāṃ tu gacchetāṃ vaiśyapārthivau vaiśyaṃ pañcaśataṃ kuryāt kṣatriyaṃ tu sahasriṇam

sanskrit_iast: ubhāvapi tu tāveva brāhmaṇyā guptayā saha viplutau śūdravaddaṇḍyau dagdhavyau vā kaṭāgninā
meaning: उभावपि तु तावेव ब्राह्मण्या गुप्तया सह विप्लुतौ शूद्रवद्दण्ड्यौ दग्धव्यौ वा कटाग्निना
text: But both these, when offending against a protected Brāhmaṇa woman, should be punished like a Śūdra, or burnt in a fire of dry grass.
iast: ubhāvapi tu tāveva brāhmaṇyā guptayā saha viplutau śūdravaddaṇḍyau dagdhavyau vā kaṭāgninā

sanskrit_iast: sahasraṃ brāhmaṇo daṇḍyo guptāṃ viprāṃ balādvrajan śatāni pañca daṇḍyaḥ syādicchantyā saha saṅgataḥ
meaning: सहस्रं ब्राह्मणो दण्ड्यो गुप्तां विप्रां बलाद्व्रजन् शतानि पञ्च दण्ड्यः स्यादिच्छन्त्या सह सङ्गतः
text: The Brāhmaṇa who has intercourse with a protected Brāhmaṇa woman by force should be fined one thousand; he who has connection with a willing one, should be fined five hundred.
iast: sahasraṃ brāhmaṇo daṇḍyo guptāṃ viprāṃ balādvrajan śatāni pañca daṇḍyaḥ syādicchantyā saha saṅgataḥ

sanskrit_iast: mauṇḍyaṃ prāṇāntikaṃ daṇḍo brāhmaṇasya vidhīyate prāṇāntako] "prāṇāntika " itareṣāṃ tu varṇānāṃ daṇḍaḥ prāṇāntiko bhavet prāṇāntako
meaning: मौण्ड्यं प्राणान्तिकं दण्डो ब्राह्मणस्य विधीयते प्राणान्तको] "प्राणान्तिक " इतरेषां तु वर्णानां दण्डः प्राणान्तिको भवेत् प्राणान्तको
text: Tonsure has been prescribed as the death-penalty for the Brāhmaṇa; for other castes the penalty would be actual death.
iast: mauṇḍyaṃ prāṇāntikaṃ daṇḍo brāhmaṇasya vidhīyate prāṇāntako] "prāṇāntika " itareṣāṃ tu varṇānāṃ daṇḍaḥ prāṇāntiko bhavet prāṇāntako

sanskrit_iast: na jātu brāhmaṇaṃ hanyāt sarvapāpeṣvapi sthitam rāṣṭrādenaṃ bahiḥ kuryāt samagradhanamakṣatam
meaning: न जातु ब्राह्मणं हन्यात् सर्वपापेष्वपि स्थितम् राष्ट्रादेनं बहिः कुर्यात् समग्रधनमक्षतम्
text: Verily he shall not kill the Brāhmaṇa, even though he be steeped in all crimes; he should banish him from the kingdom, with all his property and unhurt.
iast: na jātu brāhmaṇaṃ hanyāt sarvapāpeṣvapi sthitam rāṣṭrādenaṃ bahiḥ kuryāt samagradhanamakṣatam

sanskrit_iast: na brāhmaṇavadhādbhūyānadharmo vidyate bhuvi tasmādasya vadhaṃ rājā manasā 'pi na cintayet
meaning: न ब्राह्मणवधाद्भूयानधर्मो विद्यते भुवि तस्मादस्य वधं राजा मनसा 'पि न चिन्तयेत्
text: There is no greater crime on earth than the slaying of a Brāhmaṇa; the king shall, therefore, not even think of his death in his mind.
iast: na brāhmaṇavadhādbhūyānadharmo vidyate bhuvi tasmādasya vadhaṃ rājā manasā 'pi na cintayet

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyaścet kṣatriyāṃ guptāṃ vaiśyāṃ vā kṣatriyo vrajet yo brāhmaṇyāmaguptāyāṃ tāvubhau daṇḍamarhataḥ
meaning: वैश्यश्चेत् क्षत्रियां गुप्तां वैश्यां वा क्षत्रियो व्रजेत् यो ब्राह्मण्यामगुप्तायां तावुभौ दण्डमर्हतः
text: If a Vaiśya approaches a protected Kṣatriya woman, or the Kṣatriya a Vaiśya woman,—both these deserve the same punishment as that in the case of an unprotected Brāhmaṇa woman.
iast: vaiśyaścet kṣatriyāṃ guptāṃ vaiśyāṃ vā kṣatriyo vrajet yo brāhmaṇyāmaguptāyāṃ tāvubhau daṇḍamarhataḥ

sanskrit_iast: sahasraṃ brāhmaṇo daṇḍaṃ dāpyo gupte tu te vrajan śūdrāyāṃ kṣatriyaviśoḥ sāhasro vai bhaveddamaḥ śūdrāyā
meaning: सहस्रं ब्राह्मणो दण्डं दाप्यो गुप्ते तु ते व्रजन् शूद्रायां क्षत्रियविशोः साहस्रो वै भवेद्दमः शूद्राया
text: The Brāhmaṇa having intercourse with the said two, when protected, should be made to pay a fine of one thousand; the fine for the Kṣatriya and the Vaiśya approaching a Śūdra woman, should be one thousand.
iast: sahasraṃ brāhmaṇo daṇḍaṃ dāpyo gupte tu te vrajan śūdrāyāṃ kṣatriyaviśoḥ sāhasro vai bhaveddamaḥ śūdrāyā

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyāyāmaguptāyāṃ vaiśye pañcaśataṃ damaḥ mūtreṇa mauṇḍyamicchet tu kṣatriyo daṇḍameva vā ṛcchet tu
meaning: क्षत्रियायामगुप्तायां वैश्ये पञ्चशतं दमः मूत्रेण मौण्ड्यमिच्छेत् तु क्षत्रियो दण्डमेव वा ऋच्छेत् तु
text: In the case of the Vaiśya approaching an unprotected Kṣatriya woman, the fine shall be five hundred; but the Kṣatriya may suffer tonsure or the fine.
iast: kṣatriyāyāmaguptāyāṃ vaiśye pañcaśataṃ damaḥ mūtreṇa mauṇḍyamicchet tu kṣatriyo daṇḍameva vā ṛcchet tu

sanskrit_iast: agupte kṣatriyāvaiśye śūdrāṃ vā brāhmaṇo vrajan śatāni pañca daṇḍyaḥ syāt sahasraṃ tvantyajastriyam
meaning: अगुप्ते क्षत्रियावैश्ये शूद्रां वा ब्राह्मणो व्रजन् शतानि पञ्च दण्ड्यः स्यात् सहस्रं त्वन्त्यजस्त्रियम्
text: The Brāhmaṇa, approaching an unprotected Vaiśya or Kṣatriya woman, should be fined five hundred, and one thousand for approaching a woman of the lowest order.
iast: agupte kṣatriyāvaiśye śūdrāṃ vā brāhmaṇo vrajan śatāni pañca daṇḍyaḥ syāt sahasraṃ tvantyajastriyam

sanskrit_iast: yasya stenaḥ pure nāsti nānyastrīgo na duṣṭavāk na sāhasikadaṇḍaghno sa rājā śakralokabhāk
meaning: यस्य स्तेनः पुरे नास्ति नान्यस्त्रीगो न दुष्टवाक् न साहसिकदण्डघ्नो स राजा शक्रलोकभाक्
text: That king in whose town there is no thief, no adulterer, no defamer, no criminal, no assaulter,—attains the regions of Indra.
iast: yasya stenaḥ pure nāsti nānyastrīgo na duṣṭavāk na sāhasikadaṇḍaghno sa rājā śakralokabhāk

sanskrit_iast: eteṣāṃ nigraho rājñaḥ pañcānāṃ viṣaye svake sāṃrājyakṛt sajātyeṣu loke caiva yaśaskaraḥ
meaning: एतेषां निग्रहो राज्ञः पञ्चानां विषये स्वके सांराज्यकृत् सजात्येषु लोके चैव यशस्करः
text: The suppression of these five in his own dominions secures to the king paramount sovereignty among his peers and fame in the world.
iast: eteṣāṃ nigraho rājñaḥ pañcānāṃ viṣaye svake sāṃrājyakṛt sajātyeṣu loke caiva yaśaskaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṛtvijaṃ yastyajedyājyo yājyaṃ ca rtvik tyajedyadi śaktaṃ karmaṇyaduṣṭaṃ ca tayordaṇḍaḥ śataṃ śatam
meaning: ऋत्विजं यस्त्यजेद्याज्यो याज्यं च र्त्विक् त्यजेद्यदि शक्तं कर्मण्यदुष्टं च तयोर्दण्डः शतं शतम्
text: If a sacrificer forsakes an officiating priest, and if an officiating priest forsakes a sacrificer,—each being capable of doing the work and free from disqualifications,—their punishment is one hundred each
iast: ṛtvijaṃ yastyajedyājyo yājyaṃ ca rtvik tyajedyadi śaktaṃ karmaṇyaduṣṭaṃ ca tayordaṇḍaḥ śataṃ śatam

sanskrit_iast: na mātā na pitā na strī na putrastyāgamarhati tyajannapatitānetān rājñā daṇḍyaḥ śatāni ṣaṭ
meaning: न माता न पिता न स्त्री न पुत्रस्त्यागमर्हति त्यजन्नपतितानेतान् राज्ञा दण्ड्यः शतानि षट्
text: Neither the mother, nor the father, nor the wife, nor the son deserve to be forsaken; he who forsakes these, unless they are outcasts, should be fined six hundred by the king.
iast: na mātā na pitā na strī na putrastyāgamarhati tyajannapatitānetān rājñā daṇḍyaḥ śatāni ṣaṭ

sanskrit_iast: āśrameṣu dvijātīnāṃ kārye vivadatāṃ mithaḥ na vibrūyānnṛpo dharmaṃ cikīrṣan hitamātmanaḥ
meaning: आश्रमेषु द्विजातीनां कार्ये विवदतां मिथः न विब्रूयान्नृपो धर्मं चिकीर्षन् हितमात्मनः
text: For twice-born men disputing among themselves regarding any point relating to the orders, the king, desirous of his own welfare, shall not determine the law.
iast: āśrameṣu dvijātīnāṃ kārye vivadatāṃ mithaḥ na vibrūyānnṛpo dharmaṃ cikīrṣan hitamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathārhametānabhyarcya brāhmaṇaiḥ saha pārthivaḥ sāntvena praśamayyādau svadharmaṃ pratipādayet
meaning: यथार्हमेतानभ्यर्च्य ब्राह्मणैः सह पार्थिवः सान्त्वेन प्रशमय्यादौ स्वधर्मं प्रतिपादयेत्
text: Having, with the assistance of Brāhmaṇas, received them with due honour, the king shall, at first, pacify them with soothing words, and then explain to them their duty.
iast: yathārhametānabhyarcya brāhmaṇaiḥ saha pārthivaḥ sāntvena praśamayyādau svadharmaṃ pratipādayet

sanskrit_iast: prativeśyānuveśyau ca kalyāṇe viṃśatidvije arhāvabhojayan vipro daṇḍamarhati māṣakam
meaning: प्रतिवेश्यानुवेश्यौ च कल्याणे विंशतिद्विजे अर्हावभोजयन् विप्रो दण्डमर्हति माषकम्
text: If, at a festival where twenty twice-born men are invited, a Brāhmaṇa does not entertain his frontal and back neighbours, who are quite worthy,—he deserves to be fined one ‘māṣa.’
iast: prativeśyānuveśyau ca kalyāṇe viṃśatidvije arhāvabhojayan vipro daṇḍamarhati māṣakam

sanskrit_iast: śrotriyaḥ śrotriyaṃ sādhuṃ bhūtikṛtyeṣvabhojayan tadannaṃ dviguṇaṃ dāpyo hiraṇyaṃ caiva māṣakam airaṇyaṃ
meaning: श्रोत्रियः श्रोत्रियं साधुं भूतिकृत्येष्वभोजयन् तदन्नं द्विगुणं दाप्यो हिरण्यं चैव माषकम् ऐरण्यं
text: The Vedic scholar who does not entertain a worthy Vedic scholar at such auspicious rites, should be made to pay twice the quantity of that meal, and also a ‘māṣa’ of gold.
iast: śrotriyaḥ śrotriyaṃ sādhuṃ bhūtikṛtyeṣvabhojayan tadannaṃ dviguṇaṃ dāpyo hiraṇyaṃ caiva māṣakam airaṇyaṃ

sanskrit_iast: andho jaḍaḥ pīṭhasarpī saptatyā sthaviraśca yaḥ śrotriyeṣūpakurvaṃśca na dāpyāḥ kena cit karam
meaning: अन्धो जडः पीठसर्पी सप्तत्या स्थविरश्च यः श्रोत्रियेषूपकुर्वंश्च न दाप्याः केन चित् करम्
text: A blind man, an idiot, a cripple, an old man of seventy, and one who attends upon Vedic scholars should not be made to pay any taxes by any one.
iast: andho jaḍaḥ pīṭhasarpī saptatyā sthaviraśca yaḥ śrotriyeṣūpakurvaṃśca na dāpyāḥ kena cit karam

sanskrit_iast: śrotriyaṃ vyādhitārtau ca bālavṛddhāvakiñcanam mahākulīnamāryaṃ ca rājā saṃpūjayet sadā
meaning: श्रोत्रियं व्याधितार्तौ च बालवृद्धावकिञ्चनम् महाकुलीनमार्यं च राजा संपूजयेत् सदा
text: The king should always respect the Vedic scholar, the sick and the distressed, the infant and the aged, the indigent, the man of high family and the gentleman.
iast: śrotriyaṃ vyādhitārtau ca bālavṛddhāvakiñcanam mahākulīnamāryaṃ ca rājā saṃpūjayet sadā

sanskrit_iast: śālmalīphalake ślakṣṇe nenijyānnejakaḥ śanaiḥ na ca vāsāṃsi vāsobhirnirharenna ca vāsayet
meaning: शाल्मलीफलके श्लक्ष्णे नेनिज्यान्नेजकः शनैः न च वासांसि वासोभिर्निर्हरेन्न च वासयेत्
text: The washerman shall wash (clothes) gently on a smooth board of cotton-tree wood; he shall not carry clothes in other clothes; nor shall he allow them to be worn.
iast: śālmalīphalake ślakṣṇe nenijyānnejakaḥ śanaiḥ na ca vāsāṃsi vāsobhirnirharenna ca vāsayet

sanskrit_iast: tantuvāyo daśapalaṃ dadyādekapalādhikam ato 'nyathā vartamāno dāpyo dvādaśakaṃ damam
meaning: तन्तुवायो दशपलं दद्यादेकपलाधिकम् अतो 'न्यथा वर्तमानो दाप्यो द्वादशकं दमम्
text: The weaver shall repay ten ‘palas’ with one ‘pala’ added to it; if he acts otherwise than this, he should be made to pay a fine of twelve.
iast: tantuvāyo daśapalaṃ dadyādekapalādhikam ato 'nyathā vartamāno dāpyo dvādaśakaṃ damam

sanskrit_iast: śulkasthāneṣu kuśalāḥ sarvapaṇyavicakṣaṇāḥ kuryurarghaṃ yathāpaṇyaṃ tato viṃśaṃ nṛpo haret
meaning: शुल्कस्थानेषु कुशलाः सर्वपण्यविचक्षणाः कुर्युरर्घं यथापण्यं ततो विंशं नृपो हरेत्
text: The king shall take one-twentieth of the price of saleable commodities, that may be fixed by men who have experience of custom-houses and are experts in all kinds of merchandise.
iast: śulkasthāneṣu kuśalāḥ sarvapaṇyavicakṣaṇāḥ kuryurarghaṃ yathāpaṇyaṃ tato viṃśaṃ nṛpo haret

sanskrit_iast: rājñaḥ prakhyātabhāṇḍāni pratiṣiddhāni yāni ca tāṇi nirharato lobhāt sarvahāraṃ harennṛpaḥ
meaning: राज्ञः प्रख्यातभाण्डानि प्रतिषिद्धानि यानि च ताणि निर्हरतो लोभात् सर्वहारं हरेन्नृपः
text: Those commodities that have been proclaimed as the ‘king’s monopoly,’ and those that are forbidden,—if any one, through greed, exports these, the king shall confiscate all his property.
iast: rājñaḥ prakhyātabhāṇḍāni pratiṣiddhāni yāni ca tāṇi nirharato lobhāt sarvahāraṃ harennṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: śulkasthānaṃ pariharannakāle krayavikrayī mithyāvādī ca saṅkhyāne dāpyo 'ṣṭaguṇamatyayam
meaning: शुल्कस्थानं परिहरन्नकाले क्रयविक्रयी मिथ्यावादी च सङ्ख्याने दाप्यो 'ष्टगुणमत्ययम्
text: If one who buts and sells avoids a custom-house, and at the improper time, or makes a wrong statement in counting,—he shall be made to pay a fine eight times the amount evaded.
iast: śulkasthānaṃ pariharannakāle krayavikrayī mithyāvādī ca saṅkhyāne dāpyo 'ṣṭaguṇamatyayam

sanskrit_iast: āgamaṃ nirgamaṃ sthānaṃ tathā vṛddhikṣayāvubhau vicārya sarvapaṇyānāṃ kārayet krayavikrayau
meaning: आगमं निर्गमं स्थानं तथा वृद्धिक्षयावुभौ विचार्य सर्वपण्यानां कारयेत् क्रयविक्रयौ
text: The king shall regulate the purchase and sale of all marketable commodities after having taken into consideration their source, destination and detention, as also profit and loss.
iast: āgamaṃ nirgamaṃ sthānaṃ tathā vṛddhikṣayāvubhau vicārya sarvapaṇyānāṃ kārayet krayavikrayau

sanskrit_iast: pañcarātre pañcarātre pakṣe pakṣe 'tha vā gate kurvīta caiṣāṃ pratyakṣamarghasaṃsthāpanaṃ nṛpaḥ
meaning: पञ्चरात्रे पञ्चरात्रे पक्षे पक्षे 'थ वा गते कुर्वीत चैषां प्रत्यक्षमर्घसंस्थापनं नृपः
text: After the lapse of every five days, or after that of every fortnight, the king shall publicly fix the prices of things.
iast: pañcarātre pañcarātre pakṣe pakṣe 'tha vā gate kurvīta caiṣāṃ pratyakṣamarghasaṃsthāpanaṃ nṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tulāmānaṃ pratīmānaṃ sarvaṃ ca syāt sulakṣitam ṣaṭsu ṣaṭsu ca māseṣu punareva parīkṣayet
meaning: तुलामानं प्रतीमानं सर्वं च स्यात् सुलक्षितम् षट्सु षट्सु च मासेषु पुनरेव परीक्षयेत्
text: Scales, weights and measures should be duly marked; and they should be re-examined after every six months.
iast: tulāmānaṃ pratīmānaṃ sarvaṃ ca syāt sulakṣitam ṣaṭsu ṣaṭsu ca māseṣu punareva parīkṣayet

sanskrit_iast: paṇaṃ yānaṃ tare dāpyaṃ pauruṣo 'rdhapaṇaṃ tare pādaṃ paśuśca yoṣitca pādārdhaṃ riktakaḥ pumān pāde
meaning: पणं यानं तरे दाप्यं पौरुषो 'र्धपणं तरे पादं पशुश्च योषित्च पादार्धं रिक्तकः पुमान् पादे
text: At a ferry-crossing, a cart shall be made to pay one ‘paṇa’; one man’s burden half a ‘paṇa,’ an animal and a woman a quarter ‘paṇa,’ and an unloaded man one half of a quarter.
iast: paṇaṃ yānaṃ tare dāpyaṃ pauruṣo 'rdhapaṇaṃ tare pādaṃ paśuśca yoṣitca pādārdhaṃ riktakaḥ pumān pāde

sanskrit_iast: bhāṇḍapūrṇāni yānāni tāryaṃ dāpyāni sārataḥ riktabhāṇḍāni yat kiṃ cit pumāṃsaścaparicchadāḥ
meaning: भाण्डपूर्णानि यानानि तार्यं दाप्यानि सारतः रिक्तभाण्डानि यत् किं चित् पुमांसश्चपरिच्छदाः
text: Carts laden with commodities should be made to pay the ferry-toll according to their value; those not laden with commodities may pay a triple, as also men without luggage.
iast: bhāṇḍapūrṇāni yānāni tāryaṃ dāpyāni sārataḥ riktabhāṇḍāni yat kiṃ cit pumāṃsaścaparicchadāḥ

sanskrit_iast: dīrghādhvani yathādeśaṃ yathākālaṃ taro bhavet nadītīreṣu tadvidyāt samudre nāsti lakṣaṇam
meaning: दीर्घाध्वनि यथादेशं यथाकालं तरो भवेत् नदीतीरेषु तद्विद्यात् समुद्रे नास्ति लक्षणम्
text: For a long passage, the boat-fare should be in propor tion to the time and place; this should be understood to be the rule regarding the banks of rivers; in connection with the sea, there is no fixed rule.
iast: dīrghādhvani yathādeśaṃ yathākālaṃ taro bhavet nadītīreṣu tadvidyāt samudre nāsti lakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: garbhiṇī tu dvimāsādistathā pravrajito muniḥ brāhmaṇā liṅginaścaiva na dāpyāstārikaṃ tare
meaning: गर्भिणी तु द्विमासादिस्तथा प्रव्रजितो मुनिः ब्राह्मणा लिङ्गिनश्चैव न दाप्यास्तारिकं तरे
text: But a woman who is pregnant two months or more, an ascetic, a hermit, and brāhmaṇas in holy orders shall not be made to pay the toll at a ferry-crossing.
iast: garbhiṇī tu dvimāsādistathā pravrajito muniḥ brāhmaṇā liṅginaścaiva na dāpyāstārikaṃ tare

sanskrit_iast: yannāvi kiṃ ciddāśānāṃ viśīryetāparādhataḥ taddāśaireva dātavyaṃ samāgamya svato 'ṃśataḥ
meaning: यन्नावि किं चिद्दाशानां विशीर्येतापराधतः तद्दाशैरेव दातव्यं समागम्य स्वतो 'ंशतः
text: If anything on the boat happen to be damaged by the fault of the boatmen,—it shall be made good by the boatmen collectively, each according to his share.
iast: yannāvi kiṃ ciddāśānāṃ viśīryetāparādhataḥ taddāśaireva dātavyaṃ samāgamya svato 'ṃśataḥ

sanskrit_iast: eṣa nauyāyināmukto vyavahārasya nirṇayaḥ dāśāparādhatastoye daivike nāsti nigrahaḥ
meaning: एष नौयायिनामुक्तो व्यवहारस्य निर्णयः दाशापराधतस्तोये दैविके नास्ति निग्रहः
text: This law has been laid down in connection with suits by boat-passengers relating to the negligence of boatmen in water; there is no punishment in the case of accidents due to heaven.
iast: eṣa nauyāyināmukto vyavahārasya nirṇayaḥ dāśāparādhatastoye daivike nāsti nigrahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vāṇijyaṃ kārayedvaiśyaṃ kusīdaṃ kṛṣimeva ca paśūnāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ caiva dāsyaṃ śūdraṃ dvijanmanām
meaning: वाणिज्यं कारयेद्वैश्यं कुसीदं कृषिमेव च पशूनां रक्षणं चैव दास्यं शूद्रं द्विजन्मनाम्
text: He shall make the Vaiśya to carry on trade, money-lending, agriculture,—and cattle-trading; and the Śūdra to perform service for the twice-born castes.
iast: vāṇijyaṃ kārayedvaiśyaṃ kusīdaṃ kṛṣimeva ca paśūnāṃ rakṣaṇaṃ caiva dāsyaṃ śūdraṃ dvijanmanām

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyaṃ caiva vaiśyaṃ ca brāhmaṇo vṛttikarśitau bibhṛyādānṛśaṃsyena svāni karmāṇi kārayet
meaning: क्षत्रियं चैव वैश्यं च ब्राह्मणो वृत्तिकर्शितौ बिभृयादानृशंस्येन स्वानि कर्माणि कारयेत्
text: A Brāhmaṇa shall, through compassion, support a Vaiśya and a Kṣatriya, who are distressed for a livelihood, and should make them do his own work.
iast: kṣatriyaṃ caiva vaiśyaṃ ca brāhmaṇo vṛttikarśitau bibhṛyādānṛśaṃsyena svāni karmāṇi kārayet

sanskrit_iast: dāsyaṃ tu kārayanlobhādbrāhmaṇaḥ saṃskṛtān dvijān anicchataḥ prābhavatyādrājñā daṇḍyaḥ śatāni ṣaṭ
meaning: दास्यं तु कारयन्लोभाद्ब्राह्मणः संस्कृतान् द्विजान् अनिच्छतः प्राभवत्याद्राज्ञा दण्ड्यः शतानि षट्
text: If the Brāhmaṇa, through the sense of mastery, and out of greed, makes sanctified twice-born persons do fertile work, against their will,—he should be fined by the king six hundred.
iast: dāsyaṃ tu kārayanlobhādbrāhmaṇaḥ saṃskṛtān dvijān anicchataḥ prābhavatyādrājñā daṇḍyaḥ śatāni ṣaṭ

sanskrit_iast: śūdraṃ tu kārayeddāsyaṃ krītamakrītameva vā dāsyāyaiva hi sṛṣṭo 'sau brāhmaṇasya svayaṃbhuvā
meaning: शूद्रं तु कारयेद्दास्यं क्रीतमक्रीतमेव वा दास्यायैव हि सृष्टो 'सौ ब्राह्मणस्य स्वयंभुवा
text: But a Śūdra, whether bought or unbought, he shall make to do servile work; since it is for doing servile work for the Brāhmaṇa that he has been created by the self-born one.
iast: śūdraṃ tu kārayeddāsyaṃ krītamakrītameva vā dāsyāyaiva hi sṛṣṭo 'sau brāhmaṇasya svayaṃbhuvā

sanskrit_iast: na svāminā nisṛṣṭo 'pi śūdro dāsyādvimucyate nisargajaṃ hi tat tasya kastasmāt tadapohati
meaning: न स्वामिना निसृष्टो 'पि शूद्रो दास्याद्विमुच्यते निसर्गजं हि तत् तस्य कस्तस्मात् तदपोहति
text: Even though set free by the master, the Śūdra is not released from service; since that is innate in him, and who can release him from it?
iast: na svāminā nisṛṣṭo 'pi śūdro dāsyādvimucyate nisargajaṃ hi tat tasya kastasmāt tadapohati

sanskrit_iast: dhvajāhṛto bhaktadāso gṛhajaḥ krītadattrimau paitriko daṇḍadāsaśca saptaite dāsayonayaḥ
meaning: ध्वजाहृतो भक्तदासो गृहजः क्रीतदत्त्रिमौ पैत्रिको दण्डदासश्च सप्तैते दासयोनयः
text: There are seven kinds of slaves—(1) captured under a banner, (2) slave on food, (3) born in the house, (4) bought, (5) presented, (6) hereditary, and (7) slave by punishment.
iast: dhvajāhṛto bhaktadāso gṛhajaḥ krītadattrimau paitriko daṇḍadāsaśca saptaite dāsayonayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhāryā putraśca dāsaśca traya evādhanāḥ smṛtāḥ yat te samadhigacchanti yasya te tasya taddhanam
meaning: भार्या पुत्रश्च दासश्च त्रय एवाधनाः स्मृताः यत् ते समधिगच्छन्ति यस्य ते तस्य तद्धनम्
text: The wive, the son and the slave,—these three are declared to have no property; whatever they acquire is the property of him to whom they belong.
iast: bhāryā putraśca dāsaśca traya evādhanāḥ smṛtāḥ yat te samadhigacchanti yasya te tasya taddhanam

sanskrit_iast: visrabdhaṃ brāhmaṇaḥ śūdrāddravyaupādānamācaret na hi tasyāsti kiṃ cit svaṃ bhartṛhāryadhano hi saḥ
meaning: विस्रब्धं ब्राह्मणः शूद्राद्द्रव्यौपादानमाचरेत् न हि तस्यास्ति किं चित् स्वं भर्तृहार्यधनो हि सः
text: The Brāhmaṇa may confidently have recourse to seizing the goods of the Śūdra; as the latter has no property, and his property is meant to be seized by the master.
iast: visrabdhaṃ brāhmaṇaḥ śūdrāddravyaupādānamācaret na hi tasyāsti kiṃ cit svaṃ bhartṛhāryadhano hi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyaśūdrau prayatnena svāni karmāṇi kārayet tau hi cyutau svakarmabhyaḥ kṣobhayetāmidaṃ jagat
meaning: वैश्यशूद्रौ प्रयत्नेन स्वानि कर्माणि कारयेत् तौ हि च्युतौ स्वकर्मभ्यः क्षोभयेतामिदं जगत्
text: The king shall make the Vaiśya and the Shudra carefully to perform their duties; for by swerving from their duties they would disturb this world.
iast: vaiśyaśūdrau prayatnena svāni karmāṇi kārayet tau hi cyutau svakarmabhyaḥ kṣobhayetāmidaṃ jagat

sanskrit_iast: ahanyahanyavekṣeta karmāntān vāhanāni ca āyavyayau ca niyatāvākarān kośameva ca
meaning: अहन्यहन्यवेक्षेत कर्मान्तान् वाहनानि च आयव्ययौ च नियतावाकरान् कोशमेव च
text: Day after day he shall look after his business-centres, his conveyances, his income and expenditure regularly, and mines and treasury.
iast: ahanyahanyavekṣeta karmāntān vāhanāni ca āyavyayau ca niyatāvākarān kośameva ca

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sarvānimān rājā vyavahārān samāpayan vyapohya kilbiṣaṃ sarvaṃ prāpnoti paramāṃ gatim
meaning: एवं सर्वानिमान् राजा व्यवहारान् समापयन् व्यपोह्य किल्बिषं सर्वं प्राप्नोति परमां गतिम्
iast: evaṃ sarvānimān rājā vyavahārān samāpayan vyapohya kilbiṣaṃ sarvaṃ prāpnoti paramāṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: puruṣasya striyāścaiva dharme vartmani tiṣṭhatoḥ dharmye saṃyoge viprayoge ca dharmān vakṣyāmi śāśvatān
meaning: पुरुषस्य स्त्रियाश्चैव धर्मे वर्त्मनि तिष्ठतोः धर्म्ये संयोगे विप्रयोगे च धर्मान् वक्ष्यामि शाश्वतान्
text: I shall now expound the eternal duties of the man and woman, who keep to the righteous path, during union and separation.
iast: puruṣasya striyāścaiva dharme vartmani tiṣṭhatoḥ dharmye saṃyoge viprayoge ca dharmān vakṣyāmi śāśvatān

sanskrit_iast: asvatantrāḥ striyaḥ kāryāḥ puruṣaiḥ svairdivāniśam viṣayeṣu ca sajjantyaḥ saṃsthāpyā ātmano vaśe
meaning: अस्वतन्त्राः स्त्रियः कार्याः पुरुषैः स्वैर्दिवानिशम् विषयेषु च सज्जन्त्यः संस्थाप्या आत्मनो वशे
text: During the day and the night women should not be left to themselves by their men. If they become addicted to sensual objects, they should be kept under one’s control.
iast: asvatantrāḥ striyaḥ kāryāḥ puruṣaiḥ svairdivāniśam viṣayeṣu ca sajjantyaḥ saṃsthāpyā ātmano vaśe

sanskrit_iast: pitā rakṣati kaumāre bhartā rakṣati yauvane rakṣanti sthavire putrā na strī svātantryamarhati
meaning: पिता रक्षति कौमारे भर्ता रक्षति यौवने रक्षन्ति स्थविरे पुत्रा न स्त्री स्वातन्त्र्यमर्हति
text: The father guards her during virginity, the husband guards her in youth, the sons guard her in old age; the woman is never fit for independence.—(iii).
iast: pitā rakṣati kaumāre bhartā rakṣati yauvane rakṣanti sthavire putrā na strī svātantryamarhati

sanskrit_iast: kāle 'dātā pitā vācyo vācyaścānupayan patiḥ mṛte bhartari putrastu vācyo māturarakṣitā
meaning: काले 'दाता पिता वाच्यो वाच्यश्चानुपयन् पतिः मृते भर्तरि पुत्रस्तु वाच्यो मातुररक्षिता
text: Censurable is the father who gives her not away at the right time; censurable the husband who approaches her not; and censurable the son who, on the death of her husband, does not take care of her
iast: kāle 'dātā pitā vācyo vācyaścānupayan patiḥ mṛte bhartari putrastu vācyo māturarakṣitā

sanskrit_iast: sūkṣmebhyo 'pi prasaṅgebhyaḥ striyo rakṣyā viśeṣataḥ striyā dvayorhi kulayoḥ śokamāvaheyurarakṣitāḥ
meaning: सूक्ष्मेभ्यो 'पि प्रसङ्गेभ्यः स्त्रियो रक्ष्या विशेषतः स्त्रिया द्वयोर्हि कुलयोः शोकमावहेयुररक्षिताः
text: Women should be specially guarded against even small attachments; for, if not guarded, they would bring grief to both families.
iast: sūkṣmebhyo 'pi prasaṅgebhyaḥ striyo rakṣyā viśeṣataḥ striyā dvayorhi kulayoḥ śokamāvaheyurarakṣitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: imaṃ hi sarvavarṇānāṃ paśyanto dharmamuttamam yatante rakṣituṃ bhāryāṃ bhartāro durbalā api
meaning: इमं हि सर्ववर्णानां पश्यन्तो धर्ममुत्तमम् यतन्ते रक्षितुं भार्यां भर्तारो दुर्बला अपि
text: Looking upon this as the highest duty of all castes, even weak husbands strive to guard their wives
iast: imaṃ hi sarvavarṇānāṃ paśyanto dharmamuttamam yatante rakṣituṃ bhāryāṃ bhartāro durbalā api

sanskrit_iast: svāṃ prasūtiṃ caritraṃ ca kulamātmānameva ca svaṃ ca dharmaṃ prayatnena jāyāṃ rakṣan hi rakṣati
meaning: स्वां प्रसूतिं चरित्रं च कुलमात्मानमेव च स्वं च धर्मं प्रयत्नेन जायां रक्षन् हि रक्षति
text: He who carefully protects his wipe preserves his offspring, his character, his family, his own self, and also his religion.
iast: svāṃ prasūtiṃ caritraṃ ca kulamātmānameva ca svaṃ ca dharmaṃ prayatnena jāyāṃ rakṣan hi rakṣati

sanskrit_iast: patirbhāryāṃ saṃpraviśya garbho bhūtvaiha jāyate jāyāyāstaddhi jāyātvaṃ yadasyāṃ jāyate punaḥ
meaning: पतिर्भार्यां संप्रविश्य गर्भो भूत्वैह जायते जायायास्तद्धि जायात्वं यदस्यां जायते पुनः
text: The husband, entering the womb of his wife, becomes the embryo and is then born; the wife-hood of the ‘wife’ consists in this that the husband is re-born of her.
iast: patirbhāryāṃ saṃpraviśya garbho bhūtvaiha jāyate jāyāyāstaddhi jāyātvaṃ yadasyāṃ jāyate punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yādṛśaṃ bhajate hi strī sutaṃ sūte tathāvidham tasmāt prajāviśuddhiarthaṃ striyaṃ rakṣet prayatnataḥ
meaning: यादृशं भजते हि स्त्री सुतं सूते तथाविधम् तस्मात् प्रजाविशुद्धिअर्थं स्त्रियं रक्षेत् प्रयत्नतः
text: As the man to whom the woman clings, so the offspring that she brings forth; hence for the sake of the purity of the offspring one should carefully guard the woman.
iast: yādṛśaṃ bhajate hi strī sutaṃ sūte tathāvidham tasmāt prajāviśuddhiarthaṃ striyaṃ rakṣet prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: na kaścidyoṣitaḥ śaktaḥ prasahya parirakṣitum etairupāyayogaistu śakyāstāḥ parirakṣitum
meaning: न कश्चिद्योषितः शक्तः प्रसह्य परिरक्षितुम् एतैरुपाययोगैस्तु शक्यास्ताः परिरक्षितुम्
text: No man can guard women forcibly; they can however be guarded by the employment of these expedients.
iast: na kaścidyoṣitaḥ śaktaḥ prasahya parirakṣitum etairupāyayogaistu śakyāstāḥ parirakṣitum

sanskrit_iast: arthasya saṅgrahe caināṃ vyaye caiva niyojayet śauce dharme 'nnapaktyāṃ ca pāriṇāhyasya vekṣaṇe
meaning: अर्थस्य सङ्ग्रहे चैनां व्यये चैव नियोजयेत् शौचे धर्मे 'न्नपक्त्यां च पारिणाह्यस्य वेक्षणे
text: He shall employ her in the accumulation and disbursement of wealth, as also in cleanliness, in religious acts, in the cooking of food and in taking care of the household furniture.
iast: arthasya saṅgrahe caināṃ vyaye caiva niyojayet śauce dharme 'nnapaktyāṃ ca pāriṇāhyasya vekṣaṇe

sanskrit_iast: arakṣitā gṛhe ruddhāḥ puruṣairāptakāribhiḥ ātmānamātmanā yāstu rakṣeyustāḥ surakṣitāḥ
meaning: अरक्षिता गृहे रुद्धाः पुरुषैराप्तकारिभिः आत्मानमात्मना यास्तु रक्षेयुस्ताः सुरक्षिताः
text: Women confined in the house under trusted servants are not well guarded; really well guarded are those who guard themselves by themselves.
iast: arakṣitā gṛhe ruddhāḥ puruṣairāptakāribhiḥ ātmānamātmanā yāstu rakṣeyustāḥ surakṣitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: pānaṃ durjanasaṃsargaḥ patyā ca viraho 'ṭanam svapno 'nyagehavāsaśca nārīsaṃdūṣaṇāni ṣaṭ
meaning: पानं दुर्जनसंसर्गः पत्या च विरहो 'टनम् स्वप्नो 'न्यगेहवासश्च नारीसंदूषणानि षट्
text: Drinking, associating with wicked people, separation from her husband, rambling, sleeping and residence at other’s house are the six corrupters of women.
iast: pānaṃ durjanasaṃsargaḥ patyā ca viraho 'ṭanam svapno 'nyagehavāsaśca nārīsaṃdūṣaṇāni ṣaṭ

sanskrit_iast: naitā rūpaṃ parīkṣante nāsāṃ vayasi saṃsthitiḥ surūpaṃ vā virūpaṃ vā pumānityeva bhuñjate
meaning: नैता रूपं परीक्षन्ते नासां वयसि संस्थितिः सुरूपं वा विरूपं वा पुमानित्येव भुञ्जते
text: They care not for beauty; they have no regard for age; be he handsome or ugly, they enjoy the man simply because he is a male.
iast: naitā rūpaṃ parīkṣante nāsāṃ vayasi saṃsthitiḥ surūpaṃ vā virūpaṃ vā pumānityeva bhuñjate

sanskrit_iast: pauṃścalyāccalacittācca naisnehyācca svabhāvataḥ naiḥsnehyāc rakṣitā yatnato 'pīha bhartṛṣvetā vikurvate
meaning: पौंश्चल्याच्चलचित्ताच्च नैस्नेह्याच्च स्वभावतः नैःस्नेह्याच् रक्षिता यत्नतो 'पीह भर्तृष्वेता विकुर्वते
text: Even though carefully guarded, they injure their husbands, on account of their passion for males, of ficklemindedness and of innate want of tenderness.
iast: pauṃścalyāccalacittācca naisnehyācca svabhāvataḥ naiḥsnehyāc rakṣitā yatnato 'pīha bhartṛṣvetā vikurvate

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ svabhāvaṃ jñātvā 'sāṃ prajāpatinisargajam paramaṃ yatnamātiṣṭhet puruṣo rakṣaṇaṃ prati
meaning: एवं स्वभावं ज्ञात्वा 'सां प्रजापतिनिसर्गजम् परमं यत्नमातिष्ठेत् पुरुषो रक्षणं प्रति
text: Knowing this disposition to be innate in them, from the very creation of the lord, the man should make the highest effort to guard them.
iast: evaṃ svabhāvaṃ jñātvā 'sāṃ prajāpatinisargajam paramaṃ yatnamātiṣṭhet puruṣo rakṣaṇaṃ prati

sanskrit_iast: śayyā ''sanamalaṅkāraṃ kāmaṃ krodhamanārjavam anāryatāṃ drohabhāvaṃ kucaryāṃ ca strībhyo manurakalpayat drogdhṛbhāvaṃ
meaning: शय्या ''सनमलङ्कारं कामं क्रोधमनार्जवम् अनार्यतां द्रोहभावं कुचर्यां च स्त्रीभ्यो मनुरकल्पयत् द्रोग्धृभावं
text: Manu assigned to women sleep, sitting, ornament, lust, anger, dishonesty, malice and bad conduct.
iast: śayyā ''sanamalaṅkāraṃ kāmaṃ krodhamanārjavam anāryatāṃ drohabhāvaṃ kucaryāṃ ca strībhyo manurakalpayat drogdhṛbhāvaṃ

sanskrit_iast: nāsti strīṇāṃ kriyā mantrairiti dharme vyavasthitiḥ nirindriyā hyamantrāśca strībhyo anṛtamiti sthitiḥ striyo
meaning: नास्ति स्त्रीणां क्रिया मन्त्रैरिति धर्मे व्यवस्थितिः निरिन्द्रिया ह्यमन्त्राश्च स्त्रीभ्यो अनृतमिति स्थितिः स्त्रियो
text: For women there is no dealing with the sacred texts; such is the rule of law; the fact is that, being destitute of organs and devoid of sacred texts, women are ‘false’
iast: nāsti strīṇāṃ kriyā mantrairiti dharme vyavasthitiḥ nirindriyā hyamantrāśca strībhyo anṛtamiti sthitiḥ striyo

sanskrit_iast: tathā ca śrutayo bahvyo nigītā nigameṣvapi svālakṣaṇyaparīkṣārthaṃ tāsāṃ śṛṇuta niṣkṛtīḥ
meaning: तथा च श्रुतयो बह्व्यो निगीता निगमेष्वपि स्वालक्षण्यपरीक्षार्थं तासां शृणुत निष्कृतीः
text: So also there are many texts sung in the vedas with a view to indicate the true character of women. from among these listen to those texts that are meant to be expiatory.
iast: tathā ca śrutayo bahvyo nigītā nigameṣvapi svālakṣaṇyaparīkṣārthaṃ tāsāṃ śṛṇuta niṣkṛtīḥ

sanskrit_iast: yan me mātā pralulubhe vicarantyapativratā tan me retaḥ pitā vṛṅktāmityasyaitannidarśanam
meaning: यन् मे माता प्रलुलुभे विचरन्त्यपतिव्रता तन् मे रेतः पिता वृङ्क्तामित्यस्यैतन्निदर्शनम्
text: ‘If my mother, unfaithful unto her lord, became enamoured while roaming about,—may my father’s semen remove that from me’;—this is an example of this.
iast: yan me mātā pralulubhe vicarantyapativratā tan me retaḥ pitā vṛṅktāmityasyaitannidarśanam

sanskrit_iast: dhyāyatyaniṣṭaṃ yat kiṃ cit pāṇigrāhasya cetasā tasyaiṣa vyabhicārasya nihnavaḥ samyagucyate
meaning: ध्यायत्यनिष्टं यत् किं चित् पाणिग्राहस्य चेतसा तस्यैष व्यभिचारस्य निह्नवः सम्यगुच्यते
text: What is said here is the proper expiation for whatever ill she thinks in her mind of her husband.
iast: dhyāyatyaniṣṭaṃ yat kiṃ cit pāṇigrāhasya cetasā tasyaiṣa vyabhicārasya nihnavaḥ samyagucyate

sanskrit_iast: yādṛgguṇena bhartrā strī saṃyujyeta yathāvidhi tādṛgguṇā sā bhavati samudreṇaiva nimnagā
meaning: यादृग्गुणेन भर्त्रा स्त्री संयुज्येत यथाविधि तादृग्गुणा सा भवति समुद्रेणैव निम्नगा
text: When a woman is united in one form with a man possessed of certain qualities, she becomes herself endowed with similar qualities,—like a river united with the ocean.
iast: yādṛgguṇena bhartrā strī saṃyujyeta yathāvidhi tādṛgguṇā sā bhavati samudreṇaiva nimnagā

sanskrit_iast: akṣamālā vasiṣṭhena saṃyuktā 'dhamayonijā śāraṅgī mandapālena jagāmābhyarhaṇīyatām
meaning: अक्षमाला वसिष्ठेन संयुक्ता 'धमयोनिजा शारङ्गी मन्दपालेन जगामाभ्यर्हणीयताम्
text: The low-bornAkṣamālāunited withVaśiṣṭha, and the doe united withMandapālū, became worthy of worship.
iast: akṣamālā vasiṣṭhena saṃyuktā 'dhamayonijā śāraṅgī mandapālena jagāmābhyarhaṇīyatām

sanskrit_iast: etāścānyāśca loke 'sminnapakṛṣṭaprasūtayaḥ 'vakṛṣṭaprasūtayaḥ utkarṣaṃ yoṣitaḥ prāptāḥ svaiḥ svairbhartṛguṇaiḥ śubhaiḥ
meaning: एताश्चान्याश्च लोके 'स्मिन्नपकृष्टप्रसूतयः 'वकृष्टप्रसूतयः उत्कर्षं योषितः प्राप्ताः स्वैः स्वैर्भर्तृगुणैः शुभैः
text: These as well as other women, of low birth, have attained eminence in the world, through the good qualities of their respective husbands.
iast: etāścānyāśca loke 'sminnapakṛṣṭaprasūtayaḥ 'vakṛṣṭaprasūtayaḥ utkarṣaṃ yoṣitaḥ prāptāḥ svaiḥ svairbhartṛguṇaiḥ śubhaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: eṣauditā lokayātrā nityaṃ strīpuṃsayoḥ śubhā pretyaiha ca sukhaudarkān prajādharmānnibodhata
meaning: एषौदिता लोकयात्रा नित्यं स्त्रीपुंसयोः शुभा प्रेत्यैह च सुखौदर्कान् प्रजाधर्मान्निबोधत
text: Thus has been declared the common practice, as between husband and wife, which is always happy; now understand the laws relating to children, which are conducive to happiness here as well as after death.
iast: eṣauditā lokayātrā nityaṃ strīpuṃsayoḥ śubhā pretyaiha ca sukhaudarkān prajādharmānnibodhata

sanskrit_iast: prajanārthaṃ mahābhāgāḥ pūjārhā gṛhadīptayaḥ striyaḥ śriyaśca geheṣu na viśeṣo 'sti kaścana
meaning: प्रजनार्थं महाभागाः पूजार्हा गृहदीप्तयः स्त्रियः श्रियश्च गेहेषु न विशेषो 'स्ति कश्चन
text: There is no difference whatever between the goddess of fortune and the women who secure many blessings for the sake of bearing children, who are worthy of worship and who form the glory of their household
iast: prajanārthaṃ mahābhāgāḥ pūjārhā gṛhadīptayaḥ striyaḥ śriyaśca geheṣu na viśeṣo 'sti kaścana

sanskrit_iast: utpādanamapatyasya jātasya paripālanam pratyahaṃ lokayātrāyāḥ pratyakṣaṃ strī nibandhanam pratyarthaṃ
meaning: उत्पादनमपत्यस्य जातस्य परिपालनम् प्रत्यहं लोकयात्रायाः प्रत्यक्षं स्त्री निबन्धनम् प्रत्यर्थं
text: The begetting of the child, the nourishing of the born, and the ordinary life of the world,—of each of these things the woman is clearly the main-spring.
iast: utpādanamapatyasya jātasya paripālanam pratyahaṃ lokayātrāyāḥ pratyakṣaṃ strī nibandhanam pratyarthaṃ

sanskrit_iast: apatyaṃ dharmakāryāṇi śuśrūṣā ratiruttamā dārā 'dhīnastathā svargaḥ pitr̥̄ṇāmātmanaśca ha
meaning: अपत्यं धर्मकार्याणि शुश्रूषा रतिरुत्तमा दारा 'धीनस्तथा स्वर्गः पित्र्̥̄णामात्मनश्च ह
text: Off-spring, religious acts, faithful service, highest happiness,—all this is dependent on the wife; as also the attainment of heaven by oneself as well as by his forefathers.
iast: apatyaṃ dharmakāryāṇi śuśrūṣā ratiruttamā dārā 'dhīnastathā svargaḥ pitr̥̄ṇāmātmanaśca ha

sanskrit_iast: patiṃ yā nābhicarati manovāgdehasaṃyatā sā bhartṛlokānāpnoti sadbhiḥ sādhvīiti caucyate
meaning: पतिं या नाभिचरति मनोवाग्देहसंयता सा भर्तृलोकानाप्नोति सद्भिः साध्वीइति चौच्यते
text: The woman, who, through failure in her duty to her husband, becomes an object of contempt in the world, comes to be born as a jackal and is tormented by foul diseases.
iast: patiṃ yā nābhicarati manovāgdehasaṃyatā sā bhartṛlokānāpnoti sadbhiḥ sādhvīiti caucyate
