sanskrit_iast: vyabhicārāt tu bhartuḥ strī loke prāpnoti nindyatām sṛgālayoniṃ cāpnoti pāparogaiśca pīḍyate śṛgālayoniṃ
meaning: व्यभिचारात् तु भर्तुः स्त्री लोके प्राप्नोति निन्द्यताम् सृगालयोनिं चाप्नोति पापरोगैश्च पीड्यते शृगालयोनिं
text: The woman, who, through failure in her duty to her husband, becomes an object of contempt in the world, comes to be born as a jackal and is tormented by foul diseases.
iast: vyabhicārāt tu bhartuḥ strī loke prāpnoti nindyatām sṛgālayoniṃ cāpnoti pāparogaiśca pīḍyate śṛgālayoniṃ

sanskrit_iast: putraṃ pratyuditaṃ sadbhiḥ pūrvajaiśca maharṣibhiḥ viśvajanyamimaṃ puṇyamupanyāsaṃ nibodhata
meaning: पुत्रं प्रत्युदितं सद्भिः पूर्वजैश्च महर्षिभिः विश्वजन्यमिमं पुण्यमुपन्यासं निबोधत
text: Listen to the following disquisition regarding the son, propitious and salutary to the world, set forth by the wise patriarchs and the great sages.
iast: putraṃ pratyuditaṃ sadbhiḥ pūrvajaiśca maharṣibhiḥ viśvajanyamimaṃ puṇyamupanyāsaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: bhartari putraṃ vijānanti śrutidvaidhaṃ tu kartari bhartuḥ āhurutpādakaṃ ke cidapare kṣetriṇaṃ viduḥ
meaning: भर्तरि पुत्रं विजानन्ति श्रुतिद्वैधं तु कर्तरि भर्तुः आहुरुत्पादकं के चिदपरे क्षेत्रिणं विदुः
text: They recognise the son to be the husband’s; but in regard to one who is only the progenitor, there is diversity of opinion; some people declare the begetter, while others the owner of the soil (to be the owner of the child).
iast: bhartari putraṃ vijānanti śrutidvaidhaṃ tu kartari bhartuḥ āhurutpādakaṃ ke cidapare kṣetriṇaṃ viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣetrabhūtā smṛtā nārī bījabhūtaḥ smṛtaḥ pumān kṣetrabījasamāyogāt saṃbhavaḥ sarvadehinām
meaning: क्षेत्रभूता स्मृता नारी बीजभूतः स्मृतः पुमान् क्षेत्रबीजसमायोगात् संभवः सर्वदेहिनाम्
text: The woman has been declared to be like the ‘soil,’ and the man has been declared to be like the seed; and the production of all corporeal beings proceeds from the union of the soil and the seed.
iast: kṣetrabhūtā smṛtā nārī bījabhūtaḥ smṛtaḥ pumān kṣetrabījasamāyogāt saṃbhavaḥ sarvadehinām

sanskrit_iast: viśiṣṭaṃ kutra cidbījaṃ strīyonistveva kutra cit ubhayaṃ tu samaṃ yatra sā prasūtiḥ praśasyate
meaning: विशिष्टं कुत्र चिद्बीजं स्त्रीयोनिस्त्वेव कुत्र चित् उभयं तु समं यत्र सा प्रसूतिः प्रशस्यते
text: In some cases the seed is prominent; but in others it is the female womb; when both are equal, the offspring is highly commended.
iast: viśiṣṭaṃ kutra cidbījaṃ strīyonistveva kutra cit ubhayaṃ tu samaṃ yatra sā prasūtiḥ praśasyate

sanskrit_iast: bījasya caiva yonyāśca bījamutkṛṣṭamucyate sarvabhūtaprasūtirhi bījalakṣaṇalakṣitā
meaning: बीजस्य चैव योन्याश्च बीजमुत्कृष्टमुच्यते सर्वभूतप्रसूतिर्हि बीजलक्षणलक्षिता
text: [Prima-facie argument]—“As between the seed and the womb, the seed is declared to be superior; because the production of all things is marked by the characteristics of the seed.”
iast: bījasya caiva yonyāśca bījamutkṛṣṭamucyate sarvabhūtaprasūtirhi bījalakṣaṇalakṣitā

sanskrit_iast: yādṛśaṃ tūpyate bījaṃ kṣetre kālaupapādite tādṛg rohati tat tasmin bījaṃ svairvyañjitaṃ guṇaiḥ
meaning: यादृशं तूप्यते बीजं क्षेत्रे कालौपपादिते तादृग् रोहति तत् तस्मिन् बीजं स्वैर्व्यञ्जितं गुणैः
text: “As is the seed which is sown in the soil prepared in season, so does the seed spring forth, marked by its own qualities.”
iast: yādṛśaṃ tūpyate bījaṃ kṣetre kālaupapādite tādṛg rohati tat tasmin bījaṃ svairvyañjitaṃ guṇaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: iyaṃ bhūmirhi bhūtānāṃ śāśvatī yonirucyate na ca yoniguṇān kāṃścidbījaṃ puṣyati puṣṭiṣu
meaning: इयं भूमिर्हि भूतानां शाश्वती योनिरुच्यते न च योनिगुणान् कांश्चिद्बीजं पुष्यति पुष्टिषु
text: “This earth is called the primeval womb of things; and yet, in its development, the seed does not develop any qualities of the womb.”
iast: iyaṃ bhūmirhi bhūtānāṃ śāśvatī yonirucyate na ca yoniguṇān kāṃścidbījaṃ puṣyati puṣṭiṣu

sanskrit_iast: bhūmāvapyekakedāre kālauptāni kṛṣīvalaiḥ nānārūpāṇi jāyante bījānīha svabhāvataḥ
meaning: भूमावप्येककेदारे कालौप्तानि कृषीवलैः नानारूपाणि जायन्ते बीजानीह स्वभावतः
text: “In this world, seeds sown in season by the cultivators even in one and the same plot of land spring-forth in various forms, according to their nature”.
iast: bhūmāvapyekakedāre kālauptāni kṛṣīvalaiḥ nānārūpāṇi jāyante bījānīha svabhāvataḥ

sanskrit_iast: vrīhayaḥ śālayo mudgāstilā māṣāstathā yavāḥ yathābījaṃ prarohanti laśunānīkṣavastathā
meaning: व्रीहयः शालयो मुद्गास्तिला माषास्तथा यवाः यथाबीजं प्ररोहन्ति लशुनानीक्षवस्तथा
text: “Vrīhi-corn, rice, mudga-beans, sesamum,māṣa-beans, barley, leeks and sugar-cane are produced in accordance with the seeds.”
iast: vrīhayaḥ śālayo mudgāstilā māṣāstathā yavāḥ yathābījaṃ prarohanti laśunānīkṣavastathā

sanskrit_iast: anyaduptaṃ jātamanyadityetatnaupapadyate upyate yaddhi yadbījaṃ tat tadeva prarohati
meaning: अन्यदुप्तं जातमन्यदित्येतत्नौपपद्यते उप्यते यद्धि यद्बीजं तत् तदेव प्ररोहति
text: “It is not possible that what is sown is of one kind and what is produced is of a different kind; the seed that is produced is the same that is sown.”
iast: anyaduptaṃ jātamanyadityetatnaupapadyate upyate yaddhi yadbījaṃ tat tadeva prarohati

sanskrit_iast: tat prājñena vinītena jñānavijñānavedinā āyuṣkāmena vaptavyaṃ na jātu parayoṣiti
meaning: तत् प्राज्ञेन विनीतेन ज्ञानविज्ञानवेदिना आयुष्कामेन वप्तव्यं न जातु परयोषिति
text: [The established conclusion]—for this reason he who is intelligent, well-trained, and conversant with the sciences and the arts, should never, if he desires longevity, sow in another’s wife.
iast: tat prājñena vinītena jñānavijñānavedinā āyuṣkāmena vaptavyaṃ na jātu parayoṣiti

sanskrit_iast: atra gāthā vāyugītāḥ kīrtayanti purāvidaḥ yathā bījaṃ na vaptavyaṃ puṃsā paraparigrahe
meaning: अत्र गाथा वायुगीताः कीर्तयन्ति पुराविदः यथा बीजं न वप्तव्यं पुंसा परपरिग्रहे
text: On this point, persons conversant with ancient lore recite some ‘Gāthās’ sung byVāyu, to the effect that man should not sow his seed in what belongs to another.
iast: atra gāthā vāyugītāḥ kīrtayanti purāvidaḥ yathā bījaṃ na vaptavyaṃ puṃsā paraparigrahe

sanskrit_iast: naśyatīṣuryathā viddhaḥ khe viddhamanuvidhyataḥ tathā naśyati vai kṣipraṃ bījaṃ paraparigrahe kṣiptaṃ
meaning: नश्यतीषुर्यथा विद्धः खे विद्धमनुविध्यतः तथा नश्यति वै क्षिप्रं बीजं परपरिग्रहे क्षिप्तं
text: ‘As the arrow shot by an after-shooter hitting a wounded animal in a hole (already made) is wasted, so does the seed become wasted when sown in what belongs to another.’
iast: naśyatīṣuryathā viddhaḥ khe viddhamanuvidhyataḥ tathā naśyati vai kṣipraṃ bījaṃ paraparigrahe kṣiptaṃ

sanskrit_iast: pṛthorapīmāṃ pṛthivīṃ bhāryāṃ pūrvavido viduḥ sthāṇucchedasya kedāramāhuḥ śālyavato mṛgam
meaning: पृथोरपीमां पृथिवीं भार्यां पूर्वविदो विदुः स्थाणुच्छेदस्य केदारमाहुः शाल्यवतो मृगम्
text: People learned in ancient lore have regarded thisPṛthvī(earth) to be the wife ofPṛthu; they declare the field to belong to him who has cleared off the stalks, and the deer to him who struck the dart.
iast: pṛthorapīmāṃ pṛthivīṃ bhāryāṃ pūrvavido viduḥ sthāṇucchedasya kedāramāhuḥ śālyavato mṛgam

sanskrit_iast: etāvāneva puruṣo yatjāyā 'tmā prajaiti ha viprāḥ prāhustathā caitadyo bhartā sā smṛtāṅganā
meaning: एतावानेव पुरुषो यत्जाया 'त्मा प्रजैति ह विप्राः प्राहुस्तथा चैतद्यो भर्ता सा स्मृताङ्गना
text: The man is a man only in so far as he consists of himself, his wife and his progeny. thus it is that theBrāhmaṇashave declared that ‘the husband is declared to be the same as the wife.’
iast: etāvāneva puruṣo yatjāyā 'tmā prajaiti ha viprāḥ prāhustathā caitadyo bhartā sā smṛtāṅganā

sanskrit_iast: na niṣkrayavisargābhyāṃ bharturbhāryā vimucyate evaṃ dharmaṃ vijānīmaḥ prāk prajāpatinirmitam
meaning: न निष्क्रयविसर्गाभ्यां भर्तुर्भार्या विमुच्यते एवं धर्मं विजानीमः प्राक् प्रजापतिनिर्मितम्
text: Either by sale or by repudiation the wife is not released from her husband; such is the law that we know, as originally propounded by prajapati.
iast: na niṣkrayavisargābhyāṃ bharturbhāryā vimucyate evaṃ dharmaṃ vijānīmaḥ prāk prajāpatinirmitam

sanskrit_iast: sakṛdaṃśo nipatati sakṛt kanyā pradīyate sakṛdāha dadānīti trīṇyetāni satāṃ sakṛt dadāmīti
meaning: सकृदंशो निपतति सकृत् कन्या प्रदीयते सकृदाह ददानीति त्रीण्येतानि सतां सकृत् ददामीति
text: Once does the share fall to a man; once is a maiden given away; once does one say ‘I give’; each of these three comes only once.
iast: sakṛdaṃśo nipatati sakṛt kanyā pradīyate sakṛdāha dadānīti trīṇyetāni satāṃ sakṛt dadāmīti

sanskrit_iast: yathā go 'śvauṣṭradāsīṣu mahiṣy 'jā 'vikāsu ca nautpādakaḥ prajābhāgī tathaivānyāṅganāsvapi
meaning: यथा गो 'श्वौष्ट्रदासीषु महिष्य् 'जा 'विकासु च नौत्पादकः प्रजाभागी तथैवान्याङ्गनास्वपि
text: As with cows, mares, she-camels, slave-girls, buffaloes, she-goats and ewes, it is not the begetter who obtains the offspring,—even thus it is with the wives of others
iast: yathā go 'śvauṣṭradāsīṣu mahiṣy 'jā 'vikāsu ca nautpādakaḥ prajābhāgī tathaivānyāṅganāsvapi

sanskrit_iast: ye 'kṣetriṇo bījavantaḥ parakṣetrapravāpiṇaḥ te vai sasyasya jātasya na labhante phalaṃ kva cit
meaning: ये 'क्षेत्रिणो बीजवन्तः परक्षेत्रप्रवापिणः ते वै सस्यस्य जातस्य न लभन्ते फलं क्व चित्
text: If persons, possessing no fields, but having seeds, sow these in fields belonging to others,—they never obtain the grain of the crop that is produced.
iast: ye 'kṣetriṇo bījavantaḥ parakṣetrapravāpiṇaḥ te vai sasyasya jātasya na labhante phalaṃ kva cit

sanskrit_iast: yadanyagoṣu vṛṣabho vatsānāṃ janayetśatam gomināmeva te vatsā moghaṃ skanditamārṣabham
meaning: यदन्यगोषु वृषभो वत्सानां जनयेत्शतम् गोमिनामेव ते वत्सा मोघं स्कन्दितमार्षभम्
text: If a bull were to beget a hundred calves on others’ cows, those calves would belong to the owners of the cows, and the bull’s emissions would be in vain.
iast: yadanyagoṣu vṛṣabho vatsānāṃ janayetśatam gomināmeva te vatsā moghaṃ skanditamārṣabham

sanskrit_iast: tathaivākṣetriṇo bījaṃ parakṣetrapravāpiṇaḥ kurvanti kṣetriṇāmarthaṃ na bījī labhate phalam
meaning: तथैवाक्षेत्रिणो बीजं परक्षेत्रप्रवापिणः कुर्वन्ति क्षेत्रिणामर्थं न बीजी लभते फलम्
text: Similarly persons who have no ‘soil’ of their own—if they sow in the ‘soil’ belonging to another man, they confer benefit upon the owner of the ‘soil,’ and the owner of the seed reaps no fruit.
iast: tathaivākṣetriṇo bījaṃ parakṣetrapravāpiṇaḥ kurvanti kṣetriṇāmarthaṃ na bījī labhate phalam

sanskrit_iast: phalaṃ tvanabhisaṃdhāya kṣetriṇāṃ bījināṃ tathā pratyakṣaṃ kṣetriṇāmartho bījādyonirgalīyasī barīyasī
meaning: फलं त्वनभिसंधाय क्षेत्रिणां बीजिनां तथा प्रत्यक्षं क्षेत्रिणामर्थो बीजाद्योनिर्गलीयसी बरीयसी
text: If between the owner of the soil and the owner of the seed, there has been no compact regarding the produce, then the chops belong clearly to the owner of the soil;—the receptacle being more important than the seed.
iast: phalaṃ tvanabhisaṃdhāya kṣetriṇāṃ bījināṃ tathā pratyakṣaṃ kṣetriṇāmartho bījādyonirgalīyasī barīyasī

sanskrit_iast: kriyābhyupagamāt tvetadbījārthaṃ yat pradīyate tasyaiha bhāginau dṛṣṭau bījī kṣetrika eva ca
meaning: क्रियाभ्युपगमात् त्वेतद्बीजार्थं यत् प्रदीयते तस्यैह भागिनौ दृष्टौ बीजी क्षेत्रिक एव च
text: If however the seed is given for the purpose of sowing, after the acceptance of a compact,—in that case both, the owner of the soil and the owner of the seed, are considered to be sharers of the produce.—(r>3)
iast: kriyābhyupagamāt tvetadbījārthaṃ yat pradīyate tasyaiha bhāginau dṛṣṭau bījī kṣetrika eva ca

sanskrit_iast: oghavātāhṛtaṃ bījaṃ yasya kṣetre prarohati kṣetrikasyaiva tadbījaṃ na vaptā labhate phalam na bījī labhate phalam
meaning: ओघवाताहृतं बीजं यस्य क्षेत्रे प्ररोहति क्षेत्रिकस्यैव तद्बीजं न वप्ता लभते फलम् न बीजी लभते फलम्
text: If seed, carried away by rain or wind, germinates in a soil,—that seed belongs to the owner of the soil, and the owner of the seed does not receive the produce.
iast: oghavātāhṛtaṃ bījaṃ yasya kṣetre prarohati kṣetrikasyaiva tadbījaṃ na vaptā labhate phalam na bījī labhate phalam

sanskrit_iast: eṣa dharmo gavāśvasya dāsyuṣṭrājāvikasya ca vihaṅgamahiṣīṇāṃ ca vijñeyaḥ prasavaṃ prati
meaning: एष धर्मो गवाश्वस्य दास्युष्ट्राजाविकस्य च विहङ्गमहिषीणां च विज्ञेयः प्रसवं प्रति
text: This same law should be understood as applying to the offspring of cows, mares, slave-girls, she-camels, she-goats and ewes; as also of birds and buffaloes.
iast: eṣa dharmo gavāśvasya dāsyuṣṭrājāvikasya ca vihaṅgamahiṣīṇāṃ ca vijñeyaḥ prasavaṃ prati

sanskrit_iast: etadvaḥ sāraphalgutvaṃ bījayonyoḥ prakīrtitam ataḥ paraṃ pravakṣyāmi yoṣitāṃ dharmamāpadi
meaning: एतद्वः सारफल्गुत्वं बीजयोन्योः प्रकीर्तितम् अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि योषितां धर्ममापदि
text: Thus has been explained to you the comparative importance and non-importance of the seed and the womb; after this i am going to expound the duties of women during times of distress.
iast: etadvaḥ sāraphalgutvaṃ bījayonyoḥ prakīrtitam ataḥ paraṃ pravakṣyāmi yoṣitāṃ dharmamāpadi

sanskrit_iast: bhrāturjyeṣṭhasya bhāryā yā gurupatnyanujasya sā yavīyasastu yā bhāryā snuṣā jyeṣṭhasya sā smṛtā
meaning: भ्रातुर्ज्येष्ठस्य भार्या या गुरुपत्न्यनुजस्य सा यवीयसस्तु या भार्या स्नुषा ज्येष्ठस्य सा स्मृता
text: The wife of the elder brother is, for the younger, a ‘wife of the preceptor’; and the wife of the younger brother has been declared to be a ‘daughter-in-law’ for the elder.
iast: bhrāturjyeṣṭhasya bhāryā yā gurupatnyanujasya sā yavīyasastu yā bhāryā snuṣā jyeṣṭhasya sā smṛtā

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭho yavīyaso bhāryāṃ yavīyān vā 'grajastriyam patitau bhavato gatvā niyuktāvapyanāpadi
meaning: ज्येष्ठो यवीयसो भार्यां यवीयान् वा 'ग्रजस्त्रियम् पतितौ भवतो गत्वा नियुक्तावप्यनापदि
text: If the elder brother has recourse to the wife of the younger, or the younger brother to the wife of the elder, they become outcasts, even though ‘authorised,’—except in times of distress.
iast: jyeṣṭho yavīyaso bhāryāṃ yavīyān vā 'grajastriyam patitau bhavato gatvā niyuktāvapyanāpadi

sanskrit_iast: devarādvā sapiṇḍādvā striyā samyakniyuktayā prajaipsitā ''dhigantavyā saṃtānasya parikṣaye
meaning: देवराद्वा सपिण्डाद्वा स्त्रिया सम्यक्नियुक्तया प्रजैप्सिता ''धिगन्तव्या संतानस्य परिक्षये
text: On failure of issue, the woman, on being authorised, may obtain, in the proper manner, the desired offspring, either from her younger brother-in-law or from a ‘Sapiṇḍa’.
iast: devarādvā sapiṇḍādvā striyā samyakniyuktayā prajaipsitā ''dhigantavyā saṃtānasya parikṣaye

sanskrit_iast: vidhavāyāṃ niyuktastu ghṛtākto vāgyato niśi ekamutpādayet putraṃ na dvitīyaṃ kathaṃ cana
meaning: विधवायां नियुक्तस्तु घृताक्तो वाग्यतो निशि एकमुत्पादयेत् पुत्रं न द्वितीयं कथं चन
text: He who has been authorised in regard to a widow shall, annointed with clarified butter and with speech controlled, beget, at night, one son,—and on no account a second one.
iast: vidhavāyāṃ niyuktastu ghṛtākto vāgyato niśi ekamutpādayet putraṃ na dvitīyaṃ kathaṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: dvitīyameke prajanaṃ manyante strīṣu tadvidaḥ anirvṛtaṃ niyogārthaṃ paśyanto dharmatastayoḥ 'nirvṛttaṃ
meaning: द्वितीयमेके प्रजनं मन्यन्ते स्त्रीषु तद्विदः अनिर्वृतं नियोगार्थं पश्यन्तो धर्मतस्तयोः 'निर्वृत्तं
text: Some people, learned in the subject, admit, on the basis of propriety, of a second procreation on women,—perceiving, as they do, that the couple’s purpose of authorisation’ is not (otherwise) accomplished.
iast: dvitīyameke prajanaṃ manyante strīṣu tadvidaḥ anirvṛtaṃ niyogārthaṃ paśyanto dharmatastayoḥ 'nirvṛttaṃ

sanskrit_iast: vidhavāyāṃ niyogārthe nirvṛtte tu yathāvidhi nivṛtte guruvatca snuṣāvatca varteyātāṃ parasparam
meaning: विधवायां नियोगार्थे निर्वृत्ते तु यथाविधि निवृत्ते गुरुवत्च स्नुषावत्च वर्तेयातां परस्परम्
text: But when the purpose of the ‘authorisation’ in regard to the widow has been duly accomplished, the two should behave towards each other like an elder and like a daughter-in-law.
iast: vidhavāyāṃ niyogārthe nirvṛtte tu yathāvidhi nivṛtte guruvatca snuṣāvatca varteyātāṃ parasparam

sanskrit_iast: niyuktau yau vidhiṃ hitvā varteyātāṃ tu kāmataḥ tāvubhau patitau syātāṃ snuṣāgagurutalpagau
meaning: नियुक्तौ यौ विधिं हित्वा वर्तेयातां तु कामतः तावुभौ पतितौ स्यातां स्नुषागगुरुतल्पगौ
text: If the two persons thus ‘authorised’ renounce the law and act from carnal desire, both would become outcasts,—being like one who has intercourse with his daughter-in-law and one who defiles the bed of his elder.
iast: niyuktau yau vidhiṃ hitvā varteyātāṃ tu kāmataḥ tāvubhau patitau syātāṃ snuṣāgagurutalpagau

sanskrit_iast: nānyasmin vidhavā nārī niyoktavyā dvijātibhiḥ anyasmin hi niyuñjānā dharmaṃ hanyuḥ sanātanam
meaning: नान्यस्मिन् विधवा नारी नियोक्तव्या द्विजातिभिः अन्यस्मिन् हि नियुञ्जाना धर्मं हन्युः सनातनम्
text: By twice-born persons the widow shall not be ‘authorised’ in regard to another person; by ‘authorising’ her in regard to another, they would violate the eternal law.
iast: nānyasmin vidhavā nārī niyoktavyā dvijātibhiḥ anyasmin hi niyuñjānā dharmaṃ hanyuḥ sanātanam

sanskrit_iast: naudvāhikeṣu mantreṣu niyogaḥ kīrtyate kva cit na vivāhavidhāvuktaṃ vidhavāvedanaṃ punaḥ
meaning: नौद्वाहिकेषु मन्त्रेषु नियोगः कीर्त्यते क्व चित् न विवाहविधावुक्तं विधवावेदनं पुनः
text: Nowhere in the mantra-texts bearing upon marriage is ‘authorisation’ mentioned; nor again is the marriage of the widow mentioned in the injunction of marriage.
iast: naudvāhikeṣu mantreṣu niyogaḥ kīrtyate kva cit na vivāhavidhāvuktaṃ vidhavāvedanaṃ punaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ayaṃ dvijairhi vidvadbhiḥ paśudharmo vigarhitaḥ manuṣyāṇāmapi prokto vene rājyaṃ praśāsati
meaning: अयं द्विजैर्हि विद्वद्भिः पशुधर्मो विगर्हितः मनुष्याणामपि प्रोक्तो वेने राज्यं प्रशासति
text: During the time that King Vena was ruling over his kingdom, this reprehensible bestial practice was introduced by ignorant twice-born men among men also.
iast: ayaṃ dvijairhi vidvadbhiḥ paśudharmo vigarhitaḥ manuṣyāṇāmapi prokto vene rājyaṃ praśāsati

sanskrit_iast: sa mahīmakhilāṃ bhuñjan rājarṣipravaraḥ purā varṇānāṃ saṅkaraṃ cakre kāmaupahatacetanaḥ
meaning: स महीमखिलां भुञ्जन् राजर्षिप्रवरः पुरा वर्णानां सङ्करं चक्रे कामौपहतचेतनः
text: In ancient times that chief of royal sages, possessing the whole world, brought about the confusion of castes, having his mind beset with lust.
iast: sa mahīmakhilāṃ bhuñjan rājarṣipravaraḥ purā varṇānāṃ saṅkaraṃ cakre kāmaupahatacetanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tataḥ prabhṛti yo mohāt pramītapatikāṃ striyam niyojayatyapatyārthaṃ taṃ vigarhanti sādhavaḥ
meaning: ततः प्रभृति यो मोहात् प्रमीतपतिकां स्त्रियम् नियोजयत्यपत्यार्थं तं विगर्हन्ति साधवः
text: Since then, whenever any one, through folly, ‘authorises’ a woman whose husband is dead, to beget children,—him the good men censure.
iast: tataḥ prabhṛti yo mohāt pramītapatikāṃ striyam niyojayatyapatyārthaṃ taṃ vigarhanti sādhavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasyā mriyeta kanyāyā vācā satye kṛte patiḥ tāmanena vidhānena nijo vindeta devaraḥ
meaning: यस्या म्रियेत कन्याया वाचा सत्ये कृते पतिः तामनेन विधानेन निजो विन्देत देवरः
text: If the husband of a maiden dies after the troth has been verbally plighted,—shall her then own younger brother-in-law espouse in the following manner.
iast: yasyā mriyeta kanyāyā vācā satye kṛte patiḥ tāmanena vidhānena nijo vindeta devaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathāvidhiadhigamyaināṃ śuklavastrāṃ śucivratām mitho bhajetā prasavāt sakṛtsakṛdṛtāvṛtau
meaning: यथाविधिअधिगम्यैनां शुक्लवस्त्रां शुचिव्रताम् मिथो भजेता प्रसवात् सकृत्सकृदृतावृतौ
text: When he has, according to rule, espoused her, clad in white garments and pure in her observances, they shall approach each other once in each season, until issue.
iast: yathāvidhiadhigamyaināṃ śuklavastrāṃ śucivratām mitho bhajetā prasavāt sakṛtsakṛdṛtāvṛtau

sanskrit_iast: na dattvā kasya cit kanyāṃ punardadyādvicakṣaṇaḥ dattvā punaḥ prayacchan hi prāpnoti puruṣānṛtam
meaning: न दत्त्वा कस्य चित् कन्यां पुनर्दद्याद्विचक्षणः दत्त्वा पुनः प्रयच्छन् हि प्राप्नोति पुरुषानृतम्
text: Having given away his daughter to one man, the wise man should not give her away again. Having given her away once, if he gives her again, he incurs the guilt of ‘fraud towards men.’—‘(7l)
iast: na dattvā kasya cit kanyāṃ punardadyādvicakṣaṇaḥ dattvā punaḥ prayacchan hi prāpnoti puruṣānṛtam

sanskrit_iast: vidhivat pratigṛhyāpi tyajet kanyāṃ vigarhitām vyādhitāṃ vipraduṣṭāṃ vā chadmanā caupapāditām
meaning: विधिवत् प्रतिगृह्यापि त्यजेत् कन्यां विगर्हिताम् व्याधितां विप्रदुष्टां वा छद्मना चौपपादिताम्
text: Even after having accepted the maiden in due form, one mat repudiate her, if she be blemished, or diseased, or corrupted, or betrothed by deception.
iast: vidhivat pratigṛhyāpi tyajet kanyāṃ vigarhitām vyādhitāṃ vipraduṣṭāṃ vā chadmanā caupapāditām

sanskrit_iast: yastu doṣavatīṃ kanyāmanākhyāyaupapādayet tasya tadvitathaṃ kuryāt kanyādāturdurātmanaḥ
meaning: यस्तु दोषवतीं कन्यामनाख्यायौपपादयेत् तस्य तद्वितथं कुर्यात् कन्यादातुर्दुरात्मनः
text: If a man gives away a defective maiden, without declaring the defects, one may annul that act of the wicked girl-betrother.
iast: yastu doṣavatīṃ kanyāmanākhyāyaupapādayet tasya tadvitathaṃ kuryāt kanyādāturdurātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vidhāya vṛttiṃ bhāryāyāḥ pravaset kāryavānnaraḥ avṛttikarśitā hi strī praduṣyet sthitimatyapi
meaning: विधाय वृत्तिं भार्यायाः प्रवसेत् कार्यवान्नरः अवृत्तिकर्शिता हि स्त्री प्रदुष्येत् स्थितिमत्यपि
text: A man having business may go abroad, after having provided for the maintenance of his wife; for a wife, even though virtuous, may become corrupt, when distressed by want of subsistence.
iast: vidhāya vṛttiṃ bhāryāyāḥ pravaset kāryavānnaraḥ avṛttikarśitā hi strī praduṣyet sthitimatyapi

sanskrit_iast: vidhāya proṣite vṛttiṃ jīvenniyamamāsthitā proṣite tvavidhāyaiva jīvetśilpairagarhitaiḥ
meaning: विधाय प्रोषिते वृत्तिं जीवेन्नियममास्थिता प्रोषिते त्वविधायैव जीवेत्शिल्पैरगर्हितैः
text: When the husband has gone abroad after having provided for her subsistence, she shall live on, firmly devoted to restraint. When however he has gone without providing for it, she shall subsist by unobjectionable industries.
iast: vidhāya proṣite vṛttiṃ jīvenniyamamāsthitā proṣite tvavidhāyaiva jīvetśilpairagarhitaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: proṣito dharmakāryārthaṃ pratīkṣyo 'ṣṭau naraḥ samāḥ vidyārthaṃ ṣaḍ yaśo 'rthaṃ vā kāmārthaṃ trīṃstu vatsarān
meaning: प्रोषितो धर्मकार्यार्थं प्रतीक्ष्यो 'ष्टौ नरः समाः विद्यार्थं षड् यशो 'र्थं वा कामार्थं त्रींस्तु वत्सरान्
text: If the husband went abroad for some sacred duty, he should be awaited for eight years; if for learning, or for fame, six years; but three years, if for pleasure.
iast: proṣito dharmakāryārthaṃ pratīkṣyo 'ṣṭau naraḥ samāḥ vidyārthaṃ ṣaḍ yaśo 'rthaṃ vā kāmārthaṃ trīṃstu vatsarān

sanskrit_iast: saṃvatsaraṃ pratīkṣeta dviṣantīṃ yoṣitaṃ patiḥ dviṣāṇāṃ ūrdhvaṃ saṃvatsarāt tvenāṃ dāyaṃ hṛtvā na saṃvaset
meaning: संवत्सरं प्रतीक्षेत द्विषन्तीं योषितं पतिः द्विषाणां ऊर्ध्वं संवत्सरात् त्वेनां दायं हृत्वा न संवसेत्
text: For one year the husband shall bear with a hating wife; after the year he shall wrest her property and cease to co-habit with her.
iast: saṃvatsaraṃ pratīkṣeta dviṣantīṃ yoṣitaṃ patiḥ dviṣāṇāṃ ūrdhvaṃ saṃvatsarāt tvenāṃ dāyaṃ hṛtvā na saṃvaset

sanskrit_iast: atikrāmet pramattaṃ yā mattaṃ rogārtameva vā sā trīn māsān parityājyā vibhūṣaṇaparicchadā
meaning: अतिक्रामेत् प्रमत्तं या मत्तं रोगार्तमेव वा सा त्रीन् मासान् परित्याज्या विभूषणपरिच्छदा
text: If the wife disregards her husband who is mad, or intoxicated, or afflicted by disease, she should be deprived of ornaments and appurtenances and abandoned for three months.
iast: atikrāmet pramattaṃ yā mattaṃ rogārtameva vā sā trīn māsān parityājyā vibhūṣaṇaparicchadā

sanskrit_iast: unmattaṃ patitaṃ klībamabījaṃ pāparogiṇam na tyāgo 'sti dviṣantyāśca na ca dāyāpavartanam
meaning: उन्मत्तं पतितं क्लीबमबीजं पापरोगिणम् न त्यागो 'स्ति द्विषन्त्याश्च न च दायापवर्तनम्
text: If, however, she shows aversion to one who is mad, or an outcast, or impotent, or seedless, or afflicted with foul disease, there shall be no desertion, nor the wresting of her property.
iast: unmattaṃ patitaṃ klībamabījaṃ pāparogiṇam na tyāgo 'sti dviṣantyāśca na ca dāyāpavartanam

sanskrit_iast: madyapā 'sādhuvṛttā ca pratikūlā ca yā bhavet madyapāsatyavṛttā vyādhitā vā 'dhivettavyā hiṃsrā 'rthaghnī ca sarvadā
meaning: मद्यपा 'साधुवृत्ता च प्रतिकूला च या भवेत् मद्यपासत्यवृत्ता व्याधिता वा 'धिवेत्तव्या हिंस्रा 'र्थघ्नी च सर्वदा
text: If the wife is a drunkard, or false in conduct, or rebellious, or diseased or mischievous, or wasteful,—she should be superseded.
iast: madyapā 'sādhuvṛttā ca pratikūlā ca yā bhavet madyapāsatyavṛttā vyādhitā vā 'dhivettavyā hiṃsrā 'rthaghnī ca sarvadā

sanskrit_iast: vandhyāṣṭame 'dhivedyā'abde daśame tu mṛtaprajā ekādaśe strījananī sadyastvapriyavādinī
meaning: वन्ध्याष्टमे 'धिवेद्या'अब्दे दशमे तु मृतप्रजा एकादशे स्त्रीजननी सद्यस्त्वप्रियवादिनी
text: The barren wife shall be superseded in the eighth year; in the tenth she whose children die off; in the eleventh she who bears only daughters; but immediately she who talks harshly.
iast: vandhyāṣṭame 'dhivedyā'abde daśame tu mṛtaprajā ekādaśe strījananī sadyastvapriyavādinī

sanskrit_iast: yā rogiṇī syāt tu hitā saṃpannā caiva śīlataḥ sā 'nujñāpyādhivettavyā nāvamānyā ca karhi cit
meaning: या रोगिणी स्यात् तु हिता संपन्ना चैव शीलतः सा 'नुज्ञाप्याधिवेत्तव्या नावमान्या च कर्हि चित्
text: But if a wife, who is an invalid, is well-disposed and endowed with modesty, she may be superseded after her consent has been obtained; and in no case is she to be disgraced.
iast: yā rogiṇī syāt tu hitā saṃpannā caiva śīlataḥ sā 'nujñāpyādhivettavyā nāvamānyā ca karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: adhivinnā tu yā nārī nirgacchedruṣitā gṛhāt sā sadyaḥ saṃniroddhavyā tyājyā vā kulasaṃnidhau
meaning: अधिविन्ना तु या नारी निर्गच्छेद्रुषिता गृहात् सा सद्यः संनिरोद्धव्या त्याज्या वा कुलसंनिधौ
text: On being superseded, if a wife, in anger, should go away from the house, she shall be either immediately confined, or cast off in the presence of the family.
iast: adhivinnā tu yā nārī nirgacchedruṣitā gṛhāt sā sadyaḥ saṃniroddhavyā tyājyā vā kulasaṃnidhau

sanskrit_iast: pratiṣiddhā 'pi cedyā tu madyamabhyudayeṣvapi pratiṣedhe pibedyā tu prekṣāsamājaṃ gacchedvā sā daṇḍyā kṛṣṇalāni ṣaṭ
meaning: प्रतिषिद्धा 'पि चेद्या तु मद्यमभ्युदयेष्वपि प्रतिषेधे पिबेद्या तु प्रेक्षासमाजं गच्छेद्वा सा दण्ड्या कृष्णलानि षट्
text: If the wife, though forbidden, drinks wine even at festivals or visits, shows and assemblies, she shall be fined six ‘Kṛṣṇalas’.
iast: pratiṣiddhā 'pi cedyā tu madyamabhyudayeṣvapi pratiṣedhe pibedyā tu prekṣāsamājaṃ gacchedvā sā daṇḍyā kṛṣṇalāni ṣaṭ

sanskrit_iast: yadi svāścāparāścaiva vinderan yoṣito dvijāḥ tāsāṃ varṇakrameṇa syāj jyeṣṭhyaṃ pūjā ca veśma ca
meaning: यदि स्वाश्चापराश्चैव विन्देरन् योषितो द्विजाः तासां वर्णक्रमेण स्याज् ज्येष्ठ्यं पूजा च वेश्म च
text: When twice-born men wed women of their own as well as other castes, their seniority, honour and habitation shall be according to the order of their castes.
iast: yadi svāścāparāścaiva vinderan yoṣito dvijāḥ tāsāṃ varṇakrameṇa syāj jyeṣṭhyaṃ pūjā ca veśma ca

sanskrit_iast: bhartuḥ śarīraśuśrūṣāṃ dharmakāryaṃ ca naityakam svā caiva kuryāt sarveṣāṃ nāsvajātiḥ kathaṃ cana svā svaiva
meaning: भर्तुः शरीरशुश्रूषां धर्मकार्यं च नैत्यकम् स्वा चैव कुर्यात् सर्वेषां नास्वजातिः कथं चन स्वा स्वैव
text: Of all wives, the wife of the man’s own caste, and never that of a different caste, shall attend to the husband’s personal service, as also to his daily sacred rites.
iast: bhartuḥ śarīraśuśrūṣāṃ dharmakāryaṃ ca naityakam svā caiva kuryāt sarveṣāṃ nāsvajātiḥ kathaṃ cana svā svaiva

sanskrit_iast: yastu tat kārayen mohāt sajātyā sthitayā 'nyayā yathā brāhmaṇacāṇḍālaḥ pūrvadṛṣṭastathaiva saḥ
meaning: यस्तु तत् कारयेन् मोहात् सजात्या स्थितया 'न्यया यथा ब्राह्मणचाण्डालः पूर्वदृष्टस्तथैव सः
text: While the wife of the same caste is alive, if through folly, one causes these duties to be performed by another wife, he is a ‘Brāhmaṇa-Cāṇḍāla’, as has been held by the ancients.
iast: yastu tat kārayen mohāt sajātyā sthitayā 'nyayā yathā brāhmaṇacāṇḍālaḥ pūrvadṛṣṭastathaiva saḥ

sanskrit_iast: utkṛṣṭāyābhirūpāya varāya sadṛśāya ca aprāptāmapi tāṃ tasmai kanyāṃ dadyādyathāvidhi
meaning: उत्कृष्टायाभिरूपाय वराय सदृशाय च अप्राप्तामपि तां तस्मै कन्यां दद्याद्यथाविधि
text: One shall give his daughter in the proper form, even though she may not have attained (the age), to a bridegroom who is of exceptionally distinguished appearance, and her equal.
iast: utkṛṣṭāyābhirūpāya varāya sadṛśāya ca aprāptāmapi tāṃ tasmai kanyāṃ dadyādyathāvidhi

sanskrit_iast: kāmamā maraṇāt tiṣṭhedgṛhe kanyāṛtumatyapi na caivaināṃ prayaccet tu guṇahīnāya karhi cit
meaning: काममा मरणात् तिष्ठेद्गृहे कन्याऋतुमत्यपि न चैवैनां प्रयच्चेत् तु गुणहीनाय कर्हि चित्
text: Well might the maiden, even though she may have reached puberty, remain in the house till her death; but the father shall never give her to a man destitute of good qualities.
iast: kāmamā maraṇāt tiṣṭhedgṛhe kanyāṛtumatyapi na caivaināṃ prayaccet tu guṇahīnāya karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: trīṇi varṣāṇyudīkṣeta kumāry ṛtumatī satī ūrdhvaṃ tu kālādetasmādvindeta sadṛśaṃ patim
meaning: त्रीणि वर्षाण्युदीक्षेत कुमार्य् ऋतुमती सती ऊर्ध्वं तु कालादेतस्माद्विन्देत सदृशं पतिम्
text: Having reached puberty, the maiden may wait for three years; after that time, she shall procure a suitable husband.
iast: trīṇi varṣāṇyudīkṣeta kumāry ṛtumatī satī ūrdhvaṃ tu kālādetasmādvindeta sadṛśaṃ patim

sanskrit_iast: adīyamānā bhartāramadhigacchedyadi svayam nainaḥ kiṃ cidavāpnoti na ca yaṃ sā 'dhigacchati
meaning: अदीयमाना भर्तारमधिगच्छेद्यदि स्वयम् नैनः किं चिदवाप्नोति न च यं सा 'धिगच्छति
text: When a maiden, when not given away, herself procures a husband, she incurs no sort of sin; nor does the man whom she weds.
iast: adīyamānā bhartāramadhigacchedyadi svayam nainaḥ kiṃ cidavāpnoti na ca yaṃ sā 'dhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: alaṅkāraṃ nādadīta pitryaṃ kanyā svayaṃvarā mātṛkaṃ bhrātṛdattaṃ vā stenā syādyadi taṃ haret
meaning: अलङ्कारं नाददीत पित्र्यं कन्या स्वयंवरा मातृकं भ्रातृदत्तं वा स्तेना स्याद्यदि तं हरेत्
text: When the girl chooses her own husband, she should not take away any ornaments given to her either by her father, or mother or brother; if she did take them, she would be a thief.
iast: alaṅkāraṃ nādadīta pitryaṃ kanyā svayaṃvarā mātṛkaṃ bhrātṛdattaṃ vā stenā syādyadi taṃ haret

sanskrit_iast: pitre na dadyātśulkaṃ tu kanyāṃ ṛtumatīṃ haran sa ca svāmyādatikrāmedṛtūnāṃ pratirodhanāt
meaning: पित्रे न दद्यात्शुल्कं तु कन्यां ऋतुमतीं हरन् स च स्वाम्यादतिक्रामेदृतूनां प्रतिरोधनात्
text: When a man takes away a maiden who has reached puberty, he shall pay no nuptial fee to the father,—who would fall off from his ownership by reason of thwarting her menses.
iast: pitre na dadyātśulkaṃ tu kanyāṃ ṛtumatīṃ haran sa ca svāmyādatikrāmedṛtūnāṃ pratirodhanāt

sanskrit_iast: triṃśadvarṣo vahet kanyāṃ hṛdyāṃ dvādaśavārṣikīm tryaṣṭavarṣo 'ṣṭavarṣāṃ vā dharme sīdati satvaraḥ
meaning: त्रिंशद्वर्षो वहेत् कन्यां हृद्यां द्वादशवार्षिकीम् त्र्यष्टवर्षो 'ष्टवर्षां वा धर्मे सीदति सत्वरः
text: A man thirty years old shall marry a charming maiden twelve years old; or one twenty four years old, a damsel eight years old; in the event of his duties suffering, he may do it sooner.
iast: triṃśadvarṣo vahet kanyāṃ hṛdyāṃ dvādaśavārṣikīm tryaṣṭavarṣo 'ṣṭavarṣāṃ vā dharme sīdati satvaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: devadattāṃ patirbhāryāṃ vindate naicchayā 'tmanaḥ tāṃ sādhvīṃ bibhṛyānnityaṃ devānāṃ priyamācaran
meaning: देवदत्तां पतिर्भार्यां विन्दते नैच्छया 'त्मनः तां साध्वीं बिभृयान्नित्यं देवानां प्रियमाचरन्
text: The husband obtains his wife as a present from the gods, and not by his own wish; hence he should always support the faithful wife, thereby doing what is agreeable to the gods.
iast: devadattāṃ patirbhāryāṃ vindate naicchayā 'tmanaḥ tāṃ sādhvīṃ bibhṛyānnityaṃ devānāṃ priyamācaran

sanskrit_iast: prajanārthaṃ striyaḥ sṛṣṭāḥ saṃtānārthaṃ ca mānavaḥ tasmāt sādhāraṇo dharmaḥ śrutau patnyā sahauditaḥ
meaning: प्रजनार्थं स्त्रियः सृष्टाः संतानार्थं च मानवः तस्मात् साधारणो धर्मः श्रुतौ पत्न्या सहौदितः
text: Women were created for the purpose of child-bearing, and men for the furfose of procreation. hence it is that Religious Rites have been ordained in the Veda as common between the man and his wife.
iast: prajanārthaṃ striyaḥ sṛṣṭāḥ saṃtānārthaṃ ca mānavaḥ tasmāt sādhāraṇo dharmaḥ śrutau patnyā sahauditaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kanyāyāṃ dattaśulkāyāṃ mriyeta yadi śulkadaḥ devarāya pradātavyā yadi kanyā 'numanyate
meaning: कन्यायां दत्तशुल्कायां म्रियेत यदि शुल्कदः देवराय प्रदातव्या यदि कन्या 'नुमन्यते
text: After the nuptial fee for a girl has been paid, if the man who paid the fee dies, the girl should be given to the younger brother-in-law, in case she consents.
iast: kanyāyāṃ dattaśulkāyāṃ mriyeta yadi śulkadaḥ devarāya pradātavyā yadi kanyā 'numanyate

sanskrit_iast: ādadīta na śūdro 'pi śulkaṃ duhitaraṃ dadan śulkaṃ hi gṛhṇan kurute channaṃ duhitṛvikrayam
meaning: आददीत न शूद्रो 'पि शुल्कं दुहितरं ददन् शुल्कं हि गृह्णन् कुरुते छन्नं दुहितृविक्रयम्
text: Even a Śūdra should not take a nuptial fee, when he is giving away his daughter; by accepting a fee, what he does is disguised bartering.
iast: ādadīta na śūdro 'pi śulkaṃ duhitaraṃ dadan śulkaṃ hi gṛhṇan kurute channaṃ duhitṛvikrayam

sanskrit_iast: etat tu na pare cakrurnāpare jātu sādhavaḥ yadanyasya pratijñāya punaranyasya dīyate
meaning: एतत् तु न परे चक्रुर्नापरे जातु साधवः यदन्यस्य प्रतिज्ञाय पुनरन्यस्य दीयते
text: Good men, both ancient and modern, have never committed the act, that having promised to one they gave her to another.
iast: etat tu na pare cakrurnāpare jātu sādhavaḥ yadanyasya pratijñāya punaranyasya dīyate

sanskrit_iast: nānuśuśruma jātvetat pūrveṣvapi hi janmasu śulkasaṃjñena mūlyena channaṃ duhitṛvikrayam
meaning: नानुशुश्रुम जात्वेतत् पूर्वेष्वपि हि जन्मसु शुल्कसंज्ञेन मूल्येन छन्नं दुहितृविक्रयम्
text: Nor indeed have we heard, even in former Cycles, of the covert sale of a daughter, for a price styled “nuptial fee.”
iast: nānuśuśruma jātvetat pūrveṣvapi hi janmasu śulkasaṃjñena mūlyena channaṃ duhitṛvikrayam

sanskrit_iast: anyonyasyāvyabhicāro bhavedāmaraṇāntikaḥ eṣa dharmaḥ samāsena jñeyaḥ strīpuṃsayoḥ paraḥ
meaning: अन्योन्यस्याव्यभिचारो भवेदामरणान्तिकः एष धर्मः समासेन ज्ञेयः स्त्रीपुंसयोः परः
text: ‘May mutual fidelity continue till death’,—this, in brief should be understood as the highest duty between husband and wife.
iast: anyonyasyāvyabhicāro bhavedāmaraṇāntikaḥ eṣa dharmaḥ samāsena jñeyaḥ strīpuṃsayoḥ paraḥ

sanskrit_iast: tathā nityaṃ yateyātāṃ strīpuṃsau tu kṛtakriyau yathā nābhicaretāṃ tau viyuktāvitaretaram nāticaretāṃ
meaning: तथा नित्यं यतेयातां स्त्रीपुंसौ तु कृतक्रियौ यथा नाभिचरेतां तौ वियुक्तावितरेतरम् नातिचरेतां
text: Man and wife, after they have passed through the rites, should always so exert themselves that they may not become separated and be unfaithful to each other.
iast: tathā nityaṃ yateyātāṃ strīpuṃsau tu kṛtakriyau yathā nābhicaretāṃ tau viyuktāvitaretaram nāticaretāṃ

sanskrit_iast: eṣa strīpuṃsayorukto dharmo vo ratisaṃhitaḥ āpadyapatyaprāptiśca dāyadharmaṃ nibodhata
meaning: एष स्त्रीपुंसयोरुक्तो धर्मो वो रतिसंहितः आपद्यपत्यप्राप्तिश्च दायधर्मं निबोधत
text: Thus has been expounded to you the law relating to husband and wife, which is conducive to conjugal happiness,—as also the manner of obtaining children in times of distress; learn now the partition of inheritance.
iast: eṣa strīpuṃsayorukto dharmo vo ratisaṃhitaḥ āpadyapatyaprāptiśca dāyadharmaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: ūrdhvaṃ pituśca mātuśca sametya bhrātaraḥ samam bhajeran paitṛkaṃ rikthamanīśāste hi jīvatoḥ
meaning: ऊर्ध्वं पितुश्च मातुश्च समेत्य भ्रातरः समम् भजेरन् पैतृकं रिक्थमनीशास्ते हि जीवतोः
text: After the death of the father and of the mother, the brothers, being assembled, shall divide equally the paternal property; while the parents are alive, they have no power.
iast: ūrdhvaṃ pituśca mātuśca sametya bhrātaraḥ samam bhajeran paitṛkaṃ rikthamanīśāste hi jīvatoḥ

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭha eva tu gṛhṇīyāt pitryaṃ dhanamaśeṣataḥ śeṣāstamupajīveyuryathaiva pitaraṃ tathā
meaning: ज्येष्ठ एव तु गृह्णीयात् पित्र्यं धनमशेषतः शेषास्तमुपजीवेयुर्यथैव पितरं तथा
text: The eldest brother alone may take the entire paternal property; the rest shall live under him, just as under their father.
iast: jyeṣṭha eva tu gṛhṇīyāt pitryaṃ dhanamaśeṣataḥ śeṣāstamupajīveyuryathaiva pitaraṃ tathā

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭhena jātamātreṇa putrī bhavati mānavaḥ pitr̥̄ṇāmanṛṇaścaiva sa tasmāt sarvamarhati
meaning: ज्येष्ठेन जातमात्रेण पुत्री भवति मानवः पित्र्̥̄णामनृणश्चैव स तस्मात् सर्वमर्हति
text: By the mere birth of the eldest son, a man becomes ‘with son,’ and (hence) free from the debt toPitṛs; it is for this reason that he deserves the whole.
iast: jyeṣṭhena jātamātreṇa putrī bhavati mānavaḥ pitr̥̄ṇāmanṛṇaścaiva sa tasmāt sarvamarhati

sanskrit_iast: yasminn ṛṇaṃ saṃnayati yena cānantyamaśnute sa eva dharmajaḥ putraḥ kāmajānitarān viduḥ
meaning: यस्मिन्न् ऋणं संनयति येन चानन्त्यमश्नुते स एव धर्मजः पुत्रः कामजानितरान् विदुः
text: That son alone to whom the man transfers his debt, and through whom he attains immortality, is the ‘duty-born son;’ others are known as ‘lust-born.’
iast: yasminn ṛṇaṃ saṃnayati yena cānantyamaśnute sa eva dharmajaḥ putraḥ kāmajānitarān viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: pitaiva pālayet pūtrān jyeṣṭho bhrātr̥̄ṇ yavīyasaḥ putravatcāpi varteran jyeṣṭhe bhrātari dharmataḥ
meaning: पितैव पालयेत् पूत्रान् ज्येष्ठो भ्रात्र्̥̄ण् यवीयसः पुत्रवत्चापि वर्तेरन् ज्येष्ठे भ्रातरि धर्मतः
text: The eldest brother shall support his younger brothers, just as the father supports his sons; and the younger brothers, in duty bound, shall behave towards the eldest brother, like sons.
iast: pitaiva pālayet pūtrān jyeṣṭho bhrātr̥̄ṇ yavīyasaḥ putravatcāpi varteran jyeṣṭhe bhrātari dharmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭhaḥ kulaṃ vardhayati vināśayati vā punaḥ jyeṣṭhaḥ pūjyatamo loke jyeṣṭhaḥ sadbhiragarhitaḥ
meaning: ज्येष्ठः कुलं वर्धयति विनाशयति वा पुनः ज्येष्ठः पूज्यतमो लोके ज्येष्ठः सद्भिरगर्हितः
text: It is the eldest brother who advances the family, or ruins it; the eldest brother is worthy of the highest honour; the eldest brother is never ill-treated by good men.
iast: jyeṣṭhaḥ kulaṃ vardhayati vināśayati vā punaḥ jyeṣṭhaḥ pūjyatamo loke jyeṣṭhaḥ sadbhiragarhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo jyeṣṭho jyeṣṭhavṛttiḥ syān mātaiva sa pitaiva saḥ ajyeṣṭhavṛttiryastu syāt sa saṃpūjyastu bandhuvat
meaning: यो ज्येष्ठो ज्येष्ठवृत्तिः स्यान् मातैव स पितैव सः अज्येष्ठवृत्तिर्यस्तु स्यात् स संपूज्यस्तु बन्धुवत्
text: If the eldest brother behaves as the eldest brother, he is like a mother, and like a father. If however he does not behave like the eldest brother, he shall be honoured simply as a kinsman.
iast: yo jyeṣṭho jyeṣṭhavṛttiḥ syān mātaiva sa pitaiva saḥ ajyeṣṭhavṛttiryastu syāt sa saṃpūjyastu bandhuvat

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ saha vaseyurvā pṛthag vā dharmakāmyayā pṛthag vivardhate dharmastasmāddharmyā pṛthakkriyā
meaning: एवं सह वसेयुर्वा पृथग् वा धर्मकाम्यया पृथग् विवर्धते धर्मस्तस्माद्धर्म्या पृथक्क्रिया
text: Thus may they live either together, or separately, with a view to spiritual merit; by separate living merit prospers; hence separation is meritorious.
iast: evaṃ saha vaseyurvā pṛthag vā dharmakāmyayā pṛthag vivardhate dharmastasmāddharmyā pṛthakkriyā

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭhasya viṃśa uddhāraḥ sarvadravyācca yadvaram tato 'rdhaṃ madhyamasya syāt turīyaṃ tu yavīyasaḥ
meaning: ज्येष्ठस्य विंश उद्धारः सर्वद्रव्याच्च यद्वरम् ततो 'र्धं मध्यमस्य स्यात् तुरीयं तु यवीयसः
text: For the eldest, the additional portion shall consist of the twentieth part of the property, as also the best of all the chattels; half of that for the middlemost, and the fourth of that for the youngest.
iast: jyeṣṭhasya viṃśa uddhāraḥ sarvadravyācca yadvaram tato 'rdhaṃ madhyamasya syāt turīyaṃ tu yavīyasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭhaścaiva kaniṣṭhaśca saṃharetāṃ yathauditam ye 'nye jyeṣṭhakaniṣṭhābhyāṃ teṣāṃ syān madhyamaṃ dhanam
meaning: ज्येष्ठश्चैव कनिष्ठश्च संहरेतां यथौदितम् ये 'न्ये ज्येष्ठकनिष्ठाभ्यां तेषां स्यान् मध्यमं धनम्
text: The eldest and the youngest shall receive their property according to the rule just stated; to those other than the eldest and the youngest, would belong the middlemost share.
iast: jyeṣṭhaścaiva kaniṣṭhaśca saṃharetāṃ yathauditam ye 'nye jyeṣṭhakaniṣṭhābhyāṃ teṣāṃ syān madhyamaṃ dhanam

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāṃ dhanajātānāmādadītāgryamagrajaḥ yacca sātiśayaṃ kiṃ ciddaśataścāpnuyādvaram
meaning: सर्वेषां धनजातानामाददीताग्र्यमग्रजः यच्च सातिशयं किं चिद्दशतश्चाप्नुयाद्वरम्
text: Among the goods of every kind, the first-born shall take the best; as also anything that may be particularly good; as well as the best of ten animals.
iast: sarveṣāṃ dhanajātānāmādadītāgryamagrajaḥ yacca sātiśayaṃ kiṃ ciddaśataścāpnuyādvaram

sanskrit_iast: uddhāro na daśasvasti saṃpannānāṃ svakarmasu yat kiṃ cideva deyaṃ tu jyāyase mānavardhanam
meaning: उद्धारो न दशस्वस्ति संपन्नानां स्वकर्मसु यत् किं चिदेव देयं तु ज्यायसे मानवर्धनम्
text: But there is to be no additional share ‘out of ten,’ if all the brothers are efficient in their occupations; some little thing however shall be given to the eldest, as a mark of respect.
iast: uddhāro na daśasvasti saṃpannānāṃ svakarmasu yat kiṃ cideva deyaṃ tu jyāyase mānavardhanam

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ samuddhṛtauddhāre samānaṃśān prakalpayet uddhāre 'nuddhṛte tveṣāmiyaṃ syādaṃśakalpanā
meaning: एवं समुद्धृतौद्धारे समानंशान् प्रकल्पयेत् उद्धारे 'नुद्धृते त्वेषामियं स्यादंशकल्पना
text: After the ‘additional share’ has been thus deducted, equal shares shall be allotted. But if no additional share has been deducted, the allotment of shares shall be in this (following) manner,
iast: evaṃ samuddhṛtauddhāre samānaṃśān prakalpayet uddhāre 'nuddhṛte tveṣāmiyaṃ syādaṃśakalpanā

sanskrit_iast: ekādhikaṃ harej jyeṣṭhaḥ putro 'dhyardhaṃ tato 'nujaḥ aṃśamaṃśaṃ yavīyāṃsa iti dharmo vyavasthitaḥ
meaning: एकाधिकं हरेज् ज्येष्ठः पुत्रो 'ध्यर्धं ततो 'नुजः अंशमंशं यवीयांस इति धर्मो व्यवस्थितः
text: The eldest son shall take one share in excess; the one born next to him a share and a half; and the younger ones one share each; such is the settled law.
iast: ekādhikaṃ harej jyeṣṭhaḥ putro 'dhyardhaṃ tato 'nujaḥ aṃśamaṃśaṃ yavīyāṃsa iti dharmo vyavasthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: svebhyo 'ṃśebhyastu kanyābhyaḥ pradadyurbhrātaraḥ pṛthak svābhyaḥ svābhyastu svāt svādaṃśāccaturbhāgaṃ patitāḥ syuraditsavaḥ
meaning: स्वेभ्यो 'ंशेभ्यस्तु कन्याभ्यः प्रदद्युर्भ्रातरः पृथक् स्वाभ्यः स्वाभ्यस्तु स्वात् स्वादंशाच्चतुर्भागं पतिताः स्युरदित्सवः
text: To the maidens of the same caste, the brothers shall each severally give the fourth part of his share; those not inclined to give would be outcasts
iast: svebhyo 'ṃśebhyastu kanyābhyaḥ pradadyurbhrātaraḥ pṛthak svābhyaḥ svābhyastu svāt svādaṃśāccaturbhāgaṃ patitāḥ syuraditsavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ajāvikaṃ sekaśaphaṃ na jātu viṣamaṃ bhajet 'jāvikaṃ caikaśaphaṃ ajāvikaṃ tu viṣamaṃ jyeṣṭhasyaiva vidhīyate
meaning: अजाविकं सेकशफं न जातु विषमं भजेत् 'जाविकं चैकशफं अजाविकं तु विषमं ज्येष्ठस्यैव विधीयते
text: One shall not divide an odd goat, or sheep, or an animal with uncleft hoofs; the odd goat or sheep is declared to belong to the eldest.
iast: ajāvikaṃ sekaśaphaṃ na jātu viṣamaṃ bhajet 'jāvikaṃ caikaśaphaṃ ajāvikaṃ tu viṣamaṃ jyeṣṭhasyaiva vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: yavīyānjyeṣṭhabhāryāyāṃ putramutpādayedyadi samastatra vibhāgaḥ syāditi dharmo vyavasthitaḥ
meaning: यवीयान्ज्येष्ठभार्यायां पुत्रमुत्पादयेद्यदि समस्तत्र विभागः स्यादिति धर्मो व्यवस्थितः
text: If the younger brother begets a son on the wife of the elder, the division in that case shall be equal; such is the settled law.
iast: yavīyānjyeṣṭhabhāryāyāṃ putramutpādayedyadi samastatra vibhāgaḥ syāditi dharmo vyavasthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: upasarjanaṃ pradhānasya dharmato naupapadyate pitā pradhānaṃ prajane tasmāddharmeṇa taṃ bhajet
meaning: उपसर्जनं प्रधानस्य धर्मतो नौपपद्यते पिता प्रधानं प्रजने तस्माद्धर्मेण तं भजेत्
text: The Secondary cannot rightly be (equal to) the primary; because in procreation, the father is the primary, therefore he (the secondary) should be treated accordinc, to the law (stated before).
iast: upasarjanaṃ pradhānasya dharmato naupapadyate pitā pradhānaṃ prajane tasmāddharmeṇa taṃ bhajet

sanskrit_iast: putraḥ kaniṣṭho jyeṣṭhāyāṃ kaniṣṭhāyāṃ ca pūrvajaḥ kathaṃ tatra vibhāgaḥ syāditi cet saṃśayo bhavet
meaning: पुत्रः कनिष्ठो ज्येष्ठायां कनिष्ठायां च पूर्वजः कथं तत्र विभागः स्यादिति चेत् संशयो भवेत्
text: ‘In case the younger son is born of the elder wife, and the elder one of the younger wife,—how would the partition be made?’—If such a doubt arises,—the son born of the elder wife shall take one bull as his ‘preferential share;’ the other bulls, which are not so good, shall belong to those who are junior to him, on account of the position of their mothers.
iast: putraḥ kaniṣṭho jyeṣṭhāyāṃ kaniṣṭhāyāṃ ca pūrvajaḥ kathaṃ tatra vibhāgaḥ syāditi cet saṃśayo bhavet

sanskrit_iast: ekaṃ vṛṣabhamuddhāraṃ saṃhareta sa pūrvajaḥ tato 'pare jyeṣṭhavṛṣāstadūnānāṃ svamātṛtaḥ
meaning: एकं वृषभमुद्धारं संहरेत स पूर्वजः ततो 'परे ज्येष्ठवृषास्तदूनानां स्वमातृतः
text: ‘In case the younger son is born of the elder wife, and the elder one of the younger wife,—how would the partition be made?’—If such a doubt arises,—the son born of the elder wife shall take one bull as his ‘preferential share;’ the other bulls, which are not so good, shall belong to those who are junior to him, on account of the position of their mothers.
iast: ekaṃ vṛṣabhamuddhāraṃ saṃhareta sa pūrvajaḥ tato 'pare jyeṣṭhavṛṣāstadūnānāṃ svamātṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭhastu jāto jyeṣṭhāyāṃ haredvṛṣabhaṣoḍaśāḥ tataḥ svamātṛtaḥ śeṣā bhajeranniti dhāraṇā
meaning: ज्येष्ठस्तु जातो ज्येष्ठायां हरेद्वृषभषोडशाः ततः स्वमातृतः शेषा भजेरन्निति धारणा
text: The elder son born of the younger wife may take (fifteen cows with) a bull as the sixteenth; the others may take shares according to the seniority of their mothers; such is the settled rule.
iast: jyeṣṭhastu jāto jyeṣṭhāyāṃ haredvṛṣabhaṣoḍaśāḥ tataḥ svamātṛtaḥ śeṣā bhajeranniti dhāraṇā

sanskrit_iast: sadṛśastrīṣu jātānāṃ putrāṇāmaviśeṣataḥ na mātṛto jyaiṣṭhyamasti janmato jyaiṣṭhyamucyate
meaning: सदृशस्त्रीषु जातानां पुत्राणामविशेषतः न मातृतो ज्यैष्ठ्यमस्ति जन्मतो ज्यैष्ठ्यमुच्यते
text: Among sons born of equal wives,—if there is no other distinction,—there is no seniority on account of their mothers; seniority is declared to be by birth only.
iast: sadṛśastrīṣu jātānāṃ putrāṇāmaviśeṣataḥ na mātṛto jyaiṣṭhyamasti janmato jyaiṣṭhyamucyate

sanskrit_iast: janmajyeṣṭhena cāhvānaṃ subrahmaṇyāsvapi smṛtam yamayoścaiva garbheṣu janmato jyeṣṭhatā smṛtā
meaning: जन्मज्येष्ठेन चाह्वानं सुब्रह्मण्यास्वपि स्मृतम् यमयोश्चैव गर्भेषु जन्मतो ज्येष्ठता स्मृता
text: In theSubrahmaṇyātext also, the invocation has been declared as to be done by the son who is eldest by birth. Between sons conceived as twins, seniority has been declared to be dependent upon birth.
iast: janmajyeṣṭhena cāhvānaṃ subrahmaṇyāsvapi smṛtam yamayoścaiva garbheṣu janmato jyeṣṭhatā smṛtā

sanskrit_iast: aputro 'nena vidhinā sutāṃ kurvīta putrikām yadapatyaṃ bhavedasyāṃ tan mama syāt svadhākaram
meaning: अपुत्रो 'नेन विधिना सुतां कुर्वीत पुत्रिकाम् यदपत्यं भवेदस्यां तन् मम स्यात् स्वधाकरम्
text: He who has no son may make his daughter an ‘appointed daughter’ in the following manner: [He shall mark the declaration]—‘The child that may bk born of her shall be the performer of my funeral rites’.
iast: aputro 'nena vidhinā sutāṃ kurvīta putrikām yadapatyaṃ bhavedasyāṃ tan mama syāt svadhākaram

sanskrit_iast: anena tu vidhānena purā cakre 'tha putrikāḥ vivṛddhyarthaṃ svavaṃśasya svayaṃ dakṣaḥ prajāpatiḥ
meaning: अनेन तु विधानेन पुरा चक्रे 'थ पुत्रिकाः विवृद्ध्यर्थं स्ववंशस्य स्वयं दक्षः प्रजापतिः
text: In ancient timesDakṣa Prajāpatihimself made ‘appointed daughters’ in this same manner, for the purpose of multiplying his race.
iast: anena tu vidhānena purā cakre 'tha putrikāḥ vivṛddhyarthaṃ svavaṃśasya svayaṃ dakṣaḥ prajāpatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: dadau sa daśa dharmāya kaśyapāya trayodaśa somāya rājñe satkṛtya prītātmā saptaviṃśatim
meaning: ददौ स दश धर्माय कश्यपाय त्रयोदश सोमाय राज्ञे सत्कृत्य प्रीतात्मा सप्तविंशतिम्
text: He gave ten to Dharma, thirteen to Kaśyapa, and twenty-seven to King Soma,—having honoured them with an affectionate heart.
iast: dadau sa daśa dharmāya kaśyapāya trayodaśa somāya rājñe satkṛtya prītātmā saptaviṃśatim

sanskrit_iast: yathaivātmā tathā putraḥ putreṇa duhitā samā tasyāmātmani tiṣṭhantyāṃ kathamanyo dhanaṃ haret
meaning: यथैवात्मा तथा पुत्रः पुत्रेण दुहिता समा तस्यामात्मनि तिष्ठन्त्यां कथमन्यो धनं हरेत्
text: The son is as one’s own self, and the daughter is equal to the son; hence so long as she is there in her own real character, how can anyone else take his property?
iast: yathaivātmā tathā putraḥ putreṇa duhitā samā tasyāmātmani tiṣṭhantyāṃ kathamanyo dhanaṃ haret

sanskrit_iast: mātustu yautakaṃ yat syāt kumārībhāga eva saḥ dauhitra eva ca haredaputrasyākhilaṃ dhanam
meaning: मातुस्तु यौतकं यत् स्यात् कुमारीभाग एव सः दौहित्र एव च हरेदपुत्रस्याखिलं धनम्
text: Whatever may be the separate property of the mother is the share of the unmarried daughter alone; and the daughter’s son shall inherit the entire property of the man who has no son.
iast: mātustu yautakaṃ yat syāt kumārībhāga eva saḥ dauhitra eva ca haredaputrasyākhilaṃ dhanam

sanskrit_iast: dauhitro hyakhilaṃ rikthamaputrasya piturharet sa eva dadyāddvau piṇḍau pitre mātāmahāya ca
meaning: दौहित्रो ह्यखिलं रिक्थमपुत्रस्य पितुर्हरेत् स एव दद्याद्द्वौ पिण्डौ पित्रे मातामहाय च
text: The daughter’s son should inherit the entire property of the sonless father; he shall also offer two cakes—to the ‘father’ and to the ‘maternal grandfather.’
iast: dauhitro hyakhilaṃ rikthamaputrasya piturharet sa eva dadyāddvau piṇḍau pitre mātāmahāya ca

sanskrit_iast: pautradauhitrayorloke na viśeṣo 'sti dharmataḥ tayorhi mātāpitarau saṃbhūtau tasya dehataḥ
meaning: पौत्रदौहित्रयोर्लोके न विशेषो 'स्ति धर्मतः तयोर्हि मातापितरौ संभूतौ तस्य देहतः
text: In this world, between the son’s son and the daughter’s son there is no difference, in law; for the father and mother of each of them were both born of h is own body.
iast: pautradauhitrayorloke na viśeṣo 'sti dharmataḥ tayorhi mātāpitarau saṃbhūtau tasya dehataḥ

sanskrit_iast: putrikāyāṃ kṛtāyāṃ tu yadi putro 'nujāyate samastatra vibhāgaḥ syātjyeṣṭhatā nāsti hi striyāḥ
meaning: पुत्रिकायां कृतायां तु यदि पुत्रो 'नुजायते समस्तत्र विभागः स्यात्ज्येष्ठता नास्ति हि स्त्रियाः
text: But if a son happen to be born after the daughter has been ‘appointed,’ the division must be equal; as there is no seniority for the woman.
iast: putrikāyāṃ kṛtāyāṃ tu yadi putro 'nujāyate samastatra vibhāgaḥ syātjyeṣṭhatā nāsti hi striyāḥ

sanskrit_iast: aputrāyāṃ mṛtāyāṃ tu putrikāyāṃ kathaṃ cana dhanaṃ tat putrikābhartā haretaivāvicārayan
meaning: अपुत्रायां मृतायां तु पुत्रिकायां कथं चन धनं तत् पुत्रिकाभर्ता हरेतैवाविचारयन्
text: If the appointed daughter happen to die without a son, the husband of that appointed daughter may, without hesitation, take that property.
iast: aputrāyāṃ mṛtāyāṃ tu putrikāyāṃ kathaṃ cana dhanaṃ tat putrikābhartā haretaivāvicārayan

sanskrit_iast: akṛtā vā kṛtā vā 'pi yaṃ vindet sadṛśāt sutam pautrī mātāmahastena dadyāt piṇḍaṃ hareddhanam
meaning: अकृता वा कृता वा 'पि यं विन्देत् सदृशात् सुतम् पौत्री मातामहस्तेन दद्यात् पिण्डं हरेद्धनम्
text: Either appointed on not appointed, if a daughter bears a son to a husband of equal status, through that son does the maternal grandfather become endowed with a ‘son’s son’; he shall offer the funeral cake and inherit his property.
iast: akṛtā vā kṛtā vā 'pi yaṃ vindet sadṛśāt sutam pautrī mātāmahastena dadyāt piṇḍaṃ hareddhanam

sanskrit_iast: putreṇa lokānjayati pautreṇānantyamaśnute atha putrasya pautreṇa bradhnasyāpnoti viṣṭapam
meaning: पुत्रेण लोकान्जयति पौत्रेणानन्त्यमश्नुते अथ पुत्रस्य पौत्रेण ब्रध्नस्याप्नोति विष्टपम्
text: Through the son one conquers the worlds, through the grandson he obtains immortality, and through the son’s grandson he attains the regions of the Sun.
iast: putreṇa lokānjayati pautreṇānantyamaśnute atha putrasya pautreṇa bradhnasyāpnoti viṣṭapam

sanskrit_iast: putnāmno narakādyasmāt trāyate pitaraṃ sutaḥ tasmāt putra iti proktaḥ svayameva svayaṃbhuvā
meaning: पुत्नाम्नो नरकाद्यस्मात् त्रायते पितरं सुतः तस्मात् पुत्र इति प्रोक्तः स्वयमेव स्वयंभुवा
text: Because the Son delivers his father from the hell called ‘Put,’ therefore has he been called ‘Putra,’ ‘Deliverer from Put,’ by the Self-existent One Himself.
iast: putnāmno narakādyasmāt trāyate pitaraṃ sutaḥ tasmāt putra iti proktaḥ svayameva svayaṃbhuvā

sanskrit_iast: pautradauhitrayorloke viśeṣo naupapadyate dauhitro 'pi hyamutrainaṃ saṃtārayati pautravat
meaning: पौत्रदौहित्रयोर्लोके विशेषो नौपपद्यते दौहित्रो 'पि ह्यमुत्रैनं संतारयति पौत्रवत्
text: Between the Son’s son and the Daughter’s son there is no difference in the world; since the daughter’s son also, like the son’s son, saves the man in the next world.
iast: pautradauhitrayorloke viśeṣo naupapadyate dauhitro 'pi hyamutrainaṃ saṃtārayati pautravat

sanskrit_iast: mātuḥ prathamataḥ piṇḍaṃ nirvapet putrikāsutaḥ dvitīyaṃ tu pitustasyāstṛtīyaṃ tatpituḥ pituḥ
meaning: मातुः प्रथमतः पिण्डं निर्वपेत् पुत्रिकासुतः द्वितीयं तु पितुस्तस्यास्तृतीयं तत्पितुः पितुः
text: The son of the Appointed Daughter shall offer the first cake to his mother, the second to her father and the third to his father’s father.
iast: mātuḥ prathamataḥ piṇḍaṃ nirvapet putrikāsutaḥ dvitīyaṃ tu pitustasyāstṛtīyaṃ tatpituḥ pituḥ

sanskrit_iast: upapanno guṇaiḥ sarvaiḥ putro yasya tu dattrimaḥ sa haretaiva tadrikthaṃ saṃprāpto 'pyanyagotrataḥ
meaning: उपपन्नो गुणैः सर्वैः पुत्रो यस्य तु दत्त्रिमः स हरेतैव तद्रिक्थं संप्राप्तो 'प्यन्यगोत्रतः
text: If one has an adopted son endowed with all good qualities, he shall inherit his property, even though he may have come from another family.
iast: upapanno guṇaiḥ sarvaiḥ putro yasya tu dattrimaḥ sa haretaiva tadrikthaṃ saṃprāpto 'pyanyagotrataḥ

sanskrit_iast: gotrarikthe janayiturna hareddattrimaḥ kva cit gotrarikthānugaḥ piṇḍo vyapaiti dadataḥ svadhā
meaning: गोत्ररिक्थे जनयितुर्न हरेद्दत्त्रिमः क्व चित् गोत्ररिक्थानुगः पिण्डो व्यपैति ददतः स्वधा
text: The adopted son shall not take the family-name or the property of his progenitor; the cake follows the family-name and the property; for him therefore who gives away his son the funeral offerings cease.
iast: gotrarikthe janayiturna hareddattrimaḥ kva cit gotrarikthānugaḥ piṇḍo vyapaiti dadataḥ svadhā

sanskrit_iast: aniyuktāsutaścaiva putriṇyā 'ptaśca devarāt ubhau tau nārhato bhāgaṃ jārajātakakāmajau
meaning: अनियुक्तासुतश्चैव पुत्रिण्या 'प्तश्च देवरात् उभौ तौ नार्हतो भागं जारजातककामजौ
text: The offspring of a wife not ‘authorised,’ and the offspring obtained from her younger brother-in-law by a woman who has already got a son,—both of these are undeserving of a share; one being born of an adulterer, and the other being the product of lust.
iast: aniyuktāsutaścaiva putriṇyā 'ptaśca devarāt ubhau tau nārhato bhāgaṃ jārajātakakāmajau

sanskrit_iast: niyuktāyāmapi pumānnāryāṃ jāto 'vidhānataḥ naivārhaḥ paitṛkaṃ rikthaṃ patitautpādito hi saḥ
meaning: नियुक्तायामपि पुमान्नार्यां जातो 'विधानतः नैवार्हः पैतृकं रिक्थं पतितौत्पादितो हि सः
text: The made child of an ‘authorised’ woman, if not begotten in the prescribed manner, is not entitled to the paternal property; as he is procreated by outcasts.
iast: niyuktāyāmapi pumānnāryāṃ jāto 'vidhānataḥ naivārhaḥ paitṛkaṃ rikthaṃ patitautpādito hi saḥ

sanskrit_iast: haret tatra niyuktāyāṃ jātaḥ putro yathā 'urasaḥ kṣetrikasya tu tadbījaṃ dharmataḥ prasavaśca saḥ
meaning: हरेत् तत्र नियुक्तायां जातः पुत्रो यथा 'उरसः क्षेत्रिकस्य तु तद्बीजं धर्मतः प्रसवश्च सः
text: The son born of the ‘authorised’ woman shall inherit, like the ‘legitimate’ son; as legally that seed is of the owner of the soil and the offspring belongs to him.
iast: haret tatra niyuktāyāṃ jātaḥ putro yathā 'urasaḥ kṣetrikasya tu tadbījaṃ dharmataḥ prasavaśca saḥ

sanskrit_iast: dhanaṃ yo bibhṛyādbhrāturmṛtasya striyameva ca so 'patyaṃ bhrāturutpādya dadyāt tasyaiva taddhanam
meaning: धनं यो बिभृयाद्भ्रातुर्मृतस्य स्त्रियमेव च सो 'पत्यं भ्रातुरुत्पाद्य दद्यात् तस्यैव तद्धनम्
text: This rule refers to the case where the dead brother was one who had separated from the surviving brother; while the preceding verse was meant for that where the two brothers lived together. This is the only difference between this and the foregoing rules.
iast: dhanaṃ yo bibhṛyādbhrāturmṛtasya striyameva ca so 'patyaṃ bhrāturutpādya dadyāt tasyaiva taddhanam

sanskrit_iast: yā niyuktā 'nyataḥ putraṃ devarādvā 'pyavāpnuyāt taṃ kāmajamarikthīyaṃ vṛthautpannaṃ pracakṣate mithyautpannaṃ
meaning: या नियुक्ता 'न्यतः पुत्रं देवराद्वा 'प्यवाप्नुयात् तं कामजमरिक्थीयं वृथौत्पन्नं प्रचक्षते मिथ्यौत्पन्नं
text: If a woman, without being ‘authorised,’ bears a son either to her broth er-in-law or to some other person, that son they declare to be‘lust-born,’ ‘incapable of inheritance’ and‘born in vain.’
iast: yā niyuktā 'nyataḥ putraṃ devarādvā 'pyavāpnuyāt taṃ kāmajamarikthīyaṃ vṛthautpannaṃ pracakṣate mithyautpannaṃ

sanskrit_iast: etadvidhānaṃ vijñeyaṃ vibhāgasyaikayoniṣu bahvīṣu caikajātānāṃ nānāstrīṣu nibodhata
meaning: एतद्विधानं विज्ञेयं विभागस्यैकयोनिषु बह्वीषु चैकजातानां नानास्त्रीषु निबोधत
text: This rule should be understood as applying to partition among sons born of wives of the same caste; listen to that applying to that among sons born to one man of several and diverse wives.
iast: etadvidhānaṃ vijñeyaṃ vibhāgasyaikayoniṣu bahvīṣu caikajātānāṃ nānāstrīṣu nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇasyānupūrvyeṇa catasrastu yadi striyaḥ tāsāṃ putreṣu jāteṣu vibhāge 'yaṃ vidhiḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्यानुपूर्व्येण चतस्रस्तु यदि स्त्रियः तासां पुत्रेषु जातेषु विभागे 'यं विधिः स्मृतः
text: If to aBrāhmaṇathere be four wives in due order,—for partition among the sons born of these, the rule has been declared to be as follows.
iast: brāhmaṇasyānupūrvyeṇa catasrastu yadi striyaḥ tāsāṃ putreṣu jāteṣu vibhāge 'yaṃ vidhiḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kīnāśo govṛṣo yānamalaṅkāraśca veśma ca viprasyauddhārikaṃ deyamekāṃśaśca pradhānataḥ
meaning: कीनाशो गोवृषो यानमलङ्कारश्च वेश्म च विप्रस्यौद्धारिकं देयमेकांशश्च प्रधानतः
text: The ploughman, the breeding bull, the conveyance, the ornament, and the house shall be given as the ‘preferential share’ to theBrāhmaṇa, as also one principal share.
iast: kīnāśo govṛṣo yānamalaṅkāraśca veśma ca viprasyauddhārikaṃ deyamekāṃśaśca pradhānataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tryaṃśaṃ dāyāddharedvipro dvāvaṃśau kṣatriyāsutaḥ vaiśyājaḥ sārdhamevāṃśamaṃśaṃ śūdrāsuto haret
meaning: त्र्यंशं दायाद्धरेद्विप्रो द्वावंशौ क्षत्रियासुतः वैश्याजः सार्धमेवांशमंशं शूद्रासुतो हरेत्
text: Out of the estate theBrāhmaṇashall take three shares; the son of theKṣatriyamother two shares; the son of theVaiśyamother a share and a half; and the son of theŚūdramother one share.
iast: tryaṃśaṃ dāyāddharedvipro dvāvaṃśau kṣatriyāsutaḥ vaiśyājaḥ sārdhamevāṃśamaṃśaṃ śūdrāsuto haret

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ vā rikthajātaṃ taddaśadhā parikalpya ca dharmyaṃ vibhāgaṃ kurvīta vidhinā 'nena dharmavit
meaning: सर्वं वा रिक्थजातं तद्दशधा परिकल्प्य च धर्म्यं विभागं कुर्वीत विधिना 'नेन धर्मवित्
text: The man knowing the law shall divide the entire estate into ten parts, and then make an equitable division according to the following rule.
iast: sarvaṃ vā rikthajātaṃ taddaśadhā parikalpya ca dharmyaṃ vibhāgaṃ kurvīta vidhinā 'nena dharmavit

sanskrit_iast: caturo 'ṃśān haredviprastrīnaṃśān kṣatriyāsutaḥ vaiśyāputro hareddvyaṃśamaṃśaṃ śūdrāsuto haret
meaning: चतुरो 'ंशान् हरेद्विप्रस्त्रीनंशान् क्षत्रियासुतः वैश्यापुत्रो हरेद्द्व्यंशमंशं शूद्रासुतो हरेत्
text: TheBrāhmaṇashall take four shares, and the son of theKṣatriyamother three shares; the son of theVaiśyamother shall take two shares, and the son of theŚūdramother shall take one share.
iast: caturo 'ṃśān haredviprastrīnaṃśān kṣatriyāsutaḥ vaiśyāputro hareddvyaṃśamaṃśaṃ śūdrāsuto haret

sanskrit_iast: yadyapi syāt tu satputro 'pyasatputro 'pi vā bhavet yadyapi syāt tu satputro yadyaputro 'pi vā bhavet nādhikaṃ daśamāddadyātśūdrāputrāya dharmataḥ
meaning: यद्यपि स्यात् तु सत्पुत्रो 'प्यसत्पुत्रो 'पि वा भवेत् यद्यपि स्यात् तु सत्पुत्रो यद्यपुत्रो 'पि वा भवेत् नाधिकं दशमाद्दद्यात्शूद्रापुत्राय धर्मतः
text: Whether aBrāhmaṇahas a son or no son, he shall not, according to law, allot more than the tenth part to the son of theŚūdrawife.
iast: yadyapi syāt tu satputro 'pyasatputro 'pi vā bhavet yadyapi syāt tu satputro yadyaputro 'pi vā bhavet nādhikaṃ daśamāddadyātśūdrāputrāya dharmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇakṣatriyaviśāṃ śūdrāputro na rikthabhāk yadevāsya pitā dadyāt tadevāsya dhanaṃ bhavet
meaning: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्रापुत्रो न रिक्थभाक् यदेवास्य पिता दद्यात् तदेवास्य धनं भवेत्
text: Of theBrāhmaṇa, theKṣatriyaand theVaiśya, the son born of aŚūdrawife is not an inheritor of property; his property shall consist of whatever his father may give to him.
iast: brāhmaṇakṣatriyaviśāṃ śūdrāputro na rikthabhāk yadevāsya pitā dadyāt tadevāsya dhanaṃ bhavet

sanskrit_iast: samavarṇāsu vā jātāḥ sarve putrā dvijanmanām uddhāraṃ jyāyase dattvā bhajerannitare samam
meaning: समवर्णासु वा जाताः सर्वे पुत्रा द्विजन्मनाम् उद्धारं ज्यायसे दत्त्वा भजेरन्नितरे समम्
text: Or, all the sons of twice-born men, born of wives of the same caste, shall divide the property equally, after the others have given to the eldest his ‘preferential share.’
iast: samavarṇāsu vā jātāḥ sarve putrā dvijanmanām uddhāraṃ jyāyase dattvā bhajerannitare samam

sanskrit_iast: śūdrasya tu savarṇaiva nānyā bhāryā vidhīyate tasyāṃ jātāḥ samāṃśāḥ syuryadi putraśataṃ bhavet
meaning: शूद्रस्य तु सवर्णैव नान्या भार्या विधीयते तस्यां जाताः समांशाः स्युर्यदि पुत्रशतं भवेत्
text: For theŚūdrais ordained a wife of his own caste only, and no other; and all the sons born of her shall be entitled to equal shares, even if there be a hundred sons.
iast: śūdrasya tu savarṇaiva nānyā bhāryā vidhīyate tasyāṃ jātāḥ samāṃśāḥ syuryadi putraśataṃ bhavet

sanskrit_iast: putrān dvādaśa yānāha nr̥̄ṇāṃ svāyaṃbhuvo manuḥ teṣāṃ ṣaḍ bandhudāyādāḥ ṣaḍadāyādabāndhavāḥ
meaning: पुत्रान् द्वादश यानाह न्र्̥̄णां स्वायंभुवो मनुः तेषां षड् बन्धुदायादाः षडदायादबान्धवाः
text: Among the twelve kinds of sons that Manu sprung from the Self-existent one has mentioned,—six are kinsmen as well as heirs, and six are kinsmen, not heirs.
iast: putrān dvādaśa yānāha nr̥̄ṇāṃ svāyaṃbhuvo manuḥ teṣāṃ ṣaḍ bandhudāyādāḥ ṣaḍadāyādabāndhavāḥ

sanskrit_iast: aurasaḥ kṣetrajaścaiva dattaḥ kṛtrima eva ca gūḍhautpanno 'paviddhaśca dāyādā bāndhavāśca ṣaṭ
meaning: औरसः क्षेत्रजश्चैव दत्तः कृत्रिम एव च गूढौत्पन्नो 'पविद्धश्च दायादा बान्धवाश्च षट्
text: (1) The ‘Kānīna,’ ‘maiden-born,’ (2) the ‘Sahoḍha,’ ‘received along with the wife,’ (3) the ‘Krīta,’ ‘bought,’ (4) the ‘Paunarbhava’ ‘begotten on a remarried woman,’ (5) the ‘Svayandatta,’ ‘self-offered’ and (6) the ‘Śaudra,’ ‘Śūdra-born,’—these six are only kinsmen, not heirs.
iast: aurasaḥ kṣetrajaścaiva dattaḥ kṛtrima eva ca gūḍhautpanno 'paviddhaśca dāyādā bāndhavāśca ṣaṭ

sanskrit_iast: kānīnaśca sahoḍhaśca krītaḥ paunarbhavastathā svayaṃdattaśca śaudraśca ṣaḍadāyādabāndhavāḥ
meaning: कानीनश्च सहोढश्च क्रीतः पौनर्भवस्तथा स्वयंदत्तश्च शौद्रश्च षडदायादबान्धवाः
text: (1) The ‘Kānīna,’ ‘maiden-born,’ (2) the ‘Sahoḍha,’ ‘received along with the wife,’ (3) the ‘Krīta,’ ‘bought,’ (4) the ‘Paunarbhava’ ‘begotten on a remarried woman,’ (5) the ‘Svayandatta,’ ‘self-offered’ and (6) the ‘Śaudra,’ ‘Śūdra-born,’—these six are only kinsmen, not heirs.
iast: kānīnaśca sahoḍhaśca krītaḥ paunarbhavastathā svayaṃdattaśca śaudraśca ṣaḍadāyādabāndhavāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yādṛśaṃ phalamāpnoti kuplavaiḥ saṃtaranjalam tādṛśaṃ phalamāpnoti kuputraiḥ saṃtaraṃstamaḥ
meaning: यादृशं फलमाप्नोति कुप्लवैः संतरन्जलम् तादृशं फलमाप्नोति कुपुत्रैः संतरंस्तमः
text: The man who tries to cross the gloom with the help of bad sons obtains results similar to those obtained by one who tries to cross the water with the help of unsound boats.
iast: yādṛśaṃ phalamāpnoti kuplavaiḥ saṃtaranjalam tādṛśaṃ phalamāpnoti kuputraiḥ saṃtaraṃstamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadyekarikthinau syātāmaurasakṣetrajau sutau yasya yat paitṛkaṃ rikthaṃ sa tadgṛhṇīta naitaraḥ
meaning: यद्येकरिक्थिनौ स्यातामौरसक्षेत्रजौ सुतौ यस्य यत् पैतृकं रिक्थं स तद्गृह्णीत नैतरः
text: If the ‘Soil-born’ and the ‘body-born’ sons are both entitled to inherit the same property, each shall receive that property which belongs to his own father, and not the other.
iast: yadyekarikthinau syātāmaurasakṣetrajau sutau yasya yat paitṛkaṃ rikthaṃ sa tadgṛhṇīta naitaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: eka evaurasaḥ putraḥ pitryasya vasunaḥ prabhuḥ śeṣāṇāmānṛśaṃsyārthaṃ pradadyāt tu prajīvanam
meaning: एक एवौरसः पुत्रः पित्र्यस्य वसुनः प्रभुः शेषाणामानृशंस्यार्थं प्रदद्यात् तु प्रजीवनम्
text: The ‘legitimate’ (body-born) son is alone the owner of the paternal estate; but in order toavoid unkindness, he shall provide subsistence for the rest.
iast: eka evaurasaḥ putraḥ pitryasya vasunaḥ prabhuḥ śeṣāṇāmānṛśaṃsyārthaṃ pradadyāt tu prajīvanam

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṣṭhaṃ tu kṣetrajasyāṃśaṃ pradadyāt paitṛkāddhanāt auraso vibhajan dāyaṃ pitryaṃ pañcamameva vā
meaning: षष्ठं तु क्षेत्रजस्यांशं प्रदद्यात् पैतृकाद्धनात् औरसो विभजन् दायं पित्र्यं पञ्चममेव वा
text: When the legitimate son is dividing the paternal estate, he shall give to the ‘Kṣetraja’ son one-sixth or one-fifth part of the father’s property.
iast: ṣaṣṭhaṃ tu kṣetrajasyāṃśaṃ pradadyāt paitṛkāddhanāt auraso vibhajan dāyaṃ pitryaṃ pañcamameva vā

sanskrit_iast: aurasakṣetrajau putrau pitṛrikthasya bhāginau daśāpare tu kramaśo gotrarikthāṃśabhāginaḥ
meaning: औरसक्षेत्रजौ पुत्रौ पितृरिक्थस्य भागिनौ दशापरे तु क्रमशो गोत्ररिक्थांशभागिनः
text: The ‘body-born’ and the ‘soil-born’ are entitled to inherit the father’s property; while the other ten inherit the ‘family-title’ and a share in the property, according to their order
iast: aurasakṣetrajau putrau pitṛrikthasya bhāginau daśāpare tu kramaśo gotrarikthāṃśabhāginaḥ

sanskrit_iast: svakṣetre saṃskṛtāyāṃ tu svayamutpādayeddhi yam tamaurasaṃ vijānīyāt putraṃ prāthamakalpikam
meaning: स्वक्षेत्रे संस्कृतायां तु स्वयमुत्पादयेद्धि यम् तमौरसं विजानीयात् पुत्रं प्राथमकल्पिकम्
text: Him whom a man himself begets in his own sanctified ‘soil,’—one shall know as the ‘body-born’ (legitimate) son, (declared) to be the first in order.
iast: svakṣetre saṃskṛtāyāṃ tu svayamutpādayeddhi yam tamaurasaṃ vijānīyāt putraṃ prāthamakalpikam

sanskrit_iast: yastalpajaḥ pramītasya klībasya vyādhitasya vā svadharmeṇa niyuktāyāṃ sa putraḥ kṣetrajaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: यस्तल्पजः प्रमीतस्य क्लीबस्य व्याधितस्य वा स्वधर्मेण नियुक्तायां स पुत्रः क्षेत्रजः स्मृतः
text: If a son is born of the wife of a man, either dead or impotent or diseased, by one who has been duly ‘authorised,’—that son is declared to be ‘kṣetraja’ ‘soil-born.’
iast: yastalpajaḥ pramītasya klībasya vyādhitasya vā svadharmeṇa niyuktāyāṃ sa putraḥ kṣetrajaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mātā pitā vā dadyātāṃ yamadbhiḥ putramāpadi sadṛśaṃ prītisaṃyuktaṃ sa jñeyo dattrimaḥ sutaḥ
meaning: माता पिता वा दद्यातां यमद्भिः पुत्रमापदि सदृशं प्रीतिसंयुक्तं स ज्ञेयो दत्त्रिमः सुतः
text: When in times of distress, the mother or the father affectionately gives away, with water-libations, a worthy son,—that son is called ‘given’ (adopted).
iast: mātā pitā vā dadyātāṃ yamadbhiḥ putramāpadi sadṛśaṃ prītisaṃyuktaṃ sa jñeyo dattrimaḥ sutaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sadṛśaṃ tu prakuryādyaṃ guṇadoṣavicakṣaṇam putraṃ putraguṇairyuktaṃ sa vijñeyaśca kṛtrimaḥ
meaning: सदृशं तु प्रकुर्याद्यं गुणदोषविचक्षणम् पुत्रं पुत्रगुणैर्युक्तं स विज्ञेयश्च कृत्रिमः
text: When one appoints a son who is worthy, capable of discerning right and wrong, and endowed with filial virtues,—that son is to be known as “appointed.’
iast: sadṛśaṃ tu prakuryādyaṃ guṇadoṣavicakṣaṇam putraṃ putraguṇairyuktaṃ sa vijñeyaśca kṛtrimaḥ

sanskrit_iast: utpadyate gṛhe yastu na ca jñāyeta kasya saḥ sa gṛhe gūḍha utpannastasya syādyasya talpajaḥ
meaning: उत्पद्यते गृहे यस्तु न च ज्ञायेत कस्य सः स गृहे गूढ उत्पन्नस्तस्य स्याद्यस्य तल्पजः
text: If a son is born in a man’s house, and it is not known whose he is,—this son ‘secretly born’ in the house shall belong to him of whose wife he is born.
iast: utpadyate gṛhe yastu na ca jñāyeta kasya saḥ sa gṛhe gūḍha utpannastasya syādyasya talpajaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mātāpitṛbhyāmutsṛṣṭaṃ tayoranyatareṇa vā yaṃ putraṃ parigṛhṇīyādapaviddhaḥ sa ucyate
meaning: मातापितृभ्यामुत्सृष्टं तयोरन्यतरेण वा यं पुत्रं परिगृह्णीयादपविद्धः स उच्यते
text: If a man takes up a son deserted by his parents, or by either of them, he is called the ‘cast off son.’
iast: mātāpitṛbhyāmutsṛṣṭaṃ tayoranyatareṇa vā yaṃ putraṃ parigṛhṇīyādapaviddhaḥ sa ucyate

sanskrit_iast: pitṛveśmani kanyā tu yaṃ putraṃ janayedrahaḥ taṃ kānīnaṃ vadennāmnā voḍhuḥ kanyāsamudbhavam
meaning: पितृवेश्मनि कन्या तु यं पुत्रं जनयेद्रहः तं कानीनं वदेन्नाम्ना वोढुः कन्यासमुद्भवम्
text: If a maiden secretly bears a son in her father’s house, that son, born of a maiden, should be declared as ‘maiden-born’ by name, and to belong to the man who marries her.
iast: pitṛveśmani kanyā tu yaṃ putraṃ janayedrahaḥ taṃ kānīnaṃ vadennāmnā voḍhuḥ kanyāsamudbhavam

sanskrit_iast: yā garbhiṇī saṃskriyate jñātā 'jñātā 'pi vā satī voḍhuḥ sa garbho bhavati sahoḍha iti caucyate
meaning: या गर्भिणी संस्क्रियते ज्ञाता 'ज्ञाता 'पि वा सती वोढुः स गर्भो भवति सहोढ इति चौच्यते
text: If one marries, knowingly or unknowingly, a pregnant maiden, the child in her womb belongs to him who marries her, and is called ‘received along with the wife.’
iast: yā garbhiṇī saṃskriyate jñātā 'jñātā 'pi vā satī voḍhuḥ sa garbho bhavati sahoḍha iti caucyate

sanskrit_iast: krīṇīyādyastvapatyārthaṃ mātāpitroryamantikāt sa krītakaḥ sutastasya sadṛśo 'sadṛśo 'pi vā
meaning: क्रीणीयाद्यस्त्वपत्यार्थं मातापित्रोर्यमन्तिकात् स क्रीतकः सुतस्तस्य सदृशो 'सदृशो 'पि वा
text: If a man buys a boy, worthy or unworthy, from his father and mother, with a view to making him his son, that son is called ‘bought.’
iast: krīṇīyādyastvapatyārthaṃ mātāpitroryamantikāt sa krītakaḥ sutastasya sadṛśo 'sadṛśo 'pi vā

sanskrit_iast: yā patyā vā parityaktā vidhavā vā svayaicchayā utpādayet punarbhūtvā sa paunarbhava ucyate
meaning: या पत्या वा परित्यक्ता विधवा वा स्वयैच्छया उत्पादयेत् पुनर्भूत्वा स पौनर्भव उच्यते
text: If a woman abandoned by her husband, or a widow, of her own accord, marries again and bears a son, that son is called ‘the son of a re-married woman.’
iast: yā patyā vā parityaktā vidhavā vā svayaicchayā utpādayet punarbhūtvā sa paunarbhava ucyate

sanskrit_iast: sā cedakṣatayoniḥ syādgatapratyāgatā 'pi vā paunarbhavena bhartrā sā punaḥ saṃskāramarhati
meaning: सा चेदक्षतयोनिः स्याद्गतप्रत्यागता 'पि वा पौनर्भवेन भर्त्रा सा पुनः संस्कारमर्हति
text: In case she be still a virgin, or having gone away comes back,—she is fit to undergo re-marriage with her second husband.
iast: sā cedakṣatayoniḥ syādgatapratyāgatā 'pi vā paunarbhavena bhartrā sā punaḥ saṃskāramarhati

sanskrit_iast: mātāpitṛvihīno yastyakto vā syādakāraṇāt ātmānamarpayedyasmai svayaṃdattastu sa smṛtaḥ
meaning: मातापितृविहीनो यस्त्यक्तो वा स्यादकारणात् आत्मानमर्पयेद्यस्मै स्वयंदत्तस्तु स स्मृतः
text: If a boy, being deprived of his parents, or being abandoned by them without cause, offers himself to a man,—he is called the‘self-offered son.’
iast: mātāpitṛvihīno yastyakto vā syādakāraṇāt ātmānamarpayedyasmai svayaṃdattastu sa smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yaṃ brāhmaṇastu śūdrāyāṃ kāmādutpādayet sutam sa pārayanneva śavastasmāt pāraśavaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: यं ब्राह्मणस्तु शूद्रायां कामादुत्पादयेत् सुतम् स पारयन्नेव शवस्तस्मात् पारशवः स्मृतः
text: If aBrāhmaṇa, through lust, begets a son on aŚūdrawoman, he is as a corpse, even though living, and hence called the ‘living corpse.’
iast: yaṃ brāhmaṇastu śūdrāyāṃ kāmādutpādayet sutam sa pārayanneva śavastasmāt pāraśavaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dāsyāṃ vā dāsadāsyāṃ vā yaḥ śūdrasya suto bhavet so 'nujñāto haredaṃśamiti dharmo vyavasthitaḥ
meaning: दास्यां वा दासदास्यां वा यः शूद्रस्य सुतो भवेत् सो 'नुज्ञातो हरेदंशमिति धर्मो व्यवस्थितः
text: If a son is born to aŚūdrafrom a female slave, or from the female slave of a slave, he shall, when permitted, receive a share; such is the settled law.
iast: dāsyāṃ vā dāsadāsyāṃ vā yaḥ śūdrasya suto bhavet so 'nujñāto haredaṃśamiti dharmo vyavasthitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣetrajādīn sutānetānekādaśa yathauditān putrapratinidhīnāhuḥ kriyālopān manīṣiṇaḥ
meaning: क्षेत्रजादीन् सुतानेतानेकादश यथौदितान् पुत्रप्रतिनिधीनाहुः क्रियालोपान् मनीषिणः
text: These eleven, the ‘soil-born’ and the rest, as here described, the wise ones call ‘substitutes of a son,’—taken with a view to the failure of a religious duty.
iast: kṣetrajādīn sutānetānekādaśa yathauditān putrapratinidhīnāhuḥ kriyālopān manīṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ya ete 'bhihitāḥ putrāḥ prasaṅgādanyabījajāḥ yasya te bījato jātāstasya te naitarasya tu
meaning: य एते 'भिहिताः पुत्राः प्रसङ्गादन्यबीजजाः यस्य ते बीजतो जातास्तस्य ते नैतरस्य तु
text: Those sons born of the seed of strangers that have been described here by the way, belong to him from whose seed they are born, and not to any other person.
iast: ya ete 'bhihitāḥ putrāḥ prasaṅgādanyabījajāḥ yasya te bījato jātāstasya te naitarasya tu

sanskrit_iast: bhrātr̥̄ṇāmekajātānāmekaścet putravān bhavet sarvāṃstāṃstena putreṇa putriṇo manurabravīt
meaning: भ्रात्र्̥̄णामेकजातानामेकश्चेत् पुत्रवान् भवेत् सर्वांस्तांस्तेन पुत्रेण पुत्रिणो मनुरब्रवीत्
text: Among brothers, born of the same father, if even one have a son, Manu has declared all of them to be ‘with son,’ through that son.
iast: bhrātr̥̄ṇāmekajātānāmekaścet putravān bhavet sarvāṃstāṃstena putreṇa putriṇo manurabravīt

sanskrit_iast: sarvāsāmekapatnīnāmekā cet putriṇī bhavet sarvāstāstena putreṇa prāha putravatīrmanuḥ
meaning: सर्वासामेकपत्नीनामेका चेत् पुत्रिणी भवेत् सर्वास्तास्तेन पुत्रेण प्राह पुत्रवतीर्मनुः
text: Among all the wives of one man, if one have a son, Manu has declared all of them to be ‘with son,’ through that son.
iast: sarvāsāmekapatnīnāmekā cet putriṇī bhavet sarvāstāstena putreṇa prāha putravatīrmanuḥ

sanskrit_iast: śreyasaḥ śreyaso 'lābhe pāpīyān rikthamarhati bahavaścet tu sadṛśāḥ sarve rikthasya bhāginaḥ
meaning: श्रेयसः श्रेयसो 'लाभे पापीयान् रिक्थमर्हति बहवश्चेत् तु सदृशाः सर्वे रिक्थस्य भागिनः
text: On the failure of each superior kind of son, each next inferior one is entitled to inheritance; if there be several of the same class, all shall share the property.
iast: śreyasaḥ śreyaso 'lābhe pāpīyān rikthamarhati bahavaścet tu sadṛśāḥ sarve rikthasya bhāginaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na bhrātaro na pitaraḥ putrā rikthaharāḥ pituḥ pitā haredaputrasya rikthaṃ bhrātara eva ca
meaning: न भ्रातरो न पितरः पुत्रा रिक्थहराः पितुः पिता हरेदपुत्रस्य रिक्थं भ्रातर एव च
text: Sons alone shall inherit the father’s property, not brothers or fathers; but the father and brothers shall inherit the property of one who dies sonless.
iast: na bhrātaro na pitaraḥ putrā rikthaharāḥ pituḥ pitā haredaputrasya rikthaṃ bhrātara eva ca

sanskrit_iast: trayāṇāmudakaṃ kāryaṃ triṣu piṇḍaḥ pravartate caturthaḥ saṃpradātaiṣāṃ pañcamo naupapadyate
meaning: त्रयाणामुदकं कार्यं त्रिषु पिण्डः प्रवर्तते चतुर्थः संप्रदातैषां पञ्चमो नौपपद्यते
text: To three should water-libation be offered; to three is the cake offered; the fourth is the giver of these offerings; there can be no fifth.
iast: trayāṇāmudakaṃ kāryaṃ triṣu piṇḍaḥ pravartate caturthaḥ saṃpradātaiṣāṃ pañcamo naupapadyate

sanskrit_iast: anantaraḥ sapiṇḍādyastasya tasya dhanaṃ bhavet ata ūrdhvaṃ sakulyaḥ syādācāryaḥ śiṣya eva vā
meaning: अनन्तरः सपिण्डाद्यस्तस्य तस्य धनं भवेत् अत ऊर्ध्वं सकुल्यः स्यादाचार्यः शिष्य एव वा
text: The property shall always devolve upon him who is nearest to the (deceased) ‘Sapiṇḍa’; after these either a ‘Sakulya’; or the Spiritual Preceptor, or the pupil.
iast: anantaraḥ sapiṇḍādyastasya tasya dhanaṃ bhavet ata ūrdhvaṃ sakulyaḥ syādācāryaḥ śiṣya eva vā

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmapyabhāve tu brāhmaṇā rikthabhāginaḥ traividyāḥ śucayo dāntāstathā dharmo na hīyate
meaning: सर्वेषामप्यभावे तु ब्राह्मणा रिक्थभागिनः त्रैविद्याः शुचयो दान्तास्तथा धर्मो न हीयते
text: But, on the failure of all, the property shall be taken byBrāhmaṇas, learned in the Vedas, pure and self-controlled; in this manner the law would not be violated.
iast: sarveṣāmapyabhāve tu brāhmaṇā rikthabhāginaḥ traividyāḥ śucayo dāntāstathā dharmo na hīyate

sanskrit_iast: ahāryaṃ brāhmaṇadravyaṃ rājñā nityamiti sthitiḥ itareṣāṃ tu varṇānāṃ sarvābhāve harennṛpaḥ
meaning: अहार्यं ब्राह्मणद्रव्यं राज्ञा नित्यमिति स्थितिः इतरेषां तु वर्णानां सर्वाभावे हरेन्नृपः
text: The property of theBrāhmaṇashoved never be taken by the King,—such is the law; but in the case of other castes, the king shall take the property, in the absence of all heirs.
iast: ahāryaṃ brāhmaṇadravyaṃ rājñā nityamiti sthitiḥ itareṣāṃ tu varṇānāṃ sarvābhāve harennṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃsthitasyānapatyasya sagotrāt putramāharet tatra yadrikthajātaṃ syāt tat tasmin pratipādayet
meaning: संस्थितस्यानपत्यस्य सगोत्रात् पुत्रमाहरेत् तत्र यद्रिक्थजातं स्यात् तत् तस्मिन् प्रतिपादयेत्
text: In the case of a man dying childless, if an issue is raised from a member of the same family, all the property that there may be shall be delivered to that child.
iast: saṃsthitasyānapatyasya sagotrāt putramāharet tatra yadrikthajātaṃ syāt tat tasmin pratipādayet

sanskrit_iast: dvau tu yau vivadeyātāṃ dvābhyāṃ jātau striyā dhane tayoryadyasya pitryaṃ syāt tat sa gṛhṇīta naitaraḥ
meaning: द्वौ तु यौ विवदेयातां द्वाभ्यां जातौ स्त्रिया धने तयोर्यद्यस्य पित्र्यं स्यात् तत् स गृह्णीत नैतरः
text: But if two sons, born of two men, contend for the property in the mother’s possession, each shall take, to the exclusion of the other, what belonged to his own father.
iast: dvau tu yau vivadeyātāṃ dvābhyāṃ jātau striyā dhane tayoryadyasya pitryaṃ syāt tat sa gṛhṇīta naitaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: jananyāṃ saṃsthitāyāṃ tu samaṃ sarve sahaudarāḥ bhajeran mātṛkaṃ rikthaṃ bhaginyaśca sanābhayaḥ
meaning: जनन्यां संस्थितायां तु समं सर्वे सहौदराः भजेरन् मातृकं रिक्थं भगिन्यश्च सनाभयः
text: When the mother has died, all the uterine brothers and uterine sisters shall divide the mother’s property equally.
iast: jananyāṃ saṃsthitāyāṃ tu samaṃ sarve sahaudarāḥ bhajeran mātṛkaṃ rikthaṃ bhaginyaśca sanābhayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāstāsāṃ syurduhitarastāsāmapi yathārhataḥ tasyāṃ mātāmahyā dhanāt kiṃ cit pradeyaṃ prītipūrvakam
meaning: यास्तासां स्युर्दुहितरस्तासामपि यथार्हतः तस्यां मातामह्या धनात् किं चित् प्रदेयं प्रीतिपूर्वकम्
text: Even to the daughters of those daughters something shall be lovingly given, as is quite proper, out of the property of their maternal grandmother.
iast: yāstāsāṃ syurduhitarastāsāmapi yathārhataḥ tasyāṃ mātāmahyā dhanāt kiṃ cit pradeyaṃ prītipūrvakam

sanskrit_iast: adhyagniadhyāvāhanikaṃ dattaṃ ca prītikarmaṇi bhrātṛmātṛpitṛprāptaṃ ṣaḍvidhaṃ strīdhanaṃ smṛtam
meaning: अध्यग्निअध्यावाहनिकं दत्तं च प्रीतिकर्मणि भ्रातृमातृपितृप्राप्तं षड्विधं स्त्रीधनं स्मृतम्
text: (1) What is given before the fire, (2) what is given at the time of departure, (3) what is given in token of love, and what is received from (4) the brother, (5) the mother and (6) the father,—has been declared to be ‘Strīdhana’ (the exclusive property of the woman).
iast: adhyagniadhyāvāhanikaṃ dattaṃ ca prītikarmaṇi bhrātṛmātṛpitṛprāptaṃ ṣaḍvidhaṃ strīdhanaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: anvādheyaṃ ca yaddattaṃ patyā prītena caiva yat patyau jīvati vṛttāyāḥ prajāyāstaddhanaṃ bhavet
meaning: अन्वाधेयं च यद्दत्तं पत्या प्रीतेन चैव यत् पत्यौ जीवति वृत्तायाः प्रजायास्तद्धनं भवेत्
text: Also the gift that is subsequently made to her by her loving husband, shall go to her offspring, if she dies while her husband is living.
iast: anvādheyaṃ ca yaddattaṃ patyā prītena caiva yat patyau jīvati vṛttāyāḥ prajāyāstaddhanaṃ bhavet

sanskrit_iast: brāhmadaivārṣagāndharvaprājāpatyeṣu yadvasu aprajāyāmatītāyāṃ bhartureva tadiṣyate
meaning: ब्राह्मदैवार्षगान्धर्वप्राजापत्येषु यद्वसु अप्रजायामतीतायां भर्तुरेव तदिष्यते
text: But the property given to a woman on the ‘Āsura’ or other (inferior) forms of marriage, has been held to belong to her parents, upon her dying childless.
iast: brāhmadaivārṣagāndharvaprājāpatyeṣu yadvasu aprajāyāmatītāyāṃ bhartureva tadiṣyate
