sanskrit_iast: yat tvasyāḥ syāddhanaṃ dattaṃ vivāheṣvāsurādiṣu aprajāyāmatītāyāṃ mātāpitrostadiṣyate
meaning: यत् त्वस्याः स्याद्धनं दत्तं विवाहेष्वासुरादिषु अप्रजायामतीतायां मातापित्रोस्तदिष्यते
text: But the property given to a woman on the ‘Āsura’ or other (inferior) forms of marriage, has been held to belong to her parents, upon her dying childless.
iast: yat tvasyāḥ syāddhanaṃ dattaṃ vivāheṣvāsurādiṣu aprajāyāmatītāyāṃ mātāpitrostadiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: striyāṃ tu yadbhavedvittaṃ pitrā dattaṃ kathaṃ cana brāhmaṇī taddharet kanyā tadapatyasya vā bhavet
meaning: स्त्रियां तु यद्भवेद्वित्तं पित्रा दत्तं कथं चन ब्राह्मणी तद्धरेत् कन्या तदपत्यस्य वा भवेत्
text: The property that may have been given to a woman by her father shall be taken by the daughter of theBrāhamaṇa-caste; or it shall belong to the child of that daughter.
iast: striyāṃ tu yadbhavedvittaṃ pitrā dattaṃ kathaṃ cana brāhmaṇī taddharet kanyā tadapatyasya vā bhavet

sanskrit_iast: na nirhāraṃ striyaḥ kuryuḥ kuṭumbādbahumadhyagāt kṣ svakādapi ca vittāddhi svasya bharturanājñayā
meaning: न निर्हारं स्त्रियः कुर्युः कुटुम्बाद्बहुमध्यगात् क्ष् स्वकादपि च वित्ताद्धि स्वस्य भर्तुरनाज्ञया
text: Women shall never make a hoard out of the family-property common to many, nor out of their own property, without the husband’s permission.
iast: na nirhāraṃ striyaḥ kuryuḥ kuṭumbādbahumadhyagāt kṣ svakādapi ca vittāddhi svasya bharturanājñayā

sanskrit_iast: patyau jīvati yaḥ strībhiralaṅkāro dhṛto bhavet na taṃ bhajeran dāyādā bhajamānāḥ patanti te
meaning: पत्यौ जीवति यः स्त्रीभिरलङ्कारो धृतो भवेत् न तं भजेरन् दायादा भजमानाः पतन्ति ते
text: The ornament worn by the woman during her husband’s life-time, her heirs shall not divide; if they divide it, they become outcasts.
iast: patyau jīvati yaḥ strībhiralaṅkāro dhṛto bhavet na taṃ bhajeran dāyādā bhajamānāḥ patanti te

sanskrit_iast: anaṃśau klībapatitau jātyandhabadhirau tathā unmattajaḍamūkāśca ye ca ke cinnirindriyāḥ
meaning: अनंशौ क्लीबपतितौ जात्यन्धबधिरौ तथा उन्मत्तजडमूकाश्च ये च के चिन्निरिन्द्रियाः
text: Eunuchs and outcasts, those born blind or deaf, idiots and the dumb, as well as those deficient in any organ, are entitled to no shares.
iast: anaṃśau klībapatitau jātyandhabadhirau tathā unmattajaḍamūkāśca ye ca ke cinnirindriyāḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmapi tu nyāyyaṃ dātuṃ śaktyā manīṣiṇā grāsācchādanamatyantaṃ patito hyadadadbhavet
meaning: सर्वेषामपि तु न्याय्यं दातुं शक्त्या मनीषिणा ग्रासाच्छादनमत्यन्तं पतितो ह्यददद्भवेत्
text: But it is fair that the wise man shall give even to all these food and clothing to the best of his ability; if he does not give it at all, he becomes an outcast.
iast: sarveṣāmapi tu nyāyyaṃ dātuṃ śaktyā manīṣiṇā grāsācchādanamatyantaṃ patito hyadadadbhavet

sanskrit_iast: yadyarthitā tu dāraiḥ syāt klībādīnāṃ kathaṃ cana teṣāmutpannatantūnāmapatyaṃ dāyamarhati
meaning: यद्यर्थिता तु दारैः स्यात् क्लीबादीनां कथं चन तेषामुत्पन्नतन्तूनामपत्यं दायमर्हति
text: If the eunuch and the rest should somehow happen to have longing for a wife, the child of such of them as have issue is entitled to inheritance.
iast: yadyarthitā tu dāraiḥ syāt klībādīnāṃ kathaṃ cana teṣāmutpannatantūnāmapatyaṃ dāyamarhati

sanskrit_iast: yat kiṃ cit pitari prete dhanaṃ jyeṣṭho 'dhigacchati bhāgo yavīyasāṃ tatra yadi vidyānupālinaḥ
meaning: यत् किं चित् पितरि प्रेते धनं ज्येष्ठो 'धिगच्छति भागो यवीयसां तत्र यदि विद्यानुपालिनः
text: Whatever property the eldest brother acquires after the death of the father, a share of that shall belong to the younger brothers, if they are devoted to learning.
iast: yat kiṃ cit pitari prete dhanaṃ jyeṣṭho 'dhigacchati bhāgo yavīyasāṃ tatra yadi vidyānupālinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: avidyānāṃ tu sarveṣāmīhātaśceddhanaṃ bhavet samastatra vibhāgaḥ syādapitrya iti dhāraṇā
meaning: अविद्यानां तु सर्वेषामीहातश्चेद्धनं भवेत् समस्तत्र विभागः स्यादपित्र्य इति धारणा
text: But if all of them are unlearned, and the property is acquired by their labour,—the division in that case shall be equal, the property being not ancestral. such is the settled rule.
iast: avidyānāṃ tu sarveṣāmīhātaśceddhanaṃ bhavet samastatra vibhāgaḥ syādapitrya iti dhāraṇā

sanskrit_iast: vidyādhanaṃ tu yadyasya tat tasyaiva dhanaṃ bhavet maitryamaudvāhikaṃ caiva mādhuparkikameva ca
meaning: विद्याधनं तु यद्यस्य तत् तस्यैव धनं भवेत् मैत्र्यमौद्वाहिकं चैव माधुपर्किकमेव च
text: The gains of learning shall be the sole property of the man by whom they have been acquired; as also friendly presents, marriage.—presents, and presents received in connection with the ‘honey—mixture.’
iast: vidyādhanaṃ tu yadyasya tat tasyaiva dhanaṃ bhavet maitryamaudvāhikaṃ caiva mādhuparkikameva ca

sanskrit_iast: bhrātr̥̄ṇāṃ yastu naiheta dhanaṃ śaktaḥ svakarmaṇā sa nirbhājyaḥ svakādaṃśāt kiṃ ciddattvaupajīvanam
meaning: भ्रात्र्̥̄णां यस्तु नैहेत धनं शक्तः स्वकर्मणा स निर्भाज्यः स्वकादंशात् किं चिद्दत्त्वौपजीवनम्
text: Among brothers, if any one, being quite competent through his own profession, does not desire the property, he shall be debarred from his share, after a little has been given to him by way of maintenance.
iast: bhrātr̥̄ṇāṃ yastu naiheta dhanaṃ śaktaḥ svakarmaṇā sa nirbhājyaḥ svakādaṃśāt kiṃ ciddattvaupajīvanam

sanskrit_iast: anupaghnan pitṛdravyaṃ śrameṇa yadupārjitam svayamīhitalabdhaṃ tannākāmo dātumarhati
meaning: अनुपघ्नन् पितृद्रव्यं श्रमेण यदुपार्जितम् स्वयमीहितलब्धं तन्नाकामो दातुमर्हति
text: If one of them acquires something by his own effort, without interfering with the patrimony,—that property, being acquired by his own labour, he need not give to others, unless he himself wishes it.
iast: anupaghnan pitṛdravyaṃ śrameṇa yadupārjitam svayamīhitalabdhaṃ tannākāmo dātumarhati

sanskrit_iast: paitṛkaṃ tu pitā dravyamanavāptaṃ yadāpnuyāt na tat putrairbhajet sārdhamakāmaḥ svayamarjitam
meaning: पैतृकं तु पिता द्रव्यमनवाप्तं यदाप्नुयात् न तत् पुत्रैर्भजेत् सार्धमकामः स्वयमर्जितम्
text: If the father recovers a lost ancestral property, he shall not, unless he so wishes, share it with his sons,—being, as it is his self-acquired property.
iast: paitṛkaṃ tu pitā dravyamanavāptaṃ yadāpnuyāt na tat putrairbhajet sārdhamakāmaḥ svayamarjitam

sanskrit_iast: vibhaktāḥ saha jīvanto vibhajeran punaryadi samastatra vibhāgaḥ syāj jyaiṣṭhyaṃ tatra na vidyate
meaning: विभक्ताः सह जीवन्तो विभजेरन् पुनर्यदि समस्तत्र विभागः स्याज् ज्यैष्ठ्यं तत्र न विद्यते
text: If brothers, living together, after having divided once, happen to make a second partition, the division in that case shall be equal; in such cases there is no ‘primogeniture.’
iast: vibhaktāḥ saha jīvanto vibhajeran punaryadi samastatra vibhāgaḥ syāj jyaiṣṭhyaṃ tatra na vidyate

sanskrit_iast: yeṣāṃ jyeṣṭhaḥ kaniṣṭho vā hīyetāṃśapradānataḥ mriyetānyataro vā 'pi tasya bhāgo na lupyate
meaning: येषां ज्येष्ठः कनिष्ठो वा हीयेतांशप्रदानतः म्रियेतान्यतरो वा 'पि तस्य भागो न लुप्यते
text: If the eldest or the youngest of the brothers should be deprived of his shares,—or if either of them dies,—his share does not become lost.
iast: yeṣāṃ jyeṣṭhaḥ kaniṣṭho vā hīyetāṃśapradānataḥ mriyetānyataro vā 'pi tasya bhāgo na lupyate

sanskrit_iast: sodaryā vibhajeraṃstaṃ sametya sahitāḥ samam bhrātaro ye ca saṃsṛṣṭā bhāginyaśca sanābhayaḥ
meaning: सोदर्या विभजेरंस्तं समेत्य सहिताः समम् भ्रातरो ये च संसृष्टा भागिन्यश्च सनाभयः
text: His uterine brothers, coming together, shall divide it equally; as also the united brothers and consanguineous sisters.
iast: sodaryā vibhajeraṃstaṃ sametya sahitāḥ samam bhrātaro ye ca saṃsṛṣṭā bhāginyaśca sanābhayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo jyeṣṭho vinikurvīta lobhādbhrātr̥̄n yavīyasaḥ so 'jyeṣṭhaḥ syādabhāgaśca niyantavyaśca rājabhiḥ
meaning: यो ज्येष्ठो विनिकुर्वीत लोभाद्भ्रात्र्̥̄न् यवीयसः सो 'ज्येष्ठः स्यादभागश्च नियन्तव्यश्च राजभिः
text: If an eldest brother, through avarice, defrauds the younger ones, he shall lose his ‘seniority’ and his share, and shall also be punished by the king.
iast: yo jyeṣṭho vinikurvīta lobhādbhrātr̥̄n yavīyasaḥ so 'jyeṣṭhaḥ syādabhāgaśca niyantavyaśca rājabhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarva eva vikarmasthā nārhanti bhrātaro dhanam na cādattvā kaniṣṭhebhyo jyeṣṭhaḥ kurvīta yautakam
meaning: सर्व एव विकर्मस्था नार्हन्ति भ्रातरो धनम् न चादत्त्वा कनिष्ठेभ्यो ज्येष्ठः कुर्वीत यौतकम्
text: All brothers addicted to evil deeds are unworthy of having property; and the elder brother shall not have a separate hoard without making a contribution to his younger brothers.
iast: sarva eva vikarmasthā nārhanti bhrātaro dhanam na cādattvā kaniṣṭhebhyo jyeṣṭhaḥ kurvīta yautakam

sanskrit_iast: bhrātr̥̄ṇāmavibhaktānāṃ yadyutthānaṃ bhavet saha na putrabhāgaṃ viṣamaṃ pitā dadyāt kathaṃ cana
meaning: भ्रात्र्̥̄णामविभक्तानां यद्युत्थानं भवेत् सह न पुत्रभागं विषमं पिता दद्यात् कथं चन
text: Among undivided brothers, if there is a joint concern,—the father shall, on no account, make an unequal division among his sons.
iast: bhrātr̥̄ṇāmavibhaktānāṃ yadyutthānaṃ bhavet saha na putrabhāgaṃ viṣamaṃ pitā dadyāt kathaṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: ūrdhvaṃ vibhāgātjātastu pitryameva hareddhanam saṃsṛṣṭāstena vā ye syurvibhajeta sa taiḥ saha
meaning: ऊर्ध्वं विभागात्जातस्तु पित्र्यमेव हरेद्धनम् संसृष्टास्तेन वा ये स्युर्विभजेत स तैः सह
text: If a son is born after partition, he shall receive the property of the father alone; or if any other sons be reunited, he would share it with them.
iast: ūrdhvaṃ vibhāgātjātastu pitryameva hareddhanam saṃsṛṣṭāstena vā ye syurvibhajeta sa taiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: anapatyasya putrasya mātā dāyamavāpnuyāt mātaryapi ca vṛttāyāṃ piturmātā hareddhanam
meaning: अनपत्यस्य पुत्रस्य माता दायमवाप्नुयात् मातर्यपि च वृत्तायां पितुर्माता हरेद्धनम्
text: The property of a childless son shall be inherited by his mother; and if the mother also is dead, his father’s mother shall receive that property.
iast: anapatyasya putrasya mātā dāyamavāpnuyāt mātaryapi ca vṛttāyāṃ piturmātā hareddhanam

sanskrit_iast: ṛṇe dhane ca sarvasmin pravibhakte yathāvidhi paścāddṛśyeta yat kiṃ cit tat sarvaṃ samatāṃ nayet
meaning: ऋणे धने च सर्वस्मिन् प्रविभक्ते यथाविधि पश्चाद्दृश्येत यत् किं चित् तत् सर्वं समतां नयेत्
text: After all the assets and liabilities have been duly distributed, if something be discovered afterwards,—all this must be divided equally.
iast: ṛṇe dhane ca sarvasmin pravibhakte yathāvidhi paścāddṛśyeta yat kiṃ cit tat sarvaṃ samatāṃ nayet

sanskrit_iast: vastraṃ patramalaṅkāraṃ kṛtānnamudakaṃ striyaḥ yogakṣemaṃ pracāraṃ ca na vibhājyaṃ pracakṣate
meaning: वस्त्रं पत्रमलङ्कारं कृतान्नमुदकं स्त्रियः योगक्षेमं प्रचारं च न विभाज्यं प्रचक्षते
text: A cloth, a conveyance, an ornament, cooked food, water, women, what is conducive to welfare and pasture-ground,—these they declare to be impartible.
iast: vastraṃ patramalaṅkāraṃ kṛtānnamudakaṃ striyaḥ yogakṣemaṃ pracāraṃ ca na vibhājyaṃ pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: ayamukto vibhāgo vaḥ putrāṇāṃ ca kriyāvidhiḥ kramaśaḥ kṣetrajādīnāṃ dyūtadharmaṃ nibodhata
meaning: अयमुक्तो विभागो वः पुत्राणां च क्रियाविधिः क्रमशः क्षेत्रजादीनां द्यूतधर्मं निबोधत
text: Thus has been expounded to you Partition, and the appointing of the ‘soil-born’ and other kinds of sons in due order. Now learn the law relating to Gambling.
iast: ayamukto vibhāgo vaḥ putrāṇāṃ ca kriyāvidhiḥ kramaśaḥ kṣetrajādīnāṃ dyūtadharmaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: dyūtaṃ samāhvayaṃ caiva rājā rāṣṭrātnivārayet rājāntakaraṇāvetau dvau doṣau pṛthivīkṣitām
meaning: द्यूतं समाह्वयं चैव राजा राष्ट्रात्निवारयेत् राजान्तकरणावेतौ द्वौ दोषौ पृथिवीक्षिताम्
text: The King shall exclude from his realm Gambling and Betting; these two evils bring about the destruction of the kingdoms of princes.
iast: dyūtaṃ samāhvayaṃ caiva rājā rāṣṭrātnivārayet rājāntakaraṇāvetau dvau doṣau pṛthivīkṣitām

sanskrit_iast: prakāśametat tāskaryaṃ yaddevanasamāhvayau tayornityaṃ pratīghāte nṛpatiryatnavān bhavet
meaning: प्रकाशमेतत् तास्कर्यं यद्देवनसमाह्वयौ तयोर्नित्यं प्रतीघाते नृपतिर्यत्नवान् भवेत्
text: Gambling and Betting are open theft; the King shall always be careful in suppressing them.
iast: prakāśametat tāskaryaṃ yaddevanasamāhvayau tayornityaṃ pratīghāte nṛpatiryatnavān bhavet

sanskrit_iast: aprāṇibhiryat kriyate tatloke dyūtamucyate prāṇibhiḥ kriyate yastu sa vijñeyaḥ samāhvayaḥ
meaning: अप्राणिभिर्यत् क्रियते तत्लोके द्यूतमुच्यते प्राणिभिः क्रियते यस्तु स विज्ञेयः समाह्वयः
text: That which is done through inanimate things is called ‘Gambling’; while what is done through animate things is to be known as ‘Betting.’
iast: aprāṇibhiryat kriyate tatloke dyūtamucyate prāṇibhiḥ kriyate yastu sa vijñeyaḥ samāhvayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dyūtaṃ samāhvayaṃ caiva yaḥ kuryāt kārayeta vā tān sarvān ghātayedrājā śūdrāṃśca dvijaliṅginaḥ
meaning: द्यूतं समाह्वयं चैव यः कुर्यात् कारयेत वा तान् सर्वान् घातयेद्राजा शूद्रांश्च द्विजलिङ्गिनः
text: He who either does the gambling or betting himself, or helps others to do them,—all these the King shall strike; as also those Śūdras who assume the guise of twice-born men.
iast: dyūtaṃ samāhvayaṃ caiva yaḥ kuryāt kārayeta vā tān sarvān ghātayedrājā śūdrāṃśca dvijaliṅginaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kitavān kuśīlavān krūrān pāṣaṇḍasthāṃśca mānavān vikarmasthān śauṇḍikāṃśca kṣipraṃ nirvāsayet purāt
meaning: कितवान् कुशीलवान् क्रूरान् पाषण्डस्थांश्च मानवान् विकर्मस्थान् शौण्डिकांश्च क्षिप्रं निर्वासयेत् पुरात्
text: Gamblers, dancers, cruel men, men belonging to heretical sects, men addicted to evil deeds, dealers in wine,—these the King shall instantly banish from his town.
iast: kitavān kuśīlavān krūrān pāṣaṇḍasthāṃśca mānavān vikarmasthān śauṇḍikāṃśca kṣipraṃ nirvāsayet purāt

sanskrit_iast: ete rāṣṭre vartamānā rājñaḥ prachannataskarāḥ vikarmakriyayā nityaṃ bādhante bhadrikāḥ prajāḥ
meaning: एते राष्ट्रे वर्तमाना राज्ञः प्रछन्नतस्कराः विकर्मक्रियया नित्यं बाधन्ते भद्रिकाः प्रजाः
text: These disguised thieves, living in the King’s realm, constantly harass the well-behaved people by their evil deeds.
iast: ete rāṣṭre vartamānā rājñaḥ prachannataskarāḥ vikarmakriyayā nityaṃ bādhante bhadrikāḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: dyūtametat purā kalpe dṛṣṭaṃ vairakaraṃ mahat tasmāddyūtaṃ na seveta hāsyārthamapi buddhimān
meaning: द्यूतमेतत् पुरा कल्पे दृष्टं वैरकरं महत् तस्माद्द्यूतं न सेवेत हास्यार्थमपि बुद्धिमान्
text: In former cycles gambling has been seen to be the great source of enmity; the wise man shall therefore not have recourse to gambling, even in joke.
iast: dyūtametat purā kalpe dṛṣṭaṃ vairakaraṃ mahat tasmāddyūtaṃ na seveta hāsyārthamapi buddhimān

sanskrit_iast: pracchannaṃ vā prakāśaṃ vā tatniṣeveta yo naraḥ tasya daṇḍavikalpaḥ syādyathaiṣṭaṃ nṛpatestathā
meaning: प्रच्छन्नं वा प्रकाशं वा तत्निषेवेत यो नरः तस्य दण्डविकल्पः स्याद्यथैष्टं नृपतेस्तथा
text: If a man has recourse, either openly or secretly, to this (vice), the form of punishment inflicted upon him shall be in accordance with the King’s discretion.
iast: pracchannaṃ vā prakāśaṃ vā tatniṣeveta yo naraḥ tasya daṇḍavikalpaḥ syādyathaiṣṭaṃ nṛpatestathā

sanskrit_iast: kṣatraviśśūdrayonistu daṇḍaṃ dātumaśaknuvan ānṛṇyaṃ karmaṇā gacchedvipro dadyātśanaiḥ śanaiḥ
meaning: क्षत्रविश्शूद्रयोनिस्तु दण्डं दातुमशक्नुवन् आनृण्यं कर्मणा गच्छेद्विप्रो दद्यात्शनैः शनैः
text: TheKṣatriya, theVaiśyaand theŚūdra, when unable to pay a fine, shall discharge the liability by labour; theBrāhmaṇamay pay it by instalments.
iast: kṣatraviśśūdrayonistu daṇḍaṃ dātumaśaknuvan ānṛṇyaṃ karmaṇā gacchedvipro dadyātśanaiḥ śanaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: strībālaunmattavṛddhānāṃ daridrāṇāṃ ca rogiṇām śiphāvidalarajjuādyairvidadhyātnṛpatirdamam
meaning: स्त्रीबालौन्मत्तवृद्धानां दरिद्राणां च रोगिणाम् शिफाविदलरज्जुआद्यैर्विदध्यात्नृपतिर्दमम्
text: On women, boys, men out of their minds, the old, the poor and the sick, the king shall inflict punishment with creepers, barks, ropes and so forth.
iast: strībālaunmattavṛddhānāṃ daridrāṇāṃ ca rogiṇām śiphāvidalarajjuādyairvidadhyātnṛpatirdamam

sanskrit_iast: ye niyuktāstu kāryeṣu hanyuḥ kāryāṇi kāryiṇām dhanauṣmaṇā pacyamānāstānnissvān kārayennṛpaḥ
meaning: ये नियुक्तास्तु कार्येषु हन्युः कार्याणि कार्यिणाम् धनौष्मणा पच्यमानास्तान्निस्स्वान् कारयेन्नृपः
text: If the officers deputed to look after the business of suitors should, fired by the heat of wealth, hamper that business,—these the King shall render penniless.
iast: ye niyuktāstu kāryeṣu hanyuḥ kāryāṇi kāryiṇām dhanauṣmaṇā pacyamānāstānnissvān kārayennṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kūṭaśāsanakartr̥̄ṃśca prakṛtīnāṃ ca dūṣakān strībālabrāhmaṇaghnāṃśca hanyāddviṣsevinastathā
meaning: कूटशासनकर्त्र्̥̄ंश्च प्रकृतीनां च दूषकान् स्त्रीबालब्राह्मणघ्नांश्च हन्याद्द्विष्सेविनस्तथा
text: कूटशासनकर्तॄंश्च प्रकृतीनां च दूषकान् ।स्त्रीबालब्राह्मणघ्नांश्च हन्याद् द्विष् सेविनस्तथा ?? ॥ २३२ ॥
iast: kūṭaśāsanakartr̥̄ṃśca prakṛtīnāṃ ca dūṣakān strībālabrāhmaṇaghnāṃśca hanyāddviṣsevinastathā

sanskrit_iast: tīritaṃ cānuśiṣṭaṃ ca yatra kva cana yadbhavet kṛtaṃ taddharmato vidyātna tadbhūyo nivartayet
meaning: तीरितं चानुशिष्टं च यत्र क्व चन यद्भवेत् कृतं तद्धर्मतो विद्यात्न तद्भूयो निवर्तयेत्
text: Whatever has been finally settled and whatever punishment has been inflicted,—he shall accept as lawfully done, and shall not annul it
iast: tīritaṃ cānuśiṣṭaṃ ca yatra kva cana yadbhavet kṛtaṃ taddharmato vidyātna tadbhūyo nivartayet

sanskrit_iast: amātyāḥ prāḍvivāko vā yat kuryuḥ kāryamanyathā tat svayaṃ nṛpatiḥ kuryāt tān sahasraṃ ca daṇḍayet taṃ
meaning: अमात्याः प्राड्विवाको वा यत् कुर्युः कार्यमन्यथा तत् स्वयं नृपतिः कुर्यात् तान् सहस्रं च दण्डयेत् तं
text: If the councillors or the judge decide a case unfairly, that case the King himself shall revise and fine them one thousand.
iast: amātyāḥ prāḍvivāko vā yat kuryuḥ kāryamanyathā tat svayaṃ nṛpatiḥ kuryāt tān sahasraṃ ca daṇḍayet taṃ

sanskrit_iast: brahmahā ca surāpaśca steyī ca gurutalpagaḥ taskaro gurutalpagaḥ ete sarve pṛthag jñeyā mahāpātakino narāḥ
meaning: ब्रह्महा च सुरापश्च स्तेयी च गुरुतल्पगः तस्करो गुरुतल्पगः एते सर्वे पृथग् ज्ञेया महापातकिनो नराः
text: The slayer of aBrāhmaṇa, the drinker of wine, the thief and the violator of the preceptor’s bed,—all these individually should be known as men who have committed heinous crimes.
iast: brahmahā ca surāpaśca steyī ca gurutalpagaḥ taskaro gurutalpagaḥ ete sarve pṛthag jñeyā mahāpātakino narāḥ

sanskrit_iast: caturṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ prāyaścittamakurvatām śārīraṃ dhanasaṃyuktaṃ daṇḍaṃ dharmyaṃ prakalpayet
meaning: चतुर्णामपि चैतेषां प्रायश्चित्तमकुर्वताम् शारीरं धनसंयुक्तं दण्डं धर्म्यं प्रकल्पयेत्
text: Even on all these four, if they do not perform the expiatory penance, the king shall inflict corporal punishment along with fine, in accordance with the law.
iast: caturṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ prāyaścittamakurvatām śārīraṃ dhanasaṃyuktaṃ daṇḍaṃ dharmyaṃ prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: gurutalpe bhagaḥ kāryaḥ surāpāne surādhvajaḥ steye ca śvapadaṃ kāryaṃ brahmahaṇyaśirāḥ pumān taskare śvapadaṃ kāryaṃ
meaning: गुरुतल्पे भगः कार्यः सुरापाने सुराध्वजः स्तेये च श्वपदं कार्यं ब्रह्महण्यशिराः पुमान् तस्करे श्वपदं कार्यं
text: For violating the preceptor’s bed the sign of the female organ shall be branded; for drinking wine that of the tavern; for theft that of the dog’s foot; and for killing aBrāhmaṇathat of a headless man.
iast: gurutalpe bhagaḥ kāryaḥ surāpāne surādhvajaḥ steye ca śvapadaṃ kāryaṃ brahmahaṇyaśirāḥ pumān taskare śvapadaṃ kāryaṃ

sanskrit_iast: asaṃbhojyā hyasaṃyājyā asaṃpāṭhyā 'vivāhinaḥ careyuḥ pṛthivīṃ dīnāḥ sarvadharmabahiṣkṛtāḥ
meaning: असंभोज्या ह्यसंयाज्या असंपाठ्या 'विवाहिनः चरेयुः पृथिवीं दीनाः सर्वधर्मबहिष्कृताः
text: Debarred from entertainments, debarred from sacrifices, debarred from education, excluded from all religious acts, these shall wander over the earth; abject and despised.
iast: asaṃbhojyā hyasaṃyājyā asaṃpāṭhyā 'vivāhinaḥ careyuḥ pṛthivīṃ dīnāḥ sarvadharmabahiṣkṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: jñātisaṃbandhibhistvete tyaktavyāḥ kṛtalakṣaṇāḥ nirdayā nirṃamaskārāstan manoranuśāsanam
meaning: ज्ञातिसंबन्धिभिस्त्वेते त्यक्तव्याः कृतलक्षणाः निर्दया निर्ंअमस्कारास्तन् मनोरनुशासनम्
text: Being branded, these shall be abandoned by Kinsmen and relations, deprived of all sympathy and greetings;—such is the teaching of manu.
iast: jñātisaṃbandhibhistvete tyaktavyāḥ kṛtalakṣaṇāḥ nirdayā nirṃamaskārāstan manoranuśāsanam

sanskrit_iast: prāyaścittaṃ tu kurvāṇāḥ sarvavarṇā yathoditam pūrve varṇā yathauditam nāṅkyā rājñā lalāṭe syurdāpyāstūttamasāhasam
meaning: प्रायश्चित्तं तु कुर्वाणाः सर्ववर्णा यथोदितम् पूर्वे वर्णा यथौदितम् नाङ्क्या राज्ञा ललाटे स्युर्दाप्यास्तूत्तमसाहसम्
text: But men of the senior castes, who perform the expiatory penances, as prescribed, shall not be branded on the forehead by the king; they shall be made to pay the highest amercement.
iast: prāyaścittaṃ tu kurvāṇāḥ sarvavarṇā yathoditam pūrve varṇā yathauditam nāṅkyā rājñā lalāṭe syurdāpyāstūttamasāhasam

sanskrit_iast: āgaḥsu brāhmaṇasyaiva kāryo madhyamasāhasaḥ vivāsyo vā bhavedrāṣṭrāt sadravyaḥ saparicchadaḥ
meaning: आगःसु ब्राह्मणस्यैव कार्यो मध्यमसाहसः विवास्यो वा भवेद्राष्ट्रात् सद्रव्यः सपरिच्छदः
text: For offences committed by theBrāhmaṇathe middle-most amercement shall be inflicted on him; or he shall be banished from the kingdom, along with his goods and chattels.
iast: āgaḥsu brāhmaṇasyaiva kāryo madhyamasāhasaḥ vivāsyo vā bhavedrāṣṭrāt sadravyaḥ saparicchadaḥ

sanskrit_iast: itare kṛtavantastu pāpānyetānyakāmataḥ sarvasvahāramarhanti kāmatastu pravāsanam
meaning: इतरे कृतवन्तस्तु पापान्येतान्यकामतः सर्वस्वहारमर्हन्ति कामतस्तु प्रवासनम्
text: But others who have committed these offences unintentionally, deserve to have the entire property confiscated; and death, in the case of their being intentional.
iast: itare kṛtavantastu pāpānyetānyakāmataḥ sarvasvahāramarhanti kāmatastu pravāsanam

sanskrit_iast: nādadīta nṛpaḥ sādhurmahāpātakino dhanam ādadānastu tatlobhāt tena doṣeṇa lipyate
meaning: नाददीत नृपः साधुर्महापातकिनो धनम् आददानस्तु तत्लोभात् तेन दोषेण लिप्यते
text: The righteous King shall not appropriate the property of the man guilty of a heinous crime; if, through greed, he takes it, he becomes tainted with that guilt.
iast: nādadīta nṛpaḥ sādhurmahāpātakino dhanam ādadānastu tatlobhāt tena doṣeṇa lipyate

sanskrit_iast: apsu praveśya taṃ daṇḍaṃ varuṇāyaupapādayet śrutavṛttaupapanne vā brāhmaṇe pratipādayet
meaning: अप्सु प्रवेश्य तं दण्डं वरुणायौपपादयेत् श्रुतवृत्तौपपन्ने वा ब्राह्मणे प्रतिपादयेत्
text: He shall deposit such property in the water and offer it toVaruṇa, or bestow it on aBrāhmaṇaendowed with learning and character.
iast: apsu praveśya taṃ daṇḍaṃ varuṇāyaupapādayet śrutavṛttaupapanne vā brāhmaṇe pratipādayet

sanskrit_iast: īśo daṇḍasya varuṇo rājñāṃ daṇḍadharo hi saḥ īśaḥ sarvasya jagato brāhmaṇo vedapāragaḥ
meaning: ईशो दण्डस्य वरुणो राज्ञां दण्डधरो हि सः ईशः सर्वस्य जगतो ब्राह्मणो वेदपारगः
text: Varuṇai s the lord of punishment, as he holds the sceptre over the King; while theBrāhmaṇa, well versed in the Veda, is the lord of the whole world.
iast: īśo daṇḍasya varuṇo rājñāṃ daṇḍadharo hi saḥ īśaḥ sarvasya jagato brāhmaṇo vedapāragaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yatra varjayate rājā pāpakṛdbhyo dhanāgamam tatra kālena jāyante mānavā dīrghajīvinaḥ
meaning: यत्र वर्जयते राजा पापकृद्भ्यो धनागमम् तत्र कालेन जायन्ते मानवा दीर्घजीविनः
text: In a country where the King avoids the income of wealth from sinners, men are, in time, born to be long-lived—(246) the crops of husbandmen grow, according as they are sown; children do not die, and no mis-shaped child is born.
iast: yatra varjayate rājā pāpakṛdbhyo dhanāgamam tatra kālena jāyante mānavā dīrghajīvinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: niṣpadyante ca sasyāni yathauptāni viśāṃ pṛthak bālāśca na pramīyante vikṛtaṃ ca na jāyate
meaning: निष्पद्यन्ते च सस्यानि यथौप्तानि विशां पृथक् बालाश्च न प्रमीयन्ते विकृतं च न जायते
text: In a country where the King avoids the income of wealth from sinners, men are, in time, born to be long-lived—(246) the crops of husbandmen grow, according as they are sown; children do not die, and no mis-shaped child is born.
iast: niṣpadyante ca sasyāni yathauptāni viśāṃ pṛthak bālāśca na pramīyante vikṛtaṃ ca na jāyate

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇān bādhamānaṃ tu kāmādavaravarṇajam hanyāccitrairvadhaupāyairudvejanakarairnṛpaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणान् बाधमानं तु कामादवरवर्णजम् हन्याच्चित्रैर्वधौपायैरुद्वेजनकरैर्नृपः
text: If a low-born person intentionally harasses aBrāhmaṇa, the King shall strike him with various terror-striking forms of corporal punishment.
iast: brāhmaṇān bādhamānaṃ tu kāmādavaravarṇajam hanyāccitrairvadhaupāyairudvejanakarairnṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāvānavadhyasya vadhe tāvān vadhyasya mokṣaṇe adharmo nṛpaterdṛṣṭo dharmastu viniyacchataḥ
meaning: यावानवध्यस्य वधे तावान् वध्यस्य मोक्षणे अधर्मो नृपतेर्दृष्टो धर्मस्तु विनियच्छतः
text: The sin incurred by the king in striking one who does not deserve it, is the same as that in acquitting one who deserves to be struck; but merit accrues to him if he chastises justly.
iast: yāvānavadhyasya vadhe tāvān vadhyasya mokṣaṇe adharmo nṛpaterdṛṣṭo dharmastu viniyacchataḥ

sanskrit_iast: udito 'yaṃ vistaraśo mitho vivādamānayoḥ aṣṭādaśasu mārgeṣu vyavahārasya nirṇayaḥ
meaning: उदितो 'यं विस्तरशो मिथो विवादमानयोः अष्टादशसु मार्गेषु व्यवहारस्य निर्णयः
text: This has been expounded at length-investigation of suits between two litigants, bearing upon the eighteen titles of dispute.
iast: udito 'yaṃ vistaraśo mitho vivādamānayoḥ aṣṭādaśasu mārgeṣu vyavahārasya nirṇayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ dharmyāṇi kāryāṇi samyak kurvan mahīpatiḥ deśānalabdhānlipseta labdhāṃśca paripālayet
meaning: एवं धर्म्याणि कार्याणि सम्यक् कुर्वन् महीपतिः देशानलब्धान्लिप्सेत लब्धांश्च परिपालयेत्
text: The king thus duly doing his lawful work, may seek to acquire tracts of land not already acquired, and settle those already acquired.
iast: evaṃ dharmyāṇi kāryāṇi samyak kurvan mahīpatiḥ deśānalabdhānlipseta labdhāṃśca paripālayet

sanskrit_iast: samyakniviṣṭadeśastu kṛtadurgaśca śāstrataḥ kaṇṭakauddharaṇe nityamātiṣṭhedyatnamuttamam
meaning: सम्यक्निविष्टदेशस्तु कृतदुर्गश्च शास्त्रतः कण्टकौद्धरणे नित्यमातिष्ठेद्यत्नमुत्तमम्
text: Having duly settled his kingdom, and having built forts according to the institutes, he shall apply his best efforts to the ‘removal of thorns.’
iast: samyakniviṣṭadeśastu kṛtadurgaśca śāstrataḥ kaṇṭakauddharaṇe nityamātiṣṭhedyatnamuttamam

sanskrit_iast: rakṣanādāryavṛttānāṃ kaṇṭakānāṃ ca śodhanāt narendrāstridivaṃ yānti prajāpālanatatparāḥ
meaning: रक्षनादार्यवृत्तानां कण्टकानां च शोधनात् नरेन्द्रास्त्रिदिवं यान्ति प्रजापालनतत्पराः
text: Kings, intent upon protecting the people, go to heaven, by protecting the Well-behaved and by removing the ‘thorns’
iast: rakṣanādāryavṛttānāṃ kaṇṭakānāṃ ca śodhanāt narendrāstridivaṃ yānti prajāpālanatatparāḥ

sanskrit_iast: aśāsaṃstaskarān yastu baliṃ gṛhṇāti pārthivaḥ tasya prakṣubhyate rāṣṭraṃ svargācca parihīyate
meaning: अशासंस्तस्करान् यस्तु बलिं गृह्णाति पार्थिवः तस्य प्रक्षुभ्यते राष्ट्रं स्वर्गाच्च परिहीयते
text: If a king does not repress thieves and yet receives his taxes, his kingdom becomes perturbed and he falls off from heaven.
iast: aśāsaṃstaskarān yastu baliṃ gṛhṇāti pārthivaḥ tasya prakṣubhyate rāṣṭraṃ svargācca parihīyate

sanskrit_iast: nirbhayaṃ tu bhavedyasya rāṣṭraṃ bāhubalāśritam tasya tadvardhate nityaṃ sicyamāna iva drumaḥ
meaning: निर्भयं तु भवेद्यस्य राष्ट्रं बाहुबलाश्रितम् तस्य तद्वर्धते नित्यं सिच्यमान इव द्रुमः
text: If however his kingdom, resting on the strength of his arms, is secure from danger,—it flourishes constantly, like a well-watered tree.
iast: nirbhayaṃ tu bhavedyasya rāṣṭraṃ bāhubalāśritam tasya tadvardhate nityaṃ sicyamāna iva drumaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dvividhāṃstaskarān vidyāt paradravyāpahārakān prakāśāṃścāprakāśāṃśca cāracakṣurmahīpatiḥ
meaning: द्विविधांस्तस्करान् विद्यात् परद्रव्यापहारकान् प्रकाशांश्चाप्रकाशांश्च चारचक्षुर्महीपतिः
text: The spy-eyed king shall discover the two kinds of thieves who take away the property of other men, those that are ‘open’ and those ‘concealed’
iast: dvividhāṃstaskarān vidyāt paradravyāpahārakān prakāśāṃścāprakāśāṃśca cāracakṣurmahīpatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: prakāśavañcakāsteṣāṃ nānāpaṇyaupajīvinaḥ pracchannavañcakāstvete ye stenāṭavikādayaḥ
meaning: प्रकाशवञ्चकास्तेषां नानापण्यौपजीविनः प्रच्छन्नवञ्चकास्त्वेते ये स्तेनाटविकादयः
text: Of these, the ‘open’ cheats are those who make a living by dealing in various commodities, and the ‘concealed’ cheats are burglars, robbers in forests and so forth.
iast: prakāśavañcakāsteṣāṃ nānāpaṇyaupajīvinaḥ pracchannavañcakāstvete ye stenāṭavikādayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: utkocakāścaupadhikā vañcakāḥ kitavāstathā maṅgalādeśavṛttāśca bhadrāścaikṣaṇikaiḥ saha bhadraprekṣaṇikaiḥ saha
meaning: उत्कोचकाश्चौपधिका वञ्चकाः कितवास्तथा मङ्गलादेशवृत्ताश्च भद्राश्चैक्षणिकैः सह भद्रप्रेक्षणिकैः सह
text: Those who take bribes, dissemblers, cheats and gamblers, fortune-tellers and palmists.
iast: utkocakāścaupadhikā vañcakāḥ kitavāstathā maṅgalādeśavṛttāśca bhadrāścaikṣaṇikaiḥ saha bhadraprekṣaṇikaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: asamyakkāriṇaścaiva mahāmātrāścikitsakāḥ śilpaupacārayuktāśca nipuṇāḥ paṇyayoṣitaḥ
meaning: असम्यक्कारिणश्चैव महामात्राश्चिकित्सकाः शिल्पौपचारयुक्ताश्च निपुणाः पण्ययोषितः
text: Misbehaving High officials and physicians, art-exhibitors, and clever harlots.
iast: asamyakkāriṇaścaiva mahāmātrāścikitsakāḥ śilpaupacārayuktāśca nipuṇāḥ paṇyayoṣitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: evamādīn vijānīyāt prakāśāṃllokakaṇṭakān evamādyān nigūḍhacāriṇaścānyānanāryānāryaliṅginaḥ
meaning: एवमादीन् विजानीयात् प्रकाशांल्लोककण्टकान् एवमाद्यान् निगूढचारिणश्चान्याननार्यानार्यलिङ्गिनः
text: These and others of the same kind one should know as the open ‘thorns’ of the people; and others, who are rogues in the guise of gentlemen, as ‘dissemblers.’
iast: evamādīn vijānīyāt prakāśāṃllokakaṇṭakān evamādyān nigūḍhacāriṇaścānyānanāryānāryaliṅginaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tān viditvā sucaritairgūḍhaistatkarmakāribhiḥ cāraiścānekasaṃsthānaiḥ protsādya vaśamānayet
meaning: तान् विदित्वा सुचरितैर्गूढैस्तत्कर्मकारिभिः चारैश्चानेकसंस्थानैः प्रोत्साद्य वशमानयेत्
text: Having discovered them through well-behaved and disguised men following the same occupations, as also through spies variously disguised, he shall exterminate them and bring them under his control.
iast: tān viditvā sucaritairgūḍhaistatkarmakāribhiḥ cāraiścānekasaṃsthānaiḥ protsādya vaśamānayet

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ doṣānabhikhyāpya sve sve karmaṇi tattvataḥ kurvīta śāsanaṃ rājā samyak sārāparādhataḥ
meaning: तेषां दोषानभिख्याप्य स्वे स्वे कर्मणि तत्त्वतः कुर्वीत शासनं राजा सम्यक् सारापराधतः
text: Having truly proclaimed their crimes in connection with their respective acts, the king shall duly inflict punishment on them, in accordance with their crimes and capacities.
iast: teṣāṃ doṣānabhikhyāpya sve sve karmaṇi tattvataḥ kurvīta śāsanaṃ rājā samyak sārāparādhataḥ

sanskrit_iast: na hi daṇḍādṛte śakyaḥ kartuṃ pāpavinigrahaḥ stenānāṃ pāpabuddhīnāṃ nibhṛtaṃ caratāṃ kṣitau
meaning: न हि दण्डादृते शक्यः कर्तुं पापविनिग्रहः स्तेनानां पापबुद्धीनां निभृतं चरतां क्षितौ
text: The crimes of evil-minded thieves secretly prowling over the earth cannot be suppressed without punishment.
iast: na hi daṇḍādṛte śakyaḥ kartuṃ pāpavinigrahaḥ stenānāṃ pāpabuddhīnāṃ nibhṛtaṃ caratāṃ kṣitau

sanskrit_iast: sabhāprapā 'pūpaśālāveśamadyānnavikrayāḥ catuṣpathāṃścaityavṛkṣāḥ samājāḥ prekṣaṇāni ca
meaning: सभाप्रपा 'पूपशालावेशमद्यान्नविक्रयाः चतुष्पथांश्चैत्यवृक्षाः समाजाः प्रेक्षणानि च
text: Old gardens, forests, shops of artisans, uninhabited houses, groves and gardens;—(265)—these and similar places the king shall cause to be guarded by companies of soldiers, stationary as well as patrolling, and also by spies,—in order to keep away thieves.
iast: sabhāprapā 'pūpaśālāveśamadyānnavikrayāḥ catuṣpathāṃścaityavṛkṣāḥ samājāḥ prekṣaṇāni ca

sanskrit_iast: jīrṇaudyānānyaraṇyāni kārukāveśanāni ca śūnyāni cāpyagārāṇi vanānyupavanāni ca
meaning: जीर्णौद्यानान्यरण्यानि कारुकावेशनानि च शून्यानि चाप्यगाराणि वनान्युपवनानि च
text: Old gardens, forests, shops of artisans, uninhabited houses, groves and gardens;—(265)—these and similar places the king shall cause to be guarded by companies of soldiers, stationary as well as patrolling, and also by spies,—in order to keep away thieves.
iast: jīrṇaudyānānyaraṇyāni kārukāveśanāni ca śūnyāni cāpyagārāṇi vanānyupavanāni ca

sanskrit_iast: evaṃvidhānnṛpo deśān gulmaiḥ sthāvarajaṅgamaiḥ taskarapratiṣedhārthaṃ cāraiścāpyanucārayet
meaning: एवंविधान्नृपो देशान् गुल्मैः स्थावरजङ्गमैः तस्करप्रतिषेधार्थं चारैश्चाप्यनुचारयेत्
text: Old gardens, forests, shops of artisans, uninhabited houses, groves and gardens;—(265)—these and similar places the king shall cause to be guarded by companies of soldiers, stationary as well as patrolling, and also by spies,—in order to keep away thieves.
iast: evaṃvidhānnṛpo deśān gulmaiḥ sthāvarajaṅgamaiḥ taskarapratiṣedhārthaṃ cāraiścāpyanucārayet

sanskrit_iast: tatsahāyairanugatairnānākarmapravedibhiḥ vidyādutsādayeccaiva nipuṇaiḥ pūrvataskaraiḥ
meaning: तत्सहायैरनुगतैर्नानाकर्मप्रवेदिभिः विद्यादुत्सादयेच्चैव निपुणैः पूर्वतस्करैः
text: He shall detect and exterminate them by means of clever reformed thieves, who associate with them, follow them and become apprised of their machinations.
iast: tatsahāyairanugatairnānākarmapravedibhiḥ vidyādutsādayeccaiva nipuṇaiḥ pūrvataskaraiḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhakṣyabhojyaupadeśaiśca brāhmaṇānāṃ ca darśanaiḥ śauryakarmāpadeśaiśca kuryusteṣāṃ samāgamam
meaning: भक्ष्यभोज्यौपदेशैश्च ब्राह्मणानां च दर्शनैः शौर्यकर्मापदेशैश्च कुर्युस्तेषां समागमम्
text: They shall bring them together by means of offers of food and drink, by introducing toBrāhmaṇas, and by exhibition of martial feats.
iast: bhakṣyabhojyaupadeśaiśca brāhmaṇānāṃ ca darśanaiḥ śauryakarmāpadeśaiśca kuryusteṣāṃ samāgamam

sanskrit_iast: ye tatra naupasarpeyurmūlapraṇihitāśca ye tān prasahya nṛpo hanyāt samitrajñātibāndhavān
meaning: ये तत्र नौपसर्पेयुर्मूलप्रणिहिताश्च ये तान् प्रसह्य नृपो हन्यात् समित्रज्ञातिबान्धवान्
text: Those among them who do not come, and those who are careful in their dealings with the older men,—these the king shall attack by force and destroy, along with their friends, kinsmen and relations.
iast: ye tatra naupasarpeyurmūlapraṇihitāśca ye tān prasahya nṛpo hanyāt samitrajñātibāndhavān

sanskrit_iast: na hauḍhena vinā cauraṃ ghātayeddhārmiko nṛpaḥ sahauḍhaṃ saupakaraṇaṃ ghātayedavicārayan
meaning: न हौढेन विना चौरं घातयेद्धार्मिको नृपः सहौढं सौपकरणं घातयेदविचारयन्
text: The righteous king shall not put a thief to death unless caught with the stolen goods; when however one is caught with the stolen goods, and the implements of burglary, he may, without hesitation, put him to death.
iast: na hauḍhena vinā cauraṃ ghātayeddhārmiko nṛpaḥ sahauḍhaṃ saupakaraṇaṃ ghātayedavicārayan

sanskrit_iast: grāmeṣvapi ca ye ke ciccaurāṇāṃ bhaktadāyakāḥ bhāṇḍāvakāśadāścaiva sarvāṃstānapi ghātayet
meaning: ग्रामेष्वपि च ये के चिच्चौराणां भक्तदायकाः भाण्डावकाशदाश्चैव सर्वांस्तानपि घातयेत्
text: He shall also strike all those in a village who supply food for thieves or provide room for the goods.
iast: grāmeṣvapi ca ye ke ciccaurāṇāṃ bhaktadāyakāḥ bhāṇḍāvakāśadāścaiva sarvāṃstānapi ghātayet

sanskrit_iast: rāṣṭreṣu rakṣādhikṛtān sāmantāṃścaiva coditān abhyāghāteṣu madhyasthāñ śiṣyāccaurāniva drutam
meaning: राष्ट्रेषु रक्षाधिकृतान् सामन्तांश्चैव चोदितान् अभ्याघातेषु मध्यस्थाञ् शिष्याच्चौरानिव द्रुतम्
text: If those persons who are entrusted with the work of guarding the realm, and those vassals who have been ordered to assist, should remain neutral during the raids (against thieves), the king shall punish them speedily, like thieves.
iast: rāṣṭreṣu rakṣādhikṛtān sāmantāṃścaiva coditān abhyāghāteṣu madhyasthāñ śiṣyāccaurāniva drutam

sanskrit_iast: yaścāpi dharmasamayāt pracyuto dharmajīvanaḥ daṇḍenaiva tamapyoṣet svakāddharmāddhi vicyutam
meaning: यश्चापि धर्मसमयात् प्रच्युतो धर्मजीवनः दण्डेनैव तमप्योषेत् स्वकाद्धर्माद्धि विच्युतम्
text: If one who subsists on religion deviates from religious ordinances, he shall punish him severely by a fine,—fallen as he is from his duty.
iast: yaścāpi dharmasamayāt pracyuto dharmajīvanaḥ daṇḍenaiva tamapyoṣet svakāddharmāddhi vicyutam

sanskrit_iast: grāmaghāte hitābhaṅge pathi moṣābhidarśane śaktito nābhidhāvanto nirvāsyāḥ saparicchadāḥ
meaning: ग्रामघाते हिताभङ्गे पथि मोषाभिदर्शने शक्तितो नाभिधावन्तो निर्वास्याः सपरिच्छदाः
text: If people do not hasten to assist, to the best of their power, whenever a village is attacked, or a dyke is breaking, or a highway robbery is being committed,—they should be banished along with their chattels.
iast: grāmaghāte hitābhaṅge pathi moṣābhidarśane śaktito nābhidhāvanto nirvāsyāḥ saparicchadāḥ

sanskrit_iast: rājñaḥ kośāpahartr̥̄ṃśca pratikūleṣu ca sthitān prātikūlyeṣvavasthitān ghātayedvividhairdaṇḍairarīṇāṃ caupajāpakān
meaning: राज्ञः कोशापहर्त्र्̥̄ंश्च प्रतिकूलेषु च स्थितान् प्रातिकूल्येष्ववस्थितान् घातयेद्विविधैर्दण्डैररीणां चौपजापकान्
text: Those who rob the king’s treasuries and those who are disaffected towards him, as also those who conspire with his enemies,—the king shall strike with various forms of punishment.
iast: rājñaḥ kośāpahartr̥̄ṃśca pratikūleṣu ca sthitān prātikūlyeṣvavasthitān ghātayedvividhairdaṇḍairarīṇāṃ caupajāpakān

sanskrit_iast: saṃdhiṃ chittvā tu ye cauryaṃ rātrau kurvanti taskarāḥ saṃdhiṃ bhittvā teṣāṃ chittvā nṛpo hastau tīkṣṇe śūle niveśayet
meaning: संधिं छित्त्वा तु ये चौर्यं रात्रौ कुर्वन्ति तस्कराः संधिं भित्त्वा तेषां छित्त्वा नृपो हस्तौ तीक्ष्णे शूले निवेशयेत्
text: If thieves commit thefts at night, after breaking into a house, the king shall cut off their hands and have them impaled on a pointed stake;—(276) on the first conviction he should have two fingers of the cut-purse amputated; on the second a hand and a foot; and on the third he should be put to death.
iast: saṃdhiṃ chittvā tu ye cauryaṃ rātrau kurvanti taskarāḥ saṃdhiṃ bhittvā teṣāṃ chittvā nṛpo hastau tīkṣṇe śūle niveśayet

sanskrit_iast: aṅgulīrgranthibhedasya chedayet prathame grahe dvitīye hastacaraṇau tṛtīye vadhamarhati
meaning: अङ्गुलीर्ग्रन्थिभेदस्य छेदयेत् प्रथमे ग्रहे द्वितीये हस्तचरणौ तृतीये वधमर्हति
text: If thieves commit thefts at night, after breaking into a house, the king shall cut off their hands and have them impaled on a pointed stake;—(276) on the first conviction he should have two fingers of the cut-purse amputated; on the second a hand and a foot; and on the third he should be put to death.
iast: aṅgulīrgranthibhedasya chedayet prathame grahe dvitīye hastacaraṇau tṛtīye vadhamarhati

sanskrit_iast: agnidān bhaktadāṃścaiva tathā śastrāvakāśadān saṃnidhātr̥̄ṃśca moṣasya hanyāccauramivaiśvaraḥ
meaning: अग्निदान् भक्तदांश्चैव तथा शस्त्रावकाशदान् संनिधात्र्̥̄ंश्च मोषस्य हन्याच्चौरमिवैश्वरः
text: The king shall strike like thieves those who provide fire, offer food and supply arms and lodging, as also those who abet their escape.
iast: agnidān bhaktadāṃścaiva tathā śastrāvakāśadān saṃnidhātr̥̄ṃśca moṣasya hanyāccauramivaiśvaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: taḍāgabhedakaṃ hanyādapsu śuddhavadhena vā yadvā 'pi pratisaṃskuryāddāpyastūttamasāhasam
meaning: तडागभेदकं हन्यादप्सु शुद्धवधेन वा यद्वा 'पि प्रतिसंस्कुर्याद्दाप्यस्तूत्तमसाहसम्
text: If a man breaks open a tank, he shall be slain in the water, or by simple form of death; or, he may repair the damage and be made to fay the highest amercement.
iast: taḍāgabhedakaṃ hanyādapsu śuddhavadhena vā yadvā 'pi pratisaṃskuryāddāpyastūttamasāhasam

sanskrit_iast: koṣṭhāgārāyudhāgāradevatāgārabhedakān hastiaśvarathahartr̥̄ṃśca hanyādevāvicārayan
meaning: कोष्ठागारायुधागारदेवतागारभेदकान् हस्तिअश्वरथहर्त्र्̥̄ंश्च हन्यादेवाविचारयन्
text: Those who break into a storehouse, an armoury, or a temple, and those who steal elephants, horses and chariots,—he shall put to death without hesitation.
iast: koṣṭhāgārāyudhāgāradevatāgārabhedakān hastiaśvarathahartr̥̄ṃśca hanyādevāvicārayan

sanskrit_iast: yastu pūrvaniviṣṭasya taḍāgasyaudakaṃ haret āgamaṃ vā 'pyapāṃ bhindyāt sa dāpyaḥ pūrvasāhasam
meaning: यस्तु पूर्वनिविष्टस्य तडागस्यौदकं हरेत् आगमं वा 'प्यपां भिन्द्यात् स दाप्यः पूर्वसाहसम्
text: If a man take away the water of a tank dug in ancient times, or cut off the supply of water,—he shall be made to pay the lowest amercement.
iast: yastu pūrvaniviṣṭasya taḍāgasyaudakaṃ haret āgamaṃ vā 'pyapāṃ bhindyāt sa dāpyaḥ pūrvasāhasam

sanskrit_iast: samutsṛjedrājamārge yastvamedhyamanāpadi sa dvau kārṣāpaṇau dadyādamedhyaṃ cāśu śodhayet
meaning: समुत्सृजेद्राजमार्गे यस्त्वमेध्यमनापदि स द्वौ कार्षापणौ दद्यादमेध्यं चाशु शोधयेत्
text: If one throws filth upon the public road, except in dire necessity,—he shall pay twoKārṣāpaṇasand clean the filth immediately.
iast: samutsṛjedrājamārge yastvamedhyamanāpadi sa dvau kārṣāpaṇau dadyādamedhyaṃ cāśu śodhayet

sanskrit_iast: āpadgato 'tha vā vṛddhā garbhiṇī bāla eva vā paribhāṣaṇamarhanti tacca śodhyamiti sthitiḥ
meaning: आपद्गतो 'थ वा वृद्धा गर्भिणी बाल एव वा परिभाषणमर्हन्ति तच्च शोध्यमिति स्थितिः
text: But a person in urgent necessity, an aged person, a pregnant woman, or a child should be reprimanded and the filth should be cleaned;—such is the law.
iast: āpadgato 'tha vā vṛddhā garbhiṇī bāla eva vā paribhāṣaṇamarhanti tacca śodhyamiti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: cikitsakānāṃ sarveṣāṃ mithyāpracaratāṃ damaḥ amānuṣeṣu prathamo mānuṣeṣu tu madhyamaḥ
meaning: चिकित्सकानां सर्वेषां मिथ्याप्रचरतां दमः अमानुषेषु प्रथमो मानुषेषु तु मध्यमः
text: All physicians dealing dishonestly are liable to punishment; in the case of patients other than human, the lowest, and in that of human patients, the middlemost amercement.
iast: cikitsakānāṃ sarveṣāṃ mithyāpracaratāṃ damaḥ amānuṣeṣu prathamo mānuṣeṣu tu madhyamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṅkramadhvajayaṣṭīnāṃ pratimānāṃ ca bhedakaḥ pratikuryācca tat sarvaṃ pañca dadyātśatāni ca
meaning: सङ्क्रमध्वजयष्टीनां प्रतिमानां च भेदकः प्रतिकुर्याच्च तत् सर्वं पञ्च दद्यात्शतानि च
text: He who destroys a crossing, a flag, a pole or images, shall repair the whole of it and shall pay five hundred.
iast: saṅkramadhvajayaṣṭīnāṃ pratimānāṃ ca bhedakaḥ pratikuryācca tat sarvaṃ pañca dadyātśatāni ca

sanskrit_iast: adūṣitānāṃ dravyāṇāṃ dūṣaṇe bhedane tathā maṇīnāmapavedhe ca daṇḍaḥ prathamasāhasaḥ
meaning: अदूषितानां द्रव्याणां दूषणे भेदने तथा मणीनामपवेधे च दण्डः प्रथमसाहसः
text: For adulterating unadulterated commodities, and for breaking or wrongly boring gems, the punishment shall be the first amercement.
iast: adūṣitānāṃ dravyāṇāṃ dūṣaṇe bhedane tathā maṇīnāmapavedhe ca daṇḍaḥ prathamasāhasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: samairhi viṣamaṃ yastu caredvai mūlyato 'pi vā samāpnuyāddamaṃ pūrvaṃ naro madhyamameva vā
meaning: समैर्हि विषमं यस्तु चरेद्वै मूल्यतो 'पि वा समाप्नुयाद्दमं पूर्वं नरो मध्यममेव वा
text: The man who treats equals as unequals in value should receive the punishment of the first or the middlemost amercement.
iast: samairhi viṣamaṃ yastu caredvai mūlyato 'pi vā samāpnuyāddamaṃ pūrvaṃ naro madhyamameva vā

sanskrit_iast: bandhanāni ca sarvāṇi rājā mārge niveśayet rājamārge duḥkhitā yatra dṛśyeran vikṛtāḥ pāpakāriṇaḥ
meaning: बन्धनानि च सर्वाणि राजा मार्गे निवेशयेत् राजमार्गे दुःखिता यत्र दृश्येरन् विकृताः पापकारिणः
text: The king shall establish prisons all along the public road,—where the suffering and disfigured offenders might be seen.
iast: bandhanāni ca sarvāṇi rājā mārge niveśayet rājamārge duḥkhitā yatra dṛśyeran vikṛtāḥ pāpakāriṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: prākārasya ca bhettāraṃ parikhāṇāṃ ca pūrakam dvārāṇāṃ caiva bhaṅktāraṃ kṣiprameva pravāsayet
meaning: प्राकारस्य च भेत्तारं परिखाणां च पूरकम् द्वाराणां चैव भङ्क्तारं क्षिप्रमेव प्रवासयेत्
text: Him in who breaks the wall, or fills up the ditch, or breaks the gate—he shall instantly banish.
iast: prākārasya ca bhettāraṃ parikhāṇāṃ ca pūrakam dvārāṇāṃ caiva bhaṅktāraṃ kṣiprameva pravāsayet

sanskrit_iast: abhicāreṣu sarveṣu kartavyo dviśato damaḥ mūlakarmaṇi cānāpteḥ kṛtyāsu vividhāsu ca cānāptaiḥ
meaning: अभिचारेषु सर्वेषु कर्तव्यो द्विशतो दमः मूलकर्मणि चानाप्तेः कृत्यासु विविधासु च चानाप्तैः
text: In all cases of malevolent rites, the fine shall be two hundred; as also in a case of magic spell by persons not related, or in those of various kinds of sorcery.
iast: abhicāreṣu sarveṣu kartavyo dviśato damaḥ mūlakarmaṇi cānāpteḥ kṛtyāsu vividhāsu ca cānāptaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: abījavikrayī caiva bījautkṛṣṭā tathaiva ca maryādābhedakaścaiva vikṛtaṃ prāpnuyādvadham
meaning: अबीजविक्रयी चैव बीजौत्कृष्टा तथैव च मर्यादाभेदकश्चैव विकृतं प्राप्नुयाद्वधम्
text: He who sells what is not-seed, or picks out the seed, or transgresses the bounds (of propriety) shall suffer ‘mutilation’ as the penalty.
iast: abījavikrayī caiva bījautkṛṣṭā tathaiva ca maryādābhedakaścaiva vikṛtaṃ prāpnuyādvadham

sanskrit_iast: sarvakaṇṭakapāpiṣṭhaṃ hemakāraṃ tu pārthivaḥ pravartamānamanyāye chedayetlavaśaḥ kṣuraiḥ chedayet khaṇḍaśaḥ kṣuraiḥ
meaning: सर्वकण्टकपापिष्ठं हेमकारं तु पार्थिवः प्रवर्तमानमन्याये छेदयेत्लवशः क्षुरैः छेदयेत् खण्डशः क्षुरैः
text: If the goldsmith, the worst of all ‘thorns,’ behaves dishonestly, the king shall have him cut to pieces with razors.
iast: sarvakaṇṭakapāpiṣṭhaṃ hemakāraṃ tu pārthivaḥ pravartamānamanyāye chedayetlavaśaḥ kṣuraiḥ chedayet khaṇḍaśaḥ kṣuraiḥ

sanskrit_iast: sītādravyāpaharaṇe śastrāṇāmauṣadhasya ca kālamāsādya kāryaṃ ca rājā daṇḍaṃ prakalpayet
meaning: सीताद्रव्यापहरणे शस्त्राणामौषधस्य च कालमासाद्य कार्यं च राजा दण्डं प्रकल्पयेत्
text: For the stealing of agricultural implements, of arms or of medicines, the king shall determine the punishment, after taking into consideration the time and uses.
iast: sītādravyāpaharaṇe śastrāṇāmauṣadhasya ca kālamāsādya kāryaṃ ca rājā daṇḍaṃ prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: svāmy 'mātyau puraṃ rāṣṭraṃ kośadaṇḍau suhṛt tathā sapta prakṛtayo hyetāḥ saptāṅgaṃ rājyamucyate
meaning: स्वाम्य् 'मात्यौ पुरं राष्ट्रं कोशदण्डौ सुहृत् तथा सप्त प्रकृतयो ह्येताः सप्ताङ्गं राज्यमुच्यते
text: The master and the minister, the capital city, the people, the treasury and the army, and the ally,—these are the seven constituents; and the kingdom is described as having ‘seven limbs.’
iast: svāmy 'mātyau puraṃ rāṣṭraṃ kośadaṇḍau suhṛt tathā sapta prakṛtayo hyetāḥ saptāṅgaṃ rājyamucyate

sanskrit_iast: saptānāṃ prakṛtīnāṃ tu rājyasyāsāṃ yathākramam pūrvaṃ pūrvaṃ gurutaraṃ jānīyādvyasanaṃ mahat
meaning: सप्तानां प्रकृतीनां तु राज्यस्यासां यथाक्रमम् पूर्वं पूर्वं गुरुतरं जानीयाद्व्यसनं महत्
text: Among these seven constituents of the kingdom stated in due order, injury to each preceding one is to be regarded as more serious.
iast: saptānāṃ prakṛtīnāṃ tu rājyasyāsāṃ yathākramam pūrvaṃ pūrvaṃ gurutaraṃ jānīyādvyasanaṃ mahat

sanskrit_iast: saptāṅgasyaiha rājyasya viṣṭabdhasya tridaṇḍavat anyonyaguṇavaiśeṣyātna kiṃ cidatiricyate
meaning: सप्ताङ्गस्यैह राज्यस्य विष्टब्धस्य त्रिदण्डवत् अन्योन्यगुणवैशेष्यात्न किं चिदतिरिच्यते
text: Yet in the kingdom consisting of the ‘seven limbs’ interlaced like the ‘triple staff,’—since their qualities are mutually helpful,—no one of them is superior.
iast: saptāṅgasyaiha rājyasya viṣṭabdhasya tridaṇḍavat anyonyaguṇavaiśeṣyātna kiṃ cidatiricyate

sanskrit_iast: teṣu teṣu tu kṛtyeṣu tat tadaṅgaṃ viśiṣyate yena yat sādhyate kāryaṃ tat tasmiṃśreṣṭhamucyate
meaning: तेषु तेषु तु कृत्येषु तत् तदङ्गं विशिष्यते येन यत् साध्यते कार्यं तत् तस्मिंश्रेष्ठमुच्यते
text: Each ‘limb’ is particularly qualified for the fulfilment of a distinct purpose; and hence each is declared to be the most important in reference to that purpose which is fulfilled by its means.
iast: teṣu teṣu tu kṛtyeṣu tat tadaṅgaṃ viśiṣyate yena yat sādhyate kāryaṃ tat tasmiṃśreṣṭhamucyate

sanskrit_iast: cāreṇautsāhayogena kriyayaiva ca karmaṇām svaśaktiṃ paraśaktiṃ ca nityaṃ vidyātmahīpatiḥ vidyāt parātmanoḥ
meaning: चारेणौत्साहयोगेन क्रिययैव च कर्मणाम् स्वशक्तिं परशक्तिं च नित्यं विद्यात्महीपतिः विद्यात् परात्मनोः
text: The king shall constantly ascertain his own and his enemy’s strength through spies, through display of energy and also through the actual carrying out of undertakings.
iast: cāreṇautsāhayogena kriyayaiva ca karmaṇām svaśaktiṃ paraśaktiṃ ca nityaṃ vidyātmahīpatiḥ vidyāt parātmanoḥ

sanskrit_iast: pīḍanāni ca sarvāṇi vyasanāni tathaiva ca ārabheta tataḥ kāryaṃ sañcintya gurulāghavam
meaning: पीडनानि च सर्वाणि व्यसनानि तथैव च आरभेत ततः कार्यं सञ्चिन्त्य गुरुलाघवम्
text: The king shall begin operations after having taken into consideration all calamities and vices, and their relative importance.
iast: pīḍanāni ca sarvāṇi vyasanāni tathaiva ca ārabheta tataḥ kāryaṃ sañcintya gurulāghavam

sanskrit_iast: ārabhetaiva karmāṇi śrāntaḥ śrāntaḥ punaḥ punaḥ karmāṇyārabhamāṇaṃ hi puruṣaṃ śrīrniṣevate
meaning: आरभेतैव कर्माणि श्रान्तः श्रान्तः पुनः पुनः कर्माण्यारभमाणं हि पुरुषं श्रीर्निषेवते
text: Tired and tired, over again he should begin his operations; for fortune favours the man who undertakes operations.
iast: ārabhetaiva karmāṇi śrāntaḥ śrāntaḥ punaḥ punaḥ karmāṇyārabhamāṇaṃ hi puruṣaṃ śrīrniṣevate

sanskrit_iast: kṛtaṃ tretāyugaṃ caiva dvāparaṃ kalireva ca rājño vṛttāni sarvāṇi rājā hi yugamucyate
meaning: कृतं त्रेतायुगं चैव द्वापरं कलिरेव च राज्ञो वृत्तानि सर्वाणि राजा हि युगमुच्यते
text: The actions of the king constitute the ‘Kṛta’, the ‘Tretā’, the ‘Dvāpara’ and the ‘Kali’ cycles; as it is the king that is called the ‘cycle.’
iast: kṛtaṃ tretāyugaṃ caiva dvāparaṃ kalireva ca rājño vṛttāni sarvāṇi rājā hi yugamucyate

sanskrit_iast: kaliḥ prasupto bhavati sa jāgraddvāparaṃ yugam karmasvabhyudyatastretā vicaraṃstu kṛtaṃ yugam
meaning: कलिः प्रसुप्तो भवति स जाग्रद्द्वापरं युगम् कर्मस्वभ्युद्यतस्त्रेता विचरंस्तु कृतं युगम्
text: Asleep, he represents ‘Kali;’ awake, the ‘Dvāpara’ cycle; ready to act, the ‘Tretā’; and actually acting, the ‘Kṛta’ cycle.
iast: kaliḥ prasupto bhavati sa jāgraddvāparaṃ yugam karmasvabhyudyatastretā vicaraṃstu kṛtaṃ yugam

sanskrit_iast: indrasyārkasya vāyośca yamasya varuṇasya ca candrasyāgneḥ pṛthivyāśca tejovṛttaṃ nṛpaścaret
meaning: इन्द्रस्यार्कस्य वायोश्च यमस्य वरुणस्य च चन्द्रस्याग्नेः पृथिव्याश्च तेजोवृत्तं नृपश्चरेत्
text: The king shall emulate the energetic activity ofIndra, ofArka, ofVāyu, ofYama, ofVaruṇa, ofChandra, ofAgniand ofPṛthvī.
iast: indrasyārkasya vāyośca yamasya varuṇasya ca candrasyāgneḥ pṛthivyāśca tejovṛttaṃ nṛpaścaret

sanskrit_iast: vārṣikāṃścaturo māsān yathaindro 'bhipravarṣati tathā 'bhivarṣet svaṃ rāṣṭraṃ kāmairindravrataṃ caran
meaning: वार्षिकांश्चतुरो मासान् यथैन्द्रो 'भिप्रवर्षति तथा 'भिवर्षेत् स्वं राष्ट्रं कामैरिन्द्रव्रतं चरन्
text: As Indra showers rain during four months of the year, so shall the king, acting like Indra, shower benefits on his people.
iast: vārṣikāṃścaturo māsān yathaindro 'bhipravarṣati tathā 'bhivarṣet svaṃ rāṣṭraṃ kāmairindravrataṃ caran

sanskrit_iast: aṣṭau māsān yathā 'dityastoyaṃ harati raśmibhiḥ tathā haret karaṃ rāṣṭrātnityamarkavrataṃ hi tat
meaning: अष्टौ मासान् यथा 'दित्यस्तोयं हरति रश्मिभिः तथा हरेत् करं राष्ट्रात्नित्यमर्कव्रतं हि तत्
text: Just as during eight months,Ādityadraws up water with his rays, even so the king shall draw taxes from the people,—this being the function of arka.
iast: aṣṭau māsān yathā 'dityastoyaṃ harati raśmibhiḥ tathā haret karaṃ rāṣṭrātnityamarkavrataṃ hi tat

sanskrit_iast: praviśya sarvabhūtāni yathā carati mārutaḥ tathā cāraiḥ praveṣṭavyaṃ vratametaddhi mārutam
meaning: प्रविश्य सर्वभूतानि यथा चरति मारुतः तथा चारैः प्रवेष्टव्यं व्रतमेतद्धि मारुतम्
text: As Yama, at the approach of the proper time, restrains both friends and enemies, even so shall all men be restrained by the king; this is the function of Yama.
iast: praviśya sarvabhūtāni yathā carati mārutaḥ tathā cāraiḥ praveṣṭavyaṃ vratametaddhi mārutam

sanskrit_iast: yathā yamaḥ priyadveṣyau prāpte kāle niyacchati tathā rājñā niyantavyāḥ prajāstaddhi yamavratam
meaning: यथा यमः प्रियद्वेष्यौ प्राप्ते काले नियच्छति तथा राज्ञा नियन्तव्याः प्रजास्तद्धि यमव्रतम्
text: As Yama, at the approach of the proper time, restrains both friends and enemies, even so shall all men be restrained by the king; this is the function of Yama.
iast: yathā yamaḥ priyadveṣyau prāpte kāle niyacchati tathā rājñā niyantavyāḥ prajāstaddhi yamavratam

sanskrit_iast: varuṇena yathā pāśairbaddha evābhidṛśyate tathā pāpānnigṛhṇīyādvratametaddhi vāruṇam
meaning: वरुणेन यथा पाशैर्बद्ध एवाभिदृश्यते तथा पापान्निगृह्णीयाद्व्रतमेतद्धि वारुणम्
text: As one is always found bound up with ropes byVaruṇa, even so shall the king punish the wicked; this is the function ofVaruṇa.
iast: varuṇena yathā pāśairbaddha evābhidṛśyate tathā pāpānnigṛhṇīyādvratametaddhi vāruṇam

sanskrit_iast: paripūrṇaṃ yathā candraṃ dṛṣṭvā hṛṣyanti mānavāḥ tathā prakṛtayo yasmin sa cāndravratiko nṛpaḥ
meaning: परिपूर्णं यथा चन्द्रं दृष्ट्वा हृष्यन्ति मानवाः तथा प्रकृतयो यस्मिन् स चान्द्रव्रतिको नृपः
text: The people rejoice at seeing the full moon; the king at whose appearance the people rejoice in that manner is a king who fulfills the functions of the moon.
iast: paripūrṇaṃ yathā candraṃ dṛṣṭvā hṛṣyanti mānavāḥ tathā prakṛtayo yasmin sa cāndravratiko nṛpaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pratāpayuktastejasvī nityaṃ syāt pāpakarmasu duṣṭasāmantahiṃsraśca tadāgneyaṃ vrataṃ smṛtam
meaning: प्रतापयुक्तस्तेजस्वी नित्यं स्यात् पापकर्मसु दुष्टसामन्तहिंस्रश्च तदाग्नेयं व्रतं स्मृतम्
text: He shall always be endowed with brilliant energy and ardent in his wrath against offenders; he shall exterminate his disaffected vassals; this is the function of agni.
iast: pratāpayuktastejasvī nityaṃ syāt pāpakarmasu duṣṭasāmantahiṃsraśca tadāgneyaṃ vrataṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: yathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni dharā dhārayate samam tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni bibhrataḥ pārthivaṃ vratam
meaning: यथा सर्वाणि भूतानि धरा धारयते समम् तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि बिभ्रतः पार्थिवं व्रतम्
text: As the earth sustains all beings equally, so does the king support all beings; and this is the function ofPṛthivī.
iast: yathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni dharā dhārayate samam tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni bibhrataḥ pārthivaṃ vratam

sanskrit_iast: etairupāyairanyaiśca yukto nityamatandritaḥ stenān rājā nigṛhṇīyāt svarāṣṭre para eva ca
meaning: एतैरुपायैरन्यैश्च युक्तो नित्यमतन्द्रितः स्तेनान् राजा निगृह्णीयात् स्वराष्ट्रे पर एव च
text: Betaking himself to these and other methods, the king, ever alert, shall restrain thieves in his own realm as well as in that of others.
iast: etairupāyairanyaiśca yukto nityamatandritaḥ stenān rājā nigṛhṇīyāt svarāṣṭre para eva ca

sanskrit_iast: parāmapyāpadaṃ prāpto brāhmaṇānna prakopayet te hyenaṃ kupitā hanyuḥ sadyaḥ sabalavāhanam
meaning: परामप्यापदं प्राप्तो ब्राह्मणान्न प्रकोपयेत् ते ह्येनं कुपिता हन्युः सद्यः सबलवाहनम्
text: Even when fallen in the deepest distress, the king shall not provoke theBrāhmaṇas; for if provoked, they would ruin him, along with his army and conveyances.
iast: parāmapyāpadaṃ prāpto brāhmaṇānna prakopayet te hyenaṃ kupitā hanyuḥ sadyaḥ sabalavāhanam

sanskrit_iast: yaiḥ kṛtaḥ sarvabhakṣyo 'gnirapeyaśca mahodadhiḥ sarvabhakṣo kṣayī cāpyāyitaḥ somaḥ ko na naśyet prakopya tān
meaning: यैः कृतः सर्वभक्ष्यो 'ग्निरपेयश्च महोदधिः सर्वभक्षो क्षयी चाप्यायितः सोमः को न नश्येत् प्रकोप्य तान्
text: Who could escape ruin after having provoked those by whom fire was rendered all-devouring, by whom the ocean was made undrinkable and by whom the moon was made to wax and wane?
iast: yaiḥ kṛtaḥ sarvabhakṣyo 'gnirapeyaśca mahodadhiḥ sarvabhakṣo kṣayī cāpyāyitaḥ somaḥ ko na naśyet prakopya tān

sanskrit_iast: lokānanyān sṛjeyurye lokapālāṃśca kopitāḥ devān kuryuradevāṃśca kaḥ kṣiṇvaṃstān samṛdhnuyāt
meaning: लोकानन्यान् सृजेयुर्ये लोकपालांश्च कोपिताः देवान् कुर्युरदेवांश्च कः क्षिण्वंस्तान् समृध्नुयात्
text: Who could prosper after injuring those who, on being provoked, would create other worlds and other guardians of the regions, and who would make the Gods cease to be Gods?
iast: lokānanyān sṛjeyurye lokapālāṃśca kopitāḥ devān kuryuradevāṃśca kaḥ kṣiṇvaṃstān samṛdhnuyāt

sanskrit_iast: yānupāśritya tiṣṭhanti lokā devāśca sarvadā brahma caiva dhanaṃ yeṣāṃ ko hiṃsyāt tāñjijīviṣuḥ
meaning: यानुपाश्रित्य तिष्ठन्ति लोका देवाश्च सर्वदा ब्रह्म चैव धनं येषां को हिंस्यात् ताञ्जिजीविषुः
text: What man, desirous of living, would injure them, depending on whom the world and the gods exist, and whose wealth consists of the Veda?
iast: yānupāśritya tiṣṭhanti lokā devāśca sarvadā brahma caiva dhanaṃ yeṣāṃ ko hiṃsyāt tāñjijīviṣuḥ

sanskrit_iast: avidvāṃścaiva vidvāṃśca brāhmaṇo daivataṃ mahat praṇītaścāpraṇītaśca yathā 'gnirdaivataṃ mahat
meaning: अविद्वांश्चैव विद्वांश्च ब्राह्मणो दैवतं महत् प्रणीतश्चाप्रणीतश्च यथा 'ग्निर्दैवतं महत्
text: Learned or unlearned, theBrāhmaṇais a great divinity; just as consecrated or unconsecrated, the fire is a great divinity.
iast: avidvāṃścaiva vidvāṃśca brāhmaṇo daivataṃ mahat praṇītaścāpraṇītaśca yathā 'gnirdaivataṃ mahat

sanskrit_iast: śmaśāneṣvapi tejasvī pāvako naiva duṣyati hūyamānaśca yajñeṣu bhūya evābhivardhate
meaning: श्मशानेष्वपि तेजस्वी पावको नैव दुष्यति हूयमानश्च यज्ञेषु भूय एवाभिवर्धते
text: Even though in the cremation-ground, the brilliant fire is not defiled, and it flourishes again when libations are poured unto it at sacrifices.
iast: śmaśāneṣvapi tejasvī pāvako naiva duṣyati hūyamānaśca yajñeṣu bhūya evābhivardhate

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ yadyapyaniṣṭeṣu vartante sarvakarmasu sarvathā brāhmaṇāḥ pūjyāḥ paramaṃ daivataṃ hi tat
meaning: एवं यद्यप्यनिष्टेषु वर्तन्ते सर्वकर्मसु सर्वथा ब्राह्मणाः पूज्याः परमं दैवतं हि तत्
text: Even though in the cremation-ground, the brilliant fire is not defiled, and it flourishes again when libations are poured unto it at sacrifices.
iast: evaṃ yadyapyaniṣṭeṣu vartante sarvakarmasu sarvathā brāhmaṇāḥ pūjyāḥ paramaṃ daivataṃ hi tat

sanskrit_iast: kṣatrasyātipravṛddhasya brāhmaṇān prati sarvaśaḥ brahmaiva saṃniyantṛ syāt kṣatraṃ hi brahmasaṃbhavam
meaning: क्षत्रस्यातिप्रवृद्धस्य ब्राह्मणान् प्रति सर्वशः ब्रह्मैव संनियन्तृ स्यात् क्षत्रं हि ब्रह्मसंभवम्
text: When theKṣatriyashall become too over-bearing in every way towardsBrāhmaṇas, theBrāhmaṇahimself shall be their restraining influence; as theKṣatriyahas his source in theBrāhmaṇa.—320
iast: kṣatrasyātipravṛddhasya brāhmaṇān prati sarvaśaḥ brahmaiva saṃniyantṛ syāt kṣatraṃ hi brahmasaṃbhavam

sanskrit_iast: adbhyo 'gnirbrahmataḥ kṣatramaśmano lohamutthitam teṣāṃ sarvatragaṃ tejaḥ svāsu yoniṣu śāmyati
meaning: अद्भ्यो 'ग्निर्ब्रह्मतः क्षत्रमश्मनो लोहमुत्थितम् तेषां सर्वत्रगं तेजः स्वासु योनिषु शाम्यति
text: Fire sprang from water, theKṣatriyafrom theBrāhmaṇa, and iron from stone; the all-penetrating force of each of these becomes extinguished in its source.
iast: adbhyo 'gnirbrahmataḥ kṣatramaśmano lohamutthitam teṣāṃ sarvatragaṃ tejaḥ svāsu yoniṣu śāmyati

sanskrit_iast: nābrahma kṣatraṃ ṛdhnoti nākṣatraṃ brahma vardhate brahma kṣatraṃ ca saṃpṛktamiha cāmutra vardhate
meaning: नाब्रह्म क्षत्रं ऋध्नोति नाक्षत्रं ब्रह्म वर्धते ब्रह्म क्षत्रं च संपृक्तमिह चामुत्र वर्धते
text: TheKṣatriyaflourishes not without theBrāhmaṇa; theBrāhmaṇaprospers not without theKṣatriya; theBrāhmaṇaand theKṣatriya, when united, prosper here as also in the other world.
iast: nābrahma kṣatraṃ ṛdhnoti nākṣatraṃ brahma vardhate brahma kṣatraṃ ca saṃpṛktamiha cāmutra vardhate

sanskrit_iast: dattvā dhanaṃ tu viprebhyaḥ sarvadaṇḍasamutthitam putre rājyaṃ samāsṛjya kurvīta prāyaṇaṃ raṇe samāsādya
meaning: दत्त्वा धनं तु विप्रेभ्यः सर्वदण्डसमुत्थितम् पुत्रे राज्यं समासृज्य कुर्वीत प्रायणं रणे समासाद्य
text: Bestowing his wealth, obtained from all kinds of fines, uponBrāhmaṇas, and making over the kingdom to his son, the king shall bring about his departure in battle.
iast: dattvā dhanaṃ tu viprebhyaḥ sarvadaṇḍasamutthitam putre rājyaṃ samāsṛjya kurvīta prāyaṇaṃ raṇe samāsādya

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ caran sadā yukto rājadharmeṣu pārthivaḥ hiteṣu caiva lokasya sarvān bhṛtyānniyojayet iteṣu caiva lokebhyaḥ
meaning: एवं चरन् सदा युक्तो राजधर्मेषु पार्थिवः हितेषु चैव लोकस्य सर्वान् भृत्यान्नियोजयेत् इतेषु चैव लोकेभ्यः
text: Acting in this manner, and ever intent upon his Kingly Duties, the king shall order all his servants to work for the good of the people.
iast: evaṃ caran sadā yukto rājadharmeṣu pārthivaḥ hiteṣu caiva lokasya sarvān bhṛtyānniyojayet iteṣu caiva lokebhyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: eṣo 'khilaḥ karmavidhirukto rājñaḥ sanātanaḥ imaṃ karmavidhiṃ vidyāt kramaśo vaiśyaśūdrayoḥ
meaning: एषो 'खिलः कर्मविधिरुक्तो राज्ञः सनातनः इमं कर्मविधिं विद्यात् क्रमशो वैश्यशूद्रयोः
text: Thus has been expounded the whole of the eternal Law relating to the Duties of the King; the law relating to theVaiśyaand theŚūdrarespectively should be understood to be as follows.
iast: eṣo 'khilaḥ karmavidhirukto rājñaḥ sanātanaḥ imaṃ karmavidhiṃ vidyāt kramaśo vaiśyaśūdrayoḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyastu kṛtasaṃskāraḥ kṛtvā dāraparigraham vārtāyāṃ nityayuktaḥ syāt paśūnāṃ caiva rakṣaṇe
meaning: वैश्यस्तु कृतसंस्कारः कृत्वा दारपरिग्रहम् वार्तायां नित्ययुक्तः स्यात् पशूनां चैव रक्षणे
text: After having his sacraments performed, the Vaiśya shall take a wife and apply himself entirely to agriculture and the tending of catlle.
iast: vaiśyastu kṛtasaṃskāraḥ kṛtvā dāraparigraham vārtāyāṃ nityayuktaḥ syāt paśūnāṃ caiva rakṣaṇe

sanskrit_iast: prajāpatirhi vaiśyāya sṛṣṭvā paridade paśūn brāhmaṇāya ca rājñe ca sarvāḥ paridade prajāḥ
meaning: प्रजापतिर्हि वैश्याय सृष्ट्वा परिददे पशून् ब्राह्मणाय च राज्ञे च सर्वाः परिददे प्रजाः
text: Prajāpati, having created cattle, made them over to the Vaiśya; while to theBrāhmaṇāand theKṣatriyahe made over all creatures.
iast: prajāpatirhi vaiśyāya sṛṣṭvā paridade paśūn brāhmaṇāya ca rājñe ca sarvāḥ paridade prajāḥ

sanskrit_iast: na ca vaiśyasya kāmaḥ syānna rakṣeyaṃ paśūniti vaiśye caicchati nānyena rakṣitavyāḥ kathaṃ cana
meaning: न च वैश्यस्य कामः स्यान्न रक्षेयं पशूनिति वैश्ये चैच्छति नान्येन रक्षितव्याः कथं चन
text: The Vaiśya shall never conceive the wish—‘I will not tend cattle;’ and so long as the Vaiśya is willing, they should not be tended by any one else.
iast: na ca vaiśyasya kāmaḥ syānna rakṣeyaṃ paśūniti vaiśye caicchati nānyena rakṣitavyāḥ kathaṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: maṇimuktāpravālānāṃ lohānāṃ tāntavasya ca gandhānāṃ ca rasānāṃ ca vidyādarghabalābalam
meaning: मणिमुक्ताप्रवालानां लोहानां तान्तवस्य च गन्धानां च रसानां च विद्यादर्घबलाबलम्
text: He shall find out the relative value of gems, pearls, corals, metals, woven cloths, perfumes and condiments.
iast: maṇimuktāpravālānāṃ lohānāṃ tāntavasya ca gandhānāṃ ca rasānāṃ ca vidyādarghabalābalam

sanskrit_iast: bījānāmuptividca syāt kṣetradoṣaguṇasya ca mānayogaṃ ca jānīyāt tulāyogāṃśca sarvaśaḥ
meaning: बीजानामुप्तिविद्च स्यात् क्षेत्रदोषगुणस्य च मानयोगं च जानीयात् तुलायोगांश्च सर्वशः
text: He should be acquainted with the manner of sowing seeds, with the good and bad qualities of the soil; he should know all kinds of weights and measures.
iast: bījānāmuptividca syāt kṣetradoṣaguṇasya ca mānayogaṃ ca jānīyāt tulāyogāṃśca sarvaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sārāsāraṃ ca bhāṇḍānāṃ deśānāṃ ca guṇāguṇān lābhālābhaṃ ca paṇyānāṃ paśūnāṃ parivardhanam
meaning: सारासारं च भाण्डानां देशानां च गुणागुणान् लाभालाभं च पण्यानां पशूनां परिवर्धनम्
text: Also the excellences and defects of commodities, the advantages and disadvantages relating to countries, the profit and loss on merchandise and also cattle-breeding.
iast: sārāsāraṃ ca bhāṇḍānāṃ deśānāṃ ca guṇāguṇān lābhālābhaṃ ca paṇyānāṃ paśūnāṃ parivardhanam

sanskrit_iast: bhṛtyānāṃ ca bhṛtiṃ vidyādbhāṣāśca vividhā nṛṇām dravyāṇāṃ sthānayogāṃśca krayavikrayameva ca
meaning: भृत्यानां च भृतिं विद्याद्भाषाश्च विविधा नृणाम् द्रव्याणां स्थानयोगांश्च क्रयविक्रयमेव च
text: He shall know also the wages of servants, the several languages of men, the manner of keeping goods, and also their purchase and sale.
iast: bhṛtyānāṃ ca bhṛtiṃ vidyādbhāṣāśca vividhā nṛṇām dravyāṇāṃ sthānayogāṃśca krayavikrayameva ca

sanskrit_iast: dharmeṇa ca dravyavṛddhāvātiṣṭhedyatnamuttamam dadyācca sarvabhūtānāmannameva prayatnataḥ
meaning: धर्मेण च द्रव्यवृद्धावातिष्ठेद्यत्नमुत्तमम् दद्याच्च सर्वभूतानामन्नमेव प्रयत्नतः
text: He shall put forth his best efforts towards increasing his property in a righteous manner; and he shall zealously give food to all beings.
iast: dharmeṇa ca dravyavṛddhāvātiṣṭhedyatnamuttamam dadyācca sarvabhūtānāmannameva prayatnataḥ

sanskrit_iast: viprāṇāṃ vedaviduṣāṃ gṛhasthānāṃ yaśasvinām śuśrūṣaiva tu śūdrasya dharmo naiśreyasaḥ paraḥ param
meaning: विप्राणां वेदविदुषां गृहस्थानां यशस्विनाम् शुश्रूषैव तु शूद्रस्य धर्मो नैश्रेयसः परः परम्
text: For theŚūdrathe highest duty conducive to his best welfare is to attend upon suchBrāhmaṇahouse-holders as are learned in the Vedas and famous.
iast: viprāṇāṃ vedaviduṣāṃ gṛhasthānāṃ yaśasvinām śuśrūṣaiva tu śūdrasya dharmo naiśreyasaḥ paraḥ param

sanskrit_iast: śucirutkṛṣṭaśuśrūṣurmṛduvāganahaṅkṛtaḥ brāhmaṇādiāśrayo nityamutkṛṣṭāṃ jātimaśnute brāhmaṇāpāśrayo
meaning: शुचिरुत्कृष्टशुश्रूषुर्मृदुवागनहङ्कृतः ब्राह्मणादिआश्रयो नित्यमुत्कृष्टां जातिमश्नुते ब्राह्मणापाश्रयो
text: If he is pure, attendant upon his superiors, of gentle speech, free from pride, and always dependent upon theBrāhmaṇa,—he attains a higher caste.
iast: śucirutkṛṣṭaśuśrūṣurmṛduvāganahaṅkṛtaḥ brāhmaṇādiāśrayo nityamutkṛṣṭāṃ jātimaśnute brāhmaṇāpāśrayo

sanskrit_iast: eṣo 'nāpadi varṇānāmuktaḥ karmavidhiḥ śubhaḥ āpadyapi hi yasteṣāṃ kramaśastannibodhata
meaning: एषो 'नापदि वर्णानामुक्तः कर्मविधिः शुभः आपद्यपि हि यस्तेषां क्रमशस्तन्निबोधत
iast: eṣo 'nāpadi varṇānāmuktaḥ karmavidhiḥ śubhaḥ āpadyapi hi yasteṣāṃ kramaśastannibodhata

sanskrit_iast: adhīyīraṃstrayo varṇāḥ svakarmasthā dvijātayaḥ prabrūyādbrāhmaṇastveṣāṃ naitarāviti niścayaḥ
meaning: अधीयीरंस्त्रयो वर्णाः स्वकर्मस्था द्विजातयः प्रब्रूयाद्ब्राह्मणस्त्वेषां नैतराविति निश्चयः
text: The three twice-born castes, devoted to their duties, shall study; but of these theBrāhmaṇaalone shall expound it, not the other two; such is the established law.
iast: adhīyīraṃstrayo varṇāḥ svakarmasthā dvijātayaḥ prabrūyādbrāhmaṇastveṣāṃ naitarāviti niścayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāṃ brāhmaṇo vidyādvṛttyupāyān yathāvidhi prabrūyāditarebhyaśca svayaṃ caiva tathā bhavet
meaning: सर्वेषां ब्राह्मणो विद्याद्वृत्त्युपायान् यथाविधि प्रब्रूयादितरेभ्यश्च स्वयं चैव तथा भवेत्
text: TheBrāhmaṇashould know the means of livelihood for all men; he shall duly expound them to the others and himself do accordingly.
iast: sarveṣāṃ brāhmaṇo vidyādvṛttyupāyān yathāvidhi prabrūyāditarebhyaśca svayaṃ caiva tathā bhavet

sanskrit_iast: vaiśeṣyāt prakṛtiśraiṣṭhyātniyamasya ca dhāraṇāt saṃskārasya viśeṣācca varṇānāṃ brāhmaṇaḥ prabhuḥ
meaning: वैशेष्यात् प्रकृतिश्रैष्ठ्यात्नियमस्य च धारणात् संस्कारस्य विशेषाच्च वर्णानां ब्राह्मणः प्रभुः
text: TheBrāhmaṇais the lord of the castes, on account of his distinctive qualities, of the superiority of his origin, of his observance of restrictive rules, of the peculiar character of his sanctification.
iast: vaiśeṣyāt prakṛtiśraiṣṭhyātniyamasya ca dhāraṇāt saṃskārasya viśeṣācca varṇānāṃ brāhmaṇaḥ prabhuḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇaḥ kṣatriyo vaiśyastrayo varṇā dvijātayaḥ caturtha ekajātistu śūdro nāsti tu pañcamaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणः क्षत्रियो वैश्यस्त्रयो वर्णा द्विजातयः चतुर्थ एकजातिस्तु शूद्रो नास्ति तु पञ्चमः
text: TheBrāhmaṇa, theKṣatriyaand theVaiśyaare the three twice-born castes; the fourth is the one caste,Śūdra; there is no fifth.
iast: brāhmaṇaḥ kṣatriyo vaiśyastrayo varṇā dvijātayaḥ caturtha ekajātistu śūdro nāsti tu pañcamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarvavarṇeṣu tulyāsu patnīṣvakṣatayoniṣu ānulomyena saṃbhūtā jātyā jñeyāsta eva te
meaning: सर्ववर्णेषु तुल्यासु पत्नीष्वक्षतयोनिषु आनुलोम्येन संभूता जात्या ज्ञेयास्त एव ते
text: Among all castes, those only who are born of consorts wedded in the natural order, as virgins of equal status, are to be regarded as the same (as their father).
iast: sarvavarṇeṣu tulyāsu patnīṣvakṣatayoniṣu ānulomyena saṃbhūtā jātyā jñeyāsta eva te

sanskrit_iast: strīṣvanantarajātāsu dvijairutpāditān sutān sadṛśāneva tānāhurmātṛdoṣavigarhitān
meaning: स्त्रीष्वनन्तरजातासु द्विजैरुत्पादितान् सुतान् सदृशानेव तानाहुर्मातृदोषविगर्हितान्
text: The sons begotten by twice-born men on wives of the next lower castes, they declare to be equal, tainted as they are by the defect of their mothers.
iast: strīṣvanantarajātāsu dvijairutpāditān sutān sadṛśāneva tānāhurmātṛdoṣavigarhitān

sanskrit_iast: anantarāsu jātānāṃ vidhireṣa sanātanaḥ dvyekāntarāsu jātānāṃ dharmyaṃ vidyādimaṃ vidhim
meaning: अनन्तरासु जातानां विधिरेष सनातनः द्व्येकान्तरासु जातानां धर्म्यं विद्यादिमं विधिम्
text: Such is the eternal law relating to those born of wives of the next lower castes; know this (following) to be the right rule pertaining to those born of wives two or three degrees lower.
iast: anantarāsu jātānāṃ vidhireṣa sanātanaḥ dvyekāntarāsu jātānāṃ dharmyaṃ vidyādimaṃ vidhim

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇādvaiśyakanyāyāmambaṣṭho nāma jāyate niṣādaḥ śūdrakanyāyāṃ yaḥ pāraśava ucyate
meaning: ब्राह्मणाद्वैश्यकन्यायामम्बष्ठो नाम जायते निषादः शूद्रकन्यायां यः पारशव उच्यते
text: From theBrāhmaṇaon aVaiśyamaiden is born the ‘Ambaṣṭha’ and on aŚūdramaiden the ‘Niṣāda,’ who is called ‘Pārośora.’
iast: brāhmaṇādvaiśyakanyāyāmambaṣṭho nāma jāyate niṣādaḥ śūdrakanyāyāṃ yaḥ pāraśava ucyate

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyātśūdrakanyāyāṃ krūrācāravihāravān kṣatraśūdravapurjanturugro nāma prajāyate
meaning: क्षत्रियात्शूद्रकन्यायां क्रूराचारविहारवान् क्षत्रशूद्रवपुर्जन्तुरुग्रो नाम प्रजायते
text: From theKṣatriyaon aŚūdramaiden is born a being called ‘Ugra,’ of the stuff of theKṣatriyaandŚūdra, cruel in his deeds and dealings.
iast: kṣatriyātśūdrakanyāyāṃ krūrācāravihāravān kṣatraśūdravapurjanturugro nāma prajāyate

sanskrit_iast: viprasya triṣu varṇeṣu nṛpatervarṇayordvayoḥ vaiśyasya varṇe caikasmin ṣaḍete 'pasadāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: विप्रस्य त्रिषु वर्णेषु नृपतेर्वर्णयोर्द्वयोः वैश्यस्य वर्णे चैकस्मिन् षडेते 'पसदाः स्मृताः
text: Children of theBrāhmaṇafrom the three lower castes, of theKṣatriyafrom the two lower castes, and of the Vaiśya from the one lower caste,—these six have been declared to be ‘lowborn.’
iast: viprasya triṣu varṇeṣu nṛpatervarṇayordvayoḥ vaiśyasya varṇe caikasmin ṣaḍete 'pasadāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyādviprakanyāyāṃ sūto bhavati jātitaḥ vaiśyān māgadhavaidehau rājaviprāṅganāsutau
meaning: क्षत्रियाद्विप्रकन्यायां सूतो भवति जातितः वैश्यान् मागधवैदेहौ राजविप्राङ्गनासुतौ
text: One born from theKṣatriyaon theBrāhmaṇamaiden is ‘Sūta’ in caste; and the sons born on theKṣatriyaand theBrāhmaṇamaiden from the vaiśya are ‘Māgadha’ and ‘Vaideha’ respectively.
iast: kṣatriyādviprakanyāyāṃ sūto bhavati jātitaḥ vaiśyān māgadhavaidehau rājaviprāṅganāsutau

sanskrit_iast: śūdrādāyogavaḥ kṣattā caṇḍālaścādhamo nṛṇām vaiśyarājanyaviprāsu jāyante varṇasaṅkarāḥ
meaning: शूद्रादायोगवः क्षत्ता चण्डालश्चाधमो नृणाम् वैश्यराजन्यविप्रासु जायन्ते वर्णसङ्कराः
text: From theŚūdraon theVaiśya, theKṣatriyaand theBrāhmaṇamaiden are born the mixed castes, ‘Āyogava,’Kṣattṛand the ‘Caṇḍāla,’ the lowest of men.
iast: śūdrādāyogavaḥ kṣattā caṇḍālaścādhamo nṛṇām vaiśyarājanyaviprāsu jāyante varṇasaṅkarāḥ

sanskrit_iast: ekāntare tvānulomyādambaṣṭhaugrau yathā smṛtau kṣattṛvaidehakau tadvat prātilomye 'pi janmani
meaning: एकान्तरे त्वानुलोम्यादम्बष्ठौग्रौ यथा स्मृतौ क्षत्तृवैदेहकौ तद्वत् प्रातिलोम्ये 'पि जन्मनि
text: As the ‘Ambaṣṭha’ and the ‘ugra’ are born in the ‘natural order’ from a woman two degrees removed,—even so have been declared to be the ‘Kṣattṛ’ and the ‘Vaideha,’ though born in the ‘inverse order.’
iast: ekāntare tvānulomyādambaṣṭhaugrau yathā smṛtau kṣattṛvaidehakau tadvat prātilomye 'pi janmani

sanskrit_iast: putrā ye 'nantarastrījāḥ krameṇauktā dvijanmanām tānanantaranāmnastu mātṛdoṣāt pracakṣate
meaning: पुत्रा ये 'नन्तरस्त्रीजाः क्रमेणौक्ता द्विजन्मनाम् ताननन्तरनाम्नस्तु मातृदोषात् प्रचक्षते
text: The sons of twice-born men from women of the next lower caste, who have been enunciated in due order, are called by the name of the lower caste, on account of the taint attaching to their mothers.
iast: putrā ye 'nantarastrījāḥ krameṇauktā dvijanmanām tānanantaranāmnastu mātṛdoṣāt pracakṣate

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇādugrakanyāyāmāvṛto nāma jāyate ābhīro 'mbaṣṭhakanyāyāmāyogavyāṃ tu dhigvaṇaḥ
meaning: ब्राह्मणादुग्रकन्यायामावृतो नाम जायते आभीरो 'म्बष्ठकन्यायामायोगव्यां तु धिग्वणः
text: One born from the ‘Niṣāda’ on theŚūdrawoman is a ‘Pukkasa’ by caste; but one born from theŚūdraon the ‘Niṣāda’ woman is called the ‘Kukkaṭaka’.
iast: brāhmaṇādugrakanyāyāmāvṛto nāma jāyate ābhīro 'mbaṣṭhakanyāyāmāyogavyāṃ tu dhigvaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: āyogavaśca kṣattā ca caṇḍālaścādhamo nṛṇām prātilomyena jāyante śūdrādapasadāstrayaḥ
meaning: आयोगवश्च क्षत्ता च चण्डालश्चाधमो नृणाम् प्रातिलोम्येन जायन्ते शूद्रादपसदास्त्रयः
text: One born from the ‘Niṣāda’ on theŚūdrawoman is a ‘Pukkasa’ by caste; but one born from theŚūdraon the ‘Niṣāda’ woman is called the ‘Kukkaṭaka’.
iast: āyogavaśca kṣattā ca caṇḍālaścādhamo nṛṇām prātilomyena jāyante śūdrādapasadāstrayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyān māgadhavaidehau kṣatriyāt sūta eva tu pratīpamete jāyante pare 'pyapasadāstrayaḥ
meaning: वैश्यान् मागधवैदेहौ क्षत्रियात् सूत एव तु प्रतीपमेते जायन्ते परे 'प्यपसदास्त्रयः
text: One born from the ‘Niṣāda’ on theŚūdrawoman is a ‘Pukkasa’ by caste; but one born from theŚūdraon the ‘Niṣāda’ woman is called the ‘Kukkaṭaka’.
iast: vaiśyān māgadhavaidehau kṣatriyāt sūta eva tu pratīpamete jāyante pare 'pyapasadāstrayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: jāto niṣādātśūdrāyāṃ jātyā bhavati pukkasaḥ śūdrāj jāto niṣādyāṃ tu sa vai kukkuṭakaḥ smṛtaḥ
meaning: जातो निषादात्शूद्रायां जात्या भवति पुक्कसः शूद्राज् जातो निषाद्यां तु स वै कुक्कुटकः स्मृतः
text: One born from the ‘Niṣāda’ on theŚūdrawoman is a ‘Pukkasa’ by caste; but one born from theŚūdraon the ‘Niṣāda’ woman is called the ‘Kukkaṭaka’.
iast: jāto niṣādātśūdrāyāṃ jātyā bhavati pukkasaḥ śūdrāj jāto niṣādyāṃ tu sa vai kukkuṭakaḥ smṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣatturjātastathaugrāyāṃ śvapāka iti kīrtyate vaidehakena tvambaṣṭhyāmutpanno veṇa ucyate
meaning: क्षत्तुर्जातस्तथौग्रायां श्वपाक इति कीर्त्यते वैदेहकेन त्वम्बष्ठ्यामुत्पन्नो वेण उच्यते
text: One born from the ‘Kṣattṛ’ on the ‘Ugrā’ woman is called ‘Śvapāka;’ and one begotten by the ‘Vaideha’ on the ‘Ambaṣṭha’ woman is called ‘Veṇa.’
iast: kṣatturjātastathaugrāyāṃ śvapāka iti kīrtyate vaidehakena tvambaṣṭhyāmutpanno veṇa ucyate

sanskrit_iast: dvijātayaḥ savarṇāsu janayantyavratāṃstu yān tān sāvitrīparibhraṣṭān vrātyāniti vinirdiśet
meaning: द्विजातयः सवर्णासु जनयन्त्यव्रतांस्तु यान् तान् सावित्रीपरिभ्रष्टान् व्रात्यानिति विनिर्दिशेत्
text: The sons that the twice-born men beget on wives of equal caste, but who, not fulfilling their sacred duties, become excluded from theSāvitrī—should be designated as ‘Vrātyas’ (apostates).
iast: dvijātayaḥ savarṇāsu janayantyavratāṃstu yān tān sāvitrīparibhraṣṭān vrātyāniti vinirdiśet

sanskrit_iast: vrātyāt tu jāyate viprāt pāpātmā bhūrjakaṇṭakaḥ bhṛjjakaṇṭakaḥ āvantyavāṭadhānau ca puṣpadhaḥ śaikha eva ca
meaning: व्रात्यात् तु जायते विप्रात् पापात्मा भूर्जकण्टकः भृज्जकण्टकः आवन्त्यवाटधानौ च पुष्पधः शैख एव च
text: From the ‘Apostate’Brāhmaṇais born the evil- natured ‘Bhṛjjakaṇṭaka,’ theĀvantya, the ‘Vāṭadhāna’ the ‘Puṣpadha,’ and the ‘Śaikha.’
iast: vrātyāt tu jāyate viprāt pāpātmā bhūrjakaṇṭakaḥ bhṛjjakaṇṭakaḥ āvantyavāṭadhānau ca puṣpadhaḥ śaikha eva ca

sanskrit_iast: jhallo mallaśca rājanyādvrātyātnicchivireva ca vrātyātlicchavireva ca naṭaśca karaṇaścaiva khaso draviḍa eva ca
meaning: झल्लो मल्लश्च राजन्याद्व्रात्यात्निच्छिविरेव च व्रात्यात्लिच्छविरेव च नटश्च करणश्चैव खसो द्रविड एव च
text: And from the ‘Apostate’ Vaiśya is born the ‘Sudhanvan’ the ‘Ācārya,’ the ‘Karuṣa,’ the ‘Vijanman,’ the ‘Maitra’ and the ‘Sāttvata.’
iast: jhallo mallaśca rājanyādvrātyātnicchivireva ca vrātyātlicchavireva ca naṭaśca karaṇaścaiva khaso draviḍa eva ca

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyāt tu jāyate vrātyāt sudhanvā 'cārya eva ca kāruṣaśca vijanmā ca maitraḥ sātvata eva ca
meaning: वैश्यात् तु जायते व्रात्यात् सुधन्वा 'चार्य एव च कारुषश्च विजन्मा च मैत्रः सात्वत एव च
text: And from the ‘Apostate’ Vaiśya is born the ‘Sudhanvan’ the ‘Ācārya,’ the ‘Karuṣa,’ the ‘Vijanman,’ the ‘Maitra’ and the ‘Sāttvata.’
iast: vaiśyāt tu jāyate vrātyāt sudhanvā 'cārya eva ca kāruṣaśca vijanmā ca maitraḥ sātvata eva ca

sanskrit_iast: vyabhicāreṇa varṇānāmavedyāvedanena ca svakarmaṇāṃ ca tyāgena jāyante varṇasaṅkarāḥ
meaning: व्यभिचारेण वर्णानामवेद्यावेदनेन च स्वकर्मणां च त्यागेन जायन्ते वर्णसङ्कराः
text: ‘Confused castes’ are produced by infidelity among the castes, by the marrying of women unfit for marriage, and by the neglect of one’s duties.
iast: vyabhicāreṇa varṇānāmavedyāvedanena ca svakarmaṇāṃ ca tyāgena jāyante varṇasaṅkarāḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṅkīrṇayonayo ye tu pratilomānulomajāḥ anyonyavyatiṣaktāśca tān pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ
meaning: सङ्कीर्णयोनयो ये तु प्रतिलोमानुलोमजाः अन्योन्यव्यतिषक्ताश्च तान् प्रवक्ष्याम्यशेषतः
text: I am going to describe those persons of mixed origin who are born in the natural and in the inverse order and are mutually connected.
iast: saṅkīrṇayonayo ye tu pratilomānulomajāḥ anyonyavyatiṣaktāśca tān pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: sūto vaidehakaścaiva caṇḍālaśca narādhamaḥ māgadhaḥ tathā 'yogava eva ca kṣatrajātiśca kṣattṛjātiśca
meaning: सूतो वैदेहकश्चैव चण्डालश्च नराधमः मागधः तथा 'योगव एव च क्षत्रजातिश्च क्षत्तृजातिश्च
text: सूतो वैदेहकश्चैव चण्डालश्च नराधमः ।मागधः तथाऽयोगव एव च क्षत्रजातिश्च ?? ॥ २६ ॥
iast: sūto vaidehakaścaiva caṇḍālaśca narādhamaḥ māgadhaḥ tathā 'yogava eva ca kṣatrajātiśca kṣattṛjātiśca

sanskrit_iast: ete ṣaṭ sadṛśān varṇāñjanayanti svayoniṣu mātṛjātyāṃ prasūyante pravārāsu ca yoniṣu mātṛjātyāḥ
meaning: एते षट् सदृशान् वर्णाञ्जनयन्ति स्वयोनिषु मातृजात्यां प्रसूयन्ते प्रवारासु च योनिषु मातृजात्याः
text: These six beget similar castes on women belonging to the same caste as themselves; so also those belonging to the mother’s caste procreate (on women of the same caste), as also on women of higher castes.
iast: ete ṣaṭ sadṛśān varṇāñjanayanti svayoniṣu mātṛjātyāṃ prasūyante pravārāsu ca yoniṣu mātṛjātyāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathā trayāṇāṃ varṇānāṃ dvayorātmā 'sya jāyate ānantaryāt svayonyāṃ tu tathā bāhyeṣvapi kramāt kramaḥ
meaning: यथा त्रयाणां वर्णानां द्वयोरात्मा 'स्य जायते आनन्तर्यात् स्वयोन्यां तु तथा बाह्येष्वपि क्रमात् क्रमः
text: As to one his own alter-ego is born from two out of the three nearest castes, as also from his own caste,—even so is the order among those out of the outer circle.
iast: yathā trayāṇāṃ varṇānāṃ dvayorātmā 'sya jāyate ānantaryāt svayonyāṃ tu tathā bāhyeṣvapi kramāt kramaḥ

sanskrit_iast: te cāpi bāhyān subahūṃstato 'pyadhikadūṣitān parasparasya dāreṣu janayanti vigarhitān
meaning: ते चापि बाह्यान् सुबहूंस्ततो 'प्यधिकदूषितान् परस्परस्य दारेषु जनयन्ति विगर्हितान्
text: Those also beget on each other’s wives several ‘alien’ sons, greatly tainted and despised.
iast: te cāpi bāhyān subahūṃstato 'pyadhikadūṣitān parasparasya dāreṣu janayanti vigarhitān

sanskrit_iast: yathaiva śūdro brāhmaṇyāṃ bāhyaṃ jantuṃ prasūyate tathā bāhyataraṃ bāhyaścāturvarṇye prasūyate
meaning: यथैव शूद्रो ब्राह्मण्यां बाह्यं जन्तुं प्रसूयते तथा बाह्यतरं बाह्यश्चातुर्वर्ण्ये प्रसूयते
text: As theŚūdrabegets an ‘alien’ being on aBrāhmaṇawoman,—even so an alien propagates on females of the four castes, a still more alien being.
iast: yathaiva śūdro brāhmaṇyāṃ bāhyaṃ jantuṃ prasūyate tathā bāhyataraṃ bāhyaścāturvarṇye prasūyate

sanskrit_iast: pratikūlaṃ vartamānā bāhyā bāhyatarān punaḥ hīnā hīnān prasūyante varṇān pañcadaśaiva tu
meaning: प्रतिकूलं वर्तमाना बाह्या बाह्यतरान् पुनः हीना हीनान् प्रसूयन्ते वर्णान् पञ्चदशैव तु
text: Aliens behaving discordantly, beget fifteen castes, still more alien, disgraced and not disgraced.
iast: pratikūlaṃ vartamānā bāhyā bāhyatarān punaḥ hīnā hīnān prasūyante varṇān pañcadaśaiva tu

sanskrit_iast: prasādhanaupacārajñamadāsaṃ dāsajīvanam dāsyajīvinaṃ sairindhraṃ vāgurāvṛttiṃ sūte dasyurayogave sairandhraṃ
meaning: प्रसाधनौपचारज्ञमदासं दासजीवनम् दास्यजीविनं सैरिन्ध्रं वागुरावृत्तिं सूते दस्युरयोगवे सैरन्ध्रं
text: The ‘Dasyu’ begets on the ‘Āyogava,’ the ‘Sairandhra,’ skilled in toilet and attendance, who, though not a slave, makes his living like a slave, and also lives by catching animals.
iast: prasādhanaupacārajñamadāsaṃ dāsajīvanam dāsyajīvinaṃ sairindhraṃ vāgurāvṛttiṃ sūte dasyurayogave sairandhraṃ

sanskrit_iast: maitreyakaṃ tu vaideho mādhūkaṃ saṃprasūyate nr̥̄n praśaṃsatyajasraṃ yo ghaṇṭātāḍo 'ruṇaudaye
meaning: मैत्रेयकं तु वैदेहो माधूकं संप्रसूयते न्र्̥̄न् प्रशंसत्यजस्रं यो घण्टाताडो 'रुणौदये
text: But the ‘Vaideha’ begets theMadhūka-like‘Maitreyaka,’ who constantly praises men, and serves as the bell-ringer at sunrise.
