iast: maitreyakaṃ tu vaideho mādhūkaṃ saṃprasūyate nr̥̄n praśaṃsatyajasraṃ yo ghaṇṭātāḍo 'ruṇaudaye

sanskrit_iast: niṣādo mārgavaṃ sūte dāsaṃ naukarmajīvinam kaivartamiti yaṃ prāhurāryāvartanivāsinaḥ
meaning: निषादो मार्गवं सूते दासं नौकर्मजीविनम् कैवर्तमिति यं प्राहुरार्यावर्तनिवासिनः
text: The ‘Niṣāda’ begets the ‘Mārgava’ or ‘Dāsa,’ who subsists by working the boat, and whom the inhabitants ofĀryāvartacall ‘Kaivarta.’
iast: niṣādo mārgavaṃ sūte dāsaṃ naukarmajīvinam kaivartamiti yaṃ prāhurāryāvartanivāsinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mṛtavastrabhṛtsvnārīṣu garhitānnāśanāsu ca 'nāryāṣu bhavantyāyogavīṣvete jātihīnāḥ pṛthak trayaḥ
meaning: मृतवस्त्रभृत्स्व्नारीषु गर्हितान्नाशनासु च 'नार्याषु भवन्त्यायोगवीष्वेते जातिहीनाः पृथक् त्रयः
text: These three, devoid of caste, are severally born from ‘Āyogava’ women, who wear the clothes of the dead, are ignoble, and eat despised food.
iast: mṛtavastrabhṛtsvnārīṣu garhitānnāśanāsu ca 'nāryāṣu bhavantyāyogavīṣvete jātihīnāḥ pṛthak trayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kārāvaro niṣādāt tu carmakāraḥ prasūyate carmakāraṃ vaidehikādandhramedau bahirgrāmapratiśrayau
meaning: कारावरो निषादात् तु चर्मकारः प्रसूयते चर्मकारं वैदेहिकादन्ध्रमेदौ बहिर्ग्रामप्रतिश्रयौ
text: From the ‘Niṣāda’ is born of the ‘Kārāvara,’ who works in leather; and from the ‘Vaiḍehaka’ the ‘Andhra’ and the ‘Meda,’ who have their dwellings outside the village.
iast: kārāvaro niṣādāt tu carmakāraḥ prasūyate carmakāraṃ vaidehikādandhramedau bahirgrāmapratiśrayau

sanskrit_iast: caṇḍālāt pāṇḍusopākastvaksāravyavahāravān āhiṇḍiko niṣādena vaidehyāmeva jāyate
meaning: चण्डालात् पाण्डुसोपाकस्त्वक्सारव्यवहारवान् आहिण्डिको निषादेन वैदेह्यामेव जायते
text: On the ‘Vaideha’ woman from the ‘Caṇḍāla’ is born the ‘Paṇḍusopāka’ who deals in bamboos; as also the ‘Āhiṇḍika’ from the ‘Niṣāda.’
iast: caṇḍālāt pāṇḍusopākastvaksāravyavahāravān āhiṇḍiko niṣādena vaidehyāmeva jāyate

sanskrit_iast: caṇḍālena tu sopāko mūlavyasanavṛttimān pukkasyāṃ jāyate pāpaḥ sadā sajjanagarhitaḥ pulkasyāṃ
meaning: चण्डालेन तु सोपाको मूलव्यसनवृत्तिमान् पुक्कस्यां जायते पापः सदा सज्जनगर्हितः पुल्कस्यां
text: By the ‘Caṇḍāla,’ on the ‘Pukkasa’ woman is begotten the ‘Sopāka,’ whose livelihood consists of death, and who are wicked and despised by good people.
iast: caṇḍālena tu sopāko mūlavyasanavṛttimān pukkasyāṃ jāyate pāpaḥ sadā sajjanagarhitaḥ pulkasyāṃ

sanskrit_iast: niṣādastrī tu caṇḍālāt putramantyāvasāyinam śmaśānagocaraṃ sūte bāhyānāmapi garhitam
meaning: निषादस्त्री तु चण्डालात् पुत्रमन्त्यावसायिनम् श्मशानगोचरं सूते बाह्यानामपि गर्हितम्
text: The ‘Niṣāda’ woman bears to the ‘Caṇḍāla’ the son called ‘Antyāvasāyin,’ working in the cremation-ground, despised even by out-casts.
iast: niṣādastrī tu caṇḍālāt putramantyāvasāyinam śmaśānagocaraṃ sūte bāhyānāmapi garhitam

sanskrit_iast: saṅkare jātayastvetāḥ pitṛmātṛpradarśitāḥ prachannā vā prakāśā vā veditavyāḥ svakarmabhiḥ
meaning: सङ्करे जातयस्त्वेताः पितृमातृप्रदर्शिताः प्रछन्ना वा प्रकाशा वा वेदितव्याः स्वकर्मभिः
text: These castes, proceeding from mixtures, have been described in relation to their fathers and mothers; and whether manifest or unmanifest, they may be known by their occupations.
iast: saṅkare jātayastvetāḥ pitṛmātṛpradarśitāḥ prachannā vā prakāśā vā veditavyāḥ svakarmabhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: svajātijānantarajāḥ ṣaṭ sutā dvijadharmiṇaḥ śūdrāṇāṃ tu sadharmāṇaḥ sarve 'padhvaṃsajāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: स्वजातिजानन्तरजाः षट् सुता द्विजधर्मिणः शूद्राणां तु सधर्माणः सर्वे 'पध्वंसजाः स्मृताः
text: Six sons born or women of the same caste and of those of the next lower castes partake of the character of ‘twice-born’ persons. But all those born of violation have been declared to be of the nature ofŚūdras.
iast: svajātijānantarajāḥ ṣaṭ sutā dvijadharmiṇaḥ śūdrāṇāṃ tu sadharmāṇaḥ sarve 'padhvaṃsajāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: tapobījaprabhāvaistu te gacchanti yuge yuge utkarṣaṃ cāpakarṣaṃ ca manuṣyeṣviha janmataḥ
meaning: तपोबीजप्रभावैस्तु ते गच्छन्ति युगे युगे उत्कर्षं चापकर्षं च मनुष्येष्विह जन्मतः
text: By the force of austerities and the seed they attain higher or lower rank among men, through birth, cycle after cycle.
iast: tapobījaprabhāvaistu te gacchanti yuge yuge utkarṣaṃ cāpakarṣaṃ ca manuṣyeṣviha janmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: śanakaistu kriyālopādimāḥ kṣatriyajātayaḥ vṛṣalatvaṃ gatā loke brāhmaṇādarśanena ca brāhmaṇātikrameṇa ca
meaning: शनकैस्तु क्रियालोपादिमाः क्षत्रियजातयः वृषलत्वं गता लोके ब्राह्मणादर्शनेन च ब्राह्मणातिक्रमेण च
text: But by the omission of the sacred rites, and also by their neglect ofBrāhmaṇas, the followingKṣatriyacastes have gradually sunk to the position of the low-born.
iast: śanakaistu kriyālopādimāḥ kṣatriyajātayaḥ vṛṣalatvaṃ gatā loke brāhmaṇādarśanena ca brāhmaṇātikrameṇa ca

sanskrit_iast: pauṇḍrakāścauḍradraviḍāḥ kāmbojā yavanāḥ śakāḥ puṇḍrakāścoḍadraviḍāḥ pāradāpahlavāścīnāḥ kirātā daradāḥ khaśāḥ
meaning: पौण्ड्रकाश्चौड्रद्रविडाः काम्बोजा यवनाः शकाः पुण्ड्रकाश्चोडद्रविडाः पारदापह्लवाश्चीनाः किराता दरदाः खशाः
text: ThePuṇḍrakas, theCoḍas[=Cauḍras], theDraviḍas, theKāmbojas, the Yavanas, theŚākas, thePāradas, the Pahlavas, theCīnas, theKirātas, the Daradas and the Khaśas.
iast: pauṇḍrakāścauḍradraviḍāḥ kāmbojā yavanāḥ śakāḥ puṇḍrakāścoḍadraviḍāḥ pāradāpahlavāścīnāḥ kirātā daradāḥ khaśāḥ

sanskrit_iast: mukhabāhūrupadjānāṃ yā loke jātayo bahiḥ mlecchavācaścāryavācaḥ sarve te dasyavaḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: मुखबाहूरुपद्जानां या लोके जातयो बहिः म्लेच्छवाचश्चार्यवाचः सर्वे ते दस्यवः स्मृताः
text: All those races of the world which are outside the pale of the people ‘born of the mouth, the arms, the thighs and the feet,’—speaking the ‘barbaric’ or the ‘refined’ language—are called ‘Dasyu.’
iast: mukhabāhūrupadjānāṃ yā loke jātayo bahiḥ mlecchavācaścāryavācaḥ sarve te dasyavaḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: ye dvijānāmapasadā ye cāpadhvaṃsajāḥ smṛtāḥ te ninditairvartayeyurdvijānāmeva karmabhiḥ
meaning: ये द्विजानामपसदा ये चापध्वंसजाः स्मृताः ते निन्दितैर्वर्तयेयुर्द्विजानामेव कर्मभिः
text: The base-born sons of the ‘twice-born,’ as also those who have been declared to be ‘born of transgression,’ shall subsist by lowly services of ‘twice-born’ persons.
iast: ye dvijānāmapasadā ye cāpadhvaṃsajāḥ smṛtāḥ te ninditairvartayeyurdvijānāmeva karmabhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: sūtānāmaśvasārathyamambaṣṭhānāṃ cikitsanam vaidehakānāṃ strīkāryaṃ māgadhānāṃ vaṇikpathaḥ
meaning: सूतानामश्वसारथ्यमम्बष्ठानां चिकित्सनम् वैदेहकानां स्त्रीकार्यं मागधानां वणिक्पथः
text: ForSūtas, the management of horses and chariots; forAmbaṣṭhas, the art of healing; forVaidehakas, the service of women; and forMāgadhas, trade.
iast: sūtānāmaśvasārathyamambaṣṭhānāṃ cikitsanam vaidehakānāṃ strīkāryaṃ māgadhānāṃ vaṇikpathaḥ

sanskrit_iast: matsyaghāto niṣādānāṃ tvaṣṭistvāyogavasya ca medāndhracuñcumadgūnāmāraṇyapaśuhiṃsanam
meaning: मत्स्यघातो निषादानां त्वष्टिस्त्वायोगवस्य च मेदान्ध्रचुञ्चुमद्गूनामारण्यपशुहिंसनम्
text: ForNiṣādas, the killing of fish; forĀyogavas, carpentry; and for medas, andhras,Cuñcusand Madgus, the killing of wild animals.
iast: matsyaghāto niṣādānāṃ tvaṣṭistvāyogavasya ca medāndhracuñcumadgūnāmāraṇyapaśuhiṃsanam

sanskrit_iast: kṣattryugrapukkasānāṃ tu bilaukovadhabandhanam dhigvaṇānāṃ carmakāryaṃ veṇānāṃ bhāṇḍavādanam
meaning: क्षत्त्र्युग्रपुक्कसानां तु बिलौकोवधबन्धनम् धिग्वणानां चर्मकार्यं वेणानां भाण्डवादनम्
text: ForKṣattṛs, Ugras and Pukkasas, the killing and catching of animals living underground; forDhigvaṇas, work in leather; and forVeṇasthe beating of drums.
iast: kṣattryugrapukkasānāṃ tu bilaukovadhabandhanam dhigvaṇānāṃ carmakāryaṃ veṇānāṃ bhāṇḍavādanam

sanskrit_iast: caityadrumaśmaśāneṣu śaileṣūpavaneṣu ca vaseyurete vijñātā vartayantaḥ svakarmabhiḥ
meaning: चैत्यद्रुमश्मशानेषु शैलेषूपवनेषु च वसेयुरेते विज्ञाता वर्तयन्तः स्वकर्मभिः
text: Near worshipped trees and cremation-grounds, on hills and in groves, these shall dwell, duly marked, subsisting by their respective occupations.
iast: caityadrumaśmaśāneṣu śaileṣūpavaneṣu ca vaseyurete vijñātā vartayantaḥ svakarmabhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: caṇḍālaśvapacānāṃ tu bahirgrāmāt pratiśrayaḥ apapātrāśca kartavyā dhanameṣāṃ śvagardabham
meaning: चण्डालश्वपचानां तु बहिर्ग्रामात् प्रतिश्रयः अपपात्राश्च कर्तव्या धनमेषां श्वगर्दभम्
text: The dwelling ofCaṇḍālasand Śvapacas shall be outside the village; they shall be made ‘Apapātra,’ and their wealth shall consist of dogs and donkeys.
iast: caṇḍālaśvapacānāṃ tu bahirgrāmāt pratiśrayaḥ apapātrāśca kartavyā dhanameṣāṃ śvagardabham

sanskrit_iast: vāsāṃsi mṛtacailāni bhinnabhāṇḍeṣu bhojanam kārṣṇāyasamalaṅkāraḥ parivrajyā ca nityaśaḥ
meaning: वासांसि मृतचैलानि भिन्नभाण्डेषु भोजनम् कार्ष्णायसमलङ्कारः परिव्रज्या च नित्यशः
text: The clothes of dead bodies shall be their dress; they shall eat in broken dishes; their ornaments shall be of iron, and they shall be constantly wandering.
iast: vāsāṃsi mṛtacailāni bhinnabhāṇḍeṣu bhojanam kārṣṇāyasamalaṅkāraḥ parivrajyā ca nityaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na taiḥ samayamanvicchet puruṣo dharmamācaran vyavahāro mithasteṣāṃ vivāhaḥ sadṛśaiḥ saha
meaning: न तैः समयमन्विच्छेत् पुरुषो धर्ममाचरन् व्यवहारो मिथस्तेषां विवाहः सदृशैः सह
text: One who follows the law shall not seek intercourse with them; their transactions shall be among themselves and their marriages with their equals.
iast: na taiḥ samayamanvicchet puruṣo dharmamācaran vyavahāro mithasteṣāṃ vivāhaḥ sadṛśaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: annameṣāṃ parādhīnaṃ deyaṃ syādbhinnabhājane rātrau na vicareyuste grāmeṣu nagareṣu ca
meaning: अन्नमेषां पराधीनं देयं स्याद्भिन्नभाजने रात्रौ न विचरेयुस्ते ग्रामेषु नगरेषु च
text: Their food should be given to them, through others, and in a broken dish; they shall not wander about in villages or cities during the night.
iast: annameṣāṃ parādhīnaṃ deyaṃ syādbhinnabhājane rātrau na vicareyuste grāmeṣu nagareṣu ca

sanskrit_iast: divā careyuḥ kāryārthaṃ cihnitā rājaśāsanaiḥ abāndhavaṃ śavaṃ caiva nirhareyuriti sthitiḥ
meaning: दिवा चरेयुः कार्यार्थं चिह्निता राजशासनैः अबान्धवं शवं चैव निर्हरेयुरिति स्थितिः
text: During the day they may go about on business distinguished by royal signs; they shall carry out corpses of people without relations; such is the law.
iast: divā careyuḥ kāryārthaṃ cihnitā rājaśāsanaiḥ abāndhavaṃ śavaṃ caiva nirhareyuriti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: vadhyāṃśca hanyuḥ satataṃ yathāśāstraṃ nṛpājñayā vadhyavāsāṃsi gṛhṇīyuḥ śayyāścābharaṇāni ca
meaning: वध्यांश्च हन्युः सततं यथाशास्त्रं नृपाज्ञया वध्यवासांसि गृह्णीयुः शय्याश्चाभरणानि च
text: They shall always execute criminals, in accordance with law, under the orders of the king; and they shall take away the clothes, the beds and the ornaments of those executed.
iast: vadhyāṃśca hanyuḥ satataṃ yathāśāstraṃ nṛpājñayā vadhyavāsāṃsi gṛhṇīyuḥ śayyāścābharaṇāni ca

sanskrit_iast: varṇāpetamavijñātaṃ naraṃ kaluṣayonijam āryarūpamivānāryaṃ karmabhiḥ svairvibhāvayet
meaning: वर्णापेतमविज्ञातं नरं कलुषयोनिजम् आर्यरूपमिवानार्यं कर्मभिः स्वैर्विभावयेत्
text: The man of impure origin, who is devoid of caste, unknown, a non-Aryan, even though having the appearance of an Aryan,—one shall discover by his acts.
iast: varṇāpetamavijñātaṃ naraṃ kaluṣayonijam āryarūpamivānāryaṃ karmabhiḥ svairvibhāvayet

sanskrit_iast: anāryatā niṣṭhuratā krūratā niṣkriyātmatā puruṣaṃ vyañjayantīha loke kaluṣayonijam
meaning: अनार्यता निष्ठुरता क्रूरता निष्क्रियात्मता पुरुषं व्यञ्जयन्तीह लोके कलुषयोनिजम्
text: Snobbishness, harshness, cruelty, and proneness to neglect duties, mark the man of impure origin in this world.
iast: anāryatā niṣṭhuratā krūratā niṣkriyātmatā puruṣaṃ vyañjayantīha loke kaluṣayonijam

sanskrit_iast: pitryaṃ vā bhajate śīlaṃ māturvaubhayameva vā na kathaṃ cana duryoniḥ prakṛtiṃ svāṃ niyacchati
meaning: पित्र्यं वा भजते शीलं मातुर्वौभयमेव वा न कथं चन दुर्योनिः प्रकृतिं स्वां नियच्छति
text: The base-born man bears the character or his father, or of his mother, or of both; he can never conceal his origin.
iast: pitryaṃ vā bhajate śīlaṃ māturvaubhayameva vā na kathaṃ cana duryoniḥ prakṛtiṃ svāṃ niyacchati

sanskrit_iast: kule mukhye 'pi jātasya yasya syādyonisaṅkaraḥ saṃśrayatyeva tatśīlaṃ naro 'lpamapi vā bahu
meaning: कुले मुख्ये 'पि जातस्य यस्य स्याद्योनिसङ्करः संश्रयत्येव तत्शीलं नरो 'ल्पमपि वा बहु
text: Even when one is born in a great family, if there be a confusion regarding his parentage, he surely imbibes the traits thereof, to a greater or less extent.
iast: kule mukhye 'pi jātasya yasya syādyonisaṅkaraḥ saṃśrayatyeva tatśīlaṃ naro 'lpamapi vā bahu

sanskrit_iast: yatra tvete paridhvaṃsāj jāyante varṇadūṣakāḥ rāṣṭrikaiḥ saha tadrāṣṭraṃ kṣiprameva vinaśyati rāṣṭriyaiḥ
meaning: यत्र त्वेते परिध्वंसाज् जायन्ते वर्णदूषकाः राष्ट्रिकैः सह तद्राष्ट्रं क्षिप्रमेव विनश्यति राष्ट्रियैः
text: That kingdom in which such caste-defilers are born of criminal intercourses, speedily perishes, along with the people.
iast: yatra tvete paridhvaṃsāj jāyante varṇadūṣakāḥ rāṣṭrikaiḥ saha tadrāṣṭraṃ kṣiprameva vinaśyati rāṣṭriyaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇārthe gavārthe vā dehatyāgo 'nupaskṛtaḥ strībālābhyupapattau ca bāhyānāṃ siddhikāraṇam strībālābhyavapattau ca
meaning: ब्राह्मणार्थे गवार्थे वा देहत्यागो 'नुपस्कृतः स्त्रीबालाभ्युपपत्तौ च बाह्यानां सिद्धिकारणम् स्त्रीबालाभ्यवपत्तौ च
text: For aliens perfection is secured by unrequitedly giving up the body for the sake ofBrāhmaṇasand cows, and in defence of women and children.
iast: brāhmaṇārthe gavārthe vā dehatyāgo 'nupaskṛtaḥ strībālābhyupapattau ca bāhyānāṃ siddhikāraṇam strībālābhyavapattau ca

sanskrit_iast: ahiṃsā satyamasteyaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ etaṃ sāmāsikaṃ dharmaṃ cāturvarṇye 'bravīn manuḥ
meaning: अहिंसा सत्यमस्तेयं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः एतं सामासिकं धर्मं चातुर्वर्ण्ये 'ब्रवीन् मनुः
text: Abstention from injuring, truthfulness, abstention from unlawful appropriation, purity and control of the sense-organs,—this Manu has declared to be the sum and substance of duty for the four castes.
iast: ahiṃsā satyamasteyaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ etaṃ sāmāsikaṃ dharmaṃ cāturvarṇye 'bravīn manuḥ

sanskrit_iast: śūdrāyāṃ brāhmaṇāj jātaḥ śreyasā cet prajāyate aśreyān śreyasīṃ jātiṃ gacchatyā saptamādyugāt
meaning: शूद्रायां ब्राह्मणाज् जातः श्रेयसा चेत् प्रजायते अश्रेयान् श्रेयसीं जातिं गच्छत्या सप्तमाद्युगात्
text: If the child born from aŚūdrawoman to aBrāhmaṇagoes on being wedded to a superior person,—the inferior attains the superior caste, within the seventh generation.
iast: śūdrāyāṃ brāhmaṇāj jātaḥ śreyasā cet prajāyate aśreyān śreyasīṃ jātiṃ gacchatyā saptamādyugāt

sanskrit_iast: śūdro brāhmaṇatāmeti brāhmaṇaścaiti śūdratām kṣatriyāj jātamevaṃ tu vidyādvaiśyāt tathaiva ca
meaning: शूद्रो ब्राह्मणतामेति ब्राह्मणश्चैति शूद्रताम् क्षत्रियाज् जातमेवं तु विद्याद्वैश्यात् तथैव च
text: TheŚūdraattains the position of theBrāhmaṇaand theBrāhmaṇasinks to the position of theŚūdra; the same should be understood to be the case with the offspring of theKṣatriyaor of the vaiśya.
iast: śūdro brāhmaṇatāmeti brāhmaṇaścaiti śūdratām kṣatriyāj jātamevaṃ tu vidyādvaiśyāt tathaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: anāryāyāṃ samutpanno brāhmaṇāt tu yadṛcchayā brāhmaṇyāmapyanāryāt tu śreyastvaṃ kvaiti cedbhavet) kasya cidbhavet
meaning: अनार्यायां समुत्पन्नो ब्राह्मणात् तु यदृच्छया ब्राह्मण्यामप्यनार्यात् तु श्रेयस्त्वं क्वैति चेद्भवेत्) कस्य चिद्भवेत्
text: (question).—If a child is somehow born to aBrāhmaṇafrom a non-Aryan woman, and another is born to a non-Aryan from aBrāhmaṇawoman,—with which of these would the ‘superiority’ lie?
iast: anāryāyāṃ samutpanno brāhmaṇāt tu yadṛcchayā brāhmaṇyāmapyanāryāt tu śreyastvaṃ kvaiti cedbhavet) kasya cidbhavet

sanskrit_iast: jāto nāryāmanāryāyāmāryādāryo bhavedguṇaiḥ jāto 'pyanāryādāryāyāmanārya iti niścayaḥ
meaning: जातो नार्यामनार्यायामार्यादार्यो भवेद्गुणैः जातो 'प्यनार्यादार्यायामनार्य इति निश्चयः
text: The decision is that—‘one born to anĀryafrom aNon-Āryawoman may be anĀryain quality; but one born to aNon-āryaeven from anĀryawoman is alwaysNon-Ārya’
iast: jāto nāryāmanāryāyāmāryādāryo bhavedguṇaiḥ jāto 'pyanāryādāryāyāmanārya iti niścayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tāvubhāvapyasaṃskāryāviti dharmo vyavasthitaḥ vaiguṇyāj janmanaḥ pūrva uttaraḥ pratilomataḥ janmataḥ
meaning: तावुभावप्यसंस्कार्याविति धर्मो व्यवस्थितः वैगुण्याज् जन्मनः पूर्व उत्तरः प्रतिलोमतः जन्मतः
text: The settled law is that both these are unfit for the sacraments:—the former on account of the defect in his birth and the latter by reason of his being horn ‘in the inverse order.’
iast: tāvubhāvapyasaṃskāryāviti dharmo vyavasthitaḥ vaiguṇyāj janmanaḥ pūrva uttaraḥ pratilomataḥ janmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: subījaṃ caiva sukṣetre jātaṃ saṃpadyate yathā tathā 'ryāj jāta āryāyāṃ sarvaṃ saṃskāramarhati
meaning: सुबीजं चैव सुक्षेत्रे जातं संपद्यते यथा तथा 'र्याज् जात आर्यायां सर्वं संस्कारमर्हति
text: Just as good seed grown in a good soil turns out well, so is the child born to anĀryafrom anĀryawoman worthy of all sacraments.
iast: subījaṃ caiva sukṣetre jātaṃ saṃpadyate yathā tathā 'ryāj jāta āryāyāṃ sarvaṃ saṃskāramarhati

sanskrit_iast: bījameke praśaṃsanti kṣetramanye manīṣiṇaḥ bījakṣetre tathaivānye tatraiyaṃ tu vyavasthitiḥ
meaning: बीजमेके प्रशंसन्ति क्षेत्रमन्ये मनीषिणः बीजक्षेत्रे तथैवान्ये तत्रैयं तु व्यवस्थितिः
text: Some wise men extol the seed, while others the soil; while yet others extol both the seed and the soil. The decision on this point is as follows.
iast: bījameke praśaṃsanti kṣetramanye manīṣiṇaḥ bījakṣetre tathaivānye tatraiyaṃ tu vyavasthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: akṣetre bījamutsṛṣṭamantaraiva vinaśyati abījakamapi kṣetraṃ kevalaṃ sthaṇḍilaṃ bhavet
meaning: अक्षेत्रे बीजमुत्सृष्टमन्तरैव विनश्यति अबीजकमपि क्षेत्रं केवलं स्थण्डिलं भवेत्
text: The seed sown on barren soil perishes prematurely; and soil without the seed would be a mere barren plot.
iast: akṣetre bījamutsṛṣṭamantaraiva vinaśyati abījakamapi kṣetraṃ kevalaṃ sthaṇḍilaṃ bhavet

sanskrit_iast: yasmādbījaprabhāveṇa tiryagjā ṛṣayo 'bhavan pūjitāśca praśastāśca tasmādbījaṃ praśasyate viśiṣyate
meaning: यस्माद्बीजप्रभावेण तिर्यग्जा ऋषयो 'भवन् पूजिताश्च प्रशस्ताश्च तस्माद्बीजं प्रशस्यते विशिष्यते
text: Because through the power of the seed, those born of animals became sages, honoured and extolled, therefore it is the seed that is more important.
iast: yasmādbījaprabhāveṇa tiryagjā ṛṣayo 'bhavan pūjitāśca praśastāśca tasmādbījaṃ praśasyate viśiṣyate

sanskrit_iast: anāryamāryakarmāṇamāryaṃ cānāryakarmiṇam saṃpradhāryābravīddhātā na samau nāsamāviti
meaning: अनार्यमार्यकर्माणमार्यं चानार्यकर्मिणम् संप्रधार्याब्रवीद्धाता न समौ नासमाविति
text: Having examined theNon-āryabehaving like anĀryaand theĀryabehaving like theNon-Ārya, the creator declared ‘that these are neither equal nor unequal.’
iast: anāryamāryakarmāṇamāryaṃ cānāryakarmiṇam saṃpradhāryābravīddhātā na samau nāsamāviti

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇā brahmayonisthā ye svakarmaṇyavasthitāḥ te samyagupajīveyuḥ ṣaṭ karmāṇi yathākramam
meaning: ब्राह्मणा ब्रह्मयोनिस्था ये स्वकर्मण्यवस्थिताः ते सम्यगुपजीवेयुः षट् कर्माणि यथाक्रमम्
text: Brāhmaṇasof pureBrāhmaṇa-birth, intent upon their duties, should duly perform the six acts in due order.
iast: brāhmaṇā brahmayonisthā ye svakarmaṇyavasthitāḥ te samyagupajīveyuḥ ṣaṭ karmāṇi yathākramam

sanskrit_iast: adhyāpanamadhyayanaṃ yajanaṃ yājanaṃ tathā dānaṃ pratigrahaścaiva ṣaṭ karmāṇyagrajanmanaḥ
meaning: अध्यापनमध्ययनं यजनं याजनं तथा दानं प्रतिग्रहश्चैव षट् कर्माण्यग्रजन्मनः
text: Teaching, studying, sacrificing for oneself, sacrificing for others, giving and receiving gifts are the six functions for the ‘first born’.
iast: adhyāpanamadhyayanaṃ yajanaṃ yājanaṃ tathā dānaṃ pratigrahaścaiva ṣaṭ karmāṇyagrajanmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṇṇāṃ tu karmaṇāmasya trīṇi karmāṇi jīvikā yājanādhyāpane caiva viśuddhācca pratigrahaḥ
meaning: षण्णां तु कर्मणामस्य त्रीणि कर्माणि जीविका याजनाध्यापने चैव विशुद्धाच्च प्रतिग्रहः
text: From among these six functions, three are his means of livelihood:viz., sacrificing for others, Teaching and Receiving gifts from pure men.
iast: ṣaṇṇāṃ tu karmaṇāmasya trīṇi karmāṇi jīvikā yājanādhyāpane caiva viśuddhācca pratigrahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: trayo dharmā nivartante brāhmaṇāt kṣatriyaṃ prati adhyāpanaṃ yājanaṃ ca tṛtīyaśca pratigrahaḥ
meaning: त्रयो धर्मा निवर्तन्ते ब्राह्मणात् क्षत्रियं प्रति अध्यापनं याजनं च तृतीयश्च प्रतिग्रहः
text: From theBrāhmaṇacoming to theKṣatriya, three of these functions cease;—viz., Teaching, Sacrificing for others, and third, the Receiving of gifts.
iast: trayo dharmā nivartante brāhmaṇāt kṣatriyaṃ prati adhyāpanaṃ yājanaṃ ca tṛtīyaśca pratigrahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyaṃ prati tathaivaite nivarteranniti sthitiḥ na tau prati hi tān dharmān manurāha prajāpatiḥ prati hitān dharmān
meaning: वैश्यं प्रति तथैवैते निवर्तेरन्निति स्थितिः न तौ प्रति हि तान् धर्मान् मनुराह प्रजापतिः प्रति हितान् धर्मान्
text: For the Vaiśya also these three should cease,—such is the law; sincePrajāpatiManu has not prescribed these duties for those two (castes).
iast: vaiśyaṃ prati tathaivaite nivarteranniti sthitiḥ na tau prati hi tān dharmān manurāha prajāpatiḥ prati hitān dharmān

sanskrit_iast: śastrāstrabhṛttvaṃ kṣatrasya vaṇikpaśukṛṣirviṣaḥ ājīvanārthaṃ dharmastu dānamadhyayanaṃ yajiḥ
meaning: शस्त्रास्त्रभृत्त्वं क्षत्रस्य वणिक्पशुकृषिर्विषः आजीवनार्थं धर्मस्तु दानमध्ययनं यजिः
text: For theKṣatriyacarrying of arms and weapons, and for the Vaiśya, trade, cattle-tending and agriculture, are the means of livelihood; while giving, studying and sacrificing constitute their duty.
iast: śastrāstrabhṛttvaṃ kṣatrasya vaṇikpaśukṛṣirviṣaḥ ājīvanārthaṃ dharmastu dānamadhyayanaṃ yajiḥ

sanskrit_iast: vedābhyāso brāhmaṇasya kṣatriyasya ca rakṣaṇam vārtākarmaiva vaiśyasya viśiṣṭāni svakarmasu
meaning: वेदाभ्यासो ब्राह्मणस्य क्षत्रियस्य च रक्षणम् वार्ताकर्मैव वैश्यस्य विशिष्टानि स्वकर्मसु
text: Among their respective occupations, the most useful are—constant Vedic study for theBrāhmaṇa, protection for theKṣatriyaand commercial dealings for the Vaiśya.
iast: vedābhyāso brāhmaṇasya kṣatriyasya ca rakṣaṇam vārtākarmaiva vaiśyasya viśiṣṭāni svakarmasu

sanskrit_iast: ajīvaṃstu yathauktena brāhmaṇaḥ svena karmaṇā jīvet kṣatriyadharmeṇa sa hyasya pratyanantaraḥ
meaning: अजीवंस्तु यथौक्तेन ब्राह्मणः स्वेन कर्मणा जीवेत् क्षत्रियधर्मेण स ह्यस्य प्रत्यनन्तरः
text: If theBrāhmaṇais unable to subsist by his own occupation as described above, he may make a living by the function of theKṣatriya; since this latter is next to him.
iast: ajīvaṃstu yathauktena brāhmaṇaḥ svena karmaṇā jīvet kṣatriyadharmeṇa sa hyasya pratyanantaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: ubhābhyāmapyajīvaṃstu kathaṃ syāditi cedbhavet kṛṣigorakṣamāsthāya jīvedvaiśyasya jīvikām
meaning: उभाभ्यामप्यजीवंस्तु कथं स्यादिति चेद्भवेत् कृषिगोरक्षमास्थाय जीवेद्वैश्यस्य जीविकाम्
text: If he is unable to subsist by these two occupations, and the question arises as to how it should be,—he may live the living of the Vaiśya, having recourse to agriculture and cattle-tending.
iast: ubhābhyāmapyajīvaṃstu kathaṃ syāditi cedbhavet kṛṣigorakṣamāsthāya jīvedvaiśyasya jīvikām

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyavṛttyā 'pi jīvaṃstu brāhmaṇaḥ ksatriyo 'pi vā hiṃsāprāyāṃ parādhīnāṃ kṛṣiṃ yatnena varjayet
meaning: वैश्यवृत्त्या 'पि जीवंस्तु ब्राह्मणः क्सत्रियो 'पि वा हिंसाप्रायां पराधीनां कृषिं यत्नेन वर्जयेत्
text: But even when subsisting by the occupation of the Vaiśya, theBrāhmaṇaor theKṣatriyashall carefully avoid agriculture, which involves injury to living creatures and is dependent upon others.
iast: vaiśyavṛttyā 'pi jīvaṃstu brāhmaṇaḥ ksatriyo 'pi vā hiṃsāprāyāṃ parādhīnāṃ kṛṣiṃ yatnena varjayet

sanskrit_iast: kṛṣiṃ sādhuiti manyante sā vṛttiḥ sadvigarhitāḥ bhūmiṃ bhūmiśayāṃścaiva hanti kāṣṭhamayomukham
meaning: कृषिं साधुइति मन्यन्ते सा वृत्तिः सद्विगर्हिताः भूमिं भूमिशयांश्चैव हन्ति काष्ठमयोमुखम्
text: People think agriculture to be good; but that occupation is despised by the righteous; the iron-tipped wood injures the earth and the earthly creatures.
iast: kṛṣiṃ sādhuiti manyante sā vṛttiḥ sadvigarhitāḥ bhūmiṃ bhūmiśayāṃścaiva hanti kāṣṭhamayomukham

sanskrit_iast: idaṃ tu vṛttivaikalyāt tyajato dharmanaipuṇam viśpaṇyamuddhṛtauddhāraṃ vikreyaṃ vittavardhanam
meaning: इदं तु वृत्तिवैकल्यात् त्यजतो धर्मनैपुणम् विश्पण्यमुद्धृतौद्धारं विक्रेयं वित्तवर्धनम्
text: But there is this, that one who, under the stress of livelihood, renounces the strictness concerning his duties, may, for increasing his wealth, sell such commodities as are sold by Vaiśyas, with certain exceptions.
iast: idaṃ tu vṛttivaikalyāt tyajato dharmanaipuṇam viśpaṇyamuddhṛtauddhāraṃ vikreyaṃ vittavardhanam

sanskrit_iast: sarvān rasānapoheta kṛtānnaṃ ca tilaiḥ saha aśmano lavaṇaṃ caiva paśavo ye ca mānuṣāḥ
meaning: सर्वान् रसानपोहेत कृतान्नं च तिलैः सह अश्मनो लवणं चैव पशवो ये च मानुषाः
text: He shall avoid all savoury substances as also cooked food and sesamum, stores, salt, animals and human beings.
iast: sarvān rasānapoheta kṛtānnaṃ ca tilaiḥ saha aśmano lavaṇaṃ caiva paśavo ye ca mānuṣāḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarvaṃ ca tāntavaṃ raktaṃ śāṇakṣaumāvikāni ca api cet syuraraktāni phalamūle tathauṣadhīḥ
meaning: सर्वं च तान्तवं रक्तं शाणक्षौमाविकानि च अपि चेत् स्युररक्तानि फलमूले तथौषधीः
text: Every kind of woven article dyed (red) as well as that made of hemp, silk or wool, even when not dyed red; and also fruits and roots and medicinal herbs.
iast: sarvaṃ ca tāntavaṃ raktaṃ śāṇakṣaumāvikāni ca api cet syuraraktāni phalamūle tathauṣadhīḥ

sanskrit_iast: apaḥ śastraṃ viṣaṃ māṃsaṃ somaṃ gandhāṃśca sarvaśaḥ kṣīraṃ kṣaudraṃ dadhi ghṛtaṃ tailaṃ madhu guḍaṃ kuśān
meaning: अपः शस्त्रं विषं मांसं सोमं गन्धांश्च सर्वशः क्षीरं क्षौद्रं दधि घृतं तैलं मधु गुडं कुशान्
text: Water, weapons, poison, meat, soma and all kinds of perfume; milk, honey, curds, butter, oil, honey-wax, sugar and kuśa-grass.
iast: apaḥ śastraṃ viṣaṃ māṃsaṃ somaṃ gandhāṃśca sarvaśaḥ kṣīraṃ kṣaudraṃ dadhi ghṛtaṃ tailaṃ madhu guḍaṃ kuśān

sanskrit_iast: āraṇyāṃśca paśūn sarvān daṃṣṭriṇaśca vayāṃsi ca madyaṃ nīliṃ ca lākṣāṃ ca sarvāṃścaikaśaphāṃstathā nīlīṃ
meaning: आरण्यांश्च पशून् सर्वान् दंष्ट्रिणश्च वयांसि च मद्यं नीलिं च लाक्षां च सर्वांश्चैकशफांस्तथा नीलीं
text: All beasts of the forest, fanged animals, and birds, wine, indigo, lac, and also all one-hoofed animals.
iast: āraṇyāṃśca paśūn sarvān daṃṣṭriṇaśca vayāṃsi ca madyaṃ nīliṃ ca lākṣāṃ ca sarvāṃścaikaśaphāṃstathā nīlīṃ

sanskrit_iast: kāmamutpādya kṛṣyāṃ tu svayameva kṛṣīvalaḥ vikrīṇīta tilāṃśūdrān dharmārthamacirasthitān tilāṃśuddhān
meaning: काममुत्पाद्य कृष्यां तु स्वयमेव कृषीवलः विक्रीणीत तिलांशूद्रान् धर्मार्थमचिरस्थितान् तिलांशुद्धान्
text: But the agriculturist may, if he wishes, sell pure sesamum for sacred purposes, if he has cultivated them himself and has not kept these long.
iast: kāmamutpādya kṛṣyāṃ tu svayameva kṛṣīvalaḥ vikrīṇīta tilāṃśūdrān dharmārthamacirasthitān tilāṃśuddhān

sanskrit_iast: bhojanābhyañjanāddānādyadanyat kurute tilaiḥ kṛmibhūtaḥ śvaviṣṭhāyāṃ pitṛbhiḥ saha majjati
meaning: भोजनाभ्यञ्जनाद्दानाद्यदन्यत् कुरुते तिलैः कृमिभूतः श्वविष्ठायां पितृभिः सह मज्जति
text: If one does with sesamum anything else, except eating, anointing and giving,—he becomes a worm and plunges into the ordure of dogs, along with his ancestors.
iast: bhojanābhyañjanāddānādyadanyat kurute tilaiḥ kṛmibhūtaḥ śvaviṣṭhāyāṃ pitṛbhiḥ saha majjati

sanskrit_iast: sadyaḥ patati māṃsena lākṣayā lavaṇena ca tryaheṇa śūdro bhavati brāhmaṇaḥ kṣīravikrayāt
meaning: सद्यः पतति मांसेन लाक्षया लवणेन च त्र्यहेण शूद्रो भवति ब्राह्मणः क्षीरविक्रयात्
text: By selling meat, lac and salt, theBrāhmaṇabecomes an outcast at once; and by selling milk theBrāhmaṇabecomes aŚūdrain three days.
iast: sadyaḥ patati māṃsena lākṣayā lavaṇena ca tryaheṇa śūdro bhavati brāhmaṇaḥ kṣīravikrayāt

sanskrit_iast: itareṣāṃ tu paṇyānāṃ vikrayādiha kāmataḥ brāhmaṇaḥ saptarātreṇa vaiśyabhāvaṃ niyacchati
meaning: इतरेषां तु पण्यानां विक्रयादिह कामतः ब्राह्मणः सप्तरात्रेण वैश्यभावं नियच्छति
text: By selling, through greed, other commodities, theBrāhmaṇaassumes, in seven nights, the character of the Vaiśya.
iast: itareṣāṃ tu paṇyānāṃ vikrayādiha kāmataḥ brāhmaṇaḥ saptarātreṇa vaiśyabhāvaṃ niyacchati

sanskrit_iast: rasā rasairnimātavyā na tveva lavaṇaṃ rasaiḥ kṛtānnaṃ ca kṛtānnena tilā dhānyena tatsamāḥ
meaning: रसा रसैर्निमातव्या न त्वेव लवणं रसैः कृतान्नं च कृतान्नेन तिला धान्येन तत्समाः
text: Savoury articles should be bartered for savoury articles, but never salt for another savoury article,—cooked food, for cooked food, and sesamum for corn, in equal quantities.
iast: rasā rasairnimātavyā na tveva lavaṇaṃ rasaiḥ kṛtānnaṃ ca kṛtānnena tilā dhānyena tatsamāḥ

sanskrit_iast: jīvedetena rājanyaḥ sarveṇāpyanayaṃ gataḥ na tveva jyāyaṃsīṃ vṛttimabhimanyeta karhi cit
meaning: जीवेदेतेन राजन्यः सर्वेणाप्यनयं गतः न त्वेव ज्यायंसीं वृत्तिमभिमन्येत कर्हि चित्
text: TheKṣatriya, fallen in adverse circumstances, shall subsist by means of all this; but he shall never think of arrogating to himself the higher occupations.
iast: jīvedetena rājanyaḥ sarveṇāpyanayaṃ gataḥ na tveva jyāyaṃsīṃ vṛttimabhimanyeta karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: yo lobhādadhamo jātyā jīvedutkṛṣṭakarmabhiḥ taṃ rājā nirdhanaṃ kṛtvā kṣiprameva pravāsayet
meaning: यो लोभादधमो जात्या जीवेदुत्कृष्टकर्मभिः तं राजा निर्धनं कृत्वा क्षिप्रमेव प्रवासयेत्
text: If a man of low caste, through greed, subsists by the occupations of his superiors, him the King shall deprive of his property and quickly banish.
iast: yo lobhādadhamo jātyā jīvedutkṛṣṭakarmabhiḥ taṃ rājā nirdhanaṃ kṛtvā kṣiprameva pravāsayet

sanskrit_iast: varaṃ svadharmo viguṇo na pārakyaḥ svanuṣṭhitaḥ viguṇaḥ paradharmāt svadhiṣṭhitāt paradharmeṇa jīvan hi sadyaḥ patati jātitaḥ
meaning: वरं स्वधर्मो विगुणो न पारक्यः स्वनुष्ठितः विगुणः परधर्मात् स्वधिष्ठितात् परधर्मेण जीवन् हि सद्यः पतति जातितः
text: Better one’s own duty imperfectly performed, and not the duty of another performed perfectly; he who subsists by the function of another, instantly falls off from his caste.
iast: varaṃ svadharmo viguṇo na pārakyaḥ svanuṣṭhitaḥ viguṇaḥ paradharmāt svadhiṣṭhitāt paradharmeṇa jīvan hi sadyaḥ patati jātitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyo 'jīvan svadharmeṇa śūdravṛttyā 'pi vartayet anācarannakāryāṇi nivarteta ca śaktimān
meaning: वैश्यो 'जीवन् स्वधर्मेण शूद्रवृत्त्या 'पि वर्तयेत् अनाचरन्नकार्याणि निवर्तेत च शक्तिमान्
text: The Vaiśya, unable to subsist by his own occupations, may subsist by the occupations of theŚūdra, avoiding all improper acts. But he shall desist from them as soon as he is able to do so.
iast: vaiśyo 'jīvan svadharmeṇa śūdravṛttyā 'pi vartayet anācarannakāryāṇi nivarteta ca śaktimān

sanskrit_iast: aśaknuvaṃstu śuśrūṣāṃ śūdraḥ kartuṃ dvijanmanām putradārātyayaṃ prāpto jīvet kārukakarmabhiḥ
meaning: अशक्नुवंस्तु शुश्रूषां शूद्रः कर्तुं द्विजन्मनाम् पुत्रदारात्ययं प्राप्तो जीवेत् कारुककर्मभिः
text: TheŚūdra, unable to do service for twice-born people, and threatened with danger to his wife and sons, may subsist by the occupations of craftsmen.
iast: aśaknuvaṃstu śuśrūṣāṃ śūdraḥ kartuṃ dvijanmanām putradārātyayaṃ prāpto jīvet kārukakarmabhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yaiḥ karmabhiḥ pracaritaiḥ śuśrūṣyante dvijātayaḥ tāni kārukakarmāṇi śilpāni vividhāni ca
meaning: यैः कर्मभिः प्रचरितैः शुश्रूष्यन्ते द्विजातयः तानि कारुककर्माणि शिल्पानि विविधानि च
text: That is, those occupations of craftsmen and other arts by the performance whereof the twice-born men are best served.
iast: yaiḥ karmabhiḥ pracaritaiḥ śuśrūṣyante dvijātayaḥ tāni kārukakarmāṇi śilpāni vividhāni ca

sanskrit_iast: vaiśyavṛttimanātiṣṭhan brāhmaṇaḥ sve pathi sthitaḥ avṛttikarṣitaḥ sīdannimaṃ dharmaṃ samācaret
meaning: वैश्यवृत्तिमनातिष्ठन् ब्राह्मणः स्वे पथि स्थितः अवृत्तिकर्षितः सीदन्निमं धर्मं समाचरेत्
text: If aBrāhmaṇa, distressed through want of livelihood, pines, because resolved to stick to his own path, he does not adopt the occupation of the Vaiśya,—he shall adopt this (following) procedure.
iast: vaiśyavṛttimanātiṣṭhan brāhmaṇaḥ sve pathi sthitaḥ avṛttikarṣitaḥ sīdannimaṃ dharmaṃ samācaret

sanskrit_iast: sarvataḥ pratigṛhṇīyādbrāhmaṇastvanayaṃ gataḥ pavitraṃ duṣyatītyetaddharmato naupapadyate
meaning: सर्वतः प्रतिगृह्णीयाद्ब्राह्मणस्त्वनयं गतः पवित्रं दुष्यतीत्येतद्धर्मतो नौपपद्यते
text: Fallen into distress, theBrāhmaṇamay receive gifts from all; that anything which is pure should become defiled is not possible under the law.
iast: sarvataḥ pratigṛhṇīyādbrāhmaṇastvanayaṃ gataḥ pavitraṃ duṣyatītyetaddharmato naupapadyate

sanskrit_iast: nādhyāpanādyājanādvā garhitādvā pratigrahāt doṣo bhavati viprāṇāṃ jvalanāmbusamā hi te
meaning: नाध्यापनाद्याजनाद्वा गर्हिताद्वा प्रतिग्रहात् दोषो भवति विप्राणां ज्वलनाम्बुसमा हि ते
text: No sin attaches toBrāhmaṇas, either by teaching or by sacrificing for others or by receiving reprehensible gifts; for they are like fire and water.
iast: nādhyāpanādyājanādvā garhitādvā pratigrahāt doṣo bhavati viprāṇāṃ jvalanāmbusamā hi te

sanskrit_iast: jīvitātyayamāpanno yo 'nnamatti tatastataḥ ākāśamiva paṅkena na sa pāpena lipyate
meaning: जीवितात्ययमापन्नो यो 'न्नमत्ति ततस्ततः आकाशमिव पङ्केन न स पापेन लिप्यते
text: If a man, threatened with loss of life, eats food from stray sources, he does not become tainted with sin, just asĀkāśais not defiled by mud.
iast: jīvitātyayamāpanno yo 'nnamatti tatastataḥ ākāśamiva paṅkena na sa pāpena lipyate

sanskrit_iast: ajīgartaḥ sutaṃ hantumupāsarpadbubhukṣitaḥ na cālipyata pāpena kṣutpratīkāramācaran
meaning: अजीगर्तः सुतं हन्तुमुपासर्पद्बुभुक्षितः न चालिप्यत पापेन क्षुत्प्रतीकारमाचरन्
text: Ajīgarta, buffering from hunger, went forward to kill his son; and as he sought a remedy for hunger, he did not become tainted with sin.
iast: ajīgartaḥ sutaṃ hantumupāsarpadbubhukṣitaḥ na cālipyata pāpena kṣutpratīkāramācaran

sanskrit_iast: śvamāṃsamicchanārto 'ttuṃ dharmādharmavicakṣaṇaḥ prāṇānāṃ parirakṣārthaṃ vāmadevo na liptavān
meaning: श्वमांसमिच्छनार्तो 'त्तुं धर्माधर्मविचक्षणः प्राणानां परिरक्षार्थं वामदेवो न लिप्तवान्
text: Vāmadeva, expert in the knowledge of right and wrong, when distressed, desired to eat dog’s flesh for saving his life,—and he did not become defiled.
iast: śvamāṃsamicchanārto 'ttuṃ dharmādharmavicakṣaṇaḥ prāṇānāṃ parirakṣārthaṃ vāmadevo na liptavān

sanskrit_iast: bharadvājaḥ kṣudhārtastu saputro vijane vane bahvīrgāḥ pratijagrāha vṛdhostakṣṇo mahātapāḥ
meaning: भरद्वाजः क्षुधार्तस्तु सपुत्रो विजने वने बह्वीर्गाः प्रतिजग्राह वृधोस्तक्ष्णो महातपाः
text: Bharadvāja, a man of great austerities, accepted many cows from the carpenterVṛdhu, when tormented by hunger, along with his sons, in a lonely forest.
iast: bharadvājaḥ kṣudhārtastu saputro vijane vane bahvīrgāḥ pratijagrāha vṛdhostakṣṇo mahātapāḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣudhārtaścāttumabhyāgādviśvāmitraḥ śvajāghanīm caṇḍālahastādādāya dharmādharmavicakṣaṇaḥ
meaning: क्षुधार्तश्चात्तुमभ्यागाद्विश्वामित्रः श्वजाघनीम् चण्डालहस्तादादाय धर्माधर्मविचक्षणः
text: Viśvāmitra, expert in the knowledge of right and wrong, when tormented by hunger, proceeded to eat the haunch of a dog, receiving it from the hands of aCaṇḍāla.
iast: kṣudhārtaścāttumabhyāgādviśvāmitraḥ śvajāghanīm caṇḍālahastādādāya dharmādharmavicakṣaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: pratigrahādyājanādvā tathaivādhyāpanādapi pratigrahaḥ pratyavaraḥ pretya viprasya garhitaḥ
meaning: प्रतिग्रहाद्याजनाद्वा तथैवाध्यापनादपि प्रतिग्रहः प्रत्यवरः प्रेत्य विप्रस्य गर्हितः
text: Among Receiving of Gifts, Sacrificing and Teaching, the Receiving of Gifts is the meanest, and the most reprehensible for theBrāhmaṇa, in respect of his life after death.
iast: pratigrahādyājanādvā tathaivādhyāpanādapi pratigrahaḥ pratyavaraḥ pretya viprasya garhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yājanādhyāpane nityaṃ kriyete saṃskṛtātmanām pratigrahastu kriyate śūdrādapyantyajanmanaḥ
meaning: याजनाध्यापने नित्यं क्रियेते संस्कृतात्मनाम् प्रतिग्रहस्तु क्रियते शूद्रादप्यन्त्यजन्मनः
text: As for Teachng and Sacrificing, these are done always for only such men as have received the sacraments; while the Receiving of Gifts may be done even from the lowest-bornŚūdra.
iast: yājanādhyāpane nityaṃ kriyete saṃskṛtātmanām pratigrahastu kriyate śūdrādapyantyajanmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: japahomairapaityeno yājanādhyāpanaiḥ kṛtam pratigrahanimittaṃ tu tyāgena tapasaiva ca
meaning: जपहोमैरपैत्येनो याजनाध्यापनैः कृतम् प्रतिग्रहनिमित्तं तु त्यागेन तपसैव च
text: By the muttering of sacred texts and by the offering of libations is the guilt incurred by teaching and sacrificing removed; while that incurred by receiving gifts is removed only by renouncing them and by austerities.
iast: japahomairapaityeno yājanādhyāpanaiḥ kṛtam pratigrahanimittaṃ tu tyāgena tapasaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: śilauñchamapyādadīta vipro 'jīvan yatastataḥ pratigrahātśilaḥ śreyāṃstato 'pyuñchaḥ praśasyate
meaning: शिलौञ्छमप्याददीत विप्रो 'जीवन् यतस्ततः प्रतिग्रहात्शिलः श्रेयांस्ततो 'प्युञ्छः प्रशस्यते
text: TheBrāhmaṇa, unable to maintain himself, may take to gleaning ears of corn and picking up grains, even from stray places. Gleaning ears of corn is better than receiving gifts; and better even than picking up of grains.
iast: śilauñchamapyādadīta vipro 'jīvan yatastataḥ pratigrahātśilaḥ śreyāṃstato 'pyuñchaḥ praśasyate

sanskrit_iast: sīdadbhiḥ kupyamicchadbhirdhane vā pṛthivīpatiḥ dhanaṃ vā yācyaḥ syāt snātakairviprairaditsaṃstyāgamarhati
meaning: सीदद्भिः कुप्यमिच्छद्भिर्धने वा पृथिवीपतिः धनं वा याच्यः स्यात् स्नातकैर्विप्रैरदित्संस्त्यागमर्हति
text: IfBrāhmaṇas, who have completed their study, suffer privation, or seek to obtain wealth and riches, they may beg of the king; and if he be unwilling to give, he should be given up.
iast: sīdadbhiḥ kupyamicchadbhirdhane vā pṛthivīpatiḥ dhanaṃ vā yācyaḥ syāt snātakairviprairaditsaṃstyāgamarhati

sanskrit_iast: akṛtaṃ ca kṛtāt kṣetrādgaurajāvikameva ca hiraṇyaṃ dhānyamannaṃ ca pūrvaṃ pūrvamadoṣavat
meaning: अकृतं च कृतात् क्षेत्राद्गौरजाविकमेव च हिरण्यं धान्यमन्नं च पूर्वं पूर्वमदोषवत्
text: Uncultivated land is less reprehensible than cultivated land; and among cows, goats, sheep, gold, grains, and food each preceding one is less reprehensible than the succeeding.
iast: akṛtaṃ ca kṛtāt kṣetrādgaurajāvikameva ca hiraṇyaṃ dhānyamannaṃ ca pūrvaṃ pūrvamadoṣavat

sanskrit_iast: sapta vittāgamā dharmyā dāyo lābhaḥ krayo jayaḥ prayogaḥ karmayogaśca satpratigraha eva ca
meaning: सप्त वित्तागमा धर्म्या दायो लाभः क्रयो जयः प्रयोगः कर्मयोगश्च सत्प्रतिग्रह एव च
text: There are seven lawful sources of income: inheritance, acquisition, purchase, conquest, investment, industry and receiving of proper gifts.
iast: sapta vittāgamā dharmyā dāyo lābhaḥ krayo jayaḥ prayogaḥ karmayogaśca satpratigraha eva ca

sanskrit_iast: vidyā śilpaṃ bhṛtiḥ sevā gorakṣyaṃ vipaṇiḥ kṛṣiḥ dhṛtirbhaikṣaṃ kusīdaṃ ca daśa jīvanahetavaḥ
meaning: विद्या शिल्पं भृतिः सेवा गोरक्ष्यं विपणिः कृषिः धृतिर्भैक्षं कुसीदं च दश जीवनहेतवः
text: Learning, handicrafts, working on hire, service, cattle-tending, trade, agriculture, contentment, begging and money-lending,—these are the ten means of livelihood.
iast: vidyā śilpaṃ bhṛtiḥ sevā gorakṣyaṃ vipaṇiḥ kṛṣiḥ dhṛtirbhaikṣaṃ kusīdaṃ ca daśa jīvanahetavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇaḥ kṣatriyo vā 'pi vṛddhiṃ naiva prayojayet kāmaṃ tu khalu dharmārthaṃ dadyāt pāpīyase 'lpikām
meaning: ब्राह्मणः क्षत्रियो वा 'पि वृद्धिं नैव प्रयोजयेत् कामं तु खलु धर्मार्थं दद्यात् पापीयसे 'ल्पिकाम्
text: TheBrāhmaṇaor theKṣatriyamust not lend money on interest; but he may advance a little to unrighteous men for sacred purposes.
iast: brāhmaṇaḥ kṣatriyo vā 'pi vṛddhiṃ naiva prayojayet kāmaṃ tu khalu dharmārthaṃ dadyāt pāpīyase 'lpikām

sanskrit_iast: caturthamādadāno 'pi kṣatriyo bhāgamāpadi prajā rakṣan paraṃ śaktyā kilbiṣāt pratimucyate
meaning: चतुर्थमाददानो 'पि क्षत्रियो भागमापदि प्रजा रक्षन् परं शक्त्या किल्बिषात् प्रतिमुच्यते
text: TheKṣatriya, who, in times of distress, takes the fourth fart, becomes freed from sin, if he protects the people to the best of his ability.
iast: caturthamādadāno 'pi kṣatriyo bhāgamāpadi prajā rakṣan paraṃ śaktyā kilbiṣāt pratimucyate

sanskrit_iast: svadharmo vijayastasya nāhave syāt parālgmukhaḥ śastreṇa vaiśyān rakṣitvā dharmyamāhārayedbalim vaiśyādrakṣitvā
meaning: स्वधर्मो विजयस्तस्य नाहवे स्यात् पराल्ग्मुखः शस्त्रेण वैश्यान् रक्षित्वा धर्म्यमाहारयेद्बलिम् वैश्याद्रक्षित्वा
text: Conquest is his peculiar duty; and he should never turn back in danger; he shall realise the legal tax from the Vaiśya, after protecting the people;
iast: svadharmo vijayastasya nāhave syāt parālgmukhaḥ śastreṇa vaiśyān rakṣitvā dharmyamāhārayedbalim vaiśyādrakṣitvā

sanskrit_iast: dhānye 'ṣṭamaṃ viśāṃ śulkaṃ viṃśaṃ kārṣāpaṇāvaram karmaupakaraṇāḥ śūdrāḥ kāravaḥ śilpinastathā
meaning: धान्ये 'ष्टमं विशां शुल्कं विंशं कार्षापणावरम् कर्मौपकरणाः शूद्राः कारवः शिल्पिनस्तथा
text: From the people the tax on grains shall be one-eighth, and one-twentieth (on gold), with the minimum of one ‘Kārṣāpaṇa’;Śūdras, craftsmen and artisans discharge their dues by work.
iast: dhānye 'ṣṭamaṃ viśāṃ śulkaṃ viṃśaṃ kārṣāpaṇāvaram karmaupakaraṇāḥ śūdrāḥ kāravaḥ śilpinastathā

sanskrit_iast: śūdrastu vṛttimākāṅkṣan kṣatramārādhayedyadi ārādhayediti dhaninaṃ vā 'pyupārādhya vaiśyaṃ śūdro jijīviṣet
meaning: शूद्रस्तु वृत्तिमाकाङ्क्षन् क्षत्रमाराधयेद्यदि आराधयेदिति धनिनं वा 'प्युपाराध्य वैश्यं शूद्रो जिजीविषेत्
text: Sanskrit text, Unicode transliteration and English translation by Ganganath Jha:
iast: śūdrastu vṛttimākāṅkṣan kṣatramārādhayedyadi ārādhayediti dhaninaṃ vā 'pyupārādhya vaiśyaṃ śūdro jijīviṣet

sanskrit_iast: svargārthamubhayārthaṃ vā viprānārādhayet tu saḥ jātabrāhmaṇaśabdasya sā hyasya kṛtakṛtyatā
meaning: स्वर्गार्थमुभयार्थं वा विप्रानाराधयेत् तु सः जातब्राह्मणशब्दस्य सा ह्यस्य कृतकृत्यता
text: He shall serveBrāhmaṇaseither for the sake of heaven, or for the sake of both; when, he has attained the title of the ‘Brāhmaṇa,’ this implies the accomplishment of all his purposes.
iast: svargārthamubhayārthaṃ vā viprānārādhayet tu saḥ jātabrāhmaṇaśabdasya sā hyasya kṛtakṛtyatā

sanskrit_iast: viprasevaiva śūdrasya viśiṣṭaṃ karma kīrtyate yadato 'nyaddhi kurute tadbhavatyasya niṣphalam
meaning: विप्रसेवैव शूद्रस्य विशिष्टं कर्म कीर्त्यते यदतो 'न्यद्धि कुरुते तद्भवत्यस्य निष्फलम्
text: The service of theBrāhmaṇais described as the distinctive duty of theŚūdra; everything else that he does is fruitless for him.
iast: viprasevaiva śūdrasya viśiṣṭaṃ karma kīrtyate yadato 'nyaddhi kurute tadbhavatyasya niṣphalam

sanskrit_iast: prakalpyā tasya tairvṛttiḥ svakuṭumbādyathārhataḥ śaktiṃ cāvekṣya dākṣyaṃ ca bhṛtyānāṃ ca parigraham
meaning: प्रकल्प्या तस्य तैर्वृत्तिः स्वकुटुम्बाद्यथार्हतः शक्तिं चावेक्ष्य दाक्ष्यं च भृत्यानां च परिग्रहम्
text: They should provide out of their family, a suitable maintenance for him, after considering his own capacity, and the man’s skill and the burden of persons to be supported by him.
iast: prakalpyā tasya tairvṛttiḥ svakuṭumbādyathārhataḥ śaktiṃ cāvekṣya dākṣyaṃ ca bhṛtyānāṃ ca parigraham

sanskrit_iast: ucchiṣṭamannaṃ dātavyaṃ jīrṇāni vasanāni ca pulākāścaiva dhānyānāṃ jīrṇāścaiva paricchadāḥ
meaning: उच्छिष्टमन्नं दातव्यं जीर्णानि वसनानि च पुलाकाश्चैव धान्यानां जीर्णाश्चैव परिच्छदाः
text: Remnants of food and worn-out clothes shall be given to him; as also the grain-refuse and old furniture.
iast: ucchiṣṭamannaṃ dātavyaṃ jīrṇāni vasanāni ca pulākāścaiva dhānyānāṃ jīrṇāścaiva paricchadāḥ

sanskrit_iast: na śūdre pātakaṃ kiṃ cinna ca saṃskāramarhati nāsyādhikāro dharme 'sti na dharmāt pratiṣedhanam
meaning: न शूद्रे पातकं किं चिन्न च संस्कारमर्हति नास्याधिकारो धर्मे 'स्ति न धर्मात् प्रतिषेधनम्
text: For theŚūdrathere is no sin; nor is he worthy of any sacraments; he is not entitled to any sacred rites; but there is no prohibition against sacred rites.
iast: na śūdre pātakaṃ kiṃ cinna ca saṃskāramarhati nāsyādhikāro dharme 'sti na dharmāt pratiṣedhanam

sanskrit_iast: dharmaipsavastu dharmajñāḥ satāṃ vṛttamanuṣṭhitāḥ satāṃ dharmaṃ mantravarjyaṃ na duṣyanti praśaṃsāṃ prāpnuvanti ca mantravarjaṃ
meaning: धर्मैप्सवस्तु धर्मज्ञाः सतां वृत्तमनुष्ठिताः सतां धर्मं मन्त्रवर्ज्यं न दुष्यन्ति प्रशंसां प्राप्नुवन्ति च मन्त्रवर्जं
text: If those who, knowing their duty, and wishing to acquire merit, imitate the practices of righteous men, with the exception of reciting the sacred texts, they incur no guilt; they obtain praise.
iast: dharmaipsavastu dharmajñāḥ satāṃ vṛttamanuṣṭhitāḥ satāṃ dharmaṃ mantravarjyaṃ na duṣyanti praśaṃsāṃ prāpnuvanti ca mantravarjaṃ

sanskrit_iast: yathā yathā hi sadvṛttamātiṣṭhatyanasūyakaḥ tathā tathaimaṃ cāmuṃ ca lokaṃ prāpnotyaninditaḥ
meaning: यथा यथा हि सद्वृत्तमातिष्ठत्यनसूयकः तथा तथैमं चामुं च लोकं प्राप्नोत्यनिन्दितः
text: As theŚūdra, free from envy, maintains the right course of conduct, so does he, free from blame, gain this world and the next.
iast: yathā yathā hi sadvṛttamātiṣṭhatyanasūyakaḥ tathā tathaimaṃ cāmuṃ ca lokaṃ prāpnotyaninditaḥ

sanskrit_iast: śaktenāpi hi śūdreṇa na kāryo dhanasañcayaḥ śūdro hi dhanamāsādya brāhmaṇāneva bādhate
meaning: शक्तेनापि हि शूद्रेण न कार्यो धनसञ्चयः शूद्रो हि धनमासाद्य ब्राह्मणानेव बाधते
text: Even though he be able, theŚūdrashall not amass wealth; for having acquired wealth, theŚūdraharasses theBrāhmaṇas.
iast: śaktenāpi hi śūdreṇa na kāryo dhanasañcayaḥ śūdro hi dhanamāsādya brāhmaṇāneva bādhate

sanskrit_iast: ete caturṇāṃ varṇānāmāpaddharmāḥ prakīrtitāḥ yān samyaganutiṣṭhanto vrajanti paramaṃ gatim
meaning: एते चतुर्णां वर्णानामापद्धर्माः प्रकीर्तिताः यान् सम्यगनुतिष्ठन्तो व्रजन्ति परमं गतिम्
text: The duties or the four castes in times or distress have thus been expounded; by properly carrying out which they attain the highest state.
iast: ete caturṇāṃ varṇānāmāpaddharmāḥ prakīrtitāḥ yān samyaganutiṣṭhanto vrajanti paramaṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: eṣa dharmavidhiḥ kṛtsnaścāturvarṇyasya kīrtitaḥ ataḥ paraṃ pravakṣyāmi prāyaścittavidhiṃ śubham
meaning: एष धर्मविधिः कृत्स्नश्चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य कीर्तितः अतः परं प्रवक्ष्यामि प्रायश्चित्तविधिं शुभम्
text: Having killed a cat, an ichneumon, a blue jay, a frog, a dog, an iguana, an owl and a crow,—he shall perform the penance of the ‘Śūdra-killer.’
iast: eṣa dharmavidhiḥ kṛtsnaścāturvarṇyasya kīrtitaḥ ataḥ paraṃ pravakṣyāmi prāyaścittavidhiṃ śubham

sanskrit_iast: sāntānikaṃ yakṣyamāṇamadhvagaṃ sārvavedasam guruarthaṃ pitṛmātryarthaṃ svādhyāyārthyupatāpinaḥ
meaning: सान्तानिकं यक्ष्यमाणमध्वगं सार्ववेदसम् गुरुअर्थं पितृमात्र्यर्थं स्वाध्यायार्थ्युपतापिनः
text: He who is seeking for progeny, he who is going to perform a sacrifice, a traveller on the road, he who has given away all his belongings, he who is begging for his preceptor, he who is begging for his parents, he who is begging for study, a sick man;—(1)—these nineBrāhmaṇas, who are religious mendicants, one should know as ‘Snātakas’; to these penniless men gifts shall be given, in proportion to their learning.
iast: sāntānikaṃ yakṣyamāṇamadhvagaṃ sārvavedasam guruarthaṃ pitṛmātryarthaṃ svādhyāyārthyupatāpinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: na vai tān snātakān vidyādbrāhmaṇān dharmabhikṣukān niḥsvebhyo deyametebhyo dānaṃ vidyāviśeṣataḥ
meaning: न वै तान् स्नातकान् विद्याद्ब्राह्मणान् धर्मभिक्षुकान् निःस्वेभ्यो देयमेतेभ्यो दानं विद्याविशेषतः
text: He who is seeking for progeny, he who is going to perform a sacrifice, a traveller on the road, he who has given away all his belongings, he who is begging for his preceptor, he who is begging for his parents, he who is begging for study, a sick man;—(1)—these nineBrāhmaṇas, who are religious mendicants, one should know as ‘Snātakas’; to these penniless men gifts shall be given, in proportion to their learning.
iast: na vai tān snātakān vidyādbrāhmaṇān dharmabhikṣukān niḥsvebhyo deyametebhyo dānaṃ vidyāviśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: etebhyo hi dvijāgryebhyo deyamannaṃ sadakṣiṇam itarebhyo bahirvedi kṛtānnaṃ deyamucyate
meaning: एतेभ्यो हि द्विजाग्र्येभ्यो देयमन्नं सदक्षिणम् इतरेभ्यो बहिर्वेदि कृतान्नं देयमुच्यते
text: For these best of twice-born men, the ‘gift’ shall consist of food accompanied by a present; for others, ‘gift’ has been declared to be the cooked food given outside the sacrificial enclosure.
iast: etebhyo hi dvijāgryebhyo deyamannaṃ sadakṣiṇam itarebhyo bahirvedi kṛtānnaṃ deyamucyate

sanskrit_iast: sarvaratnāni rājā tu yathārhaṃ pratipādayet brāhmaṇān vedaviduṣo yajñārthaṃ caiva dakṣiṇām
meaning: सर्वरत्नानि राजा तु यथार्हं प्रतिपादयेत् ब्राह्मणान् वेदविदुषो यज्ञार्थं चैव दक्षिणाम्
text: OnBrāhmaṇaslearned in the veda, the king shall bestow, according to their desert, all kinds of jewels, as also presents for sacrificial performances.
iast: sarvaratnāni rājā tu yathārhaṃ pratipādayet brāhmaṇān vedaviduṣo yajñārthaṃ caiva dakṣiṇām

sanskrit_iast: kṛtadāro 'parān dārān bhikṣitvā yo 'dhigacchati ratimātraṃ phalaṃ tasya dravyadātustu saṃtatiḥ
meaning: कृतदारो 'परान् दारान् भिक्षित्वा यो 'धिगच्छति रतिमात्रं फलं तस्य द्रव्यदातुस्तु संततिः
text: If a man, having already got a wife, weds another wife, after having begged (for the requisite wealth), sexual enjoyment is his only fruit; the issue belongs to the person who gave him the wealth.
iast: kṛtadāro 'parān dārān bhikṣitvā yo 'dhigacchati ratimātraṃ phalaṃ tasya dravyadātustu saṃtatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: dhanāni tu yathāśakti vipreṣu pratipādayet vedavitsu vivikteṣu pretya svargaṃ samaśnute
meaning: धनानि तु यथाशक्ति विप्रेषु प्रतिपादयेत् वेदवित्सु विविक्तेषु प्रेत्य स्वर्गं समश्नुते
text: One should give, according to one’s ability, wealth to Brāhmaṇas learned in the Veda and living alone; (thus) one obtains after death heavenly bliss..
iast: dhanāni tu yathāśakti vipreṣu pratipādayet vedavitsu vivikteṣu pretya svargaṃ samaśnute

sanskrit_iast: yasya traivārṣikaṃ bhaktaṃ paryāptaṃ bhṛtyavṛttaye adhikaṃ vā 'pi vidyeta sa somaṃ pātumarhati
meaning: यस्य त्रैवार्षिकं भक्तं पर्याप्तं भृत्यवृत्तये अधिकं वा 'पि विद्येत स सोमं पातुमर्हति
text: For this reason, if a twice-born person possessing less wealth drinks Soma, he does not obtain its reward, even though he may have drunk Soma formerly.
iast: yasya traivārṣikaṃ bhaktaṃ paryāptaṃ bhṛtyavṛttaye adhikaṃ vā 'pi vidyeta sa somaṃ pātumarhati

sanskrit_iast: ataḥ svalpīyasi dravye yaḥ somaṃ pibati dvijaḥ sa pītasomapūrvo 'pi na tasyāpnoti tatphalam
meaning: अतः स्वल्पीयसि द्रव्ये यः सोमं पिबति द्विजः स पीतसोमपूर्वो 'पि न तस्याप्नोति तत्फलम्
text: If a wealthy man gives to other people, while his own people are living in distress,—such counterfeit virtue would be like swallowing poison, which is sweet in the beginning.
iast: ataḥ svalpīyasi dravye yaḥ somaṃ pibati dvijaḥ sa pītasomapūrvo 'pi na tasyāpnoti tatphalam

sanskrit_iast: śaktaḥ parajane dātā svajane duḥkhajīvini madhvāpāto viṣāsvādaḥ sa dharmapratirūpakaḥ
meaning: शक्तः परजने दाता स्वजने दुःखजीविनि मध्वापातो विषास्वादः स धर्मप्रतिरूपकः
text: If a man does anything for his own benefit after death, to the detriment of the persons he has got to maintain,—it becomes conducive to unhappy results while he lives as also when he dies.—(9). Aged parents, well-behaved wife and infant sons have been declared by Manu to be persons that should be maintained, even by doing a hundred evil acts.
iast: śaktaḥ parajane dātā svajane duḥkhajīvini madhvāpāto viṣāsvādaḥ sa dharmapratirūpakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bhṛtyānāmuparodhena yat karotyaurdhvadehikam tadbhavatyasukhaudarkaṃ jīvataśca mṛtasya ca
meaning: भृत्यानामुपरोधेन यत् करोत्यौर्ध्वदेहिकम् तद्भवत्यसुखौदर्कं जीवतश्च मृतस्य च
text: If a man does anything for his own benefit after death, to the detriment of the persons he has got to maintain,—it becomes conducive to unhappy results while he lives as also when he dies.—(9). Aged parents, well-behaved wife and infant sons have been declared by Manu to be persons that should be maintained, even by doing a hundred evil acts.
iast: bhṛtyānāmuparodhena yat karotyaurdhvadehikam tadbhavatyasukhaudarkaṃ jīvataśca mṛtasya ca

sanskrit_iast: yajñaścet pratiruddhaḥ syādekenāṅgena yajvanaḥ brāhmaṇasya viśeṣena dhārmike sati rājani
meaning: यज्ञश्चेत् प्रतिरुद्धः स्यादेकेनाङ्गेन यज्वनः ब्राह्मणस्य विशेषेन धार्मिके सति राजनि
text: During the reign of a righteous king, if the sacrificial rite of a sacrificer, specially of aBrāhmaṇa, be interrupted for want of one requisite,—that substance may be appropriated, for the completion of that sacrifice, from the house of a Vaiśya possessed of many cattle, who does not perform sacrifices and does not drink the Soma.
iast: yajñaścet pratiruddhaḥ syādekenāṅgena yajvanaḥ brāhmaṇasya viśeṣena dhārmike sati rājani

sanskrit_iast: yo vaiśyaḥ syādbahupaśurhīnakraturasomapaḥ kuṭumbāt tasya taddravyamāharedyajñasiddhaye
meaning: यो वैश्यः स्याद्बहुपशुर्हीनक्रतुरसोमपः कुटुम्बात् तस्य तद्द्रव्यमाहरेद्यज्ञसिद्धये
text: During the reign of a righteous king, if the sacrificial rite of a sacrificer, specially of aBrāhmaṇa, be interrupted for want of one requisite,—that substance may be appropriated, for the completion of that sacrifice, from the house of a Vaiśya possessed of many cattle, who does not perform sacrifices and does not drink the Soma.
iast: yo vaiśyaḥ syādbahupaśurhīnakraturasomapaḥ kuṭumbāt tasya taddravyamāharedyajñasiddhaye

sanskrit_iast: āharet trīṇi vā dve vā kāmaṃ śūdrasya veśmanaḥ na hi śūdrasya yajñeṣu kaścidasti parigrahaḥ
meaning: आहरेत् त्रीणि वा द्वे वा कामं शूद्रस्य वेश्मनः न हि शूद्रस्य यज्ञेषु कश्चिदस्ति परिग्रहः
text: Or, he may freely take away two or three things from the house of aŚūdra; for theŚūdrahas nothing to do with acrifices.
iast: āharet trīṇi vā dve vā kāmaṃ śūdrasya veśmanaḥ na hi śūdrasya yajñeṣu kaścidasti parigrahaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo 'nāhitāgniḥ śatagurayajvā ca sahasraguḥ ayajñaś tayorapi kuṭumbābhyāmāharedavicārayan
meaning: यो 'नाहिताग्निः शतगुरयज्वा च सहस्रगुः अयज्ञश् तयोरपि कुटुम्बाभ्यामाहरेदविचारयन्
text: If a man, possessing a hundred cows, has not laid the Fire,—or a man possessing a thousand cows, performs no sacrifices,—out of the houses of these men also, one may take away (the sacrificial requisites) without hesitation.
iast: yo 'nāhitāgniḥ śatagurayajvā ca sahasraguḥ ayajñaś tayorapi kuṭumbābhyāmāharedavicārayan

sanskrit_iast: ādānanityāccādāturāharedaprayacchataḥ tathā yaśo 'sya prathate dharmaścaiva pravardhate
meaning: आदाननित्याच्चादातुराहरेदप्रयच्छतः तथा यशो 'स्य प्रथते धर्मश्चैव प्रवर्धते
text: He shall take it also from one who always acquires, and never gives, if he refuses to give up the thing; thus does his fame spread and merit increase.
iast: ādānanityāccādāturāharedaprayacchataḥ tathā yaśo 'sya prathate dharmaścaiva pravardhate

sanskrit_iast: tathā 'iva saptame bhakte bhaktāni ṣaḍanaśnatā aśvastanavidhānena hartavyaṃ hīnakarmaṇaḥ
meaning: तथा 'इव सप्तमे भक्ते भक्तानि षडनश्नता अश्वस्तनविधानेन हर्तव्यं हीनकर्मणः
text: Likewise one who has not eaten six meals, may, for the seventh meal, take from a person whose sacred duties are neglected,—but only to this extent that it does not last till the morrow;—(16) either from the threshing yard, or from the field, or from the house, or from any place where it may be got; but if the owner questions him, he must confess it to the questioner.
iast: tathā 'iva saptame bhakte bhaktāni ṣaḍanaśnatā aśvastanavidhānena hartavyaṃ hīnakarmaṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: khalāt kṣetrādagārādvā yato vā 'pyupalabhyate ākhyātavyaṃ tu tat tasmai pṛcchate yadi pṛcchati
meaning: खलात् क्षेत्रादगाराद्वा यतो वा 'प्युपलभ्यते आख्यातव्यं तु तत् तस्मै पृच्छते यदि पृच्छति
text: Likewise one who has not eaten six meals, may, for the seventh meal, take from a person whose sacred duties are neglected,—but only to this extent that it does not last till the morrow;—(16) either from the threshing yard, or from the field, or from the house, or from any place where it may be got; but if the owner questions him, he must confess it to the questioner.
iast: khalāt kṣetrādagārādvā yato vā 'pyupalabhyate ākhyātavyaṃ tu tat tasmai pṛcchate yadi pṛcchati

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇasvaṃ na hartavyaṃ kṣatriyeṇa kadā cana dasyuniṣkriyayostu svamajīvan hartumarhati
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्वं न हर्तव्यं क्षत्रियेण कदा चन दस्युनिष्क्रिययोस्तु स्वमजीवन् हर्तुमर्हति
text: TheKṣatriyashall never appropriate the property of aBrāhmaṇa; when starving, he may appropriate the property of the robbers and of one who neglects his duties.
iast: brāhmaṇasvaṃ na hartavyaṃ kṣatriyeṇa kadā cana dasyuniṣkriyayostu svamajīvan hartumarhati

sanskrit_iast: yo 'sādhubhyo 'rthamādāya sādhubhyaḥ saṃprayacchati sa kṛtvā plavamātmānaṃ saṃtārayati tāvubhau
meaning: यो 'साधुभ्यो 'र्थमादाय साधुभ्यः संप्रयच्छति स कृत्वा प्लवमात्मानं संतारयति तावुभौ
text: He who takes wealth from the wicked and gives it to the virtuous, makes himself a raft and carries both over.
iast: yo 'sādhubhyo 'rthamādāya sādhubhyaḥ saṃprayacchati sa kṛtvā plavamātmānaṃ saṃtārayati tāvubhau

sanskrit_iast: yaddhanaṃ yajñaśīlānāṃ devasvaṃ tadvidurbudhāḥ ayajvanāṃ tu yadvittamāsurasvaṃ taducyate
meaning: यद्धनं यज्ञशीलानां देवस्वं तद्विदुर्बुधाः अयज्वनां तु यद्वित्तमासुरस्वं तदुच्यते
text: The property of persons given to perform sacrifices the learned regard as ‘the property of the gods;’ while the property of those who do not perform sacrifices is described as ‘the property of demons.’
iast: yaddhanaṃ yajñaśīlānāṃ devasvaṃ tadvidurbudhāḥ ayajvanāṃ tu yadvittamāsurasvaṃ taducyate

sanskrit_iast: na tasmin dhārayeddaṇḍaṃ dhārmikaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ kṣatriyasya hi bāliśyādbrāhmaṇaḥ sīdati kṣudhā
meaning: न तस्मिन् धारयेद्दण्डं धार्मिकः पृथिवीपतिः क्षत्रियस्य हि बालिश्याद्ब्राह्मणः सीदति क्षुधा
text: The righteous king shall inflict no punishment upon him; as it is only through the foolishness of theKṣatriyathat theBrāhmaṇasuffers from hunger.
iast: na tasmin dhārayeddaṇḍaṃ dhārmikaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ kṣatriyasya hi bāliśyādbrāhmaṇaḥ sīdati kṣudhā

sanskrit_iast: tasya bhṛtyajanaṃ jñātvā svakuṭumbān mahīpatiḥ śrutaśīle ca vijñāya vṛttiṃ dharmyāṃ prakalpayet
meaning: तस्य भृत्यजनं ज्ञात्वा स्वकुटुम्बान् महीपतिः श्रुतशीले च विज्ञाय वृत्तिं धर्म्यां प्रकल्पयेत्
text: Having ascertained the number of persons he has to maintain, and having investigated his learning and character, the king shall provide, out of his own property, a proper living for him.
iast: tasya bhṛtyajanaṃ jñātvā svakuṭumbān mahīpatiḥ śrutaśīle ca vijñāya vṛttiṃ dharmyāṃ prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: kalpayitvā 'sya vṛttiṃ ca rakṣedenaṃ samantataḥ rājā hi dharmaṣaḍbhāgaṃ tasmāt prāpnoti rakṣitāt
meaning: कल्पयित्वा 'स्य वृत्तिं च रक्षेदेनं समन्ततः राजा हि धर्मषड्भागं तस्मात् प्राप्नोति रक्षितात्
text: Having provided a living for him, the king shall protect him in every way; since he obtains, from the person thus protected, the sixth part of his spiritual merit.
iast: kalpayitvā 'sya vṛttiṃ ca rakṣedenaṃ samantataḥ rājā hi dharmaṣaḍbhāgaṃ tasmāt prāpnoti rakṣitāt

sanskrit_iast: na yajñārthaṃ dhanaṃ śūdrādvipro bhikṣeta karhi cit yajamāno hi bhikṣitvā caṇḍālaḥ pretya jāyate
meaning: न यज्ञार्थं धनं शूद्राद्विप्रो भिक्षेत कर्हि चित् यजमानो हि भिक्षित्वा चण्डालः प्रेत्य जायते
text: TheBrāhmaṇashall never beg from aŚūdrawealth for the purpose of sacrificial performances; if one perform sacrifices with wealth so begged, he is born, after death, as aCaṇḍāla.
iast: na yajñārthaṃ dhanaṃ śūdrādvipro bhikṣeta karhi cit yajamāno hi bhikṣitvā caṇḍālaḥ pretya jāyate

sanskrit_iast: yājñārthamarthaṃ bhikṣitvā yo na sarvaṃ prayacchati sa yāti bhāsatāṃ vipraḥ kākatāṃ vā śataṃ samāḥ
meaning: याज्ञार्थमर्थं भिक्षित्वा यो न सर्वं प्रयच्छति स याति भासतां विप्रः काकतां वा शतं समाः
text: If aBrāhmaṇa, having begged wealth for a sacrifice, does not spend the whole of it, he becomes, for a hundred years, aBhāsaor acrow.
iast: yājñārthamarthaṃ bhikṣitvā yo na sarvaṃ prayacchati sa yāti bhāsatāṃ vipraḥ kākatāṃ vā śataṃ samāḥ

sanskrit_iast: devasvaṃ brāhmaṇasvaṃ vā lobhenaupahinasti yaḥ sa pāpātmā pare loke gṛdhraucchiṣṭena jīvati
meaning: देवस्वं ब्राह्मणस्वं वा लोभेनौपहिनस्ति यः स पापात्मा परे लोके गृध्रौच्छिष्टेन जीवति
text: The sinful man who, through covetousness, seizes the property of the gods, or the property ofBrāhmaṇas, lives, in the other world, upon the leavings of vultures.
iast: devasvaṃ brāhmaṇasvaṃ vā lobhenaupahinasti yaḥ sa pāpātmā pare loke gṛdhraucchiṣṭena jīvati

sanskrit_iast: iṣṭiṃ vaiśvānarīṃ nityaṃ nirvapedabdaparyaye kḷptānāṃ paśusomānāṃ niṣkṛtyarthamasaṃbhave
meaning: इष्टिं वैश्वानरीं नित्यं निर्वपेदब्दपर्यये कॢप्तानां पशुसोमानां निष्कृत्यर्थमसंभवे
text: In the event of the impossibility of the performance of the prescribed Animal and Soma sacrifices, one shall always offer theVaiśvānarīsacrifice at the change of the year,—in expiation thereof.
iast: iṣṭiṃ vaiśvānarīṃ nityaṃ nirvapedabdaparyaye kḷptānāṃ paśusomānāṃ niṣkṛtyarthamasaṃbhave

sanskrit_iast: āpatkalpena yo dharmaṃ kurute 'nāpadi dvijaḥ sa nāpnoti phalaṃ tasya paratraiti vicāritam
meaning: आपत्कल्पेन यो धर्मं कुरुते 'नापदि द्विजः स नाप्नोति फलं तस्य परत्रैति विचारितम्
text: If a twice-born person performs, even in normal times, a sacred act according to the mode sanctioned for Abnormal Times,—he does not obtain its reward. Such is the well-considered opinion.
iast: āpatkalpena yo dharmaṃ kurute 'nāpadi dvijaḥ sa nāpnoti phalaṃ tasya paratraiti vicāritam

sanskrit_iast: viśvaiśca devaiḥ sādhyaiśca brāhmaṇaiśca maharṣibhiḥ āpatsu maraṇādbhītairvidheḥ pratinidhiḥ kṛtaḥ
meaning: विश्वैश्च देवैः साध्यैश्च ब्राह्मणैश्च महर्षिभिः आपत्सु मरणाद्भीतैर्विधेः प्रतिनिधिः कृतः
text: The substitute of the Primary Rule was ordained by theViśvedevas, theSādhyasand the greatBrāhmaṇaSages, when they were afraid of perishing in abnormal times.
iast: viśvaiśca devaiḥ sādhyaiśca brāhmaṇaiśca maharṣibhiḥ āpatsu maraṇādbhītairvidheḥ pratinidhiḥ kṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: prabhuḥ prathamakalpasya yo 'nukalpena vartate na sāṃparāyikaṃ tasya durmatervidyate phalam
meaning: प्रभुः प्रथमकल्पस्य यो 'नुकल्पेन वर्तते न सांपरायिकं तस्य दुर्मतेर्विद्यते फलम्
text: If one, who is able to fulfil the primary rule, adopts the substitute,—this evil-minded man does not obtain its reward in the other world.
iast: prabhuḥ prathamakalpasya yo 'nukalpena vartate na sāṃparāyikaṃ tasya durmatervidyate phalam

sanskrit_iast: na brāhmaṇo vedayeta kiṃ cidrājani dharmavit svavīryeṇaiva tāṃśiṣyān mānavānapakāriṇaḥ
meaning: न ब्राह्मणो वेदयेत किं चिद्राजनि धर्मवित् स्ववीर्येणैव तांशिष्यान् मानवानपकारिणः
text: TheBrāhmaṇaconversant with the Law shall not complain to the King; by his own power alone he shall punish the men that injure him.
iast: na brāhmaṇo vedayeta kiṃ cidrājani dharmavit svavīryeṇaiva tāṃśiṣyān mānavānapakāriṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: svavīryādrājavīryācca svavīryaṃ balavattaram tasmāt svenaiva vīryeṇa nigṛhṇīyādarīn dvijaḥ
meaning: स्ववीर्याद्राजवीर्याच्च स्ववीर्यं बलवत्तरम् तस्मात् स्वेनैव वीर्येण निगृह्णीयादरीन् द्विजः
text: His own power is more forcible than the King’s power; the twice-born man shall, therefore, punish his enemies by his own power.
iast: svavīryādrājavīryācca svavīryaṃ balavattaram tasmāt svenaiva vīryeṇa nigṛhṇīyādarīn dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: śrutīratharvāṅgirasīḥ kuryādityavicārayan vākṣastraṃ vai brāhmaṇasya tena hanyādarīn dvijaḥ
meaning: श्रुतीरथर्वाङ्गिरसीः कुर्यादित्यविचारयन् वाक्षस्त्रं वै ब्राह्मणस्य तेन हन्यादरीन् द्विजः
text: He should make use of the sacred texts of the Atharva-Veda, without hesitation. Speech indeed is theBrāhmaṇa’sweapon; by that should the twice-born strike his enemies.
iast: śrutīratharvāṅgirasīḥ kuryādityavicārayan vākṣastraṃ vai brāhmaṇasya tena hanyādarīn dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: kṣatriyo bāhuvīryeṇa taredāpadamātmanaḥ dhanena vaiśyaśūdrau tu japahomairdvijottamaḥ
meaning: क्षत्रियो बाहुवीर्येण तरेदापदमात्मनः धनेन वैश्यशूद्रौ तु जपहोमैर्द्विजोत्तमः
text: TheKṣatriyashall cut through his misfortunes by the strength of his arms; the Vaiśya and theŚūdraby their wealth, and the chief of the twice-born by muttered prayers and oblations into the fire.
iast: kṣatriyo bāhuvīryeṇa taredāpadamātmanaḥ dhanena vaiśyaśūdrau tu japahomairdvijottamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vidhātā śāsitā vaktā maitro brāhmaṇa ucyate tasmai nākuśalaṃ brūyānna śuṣkāṃ giramīrayet
meaning: विधाता शासिता वक्ता मैत्रो ब्राह्मण उच्यते तस्मै नाकुशलं ब्रूयान्न शुष्कां गिरमीरयेत्
text: TheBrāhmaṇais called the creator, the punisher, the teacher and the advisor; therefore one should not address unpleasant words to him, nor use any harsh words.
iast: vidhātā śāsitā vaktā maitro brāhmaṇa ucyate tasmai nākuśalaṃ brūyānna śuṣkāṃ giramīrayet

sanskrit_iast: na vai kanyā na yuvatirnālpavidyo na bāliśaḥ hotā syādagnihotrasya nārto nāsaṃskṛtastathā
meaning: न वै कन्या न युवतिर्नाल्पविद्यो न बालिशः होता स्यादग्निहोत्रस्य नार्तो नासंस्कृतस्तथा
text: Neither a girl, nor a youthful woman, nor a man of little learning, nor a fool, nor one distressed, nor one without the sacraments shall act as a ‘Hotṛ’ at the Agnihotra.
iast: na vai kanyā na yuvatirnālpavidyo na bāliśaḥ hotā syādagnihotrasya nārto nāsaṃskṛtastathā

sanskrit_iast: narake hi patantyete juhvantaḥ sa ca yasya tat juhvataḥ tasmādvaitānakuśalo hotā syādvedapāragaḥ
meaning: नरके हि पतन्त्येते जुह्वन्तः स च यस्य तत् जुह्वतः तस्माद्वैतानकुशलो होता स्याद्वेदपारगः
text: If these persons pour the oblations, they sink into hell, as also the person to whom the agnihotra belongs; hence the ‘Hotṛ’ shall be a person fully learned in the Veda and expert in rituals.
iast: narake hi patantyete juhvantaḥ sa ca yasya tat juhvataḥ tasmādvaitānakuśalo hotā syādvedapāragaḥ

sanskrit_iast: prājāpatyamadattvā 'śvamagnyādheyasya dakṣiṇām anāhitāgnirbhavati brāhmaṇo vibhave sati
meaning: प्राजापत्यमदत्त्वा 'श्वमग्न्याधेयस्य दक्षिणाम् अनाहिताग्निर्भवति ब्राह्मणो विभवे सति
text: If aBrāhmaṇa, possessed of wealth, does not give a ‘prājāpatya’ horse as the ‘fee’ foe the fire-laying, he becomes as good as one who has not laid the fire at all.
iast: prājāpatyamadattvā 'śvamagnyādheyasya dakṣiṇām anāhitāgnirbhavati brāhmaṇo vibhave sati

sanskrit_iast: puṇyānyanyāni kurvīta śraddadhāno jitaindriyaḥ na tvalpadakṣiṇairyajñairyajetaiha kathaṃ cana
meaning: पुण्यान्यन्यानि कुर्वीत श्रद्दधानो जितैन्द्रियः न त्वल्पदक्षिणैर्यज्ञैर्यजेतैह कथं चन
text: The man who has faith and control over his senses may perform other meritorious acts; but he shall not, on any account, perform sacrifices with small fees.
iast: puṇyānyanyāni kurvīta śraddadhāno jitaindriyaḥ na tvalpadakṣiṇairyajñairyajetaiha kathaṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇi yaśaḥ svargamāyuḥ kīrtiṃ prajāḥ paśūn hantyalpadakṣiṇo yajñastasmānnālpadhano yajet
meaning: इन्द्रियाणि यशः स्वर्गमायुः कीर्तिं प्रजाः पशून् हन्त्यल्पदक्षिणो यज्ञस्तस्मान्नाल्पधनो यजेत्
text: A sacrifice with small fees destroys the organs, honour, heaven, longevity, fame, offspring and cattle. Hence one possessed of small means shall not perform sacrifices.
iast: indriyāṇi yaśaḥ svargamāyuḥ kīrtiṃ prajāḥ paśūn hantyalpadakṣiṇo yajñastasmānnālpadhano yajet

sanskrit_iast: agnihotryapavidhyāgnīn brāhmaṇaḥ kāmakārataḥ cāndrāyaṇaṃ caren māsaṃ vīrahatyāsamaṃ hi tat
meaning: अग्निहोत्र्यपविध्याग्नीन् ब्राह्मणः कामकारतः चान्द्रायणं चरेन् मासं वीरहत्यासमं हि तत्
text: If aBrāhmaṇawho has set up the fires neglects them wilfully, he shall perform the ‘Cāndrāyaṇa’ penance for a month; as his offence is equal to the offence of killing a hero.
iast: agnihotryapavidhyāgnīn brāhmaṇaḥ kāmakārataḥ cāndrāyaṇaṃ caren māsaṃ vīrahatyāsamaṃ hi tat

sanskrit_iast: ye śūdrādadhigamyārthamagnihotramupāsate ṛtvijaste hi śūdrāṇāṃ brahmavādiṣu garhitāḥ
meaning: ये शूद्रादधिगम्यार्थमग्निहोत्रमुपासते ऋत्विजस्ते हि शूद्राणां ब्रह्मवादिषु गर्हिताः
text: Those who perform the Agnihotra, after having obtained wealth from aŚūdra, are ‘Śūdra’sPriests,’ censured among Vedic scholars.
iast: ye śūdrādadhigamyārthamagnihotramupāsate ṛtvijaste hi śūdrāṇāṃ brahmavādiṣu garhitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ satatamajñānāṃ vṛṣalāgnyupasevinām padā mastakamākramya dātā durgāṇi saṃtaret
meaning: तेषां सततमज्ञानां वृषलाग्न्युपसेविनाम् पदा मस्तकमाक्रम्य दाता दुर्गाणि संतरेत्
text: The giver shall cut across his miseries by putting his foot upon the heads of those fools who attend upon theŚūdra’sfires.
iast: teṣāṃ satatamajñānāṃ vṛṣalāgnyupasevinām padā mastakamākramya dātā durgāṇi saṃtaret

sanskrit_iast: akurvan vihitaṃ karma ninditaṃ ca samācaran prasaktaścaindriyārtheṣu prāyaścittīyate naraḥ prasajjanindriyārtheṣu
meaning: अकुर्वन् विहितं कर्म निन्दितं च समाचरन् प्रसक्तश्चैन्द्रियार्थेषु प्रायश्चित्तीयते नरः प्रसज्जनिन्द्रियार्थेषु
text: If a man does not do what is enjoined, or does what is censured, or becomes addicted to sensual objects, he becomes liable to expiatory rites.
iast: akurvan vihitaṃ karma ninditaṃ ca samācaran prasaktaścaindriyārtheṣu prāyaścittīyate naraḥ prasajjanindriyārtheṣu

sanskrit_iast: akāmataḥ kṛte pāpe prāyaścittaṃ vidurbudhāḥ kāmakārakṛte 'pyāhureke śrutinidarśanāt
meaning: अकामतः कृते पापे प्रायश्चित्तं विदुर्बुधाः कामकारकृते 'प्याहुरेके श्रुतिनिदर्शनात्
text: The learned understand Expiatory rites to pertain to cases where the sin is committed unintentionally; some people however assert on the evidence of ‘Śruti texts’ that they apply to cases of intentional offence also.
iast: akāmataḥ kṛte pāpe prāyaścittaṃ vidurbudhāḥ kāmakārakṛte 'pyāhureke śrutinidarśanāt

sanskrit_iast: akāmataḥ kṛtaṃ pāpaṃ vedābhyāsena śudhyati kāmatastu kṛtaṃ mohāt prāyaścittaiḥ pṛthagvidhaiḥ
meaning: अकामतः कृतं पापं वेदाभ्यासेन शुध्यति कामतस्तु कृतं मोहात् प्रायश्चित्तैः पृथग्विधैः
text: A sin committed unintentionally is expiated by the reciting of the Veda; while that committed intentionally, in folly, is expiated by the various forms of expiatory rites.
iast: akāmataḥ kṛtaṃ pāpaṃ vedābhyāsena śudhyati kāmatastu kṛtaṃ mohāt prāyaścittaiḥ pṛthagvidhaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: prāyaścittīyatāṃ prāpya daivāt pūrvakṛtena vā na saṃsargaṃ vrajet sadbhiḥ prāyaścitte 'kṛte dvijaḥ
meaning: प्रायश्चित्तीयतां प्राप्य दैवात् पूर्वकृतेन वा न संसर्गं व्रजेत् सद्भिः प्रायश्चित्ते 'कृते द्विजः
text: Having incurred the liability to an expiatory rite,—either by chance or by a previous deed,—a twice-born person shall not associate with righteous men, so long as the expiatory rite has not been performed.
iast: prāyaścittīyatāṃ prāpya daivāt pūrvakṛtena vā na saṃsargaṃ vrajet sadbhiḥ prāyaścitte 'kṛte dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: iha duścaritaiḥ ke cit ke cit pūrvakṛtaistathā prāpnuvanti durātmāno narā rūpaviparyayam
meaning: इह दुश्चरितैः के चित् के चित् पूर्वकृतैस्तथा प्राप्नुवन्ति दुरात्मानो नरा रूपविपर्ययम्
text: Evil-minded men suffer disfigurement,—some from evil deeds committed during the present life and others from those committed in former lives.
iast: iha duścaritaiḥ ke cit ke cit pūrvakṛtaistathā prāpnuvanti durātmāno narā rūpaviparyayam

sanskrit_iast: suvarṇacauraḥ kaunakhyaṃ surāpaḥ śyāvadantatām brahmahā kṣayarogitvaṃ dauścarmyaṃ gurutalpagaḥ
meaning: सुवर्णचौरः कौनख्यं सुरापः श्यावदन्तताम् ब्रह्महा क्षयरोगित्वं दौश्चर्म्यं गुरुतल्पगः
text: The stealer of gold has disfigured nails; the drinker of wine, black teeth; the slayer of aBrāhmaṇa, consumption; and the violator of his preceptor’s bed, a disfigured skin;—(49) the informer, a foul-smelling nose; the false caluminator, a foul-smelling mouth; the stealer of grains, a deficiency of limbs; and the adulterator, an excess of limbs;—(50) the stealer of food, dyspepsia; the stealer of words, dumbness; the stealer of clothes, leucoderma; and the stealer of horse, lameness.—(51) It is thus that idiots, the dumb, the blind, the deaf and deformed men, despised by righteous men, are born, on account of particular acts.
iast: suvarṇacauraḥ kaunakhyaṃ surāpaḥ śyāvadantatām brahmahā kṣayarogitvaṃ dauścarmyaṃ gurutalpagaḥ

sanskrit_iast: piśunaḥ pautināsikyaṃ sūcakaḥ pūtivaktratām dhānyacauro 'ṅgahīnatvamātiraikyaṃ tu miśrakaḥ
meaning: पिशुनः पौतिनासिक्यं सूचकः पूतिवक्त्रताम् धान्यचौरो 'ङ्गहीनत्वमातिरैक्यं तु मिश्रकः
text: The stealer of gold has disfigured nails; the drinker of wine, black teeth; the slayer of aBrāhmaṇa, consumption; and the violator of his preceptor’s bed, a disfigured skin;—(49) the informer, a foul-smelling nose; the false caluminator, a foul-smelling mouth; the stealer of grains, a deficiency of limbs; and the adulterator, an excess of limbs;—(50) the stealer of food, dyspepsia; the stealer of words, dumbness; the stealer of clothes, leucoderma; and the stealer of horse, lameness.—(51) It is thus that idiots, the dumb, the blind, the deaf and deformed men, despised by righteous men, are born, on account of particular acts.
iast: piśunaḥ pautināsikyaṃ sūcakaḥ pūtivaktratām dhānyacauro 'ṅgahīnatvamātiraikyaṃ tu miśrakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: annahartā 'mayāvitvaṃ maukyaṃ vāgapahārakaḥ vastrāpahārakaḥ śvaitryaṃ paṅgutāmaśvahārakaḥ
meaning: अन्नहर्ता 'मयावित्वं मौक्यं वागपहारकः वस्त्रापहारकः श्वैत्र्यं पङ्गुतामश्वहारकः
text: The stealer of gold has disfigured nails; the drinker of wine, black teeth; the slayer of aBrāhmaṇa, consumption; and the violator of his preceptor’s bed, a disfigured skin;—(49) the informer, a foul-smelling nose; the false caluminator, a foul-smelling mouth; the stealer of grains, a deficiency of limbs; and the adulterator, an excess of limbs;—(50) the stealer of food, dyspepsia; the stealer of words, dumbness; the stealer of clothes, leucoderma; and the stealer of horse, lameness.—(51) It is thus that idiots, the dumb, the blind, the deaf and deformed men, despised by righteous men, are born, on account of particular acts.
iast: annahartā 'mayāvitvaṃ maukyaṃ vāgapahārakaḥ vastrāpahārakaḥ śvaitryaṃ paṅgutāmaśvahārakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ karmaviśeṣeṇa jāyante sadvigarhitāḥ jaḍamūkāndhabadhirā vikṛtākṛtayastathā
meaning: एवं कर्मविशेषेण जायन्ते सद्विगर्हिताः जडमूकान्धबधिरा विकृताकृतयस्तथा
text: The stealer of gold has disfigured nails; the drinker of wine, black teeth; the slayer of aBrāhmaṇa, consumption; and the violator of his preceptor’s bed, a disfigured skin;—(49) the informer, a foul-smelling nose; the false caluminator, a foul-smelling mouth; the stealer of grains, a deficiency of limbs; and the adulterator, an excess of limbs;—(50) the stealer of food, dyspepsia; the stealer of words, dumbness; the stealer of clothes, leucoderma; and the stealer of horse, lameness.—(51) It is thus that idiots, the dumb, the blind, the deaf and deformed men, despised by righteous men, are born, on account of particular acts.
iast: evaṃ karmaviśeṣeṇa jāyante sadvigarhitāḥ jaḍamūkāndhabadhirā vikṛtākṛtayastathā

sanskrit_iast: caritavyamato nityaṃ prāyaścittaṃ viśuddhaye nindyairhi lakṣaṇairyuktā jāyante 'niṣkṛtenasaḥ
meaning: चरितव्यमतो नित्यं प्रायश्चित्तं विशुद्धये निन्द्यैर्हि लक्षणैर्युक्ता जायन्ते 'निष्कृतेनसः
text: Because persons with sins unexpiated are born with disgraceful marks, therefore expiatory rites shall always be performed, for the purpose of purification.
iast: caritavyamato nityaṃ prāyaścittaṃ viśuddhaye nindyairhi lakṣaṇairyuktā jāyante 'niṣkṛtenasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: brahmahatyā surāpānaṃ steyaṃ gurvaṅganāgamaḥ mahānti pātakānyāhuḥ saṃsargaścāpi taiḥ saha
meaning: ब्रह्महत्या सुरापानं स्तेयं गुर्वङ्गनागमः महान्ति पातकान्याहुः संसर्गश्चापि तैः सह
text: Brāhmaṇa-slaying, wine-drinking, theft, intercourse with the Preceptor’s wife,—are called the ‘heinous offences,’ as also association with these.
iast: brahmahatyā surāpānaṃ steyaṃ gurvaṅganāgamaḥ mahānti pātakānyāhuḥ saṃsargaścāpi taiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: anṛtaṃ ca samutkarṣe rājagāmi ca paiśunam guroścālīkanirbandhaḥ samāni brahmahatyayā
meaning: अनृतं च समुत्कर्षे राजगामि च पैशुनम् गुरोश्चालीकनिर्बन्धः समानि ब्रह्महत्यया
text: Lying for self-aggrandisement, caluminating before the king, and falsely harassing the Preceptor are equal to ‘Brāhmaṇa-killing.’
iast: anṛtaṃ ca samutkarṣe rājagāmi ca paiśunam guroścālīkanirbandhaḥ samāni brahmahatyayā

sanskrit_iast: brahmaujjhatā vedanindā kauṭasākṣyaṃ suhṛdvadhaḥ garhitānādyayorjagdhiḥ surāpānasamāni ṣaṭ
meaning: ब्रह्मौज्झता वेदनिन्दा कौटसाक्ष्यं सुहृद्वधः गर्हितानाद्ययोर्जग्धिः सुरापानसमानि षट्
text: Neglecting the Veda, reviling the Veda, bearing false witness, slaying a friend, and eating of forbidden and unfit food,—these six are equal to ‘wine-drinking.’
iast: brahmaujjhatā vedanindā kauṭasākṣyaṃ suhṛdvadhaḥ garhitānādyayorjagdhiḥ surāpānasamāni ṣaṭ

sanskrit_iast: nikṣepasyāpaharaṇaṃ narāśvarajatasya ca bhūmivajramaṇīnāṃ ca rukmasteyasamaṃ smṛtam
meaning: निक्षेपस्यापहरणं नराश्वरजतस्य च भूमिवज्रमणीनां च रुक्मस्तेयसमं स्मृतम्
text: Stealing of a deposit, or of men, horse, silver, land, diamonds and other oems,—all this has been declared to be equal to the ‘stealing of gold.’
iast: nikṣepasyāpaharaṇaṃ narāśvarajatasya ca bhūmivajramaṇīnāṃ ca rukmasteyasamaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: retaḥsekaḥ svayonīṣu kumārīṣvantyajāsu ca sakhyuḥ putrasya ca strīṣu gurutalpasamaṃ viduḥ
meaning: रेतःसेकः स्वयोनीषु कुमारीष्वन्त्यजासु च सख्युः पुत्रस्य च स्त्रीषु गुरुतल्पसमं विदुः
text: Carnal intercourse with one’s uterine sister, or with virgins, or with low-born women, or with the women of one’s friend or son,—all this they regard as equal to the ‘violating of the Preceptor’s bed.’
iast: retaḥsekaḥ svayonīṣu kumārīṣvantyajāsu ca sakhyuḥ putrasya ca strīṣu gurutalpasamaṃ viduḥ

sanskrit_iast: govadho 'yājyasaṃyājyaṃ pāradāryātmavikrayaḥ gurumātṛpitṛtyāgaḥ svādhyāyāgnyoḥ sutasya ca
meaning: गोवधो 'याज्यसंयाज्यं पारदार्यात्मविक्रयः गुरुमातृपितृत्यागः स्वाध्यायाग्न्योः सुतस्य च
text: Killing a cow, sacrificing for one unworthy to sacrifice, adultery, selling oneself, abandoning one’s father, mother, teacher, or son, or of Vedic study or Fire;
iast: govadho 'yājyasaṃyājyaṃ pāradāryātmavikrayaḥ gurumātṛpitṛtyāgaḥ svādhyāyāgnyoḥ sutasya ca

sanskrit_iast: parivittitā 'nuje 'nūḍhe parivedanameva ca tayordānaṃ ca kanyāyāstayoreva ca yājanam
meaning: परिवित्तिता 'नुजे 'नूढे परिवेदनमेव च तयोर्दानं च कन्यायास्तयोरेव च याजनम्
text: Allowing oneself to be superseded in marriage by his younger brother, superseding by the younger one’s elder brother in marriage, and the giving of one’s daughter to, or sacrificing for, these two;
iast: parivittitā 'nuje 'nūḍhe parivedanameva ca tayordānaṃ ca kanyāyāstayoreva ca yājanam

sanskrit_iast: kanyāyā dūṣaṇaṃ caiva vārdhuṣyaṃ vratalopanam taḍāgārāmadārāṇāmapatyasya ca vikrayaḥ
meaning: कन्याया दूषणं चैव वार्धुष्यं व्रतलोपनम् तडागारामदाराणामपत्यस्य च विक्रयः
text: Defiling a maiden, usury, breaking of a vow, selling a tank, a garden, one’s wife or a child.
iast: kanyāyā dūṣaṇaṃ caiva vārdhuṣyaṃ vratalopanam taḍāgārāmadārāṇāmapatyasya ca vikrayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vrātyatā bāndhavatyāgo bhṛtyādhyāpanameva ca bhṛtyā cādhyayanādānamapaṇyānāṃ ca vikrayaḥ bhṛtāccādhyayanādānam
meaning: व्रात्यता बान्धवत्यागो भृत्याध्यापनमेव च भृत्या चाध्ययनादानमपण्यानां च विक्रयः भृताच्चाध्ययनादानम्
text: Apostacy, abandoning a relative, teaching for wages, learning from a paid teacher and the selling of what should not be sold.
iast: vrātyatā bāndhavatyāgo bhṛtyādhyāpanameva ca bhṛtyā cādhyayanādānamapaṇyānāṃ ca vikrayaḥ bhṛtāccādhyayanādānam

sanskrit_iast: sarvākāreṣvadhīkāro mahāyantrapravartanam hiṃsauṣadhīnāṃ striājīvo 'bhicāro mūlakarma ca
meaning: सर्वाकारेष्वधीकारो महायन्त्रप्रवर्तनम् हिंसौषधीनां स्त्रिआजीवो 'भिचारो मूलकर्म च
text: Superintending all mines, executing large mechanical works, destroying medicinal herbs, subsisting on women, performing malevolent rites and sorcery.
iast: sarvākāreṣvadhīkāro mahāyantrapravartanam hiṃsauṣadhīnāṃ striājīvo 'bhicāro mūlakarma ca

sanskrit_iast: indhanārthamaśuṣkāṇāṃ drumāṇāmavapātanam ātmārthaṃ ca kriyārambho ninditānnādanaṃ tathā
meaning: इन्धनार्थमशुष्काणां द्रुमाणामवपातनम् आत्मार्थं च क्रियारम्भो निन्दितान्नादनं तथा
text: Cutting down green trees for purposes of fuel, the undertaking of the act (of cooking) for one’s own benefit and the eating of forbidden food.
iast: indhanārthamaśuṣkāṇāṃ drumāṇāmavapātanam ātmārthaṃ ca kriyārambho ninditānnādanaṃ tathā

sanskrit_iast: anāhitāgnitā steyaṃ ṛṇānāmanapakriyā asatśāṣtrādhigamanaṃ kauśīlavyasya ca kriyā
meaning: अनाहिताग्निता स्तेयं ऋणानामनपक्रिया असत्शाष्त्राधिगमनं कौशीलव्यस्य च क्रिया
text: Omission of fire-laying, theft, non-payment of debts, studying bad books, and the practising of the histrionic art.
iast: anāhitāgnitā steyaṃ ṛṇānāmanapakriyā asatśāṣtrādhigamanaṃ kauśīlavyasya ca kriyā

sanskrit_iast: dhānyakupyapaśusteyaṃ madyapastrīniṣevaṇam strīśūdraviśkṣatravadho nāstikyaṃ caupapātakam
meaning: धान्यकुप्यपशुस्तेयं मद्यपस्त्रीनिषेवणम् स्त्रीशूद्रविश्क्षत्रवधो नास्तिक्यं चौपपातकम्
text: Stealing grain, base metals and cattle,—intercourse with women addicted to drinking wine,—killing women,Śūdras,VaiśyasandKṣatriyas,—and heresy,—everyone of these is a ‘minor offence.’
iast: dhānyakupyapaśusteyaṃ madyapastrīniṣevaṇam strīśūdraviśkṣatravadho nāstikyaṃ caupapātakam
