sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇasya rujaḥ kṛtvā ghrātiraghreyamadyayoḥ rujaḥkṛtyaṃ jaihmyaṃ ca maithunaṃ puṃsi jātibhraṃśakaraṃ smṛtam
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्य रुजः कृत्वा घ्रातिरघ्रेयमद्ययोः रुजःकृत्यं जैह्म्यं च मैथुनं पुंसि जातिभ्रंशकरं स्मृतम्
text: Causing pain to aBrāhmaṇa,—smelling at things that should not be smelt, or at wine,—cheating—and sexual intercourse with a man,—all this is declared to lead to loss of caste.
iast: brāhmaṇasya rujaḥ kṛtvā ghrātiraghreyamadyayoḥ rujaḥkṛtyaṃ jaihmyaṃ ca maithunaṃ puṃsi jātibhraṃśakaraṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: kharāśvauṣṭramṛgaibhānāmajāvikavadhastathā saṅkarīkaraṇaṃ jñeyaṃ mīnāhimahiṣasya ca
meaning: खराश्वौष्ट्रमृगैभानामजाविकवधस्तथा सङ्करीकरणं ज्ञेयं मीनाहिमहिषस्य च
text: The killing of an ass, of a horse, of a camel, of a deer, of an elephant, of a goat, of a sheep, of fish, of a snake, and of a buffalo should be regarded as degrading the man to the rank of a ‘mixed caste.’
iast: kharāśvauṣṭramṛgaibhānāmajāvikavadhastathā saṅkarīkaraṇaṃ jñeyaṃ mīnāhimahiṣasya ca

sanskrit_iast: ninditebhyo dhanādānaṃ vāṇijyaṃ śūdrasevanam apātrīkaraṇaṃ jñeyamasatyasya ca bhāṣaṇam
meaning: निन्दितेभ्यो धनादानं वाणिज्यं शूद्रसेवनम् अपात्रीकरणं ज्ञेयमसत्यस्य च भाषणम्
text: Accepting gifts from despicable persons, trading, servingŚūdrasand the telling of a lie should be regarded as rendering one unworthy of receiving gifts.
iast: ninditebhyo dhanādānaṃ vāṇijyaṃ śūdrasevanam apātrīkaraṇaṃ jñeyamasatyasya ca bhāṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: kṛmikīṭavayohatyā madyānugatabhojanam phaledhaḥkusumasteyamadhairyaṃ ca malāvaham
meaning: कृमिकीटवयोहत्या मद्यानुगतभोजनम् फलेधःकुसुमस्तेयमधैर्यं च मलावहम्
text: The killing of insects, worms and birds,—the eating of things touched by wine,—the stealing of fruits, fuel or flowers—and inconstancy—are conducive to impurity.
iast: kṛmikīṭavayohatyā madyānugatabhojanam phaledhaḥkusumasteyamadhairyaṃ ca malāvaham

sanskrit_iast: etānyenāṃsi sarvāṇi yathauktāni pṛthak pṛthak yairyairvratairapohyante tāni samyalg nibodhata
meaning: एतान्येनांसि सर्वाणि यथौक्तानि पृथक् पृथक् यैर्यैर्व्रतैरपोह्यन्ते तानि सम्यल्ग् निबोधत
text: Learn properly now those penances b y which all these offences, severally described, become expiated.
iast: etānyenāṃsi sarvāṇi yathauktāni pṛthak pṛthak yairyairvratairapohyante tāni samyalg nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: brahmahā dvādaśa samāḥ kuṭīṃ kṛtvā vane vaset bhaikṣāśyātmaviśuddhyarthaṃ kṛtvā śavaśiro dhvajam
meaning: ब्रह्महा द्वादश समाः कुटीं कृत्वा वने वसेत् भैक्षाश्यात्मविशुद्ध्यर्थं कृत्वा शवशिरो ध्वजम्
text: TheBrāhmaṇa-slayer shall, for his purification, build a hut in the forest, live there for twelve years, subsisting on alms; making for himself a flag consisting of the head of the dead man.
iast: brahmahā dvādaśa samāḥ kuṭīṃ kṛtvā vane vaset bhaikṣāśyātmaviśuddhyarthaṃ kṛtvā śavaśiro dhvajam

sanskrit_iast: lakṣyaṃ śastrabhṛtāṃ vā syādviduṣāmicchayā 'tmanaḥ prāsyedātmānamagnau vā samiddhe triravākṣirāḥ
meaning: लक्ष्यं शस्त्रभृतां वा स्याद्विदुषामिच्छया 'त्मनः प्रास्येदात्मानमग्नौ वा समिद्धे त्रिरवाक्षिराः
text: Or, by his own will, he should become the target of armed men cognisant (of his purpose); or he may thrice throw himself headlong into blazing fire.
iast: lakṣyaṃ śastrabhṛtāṃ vā syādviduṣāmicchayā 'tmanaḥ prāsyedātmānamagnau vā samiddhe triravākṣirāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yajeta vā 'śvamedhena svarjitā gosavena vā abhijidviśvajidbhyāṃ vā trivṛtā 'gniṣṭutā 'pi vā
meaning: यजेत वा 'श्वमेधेन स्वर्जिता गोसवेन वा अभिजिद्विश्वजिद्भ्यां वा त्रिवृता 'ग्निष्टुता 'पि वा
text: Or, he may offer theAśvamedha, or the Svajit—the Gosava, or the Abhijit—Viśvajit, or the tripleAgniṣṭut.
iast: yajeta vā 'śvamedhena svarjitā gosavena vā abhijidviśvajidbhyāṃ vā trivṛtā 'gniṣṭutā 'pi vā

sanskrit_iast: japan vā 'nyatamaṃ vedaṃ yojanānāṃ śataṃ vrajet brahmahatyāpanodāya mitabhujñiyataindriyaḥ
meaning: जपन् वा 'न्यतमं वेदं योजनानां शतं व्रजेत् ब्रह्महत्यापनोदाय मितभुज्ञियतैन्द्रियः
text: Or, for the purpose of expiatingBrāhmaṇa-slay ing, he shall walk eight hundred miles, reciting one of the Vedas, eating little and controlling his senses.
iast: japan vā 'nyatamaṃ vedaṃ yojanānāṃ śataṃ vrajet brahmahatyāpanodāya mitabhujñiyataindriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sarvasvaṃ vedaviduṣe brāhmaṇāyaupapādayet dhanaṃ hi jīvanāyālaṃ gṛhaṃ vā saparicchadam
meaning: सर्वस्वं वेदविदुषे ब्राह्मणायौपपादयेत् धनं हि जीवनायालं गृहं वा सपरिच्छदम्
text: He shall make over to aBrāhmaṇalearned in the Veda, his entire property, which should be wealth sufficient for his maintenance,—or a house along with the furniture.
iast: sarvasvaṃ vedaviduṣe brāhmaṇāyaupapādayet dhanaṃ hi jīvanāyālaṃ gṛhaṃ vā saparicchadam

sanskrit_iast: haviṣyabhug vā 'nusaret pratisrotaḥ sarasvatīm japedvā niyatāhārastrirvai vedasya saṃhitām
meaning: हविष्यभुग् वा 'नुसरेत् प्रतिस्रोतः सरस्वतीम् जपेद्वा नियताहारस्त्रिर्वै वेदस्य संहिताम्
text: Or, subsisting on ‘sacrificial food,’ he may walk along each stream of theSarasvatī; or with food restrained, he may thrice recite the text of the Veda
iast: haviṣyabhug vā 'nusaret pratisrotaḥ sarasvatīm japedvā niyatāhārastrirvai vedasya saṃhitām

sanskrit_iast: kṛtavāpano nivasedgrāmānte govraje 'pi vā āśrame vṛkṣamūle vā gobrāhmaṇahite rataḥ
meaning: कृतवापनो निवसेद्ग्रामान्ते गोव्रजे 'पि वा आश्रमे वृक्षमूले वा गोब्राह्मणहिते रतः
text: Having shaved off, he may dwell at the extremity of the village, or in a cow-pen, or in a hermitage under a tree,—giving himself up to doing good to cows andBrāhmaṇas.
iast: kṛtavāpano nivasedgrāmānte govraje 'pi vā āśrame vṛkṣamūle vā gobrāhmaṇahite rataḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇārthe gavārthe vā sadyaḥ prāṇān parityajet saṃyak prāṇān mucyate brahmahatyāyā goptā gorbrāhmaṇasya ca
meaning: ब्राह्मणार्थे गवार्थे वा सद्यः प्राणान् परित्यजेत् संयक् प्राणान् मुच्यते ब्रह्महत्याया गोप्ता गोर्ब्राह्मणस्य च
text: He may give up his life unhesitatingly for the sake of a cow or aBrāhmaṇa; the protector of the cow and theBrāhmaṇabecomes absolved from the guilt ofBrāhmaṇa-killing.
iast: brāhmaṇārthe gavārthe vā sadyaḥ prāṇān parityajet saṃyak prāṇān mucyate brahmahatyāyā goptā gorbrāhmaṇasya ca

sanskrit_iast: trivāraṃ pratiroddhā vā sarvasvamavajitya vā tryavaraṃ viprasya tannimitte vā prāṇālābhe vimucyate prāṇālābhe 'pi mucyate
meaning: त्रिवारं प्रतिरोद्धा वा सर्वस्वमवजित्य वा त्र्यवरं विप्रस्य तन्निमित्ते वा प्राणालाभे विमुच्यते प्राणालाभे 'पि मुच्यते
text: If he fights at least thrice on behalf of aBrāhmaṇa, or reconquers his entire property, or gives up his life for his sake,—he becomes absolved.
iast: trivāraṃ pratiroddhā vā sarvasvamavajitya vā tryavaraṃ viprasya tannimitte vā prāṇālābhe vimucyate prāṇālābhe 'pi mucyate

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ dṛḍhavrato nityaṃ brahmacārī samāhitaḥ samāpte dvādaśe varṣe brahmahatyāṃ vyapohati
meaning: एवं दृढव्रतो नित्यं ब्रह्मचारी समाहितः समाप्ते द्वादशे वर्षे ब्रह्महत्यां व्यपोहति
text: He who remains thus firm in his vow, always chaste and with concentrated mind, shakes off the sin ofBrāhmaṇa-slaying, on the completion of the twelfth year.
iast: evaṃ dṛḍhavrato nityaṃ brahmacārī samāhitaḥ samāpte dvādaśe varṣe brahmahatyāṃ vyapohati

sanskrit_iast: śiṣṭvā vā bhūmidevānāṃ naradevasamāgame svameno 'vabhṛthasnāto hayamedhe vimucyate
meaning: शिष्ट्वा वा भूमिदेवानां नरदेवसमागमे स्वमेनो 'वभृथस्नातो हयमेधे विमुच्यते
text: Or, having confessed his guilt before the congregation of the gods of Earth and the gods of men, if he bathes at the Final Bath of the Horse-sacrifice,—he becomes absolved.
iast: śiṣṭvā vā bhūmidevānāṃ naradevasamāgame svameno 'vabhṛthasnāto hayamedhe vimucyate

sanskrit_iast: dharmasya brāhmaṇo mūlamagraṃ rājanya ucyate tasmāt samāgame teṣāmeno vikhyāpya śudhyati
meaning: धर्मस्य ब्राह्मणो मूलमग्रं राजन्य उच्यते तस्मात् समागमे तेषामेनो विख्याप्य शुध्यति
text: TheBrāhmaṇais called the root of righteousness, and theKṣatriyaits top; hence one who confesses his guilt before their congregation becomes pure.
iast: dharmasya brāhmaṇo mūlamagraṃ rājanya ucyate tasmāt samāgame teṣāmeno vikhyāpya śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: brahmaṇaḥ saṃbhavenaiva devānāmapi daivatam pramāṇaṃ caiva lokasya brahmātraiva hi kāraṇam
meaning: ब्रह्मणः संभवेनैव देवानामपि दैवतम् प्रमाणं चैव लोकस्य ब्रह्मात्रैव हि कारणम्
text: By his very birth theBrāhmaṇais a divinity even for the gods, and an authority for the people; and the Veda itself is the cause of this.
iast: brahmaṇaḥ saṃbhavenaiva devānāmapi daivatam pramāṇaṃ caiva lokasya brahmātraiva hi kāraṇam

sanskrit_iast: teṣāṃ vedavido brūyustrayo 'pyenaḥ suniṣkṛtim sā teṣāṃ pāvanāya syāt pavitrā viduṣāṃ hi vāk pavitraṃ
meaning: तेषां वेदविदो ब्रूयुस्त्रयो 'प्येनः सुनिष्कृतिम् सा तेषां पावनाय स्यात् पवित्रा विदुषां हि वाक् पवित्रं
text: If even three of them, learned in the Veda, expound the expiation for the offences, that shall suffice for their purification; as the word of learned men is purificatory.
iast: teṣāṃ vedavido brūyustrayo 'pyenaḥ suniṣkṛtim sā teṣāṃ pāvanāya syāt pavitrā viduṣāṃ hi vāk pavitraṃ

sanskrit_iast: ato 'nyatamamāsthāya vidhiṃ vipraḥ samāhitaḥ brahmahatyākṛtaṃ pāpaṃ vyapohatyātmavattayā
meaning: अतो 'न्यतममास्थाय विधिं विप्रः समाहितः ब्रह्महत्याकृतं पापं व्यपोहत्यात्मवत्तया
text: ABrāhmaṇawho, with concentrated mind, follows any one of these methods, removes, on account of his being self-possessed, the sin committed by killing aBrāhmaṇa.
iast: ato 'nyatamamāsthāya vidhiṃ vipraḥ samāhitaḥ brahmahatyākṛtaṃ pāpaṃ vyapohatyātmavattayā

sanskrit_iast: hatvā garbhamavijñātametadeva vrataṃ caret rājanyavaiśyau caijānāvātreyīmeva ca striyam
meaning: हत्वा गर्भमविज्ञातमेतदेव व्रतं चरेत् राजन्यवैश्यौ चैजानावात्रेयीमेव च स्त्रियम्
text: Having killed an unknown embryo, one should perform this same penance,—also on killing aKṣatriyaor a Vaiśya, who has performed sacrifices, or a woman of the ‘Ātreyī’ race.
iast: hatvā garbhamavijñātametadeva vrataṃ caret rājanyavaiśyau caijānāvātreyīmeva ca striyam

sanskrit_iast: uktvā caivānṛtaṃ sākṣye pratirudhya guruṃ tathā pratirabhya apahṛtya ca niḥkṣepaṃ kṛtvā ca strīsuhṛtvadham nikṣepaṃ
meaning: उक्त्वा चैवानृतं साक्ष्ये प्रतिरुध्य गुरुं तथा प्रतिरभ्य अपहृत्य च निःक्षेपं कृत्वा च स्त्रीसुहृत्वधम् निक्षेपं
text: Similarly also for telling a lie in giving evidence, for angering the preceptor, for misappropriating a trust, and for killing one’s wife or friend.
iast: uktvā caivānṛtaṃ sākṣye pratirudhya guruṃ tathā pratirabhya apahṛtya ca niḥkṣepaṃ kṛtvā ca strīsuhṛtvadham nikṣepaṃ

sanskrit_iast: iyaṃ viśuddhiruditā pramāpyākāmato dvijam kāmato brāhmaṇavadhe niṣkṛtirna vidhīyate
meaning: इयं विशुद्धिरुदिता प्रमाप्याकामतो द्विजम् कामतो ब्राह्मणवधे निष्कृतिर्न विधीयते
text: All this expiation has been laid down for killing aBrāhmaṇaunintentionally; for killing aBrāhmaṇaintentionally no atonement has been ordained.
iast: iyaṃ viśuddhiruditā pramāpyākāmato dvijam kāmato brāhmaṇavadhe niṣkṛtirna vidhīyate

sanskrit_iast: surāṃ pītvā dvijo mohādagnivarṇāṃ surāṃ pibet tayā sa kāye nirdagdhe mucyate kilbiṣāt tataḥ
meaning: सुरां पीत्वा द्विजो मोहादग्निवर्णां सुरां पिबेत् तया स काये निर्दग्धे मुच्यते किल्बिषात् ततः
text: A twice-born person, having, through folly, drunk wine, shall drink wine red-hot; he becomes freed from his guilt, when his body has been completely burnt by it.
iast: surāṃ pītvā dvijo mohādagnivarṇāṃ surāṃ pibet tayā sa kāye nirdagdhe mucyate kilbiṣāt tataḥ

sanskrit_iast: gomūtramagnivarṇaṃ vā pibedudakameva vā payo ghṛtaṃ vā 'maraṇādgośakṛdrasameva vā
meaning: गोमूत्रमग्निवर्णं वा पिबेदुदकमेव वा पयो घृतं वा 'मरणाद्गोशकृद्रसमेव वा
text: Or, he may drink red-hot cow’s urine, or water, or milk, or butter, or liquid cow-dung, until he dies.
iast: gomūtramagnivarṇaṃ vā pibedudakameva vā payo ghṛtaṃ vā 'maraṇādgośakṛdrasameva vā

sanskrit_iast: kaṇān vā bhakṣayedabdaṃ piṇyākaṃ vā sakṛtniśi surāpānāpanuttyarthaṃ vālavāsā jaṭī dhvajī
meaning: कणान् वा भक्षयेदब्दं पिण्याकं वा सकृत्निशि सुरापानापनुत्त्यर्थं वालवासा जटी ध्वजी
text: Or, for the expiation of the guilt of wine-drinking, he may, for one year, eat only once at night either pieces of grain or oil-cake, clothed in hair-cloth, with his hair matted, and carrying a sign.
iast: kaṇān vā bhakṣayedabdaṃ piṇyākaṃ vā sakṛtniśi surāpānāpanuttyarthaṃ vālavāsā jaṭī dhvajī

sanskrit_iast: surā vai malamannānāṃ pāpmā ca malamucyate tasmādbrāhmaṇarājanyau vaiśyaśca na surāṃ pibet
meaning: सुरा वै मलमन्नानां पाप्मा च मलमुच्यते तस्माद्ब्राह्मणराजन्यौ वैश्यश्च न सुरां पिबेत्
text: Wine indeed is the dirty refuse of grains, and sin also is called ‘dirt’; for this reason theBrāhmaṇa, theKṣatriyaand theVaiśyashall not drink wine.
iast: surā vai malamannānāṃ pāpmā ca malamucyate tasmādbrāhmaṇarājanyau vaiśyaśca na surāṃ pibet

sanskrit_iast: gauḍī paiṣṭī ca mādhvī ca vijñeyā trividhā surā yathaivaikā tathā sarvā na pātavyā dvijottamaiḥ
meaning: गौडी पैष्टी च माध्वी च विज्ञेया त्रिविधा सुरा यथैवैका तथा सर्वा न पातव्या द्विजोत्तमैः
text: Wine should be understood to be of three kinds: (a) distilled from molasses (Gauḍī), (b) distilled from grains (Paiṣṭī), and (c) ‘distilled from grapes’ (Mādhvī); as the one so all the rest should never be drunk by the chief of the twice-born.
iast: gauḍī paiṣṭī ca mādhvī ca vijñeyā trividhā surā yathaivaikā tathā sarvā na pātavyā dvijottamaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yakṣarakṣaḥpiśācānnaṃ madyaṃ māṃsaṃ surāsavam tadbrāhmaṇena nāttavyaṃ devānāmaśnatā haviḥ
meaning: यक्षरक्षःपिशाचान्नं मद्यं मांसं सुरासवम् तद्ब्राह्मणेन नात्तव्यं देवानामश्नता हविः
text: Intoxicants, meat, wine and distilled liquors are the food ofYakṣas,RākṣasasandPiśācas; it should not be taken by theBrāhmaṇawho partakes of the offerings to the gods.
iast: yakṣarakṣaḥpiśācānnaṃ madyaṃ māṃsaṃ surāsavam tadbrāhmaṇena nāttavyaṃ devānāmaśnatā haviḥ

sanskrit_iast: amedhye vā paten matto vaidikaṃ vā 'pyudāharet akāryamanyat kuryādvā brāhmaṇo madamohitaḥ
meaning: अमेध्ये वा पतेन् मत्तो वैदिकं वा 'प्युदाहरेत् अकार्यमन्यत् कुर्याद्वा ब्राह्मणो मदमोहितः
text: ABrāhmaṇa, stupefied by drunkenness, might tumble down upon unclean things; or he might wrongly recite the Veda; or he might do some other improper act.
iast: amedhye vā paten matto vaidikaṃ vā 'pyudāharet akāryamanyat kuryādvā brāhmaṇo madamohitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yasya kāyagataṃ brahma madyenāplāvyate sakṛt tasya vyapaiti brāhmaṇyaṃ śūdratvaṃ ca sa gacchati
meaning: यस्य कायगतं ब्रह्म मद्येनाप्लाव्यते सकृत् तस्य व्यपैति ब्राह्मण्यं शूद्रत्वं च स गच्छति
text: When the ‘Brahman’ resident in his body has been once deluged by wine, the ‘Brāhmaṇahood’ disappears and the man becomes aŚūdra.
iast: yasya kāyagataṃ brahma madyenāplāvyate sakṛt tasya vyapaiti brāhmaṇyaṃ śūdratvaṃ ca sa gacchati

sanskrit_iast: eṣā vicitrābhihitā surāpānasya niṣkṛtiḥ ata ūrdhvaṃ pravakṣyāmi suvarṇasteyaniṣkṛtim
meaning: एषा विचित्राभिहिता सुरापानस्य निष्कृतिः अत ऊर्ध्वं प्रवक्ष्यामि सुवर्णस्तेयनिष्कृतिम्
text: Thus have been described the various expiations for wine-drinking; after this I am going to expound the expiation for the theft of gold.
iast: eṣā vicitrābhihitā surāpānasya niṣkṛtiḥ ata ūrdhvaṃ pravakṣyāmi suvarṇasteyaniṣkṛtim

sanskrit_iast: suvarṇasteyakṛdvipro rājānamabhigamya tu svakarma khyāpayan brūyātmāṃ bhavānanuśāstviti
meaning: सुवर्णस्तेयकृद्विप्रो राजानमभिगम्य तु स्वकर्म ख्यापयन् ब्रूयात्मां भवाननुशास्त्विति
text: ABrāhmaṇawho has committed the theft of gold shall go to the King, and confessing his crime, shall say ‘sire, punish me.’
iast: suvarṇasteyakṛdvipro rājānamabhigamya tu svakarma khyāpayan brūyātmāṃ bhavānanuśāstviti

sanskrit_iast: gṛhītvā musalaṃ rājā sakṛddhanyāt tu taṃ svayam vadhena śudhyati steno brāhmaṇastapasaiva tu
meaning: गृहीत्वा मुसलं राजा सकृद्धन्यात् तु तं स्वयम् वधेन शुध्यति स्तेनो ब्राह्मणस्तपसैव तु
text: Taking up a club, the King himself shall strike him once. The thief becomes purified by death; but theBrāhmaṇaby penance alone.
iast: gṛhītvā musalaṃ rājā sakṛddhanyāt tu taṃ svayam vadhena śudhyati steno brāhmaṇastapasaiva tu

sanskrit_iast: tapasāpanunutsustu suvarṇasteyajaṃ malam cīravāsā dvijo 'raṇye caredbrahmahaṇo vratam
meaning: तपसापनुनुत्सुस्तु सुवर्णस्तेयजं मलम् चीरवासा द्विजो 'रण्ये चरेद्ब्रह्महणो व्रतम्
text: If a twice-born person is desirous of removing the guilt of stealing gold by means of penance, he should perform the penance prescribed for the slayer of aBrāhmaṇa,—living in a forest, clothed in rags.
iast: tapasāpanunutsustu suvarṇasteyajaṃ malam cīravāsā dvijo 'raṇye caredbrahmahaṇo vratam

sanskrit_iast: etairvratairapoheta pāpaṃ steyakṛtaṃ dvijaḥ gurustrīgamanīiyaṃ tu vratairebhirapānudet
meaning: एतैर्व्रतैरपोहेत पापं स्तेयकृतं द्विजः गुरुस्त्रीगमनीइयं तु व्रतैरेभिरपानुदेत्
text: The twice-born man shall remove the guilt caused by theft by means of these penances. That caused by intercourse with the Preceptor’s wife he shall wipe off by means of these following penances.
iast: etairvratairapoheta pāpaṃ steyakṛtaṃ dvijaḥ gurustrīgamanīiyaṃ tu vratairebhirapānudet

sanskrit_iast: gurutalpyabhibhāṣyainastapte svapyādayomaye talpe svapyād sūrmīṃ jvalantīṃ svāśliṣyen mṛtyunā sa viśudhyati vā 'śliṣyen
meaning: गुरुतल्प्यभिभाष्यैनस्तप्ते स्वप्यादयोमये तल्पे स्वप्याद् सूर्मीं ज्वलन्तीं स्वाश्लिष्येन् मृत्युना स विशुध्यति वा 'श्लिष्येन्
text: He who has violated his Preceptor’s bed shall confess his crime and lie down upon a heated iron-bed; or embrace a blazing image. By death he becomes purified.
iast: gurutalpyabhibhāṣyainastapte svapyādayomaye talpe svapyād sūrmīṃ jvalantīṃ svāśliṣyen mṛtyunā sa viśudhyati vā 'śliṣyen

sanskrit_iast: svayaṃ vā śiṣṇavṛṣaṇāvutkṛtyādhāya cāñjalau nairṛtīṃ diśamātiṣṭhedā nipātādajihmagaḥ
meaning: स्वयं वा शिष्णवृषणावुत्कृत्याधाय चाञ्जलौ नैरृतीं दिशमातिष्ठेदा निपातादजिह्मगः
text: Or, having cut off his penis and testicles, he shall take them in his joined hands and walk straight on towards the ‘region of evil spirits,’ until he falls down.
iast: svayaṃ vā śiṣṇavṛṣaṇāvutkṛtyādhāya cāñjalau nairṛtīṃ diśamātiṣṭhedā nipātādajihmagaḥ

sanskrit_iast: khaṭvāṅgī cīravāsā vā śmaśrulo vijane vane prājāpatyaṃ caret kṛcchramabdamekaṃ samāhitaḥ
meaning: खट्वाङ्गी चीरवासा वा श्मश्रुलो विजने वने प्राजापत्यं चरेत् कृच्छ्रमब्दमेकं समाहितः
text: Or, carrying a bedstead, clothed in rags, with beard grown, he shall perform, in the solitary forest, the ‘Prājāpatya’ penance, for one year, with concentrated mind.
iast: khaṭvāṅgī cīravāsā vā śmaśrulo vijane vane prājāpatyaṃ caret kṛcchramabdamekaṃ samāhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: cāndrāyaṇaṃ vā trīn māsānabhyasyenniyataindriyaḥ haviṣyeṇa yavāgvā vā gurutalpāpanuttaye
meaning: चान्द्रायणं वा त्रीन् मासानभ्यस्येन्नियतैन्द्रियः हविष्येण यवाग्वा वा गुरुतल्पापनुत्तये
text: Or, with his senses controlled, he shall perform the ‘Cāndrāyaṇa’ for three months, subsisting on ‘sacrificial food’ or on barley-gruel,—for the expiating of the sin of violating the Preceptor’s bed.
iast: cāndrāyaṇaṃ vā trīn māsānabhyasyenniyataindriyaḥ haviṣyeṇa yavāgvā vā gurutalpāpanuttaye

sanskrit_iast: etairvratairapoheyurmahāpātakino malam upapātakinastvevamebhirnānāvidhairvrataiḥ
meaning: एतैर्व्रतैरपोहेयुर्महापातकिनो मलम् उपपातकिनस्त्वेवमेभिर्नानाविधैर्व्रतैः
text: By means of these penances, the committers of heinous crimes may wipe off their sins. The committers of minor offences may do the same by their following the several forms of penances.
iast: etairvratairapoheyurmahāpātakino malam upapātakinastvevamebhirnānāvidhairvrataiḥ

sanskrit_iast: upapātakasaṃyukto goghno māsaṃ yavān pibet kṛtavāpo vasedgoṣṭhe carmaṇā tena saṃvṛtaḥ
meaning: उपपातकसंयुक्तो गोघ्नो मासं यवान् पिबेत् कृतवापो वसेद्गोष्ठे चर्मणा तेन संवृतः
text: The cow-killer, charged with a minor offence, shall drink barley for three months; and having shaved his head and covered with the skin of the cow, he shall live in the cow-pen.
iast: upapātakasaṃyukto goghno māsaṃ yavān pibet kṛtavāpo vasedgoṣṭhe carmaṇā tena saṃvṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: caturthakālamaśnīyādakṣāralavaṇaṃ mitam gomūtreṇācaret snānaṃ dvau māsau niyataindriyaḥ
meaning: चतुर्थकालमश्नीयादक्षारलवणं मितम् गोमूत्रेणाचरेत् स्नानं द्वौ मासौ नियतैन्द्रियः
text: For two months, with senses controlled, he shall eat a limited quantity of food, without any pungent salt, at the fourth meal-time; and shall bathe in cow’s urine.
iast: caturthakālamaśnīyādakṣāralavaṇaṃ mitam gomūtreṇācaret snānaṃ dvau māsau niyataindriyaḥ

sanskrit_iast: divā 'nugacchedgāstāstu tiṣṭhannūrdhvaṃ rajaḥ pibet śuśrūṣitvā namaskṛtya rātrau vīrāsanaṃ vaset vīrāsano
meaning: दिवा 'नुगच्छेद्गास्तास्तु तिष्ठन्नूर्ध्वं रजः पिबेत् शुश्रूषित्वा नमस्कृत्य रात्रौ वीरासनं वसेत् वीरासनो
text: During the day he shall follow those cows, and standing upright, inhale the dust; at night having attended and bowed to them, he shall rest in the ‘Vīrāsana’ posture.
iast: divā 'nugacchedgāstāstu tiṣṭhannūrdhvaṃ rajaḥ pibet śuśrūṣitvā namaskṛtya rātrau vīrāsanaṃ vaset vīrāsano

sanskrit_iast: tiṣṭhantīṣvanutiṣṭhet tu vrajantīṣvapyanuvrajet āsīnāsu tathā 'sīno niyato vītamatsaraḥ
meaning: तिष्ठन्तीष्वनुतिष्ठेत् तु व्रजन्तीष्वप्यनुव्रजेत् आसीनासु तथा 'सीनो नियतो वीतमत्सरः
text: Self-controlled and free from greed, he shall stand when they stand, follow them when they move, and sit when they have sat down.
iast: tiṣṭhantīṣvanutiṣṭhet tu vrajantīṣvapyanuvrajet āsīnāsu tathā 'sīno niyato vītamatsaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: āturāmabhiśastāṃ vā cauravyāghrādibhirbhayaiḥ patitāṃ paṅkalagnaṃ vā sarvaupāyair vimocayet sarvaprāṇair
meaning: आतुरामभिशस्तां वा चौरव्याघ्रादिभिर्भयैः पतितां पङ्कलग्नं वा सर्वौपायैर् विमोचयेत् सर्वप्राणैर्
text: When a cow is sick, or threatened with danger from thieves, tigers and the like, or falls, or becomes stuck in a morass, he shall rescue her with all his strength.
iast: āturāmabhiśastāṃ vā cauravyāghrādibhirbhayaiḥ patitāṃ paṅkalagnaṃ vā sarvaupāyair vimocayet sarvaprāṇair

sanskrit_iast: uṣṇe varṣati śīte vā mārute vāti vā bhṛśam na kurvītātmanastrāṇaṃ gorakṛtvā tu śaktitaḥ
meaning: उष्णे वर्षति शीते वा मारुते वाति वा भृशम् न कुर्वीतात्मनस्त्राणं गोरकृत्वा तु शक्तितः
text: In heat, in rain, in cold, or when the wind is blowing violently, he shall not shelter himself, without having sheltered the cows to the best of his ability.
iast: uṣṇe varṣati śīte vā mārute vāti vā bhṛśam na kurvītātmanastrāṇaṃ gorakṛtvā tu śaktitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ātmano yadi vā 'nyeṣāṃ gṛhe kṣetre 'tha vā khale bhakṣayantīṃ na kathayet pibantaṃ caiva vatsakam
meaning: आत्मनो यदि वा 'न्येषां गृहे क्षेत्रे 'थ वा खले भक्षयन्तीं न कथयेत् पिबन्तं चैव वत्सकम्
text: If the cow is eating anything in his own or another’s house, field or threshing-yard,—or when her calf is drinking (her milk),—he shall not say anything.
iast: ātmano yadi vā 'nyeṣāṃ gṛhe kṣetre 'tha vā khale bhakṣayantīṃ na kathayet pibantaṃ caiva vatsakam

sanskrit_iast: anena vidhinā yastu goghno gāmanugacchati sa gohatyākṛtaṃ pāpaṃ tribhirmāsairvyapohati
meaning: अनेन विधिना यस्तु गोघ्नो गामनुगच्छति स गोहत्याकृतं पापं त्रिभिर्मासैर्व्यपोहति
text: The cow-killer, who attends upon cows in this manner, wipes off, in three months, the sin accruing from the killing of a cow—(115); and after having duly performed the penance, he shall give away cows with a bull as the eleventh; in the event of these being not available, he shall offer all he possesses to persons learned in the Veda.
iast: anena vidhinā yastu goghno gāmanugacchati sa gohatyākṛtaṃ pāpaṃ tribhirmāsairvyapohati

sanskrit_iast: vṛṣabhekādaśā gāśca dadyāt sucaritavrataḥ avidyamāne sarvasvaṃ vedavidbhyo nivedayet
meaning: वृषभेकादशा गाश्च दद्यात् सुचरितव्रतः अविद्यमाने सर्वस्वं वेदविद्भ्यो निवेदयेत्
text: The cow-killer, who attends upon cows in this manner, wipes off, in three months, the sin accruing from the killing of a cow—(115); and after having duly performed the penance, he shall give away cows with a bull as the eleventh; in the event of these being not available, he shall offer all he possesses to persons learned in the Veda.
iast: vṛṣabhekādaśā gāśca dadyāt sucaritavrataḥ avidyamāne sarvasvaṃ vedavidbhyo nivedayet

sanskrit_iast: etadeva vrataṃ kuryurupapātakino dvijāḥ avakīrṇivarjyaṃ śuddhyarthaṃ cāndrāyaṇamathāpi vā 'vakīrṇivarjaṃ
meaning: एतदेव व्रतं कुर्युरुपपातकिनो द्विजाः अवकीर्णिवर्ज्यं शुद्ध्यर्थं चान्द्रायणमथापि वा 'वकीर्णिवर्जं
text: Twice-born men who have committed the minor offences, except the ‘immoral’ religious student, may, in order to purify themselves, perform this same penance, or the ‘Cāndrāyaṇa’ penance.
iast: etadeva vrataṃ kuryurupapātakino dvijāḥ avakīrṇivarjyaṃ śuddhyarthaṃ cāndrāyaṇamathāpi vā 'vakīrṇivarjaṃ

sanskrit_iast: avakīrṇī tu kāṇena gardabhena catuṣpathe pākayajñavidhānena yajeta nirṛtiṃ niśi
meaning: अवकीर्णी तु काणेन गर्दभेन चतुष्पथे पाकयज्ञविधानेन यजेत निरृतिं निशि
text: The immoral religious student shall offer, at night, toNirṛti, on the crossway, a one-eyed ass, in the manner of the ‘Pākayajña.’
iast: avakīrṇī tu kāṇena gardabhena catuṣpathe pākayajñavidhānena yajeta nirṛtiṃ niśi

sanskrit_iast: hutvā 'gnau vidhivaddhomānantataśca samaity ṛcā vātaindraguruvahnīnāṃ juhuyāt sarpiṣā 'hutīḥ
meaning: हुत्वा 'ग्नौ विधिवद्धोमानन्ततश्च समैत्य् ऋचा वातैन्द्रगुरुवह्नीनां जुहुयात् सर्पिषा 'हुतीः
text: Having, in due form, poured oblations into the fire, he shall finally pour oblations of clarified butter toVāta,Indra, Guru and Vahni, with the verse ‘sam, etc.’
iast: hutvā 'gnau vidhivaddhomānantataśca samaity ṛcā vātaindraguruvahnīnāṃ juhuyāt sarpiṣā 'hutīḥ

sanskrit_iast: kāmato retasaḥ sekaṃ vratasthasya dvijanmanaḥ atikramaṃ vratasyāhurdharmajñā brahmavādinaḥ
meaning: कामतो रेतसः सेकं व्रतस्थस्य द्विजन्मनः अतिक्रमं व्रतस्याहुर्धर्मज्ञा ब्रह्मवादिनः
text: Persons learned in the Veda and knowing the law declare that for the twice-born person keeping up his vows, the intentional emission of semen means a ‘transgression of the vow.’
iast: kāmato retasaḥ sekaṃ vratasthasya dvijanmanaḥ atikramaṃ vratasyāhurdharmajñā brahmavādinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: mārutaṃ puruhūtaṃ ca guruṃ pāvakameva ca caturo vratino 'bhyeti brāhmaṃ tejo 'vakīrṇinaḥ
meaning: मारुतं पुरुहूतं च गुरुं पावकमेव च चतुरो व्रतिनो 'भ्येति ब्राह्मं तेजो 'वकीर्णिनः
text: The spiritual power of the Religious Student, who has become ‘immoral,’ ‘goes away into the maruts, indra,Bṛhaspatiand agni.
iast: mārutaṃ puruhūtaṃ ca guruṃ pāvakameva ca caturo vratino 'bhyeti brāhmaṃ tejo 'vakīrṇinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etasminnenasi prāpte vasitvā gardabhājinam saptāgārāṃścaredbhaikṣaṃ svakarma parikīrtayan
meaning: एतस्मिन्नेनसि प्राप्ते वसित्वा गर्दभाजिनम् सप्तागारांश्चरेद्भैक्षं स्वकर्म परिकीर्तयन्
text: On this sin having been incurred, the man, clothed in the skin of the ass, shall go begging alms at seven houses; proclaiming his own deed.
iast: etasminnenasi prāpte vasitvā gardabhājinam saptāgārāṃścaredbhaikṣaṃ svakarma parikīrtayan

sanskrit_iast: tebhyo labdhena bhaikṣeṇa vartayannekakālikam upaspṛśaṃstriṣavaṇaṃ tvabdena sa viśudhyati triṣavaṇaṃ abdena
meaning: तेभ्यो लब्धेन भैक्षेण वर्तयन्नेककालिकम् उपस्पृशंस्त्रिषवणं त्वब्देन स विशुध्यति त्रिषवणं अब्देन
text: Subsisting on a single meal per day out of the alms obtained from those houses, and bathing at the three ‘extractions,’ he becomes pure after one year.
iast: tebhyo labdhena bhaikṣeṇa vartayannekakālikam upaspṛśaṃstriṣavaṇaṃ tvabdena sa viśudhyati triṣavaṇaṃ abdena

sanskrit_iast: jātibhraṃśakaraṃ karma kṛtvā 'nyatamamicchayā caret sāṃtapanaṃ kṛcchraṃ prājāpatyamanicchayā
meaning: जातिभ्रंशकरं कर्म कृत्वा 'न्यतममिच्छया चरेत् सांतपनं कृच्छ्रं प्राजापत्यमनिच्छया
text: On having intention ally done any one of those acts which cause loss of caste, one should perform aSāntapana-Kṛcchra; and thePrājāpatya, when it is done unintentionally.
iast: jātibhraṃśakaraṃ karma kṛtvā 'nyatamamicchayā caret sāṃtapanaṃ kṛcchraṃ prājāpatyamanicchayā

sanskrit_iast: saṅkarāpātrakṛtyāsu māsaṃ śodhanamaindavam 'indavaḥ malinīkaraṇīyeṣu taptaḥ syādyāvakaistryaham
meaning: सङ्करापात्रकृत्यासु मासं शोधनमैन्दवम् 'इन्दवः मलिनीकरणीयेषु तप्तः स्याद्यावकैस्त्र्यहम्
text: In the case of acts conducive to the degradation of ‘caste-mixture,’ or of those that make one unworthy of receiving gifts, purification is secured by the monthly lunar penance; and in the case of those that lead to defilement, one should mortify his body on barley-products for three days.
iast: saṅkarāpātrakṛtyāsu māsaṃ śodhanamaindavam 'indavaḥ malinīkaraṇīyeṣu taptaḥ syādyāvakaistryaham

sanskrit_iast: turīyo brahmahatyāyāḥ kṣatriyasya vadhe smṛtaḥ vaiśye 'ṣṭamāṃśo vṛttasthe śūdre jñeyastu ṣoḍaśaḥ
meaning: तुरीयो ब्रह्महत्यायाः क्षत्रियस्य वधे स्मृतः वैश्ये 'ष्टमांशो वृत्तस्थे शूद्रे ज्ञेयस्तु षोडशः
text: One fourth of the expiation for the killing of aBrāhmaṇahas been prescribed for the killing of aKṣatriya, one eighth for that of aVaiśya, and one sixteenth for that of aŚūdra,—each of these being one who is devoted to his duty.
iast: turīyo brahmahatyāyāḥ kṣatriyasya vadhe smṛtaḥ vaiśye 'ṣṭamāṃśo vṛttasthe śūdre jñeyastu ṣoḍaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: akāmatastu rājanyaṃ vinipātya dvijottamaḥ vṛṣabhekasahasrā gā dadyāt sucaritavrataḥ
meaning: अकामतस्तु राजन्यं विनिपात्य द्विजोत्तमः वृषभेकसहस्रा गा दद्यात् सुचरितव्रतः
text: If the chief of twice-born men kills aKṣatriyaunintentionally, he should duly perform the penance and give away a thousand cows and a bull.
iast: akāmatastu rājanyaṃ vinipātya dvijottamaḥ vṛṣabhekasahasrā gā dadyāt sucaritavrataḥ

sanskrit_iast: tryabdaṃ caredvā niyato jaṭī brahmahaṇo vratam vasan dūratare grāmādvṛkṣamūlaniketanaḥ
meaning: त्र्यब्दं चरेद्वा नियतो जटी ब्रह्महणो व्रतम् वसन् दूरतरे ग्रामाद्वृक्षमूलनिकेतनः
text: Or, he may perform, for three years, the penance of the ‘Brāhmaṇa-killer,’ keeping himself under control and wearing matted locks, living far off from the village, having his abode at the root of a tree.
iast: tryabdaṃ caredvā niyato jaṭī brahmahaṇo vratam vasan dūratare grāmādvṛkṣamūlaniketanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etadeva caredabdaṃ prāyaścittaṃ dvijottamaḥ pramāpya vaiśyaṃ vṛttasthaṃ dadyāccaikaśataṃ dadyādvaikaśataṃ gavām
meaning: एतदेव चरेदब्दं प्रायश्चित्तं द्विजोत्तमः प्रमाप्य वैश्यं वृत्तस्थं दद्याच्चैकशतं दद्याद्वैकशतं गवाम्
text: If aBrāhmaṇakills a righteous Vaiśya, he shall perform this same expiatory rite for one year; or he may give a hundred cows and one (bull).
iast: etadeva caredabdaṃ prāyaścittaṃ dvijottamaḥ pramāpya vaiśyaṃ vṛttasthaṃ dadyāccaikaśataṃ dadyādvaikaśataṃ gavām

sanskrit_iast: etadeva vrataṃ kṛtsnaṃ ṣaṇmāsāṃśūdrahā caret vṛṣabhekādaśā vā 'pi dadyādviprāya gāḥ sitāḥ
meaning: एतदेव व्रतं कृत्स्नं षण्मासांशूद्रहा चरेत् वृषभेकादशा वा 'पि दद्याद्विप्राय गाः सिताः
text: एतदेव व्रतं कृत्स्नं षण्मासांशूद्रहा चरेत् ) ।वृषभेकादशा वाऽपि दद्याद् विप्राय गाः सिताः ॥ १३० ॥
iast: etadeva vrataṃ kṛtsnaṃ ṣaṇmāsāṃśūdrahā caret vṛṣabhekādaśā vā 'pi dadyādviprāya gāḥ sitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: mārjāranakulau hatvā cāṣaṃ maṇḍūkameva ca śvagodhaulūkakākāṃśca śūdrahatyāvrataṃ caret
meaning: मार्जारनकुलौ हत्वा चाषं मण्डूकमेव च श्वगोधौलूककाकांश्च शूद्रहत्याव्रतं चरेत्
text: Having killed a cat, an ichneumon, a blue jay, a frog, a dog, an iguana, an owl and a crow,—he shall perform the penance of the ‘Śūdra-killer.’
iast: mārjāranakulau hatvā cāṣaṃ maṇḍūkameva ca śvagodhaulūkakākāṃśca śūdrahatyāvrataṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: payaḥ pibet trirātraṃ vā yojanaṃ vā 'dhvano vrajet upaspṛśet sravantyāṃ vā sūktaṃ vā 'bdaivataṃ japet
meaning: पयः पिबेत् त्रिरात्रं वा योजनं वा 'ध्वनो व्रजेत् उपस्पृशेत् स्रवन्त्यां वा सूक्तं वा 'ब्दैवतं जपेत्
text: Or, he may drink milk only for three days, or walk over eight hundred miles of road, or bathe in a stream, or recite the hymn addressed to the Waters.
iast: payaḥ pibet trirātraṃ vā yojanaṃ vā 'dhvano vrajet upaspṛśet sravantyāṃ vā sūktaṃ vā 'bdaivataṃ japet

sanskrit_iast: abhriṃ kārṣṇāyasīṃ dadyāt sarpaṃ hatvā dvijottamaḥ palālabhārakaṃ ṣaṇḍhe saisakaṃ caikamāṣakam
meaning: अभ्रिं कार्ष्णायसीं दद्यात् सर्पं हत्वा द्विजोत्तमः पलालभारकं षण्ढे सैसकं चैकमाषकम्
text: For killing a snake, theBrāhmaṇashould give an iron spade; and in the case of a eunuch, a load of straw and a ‘māsa’ of lead.
iast: abhriṃ kārṣṇāyasīṃ dadyāt sarpaṃ hatvā dvijottamaḥ palālabhārakaṃ ṣaṇḍhe saisakaṃ caikamāṣakam

sanskrit_iast: ghṛtakumbhaṃ varāhe tu tiladroṇaṃ tu tittirau śuke dvihāyanaṃ vatsaṃ krauñcaṃ hatvā trihāyanam
meaning: घृतकुम्भं वराहे तु तिलद्रोणं तु तित्तिरौ शुके द्विहायनं वत्सं क्रौञ्चं हत्वा त्रिहायनम्
text: For a boar, a jar of clarified butter; for a partridge, a ‘droṇa’ of sesamum; for a parrot, a two-year-old calf; and for killing a horse, a three-year-old calf.
iast: ghṛtakumbhaṃ varāhe tu tiladroṇaṃ tu tittirau śuke dvihāyanaṃ vatsaṃ krauñcaṃ hatvā trihāyanam

sanskrit_iast: hatvā haṃsaṃ balākāṃ ca bakaṃ barhiṇameva ca vānaraṃ śyenabhāsau ca sparśayedbrāhmaṇāya gām
meaning: हत्वा हंसं बलाकां च बकं बर्हिणमेव च वानरं श्येनभासौ च स्पर्शयेद्ब्राह्मणाय गाम्
text: On having killed a swan, abalākā, a crane, a peacock, a monkey, a falcon, or a vulture,—one should give a cow to aBrāhmaṇa.
iast: hatvā haṃsaṃ balākāṃ ca bakaṃ barhiṇameva ca vānaraṃ śyenabhāsau ca sparśayedbrāhmaṇāya gām

sanskrit_iast: vāso dadyāddhayaṃ hatvā pañca nīlān vṛṣān gajam ajameṣāvanaḍvāhaṃ kharaṃ hatvaikahāyanam
meaning: वासो दद्याद्धयं हत्वा पञ्च नीलान् वृषान् गजम् अजमेषावनड्वाहं खरं हत्वैकहायनम्
text: On killing a horse, one should give a garment; and five black bulls on killing an elephant; on killing a goat and a sheep, an ox; and on killing a hare, a one-year-old calf.
iast: vāso dadyāddhayaṃ hatvā pañca nīlān vṛṣān gajam ajameṣāvanaḍvāhaṃ kharaṃ hatvaikahāyanam

sanskrit_iast: kravyādāṃstu mṛgān hatvā dhenuṃ dadyāt payasvinīm akravyādān vatsatarīmuṣṭraṃ hatvā tu kṛṣṇalam
meaning: क्रव्यादांस्तु मृगान् हत्वा धेनुं दद्यात् पयस्विनीम् अक्रव्यादान् वत्सतरीमुष्ट्रं हत्वा तु कृष्णलम्
text: For killing carnivorous animals, one should give a milch-cow; and a heifer for killing those not carnivorous; and a ‘kṛṣṇala’ of gold for killing a camel.
iast: kravyādāṃstu mṛgān hatvā dhenuṃ dadyāt payasvinīm akravyādān vatsatarīmuṣṭraṃ hatvā tu kṛṣṇalam

sanskrit_iast: jīnakārmukabastāvīn pṛthag dadyādviśuddhaye caturṇāmapi varṇānāṃ nārīrhatvā 'navasthitāḥ
meaning: जीनकार्मुकबस्तावीन् पृथग् दद्याद्विशुद्धये चतुर्णामपि वर्णानां नारीर्हत्वा 'नवस्थिताः
text: For killing inconstant women of the four castes, one should give, for his purification, a leathern bag, a bow, a goat and a sheep respectively.
iast: jīnakārmukabastāvīn pṛthag dadyādviśuddhaye caturṇāmapi varṇānāṃ nārīrhatvā 'navasthitāḥ

sanskrit_iast: dānena vadhanirṇekaṃ sarpādīnāmaśaknuvan ekaikaśaścaret kṛcchraṃ dvijaḥ pāpāpanuttaye
meaning: दानेन वधनिर्णेकं सर्पादीनामशक्नुवन् एकैकशश्चरेत् कृच्छ्रं द्विजः पापापनुत्तये
text: If a twice-born person is unable to atone the sin of killing by means of gifts, he should perform the ‘Kṛcchra,’ for each act, for the purpose of removing his sin.
iast: dānena vadhanirṇekaṃ sarpādīnāmaśaknuvan ekaikaśaścaret kṛcchraṃ dvijaḥ pāpāpanuttaye

sanskrit_iast: asthimatāṃ tu sattvānāṃ sahasrasya pramāpaṇe pūrṇe cānasyanasthnāṃ tu śūdrahatyāvrataṃ caret
meaning: अस्थिमतां तु सत्त्वानां सहस्रस्य प्रमापणे पूर्णे चानस्यनस्थ्नां तु शूद्रहत्याव्रतं चरेत्
text: If one kills one thousand animals ‘with bones,’ or a full cart-load of boneless ones, he shall perform the penance for the killing of aŚūdra.
iast: asthimatāṃ tu sattvānāṃ sahasrasya pramāpaṇe pūrṇe cānasyanasthnāṃ tu śūdrahatyāvrataṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: kiṃ cideva tu viprāya dadyādasthimatāṃ vadhe anasthnāṃ caiva hiṃsāyāṃ prāṇāyāmena śudhyati
meaning: किं चिदेव तु विप्राय दद्यादस्थिमतां वधे अनस्थ्नां चैव हिंसायां प्राणायामेन शुध्यति
text: For the killing of animals with bones, he should give some trifle to aBrāhmaṇa; and for the killing of boneless animals, one becomes purified by the ‘control of breath.’
iast: kiṃ cideva tu viprāya dadyādasthimatāṃ vadhe anasthnāṃ caiva hiṃsāyāṃ prāṇāyāmena śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: phaladānāṃ tu vṛkṣāṇāṃ chedane japyaṃ ṛcśatam gulmavallīlatānāṃ ca puṣpitānāṃ ca vīrudhām
meaning: फलदानां तु वृक्षाणां छेदने जप्यं ऋच्शतम् गुल्मवल्लीलतानां च पुष्पितानां च वीरुधाम्
text: When one cuts fruit-bearing trees, shrubs, creepers, branches of trees or flowering plants, he should recite one hundredṚkverses.
iast: phaladānāṃ tu vṛkṣāṇāṃ chedane japyaṃ ṛcśatam gulmavallīlatānāṃ ca puṣpitānāṃ ca vīrudhām

sanskrit_iast: annādyajānāṃ sattvānāṃ rasajānāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ phalapuṣpaudbhavānāṃ ca ghṛtaprāśo viśodhanam
meaning: अन्नाद्यजानां सत्त्वानां रसजानां च सर्वशः फलपुष्पौद्भवानां च घृतप्राशो विशोधनम्
text: For the destroying of any kind of creatures bred in food, in sauces, in fruits or in flowers, the expiation consists in eating with clarified butter.
iast: annādyajānāṃ sattvānāṃ rasajānāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ phalapuṣpaudbhavānāṃ ca ghṛtaprāśo viśodhanam

sanskrit_iast: kṛṣtajānāmoṣadhīnāṃ jātānāṃ ca svayaṃ vane vṛthālambhe 'nugacchedgāṃ dinamekaṃ payovrataḥ
meaning: कृष्तजानामोषधीनां जातानां च स्वयं वने वृथालम्भे 'नुगच्छेद्गां दिनमेकं पयोव्रतः
text: If one needlessly cuts plants grown by cultivation, or those that spontaneously grow in the forest, he shall attend on the cow for one day, subsisting on milk only.
iast: kṛṣtajānāmoṣadhīnāṃ jātānāṃ ca svayaṃ vane vṛthālambhe 'nugacchedgāṃ dinamekaṃ payovrataḥ

sanskrit_iast: etairvratairapohyaṃ syādeno hiṃsāsamudbhavam jñānājñānakṛtaṃ kṛtsnaṃ śṛṇutānādyabhakṣaṇe
meaning: एतैर्व्रतैरपोह्यं स्यादेनो हिंसासमुद्भवम् ज्ञानाज्ञानकृतं कृत्स्नं शृणुतानाद्यभक्षणे
text: By means of these penances shall one remove the six arising from the act of slaying, either intentionally or unintentionally. Now listen to all that is involved in eating forbidden food.
iast: etairvratairapohyaṃ syādeno hiṃsāsamudbhavam jñānājñānakṛtaṃ kṛtsnaṃ śṛṇutānādyabhakṣaṇe

sanskrit_iast: ajñānādvāruṇīṃ pītvā saṃskāreṇaiva śudhyati matipūrvamanirdeśyaṃ prāṇāntikamiti sthitiḥ
meaning: अज्ञानाद्वारुणीं पीत्वा संस्कारेणैव शुध्यति मतिपूर्वमनिर्देश्यं प्राणान्तिकमिति स्थितिः
text: If one drinks wine unintentionally, he becomes pure by a sacrament; this, however, is not what should be prescribed in the case of doing the act intentionally; in which case there should be a penance involving death;—such is the settled law.
iast: ajñānādvāruṇīṃ pītvā saṃskāreṇaiva śudhyati matipūrvamanirdeśyaṃ prāṇāntikamiti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: apaḥ surābhājanasthā madyabhāṇḍasthitāstathā pañcarātraṃ pibet pītvā śaṅkhapuṣpīśṛtaṃ payaḥ
meaning: अपः सुराभाजनस्था मद्यभाण्डस्थितास्तथा पञ्चरात्रं पिबेत् पीत्वा शङ्खपुष्पीशृतं पयः
text: If one has drunk water kept in a vessel used for keeping wine, or in a pot where intoxicating drinks are kept, he shall drink, for five days, milk in whichŚaṅkhapuṣpīhas been boiled.
iast: apaḥ surābhājanasthā madyabhāṇḍasthitāstathā pañcarātraṃ pibet pītvā śaṅkhapuṣpīśṛtaṃ payaḥ

sanskrit_iast: spṛṣṭva dattvā ca madirāṃ vidhivat pratigṛhya ca śūdraucchiṣṭāśca pītvā 'paḥ kuśavāri pibet tryaham
meaning: स्पृष्ट्व दत्त्वा च मदिरां विधिवत् प्रतिगृह्य च शूद्रौच्छिष्टाश्च पीत्वा 'पः कुशवारि पिबेत् त्र्यहम्
text: If one touches wine, or offers it to another, or receives it in due form,—or if he drinks water left by aŚūdra,—he shall drink kuśa-water for three days.
iast: spṛṣṭva dattvā ca madirāṃ vidhivat pratigṛhya ca śūdraucchiṣṭāśca pītvā 'paḥ kuśavāri pibet tryaham

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇastu surāpasya gandhamāghrāya somapaḥ prāṇānapsu trirāyamya ghṛtaṃ prāśya viśudhyati
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्तु सुरापस्य गन्धमाघ्राय सोमपः प्राणानप्सु त्रिरायम्य घृतं प्राश्य विशुध्यति
text: If aBrāhmaṇawho has partaken of the Soma inhales the odour given out by a wine-drinker, he becomes pure by thrice suppressing his breath in water and eating clarified butter.
iast: brāhmaṇastu surāpasya gandhamāghrāya somapaḥ prāṇānapsu trirāyamya ghṛtaṃ prāśya viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: ajñānāt prāśya viṣmūtraṃ surāsaṃspṛṣṭameva ca punaḥ saṃskāramarhanti trayo varṇā dvijātayaḥ
meaning: अज्ञानात् प्राश्य विष्मूत्रं सुरासंस्पृष्टमेव च पुनः संस्कारमर्हन्ति त्रयो वर्णा द्विजातयः
text: The three twice-born castes, who have unwittingly swallowed ordure or urine, or anything that has been in contact with wine, are liable to re-initiation.
iast: ajñānāt prāśya viṣmūtraṃ surāsaṃspṛṣṭameva ca punaḥ saṃskāramarhanti trayo varṇā dvijātayaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vapanaṃ mekhalā daṇḍo bhaikṣacaryā vratāni ca bhaikṣyacaryā nivartante dvijātīnāṃ punaḥsaṃskārakarmaṇi
meaning: वपनं मेखला दण्डो भैक्षचर्या व्रतानि च भैक्ष्यचर्या निवर्तन्ते द्विजातीनां पुनःसंस्कारकर्मणि
text: In the performance of the Re-initiation of twice-born men, tonsure, the girdle, the staff, begging alms, and the vows are omitted.
iast: vapanaṃ mekhalā daṇḍo bhaikṣacaryā vratāni ca bhaikṣyacaryā nivartante dvijātīnāṃ punaḥsaṃskārakarmaṇi

sanskrit_iast: abhojyānāṃ tu bhuktvā 'nnaṃ strīśūdraucchiṣṭameva ca jagdhvā māṃsamabhakṣyaṃ ca saptarātraṃ yavān pibet
meaning: अभोज्यानां तु भुक्त्वा 'न्नं स्त्रीशूद्रौच्छिष्टमेव च जग्ध्वा मांसमभक्ष्यं च सप्तरात्रं यवान् पिबेत्
text: If one has eaten the food of persons of unfit food, or food left by a woman or aŚūdra,—or forbidden flesh,—he shall drink barley for seven days.
iast: abhojyānāṃ tu bhuktvā 'nnaṃ strīśūdraucchiṣṭameva ca jagdhvā māṃsamabhakṣyaṃ ca saptarātraṃ yavān pibet

sanskrit_iast: śuktāni ca kaṣāyāṃśca pītvā medhyānyapi dvijaḥ tāvadbhavatyaprayato yāvat tanna vrajatyadhaḥ
meaning: शुक्तानि च कषायांश्च पीत्वा मेध्यान्यपि द्विजः तावद्भवत्यप्रयतो यावत् तन्न व्रजत्यधः
text: If a twice-born person drinks soured liquids or decoctions,—even though they be pure,—remains impure until it has gone down.
iast: śuktāni ca kaṣāyāṃśca pītvā medhyānyapi dvijaḥ tāvadbhavatyaprayato yāvat tanna vrajatyadhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: viḍvarāhakharauṣtrāṇāṃ gomāyoḥ kapikākayoḥ prāśya mūtrapurīṣāṇi dvijaścāndrāyaṇaṃ caret
meaning: विड्वराहखरौष्त्राणां गोमायोः कपिकाकयोः प्राश्य मूत्रपुरीषाणि द्विजश्चान्द्रायणं चरेत्
text: If a twice-born person swallows the ordure or urine of the village-pig, of an ass, of a camel, of a jackal, of a monkey, or of a crow—he shall perform theCāndrāyaṇa.
iast: viḍvarāhakharauṣtrāṇāṃ gomāyoḥ kapikākayoḥ prāśya mūtrapurīṣāṇi dvijaścāndrāyaṇaṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: śuṣkāṇi bhuktvā māṃsāni bhaumāni kavakāni ca ajñātaṃ caiva sūnāsthametadeva vrataṃ caret
meaning: शुष्काणि भुक्त्वा मांसानि भौमानि कवकानि च अज्ञातं चैव सूनास्थमेतदेव व्रतं चरेत्
text: If one eats dried meat, mushrooms growing on the ground, or unrecognised meat lying in the slaughter-house—he shall perform this same penance.
iast: śuṣkāṇi bhuktvā māṃsāni bhaumāni kavakāni ca ajñātaṃ caiva sūnāsthametadeva vrataṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: kravyādasūkarauṣṭrāṇāṃ kukkuṭānāṃ ca bhakṣaṇe narakākakharāṇāṃ ca taptakṛcchraṃ viśodhanam
meaning: क्रव्यादसूकरौष्ट्राणां कुक्कुटानां च भक्षणे नरकाकखराणां च तप्तकृच्छ्रं विशोधनम्
text: For eating the meat of carnivorous animals, of pigs, of camels, of cocks, of crows, of asses, or of human flesh,—the atonement consists of theTapta-Kṛcchra.
iast: kravyādasūkarauṣṭrāṇāṃ kukkuṭānāṃ ca bhakṣaṇe narakākakharāṇāṃ ca taptakṛcchraṃ viśodhanam

sanskrit_iast: māsikānnaṃ tu yo 'śnīyādasamāvartako dvijaḥ sa trīṇyahānyupavasedekāhaṃ caudake vaset
meaning: मासिकान्नं तु यो 'श्नीयादसमावर्तको द्विजः स त्रीण्यहान्युपवसेदेकाहं चौदके वसेत्
text: If a twice-born person, who has not completed his course of study, eats food given at a monthly rite, he shall fast for three days and remain one day in water.
iast: māsikānnaṃ tu yo 'śnīyādasamāvartako dvijaḥ sa trīṇyahānyupavasedekāhaṃ caudake vaset

sanskrit_iast: brahmacārī tu yo 'śnīyān madhu māṃsaṃ kathaṃ cana vratacārī tu sa kṛtvā prākṛtaṃ kṛcchraṃ vrataśeṣaṃ samāpayet
meaning: ब्रह्मचारी तु यो 'श्नीयान् मधु मांसं कथं चन व्रतचारी तु स कृत्वा प्राकृतं कृच्छ्रं व्रतशेषं समापयेत्
text: If a person keeping a vow happens, in any way, to eat honey or meat, he should perform the originalKṛcchra, and then complete the remainder of his vow.
iast: brahmacārī tu yo 'śnīyān madhu māṃsaṃ kathaṃ cana vratacārī tu sa kṛtvā prākṛtaṃ kṛcchraṃ vrataśeṣaṃ samāpayet

sanskrit_iast: biḍālakākākhūcchiṣṭaṃ jagdhvā śvanakulasya ca keśakīṭāvapannaṃ ca pibedbrahmasuvarcalām
meaning: बिडालकाकाखूच्छिष्टं जग्ध्वा श्वनकुलस्य च केशकीटावपन्नं च पिबेद्ब्रह्मसुवर्चलाम्
text: He who eats what has been left by a cat, a crow, a rat, a dog, or an ichneumon,—or food defiled by a hair or an insect,—shall drink theBrahmasuvarcalāherb.
iast: biḍālakākākhūcchiṣṭaṃ jagdhvā śvanakulasya ca keśakīṭāvapannaṃ ca pibedbrahmasuvarcalām

sanskrit_iast: abhojyamannaṃ nāttavyamātmanaḥ śuddhimicchatā ajñānabhuktaṃ tūttāryaṃ śodhyaṃ vā 'pyāśu śodhanaiḥ
meaning: अभोज्यमन्नं नात्तव्यमात्मनः शुद्धिमिच्छता अज्ञानभुक्तं तूत्तार्यं शोध्यं वा 'प्याशु शोधनैः
text: If one desires his own purity, he should not eat improper food; when eaten unintentionally, it should be thrown out, or speedily atoned for by means of purifications.
iast: abhojyamannaṃ nāttavyamātmanaḥ śuddhimicchatā ajñānabhuktaṃ tūttāryaṃ śodhyaṃ vā 'pyāśu śodhanaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: eṣo 'nādyādanasyaukto vratānāṃ vividho vidhiḥ steyadoṣāpahartr̥̄ṇāṃ vratānāṃ śrūyatāṃ vidhiḥ
meaning: एषो 'नाद्यादनस्यौक्तो व्रतानां विविधो विधिः स्तेयदोषापहर्त्र्̥̄णां व्रतानां श्रूयतां विधिः
text: Thus has the law relating to the penances for the eating of improper food been set forth; listen now to the law relating to the penances expiatory of the sins of theft.
iast: eṣo 'nādyādanasyaukto vratānāṃ vividho vidhiḥ steyadoṣāpahartr̥̄ṇāṃ vratānāṃ śrūyatāṃ vidhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: dhānyānnadhanacauryāṇi kṛtvā kāmāddvijottamaḥ svajātīyagṛhādeva kṛcchrābdena viśudhyati
meaning: धान्यान्नधनचौर्याणि कृत्वा कामाद्द्विजोत्तमः स्वजातीयगृहादेव कृच्छ्राब्देन विशुध्यति
text: If a chief of twice-born men intentionally commits theft of grains, cooked food and wealth, from the house of a caste-fellow, he becomes pure by performing theKṛcchrafor one year.
iast: dhānyānnadhanacauryāṇi kṛtvā kāmāddvijottamaḥ svajātīyagṛhādeva kṛcchrābdena viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: manuṣyāṇāṃ tu haraṇe strīṇāṃ kṣetragṛhasya ca kūpavāpījalānāṃ ca śuddhiścāndrāyaṇaṃ smṛtam
meaning: मनुष्याणां तु हरणे स्त्रीणां क्षेत्रगृहस्य च कूपवापीजलानां च शुद्धिश्चान्द्रायणं स्मृतम्
text: For the stealing of men and women, of a field or a house, or the water of a well, or a tank,—theCāndrāyaṇahas been declared to be the expiation.
iast: manuṣyāṇāṃ tu haraṇe strīṇāṃ kṣetragṛhasya ca kūpavāpījalānāṃ ca śuddhiścāndrāyaṇaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: dravyāṇāmalpasārāṇāṃ steyaṃ kṛtvā 'nyaveśmataḥ kṛtvā 'nyaveśmani caret sāṃtapanaṃ kṛcchraṃ tanniryātyātmaśuddhaye
meaning: द्रव्याणामल्पसाराणां स्तेयं कृत्वा 'न्यवेश्मतः कृत्वा 'न्यवेश्मनि चरेत् सांतपनं कृच्छ्रं तन्निर्यात्यात्मशुद्धये
text: If one steals things of small value in the house of another, he should restore them; and for his own purification, he should perform the ‘Sāntapana Kṛcchra.’
iast: dravyāṇāmalpasārāṇāṃ steyaṃ kṛtvā 'nyaveśmataḥ kṛtvā 'nyaveśmani caret sāṃtapanaṃ kṛcchraṃ tanniryātyātmaśuddhaye

sanskrit_iast: bhakṣyabhojyāpaharaṇe yānaśayyā ''sanasya ca puṣpamūlaphalānāṃ ca pañcagavyaṃ viśodhanam
meaning: भक्ष्यभोज्यापहरणे यानशय्या ''सनस्य च पुष्पमूलफलानां च पञ्चगव्यं विशोधनम्
text: For the stealing of eatables and edibles, of a conveyance or a bed, or a seat, or of fruits, roots and flowers,—the expiation consists of the Five Products of the cow.
iast: bhakṣyabhojyāpaharaṇe yānaśayyā ''sanasya ca puṣpamūlaphalānāṃ ca pañcagavyaṃ viśodhanam

sanskrit_iast: tṛṇakāṣṭhadrumāṇāṃ ca śuṣkānnasya guḍasya ca celacarmāmiṣāṇāṃ ca trirātraṃ syādabhojanam cailacarmāmikṣāṇāṃ
meaning: तृणकाष्ठद्रुमाणां च शुष्कान्नस्य गुडस्य च चेलचर्मामिषाणां च त्रिरात्रं स्यादभोजनम् चैलचर्मामिक्षाणां
text: There should be fasting for three days, in the case of stealing grass, wood, trees, dry food, molasses, clothes, leather and meat.
iast: tṛṇakāṣṭhadrumāṇāṃ ca śuṣkānnasya guḍasya ca celacarmāmiṣāṇāṃ ca trirātraṃ syādabhojanam cailacarmāmikṣāṇāṃ

sanskrit_iast: maṇimuktāpravālānāṃ tāmrasya rajatasya ca ayaḥkāṃsyaupalānāṃ ca dvādaśāhaṃ kaṇānnatā
meaning: मणिमुक्ताप्रवालानां ताम्रस्य रजतस्य च अयःकांस्यौपलानां च द्वादशाहं कणान्नता
text: In the case of the stealing of gems, pearls, corals, copper, silver, iron, bronze and stone, one should subsist, for twelve days, on pieces of grain.
iast: maṇimuktāpravālānāṃ tāmrasya rajatasya ca ayaḥkāṃsyaupalānāṃ ca dvādaśāhaṃ kaṇānnatā

sanskrit_iast: kārpāsakīṭajaurṇānāṃ dviśaphekaśaphasya ca dveśaphekakhurasya ca pakṣigandhoṣadhīnāṃ ca rajjvāścaiva tryahaṃ payaḥ
meaning: कार्पासकीटजौर्णानां द्विशफेकशफस्य च द्वेशफेकखुरस्य च पक्षिगन्धोषधीनां च रज्ज्वाश्चैव त्र्यहं पयः
text: In the case of cotton, silk, wool, an animal with cleft hoofs, an animal with uncleft hoofs, a bird, perfumes, medicinal herbs, and a rope,—milk shall be drunk for three days.
iast: kārpāsakīṭajaurṇānāṃ dviśaphekaśaphasya ca dveśaphekakhurasya ca pakṣigandhoṣadhīnāṃ ca rajjvāścaiva tryahaṃ payaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etairvratairapoheta pāpaṃ steyakṛtaṃ dvijaḥ agamyāgamanīyaṃ tu vratairebhirapānudet
meaning: एतैर्व्रतैरपोहेत पापं स्तेयकृतं द्विजः अगम्यागमनीयं तु व्रतैरेभिरपानुदेत्
text: By means of these penances, the twice-born man shall remove the sin caused by theft; that due to approaching women who should not be approached, he shall expiate by these (following) penances.
iast: etairvratairapoheta pāpaṃ steyakṛtaṃ dvijaḥ agamyāgamanīyaṃ tu vratairebhirapānudet

sanskrit_iast: gurutalpavrataṃ kuryādretaḥ siktvā svayoniṣu sakhyuḥ putrasya ca strīṣu kumārīṣvantyajāsu ca
meaning: गुरुतल्पव्रतं कुर्याद्रेतः सिक्त्वा स्वयोनिषु सख्युः पुत्रस्य च स्त्रीषु कुमारीष्वन्त्यजासु च
text: If one has had sexual intercourse with his uterine sister, or with the wife of his friend, or of his son, or with an unmarried maiden, or with a lowest-born woman,—he should perform the penance prescribed for the ‘violation of the Preceptor’s Bed.’
iast: gurutalpavrataṃ kuryādretaḥ siktvā svayoniṣu sakhyuḥ putrasya ca strīṣu kumārīṣvantyajāsu ca

sanskrit_iast: paitṛsvaseyīṃ bhaginīṃ svasrīyāṃ mātureva ca mātuśca bhrātustanayāṃ gatvā cāndrāyaṇaṃ caret [bhrāturāptasya gatvā
meaning: पैतृस्वसेयीं भगिनीं स्वस्रीयां मातुरेव च मातुश्च भ्रातुस्तनयां गत्वा चान्द्रायणं चरेत् [भ्रातुराप्तस्य गत्वा
text: On having had intercourse with one’s sister born of his father’s sister, or of his mother’s sister, or of his mother’s full brother,—one should perform theCāndrāyaṇa.
iast: paitṛsvaseyīṃ bhaginīṃ svasrīyāṃ mātureva ca mātuśca bhrātustanayāṃ gatvā cāndrāyaṇaṃ caret [bhrāturāptasya gatvā

sanskrit_iast: etāstisrastu bhāryārthe naupayacchet tu buddhimān jñātitvenānupeyāstāḥ patati hyupayannadhaḥ
meaning: एतास्तिस्रस्तु भार्यार्थे नौपयच्छेत् तु बुद्धिमान् ज्ञातित्वेनानुपेयास्ताः पतति ह्युपयन्नधः
text: A wise man should never take these three as his wife; being blood-relations, they are not fit to be married; because by marrying them one sinks low.
iast: etāstisrastu bhāryārthe naupayacchet tu buddhimān jñātitvenānupeyāstāḥ patati hyupayannadhaḥ

sanskrit_iast: amānuṣīṣū puruṣa udakyāyāmayoniṣu retaḥ siktvā jale caiva kṛcchraṃ sāṃtapanaṃ caret
meaning: अमानुषीषू पुरुष उदक्यायामयोनिषु रेतः सिक्त्वा जले चैव कृच्छ्रं सांतपनं चरेत्
text: A man who has had sexual intercourse with nonhuman females, or with a menstruating woman,—and he who has discharged his semen in a place other than the female organ, or in water,—should perporm the ‘Sāntapana Kṛcchra.’
iast: amānuṣīṣū puruṣa udakyāyāmayoniṣu retaḥ siktvā jale caiva kṛcchraṃ sāṃtapanaṃ caret

sanskrit_iast: maithunaṃ tu samāsevya puṃsi yoṣiti vā dvijaḥ goyāne 'psu divā caiva savāsāḥ snānamācaret
meaning: मैथुनं तु समासेव्य पुंसि योषिति वा द्विजः गोयाने 'प्सु दिवा चैव सवासाः स्नानमाचरेत्
text: If a twice-born man commits an unnatural offence with a male, or has intercourse with a female, in an ox-cart, or in water, or during the day,—he should take a bath along with his clothes.
iast: maithunaṃ tu samāsevya puṃsi yoṣiti vā dvijaḥ goyāne 'psu divā caiva savāsāḥ snānamācaret

sanskrit_iast: caṇḍālāntyastriyo gatvā bhuktvā ca pratigṛhya ca patatyajñānato vipro jñānāt sāmyaṃ tu gacchati
meaning: चण्डालान्त्यस्त्रियो गत्वा भुक्त्वा च प्रतिगृह्य च पतत्यज्ञानतो विप्रो ज्ञानात् साम्यं तु गच्छति
text: If aBrāhmaṇaunintentionally approaches a woman of theCaṇḍālaor other lowest-born castes,—or eats her food, or receives her presents,—he becomes an outcast; but if he does it intentionally, he becomes her equal.
iast: caṇḍālāntyastriyo gatvā bhuktvā ca pratigṛhya ca patatyajñānato vipro jñānāt sāmyaṃ tu gacchati

sanskrit_iast: vipraduṣṭāṃ striyaṃ bhartā nirundhyādekaveśmani yat puṃsaḥ paradāreṣu taccaināṃ cārayedvratam
meaning: विप्रदुष्टां स्त्रियं भर्ता निरुन्ध्यादेकवेश्मनि यत् पुंसः परदारेषु तच्चैनां चारयेद्व्रतम्
text: If the wife is particularly corrupt, her husband should keep her confined in one room, and should make her perform that penance which has been prescribed for males in cases of adultery.
iast: vipraduṣṭāṃ striyaṃ bhartā nirundhyādekaveśmani yat puṃsaḥ paradāreṣu taccaināṃ cārayedvratam

sanskrit_iast: sā cet punaḥ praduṣyet tu sadṛśenaupamantritā sadṛśenaupayantritā ? kṛcchraṃ cāndrāyaṇaṃ caiva tadasyāḥ pāvanaṃ smṛtam
meaning: सा चेत् पुनः प्रदुष्येत् तु सदृशेनौपमन्त्रिता सदृशेनौपयन्त्रिता ? कृच्छ्रं चान्द्रायणं चैव तदस्याः पावनं स्मृतम्
text: If she happens to be corrupted again, on being solicited by a man of equal caste,—then theKṛcchraand theCāndrāyaṇawould be the means prescribed for her purification.
iast: sā cet punaḥ praduṣyet tu sadṛśenaupamantritā sadṛśenaupayantritā ? kṛcchraṃ cāndrāyaṇaṃ caiva tadasyāḥ pāvanaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: yat karotyekarātreṇa vṛṣalīsevanāddvijaḥ tadbhaikṣabhujjapannityaṃ tribhirvarṣairvyapohati
meaning: यत् करोत्येकरात्रेण वृषलीसेवनाद्द्विजः तद्भैक्षभुज्जपन्नित्यं त्रिभिर्वर्षैर्व्यपोहति
text: What a twice-born man commits by dallying with aCaṇḍālīfor one night,—that he wipes off in three years, living on alms and constantly repeating (sacred texts).
iast: yat karotyekarātreṇa vṛṣalīsevanāddvijaḥ tadbhaikṣabhujjapannityaṃ tribhirvarṣairvyapohati

sanskrit_iast: eṣā pāpakṛtāmuktā caturṇāmapi niṣkṛtiḥ patitaiḥ saṃprayuktānāmimāḥ śṛṇuta niṣkṛtīḥ
meaning: एषा पापकृतामुक्ता चतुर्णामपि निष्कृतिः पतितैः संप्रयुक्तानामिमाः शृणुत निष्कृतीः
text: Thus has the atonement tor the four kinds of sinners been set forth; now listen to these (following) expiations for those who associate with outcasts.
iast: eṣā pāpakṛtāmuktā caturṇāmapi niṣkṛtiḥ patitaiḥ saṃprayuktānāmimāḥ śṛṇuta niṣkṛtīḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃvatsareṇa patati patitena sahācaran yājanādhyāpanādyaunātna tu yānāsanāśanāt
meaning: संवत्सरेण पतति पतितेन सहाचरन् याजनाध्यापनाद्यौनात्न तु यानासनाशनात्
text: If one associates with an outcast for one year, he himself becomes an outcast; not by sacrificing for him, or teaching him, or forming a matrimonial alliance with him,—but by walking, sitting or eating.
iast: saṃvatsareṇa patati patitena sahācaran yājanādhyāpanādyaunātna tu yānāsanāśanāt

sanskrit_iast: yo yena patitenaiṣāṃ saṃsargaṃ yāti mānavaḥ sa tasyaiva vrataṃ kuryāt tatsaṃsargaviśuddhaye
meaning: यो येन पतितेनैषां संसर्गं याति मानवः स तस्यैव व्रतं कुर्यात् तत्संसर्गविशुद्धये
text: When a man associates with any one of outcasts, he should, for his own purification, perform the same penance that has been prescribed for that outcast.
iast: yo yena patitenaiṣāṃ saṃsargaṃ yāti mānavaḥ sa tasyaiva vrataṃ kuryāt tatsaṃsargaviśuddhaye

sanskrit_iast: patitasyaudakaṃ kāryaṃ sapiṇḍairbāndhavairbahiḥ nindite 'hani sāyāhne jñātiṛtvijgurusaṃnidhau
meaning: पतितस्यौदकं कार्यं सपिण्डैर्बान्धवैर्बहिः निन्दिते 'हनि सायाह्ने ज्ञातिऋत्विज्गुरुसंनिधौ
text: When one has become an outcast, hisSapiṇḍasand relations shall offer him ‘water’ outside, on an inauspicious day, in the evening, in the presence of relatives, priests and elders.
iast: patitasyaudakaṃ kāryaṃ sapiṇḍairbāndhavairbahiḥ nindite 'hani sāyāhne jñātiṛtvijgurusaṃnidhau

sanskrit_iast: dāsī ghaṭamapāṃ pūrṇaṃ paryasyet pretavat padā ahorātramupāsīrannaśaucaṃ bāndhavaiḥ saha
meaning: दासी घटमपां पूर्णं पर्यस्येत् प्रेतवत् पदा अहोरात्रमुपासीरन्नशौचं बान्धवैः सह
text: A female slave shall overturn a jar full of water with her foot, as in the case of the dead; and they, along with the relations, shall observe the ‘uncleanliness’ for the day and night.
iast: dāsī ghaṭamapāṃ pūrṇaṃ paryasyet pretavat padā ahorātramupāsīrannaśaucaṃ bāndhavaiḥ saha

sanskrit_iast: nivarteraṃśca tasmāt tu saṃbhāṣaṇasahāsane dāyādyasya pradānaṃ ca yātrā caiva hi laukikī
meaning: निवर्तेरंश्च तस्मात् तु संभाषणसहासने दायाद्यस्य प्रदानं च यात्रा चैव हि लौकिकी
text: Thenceforth shall cease all conversation with him, sitting with him, his sharing in property, as also all ordinary intercourse.
iast: nivarteraṃśca tasmāt tu saṃbhāṣaṇasahāsane dāyādyasya pradānaṃ ca yātrā caiva hi laukikī

sanskrit_iast: jyeṣṭhatā ca nivarteta jyeṣṭhāvāpyaṃ ca yaddhanam yadvasu jyeṣṭhāṃśaṃ prāpnuyāccāsya yavīyān guṇato 'dhikaḥ
meaning: ज्येष्ठता च निवर्तेत ज्येष्ठावाप्यं च यद्धनम् यद्वसु ज्येष्ठांशं प्राप्नुयाच्चास्य यवीयान् गुणतो 'धिकः
text: The right of primogeniture shall be withheld, as also the additional share of property due to the eldest; the additional share due to him as the eldest shall be obtained by his younger brother, who is superior to him in quality.
iast: jyeṣṭhatā ca nivarteta jyeṣṭhāvāpyaṃ ca yaddhanam yadvasu jyeṣṭhāṃśaṃ prāpnuyāccāsya yavīyān guṇato 'dhikaḥ

sanskrit_iast: prāyaścitte tu carite pūrṇakumbhamapāṃ navam tenaiva sārdhaṃ prāsyeyuḥ snātvā puṇye jalāśaye
meaning: प्रायश्चित्ते तु चरिते पूर्णकुम्भमपां नवम् तेनैव सार्धं प्रास्येयुः स्नात्वा पुण्ये जलाशये
text: If however the expiation has been performed, they shall bathe with him in a sacred reservoir of water and shall throw into the water a fresh jar filled with water.
iast: prāyaścitte tu carite pūrṇakumbhamapāṃ navam tenaiva sārdhaṃ prāsyeyuḥ snātvā puṇye jalāśaye

sanskrit_iast: sa tvapsu taṃ ghaṭaṃ prāsya praviśya bhavanaṃ svakam sarvāṇi jñātikāryāṇi yathāpūrvaṃ samācaret
meaning: स त्वप्सु तं घटं प्रास्य प्रविश्य भवनं स्वकम् सर्वाणि ज्ञातिकार्याणि यथापूर्वं समाचरेत्
text: Having thrown that jar into the water, he shall enter his own house and carry on, as before, all his family-functions.
iast: sa tvapsu taṃ ghaṭaṃ prāsya praviśya bhavanaṃ svakam sarvāṇi jñātikāryāṇi yathāpūrvaṃ samācaret

sanskrit_iast: etadeva vidhiṃ kuryādyoṣitsu patitāsvapi etameva vidhiṃ vastrānnapānaṃ deyaṃ tu vaseyuśca gṛhāntike
meaning: एतदेव विधिं कुर्याद्योषित्सु पतितास्वपि एतमेव विधिं वस्त्रान्नपानं देयं तु वसेयुश्च गृहान्तिके
text: This same method is to be adopted also in the case of female outcasts; but clothing, food and drink shall be supplied to them and they shall live close to the house.
iast: etadeva vidhiṃ kuryādyoṣitsu patitāsvapi etameva vidhiṃ vastrānnapānaṃ deyaṃ tu vaseyuśca gṛhāntike

sanskrit_iast: enasvibhiranirṇiktairnārthaṃ kiṃ cit sahācaret kṛtanirṇejanāṃścaiva na jugupseta karhi cit kṛtanirṇejanāṃścaitān
meaning: एनस्विभिरनिर्णिक्तैर्नार्थं किं चित् सहाचरेत् कृतनिर्णेजनांश्चैव न जुगुप्सेत कर्हि चित् कृतनिर्णेजनांश्चैतान्
text: One shall not carry on any business with unexpiated sinners; but in no case shall he despise those who have performed the expiation.
iast: enasvibhiranirṇiktairnārthaṃ kiṃ cit sahācaret kṛtanirṇejanāṃścaiva na jugupseta karhi cit kṛtanirṇejanāṃścaitān

sanskrit_iast: bālaghnāṃśca kṛtaghnāṃśca viśuddhānapi dharmataḥ śaraṇāgatahantr̥̄ṃśca strīhantr̥̄ṃśca na saṃvaset
meaning: बालघ्नांश्च कृतघ्नांश्च विशुद्धानपि धर्मतः शरणागतहन्त्र्̥̄ंश्च स्त्रीहन्त्र्̥̄ंश्च न संवसेत्
text: One shall not associate with murderers of children, ungrateful men, murderers of a refugee, and murderers of women,—even though they may have been duly purified.
iast: bālaghnāṃśca kṛtaghnāṃśca viśuddhānapi dharmataḥ śaraṇāgatahantr̥̄ṃśca strīhantr̥̄ṃśca na saṃvaset

sanskrit_iast: yeṣāṃ dvijānāṃ sāvitrī nānūcyeta yathāvidhi tāṃścārayitvā trīn kṛcchrān yathāvidhyopanāyayet
meaning: येषां द्विजानां सावित्री नानूच्येत यथाविधि तांश्चारयित्वा त्रीन् कृच्छ्रान् यथाविध्योपनाययेत्
text: Though twice-born men to whom theSāvitrīhas not been taught according to rule, should be made to perform threeKṛcchrapenances and then initiated in due form.
iast: yeṣāṃ dvijānāṃ sāvitrī nānūcyeta yathāvidhi tāṃścārayitvā trīn kṛcchrān yathāvidhyopanāyayet

sanskrit_iast: prāyaścittaṃ cikīrṣanti vikarmasthāstu ye dvijāḥ brahmaṇā ca parityaktāsteṣāmapyetadādiśet
meaning: प्रायश्चित्तं चिकीर्षन्ति विकर्मस्थास्तु ये द्विजाः ब्रह्मणा च परित्यक्तास्तेषामप्येतदादिशेत्
text: When twice-born men, who follow improper occupations, or who are abandoned by the Veda, are desirous of performing expiations—for these also this same expiation is to be prescribed.
iast: prāyaścittaṃ cikīrṣanti vikarmasthāstu ye dvijāḥ brahmaṇā ca parityaktāsteṣāmapyetadādiśet

sanskrit_iast: yadgarhitenārjayanti karmaṇā brāhmaṇā dhanam tasyautsargeṇa śudhyanti japyena tapasaiva ca
meaning: यद्गर्हितेनार्जयन्ति कर्मणा ब्राह्मणा धनम् तस्यौत्सर्गेण शुध्यन्ति जप्येन तपसैव च
text: WhenBrāhmaṇasacquire property by an objectionable act, they become pure by giving it up, and also by repeating sacred texts and performing austerities.
iast: yadgarhitenārjayanti karmaṇā brāhmaṇā dhanam tasyautsargeṇa śudhyanti japyena tapasaiva ca

sanskrit_iast: japitvā trīṇi sāvitryāḥ sahasrāṇi samāhitaḥ māsaṃ goṣṭhe payaḥ pītvā mucyate 'satpratigrahāt
meaning: जपित्वा त्रीणि सावित्र्याः सहस्राणि समाहितः मासं गोष्ठे पयः पीत्वा मुच्यते 'सत्प्रतिग्रहात्
text: Having, with concentrated mind, repeated theSāvitrīthree thousand times, and drinking milk in a cow-pen for one month, he becomes absolved from the sin of accepting an improper gift.
iast: japitvā trīṇi sāvitryāḥ sahasrāṇi samāhitaḥ māsaṃ goṣṭhe payaḥ pītvā mucyate 'satpratigrahāt

sanskrit_iast: upavāsakṛśaṃ taṃ tu govrajāt punarāgatam praṇataṃ prati pṛccheyuḥ sāmyaṃ saumyaicchasīti kim
meaning: उपवासकृशं तं तु गोव्रजात् पुनरागतम् प्रणतं प्रति पृच्छेयुः साम्यं सौम्यैच्छसीति किम्
text: When he has returned from the cow-pen, emaciated with the fast, and humble, they shall ask him—‘friend, dost thou desire equality with us?’
iast: upavāsakṛśaṃ taṃ tu govrajāt punarāgatam praṇataṃ prati pṛccheyuḥ sāmyaṃ saumyaicchasīti kim

sanskrit_iast: satyamuktvā tu vipreṣu vikiredyavasaṃ gavām gobhiḥ pravartite tīrthe kuryustasya parigraham
meaning: सत्यमुक्त्वा तु विप्रेषु विकिरेद्यवसं गवाम् गोभिः प्रवर्तिते तीर्थे कुर्युस्तस्य परिग्रहम्
text: Having said ‘Forsooth’ to theBrāhmaṇas, he shall scatter grass to the cows; whereupon they shall accord admission to him at a place hallowed by the cows.
iast: satyamuktvā tu vipreṣu vikiredyavasaṃ gavām gobhiḥ pravartite tīrthe kuryustasya parigraham

sanskrit_iast: vrātyānāṃ yājanaṃ kṛtvā pareṣāmantyakarma ca abhicāramahīnaṃ ca tribhiḥ kṛcchrairvyapohati
meaning: व्रात्यानां याजनं कृत्वा परेषामन्त्यकर्म च अभिचारमहीनं च त्रिभिः कृच्छ्रैर्व्यपोहति
text: If one sacrifices for apostates, or performs the obsequies of strangers, or malevolent rites, or theAhīnasacrifice,—he wipes it off by threeKṛcchras.
iast: vrātyānāṃ yājanaṃ kṛtvā pareṣāmantyakarma ca abhicāramahīnaṃ ca tribhiḥ kṛcchrairvyapohati

sanskrit_iast: śaraṇāgataṃ parityajya vedaṃ viplāvya ca dvijaḥ saṃvatsaraṃ yavāhārastat pāpamapasedhati
meaning: शरणागतं परित्यज्य वेदं विप्लाव्य च द्विजः संवत्सरं यवाहारस्तत् पापमपसेधति
text: If a twice-born man has abandoned a refugee, or has tampered with the Veda, he atones for that offence by living upon barley for one year.
iast: śaraṇāgataṃ parityajya vedaṃ viplāvya ca dvijaḥ saṃvatsaraṃ yavāhārastat pāpamapasedhati

sanskrit_iast: śvaśṛgālakharairdaṣṭo grāmyaiḥ kravyādbhireva ca narāśvauṣṭravarāhaiśca prāṇāyāmena śudhyati
meaning: श्वशृगालखरैर्दष्टो ग्राम्यैः क्रव्याद्भिरेव च नराश्वौष्ट्रवराहैश्च प्राणायामेन शुध्यति
text: When bitten by a dog, or a jackal, or an ass, or by a tame carnivorous animal, or by a man, or a camel, or a pig—he becomes pure by ‘breath-suppression.’
iast: śvaśṛgālakharairdaṣṭo grāmyaiḥ kravyādbhireva ca narāśvauṣṭravarāhaiśca prāṇāyāmena śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṣṭhānnakālatā māsaṃ saṃhitājapa eva vā homāśca sakalā nityamapāṅktyānāṃ viśodhanam omāśca śākalā
meaning: षष्ठान्नकालता मासं संहिताजप एव वा होमाश्च सकला नित्यमपाङ्क्त्यानां विशोधनम् ओमाश्च शाकला
text: For persons excommunicated from repasts, the purification consists in (a) eating at the sixth meal-time, reciting the Vedic text, and the daily offering of the ‘Sākala homa,’ for a month.
iast: ṣaṣṭhānnakālatā māsaṃ saṃhitājapa eva vā homāśca sakalā nityamapāṅktyānāṃ viśodhanam omāśca śākalā

sanskrit_iast: uṣṭrayānaṃ samāruhya kharayānaṃ tu kāmataḥ snātvā tu vipro digvāsāḥ prāṇāyāmena śudhyati
meaning: उष्ट्रयानं समारुह्य खरयानं तु कामतः स्नात्वा तु विप्रो दिग्वासाः प्राणायामेन शुध्यति
text: If aBrāhmaṇaintentionally hides a conveyance drawn by a camel, or one drawn by asses,—or if he bathes naked,—he becomes pure by ‘breath-suppression.’
iast: uṣṭrayānaṃ samāruhya kharayānaṃ tu kāmataḥ snātvā tu vipro digvāsāḥ prāṇāyāmena śudhyati

sanskrit_iast: vinā 'dbhirapsu vā 'pyārtaḥ śārīraṃ saṃniṣevya ca sacailo bahirāplutya gāmālabhya viśudhyati
meaning: विना 'द्भिरप्सु वा 'प्यार्तः शारीरं संनिषेव्य च सचैलो बहिराप्लुत्य गामालभ्य विशुध्यति
text: If, on being pressed, one passes bodily refuse, either without water, or in water, he becomes pure by bathing in his clothes, outside and touching a cow.
iast: vinā 'dbhirapsu vā 'pyārtaḥ śārīraṃ saṃniṣevya ca sacailo bahirāplutya gāmālabhya viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: vedauditānāṃ nityānāṃ karmaṇāṃ samatikrame snātakavratalope ca prāyaścittamabhojanam
meaning: वेदौदितानां नित्यानां कर्मणां समतिक्रमे स्नातकव्रतलोपे च प्रायश्चित्तमभोजनम्
text: For the neglect of the compulsory duties laid down by the Veda, and for the omission of the observances of the Accomplished Student,—the expiation is fasting.
iast: vedauditānāṃ nityānāṃ karmaṇāṃ samatikrame snātakavratalope ca prāyaścittamabhojanam

sanskrit_iast: huṅkāraṃ brāhmaṇasyauktvā tvaṅkāraṃ ca garīyasaḥ snātvā 'naśnannahaḥ śeṣamabhivādya prasādayet
meaning: हुङ्कारं ब्राह्मणस्यौक्त्वा त्वङ्कारं च गरीयसः स्नात्वा 'नश्नन्नहः शेषमभिवाद्य प्रसादयेत्
text: If a man has uttered the syllable ‘hum’ against aBrāhmaṇa, or has addressed a superior person as ‘thou,’—he shall bathe, fast for the rest of the day and having saluted him, shall propitiate him.
iast: huṅkāraṃ brāhmaṇasyauktvā tvaṅkāraṃ ca garīyasaḥ snātvā 'naśnannahaḥ śeṣamabhivādya prasādayet

sanskrit_iast: tāḍayitvā tṛṇenāpi kaṇṭhe vā 'badhya vāsasā vivāde vā vinirjitya praṇipatya prasādayet
meaning: ताडयित्वा तृणेनापि कण्ठे वा 'बध्य वाससा विवादे वा विनिर्जित्य प्रणिपत्य प्रसादयेत्
text: Having struck him even with a blade of grass, or having tied him in the neck with a cloth, or having defeated him in an altercation,—one shall bow to him and appease him.
iast: tāḍayitvā tṛṇenāpi kaṇṭhe vā 'badhya vāsasā vivāde vā vinirjitya praṇipatya prasādayet

sanskrit_iast: avagūrya tvabdaśataṃ sahasramabhihatya ca jighāṃsayā brāhmaṇasya narakaṃ pratipadyate
meaning: अवगूर्य त्वब्दशतं सहस्रमभिहत्य च जिघांसया ब्राह्मणस्य नरकं प्रतिपद्यते
text: If, with the intention of injuring aBrāhmaṇa, one has threatened him, he shall remain in hell for a hundred years; and for one thousand years, if he has struck him.
iast: avagūrya tvabdaśataṃ sahasramabhihatya ca jighāṃsayā brāhmaṇasya narakaṃ pratipadyate

sanskrit_iast: śoṇitaṃ yāvataḥ pāṃsūn saṅgṛhṇāti mahītale tāvantyabdasahasrāṇi tatkartā narake vaset narake vrajet
meaning: शोणितं यावतः पांसून् सङ्गृह्णाति महीतले तावन्त्यब्दसहस्राणि तत्कर्ता नरके वसेत् नरके व्रजेत्
text: As many particles of dust on the ground asBrāhmaṇa’sblood coagulates, for so many thousand years will the shedder (of that blood lie in hell.
iast: śoṇitaṃ yāvataḥ pāṃsūn saṅgṛhṇāti mahītale tāvantyabdasahasrāṇi tatkartā narake vaset narake vrajet

sanskrit_iast: avagūrya caret kṛcchramatikṛcchraṃ nipātane kṛcchrātikṛcchrau kurvīta viprasyautpādya śoṇitam
meaning: अवगूर्य चरेत् कृच्छ्रमतिकृच्छ्रं निपातने कृच्छ्रातिकृच्छ्रौ कुर्वीत विप्रस्यौत्पाद्य शोणितम्
text: On threatening aBrāhmaṇa, one shall perform theKṛcchra; on striking him, theAtikṛcchara, and on shedding his blood, both theKṛcchraand theAtikṛcchra.
iast: avagūrya caret kṛcchramatikṛcchraṃ nipātane kṛcchrātikṛcchrau kurvīta viprasyautpādya śoṇitam

sanskrit_iast: anuktaniṣkṛtīnāṃ tu pāpānāmapanuttaye śaktiṃ cāvekṣya pāpaṃ ca prāyaścittaṃ prakalpayet
meaning: अनुक्तनिष्कृतीनां तु पापानामपनुत्तये शक्तिं चावेक्ष्य पापं च प्रायश्चित्तं प्रकल्पयेत्
text: For the atonement of offences for which no expiation has been prescribed, one should fix an expiation after taking into consideration the man’s capacity and the nature of the offence.
iast: anuktaniṣkṛtīnāṃ tu pāpānāmapanuttaye śaktiṃ cāvekṣya pāpaṃ ca prāyaścittaṃ prakalpayet

sanskrit_iast: yairabhyupāyairenāṃsi mānavo vyapakarṣati tān vo 'bhyupāyān vakṣyāmi devaṛṣipitṛsevitān
meaning: यैरभ्युपायैरेनांसि मानवो व्यपकर्षति तान् वो 'भ्युपायान् वक्ष्यामि देवऋषिपितृसेवितान्
text: I am now going to describe to you those methods, adopted by gods and sages andPitṛs, by means of which a man may wipe off his sins.
iast: yairabhyupāyairenāṃsi mānavo vyapakarṣati tān vo 'bhyupāyān vakṣyāmi devaṛṣipitṛsevitān

sanskrit_iast: tryahaṃ prātastryahaṃ sāyaṃ tryahamadyādayācitam tryahaṃ paraṃ ca nāśnīyāt prājāpatyaṃ caran dvijaḥ
meaning: त्र्यहं प्रातस्त्र्यहं सायं त्र्यहमद्यादयाचितम् त्र्यहं परं च नाश्नीयात् प्राजापत्यं चरन् द्विजः
text: The twice-born, who is performing thePrājāpatya, shall eat in the morning for three days, then in the evening for three days, then for three days food got unasked, and for the next three days he shall not eat.
iast: tryahaṃ prātastryahaṃ sāyaṃ tryahamadyādayācitam tryahaṃ paraṃ ca nāśnīyāt prājāpatyaṃ caran dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: gomūtraṃ gomayaṃ kṣīraṃ dadhi sarpiḥ kuśaudakam ekarātraupavāsaśca kṛcchraṃ sāṃtapanaṃ smṛtam
meaning: गोमूत्रं गोमयं क्षीरं दधि सर्पिः कुशौदकम् एकरात्रौपवासश्च कृच्छ्रं सांतपनं स्मृतम्
text: Taking cow’s urine, cow-dung, milk, curds, clarified butter, kuśa-water, and fasting for one day,—has been declared to be ‘Sāntapana Kṛcchra’
iast: gomūtraṃ gomayaṃ kṣīraṃ dadhi sarpiḥ kuśaudakam ekarātraupavāsaśca kṛcchraṃ sāṃtapanaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: ekaikaṃ grāsamaśnīyāt tryahāṇi trīṇi pūrvavat tryahaṃ caupavasedantyamatikṛcchraṃ caran dvijaḥ
meaning: एकैकं ग्रासमश्नीयात् त्र्यहाणि त्रीणि पूर्ववत् त्र्यहं चौपवसेदन्त्यमतिकृच्छ्रं चरन् द्विजः
text: The twice-born man who is performing the ‘Atikṛcchra’ shall eat only one mouthful at each of the three times mentioned above, for three days,—and shall fast during the last three days.
iast: ekaikaṃ grāsamaśnīyāt tryahāṇi trīṇi pūrvavat tryahaṃ caupavasedantyamatikṛcchraṃ caran dvijaḥ

sanskrit_iast: taptakṛcchraṃ caran vipro jalakṣīraghṛtānilān pratitryahaṃ pibeduṣṇān sakṛtsnāyī samāhitaḥ
meaning: तप्तकृच्छ्रं चरन् विप्रो जलक्षीरघृतानिलान् प्रतित्र्यहं पिबेदुष्णान् सकृत्स्नायी समाहितः
text: TheBrāhmaṇawho is performing the ‘Tapta-Kṛcchra’ shall drink hot water, hot milk, hot clarified butter and hot air,—each for three days; bathing once and having his mind concentrated.
iast: taptakṛcchraṃ caran vipro jalakṣīraghṛtānilān pratitryahaṃ pibeduṣṇān sakṛtsnāyī samāhitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yatātmano 'pramattasya dvādaśāhamabhojanam parāko nāma kṛcchro 'yaṃ sarvapāpāpanodanaḥ
meaning: यतात्मनो 'प्रमत्तस्य द्वादशाहमभोजनम् पराको नाम कृच्छ्रो 'यं सर्वपापापनोदनः
text: If a man, self-controlled and guarded, fasts for twelve days,—this constitutes the ‘Kṛcchra’ name ‘Parāka’, which removes all sins.
iast: yatātmano 'pramattasya dvādaśāhamabhojanam parāko nāma kṛcchro 'yaṃ sarvapāpāpanodanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: ekaikaṃ hrāsayet piṇḍaṃ kṛṣṇe śukle ca vardhayet upaspṛśaṃstriṣavaṇametatcāṇḍrāyaṇaṃ smṛtam
meaning: एकैकं ह्रासयेत् पिण्डं कृष्णे शुक्ले च वर्धयेत् उपस्पृशंस्त्रिषवणमेतत्चाण्ड्रायणं स्मृतम्
text: If one reduces his food by one morsel daily during the dark half of the month, and increases it during the light half,—bathing at the three ‘extractions,’—it is what has been called the ‘Cāndrāyaṇa.’
iast: ekaikaṃ hrāsayet piṇḍaṃ kṛṣṇe śukle ca vardhayet upaspṛśaṃstriṣavaṇametatcāṇḍrāyaṇaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: etameva vidhiṃ kṛtsnamācaredyavamadhyame śuklapakṣādiniyataścaraṃścāndrāyaṇaṃ vratam
meaning: एतमेव विधिं कृत्स्नमाचरेद्यवमध्यमे शुक्लपक्षादिनियतश्चरंश्चान्द्रायणं व्रतम्
text: This same method shall be adopted in the ‘Yavamadhyama’ penance, but beginning it in the bright half of the month; and it is with a controlled mind that one should perform the ‘Cāndrāyaṇa’ penance.
iast: etameva vidhiṃ kṛtsnamācaredyavamadhyame śuklapakṣādiniyataścaraṃścāndrāyaṇaṃ vratam

sanskrit_iast: aṣṭāvaṣṭau samaśnīyāt piṇḍān madhyaṃdine sthite niyatātmā haviṣyāśī yaticāndrāyaṇaṃ caran
meaning: अष्टावष्टौ समश्नीयात् पिण्डान् मध्यंदिने स्थिते नियतात्मा हविष्याशी यतिचान्द्रायणं चरन्
text: One who performs the ‘Yati-Cāndrāyaṇa’ shall eat daily at midday eight morsels, controlling himself and eating only ‘sacrificial food.’
iast: aṣṭāvaṣṭau samaśnīyāt piṇḍān madhyaṃdine sthite niyatātmā haviṣyāśī yaticāndrāyaṇaṃ caran

sanskrit_iast: caturaḥ prātaraśnīyāt piṇḍān vipraḥ samāhitaḥ caturo 'stamite sūrye śiśucāndrāyaṇaṃ smṛtam
meaning: चतुरः प्रातरश्नीयात् पिण्डान् विप्रः समाहितः चतुरो 'स्तमिते सूर्ये शिशुचान्द्रायणं स्मृतम्
text: TheBrāhmaṇa, with concentrated mind, shall eat four morsels in the morning, and four when the sun has set this is called the ‘Śiśu-Cāndrāyaṇa.’
iast: caturaḥ prātaraśnīyāt piṇḍān vipraḥ samāhitaḥ caturo 'stamite sūrye śiśucāndrāyaṇaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: yathā kathaṃ cit piṇḍānāṃ tisro 'śītīḥ samāhitaḥ māsenāśnan haviṣyasya candrasyaiti salokatām
meaning: यथा कथं चित् पिण्डानां तिस्रो 'शीतीः समाहितः मासेनाश्नन् हविष्यस्य चन्द्रस्यैति सलोकताम्
text: If a man, with concentrated mind, somehow eats thrice eighty morsels of ‘sacrificial food,’ during a month, he attains the regions of the Moon.
iast: yathā kathaṃ cit piṇḍānāṃ tisro 'śītīḥ samāhitaḥ māsenāśnan haviṣyasya candrasyaiti salokatām

sanskrit_iast: etadrudrāstathā 'dityā vasavaścācaran vratam sarvākuśalamokṣāya marutaśca maharṣibhiḥ
meaning: एतद्रुद्रास्तथा 'दित्या वसवश्चाचरन् व्रतम् सर्वाकुशलमोक्षाय मरुतश्च महर्षिभिः
text: The Rudras,Ādityasand the Maruts, along with the Great Sages, have performed this penance, for deliverance from all evils.
iast: etadrudrāstathā 'dityā vasavaścācaran vratam sarvākuśalamokṣāya marutaśca maharṣibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: mahāvyāhṛtibhirhomaḥ kartavyaḥ svayamanvaham ahiṃsā satyamakrodhamārjavaṃ ca samācaret
meaning: महाव्याहृतिभिर्होमः कर्तव्यः स्वयमन्वहम् अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधमार्जवं च समाचरेत्
text: Homa-offerings shall be made by the man himself everyday, with the ‘Mahāvyāhṛtis,’ and he should practise harmlessness, truthfulness, freedom from anger, and mercy.
iast: mahāvyāhṛtibhirhomaḥ kartavyaḥ svayamanvaham ahiṃsā satyamakrodhamārjavaṃ ca samācaret

sanskrit_iast: trirahnastrirniśāyāṃ ca savāsā jalamāviśet strīśūdrapatitāṃścaiva nābhibhāṣeta karhi cit
meaning: त्रिरह्नस्त्रिर्निशायां च सवासा जलमाविशेत् स्त्रीशूद्रपतितांश्चैव नाभिभाषेत कर्हि चित्
text: Thrice in the day and thrice in the night shall he enter water along with his clothes; and he shall never converse with women,Śūdrasand outcasts.
iast: trirahnastrirniśāyāṃ ca savāsā jalamāviśet strīśūdrapatitāṃścaiva nābhibhāṣeta karhi cit

sanskrit_iast: sthānāsanābhyāṃ viharedaśakto 'dhaḥ śayīta vā brahmacārī vratī ca syādgurudevadvijārcakaḥ
meaning: स्थानासनाभ्यां विहरेदशक्तो 'धः शयीत वा ब्रह्मचारी व्रती च स्याद्गुरुदेवद्विजार्चकः
text: He shall pass his time standing and sitting; or if he is unable to do so, he may lie down on the ground ; he shall remain chaste, firm in vows, worshipping his elders, gods andBrāhmaṇas.
iast: sthānāsanābhyāṃ viharedaśakto 'dhaḥ śayīta vā brahmacārī vratī ca syādgurudevadvijārcakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sāvitrīṃ ca japennityaṃ pavitrāṇi ca śaktitaḥ sarveṣveva vrateṣvevaṃ prāyaścittārthamādṛtaḥ
meaning: सावित्रीं च जपेन्नित्यं पवित्राणि च शक्तितः सर्वेष्वेव व्रतेष्वेवं प्रायश्चित्तार्थमादृतः
text: He shall constantly repeat theSāvitrī, as also other purificatory texts, to the best of his ability. This shall be done with due care, in connection with all penances performed for the sake of expiation.
iast: sāvitrīṃ ca japennityaṃ pavitrāṇi ca śaktitaḥ sarveṣveva vrateṣvevaṃ prāyaścittārthamādṛtaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etairdvijātayaḥ śodhyā vratairāviṣkṛtenasaḥ anāviṣkṛtapāpāṃstu mantrairhomaiśca śodhayet
meaning: एतैर्द्विजातयः शोध्या व्रतैराविष्कृतेनसः अनाविष्कृतपापांस्तु मन्त्रैर्होमैश्च शोधयेत्
text: By means of these penances are those twice-born persons to be purified whose sins have become known; those whose sins have not become known, one shall purify by means of sacred texts and Homa-offerings.
iast: etairdvijātayaḥ śodhyā vratairāviṣkṛtenasaḥ anāviṣkṛtapāpāṃstu mantrairhomaiśca śodhayet

sanskrit_iast: khyāpanenānutāpena tapasā 'dhyayanena ca pāpakṛtmucyate pāpāt tathā dānena cāpadi
meaning: ख्यापनेनानुतापेन तपसा 'ध्ययनेन च पापकृत्मुच्यते पापात् तथा दानेन चापदि
text: By confession, by repentance, by austerity and by study is the sinner freed from sin; as also by gifts in cases of difficulty.
iast: khyāpanenānutāpena tapasā 'dhyayanena ca pāpakṛtmucyate pāpāt tathā dānena cāpadi

sanskrit_iast: yathā yathā naro 'dharmaṃ svayaṃ kṛtvā 'nubhāṣate tathā tathā tvacaivāhistenādharmeṇa mucyate
meaning: यथा यथा नरो 'धर्मं स्वयं कृत्वा 'नुभाषते तथा तथा त्वचैवाहिस्तेनाधर्मेण मुच्यते
text: As a man, having committed a misdeed, goes on proclaiming it himself, so does he become freed from that sin, as a snake from its slough.
iast: yathā yathā naro 'dharmaṃ svayaṃ kṛtvā 'nubhāṣate tathā tathā tvacaivāhistenādharmeṇa mucyate

sanskrit_iast: yathā yathā manastasya duṣkṛtaṃ karma garhati tathā tathā śarīraṃ tat tenādharmeṇa mucyate
meaning: यथा यथा मनस्तस्य दुष्कृतं कर्म गर्हति तथा तथा शरीरं तत् तेनाधर्मेण मुच्यते
text: As his mind goes on loathing the evil deed, so is his body freed from that sin.
iast: yathā yathā manastasya duṣkṛtaṃ karma garhati tathā tathā śarīraṃ tat tenādharmeṇa mucyate

sanskrit_iast: kṛtvā pāpaṃ hi saṃtapya tasmāt pāpāt pramucyate naivaṃ kuryāṃ punariti nivṛttyā pūyate tu saḥ
meaning: कृत्वा पापं हि संतप्य तस्मात् पापात् प्रमुच्यते नैवं कुर्यां पुनरिति निवृत्त्या पूयते तु सः
text: After having committed, a sin, if one repents, he becomes freed from that sin; he becomes purified by the renunciation—‘I shall not do so again.’
iast: kṛtvā pāpaṃ hi saṃtapya tasmāt pāpāt pramucyate naivaṃ kuryāṃ punariti nivṛttyā pūyate tu saḥ

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sañcintya manasā pretya karmaphalaudayam manovācmūrtibhirnityaṃ śubhaṃ karma samācaret
meaning: एवं सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा प्रेत्य कर्मफलौदयम् मनोवाच्मूर्तिभिर्नित्यं शुभं कर्म समाचरेत्
text: Having thus considered in his mind the results arising after death from his deeds, he should perform good acts, by his thought, speech and body.
iast: evaṃ sañcintya manasā pretya karmaphalaudayam manovācmūrtibhirnityaṃ śubhaṃ karma samācaret

sanskrit_iast: ajñānādyadi vā jñānāt kṛtvā karma vigarhitam tasmādvimuktimanvicchan dvitīyaṃ na samācaret
meaning: अज्ञानाद्यदि वा ज्ञानात् कृत्वा कर्म विगर्हितम् तस्माद्विमुक्तिमन्विच्छन् द्वितीयं न समाचरेत्
text: Either intentionally or unintentionally, if one has done a reprehensible act, he must not do it a second time, if he seeks absolution from the former.
iast: ajñānādyadi vā jñānāt kṛtvā karma vigarhitam tasmādvimuktimanvicchan dvitīyaṃ na samācaret

sanskrit_iast: yasmin karmaṇyasya kṛte manasaḥ syādalāghavam tasmiṃstāvat tapaḥ kuryādyāvat tuṣṭikaraṃ bhavet
meaning: यस्मिन् कर्मण्यस्य कृते मनसः स्यादलाघवम् तस्मिंस्तावत् तपः कुर्याद्यावत् तुष्टिकरं भवेत्
text: If in regard to any act that has been committed, his mind be uneasy, he shall perform the penance prescribed for it until it brings peace to his mind.
iast: yasmin karmaṇyasya kṛte manasaḥ syādalāghavam tasmiṃstāvat tapaḥ kuryādyāvat tuṣṭikaraṃ bhavet

sanskrit_iast: tapomūlamidaṃ sarvaṃ daivamānuṣakaṃ sukham tapomadhyaṃ budhaiḥ proktaṃ tapo 'ntaṃ vedadarśibhiḥ
meaning: तपोमूलमिदं सर्वं दैवमानुषकं सुखम् तपोमध्यं बुधैः प्रोक्तं तपो 'न्तं वेददर्शिभिः
text: All happiness among gods and men has been declared by the wise ones to whom the Veda was revealed to have austerity for its source, austerity for its middle and austerity for its end.
iast: tapomūlamidaṃ sarvaṃ daivamānuṣakaṃ sukham tapomadhyaṃ budhaiḥ proktaṃ tapo 'ntaṃ vedadarśibhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: brāhmaṇasya tapo jñānaṃ tapaḥ kṣatrasya rakṣaṇam vaiśyasya tu tapo vārtā tapaḥ śūdrasya sevanam
meaning: ब्राह्मणस्य तपो ज्ञानं तपः क्षत्रस्य रक्षणम् वैश्यस्य तु तपो वार्ता तपः शूद्रस्य सेवनम्
text: The ‘austerity’ for theBrāhmaṇais ‘knowledge’; the ‘austerity’ for theKṣatriyais ‘protecting’; the ‘austerity’ for the Vaiśya is ‘agriculture’; and the ‘austertty’ for the Śūdra is ‘service.’
