iast: brāhmaṇasya tapo jñānaṃ tapaḥ kṣatrasya rakṣaṇam vaiśyasya tu tapo vārtā tapaḥ śūdrasya sevanam

sanskrit_iast: ṛṣayaḥ saṃyatātmānaḥ phalamūlānilāśanāḥ tapasaiva prapaśyanti trailokyaṃ sacarācaram
meaning: ऋषयः संयतात्मानः फलमूलानिलाशनाः तपसैव प्रपश्यन्ति त्रैलोक्यं सचराचरम्
text: It is by austerity alone that self-controlled sages, subsisting on fruits, roots and air, survey the three worlds, including all moveable and immoveable beings.
iast: ṛṣayaḥ saṃyatātmānaḥ phalamūlānilāśanāḥ tapasaiva prapaśyanti trailokyaṃ sacarācaram

sanskrit_iast: auṣadhānyagado vidyā daivī ca vividhā sthitiḥ tapasaiva prasidhyanti tapasteṣāṃ hi sādhanam
meaning: औषधान्यगदो विद्या दैवी च विविधा स्थितिः तपसैव प्रसिध्यन्ति तपस्तेषां हि साधनम्
text: Medicines, drugs, science and the various kinds of divine condition are acquired by austerity; as austerity is the means of their accomplishment.
iast: auṣadhānyagado vidyā daivī ca vividhā sthitiḥ tapasaiva prasidhyanti tapasteṣāṃ hi sādhanam

sanskrit_iast: yaddustaraṃ yaddurāpaṃ yaddurgaṃ yacca duṣkaram sarvaṃ tu tapasā sādhyaṃ tapo hi duratikramam sarvaṃ tat tapasā
meaning: यद्दुस्तरं यद्दुरापं यद्दुर्गं यच्च दुष्करम् सर्वं तु तपसा साध्यं तपो हि दुरतिक्रमम् सर्वं तत् तपसा
text: What is hard to traverse, what is hard to attain, what is hard to reach, and what is hard to do,—all this is accomplished by Austerity; as Austerity is irrepressible.
iast: yaddustaraṃ yaddurāpaṃ yaddurgaṃ yacca duṣkaram sarvaṃ tu tapasā sādhyaṃ tapo hi duratikramam sarvaṃ tat tapasā

sanskrit_iast: mahāpātakinaścaiva śeṣāścākāryakāriṇaḥ tapasaiva sutaptena mucyante kilbiṣāt tataḥ
meaning: महापातकिनश्चैव शेषाश्चाकार्यकारिणः तपसैव सुतप्तेन मुच्यन्ते किल्बिषात् ततः
text: Those who have committed heinous offences, as also those who have committed other misdeeds, become freed from their sins by means of austerities well-performed.
iast: mahāpātakinaścaiva śeṣāścākāryakāriṇaḥ tapasaiva sutaptena mucyante kilbiṣāt tataḥ

sanskrit_iast: kītāścāhipataṅgāśca paśavaśca vayāṃsi ca sthāvarāṇi ca bhūtāni divaṃ yānti tapobalāt
meaning: कीताश्चाहिपतङ्गाश्च पशवश्च वयांसि च स्थावराणि च भूतानि दिवं यान्ति तपोबलात्
text: Insects, snakes, moths, animals and birds, as also immovable beings go to heaven by the force of austerities.
iast: kītāścāhipataṅgāśca paśavaśca vayāṃsi ca sthāvarāṇi ca bhūtāni divaṃ yānti tapobalāt

sanskrit_iast: yat kiṃ cidenaḥ kurvanti manovācmūrtibhirjanāḥ manovāckarmabhir tat sarvaṃ nirdahantyāśu tapasaiva tapodhanāḥ
meaning: यत् किं चिदेनः कुर्वन्ति मनोवाच्मूर्तिभिर्जनाः मनोवाच्कर्मभिर् तत् सर्वं निर्दहन्त्याशु तपसैव तपोधनाः
text: Whatever sin people commit by thought, word or deed,—all that they speedily burn away, having Austerity as their sole wealth.
iast: yat kiṃ cidenaḥ kurvanti manovācmūrtibhirjanāḥ manovāckarmabhir tat sarvaṃ nirdahantyāśu tapasaiva tapodhanāḥ

sanskrit_iast: tapasaiva viśuddhasya brāhmaṇasya divokasaḥ ijyāśca pratigṛhṇanti kāmān saṃvardhayanti ca
meaning: तपसैव विशुद्धस्य ब्राह्मणस्य दिवोकसः इज्याश्च प्रतिगृह्णन्ति कामान् संवर्धयन्ति च
text: It is only when theBrāhmaṇahas been purified by Austerities that Heavenly Beings accept their sacrifices and advance their interests.
iast: tapasaiva viśuddhasya brāhmaṇasya divokasaḥ ijyāśca pratigṛhṇanti kāmān saṃvardhayanti ca

sanskrit_iast: prajāpatiridaṃ śāstraṃ tapasaivāsṛjat prabhuḥ tathaiva vedān ṛṣayastapasā pratipedire
meaning: प्रजापतिरिदं शास्त्रं तपसैवासृजत् प्रभुः तथैव वेदान् ऋषयस्तपसा प्रतिपेदिरे
text: It was by means of Austerity that LordPrajāpatigave forth these ordinances; and it was by means of Austerity that the Sages obtained the Vedas.
iast: prajāpatiridaṃ śāstraṃ tapasaivāsṛjat prabhuḥ tathaiva vedān ṛṣayastapasā pratipedire

sanskrit_iast: ityetat tapaso devā mahābhāgyaṃ pracakṣate yadetat tapaso sarvasyāsya prapaśyantastapasaḥ puṇyamuttamam puṇyamudbhavaṃ
meaning: इत्येतत् तपसो देवा महाभाग्यं प्रचक्षते यदेतत् तपसो सर्वस्यास्य प्रपश्यन्तस्तपसः पुण्यमुत्तमम् पुण्यमुद्भवं
text: The gods, discerning that the holy origin of all this lies in austerity, thus proclaim the magnificence of Austerity.
iast: ityetat tapaso devā mahābhāgyaṃ pracakṣate yadetat tapaso sarvasyāsya prapaśyantastapasaḥ puṇyamuttamam puṇyamudbhavaṃ

sanskrit_iast: vedābhyāso 'nvahaṃ śaktyā mahāyajñakriyā kṣamā nāśayantyāśu pāpāni mahāpātakajānyapi
meaning: वेदाभ्यासो 'न्वहं शक्त्या महायज्ञक्रिया क्षमा नाशयन्त्याशु पापानि महापातकजान्यपि
text: The daily recitation of the Veda, to the best of one’s ability, the ‘Great Sacrifices,’ and ‘tolerance,’ quickly destroy all sins,—even those due to heinous offences.
iast: vedābhyāso 'nvahaṃ śaktyā mahāyajñakriyā kṣamā nāśayantyāśu pāpāni mahāpātakajānyapi

sanskrit_iast: yathaidhastejasā vahniḥ prāptaṃ nirdahati kṣaṇāt tathā jñānāgninā pāpaṃ sarvaṃ dahati vedavit
meaning: यथैधस्तेजसा वह्निः प्राप्तं निर्दहति क्षणात् तथा ज्ञानाग्निना पापं सर्वं दहति वेदवित्
text: Just as fire, in a moment, consumes with its heat the fuel placed upon it, so does the man learned in the Veda destroy all sins by the fire of knowledge.
iast: yathaidhastejasā vahniḥ prāptaṃ nirdahati kṣaṇāt tathā jñānāgninā pāpaṃ sarvaṃ dahati vedavit

sanskrit_iast: ityetadenasāmuktaṃ prāyaścittaṃ yathāvidhi ata ūrdhvaṃ rahasyānāṃ prāyaścittaṃ nibodhata
meaning: इत्येतदेनसामुक्तं प्रायश्चित्तं यथाविधि अत ऊर्ध्वं रहस्यानां प्रायश्चित्तं निबोधत
text: The due expiation of sins has thus been described; listen after this to the expiation of secret sins.
iast: ityetadenasāmuktaṃ prāyaścittaṃ yathāvidhi ata ūrdhvaṃ rahasyānāṃ prāyaścittaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: savyāhṛtipraṇavakāḥ prāṇāyāmāstu ṣoḍaśa api bhrūṇahanaṃ māsāt punantyaharahaḥ kṛtāḥ
meaning: सव्याहृतिप्रणवकाः प्राणायामास्तु षोडश अपि भ्रूणहनं मासात् पुनन्त्यहरहः कृताः
text: Sixteen ‘Breath-Suppressions’ with theVyāhṛtisand thePraṇava, performed daily, purify, in a month, even the ‘murderer of the embryo’ (Brāhmaṇa).
iast: savyāhṛtipraṇavakāḥ prāṇāyāmāstu ṣoḍaśa api bhrūṇahanaṃ māsāt punantyaharahaḥ kṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: kautsaṃ japtvā 'pa ityetadvasiṣṭhaṃ ca pratīty ṛcam māhitraṃ śuddhavatyaśca surāpo 'pi viśudhyati
meaning: कौत्सं जप्त्वा 'प इत्येतद्वसिष्ठं च प्रतीत्य् ऋचम् माहित्रं शुद्धवत्यश्च सुरापो 'पि विशुध्यति
text: Even a drinker of wine becomes pure if he recites the ‘Kautsa hymn,’ beginning with the term ‘Ap,’ or the ‘Vāsiṣṭhahymn,’ consisting of the triad of verses beginning with ‘Prati,’ or the ‘Māhitrahymn,’ or the ‘Śuddhavati verses.’
iast: kautsaṃ japtvā 'pa ityetadvasiṣṭhaṃ ca pratīty ṛcam māhitraṃ śuddhavatyaśca surāpo 'pi viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: sakṛtjaptvā 'syavāmīyaṃ śivasaṅkalpameva ca apahṛtya suvarṇaṃ tu kṣaṇādbhavati nirmalaḥ
meaning: सकृत्जप्त्वा 'स्यवामीयं शिवसङ्कल्पमेव च अपहृत्य सुवर्णं तु क्षणाद्भवति निर्मलः
text: Having stolen gold, one instantly becomes free from impurity, by reciting once the ‘Āsyavāmīyahymn’ and the ‘Śivasaṅkalpahymn.’
iast: sakṛtjaptvā 'syavāmīyaṃ śivasaṅkalpameva ca apahṛtya suvarṇaṃ tu kṣaṇādbhavati nirmalaḥ

sanskrit_iast: haviṣpāntīyamabhyasya na tamaṃ ha itīti ca japitvā pauruṣaṃ sūktaṃ mucyate gurutalpagaḥ
meaning: हविष्पान्तीयमभ्यस्य न तमं ह इतीति च जपित्वा पौरुषं सूक्तं मुच्यते गुरुतल्पगः
text: The Violator of the Preceptor’s Bed becomes absolved by repeating the ‘HaviṣpāntīyaHymn,’ (Ṛgveda, 10.88.1), the verse beginning with ‘na tamam ha’ (Ṛgveda, 10.126) and that beginning with ‘iti’ (Ṛgveda, 10.119),—and by reciting the ‘Puruṣasūkta’ (Ṛgveda, 10.90.1)
iast: haviṣpāntīyamabhyasya na tamaṃ ha itīti ca japitvā pauruṣaṃ sūktaṃ mucyate gurutalpagaḥ

sanskrit_iast: enasāṃ sthūlasūkṣmāṇāṃ cikīrṣannapanodanam avaity ṛcaṃ japedabdaṃ yat kiṃ caidamitīti vā
meaning: एनसां स्थूलसूक्ष्माणां चिकीर्षन्नपनोदनम् अवैत्य् ऋचं जपेदब्दं यत् किं चैदमितीति वा
text: एनसां स्थूलसूक्ष्माणां चिकीर्षन्नपनोदनम् ।अवेत्यर्चं जपेदब्दं यत् किं चेदमितीति वा ?? ॥ २५२ ॥
iast: enasāṃ sthūlasūkṣmāṇāṃ cikīrṣannapanodanam avaity ṛcaṃ japedabdaṃ yat kiṃ caidamitīti vā

sanskrit_iast: pratigṛhyāpratigrāhyaṃ bhuktvā cānnaṃ vigarhitam japaṃstaratsamandīyaṃ pūyate mānavastryahāt
meaning: प्रतिगृह्याप्रतिग्राह्यं भुक्त्वा चान्नं विगर्हितम् जपंस्तरत्समन्दीयं पूयते मानवस्त्र्यहात्
text: If a man has accepted a gift that should not be accepted, or has eaten reprehensible food, he becomes pure in three days, by repeating theTaratsamandīya’ verses.
iast: pratigṛhyāpratigrāhyaṃ bhuktvā cānnaṃ vigarhitam japaṃstaratsamandīyaṃ pūyate mānavastryahāt

sanskrit_iast: somāraudraṃ tu bahvenāḥ māsamabhyasya śudhyati samāmabhyasya sravantyāmācaran snānamaryamṇāmiti ca tṛcam
meaning: सोमारौद्रं तु बह्वेनाः मासमभ्यस्य शुध्यति समामभ्यस्य स्रवन्त्यामाचरन् स्नानमर्यम्णामिति च तृचम्
text: One who has committed many sins becomes purified by reciting, for a year, the ‘Somāraudrahymn’ and the three verses beginning with ‘aryamaṇam,’—while taking his bath in a stream.
iast: somāraudraṃ tu bahvenāḥ māsamabhyasya śudhyati samāmabhyasya sravantyāmācaran snānamaryamṇāmiti ca tṛcam

sanskrit_iast: abdārdhamindramityetadenasvī saptakaṃ japet apraśastaṃ tu kṛtvā 'psu māsamāsīta bhaikṣabhuk
meaning: अब्दार्धमिन्द्रमित्येतदेनस्वी सप्तकं जपेत् अप्रशस्तं तु कृत्वा 'प्सु मासमासीत भैक्षभुक्
text: An offender shall recite, for half the year, the seven verses beginning with ‘Indram’; but he who has committed a reprehensible act in water shall subsist, for one month, on alms.
iast: abdārdhamindramityetadenasvī saptakaṃ japet apraśastaṃ tu kṛtvā 'psu māsamāsīta bhaikṣabhuk

sanskrit_iast: mantraiḥ śākalahomīyairabdaṃ hutvā ghṛtaṃ dvijaḥ sugurvapyapahantyeno japtvā vā nama ity ṛcam
meaning: मन्त्रैः शाकलहोमीयैरब्दं हुत्वा घृतं द्विजः सुगुर्वप्यपहन्त्येनो जप्त्वा वा नम इत्य् ऋचम्
text: A twice-born person destroys even the most grievous sin by making Homa-offerings of clarified butter for one year, with the sacred texts related to the ‘Śākala-homa,’ or by repeating the verse beginning with ‘namaḥ.’
iast: mantraiḥ śākalahomīyairabdaṃ hutvā ghṛtaṃ dvijaḥ sugurvapyapahantyeno japtvā vā nama ity ṛcam

sanskrit_iast: mahāpātakasaṃyukto 'nugacchedgāḥ samāhitaḥ abhyasyābdaṃ pāvamānīrbhaikṣāhāro viśudhyati
meaning: महापातकसंयुक्तो 'नुगच्छेद्गाः समाहितः अभ्यस्याब्दं पावमानीर्भैक्षाहारो विशुध्यति
text: He who is polluted by a ‘heinous offence,’ should, with concentrated mind, attend upon cows; and by repeating thePāvamānīverses and subsisting on alms for one year, he becomes pure.
iast: mahāpātakasaṃyukto 'nugacchedgāḥ samāhitaḥ abhyasyābdaṃ pāvamānīrbhaikṣāhāro viśudhyati

sanskrit_iast: araṇye vā trirabhyasya prayato vedasaṃhitām mucyate pātakaiḥ sarvaiḥ parākaiḥ śodhitastribhiḥ
meaning: अरण्ये वा त्रिरभ्यस्य प्रयतो वेदसंहिताम् मुच्यते पातकैः सर्वैः पराकैः शोधितस्त्रिभिः
text: Or, if, purified by the performance of three ‘Parāka’ penances, and self-controlled, he repeats thrice the entire text of the Veda, in a forest, he becomes absolved from all sins.
iast: araṇye vā trirabhyasya prayato vedasaṃhitām mucyate pātakaiḥ sarvaiḥ parākaiḥ śodhitastribhiḥ

sanskrit_iast: tryahaṃ tūpavasedyuktastrirahno 'bhyupayannapaḥ mucyate pātakaiḥ sarvaistrirjapitvā 'ghamarṣaṇam
meaning: त्र्यहं तूपवसेद्युक्तस्त्रिरह्नो 'भ्युपयन्नपः मुच्यते पातकैः सर्वैस्त्रिर्जपित्वा 'घमर्षणम्
text: If a man fasts for, three days, and entering the water thrice a dat repeats the ‘AghamarṣaṇaHymn’ three times, he becomes absolved from all sins.
iast: tryahaṃ tūpavasedyuktastrirahno 'bhyupayannapaḥ mucyate pātakaiḥ sarvaistrirjapitvā 'ghamarṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: yathā 'śvamedhaḥ kraturājsarvapāpāpanodanaḥ tathā 'ghamarṣaṇaṃ sūktaṃ sarvapāpāpanodanam
meaning: यथा 'श्वमेधः क्रतुराज्सर्वपापापनोदनः तथा 'घमर्षणं सूक्तं सर्वपापापनोदनम्
text: Just as theAśvamedha, the king of sacrifices, is destructive of all sins, even so is the ‘AghamarṣaṇaHymn’ also destructive of all sins.
iast: yathā 'śvamedhaḥ kraturājsarvapāpāpanodanaḥ tathā 'ghamarṣaṇaṃ sūktaṃ sarvapāpāpanodanam

sanskrit_iast: hatvā lokānapīmāṃstrīnaśnannapi yatastataḥ ṛgvedaṃ dhārayan vipro nainaḥ prāpnoti kiṃ cana
meaning: हत्वा लोकानपीमांस्त्रीनश्नन्नपि यतस्ततः ऋग्वेदं धारयन् विप्रो नैनः प्राप्नोति किं चन
text: Even if he kills the three worlds, or if he eats here and there, he does not incur any sin, if he retains theṚgveda(in memory).
iast: hatvā lokānapīmāṃstrīnaśnannapi yatastataḥ ṛgvedaṃ dhārayan vipro nainaḥ prāpnoti kiṃ cana

sanskrit_iast: ṛksaṃhitāṃ trirabhyasya yajuṣāṃ vā samāhitaḥ sāmnāṃ vā sarahasyānāṃ sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate
meaning: ऋक्संहितां त्रिरभ्यस्य यजुषां वा समाहितः साम्नां वा सरहस्यानां सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते
text: One becomes absolved from all sins by reciting, with concentrated mind, three times, the text of theṚkor of theYajuṣ, or of theSāman, along with the esoteric texts.
iast: ṛksaṃhitāṃ trirabhyasya yajuṣāṃ vā samāhitaḥ sāmnāṃ vā sarahasyānāṃ sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate

sanskrit_iast: yathā mahāhradaṃ prāpya kṣiptaṃ loṣṭaṃ vinaśyati tathā duścaritaṃ sarvaṃ vede trivṛti majjati
meaning: यथा महाह्रदं प्राप्य क्षिप्तं लोष्टं विनश्यति तथा दुश्चरितं सर्वं वेदे त्रिवृति मज्जति
text: As a clod of earth, being thrown, becomes dissolved as soon as it gets into the water,—even so does all sin become engulfed in the threefold Veda.
iast: yathā mahāhradaṃ prāpya kṣiptaṃ loṣṭaṃ vinaśyati tathā duścaritaṃ sarvaṃ vede trivṛti majjati

sanskrit_iast: ṛco yajūṃṣi cānyāni sāmāni vividhāni ca eṣa jñeyastrivṛdvedo yo vedainaṃ sa vedavit
meaning: ऋचो यजूंषि चान्यानि सामानि विविधानि च एष ज्ञेयस्त्रिवृद्वेदो यो वेदैनं स वेदवित्
text: TheṚk-verses, the primevalYajuṣ-texts, and the manifoldSāman-songs,—these constitute the ‘Threefold Veda’; he who knows this is ‘learned in the Vedas.’
iast: ṛco yajūṃṣi cānyāni sāmāni vividhāni ca eṣa jñeyastrivṛdvedo yo vedainaṃ sa vedavit

sanskrit_iast: ādyaṃ yat tryakṣaraṃ brahma trayī yasmin pratiṣṭhitā sa guhyo 'nyastrivṛdvedo yastaṃ veda sa vedavit
meaning: आद्यं यत् त्र्यक्षरं ब्रह्म त्रयी यस्मिन् प्रतिष्ठिता स गुह्यो 'न्यस्त्रिवृद्वेदो यस्तं वेद स वेदवित्
iast: ādyaṃ yat tryakṣaraṃ brahma trayī yasmin pratiṣṭhitā sa guhyo 'nyastrivṛdvedo yastaṃ veda sa vedavit

sanskrit_iast: cāturvarṇyasya kṛtsno 'yamukto dharmastvayā 'naghaḥ karmaṇāṃ phalanirvṛttiṃ śaṃsa nastattvataḥ parām
meaning: चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य कृत्स्नो 'यमुक्तो धर्मस्त्वया 'नघः कर्मणां फलनिर्वृत्तिं शंस नस्तत्त्वतः पराम्
text: ‘O sinless one, the whole law for the four castes has been expounded by you; instruct us now in regard to the actual fruition of actions.’
iast: cāturvarṇyasya kṛtsno 'yamukto dharmastvayā 'naghaḥ karmaṇāṃ phalanirvṛttiṃ śaṃsa nastattvataḥ parām

sanskrit_iast: sa tānuvāca dharmātmā maharṣīn mānavo bhṛguḥ asya sarvasya śṛṇuta karmayogasya nirṇayam
meaning: स तानुवाच धर्मात्मा महर्षीन् मानवो भृगुः अस्य सर्वस्य शृणुत कर्मयोगस्य निर्णयम्
text: The righteousBhṛgu, sprung for Manu, said to the Great Sages—‘Listen to the truth regarding the relation of Actions.’
iast: sa tānuvāca dharmātmā maharṣīn mānavo bhṛguḥ asya sarvasya śṛṇuta karmayogasya nirṇayam

sanskrit_iast: śubhāśubhaphalaṃ karma manovācdehasaṃbhavam karmajā gatayo nr̥̄ṇāmuttamādhamamadhyamaḥ
meaning: शुभाशुभफलं कर्म मनोवाच्देहसंभवम् कर्मजा गतयो न्र्̥̄णामुत्तमाधममध्यमः
text: Actions proceeding from mind, speech and body are conducive to good and bad results; and the conditions of men, due to actions, are high, low and middling.
iast: śubhāśubhaphalaṃ karma manovācdehasaṃbhavam karmajā gatayo nr̥̄ṇāmuttamādhamamadhyamaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tasyaiha trividhasyāpi tryadhiṣṭhānasya dehinaḥ daśalakṣaṇayuktasya mano vidyāt pravartakam
meaning: तस्यैह त्रिविधस्यापि त्र्यधिष्ठानस्य देहिनः दशलक्षणयुक्तस्य मनो विद्यात् प्रवर्तकम्
text: Know the mind to be the instigator of all this that is connected with the body, and which is of three kinds, has three substrata and is endowed with ten distinct characteristics.
iast: tasyaiha trividhasyāpi tryadhiṣṭhānasya dehinaḥ daśalakṣaṇayuktasya mano vidyāt pravartakam

sanskrit_iast: paradravyeṣvabhidhyānaṃ manasā 'niṣṭacintanam vitathābhiniveśaśca trividhaṃ karma mānasam
meaning: परद्रव्येष्वभिध्यानं मनसा 'निष्टचिन्तनम् वितथाभिनिवेशश्च त्रिविधं कर्म मानसम्
text: Coveting the wealth or others, scheming in one’s mind about what is undesirable, adhering to a wrong notion,—these are the three forms of ‘mental action.’
iast: paradravyeṣvabhidhyānaṃ manasā 'niṣṭacintanam vitathābhiniveśaśca trividhaṃ karma mānasam

sanskrit_iast: pāruṣyamanṛtaṃ caiva paiśunyaṃ cāpi sarvaśaḥ asaṃbaddhapralāpaśca vācmayaṃ syāccaturvidham
meaning: पारुष्यमनृतं चैव पैशुन्यं चापि सर्वशः असंबद्धप्रलापश्च वाच्मयं स्याच्चतुर्विधम्
text: Abusing, lying, calumniating all men, and idle prattling,—are the pour kinds of ‘verbal action.’
iast: pāruṣyamanṛtaṃ caiva paiśunyaṃ cāpi sarvaśaḥ asaṃbaddhapralāpaśca vācmayaṃ syāccaturvidham

sanskrit_iast: adattānāmupādānaṃ hiṃsā caivāvidhānataḥ paradāraupasevā ca śārīraṃ trividhaṃ smṛtam
meaning: अदत्तानामुपादानं हिंसा चैवाविधानतः परदारौपसेवा च शारीरं त्रिविधं स्मृतम्
text: Taking what has not been given, unsanctioned killing, and intercourse with the wives of others—these have been declared to be three kinds of ‘bodily action.’
iast: adattānāmupādānaṃ hiṃsā caivāvidhānataḥ paradāraupasevā ca śārīraṃ trividhaṃ smṛtam

sanskrit_iast: mānasaṃ manasaivāyamupabhuṅkte śubhāśubham vācā vācā kṛtaṃ karma kāyenaiva ca kāyikam
meaning: मानसं मनसैवायमुपभुङ्क्ते शुभाशुभम् वाचा वाचा कृतं कर्म कायेनैव च कायिकम्
text: The good and the evil resulting from ‘mental acts,’ one experiences through the Mind alone; those of ‘verbal acts,’ through speech; and those of ‘bodily acts,’ through the body.
iast: mānasaṃ manasaivāyamupabhuṅkte śubhāśubham vācā vācā kṛtaṃ karma kāyenaiva ca kāyikam

sanskrit_iast: śarīrajaiḥ karmadoṣairyāti sthāvaratāṃ naraḥ vācikaiḥ pakṣimṛgatāṃ mānasairantyajātitām
meaning: शरीरजैः कर्मदोषैर्याति स्थावरतां नरः वाचिकैः पक्षिमृगतां मानसैरन्त्यजातिताम्
text: Through sinful acts due to the Body, man becomes inanimate; through those of Speech, a bird or a beast; and through those of Mind, he is born in the lowest caste.
iast: śarīrajaiḥ karmadoṣairyāti sthāvaratāṃ naraḥ vācikaiḥ pakṣimṛgatāṃ mānasairantyajātitām

sanskrit_iast: vāgdaṇḍo 'tha manodaṇḍaḥ kāyadaṇḍastathaiva ca yasyaite nihitā buddhau tridaṇḍīiti sa ucyate
meaning: वाग्दण्डो 'थ मनोदण्डः कायदण्डस्तथैव च यस्यैते निहिता बुद्धौ त्रिदण्डीइति स उच्यते
text: Control over Speech, control over the Mind and control over the Body,—the man in whose heart these are firmly fixed is called ‘the man of triple control.’
iast: vāgdaṇḍo 'tha manodaṇḍaḥ kāyadaṇḍastathaiva ca yasyaite nihitā buddhau tridaṇḍīiti sa ucyate

sanskrit_iast: tridaṇḍametannikṣipya sarvabhūteṣu mānavaḥ kāmakrodhau tu saṃyamya tataḥ siddhiṃ niyacchati kāmakrodhau susaṃyamya tataḥ siddhiṃ nigacchati
meaning: त्रिदण्डमेतन्निक्षिप्य सर्वभूतेषु मानवः कामक्रोधौ तु संयम्य ततः सिद्धिं नियच्छति कामक्रोधौ सुसंयम्य ततः सिद्धिं निगच्छति
text: The man who keeps this ‘triple control’ in regard to all creatures, and rightly subdues desire and anger, thereby attains success.
iast: tridaṇḍametannikṣipya sarvabhūteṣu mānavaḥ kāmakrodhau tu saṃyamya tataḥ siddhiṃ niyacchati kāmakrodhau susaṃyamya tataḥ siddhiṃ nigacchati

sanskrit_iast: yo 'syātmanaḥ kārayitā taṃ kṣetrajñaṃ pracakṣate yaḥ karoti tu karmāṇi sa bhūtātmaucyate budhaiḥ
meaning: यो 'स्यात्मनः कारयिता तं क्षेत्रज्ञं प्रचक्षते यः करोति तु कर्माणि स भूतात्मौच्यते बुधैः
text: He who is the impeller of this body, him they call the ‘Kṣetrajña,’ ‘the Conscious Being’; while he who does the acts is called by the learned, the ‘Bhūtātman,’ ‘the Material Entity.’
iast: yo 'syātmanaḥ kārayitā taṃ kṣetrajñaṃ pracakṣate yaḥ karoti tu karmāṇi sa bhūtātmaucyate budhaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: jīvasaṃjño 'ntarātmā 'nyaḥ sahajaḥ sarvadehinām yena vedayate sarvaṃ sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ ca janmasu
meaning: जीवसंज्ञो 'न्तरात्मा 'न्यः सहजः सर्वदेहिनाम् येन वेदयते सर्वं सुखं दुःखं च जन्मसु
text: An inner ‘self,’ called ‘Jīva,’ ‘Soul,’ is different,—generated along with all embodied beings, through which one experiences pleasure and fain during the several births.
iast: jīvasaṃjño 'ntarātmā 'nyaḥ sahajaḥ sarvadehinām yena vedayate sarvaṃ sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ ca janmasu

sanskrit_iast: tāvubhau bhūtasaṃpṛktau mahān kṣetrajña eva ca uccāvaceṣu bhūteṣu sthitaṃ taṃ vyāpya tiṣṭhataḥ
meaning: तावुभौ भूतसंपृक्तौ महान् क्षेत्रज्ञ एव च उच्चावचेषु भूतेषु स्थितं तं व्याप्य तिष्ठतः
text: Both of these, the ‘Great Principle’ and the ‘Conscious Being,’ united with the material substances, subsist in Him who resides in all things, pervading them all.
iast: tāvubhau bhūtasaṃpṛktau mahān kṣetrajña eva ca uccāvaceṣu bhūteṣu sthitaṃ taṃ vyāpya tiṣṭhataḥ

sanskrit_iast: asaṅkhyā mūrtayastasya niṣpatanti śarīrataḥ uccāvacāni bhūtāni satataṃ ceṣṭayanti yāḥ
meaning: असङ्ख्या मूर्तयस्तस्य निष्पतन्ति शरीरतः उच्चावचानि भूतानि सततं चेष्टयन्ति याः
text: From his body emanate innumerable forms, which constantly energise all kinds of beings.
iast: asaṅkhyā mūrtayastasya niṣpatanti śarīrataḥ uccāvacāni bhūtāni satataṃ ceṣṭayanti yāḥ

sanskrit_iast: pañcabhya eva mātrābhyaḥ pretya duṣkṛtināṃ nṛṇām pañcabhya eva bhūtebhyaḥ śarīraṃ yātanārthīyamanyadutpadyate dhruvam
meaning: पञ्चभ्य एव मात्राभ्यः प्रेत्य दुष्कृतिनां नृणाम् पञ्चभ्य एव भूतेभ्यः शरीरं यातनार्थीयमन्यदुत्पद्यते ध्रुवम्
text: In the case of misbehaved persons, there is produced out of five constituents, another strong body, for the suffering of torments, after death.
iast: pañcabhya eva mātrābhyaḥ pretya duṣkṛtināṃ nṛṇām pañcabhya eva bhūtebhyaḥ śarīraṃ yātanārthīyamanyadutpadyate dhruvam

sanskrit_iast: tenānubhūya tā yāmīḥ śarīreṇaiha yātanāḥ tāsveva bhūtamātrāsu pralīyante vibhāgaśaḥ
meaning: तेनानुभूय ता यामीः शरीरेणैह यातनाः तास्वेव भूतमात्रासु प्रलीयन्ते विभागशः
text: After they have suffered, through this body, the torments inflicted by yama, those constituents become dissolved into each of those same material elements.
iast: tenānubhūya tā yāmīḥ śarīreṇaiha yātanāḥ tāsveva bhūtamātrāsu pralīyante vibhāgaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: so 'nubhūyāsukhaudarkān doṣān viṣayasaṅgajān vyapetakalmaṣo 'bhyeti tāvevaubhau mahaujasau
meaning: सो 'नुभूयासुखौदर्कान् दोषान् विषयसङ्गजान् व्यपेतकल्मषो 'भ्येति तावेवौभौ महौजसौ
text: Having suffered the evils produced by attachment to sensual objects, and conducive to misery, he, having his sins destroyed, approaches those same two glorious ones.
iast: so 'nubhūyāsukhaudarkān doṣān viṣayasaṅgajān vyapetakalmaṣo 'bhyeti tāvevaubhau mahaujasau

sanskrit_iast: tau dharmaṃ paśyatastasya pāpaṃ cātandritau saha yābhyāṃ prāpnoti saṃpṛktaḥ pretyaiha ca sukhāsukham
meaning: तौ धर्मं पश्यतस्तस्य पापं चातन्द्रितौ सह याभ्यां प्राप्नोति संपृक्तः प्रेत्यैह च सुखासुखम्
text: Those two together carefully look into his merit and demerit, invested with which both, he obtains happiness or unhappiness, here and after death.
iast: tau dharmaṃ paśyatastasya pāpaṃ cātandritau saha yābhyāṃ prāpnoti saṃpṛktaḥ pretyaiha ca sukhāsukham

sanskrit_iast: yadyācarati dharmaṃ sa prāyaśo 'dharmamalpaśaḥ yathācarati taireva cāvṛto bhūtaiḥ svarge sukhamupāśnute
meaning: यद्याचरति धर्मं स प्रायशो 'धर्ममल्पशः यथाचरति तैरेव चावृतो भूतैः स्वर्गे सुखमुपाश्नुते
text: If he practises virtue for the most part, and vice only in a small degree, then, invested with those same material substances, he obtains happiness in heaven.
iast: yadyācarati dharmaṃ sa prāyaśo 'dharmamalpaśaḥ yathācarati taireva cāvṛto bhūtaiḥ svarge sukhamupāśnute

sanskrit_iast: yadi tu prāyaśo 'dharmaṃ sevate dharmamalpaśaḥ tairbhūtaiḥ sa parityakto yāmīḥ prāpnoti yātanāḥ
meaning: यदि तु प्रायशो 'धर्मं सेवते धर्ममल्पशः तैर्भूतैः स परित्यक्तो यामीः प्राप्नोति यातनाः
text: If on the other hand he practises vice for the most part, and virtue only in a small degree,—then, becoming deserted by those substances, he suffers the torments inflicted by Yama.
iast: yadi tu prāyaśo 'dharmaṃ sevate dharmamalpaśaḥ tairbhūtaiḥ sa parityakto yāmīḥ prāpnoti yātanāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāmīstā yātanāḥ prāpya sa jīvo vītakalmaṣaḥ tānyeva pañca bhūtāni punarapyeti bhāgaśaḥ
meaning: यामीस्ता यातनाः प्राप्य स जीवो वीतकल्मषः तान्येव पञ्च भूतानि पुनरप्येति भागशः
text: That personality, having suffered those torments inflicted by Yama, and thereby freed from sin, again enters into those very material substances, each in due proportion.
iast: yāmīstā yātanāḥ prāpya sa jīvo vītakalmaṣaḥ tānyeva pañca bhūtāni punarapyeti bhāgaśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: etā dṛṣṭvā 'sya jīvasya gatīḥ svenaiva cetasā dharmato 'dharmataścaiva dharme dadhyāt sadā manaḥ
meaning: एता दृष्ट्वा 'स्य जीवस्य गतीः स्वेनैव चेतसा धर्मतो 'धर्मतश्चैव धर्मे दध्यात् सदा मनः
text: Having recognised, in his own mind, the conditions of the Personality, due to Virtue and Vice, one should fix his heart upon Virtue.
iast: etā dṛṣṭvā 'sya jīvasya gatīḥ svenaiva cetasā dharmato 'dharmataścaiva dharme dadhyāt sadā manaḥ

sanskrit_iast: sattvaṃ rajastamascaiva trīn vidyādātmano guṇān yairvyāpyaimān sthito bhāvān mahān sarvānaśeṣataḥ
meaning: सत्त्वं रजस्तमस्चैव त्रीन् विद्यादात्मनो गुणान् यैर्व्याप्यैमान् स्थितो भावान् महान् सर्वानशेषतः
text: Know Sattva, Rajas and Tamas to be the three qualities of the Self, by means of which the Great One completely pervades all these beings.
iast: sattvaṃ rajastamascaiva trīn vidyādātmano guṇān yairvyāpyaimān sthito bhāvān mahān sarvānaśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: yo yadaiṣāṃ guṇo dehe sākalyenātiricyate sa tadā tadguṇaprāyaṃ taṃ karoti śarīriṇam
meaning: यो यदैषां गुणो देहे साकल्येनातिरिच्यते स तदा तद्गुणप्रायं तं करोति शरीरिणम्
text: Whichsoever of these qualities wholly predominates in a body, it makes the owner of that body abound in that quality.
iast: yo yadaiṣāṃ guṇo dehe sākalyenātiricyate sa tadā tadguṇaprāyaṃ taṃ karoti śarīriṇam

sanskrit_iast: sattvaṃ jñānaṃ tamo 'jñānaṃ rāgadveṣau rajaḥ smṛtam etadvyāptimadeteṣāṃ sarvabhūtāśritaṃ vapuḥ
meaning: सत्त्वं ज्ञानं तमो 'ज्ञानं रागद्वेषौ रजः स्मृतम् एतद्व्याप्तिमदेतेषां सर्वभूताश्रितं वपुः
text: ‘Sattva’ has been declared to be Knowledge, ‘Tamas,’ to be Ignorance, and ‘Rajas,’ to be Love and Hate;—such is the nature of these, all-pervading and interpenetrating all beings.
iast: sattvaṃ jñānaṃ tamo 'jñānaṃ rāgadveṣau rajaḥ smṛtam etadvyāptimadeteṣāṃ sarvabhūtāśritaṃ vapuḥ

sanskrit_iast: tatra yat prītisaṃyuktaṃ kiṃ cidātmani lakṣayet praśāntamiva śuddhābhaṃ sattvaṃ tadupadhārayet
meaning: तत्र यत् प्रीतिसंयुक्तं किं चिदात्मनि लक्षयेत् प्रशान्तमिव शुद्धाभं सत्त्वं तदुपधारयेत्
text: Whenever one perceives in himself something full of bliss, calm and pure,—he should know it to be ‘Sattva.’
iast: tatra yat prītisaṃyuktaṃ kiṃ cidātmani lakṣayet praśāntamiva śuddhābhaṃ sattvaṃ tadupadhārayet

sanskrit_iast: yat tu duḥkhasamāyuktamaprītikaramātmanaḥ tadrajo pratīpaṃ vidyāt satataṃ hāri dehinām artṛ
meaning: यत् तु दुःखसमायुक्तमप्रीतिकरमात्मनः तद्रजो प्रतीपं विद्यात् सततं हारि देहिनाम् अर्तृ
text: What is mixed with pain and brings unhappiness to the Soul,—know that to be ‘Rajas,’ imperceptible and constantly attracting embodied beings.
iast: yat tu duḥkhasamāyuktamaprītikaramātmanaḥ tadrajo pratīpaṃ vidyāt satataṃ hāri dehinām artṛ

sanskrit_iast: yat tu syān mohasaṃyuktamavyaktaṃ viṣayātmakam apratarkyamavijñeyaṃ tamastadupadhārayet
meaning: यत् तु स्यान् मोहसंयुक्तमव्यक्तं विषयात्मकम् अप्रतर्क्यमविज्ञेयं तमस्तदुपधारयेत्
text: What is mixed with stupefaction, undiscernible, of the nature of sensual objects, incapable of being reasoned about and uncognisable,—one should recognise as ‘Tamas.’
iast: yat tu syān mohasaṃyuktamavyaktaṃ viṣayātmakam apratarkyamavijñeyaṃ tamastadupadhārayet

sanskrit_iast: trayāṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ guṇānāṃ yaḥ phalaudayaḥ agryo madhyo jaghanyaśca taṃ pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ
meaning: त्रयाणामपि चैतेषां गुणानां यः फलौदयः अग्र्यो मध्यो जघन्यश्च तं प्रवक्ष्याम्यशेषतः
text: I am now going to fully describe the results,—good, middling and bad—proceeding from these three qualities.
iast: trayāṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ guṇānāṃ yaḥ phalaudayaḥ agryo madhyo jaghanyaśca taṃ pravakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: vedābhyāsastapo jñānaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ dharmakriyā 'tmacintā ca sāttvikaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam
meaning: वेदाभ्यासस्तपो ज्ञानं शौचमिन्द्रियनिग्रहः धर्मक्रिया 'त्मचिन्ता च सात्त्विकं गुणलक्षणम्
text: Vedic study, austerity, knowledge, purity, control over the organs, practice of virtue and meditation on the Soul,—are the characteristics of the quality of ‘Sattva.’
iast: vedābhyāsastapo jñānaṃ śaucamindriyanigrahaḥ dharmakriyā 'tmacintā ca sāttvikaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: ārambharucitā 'dhairyamasatkāryaparigrahaḥ viṣayaupasevā cājasraṃ rājasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam
meaning: आरम्भरुचिता 'धैर्यमसत्कार्यपरिग्रहः विषयौपसेवा चाजस्रं राजसं गुणलक्षणम्
text: Proneness to undertake work, impatience, commission of improper acts, constant addiction to sensual objects are the characteristics of the quality of ‘Rajas.’
iast: ārambharucitā 'dhairyamasatkāryaparigrahaḥ viṣayaupasevā cājasraṃ rājasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: lobhaḥ svapno 'dhṛtiḥ krauryaṃ nāstikyaṃ bhinnavṛttitā yāciṣṇutā pramādaśca tāmasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam
meaning: लोभः स्वप्नो 'धृतिः क्रौर्यं नास्तिक्यं भिन्नवृत्तिता याचिष्णुता प्रमादश्च तामसं गुणलक्षणम्
text: Avarice, drowsiness, irresolution, cruelty, disbelief, bad character, habit of begging, and inattentiveness are the characteristics of the quality of ‘Tamas.’
iast: lobhaḥ svapno 'dhṛtiḥ krauryaṃ nāstikyaṃ bhinnavṛttitā yāciṣṇutā pramādaśca tāmasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: trayāṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ guṇānāṃ triṣu tiṣṭhatām idaṃ sāmāsikaṃ jñeyaṃ kramaśo guṇalakṣaṇam
meaning: त्रयाणामपि चैतेषां गुणानां त्रिषु तिष्ठताम् इदं सामासिकं ज्ञेयं क्रमशो गुणलक्षणम्
text: This should be understood to be in brief the characteristic of these three qualities in order, as they appear at the three times.
iast: trayāṇāmapi caiteṣāṃ guṇānāṃ triṣu tiṣṭhatām idaṃ sāmāsikaṃ jñeyaṃ kramaśo guṇalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: yat karma kṛtvā kurvaṃśca kariṣyaṃścaiva lajjati taj jñeyaṃ viduṣā sarvaṃ tāmasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam
meaning: यत् कर्म कृत्वा कुर्वंश्च करिष्यंश्चैव लज्जति तज् ज्ञेयं विदुषा सर्वं तामसं गुणलक्षणम्
text: When, having done, or doing, or going to do a certain act, a man happen to feel ashamed,—every such act should be understood by the learned to be characterised by the quality of ‘Tamas.’
iast: yat karma kṛtvā kurvaṃśca kariṣyaṃścaiva lajjati taj jñeyaṃ viduṣā sarvaṃ tāmasaṃ guṇalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: yenāsmin karmanā loke khyātimicchati puṣkalām na ca śocatyasaṃpattau tadvijñeyaṃ tu rājasam
meaning: येनास्मिन् कर्मना लोके ख्यातिमिच्छति पुष्कलाम् न च शोचत्यसंपत्तौ तद्विज्ञेयं तु राजसम्
text: When, by a certain act, the man desires great fame in this world, and does not mind failure—this should be understood to partake of the quality of ‘Rajas.’
iast: yenāsmin karmanā loke khyātimicchati puṣkalām na ca śocatyasaṃpattau tadvijñeyaṃ tu rājasam

sanskrit_iast: yat sarveṇaicchati jñātuṃ yanna lajjati cācaran yena tuṣyati cātmā 'sya tat sattvaguṇalakṣaṇam
meaning: यत् सर्वेणैच्छति ज्ञातुं यन्न लज्जति चाचरन् येन तुष्यति चात्मा 'स्य तत् सत्त्वगुणलक्षणम्
text: When, however, the act is one which he wishes to understand in all its details, by doing which he does not feel ashamed, and by which his heart feels satisfied,—it is characterised by the quality of ‘Sattva.’
iast: yat sarveṇaicchati jñātuṃ yanna lajjati cācaran yena tuṣyati cātmā 'sya tat sattvaguṇalakṣaṇam

sanskrit_iast: tamaso lakṣaṇaṃ kāmo rajasastvartha ucyate sattvasya lakṣaṇaṃ dharmaḥ śraiṣṭhyameṣāṃ yathauttaram
meaning: तमसो लक्षणं कामो रजसस्त्वर्थ उच्यते सत्त्वस्य लक्षणं धर्मः श्रैष्ठ्यमेषां यथौत्तरम्
text: Pleasure is the distinguishing feature of ‘Tamas,’ ‘Wealth’ is described to be that of ‘Rajas,’ and ‘Spiritual Merit’ is the distinguishing feature of ‘Sattva,’—each succeeding one of these being superior to the preceding.
iast: tamaso lakṣaṇaṃ kāmo rajasastvartha ucyate sattvasya lakṣaṇaṃ dharmaḥ śraiṣṭhyameṣāṃ yathauttaram

sanskrit_iast: yena yastu guṇenaiṣāṃ saṃsarān pratipadyate yena yāṃstu tān samāsena vakṣyāmi sarvasyāsya yathākramam
meaning: येन यस्तु गुणेनैषां संसरान् प्रतिपद्यते येन यांस्तु तान् समासेन वक्ष्यामि सर्वस्यास्य यथाक्रमम्
text: I am now going to describe, briefly, in due order, those migratory states into which one falls through each quality from among these.
iast: yena yastu guṇenaiṣāṃ saṃsarān pratipadyate yena yāṃstu tān samāsena vakṣyāmi sarvasyāsya yathākramam

sanskrit_iast: devatvaṃ sāttvikā yānti manuṣyatvaṃ ca rājasāḥ tiryaktvaṃ tāmasā nityamityeṣā trividhā gatiḥ
meaning: देवत्वं सात्त्विका यान्ति मनुष्यत्वं च राजसाः तिर्यक्त्वं तामसा नित्यमित्येषा त्रिविधा गतिः
text: Those partaking of ‘Sattva’ reach the state of the gods, those endowed with ‘Rajas,’ the state of men, and those characterised by ‘Tamas,’ the state of beasts; such is the threefold migratory state.
iast: devatvaṃ sāttvikā yānti manuṣyatvaṃ ca rājasāḥ tiryaktvaṃ tāmasā nityamityeṣā trividhā gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: trividhā trividhaiṣā tu vijñeyā gauṇikī gatiḥ adhamā madhyamāgryā ca karmavidyāviśeṣataḥ
meaning: त्रिविधा त्रिविधैषा तु विज्ञेया गौणिकी गतिः अधमा मध्यमाग्र्या च कर्मविद्याविशेषतः
text: This threefold Migratory State due to the qualities should be understood to be again of three kinds each—high, low and middling, in accordance with the peculiar character of the act and knowledge of each man.
iast: trividhā trividhaiṣā tu vijñeyā gauṇikī gatiḥ adhamā madhyamāgryā ca karmavidyāviśeṣataḥ

sanskrit_iast: sthāvarāḥ kṛmikīṭāśca matsyāḥ sarpāḥ sakacchapāḥ paśavaśca mṛgāścaiva jaghanyā tāmasī gatiḥ
meaning: स्थावराः कृमिकीटाश्च मत्स्याः सर्पाः सकच्छपाः पशवश्च मृगाश्चैव जघन्या तामसी गतिः
text: Inanimate beings, worms, insects, fishes, snakes, tortoise, cattle and wild animals,—represent the lowest state due to the quality of ‘Tamas.’
iast: sthāvarāḥ kṛmikīṭāśca matsyāḥ sarpāḥ sakacchapāḥ paśavaśca mṛgāścaiva jaghanyā tāmasī gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: hastinaśca turaṅgāśca śūdrā mlecchāśca garhitāḥ siṃhā vyāghrā varāhāśca madhyamā tāmasī gatiḥ
meaning: हस्तिनश्च तुरङ्गाश्च शूद्रा म्लेच्छाश्च गर्हिताः सिंहा व्याघ्रा वराहाश्च मध्यमा तामसी गतिः
text: Inanimate beings, worms, insects, fishes, snakes, tortoise, cattle and wild animals,—represent the lowest state due to the quality of ‘Tamas.’
iast: hastinaśca turaṅgāśca śūdrā mlecchāśca garhitāḥ siṃhā vyāghrā varāhāśca madhyamā tāmasī gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: cāraṇāśca suparṇāśca puruṣāścaiva dāmbhikāḥ rakṣāṃsi ca piśācāśca tāmasīṣūttamā gatiḥ
meaning: चारणाश्च सुपर्णाश्च पुरुषाश्चैव दाम्भिकाः रक्षांसि च पिशाचाश्च तामसीषूत्तमा गतिः
text: Inanimate beings, worms, insects, fishes, snakes, tortoise, cattle and wild animals,—represent the lowest state due to the quality of ‘Tamas.’
iast: cāraṇāśca suparṇāśca puruṣāścaiva dāmbhikāḥ rakṣāṃsi ca piśācāśca tāmasīṣūttamā gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: jhallā mallā naṭāścaiva puruṣāḥ śastravṛttayaḥ dyūtapānaprasaktāśca jaghanyā rājasī gatiḥ
meaning: झल्ला मल्ला नटाश्चैव पुरुषाः शस्त्रवृत्तयः द्यूतपानप्रसक्ताश्च जघन्या राजसी गतिः
text: Stick-fencers, wrestlers, actors, men subsisting by the use of weapons, those addicted to gambling and drinking,—represent the lowest state among those partaking of the quality of ‘Rajas.’
iast: jhallā mallā naṭāścaiva puruṣāḥ śastravṛttayaḥ dyūtapānaprasaktāśca jaghanyā rājasī gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: rājānaḥ kṣatriyāścaiva rājñāṃ caiva purohitāḥ vādayuddhapradhānāśca madhyamā rājasī gatiḥ
meaning: राजानः क्षत्रियाश्चैव राज्ञां चैव पुरोहिताः वादयुद्धप्रधानाश्च मध्यमा राजसी गतिः
text: Kings,Kṣatriyas, priests of kings, and leading wranglers and warriors represent the middling state among those partaking of ‘Rajas.’
iast: rājānaḥ kṣatriyāścaiva rājñāṃ caiva purohitāḥ vādayuddhapradhānāśca madhyamā rājasī gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: gandharvā guhyakā yakṣā vibudhānucarāśca ye tathaivāpsarasaḥ sarvā rājasīṣūttamā gatiḥ
meaning: गन्धर्वा गुह्यका यक्षा विबुधानुचराश्च ये तथैवाप्सरसः सर्वा राजसीषूत्तमा गतिः
text: Gandharvas, Guhyakas, ‘Yakṣas,’ the attendants of the gods, and all the Apsaras, represent the high state among those partaking of ‘Rajas.’
iast: gandharvā guhyakā yakṣā vibudhānucarāśca ye tathaivāpsarasaḥ sarvā rājasīṣūttamā gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: tāpasā yatayo viprā ye ca vaimānikā gaṇāḥ nakṣatrāṇi ca daityāśca prathamā sāttvikī gatiḥ
meaning: तापसा यतयो विप्रा ये च वैमानिका गणाः नक्षत्राणि च दैत्याश्च प्रथमा सात्त्विकी गतिः
text: Ascetics and hermits,Brāhmaṇas, celestial beings, lunar asterisms, and Daityas represent the first state partaking of ‘Sattva.’
iast: tāpasā yatayo viprā ye ca vaimānikā gaṇāḥ nakṣatrāṇi ca daityāśca prathamā sāttvikī gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yajvāna ṛṣayo devā vedā jyotīṃṣi vatsarāḥ pitaraścaiva sādhyāśca dvitīyā sāttvikī gatiḥ
meaning: यज्वान ऋषयो देवा वेदा ज्योतींषि वत्सराः पितरश्चैव साध्याश्च द्वितीया सात्त्विकी गतिः
text: Sacrificers, sages, gods, vedas, luminaries, years,PitṛsandSādhyasrepresent the second state partaking of ‘Sattva.’
iast: yajvāna ṛṣayo devā vedā jyotīṃṣi vatsarāḥ pitaraścaiva sādhyāśca dvitīyā sāttvikī gatiḥ

sanskrit_iast: brahmā viśvasṛjo dharmo mahānavyaktameva ca uttamāṃ sāttvikīmetāṃ gatimāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ
meaning: ब्रह्मा विश्वसृजो धर्मो महानव्यक्तमेव च उत्तमां सात्त्विकीमेतां गतिमाहुर्मनीषिणः
text: Brahmā, creators of the universe, Dharma, the Great One, Unmanifest,—these the wise ones describe as representing the best state partaking of ‘Sattva.’
iast: brahmā viśvasṛjo dharmo mahānavyaktameva ca uttamāṃ sāttvikīmetāṃ gatimāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: eṣa sarvaḥ samuddiṣṭastriprakārasya karmaṇaḥ triḥprakārasya trividhastrividhaḥ kṛtsnaḥ saṃsāraḥ sārvabhautikaḥ
meaning: एष सर्वः समुद्दिष्टस्त्रिप्रकारस्य कर्मणः त्रिःप्रकारस्य त्रिविधस्त्रिविधः कृत्स्नः संसारः सार्वभौतिकः
text: Thus has been described the entire thrice threefold migratory process pertaining to all beings, arising out of the three kinds of action.
iast: eṣa sarvaḥ samuddiṣṭastriprakārasya karmaṇaḥ triḥprakārasya trividhastrividhaḥ kṛtsnaḥ saṃsāraḥ sārvabhautikaḥ

sanskrit_iast: indriyāṇāṃ prasaṅgena dharmasyāsevanena ca pāpān saṃyānti saṃsārānavidvāṃso narādhamāḥ
meaning: इन्द्रियाणां प्रसङ्गेन धर्मस्यासेवनेन च पापान् संयान्ति संसारानविद्वांसो नराधमाः
text: Foolish men of the lowest class go through the vilest migratory states, in consequence of being addicted to the senses and by not attending to duties.
iast: indriyāṇāṃ prasaṅgena dharmasyāsevanena ca pāpān saṃyānti saṃsārānavidvāṃso narādhamāḥ

sanskrit_iast: yāṃ yāṃ yoniṃ tu jīvo 'yaṃ yena yenaiha karmaṇā kramaśo yāti loke 'smiṃstat tat sarvaṃ nibodhata
meaning: यां यां योनिं तु जीवो 'यं येन येनैह कर्मणा क्रमशो याति लोके 'स्मिंस्तत् तत् सर्वं निबोधत
text: Into what wombs the soul enters,—and in consequence of what acts,—listen to that, in due order.
iast: yāṃ yāṃ yoniṃ tu jīvo 'yaṃ yena yenaiha karmaṇā kramaśo yāti loke 'smiṃstat tat sarvaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: bahūn varṣagaṇān ghorānnarakān prāpya tatkṣayāt saṃsārān pratipadyante mahāpātakinastvimān
meaning: बहून् वर्षगणान् घोरान्नरकान् प्राप्य तत्क्षयात् संसारान् प्रतिपद्यन्ते महापातकिनस्त्विमान्
text: Persons who have committed the heinous offences, having passed, during several years, through dreadful hells, reach, after the expiation thereof, the following migratory states.
iast: bahūn varṣagaṇān ghorānnarakān prāpya tatkṣayāt saṃsārān pratipadyante mahāpātakinastvimān

sanskrit_iast: śvasūkarakharauṣṭrāṇāṃ go 'jāvimṛgapakṣiṇām caṇḍālapukkasānāṃ ca brahmahā yoniṃ ṛcchati
meaning: श्वसूकरखरौष्ट्राणां गो 'जाविमृगपक्षिणाम् चण्डालपुक्कसानां च ब्रह्महा योनिं ऋच्छति
text: He who kills aBrāhmaṇaenters the womb of a dog, a pig, an ass, a camel, a cow, a goat, a sheep, a deer or a bird, or that of aCaṇḍālaor a Pukkasa.
iast: śvasūkarakharauṣṭrāṇāṃ go 'jāvimṛgapakṣiṇām caṇḍālapukkasānāṃ ca brahmahā yoniṃ ṛcchati

sanskrit_iast: kṛmikīṭapataṅgānāṃ viṣbhujāṃ caiva pakṣiṇām hiṃsrāṇāṃ caiva sattvānāṃ surāpo brāhmaṇo vrajet
meaning: कृमिकीटपतङ्गानां विष्भुजां चैव पक्षिणाम् हिंस्राणां चैव सत्त्वानां सुरापो ब्राह्मणो व्रजेत्
text: TheBrāhmaṇawho drinks wine shall enter the womb of worms, insects, moths, of birds feeding on ordure, or of carnivorous animals.
iast: kṛmikīṭapataṅgānāṃ viṣbhujāṃ caiva pakṣiṇām hiṃsrāṇāṃ caiva sattvānāṃ surāpo brāhmaṇo vrajet

sanskrit_iast: lūtā 'hisaraṭānāṃ ca tiraścāṃ cāmbucāriṇām hiṃsrāṇāṃ ca piśācānāṃ steno vipraḥ sahasraśaḥ
meaning: लूता 'हिसरटानां च तिरश्चां चाम्बुचारिणाम् हिंस्राणां च पिशाचानां स्तेनो विप्रः सहस्रशः
text: TheBrāhmaṇawho steals gold shall (enter) a thousand times into the womb of the spider, the snake, the lizard, of aquatic animals or of carnivorousPiśācas.
iast: lūtā 'hisaraṭānāṃ ca tiraścāṃ cāmbucāriṇām hiṃsrāṇāṃ ca piśācānāṃ steno vipraḥ sahasraśaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tṛṇagulmalatānāṃ ca kravyādāṃ daṃṣṭriṇāmapi krūrakarmakṛtāṃ caiva śataśo gurutalpagaḥ
meaning: तृणगुल्मलतानां च क्रव्यादां दंष्ट्रिणामपि क्रूरकर्मकृतां चैव शतशो गुरुतल्पगः
text: The violator of the Preceptor’s Bed is born hundreds of times as grasses, shrubs, creepers, as carnivorous and fanged animals, or as beings of cruel deeds.
iast: tṛṇagulmalatānāṃ ca kravyādāṃ daṃṣṭriṇāmapi krūrakarmakṛtāṃ caiva śataśo gurutalpagaḥ

sanskrit_iast: hiṃsrā bhavanti kravyādāḥ kṛmayo 'medhyabhakṣiṇaḥ parasparādinaḥ stenāḥ pretyāntyastrīniṣeviṇaḥ
meaning: हिंस्रा भवन्ति क्रव्यादाः कृमयो 'मेध्यभक्षिणः परस्परादिनः स्तेनाः प्रेत्यान्त्यस्त्रीनिषेविणः
text: Sanguinary persons become carnivorous animals; the eaters of impure food become worms; thieves become creatures consuming their own kind; and those having intercourse with women of the lowest caste become Pretas.
iast: hiṃsrā bhavanti kravyādāḥ kṛmayo 'medhyabhakṣiṇaḥ parasparādinaḥ stenāḥ pretyāntyastrīniṣeviṇaḥ

sanskrit_iast: saṃyogaṃ patitairgatvā parasyaiva ca yoṣitam apahṛtya ca viprasvaṃ bhavati brahmarākṣasaḥ
meaning: संयोगं पतितैर्गत्वा परस्यैव च योषितम् अपहृत्य च विप्रस्वं भवति ब्रह्मराक्षसः
text: He who associates with outcasts, he who approaches the wife of another person, and he who has stolen the property of aBrāhmaṇa, become ‘Brahmarākṣasas.’
iast: saṃyogaṃ patitairgatvā parasyaiva ca yoṣitam apahṛtya ca viprasvaṃ bhavati brahmarākṣasaḥ

sanskrit_iast: maṇimuktāpravālāni hṛtvā lobhena mānavaḥ vividhāṇi ca ratnāni jāyate hemakartṛṣu
meaning: मणिमुक्ताप्रवालानि हृत्वा लोभेन मानवः विविधाणि च रत्नानि जायते हेमकर्तृषु
text: The man who, through greed, steals gems, pearls and corals, or the various kinds of precious articles, is born among ‘Hemakartṛs.’
iast: maṇimuktāpravālāni hṛtvā lobhena mānavaḥ vividhāṇi ca ratnāni jāyate hemakartṛṣu

sanskrit_iast: dhānyaṃ hṛtvā bhavatyākhuḥ kāṃsyaṃ haṃso jalaṃ plavaḥ madhu daṃśaḥ payaḥ kāko rasaṃ śvā nakulo ghṛtam
meaning: धान्यं हृत्वा भवत्याखुः कांस्यं हंसो जलं प्लवः मधु दंशः पयः काको रसं श्वा नकुलो घृतम्
text: By stealing grains one becomes a rat; by stealing bronze, a Haṃsa; by stealing water, a Plava; by stealing honey, a gnat; by stealing milk, a crow; by stealing sweets, a dog; and by stealing clarified butter, an ichneumon.
iast: dhānyaṃ hṛtvā bhavatyākhuḥ kāṃsyaṃ haṃso jalaṃ plavaḥ madhu daṃśaḥ payaḥ kāko rasaṃ śvā nakulo ghṛtam

sanskrit_iast: māṃsaṃ gṛdhro vapāṃ madgustailaṃ tailapakaḥ khagaḥ cīrīvākastu lavaṇaṃ balākā śakunirdadhi
meaning: मांसं गृध्रो वपां मद्गुस्तैलं तैलपकः खगः चीरीवाकस्तु लवणं बलाका शकुनिर्दधि
text: For stealing meat, a vulture; for stealing fat, a cormorant; for stealing oil, a ‘Tailapāyika’ bird; for stealing salt, aCīrīvāka; and for stealing curds, a ‘Balākā’-bird.
iast: māṃsaṃ gṛdhro vapāṃ madgustailaṃ tailapakaḥ khagaḥ cīrīvākastu lavaṇaṃ balākā śakunirdadhi

sanskrit_iast: kauśeyaṃ tittirirhṛtvā kṣaumaṃ hṛtvā tu darduraḥ kārpāsatāntavaṃ krauñco godhā gāṃ vāggudo guḍam
meaning: कौशेयं तित्तिरिर्हृत्वा क्षौमं हृत्वा तु दर्दुरः कार्पासतान्तवं क्रौञ्चो गोधा गां वाग्गुदो गुडम्
text: For stealing silk, a partridge; for stealing linen, a frog; for stealing cotton-cloth, a crane; for stealing a cow, an alligator; and for stealing molasses, a ‘Vāgguda’-bird.
iast: kauśeyaṃ tittirirhṛtvā kṣaumaṃ hṛtvā tu darduraḥ kārpāsatāntavaṃ krauñco godhā gāṃ vāggudo guḍam

sanskrit_iast: chucchundariḥ śubhān gandhān patraśākaṃ tu barhiṇaḥ chucchundarīḥ śvāvit kṛtānnaṃ vividhamakṛtānnaṃ tu śalyakaḥ
meaning: छुच्छुन्दरिः शुभान् गन्धान् पत्रशाकं तु बर्हिणः छुच्छुन्दरीः श्वावित् कृतान्नं विविधमकृतान्नं तु शल्यकः
text: For stealing excellent perfumes, a musk-rat; for stealing vegetables with leaves, a peacock; for stealing cooked food of various kinds, aŚvāvit; and for stealing uncooked food a hedge-hog.
iast: chucchundariḥ śubhān gandhān patraśākaṃ tu barhiṇaḥ chucchundarīḥ śvāvit kṛtānnaṃ vividhamakṛtānnaṃ tu śalyakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: bako bhavati hṛtvā 'gniṃ gṛhakārī hyupaskaram raktāni hṛtvā vāsāṃsi jāyate jīvajīvakaḥ
meaning: बको भवति हृत्वा 'ग्निं गृहकारी ह्युपस्करम् रक्तानि हृत्वा वासांसि जायते जीवजीवकः
text: For stealing fire, a heron; for stealing utensils, a ‘Gṛhakāri’-bird; for stealing red clothes, one is born as a ‘Jīvajīvaka’-bird
iast: bako bhavati hṛtvā 'gniṃ gṛhakārī hyupaskaram raktāni hṛtvā vāsāṃsi jāyate jīvajīvakaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vṛko mṛgaibhaṃ vyāghro 'śvaṃ phalamūlaṃ tu markaṭaḥ strīṃ ṛkṣaḥ stokako vāri yānānyuṣṭraḥ paśūnajaḥ
meaning: वृको मृगैभं व्याघ्रो 'श्वं फलमूलं तु मर्कटः स्त्रीं ऋक्षः स्तोकको वारि यानान्युष्ट्रः पशूनजः
text: For stealing a deer or an elephant, a wolf; for stealing a horse, a tiger; for stealing fruits and roots, a monkey; for stealing a woman, a bear; for stealing water, a ‘Stokaka’-bird; for stealing conveyances, a camel; and for stealing cattle, a goat.
iast: vṛko mṛgaibhaṃ vyāghro 'śvaṃ phalamūlaṃ tu markaṭaḥ strīṃ ṛkṣaḥ stokako vāri yānānyuṣṭraḥ paśūnajaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yadvā tadvā paradravyamapahṛtya balātnaraḥ avaśyaṃ yāti tiryaktvaṃ jagdhvā caivāhutaṃ haviḥ
meaning: यद्वा तद्वा परद्रव्यमपहृत्य बलात्नरः अवश्यं याति तिर्यक्त्वं जग्ध्वा चैवाहुतं हविः
text: For taking by force any kind of property belonging to another, and for eating a sacrificial material that has not been offered, one inevitably becomes an animal.
iast: yadvā tadvā paradravyamapahṛtya balātnaraḥ avaśyaṃ yāti tiryaktvaṃ jagdhvā caivāhutaṃ haviḥ

sanskrit_iast: striyo 'pyetena kalpena hṛtvā doṣamavāpnuyuḥ eteṣāmeva jantūnāṃ bhāryātvamupayānti tāḥ
meaning: स्त्रियो 'प्येतेन कल्पेन हृत्वा दोषमवाप्नुयुः एतेषामेव जन्तूनां भार्यात्वमुपयान्ति ताः
text: For stealing things, women also should incur guilt on this same principle; they become the female of those same creatures that have been named.
iast: striyo 'pyetena kalpena hṛtvā doṣamavāpnuyuḥ eteṣāmeva jantūnāṃ bhāryātvamupayānti tāḥ

sanskrit_iast: svebhyaḥ svebhyastu karmabhyaścyutā varṇā hyanāpadi pāpān saṃsṛtya saṃsārān preṣyatāṃ yānti śatruṣu yānti dasyuṣu
meaning: स्वेभ्यः स्वेभ्यस्तु कर्मभ्यश्च्युता वर्णा ह्यनापदि पापान् संसृत्य संसारान् प्रेष्यतां यान्ति शत्रुषु यान्ति दस्युषु
text: If men of the various castes deviate from their occupations, under normal conditions, they migrate into vile migratory states and become servants among robbers.
iast: svebhyaḥ svebhyastu karmabhyaścyutā varṇā hyanāpadi pāpān saṃsṛtya saṃsārān preṣyatāṃ yānti śatruṣu yānti dasyuṣu

sanskrit_iast: vāntāśyulkāmukhaḥ preto vipro dharmāt svakāccyutaḥ amedhyakuṇapāśī ca kṣatriyaḥ kaṭapūtanaḥ kūṭapūtanaḥ
meaning: वान्ताश्युल्कामुखः प्रेतो विप्रो धर्मात् स्वकाच्च्युतः अमेध्यकुणपाशी च क्षत्रियः कटपूतनः कूटपूतनः
text: TheBrāhmaṇafallen off from his own duty becomes a ‘fire-mouthed’Pretafeeding on vomitings; and theKṣatriyaa ‘foul-nosed’ Preta feeding on impure substances and dead bodies.
iast: vāntāśyulkāmukhaḥ preto vipro dharmāt svakāccyutaḥ amedhyakuṇapāśī ca kṣatriyaḥ kaṭapūtanaḥ kūṭapūtanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: maitrākṣajyotikaḥ preto vaiśyo bhavati pūyabhuk maitrākṣijyotikaḥ cailāśakaśca bhavati śūdro dharmāt svakāccyutaḥ
meaning: मैत्राक्षज्योतिकः प्रेतो वैश्यो भवति पूयभुक् मैत्राक्षिज्योतिकः चैलाशकश्च भवति शूद्रो धर्मात् स्वकाच्च्युतः
text: The Vaiśya, fallen from his duty, becomes the ‘Maitrākṣajyotika’Pretafeeding on pus; and theŚūdrabecomes a ‘Cailāśaka’ Preta
iast: maitrākṣajyotikaḥ preto vaiśyo bhavati pūyabhuk maitrākṣijyotikaḥ cailāśakaśca bhavati śūdro dharmāt svakāccyutaḥ

sanskrit_iast: yathā yathā niṣevante viṣayān viṣayātmakāḥ tathā tathā kuśalatā teṣāṃ teṣūpajāyate
meaning: यथा यथा निषेवन्ते विषयान् विषयात्मकाः तथा तथा कुशलता तेषां तेषूपजायते
text: In the proportion that sensually-inclined persons go on indulging in sensual pleasures, in that same proportion their ardour for them goes on increasing.
iast: yathā yathā niṣevante viṣayān viṣayātmakāḥ tathā tathā kuśalatā teṣāṃ teṣūpajāyate

sanskrit_iast: te 'bhyāsāt karmaṇāṃ teṣāṃ pāpānāmalpabuddhayaḥ saṃprāpnuvanti duḥkhāni tāsu tāsviha yoniṣu
meaning: ते 'भ्यासात् कर्मणां तेषां पापानामल्पबुद्धयः संप्राप्नुवन्ति दुःखानि तासु तास्विह योनिषु
text: By the repetition of the said sinful acts, those men with shall understanding undergo sufferings in the various forms of existence in this world.
iast: te 'bhyāsāt karmaṇāṃ teṣāṃ pāpānāmalpabuddhayaḥ saṃprāpnuvanti duḥkhāni tāsu tāsviha yoniṣu

sanskrit_iast: tāmisrādiṣu caugreṣu narakeṣu vivartanam asipatravanādīni bandhanachedanāni ca
meaning: तामिस्रादिषु चौग्रेषु नरकेषु विवर्तनम् असिपत्रवनादीनि बन्धनछेदनानि च
text: (They also suffer) being tossed about in theTāmisraand other dreadful hells, and being bound and mangled in the ‘forest with sword-leaved trees’ and other places.
iast: tāmisrādiṣu caugreṣu narakeṣu vivartanam asipatravanādīni bandhanachedanāni ca

sanskrit_iast: vividhāścaiva saṃpīḍāḥ kākaulūkaiśca bhakṣaṇam karambhavālukātāpān kumbhīpākāṃśca dāruṇān
meaning: विविधाश्चैव संपीडाः काकौलूकैश्च भक्षणम् करम्भवालुकातापान् कुम्भीपाकांश्च दारुणान्
text: Also various forms of torments, being devoured by ravens and owls, tortures from morasses and scorching sand, and terrible boiling in jars.
iast: vividhāścaiva saṃpīḍāḥ kākaulūkaiśca bhakṣaṇam karambhavālukātāpān kumbhīpākāṃśca dāruṇān

sanskrit_iast: saṃbhavāṃśca viyonīṣu duḥkhaprāyāsu nityaśaḥ śītātapābhighātāṃśca vividhāni bhayāni ca
meaning: संभवांश्च वियोनीषु दुःखप्रायासु नित्यशः शीतातपाभिघातांश्च विविधानि भयानि च
text: Constant births in low forms of life, abounding in misery, afflictions from heat and cold, and terrors of various kinds.
iast: saṃbhavāṃśca viyonīṣu duḥkhaprāyāsu nityaśaḥ śītātapābhighātāṃśca vividhāni bhayāni ca

sanskrit_iast: asakṛdgarbhavāseṣu vāsaṃ janma ca dāruṇam bandhanāni ca kāṣṭhāni parapreṣyatvameva ca kaṣṭāni
meaning: असकृद्गर्भवासेषु वासं जन्म च दारुणम् बन्धनानि च काष्ठानि परप्रेष्यत्वमेव च कष्टानि
text: Repeated lying in wombs, agonising births, painful bondages, and slavery to others.
iast: asakṛdgarbhavāseṣu vāsaṃ janma ca dāruṇam bandhanāni ca kāṣṭhāni parapreṣyatvameva ca kaṣṭāni

sanskrit_iast: bandhupriyaviyogāṃśca saṃvāsaṃ caiva durjanaiḥ dravyārjanaṃ ca nāśaṃ ca mitrāmitrasya cārjanam
meaning: बन्धुप्रियवियोगांश्च संवासं चैव दुर्जनैः द्रव्यार्जनं च नाशं च मित्रामित्रस्य चार्जनम्
text: Inevitable decrepitude, the pangs of sickness, various forms of afflictions and unconquerable death.
iast: bandhupriyaviyogāṃśca saṃvāsaṃ caiva durjanaiḥ dravyārjanaṃ ca nāśaṃ ca mitrāmitrasya cārjanam

sanskrit_iast: jarāṃ caivāpratīkārāṃ vyādhibhiścaupapīḍanam kleśāṃśca vividhāṃstāṃstān mṛtyumeva ca durjayam
meaning: जरां चैवाप्रतीकारां व्याधिभिश्चौपपीडनम् क्लेशांश्च विविधांस्तांस्तान् मृत्युमेव च दुर्जयम्
text: Inevitable decrepitude, the pangs of sickness, various forms of afflictions and unconquerable death.
iast: jarāṃ caivāpratīkārāṃ vyādhibhiścaupapīḍanam kleśāṃśca vividhāṃstāṃstān mṛtyumeva ca durjayam

sanskrit_iast: yādṛśena tu bhāvena yadyat karma niṣevate tādṛśena śarīreṇa tat tat phalamupāśnute
meaning: यादृशेन तु भावेन यद्यत् कर्म निषेवते तादृशेन शरीरेण तत् तत् फलमुपाश्नुते
text: With whatever disposition a man performs an act, the fruit thereof he reaps with a body of that same quality.
iast: yādṛśena tu bhāvena yadyat karma niṣevate tādṛśena śarīreṇa tat tat phalamupāśnute

sanskrit_iast: eṣa sarvaḥ samuddiṣṭaḥ karmaṇāṃ vaḥ phalaudayaḥ naiḥśreyasakaraṃ karma viprasyaidaṃ nibodhata
meaning: एष सर्वः समुद्दिष्टः कर्मणां वः फलौदयः नैःश्रेयसकरं कर्म विप्रस्यैदं निबोधत
text: Thus has been indicated to you the entire manner in which results proceed from actions; now learn that act which brings about the Highest Good of theBrāhmaṇa.
iast: eṣa sarvaḥ samuddiṣṭaḥ karmaṇāṃ vaḥ phalaudayaḥ naiḥśreyasakaraṃ karma viprasyaidaṃ nibodhata

sanskrit_iast: vedābhyāsastapo jñānamindriyāṇāṃ ca saṃyamaḥ ahiṃsā gurusevā ca niḥśreyasakaraṃ param
meaning: वेदाभ्यासस्तपो ज्ञानमिन्द्रियाणां च संयमः अहिंसा गुरुसेवा च निःश्रेयसकरं परम्
text: Vedic Study, Austerity, Knowledge, Control of the Senses, Harmlessness, Service of Elders,—are the best means of attaining the Highest Good.
iast: vedābhyāsastapo jñānamindriyāṇāṃ ca saṃyamaḥ ahiṃsā gurusevā ca niḥśreyasakaraṃ param

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmapi caiteṣāṃ śubhānāmiha karmaṇām kiṃ citśreyaskarataraṃ karmauktaṃ puruṣaṃ prati
meaning: सर्वेषामपि चैतेषां शुभानामिह कर्मणाम् किं चित्श्रेयस्करतरं कर्मौक्तं पुरुषं प्रति
text: [Answer]—Of all these, Knowledge of the Self has been declared to be the most efficacious; since it is the best of all sciences, as Immortality is attained by its means.
iast: sarveṣāmapi caiteṣāṃ śubhānāmiha karmaṇām kiṃ citśreyaskarataraṃ karmauktaṃ puruṣaṃ prati

sanskrit_iast: sarveṣāmapi caiteṣāmātmajñānaṃ paraṃ smṛtam taddhyagryaṃ sarvavidyānāṃ prāpyate hyamṛtaṃ tataḥ
meaning: सर्वेषामपि चैतेषामात्मज्ञानं परं स्मृतम् तद्ध्यग्र्यं सर्वविद्यानां प्राप्यते ह्यमृतं ततः
text: [Answer]—Of all these, Knowledge of the Self has been declared to be the most efficacious; since it is the best of all sciences, as Immortality is attained by its means.
iast: sarveṣāmapi caiteṣāmātmajñānaṃ paraṃ smṛtam taddhyagryaṃ sarvavidyānāṃ prāpyate hyamṛtaṃ tataḥ

sanskrit_iast: ṣaṇṇāmeṣāṃ tu sarveṣāṃ karmaṇāṃ pretya caiha ca śreyaskarataraṃ jñeyaṃ sarvadā karma vaidikam
meaning: षण्णामेषां तु सर्वेषां कर्मणां प्रेत्य चैह च श्रेयस्करतरं ज्ञेयं सर्वदा कर्म वैदिकम्
text: Among the six aforesaid actions, the performance of ‘Vedic Acts’ should be regarded as the most efficacious for bringing about happiness in this world and as well as after death.
iast: ṣaṇṇāmeṣāṃ tu sarveṣāṃ karmaṇāṃ pretya caiha ca śreyaskarataraṃ jñeyaṃ sarvadā karma vaidikam

sanskrit_iast: vaidike karmayoge tu sarvāṇyetānyaśeṣataḥ antarbhavanti kramaśastasmiṃstasmin kriyāvidhau
meaning: वैदिके कर्मयोगे तु सर्वाण्येतान्यशेषतः अन्तर्भवन्ति क्रमशस्तस्मिंस्तस्मिन् क्रियाविधौ
text: All these are fully included, each in its turn, in a particular course of performance of the Vedic Act.
iast: vaidike karmayoge tu sarvāṇyetānyaśeṣataḥ antarbhavanti kramaśastasmiṃstasmin kriyāvidhau

sanskrit_iast: sukhābhyudayikaṃ caiva naiḥśreyasikameva ca pravṛttaṃ ca nivṛttaṃ ca dvividhaṃ karma vaidikam
meaning: सुखाभ्युदयिकं चैव नैःश्रेयसिकमेव च प्रवृत्तं च निवृत्तं च द्विविधं कर्म वैदिकम्
text: The ‘Vedic Act’ is of two kinds—(a) the ‘active,’ which is conducive to happiness and prosperity, and (b) the ‘passive,’ which is conducive to the highest good.
iast: sukhābhyudayikaṃ caiva naiḥśreyasikameva ca pravṛttaṃ ca nivṛttaṃ ca dvividhaṃ karma vaidikam

sanskrit_iast: iha cāmutra vā kāmyaṃ pravṛttaṃ karma kīrtyate niṣkāmaṃ jñātapūrvaṃ tu nivṛttamupadiśyate
meaning: इह चामुत्र वा काम्यं प्रवृत्तं कर्म कीर्त्यते निष्कामं ज्ञातपूर्वं तु निवृत्तमुपदिश्यते
text: That which is done with knowledge and brings about the fulfilment of desires either in this world, or in the next, is described as ‘active’; while that which is done with knowledge and without desires, is declared to be ‘passive.’
iast: iha cāmutra vā kāmyaṃ pravṛttaṃ karma kīrtyate niṣkāmaṃ jñātapūrvaṃ tu nivṛttamupadiśyate

sanskrit_iast: pravṛttaṃ karma saṃsevyaṃ devānāmeti sāmyatām nivṛttaṃ sevamānastu bhūtānyatyeti pañca vai
meaning: प्रवृत्तं कर्म संसेव्यं देवानामेति साम्यताम् निवृत्तं सेवमानस्तु भूतान्यत्येति पञ्च वै
text: He who devotes himself to the ‘active’ side, attains equality to the gods; while he who devotes himself to the ‘passive’ section, passes beyond the five material substances.
iast: pravṛttaṃ karma saṃsevyaṃ devānāmeti sāmyatām nivṛttaṃ sevamānastu bhūtānyatyeti pañca vai

sanskrit_iast: sarvabhūteṣu cātmānaṃ sarvabhūtāni cātmani samaṃ paśyannātmayājī svārājyamadhigacchati
meaning: सर्वभूतेषु चात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि समं पश्यन्नात्मयाजी स्वाराज्यमधिगच्छति
text: He who perceives the Self in all beings, and all beings in the Self,—and sacrifices to the Self,—attains self-sovereignty.
iast: sarvabhūteṣu cātmānaṃ sarvabhūtāni cātmani samaṃ paśyannātmayājī svārājyamadhigacchati

sanskrit_iast: yathauktānyapi karmāṇi parihāya dvijottamaḥ ātmajñāne śame ca syādvedābhyāse ca yatnavān
meaning: यथौक्तान्यपि कर्माणि परिहाय द्विजोत्तमः आत्मज्ञाने शमे च स्याद्वेदाभ्यासे च यत्नवान्
text: Having renounced even the said acts, theBrāhmaṇashall concentrate his effort on the knowledge of Self, on calmness and on the study of the Veda.
iast: yathauktānyapi karmāṇi parihāya dvijottamaḥ ātmajñāne śame ca syādvedābhyāse ca yatnavān

sanskrit_iast: etaddhi janmasāphalyaṃ brāhmaṇasya viśeṣataḥ prāpyaitat kṛtakṛtyo hi dvijo bhavati nānyathā
meaning: एतद्धि जन्मसाफल्यं ब्राह्मणस्य विशेषतः प्राप्यैतत् कृतकृत्यो हि द्विजो भवति नान्यथा
text: This represents the fulfilment of the object of one’s existence, specially for theBrāhmaṇa; it is only when he has attained this, and not otherwise, that the twice-born man has accomplished his purpose.
iast: etaddhi janmasāphalyaṃ brāhmaṇasya viśeṣataḥ prāpyaitat kṛtakṛtyo hi dvijo bhavati nānyathā

sanskrit_iast: pitṛdevamanuṣyāṇāṃ vedaścakṣuḥ sanātanam aśakyaṃ cāprameyaṃ ca vedaśāstramiti sthitiḥ
meaning: पितृदेवमनुष्याणां वेदश्चक्षुः सनातनम् अशक्यं चाप्रमेयं च वेदशास्त्रमिति स्थितिः
text: ForPitṛs, gods and men, the Veda is the eternal eye; the teaching of the Veda is beyond power and illimitable. Such is the settled fact.
iast: pitṛdevamanuṣyāṇāṃ vedaścakṣuḥ sanātanam aśakyaṃ cāprameyaṃ ca vedaśāstramiti sthitiḥ

sanskrit_iast: yā vedabāhyāḥ smṛtayo yāśca kāśca kudṛṣṭayaḥ śrutayo sarvāstā niṣphalāḥ pretya tamoniṣṭhā hi tāḥ smṛtāḥ
meaning: या वेदबाह्याः स्मृतयो याश्च काश्च कुदृष्टयः श्रुतयो सर्वास्ता निष्फलाः प्रेत्य तमोनिष्ठा हि ताः स्मृताः
text: Those ‘revealed texts’ that are outside the Veda, as also all the false theories, are useless, even when carried to perfection; as they have been declared to be founded on ‘darkness.’
iast: yā vedabāhyāḥ smṛtayo yāśca kāśca kudṛṣṭayaḥ śrutayo sarvāstā niṣphalāḥ pretya tamoniṣṭhā hi tāḥ smṛtāḥ

sanskrit_iast: utpadyante cyavante ca yānyato 'nyāni kāni cit utpadyante vinaśyanti tānyarvākkālikatayā niṣphalānyanṛtāni ca
meaning: उत्पद्यन्ते च्यवन्ते च यान्यतो 'न्यानि कानि चित् उत्पद्यन्ते विनश्यन्ति तान्यर्वाक्कालिकतया निष्फलान्यनृतानि च
text: Those other (doctrines) which spring up and perish are all worthless and false, being of modern growth.
iast: utpadyante cyavante ca yānyato 'nyāni kāni cit utpadyante vinaśyanti tānyarvākkālikatayā niṣphalānyanṛtāni ca

sanskrit_iast: cāturvarṇyaṃ trayo lokāścatvāraścāśramāḥ pṛthak bhūtaṃ bhavyaṃ bhaviṣyaṃ ca sarvaṃ vedāt prasidhyati bhūtaṃ bhavadbhaviṣyaṃ ca
meaning: चातुर्वर्ण्यं त्रयो लोकाश्चत्वारश्चाश्रमाः पृथक् भूतं भव्यं भविष्यं च सर्वं वेदात् प्रसिध्यति भूतं भवद्भविष्यं च
text: The four castes, the three worlds, the four life-stages, the past, the present and the future are each learnt from the Veda.
iast: cāturvarṇyaṃ trayo lokāścatvāraścāśramāḥ pṛthak bhūtaṃ bhavyaṃ bhaviṣyaṃ ca sarvaṃ vedāt prasidhyati bhūtaṃ bhavadbhaviṣyaṃ ca

sanskrit_iast: śabdaḥ sparśaśca rūpaṃ ca raso gandhaśca pañcamaḥ vedādeva prasūyante prasūtirguṇakarmataḥ
meaning: शब्दः स्पर्शश्च रूपं च रसो गन्धश्च पञ्चमः वेदादेव प्रसूयन्ते प्रसूतिर्गुणकर्मतः
text: Sound, touch, colour, taste, and odour as the fifth, proceed from the Veda; their production being due to the ‘secondary rites.’
iast: śabdaḥ sparśaśca rūpaṃ ca raso gandhaśca pañcamaḥ vedādeva prasūyante prasūtirguṇakarmataḥ

sanskrit_iast: bibharti sarvabhūtāni vedaśāstraṃ sanātanam tasmādetat paraṃ manye yatjantorasya sādhanam
meaning: बिभर्ति सर्वभूतानि वेदशास्त्रं सनातनम् तस्मादेतत् परं मन्ये यत्जन्तोरस्य साधनम्
text: The eternal lore of the Veda upholds all beings; f or th is reason I regard this as the best means of accomplishing the ends of every creature.
iast: bibharti sarvabhūtāni vedaśāstraṃ sanātanam tasmādetat paraṃ manye yatjantorasya sādhanam

sanskrit_iast: senāpatyaṃ ca rājyaṃ ca daṇḍanetṛtvameva ca saināpatyaṃ sarvalokādhipatyaṃ ca vedaśāstravidarhati
meaning: सेनापत्यं च राज्यं च दण्डनेतृत्वमेव च सैनापत्यं सर्वलोकाधिपत्यं च वेदशास्त्रविदर्हति
text: It is only one who knows the Vedic lore that deserves the command over armies, Kingly authority, the office of the adjudicator of punishments and sovereignty over all men.
iast: senāpatyaṃ ca rājyaṃ ca daṇḍanetṛtvameva ca saināpatyaṃ sarvalokādhipatyaṃ ca vedaśāstravidarhati

sanskrit_iast: yathā jātabalo vahnirdahatyārdrānapi drumān tathā dahati vedajñaḥ karmajaṃ doṣamātmanaḥ
meaning: यथा जातबलो वह्निर्दहत्यार्द्रानपि द्रुमान् तथा दहति वेदज्ञः कर्मजं दोषमात्मनः
text: Just as fire, having gained strength, burns even green trees, even so does the person knowing the Veda consume all the evil effects of his deeds.
iast: yathā jātabalo vahnirdahatyārdrānapi drumān tathā dahati vedajñaḥ karmajaṃ doṣamātmanaḥ

sanskrit_iast: vedaśāstrārthatattvajño yatra tatrāśrame vasan ihaiva loke tiṣṭhan sa brahmabhūyāya kalpate
meaning: वेदशास्त्रार्थतत्त्वज्ञो यत्र तत्राश्रमे वसन् इहैव लोके तिष्ठन् स ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते
text: In whatever life-stage he may be, the person who knows the true meaning of the Vedic Scriptures becomes fit for union with brahman, even while dwelling in this world.
iast: vedaśāstrārthatattvajño yatra tatrāśrame vasan ihaiva loke tiṣṭhan sa brahmabhūyāya kalpate

sanskrit_iast: ajñebhyo granthinaḥ śreṣṭhā granthibhyo dhāriṇo varāḥ dhāribhyo jñāninaḥ śreṣṭhā jñānibhyo vyavasāyinaḥ
meaning: अज्ञेभ्यो ग्रन्थिनः श्रेष्ठा ग्रन्थिभ्यो धारिणो वराः धारिभ्यो ज्ञानिनः श्रेष्ठा ज्ञानिभ्यो व्यवसायिनः
text: Better than ignoramuses are those who ‘read the books’; better than the ‘reader of books’ are those who ‘retain’ them; better than the ‘retainers’ are those who have ‘knowledge’; and better than those having ‘knowledge’ are those who act.
iast: ajñebhyo granthinaḥ śreṣṭhā granthibhyo dhāriṇo varāḥ dhāribhyo jñāninaḥ śreṣṭhā jñānibhyo vyavasāyinaḥ

sanskrit_iast: tapo vidyā ca viprasya niḥśreyasakaraṃ param tapasā kilbiṣaṃ hanti vidyayā 'mṛtamaśnute
meaning: तपो विद्या च विप्रस्य निःश्रेयसकरं परम् तपसा किल्बिषं हन्ति विद्यया 'मृतमश्नुते
text: Austerity and knowledge are conducive to the Highest Good of theBrāhmaṇa; by Austerity he destroys sins and by Knowledge he attains immortality.
iast: tapo vidyā ca viprasya niḥśreyasakaraṃ param tapasā kilbiṣaṃ hanti vidyayā 'mṛtamaśnute

sanskrit_iast: pratyakṣaṃ cānumānaṃ ca śāstraṃ ca vividhā ''gamam trayaṃ suviditaṃ kāryaṃ dharmaśuddhimabhīpsatā
meaning: प्रत्यक्षं चानुमानं च शास्त्रं च विविधा ''गमम् त्रयं सुविदितं कार्यं धर्मशुद्धिमभीप्सता
text: If one desires to obtain the correct knowledge of Dharma, he should become fully acquainted with these three:—Perception, Inference and the Scriptures of various traditions.
iast: pratyakṣaṃ cānumānaṃ ca śāstraṃ ca vividhā ''gamam trayaṃ suviditaṃ kāryaṃ dharmaśuddhimabhīpsatā

sanskrit_iast: ārṣaṃ dharmaupadeśaṃ ca vedaśāstrāvirodhinā yastarkeṇānusaṃdhatte sa dharmaṃ veda naitaraḥ
meaning: आर्षं धर्मौपदेशं च वेदशास्त्राविरोधिना यस्तर्केणानुसंधत्ते स धर्मं वेद नैतरः
text: If a man explores, by ratiocination, the Vedic teaching regarding Dharma, he alone, and no other, understands Dharma.
iast: ārṣaṃ dharmaupadeśaṃ ca vedaśāstrāvirodhinā yastarkeṇānusaṃdhatte sa dharmaṃ veda naitaraḥ

sanskrit_iast: naiḥśreyasamidaṃ karma yathauditamaśeṣataḥ mānavasyāsya śāstrasya rahasyamupadiśyate upadekṣyate
meaning: नैःश्रेयसमिदं कर्म यथौदितमशेषतः मानवस्यास्य शास्त्रस्य रहस्यमुपदिश्यते उपदेक्ष्यते
text: The act conducive to the Highest Good is as has been here fully declared. Now is going to be set forth the secret of the Teachings of Manu
iast: naiḥśreyasamidaṃ karma yathauditamaśeṣataḥ mānavasyāsya śāstrasya rahasyamupadiśyate upadekṣyate

sanskrit_iast: anāmnāteṣu dharmeṣu kathaṃ syāditi cedbhavet yaṃ śiṣṭā brāhmaṇā brūyuḥ sa dharmaḥ syādaśaṅkitaḥ
meaning: अनाम्नातेषु धर्मेषु कथं स्यादिति चेद्भवेत् यं शिष्टा ब्राह्मणा ब्रूयुः स धर्मः स्यादशङ्कितः
text: If the question should arise—“How should it be in regard to those points upon which the laws have not been declared?”—[the answer is]—what the culturedBrāhmaṇasdeclare, that shall be the undoubted law.
iast: anāmnāteṣu dharmeṣu kathaṃ syāditi cedbhavet yaṃ śiṣṭā brāhmaṇā brūyuḥ sa dharmaḥ syādaśaṅkitaḥ

sanskrit_iast: dharmeṇādhigato yaistu vedaḥ saparibṛṃhaṇaḥ te śiṣṭā brāhmaṇā jñeyāḥ śrutipratyakṣahetavaḥ
meaning: धर्मेणाधिगतो यैस्तु वेदः सपरिबृंहणः ते शिष्टा ब्राह्मणा ज्ञेयाः श्रुतिप्रत्यक्षहेतवः
text: ThoseBrāhmaṇas, by whom the Veda, along with its supplements, has been learnt in the right manner, and who are guided directly by the revealed texts,—shall be regarded as ‘cultur ed.’
iast: dharmeṇādhigato yaistu vedaḥ saparibṛṃhaṇaḥ te śiṣṭā brāhmaṇā jñeyāḥ śrutipratyakṣahetavaḥ

sanskrit_iast: daśāvarā vā pariṣadyaṃ dharmaṃ parikalpayet try 'varā vā 'pi vṛttasthā taṃ dharmaṃ na vicālayet
meaning: दशावरा वा परिषद्यं धर्मं परिकल्पयेत् त्र्य् 'वरा वा 'पि वृत्तस्था तं धर्मं न विचालयेत्
text: When an assembly consisting of at least ten men,—or of at least three men firm in their duty,—declares a certain law, one should not seek to dispute it.
iast: daśāvarā vā pariṣadyaṃ dharmaṃ parikalpayet try 'varā vā 'pi vṛttasthā taṃ dharmaṃ na vicālayet

sanskrit_iast: traividyo hetukastarkī nairukto dharmapāṭhakaḥ trayaścāśramiṇaḥ pūrve pariṣat syāddaśāvarā
meaning: त्रैविद्यो हेतुकस्तर्की नैरुक्तो धर्मपाठकः त्रयश्चाश्रमिणः पूर्वे परिषत् स्याद्दशावरा
text: A person learned in the three Vedas, a logician, an investigator, a person knowing the Nirukta, a lawyer and three men belonging to the first three life-stages, shall constitute the ‘Assembly’; which shall consist of at least ten members.
iast: traividyo hetukastarkī nairukto dharmapāṭhakaḥ trayaścāśramiṇaḥ pūrve pariṣat syāddaśāvarā

sanskrit_iast: ṛgvedavidyajurvidca sāmavedavideva ca try 'varā pariṣadjñeyā dharmasaṃśayanirṇaye
meaning: ऋग्वेदविद्यजुर्विद्च सामवेदविदेव च त्र्य् 'वरा परिषद्ज्ञेया धर्मसंशयनिर्णये
text: A person knowing theṚgveda, a person knowing the Yajurveda, and a person knowing theSāmaveda, shall be understood to form the assembly of at least three members, competent to decide doubtful points of law.
iast: ṛgvedavidyajurvidca sāmavedavideva ca try 'varā pariṣadjñeyā dharmasaṃśayanirṇaye

sanskrit_iast: eko 'pi vedaviddharmaṃ yaṃ vyavasyeddvijottamaḥ sa vijñeyaḥ paro dharmo nājñānāmudito 'yutaiḥ
meaning: एको 'पि वेदविद्धर्मं यं व्यवस्येद्द्विजोत्तमः स विज्ञेयः परो धर्मो नाज्ञानामुदितो 'युतैः
text: That which even a singleBrāhmaṇalearned in the Veda decides to be the law, shall be understood to be the highest law,—and not what is asserted by ten thousand ignoramuses.
iast: eko 'pi vedaviddharmaṃ yaṃ vyavasyeddvijottamaḥ sa vijñeyaḥ paro dharmo nājñānāmudito 'yutaiḥ

sanskrit_iast: avratānāmamantrāṇāṃ jātimātraupajīvinām sahasraśaḥ sametānāṃ pariṣattvaṃ na vidyate
meaning: अव्रतानाममन्त्राणां जातिमात्रौपजीविनाम् सहस्रशः समेतानां परिषत्त्वं न विद्यते
text: Even if thousands ofBrāhmaṇascome together,—who have not fulfilled their duties, who are ignorant of the sacred texts, who subsist merely by the name of their caste,—the character of the ‘Assembly’ cannot belong to them.
iast: avratānāmamantrāṇāṃ jātimātraupajīvinām sahasraśaḥ sametānāṃ pariṣattvaṃ na vidyate

sanskrit_iast: yaṃ vadanti tamobhūtā mūrkhā dharmamatadvidaḥ tatpāpaṃ śatadhā bhūtvā tadvaktr̥̄nanugacchati
meaning: यं वदन्ति तमोभूता मूर्खा धर्ममतद्विदः तत्पापं शतधा भूत्वा तद्वक्त्र्̥̄ननुगच्छति
text: When ignorant men, partaking of the quality of ‘Tamas,’ declare a certain act as right, without knowing what ‘right’ means,—the sin of that act falls hundred-fold upon those who propound it.
iast: yaṃ vadanti tamobhūtā mūrkhā dharmamatadvidaḥ tatpāpaṃ śatadhā bhūtvā tadvaktr̥̄nanugacchati

sanskrit_iast: etadvo 'bhihitaṃ sarvaṃ niḥśreyasakaraṃ param asmādapracyuto vipraḥ prāpnoti paramāṃ gatim
meaning: एतद्वो 'भिहितं सर्वं निःश्रेयसकरं परम् अस्मादप्रच्युतो विप्रः प्राप्नोति परमां गतिम्
text: All that is conducive to the Highest Good has been thus expounded to you; theBrāhmaṇawho has not deviated from this, attains the highest state.
iast: etadvo 'bhihitaṃ sarvaṃ niḥśreyasakaraṃ param asmādapracyuto vipraḥ prāpnoti paramāṃ gatim

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ sa bhagavān devo lokānāṃ hitakāmyayā dharmasya paramaṃ guhyaṃ mamaidaṃ sarvamuktavān
meaning: एवं स भगवान् देवो लोकानां हितकाम्यया धर्मस्य परमं गुह्यं ममैदं सर्वमुक्तवान्
text: Thus has the blessed Lord explained to me, with a desire to benefit mankind, the highest secret of Dharma.
iast: evaṃ sa bhagavān devo lokānāṃ hitakāmyayā dharmasya paramaṃ guhyaṃ mamaidaṃ sarvamuktavān

sanskrit_iast: sarvamātmani saṃpaśyet satcāsatca samāhitaḥ sarvaṃ hyātmani saṃpaśyannādharme kurute manaḥ matiṃ
meaning: सर्वमात्मनि संपश्येत् सत्चासत्च समाहितः सर्वं ह्यात्मनि संपश्यन्नाधर्मे कुरुते मनः मतिं
text: With a concentrated mind, one should perceive in the Self all things, real as well as unreal. One who perceives all things in the Self never turns his mind towards wrong.
iast: sarvamātmani saṃpaśyet satcāsatca samāhitaḥ sarvaṃ hyātmani saṃpaśyannādharme kurute manaḥ matiṃ

sanskrit_iast: ātmaiva devatāḥ sarvāḥ sarvamātmanyavasthitam ātmā hi janayatyeṣāṃ karmayogaṃ śarīriṇām
meaning: आत्मैव देवताः सर्वाः सर्वमात्मन्यवस्थितम् आत्मा हि जनयत्येषां कर्मयोगं शरीरिणाम्
text: The Self alone is all the gods; everything subsists in the Self; it is the Self that brings about the connection of these embodied beings with actions.
iast: ātmaiva devatāḥ sarvāḥ sarvamātmanyavasthitam ātmā hi janayatyeṣāṃ karmayogaṃ śarīriṇām

sanskrit_iast: khaṃ saṃniveśayet kheṣu ceṣṭanasparśane 'nilam paktidṛṣṭyoḥ paraṃ tejaḥ snehe 'po gāṃ ca mūrtiṣu
meaning: खं संनिवेशयेत् खेषु चेष्टनस्पर्शने 'निलम् पक्तिदृष्ट्योः परं तेजः स्नेहे 'पो गां च मूर्तिषु
text: One should withdraw theĀkāśainto the ten cavities, the Wind into the organs of touch and movement, the best Light into the organs of digestion and vision, the Water into the fluids, and the Earth into the solid limbs.
iast: khaṃ saṃniveśayet kheṣu ceṣṭanasparśane 'nilam paktidṛṣṭyoḥ paraṃ tejaḥ snehe 'po gāṃ ca mūrtiṣu

sanskrit_iast: manasīnduṃ diśaḥ śrotre krānte viṣṇuṃ bale haram vācyagniṃ mitramutsarge prajane ca prajāpatim
meaning: मनसीन्दुं दिशः श्रोत्रे क्रान्ते विष्णुं बले हरम् वाच्यग्निं मित्रमुत्सर्गे प्रजने च प्रजापतिम्
text: The Moon into the mind, Space into the ear,Viṣṇuinto movement, Hara into strength, Agni into speech, Mitra into the excretions, andPrajāpatiinto the generative organ.
iast: manasīnduṃ diśaḥ śrotre krānte viṣṇuṃ bale haram vācyagniṃ mitramutsarge prajane ca prajāpatim

sanskrit_iast: praśāsitāraṃ sarveṣāmaṇīyāṃsamaṇorapi rukmābhaṃ svapnadhīgamyaṃ vidyāt taṃ puruṣaṃ param
meaning: प्रशासितारं सर्वेषामणीयांसमणोरपि रुक्माभं स्वप्नधीगम्यं विद्यात् तं पुरुषं परम्
text: The ruler of all, who is minuter than the minutest atom, bright like gold, amenable to dream-cognition,—him should one know as the HighestPuruṣa.
iast: praśāsitāraṃ sarveṣāmaṇīyāṃsamaṇorapi rukmābhaṃ svapnadhīgamyaṃ vidyāt taṃ puruṣaṃ param

sanskrit_iast: etameke vadantyagniṃ manumanye prajāpatim indrameke pare prāṇamapare brahma śāśvatam
meaning: एतमेके वदन्त्यग्निं मनुमन्ये प्रजापतिम् इन्द्रमेके परे प्राणमपरे ब्रह्म शाश्वतम्
text: This some people call ‘Agni’; others, ‘Manu-Prajāpati’; others, ‘Indra’; others, ‘Prāṇa’; and yet others, ‘the Eternal Brahman.’
iast: etameke vadantyagniṃ manumanye prajāpatim indrameke pare prāṇamapare brahma śāśvatam

sanskrit_iast: eṣa sarvāṇi bhūtāni pañcabhirvyāpya mūrtibhiḥ janmavṛddhikṣayairnityaṃ saṃsārayati cakravat
meaning: एष सर्वाणि भूतानि पञ्चभिर्व्याप्य मूर्तिभिः जन्मवृद्धिक्षयैर्नित्यं संसारयति चक्रवत्
text: He it is who, interpenetrating all beings, with his five forms, makes them, revolve constantly like a wheel, through birth, growth and decay.
iast: eṣa sarvāṇi bhūtāni pañcabhirvyāpya mūrtibhiḥ janmavṛddhikṣayairnityaṃ saṃsārayati cakravat

sanskrit_iast: evaṃ yaḥ sarvabhūteṣu paśyatyātmānamātmanā sa sarvasamatāmetya brahmābhyeti paraṃ padam
meaning: एवं यः सर्वभूतेषु पश्यत्यात्मानमात्मना स सर्वसमतामेत्य ब्रह्माभ्येति परं पदम्
text: He who thus perceives the Self through the Self, in all beings, becomes equal towards all and attains the highest state, Brahman.
iast: evaṃ yaḥ sarvabhūteṣu paśyatyātmānamātmanā sa sarvasamatāmetya brahmābhyeti paraṃ padam

sanskrit_iast: ityetan mānavaṃ śāstraṃ bhṛguproktaṃ paṭhan dvijaḥ bhavatyācāravānnityaṃ yathaiṣṭāṃ prāpnuyādgatim samāptaṃ mānavaṃ dharmaśāstram
meaning: इत्येतन् मानवं शास्त्रं भृगुप्रोक्तं पठन् द्विजः भवत्याचारवान्नित्यं यथैष्टां प्राप्नुयाद्गतिम् समाप्तं मानवं धर्मशास्त्रम्
text: The twice-born man who reads these Ordinances of Manu, shall be ever equipped with virtue and shall attain whatever state he may desire.
iast: ityetan mānavaṃ śāstraṃ bhṛguproktaṃ paṭhan dvijaḥ bhavatyācāravānnityaṃ yathaiṣṭāṃ prāpnuyādgatim samāptaṃ mānavaṃ dharmaśāstram
